eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Ahin duma kɛrɛɛ­wam kɛrɛ ti Yesu Kristo nam busu wɔ, fiteeri Abraham kɔ tu Fɛmɛ Davidi fa ba juuri saŋga bo bu wuuri yiri Yesu ni. I nam n busu yaa ni.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abraham wuuri Izaki, na Izaki awu Jakɔbu, na Jakɔbu awu Yuda ni i niɛmam.

eng: Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Aram.
local: na Yuda awu Farɛsɛ ni Zara bo bu ni yo la Tama ni, na Farɛsɛ awu Hɛsarom, na Hɛsarom awu Aram.

eng: Aram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
local: na Aram awu Aminadabu, na Aminadabu awu Naasoni, na Naasoni awu Salɛmɔ.

eng: Salmon was the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
local: na Salɛmɔ awu Boosi bo i ni yo la Rahabu ni, na Boosi gusu awu Yobedi bo i ni yo la Ruti ni, na Yobedi gusu anya awu Yese.

eng: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been the wife of Uriah.
local: bo i gusu wuuri Fɛmɛ Davidi ni. Tɔ, na Fɛmɛ Davidi awu Fɛmɛ Solomon bo i ni yo la Yuriya yi ni.

eng: Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
local: na Fɛmɛ Solomon gusu awu Fɛmɛ Robowam, na Fɛmɛ Robowam awu Fɛmɛ Abiya, na Fɛmɛ Abiya awu Fɛmɛ Asafu.

eng: Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
local: na Fɛmɛ Asafu awu Fɛmɛ Yosafati, na Fɛmɛ Yosafati awu Fɛmɛ Yoram, na Fɛmɛ Yoram awu Fɛmɛ Yoziya.

eng: Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
local: na Fɛmɛ Yoziya awu Fɛmɛ Yowatam, na Fɛmɛ Yowatam awu Fɛmɛ Ahazi, na Fɛmɛ Ahazi awu Fɛmɛ Hɛzɛkiya.

eng: Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
local: na Fɛmɛ Hɛzɛkiya awu Fɛmɛ Manase, na Fɛmɛ Manase awu Fɛmɛ Amoni, na Fɛmɛ Amoni awu Fɛmɛ Yosiya.

eng: Josiah was the father of Jechoniah and his brothers. This was at the time of the exile to Babylon.
local: na Fɛmɛ Yosiya awu Fɛmɛ Yɛkoniya ni i niɛmam. Nyini saŋga n ye bu miɛɛri Yisareli­fɔm fiteeri bu asiɛn su kɔɔri Babilɔniya asiɛn su n a.

eng: After the exile to Babylon: Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
local: Bu faari bu kɔɔri Babilɔniya n dɛɛ n sin ni, na Fɛmɛ Yɛkoniya awu Salatiyeli, na Salatiyeli awu Zorobabɛli.

eng: Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
local: na Zorobabɛli awu Abiyidu, na Abiyidu awu Eliyakimu, na Eliyakimu awu Azore.

eng: Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
local: na Azore awu Sadoki, na Sadoki awu Akim, na Akim awu Eliyudu.

eng: Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
local: na Eliyudu awu Eleyaza, na Eleyaza awu Matan, na Matan awu Jakɔbu nyiŋga bekun.

eng: Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary—of whom Jesus was born, who is called the Christ.
local: na nyini Jakɔbu n awu Yosefu, Mariya hun. Mariya wuuri Yesu bo bu fere i ka Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: So there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David, fourteen generations from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen generations from the exile to Babylon to the Christ.
local: Tɔ, nyini kere ka fiteeri Abraham fa ba juuri Fɛmɛ Davidi, bu tiiri nanam buru ni nna wɔ, na fiteeri Fɛmɛ Davidi fa ba juuri saŋga bo bu miɛɛri Yisareli­fɔm kɔɔri Babilɔniya asiɛn n su ni, bu tiiri nanam buru ni nna wɔ, na fiteeri nyini saŋga n fa ba juuri saŋga bo bu wuuri Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni, bu gusu tiiri nanam buru ni nna wɔ.

eng: This is how the birth of Jesus Christ took place. When Mary his mother was engaged to Joseph, before they were married, she became pregnant by the Holy Spirit.
local: Kabo bu wuuri Yesu Kristo n yaa. Bu yo faari i ni Mariya ceeri Yosefu wɔ, ama, kana bu ba ja i kɔ i hun n dika ni, na ba fa nu ka i bita nyinsɛ bo i fite Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu wɔ.

eng: Joseph her husband was a righteous man. Because he didnt want to humiliate her, he decided to call off their engagement quietly.
local: I hun Yosefu yo ti sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, nyini ti, wɔ koro má ka i wura i yi n nyinsɛ jama nu, na wɔ fa sunsunni ka i yaki i asiri nu.

eng: As he was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, Joseph son of David, dont be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child she carries was conceived by the Holy Spirit.
local: Saŋga bo i wo birisi su n sɔ ni, na Micɛra n dɛɛ mɛrɛkɛ aba i dɔ alɛlɛ nu, aba ase i ka, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma Yosefu, má a soro sɛrɛ ka a ja Mariya, na a fa i kɔ u dika, dama Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni maari i nyinsɛ n a.

eng: She will give birth to a son, and you will call him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.
local: I wú baa­biɛsɔ na a fere i ka Yesu, i bu la ka Tii­de­fɔ, dama yiri bá yi i minɛm fite bu satiɛm nu a.

eng: Now all of this took place so that what the Lord had spoken through the prophet would be fulfilled.
local: Tɔ, nyini n kɛrɛ yoori sɔ, adena i ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n nɔaniɛ n bá yo yii­wa, kabo wɔ jɔjɔ ka.

eng: Look! A virgin will become pregnant and give birth to a son, And they will call him, Emmanuel.
local: Suŋguru bo wɔ nya si má biɛsɔ bá nyinsɛ, na i wú baa­biɛsɔ, na bu fére i ka Emanuwɛli , i bu la ka, Nyɛmɛ wo ya dɔ.

eng: When Joseph woke up, he did just as an angel from God commanded and took Mary as his wife.
local: Saŋga bo Yosefu teŋgeeri ni, na wɔ yo kabo Micɛra n dɛɛ mɛrɛkɛ n seeri i ni, na wɔ fa Mariya aba i dika.

eng: But he didnt have sexual relations with her until she gave birth to a son. Joseph called him Jesus.
local: Ama, i ni yiri asaŋga má bara ni biɛsɔ, haari Mariya ba wuuri baa­biɛsɔ, na Yosefu atun i duma ka Yesu.

eng: The Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus. In order to test him they asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm, ni Sadusi­fɔ nbem aba Yesu dɔ, na ba koro ka bu sɔ i ŋgɛɛ. Nyini ti, na ba se i ka i yo alekutura sa be bo i kere ka i yiko n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: But he replied, At evening you say, It will be nice weather because the sky is bright red.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Saŋga bo wiɛ nya ba sutɔ, na am ŋu ka aŋgoro n kɛrɛ ayo kɔkɔrɛ, a ti ka am ŋa, Ahunma dika ni bó, dama i ti kɔkɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: And in the morning you say, There will be bad weather today because the sky is cloudy. You know how to make sense of the skys appearance. But you are unable to recognize the signs that point to what the time is.
local: Nzɛn ŋgarama fuun, na am ŋu ka aŋgoro asaŋga kɔkɔrɛ ni biri, a ti ka am ŋa, Nyuma nzue tɔ́. Am koro niɛ aŋgoro n na am si sa bo i bá yo, ama, am koro si má sam bo bu suyo jina n nu n yiri bu.

eng: An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign. But it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign. Then he left them and went away.
local: Ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔ, satiɛ­yo­fɔm bo am su má Nyɛmɛ ni, ye am koro ka m yó alekutura sa be na am niɛ? Ai, m yó ma alekutura sa be na am ŋu i fieo, má ka alekutura sa n bo Nyɛmɛ yo yoori i maari Jona ŋgumi ni. I seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ yaki bu, na wɔ kɔ.

eng: When the disciples arrived on the other side of the lake, they had forgotten to bring bread.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu susu­fɔm n kpiɛ kɔ juuri kunma kpiri n bue kun dɔ ni, na bu wara afi diire sa, ba bita má kpɔnɔ nu.

eng: Jesus said to them, Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am si am ŋu sa ni Farasi­fɔm ni Sadusi­fɔm dɛɛ kpɔnɔ ayiri n bo bu fa gɔ kpɔnɔ nu na i yasu ni.

eng: They discussed this among themselves and said, We didnt bring any bread.
local: Na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ya bita má kpɔnɔ aba má n dɛɛ ti, ye i se sɔ n a.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, You people of weak faith! Why are you discussing among yourselves the fact that you dont have any bread?.
local: Na Yesu afa nu kabo i susu­fɔm n sujɔjɔ bu ŋuŋu nu ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ bo am yarada n ti kaan sɔ ni, nzu ti ye am sujɔjɔ am ŋuŋu nu ka am bita má kpɔnɔ a?.

eng: Dont you understand yet? Dont you remember the five loaves that fed the five thousand and how many baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Am nya ti má sa n bu, nzɛn sɛ? Am teŋge má su ka m yo faari kpɔnɔ nnu kpiɛkpiɛ maari minɛ akpii nnu? Ye bo i kaari ni, kandiɛ nyɛ ye am sasaari a?.

eng: And the seven loaves that fed the four thousand and how many large baskets of leftovers you gathered?.
local: Ye kpɔnɔ nso n bo m yo fa kpiɛkpiɛ maari minɛ akpii nna n di? Kandiɛ nyɛ ye am sasaari a?.

eng: Dont you know that I wasnt talking about bread? But be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Ye nzu ti ye am si má ka m sujɔjɔ má kpɔnɔ jɔrɛ a? M ŋa am si am ŋu sa ni Farasi­fɔm ni Sadusi­fɔm dɛɛ kpɔnɔ ayiri n bo bu fa gɔ kpɔnɔ nu na i yasu ni.

eng: Then they understood that he wasnt telling them to be on their guard for yeast used in making bread. No, he was telling them to watch out for the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
local: Na ba nya asi ka i suse má bu ka bu si bu ŋu sa ni Farasi­fɔm ni Sadusi­fɔm dɛɛ kpɔnɔ ayiri n wɔ, ama, i ŋa bu si bu ŋu sa fa kɔ bu keree­wa n dɛɛ atin su wɔ.

eng: Now when Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, Who do people say the Human One is.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu fa ba juuri Sizariya Filipiya dɛɛ miɛ kan kanm su ni, na wɔ bisa i susu­fɔm ka, Minɛm fa mini Adamande Wa n bu ŋma a.

eng: They replied, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others Jeremiah or one of the other prophets.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Nbem ŋa wɔrɔ la Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n a, nbem ŋa wɔrɔ la Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Eliya, nbem ŋa wɔrɔ la Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Jeremiya, ye nbem gusu ŋa a ti dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nyiŋgam n kun wɔ.

eng: He said, And what about you? Who do you say that I am.
local: Na wɔ nya abisa bu bekun ka, Ye ambɛrɛ fa m bu ŋma a.

eng: Simon Peter said, You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
local: Na Simon Petoro abɔ i nu ka, Wɔrɔ ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a, Nyɛmɛ n bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ Wa ni.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Happy are you, Simon son of Jonah, because no human has shown this to you. Rather my Father who is in heaven has shown you.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Simon Jona wa, tere­fiɛ wo u dɔ. Dama má adamande yiiri ahin jɔrɛ n bu kereeri u ka a jɔjɔ a, ama, m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni yi kereeri u a.

eng: I tell you that you are Peter. And Ill build my church on this rock. The gates of the underworld wont be able to stand against it.
local: Nyini ti, m suse u wɔ lɛɛ ka, wɔrɔ ti Petoro a, u duma bu la ka yabuɛ ni. Nyini yabuɛ n su ye m ma m jama minɛm kɛrɛ jina a, na yiwee mmɔɔ koro yo má bu pui.

eng: Ill give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Anything you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. Anything you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: M fá Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n anɔ kadombaam ma u, na deke kɛrɛ bo a ma minɛm atin ka bu yo i durunya n nu fɛn, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Na deke kɛrɛ bo a ma má minɛm atin ka bu yo i durunya n nu fɛn, i kaaki ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: Dont judge, so that you wont be judged.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Má am jɔjɔ be jɔrɛ na am jaraki i, adena má Nyɛmɛ gusu jɔjɔ ambɛrɛ jɔrɛ jaraki am.

eng: Youll receive the same judgment you give. Whatever you deal out will be dealt out to you.
local: Dama kabo am jɔjɔ am beŋgum jɔrɛ jaraki bu ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ gusu jɔjɔ am gusu jɔrɛ jaraki am a. Gɔŋgɔ bo am fa susu ma minɛm ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ gusu fá susu ma am a.

eng: Why do you see the splinter thats in your brothers or sisters eye, but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Nzu ti ye a niɛ ŋu wura mbaa n bo i wo u niwa nyu­mbaa nu, ye a niɛ ŋu má yeenzin n bo i wo u dɛɛ nyu­mbaa n nu a?.

eng: Then he ordered the disciples not to tell anybody that he was the Christ.
local: Nyini sin, na Yesu abɔbɔ i susu­fɔm ni ka má bu ma be si ka yiri ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the splinter out of your eye, when theres a log in your eye?.
local: A yo sɛ koro se u niwa ka, Ma m yi wura mbaa n bo i wo u nyu­mbaa nu ni, ye saŋga kɛrɛ yeenzin n wo u dɛɛ nyu­mbaa n nu a?.

eng: You deceive yourself! First take the log out of your eye, and then youll see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: Wɔrɔ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔ ni, du mɔ yi yeenzin n bo i wo u nyu­mbaa nu n ka, na a nyá ŋu asi nyunyumi na a yi wura mbaa n bo i wo u niwa n dɛɛ nyu­mbaa nu ni.

eng: From that time Jesus began to show his disciples that he had to go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and legal experts, and that he had to be killed and raised on the third day.
local: Fite nyini saŋga n fa kɔ, na Yesu abo i bu sukere i susu­fɔm n ka, Wɔ yo daka ka m kɔ Yɛrusalem na m kɔ di wahara dɔŋgu fite Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm dɔ na bu kún m. Ama, na i le­san cɛɛn, na Nyɛmɛ má m teŋge m yiwee n nu.

eng: At that time Herod the ruler heard the news about Jesus.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, na Fɛmɛ Herode ati Yesu ŋga.

eng: He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist. Hes been raised from the dead. This is why these miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Na wɔ se i nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm bo bu wo i nyunu n ka, Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n lɛɛ, ye wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu a, nyini ti ye i la yiko koro yo nyini alekutura sam kɛrɛ a.

eng: Then Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him: God forbid, Lord! This wont happen to you.
local: Na Petoro afa i akɔ ahɛmɛ, na wɔ jaraki i ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, má a se ka a se sɔ, Nyɛmɛ yo u kisi ni nyini sa ni. Nyini koro yo má cɛɛn be da.

eng: Dont give holy things to dogs, and dont throw your pearls in front of pigs. They will stomp on the pearls, then turn around and attack you.
local: Má am se ka am fa deke bo i ti casi fa ma caam, nzɛn am yoori sɔ, bu kpíe bu nyi fɔn tara am wɔ, na bu kán titi am nu. Sasu bekun, má am se ka am fa afere fofoem bo bu la gɔ agaya, fa tu afam. Má nyini, bu tíɛntiɛn i su wura fa nu wɔ.

eng: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Am sere deke bo am koro i Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na i má am. Am niɛniɛ deke bo am koro i dɔ, na am ŋú i. Am bobo anɔ ni, na i téke i ma am.

eng: But he turned to Peter and said, Get behind me, Satan. You are a stone that could make me stumble, for you are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Na Yesu acɛ aniɛ Petoro na wɔ tiɛn i ŋu ase i ka, Sitana, fa cinsi m bi bɛrɛ. A sukanda m atin wɔ. A sunsun má kabo Nyɛmɛ sunsun ni. A sunsun kabo adamande sunsun n wɔ.

eng: Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip.
local: Fɛmɛ Herode yo ma bu taraari Yohane nyiiri i wɔ, dama yiri Fɛmɛ Herode yo fiinri i gɔrɔ Filipu yi Herodiya jaari wɔ.

eng: For everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. And to everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sere Nyɛmɛ deke be, i má i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i niɛniɛ deke be, i ŋú i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bobo anɔ ni, i téke i ma i.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, All who want to come after me must sayno to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i su m su, wɔ yo daka ka i mi wara fi i tiɛŋgu sa koroo­wa sa, na i fa i yiwee baka jaanu n sɔ, na i su m su.

eng: Thats because John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry her.
local: Má pui ti, Yohane yo seeri Fɛmɛ Herode ka, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka a la má atin ka a fa u gɔrɔ yi ja i ŋgɔɔ su.

eng: Who among you will give your children a stone when they ask for bread?.
local: Ambɛrɛ nu, ŋma ye i wa sere i diire, ye i mi kéte na i fá yabuɛ ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.

eng: Although Herod wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd because they thought John was a prophet.
local: Má Fɛmɛ Herode yo koroori ka i kun Yohane wɔ, ama, i soroori Yuda­fɔm jama n wɔ, dama ba se ka Yohane ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i fa i ŋgɔɔ fiɛ, i mi bá fun i. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tɔɔri i ŋgɔɔ mini ni m labari kpa n dɛɛ ti, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Or give them a snake when they ask for fish?.
local: Wara, ambɛrɛ nu, ŋma ye i wa sere i jombaa ye i kéte na i fá woo ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.

eng: But at Herods birthday party Herodias daughter danced in front of the guests and thrilled Herod.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Herode baa­wu cɛɛn­kpiri di cɛɛn, na Herodiya wa suŋguru agɔ agɔrɛ fɛmɛ ni i jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, na fɛmɛ n ahore ajɔ.

eng: Then he swore to give her anything she asked.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ tan ndiɛ ase suŋguru n ka, Deke kɛrɛ bo a koro, se m, na m má u.

eng: At her mothers urging, the girl said, Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a plate.
local: Na suŋguru n ni ase i ka i kɔ se ka i koro Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n tii­awa a. Na wɔ kɔ ase fɛmɛ n ka, M koro ka na a fa Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n tii­awa ma m pɛrɛntɛ nu kisa kisa.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give good things to those who ask him.
local: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ, haari ni bo am ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, am si aceeri kpam bo am fá ce am nbaam, nzu ti ye nzɛn am sereeri am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni, i koro ma má am aceeri kpam a?.

eng: At that time Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry so they were picking heads of wheat and eating them.
local: Nyini saŋga n na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm ni sunati sin alɛ­mbaa fie be nu, Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni. Na ahɔɛ ati i susu­fɔm ni, na ba bubu be akpɛsɛ.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives? What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ nyaari durunya n nu deke n kɛrɛ, na i fuunri i ŋgɔɔ, i kpɛrɛ la bɔnɔ? Nzu deke ye sɔnɔ fá kaaki i ŋgɔɔ a?.

eng: For the Human One is about to come with the majesty of his Father with his angels. And then he will repay each one for what that person has done.
local: Dama mini Adamande Wa ni bá ba ni m Si Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa n nu, ni i mɛrɛkɛm ni, na m nyá tɔ sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kaari, kabo i juma, wara i sa­yoo­wa ti barasu.

eng: Therefore, you should treat people in the same way that you want people to treat you; this is the Law and the Prophets.
local: Nyini ti, kabo am koro ka minɛm yó ma am ni, am yo i sɔ ma bu, dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ lɛɛ, na i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ buka su.

eng: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, Look, your disciples are breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Saŋga bo Farasi­fɔm ni ŋuuri nyini sɔ ni, na ba se Yesu ka, Niɛ fɛn. Kabo u susu­fɔm ni suyo ni, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka má be yo sɔ, Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests he commanded that they give it to her.
local: Fɛmɛ n tiiri sɔ ni, na i ahore asaaki, ama, ndiɛ n bo i taanri i jama n nyunu n dɛɛ ti, wɔ koro akaaki má i nɔɔ. Na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu yo suŋguru n koroo­wa ma i.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see the Human One coming in his kingdom.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, am nbem wo fɛn bo am wu má, má ka am aba aŋu ka mini Adamande Wa ni suba ka m bá niɛ m fɛmɛya n su.

eng: Go in through the narrow gate. The gate that leads to destruction is broad and the road wide, so many people enter through it.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Am fa anɔ n bo i ti fii n na am wura. Dama atin n bo i fa minɛm kɔ saakii­biri ni, i anɔ n ti tɛtɛrɛ wɔ, na i natiiri ti má kekere, nyini dɛɛ ti, minɛm dɔŋgu fa yiri wɔ.

eng: But he said to them, Havent you read what David did when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am nya kan má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu, kabo Fɛmɛ Davidi yo yoori saŋga bo ahɔɛ tiiri i ni i minɛm na ba si má kabo bu yo ni, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: Then he had John beheaded in prison.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ ma bu akɔ akpiɛ Yohane tii dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu dɔ.

eng: They brought his head on a plate and gave it to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
local: Na ba fa i tii­awa n ada pɛrɛntɛ n nu, aba ama suŋguru ni, na yiri gusu afa akɔ ama i ni.

eng: But the gate that leads to life is narrow and the road difficult, so few people find it.
local: Ama, atin n bo i fa minɛm kɔ ŋgɔɔ woo­biri ni, i anɔ n ti fii wɔ, na i atin gusu nati ti kekereke, nyini dɛɛ ti, minɛm bo bu ŋu nyini atin n na bu su i su ni, bu sunnu má.

eng: Watch out for false prophets. They come to you dressed like sheep, but inside they are vicious wolves.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Am si am ŋu sa ni kɔrɛ­fɔm bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni. Bu yo bu ŋu bɛnlɛ ka nbɔɛm wɔ, ama, bu ti ka bedim wɔ, na bu ti tiɛ.

eng: You will know them by their fruit. Do people get bunches of grapes from thorny weeds, or do they get figs from thistles?.
local: Bu sa­yoo­wa n nu, ye am koro fa si kabo bu ti barasu a. Am aŋu ka buwe asu koto? Wara, am aŋu ka bedikantoŋga asu ndɔrɔwa? Ai.

eng: He went into Gods house and broke the law by eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat.
local: Ahɔɛ n ti, na wɔ kɔ awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ di kpɔnɔ n bo ba fa ama Nyɛmɛ ni, na wɔ ma i minɛm ni be. Ama, am si ka ya dɛɛ atin n su, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ŋgumi ti ye ni ka bu di nyini kpɔnɔ n a.

eng: But Johns disciples came and took his body and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus what had happened.
local: Na Yohane susu­fɔm n aba afa fuin n akɔ ase i, na ba kɔ ama Yesu labari.

eng: When Jesus heard about John, he withdrew in a boat to a deserted place by himself. When the crowds learned this, they followed him on foot from the cities.
local: Yesu tiiri Yohane n jɔrɛ labari n sɔ ni, na wɔ fa lee akɔ deke bo minɛ n wo má, i ni i susu­fɔm ŋgumi. Minɛm tiiri sɔ ni, na ba fite miɛ miɛm su anati bu jaa su, asa i su.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion for them and healed those who were sick.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu kɔ fiteeri lee n nu ni, na wɔ ŋu jama beberebe, na wɔ si bu aŋunfɔ, na wɔ ma tukpaki­fɔm bo bu woori dɔ n anya laifiɛ.

eng: In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit, and every rotten tree produces bad fruit.
local: Ama, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ka baka kpa kɛrɛ su suu­wa kpa a, na baka ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ su suu­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Or havent you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple treat the Sabbath as any other day and are still innocent?.
local: Sasu bekun, am nya kan má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu, kabo Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ni saaki Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ciire ni, na bu yo bu yoo­wam Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na be se má bu pui, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: That evening his disciples came and said to him, This is an isolated place and its getting late. Send the crowds away so they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n naasɔ saŋga, na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, aba ase i ka, Kisa wiɛ atɔ, ya wo boro gusu wɔ lɛɛ. Ma minɛm n atin na bu kɔ miɛ kunnu, na bu kɔ nya diire be to di.

eng: A good tree cant produce bad fruit. And a rotten tree cant produce good fruit.
local: Baka kpa koro su má suu­wa ŋgbɛɛn, na baka ŋgbɛɛn gusu koro su má suu­wa kpa.

eng: Every tree that doesnt produce good fruit is chopped down and thrown into the fire.
local: Baka kɛrɛ bo i su má suu­wa kpa, bu kpiɛ i wɔ, na bu fa ji sin nu.

eng: Therefore, you will know them by their fruit.
local: Nyini ti, bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, bu sa­yoo­wa n nu, ye am koro fa si kabo bu ti barasu a.

eng: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, deke bo i tara Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n wo fɛn.

eng: But Jesus said to them, Theres no need to send them away. You give them something to eat.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, I ti má deke bo bu kɔ pui, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii nya deke be ma bu na bu di.

eng: They replied, We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Na ba se i ka, Deke bo ya la i fɛn n kɛrɛ ti kpɔnɔ nnu wɔ, ni jombaa nnyɔ.

eng: If you had known what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice , you wouldnt have condemned the innocent.
local: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, M koro aŋunfɔ si tara bo am yo yoo­wa ma m. Nzɛn má am si nyini nɔaniɛ n bu, má am ma má bu di minɛm jɔrɛ cin bu sui, bɛrɛ bo bu la má ŋgasi be ni.

eng: Not everybody who says to me, Lord, Lord, will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do the will of my Father who is in heaven will enter.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bo fere m ka, M Mibiɛ, m Mibiɛ, ni wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu a, se má ka bo bu yo m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n dɛɛ koroo­wa ni.

eng: He said, Bring them here to me.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am fa bu ba ma m fɛn.

eng: The Human One is Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Dama mini Adamande Wa ni ti Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n Mibiɛ n a.

eng: On the Judgment Day, many people will say to me, Lord, Lord, didnt we prophesy in your name and expel demons in your name and do lots of miracles in your name.
local: Jina cɛɛn n ba juuri, minɛ dɔŋgu bá se m ka, Ya Mibiɛ, niɛ e. Ya yo faari u dɛɛ duma, na ya fa akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, na ya fa u duma n afɔn ayi ajinim afite minɛm ŋu, na ya kaaki afa u duma n ayo alekutura sam dɔŋgu.

eng: He ordered the crowds to sit down on the grass. He took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them and broke the loaves apart and gave them to his disciples. Then the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Na wɔ ma minɛm nɔɔ ka, bu tantana wura fiɛfiɛɛ­wa n su. Na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ nnu ni, ni jombaa nnyɔ ni, na wɔ yɛ i tii su aŋgoro na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi. Na wɔ nya akpiɛkpiɛ kpɔnɔ n nu afa ama i susu­fɔm ni, na bɛrɛ gusu afa ama minɛm ni.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into their synagogue.
local: Na Yesu ayasu dɔ akɔ awura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu.

eng: A man with a withered hand was there. Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they asked, Does the Law allow a person to heal on the Sabbath.
local: Sɔnɔ asaa fuin be woori dɔ. Na minɛ nbem akoro ka bu sɔ́ Yesu ŋgɛɛ na bu járaki i ka na wɔ saaki Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ciire ni. Dɔ na ba bisa i ka, Atin wo nu ka ya koro ma sɔnɔ nya laifiɛ, Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and they filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ adi, na bu kunnu ayi. Na i susu­fɔm n atambu kpɔnɔ ni jombaa sinsin n bo bu diiri na i kaari ni, na ba nya kandiɛ buru ni nnyɔ.

eng: Then Ill tell them, Ive never known you. Get away from me, you people who do wrong.
local: Na m nya se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, am fa cinsi m bi bɛrɛ. M si má am cɛɛn n be cɛɛn n be da.

eng: Everybody who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise builder who built a house on bedrock.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tiiri m dɛɛ nɔaniɛm n na i deeri i na i fa yoori juma, i mi ti ka alecira­fɔ be bo i taari i awuru na i seeri i jaa n yabuɛ su n wɔ.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It didnt fall because it was firmly set on bedrock.
local: Na nzue aba atɔ, na wawa n kɛrɛ ayi asandi, na aŋuma akpisi awuru ni, ama, wɔ tɔ má, dama i jaa jina yabuɛ su n dɛɛ ti.

eng: About five thousand men plus women and children had eaten.
local: Minɛm bo bu diiri kpɔnɔ n ni jombaa n kɛrɛ kɔ yasuuri nbiɛsɔm akpii nnu wɔ, ba kan má nbaram ni nbaatumam awura má nu.

eng: Jesus replied, Who among you has a sheep that falls into a pit on the Sabbath and will not take hold of it and pull it out?.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am nu, ŋma ye i bɔɛ ti kun n kpein ye i toori kunma ŋgoŋgo nu Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ye i mi wúra yi má i a?.

eng: How much more valuable is a person than a sheep! So the Law allows a person to do what is good on the Sabbath.
local: Adamande tara bɔɛ fieo. Nyini ti, ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka ya yo sakpa Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni.

eng: Right then, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake while he dismissed the crowds.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu ama i susu­fɔm n awura lee n nu na bu du i mɔ kpiɛ kɔ kunma kpiri n buekun, na yiri aka na i kaari jama ni.

eng: When he sent them away, he went up onto a mountain by himself to pray. Evening came and he was alone.
local: I kaariiri jama n wieeri ni, na wɔ fun akɔ kpɛrɛ be su i ŋgumi ka i kɔ sere Nyɛmɛ. Haari wiɛ ba tɔɔri kɛrɛ, i toko wo dɔ i ŋgumi.

eng: Then Jesus said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and it was made healthy, just like the other one.
local: Na wɔ nya ase sɔnɔ n ka, Teŋge u saa ni. Na wɔ teŋge i saa ni, na i saa n ayo faŋga bekun ka nyiŋga ni.

eng: Meanwhile, the boat, fighting a strong headwind, was being battered by the waves and was already far away from land.
local: Nyini saŋga na lee n yiri wo nzue n afiɛn dede dɔ, na nzue nyama sukpara i, dama aŋuma sun bu kɔ bu sin wɔ.

eng: But everybody who hears these words of mine and doesnt put them into practice will be like a fool who built a house on sand.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tiiri m dɛɛ nɔaniɛm n na wɔ de má i, afa ayo má juma, i mi ti ka sinnzin be bo i taari i awuru ŋmiɛ nu n wɔ.

eng: The Pharisees went out and met in order to find a way to destroy Jesus.
local: Dɔ na, Farasi­fɔm n afite akɔ asɛsɛ bu ŋu su, kabo bu yó na bu kun Yesu.

eng: Jesus knew what they intended to do, so he went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu siiri nu sɔ ni, na wɔ yasu bɛrɛ. Na jama beberebe asu i su, na wɔ ma bu tukpaki­fɔm kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

eng: But he ordered them not to spread the word about him.
local: Na wɔ bɔbɔ bu ka, má bu ma be si i sɔnɔ nzuin nu.

eng: Very early in the morning he came to his disciples, walking on the lake.
local: Na fa kɔ akɔnyuma ciciri su, na wɔ nati nzue n su suba bu dɔ.

eng: The rain fell, the floods came, and the wind blew and beat against that house. It fell and was completely destroyed.
local: Na nzue aba atɔ, na wawa n kɛrɛ ayi asandi, na aŋuma akpisi awuru n na i kɛrɛ abereki atɔ liim. Nyini awuru n tɔɔ­wa n tiiri tiɛ haari.

eng: When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ba jɔjɔɔri sɔ wieeri n na jama n ayo alekutura fa kɔ i keree­wa n su.

eng: because he was teaching them like someone with authority and not like their legal experts.
local: dama i keree­wa n awo i ŋgumi ni Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni. I keree­wa n nu la yiko.

eng: so that what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled.
local: I yoori sɔ, adena kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo jɔjɔɔri n yo yii­wa a, i ŋa.

eng: When the disciples saw him walking on the lake, they were terrified and said, Its a ghost! They were so frightened they screamed.
local: Saŋga bo bu ŋuuri ka i nati nzue n su ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu agaya, bu ŋa, Ŋumi lɛɛ o. Na ba bo awie, sɛrɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Just then Jesus spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Kpeŋga na Yesu ajɔjɔ bu dɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, Am ma am ahore si, mini a, má am soro sɛrɛ.

eng: Peter replied, Lord, if its you, order me to come to you on the water.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, nzɛn i ti wɔrɔ nahɔrɛ su a, se m ka m nati nzue n su bra u dɔ.

eng: Look, my Servant whom I chose, the one I love, in whom I find great pleasure . Ill put my Spirit upon him, and hell announce judgment to the Gentiles.
local: Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, M amusui­su­fɔ bo ma yi i ka i yo m sa koroo­wa n yaa. Yiri ye m koro i a, m ahore ajɔ i ŋu. M má m Awiɛwiɛ n ba wura i ŋu. Na i kɔ́ kan kere durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ kabo m nahɔrɛ atin n ti barasu.

eng: He wont argue or shout, and nobody will hear his voice in the streets.
local: I ni be sé má akuruwa, wara i tíɛntiɛn má. I káaki yo má naŋmiɛ atiŋgberem su.

eng: And Jesus said, Come. Then Peter got out of the boat and was walking on the water toward Jesus.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Bra. Na Petoro afite lee n nu sunati nzue n su sukɔ Yesu dɔ.

eng: But when Peter saw the strong wind, he became frightened. As he began to sink, he shouted, Lord, rescue me.
local: Ama, saŋga bo i ŋuuri ka aŋuma ni sukpiɛ agaya sɔ ni, na sɛrɛ akaaki ati i, kpeŋga i suwura nzue n bu. Na wɔ bo awie ase ka, M Mibiɛ, de m tii ji.

eng: He wont break a bent stalk, and he wont snuff out a smoldering wick, until he makes justice win.
local: I búbu má janja wura bo wɔ biri, na i nɔ́ má fitana bo i je má nyunyumi. I wó bɛrɛ sɔ haari na m nahɔrɛ atin n bá kunma deke n kɛrɛ.

eng: And the Gentiles will put their hope in his name.
local: Na durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ bá ti i duma ni, na bu yó i tama.

eng: They brought to Jesus a demon-possessed man who was blind and unable to speak. Jesus healed him so that he could both speak and see.
local: Nyini sin na ba fa sɔnɔ be aba Yesu dɔ, ajini ayo i anyinsin ni bobo wɔ. Na Yesu ama wɔ nya laifiɛ, na wɔ koro ajɔjɔ, na wɔ ŋu asi.

eng: Jesus immediately reached out and grabbed him, saying, You man of weak faith! Why did you begin to have doubts.
local: Kpeŋga na Yesu alaandi i saa aso i nu, na wɔ se i ka, Wɔrɔ, u yarada n ti kaan sɔ. Nzu ti ye u sunsunni ti nnyɔ nnyɔ a.

eng: All the crowds were amazed and said, This man couldnt be the Son of David, could he.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ ayo alekutura, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma n lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ a.

eng: When the Pharisees heard, they said, This man throws out demons only by the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.
local: Ama, Farasi­fɔm tiiri sɔ n na ba se ka, Bɛlazɛbuli bo i ti ajinim kɛrɛ dɛɛ fɛmɛ ni, yiri ŋgumi ma ahin sɔnɔ n yiko dɛɛ ti, ye i koro fɔn yi ajinim n a.

eng: When they got into the boat, the wind settled down.
local: Na bu nnyɔ n kɛrɛ awura lee n nu, na aŋuma n atara.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he replied, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland. Every city or house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Yesu siiri bu sunsunni n sɔ n na wɔ se bu ka, Fɛmɛya kɛrɛ bo i nya kpakpaakiiri nu wieeri, nyini fɛmɛya ni ká ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. Awuru, wara miɛ kɛrɛ gusu bo i nya kpakpaakiiri nu wieeri, nyini awuru, wara miɛ ni sándi wɔ.

eng: If Satan throws out Satan, he is at war with himself. How then can his kingdom endure?.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn má Sitana bo i ti ajinim kɛrɛ fɛmɛ n nya fɔn i ajinim fite minɛm ŋu, má nyini kere ka ba kpakpaaki nu lɛɛ. Ye i yó sɛ ye i fɛmɛya ni kóro jina a?.

eng: And if I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Tɔ, ye nzɛn am ŋa Bɛlazɛbuli maari m yiko, ye m fa fɔn yi ajinim a, ye ŋma maari am dɛɛ minɛm ni yiko ye bu bita fɔn yi bu n a? Nyini ti, kabo am minɛm yoori ni, nyini kere ka am la má nahɔrɛ.

eng: Then those in the boat worshipped Jesus and said, You must be Gods Son.
local: Na i susu­fɔm bo bu wo lee n nu n kɛrɛ akansi i, bu ŋa, A ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a, nahɔrɛ su.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the power of Gods Spirit, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni ma m yiko ye m fa fɔn yi ajinim a. Nyini n kere ka Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ wo am nu suniɛ i minɛm su lɛɛ.

eng: Can people go into a house that belongs to a strong man and steal his possessions, unless they first tie up the strong man? Then they can rob his house.
local: Bekun, i bá yo sɛ ye be koro wura sɔnɔ faŋga­fɔ dika na i fin i neŋge a, má ka wɔ tara i faŋga­fɔ acin i nyama ka? Nyini sin na i nyá koro fin i neŋge bo i wo awuru n nu.

eng: When they had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret.
local: Na ba kpiɛ nzue n aba aju awɔrɛ n bo bu fere i ka Gɛnɛsarɛti ni.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me scatters.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jina má m sin, i mi ti m kpɔ­fɔ ɔ. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo i buka má m na ya tiɛn minɛm nɔɔ, i mi sandi minɛm wɔ.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that people will be forgiven for every sin and insult to God. But insulting the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Nyini ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, Nyɛmɛ koro fa minɛm satiɛ kɛrɛ fa ce bu, haari fa kɔ saŋga ni jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ bo bu jɔjɔɔri i fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ wuraari Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ŋgasi nu, Nyɛmɛ fá má i mi ŋgasi ce má i.

eng: When the people who lived in that place recognized him, they sent word throughout that whole region, and they brought to him everyone who was sick.
local: Na dɔ­fɔm asi ka Yesu lɛɛ, na ba sunma akɔ ase awɔrɛ n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ, na ba fa tukpaki­fɔm kɛrɛ aba Yesu dɔ.

eng: And whoever speaks a word against the Human One will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven, not in this age or in the age that is coming.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ wuraari mini Adamande Wa ni ŋgasi nu, Nyɛmɛ fá i mi ŋgasi ce i. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ wuraari Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ŋgasi nu, Nyɛmɛ fá má i mi ŋgasi ce má i, fite kisa haari fa kɔ fa ba.

eng: Then they begged him that they might just touch the edge of his clothes. Everyone who touched him was cured.
local: na ba sere i jande ka i ma bu fa bu saa kan i tanlɛɛ n jaa bu. Na bo bu yoori sɔ n kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

eng: Either consider the tree good and its fruit good, or consider the tree rotten and its fruit rotten. A tree is known by its fruit.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Baka kɛrɛ bo i nya ti kpa, su suu­wa kpa a, na baka kɛrɛ gusu bo i nya ti má kpa, su suu­wa tiɛtiɛ wɔ. Dama baka kɛrɛ suu­wa, ye a fa si kabo i ti a.

eng: Children of snakes! How can you speak good things while you are evil? What fills the heart comes out of the mouth.
local: Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ka woom ni, kabo am ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm sɔ ni, am yo sɛ koro yo sakpa bekun a? Dama dekem n bo am sunsun bu am ahore su n ye am jɔjɔ bu a.

eng: Good people bring out good things from their good treasure. But evil people bring out evil things from their evil treasure.
local: Sɔnɔ kpa ni i deke kpa kɛrɛ bo i bita i, fa sakpa ba a. Na sɔnɔ tiɛ gusu ni i deke tiɛ bo i bita i, fa satiɛ ba a.

eng: I tell you that people will have to answer on Judgment Day for every useless word they speak.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, jina cɛɛn, Nyɛmɛ dí sɔnɔ kɛrɛ jɔrɛ fa kɔ jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ bo i yo jɔjɔɔri i.

eng: By your words you will be either judged innocent or condemned as guilty.
local: Dama u nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa má a fite nu a, na u nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa gusu má bu di u jɔrɛ cin u sui a.

eng: At that time some of the legal experts and the Pharisees requested of Jesus, Teacher, we would like to see a sign from you.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm nbem ase Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ya koro ka a yo alekutura sa be kere ya na ya ŋu i.

eng: But he replied, An evil and unfaithful generation searches for a sign, but it wont receive any sign except Jonahs sign.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔ, satiɛ­yo­fɔm bo am su má Nyɛmɛ ni, ye am koro ka m yó alekutura sa be na am niɛ a? Ai, m yó má alekutura sa be na am ŋu i fieo, má ka alekutura sa n bo Nyɛmɛ yo yoori i maari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Jona ni ŋgumi.

eng: Just as Jonah was in the whales belly for three days and three nights , so the Human One will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.
local: Dama kabo Jona yo woori jombaa bambaka be kunnu kɔ juuri le­san, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu ni, sɔ ye mini Adamande Wa ni bá wo asiɛn n bu kɔ ju le­san, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu a.

eng: The citizens of Nineveh will stand up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it as guilty, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching. And look, someone greater than Jonah is here.
local: Jona yo kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kereeri Ninɛva­fɔm na ba kaaki bu wo. Jina cɛɛn, bɛrɛ bá yasu jina na bu járaki ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm, dama kisa, m suse am wɔ ka, mini tara Jona, ama, am akete ka am kaaki má am wo.

eng: The queen of the South will be raised up by God at the judgment with this generation and condemn it because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Fɛmɛ bara be yo fiteeri haari i bɔsu Seba wɔ, na i bá ti Fɛmɛ Solomon dɛɛ alecira jɔjɔɔ­wa nɔaniɛ ni. Jina cɛɛn, yiri bá yasu jina na i járaki ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm nahɔrɛ su, dama kisa, m suse am wɔ ka, mini tara Fɛmɛ Solomon, ama, am ade má m nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Nzɛn ajini nya fite yakiiri sɔnɔ, i kɔ jeŋgbe bara yɛ boro kpantain nu wɔ, deke bo nzue wo má, na i kɔ niɛniɛ woo­biri bo i tana yi i ŋumi. Nzɛn wɔ nya má woo­biri.

eng: Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left. When it arrives, it finds the place vacant, cleaned up, and decorated.
local: nyini saŋga, a ti ka i ŋa, Ma m kpie m nyi kɔ m woo­biri dawa ni. Na i kaaki kɔ, i kɔ ŋu ka ba kpara dika n nyunyumi, asɛsɛ deke kɛrɛ su, be wo má nu.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning. This is the way it will be also for this evil generation.
local: Na i nyá kɔ fa ajinim nso, bo bu ti tiɛ tara yiri ni i tii, na bu kɛrɛ bá wura woo­biri dawa n nu. Nyini saŋga, a ŋu ka sɔnɔ n woo­wa ayo tiɛ atara kabo i yo ti dawa ni. Sɔ ye i bá yo ma ahin nyuma­fɔm bo bu satiɛ­yo asunnu n a.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside trying to speak with him.
local: Yesu toko wo minɛm dɔ jɔjɔ su ni, na i ni, ni i niɛmam aba aka ajina dasin, na ba se ka bu koro ka bu jɔjɔ i dɔ ɔ.

eng: Someone said to him, Look, your mother and brothers are outside wanting to speak with you.
local: Na minɛm ni kun ase i ka, Ti fɛn, u ni, ni u niɛmam jina dasin koro ka bu jɔjɔ u dɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Sɔnɔ n seeri i sɔ wieeri ni, na Yesu ase i ka, Ma m kere u bo bu ti m ni, ni m niɛmam.

eng: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples and said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Na wɔ fa i saa akere i susu­fɔm ni, na wɔ se ka, Niɛ fɛn. M ni, ni m niɛmam n yaa.

eng: Whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n dɛɛ sa koroo­wa, yiri ti m siwa, ni m niwa, ni m ni a.

eng: Then the Spirit led Jesus up into the wilderness so that the devil might tempt him.
local: Nyini sin na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa Yesu akɔ boro kpantain n nu ka Sitana kɔ yo i na i niɛ, ka i yó yiri Sitana n dɛɛ koroo­wa nzɛn i yó má.

eng: After Jesus had fasted for forty days and forty nights, he was starving.
local: Dɔ na Yesu acici i nɔɔ haari cɛɛn abura­na, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu bo i di má pui, na ahɔɛ aba ati i.

eng: The tempter came to him and said, Since you are Gods Son, command these stones to become bread.
local: Na Sitana bo bu kaaki fere i ka Ŋgɛɛ­sɔ­fɔ n aba ase i ka, Nzɛn a ti Nyɛmɛ Wa, se ahin yabuɛm n na bu kaaki diire.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread, but by every word spoken by God .
local: Ama, na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Má diire ŋgumi ma sɔnɔ ŋgɔɔ a, ama, i de nɔaniɛ kɛrɛ bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ n gusu.

eng: After that the devil brought him into the holy city and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him.
local: Na Sitana n akaaki afa Yesu akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na wɔ kɔ afa i ajina Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n tii su lenleŋge, na wɔ se i ka.

eng: Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down; for it is written, I will command my angels concerning you, and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Nzɛn a ti Nyɛmɛ Wa, ti tɔ asiɛŋgu na m niɛ. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ má i mɛrɛkɛm nɔɔ na bu niɛ u dika. Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i ka, Bu fá bu saa so u nu, na má u jaa bo yabuɛ be.

eng: Jesus replied, Again its written, Dont test the Lord your God .
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu bekun ka, Má a yo Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n na a niɛ kabo i yiko ti barasu.

eng: Then the devil brought him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
local: Nyini sin na Sitana n afa i akɔ buka be tii su lenleŋge, na wɔ kere i durunya n nu asiɛn kɛrɛ, ni kabo bu ti na bu yo nyumi barasu.

eng: He said, Ill give you all these if you bow down and worship me.
local: Na wɔ se i ka, Nzɛn a kutu suuri m, m fá nyinim n kɛrɛ ma u.

eng: Jesus responded, Go away, Satan, because its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him .
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Wɔrɔ Sitana, fa cinsi m bi bɛrɛ. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Kutu Micɛra u Nyɛmɛ ni, na a su yiri ŋgumi.

eng: The devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
local: Na Sitana n akɔ ayaki i, na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm aba i dɔ na ba ba asu i.

eng: Now when Jesus heard that John was arrested, he went to Galilee.
local: Na saŋga bo Yesu ba tiiri ka ba tara Yohane anyi i dan­saraka ni, na wɔ fite bɛrɛ akɔ Galili awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: He left Nazareth and settled in Capernaum, which lies alongside the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
local: I yasuuri Nazarɛti miɛ n nu ni, na wɔ kɔ awo Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n bo i wo dodo Galili kunma kpiri ni, na i wo Zabulon­fɔm ni Nafatali­fɔm dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu ni.

eng: This fulfilled what Isaiah the prophet said.
local: I kɔ tanaari dɔ ni, adena kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo jɔjɔɔri n yo yii­wa. I ŋa.

eng: Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, alongside the sea, across the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles.
local: Zabulon ni Nafatali­fɔm, am so am sui, ambɛrɛ bo am wo dodo kunma kpiri n bo i wo Yodan kunma n wiɛ afiiri dɛɛ bue ni, ambɛrɛ bo am wo Galili awɔrɛ n ni, deke bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm wo ni, am so am sui.

eng: the people who lived in the dark have seen a great light, and a light has come upon those who lived in the region and in shadow of death.
local: Minɛm bo bu wo awosin nu ni, ba ŋu wein bambaka ni. Na bo bu wo awosin ni yiwee nu ni, bɛrɛ ye sin ni jé ta wein ma bu a.

eng: From that time Jesus began to announce, Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Fite nyini saŋga n na Yesu abo i bu sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, i ŋa, Am kaaki am wo, dama saŋga n aju bo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su.

eng: As Jesus walked alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, because they were fishermen.
local: Na saŋga bo Yesu sunati Galili kunma kpiri n nɔɔ su ni, na wɔ ŋu minɛ nnyɔ nbem, bu ti sɔnɔ kun nbaam wɔ. Bɛrɛ la Simon bo bu fere i ka Petoro ni, ni i niɛma Andere. Bu wo bu diɛndiɛ sandi su kunma kpiri n nu, dama bu ti jombaa tara­fɔm wɔ.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am bra na am ba su m su na m má am tiɛn minɛm nɔɔ ma m, kabo am tara jombaam ni.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na ba kaba ayaki bu diɛndiɛm ni, na ba su i su.

eng: Continuing on, he saw another set of brothers, James the son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with Zebedee their father repairing their nets. Jesus called them and.
local: I nati kɔɔri i nyunu kaan, na wɔ ŋu minɛ nnyɔ nbem bekun, bu ni bu si, bu fere i ka Zɛbɛdi, bɛrɛ la Yakobo ni i niɛma Yohane. Bu ni bu si n wo lee nu suyo bu diɛndiɛm dɔŋgu, na Yesu afere bu.

eng: immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
local: Dɔ na ba kaba afite lee n nu ayaki bu si ni, na ba su i su.

eng: Jesus traveled throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues. He announced the good news of the kingdom and healed every disease and sickness among the people.
local: Na Yesu abara ayɛ Galili awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, na i sukere minɛm bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, deke bo bu sere Nyɛmɛ na bu kere minɛm Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ nu ni, na i sukan labari kpa n fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n su, na wɔ ma minɛm bo bu la tukpaki barasu barasu n kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

eng: News about him spread throughout Syria. People brought to him all those who had various kinds of diseases, those in pain, those possessed by demons, those with epilepsy, and those who were paralyzed, and he healed them.
local: Na i duma ata abara ayɛ Siriya awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, na ba sɔ minɛm bo bu la tukpaki barasu barasu kɛrɛ, ni bo bu di yaari agaya bu ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu, aba i dɔ ka i ma bu nya laifiɛ. Ajinim wo bu nbem ŋu, na nbem tɔ ŋgbiti, na nbem gusu saam ni bu jaam awu, na wɔ ma bu kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

eng: Large crowds followed him from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and from the areas beyond the Jordan River.
local: Na jama beberebe afite Galili awɔrɛ n nu, ni Miɛ Burum dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, ni Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, ni Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, ni Yodan kunma n sin dɔ, na bu kɛrɛ aba asu i su.

eng: Now when Jesus had come down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu juraari buka n su ba juuri n na jama beberebe asu i su.

eng: A man with a skin disease came, kneeled before him, and said, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Na koko­fɔ be aba i dɔ, na wɔ ba akutu i nyunu asere i ka i buka i, i ŋa, M Mibiɛ, nzɛn a koro ka a ma m nya laifiɛ, a koro ma m nya i.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand and touched him, saying, I do want to. Become clean. Instantly his skin disease was cleansed.
local: Na Yesu ateŋge i saa anana i ŋu, na wɔ se i ka, M koro. Nya laifiɛ. Na kokobi n akaba ayaki sɔnɔ n na wɔ nya laifiɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Dɔ na Yesu ase i ka, Má a se ka a se be ahin jɔrɛ ni, ama, kɔ na a kɔ fa u ŋu kere Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ ni, na a kɔ yo yoo­wa n bo Moyisi yo kereeri dawa­fɔm ni, dama nyini kere minɛm ka a nya u ŋu a.

eng: When Jesus went to Capernaum, a centurion approached.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu kɔ wuraari Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na Roma­fɔm soja kpiɛn be aba asere Yesu jande ka i buka i.

eng: pleading with him, Lord, my servant is flat on his back at home, paralyzed, and his suffering is awful.
local: i ŋa, M Mibiɛ, m gbɛfɛnɛ da awuru, i saa ni i jaa kɛrɛ awu, i sudi wahara agaya.

eng: Jesus responded, Ill come and heal him.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, M bá na m ma i nya laifiɛ.

eng: But the centurion replied, Lord, I dont deserve to have you come under my roof. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Na soja kpiɛn n ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, m ti kaan ni be a ba m dika. Ama, haari a ma nɔaniɛ n mmɔɔ, m gbɛfɛnɛ ni nyá laifiɛ.

eng: Im a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Dama mini mmɔɔ ni m tii n wo sɔnɔ saa nu wɔ, na mini gusu nya la m dɛɛ sojam bo m niɛ bu su. Na bo m nya se i ka, Kɔ fɛn, na i kɔ, na m se nyiŋga gusu ka, Bra fɛn, na i ba. Na m se m akɔɔ n gusu ka, Yo ahin, na i yo i.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he was impressed and said to the people following him, I say to you with all seriousness that even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu tiiri sɔ ni, na wɔ yo alekutura, na wɔ se minɛm bo bu su i su n ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, ma nya aŋu má sɔnɔ bo i la yarada agaya ka ahin sɔnɔ n ŋusu, haari fa saŋga ni Yisareli­fɔm mmɔɔ.

eng: I say to you that there are many who will come from east and west and sit down to eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, minɛm beberebe fíte durunya n wiɛ afiiri, ni wiɛ atɔɔri kɛrɛ, na bɛrɛ, ni Abraham, ni Izaki, ni Jakɔbu bá saŋga tana Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, na bu dí na bu nú.

eng: But the children of the kingdom will be thrown outside into the darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ama, na minɛm n bo má i ti ye ni ka bɛrɛ wo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu ni, na Nyɛmɛ fa bu tu awosin nu. Dɔ na bu sún, na bu kán bu saa.

eng: Jesus said to the centurion, Go; it will be done for you just as you have believed. And his servant was healed that very moment.
local: Na Yesu anya ase soja kpiɛn n ka, Kɔ awuru. Kabo a yo yarada ni, i yó sɔ ma u a. Na soja kpiɛn n gbɛfɛnɛ n anya laifiɛ, nyini saŋga n bo Yesu fa jɔjɔɔri ni.

eng: Jesus went home with Peter and saw Peters mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu wuraari Petoro dika n na wɔ ŋu ka Petoro sibara da sɔrɔ, i la má i ŋu, yakɔkɔ abo i.

eng: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served them.
local: Na Yesu aso i saa nu, na yakɔkɔ n ayaki i, na bara n ayasu, na wɔ kɔ ayo diire ama i.

eng: That evening people brought to Jesus many who were demon-possessed. He threw the spirits out with just a word. He healed everyone who was sick.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n naasɔ, na ba fa minɛm beberebe n bo ajinim wo bu ŋu ni, aba Yesu dɔ. Na wɔ ma ajinim nɔɔ na ba fite, na wɔ tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

eng: This happened so that what Isaiah the prophet said would be fulfilled: He is the one who took our illnesses and carried away our diseases.
local: I yoori sɔ, na nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo jɔjɔɔri i n yo yii­wa wɔ, i ŋa, Na wɔ de ya afite ya tɔrɛm, ni ya tukpaki kɛrɛ nu.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowd, he ordered his disciples to go over to the other side of the lake.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ŋuuri jama n sɔ ni, na wɔ se i susu­fɔm n ka bu kpiɛ kɔ kunma kpiri n buekun dɔ.

eng: A legal expert came and said to him, Teacher, Ill follow you wherever you go.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ kun aba Yesu dɔ, aba ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, deke kɛrɛ bo a kɔ, m sú u su wɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human One has no place to lay his head.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Ŋaakum la kunmam, na anumam gusu la tiɛɛrim, ama, mini Adamande Wa n yiri, m la má m tii daa­biri.

eng: Another man, one of his disciples, said to him, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n kun be gusu ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, ma m du mɔ kɔ yo m si sutura ka, na m nyá ba su u su.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
local: Ama, na Yesu ase i ka, Wɔrɔ su m su, na a yaki bo ba wu ni, na bu se bu beŋgu.

eng: When Jesus got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu awura lee nu, na i susu­fɔm n asu i su.

eng: A huge storm arose on the lake so that waves were sloshing over the boat. But Jesus was asleep.
local: Bu wo kɔ su sɔ ni, na aŋuma bambaka be ayasu nzue n nu, na i subo nzue gɔ lee n nu. Má lee ni suyi ni nzue wɔ. Ama, nyini saŋga na Yesu wo daafiri su.

eng: They came and woke him, saying, Lord, rescue us! Were going to drown.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n akɔ ateŋge i, na ba se i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, de ya tii ji, má nyini, ya wu awie.

eng: He said to them, Why are you afraid, you people of weak faith? Then he got up and gave orders to the winds and the lake, and there was a great calm.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nzu ti ye am soro sɛrɛ sɔ a? Ambɛrɛ, am yarada n ti kaan sɔ ɔ. Na wɔ yasu atiɛn aŋuma n ni nzue n ŋu, na dika n kɛrɛ atara jum.

eng: The people were amazed and said, What kind of person is this? Even the winds and the lake obey him.
local: Na ba yo alekutura, na ba se ka, Nzu sɔnɔmbiri barasu la ahin, bo aŋuma ni nzue n mmɔɔ de i nɔaniɛ a.

eng: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake in the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed came from among the tombs to meet him. They were so violent that nobody could travel on that road.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu kpiɛɛri kunma kpiri n kɔ juuri awɔrɛ n bo bu fere i ka Gadara n ni, na i ni minɛ nnyɔ nbem ayɛ, bu fite faŋ­kunmam nu wɔ, ajinim wo bu ŋu. Bu ti tiɛ haari bo be koro nati sin má nyini awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: They cried out, What are you going to do with us, Son of God? Have you come to torture us before the time of judgment.
local: Bu ŋuuri Yesu sɔ ni, na ba tiɛn afere i, bu ŋa, Nyɛmɛ Wa, a koro nzu ya nu fɛn a? A ba ka a ba ma ya ŋu anyum­boro wɔ ka, na saŋga n nya ju, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: Far off in the distance a large herd of pigs was feeding.
local: Nyini saŋga afam beberebe wo di su, bu waari má ni bɛrɛ.

eng: The demons pleaded with him, If you throw us out, send us into the herd of pigs.
local: na ajinim n asere Yesu ka, Nzɛn a ŋa a fɔn ya fite minɛm n ŋu, ma ya kɔ fun afam n ŋu.

eng: Then he said to the demons, Go away, and they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am kɔ. Na ba fite ayaki minɛm ni, na ba kɔ afun afam n ŋu. Dika n nu bɛrɛ, na bu kɛrɛ aŋmati acɔɔndi akɔ ato kunma kpiri nzue n nu, na nzue n adi bu.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran into the city and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
local: Afa sasa­fɔm ni ŋuuri nyini n sɔ ni, na ba ŋmati akɔ miɛ n kunnu akɔ abo ndoori, fa kɔ sa n bo wɔ yo n su. Na ba kan sa kɛrɛ bo i tuuri minɛm n bo ajinim woori bu ŋu ni.

eng: Then the whole city came out and met Jesus. When they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.
local: Na miɛ n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ pepere afite akɔ Yesu dɔ. Saŋga bo bu kɔ ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na ba sere i jande ka i fite bu awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: When Jesus finished teaching his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu jɔjɔɔri i susu­fɔm n dɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ jeŋgbe Galili awɔrɛ n nu miɛ miɛm su, sukere minɛm, na wɔ kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ.

eng: Now when John heard in prison about the things the Christ was doing, he sent word by his disciples to Jesus, asking.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, ba nyi Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n dan­saraka sɔrɔ. Na wɔ ti sam kɛrɛ bo Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n suyo bu ni, na wɔ sunma i susu­fɔm ka bu kɔ bisa i ka.

eng: Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another.
local: Wɔrɔ ye bu yo seeri ka a bá n a, nzɛn ya ŋmínda sɔnɔ fɔfɔrɛ be wɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Go, report to John what you hear and see.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am kpie am nyi kɔ se Yohane fa kɔ sam bo am ati bu na am aŋu bu ni, ka.

eng: Those who were blind are able to see . Those who were crippled are walking. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. The poor have good news proclaimed to them .
local: anyinsinm aŋu asi, wɔbukam akoro anati, koko­fɔm anya laifiɛ, asuturim ati asui, minɛm bo ba wu n ateŋge, na yaari­fɔm gusu akoro ati labari kpa ni.

eng: Happy are those who dont stumble and fall because of me.
local: Tere­fiɛ wo minɛm bo bu yo m yarada na bu la má sika be ni mini n dɔ.

eng: When Johns disciples had gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Saŋga bo Yohane susu­fɔm kaaki sukɔ ni, na Yesu ajɔjɔ jama n dɔ, fa kɔ Yohane ŋusu jɔrɛ, i ŋa, Saŋga bo am fite kɔɔri Yohane dɔ, boro kpantain n nu ni, am kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu nzu wɔ? Am kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu sɔnɔ bo i ti ka barankpaati fofoe bo aŋuma n kankan i wɔ?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who wear refined clothes are in royal palaces.
local: Nzɛn má nyini, nzu ti ye am kɔɔri a? Am ŋa am kɔ ŋu sɔnɔ bo i wura sutura ŋgbiŋgbiri wɔ? Ai, am si ka minɛm bo bu wura nyini suturam ni, bu wo nfɛmɛm dika a.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Nzɛn má nyini, nzu ti ye am kɔɔri a? Am kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ? Ɛɛn, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, am aŋu sɔnɔ bo i tara Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ.

eng: He is the one of whom it is written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Dama Yohane ye bu yo kɛrɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, M dú mɔ sunma m sunma­fɔ n na i dú u mɔ, yiri kɔ́ yo u atin n dɔŋgu se u a.

eng: I assure you that no one who has ever been born is greater than John the Baptist. Yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n la má abeŋgu, bo i tara i durunya n nu. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti kaan, Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu n tara yiri Yohane.

eng: From the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven is violently attacked as violent people seize it.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Fite saŋga bo Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n boori Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kan n bu ni, fa ba ju ni kisa, ye Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ŋuuri kekereke a, na minɛm afa anyum­boro ka bu wura nu de i.

eng: All the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John came.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ, ni Moyisi du mɔ kɛrɛɛri Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n su ka, ye Yohane nya baari a.

eng: If you are willing to accept it, he is Elijah who is to come.
local: Nzɛn am yó bu nɔaniɛm yarada, Yohane la yiri n bo bu yo kɛrɛɛri ka Eliya káaki ba n a.

eng: Let the person who has ears, hear.
local: Bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i so i sui na i ti.

eng: To what will I compare this generation? It is like a child sitting in the marketplaces calling out to others.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Ye m fá nzu fa sunsun kabo nyuma­fɔ ti a? Bu ti ka nbaatuma kan kanm bo bu tana gɔɔsu wɔ. Na bu sufere bu beŋgu.

eng: We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt mourn.
local: bu ŋa, Ya kan tere ama am, ama, am abo má kereba. Ya di sɛɛ jue ama am, ama, am asun má.

eng: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.
local: Deke bo i ti na m se sɔ n la ka, Yohane ba juuri ni, i ciciiri i nɔɔ ɔ, i di má diire, i nu má nzan yiri fieo, nyini ti, na ba se ka ajini wo i ŋu a.

eng: Yet the Human One came eating and drinking, and they say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners. But wisdom is proved to be right by her works.
local: Ama, mini Adamande Wa n ba juuri, na m ni minɛ asaŋga adi, na ya saŋga anu, na ba se ka, Am niɛ, ahin sɔnɔ n yiri, i ti ayaakpiri­fɔ ɔ, na i nu nzan nahɔrɛ su. Wɔ tara lampo­de­fɔm, ni satiɛ­yo­fɔm danfoya. Tɔ, minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu su Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ alecira n atin n su ni, bu sa­yoo­wam kere ka i ti nahɔrɛ a.

eng: Then he began to scold the cities where he had done his greatest miracles because they didnt change their hearts and lives.
local: Miɛm bo Yesu yoori alekutura sam beberebe nu ni, bu minɛm n akaaki má bu wo, nyini ti, na Yesu akan bu ndiɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin! How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives and put on funeral clothes and ashes a long time ago.
local: i ŋa, Ambɛrɛ Korazin­fɔm, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Ambɛrɛ Besayida­fɔm gusu, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Alekutura sam bo m yo maari am ni, má nzɛn m yoori bu Tiro ni Sidon miɛ kpirim n nu, má minɛm n akaaki bu wo dada kɛrɛ na bu fa pirim bɔtɔ, ni nzuin yo bu yoo­wa n bo i kere ka bu ahore asaaki, bu satiɛ yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti ni.

eng: But I say to you that Tyre and Sidon will be better off on Judgment Day than you.
local: Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, jina cɛɛn, Nyɛmɛ bá cin am sui tara Tiro ni Sidon dɛɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be thrown down to the place of the dead. After all, if the miracles that were done among you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
local: Ambɛrɛ Kapɛnam­fɔm gusu, am sunsun ka Nyɛmɛ bá yɛ am su kɔ aŋgoro wɔ? Ai, i bá yo am kaan wɔ, na i fá am cɔɔndi kɔ Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri nu. Alekutura sam bo m yo maari am ni, má nzɛn m yoori bu Sodom miɛ kpiri n nu, má kisa, miɛ n toko wo bɛrɛ haari ni nyuma.

eng: But I say to you that it will be better for the land of Sodom on the Judgment Day than it will be for you.
local: Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, jina cɛɛn, Nyɛmɛ bá cin am sui tara Sodom dɛɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm.

eng: At that time Jesus said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and have shown them to babies.
local: Nyini saŋga n na Yesu ase ka, M Si Nyɛmɛ, wɔrɔ ti aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a. M da u asi ka a fa nyini dekem bo alecira­fɔm ni si­fɔm si má i ni, ayi akere bo bu ti ka nbaatam ni.

eng: Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: M Si, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, kabo a koroori ka i yo lɛɛ.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows the Son except the Father. And nobody knows the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, M Si Nyɛmɛ afa deke kɛrɛ awura m saa nu. Be si má Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, má ka i Si Nyɛmɛ si i a, na be gusu si má i Si ni, má ka Baa ni si i a, ni minɛm bo Baa n koro ka i yi i kere bu ni, bɛrɛ si i a.

eng: Come to me, all you who are struggling hard and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase ka, Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am sɔ sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri n na am afie ni, am bra m dɔ, na m má am ŋumi­yi.

eng: Put on my yoke, and learn from me. Im gentle and humble. And you will find rest for yourselves.
local: Am de m sɔɔri ni, na am ma am wo ti kabo m dɛɛ n ti ni, dama m ti bɛnlɛ wɔ, na m yo m ŋu kaan. Mini dɔ ye am nyá ŋumi­yi a.

eng: My yoke is easy to bear, and my burden is light.
local: Dama keree­wa n bo m fa ma am n ti má kekereke, na sɔɔri n gusu bo m fa sɔ am n wo má nɔ.

eng: Then Pharisees and legal experts came to Jesus from Jerusalem and said.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ nbem, ni Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm afite Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu aba Yesu dɔ, aba abisa i ka.

eng: Boarding a boat, Jesus crossed to the other side of the lake and went to his own city.
local: Na Yesu akaaki awura lee n nu, akpiɛ akɔ i bɔsu.

eng: People brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man who was paralyzed, Be encouraged, my child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Dɔ na minɛ nbem afa sɔnɔ be aba i dɔ, i saam ni i jaam awu. Bu sɔɔri i ni i dasu kɛrɛ baari a. Yesu ŋuuri kabo bu yoori i yarada n sɔ ni, na wɔ se sɔnɔ n bo i saam ni i jaam awu n ka, M wa, ma u ahore si, dama ma fa u satiɛ ace u.

eng: Some legal experts said among themselves, This man is insulting God.
local: Dɔ na Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm nbem asunsun bu ahore su ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n sujɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu.

eng: Why are your disciples breaking the elders rules handed down to us? They dont ritually purify their hands by washing before they eat.
local: Nzu ti ye u susu­fɔm n akete ya nam sa­yoo­wa n a? Dama bu ŋmisi má bu saa ka, na bu nya di diire.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking and said, Why do you fill your minds with evil things?.
local: Yesu siiri bu sunsunni nu sɔ ni, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am bita sunsunni tiɛ am ahore su a?.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you break the command of God by keeping the rules handed down to you?.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Nzu ti ye am akete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n ye am su am dɛɛ sa­yoo­wam su a?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Bɔnɔ ti kekereke a, bo m se ka, Ma fa u satiɛ ace u, nzɛn, Yasu fa u dasu n na a nati n a?.

eng: For God said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ ama nɔɔ ka, Bu u si, ni u ni. Ye wɔ kaaki ase ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔɔri i si, ni i ni jɔrɛ tiɛ, wɔ yo daka ka bu kun i mi.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed—Get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: Ama, m kére am ka mini Adamande Wa n la yiko durunya n nu bo m fa minɛm satiɛ ni bu ŋgasi ce bu. Na wɔ se sɔnɔ n ka, Yasu fa u dasu n na a kɔ awuru.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you Im giving to God as a gift,then you dont have to honor your father.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ kere ka nzɛn sɔnɔ be la deke be bo i ti ye ni ka i fa buka i si, ni i ni, i koro se bu ka, Deke bo má m ŋa m fa ma am ni, ma fa ayi saraka ama Nyɛmɛ.

eng: The man got up and went home.
local: Na sɔnɔ n ayasu, na wɔ kɔ awuru.

eng: So you do away with Gods Law for the sake of the rules that have been handed down to you.
local: Am suse ka i mi koro kpiɛ i si, ni i ni n nyini deke n lɛɛ. Nyini n kere ka am abu Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ nɔaniɛ ni ŋgbɛɛn lɛɛ, ye am su am dɛɛ sa­yoo­wam n su a.

eng: When the crowds saw what had happened, they were afraid and praised God, who had given such authority to human beings.
local: Saŋga bo jama n ŋuuri nyini n sɔ ni, na ba yo alekutura, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ, kabo wɔ ma minɛm nyini yiko n ni.

eng: Hypocrites! Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you.
local: Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, nahɔrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo kaanri am jɔrɛ n a. Wɔ kɛrɛ i kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o.

eng: As Jesus continued on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. He said to him, Follow me, and he got up and followed him.
local: Na Yesu ayasu dɔ akɔ. I wo kɔ su sɔ ni, na wɔ ŋu lampo­de­fɔ be, bo bu fere i ka Matio, ka i tana i juma yoo­biri. Na Yesu ase i ka, Yasu bra na a ba su m su. Na wɔ yasu asu i su.

eng: This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Ahin minɛm ni, bu ma m jirima bu nɔɔ nu bɛrɛ wɔ, ama, i wo má bu ahore su.

eng: As Jesus sat down to eat in Matthews house, many tax collectors and sinners joined Jesus and his disciples at the table.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu wo Matio dika, sudi diire ni, na satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ni lampo­de­fɔm beberebe aba Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n dɔ, na ba saŋga adi diire.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human rules .
local: Bu kansi m ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, dama bu kere minɛ adamande dɛɛ keree­wam wɔ, na bu se ka nyini keree­wa n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: But when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Farasi­fɔm ŋuuri nyini sɔ ni, na ba bisa Yesu susu­fɔm n ka, Nzu ti ye am kerefɔ n ni lampo­de­fɔm, ni satiɛ­yo­fɔm saŋga di diire a.

eng: In those days John the Baptist appeared in the desert of Judea announcing.
local: Nyini saŋga n na Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n akɔ Yudiya boro kpantain n nu, kɔ sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, i ŋa.

eng: Jesus called the crowd near and said to them, Listen and understand.
local: Na Yesu afere jama n aba i bi, na wɔ se bu ka, Am so am sui na am ti jɔrɛ n bo m sujɔjɔ i ma am n bu.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Na Yesu ati bu jɔjɔɔ­wa n na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Minɛm bo bu la laifiɛ n koro má ayiri­fɔ, má ka bo bu la má laifiɛ ni koro a.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Here comes the kingdom of heaven.
local: Am kaaki am wo, dama saŋga n adodo bo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su.

eng: Its not what goes into the mouth that contaminates a person in Gods sight. Its what comes out of the mouth that contaminates the person.
local: Má diire bo i wura sɔnɔ nɔɔ nu n yo i fin a, ama, jɔjɔɔ­wa bo i fite sɔnɔ nɔɔ nu ni, yiri yo i fin a.

eng: Go and learn what this means: I want mercy and not sacrifice . I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Am kɔ na am kɔ fiiti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n nu na am ti i bu, kabo ba kɛrɛ i ka, M koro aŋunfɔ si tara bo am yo yoo­wa ma m. Ma ba má ka m bá fere minɛm bo bu ŋa bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n wɔ, ama, m baari ka m bá fere satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ.

eng: Then the disciples came and said to him, Do you know that the Pharisees were offended by what you just said.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, aba abisa i ka, A si ka u jɔrɛ n bo a jɔjɔɔri i n asun má Farasi­fɔm n nu e.

eng: At that time Johns disciples came and asked Jesus, Why do we and the Pharisees frequently fast, but your disciples never fast.
local: Na Yohane susu­fɔm n aba Yesu dɔ, aba abisa i ka, Nzu ti ye yɛrɛ ni Farasi­fɔm cici ya nɔɔ, ye u dɛɛ susu­fɔm bɛrɛ cici má.

eng: He was the one of whom Isaiah the prophet spoke when he said: The voice of one shouting in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight. .
local: Yiri Yohane n ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo jɔjɔɔri i jɔrɛ n a. I ŋa, Sɔnɔ be subo ndoori boro kpantain n nu se ka, Am sɛsɛ Mibiɛ n atin n su, na am yo i atin n dɔŋgu.

eng: Jesus replied, Every plant that my heavenly Father didnt plant will be pulled up.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Má am bɔ bu nu, bu ti ka anyinsinm wɔ. Anyinsin gusu nya bitaari i beŋgu anyinsin n kpama, bu kɛrɛ kɔ́ to kunma nu wɔ. Baka kɛrɛ bo m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n ata má i, i cín tu i wɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, The wedding guests cant mourn while the groom is still with them, can they? But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then theyll fast.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am sunsun ka konyaya­fɔm koro wo ni ahoresaaki, saŋga bo konyafan n toko wo bu dɔ ni? Ama, saŋga suba bo bu bá yi konyafan n fite bu nu, nyini saŋga n ye bu nyá cici bu nɔɔ a.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Yohane wuraari sutura bo bu faari nyɔɔma ndɔrɛ wuuri i a. Na wɔ fa jina abo i wi nu. I diire yo la lalom ni wiɛ wɔ.

eng: Then Peter spoke up, Explain this riddle to us.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, Kere ya anyundɛrɛ n bu, bo a ŋa sɔnɔ nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa yo i fin ni.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes because the patch tears away the cloth and makes a worse tear.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Be koro fa má sanvuin fɔfɔrɛ kpa tɔ má tani dawa kunma, nzɛn i yoori sɔ, sanvuin fɔfɔrɛ ni sɔ́n tani dawa n wɔ, na i téke i kunma buka su.

eng: People from Jerusalem, throughout Judea, and all around the Jordan River came to him.
local: Na minɛ afite Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, ni Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ miɛm kɛrɛ su, ni Yodan dɛɛ bue n kɛrɛ aba i dɔ.

eng: Jesus said, Dont you understand yet?.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, Ŋka ambɛrɛ gusu ati má jɔrɛ n bu lɛɛ?.

eng: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the wineskins would burst, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined. Instead, people pour new wine into new wineskins so that both are kept safe.
local: I kaaki ti sɔ ɔ bekun ka be koro fa má nzan nyii­wa faŋga­fɔ gɔ má dundu dawa bɛbɛtɛ nu, nzɛn i yoori sɔ, dundu ni tíɛ wɔ, na i ká ŋgbɛɛn, na nzan n kɛrɛ sóŋgi butu. Ama, i ti ye ni ka, bu fa nzan nyii­wa faŋga­fɔ gɔ dundu fɔfɔrɛ bii­wa nu wɔ, na bu fa taka bo pui yo má i.

eng: As they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
local: na ba kan bu ŋgasi ni bu satiɛ kɛrɛ akere Nyɛmɛ, na Yohane abiɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue Yodan kunma n nu.

eng: Dont you know that everything that goes into the mouth enters the stomach and goes out into the sewer?.
local: Deke bo i wura sɔnɔ nɔɔ nu, wura i kunnu wɔ, na i kɔ je yi i, má sɔ?.

eng: While Jesus was speaking to them, a ruler came and knelt in front of him, saying, My daughter has just died. But come and place your hand on her, and shell live.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu wo jɔjɔ su sɔ ni, na Yuda­fɔm kpiɛn be bo i niɛ bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n su n aba akutu i nyunu, ase i ka, M wa baa­bara awu, jina n nu bɛrɛ. Bra na a ba fa u saa nana i ŋu, na i nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Be careful that you dont practice your religion in front of people to draw their attention. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am si am ŋu sa, na má am yi am nyɛmɛsu sa­yoo­wa fite fofoe nu, adena minɛm ŋu i na bu kansi am. Nzɛn am yoori sɔ, am nya má ŋkatɔ be, am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n dɔ.

eng: Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to be baptized by John. He said to them, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Ama, saŋga bo i ba ŋuuri ka Farasi­fɔm ni Sadusi­fɔm dɔŋgu suba i dɔ ka i biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm bo am ti ka woom ni, ŋma seeri am ka am ŋmati fite nyini sui­cin kekereke n bo Nyɛmɛ sufa ba am dɔ n nu a?.

eng: But what goes out of the mouth comes from the heart. And thats what contaminates a person in Gods sight.
local: Ama, jɔjɔɔ­wa bo i fite sɔnɔ nɔɔ nu fite i ahore su wɔ, nyini yo i fin a.

eng: So Jesus and his disciples got up and went with him.
local: Na Yesu ayasu, na i ni yiri akɔ, na i susu­fɔm n asu su.

eng: Whenever you give to the poor, dont blow your trumpet as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets so that they may get praise from people. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn a ŋa a ce yaari­fɔm aceeri, má a bo ndoori na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti, kabo burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm yo i bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, ni miɛ kunnu atiŋgberem n su ni, dama bu yo sɔ ɔ, adena minɛm kansi bu. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, bɛrɛ ŋkatɔ awieeri lɛɛ, ye ba du mɔ anya i n a.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives.
local: Am yo sakpa bo i kere ka am akaaki am wo.

eng: Out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adultery, sexual sins, thefts, false testimonies, and insults.
local: Dama deke bo i wo sɔnɔ ahore su ma i sunsunni tiɛ a. I koro ma i nati sukun minɛm, na i suyo sakaraya, na i susaaki ajaa ciire, na i suwɔ, na i nati sudi kɔrɛ daani, na i nati susaaki minɛm duma.

eng: Then a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and touched the hem of his clothes.
local: Dɔ na bara be woori jama n nu, i bita abiɛɛri i afɔ buru ni nnyɔ lɛɛ bo i kpu na i kpiɛ má. Na wɔ sakati awura jama n nu Yesu sin, afa i saa akan Yesu tanlɛɛ n jaa bu.

eng: But when you give to the poor, dont let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
local: Ama, nzɛn a ŋa a ce yaari­fɔm aceeri, má a ma be ŋu i fieo, na bu si sa bo a suyo i ni.

eng: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in the territory of Judea during the rule of King Herod, magi came from the east to Jerusalem.
local: Saŋga bo bu wuuri Yesu miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Bɛtilɛhɛm na i wo Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu ni, nyini saŋga ni Herode ti nyini awɔrɛ n nu fɛmɛ a. Na asara­mbaa kɔrɛsi­yi­fɔm nbem afite wiɛ afiiri, aba Yɛrusalem aba abisa ka.

eng: And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Na má am sunsun ka Abraham ti am na n dɛɛ ti, na am koro fite nyini sui­cin n nu. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, Nyɛmɛ koro ma ahin yabuɛm ni kaaki nbaam ma Abraham.

eng: These contaminate a person in Gods sight. But eating without washing hands doesnt contaminate in Gods sight.
local: Nyini dekem kɛrɛ koro yo sɔnɔ fin a. Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ di diire na wɔ ŋmisi má i saa, i yo má i fin.

eng: She thought, If I only touch his robe Ill be healed.
local: Dama i seeri i kunnu ka, Haari m fa m saa kaanri i tanlɛɛ ni mmɔɔ, m nyá laifiɛ.

eng: so that you may give to the poor in secret. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: adena u aceeri n yo asiri nu. Dɔ na u Si Nyɛmɛ bo i ŋu sa kɛrɛ bo a yo i asiri nu ni, n tɔ́ u kaari.

eng: They asked, Where is the newborn king of the Jews? Weve seen his star in the east, and weve come to honor him.
local: Baa n bo ba wu i na i bá yo Yuda­fɔm fɛmɛ n wo ni wɔ? Ya ŋu i asara­mbaa wiɛ afiiri dɔ, ye ya ŋa ya bá na ya bá kutu i a.
