eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Nyɛmɛ asi i samba n nana ase baka kɛrɛ bu na i kpiɛ i ji sin nu, yiri bo i suu­wa ti má kpa ni.

eng: From there, Jesus went to the regions of Tyre and Sidon.
local: Na Yesu afite bɛrɛ akɔ awɔrɛ n bo i dodo Tiro ni Sidon miɛ kpirim ni.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw her, he said, Be encouraged, daughter. Your faith has healed you. And the woman was healed from that time on.
local: Na Yesu akaaki aniɛ aŋu i, na wɔ se i ka, M wa, ma u ahore si. Yarada n bo a yo m ni, yiri ma a nya laifiɛ a. Dika n nu bɛrɛ, na bara n anya laifiɛ.

eng: When you pray, dont be like hypocrites. They love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners so that people will see them. I assure you, thats the only reward theyll get.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Nzɛn saŋga bo am ba susere Nyɛmɛ, má am yo kabo burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm yo ni, dama bɛrɛ koro ka bu sujina bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, ni deke bo atiŋgberem jaja nu ni, na bu sere Nyɛmɛ adena minɛm ŋu bu. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, bu ŋkatɔ awieeri lɛɛ, ye ba du mɔ anya i n a.

eng: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and everyone in Jerusalem was troubled with him.
local: Fɛmɛ Herode tiiri sɔ ni, na jɔrɛ n amiɛ i ni Yɛrusalem­fɔm kɛrɛ.

eng: I baptize with water those of you who have changed your hearts and lives. The one who is coming after me is stronger than I am. Im not worthy to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
local: Mini sufa nzue biɛ am a, na am yo casi, bo i kere ka am akaaki am wo, ama, yiri bo i wo m sin suba n tara m fieo, m ti kaan ni bo m dodo i. Yiri fá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ni sin yo am casi a, kabo sɔnɔ biɛ nzue na i yo casi ni.

eng: A Canaanite woman from those territories came out and shouted, Show me mercy, Son of David. My daughter is suffering terribly from demon possession.
local: Na bara be aba Yesu dɔ, i ti má Yuda­fɔ, i ti Kanana­fɔ ɔ, bo i fite nyini awɔrɛ n nu. Na wɔ bo afere Yesu, ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma, si m aŋunfɔ. Ajinim wo m wa baa­bara ŋu, sukere i yaari agaya.

eng: When Jesus went into the rulers house, he saw the flute players and the distressed crowd.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu kɔ juuri kpiɛn n dika ni, na wɔ ŋu sɛɛ mbiɛ kan­fɔm, ni jamam ka bu wo naŋmiɛ su.

eng: He gathered all the chief priests and the legal experts and asked them where the Christ was to be born.
local: Na wɔ fere Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Ye bu wú Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ni wɔ.

eng: But when you pray, go to your room, shut the door, and pray to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees what you do in secret will reward you.
local: Ama, saŋga bo a ba susere Nyɛmɛ, wura u sɔrɔ na a nyi u anɔ, na a sere u Si Nyɛmɛ bo be ŋu má i ni, na u Si Nyɛmɛ bo i ŋu sa kɛrɛ bo a yo i asiri nu ni, nyá tɔ u kaari.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: I bita i ŋgani kpaja fieeba bo i ba bobo ŋgani n gbaŋgba n su, na i kpája yi ŋgani mbaa n fa gɔ i bondo nu, na i nyá yara kunkun n sin n bo i je na i nɔ má n nu.

eng: But he didnt respond to her at all. His disciples came and urged him, Send her away; she keeps shouting out after us.
local: Ama, Yesu abɔ má i nu. Na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, aba asere i jande, ase i ka, Fɔn bara n na i kɔ. Niɛ kabo i su ya su, yo naŋmiɛ barasu a.

eng: He said, Go away, because the little girl isnt dead but is asleep; but they laughed at him.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am fa sin fite, baa­bara n awu má, wɔ daafi wɔ. Ama, na ba siri i.

eng: They said, In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is what the prophet wrote.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Bɛtilɛhɛm bo i wo Yudiya asiɛn n su ni, dama kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n kɛrɛɛri i lɛɛ. Micɛra n ŋa o.

eng: When you pray, dont pour out a flood of empty words, as the Gentiles do. They think that by saying many words theyll be heard.
local: Na saŋga bo am ba susere Nyɛmɛ, má am yo naŋmiɛ kabo bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n yo ni, dama bu sunsun ka bu jɔjɔɔri beberebe n ye Nyɛmɛ de bu sere a.

eng: At that time Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan River so that John would baptize him.
local: Nyini saŋga n na Yesu afite Galili awɔrɛ n nu, aba Yohane dɔ Yodan kunma n nu ka Yohane biɛ i Nyɛmɛ nzue.

eng: Jesus replied, Ive been sent only to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Nyɛmɛ asunma m Yisareli­fɔm bo bu ti ka nbɔɛm na ba mini ni ŋgumi dɔ a.

eng: After he had sent the crowd away, Jesus went in and touched her hand, and the little girl rose up.
local: I fɔɔnri jama n kɛrɛ fite wieeri ni, na wɔ nya awura sɔɔ n bo baa­bara n da nu n nu, na wɔ kɔ atara i saa, na wɔ yasu.

eng: You, Bethlehem, land of Judah, by no means are you least among the rulers of Judah, because from you will come one who governs, who will shepherd my people Israel.
local: Wɔrɔ Bɛtilɛhɛm bo a wo Yuda asiɛn n su ni, a ti má miɛ kaan. Wɔrɔ ti Yuda asiɛn n su miɛm anyunuyi­fɔm kun a, dama ka fɛmɛ n bo m yí i na i bá yo m minɛ Yisareli­fɔm tii­fɔ ni, i fíte u dɔ ɔ.

eng: He called his twelve disciples and gave them authority over unclean spirits to throw them out and to heal every disease and every sickness.
local: Na Yesu afere i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni, na wɔ ma bu yiko ka bu kɔ sufɔn yi ajinim fite minɛm ŋu, na bu ma tukpaki­fɔm barasu barasu kɛrɛ nya laifiɛ.

eng: Dont be like them, because your Father knows what you need before you ask.
local: Má ambɛrɛ yo ka bɛrɛ, dama am Si Nyɛmɛ si am sa koroo­wa kɛrɛ n mmɔɔ ka, ye am nya sere i a.

eng: John tried to stop him and said, I need to be baptized by you, yet you come to me.
local: Ama, na Yohane akete ka i biɛ má Yesu Nyɛmɛ nzue ni. I ŋa, Má i ti ye ni ka wɔrɔ biɛ m Nyɛmɛ nzue a, ye nzu ti ye a ŋa mini biɛ u a.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up a mountain. He sat down and his disciples came to him.
local: Yesu ŋuuri jama n ni, na wɔ fun akɔ atana buka be su. Na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ.

eng: News about this spread throughout that whole region.
local: Na nyini labari n abara ayɛ dɔ dɛɛ awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: But she knelt before him and said, Lord, help me.
local: Na bara n aba akutu i nyunu, ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, buka m.

eng: Then Herod secretly called for the magi and found out from them the time when the star had first appeared.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Herode afere asara­mbaa kɔrɛsi­yi­fɔm n asiri nu na i si saŋga bo asara­mbaa n tiɛɛri ni.

eng: Here are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon, who is called Peter; and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee; and John his brother.
local: I jaramasa buru ni nnyɔm n bo i yiiri bu n duma n la Simon, bo bu fere i ka Petoro ni, ni i niɛma Andere, ni Yakobo ni i niɛma Yohane bo bu si la Zɛbɛdi ni.

eng: Pray like this: Our Father who is in heaven, uphold the holiness of your name.
local: Nyini ti, kabo i ti ye ni ka am sere Nyɛmɛ yaa. Ya Si, bo a wo aŋgoro ni, ma minɛm bu u duma casi ni.

eng: Jesus answered, Allow me to be baptized now. This is necessary to fulfill all righteousness. So John agreed to baptize Jesus.
local: Ama, na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Ma ya yo sɔ adena ya yo deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro ni. I seeri sɔ n na Yohane asoro su.

eng: He taught them, saying.
local: Na wɔ bo i bu ka i kere bu, i ŋa.

eng: As Jesus departed, two blind men followed him, crying out, Show us mercy, Son of David.
local: Na Yesu afite bɛrɛ, i sukɔ n na anyinsin nnyɔ nbem asu i su, subo fere i, bu ŋa, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma, si ya aŋunfɔ.

eng: He replied, It is not good to take the childrens bread and toss it to dogs.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, I ti má ye ni ka, a fa nbaatumam diire na a fa ma caam.

eng: He sent them to Bethlehem, saying, Go and search carefully for the child. When youve found him, report to me so that I too may go and honor him.
local: Na wɔ kere bu ka, Am kɔ Bɛtilɛhɛm, na am kɔ niɛniɛ nyunyumi, na nzɛn am ŋuuri baa ni, am kpie am nyi ba se m na m gusu kɔ́ kutu i.

eng: Bring in your kingdom so that your will is done on earth as its done in heaven.
local: Ma u fɛmɛya n bra. Ma u koroo­wa yo asiɛŋgu fɛn, ka alecɛɛma nu.

eng: Philip; and Bartholomew; Thomas; and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; and Thaddaeus.
local: ni Filipu, ni Batolomeyo, ni Tomasi, ni Matio bo i ti lampo­de­fɔ ni, ni Yakobo bo i si la Alafeyo ni, ni Tadayɔ.

eng: When Jesus was baptized, he immediately came up out of the water. Heaven was opened to him, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
local: Saŋga bo Yohane biɛɛri Yesu Nyɛmɛ nzue n wieeri n na i fiteeri nzue n nu ni, kpeŋga na aŋgoro ateke, na wɔ ŋu ka Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n ajura ka tiiwa dɛɛ su aba atana i ŋu.

eng: Happy are people who are hopeless, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Bɛrɛ bo bu ti yaari­fɔm bu ahore su ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti bu dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: When he came into the house, the blind men approached him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe I can do this? Yes, Lord, they replied.
local: Na saŋga bo Yesu kɔ wuraari awuru n nu ni, na anyinsinm n aba i dɔ na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am yo yarada ka m koro ma am nya laifiɛ? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ɛɛn, ya Mibiɛ, ya yo yarada.

eng: She said, Yes, Lord. But even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall off their masters table.
local: Na bara n ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, a la nahɔrɛ, ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, caam n tambu diire n bo bu fɔm di, na i tɔtɔ asiɛŋgu n na bu fa di.

eng: When they heard the king, they went; and look, the star they had seen in the east went ahead of them until it stood over the place where the child was.
local: Fɛmɛ n jɔjɔɔri bu dɔ wieeri sɔ n na ba de atin, bu wo kɔ su sɔ ni, na ba ŋu asara­mbaa n bo bu ŋuuri i wiɛ afiiri ni, ka i du bu mɔ haari na wɔ kɔ ajina awuru n bo baa n wo nu n tii su.

eng: That day Jesus went out of the house and sat down beside the lake.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n na Yesu afite awuru n nu akɔ atana Galili kunma kpiri n nɔɔ su, ka i kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kere jama ni.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Ma ya nyuma dɛɛ cɛɛn nu, ya nɔɔ nu diire.

eng: Simon the Cananaean; and Judas, who betrayed Jesus.
local: ni Simon bo i tu ŋgoni fa kɔ i asiɛn su ni, ni Yudasi Isikariyoti bo i ba tɔɔri Yesu ni.

eng: A voice from heaven said, This is my Son whom I dearly love; I find happiness in him.
local: Dɔ na wɔ ti akonvi be ŋga aŋgoro, i ŋa, Ahin ti m wa a, m koro i agaya, m ahore ajɔ i ŋu.

eng: Happy are people who grieve, because they will be made glad.
local: Bɛrɛ bo bu ahore asaaki ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ dá bu ahore a.

eng: Then Jesus touched their eyes and said, It will happen for you just as you have believed.
local: Dɔ na wɔ fa i saa akan bu nyu­mbaam, na wɔ se bu ka, Kabo am ayo m yarada ni, i yó sɔ ma am.

eng: Jesus answered, Woman, you have great faith. It will be just as you wish. And right then her daughter was healed.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, A ti bara yarada­yo­fɔ ɔ, nahɔrɛ su. Nyini ti, kabo a koro ni, i yó sɔ ma u a. Dika n nu bɛrɛ, na i wa n anya laifiɛ.

eng: When they saw the star, they were filled with joy.
local: Bu ŋuuri asara­mbaa n sɔ ni, na bu ahore ajɔ agaya.

eng: Such large crowds gathered around him that he climbed into a boat and sat down. The whole crowd was standing on the shore.
local: Na jama beberebe atiɛn bu nɔɔ i dɔ. Bu sunnuuri beberebe sɔ n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ wura lee nu atana, na bɛrɛ aka ajina nzue n nɔɔ su.

eng: Jesus sent these twelve out and commanded them, Dont go among the Gentiles or into a Samaritan city.
local: Na Yesu asunma nyini minɛ buru ni nnyɔm n na wɔ ma bu ahin nɔaniɛ ni, i ŋa, Má am kɔ miɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm miɛm n nu, wara am wura Samariya­fɔm miɛ be nu.

eng: Forgive us for the ways we have wronged you, just as we also forgive those who have wronged us.
local: Fa ya ŋgasi fa ce ya, kabo ya gusu afa ace bo bu do ya ŋgasi ni.

eng: Happy are people who are humble, because they will inherit the earth.
local: Bɛrɛ bo bu yo bu ŋu kaan ma Nyɛmɛ ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ bá fa durunya n ma bɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: Their eyes were opened. Then Jesus sternly warned them, Make sure nobody knows about this.
local: Na bu nyu­mbaam n ateke, na ba ŋu asi. Na Yesu abɔbɔ bu ni nahɔrɛ su ka, má bu ma be si sa n bo wɔ yo i ni.

eng: Jesus moved on from there along the shore of the Galilee Sea. He went up a mountain and sat down.
local: Na Yesu afite bɛrɛ, asu Galili kunma kpiri n su, na wɔ kɔ afun atana buka be su.

eng: They entered the house and saw the child with Mary his mother. Falling to their knees, they honored him. Then they opened their treasure chests and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
local: Na ba kɔ awura awuru n nu, na ba ŋu baa n ni i ni Mariya na ba kutu i, na ba yo i ansɛ. Na ba nya ateke bu kurukum, na ba yi aceerim ace i. Aceerim n la sika, ni tulaalɛɛ nva­nvani­fɔ be, i ti samɛnɛ wɔ, ni baka nyunvuin nva­nvani­fɔ.

eng: He said many things to them in parables: A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Na wɔ bo miɛɛnum na wɔ kere bu dekem beberebe. I ŋa, Dɔdɔɔri­fɔ be fiteeri sɔ ɔ, ka i kɔ sandi alɛ­mbaa.

eng: Go instead to the lost sheep, the people of Israel.
local: Ama, am kɔ Yisareli­fɔm bo bu ti ka nbɔɛm na ba mini n dɔ.

eng: And dont lead us into temptation, but rescue us from the evil one.
local: Má a ma deke be yo ya na ya wura satiɛ nu, ama, yi ya fite anyumbiri nu. Dama, u dɛɛ la fɛmɛya, ni faŋga, ni jirima saŋga kɛrɛ. Ami.

eng: Happy are people who are hungry and thirsty for righteousness, because they will be fed until they are full.
local: Bɛrɛ bo bu nyunu aboro ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su kabo sɔnɔ la konviki fa kɔ diire ni nzue busu ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ bá ma bu akunnuyi a.

eng: But they went out and spread the word about him throughout that whole region.
local: Ama, na ba kɔ abo ndoori abara ayɛ dɔ dɛɛ awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, fa kɔ sa n bo Yesu ayo i ni.

eng: Large crowds came to him, including those who were paralyzed, blind, injured, and unable to speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
local: Na jama beberebe afa wɔbukam, ni anyinsinm, ni bo bu saam ni bu jaam awu ni, ni bobom, ni tukpaki­fɔm beberebe, ka i ma bu laifiɛ, na wɔ ma bu anya laifiɛ.

eng: Because they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country by another route.
local: Nyini sin na ba fa atin be akpie bu nyi akɔ bu bɔsu, dama Nyɛmɛ ase bu alɛlɛ nu ka má bu kɔ Fɛmɛ Herode dɔ kun.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path, and birds came and ate it.
local: Saŋga bo i wo sandi su ni, na alɛ­mbaa ni nbem atɔtɔ atin n nɔɔ, na anumam aba atambu bu ami.

eng: As you go, make this announcement: The kingdom of heaven has come near.
local: Am kɔ na am kɔ kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kere bu, na am se bu ka, saŋga n adodo bo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su.

eng: If you forgive others their sins, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
local: Dama nzɛn am fa minɛm ŋgasi ce bu, am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n gusu fá am dɛɛ ce am.

eng: As they were leaving, people brought to him a man who was demon-possessed and unable to speak.
local: Saŋga bo anyinsinm n fite kɔɔri n dɛɛ sin, na minɛ nbem afa sɔnɔ be aba Yesu dɔ, ajini ayo i bobo wɔ.

eng: Happy are people who show mercy, because they will receive mercy.
local: Bɛrɛ bo bu si aŋunfɔ ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ bá si bu aŋunfɔ a.

eng: So the crowd was amazed when they saw those who had been unable to speak talking, and the paralyzed cured, and the injured walking, and the blind seeing. And they praised the God of Israel.
local: Na minɛm n ayo alekutura, ni kabo bu ŋuuri ka bobom sujɔjɔ, na minɛm bo bu saam ni bu jaam awu n anya laifiɛ, na wɔbukam sunati, na anyinsinm suŋu asi ni, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ n bo bɛrɛ Yisareli­fɔm su i ni.

eng: When the magi had departed, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, Get up. Take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod will soon search for the child in order to kill him.
local: Asara­mbaa kɔrɛsi­yi­fɔm n kɔ wieeri ni, na Micɛra n dɛɛ mɛrɛkɛ aba Yosefu dɔ alɛlɛ nu, aba ase i ka, Yasu fa baa n ni i ni na am ŋmati kɔ Ijipiti asiɛn su, má nyini Fɛmɛ Herode wo baa niɛniɛ su na i kun i. Am kɔ wo dɔ haari cɛɛn bo m bá kaaki ba ma am nɔɔ ka am yasu dɔ.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Na nbem atɔtɔ tiɛka su, deke bo fa sunnu má ni, fa n sunnu má n dɛɛ ti, wɔ cɛ má, na wɔ fi.

eng: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with skin diseases, and throw out demons. You received without having to pay. Therefore, give without demanding payment.
local: Na am ma tukpaki­fɔm nya laifiɛ, na am teŋge bo ba wu ni, na am ma koko­fɔm nya laifiɛ, na am fɔn yi ajinim fite minɛm ŋu. Kabo ma ma am ahin yiko n na ma de má pui ni, am gusu yo sɔ n na má am de pui.

eng: But if you dont forgive others, neither will your Father forgive your sins.
local: Ama, nzɛn am afa má minɛm ŋgasi ace má bu, am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n gusu fá má am dɛɛ ce má am.

eng: When Jesus had thrown out the demon, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed and said, Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
local: Yesu fɔn yiiri ajini n fiteeri sɔnɔ n ŋu wieeri n na wɔ koro ajɔjɔ. Na jama n ayo alekutura, na ba se ka, Ya nya ŋu má ahin sa n be ŋusu cɛɛn be, Yisareli asiɛn n su.

eng: Happy are people who have pure hearts, because they will see God.
local: Bɛrɛ bo bu ahore­mbaa ti casi ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama bɛrɛ bá ŋu Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: Now Jesus called his disciples and said, I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat. I dont want to send them away hungry for fear they wont have enough strength to travel.
local: Na Yesu afere i susu­fɔm ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin jama n la m aŋunfɔ, dama i nyuma le­san lɛɛ bo bu wo m dɔ na bu la má deke bo bu di. M koro má ka m yaki bu na bu kɔ awuru ni ahɔɛ, má nyini, bu nbem tɔ́ cin atin nu wɔ.

eng: Joseph got up and, during the night, took the child and his mother to Egypt.
local: Na Yosefu ayasu afa baa n ni i ni aŋmati kɔŋguɛ n kɛrɛ, na ba kɔ Ijipiti asiɛn n su.

eng: But when the sun came up, it scorched the plants, and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: ama, wiɛ ba fiteeri agaya ni, na ba waandi, dama bunlui n akpama abi má.

eng: Workers deserve to be fed, so dont gather gold or silver or copper coins for your money belts to take on your trips.
local: Má am se ka am bita ŋwaa be, haari ŋwaa kaan mmɔɔ.

eng: And when you fast, dont put on a sad face like the hypocrites. They distort their faces so people will know they are fasting. I assure you that they have their reward.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Saŋga bo am sucici am nɔɔ, má am niɛ aŋunfɔ niɛ kabo burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm yo ni, bu yo má bu ŋu nyankandi, adena minɛm ŋu ka ba cici bu nɔɔ. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, bu ŋkatɔ awieeri lɛɛ, ye ba du mɔ anya i n a.

eng: But the Pharisees said, He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Ama, na Farasi­fɔm n ase ka, Ajinim kɛrɛ dɛɛ fɛmɛ ni ma i yiko ye i koro fɔn yi ajinim n a.

eng: Happy are people who make peace, because they will be called Gods children.
local: Bɛrɛ bo bu kpata aluwa ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ bá fere bu ka i nbaam a.

eng: His disciples replied, Where are we going to get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy such a big crowd.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n abisa i ka, Ye nifansu ye ya nyá diire ma ahin jama beberebe n sɔ n na bu di na bu kunnu yi a.

eng: He stayed there until Herod died. This fulfilled what the Lord had spoken through the prophet: I have called my son out of Egypt .
local: na ba wo dɔ haari Fɛmɛ Herode aba awu. Ahin yoori sɔ ni, i ma kabo Micɛra n ma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n yo jɔjɔɔri n yo yii­wa a. I ŋa, Ma fere m wa n afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked them.
local: Na alɛ­mbaa ni nbem atɔtɔ lɔlɔɛ nu, na lɔlɔɛ n anyi akata bu ŋu.

eng: Dont take a backpack for the road or two shirts or sandals or a walking stick.
local: Ahin atin n bo am sukɔ i ni, má am se ka am bita kuruku, má am fa tanlɛɛ be buka bo am wura i n su, má am fa gbagbaa, má am fa kpama, dama juma­yo­fɔ kɛrɛ ti ye ni ka bu ma i diire wɔ.

eng: When you fast, brush your hair and wash your face.
local: Ama, nzɛn a ba sucici u nɔɔ, yo u ŋu nyankandi.

eng: Jesus traveled among all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, announcing the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness.
local: Na Yesu abara ayɛ miɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim, ni kan kanm n kɛrɛ su, na i sukere minɛm, bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, na i sukan labari kpa n fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n su, na wɔ tuntun minɛ bo tukpaki barasu barasu n kɛrɛ bita bu ni, na ba nya laifiɛ.

eng: Happy are people whose lives are harassed because they are righteous, because the kingdom of heaven is theirs.
local: Bɛrɛ bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n dɛɛ ti na minɛm kere bu wuwuruwa ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti bɛrɛ dɛɛ a.

eng: Jesus said, How much bread do you have? They responded, Seven loaves and a few fish.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, Am la kpɔnɔ nyɛ wɔ? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ya la kpɔnɔ nso wɔ, ni jombaa kan kan nbem.

eng: When Herod knew the magi had fooled him, he grew very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the children in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding territory who were two years old and younger, according to the time that he had learned from the magi.
local: Saŋga bo Fɛmɛ Herode ba faari nu ka asara­mbaa kɔrɛsi­yi­fɔm n adadaka i ni, na wɔ fa yaa agaya, na wɔ ma i sojam nɔɔ ka bu kɔ Bɛtilɛhɛm miɛ n nu, ni i ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛ n kɛrɛ su na bu kɔ kun nbaa­biɛsɔ kan kanm kɛrɛ bo ba wu bu kisa, kɔ ju afɔ nnyɔ, dama i sunsun ka saŋga n bo asara­mbaa kɔrɛsi­yi­fɔm n ŋa asara­mbaa n tiɛɛri ni, wɔ ju ka afɔ nnyɔ.

eng: Whatever city or village you go into, find somebody in it who is worthy and stay there until you go on your way.
local: Na wɔ se ka, Miɛ kpiri, wara miɛ kan kan kɛrɛ bo am kɔ wuraari nu, am niɛniɛ sɔnɔ kpa, na am kɔ wo i dɔ, haari am bá fite miɛ n nu.

eng: Other seed fell on good soil and bore fruit, in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Ama, na alɛ­mbaa ni nbem atɔtɔ asiɛn kpa su, na wɔ nyi awura mbaa zɔi zɔi.

eng: Then you wont look like you are fasting to people, but only to your Father who is present in that secret place. Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.
local: adena má minɛm si nu ka a sucici u nɔɔ, má ka u Si Nyɛmɛ bo be ŋu má i ni, na yiri u Si Nyɛmɛ bo i ŋu sa kɛrɛ bo a yo i asiri nu ni n tɔ́ u kaari.

eng: Happy are you when people insult you and harass you and speak all kinds of bad and false things about you, all because of me.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Ambɛrɛ bo minɛm bá kpiɛ am nzukɔ, na bu kére am wuwuruwa, na bu bú kɔrɛ barasu kɛrɛ sɔ am, na i kɛrɛ fite ka am ti m dɛɛm dɛɛ ti, tere­fiɛ wo am dɔ.

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he had compassion for them because they were troubled and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ŋuuri jama n na wɔ si bu aŋunfɔ, dama ba di wahara, na bu la má buka­fɔ. Bu ti ka nbɔɛm bo bu la má sasa­fɔ ɔ.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground.
local: Na Yesu ama jama n nɔɔ ka bu tana asiɛŋgu.

eng: This fulfilled the word spoken through Jeremiah the prophet.
local: Nyini kere ka, kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Jeremiya yo jɔjɔɔri n ayo yii­wa. I ŋa.

eng: When you go into a house, say, Peace.
local: Awuru kɛrɛ bo am kɔ suwura nu, am bisa bu dɔŋgu, na am se bu ka Nyɛmɛ yo bu kisi.

eng: Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i so i sui na i ti.

eng: Stop collecting treasures for your own benefit on earth, where moth and rust eat them and where thieves break in and steal them.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Má am tiɛn ajɛkɛ nɔɔ se durunya n nu fɛn, bo watarawam muka i na i kpɔrɔ, na i tɔ ŋgambulaari, na awiefɔm gusu bá wura wɔ i.

eng: Be full of joy and be glad, because you have a great reward in heaven. In the same way, people harassed the prophets who came before you.
local: Bu bá yo am sɔ, am ma am ahore jɔ agaya, dama am nyá ŋkatɔ bambaka Nyɛmɛ dɔ. I fite ka, kabo bu yo kereeri dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n wuwuruwa n lɛɛ.

eng: Then he said to his disciples, The size of the harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers.
local: Na wɔ se i susu­fɔm n ka, Fiesu diire n sunnu, ama, bo bu kɔ i tambu n sunnu má.

eng: He took the seven loaves of bread and the fish. After he gave thanks, he broke them into pieces and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the crowds.
local: Na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ nso n ni jombaa ni, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ kpiɛkpiɛ i nu, na wɔ fa ama i susu­fɔm ni, na bɛrɛ gusu afa ama jama n ka bu di.

eng: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and much grieving. Rachel weeping for her children, and she did not want to be comforted, because they were no more.
local: Ya ti awie bo ŋga Rama miɛ kpiri nu, sun ni ahoresaaki wo dɔ, bara Rakɛli susun i nbaam busu. Wɔ kete ka má be kpata i, dama i nbaam wo má bɛrɛ kun.

eng: If the house is worthy, give it your blessing of peace. But if the house isnt worthy, take back your blessing.
local: Nzɛn nyini awuru n nu­fɔm deeri am wɔfɔ, am ma nyini kisi n ka bu dɔ, ama, nzɛn ba de má am, am kaaki de nyini kisi n bu dɔ.

eng: Jesus disciples came and said to him, Why do you use parables when you speak to the crowds.
local: Nyini sin na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, aba abisa i ka, Nzu ti ye a nya ba ŋa a jɔjɔ minɛm dɔ, a bo bu miɛɛnu a.

eng: Instead, collect treasures for yourselves in heaven, where moth and rust dont eat them and where thieves dont break in and steal them.
local: Ama, am tiɛn ajɛkɛ nɔɔ se Nyɛmɛ dika n nu, bo watarawam koro muka má i, na i koro tɔ má ŋgambulaari, na awiefɔm gusu koro wura wɔ má i.

eng: You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Its good for nothing except to be thrown away and trampled under peoples feet.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Ambɛrɛ, m susu­fɔm ni, am ti ka njin wo durunya n nu a. Ama, nzɛn njin fiɛfiɛ nya ba saari wieeri, i koro yo má i fiɛfiɛ kun. Nyini saŋga ni, wɔ ka ŋgbɛɛn lɛɛ, ŋka bu fá butu wɔ, na minɛm tiɛntiɛn saŋga i ni fa.

eng: Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Nyini ti, am sere Mibiɛ n bo i dɛɛ la fie n ka i sunma juma­yo­fɔm na bu kɔ i tambu.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full. The disciples collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Bu kɛrɛ diiri, na bu kunnu ayi. Na i susu­fɔm n atambu sinsin kaa­wa n kandiɛ nso.

eng: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
local: Saŋga bo Fɛmɛ Herode ba wuuri ni, na Micɛra n dɛɛ mɛrɛkɛ aba Yosefu dɔ alɛlɛ nu Ijipiti asiɛn n su, aba ase i ka.

eng: If anyone refuses to welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet as you leave that house or city.
local: Sasu bekun, nzɛn awuru, wara miɛ bo am kɔ wuraari nu, na ba de má am wɔfɔ, wara ba soro má am su ka bu ti am nɔaniɛ ni, am fite yaki nyini awuru n wara miɛ ni, na am kpɔnkpɔn yi am jaa tuturi kɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Because they havent received the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but you have.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Ambɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ ma am asi i fɛmɛya n nu asiri jɔrɛ kɛrɛ a, ama, bɛrɛ bo ba ka ni, i ma bu si má i.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
local: Dama deke bo u ajɛkɛ wo ni, u ahore­mbaa kɛrɛ gusu wó dɔ ɔ.

eng: You are the light of the world. A city on top of a hill cant be hidden.
local: Ambɛrɛ ti wein bo i ta ma durunya n nu­fɔm a. Miɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo kpɛrɛ su, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋu i wɔ.

eng: Four thousand men ate, plus women and children.
local: Minɛm bo bu diiri diire ni, nbiɛsɔm ŋgumi kɔ yasuuri akpii nna a, ba kan má nbaram ni nbaatumam awura má nu.

eng: I assure you that it will be more bearable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on Judgment Day than it will be for that city.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, jina cɛɛn, Nyɛmɛ cín nyini miɛ n nu­fɔm sui tara Sodom ni Gomora miɛm n nu satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni.

eng: Get up, the angel said, and take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are dead.
local: Yasu fa baa n ni i ni, na am kpie am nyi kɔ Yisareli asiɛn su, dama minɛm bo bu kpiiri ka bu kun baa ni, ba wu.

eng: For those who have will receive more and they will have more than enough. But as for those who dont have, even the little they have will be taken away from them.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ nya deke be Nyɛmɛ dɔ na i mi si i bita, Nyɛmɛ búka i su wɔ, na i nyá beberebe, ama, bo i si má i bita, haari ni nyini kaan n bo i la i ni, Nyɛmɛ dé i dɔ ɔ.

eng: The eye is the lamp of the body. Therefore, if your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Ya nyu­mbaa ni ti ka fitana ma ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa n a. Nzɛn u nyu­mbaa nya ti kpa, u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ tá wein n wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand, and it shines on all who are in the house.
local: Sɔnɔ be nya so má fitana su na i fa gaŋga butu su, ama, i fa taka deke su aŋgoro wɔ na i ta wein ma awuru n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ.

eng: Look, Im sending you as sheep among wolves. Therefore, be wise as snakes and innocent as doves.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Am ti fɛn. M susunma am wɔ lɛɛ kabo nbɔɛm kɔ saŋga bedim nu barasu ni. Nyini ti, am fa ŋgɔnlɛ na am tara am ŋu ka woom, na am yo am ŋu bɛnlɛ ka tiiwam.

eng: After dismissing the crowds, Jesus got into the boat and came to the region of Magadan.
local: Na Yesu anya kaari jama ni, na ba kɔ awuru, na wɔ nya awura lee nu, akɔ awɔrɛ n bo bu fere i ka Magadan n nu.

eng: Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Israel.
local: Na Yosefu ayasu afa baa n ni i ni, na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ Yisareli asiɛn n su.

eng: This is why I speak to the crowds in parables: although they see, they dont really see; and although they hear, they dont really hear or understand.
local: Deke bo i ti na m bo bu miɛɛnu n la ka, bu niɛ wɔ, ama, bu ŋu má pui. Bu so bu sui wɔ, ama, bu ti má deke n bo bu so bu sui ka bu ti i n bu.

eng: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light in you is darkness, how terrible that darkness will be!.
local: Ama, nzɛn u nyu­mbaa nya ti má kpa, u ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ wo awosin nu wɔ lɛɛ. Nyini ti, nzɛn wein wo má u dɔ, nyini n la ka, a wo awosin bambaka bo i la má aŋusu nu lɛɛ.

eng: In the same way, let your light shine before people, so they can see the good things you do and praise your Father who is in heaven.
local: Nyini ti, am ma am wein ta durunya n nu, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋu am sakpa yoo­wa, adena bu kansi am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni.

eng: Watch out for people—because they will hand you over to councils and they will beat you in their synagogues.
local: Am si am ŋu sa, dama minɛ bá fa am wura jɔrɛ­di­fɔm saa nu, na bu fín am ŋgbere bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu.

eng: But when he heard that Archelaus ruled over Judea in place of his father Herod, Joseph was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he went to the area of Galilee.
local: Ama, saŋga bo i tiiri ka Akilawu adi i si Fɛmɛ Herode fɛmɛya n Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu ni, na sɛrɛ ati i ni kabo i kɔ wo dɔ. Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ aba i dɔ alɛlɛ nu, aba akere i kabo i yo, na wɔ yasu akɔ Galili awɔrɛ n nu akɔ awo miɛ be bo bu fere i ka Nazarɛti n su.

eng: What Isaiah prophesied has become completely true for them: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing .
local: Bu ti sɔ n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo jɔjɔ niiri bu n a, i ŋa, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, Am só am sui ni nahɔrɛ su, ama, am tí má deke bo am so am sui ka am ti i n bu. Am káaki niɛ ni nahɔrɛ su, ama, am ŋú má pui.

eng: No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Sɔnɔ kun koro saŋga su má mibiɛm nnyɔ. Nzɛn i ŋa i yó sɔ, i kpɔ́ kun wɔ, na i kóro nyiŋga ni, wara i bóro i nyi su kun n wɔ, na i bú nyiŋga ni ŋgbɛɛn. Am koro saŋga su má Nyɛmɛ ni ajɛkɛ.

eng: Dont even begin to think that I have come to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I havent come to do away with them but to fulfill them.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Fa kɔ Moyisi nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ keree­wa n su, má am sunsun ka ma ba ka m ba cɛ bu se wɔ. Ai, ma ba má ka m ba cɛ bu se wɔ, ama, ma ba ka m ma i kɛrɛ bá yo yii­wa a.

eng: They will haul you in front of governors and even kings because of me so that you may give your testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
local: Mini dɛɛ ti, bu cín am wura gominam ni nfɛmɛm saa nu. Nyini má am atin na am di m daani kere bɛrɛ a, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ.

eng: He settled in a city called Nazareth so that what was spoken through the prophets might be fulfilled: He will be called a Nazarene.
local: Nyini n kere ka, kabo Nyɛmɛ ma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n jɔjɔɔri n yo yii­wa a, fa kɔ Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n dɔ. I ŋa, Bu fére i ka Nazarɛti­fɔ ɔ.

eng: For this peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them .
local: Dama ahin minɛm tii ti kekereke, ba tɔ bu sui na má bu ti, ba kata bu nyu­mbaa na má bu ŋu. Má nyini, bu nyu­mbaa ŋú i wɔ, na bu sui tí, na bu sí bu ahore su, na bu káaki bu wo, na m má bu nya laifiɛ.

eng: I say to you very seriously that as long as heaven and earth exist, neither the smallest letter nor even the smallest stroke of a pen will be erased from the Law until everything there becomes a reality.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, kabo aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu toko wo bɛrɛ ni, bu yí má nɔaniɛ n be haari kampiti sɔ n mmɔɔ fite má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu, haari na i deke n kɛrɛ ba awieeri.

eng: Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what youll eat or what youll drink, or about your body, what youll wear. Isnt life more than food and the body more than clothes?.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Nyini ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má am tana sukoko fa kɔ am ŋgɔɔ su, kabo am dí, ni kabo am nú, na má am kaaki sukoko fa kɔ am ŋuniɛ su, kabo am nyá suturam bo am fá wura na am kára. Dama ŋgɔɔ n tara diire, na aŋuniɛ­mbaa n gusu tara suturam, má sɔ?.

eng: Whenever they hand you over, dont worry about how to speak or what you will say, because what you can say will be given to you at that moment.
local: Saŋga bo bu bá faari am wuraari jɔrɛ­di­fɔm saa nu ni, má am ma am ahore saaki fa kɔ kabo am kɔ́ jɔjɔ barasu. Nyini saŋga n ba juuri, Nyɛmɛ má am nɔaniɛ ye am jɔjɔ a.

eng: Therefore, whoever ignores one of the least of these commands and teaches others to do the same will be called the lowest in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever keeps these commands and teaches people to keep them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i buuri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n kampiti sɔ mmɔɔ ŋgbɛɛn, na i kereeri minɛm ka bɛrɛ gusu yo sɔ, bu fére i mi ka sɔnɔ kaan Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i suuri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su na i kereeri minɛm ka bu gusu yo sɔ, bu fére i mi ka sɔnɔ bambaka Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: Look at the birds in the sky. They dont sow seed or harvest grain or gather crops into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Arent you worth much more than they are?.
local: Am niɛ anumam, haari ni bo bu dɔ má ni, na bu kaaki kpiɛ má ni, bu tambu gɔ má bondo nu gusu, se am Si Nyɛmɛ ma bu diire ye bu di a. Ambɛrɛ la anyunuyi tara anumam, má sɔ?.

eng: Happy are your eyes because they see. Happy are your ears because they hear.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ la tere­fiɛ, dama am ŋu asi, na am kaaki ti asui.

eng: You arent doing the talking, but the Spirit of my Father is doing the talking through you.
local: Nɔaniɛ ni fíte má am dɔ, ama, i fíte am Si Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ Awiɛwiɛ n dɔ, ye am jɔ́jɔ a.

eng: I say to you that unless your righteousness is greater than the righteousness of the legal experts and the Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Nyini ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, kana am bá wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, má ka am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n tara Farasi­fɔm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm dɛɛ ni.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Am nu, ŋma tana koko fa kɔ i koroo­wam su ye i mi koro buka i ŋgɔɔ tintiin su haari kaan sɔ a?.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and righteous people wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni minɛm beberebe bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n kɛrɛ koroori ka bu ŋu deke bo am aŋu i ni, ama, ba ŋu má i, na ba koro ka bu ti deke bo am ati i ni, ama, ba ti má i.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to be executed. A father will turn his child in. Children will defy their parents and have them executed.
local: Gɔrɔm ni niɛmam bá tɔ bu beŋgu ma bu kpɔ­fɔm na bu kún bu, na nbaasim tɔ́ bu nbaam, na nbaam kéte bu wu­fɔm ŋu, na bu má minɛm kun bu wu­fɔm ni.

eng: You have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago, Dont commit murder, and all who commit murder will be in danger of judgment.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Am ati ka bu seeri ya nam ka, Má a kun sɔnɔ, na Nzɛn wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a kuunri sɔnɔ, bu dí u jɔrɛ.

eng: And why do you worry about clothes? Notice how the lilies in the field grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth.
local: Ye nzu ti ye am ma suturam sa miɛ am a? Am niɛ kabo wuram bo pum barasu barasu a. Bu yo má juma be, bu tu má gise.

eng: Consider then the parable of the farmer.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ni ka, Am so am sui, na m se am miɛɛnu n bu, fa kɔ dɔdɔɔri­fɔ n bo i sandiiri alɛ­mbaa n su.

eng: Everyone will hate you on account of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Mini dɛɛ ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kpɔ́ am. Ama, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a mindi deeri nyini n kɛrɛ kɔ juuri i awieeri su, Nyɛmɛ dé u tii ji.

eng: But I say to you that everyone who is angry with their brother or sister will be in danger of judgment. If they say to their brother or sister, You idiot, they will be in danger of being condemned by the governing council. And if they say, You fool, they will be in danger of fiery hell.
local: Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a faari yaa u beŋgu ŋu, wɔ yo daka ka bu di u jɔrɛ. Sasu bekun, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a seeri u beŋgu ka i ti sɔnɔ ŋgbɛɛn, wɔ yo daka ka bu fa u jina Yuda­fɔm jɔrɛ­di­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nyunu na bu di u jɔrɛ. Na nzɛn wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a seeri u beŋgu ka i ti sinnzin, Nyɛmɛ fá u wura cɛɛma sin n nu.

eng: But I say to you that even Solomon in all of his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, haari Fɛmɛ Solomon, ni i neŋge n kɛrɛ bo i yo la i ni, wɔ la má sutura bo i yo nyumi juuri nyini wura pum ni be.

eng: Whenever people hear the word about the kingdom and dont understand it, the evil one comes and carries off what was planted in their hearts. This is the seed that was sown on the path.
local: Nbem de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n fa kɔ i fɛmɛya n su kabo alɛ­mbaa n tɔtɔɔri atin nɔɔ n wɔ. Bu nya tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na bu ti má i bu, i cɛ má, na Sitana bo i ti sɔnɔ tiɛ ni, bá yi nɔaniɛ n fite bu ahore su, a ŋu ka bu wara afi i sa.

eng: Whenever they harass you in one city, escape to the next, because I assure that you will not go through all the cities of Israel before the Human One comes.
local: Saŋga bo bu bá sukere am wuwuruwa miɛ be su, am ŋmati kɔ miɛ nyiŋga su. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, am koro kan má Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ bara yɛ má Yisareli miɛm kɛrɛ su ka, ye mini Adamande Wa ni kpíe m nyi ba.

eng: Therefore, if you bring your gift to the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn a fa u aceeri aba ka a ba sere Nyɛmɛ, na a teŋge su ka u ni u beŋgu be la ŋgasi.

eng: As for the seed that was spread on rocky ground, this refers to people who hear the word and immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Nbem gusu de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kabo alɛ­mbaa tɔtɔɔri tiɛka su n wɔ. Bu nya tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, a ŋu ka ba de i ndende ni ahorejɔ.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, wont God do much more for you, you people of weak faith?.
local: Nzɛn wuram bo bu wo bɛrɛ nyuma, na ahunma a ŋú ka ba kpiɛ bu na bu yara bu, ye Nyɛmɛ niɛ bu dika, nzu ti ye i níɛ má ambɛrɛ bo am tara wuram co n a? Ambɛrɛ, am yarada n ti kaan sɔ.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, and slaves arent greater than their master.
local: Sɔnsɔn­fɔ tara má i kere­fɔ, akɔɔ gusu tara má i mibiɛ.

eng: leave your gift at the altar and go. First make things right with your brother or sister and then come back and offer your gift.
local: yaki u aceeri n yoo­wa yoo­biri n ka, na a kɔ na am kɔ yo am afiɛn dɔŋgu, na a nya ba fa u aceeri n ma Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: ama, i da má bu ahore su. Bu kaaki ti ka alɛ­mbaa n bo bu bunlui akpama abi má n wɔ. I woori bu dɔ kaan sɔ, na nzɛn wahara, wara kekereke be ba tuuri bu, fa kɔ bu Nyɛmɛ su n su, a ŋu ka ba fa ati.

eng: Therefore, dont worry and say, What are we going to eat? or What are we going to drink? or What are we going to wear.
local: Nyini ti, má am tana koko se ka, Ye ya dí nzu wɔ? wara Ya nú nzu wɔ? wara Ya wúra nzu wɔ.

eng: Its enough for disciples to be like their teacher and slaves like their master. If they have called the head of the house Beelzebul, its certain that they will call the members of his household by even worse names.
local: Nzɛn sɔnsɔn­fɔ n ba juuri i kere­fɔ n juu­biri, i ti ye ni ka i nyunu yi i tii. Na nzɛn akɔɔ n ba juuri i mibiɛ n juu­biri, i ti ye ni ka i nyunu yi i tii. Nzɛn bu nya fereeri awuru­fɔ­biɛ n ka Bɛlazɛbuli, bo bu kaaki fere i ka Sitana ni, nyini saŋga bu fére i dika­fɔm bɛrɛ kɛrɛ ka sɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Be sure to make friends quickly with your opponents while you are with them on the way to court. Otherwise, they will haul you before the judge, the judge will turn you over to the officer of the court, and you will be thrown into prison.
local: Nzɛn u ni be la jɔrɛ, na nzɛn i koro ka i fa u kɔ jɔrɛ dii­biri, jɔkɔti tara i mi n jaa, na má jɔrɛ n ju jɔrɛ dii­biri n ni, má nyini, i mi ni fá u kɔ wura jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n saa nu, na yiri gusu fá u ma dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ ni, na yiri gusu nyá nyi u.

eng: As for the seed that was spread among thorny plants, this refers to those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the false appeal of wealth choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: Nbem gusu de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kabo alɛ­mbaa n tɔtɔɔri lɔlɔɛ nu n wɔ. Bu nya tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, a ŋu ka bu sunsunni kɛrɛ wo deke bo bu yó na bu asiri káta su, na bu koro kabo bu yó na bu nyá ajɛkɛ kɛrɛ, na bu ahore bi fa kɔ durunya n nu neŋgem su, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n koro da má bu ahore su na i yo juma. Bu ti ka alɛ­mbaa n bo i wura má mbaa n wɔ.

eng: Gentiles long for all these things. Your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
local: Dama bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni, bɛrɛ sunsun sɔ saŋga kɛrɛ a. Am Si Nyɛmɛ si ka am koro nyinim n kɛrɛ.

eng: Therefore, dont be afraid of those people because nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Nyini ti, má am soro sɛrɛ ni minɛm. Dama deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i wo fiɛɛ­wa nu na bu bá ŋu má i, deke be gusu wo má bɛrɛ bo i wo asiri nu na bu bá si má i.

eng: I say to you in all seriousness that you wont get out of there until youve paid the very last penny.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u n a, nzɛn a woori dɔ na a tɔ má kaari n kɛrɛ awie má, a nyá má atin fite má dan­saraka n nu.

eng: As for what was planted on good soil, this refers to those who hear and understand, and bear fruit and produce—in one case a yield of one hundred to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of thirty to one.
local: Ama, nbem de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kabo alɛ­mbaa n tɔtɔɔri asiɛn kpa su n wɔ. Bu nya tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na bu de i, na bu fa yo juma, na i yo kpɛrɛ beberebe.

eng: Instead, desire first and foremost Gods kingdom and Gods righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
local: Nyini ti, am du mɔ kpini Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ni sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n busu ka, na i nyá ba fa nyini dekem n kɛrɛ buka am su.

eng: What I say to you in the darkness, tell in the light; and what you hear whispered, announce from the rooftops.
local: Deke kɛrɛ bo m jɔjɔ seeri am kɔŋguɛ, wɔ yo daka ka am jɔjɔ i wiɛsu wɔ. Na deke kɛrɛ bo m tuuri i asuturi am dɔ, wɔ yo daka ka am bo i ndoori na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti i.

eng: You have heard that it was said, Dont commit adultery.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Am ati ka ba se ka, Má a saaki ajaa ciire.

eng: Jesus told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like someone who planted good seed in his field.
local: Na Yesu abo bu miɛɛnu be bekun. I ŋa, Kabo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su yaa. Sɔnɔ be kɔ sandiiri nvoni i fiesu a.

eng: Therefore, stop worrying about tomorrow, because tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
local: Nyini ti, má am koko fa kɔ ahunma su, ahunma mmɔɔ kpíni i dɛɛ. Dama nyuma dɛɛ sunsunni mmɔɔ asɛ su.

eng: Dont be afraid of those who kill the body but cant kill the soul. Instead, be afraid of the one who can destroy both body and soul in hell.
local: Má am soro minɛm bo bu koro kun aŋuniɛ­mbaa n na bu koro kun má awiɛwiɛ n ni. Ama, am soro Nyɛmɛ bo i koro saaki awiɛwiɛ n ni aŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ, cɛɛma sin nu ni.

eng: But I say to you that every man who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery in his heart.
local: Ama, mini, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a niɛ bara, na u konvi wuraari i, i ti ka a saaki ajaa ciire u ahore su wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: While people were sleeping, an enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat and went away.
local: Cɛɛn n be kɔŋguɛ n sɔ ɔ, saŋga bo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ada adaafi ni, na i kpɔ­fɔ be anya ayasu akɔ afa jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni asandi nvoni fie n nu.

eng: Arent two sparrows sold for a small coin? But not one of them will fall to the ground without your Father knowing about it already.
local: Am si ka njɔrɛm nnu ŋwaa ti kaan sɔ ɔ, má sɔ? Ama, bu kun n kpein mmɔɔ tɔ́ má asiɛŋgu bo i ti má ya Si Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu.

eng: And if your right eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body be thrown into hell.
local: Nzɛn u nyu­mbaa n kun cin u wuraari satiɛ nu, lokoti yi i tu. I ti ye ni ka a fun u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue kun, ni bo a fa u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ wura cɛɛma sin nu.

eng: When the stalks sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
local: Saŋga bo nvoni n ba fi sukɔ nyinsɛ ni, na jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n gusu atu asaŋga nvoni kpa n nu.

eng: Even the hairs of your head are all counted.
local: Haari am tii­ŋmiɛ kɛrɛ mmɔɔ bo i wo am tii su ni, Nyɛmɛ si i kɛrɛ nɔ.

eng: And if your right hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better that you lose a part of your body than that your whole body go into hell.
local: Nzɛn u saa fɔmbɔrɔ cin u wuraari satiɛ nu, kaba kpiɛ i tu. I ti ye ni ka a fun u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue kun, ni bo a fa u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ wura cɛɛma sin nu.

eng: The servants of the landowner came and said to him, Master, didnt you plant good seed in your field? Then how is it that it has weeds.
local: Na awuru­fɔ­biɛ ni akɔɔm aba i dɔ, aba ase i ka, Ya mibiɛ, m ŋa ya sandiiri nvoni kpa fie n nu e. Ye jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n fiteeri ni, kɔ saŋgaari nu a.

eng: Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Nyini ti, má am soro sɛrɛ. Am la anyunuyi Nyɛmɛ dɔ tara njɔrɛm beberebe.

eng: It was said, Whoever divorces his wife must give her a divorce certificate.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Sasu bekun, ba kaaki ase am ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yaki i yi ajaa, i ti ye ni ka i mi ma bara n ajaa yaki kadasi bo i kere ka i hun ayaki i ajaa.

eng: An enemy has done this, he answered. The servants said to him, Do you want us to go and gather them.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, M kpɔ­fɔ be kɔ yoori nyini a. Na i akɔɔm n abisa i ka, Ye a koro ka ya kɔ tutu jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n butu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a.

eng: Therefore, everyone who acknowledges me before people, I also will acknowledge before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kan kereeri minɛm ka i ti m susu­fɔ, mini gusu bá kan kere m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n ka i mi ti m dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: But I say to you that whoever divorces his wife except for sexual unfaithfulness forces her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
local: Ama, mini, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i faari i yi jiiri, na wɔ yo má ka bara n akɔ somaya ye i fa i jiiri a, na nzɛn bara n kɔ jaari biɛsɔ fɔfɔrɛ, i mi n ma bara n asaaki ajaa ciire lɛɛ. Na sɔnɔ bo i faari bara n jaari, yiri gusu asaaki ajaa ciire lɛɛ.

eng: But the landowner said, No, because if you gather the weeds, youll pull up the wheat along with them.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Am yaki i, dama nzɛn am ŋa am tutu jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni ni, am sáŋga tutu i ni nvoni kpa n kɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: But everyone who denies me before people, I also will deny before my Father who is in heaven.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri minɛm nyunu ka i ti má m susu­fɔ, mini gusu bá kete m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n nyunu ka i mi ti má m dɛɛ.

eng: Again you have heard that it was said to those who lived long ago: Dont make a false solemn pledge, but you should follow through on what you have pledged to the Lord.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Am akaaki ati ka ba se ya nam ka, Má a tan ndiɛ na a kaaki wɔrɔ u nɔɔ, ama, ma u sui wo ndiɛ bo a tan i ama Micɛra n su, na a yo i.

eng: Let both grow side by side until the harvest. And at harvesttime Ill say to the harvesters,First gather the weeds and tie them together in bundles to be burned. But bring the wheat into my barn.
local: Am ma bu kɛrɛ saŋga nyi, haari nvoni n kpiɛ bá ju. Nyini saŋga na m nya se nvoni kpiɛ­fɔm ni ka bu kpiɛ i kɛrɛ na bu cin wɔrɔ jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n tiɛn i nɔɔ yara i ka. Nyini saŋga na bu nya tiɛn nvoni kpa ba gɔ m bondo nu.

eng: Dont think that Ive come to bring peace to the earth. I havent come to bring peace but a sword.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Má am sunsun ka ma ba ka m fa tana­laifiɛ ba ma durunya n nu­fɔm wɔ. Ma ba má ka m fa tana­laifiɛ ba, ama, m faari luɛ baari a.

eng: But I say to you that you must not pledge at all. You must not pledge by heaven, because its Gods throne.
local: Ama, mini, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má am tan ndiɛ mmɔɔ fieo. Má am tan aŋgoro, dama Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n woo­biri lɛɛ.

eng: He told another parable to them: The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in his field.
local: Na Yesu abo bu miɛɛnu be bekun. I ŋa, Kabo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti yaa, i ti ka alɛ­mbaa kaan kabo nyɛm mbaa ti n wɔ, bo sɔnɔ be dɔɔri i fiesu ni.

eng: Ive come to turn a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law .
local: M baari ka m ba kpura baa ni i si tii wɔ, na m kpura baa­bara ni i ni tii, na m kaaki kpura baa n ni, ni i wa yi tii.

eng: You must not pledge by the earth, because its Gods footstool. You must not pledge by Jerusalem, because its the city of the great king.
local: Má a tan asiɛŋgu gusu, dama i ti Nyɛmɛ jaa gbagbaa jinaa­biri wɔ. Má a tan Yɛrusalem, dama i ti ya Micɛra Fɛmɛ n dɛɛ miɛ kpiri wɔ.

eng: Its the smallest of all seeds. But when its grown, its the largest of all vegetable plants. It becomes a tree so that the birds in the sky come and nest in its branches.
local: Yiri ti alɛ­mbaam kɛrɛ kaan, ama, bu nya ba dɔɔri na i fiiri, i tara toro nyawam n kɛrɛ bambaka, i koro nyi na i tɛtɛrɛki asa­mbaam bo anumam koro yo bu tiɛɛrim su.

eng: Peoples enemies are members of their own households .
local: na sɔnɔ dika­fɔm káaki i kpɔ­fɔm.

eng: And you must not pledge by your head, because you cant turn one hair white or black.
local: Má a kaaki se ka a tan u tii­awa, dama a koro yo má u tii­ŋmiɛ kun mmɔɔ fofoe, wara biri.

eng: He told them another parable: The kingdom of heaven is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through all the dough.
local: Na Yesu akaaki abo bu miɛɛnu be bekun. I ŋa, Kabo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti yaa, i ti ka ayiri bo bu fa gɔ kpɔnɔ samɛnɛ nu na i yasu ni, na bara be fa gɔɔri samɛnɛ beberebe nu, na i kɛrɛ yasuuri n wɔ.

eng: Those who love father or mother more than me arent worthy of me. Those who love son or daughter more than me arent worthy of me.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i si, wara i ni tara mini, i ti má ye ni ka i mi wo m dɔ. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i wa baa­biɛsɔ, wara baa­bara tara mini, i ti má ye ni ka i mi wo m dɔ.

eng: Let your yes mean yes, and your no mean no. Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
local: Am ma am nɔaniɛ kɛrɛ yo tika. Nzɛn i ti Ɛɛn, am se ka, Ɛɛn. Nzɛn i ti Ai gusu a, am se ka, Ai. Sin nyini sin, nɔaniɛ kɛrɛ bo i buka su n fite Sitana dɔ ɔ.

eng: Jesus said all these things to the crowds in parables, and he spoke to them only in parables.
local: Ahin n kɛrɛ, Yesu boori jama n miɛɛnu wɔ. Nahɔrɛ, i se má bu pui, má ka miɛɛnu ye i bo bu a.

eng: Those who dont pick up their crosses and follow me arent worthy of me.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sɔ má i yiwee baka jaanu n na i su m su, i ti má ye ni ka i mi wo m dɔ.

eng: You have heard that it was said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth .
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Am ati ka ba se ka, Nzɛn sɔnɔ yi u nyu­mbaa, wɔrɔ gusu yi i mi dɛɛ, na nzɛn i bo tuuri u jee, wɔrɔ gusu tu i mi dɛɛ.

eng: This was to fulfill what the prophet spoke: Ill speak in parables; Ill declare what has been hidden since the beginning of the world.
local: I yoori sɔ, adena nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n yo jɔjɔɔri n yo yii­wa a. I ŋa, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, M bó bu miɛɛnu wɔ, na m má bu si deke n bo ba si má i, fite haari durunya n bumboori ni.

eng: Those who find their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives because of me will find them.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i kpíni i ŋgɔɔ busu, i mi bá fun i, ama, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tɔɔri i ŋgɔɔ mini dɛɛ ti, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: But I say to you that you must not oppose those who want to hurt you. If people slap you on your right cheek, you must turn the left cheek to them as well.
local: Ama, mini, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nzɛn sɔnɔ yoori u satiɛ, má a wɔrɔ satiɛ ma i mi. Haari nzɛn be fiinri u nyɔɔnu, kaaki bue nyiŋga n gusu ma i na i fin buka su.

eng: Jesus left the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu cin yakiiri jama n na i kɔ wuraari awuru n nu ni, na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, aba ase i ka, Ya koro ka a yi jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n dɛɛ miɛɛnu n bu kere ya.

eng: Those who receive you are also receiving me, and those who receive me are receiving the one who sent me.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de am wɔfɔ, i mi de mini wɔ lɛɛ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de mini, i mi de yiri bo i sunmaari m wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: When they wish to haul you to court and take your shirt, let them have your coat too.
local: Nzɛn be cici u lekisi ka i fa u kɔ jɔrɛ dii­biri, na i de u tanlɛɛ kaan n be i wo i bu ni, kaba yi i tii su dɛɛ bambaka n mmɔɔ buka i su.

eng: Jesus replied, The one who plants the good seed is the Human One.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Sɔnɔ n bo i sandiiri nvoni kpa n la mini Adamande Wa ni.

eng: Those who receive a prophet as a prophet will receive a prophets reward. Those who receive a righteous person as a righteous person will receive a righteous persons reward.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n dɛɛ ti, i mi nyá ŋkatɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nya i n wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na wɔ de i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n dɛɛ ti, i mi nyá ŋkatɔ kabo sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n nya i n wɔ.

eng: When they force you to go one mile, go with them two.
local: Nzɛn faŋga­fɔ be miɛɛri u ka a sɔ i sɔɔri kɔ ju ajaari natii­wa akpii kun ma i, kaba fa i kɔ ju akpii nnyɔ.

eng: The field is the world. And the good seeds are the followers of the kingdom. But the weeds are the followers of the evil one.
local: Fie n la durunya ni, na nvoni n la minɛm bo bu fa Nyɛmɛ yo bu fɛmɛ ni. Na jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n la Sitana dɛɛ minɛm ni.

eng: I assure you that everybody who gives even a cup of cold water to these little ones because they are my disciples will certainly be rewarded.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i saari nzue niniki kaan sɔ mmɔɔ, maari m susu­fɔ n bo i ti bu kɛrɛ kaan n kun, ka i mi ti m dɛɛ susu­fɔ n ti, i mi nyá i ŋkatɔ.

eng: Give to those who ask, and dont refuse those who wish to borrow from you.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i sereeri u deke be, má a kpiɛ i, na sɔnɔ bo i ŋa a fiɛ i deke be, má a kete.

eng: The enemy who planted them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the present age. The harvesters are the angels.
local: Ye kpɔ­fɔ n bo i fa jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n saŋga nvoni kpa n nu n la Sitana. Nvoni kpiɛ saŋga n la durunya n awieeri, ye nvoni n kpiɛ­fɔm la Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm ni.

eng: You have heard that it was said, You must love your neighbor and hate your enemy.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Am ati ka ba se ka, A koro sɔnɔ bo i koro u ni, na a kpɔ sɔnɔ bo i kpɔ u ni.

eng: Just as people gather weeds and burn them in the fire, so it will be at the end of the present age.
local: Kabo bu tiɛntiɛɛnri jaŋmɔŋgu nvoni n nɔɔ, na bu yaraari i ni, sɔ ye i bá yo durunya n awieeri a.

eng: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who harass you.
local: Ama, mini, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, am koro am kpɔ­fɔm, na am sere Nyɛmɛ ma bo bu kere am wuwuruwa ni.

eng: so that you will be acting as children of your Father who is in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both the evil and the good and sends rain on both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: adena am yó am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni nbaam. Dama yiri ma wiɛ fite ma satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ni sakpa­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ a, na i kaaki ma nzue tɔ ma bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, ni bo bu yo má sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n kɛrɛ.

eng: The Human One will send his angels, and they will gather out of his kingdom all things that cause people to fall away and all people who sin.
local: Mini Adamande Wa ni súnma Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm, na bu bá tambu yi satiɛ­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ, ni deke kɛrɛ bo i wura minɛm konvi na bu yo satiɛ.

eng: He will throw them into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm ni fá bu tu cɛɛma sin nu, dɔ na bu sún, na bu kán bu saa.

eng: If you love only those who love you, what reward do you have? Dont even the tax collectors do the same?.
local: Nzɛn am nya koroori minɛm bo bu koro am ŋgumi ni, nzu ŋkatɔ, ye am nya i a? Dama haari lampo­de­fɔm bo bu sisi minɛm ni mmɔɔ yo sɔ ɔ.

eng: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in their Fathers kingdom. Those who have ears should hear.
local: Na bo bu yo Nyɛmɛ sa koroo­wa n yiri nya yo wein ka wiɛ, bu Si Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu. Bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i so i sui na i ti.

eng: And if you greet only your brothers and sisters, what more are you doing? Dont even the Gentiles do the same?.
local: Na nzɛn am bisa am danfom ŋgumi ahin, am sunsun ka am ayo nzu sakpa ye be koro yo má i a? Bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni mmɔɔ koro yo sɔ, má sɔ?.

eng: Therefore, just as your heavenly Father is complete in showing love to everyone, so also you must be complete.
local: Tɔ, am ma am woo­wa n ti kpa bo i la má aŋusu, kabo am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n dɛɛ woo­wa n ti kpa bo i la má aŋusu ni.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a treasure that somebody hid in a field, which someone else found and covered up. Full of joy, the finder sold everything and bought that field.
local: Na Yesu akaaki abo bu miɛɛnu be bekun. I ŋa, Kabo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ niɛ i minɛm su yaa, i ti kabo sɔnɔ be kɔ ŋuuri ajɛkɛ be, bo ba fundu i ase fie be nu n wɔ. I ŋuuri i sɔ n na wɔ kaaki akata su, na ahorejɔ aba i dɔ agaya, na wɔ kɔ atɔ i neŋge kɛrɛ bo i la i ni, na wɔ kpie i nyi aba ato nyini asiɛn ni.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Bekun, kabo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ niɛ i minɛm su yaa, i ti kabo wata­fɔ be bo i nati niɛniɛ afere fofoem bo bu la anyunuyi agaya na i to n wɔ.

eng: When he found one very precious pearl, he went and sold all that he owned and bought it.
local: Saŋga bo i ba kɔ ŋuuri be bo i la anyunuyi na i la gɔ agaya ni, na wɔ kɔ atɔ i neŋge kɛrɛ, na wɔ fa ato i.

eng: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that people threw into the lake and gathered all kinds of fish.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Bekun, kabo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ niɛ i minɛm su yaa, i ti ka diɛndiɛ be bo bu fa sandiiri nzue nu, na bu taraari jombaa barasu barasu kɛrɛ n wɔ.

eng: When it was full, they pulled it to the shore, where they sat down and put the good fish together into containers. But the bad fish they threw away.
local: Saŋga bo diɛndiɛ n yiiri ni jombaam ni, na ba cin i afite kpɛrɛ su, na ba tana ka bu yiyi jombaam ni. Bo bu nya ŋuuri ka bu ti kpakpam ni, na bu fa bɛrɛ ji bu jombaa tara bɔtɔm nu. Na bu nya fa bo bu ti má kpam fikindi tu.

eng: Thats the way it will be at the end of the present age. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the righteous people.
local: I bá yo sɔ ɔ, durunya n awieeri, Nyɛmɛ má i mɛrɛkɛm ni nati ba yi satiɛ­yo­fɔm n fite bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ nu.

eng: and will throw the evil ones into a burning furnace. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: na i fa satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni tu cɛɛma sin nu. Dɔ na bu sún, na bu kán bu saa.

eng: Have you understood all these things?Jesus asked. They said to him, Yes.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, Am ati ahin miɛɛnu n kɛrɛ bu? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ya ti i bu.

eng: Then he said to them, Therefore, every legal expert who has been trained as a disciple for the kingdom of heaven is like the head of a household who brings old and new things out of their treasure chest.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Tɔ, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kere Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na i siiri kabo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ atin ti barasu, i mi ayo ka awuru­fɔ­biɛ bo i yi i neŋge fɔfɔrɛ ni i neŋge dawa fite i neŋge woo­biri sɔɔ n nu, na i fa ma minɛm wɔ.

eng: When Jesus finished these parables, he departed.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu boori nyini miɛɛnu kɛrɛ kɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ yasu dika n nu dɔ.

eng: When he came to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were surprised and said, Where did he get this wisdom? Where did he get the power to work miracles?.
local: Na wɔ kpie i nyi akɔ i bɔsu. Na wɔ kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ akere minɛm, bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu. Na bo bu tiiri i keree­wa n kɛrɛ ayo alekutura. Na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Nifansu ye ahin sɔnɔ n nyaari nyini alecira n ni yiko koro yo nyini alekutura sam n a?.

eng: Isnt he the carpenters son? Isnt his mother named Mary? Arent James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?.
local: Má kampenta n wa n lɛɛ? Má i ni la Mariya a? Má Yakobo, ni Yosefu, ni Simon, ni Yudasi n gɔrɔ n lɛɛ?.

eng: And his sisters, arent they here with us? Where did this man get all this.
local: Am niɛ e. Má i niɛma nbaram kɛrɛ wo ya nu fɛn n a? Ye nifansu ye ahin sɔnɔ n kɔ sɔnsɔɔnri i si n kɛrɛ a.

eng: They were repulsed by him and fell into sin. But Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns and in their own households.
local: Na ba bu i ŋgbɛɛn. Na Yesu ase bu ka, Deke n kɛrɛ bu ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ jirima a, má ka i bɔsu­fɔm, ni i gisan­nu­fɔm ŋgumi dɔ.

eng: He was unable to do many miracles there because of their disbelief.
local: I bɔsu­fɔm ayo má i yarada n dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo má alekutura sam beberebe dɔ.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he left Galilee and came to the area of Judea on the east side of the Jordan.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu jɔjɔɔri ahin jɔrɛm n kɛrɛ wieeri ni, na wɔ yasu Galili awɔrɛ n nu, akɔ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na wɔ kɔ akpiɛ Yodan kunma n buekun dɔ.

eng: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them.
local: Na jama beberebe asu i su, na wɔ ma tukpaki­fɔm bo bu wo jama n nu n anya laifiɛ.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain.
local: Le­sɛn cɛɛn sin na Yesu afa Petoro, ni Yakobo, ni yiri Yakobo niɛma Yohane, afun akɔ buka lenleŋge be su, bu ni yiri ŋgumi.

eng: Some Pharisees came to him. In order to test him, they said, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife for just any reason.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ nbem aba i dɔ, ka bu ba sɔ i ŋgɛɛ fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n su, na ba bisa i ka, Ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka sɔnɔ koro fa i yi ji deke be dɛɛ ti.

eng: He was transformed in front of them. His face shone like the sun, and his clothes became as white as light.
local: Bu woori dɔ n na ba ŋu ka i ŋuniɛ akaaki wo, na i nyunuawa aje ka wiɛ, na i tanlɛɛ n akaaki fofoe papasi.

eng: Jesus answered, Havent you read that at the beginning the creator made them male and female?.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Ye ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu sɛ wɔ? Ba kɛrɛ ka, fite durunya n bumboori, saŋga n bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri adamande ni, i yiiri biɛsɔ ni bara wɔ.

eng: Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Jesus.
local: Dɔ na ba ŋu minɛ nnyɔ nbem atiɛ bu ŋu, na bu ni Yesu subo anzama, bɛrɛ la Moyisi ni Eliya, dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni.

eng: And God said, Because of this a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife, and the two will be one flesh .
local: na wɔ se ka, Nyini dɛɛ ti, i ti ye ni ka biɛsɔ n kpaaki i gisan yaki i si ni i ni, na i ni i yi n saŋga wo. Nyini saŋga, bu minɛ nnyɔ n ayo deke kun lɛɛ.

eng: So they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Bu wo má bu ŋguŋgumi kun. Nyini ti, deke bo Nyɛmɛ anya asaŋga i awie, má sɔnɔ kpaaki i nu kun.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Lord, its good that were here. If you want, Ill make three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Na Petoro ase Yesu ka, M Mibiɛ, i ti kpa ka na ya wo fɛn. Nzɛn má a soro su, m bó nambue nsan fɛn, u dɛɛ kun, Moyisi kun, na Eliya gusu dɛɛ kun.

eng: While he was still speaking, look, a bright cloud overshadowed them. A voice from the cloud said, This is my Son whom I dearly love. I am very pleased with him. Listen to him.
local: Saŋga bo i jina wo jɔjɔɔri su n na yinyiŋgu papasi akata bu ŋu, na ba ti akonvi be ŋga yinyiŋgu n nu, na wɔ jɔjɔ bu dɔ, i ŋa, M wa koroo­wa lɛɛ bo m ahore ajɔ i ŋu. Am de i nɔaniɛ.

eng: The Pharisees said to him, Then why did Moses command us to give a divorce certificate and divorce her? .
local: Na ba bisa Yesu bekun ka, Ye nzu ti ye Moyisi yo maari atin ka, nzɛn sɔnɔ koro ka i fa i yi ji, i ti ye ni ka i mi ma bara n ajaa yaki kadasi ka, na i nya fa i ji a.

eng: Hearing this, the disciples fell on their faces, filled with awe.
local: I susu­fɔm n tiiri akonvi ni ŋga sɔ ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu agaya, na ba butu asiɛŋgu.

eng: But Jesus came and touched them. Get up, he said. Dont be afraid.
local: Na Yesu aba afa i saa akan bu, na wɔ se ka, Má am ma sɛrɛ ti am, am yasu.

eng: Jesus replied, Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because your hearts are unyielding. But it wasnt that way from the beginning.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am ahore­mbaa kekereke n dɛɛ ti, ye Moyisi yo maari am nyini atin n a. Ama, fite durunya n bumboori, i yo ti má sɔ.

eng: When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
local: Na ba yɛ bu tii su aniɛ, ba ŋu má sɔnɔ be kun, má ka Yesu ŋgumi.

eng: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, na Yesu susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, aba abisa i ka, Ŋma yo ya kɛrɛ tii Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu a.

eng: I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i faari i yi jiiri, na wɔ yo má ka bara n akɔ somaya ye i fa i jiiri, na i jaari bara fɔfɔrɛ, i mi n asaaki ajaa ciire lɛɛ.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, Dont tell anybody about the vision until the Human One is raised from the dead.
local: Na saŋga bo bu ba sujura buka n su ni, na Yesu abɔbɔ bu ka, Má am be se ka i bo sa n bo am aŋu i n sufa kere sɔnɔ be, haari fa kɔ saŋga bo mini Adamande Wa ni bá wu na Nyɛmɛ bá ma m teŋge m yiwee n nu ka.

eng: Then he called a little child over to sit among the disciples.
local: Dɔ na Yesu afere baa kaan be, aba ajina bu afiɛn.

eng: His disciples said to him, If thats the way things are between a man and his wife, then its better not to marry.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n ase i ka, Nzɛn i nya tiiri sɔ yiri, fa kɔ biɛsɔ ni bara dɔ, i ti kpa ka má be ja ajaa.

eng: The disciples asked, Then why do the legal experts say that Elijah must first come.
local: Dɔ na i susu­fɔm n abisa i ka, Ye nzu ti ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n bita jɔjɔ ka wɔ yo daka ka Eliya dú mɔ ba ka, ye Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni bá ba n a.

eng: and said, I assure you that if you dont turn your lives around and become like this little child, you will definitely not enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Na wɔ se ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, am wúra má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, má ka am kaaki am wo na am yo am ŋu ka baatuma kaan.

eng: He replied, Not everybody can accept this teaching, but only those who have received the ability to accept it.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro su ahin nɔaniɛ n su a, má ka bɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ adu mɔ afa ama bu ni.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti ka sɔnɔ be bo i yasuuri alecin ŋgarama fuun, ka i kɔ niɛniɛ npa­di­fɔm na bu kɔ yo i fie juma n wɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Elijah does come first and will restore all things.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka, Eliya dú mɔ ba na i bá sɛsɛ deke n kɛrɛ su.

eng: Those who humble themselves like this little child will be the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo i ŋu kaan ka ahin baa ni, yiri ti minɛm kɛrɛ tii Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu a.

eng: For there are eunuchs who have been eunuchs from birth. And there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by other people. And there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs because of the kingdom of heaven. Those who can accept it should accept it.
local: Dama deke bo i ti na minɛ nbem ja má ajaa n la ka, bu biɛsɔ dika yo má juma n dɛɛ ti, nbem gusu, ba sa bu wɔ, ye nbem gusu kete ajaa fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n jɔrɛ dɛɛ ti. Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro de ahin nɔaniɛ ni, i mi de i.

eng: After he agreed with the workers to pay them a denarion, he sent them into his vineyard.
local: Na i ni bɛrɛ adi gɔ, na wɔ soro su ka i tɔ bu le­kun juma­yoo­wa ŋkatɔ, jɛtɛ ŋwaa kun. Na wɔ fa bu akɔ fie n su.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they didnt know him. But they did to him whatever they wanted. In the same way the Human One is also going to suffer at their hands.
local: Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, Eliya adu mɔ aba, na minɛm asi má i, na ba yo i kabo bu koro. Sɔ ye mini Adamande Wa n gusu bá di wahara minɛm saa nu a.

eng: Whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i deeri ahin baa­kan n ŋusu m dɛɛ ti, wɔ de mini wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Then he went out around nine in the morning and saw others standing around the marketplace doing nothing.
local: Na fa kɔ kerefi ŋgɔna saŋga, na wɔ kaaki akɔ aŋu ka minɛ nbem tana gɔɔsu, bu yo má pui.

eng: Some people brought children to Jesus so that he would place his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Na minɛ nbem afa nbaatumam aba Yesu dɔ, ka i fa i saa nana bu ŋu na i sere Nyɛmɛ ma bu. Ama, na i susu­fɔm n atiɛn minɛm ni ŋu.

eng: Then the disciples realized he was telling them about John the Baptist.
local: Dɔ na i susu­fɔm n anya asi ka i jɔjɔ Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n dɛɛ jɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and be drowned in the bottom of the lake.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo u sa­yoo­wa ma ahin nbaatuma kan kanm n be bo i yo m yarada n be wuraari satiɛ nu ni, i ti má kpa. Nzɛn má bu faari ŋgani san yabuɛ bambaka ciciiri u kɔm na bu fa u kɔ tu tieku nu ka nzue di u, i ti ye tara bo a ma ahin nbaatuma kan kanm n be wura satiɛ nu ni.

eng: He said to them, You also go into the vineyard, and Ill pay you whatever is right.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am ma ya kɔ m fie n su, na am kɔ yo m juma, na m tɔ am kaari, kabo am juma­yoo­wa ti barasu.

eng: When they came to the crowd, a man met Jesus. He knelt before him.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔ nsanm ni kpieeri bu nyi ba juuri jama n bi ni, na sɔnɔ be aba akutu i nyunu.

eng: Allow the children to come to me, Jesus said. Dont forbid them, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these children.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am ma nbaatumam n bra m dɔ, má am fɔn bu. Dama bɛrɛ ŋusu sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti bu dɛɛ a.

eng: How terrible it is for the world because of the things that cause people to trip and fall into sin! Such things have to happen, but how terrible it is for the person who causes those things to happen!.
local: Durunya n nu­fɔm bá ŋu anyum­boro, dama bu yo sam bo bu ma minɛ kaaki bu sin bo m dɛɛ atin n wɔ. Nyini sam ni yo wɔ, saŋga kɛrɛ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ma nyini sam yoori ni, yiri bá ŋu anyum­boro.

eng: And they went. Again around noon and then at three in the afternoon, he did the same thing.
local: Nyini ti, na ba kɔ. Na kerefi buru ni nnyɔ saŋga, na wɔ kaaki akɔ gɔɔsu, akɔ aŋu ka minɛ nbem jina, na wɔ fa bu akɔ i fie n su. Na kerefi nsan saŋga, na wɔ yo sɔ bekun.

eng: saying, Lord, show mercy to my son. He is epileptic and suffers terribly, for he often falls into the fire or the water.
local: ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, si m wa baa­biɛsɔ n aŋunfɔ. I tɔ ŋgbiti wɔ, bo i di wahara agaya. Saŋga kɛrɛ bo i nya ba yasu i ŋu, i fa i wura sin nu wɔ, wara nzue nu.

eng: Then he blessed the children and went away from there.
local: Na wɔ fa i saa anana bu ŋu na wɔ yo bu kisi, na wɔ nya afite bɛrɛ akɔ.

eng: If your hand or your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off and throw it away. Its better to enter into life crippled or lame than to be thrown into the eternal fire with two hands or two feet.
local: Nzɛn u saa, wara u jaa cin u wura satiɛ nu a, kaba kpiɛ i fa tu na a ka asaasin, wara, ajaasin, na a nyá u ŋu kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. I ti ye ni bo a la u saa nnyɔ ni u jaa nnyɔ n kɛrɛ, na bu ba fa u tu cɛɛma sin n bo i nɔ má n nu.

eng: Around five in the afternoon he went and found others standing around, and he said to them, Why are you just standing around here doing nothing all day long.
local: Na fa kɔ naasɔ kerefi nnu saŋga, na wɔ kɔ aŋu ka minɛ nbem jina ŋgbɛɛn, bu yo má pui. Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am ba jina fɛn ŋgarama kun kɛrɛ ŋgbɛɛn sɔ, na am yo má pui a.

eng: I brought him to your disciples, but they couldnt heal him.
local: Ma fa i aba u susu­fɔm n dɔ lɛɛ ka bu ma i nya laifiɛ, ama, ba koro atuntun má i na i nya laifiɛ.

eng: A man approached him and said, Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life.
local: Cɛɛn be, na gbɛfɛnɛ be aba Yesu dɔ, aba abisa i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, m yo nzu sakpa ye m bá nya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out and throw it away. Its better to enter into life with one eye than to be cast into a burning hell with two eyes.
local: Nzɛn u nyu­mbaa kun gusu cin u wura satiɛ nu a, lokoti yi i tu na a ka anyunu kun, na a nyá u ŋu kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. I ti ye ni bo a la u nyu­mbaa nnyɔ n kɛrɛ na bu ba fa u tu cɛɛma sin nu ni.

eng: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and his disciples.
local: Na Yesu ajɔjɔ i susu­fɔm n ni jama n dɔ, na wɔ se bu ka.

eng: Because nobody has hired us, they replied. He responded, You also go into the vineyard.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Be afa má ya ka ya yo i juma. Na wɔ se bu ka bɛrɛ gusu kɔ na bu kɔ yo juma i fie n su.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Amaa. Ambɛrɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm, am la má yarada fieo. Nyuma sa ye m wo am dɔ fɛn a? Ye m yó sawɔrɛ ni ambɛrɛ kɔ ju sɛ wɔ? Am fa baa n ba ma m fɛn.

eng: Jesus said, Why do you ask me about what is good? Theres only one who is good. If you want to enter eternal life, keep the commandments.
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Nzu ti ye a bisa m fa kɔ deke bo i ti sakpa su a? Sɔnɔ kun n kpein wo bɛrɛ bo i ti kpa. Nzɛn a koro ka a nya ŋgɔɔ, su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam n su.

eng: Be careful that you dont look down on one of these little ones. I say to you that their angels in heaven are always looking into the face of my Father who is in heaven.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Má am se ka am bu ahin nbaatuma kan kanm n be ŋgbɛɛn. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, bu dɛɛ mɛrɛkɛm bo bu wo aŋgoro ni, wo m Si Nyɛmɛ nyunu saŋga kɛrɛ a. .

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees sit on Moses seat.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n ade Moyisi dɛɛ jinaa­biri ni, ye bu kere minɛm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n a.

eng: When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his manager, Call the workers and give them their wages, beginning with the last ones hired and moving on finally to the first.
local: Saŋga bo wiɛ ba tɔɔri n na fie­fɔ n fere i juma niɛsu­fɔ n na wɔ se i ka, Fere juma­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ na a tɔ bu kaari. Bo i bu tɔ bo bu baari kasin n ka, na a nya ba tɔ bo bu duuri mɔ baari ni.

eng: Then Jesus spoke harshly to the demon. And it came out of the child, who was healed from that time on.
local: Na Yesu atiɛn ajini n ŋu, na wɔ fite ayaki baa ni. Dika n nu bɛrɛ, na baa n anya laifiɛ.

eng: The man said, Which ones? Then Jesus said, Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony .
local: Na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, Nɔaniɛ maa­wa nbɔnɔm? Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Má a kun sɔnɔ. Má a saaki ajaa ciire. Má a wɔ. Má a di kɔrɛ daani.

eng: What do you think? If someone had one hundred sheep and one of them wandered off, wouldnt he leave the ninety-nine on the hillsides and go in search for the one that wandered off?.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ la nbɔɛm yaa, na bu nu kun miniiri, am sunsun ka i yo sɛ wɔ? I yáki abura­ŋgɔna ni ŋgɔna n wura dii­biri kpɛrɛ n su, na i kɔ́ niɛniɛ kun n bo wɔ mini ni, má sɔ?.

eng: Now Jesus left the temple and was going away. His disciples came to point out to him the temple buildings.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu fiteeri Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu na i wo kɔ su ni, na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, aba ase i ka i niɛ kabo Nyɛmɛ seree­biri n ti barasu a.

eng: Therefore, you must take care to do everything they say. But dont do what they do.
