local: Nyini ti, deke kɛrɛ bo bu fa kere am ni, am soro i su, ama, má am yo sam bo bu yo bu ni, dama bɛrɛ yo má sam bo bu kere ka minɛm yo bu ni.

eng: When those who were hired at five in the afternoon came, each one received a denarion.
local: Na bo bu baari ka sin n kɛrɛ aba, na wɔ tɔ bu jɛtɛ ŋwaa kun kun.

eng: Then the disciples came to Jesus in private and said, Why couldnt we throw the demon out.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, bɛrɛ ŋgumi, aba abisa i ka, Nzu ti ye yɛrɛ akoro afɔn má nyini ajini n a.

eng: Honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Bu u si ni u ni. Koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni.

eng: If he finds it, I assure you that he is happier about having that one sheep than about the ninety-nine who didnt wander off.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, nzɛn i niɛniɛ kɔ ŋuuri nyini kun ni, i ahore jɔ i ŋu tara abura­ŋgɔna ni ŋgɔna n bo ba mini má ni.

eng: He responded, Do you see all these things? I assure that no stone will be left on another. Everything will be demolished.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, M ŋa am aŋu ahinm kɛrɛ e? Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, yabuɛ kun n kpein n mmɔɔ bá nana má i beŋgu su. Bu bá bereki i kɛrɛ gɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: For they tie together heavy packs that are impossible to carry. They put them on the shoulders of others, but are unwilling to lift a finger to move them.
local: Bu cici sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri sɔ minɛm wɔ, ama, bɛrɛ ni bu tii soro má su ka bu fa bu saa kan i.

eng: Now when those hired first came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarion.
local: Saŋga bo i ba kpuraari bo bu duuri mɔ baari ni, bu sunsuunri ka bu dɛɛ ŋkatɔ tara bo bu kaari sin baari n dɛɛ n wɔ. Ama, bu tɔɔri bu kɛrɛ jɛtɛ ŋwaa kun kun wɔ, kabo bu tɔɔri kasin­fɔm ni.

eng: Because you have little faith, he said. I assure you that if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mountain, Go from here to there, and it will go. There will be nothing that you cant do.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, I fite ka am yarada n ti kaan sɔ n dɛɛ ti a. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, nzɛn am yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada bo i kɔ juuri kabo nyɛm mbaa ti ni mmɔɔ, am koro se nyini buka n ka, Fite fɛn kɔ dɔ, na i kɔ. Deke wo má bɛrɛ bo i yó kekereke ma am. .

eng: The young man replied, Ive kept all these. What am I still missing.
local: Na gbɛfɛnɛ n ase i ka, Ma su nyini nɔaniɛ maa­wa n kɛrɛ su, ye nzu kaari a.

eng: In the same way, my Father who is in heaven doesnt want to lose one of these little ones.
local: I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n dɔ, i koro má ka ahin nbaatumam kun n kpein mmɔɔ mini.

eng: Now while Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately and said, Tell us, when will these things happen? What will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu fun kɔ tanaari Oliva Buka n su ni, na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, bu ni yiri ŋgumi, na ba se i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, se ya, saŋga bɔnɔ ye ahin sam kɛrɛ bá yo a? Ye nzu deke bá kere ya ka saŋga n adodo bo a bá bekun, na durunya n bá awieeri a.

eng: Everything they do, they do to be noticed by others. They make extra-wide prayer bands for their arms and long tassels for their clothes.
local: Deke kɛrɛ bo bu yo i, bu koro ka minɛm ŋu bu wɔ, na bu kansi bu. Bu kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n wɔ, na bu yo i ka sɛbɛ, na bu fa cici bu ŋuma su, adena minɛm ŋu i. Na bu yo bu tanlɛɛ nɔɔ njɔrɛm tintiin.

eng: When they received it, they grumbled against the landowner.
local: Bu deeri ŋwaa wieeri sɔ ni, na ba ŋuŋuti fie n fɔ ni.

eng: When the disciples came together in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Human One is about to be delivered over into human hands.
local: Saŋga bo i susu­fɔm ba tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni, na Yesu ase bu ka, Bu bá tɔ mini Adamande Wa n ma minɛ nbem.

eng: Jesus said, If you want to be complete, go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come follow me.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nzɛn a koro ka u woo­wa n ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti n wɔ, kɔ na a kɔ tɔ u aja kɛrɛ, na a fa ŋwaa n ma yaari­fɔm, na a bá nya ajɛkɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na a bra, ba su m su.

eng: If your brother or sister sins against you, go and correct them when you are alone together. If they listen to you, then youve won over your brother or sister.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Nzɛn wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo u niwa yoori u satiɛ be, kɔ i dɔ na u ni yiri minɛ nnyɔ ni ŋgumi, na a se i ka wɔ yo u satiɛ. Nzɛn i tiiri i bu ka wɔ yo u satiɛ, i toko ti u niwa lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am si am ŋu sa, na má am ma be dadaka am.

eng: They love to sit in places of honor at banquets and in the synagogues.
local: Nyini minɛm n anya awura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu ni, wara ba nya afere bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ bambaka diire dii­birim ni, bu koro ka bu tana kpirikoro tanaa­birim wɔ.

eng: These who were hired last worked one hour, and they received the same pay as we did even though we had to work the whole day in the hot sun.
local: Bu ŋa, A tɔ kasin­fɔm bo ba yo má juma kpa be ni ŋkatɔ nyunyumi, na kabo a tɔɔri bu ni, a tɔ yɛrɛ gusu bo ya baari ŋgarama kun kɛrɛ, na ya ba adi yaari, na wiɛ abo ya n gusu sɔ ɔ.

eng: They will kill him. But he will be raised on the third day. And they were heartbroken.
local: na bu kún m, ama, bu kuunri m wieeri, i le­san cɛɛn, Nyɛmɛ má m teŋge m yiwee n nu. I jɔjɔɔri sɔ ni, na i susu­fɔm n ahore asaaki agaya.

eng: But when the young man heard this, he went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Saŋga bo gbɛfɛnɛ n tiiri nyini sɔ ni, na wɔ yasu akɔ ni ahoresaaki, dama i la neŋge agaya n dɛɛ ti.

eng: But if they wont listen, take with you one or two others so that every word may be established by the mouth of two or three witnesses .
local: Ama, nzɛn i keteeri, yo aniɛ na a nya sɔnɔ kun, wara minɛ nnyɔ buka u ŋu su, na am kɔ ŋu i, adena minɛ nnyɔ, wara minɛ nsan n bo ba ti jɔrɛ n yo u daani­fɔm.

eng: Many will come in my name, saying, Im the Christ. They will deceive many people.
local: Dama minɛ dɔŋgu bá fa m duma ba, na bu sé ka, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n la mini wɔ. Na bu dádaka minɛ beberebe.

eng: They love to be greeted with honor in the markets and to be addressed as Rabbi.
local: Bu koro ka minɛm suyo bu ansɛ gɔɔsu wɔ, na bu ma bu jirima, na bu fere bu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ.

eng: But he replied to one of them, Friend, I did you no wrong. Didnt I agree to pay you a denarion?.
local: Ama, na fie n fɔ n abɔ nu, ase bu kun ka, M dɔn, ti fɛn, ma sisi má am e. Bo mini si i n la ka, am soroori su ka am yo le­kun juma, na am de jɛtɛ ŋwaa kun wɔ.

eng: When they came to Capernaum, the people who collected the half-shekel temple tax came to Peter and said, Doesnt your teacher pay the temple tax.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n ba juuri Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu lampo­de­fɔm n aba Petoro dɔ, aba abisa i ka, Am kpiɛn n tɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu lampo kaari, nzɛn i tɔ má.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, I assure you that it will be very hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, i yó kekereke ni bo neŋge­fɔ wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: But if they still wont pay attention, report it to the church. If they wont pay attention even to the church, treat them as you would a Gentile and tax collector.
local: Nzɛn i keteeri bɛrɛ gusu, na a nya fa i sereya n kɔ Kristo jama n kɛrɛ dɔ. Nzɛn i nya keteeri Kristo jama ni, am fa i ka sɔnɔ bo i su má Nyɛmɛ, wara satiɛ­yo­fɔ bo wɔ kete ka i kaaki má i wo lɛɛ.

eng: You will hear about wars and reports of wars. Dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Saŋga bo am bá ti aluwa ŋga fɛn, wara bu sukun luɛ dɔ, má am ma am ahore saaki. Wɔ yo daka ka nyini sam n kɛrɛ bá yo sin ka, ama, má am niɛ ka durunya n awieeri lɛɛ.

eng: But you shouldnt be called Rabbi , because you have one teacher, and all of you are brothers and sisters.
local: Ambɛrɛ, má am ma be fere am ka Ya Kere­fɔ, dama am la kere­fɔ kun n kpein wɔ. Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ ti nibaam wɔ.

eng: Take what belongs to you and go. I want to give to this one who was hired last the same as I give to you.
local: Nyini ti, de u ŋwaa na a kɔ. M koro ka m ma kasin­fɔ n kabo m maari u n wɔ.

eng: Yes, he said. But when they came into the house, Jesus spoke to Peter first.What do you think, Simon? From whom do earthly kings collect taxes, from their children or from strangers.
local: Na Petoro abɔ i nu ka, I tɔ wanla. Saŋga bo Petoro kɔ juuri awuru n nu n na Yesu adu mɔ abisa i dɔ ka, Simon, ye wɔrɔ ŋa sɛ wɔ? Ŋmam tɔ lampo ŋwaa ma durunya n nu fɛn nfɛmɛm a, miɛnbaam nzɛn wɔfɔm.

eng: In fact, its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: M suse am wɔ bekun lɛɛ ka, kabo nyɔɔma sin misini bue nu ni, i ti má kekereke tara má kabo neŋge­fɔ yo na i wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu ni.

eng: I assure you that whatever you fasten on earth will be fastened in heaven. And whatever you loosen on earth will be loosened in heaven.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, deke kɛrɛ bo am ma minɛm atin ka bu yo i durunya n nu fɛn, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na deke kɛrɛ bo am ma má minɛm atin ka bu yo i durunya n nu, i kaaki ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: Dont call anybody on earth your father, because you have one Father, who is heavenly.
local: Má am fere be ka am si, durunya n nu fɛn, dama am la Si kun n kpein wɔ, yiri wo aŋgoro n a.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be famines and earthquakes in all sorts of places.
local: Daanfiɛ daanfiɛ bá kun luɛ ni bu beŋgum, na nfɛmɛm kún luɛ ni bu beŋgu nfɛmɛm. Na asiɛn bá kpisi awɔrɛ awɔrɛm nu, na ahɔɛ tɔ́.

eng: Dont I have the right to do what I want with what belongs to me? Or are you resentful because Im generous.
local: M la má atin ka m yo m ŋwaa kabo m koro, nzɛn sɛ? Wara, a si m kɔrɛ fa kɔ m sakpa bo ma yo i n wɔ.

eng: From strangers, he said. Jesus said to him, Then the children dont have to pay.
local: Na Petoro abɔ i nu ka, Wɔfɔm. Na Yesu ase i ka, Nzɛn i nya tiiri sɔ de, miɛnbaam tɔ má lɛɛ.

eng: When his disciples heard this, they were stunned. Then who can be saved? they asked.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu susu­fɔm n tiiri nyini n sɔ ni, na ba yo alekutura agaya, na ba bisa i ka, Ye nzɛn i ti sɔ, ŋma koro fite nu a.

eng: Again I assure you that if two of you agree on earth about anything you ask, then my Father who is in heaven will do it for you.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am bekun n a, saŋga kɛrɛ bo am minɛ nnyɔ yoori am nɔɔkun, na am sereeri m Si Nyɛmɛ deke be, i yó i ma am.

eng: Dont be called teacher , because Christ is your one teacher.
local: Má am ma be fere am ka i kpiɛn, dama am kpiɛn ti kun n kpein wɔ, yiri la Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni.

eng: But all these things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Nyini n kɛrɛ bá yo kabo bara kutu cɛɛn bumboori dɛɛ wahara n ti n wɔ.

eng: So those who are last will be first. And those who are first will be last.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ahin miɛɛnu n bu la ka, minɛm bo ba ka sin kisa ni, bá du mɔ, na bo ba du mɔ kisa n bá ka sin.

eng: But just so we dont offend them, go to the lake, throw out a fishing line and hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a shekel coin. Take it and pay the tax for both of us.
local: Ama, yɛrɛ koro má ka ya wura bu ŋgasi nu. Nyini ti, kɔ kunma kpiri n nu na a kɔ fa u kuwa n ji nu, jombaa kɛrɛ bo a du mɔ taraari i, niɛ i nɔɔ nu, a ŋú ŋwaa bo i ju mini ni wɔrɔ dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu lampo ŋwaa tɔ. Fa i na a fa kɔ tɔ mini ni wɔrɔ tii su kaari.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible for human beings. But all things are possible for God.
local: Na Yesu aniɛ bu dɔ, na wɔ se ka, Ahin ti deke bo adamande koro yo má i, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i ti má pui, dama Nyɛmɛ koro yo deke kɛrɛ.

eng: For where two or three are gathered in my name, Im there with them.
local: Dama deke kɛrɛ bo minɛ nnyɔ, wara nsan tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ m dɛɛ duma n nu, m wo bu dɔ.

eng: But the one who is greatest among you will be your servant.
local: Bo i ti am kɛrɛ tii ni, wɔ yo daka ka yiri su am a.

eng: They will arrest you, abuse you, and they will kill you. All nations will hate you on account of my name.
local: Nyini saŋga, na bu tára am ma yiko­fɔm na bu kére am wuwuruwa, na bu kún am. Mini dɛɛ ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kpɔ́ am.

eng: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the Twelve aside by themselves on the road. He told them.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm, ni jama n sukɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, bu wo kɔ su sɔ ni, na wɔ fere i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm n akɔ ahɛmɛ, na wɔ se bu ka.

eng: Then Peter replied, Look, weve left everything and followed you. What will we have.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, Tɔ, ye yɛrɛ ayaki deke kɛrɛ susu u su e. Ye ya nyá nzu wɔ.

eng: Then Peter said to Jesus, Lord, how many times should I forgive my brother or sister who sins against me? Should I forgive as many as seven times.
local: Nyini sin na Petoro aba Yesu dɔ, aba abisa i ka, M Mibiɛ, nzɛn m niwa nati yo m satiɛ saŋga kɛrɛ, kpe nyɛ ye m fa i satiɛ ce i a? Kpe nso, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low. But all who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wura i ŋu kpiri, Nyɛmɛ yó i mi kaan, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo i ŋu kaan, Nyɛmɛ wúra i mi kpiri.

eng: At that time many will fall away. They will betray each other and hate each other.
local: Nyini saŋga n na minɛ dɔŋgu bá kete m na bu su má m kun, na bu tɔ́ bu beŋgu, na bu kpɔ́ bu beŋgu.

eng: Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and legal experts. They will condemn him to death.
local: Am ti fɛn. Ya sukɔ Yɛrusalem lɛɛ, dɔ ye bu kɔ́ tara mini Adamande Wa n a, na bu fá ma Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, na bu dí m jɔrɛ, na bu sóro su ka bu kún m.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you who have followed me that, when everything is made new, when the Human One sits on his magnificent throne, you also will sit on twelve thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, saŋga bo mini Adamande Wa n ba tanaari m jirima tanaa­biri n durunya fɔfɔrɛ n nu, m má ambɛrɛ bo am asu m su ni tana fɛmɛya biɛ buru ni nnyɔm n su, na am níɛ Yisareli­fɔm anɔ buru ni nnyɔm n su.

eng: Jesus responded by speaking again in parables.
local: Na Yesu akaaki abo miɛɛnu be bekun, i ŋa.

eng: Jesus said, Not just seven times, but rather as many as seventy-seven times.
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Ai, m suse u wɔ lɛɛ ka, má kpe nso co, ama, kpe abura­soso busu nso.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You shut people out of the kingdom of heaven. You dont enter yourselves, and you wont allow those who want to enter to do so.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am kanda minɛm wɔ, adena má bu su Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n atin su, dama ambɛrɛ ni am tii kete ka am su má i atin n su, na am sukanda bo bu koro ka bu su i su ni. .

eng: Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
local: Na kɔrɛ­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni bá ba, na bu dádaka minɛm beberebe.

eng: They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be ridiculed, tortured, and crucified. But he will be raised on the third day.
local: Na bɛrɛ gusu nyá fa m wura minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm saa nu, na bu yó m anzasi, na bu fín m ŋgbere, na bu nyá kpama m baka jaanu n su na m wu, na i le­san cɛɛn, na Nyɛmɛ má m teŋge m yiwee n nu.

eng: And all who have left houses, brothers, sisters, father, mother, children, or farms because of my name will receive one hundred times more and will inherit eternal life.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yakiiri i awuru, wara i ninbaam, wara i si, ni i ni, wara i nbaam, wara i asiɛnm, mini dɛɛ ti, i mi nyá nyini n kɛrɛ ŋusu yaa i ŋgɔɔ su, na i káaki nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding party for his son.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti yaa. Fɛmɛ be yo sɛsɛɛri i wa konyaya di ni nu su fa seeri a.

eng: Therefore, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Nyini ti, kabo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti yaa. Fɛmɛ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, cɛɛn n be na wɔ se ka i bu akunta ni i akɔɔm bo bu wo i dɔ yo juma ni, na i si ŋwaa bo bu bita i.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You travel over sea and land to make one convert. But when theyve been converted, they become twice the child of hell you are.
local: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am kpiɛ tiekum wɔ, na am kɔ miɛm bo bu waarim su, adena am nya sɔnɔ kun mmɔɔ bo i káaki i wo na i su am su, na saŋga bo am nya nyaari i mi, am ma i satiɛ yoo­wa tara am dɛɛ wɔ, adena yiri dú am mɔ kɔ cɛɛma sin nu.

eng: Because disobedience will expand, the love of many will grow cold.
local: Satiɛ­yo bá sunnu n dɛɛ ti, minɛ dɔŋgu koro bá kɔ i sin.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Am minɛ dɔŋgu bo bu wo mɔ kisa ni bá ka sin, na bo bu ka sin kisa n bá du mɔ.

eng: Then the mother of Zebedees sons came to Jesus along with her sons. Bowing before him, she asked a favor of him.
local: Na Zɛbɛdi yi afa i nbaa nnyɔm n aba akutu Yesu nyunu, asere i ahɔrɔma ka i yo sa be ma i.

eng: He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding party. But they didnt want to come.
local: Na wɔ sunma i akɔɔm ka bu kɔ fere minɛm bo wɔ du mɔ abaandi bu ka bu ba konyaya di ni nu ni, ka bu bra. Ama, na ba kete ka bu ba má.

eng: When he began to settle accounts, they brought to him a servant who owed him ten thousand bags of gold.
local: Saŋga bo i boori i bu na i bu akunta ni, na ba fa i akɔɔ n kun be bo i bita i ŋwaa kaari bo i la má nɔɔ, aba i dɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you blind guides who say, If people swear by the temple, its nothing. But if people swear by the gold in the temple, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti ka anyinsinm na am kere minɛm atin ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am ŋa nzɛn sɔnɔ taanri ndiɛ fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n dɔ, i ti má pui. Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i taanri sika neŋgem n bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni, i mi ma i ndiɛ taan­wa n yo yii­wa.

eng: But the one who endures to the end will be delivered.
local: Ama, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a mindi deeri nyini n kɛrɛ kɔ juuri i awieeri, Nyɛmɛ dé u tii ji.

eng: What do you want? he asked. She responded, Say that these two sons of mine will sit, one on your right hand and one on your left, in your kingdom.
local: Na Yesu abisa i ka, A koro ka m yo nzu ma u a? Na bara n abɔ i nu ka, M koro ka a ma nɔɔ ka saŋga bo a bá diiri u fɛmɛya ni, a má m ahin nbaa nnyɔm ni atin na bu nu kun tana u saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, na kun gusu tana u saa biɛ su.

eng: Again he sent other servants and said to them, Tell those who have been invited,Look, the meal is all prepared. Ive butchered the oxen and the fattened cattle. Now everythings ready. Come to the wedding party.
local: Na wɔ kaaki asunma akɔɔ nyiŋgam ka bu kɔ se bɛrɛ n bo wɔ du mɔ abaandi bu n ka, Am bra, dama ma yo m di ni nu n siriya kɛrɛ awie. Haari ma kun m tooram, ni neŋge­ndɔrɛ bo bu la lui ni, ayo siriya kɛrɛ awie. Am bra na am ba konyaya di ni nu ni.

eng: Because the servant didnt have enough to pay it back, the master ordered that he should be sold, along with his wife and children and everything he had, and that the proceeds should be used as payment.
local: Kabo ŋwaa n sunnu sɔ na i koro tɔ má kaari n ni, na fɛmɛ n ama nɔɔ ka bu tɔ i ni i yi, ni i nbaam, ni i deke kɛrɛ bo i la i, na bu fa tɔ kaari ni.

eng: You foolish and blind people! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold holy?.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti ka anyinsin sinnzinm ni, bɔnɔ tara i beŋgu a? Sika neŋgem ni, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n bo yiri ma sika neŋgem n yo casi n a?.

eng: This gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations. Then the end will come.
local: Na bu bá kan ahin labari kpa n fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n jɔrɛ su, kere durunya n nu­fɔm, na adamande kɛrɛ tí i ka, na durunya n nya bá awieeri.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink from the cup that Im about to drink from? They said to him, We can.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am si má deke bo am susere i n ti a. Am kóro di wahara n bo m dí i ni? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ɛɛn, ya koro yo i kɛrɛ.

eng: But they paid no attention and went away—some to their fields, others to their businesses.
local: Ama, bu be mmɔɔ abɔ má bu nu. Na ba ka bu juma n yo su. Kun kɔɔri i fiesu wɔ, na nyiŋga gusu akɔ i juma yoo­biri.

eng: But the servant fell down, kneeled before him, and said, Please, be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Na akɔɔ n akutu i mibiɛ n nyunu asiɛŋgu, asere i ka, Jande, si m aŋunfɔ, na m kɔ́ niɛniɛ ŋwaa n na m bá tɔ kaari ni.

eng: You say, If people swear by the altar, its nothing. But if they swear by the gift on the altar, they are obligated to do what they swore.
local: Am kaaki ŋa, nzɛn sɔnɔ taanri Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri ni, i ti má pui. Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ taanri aceerim bo ba fa ase Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri n su ni, wɔ yo daka ka i mi ma i ndiɛ taan­wa n yo yii­wa.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing that Daniel talked about standing in the holy place.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Am bá ŋu anyumbaa ciire sa be bo Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ kpɔ i n na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Daniɛli yo jɔjɔɔri i jɔrɛ ni, ka i jina sɔɔ casi n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni. Ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kan ahin kadasi n ti i bu nyunyumi.

eng: He said to them, You will drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom my Father prepared it.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ, am dí wahara n bo m dí i ni. Ama, mini la má atin bo m má am be tana m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, na be tana m saa biɛ su. Ama, m Si Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ nyini tanaa­biri n su, afa ase minɛm bo yiri ni i tii ayi bu n a.

eng: The rest of them grabbed his servants, abused them, and killed them.
local: Na nyiŋgam n atara i akɔɔm ni, na ba kere bu wuwuruwa, na ba kun bu.

eng: The master had compassion on that servant, released him, and forgave the loan.
local: Na i mibiɛ n asi i aŋunfɔ, na wɔ fa kaari n ace i, na wɔ yaki i aji.

eng: You blind people! Which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift holy?.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti ka anyinsinm ni, bɔnɔ tara i beŋgu a? Aceerim ni, nzɛn Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri n bo i ma aceerim n yo casi n a?.

eng: then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a tiiri i ŋga na a wo Yudiya asiɛn n su, ŋmati kɔ bukam n su.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with the two brothers.
local: Saŋga bo i susu­fɔ burum n ba tiiri kabo Zɛbɛdi nbaa nnyɔm n seeri Yesu ni, na ba fa yaa bu ŋu.

eng: The king was angry. He sent his soldiers to destroy those murderers and set their city on fire.
local: Dɔ na yaa ati fɛmɛ n agaya, na wɔ sunma i sojam, na ba kɔ akun nyini minɛ kun­fɔm ni kɛrɛ, na ba yara bu miɛ ni.

eng: When that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred coins. He grabbed him around the throat and said, Pay me back what you owe me.
local: I fiteeri bɛrɛ, na i ni i beŋgu akɔɔ n kun be bo i gusu bita i kaari n ayɛ, i dɛɛ n sunnu má fieo. Na wɔ so i beŋgu n kɔm nu amiɛ i, na wɔ se i ka, Tɔ m kaari n kisa kisa bɛrɛ.

eng: Therefore, those who swear by the altar swear by it and by everything thats on it.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i taanri Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri ni, i mi atan i ni dekem kɛrɛ bo bu wo su n wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down to grab things from their houses.
local: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a wo kpata nu, má a wura gisan nu fa pui.

eng: But Jesus called them over and said, You know that those who rule the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Na Yesu afere bu kɛrɛ aba i bi, na wɔ se bu ka, Am si tenle su ka minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ, na bu yo bu ŋu nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, bɛrɛ miɛ bu minɛm a, na bu tere­tere­fɔm gusu kere kabo bu yiko ti barasu.

eng: Then he said to his servants, The wedding party is prepared, but those who were invited werent worthy.
local: Na wɔ fere i akɔɔm ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Konyaya di ni nu n ayo siriya, ama, minɛm bo m duuri mɔ baandiiri bu ka bu ba di i ni, i ti má ye ni ka bu dí i kun.

eng: Then his fellow servant fell down and begged him, Be patient with me, and Ill pay you back.
local: Na i beŋgu n akutu haari asiɛŋgu, asere i ka, Jande, si m aŋunfɔ, na m kɔ́ niɛniɛ u ŋwaa na m bá tɔ u kaari.

eng: Those who swear by the temple swear by it and by everything thats part of it.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i taanri Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni, i mi atan i ni Nyɛmɛ bo i wo nu n wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Na wɔrɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo a wo fiesu, má a kpie u nyi kɔ awuru kɔ fa u sutura be.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Ama, i ti má ye ni ka ambɛrɛ yo sɔ. Nzɛn sɔnɔ bo i koro ka i wura i ŋu kpiri am kɛrɛ nu, wɔ yo daka ka i mi yo i ŋu kaan, na i su am.

eng: Therefore, go to the roads on the edge of town and invite everyone you find to the wedding party.
local: Nyini ti, am fite kɔ atiŋgberem su, na am kɔ fere minɛm barasu kɛrɛ bo am ŋuuri bu ni, ka bu bra konyaya di ni nu ni.

eng: But he refused. Instead, he threw him into prison until he paid back his debt.
local: Ama, na wɔ kete, na wɔ kɔ anyi i beŋgu n saraka, na wɔ se i ka má ka wɔ tɔ i kaari ni.

eng: Those who swear by heaven swear by Gods throne and by the one who sits on it.
local: Na nzɛn sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i taanri aŋgoro ni, i mi atan Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya biɛ n wɔ lɛɛ, ni yiri Nyɛmɛ bo i tana su ni.

eng: When they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Bu kɔ ŋa bu dodo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na bu ŋa bu wura miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Befage ni, nyini miɛ n dodo buka n bo bu fere i ka Oliva Buka n wɔ, dama oliva bakam gɔ su wɔ. Dɔ na Yesu asunma i susu­fɔm n nu nnyɔ.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Nyini saŋga ni yó kekereke agaya ma nyinsɛ­fɔm, ni nbaram bo bu nbaam toko nu ni.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be your slave.
local: Na bo i kaaki koro ka i yo am mɔ­fɔ, i ti ye ni ka i mi yo am kɛrɛ akɔɔ.

eng: Then those servants went to the roads and gathered everyone they found, both evil and good. The wedding party was full of guests.
local: Na akɔɔm n akɔ atiŋgberem su, akɔ atiɛn minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu kɔ ŋuuri bu n nɔɔ aba, minɛ kpam ni tiɛm kɛrɛ. Na konyaya awuru n ayi ni jama beberebe.

eng: When his fellow servants saw what happened, they were deeply offended. They came and told their master all that happened.
local: I beŋgu nyiŋgam ŋuuri sa n bo wɔ yo i n sɔ ni, na bu ahore asaaki agaya, na ba kɔ ase fɛmɛ n sa n bo wɔ yo i n kɛrɛ.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You give to God a tenth of mint, dill, and cumin, but you forget about the more important matters of the Law: justice, peace, and faith. You ought to give a tenth but without forgetting about those more important matters.
local: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am yi am jakam ma Nyɛmɛ wɔ, haari ni am maakunm, ni ŋgɛtɛm, ni nvan­nvanm kɛrɛ, ama, am yo má sam bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka am yo bu n na bu la anyunuyi tara bu kɛrɛ ni. Bɛrɛ la nahɔrɛ sam, ni aŋunfɔ si, ni yarada­yo. Wɔ yo daka ka na am yo nyini sam saŋga kɛrɛ, na am fa jaka yii­wam buka su.

eng: He said to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter, you will find a donkey tied up and a colt with it. Untie them and bring them to me.
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ miɛ kaan n bo i wo am nyunu dɔ ni, am kɔ juuri, am ŋú ka kaako be minda, i wa jina i bi bɛrɛ. Am nyaŋgi i ni baa n kɛrɛ bra.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter or on the Sabbath day.
local: Am sere Nyɛmɛ ka má nyini sa n yó buke saŋga, wara Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni.

eng: just as the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: kabo mini Adamande Wa n ni m tii aba má ka minɛm su m ni, ama, m baari ka m ba su minɛm wɔ, na m wu ma bu, na m de minɛ dɔŋgu tii ji.

eng: Now when the king came in and saw the guests, he spotted a man who wasnt wearing wedding clothes.
local: Na fɛmɛ n akɔ, ka i kɔ niɛ jama ni, na wɔ ŋu ka sɔnɔ kun be wo jama n nu, i wura má konyaya sutura.

eng: His master called the first servant and said, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you appealed to me.
local: Dɔ na fɛmɛ n afere i, ase i ka, Wɔrɔ satiɛ­yo­fɔ ŋgbɛɛn ni, a sereeri m dɛɛ ti, ye m yakiiri u kaari n kɛrɛ bo a bita i n fa ceeri u a.

eng: You blind guides! You filter out an ant but swallow a camel.
local: Ambɛrɛ ti ka anyinsinm na am kere minɛm atin ni. Am kpokpo yi ŋminsɛnm bo bu ti kan kanm n wɔ, am deke nuu­wam nu, ama, na am saŋga nu nyɔɔmam bɛrɛ, am deke nuu­wam nu.

eng: If anyone says anything to you, say that their master needs them. He sent them off right away.
local: Nzɛn be bisaari am ka nzu ti ye am sunyaŋgi i a, am se i ka Mibiɛ ni koro bu a, na i má am kaaki fa bu ba kisa kisa.

eng: There will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Nyini saŋga ni wahara bo minɛ dí i, i tára wahara kɛrɛ bo ba di i asin ni, fite saŋga n bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri durunya ni, haari ni kisa. Nahɔrɛ, nyini wahara n ŋusu bá má kun.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples were going out of Jericho a large crowd followed him.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu, ni i susu­fɔm ni sufite miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Yeriko ni, na jama beberebe asu bu su.

eng: He said to him, Friend, how did you get in here without wedding clothes? But he was speechless.
local: Na fɛmɛ n abisa i ka, M dɔn, a yo sɛ wuraari awuru n nu fɛn, ye a wura má konyaya sutura a? Ama, sɔnɔ n akoro abɔ má i nu.

eng: Shouldnt you also have mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had mercy on you.
local: Má wɔ yo daka ka wɔrɔ mmɔɔ siiri u beŋgu n aŋunfɔ na a fa ce i, kabo m ŋuuri u aŋunfɔ na m fa ceeri u ni.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and plate, but inside they are full of violence and pleasure seeking.
local: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am ŋmisi am kɔpum ni taalɛm sin wɔ, ama, na bu kunnu ayi ni dekem bo am fin bu faŋga, ni anyii sa­yoo­wam wɔ.

eng: Now this happened to fulfill what the prophet said.
local: Ahin yoori sɔ ɔ, adena Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n nɔaniɛ n bo i yo jɔjɔɔri i n yo yii­wa a, i ŋa.

eng: If that time werent shortened, nobody would be rescued. But for the sake of the ones whom God chose, that time will be cut short.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ akpiɛ nyini saŋga n su. Nzɛn má wɔ kpiɛ má i su, má sɔnɔ wo má bɛrɛ bo i nya ŋgɔɔ, ama, i dɛɛ tiɛŋgu minɛm bo wɔ yi bu n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ kpiɛ saŋga n su a.

eng: When two blind men sitting along the road heard that Jesus was passing by, they shouted, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Bu sukɔ ni, anyinsin nnyɔ nbem tana atin n nɔɔ su. Saŋga bo bu tiiri ka Yesu susin ni, na ba bo afere i, bu ŋa, Ya Mibiɛ, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma, si ya aŋunfɔ.

eng: Then the king said to his servants, Tie his hands and feet and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Na fɛmɛ n ase i ngbɛfɛnɛm ka, Am tara i, na am cici i saam, ni i jaam nyama, na am fa i tu dasin dɔ, awosin n nu. Dɔ na i sún, na i kán i saa.

eng: His master was furious and handed him over to the guard responsible for punishing prisoners, until he had paid the whole debt.
local: Na yaa ati fɛmɛ n agaya, na wɔ ma bu afa nyini akɔɔ n akɔ anyi i saraka, ka i kɔ di wahara dɔ, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo i bá tɔ kaari n kɛrɛ wie.

eng: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup so that the outside of the cup will be clean too.
local: Wɔrɔ Farasi­fɔ bo a ti ka anyinsin ni, du mɔ yo u kɔpu n kunnu casi ka, na i sin n gusu nyá yo casi.

eng: Say to Daughter Zion, Look, your king is coming to you, humble and riding on a donkey, and on a colt the donkeys offspring. .
local: Se Siyon­fɔm ka, Am niɛ, am fɛmɛ ni suba n a. Wɔ yo i ŋu taraka, i fun kaako wɔ.

eng: Then if somebody says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or Hes over here, dont believe it.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, na nzɛn be bá se am ka, Am niɛ, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni wo fɛn n a, wara, Am niɛ, yiri wo dɔ n a, má am de i mi nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Now the crowd scolded them and told them to be quiet. But they shouted even louder, Show us mercy, Lord, Son of David.
local: Na jama n atiɛn bu ŋu ka bu yaki naŋmiɛ. Ama, na ba kaaki abo afere abuka su ka, Ya Mibiɛ, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma, si ya aŋunfɔ.

eng: My heavenly Father will also do the same to you if you dont forgive your brother or sister from your heart.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nzɛn am afa má am beŋgum satiɛ ace má bu am ahore su, sɔ ye m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni cín am sui a.

eng: Many people are invited, but few people are chosen.
local: Na Yesu anya ase bu ka, Minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ afere bu n sunnu, ama, bo wɔ yi bu n sunnu má.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs. They look beautiful on the outside. But inside they are full of dead bones and all kinds of filth.
local: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am ti ka faŋ­kunmam bo ba bo bu nzue fofoe kpakpari, na bu yo nyunyumi n wɔ, ama, bu kunnu yiri ayi ni fuin buwem wɔ, ni fin barasu kɛrɛ.

eng: The disciples went and did just as Jesus had ordered them.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n akɔ ayo kabo i seeri bu ni.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer great signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, even those whom God has chosen.
local: Dama kɔrɛ­fɔ nbem bá ba, na bu yó bu ŋu ka bɛrɛ ti Kristo n a, na nbem gusu yó bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm wɔ. Bu yó alekutura sam beberebe fa kere am, na nzɛn bu koro nyaari atin, bu dádaka Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛm mmɔɔ bo wɔ yi bu ni.

eng: Jesus stopped in his tracks and called to them. What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Na Yesu aka ajina, na wɔ fere bu, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am koro ka m yo nzu ma am a.

eng: Then the Pharisees met together to find a way to trap Jesus in his words.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm n afite akɔ asɛsɛ su kabo bu sɔ Yesu ŋgɛɛ, na bu nya i taraa­biri fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa su.

eng: In the same way you look righteous to people. But inside you are full of pretense and rebellion.
local: Am ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Am ma minɛm ŋu am ka am ti minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n wɔ, ama, am ahore su ayi ni burukɔɔ­nu, ni satiɛm wɔ.

eng: They brought the donkey and the colt and laid their clothes on them. Then he sat on them.
local: Na ba fa kaako n ni i wa n aba, na ba fa bu suturam anana bu ŋu, na Yesu afun atana su.

eng: Look, Ive told you ahead of time.
local: Ambɛrɛ, am ti fɛn, ma se am wɔ lɛɛ ka, na saŋga n nyá ba ju.

eng: Lord, we want to see, they replied.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ya Mibiɛ, ya koro ka a ma ya ŋu asi wɔ.

eng: They sent their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to him. Teacher, they said, we know that you are genuine and that you teach Gods way as it really is. We know that you are not swayed by peoples opinions, because you dont show favoritism.
local: Na ba sunma bu susu­fɔm, ni Fɛmɛ Herode minɛm Yesu dɔ, na ba kɔ ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ya si ka a ti nahɔrɛ se­fɔ ɔ, na a kere minɛm kabo Nyɛmɛ atin ti nahɔrɛ su, na u cɛcɛ wo má kabo minɛm jɔjɔ nu, dama a niɛ má sɔnɔ jinaa­biri ti.

eng: How terrible it will be for you legal experts and Pharisees! Hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
local: Na Yesu asa su ase ka, Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am yo faŋ­kunmam dɔŋgu fa ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm wɔ, na am yo minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n faŋ­kunmam nyunyumi.

eng: Now a large crowd spread their clothes on the road. Others cut palm branches off the trees and spread them on the road.
local: Na minɛ beberebe afa bu suturam atɛrɛki ase atin n nu, na nbem gusu abubu nyawa asese atin n nu.

eng: So if they say to you, Look, hes in the desert, dont go out. And if they say, Look, hes in the rooms deep inside the house, dont believe it.
local: Na nzɛn be bá seeri am ka, Am niɛ, Kristo ni wo boro kpantain n nu dɔ n a, má am kɔ dɔ. Wara, nzɛn be bá seeri am ka, Yiri fiɛ sɔrɔ dɔ n a, má am yo i mi yarada.

eng: Jesus had compassion on them and touched their eyes. Immediately they were able to see, and they followed him.
local: Na Yesu asi bu aŋunfɔ, na wɔ fa i saa akan bu nyu­mbaa ni, kpeŋga na ba ŋu asi, na ba su i su.

eng: So tell us what you think: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Tɔ, a ŋa sɛ wɔ? Ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka ya tɔ lampo ma Roma­fɔm Fɛmɛ Kpiri Siza, nzɛn atin wo má nu.

eng: You say, If we had lived in our ancestors days, we wouldnt have joined them in killing the prophets.
local: Na am se ka, nzɛn má ambɛrɛ yo woori bɛrɛ, am nam saŋga ni, má am kun má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n ni, kabo am nam yo yoori bu ni.

eng: The crowds in front of him and behind him shouted, Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Na jamam bo bu du i nyunu n ni bo bu wo i sin n kɛrɛ, subo kuyigindi, bu ŋa, Hosana . Ya kansi Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma ni. Tere­fiɛ wo yiri n bo i suba Micɛra n dɛɛ duma n nu n dɔ. Ya kansi Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni.

eng: Just as the lightning flashes from the east to the west, so it will be with the coming of the Human One.
local: Dama mini Adamande Wa n ba ni, i bá ti kabo nzue kpɛsɛ na i ta bara yɛ deke kɛrɛ n wɔ, fite wiɛ afiiri fa kɔ ju wiɛ atɔɔri n wɔ.

eng: Knowing their evil motives, Jesus replied, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?.
local: Ama, na Yesu asi ka bu bita sunsunni tiɛ wɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, nzu ti ye am sukoro ka am sɔ m ŋgɛɛ a?.

eng: The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: Deke kɛrɛ bo deke fuin nya wo, pɛtɛm bara yɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: And when Jesus entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred up. Who is this? they asked.
local: Yesu kɔ wuraari Yɛrusalem ni, na miɛ n abo nu, na ba bisa ka, Ye sɔnɔ bɔnɔ la ahin a.

eng: Show me the coin used to pay the tax. And they brought him a denarion.
local: Am fa ŋwaa n bo bu fa tɔ lampo kaari n bra na m niɛ. Na ba fa ŋwaa n aba ama i.

eng: You testify against yourselves that you are children of those who murdered the prophets.
local: Nyini ti, am asoro su ka am ti bɛrɛ bo bu yo kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: The crowds answered, Its the prophet Jesus from Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Na jama n abɔ nu ka, Ahin ti Yesu wɔ, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n bo i fite Nazarɛti miɛ n bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni.

eng: Now immediately after the suffering of that time the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light. The stars will fall from the sky and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Saŋga bo nyini wahara n kɛrɛ ba siinri, Awosin káta wiɛ n su, na sara n gusu tá má bo minɛm ŋu i. Na asara­mbaam fíte aŋgoro titi tɔtɔ, na aŋgoro n kɛrɛ kpísi.

eng: Whose image and inscription is this? he asked.
local: na wɔ de ŋwaa n aniɛ, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Ŋma dɛɛ tii ni i duma wo ŋwaa n su n a.

eng: Go ahead, complete what your ancestors did.
local: Tɔ, i ti ye ni ka am de am nam jinaa­biri ni, na am yo bu satiɛ yoo­wam n kɔ wie.

eng: Then Jesus went into the temple and threw out all those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Na Yesu akɔ awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ fɔn neŋge tɔ­fɔm ni to­fɔm kɛrɛ afite awuru n nu, na wɔ kaaki ŋwaa kaaki­fɔm teebirim, ni tiiwa tɔ­fɔm biɛm kɛrɛ alanlaŋga.

eng: Then the sign of the Human One will appear in the sky. At that time all the tribes of the earth will be full of sadness, and they will see the Human One coming in the heavenly clouds with power and great splendor.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, na minɛ ŋú deke be aŋgoro bo i kere ka mini Adamande Wa ni suba. Na minɛm bo bu wo durunya n nu n kɛrɛ bó awie, na bu nyá ŋu ka mini Adamande Wa ni sufite aŋgoro yinyiŋgu n nu suje piɛpiɛu suba ni yiko bambaka ni.

eng: Caesars, they replied. Then he said, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Fɛmɛ Siza dɛɛ a. Na Yesu ase bu ka, Tɔ, deke bo i ti Fɛmɛ Siza dɛɛ, am fa ma Fɛmɛ Siza, na deke bo i ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ, am fa ma Nyɛmɛ.

eng: You snakes! You children of snakes! How will you be able to escape the judgment of hell?.
local: Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm bo am ti woom na am ti ka pumpuni nbaam ni, am yo sɛ ye am koro ŋmati fite nu, bo am wura má cɛɛma sin n nu a?.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be called a house of prayer. But youve made it a hideout for crooks.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, M awuru ni, bu bá fere i ka awuru bo bu bá sere Nyɛmɛ nu wɔ. Ama, ambɛrɛ afa i yo awiefɔm fiɛɛ­biri.

eng: He will send his angels with the sound of a great trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth, from one end of the sky to the other.
local: Na bu tí ka awiɛ be sukpura ni faŋga su, na m nyá sunma Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm durunya n awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, na bu tíɛn minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu durunya n nu n kɛrɛ nɔɔ.

eng: When they heard this they were astonished, and they departed.
local: Saŋga bo bu tiiri ahin nɔaniɛ n sɔ ni, na ba miɛ, na ba kɔ ayaki i.

eng: Therefore, look, Im sending you prophets, wise people, and legal experts. Some of them you will kill and crucify. And some you will beat in your synagogues and chase from city to city.
local: Nyini ti ye m súnma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm, ni minɛ alecira­fɔm, ni kere­fɔm am dɔ n a. Am kún bu nbem, na am fá bu nbem sense baka jaanu n su, na am fín bu nbem ŋgbere, am tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, na am kɔ́ miɛ miɛm su na am kere bu wuwuruwa.

eng: People who were blind and lame came to Jesus in the temple, and he healed them.
local: Na anyinsinm, ni wɔbukam aba i dɔ, Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ ma bu anya laifiɛ.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Am fa damba baka n fa se nyaari. Saŋga bo am ba ŋu ka i nyawa akpɔnkpɔn, na i sufiɛfiɛ fɔfɔrɛ ni, am si ka dɔdɔɔri saŋga adodo lɛɛ.

eng: That same day Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n gbingbin na Sadusi­fɔ nbem aba Yesu dɔ. Bu dɛɛ keree­wa la ka Nyɛmɛ téŋge má minɛm bu yiwee n nu n a. Na ba ba abisa i jɔrɛ be, bu ŋa.

eng: Therefore, upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been poured out on the earth, from the blood of that righteous man Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ cín ambɛrɛ sui fa kɔ kabo am nam yo kuunri minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ durunya n nu. Bu yo yoori sɔ ɔ fite saŋga n bo bu kuunri sɔnɔ be bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n na bu fere i ka Abɛli ni, fa ba juuri saŋga n bo bu kuunri sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Zakariya, Berakaya wa ni. Yiri ye am kuunri i Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n ni Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n afiɛn n a.

eng: But when the chief priests and legal experts saw the amazing things he was doing and the children shouting in the temple, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were angry.
local: Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n ŋuuri sam n bo i yoori bu ni, ni kabo nbaatumam bo bu woori Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu bo tuntuunri i ka, Hosana . Ya kansi Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma ni, na yaa ati bu.

eng: In the same way, when you see all these things, you know that the Human One is near, at the door.
local: Mini Adamande Wa n ba ni bá yo sɔ ɔ. Saŋga bo am ba ŋu ka nyini sam kɛrɛ bá suyo ni, am si tenle su ka saŋga n adodo lɛɛ, i cɛ́ má kun.

eng: They asked, Teacher, Moses said, If a man who doesnt have children dies, his brother must marry his wife and produce children for his brother.
local: Ya Kere­fɔ, Moyisi yo seeri ka, nzɛn sɔnɔ wu yakiiri i yi, na i ni bara n anya má baa, wɔ yo daka ka i niɛma n fa kuna­fɔ n na bu wu nbaam ma yiri n bo wɔ wu ni.

eng: I assure you that all these things will come upon this generation.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, nyini n kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ bá cin ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm sui n a.

eng: They said to Jesus, Do you hear what these children are saying? Yes, he answered. Havent you ever read, From the mouths of babies and infants youve arranged praise for yourself? .
local: Nyini ti, na ba bisa Yesu ka, A ti kabo nbaatumam n ase ni e? Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Ɛɛn, ma ti. Am nya kan má kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n wɔ? Bo ba kɛrɛ ka, Wɔrɔ Nyɛmɛ ye a kere nbaatumam, ni nbaatuma kan kanm, kabo bu kansi u nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, ahin nyuma­fɔm busu kɛrɛ wú wie má ka, ye nyini sam kɛrɛ bá yo a.

eng: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married, then died. Because he had no children he left his widow to his brother.
local: Tɔ, sɔnɔ be yo laari nbaa­biɛsɔm nso wɔ, ya nu fɛn, na baa­kpiɛn n aja bara, na wɔ wu ayaki bara ni, ba wu má baa.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! You who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you. How often I wanted to gather your people together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Na Yesu abo awie ase ka, Keto, keto, ambɛrɛ Yɛrusalem­fɔm. Ambɛrɛ kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n a, na minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ sunmaari bu am dɔ ni, am atu bu yabuɛ. Saŋga kɛrɛ m koroori ka m tiɛn am nɔɔ, na m fa m saa bara yɛ am, kabo akɔ tiɛn i nbaam nɔɔ, i laŋkpɛtɛ nu ni, ama, am soro má m su.

eng: Then he left them and went out of the city to Bethany and spent the night there.
local: Na Yesu ayaki bu bɛrɛ, na wɔ fite miɛ n nu akɔ Betani miɛ n nu, akɔ ada dɔ.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Aŋgoro n ni asiɛŋgu n kɛrɛ bá la awieeri, ama, m dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n yiri la má awieeri cɛɛn be.

eng: The same thing happened with the second brother and the third, and in fact with all seven brothers.
local: Sɔ ye bo i tɔ i su nnyɔ su, ni nsan su n yoori a, haari fa kɔ kpuraari nso su ni.

eng: Look, your house is left to you deserted.
local: Am niɛ fɛn, Nyɛmɛ yáki am nu ji, na am miɛ n ká ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Early in the morning as Jesus was returning to the city, he was hungry.
local: I alecin ŋgarama fuun, na Yesu sukpie i nyi kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ n kunnu, na ahɔɛ ati i.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the heavenly angels and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Be si má saŋga ni, wara cɛɛn n bo m bá kpie m nyi ba ni. Haari Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm bo bu wo i dɔ ni, ni mini bo m ti i wa ni mmɔɔ si má. Má ka ya Si Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi si a.

eng: Finally, the woman died.
local: Na bara n ni i tii aba aka sin awu.

eng: I tell you, you wont see me until you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, fite kisa fa kɔ, am ŋú má m kun, má ka saŋga bo am bá se ka, Tere­fiɛ wo yiri n bo i suba Micɛra n dɛɛ duma n nu n dɔ.

eng: He saw a fig tree along the road, but when he came to it, he found nothing except leaves. Then he said to it, Youll never again bear fruit! The fig tree dried up at once.
local: Na wɔ niɛ aŋu damba baka be, atin n nɔɔ, na wɔ kɔ aniɛ ka i nyá suu­wa n be di. Ama, i kɔ juuri baka n bu ni, wɔ ŋu má pui su, se nyawa ŋgumi a. Dɔ na wɔ se baka n ka, Sin nyuma sin, wɔrɔ su má suu­wa kun. Dika n nu bɛrɛ, na baka n akaba awu.

eng: As it was in the time of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Human One.
local: Mini Adamande Wa n ba saŋga ni yó kabo sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Nowa saŋga na i yoori n wɔ.

eng: At the resurrection, which of the seven brothers will be her husband? They were all married to her.
local: Tɔ, kabo bu minɛ nso n kɛrɛ aja bara n na ba wu sɔ ni, cɛɛn bo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge minɛm bu yiwee n nu ni, bara ni ká ŋma dɛɛ tenle su a.

eng: When the disciples saw it, they were amazed. How did the fig tree dry up so fast? they asked.
local: Saŋga bo i susu­fɔm n ŋuuri nyini n sɔ ni, na ba yo alekutura. Na ba bisa i ka, Nzu ti ye baka n wuuri ndende sɔ a.

eng: In those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
local: Saŋga bo wɔmbu n nya di má durunya n ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ woori di ni nu su wɔ, na nbaram ni nbiɛsɔm suja bu beŋgum, fa kɔ juuri haari cɛɛn n bo Nowa wuraari lee bambaka n nu ni.

eng: Jesus responded, You are wrong because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am amini atin. Dama am ti má deke bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n bu, na am kaaki si má kabo Nyɛmɛ yiko n ti barasu.

eng: Jesus responded, I assure you that if you have faith and dont doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree. You will even say to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the lake. And it will happen.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada na a yo má sika, a koro yo sa n bo ma fa ayo ahin damba baka n be. Má ahin ni ŋgumi, haari nzɛn a seeri ahin buka n ka, Yasu kɔ to tieku n nu, i yó sɔ ɔ.

eng: They didnt know what was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. The coming of the Human One will be like that.
local: Minɛm ayo asi má sa bo i bá yo ni, haari cɛɛn n bo wɔmbu n ba diiri bu kɛrɛ ni. I yó sɔ ɔ, fa kɔ saŋga n bo mini Adamande Wa ni bá ba ni.

eng: At the resurrection people wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like angels from God.
local: Dama saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge minɛm bu yiwee n nu ni, ajaa wo má bɛrɛ kun. Ama, nyini cɛɛn ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kaaki ka Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm bo bu wo aŋgoro n wɔ, dama bu ja má ajaa kun.

eng: If you have faith, you will receive whatever you pray for.
local: Nzɛn am yoori yarada, deke kɛrɛ bo am sere Nyɛmɛ ka am koro i, Nyɛmɛ yó i ma am.

eng: At that time there will be two men in the field. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, minɛ nnyɔ bá wo juma yo su fiesu, na Nyɛmɛ má bu bá fa kun n kɔ yaki kun ni.

eng: As for the resurrection of the dead, havent you read what God told you.
local: Na fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge minɛm bu yiwee n nu barasu ni, am akan má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu kabo i yo seeri am n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? I ŋa.

eng: Im the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? He isnt the God of the dead but of the living.
local: Mini ti Abraham, ni Izaki, ni Jakɔbu dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ n a. Na Yesu asa su ase bu ka, Tɔ, kabo Nyɛmɛ ŋa i ti nyini minɛm n dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ ni, nyini kere ka bu toko la ŋgɔɔ lɛɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ ti má bo ba wu n dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ, ama, i ti bo bu la ŋgɔɔ ni dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: Two women will be grinding at the mill. One will be taken and the other left.
local: Na nbaram nnyɔ wo ŋgani saan­biri, na Nyɛmɛ má bu bá fa kun n kɔ yaki kun ni.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, the chief priests and elders of the people came to him as he was teaching. They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: Na Yesu akpie i nyi aba awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na i sukere minɛm. I wo kere su sɔ n na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm aba i dɔ, na ba bisa i ka, Ŋma dɛɛ yiko nu ye a yo nyini sam kɛrɛ a? Ŋma ma u nyini yiko ni, ye a yo nyini sam n a.

eng: Now when the crowd heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
local: Minɛm n tiiri i keree­wa n sɔ ni, na ba yo alekutura.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know what day the Lord is coming.
local: Tɔ, nyini ti, má am fa am ŋu ji na am daafi, dama am si má cɛɛn n bo am Mibiɛ ni bá ni.

eng: But you understand that if the head of the house knew at what time the thief would come, he would keep alert and wouldnt allow the thief to break into his house.
local: Am si tenle su ka nzɛn má awuru­fɔ­biɛ n si saŋga bo awiefɔ ni bá i dika na i bá wɔ i, má i tana ŋminda a, na awiefɔ n koro ba wura má.

eng: When the Pharisees heard that Jesus had left the Sadducees speechless, they met together.
local: Saŋga bo Farasi­fɔm tiiri ka Yesu ajɔjɔ ayi Sadusi­fɔm ni, na ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ i dɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, I have a question for you. If you tell me the answer, Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu, ase bu ka, Am ma m du mɔ bisa am bisaa­wa kun be ka, na nzɛn am bɔɔri m nu, na m gusu nyá se am deke bo m yiko n fite.

eng: Therefore, you also should be prepared, because the Human One will come at a time you dont know.
local: Nyini ti, ambɛrɛ gusu kpa am ŋu saŋga kɛrɛ, dama mini Adamande Wa ni, m bá saŋga bo am sunsun má ka m bá n wɔ.

eng: One of them, a legal expert, tested him.
local: Na bu nu kun be, bo i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ n abisa Yesu bisaa­wa be ka i sɔ i ŋgɛɛ. I ŋa.

eng: Where did John get his authority to baptize? Did he get it from heaven or from humans? They argued among themselves, If we say from heaven, hell say to us, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Ŋma maari Yohane atin ye i ba biɛɛri minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue n a? Nyɛmɛ nzɛn adamande? Na ba se akuruwa bu ŋuŋu nu, kabo bu bɔ Yesu nu barasu. Bu ŋa, Nzɛn ya se i ka Nyɛmɛ maari i atin a, i bísa ya ka ye nzu ti ye ya da má Yohane ŋu a?.

eng: Who then are the faithful and wise servants whom their master puts in charge of giving food at the right time to those who live in his house?.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Akɔɔ kpiɛn n bo i la dabuŋu na i kaaki la alecira n la ŋma a? Yiri la bo i mibiɛ n afa akɔɔ nyiŋgam awura i saa nu ka i kpaaki diire nu ma bu, saŋga bo i ti ye ni ka i ma bu ni.

eng: But we cant say from humans because were afraid of the crowd, since everyone thinks John was a prophet.
local: Ama, nzɛn ya seeri i ka adamande dɔ, ya soro sɛrɛ ka jama ni yáki má ya. Má pui ti, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ soroori su ka Yohane yo tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: Teacher, what is the greatest commandment in the Law.
local: Ya Kere­fɔ, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo i wo i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu ni, bɔnɔ tara bu kɛrɛ a.

eng: He replied, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, and with all your mind.
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Ba kɛrɛ ka, Koro Micɛra u Nyɛmɛ ni, ni u ahore­mbaa kun, ni u awiɛwiɛ, ni u sunsunni kɛrɛ.

eng: Then they replied, We dont know. Jesus also said to them, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Nyini ti, na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ya si má. Na yiri gusu ase bu ka, Mini mmɔɔ sé má am sɔnɔ bo i dɛɛ yiko nu na m yo ahin sam n kɛrɛ ni.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Nzɛn i mibiɛ n ba ŋuuri ka i suyo i juma kabo i seeri ka i yo ni, tere­fiɛ wo nyini akɔɔ n dɔ.

eng: This is the first and greatest commandment.
local: Ahin ti nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo i tara i kɛrɛ a.

eng: What do you think? A man had two sons. Now he came to the first and said, Son, go and work in the vineyard today.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, Am sunsun ka ahin miɛɛnu bu la ka sɛ wɔ lɛɛ? Sɔnɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, i la nbaa­biɛsɔm nnyɔ. Cɛɛn be na wɔ kɔ kpiɛn n dɔ, akɔ ase i ka, M wa, kɔ baka suu­wam fie n nu, na a kɔ yo juma nyuma.

eng: I assure you that he will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, mibiɛ ni fá i aja kɛrɛ wura nyini akɔɔ n saa nu wɔ, na i níɛ i dika.

eng: And the second is like it: You must love your neighbor as you love yourself .
local: Bo i tɔ su nnyɔ su n ti ka nyini n wɔ. Ka o, Koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni.

eng: No, I dont want to, he replied. But later he changed his mind and went.
local: Na baa n ase i ka, M kɔ́ má. Ama, kasin, na baa n aba akaaki i sunsunni, na wɔ kɔ.

eng: But suppose those bad servants should say to themselves, My master wont come until later.
local: Ama, nzɛn i ti akɔɔ tiɛ­fɔ, i súnsun ka i mibiɛ ni kpíe má i nyi nde.

eng: All the Law and the Prophets depend on these two commands.
local: Kabo ba kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wam n kadasi n nu, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n dɛɛ n nu ni, i kɛrɛ jina ahin nɔaniɛ maa­wa nnyɔm n su wɔ.

eng: The father said the same thing to the other son, who replied, Yes, sir. But he didnt go.
local: Na wɔ kɔ nyiŋga n dɔ, akɔ ase i gusu. Na baa n ase i ka, Ma ti, m kɔ́. Ama, wɔ kɔ má.

eng: And suppose they began to beat their fellow servants and to eat and drink with the drunks?.
local: A ŋu ka i subo akɔɔ nyiŋgam wɔ, na i sudi, na i ni nzan nu­fɔm sunu bo.

eng: Now as the Pharisees were gathering, Jesus asked them.
local: Saŋga bo Farasi­fɔ nbem ba tiɛntiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ ni, na Yesu abisa bu jɔrɛ be fa kɔ Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n su. I ŋa.

eng: Which one of these two did his fathers will? They said, The first one. Jesus said to them, I assure you that tax collectors and prostitutes are entering Gods kingdom ahead of you.
local: Tɔ, bu minɛ nnyɔm n nu, ŋma yoori i si n sa koroo­wa? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Baa­kpiɛn ni. Dɔ na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, lampo­de­fɔm ni sakaram kɛrɛ sufa Nyɛmɛ yo bu fɛmɛ yaki am.

eng: The master of those servants will come on a day when they are not expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict.
local: Dɔ, na i mibiɛ n ba kpieeri i nyi baari cɛɛn bo i sunsun má ka i bá, na i kaaki si má ba n saŋga ni.

eng: What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he? Davids son, they replied.
local: Am fa Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n bu ŋma a? Am sunsun ka i ti ŋma dɛɛ anuma a? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, I ti Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma a.

eng: He said, Then how is it that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, called him Lord when he said.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, Ye nzu ti ye Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n ba wuraari Fɛmɛ Davidi ŋu, ye i fereeri i ka i Mibiɛ a? I ŋa.

eng: He will cut them in pieces and put them in a place with the hypocrites. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Nyini saŋga n na i mibiɛ n cín i sui agaya, na i fá i bo burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm n su. Dɔ na i sún, na i kán i saa.

eng: For John came to you on the righteous road, and you didnt believe him. But tax collectors and prostitutes believed him. Yet even after you saw this, you didnt change your hearts and lives and you didnt believe him.
local: Dama ka, Yohane baari ka i bá kere am sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ama, am ayo má i yarada, na lampo­de­fɔm ni sakaram, bɛrɛ ayo i. Haari ni bo am ŋuuri kabo bu yoori yarada ni, am akaaki má am wo, na am yo i yarada.

eng: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool?.
local: Na Micɛra n ase m Mibiɛ n ka, Ba tana m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su fɛn, haari m bá má u kpɔ­fɔm kɛrɛ ba kutu u jaa bu.

eng: Listen to another parable. There was a landowner who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am so am sui na am ti miɛɛnu n be bekun. Awuru­fɔ­biɛ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, na wɔ dɔ i baka suu­wa barasu be fie, na wɔ kanda abara ayɛ fie ni. Na wɔ lokoti yabuɛ bambaka be kunma, ka i sumiɛmiɛ baka suu­wam ni nzue gɔ nu. Na wɔ ta kpafiri lenleŋge be ka bu tana su suniɛ fie n kɛrɛ su, na wɔ nya afa fie n awura i npa­di­fɔm saa nu. Na wɔ kɔ atin.

eng: When it was time for harvest, he sent his servants to the tenant farmers to collect his fruit.
local: Saŋga bo i siiri ka wɔ ka kaan na baka suu­wam n bí ni, na wɔ sunma i akɔɔm i npa­di­fɔm n dɔ ka bu kɔ de i dɛɛ bo ba nya i n bra.

eng: If David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Tɔ, nzɛn Fɛmɛ Davidi mmɔɔ ni i tii yo fereeri Kristo n ka i Mibiɛ, ye Kristo n yo sɛ kaaki yo Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma a.

eng: But the tenant farmers grabbed his servants. They beat some of them, and some of them they killed. Some of them they stoned to death.
local: Akɔɔm n kɔ juuri ni, na ba bo bu nu kun, na ba kun nyiŋga, na ba tu nyiŋga n gusu yabuɛ.

eng: Nobody was able to answer him. And from that day forward nobody dared to ask him anything.
local: Be akoro anya má nɔaniɛ be abɔ má i nu. Nyini ti, fite nyini cɛɛn n sin, be abo má su ka i bisa Yesu jɔrɛ bekun.

eng: Again he sent other servants, more than the first group. They treated them in the same way.
local: Na sɔnɔ n asunma i akɔɔ nyiŋgam bekun, bu sunnu tara ciciri dɛɛm ni, na npa­di­fɔm n ayo bu kabo bu yoori ciciri su dɛɛm ni.

eng: Finally he sent his son to them. They will respect my son, he said.
local: Na kasin, na wɔ ba asunma i wa, na wɔ se ka, Bu má m wa n yiri jirima.

eng: But when the tenant farmers saw the son, they said to each other, This is the heir. Come on, lets kill him and well have his inheritance.
local: Ama, saŋga bo npa­di­fɔm n ŋuuri ka baa ni suba ni, na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, I wa n bo i dí i aja n lɛɛ suba n a. Am bra na ya kun i na aja n kɛrɛ ká ya dɛɛ.

eng: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
local: Dɔ na ba tara i, na ba cin i atu baka suu­wa fie n ahɛmɛ, na ba kun i.

eng: When the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenant farmers.
local: Na Yesu anya abisa bu ka, Am sunsun ka fie fɔ n ba baari, i yó nyini npa­di­fɔm ni sɛ wɔ.

eng: They said, He will totally destroy those wicked farmers and rent the vineyard to other tenant farmers who will give him the fruit when its ready.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, I bá ma nyini minɛ tiɛ­fɔm n kɛrɛ wu kenkesi yiwee wɔ, na i fá fie n wura npa­di­fɔ fɔfɔrɛm saa nu, bɛrɛ bo bu má i nya i dɛɛ baka suu­wam, saŋga bo bu ba biiri ni.

eng: Jesus said to them, Havent you ever read in the scriptures, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes?.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am nya kan má kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, nzɛn sɛ? Bu ŋa, Yabuɛ n bo bondo ta­fɔm n faari i tuuri ni, yiri ye wɔ kaaki bondo n jaa yabuɛm n kɛrɛ tii a. Micɛra ni yoori i sɔ a. Ya ŋu i na wɔ yo ya alekutura.

eng: Therefore, I tell you that Gods kingdom will be taken away from you and will be given to a people who produce its fruit.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, bu dé Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n fite am dɔ, na bu fá ma minɛm bo bu bita i na i la kpɛrɛ ni. .

eng: Whoever falls on this stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kpaasiiri ahin yabuɛ n su, i mi búbu nu wɔ mumuku, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo ahin yabuɛ n tɔɔri i ŋu, i sán i mi samɛnɛ wɔ.

eng: Now when the chief priests and the Pharisees heard the parable, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
local: Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n tiiri miɛɛnu n bo Yesu boori i sɔ ni, na ba fa nu ka i boori i niiri bɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: They were trying to arrest him, but they feared the crowds, who thought he was a prophet.
local: Nyini ti, na ba kpini atin kabo bu yo na bu tara i, ama, bu soroori jama n wɔ, dama bu kɛrɛ ŋa i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ.

eng: At that time the kingdom of heaven will be like ten young bridesmaids who took their lamps and went out to meet the groom.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Saŋga bo mini Adamande Wa ni bá ba, na Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su ni, i yó ka nsuŋguru buru nbem bo bu faari bu fitanam ka bu kɔ kpa konyafan atin n wɔ.

eng: Now five of them were wise, and the other five were foolish.
local: Bu minɛ nnu tiiri alecira­fɔm wɔ, na nnu nyiŋgam n ati sinnzinm.

eng: The foolish ones took their lamps but didnt bring oil for them.
local: Na sinnzinm n afa bu fitanam, ama, ba bita má ŋguin nu.

eng: But the wise ones took their lamps and also brought containers of oil.
local: na alecira­fɔm n bɛrɛ afa ŋguin abita nu.

eng: When the groom was late in coming, they all became drowsy and went to sleep.
local: Na ba kɔ aŋminda konyafan n acɛ, wɔ ba má nde, na daafiri aba ati nsuŋgurum ni, na ba daafi.

eng: But at midnight there was a cry, Look, the groom! Come out to meet him.
local: Na jinanjin saŋga, na ba ti ka bu subo ndoori ka, Konyafan ni suba o, am fite na ya kɔ kpa i atin.

eng: Then all those bridesmaids got up and prepared their lamps.
local: Na nsuŋguru burum kɛrɛ ayasu, ayo bu fitanam dɔŋgu.

eng: But the foolish bridesmaids said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps have gone out.
local: Na sinnzinm n ase alecira­fɔm ni ka, Am bu am ŋguin n be ma ya, ya fitanam ni sunɔ.

eng: But the wise bridesmaids replied, No, because if we share with you, there wont be enough for our lamps and yours. We have a better idea. You go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
local: Na alecira­fɔm n ase bu ka, Ai, ya ŋguin ni koro ju má ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ. Am kɔ ŋguin tɔ­fɔm dɔ, na am kɔ to.

eng: But while they were gone to buy oil, the groom came. Those who were ready went with him into the wedding. Then the door was shut.
local: Na nsuŋguru sinnzinm n akɔ ka bu kɔ to ŋguin ni. Bu kɔ wieeri ni, na konyafan n aju. Na nsuŋguru nnum bo ba kpa bu ŋu ni, asu i su, awura konyaya awuru n nu. Na ba tu anɔ ni.

eng: Later the other bridesmaids came and said, Lord, lord, open the door for us.
local: Kasin, na nsuŋguru nyiŋgam bo bu kɔɔri ŋguin to ni, aba ajina anɔ n nu, abo afere i ka, Ya mibiɛ, ya mibiɛ, teke anɔ n ma ya na ya wura.

eng: But he replied, I tell you the truth, I dont know you.
local: Na konyafan n ase bu ka, Ai, nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, m si má am cɛɛn be cɛɛn be.

eng: Therefore, keep alert, because you dont know the day or the hour.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Tɔ, nyini ti, má am fa am ŋu ji na am daafi, dama am si má cɛɛn n bo m bá ni.

eng: The kingdom of heaven is like a man who was leaving on a trip. He called his servants and handed his possessions over to them.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Saŋga bo m bá ba ni, i yó kabo sɔnɔ be sukɔ atin, na i fereeri i akɔɔm, na i faari i neŋge kɛrɛ wuraari bu saa nu, ka bu niɛ i dika n wɔ.

eng: To one he gave five valuable coins, and to another he gave two, and to another he gave one. He gave to each servant according to that servants ability. Then he left on his journey.
local: Na wɔ fa ŋwaa akpaaki bu kɛrɛ nu, kabo bu koro bita i barasu. I faari ŋwaa n busu nnu maari kun wɔ, na wɔ fa busu nnyɔ n ama kun gusu, na wɔ nya afa busu kun n ama nyiŋga ni. Na wɔ kɔ atin ni.

eng: After the man left, the servant who had five valuable coins took them and went to work doing business with them. He gained five more.
local: Na bo i maari i busu nnu ni afa abo wata bu, na wɔ nya i ŋusu nnu abuka su.

eng: In the same way, the one who had two valuable coins gained two more.
local: Na bo i maari i busu nnyɔ n gusu ayo sɔ, na wɔ nya i ŋusu nnyɔ abuka su.

eng: But the servant who had received the one valuable coin dug a hole in the ground and buried his masters money.
local: Ama, bo i maari i sesee­wa kun ni kɔ funduuri kunma wɔ, na wɔ fa i dɛɛ ŋwaa n awura nu, na wɔ kata su.

eng: Now after a long time the master of those servants returned and settled accounts with them.
local: Na bu mibiɛ n akɔ acɛ dɔ, haari na wɔ ba aba ka i ba niɛ kabo bu bita i ŋwaa n barasu ni.

eng: The one who had received five valuable coins came forward with five additional coins. He said, Master, you gave me five valuable coins. Look, Ive gained five more.
local: Na bo i maari i busu nnu n afa i dɛɛ ŋwaa aba, na wɔ se i mibiɛ n ka, M mibiɛ, niɛ. A maari m ŋwaa n busu nnu wɔ, ama, ma nya i ŋusu nnu abuka su.

eng: His master replied, Excellent! You are a good and faithful servant! Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Na i mibiɛ n ase i ka, Nfan. Wɔrɔ akɔɔ kpa bo a la dabuŋu ni, kabo a kere ka a la dabuŋu niɛ deke kaan su ni, m fá dɔŋgu n mmɔɔ wura u saa nu na a niɛ i dika. Bra na a ba nya ahorejɔ n bo ma nya i n be.

eng: The second servant also came forward and said, Master, you gave me two valuable coins. Look, Ive gained two more.
local: Na bo i maari i busu nnyɔ n gusu aba ase i mibiɛ n ka, M mibiɛ, niɛ. A maari m ŋwaa n busu nnyɔ ɔ, ama, ma nya i ŋusu nnyɔ abuka su.

eng: His master replied, Well done! You are a good and faithful servant. Youve been faithful over a little. Ill put you in charge of much. Come, celebrate with me.
local: Na i mibiɛ n ase i ka, Nfan. Wɔrɔ akɔɔ kpa bo a la dabuŋu ni, kabo a kere ka a la dabuŋu niɛ deke kaan su ni, m fá dɔŋgu n mmɔɔ wura u saa nu na a niɛ i dika. Bra na a ba nya ahorejɔ n bo ma nya i n be.

eng: Now the one who had received one valuable coin came and said, Master, I knew that you are a hard man. You harvest grain where you havent sown. You gather crops where you havent spread seed.
local: Kasin, na bo i maari i sesee­wa kun ni aba, na wɔ se ka, M mibiɛ, m si u sa ka a ti sɔnɔ faŋga­fɔ ɔ. Deke bo a dɔ má i ni, a kpiɛ nyini wɔ. Na deke bo a bobo má i ni, a tiɛntiɛn nyini nɔɔ a.

eng: So I was afraid. And I hid my valuable coin in the ground. Here, you have whats yours.
local: Nyini ti ye sɛrɛ tiiri m a, na ma fundu kunma afa u ŋwaa n afiɛ i nu. Yiri yaa, de u deke.

eng: His master replied, You evil and lazy servant! You knew that I harvest grain where I havent sown and that I gather crops where I havent spread seed?.
local: Na i mibiɛ n akaaki ase i ka, Wɔrɔ akɔɔ tiɛ nviɛya­fɔ ni, a si tenle su ka deke bo ma dɔ má i ni, m kpiɛ nyini wɔ, na deke bo ma bobo má i ni, m tiɛntiɛn nyini nɔɔ a.

eng: In that case, you should have turned my money over to the bankers so that when I returned, you could give me what belonged to me with interest.
local: Tɔ, má a si ka i ti sɔ ni, má wɔ yo daka ka a fa m ŋwaa n kɔ seeri baŋkifɔm dɔ, na má m ba kpieeri m nyi baari, má m de m deke na m nya kpɛrɛ buka su.

eng: Therefore, take from him the valuable coin and give it to the one who has ten coins.
local: Dɔ na mibiɛ n ase i minɛm ka, Tɔ, am de ŋwaa ni i dɔ kisa kisa, na am fa ma akɔɔ n bo i bita ŋwaa n busu buru ni.

eng: Those who have much will receive more, and they will have more than they need. But as for those who dont have much, even the little bit they have will be taken away from them.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ nya deke na i mi si i bita, bu má i buka su wɔ, na i nyá beberebe, ama, bo i si má i bita, haari ni nyini kaan n bo i la i ni, bu cín de i dɔ ɔ.

eng: Now take the worthless servant and throw him out into the farthest darkness. People there will be weeping and grinding their teeth.
local: Ama, m ahin akɔɔ ŋgbɛɛn ni, am fa i tu awosin n nu, deke bo i sún, na i kán i saa.

eng: Now when the Human One comes in his majesty and all his angels are with him, he will sit on his majestic throne.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Saŋga bo mini Adamande Wa ni bá baari ni m jirima woo­wa n nu ni, ni Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm kɛrɛ ni, m bá tana m jirima fɛmɛya biɛ n su.

eng: All the nations will be gathered in front of him. He will separate them from each other, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
local: Na durunya n nu minɛm kɛrɛ bá tiɛn bu nɔɔ m nyunu, na m kpáaki bu nu, busu nnyɔ, kabo bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpaaki yi nbɔɛm ni sirim nu barasu ni.

eng: He will put the sheep on his right side. But the goats he will put on his left.
local: Na m má m dɛɛ minɛm bo bu ti ka nbɔɛm ni jina m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, na m má bo ba ka na bu ti ka sirim ni jina m saa biɛ su.

eng: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to look at the tomb.
local: Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n sin dɛɛ alecin, bakuɛ n bumboori, mɔnɛ cɛɛn ŋgarama fuun, na Mariya Magadala­fɔ ni, ni Mariya nyiŋga ni, akɔ ka bu kɔ niɛ Yesu faŋ­kunma ni.

eng: Then the king will say to those on his right, Come, you who will receive good things from my Father. Inherit the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world began.
local: Na mini fɛmɛ n sé bo bu wo m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su n ka, Ambɛrɛ bo m Si Nyɛmɛ ayo am kisi ni, am bra na am ba de fɛmɛya n bo wɔ sɛsɛ i su ase ama am, fite durunya n bumboori kɛrɛ ni.

eng: I was hungry and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty and you gave me a drink. I was a stranger and you welcomed me.
local: Deke bo i ti na m se am sɔ n la ka, ahɔɛ yo tiiri m, na am ama m diire. Nzuehɔɛ yo tiiri m, na am ama m nzue. M yo baari am dɔ ka wɔfɔ, na am ade m.

eng: Look, there was a great earthquake, for an angel from the Lord came down from heaven. Coming to the stone, he rolled it away and sat on it.
local: Kpeŋga na asiɛn n akpisi agaya, dama Micɛra n mɛrɛkɛ be fiteeri aŋgoro jura ba kundu yiiri yabuɛ bambaka n faŋ­kunma n anɔ n su a, na wɔ fun atana su.

eng: I was naked and you gave me clothes to wear. I was sick and you took care of me. I was in prison and you visited me.
local: M yo kaari m bu ŋgbɛɛn, na am ama m sutura. M yo daari tukpaki, na am aba aniɛ m dika. Bu yo nyiiri m dan­saraka, na am akɔ aniɛ m busu.

eng: Now his face was like lightning and his clothes as white as snow.
local: Na wɔ ta fofoe papasi ka nzue kpɛsɛɛ­wa dɛɛ su, na i sutura n kɛrɛ ayo fofoe ka cisɛn.

eng: Then those who are righteous will reply to him, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you a drink?.
local: Dɔ na m minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su ni nyá bɔ m nu ka, Ya Mibiɛ, saŋga bɔnɔ ye ya ŋuuri ka ahɔɛ ati u, ye ya maari u diire a, wara nzuehɔɛ ati u, ye ya maari u nzue a?.

eng: The guards were so terrified of him that they shook with fear and became like dead men.
local: Na sɛrɛ ati tintini­jina­fɔm ni, na bu ŋuniɛ subobo bu, na ba yo ka ba wu dɛɛ su wɔ.

eng: When did we see you as a stranger and welcome you, or naked and give you clothes to wear?.
local: Saŋga bɔnɔ tenle su, ye a baari ya dɔ ka wɔfɔ, ye ya deeri u a, wara saŋga bɔnɔ ye a kaari u bu ŋgbɛɛn, ye ya maari u sutura a?.

eng: But the angel said to the women, Dont be afraid. I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ n ase nbaram n ka, Má am ma sɛrɛ ti am. M si ka am niɛniɛ Yesu bo bu kpamaari i baka jaanu n su n wɔ.

eng: When did we see you sick or in prison and visit you.
local: Saŋga bɔnɔ ye ya ŋuuri ka a daari tukpaki, wara saŋga bɔnɔ ye bu nyiiri u dan­saraka, ye ya kɔ niɛɛri u busu a.

eng: He isnt here, because hes been raised from the dead, just as he said. Come, see the place where they laid him.
local: I wo má fɛn. Wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu, kabo i yo seeri am ni. Am ba niɛ deke bo bu faari i seeri ni.

eng: Then the king will reply to them, I assure you that when you have done it for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you have done it for me.
local: Dɔ na m nyá bɔ bu nu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, saŋga kɛrɛ bo am yo yoori sakpa fa maari m ahin ninbaa kan kanm ni kun, am yo maari mini wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Now hurry, go and tell his disciples, Hes been raised from the dead. Hes going on ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there. Ive given the message to you.
local: Am kpie am nyi na am kɔ se i susu­fɔm ni ka wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu. Am ti fɛn, i sudu am mɔ kɔ Galili awɔrɛ n nu, dɔ ye am kɔ́ ŋu i a. Ma se am lɛɛ.

eng: Then he will say to those on his left, Get away from me, you who will receive terrible things. Go into the unending fire that has been prepared for the devil and his angels.
local: Na m nyá se bo bu wo m saa biɛ su n ka, Ambɛrɛ bo m Si Nyɛmɛ ayo am ŋgbɛɛn na wɔ fa nɔɔ tiɛ ase am ŋu ni, am fa cinsi m bi bɛrɛ na am kɔ wura sin n bo i nɔ má n nu, nyini sin n ye ba tɔrɔ i afa ase Sitana ni i dɛɛ mɛrɛkɛm n a.

eng: With great fear and excitement, they hurried away from the tomb and ran to tell his disciples.
local: Na ba fite faŋ­kunma n nu ndende ni sɛrɛ, ama, ni ahorejɔ, na ba ŋmati na bu kɔ se i susu­fɔm ni.

eng: I was hungry and you didnt give me food to eat. I was thirsty and you didnt give me anything to drink.
local: Deke bo i ti na m se am sɔ n la ka, ahɔɛ yo tiiri m, ama, am ama má m diire. Nzuehɔɛ yo tiiri m, ama, am ama má m nzue.

eng: But Jesus met them and greeted them. They came and grabbed his feet and worshipped him.
local: Bu sukɔ ni, kpeŋga na bu ni Yesu ayɛ, na wɔ yo bu ansɛ. Na ba kutu i jaa bu, na ba ma i jirima.

eng: I was a stranger and you didnt welcome me. I was naked and you didnt give me clothes to wear. I was sick and in prison, and you didnt visit me.
local: M yo baari am dika, ama, am ade má m wɔfɔ. M yo kaari m bu ŋgbɛɛn, ama, am ama má m sutura. M yo daari tukpaki, wara m yo woori dan­saraka nu, ama, am akɔ aniɛ má m busu.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Dont be afraid. Go and tell my brothers that I am going into Galilee. They will see me there.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Má am ma sɛrɛ ti am. Am kɔ se m ninbaam ka bu kɔ Galili, dɔ ye bu kɔ́ ŋu m a.

eng: Then they will reply, Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and didnt do anything to help you.
local: Dɔ na bɛrɛ bo bu wo m saa biɛ su ni nyá bɔ m nu ka, Ya Mibiɛ, saŋga bɔnɔ ye ya ŋuuri u ka ahɔɛ ati u, wara nzuehɔɛ ati u, wara a baari ya wɔfɔ, wara a kaari u bu ŋgbɛɛn, wara a daari tukpaki, wara a woori dan­saraka, ye ya buka má u a.

eng: Now as the women were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the chief priests everything that had happened.
local: Saŋga bo nbaram n wo kɔ su ni, na tintini­jina­fɔm nbem akɔ miɛ n kunnu, akɔ ama Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni labari, fa kɔ sa n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo n su.

eng: Then he will answer, I assure you that when you havent done it for one of the least of these, you havent done it for me.
local: Na m nyá kaaki se bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ayo má sakpa, ama má m ahin ninbaa kan kanm ni kun, am ayo ama má mini wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: They met with the elders and decided to give a large sum of money to the soldiers.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ, afa asɛsɛ su, na ba ma tintini­jina­fɔm ni ŋwaa beberebe.

eng: And they will go away into eternal punishment. But the righteous ones will go into eternal life.
local: Na nyinim bɛrɛ kɔ́ wo sui­cin n bo i la má awieeri n nu. Ama, na bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, bɛrɛ kɔ́ wo ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n nu.

eng: They told them, Say that Jesus disciples came at night and stole his body while you were sleeping.
local: na ba se bu ka, Am se minɛm ka, saŋga bo am adaafi kɔŋguɛ n ye i susu­fɔm ni ba wɔ yiiri i a.

eng: And if the governor hears about this, we will take care of it with him so you will have nothing to worry about.
local: Na nzɛn haari gomina n ba tiiri nyini labari ni, yɛrɛ kán de am tii, na i yó má am pui.

eng: So the soldiers took the money and did as they were told. And this report has spread throughout all Judea to this very day.
local: Na tintini­jina­fɔm n ade ŋwaa n na ba kɔ ase minɛm kabo nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n seeri bu ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti ye nyini labari n abara ayɛ Yuda­fɔm dɔ, fa ba ju haari ni nyuma n a.

eng: Now the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus told them to go.
local: Na i susu­fɔ buru ni kunm n akɔ Galili awɔrɛ n nu na ba fun akɔ buka n su, deke bo Yesu seeri bu ka bu kɔ ni.
