eng: When they saw him, they worshipped him, but some doubted.
local: Saŋga bo bu kɔ ŋuuri i ni, na ba kutu ama i jirima, ama, bu nbem asunsun ka má yiri lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus came near and spoke to them, Ive received all authority in heaven and on earth.
local: Na Yesu acin adodo bu, na wɔ se bu ka, Nyɛmɛ afa aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu fɛn kɛrɛ yiko, afa awura m saa nu.

eng: Therefore, go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
local: Nyini ti, am kɔ durunya n kɛrɛ nu, na am ma daanfiɛ kɛrɛ yo m susu­fɔm. Na am biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue, Si, ni Baa, ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ duma nu.

eng: teaching them to obey everything that Ive commanded you. Look, I myself will be with you every day until the end of this present age.
local: Na am kere bu ka bu de m nɔaniɛ maa­wa n kɛrɛ bo ma fa ama am ni. Am ti fɛn, m wo am dɔ, saŋga kɛrɛ, fa kɔ haari durunya n awieeri.

eng: Jesus returned to the synagogue. A man with a withered hand was there.
local: Na Yesu akaaki akɔ tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu dɔ bekun. Sɔnɔ asaa fuin be woori dɔ.

eng: Wanting to bring charges against Jesus, they were watching Jesus closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath.
local: Na minɛm bo bu wo dɔ n atu bu nyu­mbaa aniɛ Yesu, na bu niɛ ka i túntun asaa fuin n Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, nzɛn i túntun má i, adena bu sɔ́ i ŋgɛɛ na bu járaki i ka wɔ saaki Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ciire ni.

eng: He said to the man with the withered hand, Step up where people can see you.
local: Na Yesu ase asaa fuin n ka, Ba jina afiɛn fɛn.

eng: Then he said to them, Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they said nothing.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka ya yo sakpa Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, nzɛn ya yo satiɛ wɔ? I kaaki ti ye ni ka ya de ŋgɔɔ, nzɛn ya yi ŋgɔɔ ɔ? Ama, ba bɔ má i nu.

eng: Looking around at them with anger, deeply grieved at their unyielding hearts, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did, and his hand was made healthy.
local: Na Yesu aniɛ bu nyunu abara ayɛ, na wɔ fa yaa bu ŋu, na i ahore asaaki fa kɔ bu atii kekere n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ se sɔnɔ n ka, Teŋge u saa ni. Na wɔ teŋge i saa ni, na wɔ yo faŋga bekun.

eng: At that, the Pharisees got together with the supporters of Herod to plan how to destroy Jesus.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm n akaba afite tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu akɔ Fɛmɛ Herode minɛm dɔ, na ba sɛsɛ bu ŋu su kabo bu yó na bu kún Yesu.

eng: Jesus left with his disciples and went to the lake. A large crowd followed him because they had heard what he was doing. They were from Galilee.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n afite akɔ kunma kpiri n nɔɔ su, na jama beberebe afite Galili awɔrɛ n nu asu i su. Nbem gusu fiteeri Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: Jesus began to teach beside the lake again. Such a large crowd gathered that he climbed into a boat there on the lake. He sat in the boat while the whole crowd was nearby on the shore.
local: Na Yesu akaaki akɔ Galili kunma kpiri n nɔɔ su, na wɔ bo i bu sukere minɛm Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, na jama beberebe atiɛn bu nɔɔ i dɔ. Jama n sunnuuri beberebe n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ wura lee nu atana nzue n nu, na jama n kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ fa dodo nzue n nɔɔ su.

eng: Judea, Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and the area surrounding Tyre and Sidon.
local: ni Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, ni Idumeya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na nbem gusu afite Yodan kunma n wiɛ afiiri ni, ni Tiro ni Sidon dɛɛ bue ni. Bu tiiri kabo Yesu yoori alekutura sam barasu ni, nyini ti ye bu baari i dɔ a.

eng: He said many things to them in parables. While teaching them, he said.
local: Na wɔ bo miɛɛnu, na wɔ kere bu dekem beberebe. I ŋa.

eng: Listen to this! A farmer went out to scatter seed.
local: Am ti fɛn. Dɔdɔɔri­fɔ be yo fiteeri sɔ ɔ, ka i kɔ sandi alɛ­mbaa.

eng: As he was scattering seed, some fell on the path; and the birds came and ate it.
local: Saŋga bo i wo sandi su ni, na alɛ­mbaam nbem atɔtɔ atin n nɔɔ, na anumam aba atambu bu ami.

eng: Jesus told his disciples to get a small boat ready for him so the crowd wouldnt crush him.
local: Yesu ama tukpaki­fɔm beberebe anya laifiɛ n dɛɛ ti, bo bu la tukpaki kɛrɛ koro ka bu miɛ bu nyi wɔ, na bu sakati wura na bu fa bu saa kan i. Nyini ti, na wɔ se i susu­fɔm ni ka bu kpa bu ŋu ni lee ma i, na nzɛn minɛm ba ŋa bu miɛ i ŋu na i koro wura lee n nu.

eng: Other seed fell on rocky ground where the soil was shallow. They sprouted immediately because the soil wasnt deep.
local: Na nbem atɔtɔ tiɛka su, deke bo fa sunnu má ni, na fa sunnu má n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ cɛ má, na wɔ fi.

eng: Whenever the evil spirits saw him, they fell down at his feet and shouted, You are Gods Son.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo minɛm bo ajinim wo bu ŋu n nya ŋuuri i, bu kutu i nyunu wɔ, na bu ciindi na bu se ka, Wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ Wa n a.

eng: When the sun came up, it scorched the plants; and they dried up because they had no roots.
local: ama, saŋga bo wiɛ ba fiteeri agaya ni, na wɔ waandi, dama bunlui n akpama abi má n dɛɛ ti.

eng: But he strictly ordered them not to reveal who he was.
local: Na wɔ bɔbɔ ajinim ni ni anyum­boro ka, má bu ma be si i sɔnɔ nzuin nu.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorny plants grew and choked the seeds, and they produced nothing.
local: Na alɛ­mbaa ni nbem atɔtɔ lɔlɔɛ nu, na lɔlɔɛ n anyi akata i ŋu, nyini ti, wɔ koro awura má mbaa.

eng: Jesus went up on a mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him.
local: Na Yesu afun akɔ buka be su, na wɔ fere minɛ n bo i koroori ka bu ba i dɔ ni, na ba ba i dɔ.

eng: He appointed twelve and called them apostles. He appointed them to be with him, to be sent out to preach.
local: Na wɔ yi bu nu buru ni nnyɔ, na wɔ fa bu ayo i jaramasam ka bu wo i dɔ, na i susunma bu ka bu kɔ sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ.

eng: Other seed fell into good soil and bore fruit. Upon growing and increasing, the seed produced in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case a yield of sixty to one, and in another case a yield of one hundred to one.
local: Ama, na alɛ­mbaam ni nbem atɔtɔ asiɛn kpa su, na wɔ nyi awura mbaa zɔi zɔi.

eng: and to have authority to throw out demons.
local: na wɔ ma bu yiko ka bu kɔ sufɔn yi ajinim minɛm ŋu.

eng: He said, Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i so i sui na i ti.

eng: When they were alone, the people around Jesus, along with the Twelve, asked him about the parables.
local: Na saŋga bo i kaari i ni i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni, ni minɛm bo bu wo i dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, na ba bisa i nyini miɛɛnu n bu.

eng: He appointed twelve: Peter, a name he gave Simon.
local: I jaramasa buru ni nnyɔm ni bo i yiiri bu n la Simon bo Yesu tuunri i duma ka Petoro ni.

eng: He said to them, The secret of Gods kingdom has been given to you, but to those who are outside everything comes in parables.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Ambɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ ama am asi i fɛmɛya n nu asiri jɔrɛ a, ama, minɛm bo bu su má m su ni, m bo i miɛɛnu kere bu a.

eng: Early in the morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people reached the decision to have Jesus put to death.
local: Na alecin saŋga na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n atana asɛsɛ su kabo bu yo barasu na bu kun i.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, whom he nicknamed Boanerges, which means sons of Thunder.
local: ni Yakobo ni i niɛma Yohane, bo bu si la Zɛbɛdi na Yesu kaaki tuunri bu duma ka Bowanaje , i bu la ka bu tiɛntiɛn agaya ka nzue wɔ.

eng: This is so that they can look and see but have no insight, and they can hear but not understand. Otherwise, they might turn their lives around and be forgiven.
local: adena i yó kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Bu níɛ ni nahɔrɛ su, ama, bu ŋú má pui. Bu só bu sui ni nahɔrɛ su, ama, bu tí má deke n bo bu so bu sui ka bu ti i n bu, dama nzɛn má bu tiiri i bu, má bu káaki bu wo wɔ, na Nyɛmɛ fá bu satiɛ ce bu.

eng: They bound him, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate the governor.
local: Na ba wura i jɔrɔkɔ, na ba fa i akɔ ama Pilato bo i ti Roma­fɔm gomina ni.

eng: and Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, Alphaeus son; Thaddaeus; Simon the Cananaean.
local: Bu nbem gusu la Andere, ni Filipu, ni Batolomeyo, ni Matio, ni Tomasi, ni Yakobo bo i si la Alafeyo ni, ni Tadayɔ, ni Simon bo i tu ŋgoni fa kɔ i asiɛn su ni.

eng: Dont you understand this parable? Then how will you understand all the parables?.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, Am ati má ahin miɛɛnu n bu? Ye am yó sɛ ti miɛɛnu nyiŋgam n kɛrɛ bu a?.

eng: When Judas, who betrayed Jesus, saw that Jesus was condemned to die, he felt deep regret. He returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, and.
local: Saŋga bo Yudasi bo i tɔɔri Yesu n tiiri ka ba ma nɔɔ ka bu kun Yesu ni, na wɔ yo m si n sɔ, na wɔ kaaki awɔrɔ jɛtɛ ŋwaa abura­san n aba ama Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni.

eng: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.
local: ni Yudasi Isikariyoti bo i ba tɔɔri Yesu ni.

eng: The farmer scatters the word.
local: Ahin miɛɛnu n bu la ka, dɔdɔɔri­fɔ n ti ka sɔnɔ n bo i kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n wɔ.

eng: said, I did wrong because I betrayed an innocent man. But they said, What is that to us? Thats your problem.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ma yo satiɛ kabo ma tɔ sɔnɔ bo i la má ŋgasi be na bu kun i ni. Na ba se i ka, Ya cɛcɛ bɔnɔ wo nyini nu a? U dɛɛ jɔrɛ lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus entered a house. A crowd gathered again so that it was impossible for him and his followers even to eat.
local: Na Yesu akɔ awuru, na jama aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ bekun bo i ni i susu­fɔm n akoro anya má tana bo bu di diire mmɔɔ.

eng: This is the meaning of the seed that fell on the path: When the word is scattered and people hear it, right away Satan comes and steals the word that was planted in them.
local: Minɛ nbem de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kabo alɛ­mbaa n tɔtɔɔri atin n nɔɔ n wɔ. Bu nya tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na bu de i, i cɛ má, na Sitana bá yi nɔaniɛ n fite bu ahore su, a ŋu ka bu wara afi i sa.

eng: Judas threw the silver pieces into the temple and left. Then he went and hanged himself.
local: Na Yudasi afa ŋwaa n atu Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ fite akɔ awura i kɔm nyama akun i ŋu.

eng: When his family heard what was happening, they came to take control of him. They were saying, Hes out of his mind.
local: Na saŋga bo i minɛm tiiri i ŋga ni, na ba kɔ ka bu kɔ fa i ba awuru, dama minɛm ŋa i nyunu akan i.

eng: Heres the meaning of the seed that fell on rocky ground: When people hear the word, they immediately receive it joyfully.
local: Minɛ nbem gusu de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kabo alɛ­mbaa n tɔtɔɔri tiɛka su n wɔ. Bu nya tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, a ŋu ka ba de i ndende ni ahorejɔ.

eng: The chief priests picked up the silver pieces and said, According to the Law its not right to put this money in the treasury. Since it was used to pay for someones life, its unclean.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n afa ŋwaa ni, na ba se ka, Ahin ŋwaa n ti mbunja yii­wa ŋwaa a. Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka má be fa i ŋusu fa saŋga Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru dɛɛ ŋwaa n nu.

eng: The legal experts came down from Jerusalem. Over and over they charged, Hes possessed by Beelzebul. He throws out demons with the authority of the ruler of demons.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm bo bu fiteeri Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu n ase ka, Bɛlazɛbuli bo i ti ajinim kɛrɛ dɛɛ fɛmɛ ni, wo i ŋu a. Na ba kaaki ase ka, Yiri ajinim kɛrɛ fɛmɛ ni ma i yiko ye i koro fɔn yi ajinim n a.

eng: Because they have no roots, they last for only a little while. When they experience distress or abuse because of the word, they immediately fall away.
local: ama, i da má bu ahore su. Bu kaaki ti ka alɛ­mbaa n bo i bunlui akpama abi má n wɔ. I woori bu dɔ kaan sɔ na nzɛn wahara, wara kekereke be ba tuuri bu fa kɔ bu nyɛmɛsu n su, a ŋu ka ba fa ati.

eng: So they decided to use it to buy the potters field where strangers could be buried.
local: Na ba yo anɔɔsɛ, afa ŋwaa n ato asiɛn be, bo bu fere i ka yakati ta­fɔ juma yoo­biri ni, ka bu fa yo wɔfɔm see­biri.

eng: When Jesus called them together he spoke to them in a parable: How can Satan throw Satan out?.
local: Bu seeri sɔ ni, na Yesu afere bu, na wɔ bo bu miɛɛnu. I ŋa, Sitana koro yo sɛ fɔn yi i dɛɛ ajinim minɛm ŋu a?.

eng: Others are like the seed scattered among the thorny plants. These are the ones who have heard the word.
local: Nbem gusu de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kabo alɛ­mbaa n tɔtɔɔri lɔlɔɛ nu n wɔ. Bu nya tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Thats why that field is called Field of Blood to this very day.
local: Nyini ti ye bu fere nyini asiɛn n ka Mbunja Asiɛn, haari ni nyuma sɔ n a.

eng: A kingdom involved in civil war will collapse.
local: Ya fa i ka, nzɛn fɛmɛya nya kpakpaakiiri nu wieeri, nyini fɛmɛya ni kóro jina a? Ai, i kóro jina má.

eng: but the worries of this life, the false appeal of wealth, and the desire for more things break in and choke the word, and it bears no fruit.
local: ama, bu sunsunni kɛrɛ wo kabo bu yó na bu asiri káta su, na bu koro kabo bu yó na bu nyá ajɛkɛ kɛrɛ, na bu ahore bi fa kɔ durunya n nu neŋgem su, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n koro da má bu ahore su na i yo juma. Bu ti ka alɛ­mbaa n bo i wura má mbaa n wɔ.

eng: When Jesus finished speaking all these words, he said to his disciples.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu kereeri nyini keree­wam kɛrɛ wieeri ni, na wɔ se i susu­fɔm ni ka.

eng: This fulfilled the words of Jeremiah the prophet: And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price for the one whose price had been set by some of the Israelites.
local: Dɔ na nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Jeremiya yo jɔjɔɔri n ayo yii­wa, i ŋa, Ba fa jɛtɛ ŋwaa abura­san n bo Yisareli­fɔm kɛrɛ soroori su ka bu tɔ i kaari, sɔnɔ n dɛɛ ti ni.

eng: After a few days, Jesus went back to Capernaum, and people heard that he was at home.
local: I yoori le­nnyɔ na Yesu akpie i nyi aba Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu, na labari abara ayɛ ka i wo awuru.

eng: And a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Na nzɛn awuru gusu bo i minɛm nya kpakpaakiiri nu, nyini awuru ni kóro jina a? Ai, i kóro jina má.

eng: The seed scattered on good soil are those who hear the word and embrace it. They bear fruit, in one case a yield of thirty to one, in another case sixty to one, and in another case one hundred to one.
local: Ama, minɛ nbem de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kabo alɛ­mbaa n tɔtɔɔri asiɛn kpa su n wɔ. Bu nya tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na bu de i, na bu fa yo juma na i yo kpɛrɛ beberebe.

eng: You know that the Passover is two days from now. And the Human One will be handed over to be crucified.
local: Am si ka wɔ ka le­nnyɔ ɔ, na bu di Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni. Dɔ ye bu nyá mini Adamande Wa n taraa­biri a, na bu kpáma m baka jaanu n su.

eng: and I gave them for the potters field, as the Lord commanded me .
local: na ba fa ŋwaa n ato yakati ta­fɔ juma yoo­biri, kabo Micɛra n seeri m ka bu yó lɛɛ.

eng: So many gathered that there was no longer space, not even near the door. Jesus was speaking the word to them.
local: na minɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ i dɔ agaya haari fofoe anya má anɔ n bo bu fa wura sɔɔ n nu mmɔɔ, na Yesu akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ sukere bu.

eng: If Satan rebels against himself and is divided, then he cant endure. Hes done for.
local: Nzɛn Sitana dɛɛ fɛmɛya n nya kpaakiiri nu wieeri, i kóro jina a? Ai, i kóro jina má. Nzɛn i nya yoori sɔ, i faŋga wie wɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Does anyone bring in a lamp in order to put it under a basket or a bed? Shouldnt it be placed on a lampstand?.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, Am nya ŋu ka sɔnɔ be aso fitana su na i fa gaŋga butu su, nzɛn má nyini i fa kɔ se gado bu? I fa taka deke su aŋgoro wɔ bo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro ŋu i, má sɔ?.

eng: Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Dɔ na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ bu kpiɛn n dika, bu fere i ka Kayafasi, yiri ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn a.

eng: Jesus was brought before the governor. The governor said, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Na ba fa Yesu akɔ ajina Gomina Pilato n nyunu, na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, Wɔrɔ ti Yuda­fɔm fɛmɛ n a? Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, Kabo a jɔjɔ ni, i ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: Some people arrived, and four of them were bringing to him a man who was paralyzed.
local: I wo bu kere su ni, na minɛ nbem aba i dɔ, bu nu minɛ nna sɔ sɔnɔ bo i saam ni i jaam awu.

eng: No one gets into the house of a strong person and steals anything without first tying up the strong person. Only then can the house be burglarized.
local: Be koro wura má sɔnɔ faŋga­fɔ dika na i fin i neŋge, má ka wɔ tara i faŋga acin i nyama ka, ye i nya koro fin i neŋge n bo i wo awuru n nu a.

eng: Everything hidden will be revealed, and everything secret will come out into the open.
local: Dama deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i wo fiɛɛ­wa nu, na bu bá ŋu má i, na deke be gusu wo má bɛrɛ bo i wo asiri nu, na bu bá si má i.

eng: They were plotting to arrest Jesus by cunning tricks and to kill him.
local: na ba fa sɛsɛ­su ka bu tara Yesu asiri nu na bu kun i.

eng: But he didnt answer when the chief priests and elders accused him.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm akpiɛ jɔrɛ asɔ Yesu, ama, wɔ bɔ má be nu.

eng: They couldnt carry him through the crowd, so they tore off part of the roof above where Jesus was. When they had made an opening, they lowered the mat on which the paralyzed man was lying.
local: Jama n sunnuuri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, ba koro anya má fofoe bo bu fa sɔnɔ n dodo Yesu, na ba fiiti sɔɔ n bo Yesu wo nu n aŋgoro buekun, na ba fa i ni i dasu n kɛrɛ acɔɔndi afa i ada sɔɔ n nu.

eng: I assure you that human beings will be forgiven for everything, for all sins and insults of every kind.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, M suse am nahɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ ka, Nyɛmɛ koro fa minɛm satiɛ kɛrɛ fa ce bu, haari fa kɔ saŋga jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ bo bu jɔjɔɔri i fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: Whoever has ears to listen should pay attention.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i so i sui na i ti.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the feast so there wouldnt be an uproar among the people.
local: Na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Má am ma ya yo i cɛɛn­kpiri n saŋga. Má nyini, minɛm ni kéte yo naŋmiɛ wɔ, na bu sutiɛntiɛn.

eng: Then Pilate said, Dont you hear the testimony they bring against you.
local: Na Gomina Pilato abisa i ka, A ti má jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo ba kpiɛ asɔ u ni.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Child, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yesu ŋuuri kabo bu yoori i yarada n sɔ ni, na wɔ se sɔnɔ n bo i saam ni i jaam awu n ka, M dɔn, ma fa u satiɛ ace u.

eng: But whoever insults the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. That person is guilty of a sin with consequences that last forever.
local: ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ wuraari Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ŋgasi nu, Nyɛmɛ fá má i mi ŋgasi ce má i, dama i mi ayo satiɛ bo i la má awieeri lɛɛ.

eng: He said to them, Listen carefully! God will evaluate you with the same standard you use to evaluate others. Indeed, you will receive even more.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am si am ŋu sa ni kabo am ti nɔaniɛ barasu ni. Gɔŋgɔ bo am fa susu ma minɛm ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ gusu fá susu ma am a, na bo Nyɛmɛ yó ma am ni tara sɔ n mmɔɔ.

eng: When Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease.
local: Yesu woori Betani miɛ n nu wɔ, sɔnɔ be dika, bu fere i ka Simon, i yo ti koko­fɔ ɔ.

eng: But he didnt answer, not even a single word. So the governor was greatly amazed.
local: Ama, Yesu abɔ má jɔrɛ n haari kun be mmɔɔ nu, na gomina n abirisi agaya kabo wɔ bɔ má nu ni.

eng: Some legal experts were sitting there, muttering among themselves.
local: Tɔ. Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm nbem tana bɛrɛ, na ba sunsun bu ahore su ka.

eng: He said this because the legal experts were saying, Hes possessed by an evil spirit.
local: Deke bo i ti na Yesu seeri sɔ n la ka, bɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, bu ŋa ajini wo i ŋu a.

eng: Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they dont have will be taken away from them.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ nya deke be Nyɛmɛ dɔ na i mi si i bita, Nyɛmɛ búka i su wɔ, ama, bo i si má i bita, haari ni nyini kaan n bo i la i ni, Nyɛmɛ dé i dɔ ɔ.

eng: a woman came to him with a vase made of alabaster containing very expensive perfume. She poured it on Jesus head while he was sitting at dinner.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu wo diire di su ni, na bara be aba i dɔ, ni tulaalɛɛ tuwa nva­nvani bo i la gɔ agaya. Na wɔ teke tulaalɛɛ tuwa n nɔɔ, na wɔ kaaki tulaalɛɛ n kɛrɛ agɔ Yesu tii su.

eng: It was customary during the festival for the governor to release to the crowd one prisoner, whomever they might choose.
local: Afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ, Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n nya ba ju, Roma­fɔm gomina yi dan­saraka n bo jama n koro ka i yi i ji n wɔ.

eng: Why does he speak this way? Hes insulting God. Only the one God can forgive sins.
local: Ahin sɔnɔ n la atin bɔnɔ ye i jɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu sɔ a? Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro fa minɛm satiɛ ni bu ŋgasi ce, sin Nyɛmɛ ŋu.

eng: The beginning of the good news about Jesus Christ, Gods Son.
local: Ahin jɔrɛ ni, i ti Nyɛmɛ Wa Yesu Kristo dɛɛ labari kpa n wɔ. Kabo i boori i bu yaa.

eng: His mother and brothers arrived. They stood outside and sent word to him, calling for him.
local: Na Yesu ni, ni i niɛmam aba i dɔ awuru n bo i wo nu ni, na ba ka ajina dasin asunma ka bu fere i.

eng: Then Jesus said, This is what Gods kingdom is like. Its as though someone scatters seed on the ground.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti ka kabo dɔdɔɔri­fɔ be kɔ sandi alɛ­mbaa i fiesu n wɔ. I dɛɛ la ka i sandi wɔ.

eng: Now when the disciples saw it they were angry and said, Why this waste?.
local: Yesu susu­fɔm ni ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na yaa ati bu agaya, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Nzu ti ye wɔ saaki i sɔ a?.

eng: At that time there was a well-known prisoner named Jesus Barabbas.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, sɔnɔ be woori dan­saraka n nu dɔ, bu fere i ka Barabasi, i ti má jɔrɛ bu, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si i sa.

eng: Jesus immediately recognized what they were discussing, and he said to them, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Na Yesu afa nu i ahore su fa kɔ bu sunsunni bo bu bita i n su, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am bita nyini sunsunni ni am ahore su a?.

eng: happened just as it was written about in the prophecy of Isaiah: Look, I am sending my messenger before you . He will prepare your way.
local: Nyɛmɛ yo seeri i wa n ka, Ti fɛn, m súnma m sunma­fɔ n na i dú u mɔ, yiri sɛ́sɛ u atin n su a. Yiri kɔ́ subo ndoori boro kpantain n nu se ka, Am sɛsɛ Mibiɛ n atin n su, na am yo i atin n dɔŋgu. Na Nyɛmɛ ama ba kɛrɛ nyini jɔjɔɔ­wa n kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya dɛɛ kadasi n nu.

eng: A crowd was seated around him, and those sent to him said, Look, your mother, brothers, and sisters are outside looking for you.
local: Na jama beberebe atana abara ayɛ yiri Yesu na ba se i ka, Ti fɛn. U ni, ni u niɛmam wo dasin koro u.

eng: then sleeps and wakes night and day. The seed sprouts and grows, but the farmer doesnt know how.
local: na i kɔ da kɔŋguɛ, na i teŋge alecin. Nyini saŋga n ye alɛ­mbaa n fi a, na i sunyi, i si má kabo i yoori barasu.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for a lot of money and given to the poor.
local: Má ya tɔɔri nyini tulaalɛɛ ni, má ya nyá ŋwaa beberebe, na má ya fá nyini ŋwaa n ma yaari­fɔm.

eng: When the crowd had come together, Pilate asked them, Whom would you like me to release to you, Jesus Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ.
local: Na saŋga bo jama n kɛrɛ ba tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ ni, na Gomina Pilato abisa bu ka, Am koro ka m yi ŋma ma am a, Barabasi ni, nzɛn Yesu n bo bu fere i ka Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni.

eng: Which is easier—to say to a paralyzed person, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up, take up your bed, and walk?.
local: Bɔnɔ ti kekereke a, bo m se sɔnɔ n ka, Ma fa u satiɛ ace u, nzɛn, Yasu fa u dasu n na a nati n a?.

eng: John the Baptist was in the wilderness calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Nyini sɔnɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ sunmaari i n la sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ ni. I baari boro kpantain n nu, na i sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, i ŋa, Am kaaki am wo na m biɛ am Nyɛmɛ nzue, na Nyɛmɛ fa am ŋgasi ni am satiɛ kɛrɛ ce am.

eng: He replied, Who is my mother? Who are my brothers.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am ma m kere am bo bu ti m ni, ni m niɛmam.

eng: The earth produces crops all by itself, first the stalk, then the head, then the full head of grain.
local: Asiɛn n buka alɛ­mbaa n ye i fi a. I nya fi wieeri, na i nyi, na i nyinsɛ, na i wɔrɔ, na i nya bi mbaa.

eng: But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, Why do you make trouble for the woman? Shes done a good thing for me.
local: Na Yesu asi sunsunni bo bu bita i, na wɔ se bu ka, Nzu ti ye am sukan bara ni ndiɛ a? Sakpa lɛɛ ye wɔ yo ama m n a.

eng: He knew that the leaders of the people had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, dama i siiri tenle su ka bu si Yesu kɔrɛ ti ye bu faari i ba wuraari i saa nu a.

eng: But so you will know that the Human One has authority on the earth to forgive sins—he said to the man who was paralyzed.
local: M kére am ka mini Adamande Wa n la yiko durunya n nu bo m fa minɛm satiɛ ni bu ŋgasi ce bu. Na wɔ se sɔnɔ n ka.

eng: Everyone in Judea and all the people of Jerusalem went out to the Jordan River and were being baptized by John as they confessed their sins.
local: Na Yudiya asiɛn­su­fɔm, ni Yɛrusalem miɛ nu­fɔm kɛrɛ afite aba i dɔ, na ba kan bu ŋgasi ni bu satiɛ kɛrɛ bo ba yo i akere Nyɛmɛ, na Yohane abiɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue Yodan kunma n nu.

eng: Looking around at those seated around him in a circle, he said, Look, here are my mother and my brothers.
local: Na wɔ niɛ abara ayɛ minɛm bo bu tana bara yɛɛri i ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Am niɛ. M ni, ni m niɛmam n yaa.

eng: Whenever the crop is ready, the farmer goes out to cut the grain because its harvesttime.
local: Alɛ­mbaa n ba bi ni, i kpiɛ saŋga lɛɛ, na i kpiɛ i.

eng: You always have the poor with you, but you wont always have me.
local: Am ni yaari­fɔm ni wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a, ama, mini, m wó má am dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: While he was serving as judge, his wife sent this message to him, Leave that righteous man alone. Ive suffered much today in a dream because of him.
local: Saŋga bo Gomina Pilato wo Yesu jɔrɛ­di su ni, na i yi asunma ase ka, Má a se ka a yo nyini nahɔrɛ­fɔ n sa be, dama anuma kɔŋguɛ, ma cin alɛlɛ fa kɔ nyini sɔnɔ n dɔ bo wɔ miɛ m agaya.

eng: Get up, take your mat, and go home.
local: Wɔrɔ, yasu fa u dasu n na a kɔ awuru.

eng: John wore clothes made of camels hair, with a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.
local: Yohane sutura wuraa­wa yo ti deke bo bu faari nyɔɔma ndɔrɛ wuuri i a, na wɔ fa jina abo i wi nu. I diire yo la lalom ni wiɛ wɔ.

eng: Whoever does Gods will is my brother, sister, and mother.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo Nyɛmɛ sa koroo­wa, yiri ti m siwa, ni m niwa, ni m ni a.

eng: He continued, Whats a good image for Gods kingdom? What parable can I use to explain it?.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Ya fá nzu deke fa sunsun Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n a? Wara ya fá miɛɛnu barasu bɔnɔ ye ya bó i fa kere kabo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti a?.

eng: By pouring this perfume over my body shes prepared me to be buried.
local: Tulaalɛɛ n bo wɔ kaaki agɔ m ŋu ni, i kere ka i nzee bo wɔ fa ayo m siriya afa aŋminda m yiwee cɛɛn lɛɛ.

eng: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and kill Jesus.
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n awura jama n bu sin ka bu sere Gomina Pilato ka i yí Barabasi ma bu, na i kún Yesu.

eng: Jesus raised him up, and right away he picked up his mat and walked out in front of everybody. They were all amazed and praised God, saying, Weve never seen anything like this.
local: I kaba yasu faari i dasu n wɔ, na wɔ fite jama n kɛrɛ nyu­mbaa nu. Na bu kɛrɛ ayo alekutura, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ, na ba se ka, Ya nya ŋu má ahin sa n ŋusu cɛɛn be.

eng: He announced, One stronger than I am is coming after me. Im not even worthy to bend over and loosen the strap of his sandals.
local: I kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, i ŋa, Sɔnɔ be wo m sin suba, i tara m fieo, m ti kaan ni bo m dodo i.

eng: Consider a mustard seed. When scattered on the ground, its the smallest of all the seeds on the earth.
local: I ti ka nyɛm mbaa bo bu dɔ i wɔ. Yiri ti alɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ kaan durunya n nu a.

eng: I tell you the truth that wherever in the whole world this good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, deke kɛrɛ bo bu bá kaanri Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n durunya n kɛrɛ nu, bu kán i sa­yoo­wa n na minɛm teŋge i su.

eng: The governor said, Which of the two do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, they replied.
local: Ama, na Gomina Pilato akaaki abisa jama n ka, Nyini minɛ nnyɔm ni nu, am koro ka m yi ŋma ma am a? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Barabasi.

eng: Jesus went out beside the lake again. The whole crowd came to him, and he began to teach them.
local: Na Yesu akaaki akpie i nyi akɔ Galili kunma kpiri n nɔɔ su, na jama beberebe akɔ atiɛn bu nɔɔ i dɔ, na wɔ kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ akere bu.

eng: I baptize you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mini afa nzue abiɛ am a, na am yo casi, ama, yiri fá Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wura am ŋu yo am casi a, kabo sɔnɔ biɛ nzue na i yo casi ni.

eng: but when its planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all vegetable plants. It produces such large branches that the birds in the sky are able to nest in its shade.
local: Ama, bu nya ba dɔɔri i na i fiiri, i tara toro nyawam n kɛrɛ bambaka, i koro nyi na i tɛtɛrɛki asa­mbaa asa­mbaa bo anumam koro yo bu tiɛɛrim nu.

eng: Then one of the Twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm nu kun bo bu fere i ka Yudasi Isikariyoti ni, akɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni dɔ.

eng: Pilate said, Then what should I do with Jesus who is called Christ? They all said, Crucify him.
local: Na Gomina Pilato abisa bekun ka, Ye ahin Yesu n bo bu fere i ka Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni, am koro ka m yó i sɛ wɔ? Na bu kɛrɛ abɔ i nu ka, Kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu.

eng: As he continued along, he saw Levi, Alphaeus son, sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me. Levi got up and followed him.
local: I wo natiiri su susin ni, na wɔ ŋu lampo­de­fɔ be bo bu fere i ka Levi, Alafeyo wa ni, ka i tana i juma yoo­biri, na Yesu ase i ka, Yasu bra na a ba su m su. Na wɔ yasu asu i su.

eng: About that time, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
local: Nyini saŋga n na Yesu afite Nazarɛti, miɛ n bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni, na wɔ ba Yohane dɔ, na Yohane abiɛ i Nyɛmɛ nzue Yodan kunma n nu.

eng: With many such parables he continued to give them the word, as much as they were able to hear.
local: Na Yesu afa nyini miɛɛnu n ni miɛɛnu nyiŋgam beberebe akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ akere bu, kabo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro ti i bu barasu.

eng: and said, What will you give me if I turn Jesus over to you? They paid him thirty pieces of silver.
local: akɔ abisa bu ka, Nzɛn m tɔɔri Yesu maari am, am má m nzu wɔ? Na ba kan jɛtɛ ŋwaa abura­san, afa ama i.

eng: But he said, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Na Pilato abisa ka, Ye nzu satiɛ ye wɔ yo i a? Ama, na ba kaaki atiɛn kpa agaya bekun ase ka, Kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu.

eng: Jesus sat down to eat at Levis house. Many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Jesus and his disciples. Indeed, many of them had become his followers.
local: Na Yesu akɔ Levi dika ka i kɔ di diire. Na satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ni lampo­de­fɔm beberebe asu i su. Diire n dii­biri dɔ ni, na bu dɔŋgu aba atana Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n dɔ, na ba saŋga adi diire.

eng: While he was coming up out of the water, Jesus saw heaven splitting open and the Spirit, like a dove, coming down on him.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu fiteeri nzue n nu ni, kpeŋga na wɔ ŋu ka aŋgoro n ateke, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ajura ka tiiwa dɛɛ su aba atana i ŋu.

eng: He spoke to them only in parables, then explained everything to his disciples when he was alone with them.
local: Jama n nya woori i dɔ, i se má bu pui, má ka miɛɛnu ye i bo bu a, ama, i ba kaari i ni i susu­fɔm ni, na i nya yi i bu kɛrɛ kere bu.

eng: From that time on he was looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Fite nyini cɛɛn n na Yudasi akpini atin kabo i yó na bu nya Yesu taraa­biri.

eng: Pilate saw that he was getting nowhere and that a riot was starting. So he took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd. Im innocent of this mans blood, he said. Its your problem.
local: Saŋga bo Gomina Pilato ŋuuri ka i koro yo má jɔrɛ n dɔŋgu, haari ni bo wɔ boro i nyi na i yo i dɔŋgu ni, jama n akete sukoro ka bu yo naŋmiɛ. Nyini ti, na wɔ fa nzue aŋmisi i saa, jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, na wɔ se bu ka, M saa wo má ahin sɔnɔ n yiwee nu. Am dɛɛ jɔrɛ lɛɛ.

eng: When some of the legal experts from among the Pharisees saw that he was eating with sinners and tax collectors, they asked his disciples, Why is he eating with sinners and tax collectors.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ nbem bo bu su Farasi­fɔm dɛɛ atin su ni, aŋu ka Yesu ni satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ni lampo­de­fɔm tana sudi diire, na ba bisa i susu­fɔm ni ka, Nzu ti ye Yesu ni lampo­de­fɔm, ni satiɛ­yo­fɔm saŋga di diire a.

eng: And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: Na wɔ ti akonvi be ŋga aŋgoro, i ŋa, Wɔrɔ ti m wa a, m koro u agaya, m ahore ajɔ u ŋu.

eng: Later that day, when evening came, Jesus said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n naasɔ saŋga, na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Am ma ya kpiɛ kɔ kunma kpiri n buekun dɔ.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and said, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Na cɛɛn­kpiri n bo bu di i ni kpɔnɔ bo bu gɔ má ayiri nu na i yasu ni, i le­kun cɛɛn, na Yesu susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, aba abisa ka, A koro ka ya kɔ ni kɔ sɛsɛ Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n diire n su se a.

eng: All the people replied, Let his blood be on us and on our children.
local: Na jama n kɛrɛ ase i ka, Tɔ, ma i yiwee ni ŋgasi su ya su, ni ya anumam kɛrɛ.

eng: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do. I didnt come to call righteous people, but sinners.
local: Na Yesu ati bu jɔjɔɔ­wa n na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Minɛm bo bu la laifiɛ n koro má ayiri­fɔ, má ka bo bu la má laifiɛ ni koro a. Ma ba má ka m bá fere minɛm bo bu ŋa bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n wɔ, ama, m baari satiɛ­yo­fɔm n dɛɛ ti wɔ.

eng: At once the Spirit forced Jesus out into the wilderness.
local: Nyini jinaa­biri n nu na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama Yesu akɔ boro kpantain n nu.

eng: They left the crowd and took him in the boat just as he was. Other boats followed along.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n acin ayaki jama ni, na ba ba awura lee n bo Yesu wo nu n nu, na ba kɔ. Na nbem gusu afa lee nbem na ba su i su.

eng: He replied, Go into the city, to a certain man, and say, The teacher says,My time is near. Im going to celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ miɛ n kunnu, sɔnɔ be wo dɔ, na am kɔ se i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ n ŋa o, i saŋga n aju. Na i ni i susu­fɔm ni bá di Butara cɛɛn­kpiri diire ni, u dika n nu.

eng: Then he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Dɔ na Gomina Pilato ayi Barabasi aji, na wɔ ma sojam n afin Yesu ŋgbere, na wɔ nya afa i awura bu saa nu ka bu kɔ kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu.

eng: Johns disciples and the Pharisees had a habit of fasting. Some people asked Jesus, Why do Johns disciples and the Pharisees disciples fast, but yours dont.
local: Cɛɛn be na Yohane dɛɛ susu­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm sucici bu nɔɔ, na minɛ nbem aba na ba bisa Yesu ka, Nzu ti ye Yohane susu­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm dɛɛ susu­fɔm cici bu nɔɔ, ye u dɛɛm bɛrɛ cici má.

eng: He was in the wilderness for forty days, tempted by Satan. He was among the wild animals, and the angels took care of him.
local: I woori dɔ cɛɛn abura­na, i ni boro neŋgem woori dɔ a, na Sitana ayo i na i niɛ ka, i yó yiri Sitana n dɛɛ koroo­wa, nzɛn i yó má. Ama, Yesu asoro má su. Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm aba asu i.

eng: Gale-force winds arose, and waves crashed against the boat so that the boat was swamped.
local: Na aŋuma bambaka be ayasu, na i subo nzue gɔ lee n nu. Má lee ni suyi ni nzue wɔ.

eng: The disciples did just as Jesus instructed them. They prepared the Passover.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔm n akɔ ayo kabo i seeri bu ni, na ba sɛsɛ Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n diire n su.

eng: The governors soldiers took Jesus into the governors house, and they gathered the whole company of soldiers around him.
local: Na Gomina Pilato n dɛɛ sojam n afa Yesu awura i gisan n nu, na ba tiɛn bu beŋgu sojam kɛrɛ nɔɔ abara ayɛ i.

eng: Jesus said, The wedding guests cant fast while the groom is with them, can they? As long as they have the groom with them, they cant fast.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am sunsun ka konyaya­fɔm koro cici bu nɔɔ saŋga bo konyafan n toko wo bu dɔ ni, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, saŋga bo konyafan n wo bu dɔ ni, bu yo má nyini.

eng: After John was arrested, Jesus came into Galilee announcing Gods good news.
local: Saŋga bo bu taraari Yohane nyiiri i dan­saraka n sin, na Yesu akɔ Galili awɔrɛ n nu, na i sukan labari kpa n fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin n su kere minɛm.

eng: But Jesus was in the rear of the boat, sleeping on a pillow. They woke him up and said, Teacher, dont you care that were drowning.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, Yesu afa piti asɔn i tii, ye i wo daafiri su lee n sin, na ba ba ateŋge i ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, nzɛn ya suwu, u cɛcɛ wo má ya nu fan.

eng: That evening he took his place at the table with the twelve disciples.
local: Na naasɔ saŋga, na Yesu ni i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm n aba atana ka bu di diire ni.

eng: They stripped him and put a red military coat on him.
local: Na ba yi Yesu sutura bo i wura i ni, na ba fa tanlɛɛ kɔkɔrɛ yorom be ka fɛmɛya tanlɛɛ dɛɛ su awura i.

eng: But the days will come when the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
local: Ama, saŋga suba bo bu bá yi konyafan n fite bu nu, nyini saŋga n ye bu nyá cici bu nɔɔ a.

eng: saying, Now is the time! Here comes Gods kingdom! Change your hearts and lives, and trust this good news.
local: i ŋa, Saŋga n aju bo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su. Am kaaki am wo, na am de Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n na am yo i yarada.

eng: He got up and gave orders to the wind, and he said to the lake, Silence! Be still! The wind settled down and there was a great calm.
local: Na wɔ yasu na wɔ tiɛn aŋuma n ŋu, na wɔ se nzue n ka, Da surum. Na aŋuma n atara, na dika n kɛrɛ atara jum.

eng: As they were eating he said, I assure you that one of you will betray me.
local: Saŋga bo bu tana wo diire n di su ni, na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, am nu kun bá tɔ m ma m kpɔ­fɔm a.

eng: They twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head. They put a stick in his right hand. Then they bowed down in front of him and mocked him, saying, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Na ba fa buwe aŋɔ i ka kaba dɛɛ su, na ba fa aforo i tii ka fɛmɛya kere dɛɛ su. Na ba fa kpama awura i saa nu, na ba kutu i nyunu suyo i anzasi, bu ŋa, Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ ni, ya yo u ansɛ.

eng: No one sews a piece of new, unshrunk cloth on old clothes; otherwise, the patch tears away from it, the new from the old, and makes a worse tear.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Be koro fa má sanvuin fɔfɔrɛ kpa tɔ má tani dawa kunma, nzɛn i yoori sɔ, sanvuin fɔfɔrɛ ni sɔ́n tani dawa n wɔ, na i téke i kunma n agaya buka su.

eng: As Jesus passed alongside the Galilee Sea, he saw two brothers, Simon and Andrew, throwing fishing nets into the sea, for they were fishermen.
local: Ye i ba sunati sin Galili kunma kpiri n nɔɔ su, na wɔ ŋu jombaa tara­fɔ nnyɔ nbem. Bɛrɛ la Simon ni i niɛma Andere. Bu wo bu diɛndiɛ sandi su kunma kpiri n nu.

eng: Jesus asked them, Why are you frightened? Dont you have faith yet.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nzu ti ye am soro sɛrɛ sɔ a? Am yo má m yarada.

eng: Deeply saddened, each one said to him, Im not the one, am I, Lord.
local: Na bu ahore asaaki, na bu kun kun n kɛrɛ abisa i ka, M Mibiɛ, ye m ŋa má mini yiri e.

eng: After they spit on him, they took the stick and struck his head again and again.
local: Na ba yi nanzue agɔ i ŋu, na ba de kpama ni i dɔ, na bu sufin i tii baka.

eng: No one pours new wine into old leather wineskins; otherwise, the wine would burst the wineskins and the wine would be lost and the wineskins destroyed. But new wine is for new wineskins.
local: I kaaki ti sɔ ɔ bekun ka be koro fa má nzan nyii­wa faŋga­fɔ gɔ má dundu dawa bɛbɛtɛ nu, nzɛn i yoori sɔ, dundu ni tíɛ wɔ, na i ká ŋgbɛɛn, na nzan n kɛrɛ sóŋgi. Ama, i ti ye ni ka bu fá nzan nyii­wa faŋga­fɔ gɔ dundu fɔfɔrɛ bii­wa nu.

eng: Come, follow me, he said, and Ill show you how to fish for people.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am bra na am ba su m su, na m má am tiɛn minɛm nɔɔ ma m, kabo am tara jombaa ni.

eng: Overcome with awe, they said to each other, Who then is this? Even the wind and the sea obey him.
local: Na sɛrɛ akaaki ati bu agaya, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Nzu sɔnɔmbiri barasu la ahin bo aŋuma ni nzue n mmɔɔ de i nɔaniɛ a.

eng: He replied, The one who will betray me is the one who dips his hand with me into this bowl.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am nu kun bo m ni yiri saŋga fa ya saa wura taalɛ kun nu ni, yiri tɔ́ m a.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the military coat and put his own clothes back on him. They led him away to crucify him.
local: Bu ba yoori i anzasi n wieeri ni, na ba yi tanlɛɛ kɔkɔrɛ yorom ni, na ba kaaki afa i tiɛŋgu dɛɛ sutura n awura i. Na ba nya afa i akɔ ka bu kɔ kpama i baka jaanu n su.

eng: Jesus went through the wheat fields on the Sabbath. As the disciples made their way, they were picking the heads of wheat.
local: Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn be, na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm ni sunati sin alɛ­mbaa fie be nu, na i susu­fɔm n abubu be akpɛsɛ.

eng: Right away, they left their nets and followed him.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na ba kaba ayaki bu diɛndiɛm ni, na ba su i su.

eng: As they were going out, they found Simon, a man from Cyrene. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Saŋga bo bu sukɔ ni, na bu ni Sirenefɔ biɛ be ayɛ, bu fere i ka Simon. Na sojam n amiɛ i ka i sɔ Yesu baka jaanu ni.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Mini Adamande Wa ni wú kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ase ka m wú n wɔ, ama, sɔnɔ n bo i tɔ mini Adamande Wa n ni, i mi bá ŋu anyum­boro. Má ba wu má i mi n mmɔɔ, má i ti ye.

eng: The Pharisees said to Jesus, Look! Why are they breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm n ase i ka, Niɛ fɛn. Nzu ti ye u susu­fɔm ni suyo sɔ a? Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka má be yo sɔ, Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni.

eng: After going a little farther, he saw James and John, Zebedees sons, in their boat repairing the fishing nets.
local: I nati kɔɔri mɔ kaan bekun, na wɔ ŋu minɛ nnyɔ nbem. Bɛrɛ la Yakobo ni i niɛma Yohane. Bu fere bu si ka Zɛbɛdi. Bu wo lee nu suyo bu diɛndiɛm dɔŋgu.

eng: When they came to a place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Na ba kɔ aju awɔrɛ be bo bu fere i ka Gologota ni, Gologota n bu la ka Tere­awa Woo­biri.

eng: Now Judas, who would betray him, replied, Its not me, is it, Rabbi? Jesus answered, You said it.
local: Na Yudasi bo i tɔ́ Yesu n abisa i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, m ŋa má mini yiri e? Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Kabo a jɔjɔɔri ni, i ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: He said to them, Havent you ever read what David did when he was in need, when he and those with him were hungry?.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Am nya kan má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu, kabo Fɛmɛ Davidi yo yoori saŋga bo i ni i minɛm miɛɛri diire busu n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: At that very moment he called them. They followed him, leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired workers.
local: Dɔ na wɔ fere bu ka bu su i su, na ba yaki bu si ni, ni i npaadi­fɔm n lee n nu, na ba su i su.

eng: they gave Jesus wine mixed with vinegar to drink. But after tasting it, he didnt want to drink it.
local: Na ba saŋga nzan ni ayiri wiwi barasu be ama Yesu ka i nu na i jɔ yaari ni, ama, i kaanri i nɔɔ ɔ, na wɔ kete, wɔ nu má i.

eng: During the time when Abiathar was high priest, David went into Gods house and ate the bread of the presence, which only the priests were allowed to eat. He also gave bread to those who were with him.
local: Nyini saŋga Abiyata yo ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn n a. Ahɔɛ n ti, na Fɛmɛ Davidi akɔ awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ di kpɔnɔ n bo ba fa ama Nyɛmɛ ni, na wɔ ma i minɛm be. Ama, am si ka ya dɛɛ atin n su, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ŋgumi di nyini kpɔnɔ n a.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take and eat. This is my body.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm ni wo diire n di su ni, na Yesu afa kpɔnɔ, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ bubu i nu, na wɔ fa ama bu, na wɔ se bu ka, Am de ahin kpɔnɔ n na am di, i ti m ŋuniɛ­mbaa a.

eng: Jesus and his followers went into Capernaum. Immediately on the Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and started teaching.
local: Na ba kɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Kapɛnam su. Na Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn na Yesu awura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, bo bu sere Nyɛmɛ na bu kere Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ nu ni, na wɔ kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ akere minɛm ni.

eng: After they crucified him, they divided up his clothes among them by drawing lots.
local: Na ba nya akpama i baka jaanu n su, na ba yɛ i su ajina. Bu yoori sɔ wieeri ni, na ba tu mbaa na bu si kabo bu kpaaki i suturam nu, bu ŋuŋu nu.

eng: Then he said, The Sabbath was created for humans; humans werent created for the Sabbath.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Adamande dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ yiiri Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n a, má Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ yiiri adamande a.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink from this, all of you.
local: Na wɔ kaaki afa awa ni nzan, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ fa ama bu, na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɛrɛ nu be.

eng: The people were amazed by his teaching, for he was teaching them with authority, not like the legal experts.
local: Na bu kɛrɛ ayo alekutura fa kɔ i keree­wa n su, dama i keree­wa n awo i ŋgumi ni Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni. I keree­wa n la yiko.

eng: They sat there, guarding him.
local: Nyini sin na ba ka atana suniɛ Yesu.

eng: This is why the Human One is Lord even over the Sabbath.
local: Nyini ti, Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni mmɔɔ, mini Adamande Wa ni ti i Mibiɛ a.

eng: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many so that their sins may be forgiven.
local: Ahin nzan n ti m mbunja a lɛɛ, ye m sufa ma am n a. M mbunja n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ sufa sɛsɛ­su na i di i di n a. Minɛm dɔŋgu satiɛ dɛɛ ti, ye m mbunja ni soŋgi gɔ a, na Nyɛmɛ fa bu satiɛ ce bu.

eng: Suddenly, there in the synagogue, a person with an evil spirit screamed.
local: Nyini saŋga n nu na sɔnɔ bo ajini wo i ŋu n aba bu dɔ bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na wɔ bo awie.

eng: They placed above his head the charge against him. It read, This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.
local: Na ba kɛrɛ jɔrɛ n bo bu kpiɛ sɔɔri i ni, afa abo akpama i baka jaanu n tii su, kɛrɛɛ­wa n la ka, Yesu bo i ti Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n lɛɛ.

eng: What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: ase ka, Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ, a koro nzu ya nu fɛn a? A ba ka a ba yo ya ŋgbɛɛn wɔ? M si u sa, wɔrɔ ti sɔnɔ casi n bo Nyɛmɛ asunma u n a.

eng: I tell you, I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way with you in my Fathers kingdom.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, m nú má ahin nzan bekun, má ka cɛɛn be bo m ni ambɛrɛ bá saŋga nu i fɔfɔrɛ, m Si Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu ni.

eng: They crucified with him two outlaws, one on his right side and one on his left.
local: Na ba kpama fin­fɔ nnyɔ nbem baka jaanu su adodo i, bu kpamaari kun i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, na ba kpama kun n gusu i saa biɛ su.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him.
local: Na Yesu atiɛn ajini n ŋu ase ka, Mɔ u nɔɔ bɛrɛ, na a fite yaki sɔnɔ ni.

eng: Then, after singing songs of praise, they went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Nyini sin na ba di jue akansi Nyɛmɛ, na ba fite akɔ Oliva Buka n su.

eng: Those who were walking by insulted Jesus, shaking their heads.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu sin dɔ ni, subobo bu tii, na ba kpiɛ i nzukɔ, bu ŋa.

eng: The unclean spirit shook him and screamed, then it came out.
local: Na ajini n akpisi sɔnɔ ni, na wɔ ciindi afite ayaki i.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Tonight you will all fall away because of me. This is because it is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will go off in all directions .
local: Dɔ na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ni ka, Nyuma dɛɛ kɔŋguɛ n nu, am kɛrɛ ŋmáti yaki m, dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, M bó kun bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ ni, na nbɔɛm bá sandi.

eng: and saying, So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you? Save yourself! If you are Gods Son, come down from the cross.
local: Wɔrɔ bo a ŋa a fúfu Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n na a káaki ta i le­san afiɛn n lɛɛ, de u tii ji. Nzɛn a ti Nyɛmɛ Wa nahɔrɛ su, jura baka jaanu n su.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests, along with the legal experts and the elders, were making fun of him, saying.
local: Sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n gusu yoori i anzasi a.

eng: But after Im raised up, Ill go before you to Galilee.
local: Ama, m bá teŋgeeri m yiwee n nu, m dú am mɔ kɔ Galili awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: Everyone was shaken and questioned among themselves, Whats this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands unclean spirits and they obey him.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ ayo alekutura akɔ asin su, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ee. Nzu la ahin? I ti keree­wa fɔfɔrɛ wɔ. Ahin sɔnɔ n la yiko bo i koro ma ajinim n mmɔɔ nɔɔ na bu de i.

eng: He saved others, but he cant save himself. Hes the king of Israel, so let him come down from the cross now. Then well believe in him.
local: bu ŋa, Wɔ de minɛm tii aji, ama, i sukoro de má yiri ni i tii. M ŋa i ti Yisareli­fɔm Fɛmɛ n wɔ e. Tɔ, ma i jura baka jaanu n su bra kisa kisa na ya niɛ, na ya yó i yarada.

eng: Peter replied, If everyone else stumbles because of you, Ill never stumble.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, Nzɛn ahinm kɛrɛ ŋmati yakiiri u, mini ŋmáti yaki má u da.

eng: Right away the news about him spread throughout the entire region of Galilee.
local: Nyini ti na Yesu duma akaba ata abara ayɛ Galili awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: He trusts in God, so let God deliver him now if he wants to. He said, Im Gods Son.
local: I daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu a, ye i ŋa i ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a. Tɔ, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ koro i a, ma i de i tii ji kisa na ya niɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, I assure you that, before the rooster crows tonight, you will deny me three times.
local: Na Yesu ase Petoro ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u n a, nyuma kɔŋguɛ n nu, kana akɔnyuma bá bo, a kéte kpe nsan ka a si má m.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus, James, and John went home with Simon and Andrew.
local: Saŋga bo i fiteeri tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu ni, na i ni Yakobo ni Yohane akɔ awura Simon ni Andere dika.

eng: The outlaws who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
local: Haari na fin­fɔ nnyɔm n bo bu kpamaari bu dodoori Yesu ni mmɔɔ akpiɛ i nzukɔ sɔ.

eng: Peter said, Even if I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. All the disciples said the same thing.
local: Na Petoro akaaki ase i ka, Mini sé má sɔ da. Nzɛn haari wɔ yo daka ka mini ni wɔrɔ kɛrɛ saŋga wú mmɔɔ, ŋka ya wú wɔ. Na i susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ ase sɔ.

eng: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to the disciples, Stay here while I go and pray over there.
local: Na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n ayasu akɔ aju awɔrɛ be bo bu fere i ka Gɛsɛmane ni, na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ni ka, Am tana fɛn ŋminda m, na m kɔ́ dɔ kɔ sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Wiɛsu kerefi buru ni nnyɔ saŋga na awosin akata asiɛn n kɛrɛ su, haari naasɔ kerefi nsan.

eng: Simons mother-in-law was in bed, sick with a fever, and they told Jesus about her at once.
local: Bu kɔ juuri awuru n nu ni, na ba se Yesu ka Simon sibara la má i ŋu, yakɔkɔ abo i, i da sɔrɔ.

eng: He went to her, took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.
local: Na wɔ kɔ aso i saa nu ayɛ i su atana, na yakɔkɔ n ayaki i, na wɔ kɔ ayo diire ama bu.

eng: That evening, at sunset, people brought to Jesus those who were sick or demon-possessed.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n naasɔ, dika bibiri saŋga, na minɛm afa tukpaki­fɔm kɛrɛ ni minɛm bo ajinim wo bu ŋu n aba Yesu dɔ.

eng: When he took Peter and Zebedees two sons, he began to feel sad and anxious.
local: Na wɔ fa Petoro ni Zɛbɛdi nbaa nnyɔm ni, na ba kɔ mɔ kaan. Bu kɔ juuri dɔ, na ahoresaaki aba i dɔ agaya, na i jeke ati.

eng: At about three Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani , which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Na fa kɔ nyini saŋga ni, na Yesu abo awie kpa ase ka, Eli, Eli, lama sabatani ? I bu la ka, M Nyɛmɛ, m Nyɛmɛ, nzu ti ye a yaki m aji a.

eng: The whole town gathered near the door.
local: Na miɛ n nu jama n kɛrɛ aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ i dɔ anuŋgbere n nu.

eng: Then he said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert with me.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ahoresaaki aba m dɔ agaya, i yo m ka m wu wɔ mmɔɔ. Am wo fɛn na am ŋminda m. Má am daafi.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell on his face and prayed, My Father, if its possible, take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: Na wɔ kɔ dede kaan, na wɔ butu i kunnu su. Na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ, i ŋa, M Si Nyɛmɛ, nzɛn má a soro su, yi m fite ahin anyum­boro n bo i suba m dɔ n nu. Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, má a yo kabo m koro ni, ama, yo kabo a koro ni.

eng: He healed many who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and he threw out many demons. But he didnt let the demons speak, because they recognized him.
local: Na wɔ tuntun tukpaki barasu barasu­fɔm beberebe, na ba nya laifiɛ, na wɔ fɔn ayi ajinim gusu beberebe. Wɔ du má su na ajinim n jɔjɔ, dama bu si i sa.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Hes calling Elijah.
local: Na minɛ nbem jinaari bɛrɛ, bu nbem n tiiri sɔ ni, na ba se ka, Am ti, ahin sɔnɔ n sufere Eliya.

eng: He came back to the disciples and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Couldnt you stay alert one hour with me?.
local: I sere Nyɛmɛ wieeri ni, na wɔ kpie i nyi akɔ aŋu ka i susu­fɔ nsanm ni wo daafiri su. Na wɔ bisa Petoro ka, Nzu ti ye am minɛ nsan n kɛrɛ be mmɔɔ koro kpiɛ má daafiri haari kaan sɔ mmɔɔ a.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Am kpiɛ daafiri, na am sere Nyɛmɛ adena má deke be yo am na am wura satiɛ nu. Ahore­mbaa n koro nyɛmɛsu, ama, aŋuniɛ­mbaa n ayɔrɔki.

eng: One of them ran over, took a sponge full of vinegar, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink.
local: Kpeŋga na bu nu kun aŋmati afa mɛnɛ, adɔ nzan gagaraki be nu, na wɔ fa awɔ baka su alaandi atɔ Yesu nɔɔ ka i nu.

eng: Early in the morning, well before sunrise, Jesus rose and went to a deserted place where he could be alone in prayer.
local: Na alecin akɔnyuma ciciri su, na Yesu ayasu afite akɔ dede awɔrɛ be nu i ŋgumi, na wɔ kɔ asere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: A second time he went away and prayed, My Father, if its not possible that this cup be taken away unless I drink it, then let it be what you want.
local: Na wɔ kaaki akɔ asere Nyɛmɛ bekun, i ŋa, M Si Nyɛmɛ, nzɛn i ti u dɛɛ koroya­nu ka m fite má ahin anyum­boro n bo i suba m dɔ n nu, ma u dɛɛ koroo­wa n yo.

eng: But the rest of them said, Lets see if Elijah will come and save him.
local: Ama, na nyiŋgam n ase ka, Am yaki na ya niɛ, ka Eliya bá de i tii ji.

eng: Simon and those with him tracked him down.
local: Na Simon ni i beŋgum akɔ i niɛniɛ.

eng: When they found him, they told him, Everyones looking for you.
local: Bu kɔ ŋuuri i ni, na ba se i ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ suniɛniɛ u busu.

eng: Again Jesus cried out with a loud shout. Then he died.
local: Na Yesu abo awie kpa, na wɔ nya ayo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ.

eng: Again he came and found them sleeping. Their eyes were heavy with sleep.
local: Na wɔ kpie i nyi aba i susu­fɔm n dɔ, na wɔ ba aŋu ka ba daafi bekun, dama daafiri ati bu agaya.

eng: But he left them and again went and prayed the same words for the third time.
local: Na Yesu ayaki bu bekun, na wɔ kɔ asere Nyɛmɛ kpe nsan su, ni nyini nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa kun bo i du mɔ jɔjɔɔri i ni.

eng: Look, the curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, the rocks split.
local: Dɔ na tani bambaka n bo bu fa kanda kpaakiiri Sɔɔ Casi n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu n ati nu nnyɔ, fa i aŋgoro kɔ jura i asiɛŋgu. Na asiɛn n akpisi, na yabuɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim akpakpaaki nu.

eng: He replied, Lets head in the other direction, to the nearby villages, so that I can preach there too. Thats why Ive come.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am ma ya kɔ miɛ nyiŋgam n su na m kɔ kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kere bu gusu, dama ka nyini ti ye m baari durunya n nu a.

eng: Then he came to his disciples and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Look, the time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Nyini sin, na wɔ kpie i nyi aba i susu­fɔm n dɔ, aba aŋu ka bu toko wo daafiri su, na wɔ se bu ka, Am toko daafi yi am ŋumi wɔ? Am niɛ, saŋga n aju, wɔ tɔ mini Adamande Wa ni ama satiɛ­yo­fɔm.

eng: and the bodies of many holy people who had died were raised.
local: Na faŋ­kunmam ateke, na Nyɛmɛ minɛm dɔŋgu bo ba wu n ateŋge bu yiwee nu.

eng: He traveled throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and throwing out demons.
local: Na wɔ bara ayɛ Galili awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, ye i fɔn yi ajinim minɛm ŋu a.

eng: Get up. Lets go. Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Am niɛ, sɔnɔ n bo wɔ tɔ m lɛɛ. Am yasu na ya kɔ.

eng: After Jesus resurrection they came out of their graves and went into the holy city where they appeared to many people.
local: Na ba fite faŋ­kunmam n nu, na saŋga bo Yesu ba teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni, na ba kɔ awura Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na minɛ dɔŋgu aŋu bu.

eng: When the centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus saw the earthquake and what had just happened, they were filled with awe and said, This was certainly Gods Son.
local: Na soja kpiɛn n ni i sojam n bo bu jina niɛɛri Yesu ni, na bu ŋuuri kabo asiɛn n kpisiiri barasu, ni kabo sa n kɛrɛ yoori ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu agaya, na ba se ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a nahɔrɛ su.

eng: A man with a skin disease approached Jesus, fell to his knees, and begged, If you want, you can make me clean.
local: Na koko­fɔ be aba Yesu dɔ, na wɔ ba akutu i nyunu asere i jande ka i buka i. I ŋa, Nzɛn a koro ka a ma m nya laifiɛ, a koro ma m nya i.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came. With him was a large crowd carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests and elders of the people.
local: Na saŋga bo Yesu toko wo jɔjɔɔri su ni, na Yudasi bo i ti Yesu susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm nu kun aju ni jama beberebe. Bu bita takobem ni kɔrɔmam. Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni sunmaari bu a.

eng: Many women were watching from a distance. They had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
local: Na nbaram beberebe bo bu su i su fite Galili awɔrɛ n nu na bu buka i ni, ajina dede suniɛ Yesu.

eng: Incensed, Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean.
local: Na Yesu aŋu i aŋunfɔ, na wɔ teŋge i saa anana i ŋu, na wɔ se i ka, M koro. Nya laifiɛ.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss.
local: Na Yudasi bo i tɔɔri Yesu ni akere jama n nyaari su kabo bu sí Yesu na bu tara i. I ŋa, Sɔnɔ n bo m kɔ́ biti i ni, yiri lɛɛ. Am tara i.

eng: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of Zebedees sons.
local: Bu nu nbem la Mariya bo i fite Magadala miɛ n nu ni, ni Mariya n bo i nbaam la Yakobo ni Yosefu ni, ni Zɛbɛdi yi.

eng: Just then he came to Jesus and said, Hello, Rabbi. Then he kissed him.
local: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri deke bo Yesu wo ni, na Yudasi akaba akɔ i dɔ, na wɔ se i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ. Na wɔ biti i, na wɔ yo i ansɛ.

eng: Instantly, the skin disease left him, and he was clean.
local: Na kokobi n akaba ayaki sɔnɔ n na wɔ nya laifiɛ.

eng: That evening a man named Joseph came. He was a rich man from Arimathea who had become a disciple of Jesus.
local: Na naasɔ saŋga na neŋge­fɔ be aba, i fite Aramatiya miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, bu fere i ka Yosefu. Yiri mmɔɔ ti Yesu susu­fɔm kun wɔ.

eng: But Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came to do. Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, M dɔn, yo deke n bo a koro ka a yo i ni ndende. Na minɛm n aba atara Yesu, na ba bita i kenken.

eng: Sternly, Jesus sent him away.
local: Na Yesu ama i atin ka i kɔ, na wɔ bɔbɔ i.

eng: saying, Dont say anything to anyone. Instead, go and show yourself to the priest and offer the sacrifice for your cleansing that Moses commanded. This will be a testimony to them.
local: ase i ka, Má a se ka a se be ahin jɔrɛ ni, ama, kɔ na a kɔ fa u ŋu kere Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ ni, na a kɔ yo yoo­wa n bo Moyisi yo kereeri dawa­fɔm ni, dama nyini kere minɛm ka a nya u ŋu a.

eng: One of those with Jesus reached for his sword. Striking the high priests slave, he cut off his ear.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔm n nu kun acin awɔrɔ i takobe, na wɔ kpiɛ sɔnɔ be sui, nyini sɔnɔ n ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n akɔɔ ɔ.

eng: He came to Pilate and asked for Jesus body. Pilate gave him permission to take it.
local: Na wɔ kɔ asere Gomina Pilato ka i fa Yesu ŋuniɛ n ma i. Na Gomina Pilato ama nɔɔ ka bu kɔ fa ma i.

eng: Instead, he went out and started talking freely and spreading the news so that Jesus wasnt able to enter a town openly. He remained outside in deserted places, but people came to him from everywhere.
local: Ama, na wɔ kɔ abo ndoori, na labari n abara ayɛ deke n kɛrɛ, nyini ti, Yesu akoro ayi má i ŋu fofoe nu miɛm be nu kun, i fiɛɛri i ŋu ahɛmɛ wɔ, na minɛ afite awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu aba i dɔ.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Put the sword back into its place. All those who use the sword will die by the sword.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Fa u takobe n kaaki wura i buɛ nu. Dama minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu fa takobe yo juma ni, bɛrɛ gusu bá wu ni takobe wɔ.

eng: Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
local: Na Yosefu akɔ ayi Yesu fuin n apɔɔndi nzee fɔfɔrɛ nu.

eng: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had carved out of the rock. After he rolled a large stone at the door of the tomb, he went away.
local: na wɔ kɔ ase i faŋ­kunma nu, yiri Yosefu dɛɛ tiɛŋgu yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma n bo ba lokoti i fɔfɔrɛ ni. Na wɔ nya akundu yabuɛ bambaka afa atɔ faŋ­kunma n anɔ ni, na wɔ kɔ.

eng: Or do you think that Im not able to ask my Father and he will send to me more than twelve battle groups of angels right away?.
local: A si má ka nzɛn má m sereeri m Si Nyɛmɛ, i súnma i mɛrɛkɛm asɛsɛ asɛsɛ buru ni nnyɔ, na bu bá buka m kisa kisa bɛrɛ?.

eng: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting in front of the tomb.
local: Na Mariya Magadala­fɔ ni, ni Mariya nyiŋga n atana afa bu nyunu awɔ faŋ­kunma ni.

eng: But if I did that, how would the scriptures be fulfilled that say this must happen.
local: Ama, nzɛn m nya yoori sɔ, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka wɔ yo daka ka i yó sɔ ni, i yó sɛ ye i bá yo yii­wa.

eng: Then Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like a thief? Day after day, I sat in the temple teaching, but you didnt arrest me.
local: Na Yesu abisa jama n ka, M ti fin­fɔ ɔ, ye am fa takobem ni kɔrɔmam na am ba tara m a? Cɛɛn kɛrɛ cɛɛn m ni ambɛrɛ wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu a, ye m kere minɛm a, ama, am atara má m.

eng: The next day, which was the day after Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees gathered before Pilate.
local: Na alecin fɔfɔrɛ bo i ti Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Farasi­fɔm n akɔ Gomina Pilato dɔ.

eng: But all this has happened so that what the prophets said in the scriptures might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left Jesus and ran away.
local: Ama, ahin kɛrɛ yó sɔ, adena kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm akɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n yo yii­wa a. Dɔ na i susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ aŋmati ayaki i.

eng: They said, Sir, we remember that while that deceiver was still alive he said, After three days I will arise.
local: Na ba kɔ ase i ka, Ya si, ya teŋge su ka saŋga bo nyini kɔrɛ­fɔ n yo la ŋgɔɔ ni, na wɔ se ka yiri wuuri, i le­san cɛɛn, i téŋge i yiwee n nu.

eng: Those who arrested Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest. The legal experts and the elders had gathered there.
local: Na bo bu taraari Yesu n afa i akɔ Kayafasi dɔ, yiri ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n a. I dika n nu ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ a.

eng: Therefore, order the grave to be sealed until the third day. Otherwise, his disciples may come and steal the body and tell the people, Hes been raised from the dead. This last deception will be worse than the first.
local: Nyini ti, ma nɔɔ na bu kɔ jina ŋminda i faŋ­kunma ni, fa kɔ ju haari le­san cɛɛn ni, má nyini, i susu­fɔm ni koro kɔ wɔ yi i ŋuniɛ ni, na bu bu kɔrɛ ka wɔ teŋge wɔ. Nyini saŋga, kasin dɛɛ kɔrɛ ni tára bumboori dɛɛ ni.

eng: Pilate replied, You have soldiers for guard duty. Go and make it as secure as you know how.
local: Na Gomina Pilato ase bu ka, Am fa tintini­jina­fɔm, na am ma bu kɔ jina ŋminda i faŋ­kunma n kabo am koro i barasu ni, na má be koro yo pui.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance until he came to the high priests courtyard. He entered that area and sat outside with the officers to see how it would turn out.
local: Na Petoro asu Yesu su, i woori dede kaan ni yiri, haari i kɔ wuraari Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n dika gisan nu. I wuraari gisan n nu ni, na wɔ kɔ asaŋga atana tintini­jina­fɔm nu, adena i ŋu sa n bo i yo n awieeri.

eng: Then they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.
local: Nyini ti, na ba kɔ ata atɔ faŋ­kunma n anɔ n kɛrɛ, na ba ma tintini­jina­fɔm n ajina aŋminda abara ayɛ i, adena má be koro yo pui.

eng: The chief priests and the whole council were looking for false testimony against Jesus so that they could put him to death.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm n kɛrɛ aniɛniɛ minɛm bo bu bá di kɔrɛ daani fa kɔ ŋgasi bo Yesu ado i ni, adena bu nya i taraa­biri na bu kun i.

eng: They didnt find anything they could use from the many false witnesses who were willing to come forward. But finally they found two.
local: Haari ni bo minɛ beberebe fite jina diiri kɔrɛ daani n kɛrɛ, ba nya má jɔrɛ bo bu fa kpiɛ sɔ i. Kasin na minɛ nnyɔ nbem ayasu ajina.

eng: who said, This man said, I can destroy Gods temple and rebuild it in three days.
local: na ba se ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n ŋa yiri koro fufu Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni, na i káaki ta i le­san afiɛn.

eng: Then the high priest stood and said to Jesus, Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n ayasu ajina, na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, A la má bɔnu be fa kɔ ahin jɔrɛ n bo minɛm ni akpiɛ asɔ u n su.

eng: But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, By the living God, I demand that you tell us whether you are the Christ, Gods Son.
local: Ama, na Yesu amɔ i nɔɔ. Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n ajɔjɔ i dɔ bekun, i ŋa, Kisa, m koro ka a tan Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni. Nzɛn wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ Wa n bo i ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni, se ya.

eng: You said it, Jesus replied. But I say to you that from now on youll see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds .
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Kabo a jɔjɔɔri ni, i ti sɔ ɔ. Ama, m suse am kɛrɛ wɔ lɛɛ ka, Fite nyuma fa kɔ, am bá ŋu mini Adamande Wa ni ka m tana Nyɛmɛ Yiko­fɔ n saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, na am ŋú ka m fite aŋgoro yinyiŋgu n nu suba.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Hes insulting God! Why do we need any more witnesses? Look, youve heard his insult against God.
local: Yesu bɔ nu sɔ wieeri ni, na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n asɔn i tanlɛɛ. Na wɔ se ka, Wɔ jɔjɔ awura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu. Ya koro má daani­fɔ bekun. Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ti kabo wɔ jɔjɔ awura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi n nu e.

eng: What do you think? And they answered, He deserves to die.
local: Ye am ŋa sɛ wɔ? Na bu kɛrɛ ase ka, Wɔ kan ciire jɔrɛ lɛɛ, wɔ yo daka ka i wu.

eng: Then they spit in his face and beat him. They hit him.
local: Na ba yi nanzue agɔ Yesu nyunuawa nu, na ba bo i, na ba bo i nyɔɔnu.

eng: and said, Prophesy for us, Christ! Who hit you.
local: na ba se i ka, Wɔrɔ Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni, ŋma bo u a? Kan kere ya.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. A servant woman came and said to him, You were also with Jesus the Galilean.
local: Bu wo nyini kɛrɛ su ni, na Petoro toko tana gisan n nu, na afani be aba i bi, na wɔ se Petoro ka, Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ yo woori nyini Yesu Galili­fɔ n dɔ ɔ.

eng: But he denied it in front of all of them, saying, I dont know what you are talking about.
local: Ama, na Petoro akete bu kɛrɛ nyunu ase ka, Ai, m si má nyini jɔrɛ n bo a sujɔjɔ i ni mmɔɔ.

eng: When he went over to the gate, another woman saw him and said to those who were there, This man was with Jesus, the man from Nazareth.
local: Na Petoro afite akɔ gisan n anɔ n nu, na afani nyiŋga be aba aŋu i, na wɔ se minɛm bo bu jina bɛrɛ n ka, Ahin sɔnɔ ni mmɔɔ yo woori Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n dɔ ɔ.

eng: With a solemn pledge, he denied it again, saying, I dont know the man.
local: Na Petoro akete bekun, na wɔ tan ndiɛ, i ŋa, Mini si má nyini sɔnɔ ni mmɔɔ.

eng: A short time later those standing there came and said to Peter, You must be one of them. The way you talk gives you away.
local: Wɔ cɛ má na minɛm n bo bu jina bɛrɛ n gusu ase Petoro ka, Nahɔrɛ, a ti bu kun wɔ, dama u jɔjɔɔ­wa ni kere sɔ a.

eng: Then he cursed and swore, I dont know the man! At that very moment the rooster crowed.
local: Na Petoro atan ndiɛ afa i saa awura sin nu, i ŋa, M si má nyini sɔnɔ ni. Kpeŋga na akɔnyuma abo.

eng: Peter remembered Jesus words, Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Na Petoro ateŋge su kabo Yesu seeri i ni, i ŋa Kana akɔnyuma bá bo, a kéte kpe nsan su ka a si má m. Na wɔ fite akɔ asun agaya.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to the other side of the lake, to the region of the Gerasenes.
local: Na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n akpiɛ kunma kpiri n akɔ aju Gɛrasafɔm dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out of the tombs.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu fiteeri lee n nu ni, na sɔnɔ be afite faŋ­kunma nu aba akpa i atin. Nyini sɔnɔ n nati da faŋ­kunma nu wɔ, dama ajini wo i ŋu. Sɔnɔ be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro tara i cici i ni jɔrɔkɔ n mmɔɔ kun.

eng: He had been secured many times with leg irons and chains, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one was tough enough to control him.
local: dama saŋga kɛrɛ bu bo i tobari wɔ na bu wura i jɔrɔkɔ buka su, ama, haari ni nyini n mmɔɔ i titi jɔrɔkɔ n wɔ na i kaaki bo sandi tobari n nu. Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i la faŋga koro tara i jina kun.

eng: Night and day in the tombs and the hills, he would howl and cut himself with stones.
local: Kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu kɛrɛ i wo faŋ­kunma nu sɔ ɔ, ni bukam su na i suciindi, na i sufa yabuɛ kpiɛkpiɛ i ŋu.

eng: When he saw Jesus from far away, he ran and knelt before him.
local: Na wɔ ŋu Yesu saŋga bo i toko wo dede ni yiri ni, na Yesu atiɛn ajini n ŋu ase i ka, Ajini, fite yaki nyini sɔnɔ ni. Na sɔnɔ n aŋmati akɔ akutu Yesu nyunu, na wɔ ciindi ase ka, Yesu, Micɛra Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n Wa, a koro nzu m dɔ fɛn a? M sere u wɔ, Nyɛmɛ ti, má a ma m ŋu anyum­boro.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He responded, Legion is my name, because we are many.
local: Na Yesu abisa i ka, Bu fere u sɛ? Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, Bu fere m ka Jama Beberebe­fɔ, dama ya sunnu.

eng: They pleaded with Jesus not to send them out of that region.
local: Na wɔ sere Yesu jande ka má i fɔn bu fite nyini awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside.
local: Nyini saŋga afam beberebe wo dodo bɛrɛ, bu wo buka be su sudi.

eng: Send us into the pigs! they begged. Let us go into the pigs.
local: na ajinim n asere Yesu ka, Ma ya kɔ fun afam n ŋu.

eng: Jesus gave them permission, so the unclean spirits left the man and went into the pigs. Then the herd of about two thousand pigs rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Na Yesu ama bu atin na ba fite akɔ afun afam n ŋu. Afam n ju akpii nnyɔ dika, na bu kɛrɛ aŋmati acɔɔndi akɔ ato kunma kpiri nzue n nu, na nzue n adi bu.

eng: Those who tended the pigs ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside. People came to see what had happened.
local: Afa sasa­fɔm ŋuuri nyini n sɔ ni, na ba ŋmati akɔ abo ndoori miɛ kunnu, ni ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛ kɛrɛ su. Na minɛ aba aniɛ sa n bo i yoori ni.

eng: They came to Jesus and saw the man who used to be demon-possessed. They saw the very man who had been filled with many demons sitting there fully dressed and completely sane, and they were filled with awe.
local: Na ba ba Yesu dɔ, na ba ŋu sɔnɔ n bo ajinim n yo woori i ŋu n ka i tana Yesu bi wura tanlɛɛ, ni i nyunu koroya su, na sɛrɛ ati bu.

eng: Those who had actually seen what had happened to the demon-possessed man told the others about the pigs.
local: Na minɛm bo bu ŋuuri sa n yoo­biri ni, akan jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ akere jama n fa kɔ sa n bo i tuuri sɔnɔ n bo ajinim yo woori i ŋu ni, ni sa n bo i tuuri afam ni.

eng: Then they pleaded with Jesus to leave their region.
local: Bu tiiri jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na ba nya asere Yesu jande ka i yasu bu awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: While he was climbing into the boat, the one who had been demon-possessed pleaded with Jesus to let him come along as one of his disciples.
local: Saŋga n bo Yesu suwura lee n nu na i kɔ ni, na sɔnɔ n bo ajinim yo woori i ŋu ni, asere i jande ka i ma i su i su.

eng: But Jesus wouldnt allow it. Go home to your own people, Jesus said, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and how he has shown you mercy.
local: Ama, na Yesu akete, na wɔ se i ka, Kpie u nyi kɔ awuru na a kɔ kan kere u lamɔkɔm fa kɔ sakpa n bo Mibiɛ n ayo ama u ni, ni kabo wɔ si u aŋunfɔ barasu ni.

eng: The man went away and began to proclaim in the Ten Cities all that Jesus had done for him, and everyone was amazed.
local: Na sɔnɔ n akpie i nyi akɔ akan sakpa n kɛrɛ bo Yesu ayo ama i ni, abara ayɛ Miɛ Burum dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ayo alekutura.

eng: Jesus crossed the lake again, and on the other side a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
local: Na Yesu awura lee n nu akpie i nyi akpiɛ kunma kpiri n buekun dɔ, na jama beberebe aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ i bi nzue n nɔɔ su.

eng: Jairus, one of the synagogue leaders, came forward. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm kpiɛn be wo bɛrɛ, yiri niɛ bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n su a. Bu fere i ka Yarusi. Na wɔ ba Yesu dɔ nzue n nɔɔ su na wɔ kutu i jaa bu.

eng: and pleaded with him, My daughter is about to die. Please, come and place your hands on her so that she can be healed and live.
local: na wɔ sere i jande. I ŋa, M wa suŋguru atɔ cin. Bra na a ba fa u saa nana i ŋu na i nya ŋgɔɔ ni laifiɛ.

eng: So Jesus went with him. A swarm of people were following Jesus, crowding in on him.
local: Na Yesu asu i su na ba kɔ. Na jama beberebe asu Yesu su haari bu kɔ sumiɛ i ŋu.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years.
local: Bara be woori jama n nu, i bita abiɛɛri i afɔ buru ni nnyɔ lɛɛ bo i kpu na i kpiɛ má.

eng: She had suffered a lot under the care of many doctors, and had spent everything she had without getting any better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
local: Wɔ di wahara, na wɔ saaki i ŋwaa kɛrɛ atuntun i ŋu ayiri­fɔm dɔ dede haari wɔ ba afie, ama, wɔ nya má laifiɛ se tukpaki n buka su kɔ ɔ.

eng: Because she had heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his clothes.
local: Na wɔ ti Yesu ŋga, na wɔ sakati awura jama n nu Yesu sin, afa i saa akan Yesu tanlɛɛ ni.

eng: She was thinking, If I can just touch his clothes, Ill be healed.
local: Dama i seeri i kunnu ka, Nzɛn m koro yoori aniɛ faari m saa kaanri i tanlɛɛ n mmɔɔ, m nyá laifiɛ.

eng: Her bleeding stopped immediately, and she sensed in her body that her illness had been healed.
local: Na wɔ yo sɔ n kpeŋga na i abiɛɛri n akpiɛ, na yiri ni i tii aŋu ka wɔ nya laifiɛ.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus recognized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and said, Who touched my clothes.
local: Jina n nu bɛrɛ na Yesu afa nu ka i yiko n be afite i ŋuniɛ nu akɔ ayo juma. Na wɔ kaaki aniɛ jama n nu, na wɔ bisa ka, Ŋma faari i saa kaanri m tanlɛɛ n a.

eng: His disciples said to him, Dont you see the crowd pressing against you? Yet you ask, Who touched me.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n ase i ka, Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ aŋu kabo jama n amiɛ u barasu, ye nzu ti ye a bisa ka, ŋma fa i saa kaanri u a.

eng: But Jesus looked around carefully to see who had done it.
local: Ama, na wɔ niɛ abara ayɛ na i si sɔnɔ n bo i yoori sɔ ni.

eng: The woman, full of fear and trembling, came forward. Knowing what had happened to her, she fell down in front of Jesus and told him the whole truth.
local: Na sɛrɛ ati bara ni, i jina, i ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ subobo i fa kɔ sa n bo i tuuri i ni, na wɔ ba akutu Yesu nyunu, na wɔ kan jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ ni i nahɔrɛ atin su akere i.

eng: He responded, Daughter, your faith has healed you; go in peace, healed from your disease.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, M wa, yarada n bo a yo m ni, yiri ma a nya laifiɛ a. Kɔ awuru ni ahoreda, na má u tɔrɛ n ba u ŋu kun.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking with her, messengers came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Why bother the teacher any longer.
local: Saŋga n bo Yesu toko wo jɔjɔ su ni, na minɛ nbem afite tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n kpiɛn n dika aba ase i ka, U wa n wo má bɛrɛ. Nyini ti, má a dama ya Kere­fɔ n kun.

eng: But Jesus overheard their report and said to the synagogue leader, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting.
local: Na jɔrɛ n ato Yesu sui nu, ama, na wɔ se kpiɛn n ka, Má a ma sɛrɛ ti u, wɔrɔ yo m yarada.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to follow him except Peter, James, and John, James brother.
local: Wɔ soro má su ka be su i su kun, má ka Petoro, ni Yakobo, ni i niɛma Yohane.

eng: They came to the synagogue leaders house, and he saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
local: Bu kɔ juuri kpiɛn n dika ni, na Yesu awura aŋu ka minɛ beberebe suyo naŋmiɛ, bu susun subo awie.

eng: He went in and said to them, Whats all this commotion and crying about? The child isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am suyo naŋmiɛ sun sɔ a? Baa­bara n awu má, wɔ daafi wɔ.
