eng: They laughed at him, but he threw them all out. Then, taking the childs parents and his disciples with him, he went to the room where the child was.
local: Na ba siri i. Na wɔ fɔn bu kɛrɛ afite, na wɔ fa baa­bara n si ni i ni, ni i susu­fɔ nsanm ni, na ba kɔ sɔɔ n bo baa­bara n da nu ni.

eng: Taking her hand, he said to her, Talitha koum , which means, Young woman, get up.
local: Na wɔ kɔ atara i saa, na wɔ se i ka, Talita kumi. I bu la ka, Suŋguru kaan, m ŋa a yasu.

eng: Suddenly the young woman got up and began to walk around. She was years old. They were shocked!.
local: Kpeŋga na suŋguru n ayasu anati. I yo ti afɔ buru ni nnyɔ baa a. Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu woori bɛrɛ n ayo alekutura agaya.

eng: He gave them strict orders that no one should know what had happened. Then he told them to give her something to eat.
local: Na Yesu abɔbɔ bu ni nahɔrɛ su ka má bu ma be si sa n bo wɔ yo i ni, na wɔ se ka bu ma baa n deke be na i di.

eng: Jesus left that place and came to his hometown. His disciples followed him.
local: Ye Yesu yasu kɔɔri i bɔsu na i susu­fɔm n asu i su.

eng: On the Sabbath, he began to teach in the synagogue. Many who heard him were surprised. Where did this man get all this? Whats this wisdom hes been given? What about the powerful acts accomplished through him?.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n na wɔ kɔ bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na wɔ kɔ akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ akere bu. Minɛm bo bu tiiri i keree­wa n kɛrɛ yoori alekutura. Na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Nifansu ye ahin sɔnɔ n kɔ sɔnsɔɔnri i si n kɛrɛ a? Bu maari i alecira bɔnɔ ɔ? Ye alekutura sam bo i yo bu ni, i yo sɛ yo bu a?.

eng: Isnt this the carpenter? Isnt he Marys son and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Arent his sisters here with us? They were repulsed by him and fell into sin.
local: Má Mariya wa n bo i ti kampenta n lɛɛ? Má Yakobo ni Yose ni Yuda ni Simon n gɔrɔ n lɛɛ? Am niɛ e. Má i niɛma nbaram mmɔɔ wo ya nu fɛn n a? Na ba bu i ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Jesus said to them, Prophets are honored everywhere except in their own hometowns, among their relatives, and in their own households.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Deke kɛrɛ bu ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ jirima, má ka i bɔsu­fɔm ni i lamɔkɔm ni i gisan­nu­fɔm dɔ ŋgumi.

eng: He was unable to do any miracles there, except that he placed his hands on a few sick people and healed them.
local: Nyini ti, wɔ koro ayo má alekutura sa be i bɔsu dɔ se i faari i saa nanaari tukpaki­fɔ nbem ŋu wɔ, na ba nya laifiɛ.

eng: He was appalled by their disbelief. Then Jesus traveled through the surrounding villages teaching.
local: Na wɔ yo i awarabo kabo i bɔsu­fɔm n ayo má i yarada ni. Na Yesu abara ayɛ ahɛmɛ miɛm n kɛrɛ su, na i sukere minɛm.

eng: He called for the Twelve and sent them out in pairs. He gave them authority over unclean spirits.
local: Na wɔ fere i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni, na wɔ sunma bu nnyɔ nnyɔ, na wɔ ma bu yiko ka bu kɔ sufɔn yi ajinim fite minɛm ŋu.

eng: He instructed them to take nothing for the journey except a walking stick—no bread, no bags, and no money in their belts.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Má am fa puitiri­pui kɔ ahin atin n bo am sukɔ ni, diire o, kuruku o, ŋwaa o. Ama, am fa kpama ni gbagbaa yiri. Má am se ka am fa tanlɛɛ buka bo am wura i n su.

eng: He said, Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Am kɔ juuri miɛ be su, awuru kɛrɛ bo am kɔ wuraari nu, am ka nyini awuru n nu, haari am bá fite miɛ n nu.

eng: If a place doesnt welcome you or listen to you, as you leave, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Sasu bekun miɛ bo am kɔ wuraari nu na ba de má am wɔfɔ, na ba kaaki asoro má am su ka bu ti am nɔaniɛ ni, am fite yaki nyini miɛ n ni, na am kpɔnkpɔn yi am jaa tuturi kɛrɛ. Nyini dí daani kere bu ka bu woo­wa ti má kpa.

eng: So they went out and proclaimed that people should change their hearts and lives.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n afite akɔ akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ akere minɛm ka bu kaaki bu wo.

eng: They cast out many demons, and they anointed many sick people with olive oil and healed them.
local: Na ba fɔn ayi ajinim minɛm beberebe ŋu, na ba sere Nyɛmɛ ama tukpaki­fɔm dɔŋgu, na ba fa ŋguin agɔ bu ŋu, na ba nya laifiɛ.

eng: Herod the king heard about these things, because the name of Jesus had become well-known. Some were saying, John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and this is why miraculous powers are at work through him.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Herode ati nyini sa n kɛrɛ, dama Yesu duma ata abara ayɛ deke kɛrɛ. Na minɛ nbem ase ka, Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n ye wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu a, nyini ti ye i la yiko koro yo nyini alekutura sam kɛrɛ a.

eng: Others were saying, He is Elijah. Still others were saying, He is a prophet like one of the ancient prophets.
local: Ama, na nbem ase ka, Yiri la Eliya a. Na nbem gusu ase ka, I ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ka dawa dɛɛm wɔ.

eng: But when Herod heard these rumors, he said, John, whom I beheaded, has been raised to life.
local: Ama, Fɛmɛ Herode tiiri nyini ni, na yiri ase ka, Yohane bo m kpiɛɛri i tii n ye wɔ teŋge a.

eng: He said this because Herod himself had arranged to have John arrested and put in prison because of Herodias, the wife of Herods brother Philip. Herod had married her.
local: Fɛmɛ Herode yo ma bu taraari Yohane nyiiri wɔ, dama yiri Fɛmɛ Herode afin i gɔrɔ Filipu yi Herodiya afa aja.

eng: but John told Herod, Its against the law for you to marry your brothers wife.
local: Má pui ti, dama Yohane ase Fɛmɛ Herode ka, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka a la má atin ka a fa u gɔrɔ yi ja i ŋgɔɔ su.

eng: So Herodias had it in for John. She wanted to kill him, but she couldnt.
local: Yohane seeri sɔ ni, na Herodiya afa i ŋgasi na wɔ fɔ aŋminda i kabo i nya i na i kun i. Ama, wɔ koro anya má i.

eng: This was because Herod respected John. He regarded him as a righteous and holy person, so he protected him. Johns words greatly confused Herod, yet he enjoyed listening to him.
local: dama Fɛmɛ Herode ni i tii soroori Yohane ka i ti sɔnɔ fofoe bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ. Wɔ koro má ka pui yo i. Na Fɛmɛ Herode ajimi fa kɔ nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ bo Yohane kan maari i n su, ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, i koro ka i suti i nɔaniɛ n wɔ.

eng: Finally, the time was right. It was on one of Herods birthdays, when he had prepared a feast for his high-ranking officials and military officers and Galilees leading residents.
local: Cɛɛn be sɔ ɔ na Herodiya aba anya Yohane taraa­biri. Nyini cɛɛn n ye Fɛmɛ Herode sugɔ i baa­wu cɛɛn agɔrɛ n a. Na wɔ fere i gomina minɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim, ni i soja nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Galili minɛ tere­tere­fɔm kɛrɛ ka bu ba di na bu nu.

eng: Herods daughter Herodias came in and danced, thrilling Herod and his dinner guests. The king said to the young woman, Ask me whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.
local: Agɔrɛ n nu ni, na Herodiya wa suŋguru awura agɔ akere Fɛmɛ Herode ni i jamam, na bu kɛrɛ ahore ajɔ. Dɔ ye fɛmɛ n seeri suŋguru n ka, Deke kɛrɛ bo a koro, se m, na m má u.

eng: Then he swore to her, Whatever you ask I will give to you, even as much as half of my kingdom.
local: Na wɔ tan ndiɛ ase i ka, Haari a ŋa m kpaaki m fɛmɛya n buekun ma u mmɔɔ, m fá ma u.

eng: She left the banquet hall and said to her mother, What should I ask for? John the Baptists head, Herodias replied.
local: Na suŋguru n afite akɔ abisa i ni ka, Ye m sere nzu wɔ? Na i ni n ase i ka, Kɔ se i ka a koro Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n tii­awa a.

eng: Hurrying back to the ruler, she made her request: I want you to give me John the Baptists head on a plate, right this minute.
local: Na wɔ ŋmati akpie i nyi ndende aba ase Fɛmɛ Herode n ka, M koro ka na a fa Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n tii­awa ma m pɛrɛntɛ nu kisa kisa.

eng: Although the king was upset, because of his solemn pledge and his guests, he didnt want to refuse her.
local: Fɛmɛ n tiiri sɔ ni, na i ahore asaaki agaya, ama, ndiɛ n bo i taanri i jama n nyunu n dɛɛ ti, wɔ koro akaaki má i nɔɔ.

eng: So he ordered a guard to bring Johns head. The guard went to the prison, cut off Johns head.
local: Kpeŋga na fɛmɛ n asunma soja ŋminda­fɔm n kun ka i kɔ fa Yohane tii­awa bra. Na wɔ kɔ akpiɛ i tii dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu dɔ.

eng: brought his head on a plate, and gave it to the young woman, and she gave it to her mother.
local: Na wɔ fa ada pɛrɛntɛ n nu aba ama suŋguru ni, na yiri gusu afa akɔ ama i ni ni.

eng: When Johns disciples heard what had happened, they came and took his dead body and laid it in a tomb.
local: Saŋga bo Yohane susu­fɔm tiiri sɔ n na ba ba afa fuin n akɔ ase i.

eng: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him everything they had done and taught.
local: Na jaramasam n akpie bu nyi aba i dɔ, na ba kan sam kɛrɛ bo ba yo i n akere i, ni kabo ba kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ akere minɛ barasu.

eng: Many people were coming and going, so there was no time to eat. He said to the apostles, Come by yourselves to a secluded place and rest for a while.
local: Jama n dɛɛ ti, ba nya má anyuŋkoro bo bu di diire. Nyini ti, na Yesu ase bu ka, Am ma ya kɔ deke bo minɛm wo má, na am kɔ yi am ŋumi.

eng: They departed in a boat by themselves for a deserted place.
local: Na bɛrɛ ŋgumi afa lee ni na ba kɔ.

eng: Many people saw them leaving and recognized them, so they ran ahead from all the cities and arrived before them.
local: Bu yasu sukɔ ni, na minɛm dɔŋgu aŋu bu, na ba si bu, na ba fite miɛ miɛm su aŋmati adu bu mɔ akɔ aju deke bo bu sukɔ ni.

eng: When Jesus arrived and saw a large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he began to teach them many things.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu fiteeri lee n nu ni, na wɔ ŋu jama beberebe, na wɔ si bu aŋunfɔ, dama bu ti ka nbɔɛm bo bu la má sasa­fɔ ɔ, na wɔ kere bu dekem beberebe.

eng: Late in the day, his disciples came to him and said, This is an isolated place, and its already late in the day.
local: Na saŋga bo wiɛ ba sutɔ ni, na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ aba ase i ka, Kisa, wiɛ sutɔ, ya wo boro gusu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Send them away so that they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy something to eat for themselves.
local: Ma minɛm n atin na bu kɔ miɛ kunnu ni ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛ miɛm su na bu kɔ nya diire be to di.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said to him, Should we go off and buy bread worth almost eight months pay and give it to them to eat.
local: Ama, na Yesu ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii nya deke be ma bu na bu di. Na ba se i ka, Nzɛn a ŋa ya to kpɔnɔ ma ahin jama n kɛrɛ, ya dí ŋwaa beberebe.

eng: He said to them, How much bread do you have? Take a look. After checking, they said, Five loaves of bread and two fish.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Ambɛrɛ la kpɔnɔ nyɛ wɔ? Am niɛniɛ na am niɛ. Na ba kɔ aniɛ na ba bɔ i nu ka, Kpɔnɔ nnu ni jombaa nnyɔ wo bɛrɛ a.

eng: He directed the disciples to seat all the people in groups as though they were having a banquet on the green grass.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka bu ma jama n tana asɛsɛ asɛsɛ wura fiɛfiɛɛ­wa n su.

eng: They sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.
local: Na ba tana asɛsɛ asɛsɛ yaa yaa ni aburu­nu nnu.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, broke the loaves into pieces, and gave them to his disciples to set before the people. He also divided the two fish among them all.
local: Na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ nnu ni, ni jombaa nnyɔ ni, na wɔ yɛ i tii su aŋgoro, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi. Na wɔ kpiɛkpiɛ kpɔnɔ nnu n nu, na wɔ fa ama i susu­fɔm n ka bu fa ma minɛm ni, na wɔ fa jombaa nnyɔ n gusu akpaaki bu kɛrɛ nu.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full.
local: Na bu kɛrɛ adi na bu kunnu ayi.

eng: They filled twelve baskets with the leftover pieces of bread and fish.
local: Na ba tambu kpɔnɔ, ni jombaa sinsin bo bu diiri na i kaanri ni, na ba nya kandiɛ buru ni nnyɔ.

eng: About five thousand had eaten.
local: Minɛm bo bu diiri kpɔnɔ n ni jombaa n kɛrɛ yasuuri nbiɛsɔm akpii nnu wɔ.

eng: Right then, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and go ahead to the other side of the lake, toward Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu ama i susu­fɔm n awura lee n nu na bu du i mɔ kpiɛ kɔ Besayida miɛ nu kunma kpiri n buekun, na yiri aka na i kaari jama ni.

eng: After saying good-bye to them, Jesus went up onto a mountain to pray.
local: I kaari bu wieeri ni, na wɔ fun akɔ buka n su ka i kɔ sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Evening came and the boat was in the middle of the lake, but he was alone on the land.
local: Saŋga bo ale ba saanri ni, lee n aju kunma kpiri n afiɛn, na Yesu ŋgumi aka kunma kpiri n sin.

eng: He saw his disciples struggling. They were trying to row forward, but the wind was blowing against them. Very early in the morning, he came to them, walking on the lake. He intended to pass by them.
local: Na wɔ ŋu ka i susu­fɔm n sudi wahara lee n kaan­biri, dama aŋuma sun bu kɔ bu sin wɔ. Na fa kɔ akɔnyuma ciciri su, na wɔ nati nzue n su suba bu dɔ, na wɔ yo ka i susin bu ŋu.

eng: When they saw him walking on the lake, they thought he was a ghost and they screamed.
local: Saŋga bo bu ŋuuri ka i nati nzue n su ni, na ba sunsun ka, ŋumi a, na ba bo awie.

eng: Seeing him was terrifying to all of them. Just then he spoke to them, Be encouraged! Its me. Dont be afraid.
local: Bu kɛrɛ ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu agaya. Kpeŋga na Yesu ajɔjɔ bu dɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, Am ma am ahore si, mini a, má am soro sɛrɛ.

eng: He got into the boat, and the wind settled down. His disciples were so baffled they were beside themselves.
local: Na wɔ wura lee n nu, na aŋuma n atara. Na i susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ ayo alekutura agaya.

eng: Thats because they hadnt understood about the loaves. Their minds had been closed so that they resisted Gods ways.
local: Dama ba koro ati má i bu kabo i la yiko koro faari kpɔnɔ nnu maari jama beberebem ni, bu ahore­mbaa ti kekereke n dɛɛ ti.

eng: When Jesus and his disciples had crossed the lake, they landed at Gennesaret, anchored the boat.
local: Na ba kpiɛ nzue n aba aju awɔrɛ n bo bu fere i ka Gɛnɛsarɛti n nu, na ba minda lee n ahɛmɛ.

eng: and came ashore. People immediately recognized Jesus.
local: Bu fiteeri lee n nu kpeŋga ni, na minɛm n asi ka Yesu lɛɛ.

eng: and ran around that whole region bringing sick people on their mats to wherever they heard he was.
local: Na ba ŋmati abara ayɛ miɛm n kɛrɛ su, na deke kɛrɛ bo bu tiiri i ŋga, bu sɔ bu tukpaki­fɔm ni bu dasum kɛrɛ ba i dɔ ɔ.

eng: Wherever he went—villages, cities, or farming communities—they would place the sick in the marketplaces and beg him to allow them to touch even the hem of his clothing. Everyone who touched him was healed.
local: Na nzɛn i kɔɔri ahɛmɛ miɛm wara miɛ kunnu, deke kɛrɛ bo i fa, bu fa bu tukpaki­fɔm ba i dɔ sɔ ɔ, na bu sere i jande ka i ma bu fa bu saa kan i tanlɛɛ n jaa bu. Na bo bu yoori sɔ n kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

eng: The Pharisees and some legal experts from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm nbem bo bu fite Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Farasi­fɔm n aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ Yesu dɔ.

eng: They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands. They were eating without first ritually purifying their hands through washing.
local: Na ba ŋu ka Yesu susu­fɔm n nbem sudi diire bu saa fin, ba ŋmisi má bu saa kabo bu nana sa­yoo­wa ti barasu ni.

eng: The Pharisees and all the Jews dont eat without first washing their hands carefully. This is a way of observing the rules handed down by the elders.
local: Dama Farasi­fɔm ni Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ la bu saa ŋmisi sa­yoo­wa bo i ti bu nana sa a. Bu nya ŋa bu di diire, bu du mɔ yo nyini sa n wɔ ka, na bu nya di.

eng: Upon returning from the marketplace, they dont eat without first immersing themselves. They observe many other rules that have been handed down, such as the washing of cups, jugs, pans, and sleeping mats.
local: Nzɛn haari bu fiteeri gɔ ba juuri awuru, bu di má neŋgem bo ba fa aba ni, má ka ba ŋmisi i ka, na bu yo bu nana sa­yoo­wa bo bu yo na bu di ni. Bu sa­yoo­wam sunnu. Bu nbem la ka bu ŋmisi awam ni dundum ni ayabam wɔ.

eng: So the Pharisees and legal experts asked Jesus, Why are your disciples not living according to the rules handed down by the elders but instead eat food with ritually unclean hands.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n abisa Yesu ka, Nzu ti ye u susu­fɔm n su má ya nam sa­yoo­wa n su, ye bu di diire bu saa fin a.

eng: He replied, Isaiah really knew what he was talking about when he prophesied about you hypocrites. He wrote, This people honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far away from me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo kaanri am jɔrɛ ka am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm n a. Wɔ kɛrɛ i kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, Ahin minɛm ni, bu ma m jirima bu nɔɔ nu bɛrɛ wɔ, ama, i wo má bu ahore su.

eng: Their worship of me is empty since they teach instructions that are human words.
local: Bu kansi m ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, dama bu kere minɛ adamande dɛɛ keree­wam wɔ, na bu se ka nyini keree­wa n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: You ignore Gods commandment while holding on to rules created by humans and handed down to you.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am acɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n ase buekun, na am nya susu adamande dɛɛ sa­yoo­wam su.

eng: Jesus continued, Clearly, you are experts at rejecting Gods commandment in order to establish these rules.
local: Na wɔ se bu bekun ka, Am la nahɔrɛ bo am akpiɛ mbɛnlɛ ayaki Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n na am asu am dɛɛ sa­yoo­wam su ni.

eng: Moses said, Honor your father and your mother, and The person who speaks against father or mother will certainly be put to death .
local: Dama Moyisi ase ka, Bu u si ni u ni. Na wɔ kaaki ase ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔɔri i si, ni i ni jɔrɛ tiɛ, wɔ yo daka ka bu kun i mi.

eng: But you say, If you tell your father or mother,Everything Im expected to contribute to you is corban .
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ kere ka nzɛn sɔnɔ be la deke be bo i ti ye ni ka i fa buka i si ni i ni, i koro se bu ka I ti Koroban wɔ. I bu la ka, Deke bo má m fa ma am ni, ma fa ayi saraka ama Nyɛmɛ.

eng: then you are no longer required to care for your father or mother.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, am akere ka i mi n koro buka má i si n ni i ni n lɛɛ.

eng: In this way you do away with Gods word in favor of the rules handed down to you, which you pass on to others. And you do a lot of other things just like that.
local: Nyini n kere ka am abu Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n ŋgbɛɛn lɛɛ, ye am su am dɛɛ sa­yoo­wam n bo am kere bu n su a. Na am la ahin sa­yoo­wam dɔŋgu bo am kaaki yo bu sɔ ɔ.

eng: Then Jesus called the crowd again and said, Listen to me, all of you, and understand.
local: Na Yesu akaaki afere jama n aba i bi bekun, na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɛrɛ so am sui na am ti jɔrɛ n bo m sujɔjɔ ma am n bu.

eng: Nothing outside of a person can enter and contaminate a person in Gods sight; rather, the things that come out of a person contaminate the person.
local: Deke bo i wura sɔnɔ nɔɔ nu koro yo má i mi fin, ama, deke bo i fite i mi ahore su yo i mi fin a. .

eng: After leaving the crowd, he entered a house where his disciples asked him about that riddle.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ba cinsi yakiiri jama n na i wuraari awuru n nu ni, na i susu­fɔm n abisa i nyini anyundɛrɛ n bu.

eng: He said to them, Dont you understand either? Dont you know that nothing from the outside that enters a person has the power to contaminate?.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ŋka ambɛrɛ gusu ati má jɔrɛ n bu lɛɛ? Ambɛrɛ si má ka deke bo i wura sɔnɔ nɔɔ nu koro yo má i fin.

eng: Thats because it doesnt enter into the heart but into the stomach, and it goes out into the sewer. By saying this, Jesus declared that no food could contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: dama deke bo sɔnɔ di, i wura má i ahore su se i wura i kunnu wɔ, na i kaaki je yi i, má sɔ? Yesu afa ahin jɔjɔɔ­wa n akere ka diire be la má ciire.

eng: Its what comes out of a person that contaminates someone in Gods sight, he said.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Deke bo i fite sɔnɔ ahore su, nyini yo i fin a.

eng: Its from the inside, from the human heart, that evil thoughts come: sexual sins, thefts, murders.
local: Dama deke bo i wo i ahore su ma i sunsunni tiɛ a. I koro ma i nati suyo sakaraya, na i suwɔ, na i sukun minɛ.

eng: adultery, greed, evil actions, deceit, unrestrained immorality, envy, insults, arrogance, and foolishness.
local: na i susaaki ajaa ciire, na i nyunu suboro neŋge kpini su, na i suyo satiɛtiɛm, na i sudadaka minɛm, na i suyo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn bo i cɛcɛ wo má nu, na i susi kɔrɛ, na i nati susaaki minɛm duma, na i la karambani, na i nati suyo sinnzinya sam.

eng: All these evil things come from the inside and contaminate a person in Gods sight.
local: Nyini satiɛ n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ nya fite sɔnɔ ahore su, nyini ma i mi kaaki fin­di­fɔ a.

eng: Jesus left that place and went into the region of Tyre. He didnt want anyone to know that he had entered a house, but he couldnt hide.
local: Na Yesu ayasu akɔ awɔrɛ n bo i wo dodo Tiro ni Sidon miɛ kpirim ni, na wɔ kɔ awura awuru be nu bɛnlɛ ka má sɔnɔ be ŋu i. Ama, wɔ koro afiɛ má i ŋu.

eng: In fact, a woman whose young daughter was possessed by an unclean spirit heard about him right away. She came and fell at his feet.
local: Na bara be ati i ŋga na wɔ ba ndende aba akutu i jaa bu. Ajini wo i wa baa­bara ŋu.

eng: The woman was Greek, Syrophoenician by birth. She begged Jesus to throw the demon out of her daughter.
local: Bara n ti má Yuda­fɔ, i ti Fonisiya­fɔ ɔ, bo i fite Siriya awɔrɛ n nu wɔ. Na wɔ sere Yesu jande ka i fɔn yi ajini n bo i wo i wa n ŋu ni.

eng: He responded, The children have to be fed first. It isnt right to take the childrens bread and toss it to the dogs.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Ma nbaatumam n du mɔ di na bu kunnu yi ka, dama i ti má ye ni ka a fa nbaatumam diire na a fa ma caam.

eng: But she answered, Lord, even the dogs under the table eat the childrens crumbs.
local: Ama, na bara n ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, a la nahɔrɛ, ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, caam n tambu nbaatumam n diire n bo bu di na i tɔtɔ asiɛŋgu n na bu fa di.

eng: Good answer! he said. Go on home. The demon has already left your daughter.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, U nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n dɛɛ ti, kɔ awuru. Ajini n afite ayaki u wa ni.

eng: When she returned to her house, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon gone.
local: Na wɔ kɔ awuru akɔ aŋu ka i wa n da i dasu su, ajini n afite ayaki i.

eng: After leaving the region of Tyre, Jesus went through Sidon toward the Galilee Sea through the region of the Ten Cities.
local: Na Yesu ayasu Tiro awɔrɛ n nu akɔ aju Sidon miɛ kpiri n nu, na wɔ fa bɛrɛ asin Miɛ Burum dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, aba aju Galili kunma kpiri n nɔɔ su.

eng: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly speak, and they begged him to place his hand on the man for healing.
local: Na minɛ nbem afa sɔnɔ be aba Yesu dɔ, i ti asuturi wɔ, na i koro jɔjɔ má nyunyumi. Na ba sere Yesu jande ka i fa i saa nana i ŋu na i nya laifiɛ.

eng: Jesus took him away from the crowd by himself and put his fingers in the mans ears. Then he spit and touched the mans tongue.
local: Na Yesu afa sɔnɔ n afite jama n nu, i ni yiri ŋgumi, na wɔ fa i saa marakam awura i suim nu, na wɔ yi nanzue agɔ i saa su afa akan sɔnɔ n taferema.

eng: Looking into heaven, Jesus sighed deeply and said, Ephphatha, which means, Open up.
local: na Yesu ayɛ i tii su aniɛ aŋgoro na wɔ ŋuundi, na wɔ se sɔnɔ n ka, Efata. Nyini la ka, Teke.

eng: At once, his ears opened, his twisted tongue was released, and he began to speak clearly.
local: Jina n nu bɛrɛ, na sɔnɔ n sui n ateke, na i taferema n gusu akaaki anyaŋgi nu, na wɔ koro ajɔjɔ nyunyumi.

eng: Jesus gave the people strict orders not to tell anyone. But the more he tried to silence them, the more eagerly they shared the news.
local: Na Yesu abɔbɔ bu ka má bu ma be ti ka wɔ tuntun sɔnɔ ni, ama, kabo i bɔbɔɔri bu barasu ni, bu boori ndoori n gusu sɔ ɔ bara yɛɛri a.

eng: People were overcome with wonder, saying, He does everything well! He even makes the deaf to hear and gives speech to those who cant speak.
local: Na minɛ ayo alekutura haari akɔ asin su, bu ŋa, I sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ kaba ti kpa a. I ma asuturim ti asui wɔ, na bobom jɔjɔ.

eng: In those days there was another large crowd with nothing to eat. Jesus called his disciples and told them.
local: Nyini saŋga n na jama beberebe aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ Yesu dɔ bekun, bu la má deke bo bu di, na wɔ fere i susu­fɔm ni, na wɔ se bu ka.

eng: I feel sorry for the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat.
local: Ahin jama n la m aŋunfɔ, dama i nyuma le­san lɛɛ bo bu wo m dɔ na bu la má deke bo bu di.

eng: If I send them away hungry to their homes, they wont have enough strength to travel, for some have come a long distance.
local: M koro yaki má bu na bu kɔ awuru ni ahɔɛ, má nyini, bu tɔ́ cin atin nu wɔ, dama bu nbem fite dede.

eng: His disciples responded, How can anyone get enough food in this wilderness to satisfy these people.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n ase i ka, Ye nifansu ye ya koro nya diire ahin boro n nu fɛn ma ahin minɛm n kɛrɛ na bu kunnu yi a.

eng: Jesus asked, How much bread do you have? They said, Seven loaves.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am la kpɔnɔ nyɛ wɔ? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Nso.

eng: He told the crowd to sit on the ground. He took the seven loaves, gave thanks, broke them apart, and gave them to his disciples to distribute; and they gave the bread to the crowd.
local: Na wɔ ma jamam n nɔɔ ka bu tana asiɛŋgu, na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ nso n na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi. Na wɔ kpiɛkpiɛ i nu ama i susu­fɔm n ka bu fa ma jama ni. Na bɛrɛ gusu afa akpakpaaki bu nu.

eng: They also had a few fish. He said a blessing over them, then gave them to the disciples to hand out also.
local: Bu la jombaa kan kan nbem gusu, na wɔ fa ada Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ fa ama i susu­fɔm n ka bu fa nyini n gusu ma jama ni.

eng: They ate until they were full. They collected seven baskets full of leftovers.
local: Bu diiri na bu kunnu ayi. Na i susu­fɔm n atambu sinsin kaa­wa n kandiɛ nso.

eng: This was a crowd of about four thousand people! Jesus sent them away.
local: Jama ni ju akpii nna dika. Na Yesu anya akaari bu na ba kɔ awuru.

eng: then got into a boat with his disciples and went over to the region of Dalmanutha.
local: Na i ni i susu­fɔm n awura lee akɔ Damanuta dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: The Pharisees showed up and began to argue with Jesus. To test him, they asked for a sign from heaven.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ nbem aba i dɔ, na ba se akuruwa ni Yesu. Bu koroori ka i yo alekutura sa be bo i kere ka i yiko n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, adena bu nya i ŋgɛɛ sɔɔ­biri.

eng: With an impatient sigh, Jesus said, Why does this generation look for a sign? I assure you that no sign will be given to it.
local: Na wɔ ŋuundi ni dimi i ahore su, na wɔ se ka, Nzu ti ye ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm koro ka m yo alekutura sa na am ŋu i a? M suse am nahɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ ka, m yó má alekutura sa ma má ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm fuu.

eng: Leaving them, he got back in the boat and crossed to the other side of the lake.
local: Na wɔ yasu ayaki bu, na wɔ wura lee n nu akpiɛ akɔ kunma n buekun dɔ.

eng: Jesus disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, so they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n wara afi diire sa, ba bita má pui, má ka kpɔnɔ kun n kpein woori lee n nu a.

eng: He gave them strict orders: Watch out and be on your guard for the yeast of the Pharisees as well as the yeast of Herod.
local: Na Yesu atu bu fɔ, i ŋa, Am si am ŋu sa ni Farasi­fɔm, ni Fɛmɛ Herode dɛɛ kpɔnɔ ayiri n bo bu fa gɔ kpɔnɔ n nu na i yasu ni.

eng: The disciples discussed this among themselves, He said this because we have no bread.
local: Na ba jɔjɔ bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ya la má kpɔnɔ n ti, ye i su se sɔ n a.

eng: Jesus knew what they were discussing and said, Why are you talking about the fact that you dont have any bread? Dont you grasp what has happened? Dont you understand? Are your hearts so resistant to what God is doing?.
local: Na Yesu afa nu kabo i susu­fɔm n sujɔjɔ bu ŋuŋu nu ni, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am sujɔjɔ ka am bita má kpɔnɔ a? Am nya si má deke bo m sujɔjɔ i ni, am nya ti má sa n bu? Am ahore­mbaa ti kekereke wanla.

eng: Dont you have eyes? Why cant you see? Dont you have ears? Why cant you hear? Dont you remember?.
local: I baari sɛ ye am la anyumbaa na am ŋu má asi a? Ye am la asui na am ti má? Am teŋge má su ka.

eng: When I broke five loaves of bread for those five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Twelve.
local: m yo faari kpɔnɔ nnu kpiɛkpiɛ maari minɛ akpii nnu? Ye bo i kaari ni, kandiɛ nyɛ ye am sasaari a? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Kandiɛ buru ni nnyɔ.

eng: And when I broke seven loaves of bread for those four thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you gather? They answered, Seven.
local: Na Yesu akaaki abisa bu bekun ka, Ye kpɔnɔ nso n bo m yo fa kpiɛkpiɛ maari minɛ akpii nna n di? Sinsin kandiɛ nyɛ ye am sasaari a? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Kandiɛ nso.

eng: Jesus said to them, And you still dont understand.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Haari ni kisa, am nya ti má i bu.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to Bethsaida. Some people brought a blind man to Jesus and begged him to touch and heal him.
local: Bu kɔ juuri Besayida miɛ n nu, na minɛ nbem afa anyinsin be aba Yesu dɔ, na ba sere i jande ka i fa i saa nana i ŋu na i nya laifiɛ.

eng: Taking the blind mans hand, Jesus led him out of the village. After spitting on his eyes and laying his hands on the man, he asked him, Do you see anything.
local: Na wɔ so sɔnɔ n saa nu afa i afite miɛ n nu, na wɔ yi nanzue agɔ i nyu­mbaa n nu. Na wɔ fa i saa anana i ŋu, na wɔ bisa i ka, A niɛ, a ŋu deke be e.

eng: The man looked up and said, I see people. They look like trees, only they are walking around.
local: Na anyinsin n ayɛ i tii su aniɛ, na wɔ se Yesu ka, M ŋu minɛm ka bu nati, ama, bu ti ka bakam wɔ.

eng: Then Jesus placed his hands on the mans eyes again. He looked with his eyes wide open, his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.
local: Na Yesu akaaki afa i saa anana i nyu­mbaa n su bekun, na anyinsin n aniɛ gbirim, na wɔ ŋu ka i nyu­mbaam n ateke, na wɔ ŋu deke kɛrɛ casi.

eng: Then Jesus sent him home, saying, Dont go into the village.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, To atin nu kɔ awuru, na má a se ka a fa miɛ n nu.

eng: Jesus and his disciples went into the villages near Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, Who do people say that I am.
local: Na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n ayasu ka bu kɔ Sizariya Filipu miɛ kan kanm su. Bu wo natiiri su ni, na wɔ bisa i susu­fɔm n ka, Minɛm fa m bu ŋma a.

eng: They told him, Some say John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others one of the prophets.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Bu ŋa wɔrɔ ti Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n a, nbem ŋa wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Eliya a, ye nbem gusu ŋa a ti dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nyiŋgam n kun wɔ.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, You are the Christ.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu bekun ka, Ye ambɛrɛ fa m bu ŋma a? Na Petoro abɔ i nu ka, Wɔrɔ ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone about him.
local: Na Yesu abɔbɔ bu ka má bu ma be si ka yiri ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a.

eng: Jesus continued, I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom arrive in power.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, am nbem wo nu fɛn bo am wú má, má ka am aba aŋu ka Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n aba ni yiko.

eng: Then Jesus began to teach his disciples: The Human One must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts, and be killed, and then, after three days, rise from the dead.
local: Nyini sin na wɔ bo i bu sukere i susu­fɔm n ka, Wɔ yo daka ka mini Adamande Wa n di wahara dɔŋgu, na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm kɛrɛ bá kete m ŋu na bu kún m. Ama, le­san cɛɛn sin na m téŋge m yiwee n nu.

eng: Six days later Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and brought them to the top of a very high mountain where they were alone. He was transformed in front of them.
local: Le­sɛn cɛɛn sin na Yesu afa Petoro ni Yakobo ni Yohane afun buka lenleŋge be su, bu ni yiri ŋgumi. Bu wo dɔ n na ba ŋu ka i ŋuniɛ akaaki wo.

eng: He said this plainly. But Peter took hold of Jesus and, scolding him, began to correct him.
local: Na wɔ kan sa n kɛrɛ bu kpain akere bu. Na Petoro afa i akɔ ahɛmɛ, na wɔ jaraki i ase i ka, Má a se ka a kan nyini jɔrɛ ni.

eng: and his clothes were amazingly bright, brighter than if they had been bleached white.
local: na i tanlɛɛ n akaaki fofoe papasi bo i ŋusu nya má durunya n nu.

eng: When Jesus and his followers approached Jerusalem, they came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives. Jesus gave two disciples a task.
local: Bu kɔ ŋa bu dodo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na bu ŋa bu wura miɛm bo bu fere bu ka Befage ni Betani n ni, nyini miɛm n dodo buka n bo bu fere i ka Oliva Buka n wɔ, dama oliva bakam gɔ su wɔ. Dɔ na Yesu asunma i susu­fɔm n nu nnyɔ.

eng: Jesus turned and looked at his disciples, then sternly corrected Peter: Get behind me, Satan. You are not thinking Gods thoughts but human thoughts.
local: Ama, na Yesu acɛ aniɛ i susu­fɔm ni, na wɔ tiɛn Petoro ŋu ase i ka, Sitana, fa cinsi m bi bɛrɛ. A sunsun má kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni. A sunsun kabo adamande koro n wɔ.

eng: Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
local: Dɔ na minɛ nnyɔ nbem atiɛ bu ŋu, na bu ni Yesu subo anzama. Bɛrɛ la Eliya ni Moyisi, dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni.

eng: saying to them, Go into the village over there. As soon as you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ miɛ kaan n bo i wo am nyunu dɔ ni, am kɔ ŋa am wura miɛ n nu, am ŋú ka kaako­baa be minda, be nya fun má i. Am nyaŋgi i bra.

eng: After calling the crowd together with his disciples, Jesus said to them, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross, and follow me.
local: Na Yesu afere jama n ni i susu­fɔm n na wɔ se bu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i su m su, wɔ yo daka ka i mi wara fi i tiɛŋgu sa koroo­wa sa, na i fa i yiwee baka jaanu n sɔ, na i su m su.

eng: Peter reacted to all of this by saying to Jesus, Rabbi, its good that were here. Lets make three shrines—one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
local: Na sɛrɛ ati Petoro ni susu­fɔ nyiŋgam ni, na Petoro ajimi agaya na i nɔɔ afinfin i. Nyini ti na wɔ se Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, i ti kpa ka ya wo fɛn. Ma ya bo nambue nsan, u dɛɛ kun, Moyisi dɛɛ kun, na Eliya gusu dɛɛ kun.

eng: If anyone says to you, Why are you doing this? say, Its master needs it, and he will send it back right away.
local: Nzɛn be bisaari am ka nzu ti ye am sunyaŋgi i a, am se i ka am Mibiɛ ni koro i a, ama, i má am kaaki fa i kpie i nyi ba kisa kisa.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me and because of the good news will save them.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i fa i ŋgɔɔ fiɛ, i mi bá fun i. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tɔɔri i ŋgɔɔ mini ni m dɛɛ labari kpa n dɛɛ ti, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Then a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice spoke from the cloud, This is my Son, whom I dearly love. Listen to him.
local: Na yinyiŋgu akata bu ŋu, na ba ti akonvi be ŋga yinyiŋgu n nu, na wɔ jɔjɔ bu dɔ. I ŋa, M wa koroo­wa lɛɛ. Am de i nɔaniɛ.

eng: They went and found a colt tied to a gate outside on the street, and they untied it.
local: Na ba kɔ, na ba kɔ aŋu ka kaako­baa n minda anɔ be nu atiŋgbere n su, na ba nyaŋgi i.

eng: Why would people gain the whole world but lose their lives?.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ nyaari durunya n nu deke n kɛrɛ na i fuunri i ŋgɔɔ, i kpɛrɛ la bɔnɔ?.

eng: Suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them except Jesus.
local: Bu tiiri jɔjɔɔ­wa n sɔ ni, kpeŋga na ba niɛniɛ abara ayɛ, ama, ba ŋu má sɔnɔ be kun, má ka Yesu ŋgumi wo bu dɔ a.

eng: Some people standing around said to them, What are you doing, untying the colt.
local: Na minɛ n bo bu jina dɔ n abisa bu ka, Am suyo nzu lɛɛ, am sunyaŋgi kaako­baa n kɔ ni wɔ.

eng: What will people give in exchange for their lives?.
local: Nzu deke ye sɔnɔ koro fa kaaki i ŋgɔɔ a?.

eng: As they were coming down the mountain, he ordered them not to tell anyone what they had seen until after the Human One had risen from the dead.
local: Na saŋga bo bu ba sujura buka n su ni, na Yesu abɔbɔ bu ka, Má am be se ka i bo sa n bo am aŋu i n su fa kere sɔnɔ be, haari fa kɔ saŋga bo mini Adamande Wa ni bá wu na m téŋge m yiwee n nu ka.

eng: They told them just what Jesus said, and they left them alone.
local: Na ba se minɛm n kabo Yesu seeri bu ni, na ba ma bu atin.

eng: Jesus spoke to them in parables. A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the winepress, and built a tower. Then he rented it to tenant farmers and took a trip.
local: Na Yesu abo bu miɛɛnum. I ŋa, Sɔnɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, na wɔ dɔ i baka suu­wa barasu be fie, na wɔ kanda abara ayɛ fie ni. Na wɔ lokoti yabuɛ bambaka be kunma, ka i sumiɛmiɛ baka suu­wam ni nzue gɔ nu. Na wɔ ta kpafiri lenleŋge be ka bu tana su suniɛ fie n kɛrɛ su, na wɔ nya afa fie n awura i npa­di­fɔm saa nu. Na wɔ kɔ atin.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words in this unfaithful and sinful generation, the Human One will be ashamed of that person when he comes in the Fathers glory with the holy angels.
local: Nyuma­fɔm ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ, bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ. Ambɛrɛ bo am wo bu nu, na nyinsɛ ti am fa kɔ mini ni m nɔaniɛ n dɛɛ ti ni, sɔ ye nyinsɛ tí mini Adamande Wa n gusu fa kɔ am su, saŋga bo m bá ba ni m Si Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa n nu, ni i mɛrɛkɛ casim ni.

eng: So they kept it to themselves, wondering, Whats this rising from the dead.
local: Na ba de i nɔaniɛ ni, ama, na ba bakati abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ye nzu la ka, i bá wu na i téŋge i yiwee n nu a.

eng: Jesus left that place and went beyond the Jordan and into the region of Judea. Crowds gathered around him again and, as usual, he taught them.
local: Yesu yasuuri Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu, na wɔ kɔ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na wɔ kpiɛ akɔ Yodan kunma n buekun dɔ fan, na jama beberebe ayandi aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ i dɔ. Na wɔ kere bu kabo saŋga kɛrɛ na i kere bu barasu ni.

eng: They brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes upon it, and he sat on it.
local: Na ba fa kaako­baa n aba Yesu dɔ, na ba fa bu suturam anana kaako­baa n ŋu, na Yesu afun atana su.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
local: Saŋga ba juuri bo i si ka baka suu­wam n abi ni, na wɔ sunma i akɔɔm kun i npa­di­fɔm n dɔ ka i kɔ de i dɛɛ bo ba nya i n bra.

eng: They asked Jesus, Why do the legal experts say that Elijah must come first.
local: Na ba bisa Yesu ka, Ye nzu ti ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n bita jɔjɔ ka Eliya dú mɔ ba ka, ye Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni bá ba n a.

eng: Some Pharisees came and, trying to test him, they asked, Does the Law allow a man to divorce his wife.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ nbem aba i dɔ ka bu sɔ i ŋgɛɛ fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ n jɔjɔɔ­wa su, na ba bisa i ka, Ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka sɔnɔ koro fa i yi ji i.

eng: But they grabbed the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
local: Akɔɔ n kɔ juuri ni, na ba tara i abo i, na ba fɔn i akpie i nyi i saa ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Many people spread out their clothes on the road while others spread branches cut from the fields.
local: Na minɛ beberebe afa bu suturam atɛrɛki ase atin n nu, na nbem gusu abubu nyawa asese atin n nu.

eng: He answered, Elijah does come first to restore all things. Why was it written that the Human One would suffer many things and be rejected?.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka Eliya dú mɔ ba na i bá sɛsɛ deke n kɛrɛ su. Bu kɛrɛɛri sɛ Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu fa kɔ Adamande Wa n dɔ a? Ba kɛrɛ ka i bá di wahara agaya, na minɛ bú i ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Jesus answered, What did Moses command you.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Nɔaniɛ maa­wa bɔnɔ ye Moyisi yo fa maari am a.

eng: Again the landowner sent another servant to them, but they struck him on the head and treated him disgracefully.
local: Na fie n fɔ n asunma i akɔɔ n kun bekun, na ba tara i abo i atiɛ i tii, na ba kpa i nyinyɛ.

eng: Those in front of him and those following were shouting, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord!.
local: Na bo bu du i nyunu n ni bo bu wo i sin n kɛrɛ subo kuyigindi, bu ŋa, Hosana . Ya kansi Nyɛmɛ. Tere­fiɛ wo yiri n bo i suba Micɛra n dɛɛ duma n nu n dɔ.

eng: In fact, I tell you that Elijah has come, but they did to him whatever they wanted, just as it was written about him.
local: Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, Eliya adu mɔ aba, na minɛm ayo i kabo bu koro, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu lɛɛ.

eng: They said, Moses allowed a man to write a divorce certificate and to divorce his wife.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Moyisi yo maari atin ka nzɛn sɔnɔ koro ka i fa i yi ji, i ti ye ni ka i mi ma bara n ajaa yaki kadasi ka, na i nya fa i ji.

eng: He sent another one; that one they killed. The landlord sent many other servants, but the tenants beat some and killed others.
local: Na wɔ kaaki asunma kun bekun, na ba kun yiri. Akɔɔ kɛrɛ bo fie n fɔ n nya sunmaari i a, a ŋu ka bu bo i wɔ, wara bu kun i wɔ.

eng: Blessings on the coming kingdom of our ancestor David! Hosanna in the highest.
local: Tere­fiɛ wo ya si Davidi fɛmɛya n bo i suba n dɔ. Ya kansi Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni.

eng: Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. After he looked around at everything, because it was already late in the evening, he returned to Bethany with the Twelve.
local: Na Yesu awura Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na wɔ kɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. I kɔ juuri dɔ na wɔ niɛ abara ayɛ deke n kɛrɛ. Nyini saŋga wiɛ atɔ, nyini ti, na i ni i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm n akpie bu nyi akɔ Betani.

eng: When Jesus, Peter, James, and John approached the other disciples, they saw a large crowd surrounding them and legal experts arguing with them.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔ nsanm ni kpieeri bu nyi ba juuri i susu­fɔ nyiŋgam bi ni, na ba ŋu ka jama beberebe atiɛn bu nɔɔ abara ayɛ bu. Ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n nbem bita bu ni akuruwa.

eng: Now the landowner had one son whom he loved dearly. He sent him last, thinking, They will respect my son.
local: Sɔnɔ kun n kpein be ba kaari a, yiri la i wa koroo­wa ni. Yiri ye i ba kaari sin sunmaari a, na wɔ se ka, Bu má m wa n yiri jirima.

eng: Jesus said to them, He wrote this commandment for you because of your unyielding hearts.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am ahore­mbaa kekereke n dɛɛ ti ye Moyisi yo kɛrɛɛri nyini nɔaniɛ maa­wa n maari am a.

eng: The next day, after leaving Bethany, Jesus was hungry.
local: Na i alecin na ba yasu Betani miɛ n nu sukɔ, na ahɔɛ ati Yesu.

eng: Suddenly the whole crowd caught sight of Jesus. They ran to greet him, overcome with excitement.
local: Jama n ŋuuri Yesu sɔ ni, na ba yo alekutura, na ba bo nu aŋmati akpa i atin ayo i ansɛ.

eng: But those tenant farmers said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
local: Ama, na nyini npa­di­fɔm n ase bu ŋuŋu nu ka, I wa n bo i dí i aja n lɛɛ suba n a. Am bra na ya kun i na aja n kɛrɛ ká ya dɛɛ.

eng: At the beginning of creation, God made them male and female .
local: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka fite durunya n bumboori, Nyɛmɛ yiiri biɛsɔ ni bara a.

eng: From far away, he noticed a fig tree in leaf, so he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing except leaves, since it wasnt the season for figs.
local: Na wɔ niɛ aŋu damba baka be ni i nyawa, ka i wo dede, na wɔ kɔ aniɛ ka i nyá suu­wa n be di. Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri baka n bu ni, wɔ ŋu má pui su, baka n jina ni nyawa ŋgumi wɔ, dama nyini saŋga n ti má damba su saŋga.

eng: Jesus asked them, What are you arguing about.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am se nzu akuruwa a.

eng: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
local: Dɔ na ba tara i, na ba kun i, na ba cin i afite fie n nu afa i atu.

eng: Because of this, a man should leave his father and mother and be joined together with his wife.
local: Na wɔ se ka, Nyini dɛɛ ti, i ti ye ni ka biɛsɔ n kpaaki i gisan yaki i si ni i ni, na i ni i yi saŋga wo.

eng: So he said to it, No one will ever again eat your fruit! His disciples heard this.
local: Na wɔ se baka n ka, Sin nyuma sin, sɔnɔ di má u suu­wa kun. I seeri sɔ n na i susu­fɔm n ati.

eng: Someone from the crowd responded, Teacher, I brought my son to you, since he has a spirit that doesnt allow him to speak.
local: Sɔnɔ be woori jama n nu na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ma fa m wa baa­biɛsɔ aba u dɔ ka a tuntun i ma m, dama ajini bobom wo i ŋu.

eng: So what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
local: Na Yesu anya ase bu ka, Am sunsun ka fie n fɔ ni yó sɛ wɔ? I bá kun nyini npa­di­fɔm n kɛrɛ wɔ, na i fá fie n wura minɛ fɔfɔrɛm saa nu.

eng: and the two will be one flesh. So they are no longer two but one flesh.
local: Nyini saŋga bu minɛ nnyɔ n ayo deke kun lɛɛ. Bu wo má bu ŋguŋgumi kun.

eng: They came into Jerusalem. After entering the temple, he threw out those who were selling and buying there. He pushed over the tables used for currency exchange and the chairs of those who sold doves.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm kɔ juuri Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na wɔ kɔ awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ fɔn neŋge tɔ­fɔm ni to­fɔm kɛrɛ afite awuru n nu, na wɔ kaaki ŋwaa kaaki­fɔm n teebirim n ni tiiwa tɔ­fɔm n biɛm kɛrɛ alanlaŋga.

eng: Wherever it overpowers him, it throws him into a fit. He foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and stiffens up. So I spoke to your disciples to see if they could throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo ajini n anya aba afa i, a ŋu ka wɔ fa i abo asiɛŋgu, na i nanzue sufite fufuku, na i sukpɛsɛ i jee, na i kese i ŋu. Ma sere u susu­fɔm n ka bu fɔn yi ajini ni, ama, ba kura má nu.

eng: Havent you read this scripture, The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
local: Na wɔ sa su ase ka, Ambɛrɛ ni am tii akan Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu aŋu i kabo ba kɛrɛ i barasu. Bu ŋa, Yabuɛ n bo bondo ta­fɔm n faari i tuuri ni, yiri ye wɔ kaaki bondo n jaa yabuɛm kɛrɛ tii a.

eng: Therefore, humans must not pull apart what God has put together.
local: Nyini ti, deke bo Nyɛmɛ anya asaŋga i awie, má sɔnɔ kpaaki i nu kun.

eng: He didnt allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
local: Wɔ soro má su na be sɔ sɔɔri nati sin Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu.

eng: Jesus answered them, You faithless generation, how long will I be with you? How long will I put up with you? Bring him to me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Amaa. Ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm, am la má yarada fieo. Nyuma sa ye m wo am dɔ fɛn a? Ye m yó sawɔrɛ ni ambɛrɛ kɔ ju sɛ wɔ? Am fa baa n ba ma m.

eng: The Lord has done this, and its amazing in our eyes? .
local: Micɛra ni yoori i sɔ a. Ya ŋu i, na wɔ yo ya alekutura.

eng: Inside the house, the disciples asked him again about this.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm ba juuri awuru ni, na ba bisa i nyini nɔaniɛ n bu.

eng: He taught them, Hasnt it been written, My house will be called a house of prayer for all nations? But youve turned it into a hideout for crooks .
local: Na wɔ kere minɛm ka, Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, M awuru ni, bu bá fere i ka awuru n bo bu bá sere Nyɛmɛ n nu fa ma durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ wɔ. Ama, ambɛrɛ afa i ayo fin­fɔm woo­biri.

eng: They brought him. When the spirit saw Jesus, it immediately threw the boy into a fit. He fell on the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.
local: Na ba fa baa ni aba i dɔ, na saŋga bo ajini n ŋuuri Yesu ni, kpeŋga na wɔ kpisi baa ni, na wɔ fa i abo asiɛŋgu, na wɔ da sukundu, na nanzue fufuku sufite i nɔɔ nu.

eng: They wanted to arrest Jesus because they knew that he had told the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n akpini atin kabo bu yo na bu tara i, dama bu faari nu ka miɛɛnu n bo i boori i ni, i niiri bɛrɛ wɔ, ama, bu soroori jama n dɛɛ ti, na ba yasu akɔ ayaki i.

eng: He said to them, Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i faari i yi jiiri, na i jaari bara fɔfɔrɛ, i mi n asaaki ajaa ciire ni i yi n bo wɔ fa i aji n lɛɛ.

eng: The chief priests and legal experts heard this and tried to find a way to destroy him. They regarded him as dangerous because the whole crowd was enthralled at his teaching.
local: Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n tiiri sa n bo Yesu yoori i ni, na ba kpini atin kabo bu yó na bu kún i, dama ba soro i. Deke bo i ti na bu soroori i n la ka, minɛ dɔŋgu tiiri i keree­wa n na ba yo alekutura.

eng: Jesus asked his father, How long has this been going on? He said, Since he was a child.
local: Na Yesu abisa i si n ka, Saŋga bɔnɔ ye tukpaki n taraari i a? Na si n abɔ i nu ka, I kaan nu kɛrɛ a.

eng: They sent some of the Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap him in his words.
local: Nyini sin na nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n asunma Farasi­fɔm ni Fɛmɛ Herode minɛ nbem na bu ba sɔ Yesu ŋgɛɛ na bu ti i nɔaniɛ.

eng: and if a wife divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.
local: Sasu bekun, bara kɛrɛ bo i yakiiri i hun ajaa, na i kɔ jaari biɛsɔ fɔfɔrɛ, nyini bara n gusu asaaki ajaa ciire lɛɛ.

eng: When it was evening, Jesus and his disciples went outside the city.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n naasɔ, na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n afite miɛ n nu.

eng: It has often thrown him into a fire or into water trying to kill him. If you can do anything, help us! Show us compassion.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo i nya ba yasu i ŋu, i fa i wura sin nu wɔ, wara nzue nu ka i kun i. Nyini ti, nzɛn a koro buka ya, si ya aŋunfɔ na a buka ya.

eng: They came to him and said, Teacher, we know that youre genuine and you dont worry about what people think. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is. Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay taxes or not.
local: Na ba ba ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ya si ka a ti nahɔrɛ se­fɔ ɔ, u cɛcɛ wo má kabo minɛ jɔjɔ nu, dama a niɛ má sɔnɔ jinaa­biri ti, ama, a kere minɛm wɔ nahɔrɛ su kabo Nyɛmɛ atin ti barasu. Tɔ, ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka ya tɔ lampo ma Roma­fɔm Fɛmɛ Kpiri Siza, nzɛn atin wo má nu.

eng: People were bringing children to Jesus so that he would bless them. But the disciples scolded them.
local: Minɛm sufa bu nbaam ba Yesu dɔ ka i fa i saa nana bu ŋu na i yo bu kisi, ama, na i susu­fɔm n atiɛn bu ŋu.

eng: Early in the morning, as Jesus and his disciples were walking along, they saw the fig tree withered from the root up.
local: Alecin ŋgarama bu ba susin sukɔ na ba ŋu ka damba baka n awu akɔ akpura haari i bunlui.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deceit, he said to them, Why are you testing me? Bring me a coin. Show it to me.
local: Ama, na Yesu asi ka bu ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm wɔ, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am sukoro ka am sɔ m ŋgɛɛ a? Am fa ŋwaa bra na m niɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, If you can do anything? All things are possible for the one who has faith.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nzu ti ye a ŋa nzɛn m koro buka u a? Deke be ti má kekereke ma má minɛm bo bu yo Nyɛmɛ yarada ni.

eng: Peter remembered and said to Jesus, Rabbi, look how the fig tree you cursed has dried up.
local: Na Petoro ateŋge su kabo Yesu seeri baka n ni, na wɔ se i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, niɛ. Damba baka n bo anuma na a jɔjɔ gɔɔri i ŋu ni, wɔ wu.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he grew angry and said to them, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Yesu ŋuuri i susu­fɔm sa­yoo­wa n sɔ ni, na yaa ati i, na wɔ se bu ka, Am ma nbaatumam n bra m dɔ, má am fɔn bu. Dama bɛrɛ ŋusu sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti bu dɛɛ a.

eng: And they brought one. He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this? Caesars, they replied.
local: Na ba fa ŋwaa n aba ama i. Na wɔ de ŋwaa n aniɛ, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Ŋma dɛɛ tii ni i duma wo ŋwaa n su n a? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Fɛmɛ Siza dɛɛ a.

eng: At that the boys father cried out, I have faith; help my lack of faith.
local: Kpeŋga na baa n si asa Yesu nɔɔ su ka, Ma yo yarada, ama, i sunnu má. Buka m na i sunnu.

eng: Jesus responded to them, Have faith in God!.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am yo Nyɛmɛ yarada.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a de má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu jɔrɛ kabo baatuma de deke i saa nnyɔ ni, a wúra má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: Jesus said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God. His reply left them overcome with wonder.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Deke bo i ti Fɛmɛ Siza dɛɛ, am fa ma Fɛmɛ Siza, na deke bo i ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ, am fa ma Nyɛmɛ. Na ba miɛ ni i nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Noticing that the crowd had surged together, Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, Mute and deaf spirit, I command you to come out of him and never enter him again.
local: Na Yesu aŋu ka jama n suŋmati ba i dɔ, na wɔ tiɛn ajini n ŋu, i ŋa, Wɔrɔ ajini n bo a ma baa n ayo bobo ni asuturi ni, mini suse u ka a fite yaki i a, na má a kpie u nyi ba i dɔ kun.

eng: I assure you that whoever says to this mountain, Be lifted up and thrown into the sea—and doesnt waver but believes that what is said will really happen—it will happen.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a se ahin buka n ka, Yasu kɔ to tieku n nu, na a yo má sika na a yo yarada ka deke n bo a seeri i ni, i yó, Nyɛmɛ yó i ma u.

eng: Then he hugged the children and blessed them.
local: Na wɔ kuku nbaatumam ni i saa nu, na wɔ fa i saa anana bu ŋu, na wɔ yo bu kisi.

eng: Sadducees, who deny that there is a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm asɛsɛ barasu nbem bo bu fere bu ka Sadusi­fɔm ni, aba Yesu dɔ. Bu dɛɛ keree­wa la ka Nyɛmɛ téŋge má minɛm bu yiwee n nu n a. Na ba ba abisa i jɔrɛ be, bu ŋa.

eng: After screaming and shaking the boy horribly, the spirit came out. The boy seemed to be dead; in fact, several people said that he had died.
local: Kpeŋga na wɔ ciindi na wɔ kpisi baa n agaya, na wɔ fite ayaki i, na baa n aka ada ka wɔ wu dɛɛ su. Na minɛm n dɔŋgu ase ka wɔ wu wɔ.

eng: Therefore I say to you, whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you will receive it, and it will be so for you.
local: Nyini ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, deke kɛrɛ bo am sere Nyɛmɛ ka am koro i, am yo yarada ka am anya i, Nyɛmɛ yó i ma am wɔ.

eng: As Jesus continued down the road, a man ran up, knelt before him, and asked, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu ayasu dɔ sukɔ. I wo kɔ n su ni, na sɔnɔ be aŋmati aba akutu i nyunu, na wɔ bisa i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ sɔnɔ kpa, m yó sɛ ye m bá nya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies , leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
local: Ya Kere­fɔ, Moyisi yo kɛrɛɛri nɔaniɛ be maari ya, ka nzɛn sɔnɔ wu yakiiri i yi, na i ni bara n anya má baa, wɔ yo daka ka i niɛma n fa kuna­fɔ n na bu wu nbaam ma gɔrɔ n bo wɔ wu ni.

eng: But Jesus took his hand, lifted him up, and he arose.
local: Ama, na Yesu atara i saa ayɛ i su, na wɔ jina.

eng: And whenever you stand up to pray, if you have something against anyone, forgive so that your Father in heaven may forgive you your wrongdoings.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ba susere Nyɛmɛ, na be ado am ŋgasi, am fa i mi ni ŋgasi ce i ka, adena am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n gusu fá am dɛɛ ce am. .

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Nzu ti ye a fere m ka sɔnɔ kpa a? Be ti má kpa sin Nyɛmɛ ŋu.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman; when he died, he left no children.
local: Tɔ, sɔnɔ be yo laari nbaa­biɛsɔm nso, na baa­kpiɛn n aja bara, na wɔ wu ayaki bara ni, ba nya má baa.

eng: After Jesus went into a house, his disciples asked him privately, Why couldnt we throw this spirit out.
local: Yesu ba wuraari awuru n nu ni, na i susu­fɔm n aba i dɔ, bɛrɛ ŋgumi, abisa i ka, Nzu ti ye yɛrɛ akoro afɔn má nyini ajini n a.

eng: Jesus and his disciples entered Jerusalem again. As Jesus was walking around the temple, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders came to him.
local: Bu fa ba juuri Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu bekun, na Yesu awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na i sunati bara yɛ i. I wo dɔ sɔ ɔ n na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n aba i dɔ.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit murder. Dont commit adultery. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony . Dont cheat. Honor your father and mother .
local: Ye m ŋa a si Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni e, bu ŋa, Má a kun sɔnɔ. Má a saaki ajaa ciire. Má a wɔ. Má a di kɔrɛ daani. Má a sisi sɔnɔ. Bu u si ni u ni.

eng: The second married her and died without leaving any children. The third did the same.
local: Na bo i tɔ i su n afa kuna­fɔ n aja, na yiri gusu awu ayaki bara ni, ba nya má baa. Na bo i tɔ i su nsan su n gusu afa i aja bekun.

eng: Jesus answered, Throwing this kind of spirit out requires prayer.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Be koro fɔn má nyini ajini n ŋusu sɔ, má ka nyɛmɛsere.

eng: They asked, What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority to do them.
local: na ba bisa i ka, Ŋma dɛɛ yiko nu ye a yo nyini sam kɛrɛ a? Ŋma ma u nyini yiko n ye a yo nyini sam n a.

eng: Teacher, he responded, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Na sɔnɔ n abɔ i nu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ma su nyini nɔaniɛ maa­wa n kɛrɛ su haari m kaan nu.

eng: None of the seven left any children. Finally, the woman died.
local: I yo sɔ ɔ, na bu nso n kɛrɛ aja bara n abara ayɛ, na bu kɛrɛ awu ayaki i, ba nya má baa, na bara n gusu aba aka sin awu.

eng: From there Jesus and his followers went through Galilee, but he didnt want anyone to know it.
local: Na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n ayasu bɛrɛ akɔ asin Galili awɔrɛ n nu. Wɔ koro má ka be si i woo­biri.

eng: Jesus looked at him carefully and loved him. He said, You are lacking one thing. Go, sell what you own, and give the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Na Yesu aniɛ sɔnɔ n na wɔ koro i agaya, na wɔ se i ka, Deke kun ye wɔ kpiɛ u su a. Kɔ na a kɔ tɔ u aja kɛrɛ, na a fa ŋwaa n ma yaari­fɔm, na a bá nya ajɛkɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na a bra ba su m su.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have a question for you. Give me an answer, then Ill tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am ma m du mɔ bisa am bisaa­wa kun be ka, na nzɛn am bɔɔri m nu, na m gusu nyá se am deke bo m yiko n fite.

eng: At the resurrection, when they all rise up, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Kabo bu minɛ nso n kɛrɛ aja bara n na ba wu sɔ ni, cɛɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge minɛm bu yiwee n nu ni, bara ni ká ŋma dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: This was because he was teaching his disciples, The Human One will be delivered into human hands. They will kill him. Three days after he is killed he will rise up.
local: dama i sukere i susu­fɔm wɔ. Na wɔ se bu ka, Bu bá tɔ mini Adamande Wa n ma minɛ nbem na bu kún m, ama, bu kuunri m wieeri, i le­san cɛɛn sin, na m téŋge m yiwee n nu.

eng: But the man was dismayed at this statement and went away saddened, because he had many possessions.
local: Yesu seeri i sɔ n na i ŋuniɛ ayɔrɔki i. Na wɔ kɔ awuru ni ahoresaaki, dama i la neŋge agaya n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin? Answer me.
local: Ŋma maari Yohane atin ye i ba biɛɛri minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue n a? Nyɛmɛ nzɛn adamande? Am bɔ m nu.

eng: Jesus said to them, Isnt this the reason you are wrong, because you dont know either the scriptures or Gods power?.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am amini atin. Deke bo i ti na am amini atin n la ka, am ti má deke bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n bu, na am kaaki si má kabo i yiko n ti barasu.

eng: But they didnt understand this kind of talk, and they were afraid to ask him.
local: I jɔjɔɔri sɔ ni, i susu­fɔm n ati má i bu, ama, sɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, ba koro abisa má i jɔrɛ be.

eng: Looking around, Jesus said to his disciples, It will be very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Na Yesu aniɛ i susu­fɔm n abara ayɛ, na wɔ se bu ka, I yó kekereke bo neŋge­fɔ wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: They argued among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Then why didnt you believe him.
local: Na ba se akuruwa bu ŋuŋu nu, kabo bu bɔ Yesu nu barasu. Bu ŋa, Nzɛn ya seeri i ka Nyɛmɛ maari i atin a, i bísa ya ka, ye nzu ti ye ya da má Yohane ŋu a?.

eng: When people rise from the dead, they wont marry nor will they be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like Gods angels.
local: Dama saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ má minɛm teŋge bu yiwee n nu ni, ajaa wo má bɛrɛ kun. Ama, nyini cɛɛn ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ káaki ka Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm bo bu wo aŋgoro n wɔ.

eng: They entered Capernaum. When they had come into a house, he asked them, What were you arguing about during the journey.
local: Na ba kɔ aju Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu, na saŋga bo bu wo awuru n nu ni, na Yesu abisa i susu­fɔm n ka, Am wo atin n nu suba ni, am se nzu akuruwa a.

eng: His words startled the disciples, so Jesus told them again, Children, its difficult to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n ayo alekutura ni nyini jɔjɔɔ­wa ni. Ama, na Yesu ase bu bekun ka, Njam, i ti kekereke ka neŋge­fɔm wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: But we cant say, Its of earthly origin. They said this because they were afraid of the crowd, because they all thought John was a prophet.
local: Ama, nzɛn ya se i ka adamande dɔ? Dɔ gusu na ba soro jama ni. Má pui ti, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ soroori su ka Yohane yo ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: As for the resurrection from the dead, havent you read in the scroll from Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God said to Moses, I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?.
local: Na fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge minɛm bu yiwee n nu ni, am kaanri Moyisi dɛɛ kadasi ni, nzɛn am akan má i? Kabo i kɛrɛɛri fa kɔ saŋga bo sin n jeeri buwe baka n su n na Nyɛmɛ jɔjɔɔri i dɔ ni, i ŋa, Mini ti Abraham, ni Izaki, ni Jakɔbu dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ n a.

eng: They didnt respond, since on the way they had been debating with each other about who was the greatest.
local: Ama, na bu kɛrɛ atɔ amuna, dama bu wo atin n nu dɔ ni, na ba se akuruwa bu ŋuŋu nu fa kɔ sɔnɔ bo i tara bu kɛrɛ bu nu.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Kabo nyɔɔma sin misini bue nu ni, i ti má kekereke tara má kabo neŋge­fɔ yo na i wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu ni.

eng: They answered Jesus, We dont know. Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Nyini ti, na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ya si má. Na yiri gusu ase bu ka, Mini mmɔɔ sé má am sɔnɔ bo i dɛɛ yiko nu na m yo ahin sam n kɛrɛ ni.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. You are seriously mistaken.
local: Na Yesu asa su ase bu ka, Kabo Nyɛmɛ ŋa i ti nyini minɛm n dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ ni, nyini kere ka bu toko la ŋgɔɔ lɛɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ ti má bo ba wu n dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ, ama, i ti bo bu la ŋgɔɔ n dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ a. Nyini ti, kabo am ŋa Nyɛmɛ téŋge má minɛm bu yiwee n nu, am amini fieo.

eng: He sat down, called the Twelve, and said to them, Whoever wants to be first must be least of all and the servant of all.
local: Na wɔ tana, na wɔ fere bɛrɛ i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i kaaki mɔ­fɔ, wɔ yo daka ka i mi yo i ŋu kaan tara am kɛrɛ na i kaaki yo am kɛrɛ dɛɛ amusui­su­fɔ.

eng: They were shocked even more and said to each other, Then who can be saved.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n amiɛ akɔ asin su, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ye nzɛn i ti sɔ e, ŋma koro fite nu a.

eng: One of the legal experts heard their dispute and saw how well Jesus answered them. He came over and asked him, Which commandment is the most important of all.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm kun aŋu kabo Sadusi­fɔm ni Yesu seeri bu beŋgu akuruwa ni, i tiiri kabo Yesu bɔɔri bu nu nyunyumi sɔ ni, na wɔ ba abisa i ka, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, bɔnɔ tara bu kɛrɛ a.

eng: Jesus reached for a little child, placed him among the Twelve, and embraced him. Then he said.
local: Na wɔ fa baa kaan be aba ajina bu afiɛn, na wɔ biti i, na wɔ se bu ka.

eng: Jesus looked at them carefully and said, Its impossible with human beings, but not with God. All things are possible for God.
local: Na Yesu aniɛ bu dɔ na wɔ se ka, I ti deke bo adamande koro yo má i, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i ti má pui, dama Nyɛmɛ koro yo deke kɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, The most important one is Israel, listen! Our God is the one Lord.
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Nɔaniɛ n bo i tara bu kɛrɛ n yaa, ba kɛrɛ ka, Ambɛrɛ Yisareli­fɔm, am ti fɛn, ya Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n ti kun wɔ.

eng: Whoever welcomes one of these children in my name welcomes me; and whoever welcomes me isnt actually welcoming me but rather the one who sent me.
local: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo ade ahin baa­kan n ŋusu m dɛɛ ti, a deeri mini wɔ lɛɛ. Bekun, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo a nya deeri mini, a de m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i sunmaari m wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Peter said to him, Look, weve left everything and followed you.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, Tɔ, ye yɛrɛ ayaki deke kɛrɛ susu u su e.

eng: and you must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your mind, and with all your strength.
local: Koro Micɛra u Nyɛmɛ ni, ni u ahore­mbaa kun, ni u awiɛwiɛ, ni u sunsunni, ni u faŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: John said to Jesus, Teacher, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he wasnt following us.
local: Na Yohane ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ya ŋuuri ka sɔnɔ be afa u duma sufa fɔn yi ajinim fite minɛm ŋu, ye ya ŋa ya yaki má i, dama i wo má ya nu n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Jesus said, I assure you that anyone who has left house, brothers, sisters, mother, father, children, or farms because of me and because of the good news.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yakiiri i awuru, wara i ninbaam, wara i ni, wara i si, wara i nbaam, wara i asiɛn, mini ni m labari kpa n dɛɛ ti.

eng: The second is this, You will love your neighbor as yourself . No other commandment is greater than these.
local: Bo i tɔ su nnyɔ n la ka o, Koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni. Nɔaniɛ maa­wa be wo má bɛrɛ bo i tara ahin nnyɔm ni.

eng: As Jesus left the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look! What awesome stones and buildings.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu sufite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni, na i susu­fɔm n nu kun be aba ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, niɛ nyini yabuɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim ni. Niɛ kabo bu ti a. Niɛ nyini awuru bambaka ni. Niɛ kabo ba ta i barasu a.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont stop him. No one who does powerful acts in my name can quickly turn around and curse me.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Má am se ka am kanda i, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nya sufa m duma n yo alekutura sam, i mi bá koro kaaki jɔjɔ má jɔrɛ tiɛ fa kɔ m dɔ.

eng: will receive one hundred times as much now in this life—houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and farms —and in the coming age, eternal life.
local: i mi nyá nyinim sɔ ŋusu yaa i ŋgɔɔ su. I nyá awurum, ni ninbaam, ni nim, ni nbaam, ni asiɛnm, na minɛm má i di wahara, na fa kɔ fa ba i nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri.

eng: The legal expert said to him, Well said, Teacher. You have truthfully said that God is one and there is no other besides him.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ n ase Yesu ka, Aŋgba. Ya Kere­fɔ, i nahɔrɛ atin su lɛɛ ye a kan i n a. Ya Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n ti kun wɔ. Nyɛmɛ be wo má bɛrɛ sin i ŋu.

eng: Jesus responded, Do you see these enormous buildings? Not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, M ŋa a ŋu nyini ataari ŋgbiŋgbirim n e? Yabuɛ kun n kpein n mmɔɔ bá nana má i beŋgu su. Bu bá bereki i kɛrɛ gɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: But many who are first will be last. And many who are last will be first.
local: Ama, minɛ dɔŋgu bo bu wo mɔ kisa ni bá ka sin, na bo bu ka sin kisa n bá du mɔ.

eng: Whoever isnt against us is for us.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i fite má ya sin, i mi jina ya sin wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: And to love God with all of the heart, a full understanding, and all of ones strength, and to love ones neighbor as oneself is much more important than all kinds of entirely burned offerings and sacrifices.
local: Wɔ kaaki ayo daka ka ya koro Nyɛmɛ ni ya ahore­mbaa kun, ni ya sunsunni, ni ya faŋga kɛrɛ, na ya kaaki koro ya beŋgu kabo ya koro ya ŋu ni, nyini nnyɔm n ti ye tara bo ya yo tiɛm, ni bo ya yi sarakam ma Nyɛmɛ ni.

eng: Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately.
local: Na Yesu akɔ afun atana Oliva Buka n su suniɛ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni. Na saŋga bo yiri ni Petoro ni Yakobo ni Yohane ni Andere woori bu ŋgumi ni, na ba bisa i ka.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, with Jesus in the lead. The disciples were amazed while the others following behind were afraid. Taking the Twelve aside again, he told them what was about to happen to him.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm sukɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na Yesu adu bu mɔ sunati, na i susu­fɔm n ajimi, na sɛrɛ ati minɛm bo bu suuri i su ni, na wɔ kaaki afere i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm n akɔ ahɛmɛ, na wɔ jɔjɔ bu dɔ bekun fa kɔ sa n bo i bá tu i ni.

eng: I assure you that whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will certainly be rewarded.
local: M suse am nahɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ, ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i maari am nzue ka am nu dama am su mini dɛɛ ti, i mi nyá i ŋkatɔ.

eng: When Jesus saw that he had answered with wisdom, he said to him, You arent far from Gods kingdom. After that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
local: Na Yesu aŋu ka sɔnɔ n abɔ i nu kpa, na wɔ se i ka, A waari má ni Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya ni. Nyini sin, be abo má su ka i bisa Yesu jɔrɛ bekun.

eng: Tell us, when will these things happen? What sign will show that all these things are about to come to an end.
local: Ya Kere­fɔ, se ya, saŋga bɔnɔ ye ahin sam n kɛrɛ bá yo a? Ye nzu deke bá kere ya ka saŋga n adodo bo nyini sam n kɛrɛ bá yo a.

eng: Look! he said. Were going up to Jerusalem. The Human One will be handed over to the chief priests and the legal experts. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: I ŋa, Am ti fɛn. Ya sukɔ Yɛrusalem lɛɛ, dɔ ye bu kɔ́ tara mini Adamande Wa n a, na bu fá m ma Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, na bu dí m jɔrɛ, na bu sóro su ka bu kún m. Na bɛrɛ gusu nyá fa m wura minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm saa nu.

eng: As for whoever causes these little ones who believe in me to trip and fall into sin, it would be better for them to have a huge stone hung around their necks and to be thrown into the lake.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo u sa­yoo­wa ma ahin nbaatuma kan kanm n be bo i yo m yarada n be wuraari satiɛ nu, i ti má kpa. Nzɛn má bu faari ŋgani san yabuɛ bambaka ciciiri u kɔm, na bu fa u kɔ tu tieku nu, i ti ye tara bo a ma ahin nbaatuma kan kanm n be wura satiɛ nu ni.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, Why do the legal experts say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu woori Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu sukere Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na wɔ bisa minɛm jɔrɛ be fa kɔ Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n su. I ŋa, Nzu ti ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n bita jɔjɔ ka Kristo n ti Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma a?.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that no one deceives you.
local: Na Yesu anya ase bu ka, Am si am ŋu sa na má am ma be dadaka am.

eng: They will ridicule him, spit on him, torture him, and kill him. After three days, he will rise up.
local: na bu yó m anzasi, na bu yí nanzue gɔ m ŋu, na bu fín m ŋgbere, na bu nyá kun m, na i le­san cɛɛn sin, na m téŋge m yiwee n nu.

eng: If your hand causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter into life crippled than to go away with two hands into the fire of hell, which cant be put out.
local: Nzɛn u saa n be cin u wura satiɛ nu a, kaba kpiɛ i na a ka asaasin, na a nyá u ŋu kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. I ti ye ni bo a la u saa nnyɔ n kɛrɛ na a wura cɛɛma sin n bo i nɔ má n nu.

eng: David himself, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side until I turn your enemies into your footstool.
local: Dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n yo wuraari Fɛmɛ Davidi ŋu ni, na yiri ni i tii ase ka, Na Micɛra ase m Mibiɛ n ka, Ba tana m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su fɛn, haari m bá ma u kpɔ­fɔm kɛrɛ ba kutu u jaa bu.

eng: Many people will come in my name, saying, Im the one! They will deceive many people.
local: Dama minɛ dɔŋgu bá fa m duma n ba, na bu sé ka, Yiri la mini n a. Na bu dádaka minɛ beberebe.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, came to Jesus and said, Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask.
local: Na Zɛbɛdi nbaam Yakobo ni Yohane aba Yesu dɔ aba ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ya koro ka deke kɛrɛ bo ya sere u, a yo i ma ya.

eng: If your foot causes you to fall into sin, chop it off. Its better for you to enter life lame than to be thrown into hell with two feet.
local: Nzɛn u jaa n be cin u wura satiɛ nu a, kaba kpiɛ i na a ka ajaasin, na a nyá u ŋu kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. I ti ye ni bo a la u jaa nnyɔ n kɛrɛ na bu ba fa u tu cɛɛma sin nu.

eng: David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be Davids son? The large crowd listened to him with delight.
local: Tɔ, Fɛmɛ Davidi mmɔɔ ni i tii fereeri Kristo n ka i Mibiɛ wɔ, ye Kristo n yo sɛ kaaki yo Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma a? Ye jama beberebe n jina tiiri Yesu nɔaniɛ n ni ahorejɔ a.

eng: When you hear of wars and reports of wars, dont be alarmed. These things must happen, but this isnt the end yet.
local: Saŋga bo am bá ti aluwa ŋga fɛn, wara bu sukun luɛ dɔ, má am ma am ahore saaki. Wɔ yo daka ka nyini n kɛrɛ bá yo ka, ama, má am niɛ ka durunya n awieeri lɛɛ.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? he asked.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am koro ka m yo nzu ma am a.

eng: If your eye causes you to fall into sin, tear it out. Its better for you to enter Gods kingdom with one eye than to be thrown into hell with two.
local: Nzɛn u nyu­mbaa kun gusu cin u wura satiɛ nu a, lokoti yi i tu, na a ka anyunu kun, na a wúra Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu. I ti ye ni bo a la u nyu­mbaa n nnyɔ n kɛrɛ na bu ba fa u tu cɛɛma sin nu.

eng: As he was teaching, he said, Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They want to be greeted with honor in the markets.
local: I woori bu kere n su ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Am si am ŋu sa ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, deke bo bu koro i n la ka, bu wura sutura ŋgbiŋgbirim wɔ, na bu nati sujeŋgbe, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ suyo bu ansɛ gɔɔsu.

eng: Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other, and there will be earthquakes and famines in all sorts of places. These things are just the beginning of the sufferings associated with the end.
local: Daanfiɛ daanfiɛ bá kun luɛ ni bu beŋgum, na nfɛmɛm kún luɛ ni bu beŋgu nfɛmɛm. Na asiɛn bá kpisi awɔrɛ awɔrɛm nu, na ahɔɛ tɔ́. Nyini n kɛrɛ bá yo kabo bara kutu cɛɛn bumboori dɛɛ wahara n ti n wɔ.

eng: They said, Allow one of us to sit on your right and the other on your left when you enter your glory.
local: Na ba se i ka, Saŋga bo a bá tana u jirima tanaa­biri ni, ya koro ka a ma ya kun tana u saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, na kun gusu tana u saa biɛ su.

eng: Thats a place where worms dont die and the fire never goes out .
local: Dɔ dɛɛ kakabam ni bo bu wura minɛ bo ba wu ni, bɛrɛ wu má cɛɛn be, na sin n gusu bo i je nɔ má.

eng: Watch out for yourselves. People will hand you over to the councils. You will be beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me so that you can testify before them.
local: Am si am ŋu sa, dama bu bá fa am wura jɔrɛ­di­fɔm saa nu, na bu bó am bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu. Mini dɛɛ ti, na bu dí am jɔrɛ gominam ni nfɛmɛm nyunu. Nyini má am atin, na am di m daani kere bu a.

eng: They long for places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Nyini minɛm n anya awura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu ni, wara ba nya afere bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ bambaka diire dii­birim ni, bu koro ka bu tana kpirikoro tanaa­birim wɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont know what youre asking! Can you drink the cup I drink or receive the baptism I receive.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am si má deke n bo am susere i n ti a. Am kóro di wahara n bo m dí i ni, am káaki koro wu yiwee n bo m wú i ni, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: Everyone will be salted with fire.
local: Kabo bu fa njin nyina saraka neŋge kɛrɛ su fa ma Nyɛmɛ, na i yo casi barasu ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ cín sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sui na i yó casi.

eng: First, the good news must be proclaimed to all the nations.
local: Ama, wɔ yo daka ka daanfiɛ kɛrɛ du mɔ ti labari kpa n ka, ye durunya n awieeri bá ju a.

eng: We can, they answered. Jesus said, You will drink the cup I drink and receive the baptism I receive.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ɛɛn, ya kóro yo i kɛrɛ. Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ. Am dí wahara n bo m dí i ni, na am káaki wu yiwee n bo m wú i ni.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Nyini minɛm n sɔ fin nkuna­fɔm neŋgem wɔ, na bu jina sere Nyɛmɛ cɛ, adena minɛm ŋu ka bu ti minɛ kpam wɔ. Jina cɛɛn, bɛrɛ bá nya sui­cin tara minɛm kɛrɛ.

eng: Salt is good; but if salt loses its saltiness, how will it become salty again? Maintain salt among yourselves and keep peace with each other.
local: Ya si ka njin yo fiɛ, ama, nzɛn i fiɛfiɛ nya ba saari wieeri, i koro yo má fiɛfiɛ kun. Am ma am wo yo fiɛ ka njin am beŋgu nu, na am wo ni tanalaifiɛ.

eng: When they haul you in and hand you over, dont worry ahead of time about what to answer or say. Instead, say whatever is given to you at that moment, for you arent doing the speaking but the Holy Spirit is.
local: Saŋga n bo bu bá faari am kɔɔri jinaa­biri, na bu kɔ di am jɔrɛ ni, má am ma am ahore saaki fa kɔ kabo am kɔ́ jɔjɔ barasu. Nɔaniɛ kɛrɛ bo i tɔ́ am nɔɔ nyini saŋga ni, am jɔjɔ i, dama i fíte Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ ɔ, i fíte má am dɔ.

eng: but to sit at my right or left hand isnt mine to give. It belongs to those for whom it has been prepared.
local: Ama, mini la má atin bo m má am be tana m fɔmbɔrɔ su, na be tana m biɛ su. Ama, Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ nyini tanaa­biri n su, afa ase ama minɛm bo yiri ni i tii ayi bu n a.

eng: Jesus sat across from the collection box for the temple treasury and observed how the crowd gave their money. Many rich people were throwing in lots of money.
local: Na Yesu akɔ atana adodo alaka bo minɛm yi bu saraka ŋwaa wura nu ni, na i suniɛ kabo minɛm yi saraka barasu. Neŋge­fɔm dɔŋgu yiiri ŋwaa beberebe wuraari saraka n nu.

eng: Brothers and sisters will hand each other over to death. A father will turn in his children. Children will rise up against their parents and have them executed.
local: Gɔrɔm ni niɛmam bá tɔ bu beŋgu ma bu kpɔ­fɔm na bu kún bu, na sim tɔ́ bu nbaam, na nbaam kéte bu wu­fɔm ŋu, na bu má minɛm kun bu wu­fɔm ni.

eng: Now when the other ten disciples heard about this, they became angry with James and John.
local: Saŋga bo i susu­fɔ burum ni tiiri kabo Yakobo ni Yohane n seeri Yesu ni, na ba fa yaa bu ŋu.

eng: One poor widow came forward and put in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Na kuna­fɔ yaari­fɔ be gusu aba ayi ŋwaa kaan be bo i koro to má pui awura saraka n nu.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name. But whoever stands firm until the end will be saved.
local: Ama, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a mindi deeri nyini n kɛrɛ kɔ ju i awieeri, Nyɛmɛ dé u tii ji.
