eng: Jesus called them over and said, You know that the ones who are considered the rulers by the Gentiles show off their authority over them and their high-ranking officials order them around.
local: Na Yesu afere bu kɛrɛ aba i bi, na wɔ se bu ka, Am si tenle su ka minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n na bu yo bu ŋu nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, bɛrɛ miɛ bu minɛm a, na bu tere­tere­fɔm gusu kere kabo bu yiko ti barasu.

eng: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than everyone whos been putting money in the treasury.
local: Dɔ na Yesu afere i susu­fɔm n na wɔ se bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, nyini kuna­fɔ n ayi ŋwaa atara minɛm n kɛrɛ.

eng: When you see the disgusting and destructive thing standing where it shouldnt be , then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Am bá ŋu anyumbaa ciire sa be bo Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ kpɔ i, ka bu má i jina Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, deke bo wɔ yo má daka ka i jina, na i sáaki i ni. Ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kan ahin kadasi n ti i bu nyunyumi. Nyini saŋga ni, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a tiiri i ŋga, na a wo Yudiya asiɛn n su, ŋmati kɔ bukam n su.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Whoever wants to be great among you will be your servant.
local: Ama, wɔ ti má ye ni ka ambɛrɛ yo sɔ. Nzɛn sɔnɔ be koro ka i wura i ŋu kpiri am kɛrɛ nu, i ti ye ni ka i mi yo i ŋu kaan, na i su am.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had, even what she needed to live on.
local: Dama neŋge­fɔm la ŋwaa beberebe n dɛɛ ti, ye ba yi be a. Ama, nyini kuna­fɔ n ni i yaari n kɛrɛ, i deke kɛrɛ bo i la i lɛɛ, ye wɔ yi i n a.

eng: Those on the roof shouldnt come down or enter their houses to grab anything.
local: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a wo kpata nu, má a wura gisan n nu fa pui.

eng: Whoever wants to be first among you will be the slave of all.
local: Na bo i kaaki koro ka i yo am mɔ­fɔ, i ti ye ni ka i mi yo am kɛrɛ akɔɔ.

eng: Those in the field shouldnt come back to grab their clothes.
local: Na wɔrɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo a wo fiesu, má a kpie u nyi kɔ awuru kɔ fa u sutura be.

eng: for the Human One didnt come to be served but rather to serve and to give his life to liberate many people.
local: Dama mini Adamande Wa n ni m tii aba má ka minɛm su m wɔ, ama, m baari ka m ba su minɛm wɔ, na m wu ma bu, na m de minɛm dɔŋgu tii ji.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant and for women who are nursing their children.
local: Nyini saŋga ni yó kekere agaya ma nyinsɛ­fɔm, ni nbaram bo bu nbaam toko nu ni.

eng: Jesus and his followers came into Jericho. As Jesus was leaving Jericho, together with his disciples and a sizable crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, Timaeus son, was sitting beside the road.
local: Na ba kɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Yeriko ni. Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm, ni jama beberebe n sufite miɛ n nu ni, anyinsin be tana atin n nɔɔ wo sere su. Bu fere i ka Batimeyo, Timeyo dɛɛ wa.

eng: When he heard that Jesus of Nazareth was there, he began to shout, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: I tiiri ka Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ ni susin ni, na wɔ tiɛn afere i, i ŋa, Yesu, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma, si m aŋunfɔ.

eng: Pray that it doesnt happen in winter.
local: Am sere Nyɛmɛ ka má nyini sa n yó buke saŋga.

eng: In those days there will be great suffering such as the world has never before seen and will never again see.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, wahara bo minɛ dí i, i tára wahara kɛrɛ bo ba di i asin, fite saŋga n bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri durunya ni haari ni kisa, nyini wahara n ŋusu bá má kun.

eng: Many scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Na jama n atiɛn i ŋu ka i yaki naŋmiɛ. Ama, na wɔ kaaki atiɛn kpa abuka su ka, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma, si m aŋunfɔ.

eng: If the Lord hadnt shortened that time, no one would be rescued. But for the sake of the chosen ones, the ones whom God chose, he has cut short the time.
local: Ama, Micɛra n akpiɛ nyini saŋga n su. Nzɛn má wɔ kpiɛ má i su, má sɔnɔ wo má bɛrɛ bo i nyá ŋgɔɔ, ama, i dɛɛ tiɛŋgu minɛm bo wɔ yi bu n dɛɛ ti, wɔ kpiɛ saŋga n su a.

eng: Then if someone says to you, Look, heres the Christ, or There he is, dont believe it.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, nzɛn be bá se am ka, Am niɛ, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni wo fɛn n a, wara Am niɛ, yiri wo dɔ n a, má am de i mi nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Jesus stopped and said, Call him forward. They called the blind man, Be encouraged! Get up! Hes calling you.
local: Na Yesu aka ajina, na wɔ se bu ka, Am fere i ma m. Na ba fere anyinsin ni, na ba se i ka, Ma u ahore si na a yasu, i fere u.

eng: False christs and false prophets will appear, and they will offer signs and wonders in order to deceive, if possible, those whom God has chosen.
local: Dama kɔrɛ­fɔ nbem bá ba na bu yó bu ŋu ka bɛrɛ ti Kristo n a, na nbem gusu yó bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm wɔ. Bu yó alekutura sam beberebe fa kere am, na nzɛn bu koro nyaari atin, bu dádaka Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛm bo wɔ yi bu ni.

eng: But you, watch out! Ive told you everything ahead of time.
local: Ambɛrɛ, ma se am i kɛrɛ wɔ lɛɛ ka, na saŋga n nyá ba ju. Am si am ŋu sa.

eng: Throwing his coat to the side, he jumped up and came to Jesus.
local: Na wɔ fikindi i zaba n atu, na wɔ warakpin, na wɔ ŋmati akɔ Yesu dɔ.

eng: In those days, after the suffering of that time, the sun will become dark, and the moon wont give its light.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Saŋga bo nyini wahara n kɛrɛ ba siinri, Awosin káta wiɛ n su, na sara n gusu tá má bo minɛm ŋu i.

eng: The stars will fall from the sky, and the planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken.
local: Na asara­mbaam fíte aŋgoro titi tɔtɔ, na aŋgoro n kɛrɛ kpísi.

eng: Jesus asked him, What do you want me to do for you? The blind man said, Teacher, I want to see.
local: I kɔ juuri dɔ n na Yesu abisa i ka, A koro ka m yo nzu ma u a? Na anyinsin n abɔ i nu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, m koro ka a ma m ŋu asi wɔ.

eng: Jesus said, Go, your faith has healed you. At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus on the way.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Kɔ. Yarada n bo a yo m ni, nyini ma a nya laifiɛ a. Kpeŋga na wɔ kaba aŋu asi, na wɔ su i su akɔ.

eng: Then they will see the Human One coming in the clouds with great power and splendor.
local: Na minɛm nyá ŋu ka mini Adamande Wa ni sufite aŋgoro yinyiŋgu n nu suje piɛpiɛu suba ni yiko bambaka.

eng: Then he will send the angels and gather together his chosen people from the four corners of the earth, from the end of the earth to the end of heaven.
local: Na m nyá sunma Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm durunya n awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, na bu tíɛntiɛn Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu durunya n nu n kɛrɛ nɔɔ.

eng: Learn this parable from the fig tree. After its branch becomes tender and it sprouts new leaves, you know that summer is near.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Am fa damba baka n fa se nyaari. Saŋga bo am ba ŋu ka i nyawa akpɔnkpɔn, na i sufiɛfiɛ fɔfɔrɛ ni, am si ka dɔdɔɔri saŋga adodo lɛɛ.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that hes near, at the door.
local: Mini Adamande Wa n ba ni bá yo sɔ ɔ. Saŋga bo ambɛrɛ gusu bá ŋu ka nyini sam kɛrɛ bá suyo ni, am si tenle su ka saŋga n adodo lɛɛ, i cɛ́ má kun.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until all these things happen.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, ka bo bu la ŋgɔɔ kisa n wú wie má ka, ye nyini sam kɛrɛ bá yo a.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ bá la awieeri, ama, m dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n yiri la má awieeri cɛɛn be.

eng: But nobody knows when that day or hour will come, not the angels in heaven and not the Son. Only the Father knows.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Be si má saŋga ni, wara cɛɛn n bo m bá kpie m nyi ba ni. Haari Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm n bo bu wo i dɔ ni, ni mini bo m ti i wa n mmɔɔ si má. Má ka ya Si Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi si a.

eng: Watch out! Stay alert! You dont know when the time is coming.
local: Nyini ti, am si am ŋu sa. Má am fa am ŋu ji, dama am si má saŋga n bo m bá ni.

eng: It is as if someone took a trip, left the household behind, and put the servants in charge, giving each one a job to do, and told the doorkeeper to stay alert.
local: Mini Adamande Wa n baawa ni bá yo ka sɔnɔ be ye wɔ yasu i dika ka i kɔ atin. Saŋga bo i ba sukɔ ni, na wɔ fa i awuru n awura i akɔɔm saa nu, na wɔ ma bu kɛrɛ ni bu juma bo bu yo. Na wɔ se daŋminda­fɔ n ka má i fa i ŋu ji haari fa kɔ saŋga bo i bá ba.

eng: Therefore, stay alert! You dont know when the head of the household will come, whether in the evening or at midnight, or when the rooster crows in the early morning or at daybreak.
local: Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ si má saŋga bo mini Awuru­fɔ­biɛ ni bá ba. Nzɛn ŋgarama a? Naasɔ ɔ? Kɔŋguɛ jinanjin? Akɔnyuma bo saŋga? Be si má. Nyini ti, má am fa am ŋu ji.

eng: Dont let him show up when you werent expecting and find you sleeping.
local: na má am daafi na m bá fura am nu.

eng: What I say to you, I say to all: Stay alert.
local: Deke bo m sujɔjɔ se am ni, m jɔjɔ ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ pepere wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má am fa am ŋu ji na am daafi.

eng: It was two days before Passover and the Festival of Unleavened Bread. The chief priests and legal experts through cunning tricks were searching for a way to arrest Jesus and kill him.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, wɔ ka le­nnyɔ ɔ, na bu di bu cɛɛn­kpiri nbem. Bu fere kun n ka Butara cɛɛn­kpiri, bo bu di i na bu teŋge su kabo Nyɛmɛ yo deeri bu nam tii fiteeri Ijipiti­fɔm asiɛn su ni. Na kun la bo bu di i ni kpɔnɔ bo bu gɔ má ayiri nu na i yasu ni. Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, aniɛniɛ atin kabo bu yó barasu na bu tára Yesu kun i fiɛɛ­wa nu.

eng: But they agreed that it shouldnt happen during the festival; otherwise, there would be an uproar among the people.
local: Na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Má am ma ya yo i cɛɛn­kpiri n saŋga. Má nyini, minɛm ni kéte yo naŋmiɛ wɔ, na bu sutiɛntiɛn.

eng: Jesus was at Bethany visiting the house of Simon, who had a skin disease. During dinner, a woman came in with a vase made of alabaster and containing very expensive perfume of pure nard. She broke open the vase and poured the perfume on his head.
local: Yesu woori Betani miɛ n nu wɔ, sɔnɔ be dika, bu fere i ka Simon, i yo ti koko­fɔ ɔ. Saŋga bo Yesu wo diire di su ni, na bara be aba ni tulaalɛɛ tuwa nva­nvani bo i la gɔ agaya. Na wɔ bo abu tulaalɛɛ tuwa n nɔɔ, na wɔ kaaki tulaalɛɛ n kɛrɛ agɔ Yesu tii su.

eng: Some grew angry. They said to each other, Why waste the perfume?.
local: Na yaa ati minɛm ni nbem bo bu wo dɔ ni, na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Nzu ti ye wɔ saaki tulaalɛɛ n sɔ a?.

eng: This perfume could have been sold for almost a years pay and the money given to the poor. And they scolded her.
local: Má ya tɔɔri i, má ya nyá ŋwaa beberebe, na ya fá ma yaari­fɔm. Na ba kan bara ni ndiɛ agaya.

eng: Jesus said, Leave her alone. Why do you make trouble for her? She has done a good thing for me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am yaki i bɛnlɛ. Má am kan i ndiɛ. Sakpa lɛɛ ye wɔ yo ama m n a.

eng: You always have the poor with you; and whenever you want, you can do something good for them. But you wont always have me.
local: Am ni yaari­fɔm ni wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a, na nzɛn saŋga be am ba koro ka am yo bu sakpa, am yo bu. Ama, mini, m wo má am dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body ahead of time for burial.
local: Wɔ yo i dɛɛ faŋga nyaari lɛɛ, wɔ kaaki tulaalɛɛ n agɔ m ŋu ka i dɛɛ nzee bo wɔ fa ayo m siriya fa kɔ m yiwee cɛɛn lɛɛ.

eng: I tell you the truth that, wherever in the whole world the good news is announced, what shes done will also be told in memory of her.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, deke kɛrɛ bo bu bá kaanri Nyɛmɛ labari kpa ni durunya n kɛrɛ nu, bu kán i sa­yoo­wa n na minɛm teŋge i su.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to give Jesus up to them.
local: Na Yudasi Isikariyoti bo i ti Yesu susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni kun be ni, akɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni dɔ, ka i kɔ tɔ Yesu ma bu.

eng: When they heard it, they were delighted and promised to give him money. So he started looking for an opportunity to turn him in.
local: Bu tiiri labari n sɔ ni, na bu ahore ajɔ, na bu gusu atan ndiɛ ama i ka bu má i ŋwaa. Na Yudasi akpini atin kabo i yó barasu na bu nya Yesu taraa­biri.

eng: On the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was sacrificed, the disciples said to Jesus, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover meal.
local: Na cɛɛn­kpiri n bo bu di i ni kpɔnɔ bo bu gɔ má ayiri nu na i yasu ni, i le­kun cɛɛn, nyini cɛɛn ye bu kun nbɔmbaam bo bu di na bu teŋge Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n su n a, na Yesu susu­fɔm n abisa i ka, A koro ka ya kɔ ni kɔ sɛsɛ Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n diire n su se a.

eng: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, Go into the city. A man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him.
local: Na Yesu afere bu nu nnyɔ na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ miɛ n kunnu. Am ni sɔnɔ be yɛ́. I sɔ nzue dundu. Am su i su.

eng: Wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, The teacher asks,Where is my guest room where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: kɔ awuru n bo i kɔ́ wura nu ni, na am bisa awuru­fɔ­biɛ n ka, Ya Kere­fɔ n ŋa, sɔɔ bɔnɔ nu ye i ni i susu­fɔm ni dí Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n diire n a.

eng: He will show you a large room upstairs already furnished. Prepare for us there.
local: Na i kére am aŋgoro sɔɔ bambaka n bo ba yo i dɔŋgu ase ni, dɔ ye am sɛ́sɛ diire n su se ya.

eng: The disciples left, came into the city, found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: Na i susu­fɔm ni akɔ miɛ n nu, na ba kɔ aŋu deke n kɛrɛ sɔ, kabo Yesu seeri bu ni, na ba sɛsɛ Butara cɛɛn­kpiri diire n su.

eng: That evening, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.
local: Na naasɔ saŋga, na Yesu ni i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni kɛrɛ aba dɔ.

eng: During the meal, Jesus said, I assure you that one of you will betray me—someone eating with me.
local: Na saŋga bo bu tana wo diire n di su ni, na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, am nu kun bá tɔ m ma m kpɔ­fɔm a. I mi ni mini susaŋga di diire n a.

eng: Deeply saddened, they asked him, one by one, Its not me, is it.
local: Na bu ahore asaaki, na bu kun kun n kɛrɛ abisa i ka, Ye m ŋa má mini yiri e.

eng: Jesus answered, Its one of the Twelve, one who is dipping bread with me into this bowl.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Ambɛrɛ buru ni nnyɔm nu kun yó nyini a, i mi ni mini susaŋga fa ya saa wura taalɛ kun nu a.

eng: The Human One goes to his death just as it is written about him. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays the Human One! It would have been better for him if he had never been born.
local: Mini Adamande Wa ni wú kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ase ka m wú n wɔ, ama, sɔnɔ n bo i tɔ́ mini Adamande Wa n ni, i mi bá ŋu anyum­boro. Má ba wu má i mi n mmɔɔ, má i ti ye.

eng: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed it, broke it, and gave it to them, and said, Take; this is my body.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm ni wo diire n di su ni, na Yesu afa kpɔnɔ, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ bubu i nu, na wɔ fa ama bu, na wɔ se bu ka, Am de ahin kpɔnɔ n na am di, i ti m ŋuniɛ­mbaa a.

eng: He took a cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
local: Na wɔ kaaki afa awa ni nzan, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ fa ama bu, na bu kɛrɛ anu be.

eng: He said to them, This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin nzan n ti m mbunja lɛɛ, ye m sufa ma am n a. Yiri ye Nyɛmɛ sufa sɛsɛ­su na i di i di n a. Minɛm dɔŋgu satiɛ dɛɛ ti, ye m mbunja ni soŋgi gɔ a.

eng: I assure you that I wont drink wine again until that day when I drink it in a new way in Gods kingdom.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, m nú má nzan be kun, má ka cɛɛn n bo m bá nu i fɔfɔrɛ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu ni.

eng: After singing songs of praise, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
local: Nyini sin na ba di jue akansi Nyɛmɛ, na ba fite akɔ Oliva Buka n su.

eng: Jesus said to them, You will all falter in your faithfulness to me. It is written, I will hit the shepherd, and the sheep will go off in all directions .
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Am kɛrɛ bá ŋmati yaki m, dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, M bó kun bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ ni, na nbɔɛm bá sandi.

eng: But after Im raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.
local: Ama, m bá teŋgeeri m yiwee n nu ni, m dú am mɔ kɔ Galili awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: Peter said to him, Even if everyone else stumbles, I wont.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, Nzɛn ahinm kɛrɛ ŋmati yakiiri u, mini ŋmáti yaki má u da.

eng: But Jesus said to him, I assure you that on this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.
local: Na Yesu ase Petoro ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u a, nyuma kɔŋguɛ n nu, kana akɔnyuma bá bo kpe nnyɔ su, a kéte kpe nsan ka a si má m.

eng: But Peter insisted, If I must die alongside you, I wont deny you. And they all said the same thing.
local: Na Petoro abo i hoe ase i ka, Mini sé má sɔ da. Nzɛn haari wɔ yo daka ka mini ni wɔrɔ kɛrɛ sáŋga wu mmɔɔ, ya wú ɔ. Na i susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ ase sɔ.

eng: Jesus and his disciples came to a place called Gethsemane. Jesus said to them, Sit here while I pray.
local: Na ba yasu akɔ aju awɔrɛ be bo bu fere i ka Gɛsɛmane ni, na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ni ka, Am tana fɛn ŋminda m, na m kɔ́ sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: He took Peter, James, and John along with him. He began to feel despair and was anxious.
local: Na wɔ fa Petoro ni Yakobo ni Yohane na ba kɔ mɔ kaan. Bu kɔ juuri dɔ na ahoresaaki aba i dɔ agaya, na i jeke ati.

eng: He said to them, Im very sad. Its as if Im dying. Stay here and keep alert.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ahoresaaki aba m dɔ agaya, i yo m ka m wú ɔ mmɔɔ. Am wo fɛn na am ŋminda m. Má am daafi.

eng: Then he went a short distance farther and fell to the ground. He prayed that, if possible, he might be spared the time of suffering.
local: Na wɔ kɔ dede kaan, na wɔ butu i kunnu su, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ka nzɛn má i koro ma ahin anyum­boro n sin yaki i.

eng: He said, Abba, Father, for you all things are possible. Take this cup of suffering away from me. However—not what I want but what you want.
local: I ŋa, Abba , i bu la ka, M Si, wɔrɔ koro yo sa kɛrɛ a. Nyini ti, yi m fite ahin anyum­boro n bo i suba m dɔ n nu. Ama, má a yo kabo m koro ni, se kabo a koro ni.

eng: He came and found them sleeping. He said to Peter, Simon, are you asleep? Couldnt you stay alert for one hour?.
local: I sere Nyɛmɛ wieeri ni, na wɔ kpie i nyi akɔ aŋu ka i susu­fɔ nsanm ni wo daafiri su. Na wɔ teŋge bu na wɔ se Simon Petoro ka, Simon, a daafi wɔ? A koro kpiɛ má daafiri, haari kaan sɔ mmɔɔ.

eng: Stay alert and pray so that you wont give in to temptation. The spirit is eager, but the flesh is weak.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Am kpiɛ daafiri, na am sere Nyɛmɛ, adena má deke be yo am na am wura satiɛ nu. Ahore­mbaa n koro nyɛmɛsu, ama, aŋuniɛ­mbaa n ayɔrɔki.

eng: Again, he left them and prayed, repeating the same words.
local: Na wɔ kaaki akɔ asere Nyɛmɛ bekun ni nyini nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa kun bo i du mɔ jɔjɔɔri ni.

eng: And, again, when he came back, he found them sleeping, for they couldnt keep their eyes open, and they didnt know how to respond to him.
local: Na wɔ kpie i nyi aba i susu­fɔm n dɔ, na wɔ ba aŋu ka ba daafi bekun, dama daafiri ati bu agaya. Ba koro ase má i pui.

eng: He came a third time and said to them, Will you sleep and rest all night? Thats enough! The time has come for the Human One to be betrayed into the hands of sinners.
local: Na wɔ kɔ asere Nyɛmɛ kpe nsan su, akaaki aba aŋu ka bu toko wo daafiri su, na wɔ se bu ka, Am toko daafi yi am ŋumi wɔ? Wɔ sɛ su. Saŋga n aju, wɔ tɔ mini Adamande Wa ni ama satiɛ­yo­fɔm.

eng: Get up! Lets go! Look, here comes my betrayer.
local: Am niɛ, sɔnɔ n bo wɔ tɔ m lɛɛ. Am yasu na ya kɔ.

eng: Suddenly, while Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, came with a mob carrying swords and clubs. They had been sent by the chief priests, legal experts, and elders.
local: Na saŋga bo Yesu toko wo jɔjɔɔri su ni, na Yudasi bo i ti Yesu susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm nu kun aju ni jama beberebe. Bu bita takobem ni kɔrɔmam. Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni sunmaari bu a.

eng: His betrayer had given them a sign: Arrest the man I kiss, and take him away under guard.
local: Na Yudasi bo i tɔɔri Yesu ni akere jama n nyaari su kabo bu sí Yesu na bu tara i. I ŋa, Sɔnɔ n bo m kɔ́ biti i ni, yiri lɛɛ. Am tara i, na am bita i kenken, na am fa i kɔ.

eng: As soon as he got there, Judas said to Jesus, Rabbi! Then he kissed him.
local: Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri ni, i kaba kɔɔri Yesu dɔ ɔ, na wɔ bo afere i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ. Na wɔ biti i, na wɔ yo i ansɛ.

eng: Then they came and grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
local: Na minɛm n atara Yesu na ba bita i kenken.

eng: One of the bystanders drew a sword and struck the high priests slave and cut off his ear.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔm bo bu jina bɛrɛ n nu kun acin ayi i takobe, na wɔ kpiɛ sɔnɔ be sui, nyini sɔnɔ n ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n akɔɔ ɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, like an outlaw?.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, M ti fin­fɔ ɔ ye am fa takobem ni kɔrɔmam na am ba tara m a?.

eng: Day after day, I was with you, teaching in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But let the scriptures be fulfilled.
local: Cɛɛn kɛrɛ cɛɛn m ni ambɛrɛ wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu a, ye m kere minɛm a, ama, am atara má m. Tɔ, i ti ye ni ka kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka bu yó m ni yo yii­wa.

eng: And all his disciples left him and ran away.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ aŋmati ayaki i.

eng: One young man, a disciple, was wearing nothing but a linen cloth. They grabbed him.
local: Na gbɛfɛnɛ be gusu su Yesu su. I kara tani ŋgumi wɔ. Na ba laandi ka bu tara i.

eng: but he left the linen cloth behind and ran away naked.
local: na wɔ foro bu saa aŋmati ayaki tani ni, i bu ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and legal experts gathered.
local: Na ba fa Yesu akɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n dika, na Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm kɛrɛ aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ. Bɛrɛ la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni.

eng: Peter followed him from a distance, right into the high priests courtyard. He was sitting with the guards, warming himself by the fire.
local: Na Petoro asu Yesu su, ama, i woori dede kaan ni yiri, na wɔ kɔ awura Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n dika gisan nu. Na wɔ kɔ asaŋga atana tintini­jina­fɔm n nu atun sin.

eng: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for testimony against Jesus in order to put him to death, but they couldnt find any.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm n kɛrɛ aniɛniɛ minɛm bo bu bá di daani fa kɔ ŋgasi bo Yesu ado i ni, adena bu nya i taraa­biri na bu kun i, ama, ba nya má jɔrɛ bo bu fa kpiɛ sɔ i.

eng: Many brought false testimony against him, but they contradicted each other.
local: Na daani di­fɔm beberebe afite ajina akpiɛ kɔrɛ asɔ Yesu, ama, bu nɔaniɛ n ayo má kun.

eng: Some stood to offer false witness against him, saying.
local: Na minɛ nbem gusu ayasu ajina akpiɛ kɔrɛ asɔ Yesu.

eng: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple, constructed by humans, and within three days I will build another, one not made by humans.
local: bu ŋa, Ya tiiri ka i ŋa, i fúfu ahin Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n bo minɛm taari i ni, na le­san afiɛn, na i káaki ta fɔfɔrɛ bo i ti má kabo adamande ataari ti ni.

eng: But their testimonies didnt agree even on this point.
local: Haari ni nyini n mmɔɔ, bu nɔaniɛ ni ayo má kun.

eng: Then the high priest stood up in the middle of the gathering and examined Jesus. Arent you going to respond to the testimony these people have brought against you.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn ni ayasu ajina tere­tere­fɔm n afiɛn, na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, A la má bɔnu be fa kɔ ahin jɔrɛ n bo minɛm ni akpiɛ asɔ u n su.

eng: But Jesus was silent and didnt answer. Again, the high priest asked, Are you the Christ, the Son of the blessed one.
local: Ama, na Yesu amɔ i nɔɔ, wɔ bɔ má jɔrɛ be nu. Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn ni abisa i bekun ka, Wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ n bo ya kɛrɛ kansi i n dɛɛ Wa, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a.

eng: Jesus said, I am. And you will see the Human One sitting on the right side of the Almighty and coming on the heavenly clouds.
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Mini lɛɛ. Na am bá ŋu mini Adamande Wa ni, ka m tana Nyɛmɛ Yiko­fɔ n saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, na am ŋú ka m fite aŋgoro yinyiŋgu n nu suba.

eng: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, Why do we need any more witnesses?.
local: Yesu bɔ nu sɔ wieeri ni, na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn ni asɔn i tanlɛɛ. Na wɔ se ka, Ya koro má daani­fɔ bekun.

eng: Youve heard his insult against God. What do you think? They all condemned him. He deserves to die.
local: Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ti kabo wɔ jɔjɔ awura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi n nu e. Ye am ŋa sɛ wɔ? Na bu kɛrɛ ase ka wɔ kan ciire jɔrɛ, nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka i wu.

eng: Some began to spit on him. Some covered his face and hit him, saying, Prophesy! Then the guards took him and beat him.
local: Bu kaanri sɔ wieeri ni, na bu nbem ayi nanzue agɔ Yesu ŋu, na ba fa sanvuin acin akata i nyu­mbaa, na ba bobo i, na ba se i ka, Wɔrɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ni, kan kere ya sɔnɔ n bo i boori u ni. Na tintini­jina­fɔm n gusu afinfin i nyɔɔnu.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter was below in the courtyard. A woman, one of the high priests servants, approached.
local: Bu wo nyini kɛrɛ su ni, na Petoro toko wo gisan n nu, na afani be aba i bi, yiri ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n dɛɛ afani kun a.

eng: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus.
local: Saŋga bo i niɛ ŋuuri Petoro ka i wo sin tuun­biri n ni, na wɔ niɛ Petoro nyunu dika gbirim, na wɔ se i ka, Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ yo woori nyini Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n dɔ ɔ.

eng: But he denied it, saying, I dont know what youre talking about. I dont understand what youre saying. And he went outside into the outer courtyard. A rooster crowed.
local: Na Petoro akete, i ŋa, Ai, m si má nyini jɔrɛ n bo a sujɔjɔ i ni. Na wɔ fite gisan n nu aba lɛɛ n nu. Nyini saŋga ni na akɔnyuma abo kpe­kun.

eng: The female servant saw him and began a second time to say to those standing around, This man is one of them.
local: Na afani n aba aŋu i dɔ, na wɔ kaaki ase minɛm bo bu jina bɛrɛ ni ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n ti bu nu kun wɔ.

eng: But he denied it again. A short time later, those standing around again said to Peter, You must be one of them, because you are also a Galilean.
local: Ama, na Petoro akete bekun. Wɔ cɛ má na bo bu jina bɛrɛ n gusu ase Petoro ka, Nahɔrɛ a, a ti bu nu kun wɔ, dama wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ti Galili­fɔ ɔ.

eng: But he cursed and swore, I dont know this man youre talking about.
local: Na wɔ tan ndiɛ afa i saa awura sin nu, i ŋa, M si má sɔnɔ n bo am sujɔjɔ i jɔrɛ ni.

eng: At that very moment, a rooster crowed a second time. Peter remembered what Jesus told him, Before a rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times. And he broke down, sobbing.
local: Kpeŋga na akɔnyuma n abo kpe nnyɔ su, na Petoro ateŋge su kabo Yesu seeri i ni, i ŋa, Kana akɔnyuma bá bo kpe nnyɔ su, a kéte kpe nsan su ka a si má m. Na i ahore asaaki, na wɔ sun.

eng: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they could go and anoint Jesus dead body.
local: Na naasɔ saŋga bo Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n asin ni, na Mariya Magadala­fɔ ni, ni Salome, ni Mariya n bo i wa la Yakobo ni, aba ato tulaalɛɛ nva­nvani ka bu fa kɔ gɔ Yesu ŋuniɛ n su.

eng: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they came to the tomb.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm bakuɛ n bumboori, mɔnɛ cɛɛn ŋgarama fuun, fa kɔ wiɛ tiɛ saŋga, na ba kɔ Yesu faŋ­kunma n su.

eng: They were saying to each other, Whos going to roll the stone away from the entrance for us.
local: Saŋga bo bu sukɔ ni, na ba bisa bu beŋgu ka, Ŋma kɔ́ kundu yi yabuɛ n bo i tɔ faŋ­kunma n anɔ n ma ya.

eng: When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away.
local: Deke bo i ti na bu seeri sɔ n la ka, yabuɛ n ti bambaka agaya. Na ba tu bu tii aniɛ aŋu ka yabuɛ n tɔ má anɔ ni.

eng: At daybreak, the chief priests—with the elders, legal experts, and the whole Sanhedrin—formed a plan. They bound Jesus, led him away, and turned him over to Pilate.
local: Na alecin saŋga na Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ. Bɛrɛ la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni. Na ba tana asɛsɛ su kabo bu yó Yesu. Na ba wura i jɔrɔkɔ, na ba fa i akɔ ama Gomina Pilato.

eng: Going into the tomb, they saw a young man in a white robe seated on the right side; and they were startled.
local: Na ba wura yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma n nu aŋu ka gbɛfɛnɛ be tana nu saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, i wura tanlɛɛ fofoe yorom be, na ba yo alekutura.

eng: Pilate questioned him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Na Gomina Pilato abisa i ka, Wɔrɔ ti Yuda­fɔm fɛmɛ n a? Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, Kabo a jɔjɔ ni, i ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: But he said to them, Dont be alarmed! You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised. He isnt here. Look, heres the place where they laid him.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Má am yo alekutura. M si ka am niɛniɛ Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n bo bu kpamaari i baka jaanu n su n wɔ. Wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu. I wo má fɛn. Am niɛ, deke bo bu faari i seeri n yaa.

eng: The chief priests were accusing him of many things.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni akpiɛ jɔrɛ beberebe asɔ i.

eng: Go, tell his disciples, especially Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.
local: Am kpie am nyi na am kɔ se Petoro ni nyiŋgam n kɛrɛ ka wɔ du am mɔ sukɔ Galili, dɔ ye am kɔ́ ŋu i a, dama kabo i yo seeri am lɛɛ.

eng: Pilate asked him again, Arent you going to answer? What about all these accusations.
local: Na Gomina Pilato abisa i bekun ka, A la má nɔaniɛ bo a bɔ bu nu? Niɛ jɔrɛ beberebe bo ba kpiɛ asɔ u a.

eng: Overcome with terror and dread, they fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. .
local: Na ba fite faŋ­kunma n nu ni ŋmatiiri, na bu ŋuniɛ subobo bu ni sɛrɛ. Ba bo má su ase má be, dama sɛrɛ ati bu agaya. .

eng: They promptly reported all of the young mans instructions to those who were with Peter. Afterward, through the work of his disciples, Jesus sent out, from the east to the west, the sacred and undying message of eternal salvation. Amen. .
local: Mɔnɛ cɛɛn ŋgarama fuun ye Yesu teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu a. I du mɔ yiiri i ŋu kereeri Mariya Magadala­fɔ n wɔ. Yiri la bara n bo Yesu fɔn yiiri ajini nsom i ŋu ni.

eng: But Jesus gave no more answers, so that Pilate marveled.
local: Ama, Yesu abɔ má jɔrɛ be nu, na Gomina Pilato abirisi kabo Yesu abɔ má nu ni.

eng: She went and reported to the ones who had been with him, who were mourning and weeping.
local: Na wɔ kɔ atu Yesu susu­fɔm ni ka bu wo ahoresaaki ni sun su, na wɔ se bu ka wɔ ŋu Yesu.

eng: During the festival, Pilate released one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
local: Afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n nya ba ju ni, Gomina Pilato yi dan­saraka n bo jama n sere i ka i yi i ji n wɔ.

eng: But even after they heard the news, they didnt believe that Jesus was alive and that Mary had seen him.
local: Saŋga bo i seeri bu ka Yesu ateŋge i yiwee n nu na wɔ ŋu i ni, ba de má i nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: A man named Barabbas was locked up with the rebels who had committed murder during an uprising.
local: Nyini saŋga ni sɔnɔ be woori dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu dɔ, bu fere i ka Barabasi. Saŋga be minɛm yo keteeri gomina n ŋu wɔ, na ba tu luɛ, na i ni i beŋgu ketema­fɔ nbem akun minɛm, nyini ti, na ba tara bu anyi bu dan­saraka.

eng: After that he appeared in a different form to two of them who were walking along in the countryside.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu susu­fɔ nnyɔ nbem afite Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu sukɔ ahɛmɛ, na wɔ yi i ŋu akere bu, bo ba koro afa má nu nde ka yiri lɛɛ.

eng: The crowd pushed forward and asked Pilate to release someone, as he regularly did.
local: Tɔ. Kabo afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ Gomina Pilato n yi dan­saraka n kun ma bu ni, na jama n aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ i bi na bu kere sɔnɔ n bo i yí ma bu ni.

eng: Pilate answered them, Do you want me to release to you the king of the Jews.
local: Na Gomina Pilato abisa bu ka, Am koro ka m yi Yuda­fɔm fɛmɛ ni ma am a.

eng: When they returned, they reported it to the others, but they didnt believe them.
local: Na ba kpie bu nyi aba ase nyiŋgam n ka ba ŋu Yesu, ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ba de má.

eng: He knew that the chief priests had handed him over because of jealousy.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, dama i si tenle su ka Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni si Yesu kɔrɛ ti ye bu faari i ba wuraari i saa nu a.

eng: Finally he appeared to the eleven while they were eating. Jesus criticized their unbelief and stubbornness because they didnt believe those who saw him after he was raised up.
local: Nyini kɛrɛ sin na saŋga bo i susu­fɔ buru ni kunm n wo diire di su ni, na wɔ yi i ŋu akere bu. Na wɔ jaraki bu kabo bu ahore­mbaa ti kekereke ni, ni kabo ba yo má yarada ni minɛm bo bu ŋuuri i saŋga bo i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu na bu ba seeri bu ni.

eng: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n awura jama n bu sin ka bu se Gomina Pilato ka i yi Barabasi ji ma bu.

eng: He said to them, Go into the whole world and proclaim the good news to every creature.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ durunya n kɛrɛ nu na am kɔ kan m labari kpa n kere adamande kɛrɛ.

eng: Pilate replied, Then what do you want me to do with the one you call king of the Jews.
local: Na Gomina Pilato abisa bu ka, Ye ahin sɔnɔ n bo am fere i ka Yuda­fɔm fɛmɛ ni, am koro ka m yo i sɛ wɔ.

eng: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever doesnt believe will be condemned.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori m yarada, na am biɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ nzue, Nyɛmɛ dé i tii ji, ama, bo ba yo má yarada ni, Nyɛmɛ dí bu jɔrɛ na i yó bu ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: They shouted back, Crucify him.
local: Na jama n atiɛn ase ka, Kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu.

eng: These signs will be associated with those who believe: they will throw out demons in my name. They will speak in new languages.
local: Na bo bu yoori yarada, bu nyá yiko bo bu koro yó ahin alekutura sam ni. Bu fá m duma fɔn yi ajinim fite minɛm n ŋu, na bu jɔ́jɔ aniɛ barasu barasu bo bu si má bu.

eng: Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? They shouted even louder, Crucify him.
local: Na Gomina Pilato ase bu ka, Ye nzu satiɛ ye wɔ yo i a? Ama, na ba kaaki atiɛn agaya bekun ase ka, Kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu.

eng: They will pick up snakes with their hands. If they drink anything poisonous, it will not hurt them. They will place their hands on the sick, and they will get well.
local: na nzɛn bu taraari woom wara bu diiri neŋge dii­wa n mmɔɔ, i yó má bu pui. Bu fá bu saa nana tukpaki­fɔm ŋu, na bu nyá laifiɛ.

eng: Pilate wanted to satisfy the crowd, so he released Barabbas to them. He had Jesus whipped, then handed him over to be crucified.
local: Dɔ na Gomina Pilato akoro ka i jɔ jama n ahore, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ yi Barabasi aji, na wɔ ma sojam afin Yesu ŋgbere, na wɔ nya afa i awura bu saa nu ka bu kɔ kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu.

eng: After the Lord Jesus spoke to them, he was lifted up into heaven and sat down on the right side of God.
local: Saŋga bo Mibiɛ Yesu jɔjɔɔri bu dɔ wieeri ni, na Nyɛmɛ afa i afun akɔ aŋgoro akɔ atana i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

eng: The soldiers led Jesus away into the courtyard of the palace known as the governors headquarters, and they called together the whole company of soldiers.
local: Na ba fa Yesu awura gomina n gisan n nu, na ba fere bu beŋgu sojam kɛrɛ ka bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ.

eng: But they went out and proclaimed the message everywhere. The Lord worked with them, confirming the word by the signs associated with them.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n akɔ deke n kɛrɛ kɔ sukan i labari kpa ni, na Mibiɛ n awo bu dɔ abuka bu, na wɔ ma bu yiko, na ba yo alekutura sam bo i kere ka bu labari kpa kaan­wa n kɛrɛ ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: They dressed him up in a purple robe and twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on him.
local: Na ba wura Yesu tanlɛɛ kɔkɔrɛ yorom ka fɛmɛya tanlɛɛ su, na ba fa buwe aŋɔ i ka kaba dɛɛ su, na ba fa aforo i tii ka fɛmɛya kere dɛɛ su, adena bu yo i anzasi.

eng: They saluted him, Hey! King of the Jews.
local: Na ba yo bu ŋu ka bu suma i jirima, bu ŋa, Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ ni, ya yo u ansɛ.

eng: Again and again, they struck his head with a stick. They spit on him and knelt before him to honor him.
local: Na bu sufinfin i tii baka, na bu suyi nanzue gɔ i ŋu, na bu sukutu i nyunu.

eng: When they finished mocking him, they stripped him of the purple robe and put his own clothes back on him. Then they led him out to crucify him.
local: Bu ba yoori i anzasi n wieeri ni, na ba yi tanlɛɛ kɔkɔrɛ yorom ni, na ba kaaki afa i tiɛŋgu dɛɛ sutura n awura i. Na ba nya afa i akɔ ka bu kɔ kpama i baka jaanu n su.

eng: Simon, a man from Cyrene, Alexander and Rufus father, was coming in from the countryside. They forced him to carry his cross.
local: Saŋga bo bu sukɔ ni, na bu ni Sirenefɔ biɛ be ayɛ, bu fere i ka Simon, Alɛkizanda ni Rufusu si lɛɛ. I fite ahɛmɛ suba miɛ kunnu, na sojam n amiɛ i ka i sɔ Yesu baka jaanu ni.

eng: They brought Jesus to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
local: Na ba fa Yesu akɔ deke bo bu fere i ka Gologota ni, Gologota n bu la ka Tere­awa Woo­biri.

eng: They tried to give him wine mixed with myrrh, but he didnt take it.
local: Na minɛ nbem asaŋga nzan ni ayiri barasu be ama i ka i nu, na i jɔ yaari, ama, wɔ nu má i.

eng: They crucified him. They divided up his clothes, drawing lots for them to determine who would take what.
local: Bu yoori nyini n kɛrɛ wieeri ni, na ba nya akpama i baka jaanu n su, na ba yɛ i su ajina, na ba tu mbaa na bu si kabo bu kpaaki i suturam nu, bu ŋuŋu nu.

eng: It was nine in the morning when they crucified him.
local: Bu kpamaari i baka jaanu n su ŋgarama kerefi ŋgɔna a.

eng: In the fifteenth year of the rule of the emperor Tiberius—when Pontius Pilate was governor over Judea and Herod was ruler over Galilee, his brother Philip was ruler over Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias was ruler over Abilene.
local: Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri bo bu fere i ka Siza Tiberiyosi n diiri i fɛmɛya ni, afɔ buru ni nnu, nyini saŋga ye sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Pɔntiyo Pilato n ti Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu gomina, na sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Herode n niɛ Galili awɔrɛ n nu su, na i gɔrɔ bo bu fere i ka Filipu n gusu niɛ awɔrɛ nnyɔ nbem bo bu fere bu ka Iturayɔ ni Tarakonitidɔ n su, na sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Lusaniya n gusu niɛ awɔrɛ n bo bu fere i ka Abilene n su.

eng: The notice of the formal charge against him was written, The king of the Jews.
local: Na ba kɛrɛ jɔrɛ n bo bu kpiɛ sɔɔri i ni, na ba fa abo akpama i baka jaanu n tii su, kɛrɛɛ­wa n la ka, Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n lɛɛ.

eng: during the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas—Gods word came to John son of Zechariah in the wilderness.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, Anasi ni Kayafasi jina Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn n jinaa­biri a. Na Nyɛmɛ afa i nɔaniɛ n ama Yohane Zakariya wa, boro kpantain n nu.

eng: They crucified two outlaws with him, one on his right and one on his left.
local: Na ba kpama fin­fɔ nnyɔ nbem baka jaanu su adodo i, bu kpamaari kun i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su wɔ, na ba kpama kun n gusu i saa biɛ su.

eng: John went throughout the region of the Jordan River, calling for people to be baptized to show that they were changing their hearts and lives and wanted God to forgive their sins.
local: Nyini ti, na Yohane akɔ Yodan kunma n awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ n nu sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, i ŋa, Am kaaki am wo na m biɛ am Nyɛmɛ nzue, na Nyɛmɛ fa am ŋgasi ni am satiɛ kɛrɛ ce am.

eng: This is just as it was written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, A voice crying out in the wilderness: Prepare the way for the Lord; make his paths straight.
local: Sɔ ye wɔ yo yii­wa, kabo Nyɛmɛ ma ba kɛrɛ i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya dɛɛ kadasi n nu ka, Sɔnɔ be subo ndoori boro kpantain n nu ka, Am sɛsɛ Mibiɛ n atin su, na am yo i atin n dɔŋgu.

eng: People walking by insulted him, shaking their heads and saying, Ha! So you were going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, were you?.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu sin dɔ ni subobo bu tii, na bu sukpiɛ i nzukɔ, bu ŋa, Ɛɛnhɛɛn, wɔrɔ bo a ŋa a fúfu Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n na a káaki ta i le­san afiɛn n lɛɛ.

eng: Every valley will be filled, and every mountain and hill will be leveled. The crooked will be made straight and the rough places made smooth.
local: Na bu bá bereki bukam n ni kpɛrɛm kɛrɛ, na bu bá tɔtɔ kunma kunma kɛrɛ bo i wo buka n bu, na atin kɛrɛ bo wɔ biri, bu bá teŋge, na bu bá tɔtɔ i kunma kunma n kɛrɛ.

eng: Save yourself and come down from that cross.
local: jura baka jaanu n su na a de u tii ji.

eng: All humanity will see Gods salvation. .
local: Na adamande kɛrɛ bá ŋu kabo Nyɛmɛ dé bu tii ji barasu ni.

eng: In the same way, the chief priests were making fun of him among themselves, together with the legal experts. He saved others, they said, but he cant save himself.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni gusu suyo i anzasi bu ŋuŋu nu, bu ŋa, Wɔ de minɛm tii aji, ama, i sukoro de má yiri ni i tii.

eng: Then John said to the crowds who came to be baptized by him, You children of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the angry judgment that is coming soon?.
local: Na jama beberebe afite aba Yohane dɔ ka i biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue. Na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm bo am ti ka woom ni, ŋma seeri am ka am ŋmati fite sui­cin kekereke n bo Nyɛmɛ sufa ba am dɔ n nu a?.

eng: Let the Christ, the king of Israel, come down from the cross. Then well see and believe. Even those who had been crucified with Jesus insulted him.
local: Am ma ya niɛ Yisareli­fɔm Fɛmɛ n bo i ŋa i ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni, nzɛn i koro juraari baka jaanu n su kisa kisa, ya yó i yarada. Na minɛ nnyɔm ni bo bu kpamaari bu dodoori i ni mmɔɔ akpiɛ i nzukɔ.

eng: Produce fruit that shows you have changed your hearts and lives. And dont even think about saying to yourselves, Abraham is our father. I tell you that God is able to raise up Abrahams children from these stones.
local: Am yo sakpa bo i kere ka am akaaki am wo. Na má am sunsun ka, Abraham ti am na dɛɛ ti, na am koro fite nyini sui­cin n nu. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, Nyɛmɛ koro ma ahin yabuɛm ni kaaki nbaam ma Abraham.

eng: The ax is already at the root of the trees. Therefore, every tree that doesnt produce good fruit will be chopped down and tossed into the fire.
local: Nyɛmɛ asi i samba n nana ase baka kɛrɛ bu na i kpiɛ i ji sin nu, yiri bo i suu­wa ti má kpa ni.

eng: From noon until three in the afternoon the whole earth was dark.
local: Wiɛsu kerefi buru ni nnyɔ saŋga na awosin akata asiɛn n kɛrɛ su, haari naasɔ kerefi nsan.

eng: At three, Jesus cried out with a loud shout, Eloi, eloi, lama sabachthani, which means, My God, my God, why have you left me.
local: Na nyini saŋga ni, na Yesu abo awie kpa ase ka, Eli, Eli, lama sabatani ? I bu la ka, M Nyɛmɛ, m Nyɛmɛ, nzu ti ye a yaki m aji a.

eng: After hearing him, some standing there said, Look! Hes calling Elijah.
local: Na minɛm nbem jinaari bɛrɛ, bu nbem n tiiri sɔ ni, na ba se ka, Am so am sui na am ti, i sufere Eliya.

eng: Someone ran, filled a sponge with sour wine, and put it on a pole. He offered it to Jesus to drink, saying, Lets see if Elijah will come to take him down.
local: Na bu kun nu aŋmati afa mɛnɛ adɔ nzan gagaraki be nu, na wɔ fa awɔ baka su alaandi atɔ Yesu nɔɔ ka i nu, na wɔ se ka, Am yaki i na ya niɛ, ka Eliya bá jura i baka jaanu n su.

eng: The crowds asked him, What then should we do.
local: Na jama n abisa Yohane ka, Ye ya yo sɛ wɔ lɛɛ kisa n a.

eng: But Jesus let out a loud cry and died.
local: Na Yesu abo awie kpa kpe­kun, na wɔ nya ayo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ.

eng: He answered, Whoever has two shirts must share with the one who has none, and whoever has food must do the same.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la tanlɛɛ nnyɔ, i mi ma sɔnɔ bo i la má be n kun, na bo i la diire gusu yo sɔ.

eng: The curtain of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom.
local: Na tani bambaka n bo bu fa kpaakiiri sɔɔ casi n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu n ati nu nnyɔ, fa i aŋgoro kɔ jura asiɛŋgu.

eng: Even tax collectors came to be baptized. They said to him, Teacher, what should we do.
local: Na lampo­de­fɔ nbem aba i dɔ ka i biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue, na ba bisa i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ye ya yo sɛ wɔ.

eng: When the centurion, who stood facing Jesus, saw how he died, he said, This man was certainly Gods Son.
local: Na soja kpiɛn n bo i jinaari baka jaanu n nyunu ŋuuri kabo Yesu boori awie na i wuuri barasu ni, na wɔ se ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a nahɔrɛ su.

eng: He replied, Collect no more than you are authorized to collect.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Má am de ŋwaa kɔ tara kabo i ti ye ni ka am de ni.

eng: Some women were watching from a distance, including Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and Salome.
local: Na nbara nbem ajina dede suniɛ Yesu. Bu nu nbem la Mariya bo i fite Magadala miɛ n nu ni, ni Salome, ni Mariya n bo i nbaam la Yakobo kaan n ni Yose.

eng: Soldiers asked, What about us? What should we do? He answered, Dont cheat or harass anyone, and be satisfied with your pay.
local: Na soja nbem gusu aba i dɔ aba abisa i ka, Ye yɛrɛ di, ya yó sɛ wɔ? Na wɔ se bu ka, Má am miɛ be de ŋwaa i dɔ, na má am tun be nzuin. Am ma am nyunu yi am ŋkatɔ.

eng: In those days Caesar Augustus declared that everyone throughout the empire should be enrolled in the tax lists.
local: Nyini saŋga na Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Siza Agusito ni, ama nɔɔ ka bu kɛrɛ i minɛm kɛrɛ duma.

eng: The people were filled with expectation, and everyone wondered whether John might be the Christ.
local: Na minɛm bo bu tana niɛ Kristo n atin nu ka i ba ni, yiri Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni, bu sunsun ka, beni, kisa Yohane ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a.

eng: When Jesus was in Galilee, these women had followed and supported him, along with many other women who had come to Jerusalem with him.
local: Bɛrɛ yo suuri Yesu su wɔ saŋga bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu na bu subuka i. Nbaram beberebe gusu yo woori dɔ bo bu suuri i su baari Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: This first enrollment occurred when Quirinius governed Syria.
local: Nyini saŋga ni tiiri ciciri su dɛɛ minɛm duma kɛrɛɛ­wa n ŋusu a. Nyini saŋga n ye sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Kurenɔsi n ti Siriya awɔrɛ n nu gomina n a.

eng: John replied to them all, I baptize you with water, but the one who is more powerful than me is coming. Im not worthy to loosen the strap of his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
local: Yohane siiri nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ se bu ka, Mini sufa nzue biɛ am a, na am yo casi, ama, bo i tara m fieo wo m sin suba. M ti kaan ni bo m dodo i. Yiri fá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ni sin yo am casi a, kabo sɔnɔ biɛ nzue na i yo casi ni.

eng: Since it was late in the afternoon on Preparation Day, just before the Sabbath.
local: Kabo nyini cɛɛn n tiiri Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n kpokpo cɛɛn ni, i naasɔ wiɛ.

eng: Everyone went to their own cities to be enrolled.
local: Nyini ti, na deke bo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wo na ba kɔ bu na miɛ su na bu kɔ kɛrɛ bu duma.

eng: The shovel he uses to sift the wheat from the husks is in his hands. He will clean out his threshing area and bring the wheat into his barn. But he will burn the husks with a fire that cant be put out.
local: I bita i ŋgani bakiti fieeba suba, na i bá bakiti yi ŋgani mbaa n busu gɔ i bondo nu, na i nya yára kunkun n sin n bo i je na i nɔ má n nu.

eng: Joseph from Arimathea dared to approach Pilate and ask for Jesus body.
local: na Yosefu bo i fite Aramatiya miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na wɔ fa ahoresi na wɔ kɔ aŋu Gomina Pilato, na wɔ sere i ka i fa Yesu ŋuniɛ n ma i. Yosefu yo ti sɔnɔ jirima­fɔ ɔ Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm n nu, na i sunsunni kɛrɛ yo la ka, i ŋminda saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ bá di i fɛmɛya n wɔ.

eng: Since Joseph belonged to Davids house and family line, he went up from the city of Nazareth in Galilee to Davids city, called Bethlehem, in Judea.
local: Dɔ na Yosefu gusu afite Nazarɛti miɛ n bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni, na i kɔ kɛrɛ i duma. I kɔɔri miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Bɛtilɛhɛm na i wo Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu wɔ, dɔ ye bu wuuri Fɛmɛ Davidi a. Yosefu ti Fɛmɛ Davidi dɛɛ busu nu­fɔ n ti ye i kɔɔri awɔrɛ n nu dɔ a.

eng: With many other words John appealed to them, proclaiming good news to the people.
local: Sɔ ye Yohane kaanri labari kpa n kereeri minɛm a, na wɔ jɔjɔ jɔrɛ dɔŋgu ase bu ka bu kaaki bu wo.

eng: Pilate wondered if Jesus was already dead. He called the centurion and asked him whether Jesus had already died.
local: Gomina Pilato tiiri kabo wɔ se ni, na wɔ yo alekutura kabo Yesu akaba awu ni. Na wɔ sunma afere soja kpiɛn n na i bisa i na i ti ka Yesu wuuri ni, wɔ cɛ?.

eng: He went to be enrolled together with Mary, who was promised to him in marriage and who was pregnant.
local: I ni Mariya bo ba fa ace i ni kɔɔri a. Nyini saŋga ni, Mariya bita nyinsɛ.

eng: But Herod the ruler had been criticized harshly by John because of Herodias, Herods brothers wife, and because of all the evil he had done.
local: Ama, na Yohane akan Fɛmɛ Herode ndiɛ ka wɔ fa i gɔrɔ yi Herodiya aja i hun ŋgɔɔ su, na wɔ kaaki ayo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn beberebe.

eng: When he learned from the centurion that Jesus was dead, Pilate gave the dead body to Joseph.
local: I tiiri soja n nɔaniɛ wieeri ni, na wɔ ma Yosefu atin ka i kɔ fa i ŋuniɛ ni.

eng: While they were there, the time came for Mary to have her baby.
local: Bu wo dɔ n na saŋga n aju bo Mariya wu baa.

eng: He added this to the list of his evil deeds: he locked John up in prison.
local: I satiɛ bo i ti tiɛ tara i kɛrɛ n la ka i taraari Yohane nyiiri i dan­saraka a.

eng: He bought a linen cloth, took Jesus down from the cross, wrapped him in the cloth, and laid him in a tomb that had been carved out of rock. He rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
local: Na Yosefu ato nzee, na wɔ yi fuin n baka jaanu n su, na wɔ fa nzee ni apɔɔndi i. Na wɔ kɔ afa i awura yabuɛ buɛ bo ba lokoti i ayo i faŋ­kunma n nu, na wɔ fa i ase nu, na wɔ kundu yabuɛ bambaka afa atɔ faŋ­kunma n anɔ ni.

eng: She gave birth to her firstborn child, a son, wrapped him snugly, and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the guestroom.
local: Ba nya má fofoe wɔfɔm daa­biri n nu bo bu da. Nyini ti, na ba kɔ awura deke bo bu fa neŋge­ndɔrɛm wura, dɔ na wɔ wu i suŋguru baa, baa­biɛsɔ. Na wɔ fa baa n apɔɔndi sanvuin nu, na wɔ fa i ada neŋge­ndɔrɛm diire dii­biri alaka nu.

eng: When everyone was being baptized, Jesus also was baptized. While he was praying, heaven was opened.
local: Saŋga bo Yohane biɛɛri minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, na wɔ biɛ Yesu gusu. Bu biɛɛri i wieeri, na i susere Nyɛmɛ ni, dɔ na aŋgoro n ateke.

eng: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was buried.
local: Na Mariya Magadala­fɔ ni, ni Mariya n bo i wa la Yose ni ajina aniɛ aŋu deke bo bu seeri Yesu ni.

eng: Nearby shepherds were living in the fields, guarding their sheep at night.
local: Nbɔɛ­sasa­fɔm nbem wo nyini awɔrɛ n nu dɔ suniɛ bu nbɔɛm dika, kɔŋguɛ.

eng: and the Holy Spirit came down on him in bodily form like a dove. And there was a voice from heaven: You are my Son, whom I dearly love; in you I find happiness.
local: na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ajura aba atana i ŋu ka tiiwa dɛɛ su. Na wɔ ti akonvi be ŋga aŋgoro, i ŋa, Wɔrɔ ti m wa a, m koro u agaya, m ahore ajɔ u ŋu.

eng: Jesus was about years old when he began his ministry. People supposed that he was the son of Joseph son of Heli.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu boori i juma n bu ni, nyini saŋga i la afɔ abura­san. Minɛ yo sunsun ka i si la Yosefu bo i ti Heli wa n wɔ, na Heli si la.

eng: The Lords angel stood before them, the Lords glory shone around them, and they were terrified.
local: na Micɛra n mɛrɛkɛ akaba atiɛ bu ŋu, na Micɛra n ama i wein ata abara ayɛ bu, na sɛrɛ ati bu agaya.

eng: son of Matthat son of Levi son of Melchi son of Jannai son of Joseph.
local: Matati, na Matati si la Levi, na Levi si la Mɛlɛki, na Mɛlɛki si la Yanayi, na Yanayi si la Yosefu nyiŋga n bo.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid! Look! I bring good news to you—wonderful, joyous news for all people.
local: ama, na mɛrɛkɛ n ase bu ka, Má am ma sɛrɛ ti am. Ma ba ka m ba ma am labari kpa a. Nyini labari ni, yiri má minɛm kɛrɛ ahore jɔ agaya.

eng: son of Mattathias son of Amos son of Nahum son of Esli son of Naggai.
local: i si la Matatiya ni, na Matatiya si la Amosi, na Amosi si la Nahumu, na Nahumu si la Esili, na Esili si la Nagayi.

eng: son of Maath son of Mattathias son of Semein son of Josech son of Joda.
local: na Nagayi si la Maati, na Maati si la Matatiya nyiŋga n bo i si la Sɛmɛni ni, na Sɛmɛni si la Yoseki, na Yoseki si la Yoda.

eng: Many people have already applied themselves to the task of compiling an account of the events that have been fulfilled among us.
local: Ya si Teyofilosi, minɛ dɔŋgu boroori bu nyi na ba kɛrɛ sam bo bu yoori ya nu fɛn n afa ase.

eng: Your savior is born today in Davids city. He is Christ the Lord.
local: Ahin kɔŋguɛ n nu bɛrɛ, ba wu am Tii­de­fɔ ni, Fɛmɛ Davidi bɔsu n nu, yiri ti Kristo n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i n a, am Mibiɛ ni.

eng: son of Joanan son of Rhesa son of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel son of Neri.
local: na Yoda si la Yowanani, na Yowanani si la Resa, na Resa si la Zorobabɛli, na Zorobabɛli si la Salatiyeli, na Salatiyeli si la Niɛri.

eng: They used what the original eyewitnesses and servants of the word handed down to us.
local: Kabo jɔrɛ n ti na bu kɛrɛɛri i ni, yiri ye minɛm seeri ya n a, nyini minɛm bo bu woori i bumboori ni, bɛrɛ ŋuuri i a, bɛrɛ kaanri a.

eng: This is a sign for you: you will find a newborn baby wrapped snugly and lying in a manger.
local: Deke bo i kere am na am sí nu n la ka, am kɔ́ ŋu ka ba fa i apɔɔndi sanvuin nu afa i ada neŋge­ndɔrɛm diire dii­biri alaka nu.

eng: son of Melchi son of Addi son of Cosam son of Elmadam son of Er.
local: na Niɛri si la Mɛlɛki nyiŋga n bo i si la Adi ni, na Adi si la Kosamu, na Kosamu si la Elimadamu, na Elimadamu si la Eri.

eng: Now, after having investigated everything carefully from the beginning, I have also decided to write a carefully ordered account for you, most honorable Theophilus.
local: Tɔ, kabo mini ni m tii ayi kɔrɛsi aniɛ sereya n kɛrɛ, fite saŋga n bo jɔrɛ n boori i bu ni, ma ŋu ka i ti kpa ka mini gusu kɛrɛ i kɛrɛ sɛsɛ i su na i kɔ atin su fa ma wɔrɔ Teyofilosi bo a la anyunuyi ni.

eng: Suddenly a great assembly of the heavenly forces was with the angel praising God. They said.
local: Jinaa­biri n nu bɛrɛ na mɛrɛkɛm beberebe akaba afite aŋgoro abuka nyini kun n su, na bu sukansi Nyɛmɛ, bu ŋa.

eng: son of Joshua son of Eliezer son of Jorim son of Matthat son of Levi.
local: na Eri si la Yosuwa, na Yosuwa si la Eliyɛza, na Eliyɛza si la Yorimu, na Yorimu si la Matati nyiŋga bekun, na nyini Matati n si la Levi nyiŋga bekun.

eng: I want you to have confidence in the soundness of the instruction you have received.
local: adena a si ka bo ba kan akere u n kɛrɛ ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: Glory to God in heaven, and on earth peace among those whom he favors.
local: Kansi wo Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n dɔ. Na ahoreda wo minɛm bo bu wo asiɛŋgu fɛn n dɔ na Nyɛmɛ ahore ajɔ bu ŋu ni.

eng: son of Simeon son of Judah son of Joseph son of Jonam son of Eliakim.
local: na nyini Levi n si la Simeyon, na Simeyon si la Yuda, na Yuda si la Yosefu nyiŋga n bo i si la Yonamu ni, na Yonamu si la Eliyakimu.

eng: During the rule of King Herod of Judea there was a priest named Zechariah who belonged to the priestly division of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was a descendant of Aaron.
local: Saŋga bo Herode yo ti Yudiya fɛmɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ be wo bɛrɛ, bu fere i ka Zakariya. I fite Abiya dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm asɛsɛ n nu wɔ. Bu fere i yi duma ka Elizabɛti, yiri mmɔɔ ti Aarɔn busu nu­fɔ ɔ.

eng: When the angels returned to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, Lets go right now to Bethlehem and see whats happened. Lets confirm what the Lord has revealed to us.
local: Saŋga bo mɛrɛkɛm n fiteeri bu dɔ kpieeri bu nyi kɔɔri aŋgoro ni, na nbɔɛ­sasa­fɔm ni ase bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Am ma ya kɔ Bɛtilɛhɛm miɛ n nu na ya kɔ ŋu deke n bo wɔ yo na Micɛra n ayi akere ya ni.

eng: son of Melea son of Menna son of Mattatha son of Nathan son of David.
local: na Eliyakimu si la Mɛliya, na Mɛliya si la Mɛnna, na Mɛnna si la Matata, na Matata si la Natani, na Natani si la Fɛmɛ Davidi.

eng: son of Jesse son of Obed son of Boaz son of Sala son of Nahshon.
local: na Fɛmɛ Davidi si la Jese, na Jese si la Yobedi, na Yobedi si la Boosi, na Boosi si la Sala, na Sala si la Naasoni.

eng: son of Amminadab son of Admin son of Arni son of Hezron son of Perez son of Judah.
local: na Naasoni si la Aminadabu, na Aminadabu si la Adimini, na Adimini si la Arani, na Arani si la Hɛsarom, na Hɛsarom si la Farɛsɛ, na Farɛsɛ si la Yuda nyiŋga n bo.

eng: They were both righteous before God, blameless in their observance of all the Lords commandments and regulations.
local: Bɛrɛ nnyɔm kɛrɛ yo ti minɛm bo bu su Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su wɔ, na bu deeri i nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, na bu yoori dekem kɛrɛ bo i se ka minɛm yo i ni.

eng: They went quickly and found Mary and Joseph, and the baby lying in the manger.
local: Nyini ti, na ba kɔ ndende, na ba kɔ aŋu Mariya ni Yosefu, na ba ŋu baa n gusu ka i da neŋge­ndɔrɛm diire dii­biri alaka n nu.

eng: son of Jacob son of Isaac son of Abraham son of Terah son of Nahor.
local: i si la Jakɔbu ni, na Jakɔbu si la Izaki, na Izaki si la Abraham, na Abraham si la Tara, na Tara si la Nakoro.

eng: They had no children because Elizabeth was unable to become pregnant and they both were very old.
local: Ama, ba wu má baa, dama Elizabɛti ti ŋɔrɔka a, na bekun i ni i hun n kɛrɛ ayo nkpiɛnm agaya.

eng: When they saw this, they reported what they had been told about this child.
local: Saŋga bo bu kɔ ŋuuri baa n ni, na ba kan nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ bo mɛrɛkɛ n fa maari bu fa kɔ baa n ŋusu jɔrɛ n akere minɛm.

eng: Everyone who heard it was amazed at what the shepherds told them.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu tiiri kabo nbɔɛ­sasa­fɔm seeri bu ni, n ayo alekutura.

eng: One day Zechariah was serving as a priest before God because his priestly division was on duty.
local: Cɛɛn n be, na Zakariya awo Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n Sɔɔ Casi n nu suyo i Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ juma, dama nyini cɛɛn n ti bu dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm cɛɛn wɔ.

eng: son of Serug son of Reu son of Peleg son of Eber son of Shelah.
local: na Nakoro si la Sɛruku, na Sɛruku si la Ragawu, na Ragawu si la Falɛki, na Falɛki si la Ebera, na Ebera si la Sala nyiŋga bekun.

eng: son of Cainan son of Arphaxad son of Shem son of Noah son of Lamech.
local: na nyini Sala n si la Keena, na Keena si la Arafakadi, na Arafakadi si la Semu, na Semu si la Nowa, na Nowa si la Lamɛki.

eng: son of Methuselah son of Enoch son of Jared son of Mahalalel son of Cainan.
local: na Lamɛki si la Matusala, na Matusala si la Henoki, na Henoki si la Yarɛti, na Yarɛti si la Malɛheeli, na Malɛheeli si la Keena nyiŋga bekun.

eng: Mary committed these things to memory and considered them carefully.
local: Na Mariya afa nyini sereya n kɛrɛ bo i yoori ni ase i ahore su.

eng: Following the customs of priestly service, he was chosen by lottery to go into the Lords sanctuary and burn incense.
local: Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm la bu nana sa­yoo­wa be. Bu tu mbaa a, na bu yi bu nu kun. Nyini cɛɛn ni, bu yiiri Zakariya a, ka i wura bu Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n Sɔɔ Casi n nu, na i kunkun ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani. Nyini ti, na wɔ kɔ awura nu.

eng: son of Enos son of Seth son of Adam son of God.
local: na Keena n si la Enosi, na Enosi si la Seti, na Seti si la Adam, na Adam si la Nyɛmɛ.

eng: The shepherds returned home, glorifying and praising God for all they had heard and seen. Everything happened just as they had been told.
local: Na nbɔɛ­sasa­fɔm n akpie bu nyi akɔ bu nbɔɛm n bi, na bu sukansi Nyɛmɛ na bu subo tuntun i fa kɔ deke n kɛrɛ bo ba ŋu i na ba ti i n dɛɛ ti. Deke n kɛrɛ yoori kabo mɛrɛkɛ n seeri bu n wɔ.

eng: All the people who gathered to worship were praying outside during this hour of incense offering.
local: Ayiri nva­nvani n kunkun saŋga ba juuri ni, na jama kɛrɛ aka gisan nu susere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: When eight days had passed, Jesus parents circumcised him and gave him the name Jesus. This was the name given to him by the angel before he was conceived.
local: Saŋga bo le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn n ba juuri ni, na ba wura baa n kɛnkɛnnɛ, na ba tun i duma ka Yesu. Duma n bo mɛrɛkɛ n fa maari i ka, na i ni nyinsɛɛri i n lɛɛ.

eng: An angel from the Lord appeared to him, standing to the right of the altar of incense.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ aba ayi i ŋu akere Zakariya, i jina Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri fɔmbɔrɔ su, deke bo wɔ kunkun ayiri nva­nvani n ni.

eng: When the time came for their ritual cleansing, in accordance with the Law from Moses, they brought Jesus up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. .
local: Na cɛɛn n aju bo Yosefu ni Mariya kɔ yo bu fin ŋmisi sa­yoo­wa kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i fa maari Moyisi n kereeri ka bu yo ni. Nyini ti, na ba fa baa n akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu na bu kɔ fa i wura Micɛra n saa nu.

eng: When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled and overcome with fear.
local: I ŋuuri mɛrɛkɛ n sɔ ni, na wɔ bo biri, na sɛrɛ ati i.

eng: Its written in the Law of the Lord, Every firstborn male will be dedicated to the Lord.
local: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Micɛra n nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu ni, ba kɛrɛ ka, Baa­biɛsɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti i ni suŋguru baa, i ti ye ni ka bu fa i kɔ ma Micɛra ni, na i yó i dɛɛ.

eng: They offered a sacrifice in keeping with whats stated in the Law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons .
local: Na ba kɔ ayo yoo­wa, kabo Micɛra n nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n akere ka bu yo i ni, bu faari bumbuku wara tiiwa nnyɔ yoori yoo­wa n a.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Zechariah. Your prayers have been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will give birth to your son and you must name him John.
local: Ama, na mɛrɛkɛ n ase i ka, Zakariya, má a ma sɛrɛ ti u. Nyɛmɛ ade u sere ni. U yi Elizabɛti bá wu baa­biɛsɔ, na a tún i duma ka Yohane.

eng: He will be a joy and delight to you, and many people will rejoice at his birth.
local: Baa n bo i wú i n dɛɛ ti, u ahore jɔ́ agaya kɔ sin su, na minɛ dɔŋgu gusu ahore jɔ́.

eng: for he will be great in the Lords eyes. He must not drink wine and liquor. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit even before his birth.
local: Baa ni bá yo sɔnɔ bambaka a Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Wɔ yo má daka ka i nu nzan fieo. Haari i ni kunnu, ye Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni wúra i ŋu a.

eng: A man named Simeon was in Jerusalem. He was righteous and devout. He eagerly anticipated the restoration of Israel, and the Holy Spirit rested on him.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, sɔnɔ be wo Yɛrusalem, bu fere i ka Simeyon. I yo ti sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, na i ti sɔnɔ bo i soro Nyɛmɛ, na i ŋminda fa kɔ saŋga bo Yisareli­fɔm nya bu tii­de n wɔ. Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo i ŋu.

eng: He will bring many Israelites back to the Lord their God.
local: Na i má Yisareli busu nu­fɔm dɔŋgu kpie bu nyi ba yiri Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n bo i ti ye ni ka bu su i n dɔ.

eng: The Holy Spirit revealed to him that he wouldnt die before he had seen the Lords Christ.
local: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama wɔ si ka i wú má, má ka i nyu­mbaa aba aŋu Kristo ni, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Micɛra n ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: He will go forth before the Lord, equipped with the spirit and power of Elijah. He will turn the hearts of fathers back to their children, and he will turn the disobedient to righteous patterns of thinking. He will make ready a people prepared for the Lord.
local: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n faŋga wó yiri ŋu ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Eliya a, yiri dú Micɛra nyunu a, adena i yó nbaasim ni bu nbaam afiɛn dɔŋgu a, na i káaki minɛ ketee­wam sunsunni, na bu yó minɛ kpam, na i sɛ́sɛ Nyɛmɛ minɛm su se i.

eng: Led by the Spirit, he went into the temple area. Meanwhile, Jesus parents brought the child to the temple so that they could do what was customary under the Law.
local: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama Simeyon akɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Nyini saŋga n ye Yesu si ni i ni gusu afa i akɔ ka bu kɔ yo yoo­wa kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n akere ka bu yo n a.

eng: Zechariah said to the angel, How can I be sure of this? My wife and I are very old.
local: Na Zakariya ase Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ n ka, Nzu deke ye m fá si ka jɔrɛ n bo a sujɔjɔ i ni yó nahɔrɛ a? Dama ma yo kpiɛn, m yi gusu ayo buke.

eng: Simeon took Jesus in his arms and praised God. He said.
local: Simeyon ŋuuri baa n sɔ ni, na wɔ kaba ade baa ni i saa nu, na wɔ kansi Nyɛmɛ, i ŋa.

eng: The angel replied, I am Gabriel. I stand in Gods presence. I was sent to speak to you and to bring this good news to you.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ase i ka, Bu fere m ka Gabiriyeli. Mini wo Nyɛmɛ nyunu a. Ye wɔ sunma m u dɔ ka m fa ahin labari kpa n ba ma u a.

eng: Now, master, let your servant go in peace according to your word.
local: M Mibiɛ, u nɔaniɛ n bo a yo seeri m ni, ayo yii­wa, ma mini u akɔɔ n atin kisa na m fíte durunya n nu ni ahoreda.

eng: Know this: What I have spoken will come true at the proper time. But because you didnt believe, you will remain silent, unable to speak until the day when these things happen.
local: Ti fɛn. Nɔaniɛ n bo m sujɔjɔ i ni bá yo nahɔrɛ wɔ, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i yo ni. Ama, a yo má m nɔaniɛ n yarada dɛɛ ti, a tɔ́ amuna, na a kóro jɔjɔ má haari cɛɛn bo a bá ŋu ka nɔaniɛ n bo m sujɔjɔ i n ayo nahɔrɛ.

eng: because my eyes have seen your salvation.
local: Dama kisa mini ni m tii nyu­mbaa aŋu Tii­de­fɔ ni.

eng: Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and they wondered why he was in the sanctuary for such a long time.
local: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ minɛm n toko jina gisan nu ŋminda Zakariya, na ba birisi deke bo i ti na wɔ cɛ Sɔɔ Casi n nu sɔ ni.

eng: You prepared this salvation in the presence of all peoples.
local: yiri ye a sɛsɛ i su, ye a ma minɛm kɛrɛ nyu­mbaa aŋu i a.

eng: When he came out, he was unable to speak to them. They realized he had seen a vision in the temple, for he gestured to them and couldnt speak.
local: Saŋga bo i ba fiteeri ni, wɔ koro ajɔjɔ má bu dɔ. Dɔ na ba fa nu ka wɔ ŋu alekutura deke be Sɔɔ Casi n nu. Dama wɔ koro ateke má i nɔɔ ajɔjɔ má, se i fa i saa jɔjɔ bu dɔ a.

eng: Its a light for revelation to the Gentiles and a glory for your people Israel.
local: Yiri ti wein n a, na i kére minɛm u atin ni, bɛrɛ bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, na i wúra Yisareli­fɔm kpiri, bɛrɛ bo bu ti u dɛɛ minɛm ni.

eng: When he completed the days of his priestly service, he returned home.
local: Saŋga bo Zakariya ba yoori i juma kɛrɛ wieeri Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni, na wɔ kpie i nyi akɔ i bɔsu.

eng: His father and mother were amazed by what was said about him.
local: Simeyon jɔjɔɔri baa n jɔrɛ sɔ ni, na i si ni i ni ayo alekutura.

eng: Afterward, his wife Elizabeth became pregnant. She kept to herself for five months, saying.
local: Wɔ cɛ má, na i yi Elizabɛti anyinsɛ. Wɔ yi má i ŋu fofoe nu haari sara nnu.

eng: Simeon blessed them and said to Mary his mother, This boy is assigned to be the cause of the falling and rising of many in Israel and to be a sign that generates opposition.
local: Na Simeyon asere Nyɛmɛ, na wɔ yo bu kisi. Na wɔ se baa n ni ka, Ahin baa n ye Nyɛmɛ ayi i ka i má Yisareli­fɔm dɔŋgu kpaasi, na dɔŋgu gusu yásu n a. Yiri yó deke bo i ti alekutura sa, na minɛm dɔŋgu bá kete i.

eng: This is the Lords doing. He has shown his favor to me by removing my disgrace among other people.
local: Na wɔ se ka, Kisa, ya Micɛra n aniɛ aŋu m aŋunfɔ. Wɔ yi m afite nyinsɛ bambaka nu.

eng: so that the inner thoughts of many will be revealed. And a sword will pierce your innermost being too.
local: Sɔ ye i bá yi bu asiri nu sunsunni tiɛm kɛrɛ fite fofoe nu a. Na yiri ti, u ahore sáaki agaya, kabo takobe kpiɛ sɔnɔ na i yo ya ni.

eng: When Elizabeth was six months pregnant, God sent the angel Gabriel to Nazareth, a city in Galilee.
local: Saŋga bo Elizabɛti nyinsɛ n ba juuri sara nsɛn ni, na Nyɛmɛ asunma mɛrɛkɛ Gabiriyeli miɛ be bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu, na bu fere i ka Nazarɛti ni.

eng: There was also a prophet, Anna the daughter of Phanuel, who belonged to the tribe of Asher. She was very old. After she married, she lived with her husband for seven years.
local: Nyini saŋga n nu nɔ ɔ, na bara be, i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ, na i gusu wo dɔ, na wɔ ba baa n bi. I duma la Anna, na bu fere i si ka Fanuwɛli, Aseri dɛɛ busu nu­fɔa. Bara n ti buke wɔ nahɔrɛ su, i jaari i hun, bu afɔ nso ye i kaari kuna­fɔ a. Nyini saŋga ni, i la afɔ abura­mɔcuɛ ni nna. Wɔ kpaaki ayaki má Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni, i cici i nɔɔ sɔ ɔ, na i kɔ susere Nyɛmɛ, wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ. I ba juuri baa n bi ni, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi na yiri gusu ajɔjɔ baa n jɔrɛ akere minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ŋminda ka Nyɛmɛ sunma Tii­de­fɔ n na i de Yisareli­fɔm tii ji ni.

eng: to a virgin who was engaged to a man named Joseph, a descendant of Davids house. The virgins name was Mary.
local: ka i kɔ fa labari ma suŋguru be bo bu fere i ka Mariya ni. Nyini saŋga, ba fa i ace biɛsɔ be, bu fere i ka Yosefu, i fite Fɛmɛ Davidi dɛɛ busu n nu wɔ. I ni Mariya nya saŋga da má, dama wɔ nya ja má i.

eng: When Mary and Joseph had completed everything required by the Law of the Lord, they returned to their hometown, Nazareth in Galilee.
local: Saŋga bo Yosefu ni Mariya yoori yoo­wa n kɛrɛ bo Micɛra n nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka bu yo n wieeri ni, na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ bu bɔsu Nazarɛti bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni.

eng: When the angel came to her, he said, Rejoice, favored one! The Lord is with you.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n awura i ŋu, na wɔ se i ka, Suŋguru, m yo u ansɛ ni tere­fiɛ. A di Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma, na i kaaki wo u dɔ.

eng: The child grew up and became strong. He was filled with wisdom, and Gods favor was on him.
local: Na baa n anyi, na wɔ nya faŋga ni alecira, na Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma awo i dɔ agaya.

eng: She was confused by these words and wondered what kind of greeting this might be.
local: Mariya tiiri ahin nɔaniɛ n ni, na wɔ yo i alekutura, na wɔ birisi ase ka, Ahin ansɛ yoo­wa n bu la ka sɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Each year his parents went to Jerusalem for the Passover Festival.
local: Afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ, Yesu si ni i ni kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na bu kɔ di Butara cɛɛn­kpiri, bo bu di i na bu teŋge su kabo Nyɛmɛ yo deeri bu nam tii fiteeri Ijipiti­fɔm asiɛn su ni.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Mary. God is honoring you.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ase i ka, Mariya, má a ma sɛrɛ ti u. A di Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma beberebe.

eng: When he was years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to their custom.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ba juuri afɔ buru ni nnyɔ ni, na ba kɔ na bu kɔ di cɛɛn­kpiri n kabo bu yo afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ ni.

eng: Look! You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will name him Jesus.
local: So u sui na a ti. M ŋa a bá nyinsɛ, na a wú baa­biɛsɔ, na a tún i duma ka Yesu.

eng: He will be great and he will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of David his father.
local: I bá yo sɔnɔ bambaka, na bu fere i ka, Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri n Wa. Ya Micɛra Nyɛmɛ fá i na Davidi fɛmɛya biɛ ma i.

eng: After the festival was over, they were returning home, but the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. His parents didnt know it.
local: Bu ba diiri cɛɛn­kpiri n wieeri na bu sukpie bu nyi kɔ awuru ni, na Yesu yiri aka Yɛrusalem dɔ. I si ni i ni asi má ka wɔ ka dɔ.

eng: He will rule over Jacobs house forever, and there will be no end to his kingdom.
local: na i yó Jakɔbu busu nu­fɔm fɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ, na i dɛɛ fɛmɛya ni lá má awieeri.

eng: Supposing that he was among their band of travelers, they journeyed on for a full day while looking for him among their family and friends.
local: Bu sunsunni la ka i wo abeŋgu natiiri­fɔm n nu wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, bu wo kɔ su wɔ haari wiɛ kɔ sutɔ. Dɔ na ba bo i bu suniɛniɛ i, bu lamɔsim ni bu danfom nu.

eng: Then Mary said to the angel, How will this happen since I havent had sexual relations with a man.
local: Na Mariya abisa mɛrɛkɛ n ka, Ye ahin yó sɛ koro yo a? Dama m nya si má biɛsɔ.

eng: When they didnt find Jesus, they returned to Jerusalem to look for him.
local: Ama, ba ŋu má i, na ba nya kpie bu nyi akɔ Yɛrusalem na bu kɔ niɛniɛ i.

eng: The angel replied, The Holy Spirit will come over you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore, the one who is to be born will be holy. He will be called Gods Son.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n abɔ i nu ka, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni bá wura u ŋu, na Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n bá wo u dɔ. Nyini ti, baa n bo a wú i ni, i bá yo baa casi wɔ, na bu fére i ka Nyɛmɛ Wa.

eng: After three days they found him in the temple. He was sitting among the teachers, listening to them and putting questions to them.
local: I le­san cɛɛn na ba kɔ aŋu ka i tana Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm afiɛn suso i sui, na i subisa bisaa­wam.

eng: Look, even in her old age, your relative Elizabeth has conceived a son. This woman who was labeled unable to conceive is now six months pregnant.
local: Niɛ fɛn, Elizabɛti bo i ti u lamɔkɔ ni, yiri bo bu ŋa i ti ŋɔrɔka ni, haari ni bo wɔ yo buke ni, wɔ nyinsɛ, i sara nsɛn lɛɛ.

eng: Everyone who heard him was amazed by his understanding and his answers.
local: Na minɛm bo bu tiiri i nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ ayo alekutura, fa kɔ kabo i la si na i bɔ nɔaniɛ bisaa­wam nu barasu ni.

eng: Nothing is impossible for God.
local: Pui wo má bɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro yo má i.

eng: Then Mary said, I am the Lords servant. Let it be with me just as you have said. Then the angel left her.
local: Na Mariya ase i ka, Tɔ, m ti Micɛra n afani wɔ. Ma nyini sam kɛrɛ yo ma m kabo a se ni. Na mɛrɛkɛ n akɔ ayaki i.

eng: When his parents saw him, they were shocked. His mother said, Child, why have you treated us like this? Listen! Your father and I have been worried. Weve been looking for you.
local: Na saŋga bo i si ni i ni ba ŋuuri i n na ba miɛ, na i ni abisa i ka, M wa, nzu ti ye a yo ya sɔ a? A ma mini ni u si ahore asaaki akɔ asin su, haari ya nati wo u niɛniɛ su.

eng: Mary got up and hurried to a city in the Judean highlands.
local: Nyini dɛɛ sin, wɔ cɛ má, na Mariya akpa i ŋu, na wɔ yasu ndende akɔ Zakariya ni i yi Elizabɛti bɔsu ka i kɔ niɛ bu busu. Nyini miɛ n wo Yudiya asiɛn n su bukam n afiɛn wɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Why were you looking for me? Didnt you know that it was necessary for me to be in my Fathers house.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nzu ti ye am nati niɛniɛ m busu a? Am si má ka wɔ yo daka ka m wo m Si awuru n nu wɔ.

eng: She entered Zechariahs home and greeted Elizabeth.
local: Na wɔ kɔ awura Zakariya dika, na wɔ yo Elizabɛti ansɛ.

eng: When Elizabeth heard Marys greeting, the child leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Elizabɛti tiiri Mariya ansɛ yoo­wa n ni, na baa n bo i wo i kunnu n akpisi i ŋu. Dɔ na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura Elizabɛti ŋu.

eng: But they didnt understand what he said to them.
local: Ama, ba ti má nɔaniɛ n bo i seeri bu nu n bu.

eng: With a loud voice she blurted out, God has blessed you above all women, and he has blessed the child you carry.
local: na wɔ bo awie kpa, i ŋa, Nyɛmɛ ayo u kisi atara nbaram kɛrɛ, wɔ kaaki ayo baa n bo a bita i nyinsɛ n kisi.

eng: Why do I have this honor, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?.
local: Nzu jirima la ahin n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama m n a, ka m Mibiɛ dɛɛ ni aba aniɛ m busu a?.

eng: As soon as I heard your greeting, the baby in my womb jumped for joy.
local: Dama m tiiri u ansɛ yoo­wa n na baa n bo i wo m kunnu ni, akaba akpisi i ŋu ni akunnujɔ.

eng: Jesus went down to Nazareth with them and was obedient to them. His mother cherished every word in her heart.
local: Na Yesu asu bu su, na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ Nazarɛti miɛ n nu, na wɔ de bu nɔaniɛ. Na i ni afa nyini sam kɛrɛ ase i ahore su.

eng: Happy is she who believed that the Lord would fulfill the promises he made to her.
local: Tere­fiɛ wo u dɔ, dama a yo yarada ka Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ n bo wɔ kan akere u ni, i bá yo yii­wa aŋgba.

eng: Mary said, With all my heart I glorify the Lord!.
local: Na Mariya ase ka, M kansi Mibiɛ ni m ahore­mbaa su.

eng: Jesus matured in wisdom and years, and in favor with God and with people.
local: Na Yesu sunyi, na i alecira subuka su, na i sa­yoo­wam susun Nyɛmɛ ni adamande kɛrɛ nu.

eng: In the depths of who I am I rejoice in God my savior.
local: M ahore ajɔ Nyɛmɛ ŋu, yiri bo i ti m Tii­de­fɔ ni.

eng: He has looked with favor on the low status of his servant. Look! From now on, everyone will consider me highly favored.
local: Haari ni bo m ti i afani n kɛrɛ ni, i wara afi má m sa. Fite nyuma fa kɔ, minɛm kɛrɛ bá fere m ka tere­fiɛ­fɔ.

eng: because the mighty one has done great things for me. Holy is his name.
local: Dama ka Micɛra yiko kɛrɛ fɔ ni ayo sakpa beberebe ama m, i duma ti casi.

eng: He shows mercy to everyone, from one generation to the next, who honors him as God.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga i si aŋunfɔ ɔ, i si minɛm bo bu soro i n kɛrɛ aŋunfɔ ɔ.

eng: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered those with arrogant thoughts and proud inclinations.
local: Wɔ fa i yiko asaa ni, afɔn asandi karambani­fɔm, ni bu sunsunni tiɛ n kɛrɛ.

eng: He has pulled the powerful down from their thrones and lifted up the lowly.
local: Wɔ yi nfɛmɛ bambakam afite bu fɛmɛya biɛm n su, na wɔ yɛ tarakam saa su.

eng: He has filled the hungry with good things and sent the rich away empty-handed.
local: Wɔ ma ahɔɛ­fɔm neŋge kpakpam na bu kunnu ayi, na wɔ fɔn neŋge­fɔm na ba ka bu saa ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: He has come to the aid of his servant Israel, remembering his mercy.
local: Sɛsɛ­su n bo i yo faari na i diiri i di ni ya nam ni, wɔ ma i ayo yii­wa, ye wɔ ba abuka Yisareli­fɔm bo bu su i ni. I wara afi má Abraham sa. Wɔ si i ni i sin­fɔm kɛrɛ aŋunfɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her home.
local: Na Mariya aka Elizabɛti bi, sara nsan, na wɔ nya aba akaaki akɔ i bɔsu.

eng: When the time came for Elizabeth to have her child, she gave birth to a boy.
local: Elizabɛti kutu cɛɛn ni, na wɔ wu baa­biɛsɔ.

eng: Her neighbors and relatives celebrated with her because they had heard that the Lord had shown her great mercy.
local: Na Elizabɛti tana­fɔm, ni i lamɔsim tiiri ka Micɛra n ayo i sakpa bo i la má aŋusu ni, na bu ni yiri kɛrɛ kunnu ajɔ.

eng: On the eighth day, it came time to circumcise the child. They wanted to name him Zechariah because that was his fathers name.
local: Baa n le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn n ba juuri ni, na ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ na bu wura i kɛnkɛnnɛ, na bu tun i duma. Má bu koro ka bu fa i si Zakariya duma fa tun i a.

eng: But his mother replied, No, his name will be John.
local: Ama, na i ni ase ka, Ai, bu fére i ka Yohane wɔ.

eng: They said to her, None of your relatives have that name.
local: Na ba se i ka, U lamɔsi nu, bu fere ŋma sɔ a.

eng: Then they began gesturing to his father to see what he wanted to call him.
local: Na ba fa bu saa ajɔjɔ baa n si n dɔ, na bu si duma bo i koro ka bu fa tun i wa ni.

eng: After asking for a tablet, he surprised everyone by writing, His name is John.
local: Dɔ na Zakariya afa i saa akere ka, bu má i deke be na i kɛrɛ, na wɔ de i na wɔ kɛrɛ ka, I duma la Yohane. Na nyini jɔrɛ n ayo bu kɛrɛ alekutura.

eng: At that moment, Zechariah was able to speak again, and he began praising God.
