local: Jina n nu bɛrɛ, na Nyɛmɛ ateke i nɔɔ na wɔ bo i bu ajɔjɔ bekun, na i sukansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: All their neighbors were filled with awe, and everyone throughout the Judean highlands talked about what had happened.
local: Na sɛrɛ ati bu tana­fɔm kɛrɛ. Na minɛm akan nyini sa n bo i yoori n jɔrɛ abara ayɛ Yudiya awɔrɛ n bukam n kɛrɛ su.

eng: All who heard about this considered it carefully. They said, What then will this child be? Indeed, the Lords power was with him.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu tiiri i n afa jɔrɛ n ase bu ahore su na bu susunsun, na ba bisa bu ŋu ka, Baa n ba nyiiri, i yó nzu baa lɛɛ? Bu seeri sɔ ni, dama bu kɛrɛ aŋu ka Micɛra n yiko n wo i ŋu.

eng: Johns father Zechariah was filled with the Holy Spirit and prophesied.
local: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura baa n si Zakariya ŋu, na wɔ kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ, i ŋa.

eng: Bless the Lord God of Israel because he has come to help and has delivered his people.
local: Am ma ya kansi ya Micɛra ni, yiri bo i ti Yisareli­fɔm Nyɛmɛ ni. Dama ka wɔ ba aniɛ i minɛm busu, na wɔ de bu tii aji.

eng: He has raised up a mighty savior for us in his servant Davids house.
local: Wɔ ma ya tii­de­fɔ faŋga­fɔ, i fite i amusui­su­fɔ Fɛmɛ Davidi dɛɛ busu n nu wɔ.

eng: just as he said through the mouths of his holy prophets long ago.
local: Nyɛmɛ yo maari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ casim jɔjɔɔri dawa saŋga.

eng: He has brought salvation from our enemies and from the power of all those who hate us.
local: ka i bá de ya tii fite ya kpɔ­fɔm, ni bo bu koro má ya n saa nu.

eng: He has shown the mercy promised to our ancestors, and remembered his holy covenant.
local: Sɔ ye i kereeri ka i si ya nam aŋunfɔ, na i wara fi má i sɛsɛ­su casi n sa a.

eng: the solemn pledge he made to our ancestor Abraham. He has granted.
local: I yo faari nyini sɛsɛ­su n na wɔ di i di ni ya na Abraham ka i de ya tii fite ya kpɔ­fɔm saa nu, adena ya koro su i bo ya soro má sɛrɛ.

eng: in holiness and righteousness in Gods eyes, for as long as we live.
local: na ya yo casi na ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin n su i dɔ, haari ni ya ŋgɔɔ awieeri.

eng: You, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the Lord to prepare his way.
local: Wɔrɔ, m wa ni, bu bá fere u ka Micɛra Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ. Dama a dú ya Micɛra n mɔ na a kɔ́ sɛsɛ i atin su ŋminda i.

eng: You will tell his people how to be saved through the forgiveness of their sins.
local: Na a má i minɛm si ka i fá bu satiɛ ce bu n dɛɛ ti, ye i dé bu tii ji a.

eng: Because of our Gods deep compassion, the dawn from heaven will break upon us.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ si ya aŋunfɔ agaya n dɛɛ ti, i má aŋgoro wein n ta ma ya. Nyini wein n ti kabo wiɛ fite na i ti n wɔ.

eng: to give light to those who are sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide us on the path of peace.
local: na i má wein ta ma minɛm bo bu wo awosin nu na bu soro yiwee sɛrɛ ni. Na i bá kere ya atin bo i ma ahoreda.

eng: The child grew up, becoming strong in character. He was in the wilderness until he began his public ministry to Israel.
local: Na baa n anyi, na Nyɛmɛ ayo i sunsunni faŋga, na wɔ kɔ awo boro kpantain nu dɔ i ŋumi, haari cɛɛn n bo i ba yiiri i ŋu fiteeri fofoe nu kere Yisareli­fɔm ni.

eng: Jesus returned from the Jordan River full of the Holy Spirit, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness.
local: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n woori Yesu ŋu agaya, na wɔ fite Yodan kunma n awɔrɛ n nu, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa i akɔ boro kpantain n nu kɔ sukere i atin.

eng: There he was tempted for forty days by the devil. He ate nothing during those days and afterward Jesus was starving.
local: I woori dɔ cɛɛn abura­na, na Sitana ayo i na i niɛ ka, i yó yiri Sitana n dɛɛ koroo­wa, nzɛn i yó má i. Nyini cɛɛn abura­na n kɛrɛ ni, wɔ di má pui, nyini dɛɛ sin, na ahɔɛ aba ati i.

eng: The devil said to him, Since you are Gods Son, command this stone to become a loaf of bread.
local: Na Sitana n ase i ka, Nzɛn a ti Nyɛmɛ Wa, se ahin yabuɛ n na i kaaki diire.

eng: Jesus replied, Its written, People wont live only by bread .
local: Ama, na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, má diire ŋgumi ma sɔnɔ ŋgɔɔ a.

eng: Next the devil led him to a high place and showed him in a single instant all the kingdoms of the world.
local: Na Sitana afa i afun akɔ aŋgoro lenleŋge, na i nyu­mbaa­mbaa n nu bɛrɛ, na wɔ kere i durunya n nu asiɛn n kɛrɛ.

eng: The devil said, I will give you this whole domain and the glory of all these kingdoms. Its been entrusted to me and I can give it to anyone I want.
local: Na wɔ se i ka, Wɔrɔ ye m má a niɛ ahin asiɛn n kɛrɛ su a, na m fá bu ajɛkɛm kɛrɛ ma u, dama Nyɛmɛ afa i kɛrɛ awura m saa nu, na mini gusu koro fa ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo m koro.

eng: Therefore, if you will worship me, it will all be yours.
local: Nzɛn a kutu suuri m, m fá nyinim kɛrɛ ma u.

eng: Jesus answered, Its written, You will worship the Lord your God and serve only him. .
local: Na Yesu ase i bekun ka, Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Kutu Micɛra u Nyɛmɛ ni, na a su yiri ŋgumi.

eng: The devil brought him into Jerusalem and stood him at the highest point of the temple. He said to him, Since you are Gods Son, throw yourself down from here.
local: Na Sitana n akaaki afa Yesu akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na wɔ fa i akɔ ajina Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n tii su lenleŋge, na wɔ se i ka, Nzɛn a ti Nyɛmɛ Wa, fite fɛn ti tɔ asiɛŋgu na m niɛ.

eng: for its written: He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you.
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ má i mɛrɛkɛm nɔɔ ka bu niɛ u dika na bu kanda u.

eng: and they will take you up in their hands so that you wont hit your foot on a stone. .
local: Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i ka, Bu fá bu saa so u nu, na má u jaa bo yabuɛ be.

eng: Jesus answered, Its been said, Dont test the Lord your God. .
local: Ama, na Yesu akaaki ase i ka, Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Má a yo Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n na a niɛ kabo i yiko ti barasu.

eng: After finishing every temptation, the devil departed from him until the next opportunity.
local: Saŋga bo Sitana n yoori Yesu yoo­wa barasu n kɛrɛ na i niɛ i ni, na wɔ kɔ ayaki Yesu ka.

eng: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news about him spread throughout the whole countryside.
local: Na Yesu akpie i nyi akɔ Galili awɔrɛ n nu, ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu. Na i labari abara ayɛ nyini awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ pepere nu.

eng: He taught in their synagogues and was praised by everyone.
local: Na wɔ kere minɛm Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ akansi i.

eng: Jesus went to Nazareth, where he had been raised. On the Sabbath he went to the synagogue as he normally did and stood up to read.
local: Na Yesu akɔ Nazarɛti miɛ n nu, dɔ ye bu taari i a, na wɔ nyi, na Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n be, na wɔ kɔ bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, kabo i nati kɔ ni. Na wɔ yasu ajina ka i kan Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi ni.

eng: The synagogue assistant gave him the scroll from the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the place where it was written.
local: na ba fa Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya dɛɛ kadasi n ama i. Na wɔ teke i, na wɔ niɛ aŋu awɔrɛ n bo ba kɛrɛ i nu ka.

eng: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me . He has sent me to preach good news to the poor, to proclaim release to the prisoners and recovery of sight to the blind, to liberate the oppressed.
local: Micɛra n Awiɛwiɛ n wo m dɔ, dama wɔ yi m ka m kan labari kpa n kere yaari­fɔm. Na wɔ sunma m ka m kɔ minɛm bo ba tara bu domuɛm n dɔ, na m se bu ka m bá de bu tii ji, na m kɔ anyinsinm dɔ, na m se bu ka m bá teke bu nyu­mbaa, na m kɔ minɛm bo bu bita bu miɛ n dɔ, na m se bu ka m bá de bu tii ji.

eng: and to proclaim the year of the Lords favor.
local: na m bo ndoori ka, afɔ n nu Micɛra ni má i minɛm di i ahɔrɔma.

eng: He rolled up the scroll, gave it back to the synagogue assistant, and sat down. Every eye in the synagogue was fixed on him.
local: Na Yesu akan awie, na wɔ kata Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n su, na wɔ fa ama i bita­fɔ ni, na wɔ tana. Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu n afa bu nyu­mbaa akpama i ŋu.

eng: He began to explain to them, Today, this scripture has been fulfilled just as you heard it.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nyuma dɛɛ cɛɛn n nu, ahin Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n bo ma kan i na am ati i ni, ayo yii­wa.

eng: Everyone was raving about Jesus, so impressed were they by the gracious words flowing from his lips. They said, This is Josephs son, isnt it.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ akan kpa ama i, na ba yo alekutura kabo i nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n ti kpa barasu ni, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Má Yosefu wa n lɛɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Undoubtedly, you will quote this saying to me: Doctor, heal yourself. Do here in your hometown what weve heard you did in Capernaum.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, M si tenle ka am bó m ahin anyundɛrɛ ni ka, Ayiri­fɔ, tuntun u ŋu. Na am sé m ka, Sam n bo ya tiiri ka a yoori bu Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu ni, yo bu u bɔsu fɛn gusu na ya niɛ.

eng: He said, I assure you that no prophet is welcome in the prophets hometown.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ la má jirima haari kaan sɔ mmɔɔ i bɔsu­fɔm dɔ.

eng: And I can assure you that there were many widows in Israel during Elijahs time, when it didnt rain for three and a half years and there was a great food shortage in the land.
local: Am teŋge su kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Eliya dɛɛ saŋga n na nzue atɔ má afɔ nsan ni kpaaki nu, na ahɔɛ bambaka tɔɔri ni. I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka, nyini saŋga, kuna­fɔm beberebe woori Yisareli asiɛn n su.

eng: Yet Elijah was sent to none of them but only to a widow in the city of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
local: ama, Nyɛmɛ asunma má Eliya nyini kuna­fɔm be dɔ, se i sunmaari i kuna­fɔ kun be dɔ a, i wo miɛ be bo bu fere i ka Zarɛfati na i ti má Yuda­fɔm miɛ kpiri n wɔ, i wo Sidon dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu wɔ.

eng: There were also many persons with skin diseases in Israel during the time of the prophet Elisha, but none of them were cleansed. Instead, Naaman the Syrian was cleansed.
local: Sasu bekun, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Elisa dɛɛ saŋga, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka kokobi yo taraari minɛm beberebe Yisareli asiɛn n su, ama, Nyɛmɛ ama wɔ tuntun má bu kun n kpein mmɔɔ, se i tuntuunri koko­fɔ kun be wɔ, bu fere i ka Naama, i ti má Yuda­fɔ, i fite Siriya awɔrɛ nu wɔ.

eng: When they heard this, everyone in the synagogue was filled with anger.
local: Minɛm bo bu wo sɔɔ n nu n tiiri sɔ ni, na yaa ati bu agaya.

eng: They rose up and ran him out of town. They led him to the crest of the hill on which their town had been built so that they could throw him off the cliff.
local: Na ba yasu aso i nu, acin i afite miɛ n nu akɔ kpɛrɛ n bo miɛ n wo su ni, ka bu fa i ji buka n bu asiɛŋgu.

eng: But he passed through the crowd and went on his way.
local: Ama, na wɔ nati asin jama n afiɛn akɔ.

eng: Jesus went down to the city of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people each Sabbath.
local: Dɔ na wɔ kɔ miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Kapɛnam na i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni, na Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, na wɔ kɔ awura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu sukere minɛm.

eng: They were amazed by his teaching because he delivered his message with authority.
local: Na ba yo alekutura fa kɔ i keree­wa n su, dama i jɔjɔɔ­wa n la yiko.

eng: A man in the synagogue had the spirit of an unclean demon. He screamed.
local: Na sɔnɔ be wo tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu dɔ, ajini wo i ŋu, na wɔ bo awie kpa ase ka.

eng: Hey! What have you to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are. You are the holy one from God.
local: Oi, Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ, a koro nzu ya nu fɛn a? A ba ka a ba yo ya ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? M si u sa, wɔrɔ ti sɔnɔ casi bo Nyɛmɛ asunma u n a.

eng: Silence! Jesus said, speaking harshly to the demon. Come out of him! The demon threw the man down before them, then came out of him without harming him.
local: Na Yesu atiɛn ajini n ŋu ase ka, Mɔ u nɔɔ bɛrɛ na a fite yaki sɔnɔ ni. Na ajini n akpisi sɔnɔ n abo asiɛŋgu jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, na ba fite ayaki sɔnɔ ni, ba ma má i mandim be.

eng: They were all shaken and said to each other, What kind of word is this, that he can command unclean spirits with authority and power, and they leave.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ ayo alekutura, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Nzu alekutura nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n la ahin? Dama i la yiko nahɔrɛ su bo i koro ma ajinim nɔɔ na bu fite yaki sɔnɔ.

eng: Reports about him spread everywhere in the surrounding region.
local: Na i labari n abara ayɛ nyini awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: After leaving the synagogue, Jesus went home with Simon. Simons mother-in-law was sick with a high fever, and the family asked Jesus to help her.
local: Na Yesu afite tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na wɔ kɔ awura Simon dika, na ba se i ka Simon sibara la má i ŋu, yakɔkɔ abo i agaya.

eng: He bent over her and spoke harshly to the fever, and it left her. She got up at once and served them.
local: Na Yesu akɔ ajina i bi, na wɔ ma yakɔkɔ n nɔɔ ka i fite yaki bara ni. Na wɔ fite ayaki i, na bara n akaba ayasu, na wɔ kɔ ayo diire ama bu.

eng: When the sun was setting, everyone brought to Jesus relatives and acquaintances with all kinds of diseases. Placing his hands on each of them, he healed them.
local: Na naasɔ dika bibiri saŋga, na minɛm n afa tukpaki barasu barasu fɔm kɛrɛ aba Yesu dɔ ka i tuntun bu, na wɔ fa i saa anana bu ŋu, na bu kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

eng: Demons also came out of many people. They screamed, You are Gods Son. But he spoke harshly to them and wouldnt allow them to speak because they recognized that he was the Christ.
local: Na wɔ fɔn ayi ajinim n gusu afite minɛm beberebe ŋu, na ajinim n acindi, na ba se i ka, Wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ Wa n a. Ama, na Yesu akan ajinim n ndiɛ, wɔ du má su na bu jɔjɔ, dama bu si ka yiri ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: When daybreak arrived, Jesus went to a deserted place. The crowds were looking for him. When they found him, they tried to keep him from leaving them.
local: Ale ba ciinri, na Yesu afite miɛ n nu akɔ awo awɔrɛ be nu i ŋgumi. Na minɛm n akɔ i niɛniɛ, na saŋga bo bu kɔ ŋuuri i ni, na ba kpini atin kabo bu yo na má i kpaaki yaki bu.

eng: But he said to them, I must preach the good news of Gods kingdom in other cities too, for this is why I was sent.
local: Ama, na wɔ se bu ka, Wɔ yo daka ka m kɔ miɛ nyiŋgam gusu su na m kɔ kan labari kpa fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n su kere bu gusu, dama ka nyini ti ye Nyɛmɛ sunmaari m a.

eng: So he continued preaching in the Judean synagogues.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ bara ayɛ sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ni, tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu bu awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: One day Jesus was standing beside Lake Gennesaret when the crowd pressed in around him to hear Gods word.
local: Cɛɛn be na Yesu jina Gɛnɛsarɛti kunma kpiri n nɔɔ su, na jama beberebe abara ayɛ i, na bu susun bu beŋgu ka bu ju i bi na bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Jesus saw two boats sitting by the lake. The fishermen had gone ashore and were washing their nets.
local: Dɔ na wɔ ŋu lee nnyɔ nbem ka bu wo kunma n nɔɔ su. Jombaa tara­fɔm n afite ayaki leem wo bu diɛndiɛm ŋmisi su.

eng: Jesus boarded one of the boats, the one that belonged to Simon, then asked him to row out a little distance from the shore. Jesus sat down and taught the crowds from the boat.
local: Na Yesu awura lee n kun nu, nyini lee n ti Simon dɛɛ wɔ, na Yesu ase Simon ka i sun lee n kɔ kaan. Na Yesu atana lee n nu, na i sukere jama ni.

eng: When he finished speaking to the crowds, he said to Simon, Row out farther, into the deep water, and drop your nets for a catch.
local: Saŋga bo i kere wieeri ni, na wɔ se Simon ka, Sun lee n na i kɔ ŋgoŋgo n nu, na am sandi am diɛndiɛm na am tara jombaa.

eng: Simon replied, Master, weve worked hard all night and caught nothing. But because you say so, Ill drop the nets.
local: Na Simon ase Yesu ka, M Mɔfɔ, ya yo jumam ni anyum­boro anuma kɔŋguɛ kɛrɛ fa ba ju haari ni kisa, ya tara má pui. Ama, nzɛn a se sɔ yiri, m fá diɛndiɛm n sandi nzue n nu.

eng: So they dropped the nets and their catch was so huge that their nets were splitting.
local: Na ba fa diɛndiɛm n asandi nzue n nu, na ba tara jombaa beberebe haari má diɛndiɛm ni jinaari ka bu sɔ́n ɔ.

eng: They signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. They filled both boats so full that they were about to sink.
local: Nyini ti, ba fa bu saa apaati bu lamɔkɔm bo bu wo lee nyiŋga n nu ka bu ba buka bu. Na ba yiyi jombaam n agɔ lee nnyɔ n nu, na ba yi haari leem ni jinaari ka bu sa nzue wɔ.

eng: When Simon Peter saw the catch, he fell at Jesus knees and said, Leave me, Lord, for Im a sinner.
local: Saŋga bo Simon Petoro ŋuuri nyini sɔ ni, na wɔ kɔ akutu Yesu jaa bu ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, cinsi m bi bɛrɛ, dama m ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ.

eng: Peter and those with him were overcome with amazement because of the number of fish they caught.
local: Deke bo i ma i seeri sɔ n la ka, i ni nyiŋgam kɛrɛ ayo alekutura fa kɔ kabo ba tara nyini jombaa beberebe n barasu ni.

eng: James and John, Zebedees sons, were Simons partners and they were amazed too. Jesus said to Simon, Dont be afraid. From now on, you will be fishing for people.
local: Yakobo ni Yohane gusu woori bɛrɛ, bu si la Zɛbɛdi, bu ni Simon saŋga tara jombaa n a. Na bu gusu ayo alekutura. Na Yesu ase Simon ka, Má a ma sɛrɛ ti u, fite kisa fa kɔ, m má a sutiɛn minɛm nɔɔ ma m, kabo a tara jombaa ni.

eng: As soon as they brought the boats to the shore, they left everything and followed Jesus.
local: Na ba cin ayi lee n afite nzue n ahɛmɛ, na ba yaki deke n kɛrɛ bɛrɛ, na ba su i su.

eng: Jesus was in one of the towns where there was also a man covered with a skin disease. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face and begged, Lord, if you want, you can make me clean.
local: Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, na Yesu wo miɛ be su, koko­fɔ be wo nyini miɛ n nu. Saŋga bo i ŋuuri Yesu sɔ ni, na wɔ ŋmati aba akutu, na wɔ fa i nyunu abutu asiɛŋgu, na wɔ sere i jande ka, M Mibiɛ, nzɛn a koro ka a ma m nya laifiɛ, a koro ma m nya i.

eng: Jesus reached out his hand, touched him, and said, I do want to. Be clean. Instantly, the skin disease left him.
local: Na Yesu ateŋge i saa anana i ŋu na wɔ se i ka, M koro. Nya laifiɛ. Na kokobi n akaba ayaki sɔnɔ ni.

eng: Jesus ordered him not to tell anyone. Instead, Jesus said, go and show yourself to the priest and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses instructed. This will be a testimony to them.
local: Na Yesu abɔbɔ i ase i ka, Má a se ka a se be, ama, kɔ na a kɔ fa u ŋu kere Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ ni, na a kɔ yo yoo­wa n bo Moyisi yo kereeri dawa­fɔm ni, dama nyini kere minɛm ka a nya u ŋu a.

eng: News of him spread even more and huge crowds gathered to listen and to be healed from their illnesses.
local: Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, na i labari abara ayɛ deke n kɛrɛ, na minɛm aba i dɔ na bu ba ti Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kaan­wa n na i tuntun bu tukpaki barasu barasu kɛrɛ na bu nya laifiɛ.

eng: But Jesus would withdraw to deserted places for prayer.
local: Ama, yiri ba yo, i yi i ŋu kɔ deke bo minɛm wo má n wɔ, na i kɔ sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: One day when Jesus was teaching, Pharisees and legal experts were sitting nearby. They had come from every village in Galilee and Judea, and from Jerusalem. Now the power of the Lord was with Jesus to heal.
local: Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, Yesu wo minɛm kere su, na Farasi­fɔm nbem, ni Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm tana bɛrɛ. Bu fiteeri miɛ miɛm bo bu wo Galili ni Yudiya awɔrɛm n nu, ni Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu baari a. Na Nyɛmɛ ama Yesu yiko bo i tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu nya laifiɛ.

eng: Some men were bringing a man who was paralyzed, lying on a cot. They wanted to carry him in and place him before Jesus.
local: Dɔ na minɛ nbem asɔ sɔnɔ be ni i dasu aba, i saam ni i jaam awu, na bu sumiɛ bu nyi ka bu fa i wura sɔɔ n nu wɔ, na bu fa i se Yesu nyunu.

eng: but they couldnt reach him because of the crowd. So they took him up on the roof and lowered him—cot and all—through the roof tiles into the crowded room in front of Jesus.
local: Ama, jama n sunnuuri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, ba koro anya má atin. Nyini ti, na ba fa i afun sɔɔ n tii su, na ba fiiti anya fofoe, na ba cɔɔndi i ni i dasu n kɛrɛ ase jama n afiɛn Yesu nyunu.

eng: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, Friend, your sins are forgiven.
local: Yesu ŋuuri kabo bu yoori i yarada n sɔ ni, na wɔ se biɛ n ka, Ma fa u satiɛ ace u.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees began to mutter among themselves, Who is this who insults God? Only God can forgive sins.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm n abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Sɔnɔ bɔnɔ la ahin bo i jɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu sɔ a? Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro fa minɛm satiɛ ni bu ŋgasi ce bu, sin Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi ŋu.

eng: Jesus recognized what they were discussing and responded, Why do you fill your minds with these questions?.
local: Na Yesu afa nu fa kɔ bu sunsunni bo bu bita i n su, na wɔ se bu ka, Nzu ti ye am bita nyini sunsunni ni am ahore su a?.

eng: Which is easier—to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Get up and walk?.
local: Bɔnɔ ti kekereke a, bo m se ka, Ma fa u satiɛ ace u, nzɛn, Yasu nati n a?.

eng: But so that you will know that the Human Onehas authority on the earth to forgive sins—Jesus now spoke to the man who was paralyzed,I say to you, get up, take your cot, and go home.
local: M kére am ka mini Adamande Wa n la yiko durunya n nu bo m fa satiɛ ni ŋgasi ce. Na wɔ se sɔnɔ n bo i saam ni i jaam awu n ka, Wɔrɔ, yasu fa u dasu n na a kɔ awuru.

eng: Right away, the man stood before them, picked up his cot, and went home, praising God.
local: Kpeŋga na sɔnɔ n ayasu jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, na wɔ fa i dasu n na i sukansi Nyɛmɛ sukɔ awuru.

eng: All the people were beside themselves with wonder. Filled with awe, they glorified God, saying, Weve seen unimaginable things today.
local: Na bu kɛrɛ ayo alekutura agaya, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ, na sɛrɛ ati bu, bu ŋa, Nyuma, ya ŋu alekutura sam bo ya nya ŋu má i.

eng: Afterward, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at a kiosk for collecting taxes. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Nyini sin, na Yesu afite bɛrɛ, na wɔ kɔ aŋu lampo­de­fɔ be bo bu fere i ka Levi, i tana i juma yoo­biri. Na Yesu ase i ka, Yasu bra na a ba su m su.

eng: Levi got up, left everything behind, and followed him.
local: Na Levi ayaki deke n kɛrɛ, ayasu asu i su.

eng: Then Levi threw a great banquet for Jesus in his home. A large number of tax collectors and others sat down to eat with them.
local: Na Levi afa Yesu akɔ i dika, na wɔ kɔ ayo di ni nu bambaka, na ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ adi, bu ni lampo­de­fɔm beberebe, ni minɛ nyiŋga nbem.

eng: The Pharisees and their legal experts grumbled against his disciples. They said, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ nbem ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm nbem bo bu ni bɛrɛ wo ni, aŋuŋuti i, na ba bisa Yesu susu­fɔm n ka, Nzu ti ye am ni lampo­de­fɔm, ni satiɛ­yo­fɔm saŋga di diire na am nu nzan a.

eng: Jesus answered, Healthy people dont need a doctor, but sick people do.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Minɛm bo bu la laifiɛ n koro má ayiri­fɔ, má ka bo bu la má laifiɛ ni koro a.

eng: I didnt come to call righteous people but sinners to change their hearts and lives.
local: Ma ba má ka m ba fere minɛm bo bu ŋa bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n wɔ, ama, m baari ka m ba fere satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ, na bu kaaki bu wo.

eng: Some people said to Jesus, The disciples of John fast often and pray frequently. The disciples of the Pharisees do the same, but your disciples are always eating and drinking.
local: Na minɛ nbem aba abisa Yesu ka, Nzu ti ye Yohane dɛɛ susu­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm dɛɛm cici bu nɔɔ na bu sere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga ye u dɛɛm bɛrɛ di ye bu nu a.

eng: Jesus replied, You cant make the wedding guests fast while the groom is with them, can you?.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Am koro miɛ má konyaya­fɔm ka bu cici bu nɔɔ, saŋga bo konyafan n toko wo bu dɔ ni.

eng: The days will come when the groom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
local: Ama, saŋga suba bo bu bá yi konyafan n fite bu nu, nyini saŋga n ye bu nyá cici bu nɔɔ a.

eng: Then he told them a parable. No one tears a patch from a new garment to patch an old garment. Otherwise, the new garment would be ruined, and the new patch wouldnt match the old garment.
local: Na Yesu abo ahin miɛɛnu n akere bu, i ŋa, Sɔnɔ koro fa má sanvuin fɔfɔrɛ kpa tɔ má tani dawa kunma, nzɛn i yoori sɔ, sanvuin fɔfɔrɛ ni sɔ́n dawa n wɔ, i tɔ́ má su.

eng: Nobody pours new wine into old wineskins. If they did, the new wine would burst the wineskins, the wine would spill, and the wineskins would be ruined.
local: I kaaki ti sɔ ɔ bekun ka be koro fa má nzan nyii­wa faŋga­fɔ gɔ má dundu dawa bɛbɛtɛ nu, nzɛn i yoori sɔ, dundu ni tíɛ wɔ, na i ká ŋgbɛɛn, na nzan n kɛrɛ sóŋgi butu.

eng: Instead, new wine must be put into new wineskins.
local: Ama, i ti ye ni ka bu fa nzan nyii­wa faŋga­fɔ gɔ dundu fɔfɔrɛ bii­wa nu.

eng: No one who drinks a well-aged wine wants new wine, but says, The well-aged wine is better.
local: Sɔnɔ nya nuuri nzan nyii­wa wieeri, i koro má nzan mɔnɛ kun. A tí ka i ŋa, Nzan nyii­wa ni ti kpa a.

eng: One Sabbath, as Jesus was going through the wheat fields, his disciples were picking the heads of wheat, rubbing them in their hands, and eating them.
local: Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn be, na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm ni sunati sin alɛ­mbaa fie be nu, na i susu­fɔm n abubu be, na ba nunuka i bu saa nu akpɛsɛ.

eng: Some Pharisees said, Why are you breaking the Sabbath law.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ nbem abisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am suyo sɔ a? Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka má be yo sɔ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn.

eng: Jesus replied, Havent you read what David and his companions did when they were hungry?.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, Am nya kan má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu, kabo Fɛmɛ Davidi yo yoori saŋga bo ahɔɛ tiiri i ni i minɛm na ba si má kabo bu yo n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: He broke the Law by going into Gods house and eating the bread of the presence, which only the priests can eat. He also gave some of the bread to his companions.
local: Ahɔɛ n ti, na wɔ kɔ awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ n bo ba fa ama Nyɛmɛ ni, na wɔ di, na wɔ ma i minɛm be. Ama, am si ka ya dɛɛ atin n su, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ŋgumi di nyini kpɔnɔ n a.

eng: Then he said to them, The Human Oneis Lord of the Sabbath.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Mini Adamande Wa ni ti Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n Mibiɛ a.

eng: On another Sabbath, Jesus entered a synagogue to teach. A man was there whose right hand was withered.
local: Na Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n be bekun, na Yesu akɔ awura tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na wɔ kere minɛm. Sɔnɔ be woori dɔ, i saa fɔmbɔrɔ awu.

eng: The legal experts and the Pharisees were watching him closely to see if he would heal on the Sabbath. They were looking for a reason to bring charges against him.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm nbem woori dɔ, na ba koro ka bu nya ŋgɛɛ bo bu sɔ Yesu na bu nya i taraa­biri. Nyini ti, ba tu bu nyu­mbaa aniɛ Yesu, na bu niɛ ka i túntun sɔnɔ asaa fuin n Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, nzɛn i túntun má i.

eng: Jesus knew their thoughts, so he said to the man with the withered hand, Get up and stand in front of everyone. He got up and stood there.
local: Ama, na Yesu asi bu sunsunni bo bu bita i, na wɔ se sɔnɔ asaa fuin n ka, Yasu bra ba jina afiɛn fɛn. Na sɔnɔ n ayasu akɔ ajina dɔ.

eng: Jesus said to the legal experts and Pharisees, Heres a question for you: Is it legal on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it.
local: Na Yesu abisa bu ka, Ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka ya yo sakpa Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, nzɛn ya yo satiɛ wɔ? I kaaki ti ye ni ka ya de ŋgɔɔ, nzɛn ya kun wɔ.

eng: Looking around at them all, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. So he did and his hand was made healthy.
local: Na Yesu aniɛ bu nyunu abara ayɛ, na wɔ nya ase sɔnɔ asaa fuin n ka, Teŋge u saa ni. Na sɔnɔ n ayo sɔ, na i saa n ayo faŋga bekun.

eng: They were furious and began talking with each other about what to do to Jesus.
local: Na yaa ati bu agaya, na ba bo i bu afa sɛsɛ­su kabo bu yó Yesu barasu.

eng: During that time, Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and he prayed to God all night long.
local: Nyini saŋga n na Yesu afun akɔ buka be su ka i kɔ sere Nyɛmɛ, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ nyini kɔŋguɛ n kɛrɛ.

eng: At daybreak, he called together his disciples. He chose twelve of them whom he called apostles.
local: Saŋga bo ale ba ciinri ni, na wɔ fere i susu­fɔm ni, na wɔ yi bu nu, buru ni nnyɔ, na wɔ fa bu ayo i jaramasam.

eng: Simon, whom he named Peter; his brother Andrew; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew.
local: Bɛrɛ la Simon bo Yesu tuunri i duma ka Petoro ni, ni i niɛma bo bu fere i ka Andere ni, ni Yakobo, ni Yohane, ni Filipu, ni Batolomeyo.

eng: Matthew; Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called a zealot.
local: ni Matio, ni Tomasi, ni Yakobo bo i si la Alafeyo ni, ni Simon bo i tu ŋgoni fa kɔ i asiɛn su ni.

eng: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.
local: ni Yudasi bo i si la Yakobo ni, ni Yudasi Isikariyoti bo i ba tɔɔri Yesu ni.

eng: Jesus came down from the mountain with them and stood on a large area of level ground. A great company of his disciples and a huge crowd of people from all around Judea and Jerusalem and the area around Tyre and Sidon joined him there.
local: Na Yesu ni i jaramasam n acɔɔndi buka n aba ajina awɔrɛ be nu, i susu­fɔm nyiŋgam beberebe woori dɔ, ni jama beberebe. Bu fiteeri Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu wɔ, ni Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, ni Tiro ni Sidon awɔrɛ n bo i wo tieku n nɔɔ su ni.

eng: They came to hear him and to be healed from their diseases, and those bothered by unclean spirits were healed.
local: Bu baari ka bu ba ti i keree­wa n wɔ, na i tuntun bu, na minɛm bo ajinim wo bu ŋu kere bu yaari n gusu aba, na wɔ tuntun bu, na ba nya laifiɛ.

eng: The whole crowd wanted to touch him, because power was going out from him and he was healing everyone.
local: Minɛm kɛrɛ boro bu nyi wɔ, na bu fa bu saa kan i, dama yiko fite i dɔ ye bu kɛrɛ nya laifiɛ a.

eng: Jesus raised his eyes to his disciples and said: Happy are you who are poor, because Gods kingdom is yours.
local: Na Yesu aniɛ i susu­fɔm, na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ bo am ti yaari­fɔm ni, tere­fiɛ wo am dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti am dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Happy are you who hunger now, because you will be satisfied. Happy are you who weep now, because you will laugh.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo ahɔɛ ati am kisa ni, tere­fiɛ wo am dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ bá ma am akunnuyi. Ambɛrɛ bo am ahore asaaki kisa ni, tere­fiɛ wo am dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ bá ma am ahoreda.

eng: Happy are you when people hate you, reject you, insult you, and condemn your name as evil because of the Human One.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo minɛm bá kpɔ am, na bu kéte am, na bu kpíɛ am nzukɔ, na bu sáaki am duma ka am ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, nzɛn i kɛrɛ fite ka mini Adamande Wa n dɛɛ ti, tere­fiɛ wo am dɔ.

eng: Rejoice when that happens! Leap for joy because you have a great reward in heaven. Their ancestors did the same things to the prophets.
local: Bu bá yoori am sɔ, am ma am ahore jɔ, na am tutu am kɔm. Dama am bá nya ŋkatɔ bambaka Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Kabo bu nam yo yoori dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n lɛɛ.

eng: But how terrible for you who are rich, because you have already received your comfort.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ bo am la ajɛkɛ beberebe kisa ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro bambaka, dama am adu mɔ ayo am koroo­wa n dɛɛ ti.

eng: How terrible for you who have plenty now, because you will be hungry. How terrible for you who laugh now, because you will mourn and weep.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am adi na am kunnu ayi kisa ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro bambaka, dama ahɔɛ bá ti am. Ambɛrɛ bo am siri kisa ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro bambaka, dama am bá sun haari na am bá nya má nyunvuin.

eng: How terrible for you when all speak well of you. Their ancestors did the same things to the false prophets.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kansi am ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro bambaka, dama sɔ ye bu nam yo kansiiri kɔrɛ­fɔm n bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n a.

eng: But I say to you who are willing to hear: Love your enemies. Do good to those who hate you.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ama, m se ambɛrɛ bo am suso am sui na am ti n wɔ lɛɛ ka, am koro am kpɔ­fɔm, na am yo sakpa ma bo bu yo am satiɛ ni.

eng: Bless those who curse you. Pray for those who mistreat you.
local: Minɛm bo bu jɔjɔ nɔaniɛ tiɛ gɔ am ŋu ni, am yo bu kisi, na minɛm bo bu kere am wuwuruwa ni, am kaaki sere Nyɛmɛ ma bu.

eng: If someone slaps you on the cheek, offer the other one as well. If someone takes your coat, dont withhold your shirt either.
local: Nzɛn be fin u nyɔɔnu, kaaki bue nyiŋga n gusu ma i na i fin i buka su, na nzɛn be de u tanlɛɛ bambaka, kaba fa u tanlɛɛ kaan n bo i wo i bu mmɔɔ n buka i su.

eng: Give to everyone who asks and dont demand your things back from those who take them.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sere u deke be, má a kpiɛ i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i fa u deke be, má a bisa i busu kun.

eng: Treat people in the same way that you want them to treat you.
local: Kabo am koro ka minɛm yo ma am ni, am yo i sɔ ma bu.

eng: If you love those who love you, why should you be commended? Even sinners love those who love them.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Nzɛn am nya koroori minɛm bo bu koro am ni ŋgumi, i kansi la bɔnɔ ɔ, ye am ba nya i a? Dama haari satiɛ­yo­fɔm mmɔɔ yo sɔ ɔ.

eng: If you do good to those who do good to you, why should you be commended? Even sinners do that.
local: Na nzɛn am nya yoori sakpa maari minɛm bo bu yo sakpa ma am ni, i kansi la bɔnɔ ɔ? Dama haari satiɛ­yo­fɔm mmɔɔ yo sɔ ɔ.

eng: If you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, why should you be commended? Even sinners lend to sinners expecting to be paid back in full.
local: Na nzɛn am fa neŋge fiɛ minɛm bo am sunsun ka bu bá tɔ am kaari ni, i kansi la bɔnɔ ɔ, ye am bá nya i a? Dama haari satiɛ­yo­fɔm mmɔɔ fiɛ bu beŋgu satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ, na bu bá de i ŋusu sɔ.

eng: Instead, love your enemies, do good, and lend expecting nothing in return. If you do, you will have a great reward. You will be acting the way children of the Most High act, for he is kind to ungrateful and wicked people.
local: Ama, wɔ yo daka ka am koro am kpɔ­fɔm, na am yo sakpa ma bu, na má am fiɛ be neŋge be, na am sunsun ka i tɔ́ kaari. Dɔ ye am nyá i ŋkatɔ bambaka a, na am yó Micɛra Nyɛmɛ Kpiri ni nbaam. Dama yiri yo sakpa ma satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ni bo bu si má aye ni.

eng: Be compassionate just as your Father is compassionate.
local: Am si aŋunfɔ kabo am Si Nyɛmɛ si aŋunfɔ ni.

eng: Dont judge, and you wont be judged. Dont condemn, and you wont be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Má am jɔjɔ be jɔrɛ na am jaraki i, na Nyɛmɛ gusu dí má am jɔrɛ na i járaki am. Má am ma bu di be jɔrɛ cin i sui, na Nyɛmɛ gusu dí má am jɔrɛ cin má am sui. Am fa minɛm satiɛ ce bu, na Nyɛmɛ gusu fá am dɛɛ ce am.

eng: Give, and it will be given to you. A good portion—packed down, firmly shaken, and overflowing—will fall into your lap. The portion you give will determine the portion you receive in return.
local: Am ma minɛm deke bo bu koro, na Nyɛmɛ gusu má am. Kabo Nyɛmɛ má am n la ka, i fá gɔŋgɔ dɔŋgu susu ma am a, na i yí kɔ butu. Nyini ti, gɔŋgɔ bo am fa susu ma minɛm ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ gusu fá susu ma am a.

eng: Jesus also told them a riddle. A blind person cant lead another blind person, right? Wont they both fall into a ditch?.
local: Na Yesu abo ahin anyundɛrɛ n akere bu, i ŋa, Anyinsin koro bita má i beŋgu anyinsin kpama, nzɛn i yoori sɔ, bu nnyɔ n kɛrɛ kɔ́ to kunma nu wɔ.

eng: Disciples arent greater than their teacher, but whoever is fully prepared will be like their teacher.
local: Sɔnsɔn­fɔ tara má i kere­fɔ, ama, saŋga bo wɔ ba asɔnsɔn juma n awie, i ni i kere­fɔ n kɛrɛ asɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Why do you see the splinter in your brothers or sisters eye but dont notice the log in your own eye?.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye a niɛ ŋu wura mbaa n bo i wo u niwa nyu­mbaa nu, ye a niɛ ŋu má yeenzin n bo i wo u dɛɛ nyu­mbaa n nu a?.

eng: How can you say to your brother or sister, Brother, Sister, let me take the splinter out of your eye, when you dont see the log in your own eye? You deceive yourselves! First take the log out of your eye, and then you will see clearly to take the splinter out of your brothers or sisters eye.
local: A yo sɛ koro se u niwa ka, Ma m yi wura mbaa n bo i wo u nyu­mbaa nu ni, ye a ŋu má yeenzin n bo i wo u dɛɛ nyu­mbaa n nu n a? Wɔrɔ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔ ni, du mɔ yi yeenzin n bo i wo u nyu­mbaa nu n ka, na a nyá ŋu asi nyunyumi, na a yí wura mbaa n bo i wo u niwa n dɛɛ nyu­mbaa nu ni.

eng: A good tree doesnt produce bad fruit, nor does a bad tree produce good fruit.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Baka bo i ti kpa su má suu­wa tiɛ, na baka bo i ti má kpa gusu su má suu­wa kpa.

eng: Each tree is known by its own fruit. People dont gather figs from thorny plants, nor do they pick grapes from prickly bushes.
local: Baka kɛrɛ suu­wa kere kabo baka n ti barasu a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka a koro titi má damba buwe baka su. A kaaki koro titi má cacabu kpanji baka su.

eng: A good person produces good from the good treasury of the inner self, while an evil person produces evil from the evil treasury of the inner self. The inner self overflows with words that are spoken.
local: Sɔnɔ kpa ni i deke kpa kɛrɛ bo i bita i ahore su, fa sakpa ba a. Na sɔnɔ tiɛ gusu ni i deke tiɛ bo i bita i ahore su, fa satiɛ ba a. Dama dekem bo am sunsun bu am ahore su saŋga kɛrɛ n ye am jɔjɔ bu a.

eng: Why do you call me Lord, Lord and dont do what I say?.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Nzu ti ye am fere m ka, Ya Mibiɛ, ya Mibiɛ, ye am yo má deke bo m se ka am yo a?.

eng: Ill show what its like when someone comes to me, hears my words, and puts them into practice.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i baari m dɔ, na i tiiri m nɔaniɛm n na i deeri bu na i fa yoori juma, m kére am kabo i mi ti barasu.

eng: Its like a person building a house by digging deep and laying the foundation on bedrock. When the flood came, the rising water smashed against that house, but the water couldnt shake the house because it was well built.
local: I mi ti ka sɔnɔ bo i ŋa i tá awuru na i fundu kunma ŋgoŋgo na i kɔ kpura yabuɛ, na i se sɔɔ n jaa. Wɔmbu koro wura na nzuetinm kɛrɛ koro yi sandi ba kpura awuru ni, i yó má i pui, dama ataari n ayo faŋga n ti.

eng: But those who dont put into practice what they hear are like a person who built a house without a foundation. The floodwater smashed against it and it collapsed instantly. It was completely destroyed.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tiiri m nɔaniɛm n na wɔ de má i, afa ayo má juma, i mi ti ka sɔnɔ bo i taari i awuru na wɔ se má i jaa yabuɛ su n wɔ. Wɔmbu ba wuraari na nzuetinm kɛrɛ ba yi kpura nyini awuru ni, na i kaba tɔ faŋga su bo i busu ŋu má n wɔ.

eng: After Jesus finished presenting all his words among the people, he entered Capernaum.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu jɔjɔɔri minɛm dɔ ni nyini nɔaniɛm n kɛrɛ wieeri ni, na wɔ kɔ Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: A centurion had a servant who was very important to him, but the servant was ill and about to die.
local: Na Roma­fɔm soja kpiɛn be wo dɔ, i la i akɔɔ bo i koro i agaya, wɔ da tukpaki sukoro ka i wu.

eng: When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish elders to Jesus to ask him to come and heal his servant.
local: Soja kpiɛn n tiiri Yesu ŋga ni, na wɔ sunma Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm nbem ka bu kɔ sere i ka i ba tuntun i akɔɔ n na i nya laifiɛ.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they earnestly pleaded with Jesus. He deserves to have you do this for him, they said.
local: Na ba kɔ Yesu dɔ akɔ asere i jande ka, I ti ye ni ka a ba buka ahin soja kpiɛn ni.

eng: He loves our people and he built our synagogue for us.
local: Dama i koro ya minɛm n agaya haari wɔ ta tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ ama ya.

eng: Jesus went with them. He had almost reached the house when the centurion sent friends to say to Jesus, Lord, dont be bothered. I dont deserve to have you come under my roof.
local: Dɔ na Yesu asu bu su akɔ. Saŋga bo bu kɔ dodoori awuru n kaan sɔ ni, na soja kpiɛn n asunma i danfom Yesu dɔ, bu kɔ juuri ni, na ba se i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, soja kpiɛn n ŋa má a fie u ŋu. I ŋa i ti kaan ni bo a ba i dika.

eng: In fact, I didnt even consider myself worthy to come to you. Just say the word and my servant will be healed.
local: Nyini ti ye i ŋa wɔ bu má ka i ti pui bo i ba u nyunu n a. Ama, haari a maari nɔaniɛ n mmɔɔ, i akɔɔ ni nyá laifiɛ.

eng: Im also a man appointed under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to one, Go, and he goes, and to another, Come, and he comes. I say to my servant, Do this, and the servant does it.
local: Dama yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii wo sɔnɔ saa nu wɔ, na yiri gusu nya la i dɛɛ sojam bo i niɛ bu su. Na bo i nya se i ka, Kɔ fɛn, na i kɔ, na i se nyiŋga gusu ka, Bra fɛn, na i ba. Na i se i akɔɔ n gusu ka, Yo ahin, na i yo i.

eng: When Jesus heard these words, he was impressed with the centurion. He turned to the crowd following him and said, I tell you, even in Israel I havent found faith like this.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu tiiri sɔ ni, na wɔ yo i alekutura, na wɔ kaaki aniɛ jama n bo bu su i su n na wɔ se ka, M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, ma nya aŋu má sɔnɔ bo i la yarada agaya ka ahin sɔnɔ n ŋusu, haari fa saŋga ni Yisareli­fɔm mmɔɔ.

eng: When the centurions friends returned to his house, they found the servant restored to health.
local: Na minɛm bo soja kpiɛn n sunmaari bu n akpie bu nyi akɔ i dika. Bu kɔ juuri, na ba ŋu ka i akɔɔ n anya laifiɛ.

eng: A little later Jesus went to a city called Nain. His disciples and a great crowd traveled with him.
local: Nyini sin, wɔ cɛ má, na Yesu akɔ miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Nayin n su, i ni i susu­fɔm, ni jama beberebe kɔɔri a.

eng: As he approached the city gate, a dead man was being carried out. He was his mothers only son, and she was a widow. A large crowd from the city was with her.
local: Saŋga bo i kɔ ŋa i wura miɛ n nu ni, kpeŋga i ni minɛm ayɛ, sɔnɔ be awu miɛ n nu, bu susɔ i fite na bu kɔ se i. Sɔnɔ n bo wɔ wu n ti bara kuna­fɔ be wa a, i wa kɛrɛ la yiri ŋgumi kpein wɔ. Na jama beberebe afite miɛ n nu susu kunafɔ n su.

eng: When he saw her, the Lord had compassion for her and said, Dont cry.
local: Mibiɛ Yesu n ŋuuri kuna­fɔ n sɔ ni, na aŋunfɔ ati i, na wɔ se i ka, Má a sun.

eng: He stepped forward and touched the stretcher on which the dead man was being carried. Those carrying him stood still. Jesus said, Young man, I say to you, get up.
local: Na Yesu akɔ afa i saa akan daŋgu n bo fuin n da su ni, na minɛm bo bu sɔ i n aka ajina. Na Yesu ase ka, Gbɛfɛnɛ, m suse u wɔ lɛɛ, yasu.

eng: The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother.
local: Dɔ na gbɛfɛnɛ n ayasu atana na wɔ jɔjɔ minɛm dɔ, na Yesu akaaki afa i ama i ni.

eng: Awestruck, everyone praised God. A great prophet has appeared among us, they said. God has come to help his people.
local: Na sɛrɛ ati sɔnɔ kɛrɛ, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ, na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ bambaka be aba ya dɔ. Nyɛmɛ aba aniɛ i minɛm busu.

eng: This news about Jesus spread throughout Judea and the surrounding region.
local: Sa n bo Yesu yoori i n dɛɛ ti, na i labari n abara ayɛ Yudiya awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ, ni awɔrɛm kɛrɛ bo bu dodo bɛrɛ ni.

eng: Johns disciples informed him about all these things. John called two of his disciples.
local: Saŋga bo Yohane dɛɛ susu­fɔm ba kaanri nyini jɔrɛm kɛrɛ kereeri i ni, na wɔ fere bu minɛ nnyɔ.

eng: and sent them to the Lord. They were to ask him, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: na wɔ sunma bu Mibiɛ Yesu n dɔ ka bu kɔ bisa i ka, Wɔrɔ ye bu yo seeri ka a bá n a, nzɛn ya ŋminda sɔnɔ fɔfɔrɛ be wɔ.

eng: When they reached Jesus, they said, John the Baptist sent us to you. He asks, Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for someone else.
local: Bu ba juuri Yesu dɔ n na ba se i ka, Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ ni sunmaari ya u dɔ a, na ya ba bisa u ka, Wɔrɔ ye bu yo seeri ka a bá n a, nzɛn ya ŋminda sɔnɔ fɔfɔrɛ be wɔ.

eng: Right then, Jesus healed many of their diseases, illnesses, and evil spirits, and he gave sight to a number of blind people.
local: Nyini saŋga n nu, na Yesu atuntun minɛ beberebe bo bu la tukpaki barasu barasum, na wɔ fɔn ayi ajinim afite minɛm ŋu, na wɔ ma anyinsinm beberebe aŋu asi.

eng: Then he replied to Johns disciples, Go, report to John what you have seen and heard. Those who were blind are able to see. Those who were crippled now walk. People with skin diseases are cleansed. Those who were deaf now hear. Those who were dead are raised up. And good news is preached to the poor.
local: Na Yesu ase minɛm bo Yohane sunmaari bu n ka, Am kpie am nyi na am kɔ se Yohane fa kɔ sam bo am ati bu na am aŋu bu ni, ka anyinsinm aŋu asi, wɔbukam akoro anati, koko­fɔm anya laifiɛ, asuturim ati asui, minɛm bo ba wu n ateŋge, na yaari­fɔm gusu akoro ati labari kpa ni.

eng: Happy is anyone who doesnt stumble along the way because of me.
local: Tere­fiɛ wo minɛm bo bu yo m yarada na bu la má sika be, ni mini n dɔ.

eng: After Johns messengers were gone, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John. What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A stalk blowing in the wind?.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm bo Yohane sunmaari bu n kɔ wieeri ni, na Yesu ajɔjɔ jama n dɔ, fa kɔ Yohane ŋusu jɔrɛ. I ŋa, Saŋga bo am fite kɔɔri Yohane dɔ boro kpantain n nu ni, am kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu nzu wɔ? Am kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu sɔnɔ bo i ti ka barankpaati fofoe bo aŋuma n kankan i wɔ?.

eng: What did you go out to see? A man dressed up in refined clothes? Look, those who dress in fashionable clothes and live in luxury are in royal palaces.
local: Nzɛn má nyini, am fite kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu nzu wɔ? Am ŋa am kɔ ŋu sɔnɔ bo i wura sutura kpakpa a, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, am si ka minɛm bo bu wura nyini suturam na bu di fiɛfiɛ ni, bu wo nfɛmɛm dika a.

eng: What did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
local: Nzɛn má nyini, am kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu nzu wɔ? Am kɔɔri ka am kɔ ŋu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ɛɛn, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, am aŋu sɔnɔ bo i tara Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ.

eng: He is the one of whom its written: Look, Im sending my messenger before you, who will prepare your way before you .
local: Dama Yohane ye bu yo kɛrɛɛri i ŋusu jɔrɛ, Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, M dú mɔ sunma m sunma­fɔ n na i dú u mɔ, yiri kɔ́ yo atin n dɔŋgu se u a. .

eng: I tell you that no greater human being has ever been born than John. Yet whoever is least in Gods kingdom is greater than he.
local: Dɔ na Yesu ase bu ka, M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, sɔnɔ wo má durunya n nu fɛn bo i tara Yohane. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti kaan Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu n tara yiri Yohane.

eng: Everyone who heard this, including the tax collectors, acknowledged Gods justice because they had been baptized by John.
local: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu woori dɔ ni, haari fa saŋga ni lampo­de­fɔm ni, tiiri kabo Yesu jɔjɔɔri ni, na ba soro su ka Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ atin ni ti atin nahɔrɛ a, dama bu ma Yohane adu mɔ abiɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue.

eng: But the Pharisees and legal experts rejected Gods will for themselves because they hadnt been baptized by John.
local: Ama, na Farasi­fɔm ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n akete kabo Nyɛmɛ kereeri ka bu yo ni, dama ba kete ka má Yohane biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue.

eng: To what will I compare the people of this generation? Jesus asked. What are they like?.
local: Na Yesu ase bekun ka, Ye m fá nzu fa sunsun kabo nyuma­fɔ ti a? Bu ti ka nzu wɔ?.

eng: They are like children sitting in the marketplace calling out to each other, We played the flute for you and you didnt dance. We sang a funeral song and you didnt cry.
local: Bu ti ka nbaatuma kan kanm bo bu tana gɔɔsu wɔ, na bu sufere bu beŋgu, bu ŋa, Ya kan tere ama am, ama, am abo má kereba. Ya di sɛɛ jue ama am, ama, am asun má.

eng: John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, He has a demon.
local: Deke bo i ti na m se sɔ n la ka, Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n ba juuri ni, i ciciiri i nɔɔ ɔ, i di má diire, i nu má nzan yiri fieo, nyini ti, na am ase ka ajini wo i ŋu.

eng: Yet the Human Onecame eating and drinking, and you say, Look, a glutton and a drunk, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
local: Ama, mini Adamande Wa n ba juuri, na m ni minɛ asaŋga adi, na ya saŋga anu, na am ase ka, Am niɛ, ahin sɔnɔ n yiri, i ti ayaakpiri­fɔ ɔ, na i nu nzan nahɔrɛ su. Wɔ tara lampo­de­fɔm, ni satiɛ­yo­fɔm danfoya.

eng: But wisdom is proved to be right by all her descendants.
local: Tɔ, minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu su Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ alecira n atin n su ni, bɛrɛ kere ka i ti nahɔrɛ a.

eng: One of the Pharisees invited Jesus to eat with him. After he entered the Pharisees home, he took his place at the table.
local: Farasi­fɔ be yo baandiiri Yesu a, ka i bra i dika ba di diire, na Yesu akɔ i dika, na wɔ kɔ awura ka i di diire ni.

eng: Meanwhile, a woman from the city, a sinner, discovered that Jesus was dining in the Pharisees house. She brought perfumed oil in a vase made of alabaster.
local: Bara be woori nyini miɛ n nu, i ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ. I tiiri ka Yesu wo Farasi­fɔ n dika n wo diire di su, na wɔ fa tulaalɛɛ nva­nvani tuwa kun aba.

eng: Standing behind him at his feet and crying, she began to wet his feet with her tears. She wiped them with her hair, kissed them, and poured the oil on them.
local: Na wɔ ba ajina Yesu jaa dika, na i susun, na i nyunvuin sugɔ Yesu jaa su. Na wɔ kutu aso i jaa nu, na wɔ fa i tii­ŋmiɛ tintiin anunuka i, na wɔ kaaki tulaalɛɛ n agɔ i jaa nnyɔ n kɛrɛ su.

eng: When the Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw what was happening, he said to himself, If this man were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him. He would know that she is a sinner.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ n bo i baandiiri Yesu n ase i kunnu ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n ti má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ, nzɛn má i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ, má i si ahin bara n bo i sufa i saa kan i n kabo i woo­wa ti barasu, má i si ka i ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Simon, I have something to say to you. Teacher, speak, he said.
local: Dɔ na Yesu ase i ka, Simon, m koro ka m se u jɔrɛ be. Na wɔ se Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, se m.

eng: A certain lender had two debtors. One owed enough money to pay five hundred people for a days work.The other owed enough money for fifty.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Minɛ nnyɔ nbem wo bɛrɛ a, na ba kɔ ŋwaa­fɔ be dɔ akɔ afiɛ ŋwaa. Kun dɛɛ kaari n sunnu má, ama, kun n yiri dɛɛ sunnu, i kpaaki ciciri dɛɛ n ŋusu buru.

eng: When they couldnt pay, the lender forgave the debts of them both. Which of them will love him more.
local: Bu minɛ nnyɔ n nu, bu be mmɔɔ akoro atɔ má ŋwaa­fɔ n kaari ni, nyini ti, na wɔ yaki ŋwaa n ace bu. Ye a sunsun ka bu minɛ nnyɔ n nu, ŋma koro ŋwaa­fɔ n agaya.

eng: Simon replied, I suppose the one who had the largest debt canceled. Jesus said, You have judged correctly.
local: Na Simon abɔ Yesu nu ka, Mini dɔ, m sunsun ka sɔnɔ n bo i kaari sunnuuri ni, yiri koro i agaya. Na Yesu ase i ka, A la nahɔrɛ.

eng: Jesus turned to the woman and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? When I entered your home, you didnt give me water for my feet, but she wet my feet with tears and wiped them with her hair.
local: Dɔ na Yesu acɛ aniɛ bara ni, na wɔ se Simon ka, M ŋa a ŋu ahin bara ni e? M ba juuri u dika n nu ni, a ma má m nzue ka m ŋmisi m jaa, ama, yiri afa i nyunvuin aŋmisi m jaa, ye wɔ fa i tii­ŋmiɛ n anunuka m jaa n na i wu.

eng: You didnt greet me with a kiss, but she hasnt stopped kissing my feet since I came in.
local: Bekun, a biti má m na a yo m ansɛ ni ba mmɔɔ, ama, kabo m ba juuri, yiri akere kabo i koro m, wɔ yaki má m jaa so nu.

eng: You didnt anoint my head with oil, but she has poured perfumed oil on my feet.
local: A fa má ŋguin ka a kpakpa má m tii, ama, yiri afa tulaalɛɛ akaaki agɔ m jaa su.

eng: This is why I tell you that her many sins have been forgiven; so she has shown great love. The one who is forgiven little loves little.
local: Nyini ti, m suse u wɔ lɛɛ ka, i satiɛ sunnu ye Nyɛmɛ afa i kɛrɛ ace i a, nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ kere m i koro agaya. Ama, sɔnɔ bo i satiɛ sunnu má na Nyɛmɛ afa ace i a, i mi koro mmɔɔ sunnu má.

eng: Then Jesus said to her, Your sins are forgiven.
local: Dɔ na Yesu ase bara n ka, Ma fa u satiɛ kɛrɛ ace u.

eng: The other table guests began to say among themselves, Who is this person that even forgives sins.
local: Minɛ nyiŋgam n bo bɛrɛ gusu tana wo diire n di su n tiiri sɔ ni, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ŋma la ahin sɔnɔmbiri n bo i koro fa minɛm satiɛ ce bu a.

eng: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bara n ka, Yarada n bo a yo m ni, nyini ma a nya u tii a. Kɔ awuru ni ahoreda.

eng: Soon afterward, Jesus traveled through the cities and villages, preaching and proclaiming the good news of Gods kingdom. The Twelve were with him.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu ajeŋgbe miɛ kpirim, ni ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛ miɛm su, na i sukan labari kpa n fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n su. I ni i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni kɔɔri a.

eng: along with some women who had been healed of evil spirits and sicknesses. Among them were Mary Magdalene.
local: Na nbara beberebe nbem gusu asu i su. Bu nbem ye i tuntuunri bu tukpaki barasu barasu n a. Bu kun ye i fɔn yiiri ajinim nso fiteeri i ŋu n a, bu fere i ka Mariya Magadala­fɔ ni.

eng: Joanna , Susanna, and many others who provided for them out of their resources.
local: ni kun bo bu fere i ka Yowana, na i hun la sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Cusa na i niɛ Fɛmɛ Herode dika n kɛrɛ su ni, ni bara bo bu fere i ka Susanna ni. Nyini nbaram kɛrɛ fa bu tiɛŋgu deke bo bu la i wɔ, na ba fa abuka Yesu ni i susu­fɔm ni.

eng: When a great crowd was gathering and people were coming to Jesus from one city after another, he spoke to them in a parable.
local: Na jama beberebe atiɛn bu nɔɔ Yesu dɔ, bu fiteeri miɛ su miɛ su baari a. Na wɔ bo bu ahin miɛɛnu ni, i ŋa.

eng: A farmer went out to scatter his seed. As he was scattering it, some fell on the path where it was crushed, and the birds in the sky came and ate it.
local: Dɔdɔɔri­fɔ be fiteeri sɔ ɔ, ka i kɔ sandi alɛ­mbaa. Saŋga bo i wo sandi su ni, na nbem atɔtɔ atin n nɔɔ, na minɛm atiɛntiɛn su asin, na anumam aba atambu bu ami.

eng: Other seed fell on rock. As it grew, it dried up because it had no moisture.
local: Na nbem atɔtɔ tiɛka su, na wɔ fi, wɔ cɛ má na wɔ waandi, dama niniki awo má asiɛn n bu n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Other seed fell among thorny plants. The thorns grew with the plants and choked them.
local: Na alɛ­mbaa ni nbem atɔtɔ lɔlɔɛ nu, na lɔlɔɛ n anyi akata i ŋu.

eng: Still other seed landed on good soil. When it grew, it produced one hundred times more grain than was scattered. As he said this, he called out, Everyone who has ears should pay attention.
local: Ama, na nbem atɔtɔ asiɛn kpa su, na wɔ nyi awura mbaa zɔi zɔi. Yesu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ nya atiɛn kpa ase ka, Bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i so i sui na i ti.

eng: His disciples asked him what this parable meant.
local: Dɔ na Yesu susu­fɔm n abisa i nyini miɛɛnu n bu.

eng: He said, You have been given the mysteries of Gods kingdom, but these mysteries come to everyone else in parables so that when they see, they cant see, and when they hear, they cant understand .
local: na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Ambɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ ama am asi i fɛmɛya n nu asiri jɔrɛ kɛrɛ a, ama, bo ba ka ni, m bobo i miɛɛnu kere bu a. Adena i yó kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Bu níɛ wɔ, ama, bu ŋú má pui. Bu só bu sui wɔ, ama, bu tí má deke n bo bu so bu sui ka bu ti i n bu.

eng: The parable means this: The seed is Gods word.
local: Na Yesu asa su ase bu ka, Tɔ, miɛɛnu n bo m boori i n bu yaa. Alɛ­mbaa n ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: The seed on the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and steals the word from their hearts so that they wont believe and be saved.
local: Alɛ­mbaa n bo bu tɔtɔɔri atin nɔɔ n la minɛm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, i cɛ má, na Sitana bá yi i fite bu ahore su, adena má bu yó yarada na bu nyá tii­de.

eng: The seed on the rock are those who receive the word joyfully when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a while but fall away when they are tempted.
local: Alɛ­mbaa n bo bu tɔtɔɔri tiɛka su n la minɛm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na bu de i ni ahorejɔ, ama, i da má bu ahore su. Bu kaaki ti ka alɛ­mbaa n bo i bunlui akpama abi má n wɔ. I woori bu dɔ kaan sɔ, na saŋga bo kekereke be ba tuuri bu, a ŋu ka ba fa ati.

eng: As for the seed that fell among thorny plants, these are the ones who, as they go about their lives, are choked by the concerns, riches, and pleasures of life, and their fruit never matures.
local: Alɛ­mbaa n bo bu tɔtɔɔri lɔlɔɛ nu n la minɛm bo bu nya tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni na bu su i atin su, a ŋu ka bu sunsunni kɛrɛ wo kabo bu yó na bu asiri káta su, na bu nyá durunya n nu ajɛkɛm, na bu yó bu koroo­wa, a ŋu ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n koro da má bu ahore su na i yo juma. Bu ti ka alɛ­mbaa bo i bi má n wɔ.

eng: The seed that fell on good soil are those who hear the word and commit themselves to it with a good and upright heart. Through their resolve, they bear fruit.
local: Ama, alɛ­mbaa n bo bu tɔtɔɔri asiɛn kpa su n la minɛm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na bu de i ni bu ahore fofoe nahɔrɛ su, na bu de i ni bu saa nnyɔ, na bu mindi bita i, na bu fa i yo juma ni.

eng: No one lights a lamp and then covers it with a bowl or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on top of a lampstand so that those who enter can see the light.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Sɔnɔ be nya so má fitana su na i fa gaŋga butu su, wara i fa se gado bu, ama, i fa taka deke su aŋgoro wɔ, adena minɛm bo bu ba wura awuru n nu n koro ŋu asi.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be exposed. Nor is anything concealed that wont be made known and brought to the light.
local: Deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i wo fiɛɛ­wa nu na bu bá ŋu má i, deke be gusu wo má bɛrɛ bo i wo asiri nu na bu bá si má i.

eng: Therefore, listen carefully. Those who have will receive more, but as for those who dont have, even what they seem to have will be taken away from them.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am si am ŋu sa ni kabo am ti nɔaniɛ n barasu ni. Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ nya deke be Nyɛmɛ dɔ na i mi si i bita, Nyɛmɛ búka i su wɔ, ama, bo i si má i bita, haari ni nyini kaan n bo i sunsun ka wɔ nya i ni, Nyɛmɛ dé i dɔ ɔ.

eng: Jesus mother and brothers came to him but were unable to reach him because of the crowd.
local: Na Yesu ni, ni i niɛmam aba i dɔ, ama, jama sunnuuri i dɔ n dɛɛ ti, ba koro aju má i bi.

eng: Someone told him, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you.
local: Na be ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, u ni, ni u niɛmam jina dasin koro u.

eng: He replied, My mother and brothers are those who listen to Gods word and do it.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, M ni, ni m niɛmam la minɛm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na bu de i na bu fa yo juma n wɔ.

eng: One day Jesus and his disciples boarded a boat. He said to them, Lets cross over to the other side of the lake. So they set sail.
local: Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n awura lee nu, na wɔ se bu ka, Am ma ya kpiɛ kɔ kunma kpiri n buekun dɔ. Nyini ti, na ba yasu na bu kɔ.

eng: While they were sailing, he fell asleep. Gale-force winds swept down on the lake. The boat was filling up with water and they were in danger.
local: Bu wo kɔ su ni, na Yesu adaafi, na aŋuma bambaka be ayasu na i subo nzue gɔ lee n nu. Má lee ni suyi ni nzue wɔ, na anyum­boro atu bu.

eng: So they went and woke Jesus, shouting, Master, Master, were going to drown! But he got up and gave orders to the wind and the violent waves. The storm died down and it was calm.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔm n akɔ ateŋge i, na ba se i ka, Ya Mɔ­fɔ, ya Mɔ­fɔ e, ya wu awie. Na Yesu ayasu, na wɔ tiɛn nzue n ni aŋuma n bo i subo n ŋu, na ba tara, na dika n atara jum.

eng: He said to his disciples, Where is your faith? Filled with awe and wonder, they said to each other, Who is this? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him.
local: Dɔ na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ni ka, Nzu ti ye am yo má m yarada a? Na ba yo alekutura na sɛrɛ akaaki ati bu agaya, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Nzu sɔnɔmbiri barasu la ahin bo i ma aŋuma ni nzue mmɔɔ nɔɔ na bu de i a.

eng: Jesus and his disciples sailed to the Gerasenes land, which is across the lake from Galilee.
local: Na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n akpiɛ kunma kpiri n akɔ aju Gɛrasafɔm dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na kunma kpiri n wo i ni Galili dɛɛ awɔrɛ n afiɛn ni.

eng: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, a certain man met him. The man was from the city and was possessed by demons. For a long time, he had lived among the tombs, naked and homeless.
local: Yesu fiteeri lee n nu ni, na miɛ n nu sɔnɔ be aba akpa i atin, ajinim wo i ŋu, wɔ cɛ, i nati i bu ŋgbɛɛn wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, i da má awuru, má ka faŋ­kunma nu.

eng: When he saw Jesus, he shrieked and fell down before him. Then he shouted, What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, dont torture me.
local: I ŋuuri Yesu ni, na wɔ bo awie kpa, na wɔ kutu haari Yesu jaa bu, na wɔ ciindi ase ka, Yesu, Micɛra Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n Wa, a koro nzu ya nu fɛn a? M sere u wɔ ka, má a ma m ŋu anyum­boro.

eng: He said this because Jesus had already commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had taken possession of him, so he would be bound with leg irons and chains and placed under guard. But he would break his restraints, and the demon would force him into the wilderness.
local: Deke bo i ti na i seeri sɔ n la ka, Yesu ama ajinim n nɔɔ ka bu fite yaki i. Saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga ajinim n fun i ŋu wɔ, haari ni bo bu niɛ i dika, na bu bo i tobari, na bu wura i jɔrɔkɔ buka su ni, i sandi i kɛrɛ wɔ, na ajinim n fa i kɔ boro kpantain n nu.

eng: Jesus asked him, What is your name? Legion, he replied, because many demons had entered him.
local: Na Yesu abisa i ka, Bu fere u sɛ? Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, Bu fere m ka Jama Beberebe­fɔ. Deke bo i ti na i seeri sɔ n la ka, ajinim beberebe wo i ŋu wɔ.

eng: They pleaded with him not to order them to go back into the abyss.
local: Na ba sere Yesu jande ka má i fɔn bu kɔ wura kunma n bo i kɔ ŋgoŋgo na i la má awieeri n nu.

eng: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the hillside. The demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs. Jesus gave them permission.
local: Nyini saŋga afam beberebe wo dodo bɛrɛ, bu wo buka be su sudi, na ajinim n asere Yesu ka i ma bu kɔ fun afam n ŋu, na wɔ ma bu atin.

eng: and the demons left the man and entered the pigs. The herd rushed down the cliff into the lake and drowned.
local: Na ba fite ayaki sɔnɔ ni, na ba kɔ afun afam n ŋu. Dika n nu bɛrɛ, na bu kɛrɛ aŋmati acɔɔndi akɔ ato kunma kpiri nzue n nu, na nzue n adi bu.

eng: When those who tended the pigs saw what happened, they ran away and told the story in the city and in the countryside.
local: Afa sasa­fɔm n ŋuuri nyini n sɔ ni, na ba ŋmati akɔ abo ndoori miɛ kunnu, ni ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛ kɛrɛ su.

eng: People came to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone. He was sitting at Jesus feet, fully dressed and completely sane. They were filled with awe.
local: Na minɛm aba na bu ba niɛ sa n bo wɔ yo ni, na ba ba Yesu dɔ, aŋu sɔnɔ n bo ajinim n yo woori i ŋu n ka i tana Yesu bi wura tanlɛɛ, ni i nyunu koroya su. Dɔ na sɛrɛ ati bu.

eng: Those people who had actually seen what had happened told them how the demon-possessed man had been delivered.
local: Na minɛm n bo bu ŋuuri sa n yoo­biri ni, akan jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ akere jama n kabo i ba yoori na sɔnɔ n bo ajinim yo woori i ŋu n anya laifiɛ ni.

eng: Then everyone gathered from the region of the Gerasenes asked Jesus to leave their area because they were overcome with fear. So he got into the boat and returned across the lake.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu fite nyini awɔrɛ n nu n asere Yesu jande ka, i yasu bu awɔrɛ n nu bɛrɛ, dama sɛrɛ ati bu agaya. Na Yesu ayasu awura lee nu ka i kɔ.

eng: The man from whom the demons had gone begged to come along with Jesus as one of his disciples. Jesus sent him away, saying.
local: Dɔ na sɔnɔ n bo ajinim afite ayaki i n asere Yesu ka, i ma i su i su. Ama, Yesu asoro má i su, na wɔ se i ka.

eng: Return home and tell the story of what God has done for you. So he went throughout the city proclaiming what Jesus had done for him.
local: Kpie u nyi kɔ awuru, na a kɔ kan kere minɛm fa kɔ sakpa n bo Nyɛmɛ ayo ama u n su. Na sɔnɔ n akpie i nyi akɔ akan akere miɛ n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ fa kɔ sakpa n kɛrɛ bo Yesu ayo ama i ni.

eng: When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had been waiting for him.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu kpieeri i nyi kɔ juuri kunma kpiri n buekun dɔ ni, i kɔ ŋu ka jama n atiɛn bu nɔɔ suŋminda i wɔ, na ba yo i ansɛ ni ba.

eng: A man named Jairus, who was a synagogue leader, came and fell at Jesus feet. He pleaded with Jesus to come to his house.
local: Dɔ na sɔnɔ be aba Yesu dɔ, yiri ti kpiɛn bo i niɛ bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n su a, bu fere i ka Yarusi. Na wɔ ba akutu asiɛŋgu Yesu jaa bu asere i ka, jande i bra i dika.

eng: because his only daughter, a twelve-year-old, was dying. As Jesus moved forward, he faced smothering crowds.
local: dama i wa baa­bara kun n kpein bo i la i n suwu, i la afɔ buru ni nnyɔ ɔ. Yesu wo kɔ su n na jama n amiɛ i ŋu agaya.

eng: A woman was there who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent her entire livelihood on doctors, but no one could heal her.
local: Bara be woori jama n nu, i bita abiɛɛri i afɔ buru ni nnyɔ lɛɛ, bo i kpu na i kpiɛ má, ama, be akoro atuntun má i na i nya laifiɛ.

eng: She came up behind him and touched the hem of his clothes, and at once her bleeding stopped.
local: Na wɔ sakati awura jama n nu Yesu sin, afa i saa akan Yesu tanlɛɛ n jaa bu ni, kpeŋga na abiɛɛri n ajina.

eng: Who touched me? Jesus asked. When everyone denied it, Peter said, Master, the crowds are surrounding you and pressing in on you.
local: Na Yesu abisa ka, Ŋma fa i saa kaanri m a? Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ase ka ba si má, na Petoro ase i ka, Ya Mɔ­fɔ, wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ŋu kabo jama n abara ayɛ na bu miɛ u ŋu barasu.

eng: But Jesus said, Someone touched me. I know that power has gone out from me.
local: Ama, na Yesu ase ka, Sɔnɔ be afa i saa akan m, dama ma fa nu ka m yiko n be afite m ŋuniɛ nu akɔ ayo juma.

eng: When the woman saw that she couldnt escape notice, she came trembling and fell before Jesus. In front of everyone, she explained why she had touched him and how she had been immediately healed.
local: Na bara n aŋu ka i koro fiɛ má i ŋu, na wɔ ba ajina Yesu nyunu, i ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ subobo i. Na wɔ kutu haari Yesu jaa bu akan jɔrɛ n akere i, jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, kabo i ba yoori na i kaba nyaari laifiɛ ni.

eng: Daughter, your faith has healed you, Jesus said. Go in peace.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, M wa, yarada n bo ayo m ni, yiri ma a nya laifiɛ a. Kɔ awuru ni ahoreda.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from the synagogue leaders house, saying to Jairus, Your daughter has died. Dont bother the teacher any longer.
local: Saŋga n bo Yesu toko wo jɔjɔ su ni, na sɔnɔ be afite tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n kpiɛn n dika aba ase i ka, U wa n wo má bɛrɛ. Nyini ti, má a dama ya Kere­fɔ n kun.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he responded, Dont be afraid; just keep trusting, and she will be healed.
local: Ama, na Yesu ati jɔrɛ n na wɔ se baa n si n ka, Má a ma sɛrɛ ti u, wɔrɔ yo m yarada, i nyá laifiɛ.

eng: When he came to the house, he didnt allow anyone to enter with him except Peter, John, and James, and the childs father and mother.
local: Bu kɔ juuri kpiɛn n dika n nu ni, wɔ soro má su, na be wura buka i su, má ka Petoro, ni Yakobo, ni i niɛma Yohane, ni baa n si, ni i ni.

eng: They were all crying and mourning for her, but Jesus said, Dont cry. She isnt dead. Shes only sleeping.
local: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu woori dɔ n wo awie bo su wɔ, na bu sukoko. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Yesu ase ka, Má am sun, wɔ wu má, wɔ daafi wɔ.

eng: They laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
local: Na ba siri i, dama bu si ka wɔ wu wɔ.

eng: Taking her hand, Jesus called out, Child, get up.
local: Ama, na Yesu akɔ atara i saa, na wɔ tiɛn kpa ase i ka, M wa, yasu jina.

eng: Her life returned and she got up at once. He directed them to give her something to eat.
local: Na wɔ nya ŋgɔɔ bekun, na wɔ kaba ayasu ajina, na Yesu ase ka bu ma baa n deke be na i di.

eng: Her parents were beside themselves with joy, but he ordered them to tell no one what had happened.
local: Na baa n si, ni i ni ayo alekutura akɔ asin su, ama, na Yesu abɔbɔ bu ni nahɔrɛ su ka, má bu ma be si sa n bo wɔ yo ni.

eng: Jesus called the Twelve together and he gave them power and authority over all demons and to heal sicknesses.
local: Na Yesu afere i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni, na wɔ tiɛn bu nɔɔ, na wɔ ma bu yiko ka bu kɔ sufɔn yi ajini barasu barasum kɛrɛ fite minɛm ŋu, na bu ma tukpaki­fɔm nya laifiɛ.

eng: He sent them out to proclaim Gods kingdom and to heal the sick.
local: Dɔ na wɔ sunma bu ka bu kɔ kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su ni, na bu tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu nya laifiɛ.

eng: He told them, Take nothing for the journey—no walking stick, no bag, no bread, no money, not even an extra shirt.
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Má am fa puitiri­pui kɔ ahin atin n bo m susunma am ni, kpama o, kuruku o, diire o, ŋwaa o. Má am fa tanlɛɛ be mmɔɔ buka bo am wura i n su.

eng: Whatever house you enter, remain there until you leave that place.
local: Am kɔ juuri miɛ be su, na awuru kɛrɛ bo am kɔ wuraari nu, am ka nyini awuru n nu haari am bá fite miɛ n nu.

eng: Wherever they dont welcome you, as you leave that city, shake the dust off your feet as a witness against them.
local: Sasu bekun, miɛ bo am kɔ wuraari nu, na ba de má am wɔfɔ, am fite yaki nyini miɛ ni, na am kpɔnkpɔn yi am jaa tuturi kɛrɛ. Nyini dí daani kere bu ka bu woo­wa ti má kpa.

eng: They departed and went through the villages proclaiming the good news and healing people everywhere.
local: Na i jaramasam n akɔ ajeŋgbe abara ayɛ miɛ miɛm su akan labari kpa n jɔrɛ akere minɛm deke n kɛrɛ bo bu kɔɔri ni, na ba tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na ba nya laifiɛ.

eng: Herod the ruler heard about everything that was happening. He was confused because some people were saying that John had been raised from the dead.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Herode ati sa n kɛrɛ bo Yesu ayo i ni, na wɔ birisi, dama minɛ nbem ŋa Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n ye wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu a.

eng: others that Elijah had appeared, and still others that one of the ancient prophets had come back to life.
local: ye nbem ŋa Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Eliya ye wɔ yi i ŋu afite fofoe nu, na nbem gusu ase ka dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nyiŋga n be ye wɔ kaaki ateŋge aba a.

eng: Herod said, I beheaded John, so now who am I hearing about? Herod wanted to see him.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Herode ase ka, M ma ba kpiɛ Yohane tii e, ye ŋma la ahin sɔnɔ n bo m ti i sa­yoo­wam ŋga sɔ n a? Na wɔ boro i nyi ka i ŋu Yesu.

eng: When the apostles returned, they described for Jesus what they had done. Taking them with him, Jesus withdrew privately to a city called Bethsaida.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu jaramasam n kpieeri bu nyi baari i dɔ ni, na ba kan sam kɛrɛ bo ba yo i n akere i. Na wɔ fa bu na ba kɔ awo miɛ be su awɔrɛ be nu bɛrɛ ŋgumi. Bu fere miɛ n ka Besayida.

eng: When the crowds figured it out, they followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them about Gods kingdom, and healed those who were sick.
local: Jama n tiiri Yesu ŋga sɔ ni, na ba sa i su akɔ dɔ. Na wɔ de bu, na wɔ kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su barasu. Na wɔ tuntun tukpaki­fɔm, na ba nya laifiɛ.

eng: When the day was almost over, the Twelve came to him and said, Send the crowd away so that they can go to the nearby villages and countryside and find lodging and food, because we are in a deserted place.
local: Saŋga bo wiɛ ba sukɔ tɔ ni, na i jaramasam n aba i dɔ aba ase i ka, Ma minɛm n atin na bu kɔ miɛ kunnu ni ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛ miɛm su na bu kɔ niɛniɛ diire di na bu nya daa­biri da, dama deke bo ya wo ni, ya wo boro wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: He replied, You give them something to eat. But they said, We have no more than five loaves of bread and two fish—unless we go and buy food for all these people.
local: Ama, na Yesu ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii nya deke be ma bu na bu di. Na ba se i ka, Deke bo ya la i kɛrɛ la kpɔnɔ nnu wɔ, ni jombaa nnyɔ, i jú má. A koro ka ya kɔ to diire ba ma ahin jama n kɛrɛ wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Seat them in groups of about fifty.
local: Deke bo i ti na bu seeri sɔ n la ka, nbiɛsɔm kɔ yasuuri minɛ akpii nnu wɔ. Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Am ma bu tana asɛsɛ asɛsɛ aburu­nu nnu na bu di.

eng: They did so, and everyone was seated.
local: I susu­fɔm ni maari bu kɛrɛ tanaari sɔ wieeri ni.

eng: He took the five loaves and the two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed them, and broke them and gave them to the disciples to set before the crowd.
local: na Yesu afa kpɔnɔ nnu ni, ni jombaa nnyɔ ni, na wɔ yɛ i tii su aŋgoro, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ kpiɛkpiɛ i kɛrɛ n nu, na wɔ fa ama i susu­fɔm ni ka bu fa ma minɛm ni.

eng: Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples filled twelve baskets with the leftovers.
local: Na bu kɛrɛ adi, na bu kunnu ayi. Na ba tambu kpɔnɔ, ni jombaa sinsin bo bu diiri na i kaari ni, na ba nya kandiɛ buru ni nnyɔ.

eng: Once when Jesus was praying by himself, the disciples joined him, and he asked them, Who do the crowds say that I am.
local: Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, Yesu wo nyɛmɛsere su i ŋgumi, i susu­fɔm ni wo i bi. I sereeri Nyɛmɛ wieeri n na wɔ bisa bu ka, Minɛm fa m bu ŋma a.

eng: They answered, John the Baptist, others Elijah, and still others that one of the ancient prophets has come back to life.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Bu ŋa wɔrɔ ti Yohane Nyɛmɛ Nzue Biɛ­fɔ n a, nbem ŋa wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Eliya, ye nbem gusu ŋa wɔrɔ ti dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nyiŋga n be ye wɔ teŋge aba a.

eng: He asked them, And what about you? Who do you say that I am? Peter answered, The Christ sent from God.
local: Na wɔ nya abisa bu bekun ka, Ye ambɛrɛ fa m bu ŋma a? Na Petoro abɔ i nu ka, Wɔrɔ ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell this to anyone.
local: Na Yesu abɔbɔ bu ka, má bu se ka bu se be jɔrɛ n bo Petoro ajɔjɔ i ni.

eng: He said, The Human Onemust suffer many things and be rejected—by the elders, chief priests, and the legal experts—and be killed and be raised on the third day.
local: i ŋa, Wɔ yo daka ka mini Adamande Wa n di wahara dɔŋgu, na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm kɛrɛ bá kete m ŋu, na bu kún m. Ama, na i le­san cɛɛn, na Nyɛmɛ má m teŋge m yiwee n nu.

eng: Jesus said to everyone, All who want to come after me must say no to themselves, take up their cross daily, and follow me.
local: Na wɔ se bɛrɛ kɛrɛ pepere ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i su m su, wɔ yo daka ka i mi wara fi i tiɛŋgu sa koroo­wa sa, na i fá i yiwee baka jaanu n sɔ, na i su m su cɛɛn kɛrɛ cɛɛn.

eng: All who want to save their lives will lose them. But all who lose their lives because of me will save them.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i fa i ŋgɔɔ fiɛ, i mi bá fun i. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tɔɔri i ŋgɔɔ mini dɛɛ ti, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: What advantage do people have if they gain the whole world for themselves yet perish or lose their lives?.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ nyaari durunya n nu deke n kɛrɛ na i fuunri i ŋgɔɔ na i kaari ŋgbɛɛn, i kpɛrɛ la bɔnɔ?.

eng: Whoever is ashamed of me and my words, the Human Onewill be ashamed of that person when he comes in his glory and in the glory of the Father and of the holy angels.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo nyinsɛ ti i fa kɔ mini ni m nɔaniɛ n su, sɔ ye nyinsɛ tí mini Adamande Wa n gusu fa kɔ bu su a, saŋga bo m bá ba ni m jirima woo­wa n nu, ni m Si Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ jirima woo­wa ni, ni i mɛrɛkɛ casim ni.

eng: I assure you that some standing here wont die before they see Gods kingdom.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, am nbem wo nu fɛn bo am wú má, má ka am aba aŋu ka Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n aba.

eng: About eight days after Jesus said these things, he took Peter, John, and James, and went up on a mountain to pray.
local: Yesu jɔjɔɔri nyinim kɛrɛ wieeri sɔ ni, fa kɔ i le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn, na wɔ fa Petoro ni Yohane ni Yakobo na ba fun akɔ buka be su ka bu kɔ sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: As he was praying, the appearance of his face changed and his clothes flashed white like lightning.
local: I wo nyɛmɛsere n su ni, na i nyunu­awa akaaki wo, na i tanlɛɛ n akaaki fofoe papasi.

eng: Two men, Moses and Elijah, were talking with him.
local: Dɔ na minɛm nnyɔ nbem atiɛ bu ŋu, bu ni Yesu subo anzama. Bɛrɛ la Moyisi ni Eliya, dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni.

eng: They were clothed with heavenly splendor and spoke about Jesus departure, which he would achieve in Jerusalem.
local: Na bɛrɛ gusu ata fofoe papasi, na bu sujɔjɔ i dɔ fa kɔ kabo i saŋga bá ju bo i kɔ́ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na i yoori sɔ na Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa n yó yii­wa.

eng: Peter and those with him were almost overcome by sleep, but they managed to stay awake and saw his glory as well as the two men with him.
local: Nyini saŋga, Petoro ni i beŋgum wo daafiri su, bu ba teŋgeeri n na ba ŋu kabo Yesu woo­wa n akaaki barasu, i ni minɛ nnyɔm ni jina.

eng: As the two men were about to leave Jesus, Peter said to him, Master, its good that were here. We should construct three shrines: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah—but he didnt know what he was saying.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm n ba sukɔ yaki Yesu ni, na Petoro ase i ka, Ya Mɔ­fɔ, i ti kpa ka ya wo fɛn. Ma ya bo nambue nsan, u dɛɛ kun, Moyisi dɛɛ kun, na Eliya gusu dɛɛ kun. I jɔjɔ ɔ, wɔ si má kabo i seeri ni.

eng: Peter was still speaking when a cloud overshadowed them. As they entered the cloud, they were overcome with awe.
local: I toko wo jɔjɔɔri su n na yinyiŋgu akata bu ŋu liim, na sɛrɛ ati i susu­fɔm ni, saŋga bo yinyiŋgu n sukata bu ŋu ni.

eng: Then a voice from the cloud said, This is my Son, my chosen one. Listen to him.
local: Na ba ti akonvi be ŋga yinyiŋgu n nu, i ŋa, M wa lɛɛ, yiri ye ma yi i a. Am de i nɔaniɛ.

eng: Even as the voice spoke, Jesus was found alone. They were speechless and at the time told no one what they had seen.
local: Bu tiiri jɔjɔɔ­wa n sɔ ni, bu ŋu ka Yesu ŋgumi jina a. Nyini saŋga ni, i susu­fɔm n abo má su akan akere má be fa kɔ sam n bo bu ŋuuri i n su.

eng: The next day, when Jesus, Peter, John, and James had come down from the mountain, a large crowd met Jesus.
local: Na i alecin, na Yesu ni i susu­fɔ nsanm ni sujura buka n su ni, na jama beberebe akpa i atin.

eng: A man from the crowd shouted, Teacher, I beg you to take a look at my son, my only child.
local: Dɔ na sɔnɔ be awo jama n nu atiɛn ase ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, m sere u wɔ, si m wa baa­biɛsɔ n aŋunfɔ, m wa kɛrɛ lɛɛ.

eng: Look, a spirit seizes him and, without any warning, he screams. It shakes him and causes him to foam at the mouth. It tortures him and rarely leaves him alone.
local: Niɛ, ajini wo i ŋu, saŋga kɛrɛ bo i ba ŋa i yasu i ŋu, i kaba bo awie kungere­kun wɔ, na i fa i bo asiɛŋgu, na i nanzue sufite fufuku, i bita i kere i wahara sɔ ɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, i koro má ka i fite yaki i.

eng: I begged your disciples to throw it out, but they couldnt.
local: Ma sere u susu­fɔm ni lɛɛ ka bu fɔn yi ajini ni, ama, ba kura má nu.

eng: Jesus answered, You faithless and crooked generation, how long will I be with you and put up with you? Bring your son here.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Amaa. Ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔ satiɛ­yo­fɔm, am la má yarada fieo. Nyuma sa ye m wo am dɔ fɛn a? Ye m yó sawɔrɛ ni ambɛrɛ kɔ ju sɛ wɔ? Dɔ na wɔ se baa n si ka, Fa baa n bra fɛn.

eng: While he was coming, the demon threw him down and shook him violently. Jesus spoke harshly to the unclean spirit, healed the child, and gave him back to his father.
local: Saŋga bo baa ni suba Yesu dɔ ni, na ajini n abo i aji asiɛŋgu, na wɔ da sukpisi. Na Yesu atiɛn ajini n ŋu na baa n anya laifiɛ, na wɔ kaaki afa i ama i si.

eng: Everyone was overwhelmed by Gods greatness. While everyone was marveling at everything he was doing, Jesus said to his disciples.
