local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ amiɛ fa kɔ yiko bambaka bo Nyɛmɛ la i ni. Sam n kɛrɛ bo Yesu woori bu yo su n dɛɛ ti, nyini ma minɛ kɛrɛ yoori alekutura n a. Dɔ na wɔ se i susu­fɔm ka.

eng: Take these words to heart: the Human Oneis about to be delivered into human hands.
local: Ambɛrɛ, am so am sui na am ti deke bo m koro ka m se am ni. Bu bá tɔ mini Adamande Wa n ma minɛ nbem.

eng: They didnt understand this statement. Its meaning was hidden from them so they couldnt grasp it. And they were afraid to ask him about it.
local: Ama, i jɔjɔɔri sɔ ni, i susu­fɔm n ati má i bu. Nyɛmɛ ayi má i nyi akere má bu, nyini ti ye ba koro ati má i bu. Ama, sɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, ba koro abisa má i fa kɔ nyini jɔrɛ n su.

eng: An argument arose among the disciples about which of them was the greatest.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n ase akuruwa bu ŋuŋu nu fa kɔ sɔnɔ bo i tara bu kɛrɛ ni.

eng: Aware of their deepest thoughts, Jesus took a little child and had the child stand beside him.
local: Yesu siiri bu sunsunni bo bu bita i n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ fa baa kaan be ajina bu afiɛn i bi.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Whoever welcomes this child in my name welcomes me. Whoever welcomes me, welcomes the one who sent me. Whoever is least among you all is the greatest.
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a deeri ahin baa­kan n ŋusu m dɛɛ ti, a deeri mini wɔ lɛɛ. Bekun, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo a nya deeri mini, a deeri m si bo wɔ sunma m wɔ lɛɛ. Dama bo am sunsun ka i ti kaan am kɛrɛ nu ni, yiri ti kpiri tara am kɛrɛ a.

eng: John replied, Master, we saw someone throwing demons out in your name, and we tried to stop him because he isnt in our group of followers.
local: Dɔ na Yohane ase i ka, Ya Mɔ­fɔ, ya ŋuuri ka sɔnɔ be afa u duma sufa fɔn yi ajinim fite minɛm ŋu, ye ya ŋa ya yáki má i, dama i wo má ya nu n dɛɛ ti.

eng: But Jesus replied, Dont stop him, because whoever isnt against you is for you.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Má am se ka am kanda i, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i fite má am sin, i mi jina am sin wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: As the time approached when Jesus was to be taken up into heaven, he determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Saŋga n ba dodoori bo Nyɛmɛ má Yesu fun kɔ aŋgoro ni, na wɔ bu i tii nu ka i ti ye ni ka i kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: He sent messengers on ahead of him. Along the way, they entered a Samaritan village to prepare for his arrival.
local: Na wɔ sunma sunma­fɔm Samariya ahɛmɛ miɛ be su ka bu kɔ suyo deke kɛrɛ siriya ŋminda i.

eng: but the Samaritan villagers refused to welcome him because he was determined to go to Jerusalem.
local: Ama, na Samariya­fɔm n akete ka bu de má i, dama ka i susin kɔ Yɛrusalem n dɛɛ ti.

eng: When the disciples James and John saw this, they said, Lord, do you want us to call fire down from heaven to consume them.
local: Yakobo ni Yohane ŋuuri nyini n sɔ ni, na ba se ka, Ya Mibiɛ, a koro ka ya ma sin fite aŋgoro ba di bu, nzɛn sɛ a.

eng: But Jesus turned and spoke sternly to them.
local: Na Yesu acɛ aniɛ, na wɔ jaraki bu.

eng: and they went on to another village.
local: Dɔ na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n asin akɔ miɛ nyiŋga be su.

eng: As Jesus and his disciples traveled along the road, someone said to him, I will follow you wherever you go.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n wo kɔ su ni, na sɔnɔ be aba ase Yesu ka, Deke kɛrɛ bo a kɔ, m sú u su wɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, Foxes have dens and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Human Onehas no place to lay his head.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Ŋaakum la kunmam, na anumam gusu la tiɛɛrim, ama, mini Adamande Wa n yiri, m la má m tii daa­biri.

eng: Then Jesus said to someone else, Follow me. He replied, Lord, first let me go and bury my father.
local: Na wɔ se sɔnɔ nyiŋga be ka, Su m su. Ama, na sɔnɔ n ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, ma m du mɔ kɔ yo m si sutura ka, na m nyá ba su u su.

eng: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead. But you go and spread the news of Gods kingdom.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Yaki bo ba wu ni, na bu se bu beŋgu, wɔrɔ kɔ na a kɔ kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su ni.

eng: Someone else said to Jesus, I will follow you, Lord, but first let me say good-bye to those in my house.
local: Na sɔnɔ nyiŋga be bekun ase Yesu ka, M Mibiɛ, m bá su u su, ama, ma m kɔ kaari m dika­fɔm ka, na m bá.

eng: Jesus said to him, No one who puts a hand on the plow and looks back is fit for Gods kingdom.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti ka toora dɔdɔɔri­fɔ na i wo dɔ su, na i kaaki niɛ i sin, i ti má ye ni ka Nyɛmɛ yo i mi fɛmɛ.

eng: After these things, the Lord commissioned seventy-two others and sent them on ahead in pairs to every city and place he was about to go.
local: Nyini sin na Mibiɛ Yesu n ayi i susu­fɔ abura­so ni nnyɔ nyiŋga nbem, na wɔ sunma bu nnyɔ nnyɔ ka bu du mɔ kɔ miɛ miɛm kɛrɛ bo yiri ni i tii ase ka i bá kɔ su ni.

eng: He said to them, The harvest is bigger than you can imagine, but there are few workers. Therefore, plead with the Lord of the harvest to send out workers for his harvest.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Fiesu diire n sunnu, ama, bo bu kɔ i tambu n sunnu má. Nyini ti, am sere Mibiɛ n bo i dɛɛ la fie n ka i sunma juma­yo­fɔm na bu kɔ i tambu.

eng: Go! Be warned, though, that Im sending you out as lambs among wolves.
local: Ama, am ti fɛn. Am kɔ. M susunma am wɔ lɛɛ, kabo nbɔmbaam kɔ saŋga bedim nu barasu ni.

eng: Carry no wallet, no bag, and no sandals. Dont even greet anyone along the way.
local: Má am se ka am fa ŋwaa, wara kuruku, wara gbagbaa, má am se ka am jina atin nu yo be mmɔɔ ansɛ.

eng: Whenever you enter a house, first say, May peace be on this house.
local: Awuru kɛrɛ bo am kɔ wuraari nu, am du mɔ se ka, Nyɛmɛ ma am tana­laifiɛ.

eng: If anyone there shares Gods peace, then your peace will rest on that person. If not, your blessing will return to you.
local: Nzɛn tana­laifiɛ­fɔ wo nyini awuru n nu, am ma am tana­laifiɛ n ka i mi n dɔ, ama, nzɛn wɔ ti má sɔ, Nyɛmɛ dé tana­laifiɛ ni i mi n dɔ.

eng: Remain in this house, eating and drinking whatever they set before you, for workers deserve their pay. Dont move from house to house.
local: Awuru bo am kɔ wuraari nu na bu deeri am wɔfɔ, am ka nyini awuru n nu, na am di na am nu deke kɛrɛ bo bu fa ma am, dama i ti ye ni ka juma­yo­fɔ nya i juma ni ŋkatɔ. Má am nati awuru kɛrɛ nu.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and its people welcome you, eat what they set before you.
local: Am kɔ juuri miɛ be su na bu deeri am wɔfɔ, am di deke kɛrɛ bo bu fa ma am.

eng: Heal the sick who are there, and say to them, Gods kingdom has come upon you.
local: Am tuntun tukpaki­fɔm bo bu wo dɔ n na bu nya laifiɛ, na am se bu ka, Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su n adodo am lɛɛ.

eng: Whenever you enter a city and the people dont welcome you, go out into the streets and say.
local: Ama, nzɛn am kɔ juuri miɛ be su na ba de má am wɔfɔ, am fite kɔ atiŋgbere n su, na am se bu ka.

eng: As a complaint against you, we brush off the dust of your city that has collected on our feet. But know this: Gods kingdom has come to you.
local: Haari am miɛ n tuturi mmɔɔ mata má ya jaa, ya kpɔnkpɔn yi i wɔ, nyini di daani kere ka am woo­wa ti má kpa. Ama, i ti ye ni ka am si ka saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su n adodo lɛɛ.

eng: I assure you that Sodom will be better off on Judgment Day than that city.
local: Nyini ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, jina cɛɛn, nyini miɛ n nu­fɔm, Nyɛmɛ cín bu sui tara Sodom miɛ kpiri n nu satiɛ­yo­fɔm.

eng: How terrible it will be for you, Chorazin. How terrible it will be for you, Bethsaida. If the miracles done among you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have changed their hearts and lives long ago. They would have sat around in funeral clothes and ashes.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Tɔ, ambɛrɛ Korazin­fɔm, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Ambɛrɛ Besayida­fɔm gusu, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Alekutura sam bo m yo maari am ni, má nzɛn m yoori bu Tiro, ni Sidon miɛ kpirim n nu, má minɛm n akaaki bu wo dada kɛrɛ na bu fa pirim bɔtɔ, ni nzuin yo bu yoo­wa n bo i kere ka bu ahore asaaki, bu satiɛ yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti ni.

eng: But Tyre and Sidon will be better off at the judgment than you.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ, Nyɛmɛ bá cin am sui tara Tiro ni Sidon dɛɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm.

eng: And you, Capernaum, will you be honored by being raised up to heaven? No, you will be cast down to the place of the dead.
local: Ambɛrɛ Kapɛnam­fɔm gusu, am sunsun ka Nyɛmɛ bá yɛ am su kɔ aŋgoro wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai. I bá yo am kaan wɔ, na i fá am cɔɔndi kɔ Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri.

eng: Whoever listens to you listens to me. Whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects the one who sent me.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de am nɔaniɛ, i mi de m dɛɛ nɔaniɛ wɔ lɛɛ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kete am nɔaniɛ, i mi kete m dɛɛ nɔaniɛ wɔ lɛɛ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kete m dɛɛ nɔaniɛ, i mi kete yiri n bo i sunmaari m wɔ lɛɛ. I jɔjɔ wieeri, na ba kɔ.

eng: The seventy-two returned joyously, saying, Lord, even the demons submit themselves to us in your name.
local: Saŋga bo i susu­fɔ abura­so ni nnyɔm ni kpieeri bu nyi baari Yesu dɔ ni, na bu ahore ajɔ agaya, na ba se i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, haari ajinim mmɔɔ de ya nɔaniɛ wɔ, nzɛn ya nya atiɛn bu ŋu u dɛɛ duma n nu.

eng: Jesus replied, I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, M ŋuuri Sitana ka wɔ fite aŋgoro atɔ, kabo nzue kpɛsɛ na i ti barasu n wɔ.

eng: Look, I have given you authority to crush snakes and scorpions underfoot. I have given you authority over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Am ti fɛn. Ma ma am yiko adena am tiɛntiɛn woom ni bundaanim ŋu, na am kunma yiri kpɔ­fɔ n faŋga kɛrɛ na pui yó má am.

eng: Nevertheless, dont rejoice because the spirits submit to you. Rejoice instead that your names are written in heaven.
local: Haari ni nyini kɛrɛ ni, má am ma am ahore jɔ ka ajinim de am nɔaniɛ ŋgumi n dɛɛ ti, ama, am ma am ahore jɔ ka ba kɛrɛ am duma ase Nyɛmɛ dika.

eng: At that very moment, Jesus overflowed with joy from the Holy Spirit and said, I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because youve hidden these things from the wise and intelligent and shown them to babies. Indeed, Father, this brings you happiness.
local: Nyini saŋga n na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama Yesu ahorejɔ akɔ sin su, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ase ka, M Si Nyɛmɛ, wɔrɔ ti aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a. M da u asi ka a fa nyini dekem bo alecira­fɔm ni si­fɔm si má i ni, ayi akere bo bu ti ka nbaatam ni. M Si, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, kabo a koroori ka i yo lɛɛ.

eng: My Father has handed all things over to me. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, or who the Father is except the Son and anyone to whom the Son wants to reveal him.
local: Yesu sereeri Nyɛmɛ n wieeri ni, na wɔ se ka, M Si afa deke kɛrɛ awura m saa nu. Be si má Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, má ka i Si Nyɛmɛ ni si i a, na be gusu si má i Si ni, má ka Baa ni si i a, ni minɛm bo Baa n koro ka i yi i kere bu ni, bɛrɛ si i a.

eng: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, Happy are the eyes that see what you see.
local: Dɔ na Yesu acɛ aniɛ i susu­fɔm ni, na wɔ jɔjɔ bɛrɛ ŋgumi dɔ, i ŋa, Ambɛrɛ la tere­fiɛ ka am aŋu dekem bo am ŋu bu ni.

eng: I assure you that many prophets and kings wanted to see what you see and hear what you hear, but they didnt.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni nfɛmɛm beberebe koroori ka bu ŋu deke n bo am ŋu i ni, ama, ba ŋu má i, na ba koro ka bu ti deke n bo am ti i ni, ama, ba ti má i.

eng: A legal expert stood up to test Jesus. Teacher, he said, what must I do to gain eternal life.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ be aba Yesu dɔ ka i ba sɔ i ŋgɛɛ. Na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ye m yo sɛ wɔ, ye m nyá ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: Jesus replied, What is written in the Law? How do you interpret it.
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Ba kɛrɛ i sɛ wɔ, Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu a? A ti i bu sɛ wɔ.

eng: He responded, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your being, with all your strength, and with all your mind, and love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Na sɔnɔ n abɔ Yesu nu ka, Ba kɛrɛ ka, Koro Micɛra u Nyɛmɛ ni, ni u ahore­mbaa kun, ni u awiɛwiɛ, ni u faŋga, ni u sunsunni kɛrɛ, na a koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni.

eng: Jesus said to him, You have answered correctly. Do this and you will live.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, A kan nahɔrɛ wɔ, yo sɔ na a nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: But the legal expert wanted to prove that he was right, so he said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor.
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ n akoro ka i yo i ŋu ka i ti sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, nyini ti, na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, M beŋgu la ŋma a.

eng: Jesus replied, A man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho. He encountered thieves, who stripped him naked, beat him up, and left him near death.
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Sɔnɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, na wɔ yasu Yɛrusalem ka i kɔ miɛ kpiri be su, bu fere dɔ ka Yeriko, na atin bujaanu­fɔm akɔ atara i ade i neŋge kɛrɛ, na ba bo i akɔ ayaki i ŋgɔɔ n kaan sɔ ɔ.

eng: Now it just so happened that a priest was also going down the same road. When he saw the injured man, he crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: I ba yoori ka Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn be su nyini atin n su, ama, saŋga bo i kɔ ŋuuri sɔnɔ n bo atin bujaanu­fɔm abo i ni, na wɔ san asin ayaki i.

eng: Likewise, a Levite came by that spot, saw the injured man, and crossed over to the other side of the road and went on his way.
local: I tiiri sɔ bekun, na Levi busu nu­fɔ be susin bɛrɛ, na wɔ ŋu i, na i gusu asan asin.

eng: A Samaritan, who was on a journey, came to where the man was. But when he saw him, he was moved with compassion.
local: Ama, na Samariya­fɔ be gusu ba bita nyini atin ni, na wɔ ba aŋu sɔnɔ ni, i ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na aŋunfɔ ati i.

eng: The Samaritan went to him and bandaged his wounds, tending them with oil and wine. Then he placed the wounded man on his own donkey, took him to an inn, and took care of him.
local: Na wɔ kɔ sɔnɔ n bi, na wɔ fa ŋguin ni nzan afa aŋmisi kambuem ni, na wɔ cici i, na wɔ fa sɔnɔ n na wɔ ma wɔ tana i kaako su, na wɔ fa i akɔ wɔfɔm dee­biri, na wɔ kɔ aniɛ i dika.

eng: The next day, he took two full days worth of wages and gave them to the innkeeper. He said, Take care of him, and when I return, I will pay you back for any additional costs.
local: Alecin na wɔ yi ŋwaa be ama sɔnɔ bo i niɛ awuru n su ni. Na wɔ se i ka, De na a niɛ i dika ma m, na m ba kpieeri m nyi baari, m tɔ́ deke kɛrɛ bo a saaki i afa aniɛ i dika ni.

eng: Jesus was praying in a certain place. When he finished, one of his disciples said, Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples.
local: Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, na Yesu wo nyɛmɛsere su awɔrɛ be nu. I ba sere wieeri ni, na i susu­fɔm ni kun ase i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, kere ya nyɛmɛsere atin, kabo Yohane kereeri i dɛɛ susu­fɔm ni.

eng: What do you think? Which one of these three was a neighbor to the man who encountered thieves.
local: Tɔ, u sunsunni nu, nyini minɛ nsanm n nu, bɔnɔ kere ka i ti sɔnɔ n beŋgu a.

eng: Jesus told them, When you pray, say: Father, uphold the holiness of your name. Bring in your kingdom.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Saŋga bo am ba susere Nyɛmɛ, am se ka o, Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ma minɛm bu u duma casi ni. Ma u fɛmɛya n bra.

eng: Then the legal expert said, The one who demonstrated mercy toward him. Jesus told him, Go and do likewise.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ n abɔ i nu ka, Sɔnɔ n bo i siiri i aŋunfɔ ni. Na Yesu ase i ka, Kɔ na a kɔ yo sɔ.

eng: Give us the bread we need for today.
local: Ma ya nɔɔ nu diire cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn.

eng: While Jesus and his disciples were traveling, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed him as a guest.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n wo kɔ su ni, na ba kɔ aju miɛ be su, bara be woori dɔ, bu fere i ka Marata, na wɔ de Yesu wɔfɔ i dika.

eng: Forgive us our sins, for we also forgive everyone who has wronged us. And dont lead us into temptation.
local: Fa ya satiɛ ce ya, dama yɛrɛ gusu fa ce bo bu do ya ŋgasi n wɔ. Má a ma deke be yo ya na ya wura satiɛ nu.

eng: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lords feet and listened to his message.
local: Marata la i niɛma be, bu fere i ka Mariya, na wɔ tana Yesu bi, na i suti kabo Yesu kere barasu ni.

eng: By contrast, Martha was preoccupied with getting everything ready for their meal. So Martha came to him and said, Lord, dont you care that my sister has left me to prepare the table all by myself? Tell her to help me.
local: Ama, na wɔ yo Marata ahoresaaki kabo yiri ŋgumi wo juma yo su ni, nyini ti, na wɔ ba ase Yesu ka, M Mibiɛ, u cɛcɛ wo má nu ka m niɛma n ayaki juma n kɛrɛ aji mini ŋgumi saa su, nzɛn sɛ? Se i ka i ba buka m.

eng: He also said to them, Imagine that one of you has a friend and you go to that friend in the middle of the night. Imagine saying, Friend, loan me three loaves of bread.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Ma m fa i ka, nzɛn am nu kun kɔɔri i danfo dika kɔŋguɛ jinanjin, na i kɔ se i ka, M danfo, fiɛ m kpɔnɔ nsan.

eng: The Lord answered, Martha, Martha, you are worried and distracted by many things.
local: Na Mibiɛ n abɔ i nu ka, M danfo Marata, a fa juma beberebe ata u ŋu nyi, ye a suwaraki a.

eng: because a friend of mine on a journey has arrived and I have nothing to set before him.
local: M danfo atin natiiri­fɔ be aba m dika jina n nu bɛrɛ lɛɛ, m gusu la má diire bo m fá ma i.

eng: Imagine further that he answers from within the house, Dont bother me. The door is already locked, and my children and I are in bed. I cant get up to give you anything.
local: Na yiri n bo i wo sɔrɔ n ase ka, Má a miɛ m. Ma tu anɔ awie, na m nbaam da a. M koro yasu má na m má u pui.

eng: One thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the better part. It wont be taken away from her.
local: ama, deke kun n kpein be akpiɛ u su a. Mariya yiri, deke bo i ti kpa tara i kɛrɛ ye wɔ yi i n a, na be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro de i dɔ.

eng: I assure you, even if he wouldnt get up and help because of his friendship, he will get up and give his friend whatever he needs because of his friends brashness.
local: Tɔ, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má ka am ti danfoya n dɛɛ ti ye i yásu na i ma u kpɔnɔ n a, ama, i bá yasu na i ma u deke kɛrɛ bo a koro, dama ka a fie ni sere n dɛɛ ti.

eng: And I tell you: Ask and you will receive. Seek and you will find. Knock and the door will be opened to you.
local: Nyini ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, am sere deke bo am koro i Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na i má am. Am niɛniɛ deke bo am koro i dɔ, na am ŋú i. Am bobo anɔ ni, na i téke i ma am.

eng: Some who were present on that occasion told Jesus about the Galileans whom Pilate had killed while they were offering sacrifices.
local: Nyini saŋga n na minɛ nbem wo dɔ, na ba se Yesu kabo Galili­fɔ nbem yo kɔɔri ka bu kɔ yo yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ, na bu wo yo su ni, na Gomina Pilato gusu ama minɛm aba akun bu.

eng: Everyone who asks, receives. Whoever seeks, finds. To everyone who knocks, the door is opened.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sere deke be Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i má i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i niɛniɛ deke be, i ŋú i wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bobo anɔ ni, i téke i ma i.

eng: He replied, Do you think the suffering of these Galileans proves that they were more sinful than all the other Galileans?.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ye kabo bu kuunri nyini Galili­fɔm ni, am sunsun ka nyini kere ka bu dɛɛ satiɛ yoo­wa tara Galili­fɔm kɛrɛ dɛɛ wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Ai, i ti má sɔ fieo. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nzɛn am akaaki má am wo, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ bá ka ŋgbɛɛn sɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Which father among you would give a snake to your child if the child asked for a fish?.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti nbaasim ni, ŋma ye i wa sere i jombaa ye i mi kéte na i fá woo ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.

eng: What about those eighteen people who were killed when the tower of Siloam fell on them? Do you think that they were more guilty of wrongdoing than everyone else who lives in Jerusalem?.
local: Ye minɛ buru ni mɔcuɛm bo Silowam dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu kobe bambaka n tɔ gɔɔri bu ŋu n di? Am sunsun ka i kere ka bu dɛɛ satiɛ yoo­wa tara Yɛrusalem­fɔm kɛrɛ dɛɛ wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: If a child asked for an egg, what father would give the child a scorpion?.
local: Wara, ambɛrɛ nu ŋma ye i wa sere i karafɔ ye i kéte na i fá bundaani ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.

eng: No, I tell you, but unless you change your hearts and lives, you will die just as they did.
local: Ai, i ti má sɔ fieo. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nzɛn am akaaki má am wo, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ bá ka ŋgbɛɛn sɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: If you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will the heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him.
local: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ, haari ni bo am ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, am si aceeri kpam bo am fa ce am nbaam, nzu ti ye nzɛn am sereeri am Si Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni, i koro ma má am Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a.

eng: Jesus told this parable: A man owned a fig tree planted in his vineyard. He came looking for fruit on it and found none.
local: Dɔ na Yesu abo bu ahin miɛɛnu ni, i ŋa, Sɔnɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, ye damba baka jina i baka suu­wa barasu barasum fie nu. Na wɔ kɔ aniɛniɛ ka i niɛ, beni i ŋú suu­wa su, ama, wɔ ŋu má puitiri­pui su.

eng: Jesus was throwing out a demon that causes muteness. When the demon was gone, the man who couldnt speak began to talk. The crowds were amazed.
local: Saŋga be na Yesu afɔn ayi ajini afite sɔnɔ be ŋu, wɔ yo i bobo. Ajini n fite yakiiri sɔnɔ n sɔ ni, na wɔ koro ajɔjɔ. Na jama n ayo alekutura.

eng: He said to his gardener, Look, Ive come looking for fruit on this fig tree for the past three years, and Ive never found any. Cut it down! Why should it continue depleting the soils nutrients.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ kɔ ase fie n niɛ­fɔ n ka, Niɛ, afɔ kun kun sɔ nsan lɛɛ, bo m ba niɛ ahin damba baka n su ka, beni m ŋú suu­wa a, ama, m ŋu má pui su. Nyini ti, kpiɛ i ji. Nzu ti ye i jina bɛrɛ susaaki asiɛn n a.

eng: The gardener responded, Lord, give it one more year, and I will dig around it and give it fertilizer.
local: Ama, na fie n niɛ­fɔ n abɔ i nu ka, M mibiɛ, yaki i bɛnlɛ, na ya níɛ i kɔ ju afɔ kun bekun na ya niɛ. M fúndu kunma bara yɛ i bu wɔ, na m gɔ́ i nanyɛti.

eng: But some of them said, He throws out demons with the authority of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons.
local: Ama, na minɛ nbem ase ka, Bɛlazɛbuli bo i ti ajinim kɛrɛ dɛɛ fɛmɛ n na bu kaaki fere i ka Sitana ni, yiri ma ahin sɔnɔ n yiko ye i koro fɔn yi ajinim a.

eng: Maybe it will produce fruit next year; if not, then you can cut it down.
local: Dɔ na nzɛn baka n suuri suu­wa, i ti kpa, nzɛn wɔ su má, na a koro ma m kpiɛ i ji.

eng: Others were testing him, seeking a sign from heaven.
local: Na nbem gusu akoro ka bu sɔ i ŋgɛɛ. Nyini ti, na ba se i ka i yo alekutura sa be bo i kere ka i yiko n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: Because Jesus knew what they were thinking, he said to them, Every kingdom involved in civil war becomes a wasteland, and a house torn apart by divisions will collapse.
local: Ama, Yesu siiri bu sunsunni n sɔ ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Fɛmɛya kɛrɛ bo i nya kpakpaakiiri nu wieeri, nyini fɛmɛya ni ká ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. Awuru gusu kɛrɛ bo i kpakpaakiiri nu wieeri, nyini awuru ni sándi wɔ.

eng: Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath.
local: Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, na Yesu wo minɛm kere su, tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm kun nu.

eng: If Satan is at war with himself, how will his kingdom endure? I ask this because you say that I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul.
local: Na nzɛn Sitana dɛɛ fɛmɛya n nya kpaakiiri nu wieeri, i kóro jina a? Ai, i kóro jina má. Deke bo i ti na m se sɔ n la ka, am ŋa Bɛlazɛbuli ma m yiko ye m koro fa fɔn yi ajinim a.

eng: A woman was there who had been disabled by a spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and couldnt stand up straight.
local: Bara be woori dɔ, ajini wo i ŋu, wɔ bu i wi, i afɔ buru ni mɔcuɛ lɛɛ, i koro yasu jina má.

eng: If I throw out demons by the authority of Beelzebul, then by whose authority do your followers throw them out? Therefore, they will be your judges.
local: Tɔ, ye nzɛn am ŋa Bɛlazɛbuli maari m yiko, ye m fa fɔn yi ajinim a, ye ŋma maari am dɛɛ minɛm ni yiko ye bu bita fɔn yi bu n a? Nyini ti, kabo am minɛm yoori ni, nyini kere ka am la má nahɔrɛ.

eng: When he saw her, Jesus called her to him and said, Woman, you are set free from your sickness.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ŋuuri i n na wɔ bo afere i, na wɔ se i ka, Bara, a nya u tii afite u tukpaki n nu.

eng: He placed his hands on her and she straightened up at once and praised God.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu suse sɔ n na wɔ fa i saa anana i ŋu, kpeŋga na wɔ teŋge i ŋu ajina, na wɔ kansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: But if I throw out demons by the powerof God, then Gods kingdom has already overtaken you.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko nu ye mini fɔn yi ajinim a. Nyini n kere ka, Nyɛmɛ wo am nu suniɛ i minɛm su lɛɛ.

eng: The synagogue leader, incensed that Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, responded, There are six days during which work is permitted. Come and be healed on those days, not on the Sabbath day.
local: Ama, na yaa ati tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n kpiɛn ni, ka Yesu atuntun sɔnɔ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni. Na wɔ se minɛm ni ka, Ya la le­sɛn bo wɔ yo daka ka ya fa yo juma, nyini ti, am bra nyini cɛɛnm ni, na bu bá tuntun am, ama, má Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n yiri.

eng: When a strong man, fully armed, guards his own palace, his possessions are secure.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ faŋga­fɔ nya faari i biɛsɔya neŋge kɛrɛ jina ŋmindaari i dika, pui koro yo má i awuru n nu neŋge be.

eng: One Sabbath, when Jesus went to share a meal in the home of one of the leaders of the Pharisees, they were watching him closely.
local: Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, na Farasi­fɔm kpiɛn be afere Yesu ka i bra i dika na bu di diire, na Yesu akɔ. Saŋga bo i wo diire n di su ni, na minɛ aba atana suyi i kɔrɛsi.

eng: The Lord replied, Hypocrites! Dont each of you on the Sabbath untie your ox or donkey from its stall and lead it out to get a drink?.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni. Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, am ŋma nu ye i nyaŋgi má i naani, wara i kaako, na i kɔ ma i nzue a?.

eng: But as soon as a stronger one attacks and overpowers him, the stronger one takes away the armor he had trusted and divides the stolen goods.
local: Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ nyiŋga nya taraari i faŋga, i koro tu ŋgoni ni yiri na i kunma i faŋga, na i de i biɛsɔya neŋge n bo i daafi i ŋu ni, na i fin i neŋge kɛrɛ kɔ na i ni i beŋgum kɔ kpaaki nu.

eng: A man suffering from an abnormal swelling of the body was there.
local: Kpeŋga na sɔnɔ be ayasu ajina Yesu nyunu, i saam ni i jaam kɛrɛ awura.

eng: Then isnt it necessary that this woman, a daughter of Abraham, bound by Satan for eighteen long years, be set free from her bondage on the Sabbath day.
local: Ye ahin bara ni mmɔɔ ti Abraham busu nu­fɔɔ, ye Sitana afa i ayo i dɛɛ, afɔ buru ni mɔcuɛ lɛɛ, wɔ yo má daka ka m tuntun i Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n na i nya laifiɛ, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: Whoever isnt with me is against me, and whoever doesnt gather with me, scatters.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jina má m sin, i mi ti m kpɔ­fɔ ɔ. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo i buka má m na ya tiɛn minɛm nɔɔ, i mi sandi minɛm wɔ.

eng: Jesus asked the lawyers and Pharisees, Does the Law allow healing on the Sabbath or not.
local: Yesu ŋuuri sɔnɔ n sɔ ni, na wɔ bisa Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, ni Farasi­fɔm n ka, Ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka ya tuntun tukpaki­fɔ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, nzɛn atin wo má nu.

eng: When he said these things, all his opponents were put to shame, but all those in the crowd rejoiced at all the extraordinary things he was doing.
local: I seeri sɔ n na wɔ wura i kpɔ­fɔm ni nyinsɛ, ama, na minɛm n bɛrɛ ahore ajɔ fa kɔ alekutura sam bo wɔ yo bu n su.

eng: When an unclean spirit leaves a person, it wanders through dry places looking for a place to rest. But it doesnt find any. Then it says, Ill go back to the house I left.
local: Nzɛn ajini nya fite yakiiri sɔnɔ, i kɔ jeŋgbe bara yɛ boro kpantain nu wɔ, deke bo nzue wo má, na i kɔ niɛniɛ woo­biri bo i tana yi i ŋumi. Nzɛn wɔ nya má woo­biri, nyini saŋga, a ti ka i ŋa, Ma m kpie m nyi kɔ m woo­biri dawa ni.

eng: But they said nothing. Jesus took hold of the sick man, cured him, and then let him go.
local: Ama, ba bɔ má i nu, na Yesu ama sɔnɔ n anya laifiɛ, na wɔ ma i atin na wɔ kɔ.

eng: Jesus asked, What is Gods kingdom like? To what can I compare it?.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti ka nzu wɔ? Ye m fá nzu deke fa sunsun kabo i ti a?.

eng: When a crowd of thousands upon thousands had gathered so that they were crushing each other, Jesus began to speak first to his disciples: Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees—I mean, the mismatch between their hearts and lives.
local: Saŋga n bo nyini n kɛrɛ sukɔ su ni, na jama beberebe aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ haari bu kɔ sutiɛntiɛn bu beŋgu ŋu. Na Yesu abo i bu ka i jɔjɔ, na wɔ du mɔ ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Am si am ŋu sa ni Farasi­fɔm dɛɛ kpɔnɔ ayiri n bo bu fa gɔ kpɔnɔ nu na i yasu ni. M suni bu dɛɛ burukɔɔ­nu sa­yoo­wam wɔ.

eng: When it arrives, it finds the house cleaned up and decorated.
local: Na i kaaki kɔ, i kɔ ŋu ka ba kpara dika n nyunyumi, asɛsɛ deke kɛrɛ su.

eng: He said to them, Suppose your child or ox fell into a ditch on the Sabbath day. Wouldnt you immediately pull it out.
local: Dɔ na wɔ nya abisa bu ka, Am nu ŋma ye nzɛn i wa baa­biɛsɔ, wara i naani, toori bura nu, Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, ye i mi kába yi má i fite má.

eng: Its like a mustard seed that someone took and planted in a garden. It grew and developed into a tree and the birds in the sky nested in its branches.
local: I ti ka alɛ­mbaa kaan kabo nyɛm mbaa ti n wɔ, bo sɔnɔ be dɔɔri i fiesu ni, na wɔ fi, na wɔ nyi atɛtɛrɛki asa­mbaa asa­mbaa, na anumam akoro ayo bu tiɛɛrim su.

eng: Nothing is hidden that wont be revealed, and nothing is secret that wont be brought out into the open.
local: Deke be wo má fiɛɛ­wa nu bo bu bá ŋu má i, deke be gusu wo má asiri nu bo bu bá si má i.

eng: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and make their home there. That person is worse off at the end than at the beginning.
local: Na i nyá kɔ fa ajinim nso, bo bu ti tiɛ tara yiri ni i tii, na bu kɛrɛ bá wura woo­biri dawa n nu. Nyini saŋga, a ŋu ka sɔnɔ n woo­wa ayo tiɛ atara kabo i yo ti dawa ni.

eng: But they had no response.
local: Ama, ba koro abɔ má i ahin bisaa­wa n nu.

eng: Again he said, To what can I compare Gods kingdom?.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu bekun ka, Ye m fá nzu deke fa sunsun kabo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti a?.

eng: Therefore, whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light, and whatever you have whispered in the rooms deep inside the house will be announced from the rooftops.
local: Nyini ti, deke kɛrɛ bo am jɔjɔɔri i kɔŋguɛ, bu bá jɔjɔ i wiɛsu wɔ. Na deke kɛrɛ bo am tuuri i asuturi sɔrɔ, bu bá bo i ndoori wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ tí i.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, a certain woman in the crowd spoke up: Happy is the mother who gave birth to you and who nursed you.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, bara be wo jama n nu, na wɔ se Yesu ka, Tere­fiɛ wo bara n bo i wuuri u na i maari u nyufini n dɔ.

eng: When Jesus noticed how the guests sought out the best seats at the table, he told them a parable.
local: Na Yesu aŋu kabo wɔfɔm nbem suniɛniɛ kpirikoro tanaa­biri na bu tana ni, dɔ na wɔ bo ahin miɛɛnu ni, akere bu kɛrɛ.

eng: Its like yeast, which a woman took and hid in a bushel of wheat flour until the yeast had worked its way through the whole.
local: I ti ka ayiri bo bu fa gɔ kpɔnɔ samɛnɛ nu na i yasu ni, na bara be fa gɔɔri samɛnɛ beberebe nu, na i kɛrɛ yasuuri n wɔ.

eng: I tell you, my friends, dont be terrified by those who can kill the body but after that can do nothing more.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, M danfom, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má am soro minɛm bo bu koro kun aŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, na nyini sin, bu koro yo má pui kun ni.

eng: But he said, Happy rather are those who hear Gods word and put it into practice.
local: Ama, na Yesu ase i ka, Tere­fiɛ wo minɛm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na bu de i ni dɔ a.

eng: When someone invites you to a wedding celebration, dont take your seat in the place of honor. Someone more highly regarded than you could have been invited by your host.
local: I ŋa, Saŋga kɛrɛ bo sɔnɔ be ba fere u ka a bra ba di konyaya diire i dika, a kɔ, má a tana jirima tanaa­biri ni, a yoori sɔ, a kɔ ŋu ka wɔ fere sɔnɔ be bo i tara u.

eng: Jesus traveled through cities and villages, teaching and making his way to Jerusalem.
local: Yesu sukɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n su ni, na wɔ nati asin miɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim ni kan kanm su, sukere minɛm.

eng: Ill show you whom you should fear: fear the one who, after you have been killed, has the authority to throw you into hell. Indeed, I tell you, thats the one you should fear.
local: Ama, m kére am sɔnɔ bo i ti ye ni ka am soro i. Am soro Nyɛmɛ, yiri koro kun aŋuniɛ­mbaa n a, na i kaaki la yiko bo i koro fa i ji cɛɛma sin nu. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, yiri ti sɔnɔ bo am soro i a.

eng: When the crowds grew, Jesus said, This generation is an evil generation. It looks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except Jonahs sign.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm sutiɛn bu nɔɔ bara yɛ Yesu sɔ ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ. Am koro ka m yo alekutura sa na am ŋu i wɔ, ama, m yó má i, má ka alekutura sa n bo Nyɛmɛ yo yoori i maari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Jona ni ŋgumi.

eng: The host who invited both of you will come and say to you, Give your seat to this other person. Embarrassed, you will take your seat in the least important place.
local: nyini saŋga n na sɔnɔ n bo wɔ fere am kɛrɛ ni, yiri nyá ba u dɔ na i bá se u ka, M dɔn, yasu ma ahin sɔnɔ n tanaa­biri. Nyini saŋga, a ŋu ka wɔ wura u nyinsɛ lɛɛ, na a kɔ́ kaan kɔɔ­biri.

eng: Someone said to him, Lord, will only a few be saved? Jesus said to them.
local: Na sɔnɔ be abisa i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ dé bu tii ji ni, bu sunnu, nzɛn bu sunnu má? Na Yesu ase bu ka.

eng: Arent five sparrows sold for two small coins?Yet not one of them is overlooked by God.
local: Am si ka njɔrɛm nnu ŋwaa ti kaan sɔ ɔ, má sɔ? Ama, bu kun n kpein mmɔɔ wo má bɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ niɛ má i dika.

eng: Just as Jonah became a sign to the people of Nineveh, so the Human Onewill be a sign to this generation.
local: I ti sɔ lɛɛ, kabo sa n bo i yo tuuri Jona n kereeri Ninɛva­fɔm ka Nyɛmɛ sunmaari i ni, sɔ ye sa n bo i bá tu mini Adamande Wa n gusu kére ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm ka Nyɛmɛ asunma m a.

eng: Instead, when you receive an invitation, go and sit in the least important place. When your host approaches you, he will say, Friend, move up here to a better seat. Then you will be honored in the presence of all your fellow guests.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ fereeri u ka a bra i dika ba di diire, a kɔ juuri, du mɔ kɔ tana kaan tanaa­biri ka. Dɔ na sɔnɔ n bo wɔ fere u ni, yiri nyá ba u dɔ na i bá se u ka, M dɔn, yasu bra na a ba tana ahin tanaa­biri nyunyumi ni. Nyini n sɔ koro wura u kpiri, u beŋgu wɔfɔm kɛrɛ n nyunu a.

eng: Make every effort to enter through the narrow gate. Many, I tell you, will try to enter and wont be able to.
local: Am boro am nyi na am fa anɔ n bo i ti fii n na am wura, dama m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, minɛ beberebe bá koro ka bu wura, ama, bu bá koro wura má.

eng: Even the hairs on your head are all counted. Dont be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
local: Haari am tii­ŋmiɛ mmɔɔ bo i wo am tii su ni, Nyɛmɛ si i kɛrɛ nɔ. Nyini ti, má am soro sɛrɛ. Am la anyunuyi Nyɛmɛ dɔ tara njɔrɛm beberebe.

eng: The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with the people of this generation and condemn them, because she came from a distant land to hear Solomons wisdom. And look, someone greater than Solomon is here.
local: Fɛmɛ bara be yo fiteeri haari i bɔsu Seba wɔ, na i ba ti Fɛmɛ Solomon dɛɛ ŋgɔnlɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa nɔaniɛ ni. Jina cɛɛn, yiri bá yasu jina na i járaki ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm nahɔrɛ su, dama kisa, m suse am wɔ ka, mini tara Fɛmɛ Solomon, ama, am ade má m nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wura i ŋu kpiri, Nyɛmɛ yó i mi kaan wɔ, na sɔnɔ bo i yo i ŋu kaan, Nyɛmɛ wúra i mi kpiri.

eng: Once the owner of the house gets up and shuts the door, then you will stand outside and knock on the door, saying, Lord, open the door for us. He will reply, I dont know you or where you are from.
local: Saŋga n ba juuri, awuru­fɔ­biɛ ni yásu nyi anɔ n wɔ, nyini saŋga n na am jína anɔ n su, subobo, na am sére i jande ka, Ya Mibiɛ, teke anɔ n ma ya. Na i gusu nyá se am ka, Ai, m si má am sa.

eng: I tell you, everyone who acknowledges me before humans, the Human Onewill acknowledge before Gods angels.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kan kereeri minɛm ka i ti m susu­fɔ, mini Adamande Wa n gusu bá kan kere Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm n ka i mi ti m dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: All the tax collectors and sinners were gathering around Jesus to listen to him.
local: Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, na lampo­de­fɔm ni satiɛ­yo­fɔm beberebe aba Yesu dɔ na bu ba ti i keree­wam ni.

eng: The people of Nineveh will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they changed their hearts and lives in response to Jonahs preaching—and one greater than Jonah is here.
local: Jona yo kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kereeri Ninɛva­fɔm na ba kaaki bu wo. Jina cɛɛn, bɛrɛ bá yasu jina na bu járaki ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm, dama kisa, m suse am wɔ ka, mini tara Jona, ama, am akete ka am kaaki má am wo.

eng: Then Jesus said to the person who had invited him, When you host a lunch or dinner, dont invite your friends, your brothers and sisters, your relatives, or rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return and that will be your reward.
local: Dɔ na Yesu anya ase sɔnɔ n bo i baandiiri i n ka, Saŋga bo a ŋa a fere minɛm na bu ba di diire u dika, má a fere u danfom, wara u ninbaam, wara u lamɔyam, wara u beŋgum bo bu la ajɛkɛ, nzɛn a yoori sɔ, bɛrɛ gusu bá fere u cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, nyini saŋga ni, ba tɔ u kaari awie lɛɛ.

eng: Then you will begin to say, We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
local: Dɔ na am sé i ka, Ya ni wɔrɔ yo diiri a, na ya nu, na a kere minɛm ni yɛrɛ kɛrɛ, ya bɔsu dɔ.

eng: But the one who rejects me before others will be rejected before Gods angels.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo i keteeri minɛm nyunu ka i ti má m susu­fɔ, mini Adamande Wa n gusu bá kete Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm nyunu ka i mi ti má m dɛɛ.

eng: The Pharisees and legal experts were grumbling, saying, This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm ni Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n aŋuŋuti, bu ŋa, Ahin sɔnɔ n de satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ, haari na i ni bɛrɛ saŋga di diire wɔ mmɔɔ.

eng: People dont light a lamp and then put it in a closet or under a basket. Rather, they place the lamp on a lampstand so that those who enter the house can see the light.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Sɔnɔ koro so má fitana su na i fa se fiɛɛ­wa nu, wara i fa gaŋga butu su. Ama, i fa taka deke su aŋgoro wɔ, adena minɛm bo bu ba wura awuru n nu n koro ŋu asi.

eng: Instead, when you give a banquet, invite the poor, crippled, lame, and blind.
local: Ama, saŋga kɛrɛ bo a ŋa a baandi minɛ na bu ba di diire u dika, baandi yaari­fɔm, ni wɔbukam, ni bo bu saam ni bu jaam awu ni, ni anyinsinm.

eng: He will respond, I dont know you or where you are from. Go away from me, all you evildoers.
local: Ama, na i káaki se am bekun ka, Ambɛrɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, m si má am sa fieo, am cinsi m bi bɛrɛ.

eng: Anyone who speaks a word against the Human Onewill be forgiven, but whoever insults the Holy Spirit wont be forgiven.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ wuraari mini Adamande Wa ni ŋgasi nu, Nyɛmɛ fá i mi ŋgasi ce i, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ wuraari Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ŋgasi nu, Nyɛmɛ fá má i mi ŋgasi ce má i.

eng: Jesus told them this parable.
local: Dɔ na wɔ bo bu ahin miɛɛnu ni, i ŋa.

eng: Your eye is the lamp of your body. When your eye is healthy, your whole body is full of light. But when your eye is bad, your whole body is full of darkness.
local: Ya nyu­mbaa ni ti ka fitana ma ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa n a. Nzɛn u nyu­mbaa nya ti kpa, u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ ta wein n wɔ lɛɛ. Ama, nzɛn i nya ti má kpa, u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ wo awosin nu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: And you will be blessed because they cant repay you. Instead, you will be repaid when the just are resurrected.
local: A nya yoori sɔ, tere­fiɛ wo u dɔ. Bɛrɛ koro tɔ má u kaari, ama, Nyɛmɛ bá tɔ u kaari a, cɛɛn bo minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n bá teŋge bu yiwee n nu ni.

eng: There will be weeping and grinding of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in Gods kingdom, but you yourselves will be thrown out.
local: Na am ŋú ka Abraham, ni Izaki, ni Jakɔbu, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, ka bu wo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, na kabo ba fa ambɛrɛ atu dasin ni, dɔ na am sún, na am kán am saa.

eng: When they bring you before the synagogues, rulers, and authorities, dont worry about how to defend yourself or what you should say.
local: Saŋga bo bu ba faari am baari bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu, wara nfɛmɛm ni nfaŋga­fɔm nyunu ka bu di am jɔrɛ, má am ma am ahore saaki fa kɔ kabo am jɔjɔ barasu.

eng: Suppose someone among you had one hundred sheep and lost one of them. Wouldnt he leave the other ninety-nine in the pasture and search for the lost one until he finds it?.
local: Nzɛn ambɛrɛ be nu la i nbɔɛm yaa, na kun miniiri, i mi yó sɛ wɔ? I yáki nbɔɛ abura­ŋgɔna ni ŋgɔnam ni boro n nu wɔ, na i kɔ́ niɛniɛ kun n bo wɔ mini ni haari i bá ŋu i.

eng: Therefore, see to it that the light in you isnt darkness.
local: Nyini ti, si u ŋu sa na má a se ka wein wo u dɔ, ama, i ti awosin wɔ.

eng: When one of the dinner guests heard Jesus remarks, he said to Jesus, Happy are those who will feast in Gods kingdom.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ n na wɔfɔm nu kun ase i ka, Ŋka tere­fiɛ wo minɛm bo bu bá tana Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu di diire n dɔ.

eng: People will come from east and west, north and south, and sit down to eat in Gods kingdom.
local: Na minɛm fíte durunya n awɔrɛ nna n kɛrɛ nu, na bu bá tana Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, na bu dí na bu nú.

eng: The Holy Spirit will tell you at that very moment what you must say.
local: Dama nyini saŋga n ba juuri, Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni kére am nɔaniɛ kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo daka ka am jɔjɔ i a.

eng: And when he finds it, he is thrilled and places it on his shoulders.
local: Saŋga bo i ba ŋuuri wieeri ni, na i ahore jɔ́ na i fá i fa bo i bɛtɛ.

eng: If your whole body is full of light—with no part darkened—then it will be as full of light as when a lamp shines brightly on you.
local: Nzɛn u ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ woori wein nu, na awosin be awo má nu, i tá a papasi, kabo fitana sin ta wura u ni.

eng: Jesus replied, A certain man hosted a large dinner and invited many people.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Sɔnɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, na wɔ yo diire beberebe, na wɔ baandi jama beberebe ka bu ba di diire ni.

eng: Look! Those who are last will be first and those who are first will be last.
local: Am ti fɛn. Minɛm bo bu wo mɔ kisa ni, bá ka sin, na bo bu ka sin kisa n bá du mɔ.

eng: Someone from the crowd said to him, Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
local: Sɔnɔ be woori jama n nu, na wɔ se Yesu ka, M Kere­fɔ, se m gɔrɔ ka i kpaaki aja bo ya si ayaki i ama ya n nu, na i fa m dɛɛ ma m.

eng: When he arrives home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost sheep.
local: na i fá i fa ba awuru. Dɔ na i fére i danfom ni i tana­fɔm kɛrɛ na bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ, na i sé bu ka, M ahore ajɔ ka ma ŋu m bɔɛ n bo i miniiri ni. Nyini ti, am ma m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ kunnu jɔ.

eng: While Jesus was speaking, a Pharisee invited him to share a meal with him, so Jesus went and took his place at the table.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu jɔjɔ wieeri sɔ ni, na Farasi­fɔ be asere i ka i bra na bu di diire i dika, na Yesu akɔ awura ka i di diire ni.

eng: When it was time for the dinner to begin, he sent his servant to tell the invited guests, Come! The dinner is now ready.
local: Na saŋga n bo diire n ba yoori siriya ni, na wɔ sunma i akɔɔ ka i kɔ se minɛm n bo wɔ du mɔ abaandi bu n ka, Am bra, deke kɛrɛ ayo siriya.

eng: At that time, some Pharisees approached Jesus and said, Go! Get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you.
local: Nyini saŋga n na Farasi­fɔ nbem aba Yesu dɔ aba ase i ka, I ti ye ni ka a ŋmati fite fɛn, na a kɔ deke be, dama Fɛmɛ Herode koro ka i kun u wɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me as judge or referee between you and your brother.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Wɔrɔ, ŋma ayi m, ka m di am jɔrɛ na m kpaaki am aja nu ma am a.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who changes both heart and life than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need to change their hearts and lives.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, i tí sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɔnɔ kun kaakiiri i wo, ahorejɔ wó Nyɛmɛ dika n nu tara minɛ abura­ŋgɔna ni ŋgɔna bo bu ŋa bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ na wɔ yo má daka ka bu kaaki bu wo ni.

eng: When the Pharisee saw that Jesus didnt ritually purify his hands by washing before the meal, he was astonished.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ n abirisi kabo Yesu aŋmisi má i ŋu ka, ye i sudi diire ni.

eng: One by one, they all began to make excuses. The first one told him, I bought a farm and must go and see it. Please excuse me.
local: Na bu kun kun kɛrɛ asere atin ka bu koro ba má diire n di. Na ciciri su dɛɛ sɔnɔ n ase akɔɔ n ka, Jande, m sere u wɔ, ma to asiɛn, i ti ye ni ka m kɔ niɛ kabo i ti wɔ, m koro ba má.

eng: Jesus said to them, Go, tell that fox, Look, Im throwing out demons and healing people today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will complete my work.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am kɔ na am kɔ se nyini ŋaaku sɔnɔmbiri ka o, Ti fɛn. M sufɔn ajinim fite minɛm ŋu wɔ, na m ma tukpaki­fɔm nya laifiɛ, nyuma ni ahunma ŋgumi wɔ, na ahiɛni na m wíe m juma.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Watch out! Guard yourself against all kinds of greed. After all, ones life isnt determined by ones possessions, even when someone is very wealthy.
local: Na Yesu ase jama n kɛrɛ ka, Am niɛ am tii su dɔŋgu na am si am ŋu sa fa kɔ anyii barasu kɛrɛ sa, dama sɔnɔmbiri neŋge ti má i ŋgɔɔ, haari i mi n koro i la i ka sɛ.

eng: Or what woman, if she owns ten silver coins and loses one of them, wont light a lamp and sweep the house, searching her home carefully until she finds it?.
local: Na Yesu abo bu miɛɛnu bekun, i ŋa, Nzɛn bara be la i jɛtɛ ŋwaa buru, na kun miniiri, i mi yó sɛ? I só sin su wɔ, na i kpára bara yɛ awuru n nu, na i níɛniɛ deke n kɛrɛ, haari i bá ŋu i.

eng: The Lord said to him, Now, you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and platter, but your insides are stuffed with greed and wickedness.
local: Na Mibiɛ n ase i ka, Ambɛrɛ Farasi­fɔm, am ŋmisi am kɔpu ni am taalɛ sin wɔ, ama, am kunnu ayi ni anyii sa­yoo­wam, ni sunsunni tiɛm wɔ.

eng: Another said, I bought five teams of oxen, and Im going to check on them. Please excuse me.
local: Na nyiŋga n ase ka, Ma to m tooram asɛsɛ busu nnu, m koro ka m kɔ fa bu yo juma kisa kisa na m niɛ, m sere u wɔ jande, m koro ba má.

eng: However, its necessary for me to travel today, tomorrow, and the next day because its impossible for a prophet to be killed outside of Jerusalem.
local: Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, wɔ yo daka ka m sukɔ ɔ, nyuma, ni ahunma, ni ahiɛni, dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ kɛrɛ ti ye ni ka bu kun i Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ.

eng: Then he told them a parable: A certain rich mans land produced a bountiful crop.
local: Dɔ na Yesu abo bu ahin miɛɛnu ni, i ŋa, Sɔnɔ neŋge­fɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, na wɔ dɔ i fie, na wɔ nya diire beberebe.

eng: When she finds it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, Celebrate with me because Ive found my lost coin.
local: Na saŋga bo i ba ŋuuri ni, i fére i danfom ni i tana­fɔm kɛrɛ, na bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ, na i sé bu ka, M ahore ajɔ ka ma ŋu m ŋwaa n bo i miniiri ni. Nyini ti, am ma m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ kunnu jɔ.

eng: Foolish people! Didnt the one who made the outside also make the inside?.
local: Ambɛrɛ sinnzinm ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka Nyɛmɛ yoori deke kɛrɛ kunnu ni i sin a.

eng: Another said, I just got married, so I cant come.
local: Na nyiŋga n gusu ase ka, Ma ja bara kisa lɛɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti, m koro ba má.

eng: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those who were sent to you! How often I have wanted to gather your people just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you didnt want that.
local: Na Yesu abo awie ase ka, Oi, ambɛrɛ Yɛrusalem­fɔm. Ambɛrɛ kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n a, na minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ sunmaari bu am dɔ ni, am atu bu yabuɛ. Saŋga kɛrɛ m koro ka m tiɛn am nɔɔ, na m fa m saa bara yɛ am, kabo akɔ tiɛn i nbaam nɔɔ, i laŋkpɛtɛ nu ni, ama, am soro má m su.

eng: He said to himself, What will I do? I have no place to store my harvest!.
local: Na wɔ sunsun i tii nu na wɔ se ka, M la má deke bo m fa ahin diire n kɛrɛ se. Ye m yó sɛ wɔ.

eng: In the same way, I tell you, joy breaks out in the presence of Gods angels over one sinner who changes both heart and life.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn satiɛ­yo­fɔ kun kaakiiri i wo, ahorejɔ wó Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm n dɔ.

eng: When he returned, the servant reported these excuses to his master. The master of the house became angry and said to his servant, Go quickly to the citys streets, the busy ones and the side streets, and bring the poor, crippled, blind, and lame.
local: Na akɔɔ n akpie i nyi akɔ ase i mibiɛ n kabo minɛm kɛrɛ ase i ni. Dɔ na yaa ati awuru­fɔ­biɛ n agaya, na wɔ se yiri akɔɔ n ka, Yo ndende na a kɔ miɛ kunnu atiŋgberem kɛrɛ su, na a kɔ fere yaari­fɔm, ni wɔbukam, ni anyinsinm, ni minɛm bo bu saam ni bu jaam awu ni, na a fa bu bra.

eng: Therefore, give to those in need from the core of who you are and you will be clean all over.
local: Má nzɛn am fa deke bo i wo am kɔpu ni am taalɛ kunnu ma yaari­fɔm, má deke kɛrɛ bo am yo i, má i yo casi ma am wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: Look, your house is abandoned. I tell you, you wont see me until the time comes when you say, Blessings on the one who comes in the Lords name .
local: Am niɛ fɛn, Nyɛmɛ yáki am nu ji. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, fite kisa fa kɔ, am ŋú má m kun, má ka saŋga bo am bá se ka, Tere­fiɛ wo yiri n bo i suba Micɛra n dɛɛ duma n nu n dɔ.

eng: Then he thought, Heres what Ill do. Ill tear down my barns and build bigger ones. Thats where Ill store all my grain and goods.
local: Na wɔ se ka, Kabo m yó yaa, m sún m bondom kɛrɛ ji wɔ, na m tá ŋgbiŋgbirim, na m fá m diire n ni neŋge kɛrɛ bo m la bu n wura nu.

eng: Jesus said, A certain man had two sons.
local: Na Yesu akaaki abo bu miɛɛnu be bekun, i ŋa, Sɔnɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, i la nbaa­biɛsɔm nnyɔ.

eng: The servant said, Master, your instructions have been followed and there is still room.
local: Wɔ cɛ má na akɔɔ n akɔ aba, na wɔ se ka, M mibiɛ, ma yo kabo a seeri ka m yo ni, ama, fofoe toko ka.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You give a tenth of your mint, rue, and garden herbs of all kinds, while neglecting justice and love for God. These you ought to have done without neglecting the others.
local: Ambɛrɛ Farasi­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro, dama am yi am jakam ma Nyɛmɛ wɔ, haari ni am maakunm, ni ŋgɛtɛm, ni nvan­nvanm kɛrɛ, ama, am yo má nahɔrɛ sam, na am kaaki koro má Nyɛmɛ. Wɔ yo daka ka am yo nyini sam saŋga kɛrɛ, na am fa jaka yii­wam buka su.

eng: Ill say to myself, You have stored up plenty of goods, enough for several years. Take it easy! Eat, drink, and enjoy yourself.
local: Na m nyá se ka ma di tere­fiɛ, puitiri­pui kpíɛ má m su kun, m tána a, ahin afɔ nyɛ nyɛ n kɛrɛ, na m dí na m nú na m ahore jɔ́.

eng: The younger son said to his father, Father, give me my share of the inheritance. Then the father divided his estate between them.
local: Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, na kaan n ase i si n ka, M si, kpaaki u aja kɛrɛ nu na a fa m dɛɛ ma m kisa. Na i si n akpaaki aja n nu, na wɔ fa bu nnyɔ n kɛrɛ dɛɛ ama bu.

eng: The master said to the servant, Go to the highways and back alleys and urge people to come in so that my house will be filled.
local: Na awuru­fɔ­biɛ n ase i ka, Fite kɔ miɛ n ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛ, ni i kɔrɔkam kɛrɛ nu, na a kɔ fere minɛm ka bu bra, adena m awuru n yi ni jama.

eng: How terrible for you Pharisees! You love the most prominent seats in the synagogues and respectful greetings in the marketplaces.
local: Ambɛrɛ Farasi­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am koro ka am tana kpirikoro tanaa­birim wɔ, am tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, na am koro ka minɛm yo am ansɛ gɔɔsu wɔ, na bu ma am jirima.

eng: But God said to him, Fool, tonight you will die. Now who will get the things you have prepared for yourself.
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ase i ka, Wɔrɔ sinnzin ŋgbɛɛn ni. Ahin kɔŋguɛ n nu bɛrɛ, m yí u ŋgɔɔ n fite u dɔ. Na m níɛ ka nyini neŋge n kɛrɛ bo a tiɛn i nɔɔ ni ká ŋma dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Soon afterward, the younger son gathered everything together and took a trip to a land far away. There, he wasted his wealth through extravagant living.
local: Wɔ cɛ má, na kaan n atɔ i dɛɛ neŋge n kɛrɛ bo i si n fa maari i ni. I nyaari ŋwaa n wieeri sɔ ni, na wɔ yasu akɔ asiɛn dede be su. Dɔ na wɔ kɔ adi ŋwaa ni ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn haari na i kɛrɛ aba awie.

eng: I tell you, not one of those who were invited will taste my dinner.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nyini minɛm kɛrɛ bo m baandiiri bu na ba ba má ni, bɛrɛ kun n kpein mmɔɔ nɔɔ kán má m diire n kun.

eng: How terrible for you! You are like unmarked graves, and people walk on them without recognizing it.
local: Am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am ti ka faŋ­kunmam bo bu la má nyaari see­wa be n wɔ, na minɛm nati su na bu si má ka faŋ­kunma lɛɛ.

eng: This is the way it will be for those who hoard things for themselves and arent rich toward God.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bekun ka, I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, fa kɔ minɛm bo bu kpini neŋge beberebe fa se durunya n nu fɛn ni, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɔ, bu la má pui.

eng: When he had used up his resources, a severe food shortage arose in that country and he began to be in need.
local: Saŋga bo i ba diiri ŋwaa n kɛrɛ wieeri n na ahɔɛ bambaka be atɔ nyini asiɛn n su. Dɔ, wɔ la má pui bo i di kun.

eng: Large crowds were traveling with Jesus. Turning to them, he said.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu wo kɔ su ni, na jama beberebe asu i su, na wɔ cɛ aniɛ bu, na wɔ se bu ka.

eng: One of the legal experts responded, Teacher, when you say these things, you are insulting us too.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n kun ase Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, nzɛn a seeri sɔ, a kpiɛ nzukɔ asaŋga ya kɛrɛ mmɔɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Then Jesus said to his disciples, Therefore, I say to you, dont worry about your life, what you will eat, or about your body, what you will wear.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Nyini ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má am tana sukoko fa kɔ am ŋgɔɔ su, kabo am dí, na má am kaaki sukoko fa kɔ am ŋuniɛ su, kabo am nyá suturam bo am fá wura na am kára.

eng: He hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ fa i ŋu akɔ awura nyini asiɛn n su miɛbaa be saa nu, ka i yo juma ma i mi ni, adena i nya diire di. Dɔ na miɛbaa n afa i akɔ i fiesu ka i kɔ suniɛ i afam dika.

eng: Whoever comes to me and doesnt hate father and mother, spouse and children, and brothers and sisters—yes, even ones own life—cannot be my disciple.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ba m dɔ, i koro yo má m susu­fɔ, má ka i mi akoro m atara i si, ni i ni, ni i yi, ni i nbaam, ni i ninbaam, ni i sinbaam, ni haari yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii.

eng: Jesus said, How terrible for you legal experts too! You load people down with impossible burdens and you refuse to lift a single finger to help them.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am cici sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri sɔ minɛm wɔ, ama, ambɛrɛ ni am tii soro má su ka am fa am saa kan i.

eng: There is more to life than food and more to the body than clothing.
local: Dama ŋgɔɔ tara diire, na aŋuniɛ­mbaa n gusu tara suturam.

eng: He longed to eat his fill from what the pigs ate, but no one gave him anything.
local: Na ahɔɛ aba ati i agaya, haari i ba koro ka i di afam dɛɛ diire n be mmɔɔ ɔ, ama, be ama má i deke be na i di.

eng: Whoever doesnt carry their own cross and follow me cannot be my disciple.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro sɔ má i yiwee baka jaanu n na i su m su, i mi koro yo má m susu­fɔ.

eng: How terrible for you! You built memorials to the prophets, whom your ancestors killed.
local: Ambɛrɛ bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am yo faŋ­kunmam dɔŋgu fa ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm wɔ, bɛrɛ bo am nam yo kuunri bu ni.

eng: Consider the ravens: they neither plant nor harvest, they have no silo or barn, yet God feeds them. You are worth so much more than birds!.
local: Am niɛ anumam, haari ni bo bu dɔ má ni, na bu kaaki kpiɛ má ni, bu la má bondo gusu, bo bu fa diire wura nu, se Nyɛmɛ ma bu diire ye bu di a. Ambɛrɛ la anyunuyi tara anumam e.

eng: When he came to his senses, he said, How many of my fathers hired hands have more than enough food, but Im starving to death!.
local: Kasin, na wɔ ba aŋu i ŋu ŋgbɛɛn fa kɔ sa n bo wɔ yo i n su, na wɔ bu i tii nu, na wɔ se i ŋu ka, M si npa­di­fɔm kɛrɛ di diire wɔ na i ka, ama, mini ba wo fɛn suwu ni ahɔɛ n a.

eng: If one of you wanted to build a tower, wouldnt you first sit down and calculate the cost, to determine whether you have enough money to complete it?.
local: Ma m fa i ka sɔnɔ ŋa i ta awuru, i du mɔ tana wɔ, na i bu akunta ka, na i niɛ ka i la ŋwaa bo i koro ju awuru n ta.

eng: In this way, you testify that you approve of your ancestors deeds. They killed the prophets, and you build memorials!.
local: Nyini kere ka ambɛrɛ ni am tii saa woori sa n bo am nam yo yoori i n nu lɛɛ. Bɛrɛ kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n a, na ambɛrɛ ata bu faŋ­kunmam.

eng: Who among you by worrying can add a single moment to your life?.
local: Am nu, ŋma tana koko fa kɔ i koroo­wam su ye i mi koro buka i ŋgɔɔ tintiin su haari kaan sɔ a? Ai, be wo má bɛrɛ.

eng: I will get up and go to my father, and say to him,Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
local: M kpíe m nyi kɔ m si dɔ ɔ, na m kɔ́ se i ka, M si, ma do Nyɛmɛ ni wɔrɔ kɛrɛ ŋgasi.

eng: Otherwise, when you have laid the foundation but couldnt finish the tower, all who see it will begin to belittle you.
local: Nzɛn i ŋwaa ju má bo i ta awuru n wie, na i boori i bu na wɔ wie má i, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sin ŋuuri sa n bo wɔ yo i ni, i mi síri wɔ.

eng: Therefore, Gods wisdom has said, I will send prophets and apostles to them and they will harass and kill some of them.
local: Nyini ti, ye Nyɛmɛ Alecira­fɔ n seeri ka, M súnma m nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm, ni m jaramasam bu dɔ, na bu kún nbem, na bu kére nbem wuwuruwa.

eng: If you cant do such a small thing, why worry about the rest?.
local: Nzɛn am koro yo má nyini deke n kaan n be sɔ, nzu ti ye dekem bo bu kaari n kɛrɛ miɛ am a?.

eng: I no longer deserve to be called your son. Take me on as one of your hired hands.
local: I ti má ye ni ka a fere m ka u wa kun, fa m yo u npa­di­fɔm ni kun.

eng: They will say, Heres the person who began construction and couldnt complete it.
local: na bu sé ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n boori i awuru n bu wɔ, ama, wɔ koro awie má i.

eng: As a result, this generation will be charged with the murder of all the prophets since the beginning of time.
local: Nyini ti, Nyɛmɛ cín ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm sui fa kɔ kabo am nam yo kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm, fiteeri durunya n bumboori fa ba ju haari ni kisa ni.

eng: Notice how the lilies grow. They dont wear themselves out with work, and they dont spin cloth. But I say to you that even Solomon in all his splendor wasnt dressed like one of these.
local: Am niɛ kabo wuram bo pum barasu barasu a. Bu yo má juma be, bu kaaki tu má gise. Ama, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, haari Fɛmɛ Solomon ni i neŋge n kɛrɛ bo i yo la i ni, wɔ la má sutura bo i yo nyumi juuri nyini wura pum ni be.

eng: So he got up and went to his father. While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion. His father ran to him, hugged him, and kissed him.
local: Dɔ na wɔ yasu na i kpie i nyi kɔ i si dɔ. Saŋga bo i toko wo dede suba ni, na i si n aŋu i, na wɔ si i aŋunfɔ agaya. Na wɔ ŋmati akpa i atin, na wɔ fa i saa abara ayɛ i, na wɔ biti i.

eng: Or what king would go to war against another king without first sitting down to consider whether his ten thousand soldiers could go up against the twenty thousand coming against him?.
local: Ma m kaaki fa i ka nzɛn fɛmɛ bo i la i sojam akpii buru ye i koro ka i kɔ luɛ ni i beŋgu fɛmɛ be bo yiri la sojam akpii abura­nyɔ, i tána sunsun wɔ ka, na i niɛ ka i koro kun luɛ ni i beŋgu ni.

eng: This includes the murder of every prophet—from Abel to Zechariah—who was killed between the altar and the holy place. Yes, Im telling you, this generation will be charged with it.
local: Bu yo yoori sɔ ɔ, fite saŋga n bo bu kuunri sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Abɛli n fa ba juuri saŋga n bo bu kuunri sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Zakariya ni. Yiri ye bu kuunri i Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n ni Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n afiɛn n a. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, nyini sam kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ bá cin ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm sui n a.

eng: If God dresses grass in the field so beautifully, even though its alive today and tomorrow its thrown into the furnace, how much more will God do for you, you people of weak faith!.
local: Nzɛn wuram bo bu wo bɛrɛ nyuma, na ahunma a ŋú ka ba kpiɛ bu na bu yara bu, ye Nyɛmɛ niɛ bu dika, nzu ti ye i níɛ má ambɛrɛ bo am tara wuram co n a? Ambɛrɛ, am la má yarada fieo.

eng: Then his son said, Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I no longer deserve to be called your son.
local: Na baa n ase i si n ka, M si, ma do Nyɛmɛ ni wɔrɔ kɛrɛ ŋgasi. I ti má ye ni ka a fere m ka u wa kun.

eng: And if he didnt think he could win, he would send a representative to discuss terms of peace while his enemy was still a long way off.
local: Nzɛn i ŋuuri ka i koro kɔrɔ má nu, i dú mɔ sunma sɔnɔ ɔ, na i kpá i beŋgu fɛmɛ n atin na i kɔ́ sere i ka, i má bu yo jɔrɛ n dɔŋgu.

eng: How terrible for you legal experts! You snatched away the key of knowledge. You didnt enter yourselves, and you stood in the way of those who were entering.
local: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am akere má minɛm Nyɛmɛ atin, kabo i ti ye ni ka am kere bu ni, na ambɛrɛ ni am tii akete ka am su má i atin n su, ye am akanda bo bu koro ka bu su i su ni.

eng: Dont chase after what you will eat and what you will drink. Stop worrying.
local: Nyini ti, saŋga kɛrɛ, má am ma am sunsunni wo deke bo am dí, ni deke bo am nú su. Má am tana koko fieo.

eng: But the father said to his servants, Quickly, bring out the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet!.
local: Ama, na i si n afere i akɔɔm, na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ ndende na am kɔ niɛ suturam kɛrɛ nu bo i ti kpa, na am fa ba wura i, na am fa ŋgaa ba wura i saa maraka, na am fa gbagbaa ba wura i jaa.

eng: In the same way, none of you who are unwilling to give up all of your possessions can be my disciple.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ni ambɛrɛ gusu dɔ, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wara afi má i tiɛŋgu deke, i mi koro yo má m susu­fɔ.

eng: As he left there, the legal experts and Pharisees began to resent him deeply and to ask him pointed questions about many things.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu yasuuri nyini dika n nu ni, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n afa i kpɔ agaya, na ba bisa i bisaa­wam beberebe.

eng: All the nations of the world long for these things. Your Father knows that you need them.
local: Dama durunya n nu­fɔm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni, bɛrɛ sunsun sɔ ɔ saŋga kɛrɛ. Am Si Nyɛmɛ si ka am koro nyini dekem ni.

eng: Fetch the fattened calf and slaughter it. We must celebrate with feasting.
local: Na am nya kɔ niɛ naanim n nu, na am tara bo i la anyunuyi bra, na am ba kpiɛ i kɔm yo i dɔŋgu, na am ma ya di na ya nu.

eng: Salt is good. But if salt loses its flavor, how will it become salty again?.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Njin yo fiɛ, ama, nzɛn i fiɛfiɛ nya ba saari wieeri, i koro yo má fiɛfiɛ kun.

eng: They plotted against him, trying to trap him in his words.
local: na ba boro bu nyi na bu sɔ i ŋgɛɛ, adena i jɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu.

eng: Jesus also said to the disciples, A certain rich man heard that his household manager was wasting his estate.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Biɛ neŋge­fɔ be woori bɛrɛ a, i la i amusui­su­fɔ kpiɛn bo yiri niɛ i neŋge n kɛrɛ su a. Na minɛm aba neŋge­fɔ n dɔ aba se i ka i amusui­su­fɔ kpiɛn ni susaaki i neŋgem ni.

eng: Instead, desire his kingdom and these things will be given to you as well.
local: Má am yo sɔ, ama, am kpini Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n busu ka, na i nyá ba fa nyini dekem n buka am su.

eng: because this son of mine was dead and has come back to life! He was lost and is found! And they began to celebrate.
local: Dama m ahin wa ni, i wuuri wɔ, ye wɔ teŋge a, i miniiri wɔ, ye ma ŋu i a. Dɔ na ba bo i bu ni di ni nu.

eng: It has no value, neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. People throw it away. Whoever has ears to hear should pay attention.
local: I koro buka má asiɛn ni mmɔɔ na i kaaki koro buka má nanyɛti na i yo diire, ŋka bu fa butu wɔ. Bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti.

eng: He called the manager in and said to him, What is this I hear about you? Give me a report of your administration because you can no longer serve as my manager.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ fere i, na wɔ se i ka, M suti jɔrɛ nbem fa kɔ u dɔ. Nyini ti, ba bu akunta kere m na m si kabo a bita m neŋgem barasu, dama a koro niɛ má bu dika kun.

eng: Dont be afraid, little flock, because your Father delights in giving you the kingdom.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Má am soro sɛrɛ, haari ni bo am ti ka nbɔɛm bo bu sunnu má ni, dama am Si Nyɛmɛ ayi am ka am ni yiri sáŋga di i fɛmɛya n a.

eng: Now his older son was in the field. Coming in from the field, he approached the house and heard music and dancing.
local: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ baa­kpiɛn n toko wo fiesu. I ba suba awuru, na saŋga bo i ba dodoori awuru n na wɔ ti ka bu wo agɔrɛ gɔ su ni jue.

eng: The household manager said to himself, What will I do now that my master is firing me as his manager? Im not strong enough to dig and too proud to beg.
local: Na amusui­su­fɔ kpiɛn n ase i tii nu ka, Ye kisa na m mibiɛ ni sufɔn m fite m juma n nu ni, m yó sɛ wɔ? M la má faŋga bo m koro dɔ, m gusu nyunu asin ni bo m nati susere.

eng: Sell your possessions and give to those in need. Make for yourselves wallets that dont wear out—a treasure in heaven that never runs out. No thief comes near there, and no moth destroys.
local: Am tɔ am deke bo am la i, na am fa ŋwaa n ma yaari­fɔm. Am kpini ajɛkɛ see­biri bo i saaki má n busu. Am fa ajɛkɛ bo i kpiɛ má su n fa se Nyɛmɛ dika n nu, dɔ ye awiefɔ koro wura wɔ má i a, na watarawam gusu koro muka má i.

eng: He called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
local: Dɔ na wɔ fere i si akɔɔm kun, na wɔ bisa i ka, Nzu sa yoori awuru a.

eng: I know what Ill do so that, when I am removed from my management position, people will welcome me into their houses.
local: Ɛɛnhɛɛn, kisa m si kabo m yo, na haari nzɛn m ba fuunri m juma ni, m fún má m danfom bo m kɔ́ wo bu dɔ.

eng: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be too.
local: Dama deke bo am ajɛkɛ wo ni, am ahore­mbaa kɛrɛ gusu wó dɔ ɔ.

eng: The servant replied, Your brother has arrived, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he received his son back safe and sound.
local: Na akɔɔ n abɔ i nu ka, U niɛma ye wɔ ba awuru a, ye u si ama nɔɔ ye ya kpiɛ naani bo i la anyunuyi n kɔm, dama u niɛma n aba awuru ni laifiɛ n ti.

eng: One by one, the manager sent for each person who owed his master money. He said to the first, How much do you owe my master.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ fere minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu bita i mibiɛ n kaari n kun kun. Na wɔ bisa ciciri su dɛɛ sɔnɔ n ka, A bita m mibiɛ n kaari nyɛ wɔ.

eng: Be dressed for service and keep your lamps lit.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Am kpa am ŋu wɔ, na am so am fitana suŋminda.

eng: Then the older son was furious and didnt want to enter in, but his father came out and begged him.
local: Na yaa ati baa­kpiɛn ni agaya, haari na wɔ kete ka i wura má awuru n nu. Nyini ti, na i si n afite aba asere i jande ka i wura.

eng: He said, Nine hundred gallons of olive oil.The manager said to him, Take your contract, sit down quickly, and write four hundred fifty gallons.
local: Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, M bita i ŋguin nzue sɔ gaŋga yaa kun wɔ. Na amusui­su­fɔ kpiɛn n ase i ka, De u kaari kadasi ni, na a tana, na a kɛrɛ ka a bita gaŋga aburu­nu wɔ.

eng: Be like people waiting for their master to come home from a wedding celebration, who can immediately open the door for him when he arrives and knocks on the door.
local: Am yo sɔ ɔ, ka minɛm bo bu tana ŋminda bu mibiɛ bo wɔ kɔ konyaya, na i bá n wɔ, na saŋga bo i ba boboori anɔ n kpeŋga ni, bu kába teke i wɔ na i wúra.

eng: He answered his father, Look, Ive served you all these years, and I never disobeyed your instruction. Yet youve never given me as much as a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
local: Ama, na wɔ se i si n ka, Ti fɛn, afɔ nyɛ nyɛ lɛɛ bo m wo u dɔ yo juma ma u, ka m ti akɔɔ ɔ, ma kete má u nɔaniɛ be. A koro ama má m puitiri­pui, haari siri­mbaa mmɔɔ ka m gusu fa na m ni m danfom fa di na ya nu.

eng: Then the manager said to another, How much do you owe? He said, One thousand bushels of wheat.He said, Take your contract and write eight hundred.
local: Na wɔ nya abisa nyiŋga gusu ka, Ye wɔrɔ di, a bita kaari nyɛ wɔ? Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, M bita i alɛ­mbaa pirim bɔtɔ yaa kun wɔ. Na amusui­su­fɔ kpiɛn n ase i gusu ka, De u kaari kadasi na a kɛrɛ ka a bita pirim bɔtɔ abura­mɔcuɛ wɔ.

eng: Happy are those servants whom the master finds waiting up when he arrives. I assure you that, when he arrives, he will dress himself to serve, seat them at the table as honored guests, and wait on them.
local: Tere­fiɛ wo nyini akɔɔm n dɔ, nzɛn bu mibiɛ n ba ŋu ka bu toko tana ŋminda i. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, i má bɛrɛ tana a, na yiri ni i tii fá diire ba ma bu na bu di.

eng: But when this son of yours returned, after gobbling up your estate on prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.
local: Ama, u ahin wa n yiri, wɔ fa u aja bo a fa ama i n akɔ boro, na wɔ kɔ ayo i koroo­wa ni nbara­sakaram. Haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ ni, i kpieeri i nyi ba juuri awuru ni, a ma nɔɔ ka bu kpiɛ naani bo i la anyunuyi n kɔm ma i.

eng: The master commended the dishonest manager because he acted cleverly. People who belong to this world are more clever in dealing with their peers than are people who belong to the light.
local: I mibiɛ ŋuuri sɔ ni, na wɔ kansi i fa kɔ nyini ŋgɔnlɛ bo i la i n su, haari ni bo i la má dabuŋu ni. Kabo i ti lɛɛ, durunya n nu­fɔm la ŋgɔnlɛ fa kɔ durunya n nu dɛɛ sa­yoo­wam n su tara minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ ma bu wo wein n nu ni.

eng: Happy are those whom he finds alert, even if he comes at midnight or just before dawn.
local: Nzɛn haari i kpieeri i nyi ba juuri kɔŋguɛ jinanjin, wara akɔnyuma bo saŋga, na i ba ŋu ka bu toko tana ŋminda i, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ.

eng: Then his father said, Son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
local: Na i si n ase i ka, M wa, saŋga kɛrɛ mini ni wɔrɔ wo bɛrɛ a, deke kɛrɛ bo i ti m dɛɛ ti u dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Jesus said to his disciples, Things that cause people to trip and fall into sin must happen, but how terrible it is for the person through whom they happen.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Sam nbem bo bu yo na bu fa minɛ wura satiɛ nu ni, nyini sam ni bá yo saŋga kɛrɛ, ama, anyum­boro wo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ma nyini sam yoori n dɔ.

eng: I tell you, use worldly wealth to make friends for yourselves so that when its gone, you will be welcomed into the eternal homes.
local: Na Yesu ase bu bekun ka, M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, am fa durunya n nu dɛɛ fin ajɛkɛ bo am la i n fa tara danfoya, adena ajɛkɛ n ba ŋmati yakiiri am, na minɛm bá de am wura woo­biri kpa bo i la má awieeri n nu.

eng: But know this, if the homeowner had known what time the thief was coming, he wouldnt have allowed his home to be broken into.
local: Am si tenle ka nzɛn má awuru­fɔ­biɛ n si saŋga bo awiefɔ ni bá i dika na i ba wɔ i, má i tana ŋminda a, na awiefɔ n koro ba wura má.

eng: But we had to celebrate and be glad because this brother of yours was dead and is alive. He was lost and is found.
local: Ama, wɔ yo daka ka ya di na ya nu na ya gɔ agɔrɛ, dama u niɛma ni, i wuuri wɔ, ye wɔ teŋge a, i miniiri wɔ, ye ma ŋu i a.

eng: It would be better for them to be thrown into a lake with a large stone hung around their neck than to cause one of these little ones to trip and fall into sin.
local: Nzɛn má bu faari ŋgani san yabuɛ ciciiri i mi kɔm na bu fa i kɔ tuuri tieku nu mmɔɔ, má i ti ye tara bo i mi ma ahin yarada­yo­fɔm n be wura satiɛ nu.

eng: Whoever is faithful with little is also faithful with much, and the one who is dishonest with little is also dishonest with much.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo minɛm koro daafi i ŋu ka i bita deke kaan ni nahɔrɛ atin, yiri ye bu koro daafi i ŋu ka i bita deke bambaka mmɔɔ ni nahɔrɛ atin a. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo bu koro daafi má i ŋu ka i bita deke kaan ni nahɔrɛ atin, bu koro daafi má i mi ŋu ka i bita deke bambaka mmɔɔ.

eng: You also must be ready, because the Human Oneis coming at a time when you dont expect him.
local: Ambɛrɛ gusu kpa am ŋu saŋga kɛrɛ, dama mini Adamande Wa ni bá saŋga bo am sunsun má ka m bá n wɔ.

eng: Watch yourselves! If your brother or sister sins, warn them to stop. If they change their hearts and lives, forgive them.
local: Nyini ti, am si am ŋu sa. Nzɛn wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo u niwa yoori u satiɛ be, jaraki i, na nzɛn i kaakiiri i wo, na a fa i satiɛ n ce i.

eng: If you havent been faithful with worldly wealth, who will trust you with true riches?.
local: Tɔ, nzɛn ambɛrɛ la má dabuŋu bo am koro bita durunya n nu dɛɛ fin ajɛkɛm ni nahɔrɛ atin, ŋma fá Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ajɛkɛm wura am saa nu?.

eng: Peter said, Lord, are you telling this parable for us or for everyone.
local: I jɔjɔɔri sɔ n na Petoro abisa i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, a bo ahin miɛɛnu n ni yɛrɛ wɔ, nzɛn a ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: Even if someone sins against you seven times in one day and returns to you seven times and says, I am changing my ways, you must forgive that person.
local: Nzɛn i yoori u satiɛ kɔ jinaari haari kpe nso mmɔɔ wiɛ kun nu, na i baari u dɔ ba seeri u ka, Ma yo u satiɛ, fa i satiɛ n ce i.

eng: If you havent been faithful with someone elses property, who will give you your own?.
local: Na nzɛn am la má dabuŋu bo am koro bita be dɛɛ ajɛkɛm ni nahɔrɛ atin, ŋma fá deke bo i bá yo am tiɛŋgu dɛɛ ma am a?.

eng: The Lord replied, Who are the faithful and wise managers whom the master will put in charge of his household servants, to give them their food at the proper time?.
local: Na Yesu abisa i ka, Amusui­su­fɔ kpiɛn n bo i la dabuŋu na i kaaki la alecira n la ŋma a? Yiri la bo i mibiɛ n afa amusui­su­fɔ nyiŋgam awura i saa nu ka i kpaaki diire nu ma bu, saŋga bo i ti ye ni ka i ma bu ni.

eng: The apostles said to the Lord, Increase our faith.
local: Na Yesu jaramasam n ase i ka, Buka ya na ya yarada yo bii­wa.

eng: No household servant can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be loyal to the one and have contempt for the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.
local: Sɔnɔ kun koro saŋga su má mibiɛm nnyɔ. Nzɛn i ŋa i yó sɔ, i kpɔ́ kun n wɔ, na i kóro nyiŋga ni, wara, i bóro i nyi su kun wɔ, na i bú nyiŋga ni ŋgbɛɛn. Am koro saŋga su má Nyɛmɛ ni ajɛkɛ.

eng: Happy are the servants whom the master finds fulfilling their responsibilities when he comes.
local: Nzɛn i mibiɛ n ba ŋuuri ka i suyo juma n kabo i seeri i ka i yo i ni, tere­fiɛ wo nyini amusui­su­fɔ n dɔ.

eng: The Lord replied, If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea, and it would obey you.
local: Na Mibiɛ n ase bu ka, Nzɛn má am la yarada bo i kɔ ju kabo nyɛm mbaa ti ni mmɔɔ, má am koro se ahin koto baka n ka, Kpekiti ni u bunlui n kɛrɛ na a kɔ jina tieku n nu, má i de am nɔaniɛ n wɔ.

eng: The Pharisees, who were money-lovers, heard all this and sneered at Jesus.
local: Saŋga bo Farasi­fɔm n tiiri ahin n kɛrɛ n sɔ ni, na ba siri i, dama bu ti ŋwaa koro­fɔm wɔ.

eng: I assure you that the master will put them in charge of all his possessions.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, nyini amusui­su­fɔ n mibiɛ ni fá i aja kɛrɛ wura i saa nu wɔ, na i níɛ i dika.

eng: Would any of you say to your servant, who had just come in from the field after plowing or tending sheep, Come! Sit down for dinner?.
local: Ma m fa i ka, am nu, nzɛn be la akɔɔ na i wo fiesu wara i sasa nbɔɛm wɔ, na i ba baari awuru, am nu ŋma ye i kaba se akɔɔ n ka, Ba tana na a di diire a?.

eng: He said to them, You are the ones who justify yourselves before other people, but God knows your hearts. What is highly valued by people is deeply offensive to God.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ ŋa am ti minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ na minɛm ŋu am n a, ama, Nyɛmɛ yiri si am ahore su kɛrɛ. Dama deke bo adamande sunsun ka i ŋusu nya má ni, nyini ye Nyɛmɛ kpɔ i a.

eng: Jesus was telling them a parable about their need to pray continuously and not to be discouraged.
local: Dɔ na Yesu abo miɛɛnu akere i susu­fɔm n ka wɔ yo daka ka bu sere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ, na má bu yaki i sere.

eng: But suppose that these servants should say to themselves, My master is taking his time about coming. And suppose they began to beat the servants, both men and women, and to eat, drink, and get drunk.
local: Ama, nzɛn i ti amusui­su­fɔ tiɛ­fɔ, i súnsun ka i mibiɛ ni kpíe má i nyi ba má nde. A ŋu ka i subo amusui­su­fɔ nyiŋgam ni, bu nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram kɛrɛ, na i sudi na i sunu nzan bo.

eng: Wouldnt you say instead, Fix my dinner. Put on the clothes of a table servant and wait on me while I eat and drink. After that, you can eat and drink?.
local: Am be se má i sɔ da. Ama, kabo a se i n la ka, Yo m diire siriya, na a jina ŋminda na m di na m nu ka, na a nya kɔ yo u dɛɛ di.

eng: Until John, there was only the Law and the Prophets. Since then, the good news of Gods kingdom is preached, and everyone is urged to enter it.
local: Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n bo Moyisi ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛɛri i n wo bɛrɛ ka, ye Yohane nya baari a, nyini n sin, na ba kan labari kpa n akere minɛm fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n su, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ suboro i nyi ka i kpini atin na i wura nu.

eng: He said, In a certain city there was a judge who neither feared God nor respected people.
local: I ŋa, Jɔrɛ­di­fɔ be woori miɛ be su a, i soro má Nyɛmɛ, na i kaaki bu má be.

eng: The master of those servants would come on a day when they werent expecting him, at a time they couldnt predict. The master will cut them into pieces and assign them a place with the unfaithful.
local: dɔ na i mibiɛ n bá kpie i nyi ba cɛɛn bo i sunsun má ka i bá, na i kaaki si má ba n saŋga ni. Nyini saŋga n na i mibiɛ n cín i sui agaya, na i fá i bo minɛm bo bu yo má i yarada n su.

eng: You wont thank the servant because the servant did what you asked, will you?.
local: Nzɛn u akɔɔ n deeri u nɔaniɛ na i yoori juma bo a se i ka i yo i ni, a se má i ka, M da u asi.

eng: Its easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for the smallest stroke of a pen in the Law to drop out.
local: I ti má kekereke na aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ bá mini, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kampiti sɔ mmɔɔ ká má ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: In that city there was a widow who kept coming to him, asking, Give me justice in this case against my adversary.
local: Na bara kuna­fɔ be awo nyini miɛ n su, saŋga kɛrɛ i ba jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n dɔ ɔ, na i ba sere i ka, Jɔrɛ bo mini ni m kpɔ­fɔ n bita i ni, buka m na m nya nahɔrɛ.

eng: That servant who knew his masters will but didnt prepare for it or act on it will be beaten severely.
