local: Na nzɛn amusui­su­fɔ n si deke bo i mibiɛ n koro ka i yo i, na wɔ yo má siriya na i yo deke bo i mibiɛ n koro ka i yo ni, i bó i wɔ nyunyumi.

eng: In the same way, when you have done everything required of you, you should say, We servants deserve no special praise. We have only done our duty.
local: I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ambɛrɛ gusu dɔ, saŋga bo am ayo juma n kɛrɛ bo am mibiɛ n ase ka am yo i ni, am se ka, Yɛrɛ ti akɔɔm wɔ, ya ti má ye ni ka bu kansi ya, dama ya juma lɛɛ, ye ya yo i n a.

eng: Any man who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and a man who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i faari i yi jiiri na i jaari bara fɔfɔrɛ, i mi n asaaki ajaa ciire lɛɛ. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jaari bara bo wɔ fite i hun ajaa bu, i mi n asaaki ajaa ciire lɛɛ.

eng: For a while he refused but finally said to himself, I dont fear God or respect people.
local: Na wɔ cɛ bo jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n bɔ má i n nu. Ama, na kasin, na wɔ ba asunsun i tii nu, na wɔ se ka, Haari ni bo m soro má Nyɛmɛ na m bu má be ni.

eng: The one who didnt know the masters will but who did things deserving punishment will be beaten only a little. Much will be demanded from everyone who has been given much, and from the one who has been entrusted with much, even more will be asked.
local: Ama, nzɛn amusui­su­fɔ bo i si má deke bo i mibiɛ n koro ka i yo i, na i yoori deke be bo i ti ye ni ka i cin i sui, i dɛɛ sui­cin n ti kaan sɔ ɔ. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo bu ce i aceeri beberebe, i ti ye ni ka i mi fa yo deke bo wɔ yo daka ka i yo i, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ce i aceeri beberebe agaya, i ti ye ni ka i mi su i kɔ tara sɔ.

eng: On the way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu sukɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na wɔ sin Samariya ni Galili awɔrɛm n afiɛn.

eng: There was a certain rich man who clothed himself in purple and fine linen, and who feasted luxuriously every day.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Neŋge­fɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga, i wura sutura kpa kpa bo i la gɔ ɔ, na i sudi i koroo­wa cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn.

eng: Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through town.
local: Yesu kɔ wuraari Yeriko miɛ kpiri n nu na wɔ koro ka i sin.

eng: but I will give this widow justice because she keeps bothering me. Otherwise, there will be no end to her coming here and embarrassing me.
local: ama, kabo saŋga kɛrɛ na ahin kuna­fɔ n bita kpiɛ m daafiri n dɛɛ ti, m má i nya nahɔrɛ wɔ. Nzɛn ma yo má sɔ, i suba m dɔ sɔ ɔ, haari i bá ma m wara yi m ŋu.

eng: I came to cast fire upon the earth. How I wish that it was already ablaze!.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Ma fa Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n aba ka i yara durunya n nu­fɔm ka sin dɛɛ su wɔ. Má nyini sin n jeeri kisa, má m koro.

eng: As he entered a village, ten men with skin diseases approached him. Keeping their distance from him.
local: I kɔ ŋa i wura miɛ kaan be nu, na i ni koko­fɔ buru nbem ayɛ. Na ba jina dede.

eng: A man there named Zacchaeus, a ruler among tax collectors, was rich.
local: Biɛ be woori dɔ, bu fere i ka Zakayosi, i ti lampo­de­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn wɔ, na i la ajɛkɛ agaya.

eng: At his gate lay a certain poor man named Lazarus who was covered with sores.
local: Na yaari­fɔ be gusu wo bɛrɛ, bu fere i ka Lazarosi, kambue abara ayɛ i deke kɛrɛ. Na bu sufa i ba tana neŋge­fɔ n anɔ nu.

eng: The Lord said, Listen to what the unjust judge says.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Ye m ŋa am ati kabo nyini jɔrɛ­di­fɔ findi­fɔ n seeri ni.

eng: I have a baptism I must experience. How I am distressed until its completed!.
local: Ama, m la wahara be bo i ti ye ni ka m di i, má ka ma di i awie, m ahore dá má.

eng: they raised their voices and said, Jesus, Master, show us mercy.
local: atiɛn kpa ka, Ya Mɔ­fɔ Yesu, si ya aŋunfɔ.

eng: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but, being a short man, he couldnt because of the crowd.
local: Na wɔ boro i nyi ka i ŋu Yesu na i si i. Ama, jama asunnu agaya n dɛɛ ti, wɔ koro aŋu má i, dama i ti sɔnɔ tika a.

eng: Lazarus longed to eat the crumbs that fell from the rich mans table. Instead, dogs would come and lick his sores.
local: adena neŋge­fɔ n ba sudi diire, na nzɛn be tɔtɔ asiɛŋgu, na i gusu fa di. Haari na caam ba sutafi i kambue n nu.

eng: Wont God provide justice to his chosen people who cry out to him day and night? Will he be slow to help them?.
local: Ye nzu ti ye Nyɛmɛ búka má i dɛɛ minɛm bo bu sun fere i kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu kɛrɛ a, na bu nyá nahɔrɛ a? I cɛ́ má ni bu buka.

eng: Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No, I tell you, I have come instead to bring division.
local: Am sunsun ka ma fa tana­laifiɛ aba durunya n nu a? Ai, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, ma fa má tana­laifiɛ baari a, ama, ma fa kpakpaaki­nu aba a.

eng: When Jesus saw them, he said, Go, show yourselves to the priests. As they left, they were cleansed.
local: I ŋuuri bu sɔ n na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ na am kɔ fa am ŋu kere Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ni, na bu fiiti am na bu niɛ. Na ba de atin sukɔ, bu wo kɔ su n na bu kɛrɛ aŋu ka ba nya laifiɛ.

eng: So he ran ahead and climbed up a sycamore tree so he could see Jesus, who was about to pass that way.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ ŋmati adu jama n mɔ akɔ afun baka na i ŋu i, dama wɔ ŋu ka Yesu fá nyini baka n bu sin a.

eng: The poor man died and was carried by angels to Abrahams side. The rich man also died and was buried.
local: Na sɔnɔ yaari­fɔ n aba awu, na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm aba afa i akɔ aŋgoro Abraham bi. Na neŋge­fɔ n gusu aba awu na ba se i.

eng: I tell you, he will give them justice quickly. But when the Human Onecomes, will he find faithfulness on earth.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, i búka bu wɔ na bu nya nahɔrɛ ndende. Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, saŋga bo mini Adamande Wa n ba baari, m bá ŋu minɛm bo bu toko yo m yarada, nzɛn sɛ a? Ai, m ŋú má be.

eng: From now on, a household of five will be divided—three against two and two against three.
local: Fite kisa fa kɔ, awuru kun nu­fɔm minɛ nnu kpákpaaki nu. Minɛ nsan tí bu busu bu ŋgumi wɔ, na minɛ nnyɔ tí bu busu bu ŋgumi.

eng: On one of the days when Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests, legal experts, and elders approached him.
local: Cɛɛn be sɔ ɔ, Yesu wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu sukere minɛm, na i sukan labari kpa n kere bu. I wo kere su n na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm aba i dɔ.

eng: One of them, when he saw that he had been healed, returned and praised God with a loud voice.
local: Dɔ na bu nu kun ŋuuri ka wɔ nya laifiɛ sɔ ni, na wɔ kpie i nyi aba Yesu dɔ sutiɛn kpa sukansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: When Jesus came to that spot, he looked up and said, Zacchaeus, come down at once. I must stay in your home today.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ba juuri nyini awɔrɛ n nu n na wɔ niɛ aŋgoro, na wɔ se i ka, Zakayosi, jura bra ndende, dama wɔ yo daka ka m yo u wɔfɔ ɔ nyuma.

eng: While being tormented in the place of the dead, he looked up and saw Abraham at a distance with Lazarus at his side.
local: na yiri akɔ Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri. I wo wahara di su dɔ sɔ ni, na wɔ yɛ i tii su aŋu Abraham dede dɔ, Lazarosi wo i bi.

eng: Jesus told this parable to certain people who had convinced themselves that they were righteous and who looked on everyone else with disgust.
local: Na Yesu akaaki abo ahin miɛɛnu n akere minɛm bo bu sunsun ka bɛrɛ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ na bu bu má be ni.

eng: Father will square off against son and son against father; mother against daughter and daughter against mother; and mother-in-law against daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against mother-in-law.
local: Baa­biɛsɔ ni i si koro má bu beŋgu jɔrɛ, na baa­bara ni i ni koro má bu beŋgu jɔrɛ, na baa n ni, ni i wa yi koro má bu beŋgu jɔrɛ.

eng: They said, Tell us: What kind of authority do you have for doing these things? Who gave you this authority.
local: aba ase i ka, Ŋma dɛɛ yiko nu ye a yo nyini sam kɛrɛ a? Kan na ya ti. Ŋma ma u nyini yiko ni, ye a yo nyini sam n a.

eng: He fell on his face at Jesus feet and thanked him. He was a Samaritan.
local: Na wɔ ba akutu haari Yesu jaa bu asiɛŋgu, na wɔ da i asi. Nyini sɔnɔ n ti Samariya­fɔ ɔ.

eng: So Zacchaeus came down at once, happy to welcome Jesus.
local: Na wɔ jura ndende, na wɔ kɔ ade i wɔfɔ ni ahorejɔ bambaka.

eng: He shouted, Father Abraham, have mercy on me. Send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because Im suffering in this flame.
local: Dɔ na wɔ bo afere Abraham ase i ka, Ya si Abraham, si m aŋunfɔ. Na a sunma Lazarosi na i fa i saa maraka dɔ nzue nu na i fa ba dɔ m taferema, dama m wo wahara bambaka nu wɔ, sin nu fɛn.

eng: Two people went up to the temple to pray. One was a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
local: I ŋa, Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, na minɛ nnyɔ nbem akɔ ka bu kɔ sere Nyɛmɛ, Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Kun n ti Farasi­fɔ ɔ, na nyiŋga n ti lampo­de­fɔ.

eng: Jesus also said to the crowds, When you see a cloud forming in the west, you immediately say, Its going to rain. And indeed it does.
local: Na Yesu ase jama n ka, Saŋga bo am ŋu ka nzue yinyiŋgu atiɛn i nɔɔ wiɛ afiiri, a ti ka am ŋa, nzue bá nyuma, a ŋu ka i bá a gusu.

eng: He replied, I have a question for you. Tell me.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Am ma m du mɔ bisa am bisaa­wa kun be ka. Kisa, ambɛrɛ kan na m ti.

eng: Jesus replied, Werent ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?.
local: Dɔ na Yesu abisa ka, Má minɛ buru nyaari laifiɛ a, ye ŋgɔna nyiŋgam n wo ni?.

eng: Everyone who saw this grumbled, saying, He has gone to be the guest of a sinner.
local: Na bo bu ŋuuri i n kɛrɛ aŋuŋuti. Bu ŋa, Ahin sɔnɔ n akɔ na i kɔ da satiɛ­yo­fɔ dika.

eng: But Abraham said, Child, remember that during your lifetime you received good things, whereas Lazarus received terrible things. Now Lazarus is being comforted and you are in great pain.
local: Ama, na Abraham ase i ka, Teŋge su ka saŋga bo a la ŋgɔɔ wo durunya n nu ni, a yoori u koroo­wa a, na Lazarosi yiri adi durunya n nu wahara n kɛrɛ. Ama, kisa yiri wo fiɛ di su, ye wɔrɔ wo wahara di su n a.

eng: The Pharisee stood and prayed about himself with these words, God, I thank you that Im not like everyone else—crooks, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ n ajina i ŋgumi abo i hoe asere Nyɛmɛ ka, Micɛra Nyɛmɛ, m da u asi ka m ti má kabo minɛ nyiŋgam ti barasu ni. M ti má anyii­fɔ, m ti má findi­fɔ, m saaki má ajaa ciire, haari m ti má ka nyini lampo­de­fɔ n bo i jina dɔ ni.

eng: And when a south wind blows, you say, A heat wave is coming. And it does.
local: Na saŋga bo am ŋu ka aŋuma sukpiɛ fite nyunu ndiɛma biɛ su, a ti ka am ŋa, nyuma wiɛ fíte agaya, a ŋu ka i fíte wɔ.

eng: Was Johns baptism of heavenly or of human origin.
local: ŋma maari Yohane atin ye i ba biɛɛri minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue n a? Nyɛmɛ nzɛn adamande.

eng: No one returned to praise God except this foreigner.
local: Nzu ti ye ahin sɔnɔ n bo i ti má Yuda­fɔ ni ŋgumi aba ka i ba da Nyɛmɛ asi a.

eng: Zacchaeus stopped and said to the Lord, Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor. And if I have cheated anyone, I repay them four times as much.
local: Na Zakayosi ayasu ajina, na wɔ se Mibiɛ n ka, M Mibiɛ, ti fɛn. M kpáaki m ajɛkɛ kɛrɛ nu busu nnyɔ, na m fá buekun n ma yaari­fɔm. Na nzɛn ma sisi be, m tɔ́ i sɔ ŋusu nna, na m fá ma i mi.

eng: Moreover, a great crevasse has been fixed between us and you. Those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot. Neither can anyone cross from there to us.
local: Bekun, kunma ŋgoŋgo wo u ni yɛrɛ afiɛn, bo be koro fite má fɛn na i kɔ u bi. Be gusu koro fite má u bi dɔ na i ba ya dɔ fɛn.

eng: I fast twice a week. I give a tenth of everything I receive.
local: Le­so kun kun kɛrɛ nu, m cici m nɔɔ kpe nnyɔ nnyɔ ɔ, na deke buru kɛrɛ bo m la i, m yi i kun kun ma u wɔ.

eng: Hypocrites! You know how to interpret conditions on earth and in the sky. How is it that you dont know how to interpret the present time?.
local: Ambɛrɛ burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, nzu dɛɛ ti ye am koro niɛ aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu na am si sam bo bu bá yo n a, ye am niɛ ye am koro si má sam bo bu suyo ahin saŋga n bu a.

eng: They discussed among themselves, If we say, Its of heavenly origin, hell say, Why didnt you believe him.
local: Na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu kabo bu bɔ i nu barasu, bu ŋa, Nzɛn ya seeri i ka Nyɛmɛ maari i atin a, i bísa ya ka, ye nzu ti ye ya da má Yohane ŋu a?.

eng: Then Jesus said to him, Get up and go. Your faith has healed you.
local: Dɔ na wɔ se sɔnɔ n ka, Yarada n bo a yo m ni, yiri ma a nya laifiɛ a. Yasu na a kɔ awuru.

eng: Jesus said to him, Today, salvation has come to this household because he too is a son of Abraham.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nyuma dɛɛ cɛɛn n nu, Nyɛmɛ ade ahin awuru n nu­fɔm tii aji, dama ahin sɔnɔ ni mmɔɔ ti Abraham busu nu­fɔɔ.

eng: The rich man said, Then I beg you, Father, send Lazarus to my fathers house.
local: Na neŋge­fɔ n ase ka, Ya si Abraham, m sere u wɔ, ka a sunma Lazarosi na i kɔ m si awuru n nu.

eng: But the tax collector stood at a distance. He wouldnt even lift his eyes to look toward heaven. Rather, he struck his chest and said, God, show mercy to me, a sinner.
local: Ama, na lampo­de­fɔ n ajina dede, haari wɔ koro má ka i yɛ i tii su niɛ aŋgoro mmɔɔ, na wɔ fa i saa ataka i beŋgu nu, na wɔ se ka, Micɛra Nyɛmɛ, m ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, si m aŋunfɔ.

eng: And why dont you judge for yourselves what is right?.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Nzu ti ye ambɛrɛ ni am tii sunsun má na am si sa bo i kɔ atin su a?.

eng: But if we say, Its of human origin, all the people will stone us to death because they are convinced that John was a prophet.
local: Ama, nzɛn ya seeri i ka adamande dɔ, minɛm kɛrɛ tú ya yabuɛ wɔ, dama bu kɛrɛ soroori su ka Yohane yo tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: Pharisees asked Jesus when Gods kingdom was coming. He replied, Gods kingdom isnt coming with signs that are easily noticed.
local: Na Farasi­fɔ nbem abisa Yesu ka, Ye saŋga bɔnɔ ye Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su a? Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Sa be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro kere am saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su.

eng: The Human Onecame to seek and save the lost.
local: Nahɔrɛ, mini Adamande Wa n baari ka m bá niɛniɛ minɛm bo ba mini n wɔ na m dé bu tii ji.

eng: I have five brothers. He needs to warn them so that they dont come to this place of agony.
local: m ninbaam nnu wo dɔ, na i kɔ bɔbɔ bu ka bu si bu ŋu sa na má bu bá wo ahin wahara bambaka woo­biri n bo m wo ni.

eng: I tell you, this person went down to his home justified rather than the Pharisee. All who lift themselves up will be brought low, and those who make themselves low will be lifted up.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nyini minɛ nnyɔm ni sereeri Nyɛmɛ wieeri, na bu kɔ juuri awuru ni, Nyɛmɛ deeri lampo­de­fɔ ni ka i ti sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, má Farasi­fɔ ni. Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wura i ŋu kpiri, Nyɛmɛ yó i kaan wɔ, ama, na sɔnɔ bo i yo i ŋu kaan, Nyɛmɛ wúra i mi kpiri wɔ.

eng: As you are going to court with your accuser, make your best effort to reach a settlement along the way. Otherwise, your accuser may bring you before the judge, and the judge hand you over to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.
local: Nzɛn u ni be la jɔrɛ, na nzɛn i koro ka i fa u kɔ jɔrɛ­di­fɔ dɔ, jɔkɔti tara i mi n jaa, na má jɔrɛ n ju jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n dɔ, má nyini, i mi ni cín u kɔ jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n dɔ, na yiri gusu fá u ma dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ ni, na yiri gusu nyá nyi u.

eng: Looking up, Jesus saw rich people throwing their gifts into the collection box for the temple treasury.
local: Tɔ, na Yesu aniɛ aŋu kabo neŋge­fɔm suyi bu ŋwaa wura alaka bo minɛm yi saraka wura nu ni.

eng: They answered that they didnt know where it came from.
local: Nyini ti, na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ya si má sɔnɔ bo i maari i atin.

eng: Nor will people say, Look, here it is! or There it is! Dont you see? Gods kingdom is already among you.
local: Be se má ka, Am niɛ, yiri yaa fɛn, wara, Yiri lɛɛ dɔ. Dama m se am nahɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ ka, Nyɛmɛ wo am nu fɛn jina n nu, suniɛ i minɛm su.

eng: As they listened to this, Jesus told them another parable because he was near Jerusalem and they thought Gods kingdom would appear right away.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm ni toko so bu sui ti ahin jɔrɛm bo Yesu sujɔjɔ bu ni, na Yesu akaaki abo bu miɛɛnu be, dama nyini saŋga ni wɔ ka kaan sɔ na i wúra Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na minɛm asunsun ka saŋga n akaba aju bo Nyɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su lɛɛ.

eng: Abraham replied, They have Moses and the Prophets. They must listen to them.
local: Na Abraham ase i ka, Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n bo Moyisi ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ maari bu ni, i wo bu dɔ. Wɔ yo daka ka bu so bu sui na bu ti i bu, na bu si bu ŋu sa.

eng: People were bringing babies to Jesus so that he would bless them. When the disciples saw this, they scolded them.
local: Cɛɛn be na minɛ nbem sufa bu nbaam ba Yesu dɔ ka i fa i saa nana bu ŋu na i yo bu kisi. I susu­fɔm n ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na ba tiɛn minɛm n ŋu.

eng: I tell you, you wont get out of there until you have paid the very last cent.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nzɛn a woori dɔ, na a tɔ má kaari n kɛrɛ awie má, a nyá má atin fite má dan­saraka n nu.

eng: He also saw a poor widow throw in two small copper coins worth a penny.
local: Kuna­fɔ yaari­fɔ be gusu woori dɔ, na wɔ ba ayi ŋwaa kaan be bo i koro to má pui awura saraka n nu, na Yesu aŋu i.

eng: Then Jesus replied, Neither will I tell you what kind of authority I have to do these things.
local: Na yiri gusu ase bu ka, Mini mmɔɔ sé má am sɔnɔ bo i dɛɛ yiko nu na m yo ahin sam n kɛrɛ ni.

eng: Then Jesus said to the disciples, The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Human One,and you wont see it.
local: Na wɔ se i susu­fɔm ni ka, Saŋga suba bo am bá koro ka am ŋu mini Adamande Wa n ba saŋga ni, haari cɛɛn kun mmɔɔ, ama, am ŋú má i.

eng: He said, A certain man who was born into royalty went to a distant land to receive his kingdom and then return.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ se bu ka, Fɛmɛ baa be yo woori bɛrɛ a, cɛɛn be sɔ ɔ, na wɔ yasu ka i kɔ asiɛn dede be su ka i kɔ di fɛmɛya na i kpie i nyi ba.

eng: The rich man said, No, Father Abraham! But if someone from the dead goes to them, they will change their hearts and lives.
local: Na neŋge­fɔ n ase i ka, Ya si Abraham, bu dé má i. Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ fiteeri minɛm bo ba wu n dɔ kɔɔri bu dɔ, bu káaki bu wo.

eng: Then Jesus called them to him and said, Allow the children to come to me. Dont forbid them, because Gods kingdom belongs to people like these children.
local: Ama, na Yesu afere nbaatumam ni, na wɔ se ka, Am ma nbaatumam n bra m dɔ, má am fɔn bu. Dama bɛrɛ ŋusu sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti bu dɛɛ a.

eng: Jesus told the people this parable: A certain man planted a vineyard, rented it to tenant farmers, and went on a trip for a long time.
local: Dɔ na Yesu abo minɛm ahin miɛɛnu ni, i ŋa, Sɔnɔ be yo woori bɛrɛ a, na wɔ dɔ i baka suu­wa barasu be fie. Na wɔ nya afa awura i npa­di­fɔm saa nu, na wɔ kɔ atin akɔ acɛ.

eng: He said, I assure you that this poor widow has put in more than them all.
local: Dɔ na wɔ se ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, nyini kuna­fɔ n ayi ŋwaa atara minɛm kɛrɛ.

eng: People will say to you, Look there! or Look here! Dont leave or go chasing after them.
local: Nbem bá ba na bu sé am ka, Am niɛ, yiri lɛɛ dɔ, wara, Yiri yaa fɛn. Ama, má am fite kɔ su bu su.

eng: He called together ten servants and gave each of them money worth four months wages.He said, Do business with this until I return.
local: Kana i ba kɔ ni, na wɔ fere i akɔɔm buru, na wɔ fa ŋwaa akpaaki bu kɛrɛ nu, na wɔ se bu ka, M kɔ wieeri, ambɛrɛ si kabo am yo ahin ŋwaa n a, haari m bá kpie m nyi ba.

eng: Abraham said, If they dont listen to Moses and the Prophets, then neither will they be persuaded if someone rises from the dead.
local: Ama, na Abraham ase i ka, Nzɛn ba so má bu sui na bu ti Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n bo Moyisi ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ maari bu ni, haari nzɛn sɔnɔ teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu mmɔɔ baari, bu dé má i.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt welcome Gods kingdom like a child will never enter it.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a de má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu jɔrɛ kabo baatuma de deke i saa nnyɔ ni, a wúra má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu fieo.

eng: When it was time, he sent a servant to collect from the tenants his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants sent him away, beaten and empty-handed.
local: Saŋga ba juuri bo i si ka baka suu­wam n abi ni, na wɔ sunma i akɔɔm kun i npa­di­fɔm n dɔ, ka i kɔ de i dɛɛ bo ba nya i n bra. Akɔɔ n kɔ juuri ni, na ba tara i abo i, na ba fɔn i akpie i nyi i saa ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: All of them are giving out of their spare change. But she from her hopeless poverty has given everything she had to live on.
local: Dama neŋge­fɔm la ŋwaa beberebe n dɛɛ ti, ye ba yi be a. Ama, nyini kuna­fɔ n ni i yaari n kɛrɛ, i deke kɛrɛ bo i la i lɛɛ ye wɔ yi i n a.

eng: The Human Onewill appear on his day in the same way that a flash of lightning lights up the sky from one end to the other.
local: Dama mini Adamande Wa n ba cɛɛn ni, i tí kabo nzue kpɛsɛ na i ta papasi bara yɛ deke kɛrɛ n wɔ.

eng: His citizens hated him, so they sent a representative after him who said, We dont want this man to be our king.
local: Nyini sɔnɔ n gusu, i minɛm akoro má ka i yo bu fɛmɛ, bu kpɔ i wɔ, nyini ti, na ba sunma minɛm ka bu kɔ se ka, Ya koro má ka ahin sɔnɔ n bá yo ya fɛmɛ.

eng: A certain ruler asked Jesus, Good Teacher, what must I do to obtain eternal life.
local: Na Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm be abisa Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ sɔnɔ kpa, m yó sɛ ye m bá nya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: The man sent another servant. But they beat him, treated him disgracefully, and sent him away empty-handed as well.
local: Na fie n fɔ n asunma i akɔɔ n kun bekun, na ba tara i abo i, na ba kpa i nyinyɛ, na ba ma wɔ kpie i nyi i saa ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Some people were talking about the temple, how it was decorated with beautiful stones and ornaments dedicated to God. Jesus said.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔ nbem ajɔjɔ fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n su, kabo ba ta i ni yabuɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim ni aceerim bo bu fa ba ce Nyɛmɛ na i yo nyumi barasu ni. Bu wo jɔjɔ n su sɔ ni, na Yesu ase ka.

eng: After receiving his kingdom, he returned and called the servants to whom he had given the money to find out how much they had earned.
local: Na sɔnɔ n akɔ adi fɛmɛya ni, na wɔ kpie i nyi aba. I ba juuri kpeŋga i kaba tiɛɛnri i akɔɔm ni nɔɔ ɔ, adena i si kpɛrɛ bo ba nya i fite i ŋwaa bo i fa maari bu n nu.

eng: However, first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
local: Ama, kana ahin kɛrɛ bá yo, wɔ yo daka ka m du mɔ di wahara beberebe, na ambɛrɛ nyuma­fɔm bá kete m.

eng: Jesus replied, Why do you call me good? No one is good except the one God.
local: Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, Nzu ti ye a fere m ka sɔnɔ kpa a? Be ti má kpa sin Nyɛmɛ ŋu.

eng: He sent a third servant. They wounded this servant and threw him out.
local: Kpe nsan su ni, na wɔ sunma akɔɔ nyiŋga, na ba tara i abo i, na ba ma i mandim, na ba fa i atu baka suu­wam fie n ahɛmɛ.

eng: As for the things you are admiring, the time is coming when not even one stone will be left upon another. All will be demolished.
local: Nyini dekem bo am ŋu bu ni, saŋga suba bo yabuɛ kun n kpein n mmɔɔ bá nana má i beŋgu su. Bu bá bereki i kɛrɛ gɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: The first servant came forward and said, Your money has earned a return of one thousand percent.
local: Na akɔɔ n bo i du mɔ baari n ase i ka, M mibiɛ, u ŋwaa n bo a fa maari m ni, ma nya i ŋusu buru abuka su.

eng: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be during the days of the Human One.
local: Mini Adamande Wa n ba saŋga ni yó kabo ya na Nowa saŋga na i yoori n wɔ.

eng: You know the commandments: Dont commit adultery. Dont murder. Dont steal. Dont give false testimony. Honor your father and mother. .
local: Ye m ŋa a si Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni e, bu ŋa, Má a saaki ajaa ciire. Má a kun sɔnɔ. Má a wɔ. Má a di kɔrɛ daani. Bu u si ni u ni.

eng: The owner of the vineyard said, What should I do? Ill send my son, whom I love dearly. Perhaps they will respect him.
local: Dɔ na fie n fɔ n ase ka, Ye m yó sɛ wɔ lɛɛ kisa? M súnma m tiɛŋgu wa koroo­wa n wɔ, beni, bu má yiri jirima.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, when will these things happen? What sign will show that these things are about to happen.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔm n abisa i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, saŋga bɔnɔ ye ahin sam kɛrɛ bá yo a? Ye nzu deke bá kere ya ka saŋga n adodo bo ahin sam n kɛrɛ bá yo a.

eng: The king replied, Excellent! You are a good servant. Because you have been faithful in a small matter, you will have authority over ten cities.
local: Na i mibiɛ n ase i ka, Nfan. Wɔrɔ akɔɔ kpa, kabo a kere ka a la dabuŋu niɛ deke kaan su ni, m má a niɛ miɛm buru su.

eng: People were eating, drinking, marrying, and being given in marriage until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ woori di ni nu su wɔ, na nbaram ni nbiɛsɔm suja bu beŋgum, fa kɔ juuri haari cɛɛn n bo Nowa wuraari lee bambaka n nu, na wɔmbu n ba diiri deke kɛrɛ n wɔ.

eng: Then the ruler said, Ive kept all of these things since I was a boy.
local: Na sɔnɔ n ase i ka, Ma su nyini nɔaniɛ maa­wam n kɛrɛ su haari m kaan nu.

eng: But when they saw him, they said to each other, This is the heir. Lets kill him so the inheritance will be ours.
local: Ama, saŋga bo npa­di­fɔm n ŋuuri ka baa ni suba ni, na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, I wa n bo i dí i aja n lɛɛ suba n a. Am bra na ya kun i, adena aja n kɛrɛ ká ya dɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Watch out that you arent deceived. Many will come in my name, saying, Im the one! and Its time! Dont follow them.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am si am ŋu sa, na má am ma be dadaka am. Dama minɛ dɔŋgu bá fa m duma ba, na bu sé ka, Yiri la mini a. Na bu káaki se am ka, Saŋga n aju lɛɛ. Ama, má am su bu su.

eng: The second servant came and said, Master, your money has made a return of five hundred percent.
local: Na akɔɔ n bo i tɔ su nnyɔ su n aba, na wɔ se ka, M mibiɛ, u ŋwaa n bo a fa maari m ni, ma nya i ŋusu nnu abuka su.

eng: Likewise in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, and building.
local: I yo sɔ ɔ lɛɛ bekun, ka Loti dɛɛ saŋga ni. Nyini saŋga ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ woori di ni nu su wɔ, na nbem sutɔ, na nbem suto, na nbem sudɔ dɔdɔɔri, na nbem suta fa.

eng: When Jesus heard this, he said, Theres one more thing. Sell everything you own and distribute the money to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. And come, follow me.
local: Yesu tiiri sɔ n na wɔ se i ka, Deke kun be ye wɔ kpiɛ u su a. Kɔ na a kɔ tɔ u aja n kɛrɛ, na a fa ŋwaa n ma yaari­fɔm, na a bá nya ajɛkɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na a bra ba su m su.

eng: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What will the owner of the vineyard do to them?.
local: Dɔ na ba cin i atu baka suu­wam fie n ahɛmɛ, na ba kun i. Na Yesu anya abisa bu ka, Am sunsun ka fie n fɔ ni yó bu sɛ wɔ?.

eng: When you hear of wars and rebellions, dont be alarmed. These things must happen first, but the end wont happen immediately.
local: Saŋga bo am bá ti aluwa ŋga fɛn, wara bu sukun luɛ dɔ, má am ma sɛrɛ ti am. Dama wɔ yo daka ka nyini sam n kɛrɛ bá yo sin ka, ama, i kere má ka durunya n awieeri lɛɛ.

eng: To this one, the king said, You will have authority over five cities.
local: Na wɔ se nyini n gusu ka, M má a niɛ miɛm nnu su.

eng: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
local: Cɛɛn n bo Loti fa yasuuri Sodom miɛ kpiri n nu ni, nyini cɛɛn n ye sin ni ferewita sin fiteeri aŋgoro a, na wɔ ba atɔ miɛ n nu adi deke n kɛrɛ.

eng: When he heard these words, the man became sad because he was extremely rich.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ n tiiri sɔ n na i ahore asaaki, dama i la neŋge agaya n dɛɛ ti.

eng: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others. When the people heard this, they said, May this never happen.
local: I bá kun nyini npa­di­fɔm n kɛrɛ wɔ, na i fá fie n wura minɛ fɔfɔrɛm saa nu. Saŋga bo minɛm ni tiiri sɔ n na ba se ka, Nyɛmɛ yo ya kisi ni nyini.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, Nations and kingdoms will fight against each other.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Daanfiɛ daanfiɛ bá kun luɛ ni bu beŋgum, na nfɛmɛm kún luɛ ni bu beŋgu nfɛmɛm.

eng: The Festival of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching.
local: Saŋga n ba dodoori bo bu di bu cɛɛn­kpiri n bo bu di i ni kpɔnɔ bo bu gɔ má ayiri nu na i yasu ni. Yiri ye bu kaaki fere i ka Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n a.

eng: Another servant came and said, Master, here is your money. I wrapped it up in a scarf for safekeeping.
local: Na akɔɔm n kun be aba ase ka, M mibiɛ, u ŋwaa n bo a fa maari m yaa, m fa pɔɔndiiri sanvuin nu fa seeri wɔ.

eng: Thats the way it will be on the day the Human Oneis revealed.
local: Kabo cɛɛn n bo mini Adamande Wa ni bá yi m ŋu kere lɛɛ.

eng: When Jesus saw this, he said, Its very hard for the wealthy to enter Gods kingdom!.
local: Na Yesu aniɛ sɔnɔ n nyunu, na wɔ se i ka, I ti kekereke bo neŋge­fɔ wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: Staring at them, Jesus said, Then what is the meaning of this text of scripture: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?.
local: Na Yesu acɛ aniɛ bu dɔ na wɔ se bu ka, Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, am tiiri i bu sɛ wɔ? Bu ŋa, Yabuɛ n bo bondo ta­fɔm n faari i tuuri ni, yiri ye wɔ kaaki bondo n jaa yabuɛm kɛrɛ tii a.

eng: There will be great earthquakes and wide-scale food shortages and epidemics. There will also be terrifying sights and great signs in the sky.
local: Na asiɛn bá kpisi agaya, na ahɔɛ tɔ́, na alɔɔba tɔ́ awɔrɛ awɔrɛm nu, na ya bá ŋu anyumbaa ciire sa nbem ka bu sutiɛ fite aŋgoro n nu.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were looking for a way to kill Jesus, because they were afraid of the people.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, aniɛniɛ atin kabo bu yo barasu na bu kun Yesu, dama bu soroori minɛm ni ahore wɔ.

eng: I was afraid of you because you are a stern man. You withdraw what you havent deposited and you harvest what you havent planted.
local: M soroori sɛrɛ wɔ, dama a ti u sɔnɔ faŋga­fɔ ɔ. A de deke bo i ti má u dɛɛ, na a kaaki kpiɛ deke bo a dɔ má i.

eng: On that day, those on the roof, whose possessions are in the house, shouldnt come down to grab them. Likewise, those in the field shouldnt turn back.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, Nyini cɛɛn ni, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a wo kpata nu, na u neŋge wo awuru n nu, má a wura gisan nu fa pui. I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo a wo fiesu, má a kpie u nyi kɔ awuru.

eng: Its easier for a camel to squeeze through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Kabo nyɔɔma sin misini bue nu ni, i ti má kekereke tara má kabo neŋge­fɔ yo na i wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: Everyone who falls on that stone will be crushed. And the stone will crush the person it falls on.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kpaasiiri nyini yabuɛ n su, i mi búbu nu wɔ mumuku, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo nyini yabuɛ n tɔɔri i ŋu, i sán i mi samɛnɛ wɔ.

eng: But before all this occurs, they will take you into custody and harass you because of your faith. They will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
local: Ama, kana ahin sam kɛrɛ bá yo ni, bu tára am, na bu kére am wuwuruwa, na bu fá am wura yiko­fɔm saa nu, na bu dí am jɔrɛ bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu, na bu nyí am dan­saraka, na bu fá am wura nfɛmɛm ni gominam saa nu, na bu fá am jina bu nyunu, mini dɛɛ ti.

eng: Then Satan entered Judas, called Iscariot, who was one of the Twelve.
local: Na Sitana awura Yudasi Isikariyoti ni, yiri ti Yesu susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni kun be a.

eng: The king replied, I will judge you by the words of your own mouth, you worthless servant! You knew, did you, that Im a stern man, withdrawing what I didnt deposit, and harvesting what I didnt plant?.
local: Na wɔ se akɔɔ n ka, Wɔrɔ akɔɔ tiɛ­fɔ ni, u nɔaniɛ n bo a jɔjɔ i n ye m fá yo u ŋgbɛɛn a. A ŋa a si tenle ka m ti m sɔnɔ faŋga­fɔ ɔ, na m de deke bo i ti má m dɛɛ, na m kaaki kpiɛ deke bo ma dɔ má i, má sɔ?.

eng: Remember Lots wife!.
local: Am teŋge su fa kɔ sa bo i yoori Loti yi ni.

eng: Those who heard this said, Then who can be saved.
local: Minɛm ni tiiri sɔ ni, na ba bisa ka, Ye nzɛn i ti sɔ e, ŋma koro fite nu a.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests wanted to arrest him right then because they knew he had told this parable against them. But they feared the people.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n akaba akpini atin kabo bu yo na bu tara i, dama bu faari nu ka miɛɛnu n bo i boori i ni, i bo niiri bɛrɛ wɔ, ama, bu soroori minɛm dɛɛ ti, na ba yaki i.

eng: This will provide you with an opportunity to testify.
local: Ahin má am atin a, na am kóro kan labari kpa n kere bu.

eng: He went out and discussed with the chief priests and the officers of the temple guard how he could hand Jesus over to them.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ kɔ asɛsɛ su ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n tintini­jina­fɔ nkpiɛnm ni, kabo i tɔ́ Yesu ma bu ni.

eng: Why then didnt you put my money in the bank? Then when I arrived, at least I could have gotten it back with interest.
local: Tɔ, ye nzu ti ye a fa má m ŋwaa n akɔ ase má baŋkifɔm dɔ a? Na má m ba kpieeri m nyi baari, má m nya kpɛrɛ buka su.

eng: Whoever tries to preserve their life will lose it, but whoever loses their life will preserve it.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i kpíni i ŋgɔɔ busu, i mi bá fun i, ama, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tɔɔri i ŋgɔɔ maari m, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, What is impossible for humans is possible for God.
local: Na wɔ bɔ nu ka, Deke bo adamande koro yo má i, Nyɛmɛ koro yo i.

eng: The legal experts and chief priests were watching Jesus closely and sent spies who pretended to be sincere. They wanted to trap him in his words so they could hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
local: Nyini ti, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n akpini atin kabo bu yo na bu nya Yesu taraa­biri. Na ba bo minɛ nbem ajaa­kpiɛn ka bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti minɛ kpam wɔ, na bu ba sɔ i ŋgɛɛ na bu bisa i nɔaniɛ nbem, adena bu koro fa i wura gomina yiko­fɔ n saa nu.

eng: Make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance.
local: Ama, am du mɔ yo am sunsunni nu na má am ma i miɛ am, kabo bu kɔ bisa am na am jɔjɔ barasu.

eng: They were delighted and arranged payment for him.
local: Bu tiiri nyini n sɔ ni, na bu ahore ajɔ, na ba se ka bu má i ŋwaa.

eng: He said to his attendants, Take his money and give it to the one who has ten times as much.
local: Dɔ na wɔ se bo bu jina dɔ n kɛrɛ ka, Am de ŋwaa n fite i dɔ, na am fa ma akɔɔ n bo wɔ nya m ŋwaa n ŋusu buru abuka su ni.

eng: I tell you, on that night two people will be in the same bed: one will be taken and the other left.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ, minɛ nnyɔ ɔ dá dasu kun su wo daafiri su a, na Nyɛmɛ má bu bá fa kun n kɔ yaki kun ni.

eng: Peter said, Look, we left everything we own and followed you.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, Tɔ, ye yɛrɛ ayaki ya dekem kɛrɛ susu u su e.

eng: They asked him, Teacher, we know that you are correct in what you say and teach. You dont show favoritism but teach Gods way as it really is.
local: Na nyini minɛm n ase Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ya si ka u nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa ni u keree­wa kɛrɛ ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, na a tantambu má nu, ama, a kere minɛm wɔ nahɔrɛ su kabo Nyɛmɛ atin ti barasu.

eng: Ill give you words and wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to counter or contradict.
local: Dama mini má am alecira nɔaniɛ bo am jɔ́jɔ a, na am kpɔ­fɔm be mmɔɔ kóro la má nɔaniɛ be bo bu kóro jɔjɔ bo am asi, wara bu fá ta am nyi.

eng: He agreed and began looking for an opportunity to hand Jesus over to them—a time when the crowds would be absent.
local: Na Yudasi asoro su, na wɔ kpini atin na i tɔ́ Yesu ma bu, saŋga bo jama wo má i dɔ ni.

eng: But Master, they said, he already has ten times as much.
local: Ama, na ba se i ka, Ya mibiɛ, ye yiri du mɔ la u ŋwaa n ŋusu buru e.

eng: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.
local: Na nbaram nnyɔ kási wo ŋgani san su, na Nyɛmɛ má bu bá fa kun n kɔ yaki kun ni. .

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you that anyone who has left house, husband, wife, brothers, sisters, parents, or children because of Gods kingdom.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yakiiri i awuru, wara i yi, wara i ninbaam, wara i wu­fɔm, wara i nbaam, Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Does the Law allow people to pay taxes to Caesar or not.
local: Tɔ, ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo nu ka ya tɔ lampo ma Roma­fɔm Fɛmɛ Kpiri Siza, nzɛn atin wo má nu.

eng: You will be betrayed by your parents, brothers and sisters, relatives, and friends. They will execute some of you.
local: Haari am wu­fɔm, ni am ninbaam, ni am lamɔsim, ni am danfom mmɔɔ bá tɔ am ma nfaŋga­fɔm, na bu kún am nbem.

eng: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived, when the Passover had to be sacrificed.
local: Na cɛɛn­kpiri cɛɛn n aju, cɛɛn n bo bu di kpɔnɔ bo bu gɔ má ayiri nu na i yasu ni. Nyini cɛɛn n ye wɔ yo daka ka bu kpiɛ nbɔmbaam kɔm n a, na bu fa teŋge Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n su.

eng: He replied, I say to you that everyone who has will be given more, but from those who have nothing, even what they have will be taken away.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la deke be, bu má i be buka su wɔ. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la má pui, haari ni nyini kaan n bo i la i ni, bu cín de i dɔ ɔ.

eng: The disciples asked, Where, Lord? Jesus said, The vultures gather wherever theres a dead body.
local: I susu­fɔm tiiri sɔ ni, na ba bisa i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, nifansu ye ahin sam kɛrɛ bá yo a? Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Deke kɛrɛ bo deke fuin nya wo, pɛtɛm bara yɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: will receive many times more in this age and eternal life in the coming age.
local: i mi nyá nyinim ŋusu sɔ yaa i ŋgɔɔ su, na fa kɔ fa ba, i nyá ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: Since Jesus recognized their deception, he said to them.
local: Ama, na wɔ fa nu ka bu koro ka bu kpiɛ i mbɛnlɛ wɔ, na wɔ se bu ka.

eng: Everyone will hate you because of my name.
local: Mini dɛɛ ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kpɔ́ am.

eng: Jesus sent Peter and John with this task: Go and prepare for us to eat the Passover meal.
local: Na Yesu asunma Petoro ni Yohane, i ŋa, Am kɔ sɛsɛ Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n diire n su na ya ba di.

eng: As for my enemies who dont want me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them before me.
local: Ama, m ahin kpɔ­fɔm bɛrɛ yiri, má ba koro má ka m yo bu fɛmɛ, am fa bu ba jina m nyunu fɛn na am kun bu kɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus took the Twelve aside and said, Look, were going up to Jerusalem, and everything written about the Human Oneby the prophets will be accomplished.
local: Na Yesu afere i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm n akɔ ahɛmɛ, na wɔ se bu ka, Am ti fɛn. Ya sukɔ Yɛrusalem lɛɛ, dɔ ye bu kɔ́ yo mini Adamande Wa n kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm akɛrɛ ka bu yó m n a.

eng: Show me a coin.Whose image and inscription does it have on it? Caesars, they replied.
local: Am fa ŋwaa bra na m niɛ. Bu fa baari ni, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am niɛ ahin ŋwaa ni. Ŋma dɛɛ tii ni i duma wo su a? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Fɛmɛ Siza dɛɛ a.

eng: Still, not a hair on your heads will be lost.
local: Ama, am tii­ŋmiɛ kun n kpein mmɔɔ ká má ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: They said to him, Where do you want us to prepare it.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, A koro ka ya kɔ ni kɔ sɛsɛ diire n su a.

eng: After Jesus said this, he continued on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ du bu nyunu na bu kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: He will be handed over to the Gentiles. He will be ridiculed, mistreated, and spit on.
local: Na bu fá m wura minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm saa nu, na bu yó m anzasi, na bu kpíɛ m nzukɔ, na bu yí nanzue gɔ m ŋu.

eng: He said to them, Give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and to God what belongs to God.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Tɔ, deke bo i ti Fɛmɛ Siza dɛɛ, am fa ma Fɛmɛ Siza, na deke bo i ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ, am fa ma Nyɛmɛ.

eng: By holding fast, you will gain your lives.
local: Nzɛn am mindi deeri nyini n kɛrɛ, am nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, When you go into the city, a man carrying a water jar will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Am ti fɛn, am kɔ ŋa am wura miɛ n nu, am ni sɔnɔ be kába yɛ, i sɔ dundu ni nzue. Am su i su kɔ wura awuru n bo i kɔ wura nu ni.

eng: As Jesus came to Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he gave two disciples a task.
local: I ba dodoori miɛm bo bu fere bu ka Befage ni Betani, nyini miɛm ni dodo buka n bo bu fere i ka Oliva Buka n wɔ, dama oliva bakam gɔ su wɔ. Dɔ na wɔ sunma i susu­fɔm n nu nnyɔ.

eng: After torturing him, they will kill him. On the third day, he will rise up.
local: na bu fín m ŋgbere, na bu nyá kun m, na i le­san cɛɛn, na m téŋge m yiwee n nu.

eng: They couldnt trap him in his words in front of the people. Astonished by his answer, they were speechless.
local: Dɔ na ba ŋu ka bu koro nya má i taraa­biri minɛm nyunu bɛrɛ, ama, kabo i bɔɔri bu nu n dɛɛ ti, na ba miɛ, na ba tɔ amuna.

eng: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that its destruction is close at hand.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Saŋga bo am bá ŋu ka sojam abara ayɛ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n ni, am si tenle su ka i cɛ́ má, bu sáaki miɛ n kɛrɛ lɛɛ.

eng: Say to the owner of the house, The teacher says to you,Where is the guestroom where I can eat the Passover meal with my disciples.
local: Na am bisa awuru­fɔ­biɛ n ka, Ya Kere­fɔ n ŋa, sɔɔ bɔnɔ nu ye i ni i susu­fɔm ni dí Butara cɛɛn­kpiri diire n a.

eng: He said, Go into the village over there. When you enter it, you will find tied up there a colt that no one has ever ridden. Untie it and bring it here.
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ miɛ kaan n bo i wo am nyunu dɔ ni, am kɔ ŋa am wura miɛ n nu, am ŋú ka kaako­baa be minda, be nya fun má i. Am nyaŋgi i bra fɛn.

eng: But the Twelve understood none of these words. The meaning of this message was hidden from them and they didnt grasp what he was saying.
local: Ama, i susu­fɔm n ati má i jɔjɔɔ­wa n be bu. Nyɛmɛ ayi má i nyi akere má bu, nyini ti, ba koro ati má i bu.

eng: Some Sadducees, who deny that theres a resurrection, came to Jesus and asked.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm asɛsɛ barasu nbem bo bu fere bu ka Sadusi­fɔm ni, aba Yesu dɔ. Bu dɛɛ keree­wa la ka Nyɛmɛ téŋge má minɛm bu yiwee nu n a. Na ba ba abisa i jɔrɛ be, bu ŋa.

eng: At that time, those in Judea must flee to the mountains, those in the city must escape, and those in the countryside must not enter the city.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a tiiri i ŋga na a wo Yudiya asiɛn n su, ŋmati kɔ bukam n su. Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a wo miɛ n nu, ŋmati fite nu. Na wɔrɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo a wo miɛ n ahɛmɛ, má a kpie u nyi kɔ miɛ n nu kun.

eng: He will show you a large upstairs room, already furnished. Make preparations there.
local: Na i kére am aŋgoro sɔɔ bambaka n bo ba yo i kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ni, dɔ ye am sɛsɛ diire n su se ya.

eng: If anyone asks, Why are you untying it? just say, Its master needs it.
local: Nzɛn be bisaari am ka nzu ti ye am sunyaŋgi i a, am se i ka Mibiɛ ni koro i a.

eng: As Jesus came to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the road begging.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu kɔ sudodo miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Yeriko ni, na anyinsin be tana atin n nɔɔ su wo sere su.

eng: Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a mans brother dies leaving a widow but no children, the brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother .
local: Ya Kere­fɔ, Moyisi yo kɛrɛɛri nɔaniɛ be maari ya, ka nzɛn sɔnɔ wu yakiiri i yi na i ni bara n anya má baa, wɔ yo daka ka i niɛma n fa kuna­fɔ n na bu wu nbaam ma gɔrɔ n bo wɔ wu ni.

eng: These are the days of punishment, when everything written will find its fulfillment.
local: Dama ahin sui­cin kekereke saŋga ni yó yii­wa, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n kɛrɛ.

eng: They went and found everything just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover meal.
local: I jɔjɔ wieeri ni, na ba kɔ aŋu deke n kɛrɛ sɔ kabo Yesu seeri bu ni, na ba sɛsɛ Butara cɛɛn­kpiri diire n su.

eng: Those who had been sent found it exactly as he had said.
local: Na bɛrɛ n bo i sunmaari bu n akɔ, akɔ aŋu kaako­baa n ka i minda kabo i seeri bu ni.

eng: When the man heard the crowd passing by, he asked what was happening.
local: I tiiri ka jama n wo sin su ni, na wɔ bisa ka, Nzu suyo a.

eng: Now there were seven brothers. The first man married a woman and then died childless.
local: Tɔ, sɔnɔ be yo laari nbaa­biɛsɔm nso a, na baa­kpiɛn n aja bara, na wɔ wu ayaki bara ni, ba nya má baa.

eng: How terrible it will be at that time for women who are pregnant or for women who are nursing their children. There will be great agony on the earth and angry judgment on this people.
local: Nyini saŋga ni yó kekereke agaya ma nyinsɛ­fɔm, ni nbaram bo bu nbaam toko nu ni. Dama minɛm n bo bu wo ahin asiɛn n su ni dí yaari agaya, na Nyɛmɛ cín bu sui.

eng: When the time came, Jesus took his place at the table, and the apostles joined him.
local: Saŋga n ba juuri, na Yesu ni i jaramasam n aba atana ka bu di diire ni.

eng: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why are you untying the colt.
local: Saŋga bo bu sunyaŋgi i ni, na i fɔm n abisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am sunyaŋgi kaako­baa n a.

eng: They told him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing by.
local: Na ba se i ka, Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ ni susin a.

eng: The second.
local: Sɔ ye bo i tɔ i su nnyɔ su.

eng: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be plundered by Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are concluded.
local: Na bu kún nbem luɛ nu, na bu tára nyiŋgam domuɛ, na bu fá bu kɔ asiɛn asiɛnm n kɛrɛ su, na minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni sáaki miɛ n kɛrɛ haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo bu yiko bá wie.

eng: He said to them, I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Kabo m koroori lɛɛ, m boroori m nyi wɔ ka m ni ambɛrɛ saŋga di ahin Butara cɛɛn­kpiri diire n ka, na m nyá di m wahara ni.

eng: They replied, Its master needs it.
local: Na ba bɔ bu nu ka, Mibiɛ ni koro i a.

eng: The blind man shouted, Jesus, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Dɔ na wɔ tiɛn afere i, i ŋa, Yesu, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma, si m aŋunfɔ.

eng: and then the third brother married her. Eventually all seven married her, and they all died without leaving any children.
local: ni nsan su n yoori a, haari fa kɔ kpuraari nso su ni, bu be mmɔɔ anya má baa ye bu wuuri a.

eng: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars. On the earth, there will be dismay among nations in their confusion over the roaring of the sea and surging waves.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Na alekutura sam nyá ba tiɛ fite wiɛ n ni sara n, ni asara­mbaam kɛrɛ nu. Na durunya n nu fɛn, asiɛn asiɛnm n su­fɔm kɛrɛ ahore tánda, na sɛrɛ tí bu, fa kɔ kabo tieku nzue n bo barasu, na i yo naŋmiɛ agaya ni.

eng: I tell you, I wont eat it until it is fulfilled in Gods kingdom.
local: Dama m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, m dí má ahin diire n kun, má ka i bá yo yii­wa Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: They brought it to Jesus, threw their clothes on the colt, and lifted Jesus onto it.
local: Na ba fa i aba Yesu dɔ, na minɛm n atɛrɛki bu suturam anana kaako­baa n ŋu, na ba buka Yesu na wɔ fun.

eng: Those leading the procession scolded him, telling him to be quiet, but he shouted even louder, Son of David, show me mercy.
local: Na minɛm n atiɛn i ŋu ka i yaki naŋmiɛ. Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, na wɔ kaaki atiɛn kpa abuka su ka, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma, si m aŋunfɔ.

eng: Finally, the woman died too.
local: Kasin na bara n gusu aba awu.

eng: The planets and other heavenly bodies will be shaken, causing people to faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world.
local: Sam bo bu bá yo durunya n nu n dɛɛ ti, nyini má sɛrɛ ti minɛm na bu tɔ́ cin, dama aŋgoro n kɛrɛ kpísi wɔ.

eng: After taking a cup and giving thanks, he said, Take this and share it among yourselves.
local: Dɔ na wɔ fa awa ni nzan, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ se bu ka, Am de na am nu, na am fa ma am beŋgu.

eng: As Jesus rode along, they spread their clothes on the road.
local: I fun tanaari su wieeri n na i wo kɔ su ni, na minɛm n afa bu suturam atɛrɛki ase atin n nu.

eng: Jesus stopped and called for the man to be brought to him. When he was present Jesus asked.
local: Yesu tiiri sɔ n na wɔ ka ajina, na wɔ se ka bu fa i bra i dɔ. Na ba fere i ama i. I ba dodoori ni, na Yesu abisa i ka.

eng: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? All seven were married to her.
local: Tɔ, kabo bu minɛ nso n kɛrɛ aja bara n na ba wu sɔ ni, cɛɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge minɛm bu yiwee n nu ni, bara ni ká ŋma dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: The whole assembly got up and led Jesus to Pilate and.
local: Na bu tere­tere­fɔm n kɛrɛ bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ n ayasu, na ba fa Yesu akɔ ama Gomina Pilato.

eng: Then they will see the Human Onecoming on a cloud with power and great splendor.
local: Na minɛm nyá ŋu ka mini Adamande Wa ni sufite aŋgoro yinyiŋgu n nu suje piɛpiɛu suba ni yiko bambaka.

eng: I tell you that from now on I wont drink from the fruit of the vine until Gods kingdom has come.
local: M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, m nú má ahin nzan n kun, má ka cɛɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su.

eng: As Jesus approached the road leading down from the Mount of Olives, the whole throng of his disciples began rejoicing. They praised God with a loud voice because of all the mighty things they had seen.
local: Saŋga bo i ba dodoori Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, fa kɔ awɔrɛ n bo i atin kaba cɔɔndi Oliva Buka n su ni, na i susu­fɔm beberebe sukansi Nyɛmɛ ni ahorejɔ, na bu subo kuyigindi, dama ba ŋu alekutura sakpa­kpam bo wɔ yo i n dɛɛ ti.

eng: What do you want me to do for you? He said, Lord, I want to see.
local: A koro ka m yo nzu ma u a? Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, M Mibiɛ, m koro ka a ma m ŋu asi wɔ.

eng: Jesus said to them, People who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Nbaram ni nbiɛsɔm bo bu wo bɛrɛ kisa ni ja ajaa.

eng: began to accuse him. They said, We have found this man misleading our people, opposing the payment of taxes to Caesar, and claiming that he is the Christ, a king.
local: Na ba bo i bu sujaraki i, bu ŋa, Ya kɔ aŋu ka ahin sɔnɔ ni sudaka ya minɛm ka bu kete gomina ni, na má bu tɔ lampo kaari ma Fɛmɛ Kpiri Siza, i ŋa ka yiri ni i tii ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a, nyini la ka i ti fɛmɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Now when these things begin to happen, stand up straight and raise your heads, because your redemption is near.
local: Saŋga bo nyini sam n kɛrɛ bá suyo ni, am yasu na am yɛ am tii su ni ahoresi, dama saŋga n adodo bo Nyɛmɛ bá de am tii lɛɛ.

eng: After taking the bread and giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.
local: Na Yesu akaaki afa kpɔnɔ, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ bubu i nu, na wɔ fa ama bu, na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin kpɔnɔ n ti m ŋuniɛ­mbaa lɛɛ, ye m sufa ma am n a. Am yo ahin sɔ adena am fa teŋge m su.

eng: They said, Blessings on the king who comes in the name of the Lord. Peace in heaven and glory in the highest heavens.
local: Bu ŋa, Tere­fiɛ wo fɛmɛ n dɔ, Yiri n bo i suba Micɛra n dɛɛ duma n nu ni. Ahoreda wo aŋgoro, na kansi wo Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n dɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Receive your sight! Your faith has healed you.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Ŋu asi bekun. Yarada n bo a yo m ni, nyini ma a nya laifiɛ a.

eng: But those who are considered worthy to participate in that age, that is, in the age of the resurrection from the dead, wont marry nor will they be given in marriage.
local: ama, nbaram ni nbiɛsɔm bo Nyɛmɛ aŋu ka bɛrɛ ti ye ni ka bu bá teŋge bu yiwee n nu fa kɔ fa ba ni, bɛrɛ ja má ajaa kun.

eng: Pilate asked him, Are you the king of the Jews? Jesus replied, Thats what you say.
local: Na Gomina Pilato abisa i ka, Wɔrɔ ti Yuda­fɔm fɛmɛ n a? Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, Kabo a jɔjɔ ni, i ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: Jesus told them a parable: Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
local: Dɔ na Yesu abo ahin miɛɛnu n akere bu. I ŋa, Am niɛ damba baka ni baka nyiŋgam kɛrɛ.

eng: In the same way, he took the cup after the meal and said, This cup is the new covenant by my blood, which is poured out for you.
local: Bu diiri diire n wieeri ni, na wɔ kaaki afa awa ni nzan ni, na wɔ yo sɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin nzan n ti sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ sufa m mbunja n sudi i di n a. Ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye m mbunja ni soŋgi gɔ a.

eng: At once he was able to see, and he began to follow Jesus, praising God. When all the people saw it, they praised God too.
local: Kpeŋga na wɔ kaba aŋu asi, na wɔ su i su, sukansi Nyɛmɛ. Jama n ŋuuri i sɔ n na bu kɛrɛ gusu akansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Some of the Pharisees from the crowd said to Jesus, Teacher, scold your disciples! Tell them to stop.
local: Dɔ na Farasi­fɔm nbem bo bu wo jama n nu n ase Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, tiɛn u susu­fɔm n ŋu ka bu yaki naŋmiɛ.

eng: They can no longer die, because they are like angels and are Gods children since they share in the resurrection.
local: dama bu wu má kun. Bu wo bɛrɛ ka Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm wɔ. Bu ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam wɔ, dama ka ba teŋge bu yiwee n nu.

eng: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, I find no legal basis for action against this man.
local: Dɔ na Gomina Pilato ase Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni jama n kɛrɛ ka, Ma ŋu má ŋgasi bo ahin sɔnɔ n ado i.

eng: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
local: Saŋga bo am nya ba ŋu ka bu sufiɛfiɛ nyawa fɔfɔrɛ ni, ambɛrɛ ni am tii si ka dɔdɔɔri saŋga adodo lɛɛ.

eng: But look! My betrayer is with me; his hand is on this table.
local: Ama, am niɛ. Sɔnɔ n bo i tɔ́ m ni wo diire n dii­biri fɛn, m ni yiri susaŋga di diire n a.

eng: He answered, I tell you, if they were silent, the stones would shout.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, nzɛn má bɛrɛ yakiiri naŋmiɛ, má am ŋu ka yabuɛm bo bu da bɛrɛ n ayasu suyo naŋmiɛ.

eng: Even Moses demonstrated that the dead are raised—in the passage about the burning bush, when he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob .
local: Na fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge minɛm bu yiwee n nu ni, Moyisi mmɔɔ yo kereeri ka i ti sɔ ɔ. Wɔ kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu, fa kɔ saŋga bo sin n jeeri buwe baka n su ni, ka, Micɛra ni ti Abraham, ni Izaki, ni Jakɔbu dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: But they objected strenuously, saying, He agitates the people with his teaching throughout Judea—starting from Galilee all the way here.
local: Ama, na ba jina kun su, na ba jaraki i, bu ŋa, I keree­wa n ama Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu minɛm kɛrɛ ahore ayasu. I boori i bu Galili awɔrɛ n nu wɔ, ye kisa wɔ fa aba fɛn n a.

eng: In the same way, when you see these things happening, you know that Gods kingdom is near.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su ni, i yó sɔ ɔ. Saŋga bo ambɛrɛ gusu bá ŋu ka nyini sam kɛrɛ suyo ni, am si tenle ka i cɛ́ má, Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su lɛɛ.

eng: The Human Onegoes just as it has been determined. But how terrible it is for that person who betrays him.
local: Mini Adamande Wa ni wú kabo Nyɛmɛ adu mɔ acici ka m wú n wɔ, ama, sɔnɔ n bo i tɔ m ni, i mi bá ŋu anyum­boro.

eng: As Jesus came to the city and observed it, he wept over it.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu ba dodoori Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n na i ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na wɔ sun miɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: He isnt the God of the dead but of the living. To him they are all alive.
local: Na Yesu asa su ase bu ka, Tɔ, kabo Micɛra n ti nyini minɛm dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ ni, nyini kere ka bu toko la ŋgɔɔ lɛɛ. Nyɛmɛ ti má bo ba wu n dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ, i ti bo bu la ŋgɔɔ ni dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ a, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋgɔɔ fite yiri dɔ a.

eng: Hearing this, Pilate asked if the man was a Galilean.
local: Gomina Pilato tiiri sɔ ni, na wɔ bisa ka, Sɔnɔ n ti Galili­fɔ ɔ.

eng: I assure you that this generation wont pass away until everything has happened.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, bo bu la ŋgɔɔ kisa ni wú wie má ka, ye ahin sam kɛrɛ bá yo a.

eng: They began to argue among themselves about which of them it could possibly be who would do this.
local: Dɔ na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ye ya nu ŋma koro yo ahin sa n a.

eng: He said, If only you knew on this of all days the things that lead to peace. But now they are hidden from your eyes.
local: na wɔ se ka, Nzɛn má nyuma dɛɛ cɛɛn n nu am si sam bo bu fa ahoreda ba ni, má i ti má sɔ. Ama, kabo i ti ni, am koro ŋu má nyini sam ni.

eng: Some of the legal experts responded, Teacher, you have answered well.
local: Dɔ na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ nbem ayasu ase i ka, Nfan. Ya Kere­fɔ, u bɔ nu n ti kpa.

eng: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
local: Aŋgoro n ni asiɛŋgu n kɛrɛ bá la awieeri, ama, m dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n yiri, i la má awieeri cɛɛn be.

eng: When he learned that Jesus was from Herods district, Pilate sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
local: I tiiri ka i fite dɔ ni, na wɔ ma ba fa i akɔ Fɛmɛ Herode dɔ, dama yiri yo niɛ nyini awɔrɛ n su a. Nyini saŋga ni, Fɛmɛ Herode gusu woori Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: An argument broke out among the disciples over which one of them should be regarded as the greatest.
local: Na Yesu jaramasam n ase akuruwa bu ŋuŋu nu fa kɔ sɔnɔ bo i tara bu kɛrɛ bu nu.

eng: The time will come when your enemies will build fortifications around you, encircle you, and attack you from all sides.
local: Saŋga ni suba bo am kpɔ­fɔm bá ta kanda bara yɛ am deke kɛrɛ na am kóro fite má nu.

eng: No one dared to ask him anything else.
local: Nyini sin, be abo má su ka i bisa i jɔrɛ bekun.

eng: Take care that your hearts arent dulled by drinking parties, drunkenness, and the anxieties of day-to-day life. Dont let that day fall upon you unexpectedly.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Am si am ŋu sa, na má am susaaki am saŋga sutiɛn am nɔɔ sunu nzan subo, na má am ma durunya n nu sa koroo­wa miɛ am, na má m ba ni fúra am nu.

eng: Herod was very glad to see Jesus, for he had heard about Jesus and had wanted to see him for quite some time. He was hoping to see Jesus perform some sign.
local: Fɛmɛ Herode ŋuuri Yesu ni, na wɔ yo i fiɛ akɔ asin su, dama wɔ ti i ŋga acɛ, na wɔ koro ka i ŋu i na i yo alekutura sa nbem na i niɛ.

eng: But Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles rule over their subjects, and those in authority over them are called friends of the people.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ nfɛmɛm, bɛrɛ miɛ bu minɛm a, na bɛrɛ bo bu la yiko minɛm ŋu ni, bu fere bu ka buka­fɔm.

eng: They will crush you completely, you and the people within you. They wont leave one stone on top of another within you, because you didnt recognize the time of your gracious visit from God.
local: Bu kún am ni am nbaam, na bu fúfu am ahin miɛ n kɛrɛ pepere, haari yabuɛ kun n kpein mmɔɔ bá nana má i beŋgu su. Ahin n kɛrɛ bá yo sɔ, dama am asi má saŋga n bo Nyɛmɛ baari ka i ba de am tii ji ni.

eng: Jesus said to them, Why do they say that the Christ is Davids son?.
local: Na Yesu ajɔjɔ minɛm dɔ fa kɔ Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n su, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Ye nzu ti ye bu bita jɔjɔ ka Kristo n ti Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma a?.

eng: like a trap. It will come upon everyone who lives on the face of the whole earth.
local: kabo toroku tara deke ni. Dama nyini cɛɛn bá fura minɛm bo bu wo durunya n nu wɔ.

eng: Herod questioned Jesus at length, but Jesus didnt respond to him.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ bisa Yesu bisaa­wam beberebe, ama, wɔ bɔ má i nu.

eng: But thats not the way it will be with you. Instead, the greatest among you must become like a person of lower status and the leader like a servant.
local: Ama, má ambɛrɛ yo sɔ. Sɔnɔ bo i tara am kɛrɛ am nu ni, i ti ye ni ka i mi yo i ŋu kaan, na bo i ti am mɔ­fɔ n gusu, i ti ye ni ka i mi yo i ŋu kaan na i su am.

eng: When Jesus entered the temple, he threw out those who were selling things there.
local: Dɔ na Yesu akɔ awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ bo i bu sufɔn yi wata­fɔm.

eng: David himself says in the scroll of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right side.
local: Dama kabo ba kɛrɛ i Kansi Jue kɛrɛɛ­wam n nu ni, Fɛmɛ Davidi ni i tii ase ka, Na Micɛra n ase m Mibiɛ n ka, Ba tana m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su fɛn.

eng: Stay alert at all times, praying that you are strong enough to escape everything that is about to happen and to stand before the Human One.
local: Má am fa am ŋu ji, am sere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ka i ma am faŋga na am kóro fite nyini kekereke n kɛrɛ bo bu suba n nu, na am bá koro jina mini Adamande Wa n nyunu.

eng: The chief priests and the legal experts were there, fiercely accusing Jesus.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n afite ajina sujaraki i ni anyum­boro su.

eng: So which one is greater, the one who is seated at the table or the one who serves at the table? Isnt it the one who is seated at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i tana di diire ni, ni bo i su amusui ni, ŋma tara i beŋgu a? Yiri la bo i tana di ni, má sɔ ɔ? Ama, mini wo am nu ka sɔnɔ bo i su amusui wɔ.

eng: He said to them, Its written, My house will be a house of prayer, but you have made it a hideout for crooks. .
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, M awuru ni, bu bá fere i ka awuru bo bu bá sere Nyɛmɛ nu. Ama, ambɛrɛ afa i ayo awiefɔm fiɛɛ­biri.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
local: haari m bá ma u kpɔ­fɔm kɛrɛ ba kutu u jaa bu.

eng: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, but he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
local: Ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, Yesu kɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu wɔ, na i kɔ sukere minɛm, ale nya ba san, na i kɔ Oliva Buka n su.

eng: Herod and his soldiers treated Jesus with contempt. Herod mocked him by dressing Jesus in elegant clothes and sent him back to Pilate.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Herode ni i sojam n ayo Yesu anzasi, na ba bu i ŋgbɛɛn, na ba fa tanlɛɛ nyunyumi awura i, na ba fa i akpie i nyi akɔ Gomina Pilato dɔ.

eng: You are the ones who have continued with me in my trials.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo m woori kekereke nu, ambɛrɛ jinaari m sin a.

eng: Jesus was teaching daily in the temple. The chief priests, the legal experts, and the foremost leaders among the people were seeking to kill him.
local: Cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, Yesu kere minɛm wɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm n akoro ka bu kun i.

eng: Since David calls him Lord, how can he be Davids son.
local: Tɔ, Fɛmɛ Davidi mmɔɔ fereeri Kristo n ka i Mibiɛ wɔ, ye Kristo n yo sɛ kaaki yo Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma a.

eng: All the people rose early in the morning to hear him in the temple area.
local: Ale kɛrɛ bo i cin ŋgarama fuun, minɛm ni ba i dɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu wɔ, na bu ti i keree­wam ni.

eng: Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that day. Before this, they had been enemies.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, Fɛmɛ Herode ni Gomina Pilato yo kpɔ bu beŋgu wɔ, ama, nyini cɛɛn n gbingbin na ba kaaki danfom.

eng: And I confer royal power on you just as my Father granted royal power to me.
local: Kabo m Si Nyɛmɛ ama m atin ka m di fɛmɛya ni, sɔ ye mini gusu fá ma am a.

eng: However, they couldnt find a way to do it because all the people were enthralled with what they heard.
local: ama, ba koro anya má atin, dama minɛm n kɛrɛ koroori ka bu so bu sui wɔ, na bu ti i keree­wam ni.

eng: In the presence of all the people, Jesus said to his disciples.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm n kɛrɛ jina wo i nɔaniɛ n ti su sɔ ni, na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka.

eng: Then Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people.
local: Na Gomina Pilato afere Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm ni, ni minɛm ni.

eng: Thus you will eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and you will sit on thrones overseeing the twelve tribes of Israel.
local: na am dí na am nú m fɛmɛya n nu, na am tána fɛmɛya biɛm su, na am níɛ Yisareli­fɔm anɔ buru ni nnyɔm n su.

eng: Watch out for the legal experts. They like to walk around in long robes. They love being greeted with honor in the markets. They long for the places of honor in the synagogues and at banquets.
local: Am si am ŋu sa ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm ni, deke bo bu koro i n la ka, bu wura sutura ŋgbiŋgbirim wɔ, na bu nati sujeŋgbe, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ suyo bu ansɛ gɔɔsu. Nyini minɛm n anya awura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu ni, wara ba nya afere bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ bambaka diire dii­birim ni, bu koro ka bu tana kpirikoro tanaa­birim wɔ.

eng: He said to them, You brought this man before me as one who was misleading the people. I have questioned him in your presence and found nothing in this mans conduct that provides a legal basis for the charges you have brought against him.
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Am faari ahin sɔnɔ n baari m dɔ, na am ase ka i sukere minɛm na bu kete gomina n wɔ. Mini ni m tii aniɛ jɔrɛ n nu am nyunu fɛn, ama, ma ŋu má ŋgasi bo wɔ do i.

eng: They are the ones who cheat widows out of their homes, and to show off they say long prayers. They will be judged most harshly.
local: Nyini minɛm n sɔ fin nkuna­fɔm neŋgem wɔ, na bu jina sere Nyɛmɛ cɛ, adena minɛm ŋu ka bu ti minɛ kpam wɔ. Jina cɛɛn, bɛrɛ bá nya sui­cin tara minɛm kɛrɛ.

eng: Simon, Simon, look! Satan has asserted the right to sift you all like wheat.
local: Na Yesu ase Simon ka, Simon, ti fɛn. Sitana asere atin ka i kpaja am kabo bu kpaja ŋgani n wɔ.

eng: Neither did Herod, because Herod returned him to us. Hes done nothing that deserves death.
local: Fɛmɛ Herode mmɔɔ ni i tii aŋu má i ŋgasi be, nyini dɛɛ ti ye wɔ kaaki i aba ama ya n a. Nahɔrɛ, ahin sɔnɔ n ado má ŋgasi be bo bu kun i.

eng: However, I have prayed for you that your faith wont fail. When you have returned, strengthen your brothers and sisters.
local: Ama, ma sere Nyɛmɛ ama u na má a yaki m yarada­yo. Na saŋga bo a ba kaaki ba suuri m su bekun ni, buka u ninbaam ni, na bu nya ahoresi.

eng: Peter responded, Lord, Im ready to go with you, both to prison and to death.
local: Na wɔ se i ka, M Mibiɛ, m ŋmáti yaki má u da, ŋka bu sáŋga nyi wɔrɔ ni mini kɛrɛ wɔ, na ya sáŋga wu.

eng: Therefore, Ill have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Nyini ti, m má bu fin i ŋgbere na m yáki i na i kɔ. .

eng: But with one voice they shouted, Away with this man! Release Barabbas to us. .
local: Ama, na jama n kɛrɛ atiɛn kungere­kun ase ka, Yi ahin sɔnɔ ni ŋgɔɔ, na a yi Barabasi ma ya.

eng: Jesus replied, I tell you, Peter, the rooster wont crow today before you have denied three times that you know me.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Petoro, m suse u wɔ lɛɛ ka, nyuma, kana akɔnyuma bá bo, a kéte kpe nsan su ka a si má m.

eng: Barabbas had been thrown into prison because of a riot that had occurred in the city, and for murder.
local: Yiri Barabasi ni i beŋgu ketema­fɔ nbem yo kpiɛɛri jɔrɛ, Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba di aluwa na ba kun minɛ. Nyini ti ye bu taraari i dan­saraka n a.

eng: Jesus said to them, When I sent you out without a wallet, bag, or sandals, you didnt lack anything, did you? They said, Nothing.
local: Na Yesu abisa i susu­fɔm n ka, Saŋga bo m sunmaari am na am abita má ŋwaa kuruku, wara kuruku, wara gbagbaa ni, nzu kpiɛɛri am su a? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Pui akpiɛ má ya su.

eng: Pilate addressed them again because he wanted to release Jesus.
local: Gomina Pilato koroori ka i yaki Yesu nu ji n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ kaaki ajɔjɔ jama n dɔ bekun.

eng: They kept shouting out, Crucify him! Crucify him.
local: Ama, na bu sutiɛntiɛn suse ka, Kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu. Kpama i.

eng: Then he said to them, But now, whoever has a wallet must take it, and likewise a bag. And those who dont own a sword must sell their clothes and buy one.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ama, kisa, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a la ŋwaa kuruku, wara kuruku, fa i, na wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a la má takobe, tɔ u tanlɛɛ na a fa ŋwaa n to be.

eng: I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in relation to me: And he was counted among criminals .Indeed, whats written about me is nearing completion.
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Bu faari m buuri satiɛ­yo­fɔm kun wɔ. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, wɔ yo daka ka, mini dɛɛ ti, na i yo yii­wa. Nahɔrɛ, deke n bo bu kɛrɛ niiri mini ni, i suyo yii­wa.

eng: For the third time, Pilate said to them, Why? What wrong has he done? Ive found no legal basis for the death penalty in his case. Therefore, I will have him whipped, then let him go.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu kpe nsan su ka, Ye nzu satiɛ ye wɔ yo i a? Mini aŋu má satiɛ be bo wɔ yo i na bu kun i. M má bu fin i ŋgbere wɔ, na m yáki i na i kɔ.

eng: They said to him, Lord, look, here are two swords. He replied, Enough of that.
local: Na ba se i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, niɛ. Takobe nnyɔ yaa fɛn. Na wɔ se bu ka, Wɔ sɛ su.

eng: But they were adamant, shouting their demand that Jesus be crucified. Their voices won out.
local: Ama, na ba kaaki atiɛn kpa amiɛ i ka i kpama Yesu baka jaanu n su. Na bu naŋmiɛ yoo­wa agaya n akunma Gomina Pilato.

eng: Jesus left and made his way to the Mount of Olives, as was his custom, and the disciples followed him.
local: Na Yesu afite miɛ n nu, na wɔ kɔ Oliva Buka n su, kabo i nati yo ni, na i susu­fɔm n asu i su.

eng: When he arrived, he said to them, Pray that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri dɔ ni, na wɔ se i susu­fɔm n ka, Am sere Nyɛmɛ, adena má deke be yo am na am wura satiɛ nu.

eng: He withdrew from them about a stones throw, knelt down, and prayed.
local: Dɔ na wɔ yaki bu, na wɔ kɔ mɔ kaan, na wɔ kutu asere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Pilate issued his decision to grant their request.
local: Na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu yo Yesu kabo minɛm koro ni.

eng: He released the one they asked for, who had been thrown into prison because of a riot and murder. But he handed Jesus over to their will.
local: Na wɔ yi sɔnɔ n bo minɛm n koroori i n aji, yiri bo i kpiniiri jɔrɛ na i kuunri minɛm na bu nyiiri i dan­saraka ni. Ama, Yesu yiri, na wɔ ma bu nɔɔ ka bu kun i, kabo Yuda­fɔm koro ni.

eng: He said, Father, if its your will, take this cup of suffering away from me. However, not my will but your will must be done.
local: I ŋa, M Si Nyɛmɛ, nzɛn i ti u koroya­nu, yi m fite ahin anyum­boro n bo i suba m dɔ n nu. Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, má a yo kabo m koro ni, ama, se kabo a koro ni.

eng: Then a heavenly angel appeared to him and strengthened him.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be atiɛ afite aŋgoro aba asi i ahore.

eng: As they led Jesus away, they grabbed Simon, a man from Cyrene, who was coming in from the countryside. They put the cross on his back and made him carry it behind Jesus.
local: Na sojam n afa Yesu afite, na saŋga bo bu wo kɔ su ni, na bu ni biɛ be ayɛ, bu fere i ka Simon, i ti Sirenefɔ ɔ. I fite ahɛmɛ suba miɛ kunnu, na sojam n amiɛ i ka i sɔ baka jaanu n su Yesu su.

eng: He was in anguish and prayed even more earnestly. His sweat became like drops of blood falling on the ground.
local: Ahoretanda n bo i tuuri i n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro abuka su, na i aŋuye sukpinkpin asiɛŋgu ka mbunja dɛɛ su.

eng: A huge crowd of people followed Jesus, including women, who were mourning and wailing for him.
local: Na jama beberebe asu i su, bu nbem ti nbaram wɔ, na bu susun, na bu sukoko, i dɛɛ ti.

eng: When he got up from praying, he went to the disciples. He found them asleep, overcome by grief.
local: I sereeri Nyɛmɛ n wieeri ni, na wɔ yasu akpie i nyi akɔ i susu­fɔm n dɔ, i kɔ ŋuuri ka bu wo daafiri su, dama bu ahore asaaki agaya n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Jesus turned to the women and said, Daughters of Jerusalem, dont cry for me. Rather, cry for yourselves and your children.
local: Na Yesu acɛ aniɛ bu, na wɔ se bu ka, Yɛrusalem nbaram, má am sun mini dɛɛ ti, ama, am sun ambɛrɛ ni am nbaam dɛɛ ti.

eng: The time will come when they will say, Happy are those who are unable to become pregnant, the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never nursed a child.
local: Am ti fɛn, saŋga ni suba bo minɛm bá se ka, Tere­fiɛ wo ŋɔrɔkam n dɔ, ni bo bu nya wu má nbaam ni, ni bo nbaam nya nu má bu nyufini ni.

eng: He said to them, Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you wont give in to temptation.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nzu ti ye am da daafi a? Am yasu jina na am sere Nyɛmɛ, adena má deke be yo am na am wura satiɛ nu.

eng: Then they will say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us.
local: Nyini saŋga n ye minɛm sé ka, Nzɛn má bukam bereki gɔ ya ŋu, na kpɛrɛm kata ya ŋu, má i ti ye.

eng: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd appeared, and the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
local: Na saŋga bo i toko wo jɔjɔɔri n su ni, na jama be aba aju. Yudasi bo i ti i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm nu kun ni, duuri bu mɔ a, na wɔ kɔ Yesu dɔ ka i kɔ biti i.

eng: If they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry.
local: Nzɛn bu faari baka mɔnɛ wuraari sin nu na i jeeri, ye baka wuruu­wa yiri yó sɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: They also led two other criminals to be executed with Jesus.
local: Na ba fa satiɛ­yo­fɔ nnyɔ nbem gusu akɔ ka bu kɔ kun bu ni Yesu kɛrɛ.

eng: When they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right and the other on his left.
local: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri dika be bo bu fere i ka Tere­awa woo­biri ni, na ba kpama Yesu baka jaanu n su, na ba yɛ i su ajina. Na ba kpama satiɛ­yo­fɔ nnyɔ nbem baka jaanu n su adodo i, bu kpamaari kun i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su wɔ, na ba kpama kun n gusu i saa biɛ su.

eng: Jesus said to him, Judas, would you betray the Human Onewith a kiss.
local: ama, na Yesu abisa i ka, Yudasi, a biti m ye a ma bu nya mini Adamande Wa n taraa­biri a.

eng: When those around him recognized what was about to happen, they said, Lord, should we fight with our swords.
local: Saŋga bo i susu­fɔm bo bu wo i dɔ n ŋuuri sa n bo i suba ni, na ba bisa i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, ya kpiɛ bu takobe, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Father, forgive them, for they dont know what theyre doing. They drew lots as a way of dividing up his clothing.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, M Si Nyɛmɛ, fa bu satiɛ ce bu, dama bu si má sa n bo bu suyo i ni. Na ba tu mbaa na bu si kabo bu kpaaki i suturam nu.

eng: The people were standing around watching, but the leaders sneered at him, saying, He saved others. Let him save himself if he really is the Christ sent from God, the chosen one.
local: Na minɛm n ajina dɔ suniɛ i, na Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm mmɔɔ ayo i anzasi. Bu ŋa, I ŋa i koro de minɛm tii ji. Nzɛn yiri ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi i ni, ma i de i ŋu tii ji.

eng: One of them struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Na bu nu kun akpiɛ ayi sɔnɔ be sui, i saa fɔmbɔrɔ dɛɛ ni, nyini sɔnɔ n ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ n kɛrɛ kpiɛn n akɔɔ ɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, Stop! No more of this! He touched the slaves ear and healed him.
local: Ama, na Yesu atiɛn i susu­fɔm n ŋu ka, Má am yo sɔ kun. Na wɔ fa i saa akan sɔnɔ n sui ni, na wɔ nya laifiɛ.

eng: The soldiers also mocked him. They came up to him, offering him sour wine.
local: Na sojam n gusu ayo i anzasi, na ba fa nzan gagaraki be ama i ka i nu.

eng: and saying, If you really are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
local: na ba se ka, Nzɛn wɔrɔ ti Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n a, de u tii ji.

eng: Then Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who had come to get him, Have you come with swords and clubs to arrest me, as though I were a thief?.
local: Dɔ na Yesu ase Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni tintini­jina­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm kɛrɛ bo bu baari ka bu ba tara i n ka, M ti awiefɔ ɔ, ye am fa takobem ni kɔrɔmam na am ba tara m a?.

eng: Above his head was a notice of the formal charge against him. It read This is the king of the Jews.
local: Na ba kɛrɛ kɛrɛɛ­wa be abo akpama i baka jaanu n tii su, kɛrɛɛ­wa n la ka, Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n lɛɛ.

eng: One of the criminals hanging next to Jesus insulted him: Arent you the Christ? Save yourself and us.
local: Na satiɛ­yo­fɔ nnyɔm bo bu saŋga kpamaari bu ni Yesu ni, bu nu kun atɔ i ŋu ni nzukɔ, i ŋa, Má wɔrɔ ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a? De u tii ji na a de yɛrɛ gusu ji.

eng: Responding, the other criminal spoke harshly to him, Dont you fear God, seeing that youve also been sentenced to die?.
local: Ama, na nyiŋga n ajaraki i, i ŋa, A soro má Nyɛmɛ wɔ? M ŋa ya dɛɛ sui­cin ni i dɛɛ n kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ.

eng: Day after day I was with you in the temple, but you didnt arrest me. But this is your time, when darkness rules.
local: Cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn m ni ambɛrɛ wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu a, am atara má m. Ama, kisa ti saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ ama atin, na am ni Sitana bo i wo awosin nu n kere am yiko a.

eng: We are rightly condemned, for we are receiving the appropriate sentence for what we did. But this man has done nothing wrong.
local: Ya dɛɛ sui­cin n yiri, i ti deke bo ya de i wɔ, dama kabo i ti ye ni ka bu yo ya lɛɛ, ama, yiri ado má ŋgasi be.

eng: Then he said, Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase ka, Yesu, a ba baari ni u fɛmɛya ni, teŋge m su.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that today you will be with me in paradise.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u n a, nyuma dɛɛ cɛɛn n nu, a bá wo m dɔ alezanda nu.

eng: After they arrested Jesus, they led him away and brought him to the high priests house. Peter followed from a distance.
local: Na ba tara Yesu afa i akɔ awura Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn n dika, na Petoro asu bu su, ama, i woori dede kaan ni bɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: When they lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter sat among them.
local: Minɛ nbem atɔrɔ sin gisan n afiɛn, atana abara ayɛ i, na Petoro akɔ abuka bu su.

eng: It was now about noon, and darkness covered the whole earth until about three oclock.
local: Na fa kɔ wiɛsu kerefi buru ni nnyɔ saŋga, wiɛ n afite má na awosin akata asiɛn n kɛrɛ su haari naasɔ kerefi nsan, na tani bambaka n bo bu fa kanda kpaakiiri Sɔɔ Casi n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu n ati nu nnyɔ, i afiɛn.

eng: Crying out in a loud voice, Jesus said, Father, into your hands I entrust my life . After he said this, he breathed for the last time.
local: Na Yesu abo awie kpa, i ŋa, M Si Nyɛmɛ, ma fa m ŋu awura u saa nu. I seeri sɔ wieeri n na wɔ yo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ.

eng: Then a servant woman saw him sitting in the firelight. She stared at him and said, This man was with him too.
local: I tanaari na sin wein n gɔɔri i ŋu ni, na afani be aŋu i. Na wɔ niɛ i nyunu dika gbirim, na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin sɔnɔ ni mmɔɔ ti i susu­fɔ ɔ.

eng: When the centurion saw what happened, he praised God, saying, Its really true: this man was righteous.
local: Saŋga bo soja kpiɛn ŋuuri sa n bo wɔ yo sɔ ni, na wɔ kansi Nyɛmɛ, na wɔ se ka, Nahɔrɛ su, ahin sɔnɔ n tiiri sɔnɔ fofoe wɔ.

eng: All the crowds who had come together to see this event returned to their homes beating their chests after seeing what had happened.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ jina wo i niɛ su ni, saŋga bo bu ŋuuri sa n bo wɔ yo n sɔ ni, na bu kɛrɛ afa bu saa ataka i beŋgu nu, na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ awuru.

eng: But Peter denied it, saying, Woman, I dont know him.
local: Ama, na wɔ kete, i ŋa, Bara, m si má i.

eng: And everyone who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance observing these things.
local: Na Yesu danfom n kɛrɛ, ni nbaram bo bu suuri i su fiteeri Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni, ajina dede kaan suniɛ sam n kɛrɛ bo bu yoori ni.

eng: A little while later, someone else saw him and said, You are one of them too. But Peter said, Man, Im not.
local: Nyini sin, wɔ cɛ má, na sɔnɔ be gusu aba aŋu i, na wɔ se i ka, Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ti bu kun wɔ. Ama, na wɔ se i ka, Wɔrɔ, m ti má bu kun.
