eng: An hour or so later, someone else insisted, This man must have been with him, because he is a Galilean too.
local: Na fa kɔ saŋga su bekun, na sɔnɔ kun be ajina su kekereke ase ka, Nahɔrɛ, ahin sɔnɔ n ti bu kun wɔ, dama yiri gusu ti Galili­fɔ ɔ.

eng: Peter responded, Man, I dont know what you are talking about! At that very moment, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
local: Ama, na wɔ se i ka, Wɔrɔ, m si má ahin jɔrɛ n bo a sujɔjɔ i ni mmɔɔ. Saŋga bo i toko wo jɔjɔɔri n su ni, na akɔnyuma akaba abo.

eng: Now there was a man named Joseph who was a member of the council. He was a good and righteous man.
local: Sɔnɔ be woori bɛrɛ, bu fere i ka Yosefu, i ti Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm nu kun wɔ, na i ti sɔnɔ kpa, na i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: He hadnt agreed with the plan and actions of the council. He was from the Jewish city of Arimathea and eagerly anticipated Gods kingdom.
local: Wɔ soro má su ni sunsunni n bo i beŋgum faari i na bu yoori Yesu barasu ni. I fite miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Aramatiya na i wo Yudiya awɔrɛ nu n wɔ, na i dɛɛ la ka, i ŋminda wɔ, fa kɔ saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ bá niɛ i minɛm su.

eng: The Lord turned and looked straight at Peter, and Peter remembered the Lords words: Before a rooster crows today, you will deny me three times.
local: Na Mibiɛ Yesu n acɛ aniɛ Petoro nyunu, na wɔ teŋge su kabo Mibiɛ n seeri i ni, i ŋa, Nyuma, kana akɔnyuma bá bo, a kéte kpe nsan su ka a si má m.

eng: This man went to Pilate and asked for Jesus body.
local: Na wɔ kɔ Gomina Pilato dɔ, na wɔ kɔ asere i ka i fa Yesu ŋuniɛ n ma i.

eng: Taking it down, he wrapped it in a linen cloth and laid it in a tomb carved out of the rock, in which no one had ever been buried.
local: Na wɔ kɔ ayi fuin ni baka jaanu n su, na wɔ fa nzee n apɔɔndi i. Na wɔ kɔ afa i awura yabuɛ buɛ bo ba lokoti i ayo i faŋ­kunma n nu na ba nya ase má sɔnɔ be nu ni, na wɔ fa i ase nu.

eng: And Peter went out and cried uncontrollably.
local: Na wɔ fite akɔ asun agaya.

eng: It was the Preparation Day for the Sabbath, and the Sabbath was quickly approaching.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n tiiri Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n kpokpo cɛɛn wɔ, i naasɔ, wɔ ka kaan na bu di Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni.

eng: The men who were holding Jesus in custody taunted him while they beat him.
local: Na minɛm bo bu bita Yesu ni, ayo i anzasi, na ba bo i.

eng: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph. They saw the tomb and how Jesus body was laid in it.
local: Nbaram bo bu suuri Yesu su fiteeri Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni, bɛrɛ suuri Yosefu su, na ba kɔ aŋu yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma ni, ni kabo bu seeri fuin n barasu.

eng: They blindfolded him and asked him repeatedly, Prophesy! Who hit you.
local: Na ba fa sanvuin acin akata i nyu­mbaa, na ba bisa i ka, Wɔrɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ni, ŋma bo u a? Kan kere ya.

eng: Insulting him, they said many other horrible things against him.
local: Na ba kpiɛ i nzukɔ barasu barasu n kɛrɛ.

eng: then they went away and prepared fragrant spices and perfumed oils. They rested on the Sabbath, in keeping with the commandment.
local: Na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ awuru ka bu kɔ kpa bu ŋu ni ŋguin nva­nvanim ni tulaalɛɛm. Na alecin Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, na ba yi bu ŋumi kabo Nyɛmɛ maari Moyisi nɔɔ ka bu yo ni.

eng: As morning came, the elders of the people, both chief priests and legal experts, came together, and Jesus was brought before their council.
local: Na ale acin, na Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm atiɛn bu nɔɔ, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n gusu buka su, na ba fa Yesu aba bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ n nu.

eng: They said, If you are the Christ, tell us! He answered, If I tell you, you wont believe.
local: Na ba se i ka, Nzɛn a ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a, se ya. Na wɔ se bu ka, Nzɛn m seeri am ka yiri la mini, am dé má.

eng: And if I ask you a question, you wont answer.
local: na nzɛn m bisaari am bisaa­wa, am bɔ́ má m nu.

eng: But from now on, the Human One will be seated on the right side of the power of God. .
local: Ama, fite kisa fa kɔ, mini Adamande Wa ni bá tana Nyɛmɛ Yiko­fɔ n saa fɔmbɔrɔ su wɔ.

eng: They all said, Are you Gods Son, then? He replied, You say that I am.
local: Dɔ na bu kɛrɛ abisa i ka, Ŋka wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ Wa n a? Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Kabo am jɔjɔɔri ni, i ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: Then they said, Why do we need further testimony? Weve heard it from his own lips.
local: Na ba se ka, Ya koro má daani­fɔ be kun. Yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii ati kabo wɔ se ni.

eng: Very early in the morning on the first day of the week, the women went to the tomb, bringing the fragrant spices they had prepared.
local: Na alecin, Yuda­fɔm bakuɛ n bumboori, mɔnɛ cɛɛn ŋgarama fuun, na nbaram n afa tulaalɛɛ nva­nvani bo ba kpini ase ni, na ba fa akɔ yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma n su.

eng: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
local: Bu kɔ juuri dɔ n na ba ŋu ka yabuɛ bambaka n akundu ayaki faŋ­kunma n anɔ ni.

eng: but when they went in, they didnt find the body of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ama, bu wuraari faŋ­kunma n nu ni, ba ŋu má Mibiɛ Yesu ŋuniɛ ni.

eng: They didnt know what to make of this. Suddenly, two men were standing beside them in gleaming bright clothing.
local: na ba miɛ aka ajina gbirim, kpeŋga na ba ŋu ka nbiɛsɔm nnyɔ jina, bu wura sutura fofoe papasi.

eng: The women were frightened and bowed their faces toward the ground, but the men said to them, Why do you look for the living among the dead?.
local: Na sɛrɛ ati nbaram n agaya, na ba biri bu tii haari asiɛŋgu, na minɛm n abisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am suniɛniɛ fuinm nu na am ŋu sɔnɔ bo i la ŋgɔɔ a?.

eng: He isnt here, but has been raised. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee.
local: I wo má fɛn. Wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu. Am teŋge su kabo i yo seeri am ni, saŋga bo i yo wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni.

eng: that the Human One must be handed over to sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again.
local: i ŋa, Wɔ yo daka ka bu bá fa mini Adamande Wa n wura satiɛ­yo­fɔm saa nu, na bu kpáma m baka jaanu n su, ama, na le­san cɛɛn na m téŋge m yiwee n nu.

eng: Then they remembered his words.
local: Dɔ na ba teŋge Yesu nɔaniɛ bo i yo seeri bu n su.

eng: When they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and all the others.
local: na ba fite faŋ­kunma n nu, na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ akan sam n kɛrɛ bo ba ŋu i ni, akere i susu­fɔ buru ni kunm ni, ni nyiŋgam kɛrɛ.

eng: It was Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them who told these things to the apostles.
local: Nbaram ni la Mariya bo i fite Magadala miɛ n nu ni, ni Yowana, ni Yakobo dɛɛ ni Mariya, ni bu beŋgu nbara nbem, bɛrɛ kaanri nyini sam n kɛrɛ kereeri i jaramasam n a.

eng: Their words struck the apostles as nonsense, and they didnt believe the women.
local: Ama, bu tiiri jɔrɛ n bo nbaram kan kereeri bu ni, bu sunsuunri ka i ti jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, ba yo má yarada.

eng: But Peter ran to the tomb. When he bent over to look inside, he saw only the linen cloth. Then he returned home, wondering what had happened.
local: Ama, na Petoro ayasu aŋmati akɔ faŋ­kunma n nu, i kɔ juuri n na wɔ kasi aniɛ faŋ­kunma n nu, wɔ ŋu má pui, má ka nzee n ŋgumi da. Na wɔ kpie i nyi aba awuru, na wɔ birisi fa kɔ sa n bo wɔ yo n su.

eng: On that same day, two disciples were traveling to a village called Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.
local: Nyini cɛɛn ni, na Yesu susu­fɔ nnyɔ nbem sukɔ miɛ kaan be bo bu fere i ka Ɛmɔsi n su, i ni Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n waari má agaya.

eng: They were talking to each other about everything that had happened.
local: Bu wo kɔ su ni, na bu subo anzama fa kɔ sa n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo n su.

eng: While they were discussing these things, Jesus himself arrived and joined them on their journey.
local: Bu nati wo nyini anzama n bo su ni akuruwa ni, na Yesu ni i tii aba atu bu atin nu.

eng: They were prevented from recognizing him.
local: na ba ŋu i, ama, ba koro asi má ka yiri lɛɛ.

eng: He said to them, What are you talking about as you walk along? They stopped, their faces downcast.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am wo natiiri n su atin n nu ni, am boori nzu anzama n a? Na ba ka ajina gbirim ni ahoresaaki.

eng: The one named Cleopas replied, Are you the only visitor to Jerusalem who is unaware of the things that have taken place there over the last few days.
local: Na bu nu kun be bo bu fere i ka Kɛlɔpasi n abɔ i nu ka, I yo sɛ ye wɔfɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu n si sam bo ba yo nyini le­nnyɔ n nu bɛrɛ, ye wɔrɔ ŋgumi si má.

eng: He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things about Jesus of Nazareth. Because of his powerful deeds and words, he was recognized by God and all the people as a prophet.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Nzu sam? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n jɔrɛ. I yo tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ bo i la yiko wɔ, fa kɔ i alekutura sam ni i keree­wam su, na i sa­yoo­wam sun Nyɛmɛ ni minɛm n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: But our chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni tere­tere­fɔm n ama ba di i jɔrɛ ka bu kun i, na ba kpama i baka jaanu n su.

eng: We had hoped he was the one who would redeem Israel. All these things happened three days ago.
local: Ama, yɛrɛ yo laari tama ka yiri ye wɔ ba ka i bá de Yisareli­fɔm tii ji a. Deke bo i buka nyinim kɛrɛ su n la ka, nyini sam kɛrɛ yoori, i nyuma le­san lɛɛ.

eng: But theres more: Some women from our group have left us stunned. They went to the tomb early this morning.
local: Haari ni nyini n mmɔɔ, ya nbaram nbem ba kaanri alekutura jɔrɛ be kereeri ya. Bu kɔɔri yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma n nu wɔ ŋgarama fuun.

eng: and didnt find his body. They came to us saying that they had even seen a vision of angels who told them he is alive.
local: ama, ba ŋu má fuin n dɔ. Bu kpieeri bu nyi ba juuri ni, na ba se ya ka, bu kɔ ŋuuri Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm ka alɛlɛ nu wɔ, faŋ­kunma n nu, na ba se bu ka i la ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found things just as the women said. They didnt see him.
local: Na ya nu nbem gusu akɔ faŋ­kunma n nu, bu kɔ ŋuuri deke n kɛrɛ sɔ ɔ, kabo nbaram seeri ni, ama, ba ŋu má yiri.

eng: Then Jesus said to them, You foolish people! Your dull minds keep you from believing all that the prophets talked about.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ, ŋgɔnlɛ akpiɛ am su wɔ, nzɛn sɛ, ye dada kɛrɛ am nya yo má yarada, fa kɔ jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm jɔjɔɔri n su?.

eng: Wasnt it necessary for the Christ to suffer these things and then enter into his glory.
local: Am si má ka wɔ yo daka ka Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n di nyini wahara n kɛrɛ ka, na Nyɛmɛ wura i kpiri.

eng: Then he interpreted for them the things written about himself in all the scriptures, starting with Moses and going through all the Prophets.
local: I seeri sɔ n na wɔ kan i ŋusu jɔrɛ akere bu, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n kɛrɛ nu ni, i boori i bu ni Moyisi dɛɛ kɛrɛɛ­wam wɔ, na wɔ nya akan kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ kɛrɛɛri ni.

eng: When they came to Emmaus, he acted as if he was going on ahead.
local: Bu ba kɔ suju miɛ n bo bu sukɔ su ni, na Yesu ayo ka yiri susin kɔ mɔ.

eng: But they urged him, saying, Stay with us. Its nearly evening, and the day is almost over. So he went in to stay with them.
local: ama, na ba sere i ni anyum­boro, na ba se i ka, Ba yo ya wɔfɔ. Wiɛ atɔ, ale susan lɛɛ. Nyini ti, na wɔ wura awuru n nu ka i da bu dɔ.

eng: After he took his seat at the table with them, he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.
local: Saŋga bo i tanaari na bu kɛrɛ di diire n ni, na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ bubu i nu afa ama bu.

eng: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.
local: Dɔ na bu nyunu ateke i ŋu, na ba si i, kpeŋga na wɔ mini ayaki bu.

eng: They said to each other, Werent our hearts on fire when he spoke to us along the road and when he explained the scriptures for us.
local: Na ba se bu beŋgu ka, Saŋga bo i jɔjɔɔri ya dɔ atin n nu na i kaanri Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n bu kereeri ya ni, i maari ya ahorejɔ kɔ sin su wɔ, má sɔ.

eng: They got up right then and returned to Jerusalem. They found the eleven and their companions gathered together.
local: Na ba kaba ayasu bɛrɛ akpie bu nyi akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba kɔ aŋu ka i susu­fɔ buru ni kunm ni, ni nyiŋgam n atiɛn bu nɔɔ.

eng: They were saying to each other, The Lord really has risen! He appeared to Simon.
local: bu suse ka, Ya Mibiɛ n ateŋge nahɔrɛ su. Wɔ yi i ŋu akere Simon.

eng: Then the two disciples described what had happened along the road and how Jesus was made known to them as he broke the bread.
local: Na susu­fɔ nnyɔm n akan sa n bo i yoori atin n nu dɔ ni, ni kabo bu siiri ka yiri ni, saŋga bo i bubuuri kpɔnɔ n nu maari bu ni.

eng: While they were saying these things, Jesus himself stood among them and said, Peace be with you.
local: Saŋga bo susu­fɔ nnyɔm n wo jɔrɛ n kan su sɔ ni, na Yesu ni i tii akaba aba ajina bu afiɛn, na wɔ se bu ka, Nyɛmɛ ma am ahoreda.

eng: They were terrified and afraid. They thought they were seeing a ghost.
local: Na sɛrɛ ati bu agaya, dama bu sunsun ka ŋumi ye ba ŋu i a.

eng: He said to them, Why are you startled? Why are doubts arising in your hearts?.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Nzu deke miɛ am a? Nzu ti ye am yo sika?.

eng: Look at my hands and my feet. Its really me! Touch me and see, for a ghost doesnt have flesh and bones like you see I have.
local: Am niɛ m saam ni m jaam, mini ni m tii lɛɛ. Am fa am saa kan m na am niɛ, ŋumi la má aŋuniɛ­mbaa ni buwem kabo am ŋu ka m la i ni.

eng: As he said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
local: I seeri sɔ wieeri n na wɔ fa i saam ni i jaam deke bo bu boori i bulaari n afa akere bu.

eng: Because they were wondering and questioning in the midst of their happiness, he said to them, Do you have anything to eat.
local: I yoori bu alekutura na bu ahore jɔ kɔ siinri su ni, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ, ba koro ayo má yarada. Dɔ na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am la diire be fɛn bo am di.

eng: They gave him a piece of baked fish.
local: Na ba fa jombaa n bo ba tun i n be ama i.

eng: Taking it, he ate it in front of them.
local: na wɔ de akpɛsɛ bu nyu­mbaa nu bɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you—that everything written about me in the Law from Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin nɔaniɛm bo m yo jɔjɔɔri bu saŋga bo m toko wo am dɔ n lɛɛ, ka, wɔ yo daka ka m ŋusu jɔrɛ kɛrɛ yo yii­wa, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i fa maari Moyisi dɛɛ kadasi n nu, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ n nu, ni kansi jue kɛrɛɛ­wam n nu ni.

eng: Then he opened their minds to understand the scriptures.
local: Na wɔ teke bu nyi na bu si Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n bu.

eng: He said to them, This is what is written: the Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day.
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n lɛɛ, ka, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni bá di wahara, na bu kún i, na le­san cɛɛn na i téŋge i yiwee n nu.

eng: and a change of heart and life for the forgiveness of sins must be preached in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
local: Bu bó i bu Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na bu kán labari kpa n jɔrɛ i duma nu kere daanfiɛ kɛrɛ, ka, bu kaaki bu wo na Nyɛmɛ fá bu satiɛ ce bu.

eng: You are witnesses of these things.
local: Ambɛrɛ dí nyini sam daani a.

eng: Look, Im sending to you what my Father promised, but you are to stay in the city until you have been furnished with heavenly power.
local: Am ti fɛn, mini bá sunma aceeri n bo m Si Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma am, na i bá wura am ŋu n a. Ama, ambɛrɛ ŋmínda miɛ n kunnu fɛn, haari saŋga bo yiko ni bá fite aŋgoro ba wura am ŋu.

eng: He led them out as far as Bethany, where he lifted his hands and blessed them.
local: Na wɔ fa bu afite miɛ n kunnu akɔ aju Betani miɛ n ahɛmɛ, dɔ na wɔ yɛ i saa su na wɔ yo kisi agɔ bu ŋu.

eng: As he blessed them, he left them and was taken up to heaven.
local: I wo bu kisi n yo su sɔ ni, na wɔ yaki bu, na Nyɛmɛ ama wɔ fun akɔ aŋgoro.

eng: They worshipped him and returned to Jerusalem overwhelmed with joy.
local: Na ba kutu asu i, na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni ahorejɔ bambaka.

eng: And they were continuously in the temple praising God.
local: Na ba kɔ awo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu sukansi Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God.
local: Kana Nyɛmɛ ba yi durunya ni, be woori bɛrɛ bo bu fere i ka Nɔaniɛ. I ni Nyɛmɛ woori bɛrɛ a. Yiri Nɔaniɛ ni ti Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: The Word was with God in the beginning.
local: Fite i bumboori n kɛrɛ, yiri ni Nyɛmɛ wo bɛrɛ a.

eng: Everything came into being through the Word, and without the Word nothing came into being. What came into being.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ ama Nɔaniɛ n ayi deke n kɛrɛ. Deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo wɔ yi má i.

eng: through the Word was life, and the life was the light for all people.
local: Ŋgɔɔ fite yiri Nɔaniɛ ni dɔ a. Nyini ŋgɔɔ ni fa wein ba tekeeri adamande nyi a.

eng: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness doesnt extinguish the light.
local: Na wein n aje awosin nu, na awosin n akoro akunma má wein ni.

eng: A man named John was sent from God.
local: Nyɛmɛ yo sunmaari sɔnɔ be, bu fere i ka Yohane.

eng: He came as a witness to testify concerning the light, so that through him everyone would believe in the light.
local: I baari ka i ba di nyini wein n daani wɔ, na i kan i jɔrɛ kere minɛm, adena sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tiiri kabo i kan ni, na bu daari nyini wein n ŋu.

eng: He himself wasnt the light, but his mission was to testify concerning the light.
local: Yohane n mmɔɔ ni i tii yo ti má wein ni, ama, i baari ka i ba di wein n daani wɔ.

eng: The true light that shines on all people was coming into the world.
local: Nyini wein ni ti wein nahɔrɛ su n a, bo wɔ ba durunya n nu, na i teke adamande kɛrɛ nyi.

eng: The light was in the world, and the world came into being through the light, but the world didnt recognize the light.
local: Yiri n bo bu fere i ka Nɔaniɛ n yo woori durunya n nu wɔ, Nyɛmɛ faari yiri yiiri durunya n a, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ, durunya n nu­fɔm asi má i.

eng: The light came to his own people, and his own people didnt welcome him.
local: Na wɔ ba i tiɛŋgu dɛɛ asiɛn n su minɛm dɔ, na ba kete ka bu de má i.

eng: But those who did welcome him, those who believed in his name, he authorized to become Gods children.
local: Ama, na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu deeri i, na bu daari i ŋu ni, na wɔ ma bu atin ka bu kaaki Nyɛmɛ nbaam.

eng: born not from blood nor from human desire or passion, but born from God.
local: Bu Nyɛmɛ nbaa kaakii­wam n ti má ka bu wu bu ni adamande mbunja a, nzɛn má nyini, i ti má ka biɛsɔ ni bara saŋga a ye bu wu bu a, ama, Nyɛmɛ ni i tii, ti bu Si a.

eng: The Word became flesh and made his home among us. We have seen his glory, glory like that of a fathers only son, full of grace and truth.
local: Na yiri Nɔaniɛ n akaaki adamande aba awo ya dɔ. Ahɔrɔma ni nahɔrɛ wo i dɔ beberebe. Ya ŋu i jirima woo­wa ni, nyini jirima woo­wa n ye Nyɛmɛ afa ama i Wa kun n kpein n a.

eng: John testified about him, crying out, This is the one of whom I said, He who comes after me is greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Yohane diiri i daani kereeri minɛm, na wɔ tiɛn ase ka, Nyini sɔnɔ n bo m yo seeri am ka i wo m sin suba na i tara m fieo n lɛɛ, dama kana bu ba wuuri m ni, yiri wo bɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: From his fullness we have all received grace upon grace.
local: I ahɔrɔma beberebe n dɛɛ ti, ye ya di i ahɔrɔma, na ya toko di i buka su.

eng: as the Law was given through Moses, so grace and truth came into being through Jesus Christ.
local: Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni maari Moyisi ka i fa kere Yisareli­fɔm, ama, Yesu Kristo aba ka i ba kere ya kabo Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n ni i nahɔrɛ n ti barasu wɔ.

eng: No one has ever seen God. God the only Son, who is at the Fathers side, has made God known.
local: Be nya ŋu má Nyɛmɛ, má ka i Wa kun n kpein n bo i ni i Si Nyɛmɛ n ti kun ni, na i wo Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, yiri ye wɔ kere ya kabo Nyɛmɛ ti a.

eng: This is Johns testimony when the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, Who are you.
local: Ahin ti daani dii­wa n bo Yohane yo diiri i, saŋga bo Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n bo bu wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu n sunmaari Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ni bu buka­fɔm ka bu kɔ bisa Yohane ka, Wɔrɔ la ŋma a.

eng: John confessed , Im not the Christ.
local: Yohane afiɛ má bu jɔrɛ be su, na wɔ kan i kɛrɛ kpain akere bu. I ŋa, Má mini ti Kristo ni, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ŋa i súnma i n a.

eng: They asked him, Then who are you? Are you Elijah? John said, Im not. Are you the prophet? John answered, No.
local: Na ba bisa i bekun ka, Ye wɔrɔ la ŋma a? Wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Eliya n a? Na wɔ se bu ka, Má yiri la mini. Na ba kaaki abisa i ka, Wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n a? I ŋa, Ai.

eng: They asked, Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself.
local: Na ba sa su abisa i ka, Ye wɔrɔ la ŋma a? Ma ya nɔaniɛ na ya kɔ se bo bu sunmaari ya ni. Wɔrɔ ŋa, wɔrɔ la ŋma tenle su a.

eng: John replied, I am a voice crying out in the wilderness, Make the Lords path straight, just as the prophet Isaiah said.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Sɔnɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo kɛrɛɛri i jɔrɛ ka i bá subo ndoori boro kpantain nu n ka, Am yo Mibiɛ n atin n dɔŋgu. Yiri la mini.

eng: Those sent by the Pharisees.
local: Minɛm bo bu bisaari Yohane bisaa­wa ni, Farasi­fɔm bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm asɛsɛ be ni sunmaari bu a.

eng: asked, Why do you baptize if you arent the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet.
local: Na ba kaaki abisa i ka, A ŋa a ti má Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni, a kaaki ti má Eliya, na a kaaki ti má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n ni, ye nzu ti ye a biɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue a.

eng: John answered, I baptize with water. Someone greater stands among you, whom you dont recognize.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Mini biɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue wɔ. Ama, sɔnɔ be wo am nu fɛn bo ambɛrɛ si má i.

eng: He comes after me, but Im not worthy to untie his sandal straps.
local: I wo m sin suba, m ti kaan ni bo m dodo i mmɔɔ.

eng: This encounter took place across the Jordan in Bethany where John was baptizing.
local: Ahin sa n kɛrɛ yo yoori miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Betani n nu wɔ, i wo Yodan kunma n wiɛ afiiri, deke bo Yohane yo biɛɛri minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue n wɔ.

eng: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!.
local: Alecin fɔfɔrɛ na Yohane aŋu ka Yesu suba i dɔ, na wɔ se ka, Am niɛ, Nyɛmɛ bɔmbaa n bo wɔ ba ka i ba yi durunya n nu­fɔm satiɛ n lɛɛ.

eng: This is the one about whom I said, He who comes after me is really greater than me because he existed before me.
local: Yiri, ye m yo seeri am ka sɔnɔ n bo i wo m sin suba na i tara m fieo n lɛɛ, dama i yo woori bɛrɛ wɔ ka, ye bu wuuri m a, yiri lɛɛ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but I came baptizing with water so that he might be made known to Israel.
local: Mini mmɔɔ ni m tii yo si má i, m dɛɛ la ka m biɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue wɔ, adena ambɛrɛ Yisareli­fɔm si i.

eng: John testified, I saw the Spirit coming down from heaven like a dove, and it rested on him.
local: Na Yohane adi daani ase minɛm ka, Ma ŋu ka Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afite aŋgoro ajura ka tiiwa dɛɛ su aba atana i ŋu, na wɔ wo i dɔ.

eng: Even I didnt recognize him, but the one who sent me to baptize with water said to me, The one on whom you see the Spirit coming down and resting is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.
local: Mini mmɔɔ ni m tii yo si má i, ama, Nyɛmɛ bo i sunmaari m ka m ba biɛ minɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, yiri seeri m ka, A bá ŋu ka m Awiɛwiɛ ni júra ba tana sɔnɔ be ŋu, na i wó i dɔ, yiri má m Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ba wura minɛm ŋu a.

eng: I have seen and testified that this one is Gods Son.
local: Ma ŋu i sɔ ye m sudi daani kere minɛ a, ka yiri ti Nyɛmɛ Wa n nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: The next day John was standing again with two of his disciples.
local: Na alecin fɔfɔrɛ bekun na Yohane ni i susu­fɔm ni nnyɔ jina.

eng: When he saw Jesus walking along he said, Look! The Lamb of God.
local: Na wɔ niɛ aŋu ka Yesu wo natiiri su susin, na wɔ se ka, Am niɛ, Nyɛmɛ Bɔmbaa n lɛɛ.

eng: The two disciples heard what he said, and they followed Jesus.
local: I susu­fɔ nnyɔm n tiiri sɔ n na ba kɔ asu Yesu su.

eng: When Jesus turned and saw them following, he asked, What are you looking for? They said, Rabbi , where are you staying.
local: Na Yesu akaaki aniɛ aŋu ka bu susu i su, na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am koro nzu wɔ? Na ba bisa i ka, Rabi , a da ni wɔ? Rabi bu la ka, ya Kere­fɔ.

eng: He replied, Come and see. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they remained with him that day. It was about four oclock in the afternoon.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am bra na am ba niɛ. Nyini saŋga ni, naasɔ kerefi nna abo, na ba su i su, na ba kɔ aŋu deke bo i wo ni. Na ba ka i dɔ nyini cɛɛn ni.

eng: One of the two disciples who heard what John said and followed Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
local: Nyini minɛ n nnyɔm n bo bu yo tiiri kabo Yohane seeri n na bu suuri Yesu su ni, bu nu kun ye bu fere i ka Andere n a, bo i gɔrɔ la Simon Petoro ni.

eng: He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, We have found the Messiah .
local: Nyini sin na Andere akɔ aŋu i gɔrɔ Simon, na wɔ se i ka, Ya ŋu Mɛsaya ni. Mɛsaya bu la ka Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: He led him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas .
local: Na wɔ fa i aba Yesu dɔ. Na Yesu aniɛ i nyunu, na wɔ se i ka, Bu fere u ka Simon wɔ, wɔrɔ ti Yohane wa n a, ama, bu bá fere u ka Kefasi. I bu la ka yabuɛ, i ni Petoro kɛrɛ ti duma kun wɔ.

eng: The next day Jesus wanted to go into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, Follow me.
local: Alecin fɔfɔrɛ na Yesu ayasu ka i kɔ Galili dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu. Na wɔ kɔ aŋu sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Filipu ni, na wɔ se i ka, Ba su m su.

eng: Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.
local: Filipu bɔsu la miɛ be bo bu fere i ka Besayida n wɔ. I ni Andere ni Petoro kɛrɛ fite miɛ kun su wɔ.

eng: Philip found Nathanael and said to him, We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law and the Prophets: Jesus, Josephs son, from Nazareth.
local: Na Filipu gusu akɔ aŋu sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Nataniɛli, na wɔ se i ka, Ya ŋu sɔnɔ n bo Moyisi yo kɛrɛɛri i jɔrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa kadasi n nu ni, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm gusu yo kɛrɛɛri i jɔrɛ bu dɛɛ kadasi n nu ni. Yiri la Yesu n bo i fite Nazarɛti ni, na i si la sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Yosefu ni.

eng: Nathanael responded, Can anything from Nazareth be good? Philip said, Come and see.
local: Na Nataniɛli abisa i ka, Deke kpa koro fite Nazarɛti dɛɛ miɛ n gusu nu? Na Filipu ase i ka, Bra na a ba niɛ.

eng: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, Here is a genuine Israelite in whom there is no deceit.
local: Nyini saŋga na Yesu aŋu ka Nataniɛli suba i dɔ, na wɔ se ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n ti Yisareli­fɔ baa a nahɔrɛ su, bo sa be wo má i ŋu.

eng: Nathanael asked him, How do you know me? Jesus answered, Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.
local: Na Nataniɛli abisa i ka, A yo sɛ siiri m a? Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, M ŋuuri u saŋga bo a toko tana damba baka n bu n wɔ, nyini sin ye Filipu fereeri u n a.

eng: Nathanael replied, Rabbi, you are Gods Son. You are the king of Israel.
local: Na Nataniɛli ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ Wa n a, nahɔrɛ su. Wɔrɔ ti Yisareli­fɔm Fɛmɛ n a.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you believe because I told you that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these!.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Bo ma se u ka m ŋuuri u damba baka n bu n ti ye a yo m yarada, nzɛn sɛ? A bá ŋu alekutura sam bo bu tara sɔ.

eng: I assure you that you will see heaven open and Gods angels going up to heaven and down to earth on the Human One.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a. Am bá ŋu kabo aŋgoro bá teke na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm bá sufun kɔ aŋgoro, na bu bá sujura ba mini Adamande Wa n dɔ.

eng: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus mother was there, and.
local: Nyini sin, i le­san cɛɛn na konyaya be awo Kana, miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni. Na Yesu ni gusu akɔ konyaya n nu.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were also invited to the celebration.
local: Na ba fere Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n ka bu gusu ba di konyaya n be.

eng: When the wine ran out, Jesus mother said to him, They dont have any wine.
local: Saŋga bo konyaya­fɔm nzan n ba kpiɛɛri bu su ni, na Yesu ni aba ase i ka, Bu nzan n awie.

eng: Jesus replied, Woman, what does that have to do with me? My time hasnt come yet.
local: Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, M ni, ye a ŋa m yo sɛ wɔ? M saŋga nya ju má.

eng: His mother told the servants, Do whatever he tells you.
local: Na i ni ase awuru­nbaam n ka, Am yo deke kɛrɛ bo i se am.

eng: Nearby were six stone water jars used for the Jewish cleansing ritual, each able to hold about twenty or thirty gallons.
local: Nyini dika n nu, yabuɛ cuŋgu nsɛn jina bɛrɛ, bo i kun kun kɛrɛ koro de nzue gaŋga nnu wara nsɛn. Yuda­fɔm la bu yoo­wa be, bu fa nyini cuŋgum n nu nzue ni, ye bu bita ŋmisi bu fin a.

eng: Jesus said to the servants, Fill the jars with water, and they filled them to the brim.
local: Na Yesu ase awuru­nbaam n ka, Am sa nzue gɔ cuŋgum n nu na bu yi. Na ba gɔ i kɛrɛ nzue na ba yi.

eng: Then he told them, Now draw some from them and take it to the headwaiter, and they did.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, Kisa am sa be kɔ ma konyafan danfo ni. Na ba sa be akɔ ama i.

eng: The headwaiter tasted the water that had become wine. He didnt know where it came from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. The headwaiter called the groom.
local: Bu sa kɔ maari i ni, na nzue n akaaki nzan. Na wɔ fa akan i nɔɔ. Wɔ si má deke bo nyini nzan n fite. Ama, awuru­nbaam bo bu saari nzue ni, bɛrɛ si deke bo i fite. Na wɔ fere konyafan ni.

eng: and said, Everyone serves the good wine first. They bring out the second-rate wine only when the guests are drinking freely. You kept the good wine until now.
local: ase i ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nya ŋa bu ma nzan, bu du mɔ fa nzan fiɛfiɛ n ma jama n a ka, na ba nya aba anu abo ni, na bu nya fa bo i yo má fiɛ n ma bu, ama, wɔrɔ, a fa bo i yo fiɛ n aka sin a.

eng: This was the first miraculous sign that Jesus did in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.
local: Kabo Yesu maari nzue kaaki nzan ni, nyini tiiri i ciciri su dɛɛ alekutura sa. I yoori nyini sa n Kana, bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu n wɔ, na i kere kabo i la yiko n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na i susu­fɔm n ayo i yarada.

eng: After this, Jesus and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples went down to Capernaum and stayed there for a few days.
local: Nyini sin, na i ni i ni, ni i niɛmam, ni i susu­fɔm n akɔ miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Kapɛnam, na ba kɔ ayo le­nnyɔ dɔ.

eng: It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Wɔ ka kaan na Yuda­fɔm di bu cɛɛn­kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni. Na Yesu akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: He found in the temple those who were selling cattle, sheep, and doves, as well as those involved in exchanging currency sitting there.
local: Na wɔ kɔ awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu aŋu ka minɛ wo dɔ sutɔ naanim, ni nbɔɛm, ni bumbuku tiiwam, na ŋwaa kaaki­fɔm gusu tana dɔ.

eng: He made a whip from ropes and chased them all out of the temple, including the cattle and the sheep. He scattered the coins and overturned the tables of those who exchanged currency.
local: Na wɔ fa nyama aŋɔ i ka ŋgbere dɛɛ su, na wɔ fa afɔn ayi nbɔɛm n ni naanim n kɛrɛ afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Na wɔ sandi ŋwaa kaaki­fɔm ŋwaa n kɛrɛ abutu, na wɔ nya akaaki bu teebirim kɛrɛ alanlaŋga.

eng: He said to the dove sellers, Get these things out of here! Dont make my Fathers house a place of business.
local: Na wɔ se minɛm bo bu tɔ bumbuku tiiwam n ka, Am fa am bumbukum n fite fɛn. Má am se ka am fa m Si Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n yo gɔ kun.

eng: His disciples remembered that it is written, Passion for your house consumes me.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n aba ateŋge su ka ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, M Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ, m nyunu aboro u awuru n jɔrɛ su, nyini kún m a.

eng: Then the Jewish leaders asked him, By what authority are you doing these things? What miraculous sign will you show us.
local: Na Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛnm abisa i ka, A yó nzu alekutura sa bo i kere ya ka a la atin bo a yo sɔ a.

eng: Jesus answered, Destroy this temple and in three days Ill raise it up.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am bo fufu ahin Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni, na le­san afiɛn m káaki ta i bekun.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, It took forty-six years to build this temple, and you will raise it up in three days.
local: Na ba se i ka, Afɔ abura­na ni nsɛn ye bu fa taari ahin Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n a. I yo sɛ ye wɔrɔ ba se ka a káaki ta i le­san afiɛn a.

eng: But the temple Jesus was talking about was his body.
local: I yo kaanri sɔ ni, i faari i ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni fa sunsun Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n a.

eng: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered what he had said, and they believed the scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
local: Na saŋga bo i ba wu teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni, na i susu­fɔm n ateŋge su ka i yo seeri sɔ, na ba de nɔaniɛ n bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, na ba yo Yesu jɔjɔɔ­wa n yarada.

eng: While Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Passover Festival, many believed in his name because they saw the miraculous signs that he did.
local: Saŋga bo i wo Yɛrusalem na bu sudi Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni, i yoori alekutura sam, na minɛ dɔŋgu aŋu i, na ba yo i yarada.

eng: But Jesus didnt trust himself to them because he knew all people.
local: Ama, yiri afa má i ŋu agɔ má bu ŋu, dama i si kabo adamande woo­wa ti.

eng: He didnt need anyone to tell him about human nature, for he knew what human nature was.
local: Má pui ti, yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii si kabo adamande ahore su ti barasu, má be se i a.

eng: There was a Pharisee named Nicodemus, a Jewish leader.
local: Cɛɛn be kɔŋguɛ sɔ ɔ, na Yuda­fɔm kpiɛn be aba Yesu dɔ, bu fere i ka Nikodemo. I ti Farasi­fɔm ni kun be wɔ. Na wɔ se Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ya si ka a ti kere­fɔ n bo a fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, dama be koro yo má alekutura sa bo a yo bu ni, má ka Nyɛmɛ wo i mi dɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born anew, its not possible to see Gods kingdom.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u a. Sɔnɔ koro fa má Nyɛmɛ yo má i fɛmɛ, má ka ba wu i fɔfɔrɛ.

eng: Nicodemus asked, How is it possible for an adult to be born? Its impossible to enter the mothers womb for a second time and be born, isnt it.
local: Na Nikodemo abisa i ka, Sɔnɔ nyunu ati, bu yo sɛ koro kaaki wu i fɔfɔrɛ a? I koro kaaki wura i ni kunnu na bu wu i bekun.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, its not possible to enter Gods kingdom.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u a, sɔnɔ koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, má ka ba wu i fɔfɔrɛ fa kɔ nzue biɛɛ­wa ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ atin n su.

eng: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.
local: Deke bo adamande awu i, i ti adamande wɔ, na deke bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awu i, i ti awiɛwiɛ wɔ.

eng: Dont be surprised that I said to you, You must be born anew.
local: Má a ma i yo u alekutura fa kɔ kabo ma se u ka wɔ yo daka ka bu wu am fɔfɔrɛ ni.

eng: Gods Spirit blows wherever it wishes. You hear its sound, but you dont know where it comes from or where it is going. Its the same with everyone who is born of the Spirit.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ a ti ka aŋuma kpiɛ kɔ ɔ, ye a ti i naŋmiɛ ŋga a, ama, a si má deke bo i fite, na a kaaki si má deke bo i sukɔ, nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awu i ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: Nicodemus said, How are these things possible.
local: Na Nikodemo abisa Yesu ka, I yo sɛ ye ahin koro yo a.

eng: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel and you dont know these things?.
local: Na Yesu akaaki abisa i ka, Wɔrɔ bo a kere Yisareli­fɔm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na bu nyunu yi u ni, i yo sɛ ye a si má ahin n a?.

eng: I assure you that we speak about what we know and testify about what we have seen, but you dont receive our testimony.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u a, sa bo ya si i, ye ya jɔjɔ i jɔrɛ a, na sa bo ya nyu­mbaa aŋu i, ye ya di i daani kere minɛm a, ama, am akete ya daani dii­wa ni.

eng: If I have told you about earthly things and you dont believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?.
local: M suse am sam bo bu wo durunya n nu fɛn wɔ lɛɛ, ama, am sude má i, ye m ba seeri am sam bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, am yó sɛ de i a?.

eng: No one has gone up to heaven except the one who came down from heaven, the Human One.
local: Be nya fun má Nyɛmɛ dɔ, má ka mini Adamande Wa n bo m woori i dɔ na ma jura aba ni.

eng: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so must the Human One be lifted up.
local: Kabo Moyisi yo faari jawii yoori i ka woo dɛɛ su n na i fa i bara yɛɛri baka n na i yɛɛri i su kpamaari boro kpantain n nu ni, wɔ yo daka ka sɔ ye minɛm bá yɛ mini Adamande Wa n su a.

eng: so that everyone who believes in him will have eternal life.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori m yarada, i nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who believes in him wont perish but will have eternal life.
local: Nyɛmɛ akoro durunya n nu­fɔm agaya n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ fa i kunnu Wa kun n kpein n afa ama bu, adena sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo i yarada, i mi ká má ŋgbɛɛn, ama, i nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: God didnt send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world might be saved through him.
local: Nyɛmɛ asunma má i Wa n durunya n nu ka i ba di bu jɔrɛ cin bu sui wɔ, ama, wɔ sunma i ka i ba de bu tii ji wɔ.

eng: Whoever believes in him isnt judged; whoever doesnt believe in him is already judged, because they dont believe in the name of Gods only Son.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i daafiiri Nyɛmɛ Wa n ŋu, Nyɛmɛ dí má i mi n jɔrɛ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ da má i ŋu, Nyɛmɛ adu mɔ adi i jɔrɛ ka, dama ka wɔ da má Nyɛmɛ Wa kun n kpein n ŋu n dɛɛ ti.

eng: This is the basis for judgment: The light came into the world, and people loved darkness more than the light, for their actions are evil.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ di bu jɔrɛ n la ka, wein n aba durunya n nu­fɔm dɔ, ama, bu satiɛ yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti, ba koro awosin n atara wein ni.

eng: All who do wicked things hate the light and dont come to the light for fear that their actions will be exposed to the light.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ kpɔ wein n wɔ, i koro má ka i kɔ wein n nu, na i satiɛm fite fofoe nu.

eng: Whoever does the truth comes to the light so that it can be seen that their actions were done in God.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nahɔrɛ atin su, i fite fofoe nu wɔ, na minɛ kɛrɛ ŋu ka i sa­yoo­wa n su Nyɛmɛ atin n su wɔ.

eng: After this Jesus and his disciples went into Judea, where he spent some time with them and was baptizing.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n akɔ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu. Na ba kɔ adi le­nnyɔ dɔ, na i subiɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue.

eng: John was baptizing at Aenon near Salem because there was a lot of water there, and people were coming to him and being baptized. .
local: Nyini saŋga n na Yohane gusu subiɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue. I wo Ayinon miɛ n nu fa dodo Salim awɔrɛ n nu, dama nzue sunnu dɔ n dɛɛ ti. Na minɛm suba i dɔ na i subiɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue.

eng: John hadnt yet been thrown into prison.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, ba nya anyi má Yohane dan­saraka.

eng: A debate started between Johns disciples and a certain Jew about cleansing rituals.
local: Na Yohane susu­fɔm ni Yuda­fɔ be awura akuruwa fa kɔ bu fin kpara n dɛɛ yoo­wa atin su.

eng: They came to John and said, Rabbi, look! The man who was with you across the Jordan, the one about whom you testified, is baptizing and everyone is flocking to him.
local: Na ba kɔ Yohane dɔ akɔ ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, niɛ. Sɔnɔ n bo i yo baari u dɔ Yodan kunma n wiɛ afiiri n na a yo diiri i ŋu daani kereeri minɛ ni, yiri wo dɔ subiɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue n a, bu kɛrɛ sukɔ i dɔ ɔ.

eng: John replied, No one can receive anything unless it is given from heaven.
local: Na Yohane ase bu ka, Sɔnɔ koro nya má deke be, má ka Nyɛmɛ afa nyini deke n ama i.

eng: You yourselves can testify that I said that Im not the Christ but that Im the one sent before him.
local: Ambɛrɛ ni am tii woori m daani ka m ŋa má mini ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a, ama, Nyɛmɛ sunmaari m ka m du i mɔ ɔ.

eng: The groom is the one who is getting married. The friend of the groom stands close by and, when he hears him, is overjoyed at the grooms voice. Therefore, my joy is now complete.
local: Bu nya jaari konya, i ti konyafan n dɛɛ wɔ, ama, na konyafan n danfo jina suso i sui ti deke bo konyafan ni jɔjɔ se i, nyini saŋga na i danfo n ahore jɔ. Nyini ahorejɔ kun ni, wo mini gusu dɔ a.

eng: He must increase and I must decrease.
local: Wɔ yo daka ka i dɛɛ duma n fun, na m dɛɛ n kpiɛ su.

eng: The one who comes from above is above all things. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all things.
local: Yiri bo i fite aŋgoro n tara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ. Na bo i fite durunya n nu fɛn ti durunya n nu fɛn fɔ ɔ, na i jɔjɔɔ­wa kɛrɛ ti durunya n nu fɛn jɔrɛ wɔ. Ama, yiri n bo i fite aŋgoro n tara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

eng: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
local: Deke bo wɔ ŋu i, ni bo wɔ ti i, ye i di i daani kere minɛ a, ama, be de má i daani dii­wa ni.

eng: Whoever accepts his testimony confirms that God is true.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i deeri i daani dii­wa n asoro su akere ka Nyɛmɛ ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ lɛɛ.

eng: The one whom God sent speaks Gods words because God gives the Spirit generously.
local: Dama yiri n bo Nyɛmɛ asunma i ni, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ye i kan i a, dama Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama i bo i la má aŋusu.

eng: The Father loves the Son and gives everything into his hands.
local: Nyɛmɛ koro i Wa ni, na wɔ fa deke n kɛrɛ awura i saa nu.

eng: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever doesnt believe in the Son wont see life, but the angry judgment of God remains on them.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i daari Nyɛmɛ Wa n ŋu, i la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kete i nɔaniɛ ni, i nyá má ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, ama, Nyɛmɛ fá yaa cin i sui.

eng: Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that he was making more disciples and baptizing more than John .
local: Tɔ. Na Farasi­fɔm n ati ka Yesu sunya minɛm dɔŋgu bo bu susu i su na i biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue tara Yohane. Ama, má Yesu ni i tii biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue n a, i susu­fɔm ni biɛ bu a. Saŋga bo Yesu faari nu sɔ ni.

eng: Therefore, he left Judea and went back to Galilee.
local: na wɔ yasu Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu akaaki ka i kɔ Galili dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu bekun.

eng: Jesus had to go through Samaria.
local: I sukɔ ni, wɔ yo daka ka i fa Samariya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu sin a.

eng: He came to a Samaritan city called Sychar, which was near the land Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
local: Na wɔ kɔ aju Samariya miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Suka n su, na i wo dodo asiɛn n bo Jakɔbu yo fa maari i wa Yosefu dawa saŋga ni.

eng: Jacobs well was there. Jesus was tired from his journey, so he sat down at the well. It was about noon.
local: Jakɔbu bura n wo nyini dika n nu wɔ. Natiiri fie n dɛɛ ti, na Yesu akɔ atana bura n su. Nyini saŋga wiɛ abi kenken.

eng: A Samaritan woman came to the well to draw water. Jesus said to her, Give me some water to drink.
local: Na Samariya bara be aba ka i ba sa nzue, na Yesu ase i ka, Ma m nzue n be na m nu.

eng: His disciples had gone into the city to buy him some food.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, i susu­fɔm n akɔ miɛ kunnu ka bu kɔ to diire.

eng: The Samaritan woman asked, Why do you, a Jewish man, ask for something to drink from me, a Samaritan woman.
local: Na bara n ase i ka, Ee, wɔrɔ, a ti Yuda­fɔ e, ye m gusu ti m Samariya­fɔ bara. Nzu ti ye a se m ka m ma u nzue a? Dama Yuda­fɔm ni Samariya­fɔm tɔ má pui.

eng: Jesus responded, If you recognized Gods gift and who is saying to you, Give me some water to drink, you would be asking him and he would give you living water.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nzɛn má a si Nyɛmɛ aceeri ni, na má a si sɔnɔ n bo i susere u nzue ni, má wɔrɔ sere i a, na i má u nzue bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, you dont have a bucket and the well is deep. Where would you get this living water?.
local: Na bara n ase i ka, Ya si, bura n wo ŋgo, u gusu la má goroka, ye a nya nyini nzue n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n ni wɔ?.

eng: You arent greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave this well to us, and he drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.
local: Wɔrɔ, ŋka a ŋa a tara ya na Jakɔbu n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Yiri maari ya nyini bura n a. I ni i nbaam fa saŋga ni i neŋge­ndɔrɛm kɛrɛ yo nuuri nyini bura n nu wɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nu ahin nzue ni, nzuehɔɛ tí i mi bekun.

eng: but whoever drinks from the water that I will give will never be thirsty again. The water that I give will become in those who drink it a spring of water that bubbles up into eternal life.
local: ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nu nzue n bo m má i ni, nzuehɔɛ tí má i mi kun fieo, dama nzue n bo m má i ni, i wó i kunnu tiɛ kabo bura nzue tiɛ n wɔ, na i má i ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: The woman said to him, Sir, give me this water, so that I will never be thirsty and will never need to come here to draw water.
local: Na bara n ase i ka, Ya si, ma m nyini nzue n be na m nu, adena má nzuehɔɛ ti m kun, na má m kaaki ba sa nzue fɛn kun.

eng: Jesus said to her, Go, get your husband, and come back here.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Kɔ fere u hun na am bra.

eng: The woman replied, I dont have a husband. You are right to say, I dont have a husband, Jesus answered.
local: Na bara n ase Yesu ka, M la má hun. Na Yesu ase i ka, Kabo a ŋa a la má hun ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: Youve had five husbands, and the man you are with now isnt your husband. Youve spoken the truth.
local: Dama nbiɛsɔm nnu ye a fite bu ajaa bu n a, na haari bo u ni yiri wo bɛrɛ kisa mmɔɔ ni, i ti má u hun. A se nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: The woman said, Sir, I see that you are a prophet.
local: Na bara n ase i ka, Ya si, ma ŋu ka a ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ.

eng: Our ancestors worshipped on this mountain, but you and your people say that it is necessary to worship in Jerusalem.
local: Ya na Samariya­fɔm yo suuri Nyɛmɛ ahin buka n su wɔ. Ama, ambɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm ŋa wɔ yo daka ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ su Nyɛmɛ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Believe me, woman, the time is coming when you and your people will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Bara, de bo m suse u ni, saŋga suba bo am sú má ya Si Nyɛmɛ ahin buka n su, wara Yɛrusalem miɛ n nu.

eng: You and your people worship what you dont know; we worship what we know because salvation is from the Jews.
local: Ambɛrɛ Samariya­fɔm ŋa am su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, ama, am si má i. Ama, yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm si i, ye ya su i a. Dama tii­de n fite Yuda­fɔm dɔ ɔ.

eng: But the time is coming—and is here!—when true worshippers will worship in spirit and truth. The Father looks for those who worship him this way.
local: Saŋga suba bo wɔ ju awie, na bo bu su ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni, i Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni búka bu ye bu sú i ni nahɔrɛ su a. Dama nyini minɛm ŋusu ye ya Si Nyɛmɛ koro ka bu su i a.

eng: God is spirit, and it is necessary to worship God in spirit and truth.
local: Nyɛmɛ ti awiɛwiɛ wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, bo bu su i ni, wɔ yo daka ka i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n búka bu na bu sú i ni nahɔrɛ su.

eng: The woman said, I know that the Messiah is coming, the one who is called the Christ. When he comes, he will teach everything to us.
local: Na bara n ase i ka, Tɔ, m si ka Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni bá. I ba juuri, i kán ya deke kɛrɛ kere ya.

eng: Jesus said to her, I Am—the one who speaks with you.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Mini bo m sujɔjɔ u dɔ ni, mini lɛɛ.

eng: Just then, Jesus disciples arrived and were shocked that he was talking with a woman. But no one asked, What do you want? or Why are you talking with her.
local: Nyini saŋga n nu na Yesu susu­fɔm n afite miɛ n kunnu aju, na jɔrɛ n amiɛ bu kabo Yesu ni bara n wo jɔjɔ su ni. Ama, bu be abisa má bara n ka, A koro nzu i dɔ a? Be gusu abisa má Yesu ka, Nzu ti ye a sujɔjɔ bara n dɔ a.

eng: The woman put down her water jar and went into the city. She said to the people.
local: Na bara n ayaki i dundu n bɛrɛ, na wɔ kpie i nyi akɔ miɛ n kunnu akɔ ase minɛm ka.

eng: Come and see a man who has told me everything Ive done! Could this man be the Christ.
local: Am ba niɛ sɔnɔ be bo wɔ se m m tii su sa kɛrɛ bo ma yo i. Beni, kisa, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n lɛɛ fan.

eng: They left the city and were on their way to see Jesus.
local: Na minɛm n afite miɛ n nu, na bu kɔ Yesu dɔ.

eng: In the meantime the disciples spoke to Jesus, saying, Rabbi, eat.
local: Bara n kɔɔri n dɛɛ sin, na Yesu susu­fɔm n asere i ka, Jande, ya Kere­fɔ, di diire.

eng: Jesus said to them, I have food to eat that you dont know about.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, M la diire bo m di bo am si má i.

eng: The disciples asked each other, Has someone brought him food.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Be afa diire aba ama i wɔ di wɔ, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, I am fed by doing the will of the one who sent me and by completing his work.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, M diire n la ka, m yo yiri bo wɔ sunma m dɛɛ koroo­wa na m yó i juma n wie.

eng: Dont you have a saying, Four more months and then its time for harvest? Look, I tell you: open your eyes and notice that the fields are already ripe for the harvest.
local: Na wɔ bo bu anyundɛrɛ ase ka, Am ŋa wɔ ka sara nna na am tambu fiesu neŋgem. Ama, mini suse am wɔ lɛɛ, am tu am tii na am niɛ, fiesu diire n abi ni kpiɛ.

eng: Those who harvest are receiving their pay and gathering fruit for eternal life so that those who sow and those who harvest can celebrate together.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i kpiɛ diire ni, i nya i ŋkatɔ, i ti ka sɔnɔ bo i tiɛn minɛ nɔɔ, na bu nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni, dɔ­fɔ ni kpiɛ­fɔ n kɛrɛ nya ahorejɔ.

eng: This is a true saying, that one sows and another harvests.
local: Be bu ŋa Be dɔ, na be kpiɛ ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: I have sent you to harvest what you didnt work hard for; others worked hard, and you will share in their hard work.
local: Ambɛrɛ yiri, mini asunma am a, ka am kɔ kpiɛ diire n bo am adɔ má i ni. I ti ka nbem adu mɔ akan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n akere minɛm, ye am dɛɛ la ka am ma bu yo m yarada. Nbem dɛɛ juma­yoo­wa ye am sudi i kpɛrɛ n a.

eng: Many Samaritans in that city believed in Jesus because of the womans word when she testified, He told me everything Ive ever done.
local: Bara n seeri minɛm bo bu fite miɛ n nu n kabo Yesu ase i tii su sa kɛrɛ n dɛɛ ti, na Samariya­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu fite miɛ n nu n ada Yesu ŋu.

eng: So when the Samaritans came to Jesus, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
local: Nyini ti, bu ba juuri Yesu dɔ ni, na ba sere i ka i ka bu dɔ, na wɔ kɔ awo bu dɔ cɛɛn nnyɔ.

eng: Many more believed because of his word.
local: Na minɛ dɔŋgu ada i ŋu abuka su fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n ti.

eng: and they said to the woman, We no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves and know that this one is truly the savior of the world.
local: Na ba se bara n ka, Kisa, má u dɛɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n ŋgumi ti ye ya da i ŋu a, ama, yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii ati i nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa, na ya si nu ka ahin sɔnɔ n ti bo i baari ka i ba de durunya n nu­fɔm tii wɔ, nahɔrɛ su.

eng: After two days Jesus left for Galilee. .
local: Yesu woori bu dɔ cɛɛn nnyɔ n sin, na wɔ yasu bɛrɛ akɔ Galili dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: Jesus himself had testified that prophets have no honor in their own country.
local: Dama yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii adu mɔ akan ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ la má jirima i tiɛŋgu bɔsu.

eng: When he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen all the things he had done in Jerusalem during the festival, for they also had been at the festival.
local: Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri Galili ni, na ba de i ni bu sa nnyɔ, dama bu yo ŋuuri kabo i yoori alekutura sam cɛɛn­kpiri n saŋga, Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, dama bɛrɛ mmɔɔ yo kɔɔri nyini cɛɛn­kpiri n di ti.

eng: He returned to Cana in Galilee where he had turned the water into wine. In Capernaum there was a certain royal official whose son was sick.
local: Na Yesu akaaki akɔ Kana, miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu ni, deke bo i yo maari nzue kaakiiri nzan ni. Fɛmɛ n kpiɛn be woori dɔ. I wa be la má laifiɛ, i wo Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: When he heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went out to meet him and asked Jesus if he would come and heal his son, for his son was about to die.
local: Na saŋga bo kpiɛn n tiiri ka Yesu afite Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu akɔ aju Galili ni, na wɔ kɔ Yesu dɔ akɔ asere i ka i wa suwu, i ma bu kɔ na i kɔ tuntun i, na i nya laifiɛ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Unless you see miraculous signs and wonders, you wont believe.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Ma ŋu ka nzɛn am aŋu má alekutura sam, am yo má m yarada.

eng: The royal official said to him, Lord, come before my son dies.
local: Na kpiɛn n ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, bra ndende, má nyini, a cɛɛri, a bá ŋu ka m wa n awu.

eng: Jesus replied, Go home. Your son lives. The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and set out for his home.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Kɔ. U wa n anya laifiɛ. Na wɔ de Yesu nɔaniɛ ni, na wɔ kaaki akɔ.

eng: While he was on his way, his servants were already coming to meet him. They said, Your son lives.
local: Na saŋga bo i sukɔ awuru ni, na i akɔɔm akpa i atin aba ase i ka, U wa n anya laifiɛ.

eng: So he asked them at what time his son had started to get better. And they said, The fever left him yesterday at about one oclock in the afternoon.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Saŋga bɔnɔ ye i ŋuniɛ n jɔɔri a? Na ba se i ka, Anuma wiɛsu kerefi kun saŋga ye i ŋuniɛ n jɔɔri a.

eng: Then the father realized that this was the hour when Jesus had said to him, Your son lives. And he and his entire household believed in Jesus.
local: Dɔ na baa n si n ateŋge su ka, nyini saŋga n nu lɛɛ, ye Yesu seeri i ka i wa n anya laifiɛ n a. Na i ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ ada Yesu ŋu.

eng: This was the second miraculous sign Jesus did while going from Judea to Galilee.
local: Kabo Yesu ma baa n nyaari laifiɛ ni, nyini tiiri i alekutura sa nnyɔ su bo i yoori saŋga bo i fite Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu kɔɔri Galili n a, na i kere kabo i la yiko n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: After this there was a Jewish festival, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu na i kɔ di Yuda­fɔm cɛɛn­kpiri be.

eng: In Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate in the north city wall is a pool with the Aramaic name Bethsaida. It had five covered porches.
local: Kunma be da dodoori anɔ n bo bu fa nbɔɛm fa sin wura miɛ n nu ni. Hiburu aniɛ nu bu fere nyini kunma n ka Besata, i la kpatam nnu wɔ.

eng: and a crowd of people who were sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed sat there.
local: Landuri­fɔm beberebe dada kpatam n nu, bu nu nbem ti anyinsinm, nbem ti kujanda­fɔm, na bu nbem gusu ŋuniɛ buekun awu bo bu koro yasu má. .

eng: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.
local: Sɔnɔ be woori bu nu bo wɔ da tukpaki i afɔ abura­san ni mɔcuɛ lɛɛ.

eng: When Jesus saw him lying there, knowing that he had already been there a long time, he asked him, Do you want to get well.
local: Yesu ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na wɔ fa nu ka sɔnɔ n tukpaki n acɛ i ŋu, na wɔ bisa i ka, A koro ka a nya laifiɛ wɔ.

eng: The sick man answered him, Sir, I dont have anyone who can put me in the water when it is stirred up. When Im trying to get to it, someone else has gotten in ahead of me.
local: Na tukpaki­fɔ n ase Yesu ka, M Mibiɛ, m la má buka­fɔ be bo nzue n nya ba kankan, na i kuku m wura nu. Saŋga kɛrɛ bo nzue n nya ba kankan, na m ba ŋa m wura nu, a ŋu ka be adu m mɔ.

eng: Jesus said to him, Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Yasu, fa u dasu n na a nati.

eng: Immediately the man was well, and he picked up his mat and walked. Now that day was the Sabbath.
local: Jinaa­biri n nu bɛrɛ na sɔnɔ n anya laifiɛ, na wɔ yasu afa i dasu n sunati. Nyini cɛɛn n bo Yesu tuntuunri sɔnɔ n ti Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wɔ.

eng: The Jewish leaders said to the man who had been healed, Its the Sabbath; you arent allowed to carry your mat.
local: Nyini ti na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ase sɔnɔ n bo wɔ nya laifiɛ n ka, Nyuma ti Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wɔ. Ya dɛɛ atin n su, atin wo má nu bo a sɔ u dasu ni.

eng: He answered, The man who made me well said to me, Pick up your mat and walk.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Sɔnɔ n bo i tuntuunri m ni, yiri seeri m ka m fa m dasu na m nati a.

eng: They inquired, Who is this man who said to you, Pick it up and walk.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, Nyini sɔnɔ n la ŋma a, ye i seeri u ka a fa u dasu nati a.

eng: The man who had been cured didnt know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away from the crowd gathered there.
local: Ama, na sɔnɔ n bo wɔ nya laifiɛ n akoro asi má i mi n bo i tuntuunri i ni, dama Yesu awura jama n nu akɔ.

eng: Later Jesus found him in the temple and said, See! You have been made well. Dont sin anymore in case something worse happens to you.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu aba aŋu i, Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ se i ka, Ti fɛn. Kisa a nya laifiɛ, má a yo satiɛ kun. Má nyini, a dí wahara bo i tára nyini n ŋusu.

eng: The man went and proclaimed to the Jewish leaders that Jesus was the man who had made him well.
local: Na sɔnɔ n akɔ ase Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni ka Yesu tuntuunri i a.

eng: As a result, the Jewish leaders were harassing Jesus, since he had done these things on the Sabbath.
local: Nyini ti, na ba kere Yesu wuwuruwa, dama wɔ tuntun sɔnɔ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Jesus replied, My Father is still working, and I am working too.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, M Si toko yo juma haari ba ju ni nyuma, nyini ti ye m gusu yo a.

eng: For this reason the Jewish leaders wanted even more to kill him—not only because he was doing away with the Sabbath but also because he called God his own Father, thereby making himself equal with God.
local: Yesu jɔjɔɔri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n aboro bu nyi abuka su ka bu kun i. Má ka bo wɔ saaki Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ciire n ŋgumi dɛɛ ti, ye bu kpi ka bu kun i a, ama, bo wɔ kaaki ase ka Nyɛmɛ ti i Si n dɛɛ ti. Nyini kere ka wɔ fa i ŋu abu Nyɛmɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus responded to the Jewish leaders, I assure you that the Son cant do anything by himself except what he sees the Father doing. Whatever the Father does, the Son does likewise.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, mini Nyɛmɛ Wa n ni m tii, m koro bo má m tiɛɛnu yo má pui, má ka deke bo m ŋu ka m Si yo i ni, ye mini gusu yo i a.

eng: The Father loves the Son and shows him everything that he does. He will show him greater works than these so that you will marvel.
local: Dama m Si Nyɛmɛ koro mini i Wa ni, na i sukere m deke kɛrɛ bo i yo i ni, na i bá kere m sakpa­kpam bo m yó bu tara sɔ, na i yó am alekutura.

eng: As the Father raises the dead and gives life, so too does the Son give life to whomever he wishes.
local: Kabo m Si Nyɛmɛ yɛ minɛm bo ba wu n su n na i ma bu ŋgɔɔ ni, sɔ ye mini gusu ma minɛm bo ma yi bu ni ŋgɔɔ a.

eng: The Father doesnt judge anyone, but he has given all judgment to the Son.
local: Dama m Si Nyɛmɛ ni i tii di má be jɔrɛ, ama, wɔ fa jɔrɛ­di atin kɛrɛ ama mini i Wa n wɔ.

eng: so that everyone will honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever doesnt honor the Son doesnt honor the Father who sent him.
local: adena sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bu m kabo bu bu m Si ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bu má m, i mi abu má m Si bo wɔ sunma m n lɛɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever hears my word and believes in the one who sent me has eternal life and wont come under judgment but has passed from death into life.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti m nɔaniɛ n na i de i, na i da Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ sunma m n ŋu, i mi la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri, na m dí má i mi jɔrɛ. I mi afite yiwee nu anya ŋgɔɔ lɛɛ.

eng: I assure you that the time is coming—and is here!—when the dead will hear the voice of Gods Son, and those who hear it will live.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m kaaki suse am a, ka saŋga suba bo wɔ ju awie, na minɛ bo ba wu ni tí mini Nyɛmɛ Wa n nɔaniɛ ni, na bo bu tiiri i na bu deeri i, bu nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Just as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
local: Dama kabo ŋgɔɔ fite yiri m Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, sɔ ye wɔ ma mini i Wa n gusu yiko ka m ma minɛm ŋgɔɔ a.

eng: He gives the Son authority to judge, because he is the Human One.
local: na wɔ kaaki ama m atin ka m di minɛ jɔrɛ, dama m ti Adamande Wa a.

eng: Dont be surprised by this, because the time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice.
local: Má am ma ahin jɔjɔɔ­wam n yo am alekutura. Saŋga suba bo minɛ bo ba wu n kɛrɛ tí m nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Those who did good things will come out into the resurrection of life, and those who did wicked things into the resurrection of judgment.
local: na bu téŋge bu yiwee n nu. Minɛm bo bu yo yoori sakpa ni, bu téŋge na bu nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri, na minɛm bo bu gusu yo yoori satiɛ ni, bu téŋge na bu nya sui­cin.

eng: I cant do anything by myself. Whatever I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just. I dont seek my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
local: Na Yesu asa su ase ka, M koro bo má m tiɛɛnu yo má pui. Kabo Nyɛmɛ se m ni, sɔ ye m di minɛm jɔrɛ a. Nyini ti, m jɔrɛ dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama m yo má m dɛɛ koroo­wa, ama, m yo yiri n bo wɔ sunma m n dɛɛ koroo­wa a.

eng: If I testify about myself, my testimony isnt true.
local: Nzɛn m di m ŋu daani kere am, am de má ka i ti nahɔrɛ.

eng: There is someone else who testifies about me, and I know his testimony about me is true.
local: ama, be wo bɛrɛ bo i di m ŋu daani. Yiri la m Si Nyɛmɛ. Na m si ka i daani dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: You sent a delegation to John, and he testified to the truth.
local: Am yo sunmaari minɛm Yohane dɔ, na wɔ se am nahɔrɛ, kabo m ti barasu.

eng: Although I dont accept human testimony, I say these things so that you can be saved.
local: M de má adamande daani dii­wa, ama, m kan kabo Yohane seeri am ni, adena am nya am tii.

eng: John was a burning and shining lamp, and, at least for a while, you were willing to celebrate in his light.
local: Yohane yo ti ka fitana bo i je na i ta a, na ambɛrɛ ayo asoro su ka am wo i wein nu ni ahorejɔ fa kɔ saŋga su.

eng: I have a witness greater than Johns testimony. The Father has given me works to do so that I might complete them. These works I do testify about me that the Father sent me.
local: Ama, m la deke bo i di m ŋu daani bo i tara Yohane dɛɛ ni, yiri la sam bo m Si Nyɛmɛ akere m ka m yo bu wie ni. Nyini sam ni di m ŋu daani ka m Si asunma m a.

eng: And the Father who sent me testifies about me. You have never even heard his voice or seen his form.
local: Na m Si bo wɔ sunma m ni, yiri gusu adi m ŋu daani a. Am nya ti má i konvi, am kaaki nya ŋu má i kabo i ti barasu.

eng: and you dont have his word dwelling with you because you dont believe the one whom he has sent.
local: na i nɔaniɛ wo má am ahore su, dama wɔ sunma m am dɔ, na am ada má m ŋu.

eng: Examine the scriptures, since you think that in them you have eternal life. They also testify about me.
local: Am kan Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n wɔ, na am fiiti i nu, dama am sunsun ka nyini kere am atin bo am fa na am nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri a, ama, nyini kɛrɛɛ­wam nu di m ŋu daani a.

eng: yet you dont want to come to me so that you can have life.
local: Haari ni nyini n mmɔɔ am akete ka am ba má m dɔ na am ba nya ŋgɔɔ.

eng: I dont accept praise from people.
local: M koro má kansi adamande dɔ.

eng: but I know you, that you dont have Gods love in you.
local: M si am sa, na m si ka, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koro n wo má am ahore su.

eng: I have come in my Fathers name, and you dont receive me. If others come in their own name, you receive them.
local: Mini ade m Si jinaa­biri aba, ye am ade má m, ama, nzɛn be baari ni i tiɛŋgu ba, am dé yiri wɔ.

eng: How can you believe when you receive praise from each other but dont seek the praise that comes from the only God?.
local: Ambɛrɛ, am koro am ŋuŋu nu kansi wɔ, na bu susu am, ama, am koro má kansi yiri n ŋgumi bo i ti Nyɛmɛ n dɔ, nyini ti, i yo sɛ ye am koro da m ŋu a?.

eng: Dont think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, the one in whom your hope rests.
local: Má am sunsun ka mini kɔ́ jaraki am m Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ a. Am jaraki­fɔ la Moyisi, bo am la tama ka yiri búka am ni.

eng: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, because Moses wrote about me.
local: Moyisi yo kɛrɛɛri m ŋusu jɔrɛ wɔ. Nyini ti, nzɛn má am de i nɔaniɛ ni, má am de m gusu dɛɛ ni.

eng: If you dont believe the writings of Moses, how will you believe my words.
local: Ama, am ade má kabo i yo kɛrɛɛri i ni, i yó sɛ ye am koro de bo m suse am n a.

eng: After this Jesus went across the Galilee Sea .
local: Nyini sin, na Yesu akpiɛ akɔ Galili kunma kpiri n bo bu kaaki fere i ka Tiberiya n buekun dɔ.

eng: A large crowd followed him, because they had seen the miraculous signs he had done among the sick.
local: Nyini saŋga, wɔ ka kaan, na Yuda­fɔm di bu Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni. Na jama beberebe asu i su, dama ba ŋu kabo wɔ yo alekutura sam atuntun tukpaki­fɔm na ba nya laifiɛ ni. Na Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n afun akɔ atana buka be su.

eng: Jesus looked up and saw the large crowd coming toward him. He asked Philip, Where will we buy food to feed these people.
local: Na Yesu atu i tii aniɛ aŋu ka jama beberebe suba i dɔ, na wɔ bisa Filipu ka, Ya nya diire nifansu wɔ, na ya to ma ahin minɛm kɛrɛ a.

eng: Jesus said this to test him, for he already knew what he was going to do.
local: Yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii si kabo i yó barasu, ama, i subo Filipu kunnu na i niɛ wɔ.

eng: Philip replied, More than a half years salary worth of food wouldnt be enough for each person to have even a little bit.
local: Na Filipu ase i ka, Haari nzɛn ya faari ŋwaa beberebe toori diire maari bu, i jú má bo bu kɛrɛ koro nya kan kan di.

eng: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peters brother, said.
local: Na i susu­fɔm kun bo bu fere i ka Andere, na i gɔrɔ la Simon Petoro n ase Yesu ka.

eng: A youth here has five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that for a crowd like this.
local: Gbɛfɛnɛ kaan be wo ya nu fɛn, i bita kpɔnɔ nnu ni jombaa nnyɔ, ama, i koro ju má ahin minɛm kɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus said, Have the people sit down. There was plenty of grass there. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
local: Wura mɔnɛ sunnu bɛrɛ, na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ka, Am ma minɛm kɛrɛ tantana wura n su. Na ba ma minɛm kɛrɛ atantana. Nbiɛsɔm ŋgumi kɔ yasuuri minɛ akpii nnu wɔ.

eng: Then Jesus took the bread. When he had given thanks, he distributed it to those who were sitting there. He did the same with the fish, each getting as much as they wanted.
local: Na Yesu afa kpɔnɔ n na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ kpakpaaki i nu ama minɛm bo bu tantana ni, na wɔ kaaki afa jombaa n ayo i sɔ, na bu kɛrɛ anya kabo bu koro.

eng: When they had plenty to eat, he said to his disciples, Gather up the leftover pieces, so that nothing will be wasted.
local: Bu kɛrɛ diiri na bu kunnu yiiri ni, na wɔ se i susu­fɔm n ka, Am tiɛntiɛn sinsin n bo ba di i na wɔ ka n nɔɔ. Má am ma be ka ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves that had been left over by those who had eaten.
local: Na ba tiɛntiɛn kpɔnɔ sinsin n bo ba di i na wɔ ka n nɔɔ, na wɔ yi kandiɛ buru ni nnyɔ.

eng: When the people saw that he had done a miraculous sign, they said, This is truly the prophet who is coming into the world.
local: Minɛm n ŋuuri nyini alekutura sa n bo Yesu yoori ni, na ba se ka, Nahɔrɛ, ahin sɔnɔ n ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n bo bu seeri ka i bá durunya n nu n a.

eng: Jesus understood that they were about to come and force him to be their king, so he took refuge again, alone on a mountain.
local: Yesu faari nu ka bu koro ka bu tara i faŋga, na bu fa i yo bu fɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ yasu afite bu nu yiri ŋgumi, akɔ buka n su.

eng: When evening came, Jesus disciples went down to the lake.
local: I naasɔ saŋga, na Yesu susu­fɔm n ajura akɔ kunma kpiri n nu.

eng: They got into a boat and were crossing the lake to Capernaum. It was already getting dark and Jesus hadnt come to them yet.
local: na ba kɔ awura lee nu ka bu kaaki kpiɛ kɔ Kapɛnam miɛ kpiri n nu. Ale aba asan kɛrɛ, na Yesu nya ba má bu dɔ.

eng: The water was getting rough because a strong wind was blowing.
local: na aŋuma sukpiɛ faŋga su, na nzue nyama gusu suyasu.

eng: When the wind had driven them out for about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He was approaching the boat and they were afraid.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, i susu­fɔm n wo nzue n su akɔ aju kunma kpiri n afiɛn, na ba ŋu Yesu ka i nati nzue n su suba dodo lee ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu.

eng: He said to them, I Am. Dont be afraid.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Mini a, má am ma sɛrɛ ti am.

eng: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and just then the boat reached the land where they had been heading.
local: Na ba ma wɔ wura lee n nu. Jinaa­biri n nu bɛrɛ, na ba ju deke bo bu sukɔ ni.

eng: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one boat had been there. They knew Jesus hadnt gone with his disciples, but that the disciples had gone alone.
local: Alecin na jama n bo bu kaari kunma kpiri n buekun dɔ n afa nu ka lee kun n kpein woori kunma n su anuma n a. Na ba si nu ka Yesu susu­fɔm n ŋgumi wuraari lee n nu kɔɔri a, ama, yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii awura má nu.

eng: Some boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they had eaten the bread over which the Lord had given thanks.
local: Na lee nbem afite miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Tiberiya n aba aju kunma n nɔɔ su, fa dodo deke bo Yesu sereeri Nyɛmɛ gɔɔri kpɔnɔ n su maari minɛm na bu diiri ni.

eng: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
local: Na jama n aniɛ, ba ŋu má Yesu ni i susu­fɔm n bɛrɛ, nyini ti, na ba wura leem n nu akɔ Kapɛnam kɔ suniɛniɛ Yesu busu.

eng: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, Rabbi, when did you get here.
local: Minɛm n kɔ ŋuuri Yesu kunma kpiri n buekun dɔ ni, na ba bisa i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, saŋga bɔnɔ ye a baari fɛn a.

eng: Jesus replied, I assure you that you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate all the food you wanted.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, má alekutura sam bo m yoori bu n ti ye am suniɛniɛ m busu a, ama, kabo m maari am diire na am kunnu ayi n dɛɛ ti a.

eng: Dont work for the food that doesnt last but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Human One will give you. God the Father has confirmed him as his agent to give life.
local: Má am ma am nyunu boro diire bo i saaki n busu, ama, am ma am nyunu boro diire bo i wie má, na i ma ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n busu. Mini Adamande Wa ni má am nyini diire n a, dama mini ŋgumi, ye m Si Nyɛmɛ ase m nyaari akere ka m ti i dɛɛ a.

eng: They asked, What must we do in order to accomplish what God requires.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, Ya yo sɛ koro yo juma n bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka ya yo i n a.

eng: Jesus replied, This is what God requires, that you believe in him whom God sent.
local: Na Yesu abɔ bu nu ka, Juma n bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka am yo i n la ka, am yo mini bo wɔ sunma m yarada.

eng: They asked, What miraculous sign will you do, that we can see and believe you? What will you do?.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, Ye a yó nzu alekutura sa kere ya, na ya ŋu i na ya yo u yarada? A yó sa bɔnɔ ɔ?.

eng: Our ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat .
local: Dama saŋga bo ya nam yo woori boro kpantain nu ni, bu diiri diire be bo bu fere i ka mana n wɔ. Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu lɛɛ, ka yiri maari bu diire n bo i fite aŋgoro ye bu diiri a.

eng: Jesus told them, I assure you, it wasnt Moses who gave the bread from heaven to you, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, má Moyisi yo maari nyini diire n bo i fite aŋgoro n a, ama, m Si Nyɛmɛ maari am diire n bo i fite aŋgoro nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: The bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.
local: Dama diire n bo Nyɛmɛ ma n la bo i fite aŋgoro, na i ba ma durunya n nu­fɔm ŋgɔɔ ni.

eng: They said, Sir, give us this bread all the time.
local: Na ba se i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, ŋka a ma ya nyini diire n saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Mini ti diire n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ba m dɔ, ahɔɛ ti má i mi, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, nzuehɔɛ ti má i mi kun.

eng: But I told you that you have seen me and still dont believe.
local: Ama, ma se am, haari ni bo am aŋu m n kɛrɛ, am suyo má m yarada.

eng: Everyone whom the Father gives to me will come to me, and I wont send away anyone who comes to me.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo m Si fa i maari m, i bá m dɔ, na wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a ba m dɔ, m kéte má u fieo.

eng: I have come down from heaven not to do my will, but the will of him who sent me.
local: Dama ma fite má aŋgoro aba má ka m ba yo m dɛɛ koroo­wa, ama, m baari ka m ba yo Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ sunma m dɛɛ koroo­wa a.

eng: This is the will of the one who sent me, that I wont lose anything he has given me, but I will raise it up at the last day.
local: Yiri n bo wɔ sunma m dɛɛ koroo­wa la ka, minɛm kɛrɛ bo wɔ fa ama m ni, má m ma bu be mmɔɔ mini, ama, i koro ka m teŋge bu kɛrɛ jina cɛɛn na bu nya ŋgɔɔ.

eng: This is my Fathers will: that all who see the Son and believe in him will have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Dama m Si koroo­wa la ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋu mini i Wa n na i yo m yarada, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri, na m téŋge i jina cɛɛn.

eng: The Jewish opposition grumbled about him because he said, I am the bread that came down from heaven.
local: I seeri ka yiri ti diire n bo wɔ fite aŋgoro aba n dɛɛ ti, na minɛm ni suŋuŋuti i.

eng: They asked, Isnt this Jesus, Josephs son, whose mother and father we know? How can he now say, I have come down from heaven.
local: Na ba se ka, Má Yosefu wa Yesu n lɛɛ? Ya si i si ni i ni kɛrɛ e. Ye i baari sɛ, ye i ŋa i fiteeri aŋgoro baari a.

eng: Jesus responded, Dont grumble among yourselves.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Má am ŋuŋuti am ŋuŋu nu.

eng: No one can come to me unless they are drawn to me by the Father who sent me, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Be koro ba má m dɔ, má ka m Si Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ sunma m ni, acin i mi adodo m, na m téŋge i mi jina cɛɛn.

eng: It is written in the Prophets, And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to the Father and learned from him comes to me.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm yo kɛrɛɛri lɛɛ, ka, Nyɛmɛ kére sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nyaari a. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti Nyɛmɛ keree­wa n na i siiri nu, i mi bá m dɔ.

eng: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.
local: Nyini kere má ka be adu mɔ aŋu Nyɛmɛ wɔ lɛɛ, má ka mini ŋgumi bo m fite m Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ n aŋu i a.

eng: I assure you, whoever believes has eternal life.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, i la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri.

eng: I am the bread of life.
local: Mini ti diire n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n a.

eng: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness and they died.
local: Am nam yo diiri diire n bo bu fere i ka mana n boro kpantain nu, ama, na ba wu.

eng: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that whoever eats from it will never die.
local: Ama, ahin diire n ti diire barasu be bo i fite aŋgoro wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri i, i wú má.

eng: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats this bread will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.
local: Mini ti diire n bo wɔ fite aŋgoro aba, na i ma ŋgɔɔ n a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di ahin diire ni, i nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri. Diire n bo m má n la m ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, bo m fá ma durunya n nu­fɔm, na bu nyá ŋgɔɔ ni.

eng: Then the Jews debated among themselves, asking, How can this man give us his flesh to eat.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na yaa ati bu, na bu suse akuruwa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ahin sɔnɔ ni yo sɛ koro fa i ŋuniɛ ma ya na ya di a.

eng: Jesus said to them, I assure you, unless you eat the flesh of the Human One and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, má ka am di mini Adamande Wa n ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, na am nu m dɛɛ mbunja ni, ambɛrɛ ni am tii la má ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di m dɛɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, na i nu m mbunja ni, i mi la ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, na m téŋge i mi jina cɛɛn.

eng: My flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
local: Dama m dɛɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, ti deke bo i ti diire nahɔrɛ su a, na m mbunja n ti deke bo i ti nuu­wa nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me and I in them.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di m dɛɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, na i nu m mbunja ni, i mi wo ni mini, na mini gusu wo ni yiri lɛɛ.

eng: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me lives because of me.
local: Yiri m Si bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni sunmaari m a, i dɛɛ ti, ye m la ŋgɔɔ a, sɔ ye sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di m ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, mini dɛɛ ti, ye i nyá ŋgɔɔ a.

eng: This is the bread that came down from heaven. It isnt like the bread your ancestors ate, and then they died. Whoever eats this bread will live forever.
local: Ahin ni ti diire n bo wɔ fite aŋgoro aba n a, i ti má ka diire n bo am nam yo diiri na i sin na bu wuuri ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di ahin diire ni, i nyá ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri.

eng: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
local: Yesu kereeri nyini keree­wa n kɛrɛ saŋga bo i wo Kapɛnam bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ nu n wɔ.

eng: Many of his disciples who heard this said, This message is harsh. Who can hear it.
local: Yesu susu­fɔm dɔŋgu tiiri i jɔjɔɔ­wa n sɔ ni, na ba se ka, Ahin keree­wa n ti kekereke. Ŋma koro de i a.

eng: Jesus knew that the disciples were grumbling about this and he said to them, Does this offend you?.
local: Na Yesu afa nu ka bu suŋuŋuti fa kɔ i keree­wa n su, na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin jɔjɔɔ­wa n dɛɛ ti ye am koro ka am kaaki am sin n a?.

eng: What if you were to see the Human One going up where he was before?.
local: Ye nzɛn má am ŋu ka mini Adamande Wa n sukaaki sufun kɔ aŋgoro, deke bo m fite baari ni, má am se sɛ wɔ lɛɛ?.

eng: The Spirit is the one who gives life and the flesh doesnt help at all. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
local: Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ma minɛ ŋgɔɔ a. Adamande yiko be koro ma má ŋgɔɔ. Nɔaniɛ n bo ma jɔjɔ akere am ni, yiri ma am nya Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n a.

eng: Yet some of you dont believe.Jesus knew from the beginning who wouldnt believe and the one who would betray him.
local: Ama, am be n mmɔɔ ada má m ŋu. Má pui ti, fite saŋga bo Yesu boori i juma n bu ni, ye i si bo bu yo má i yarada n a, ni sɔnɔ n bo i bá tɔ i ni.

eng: He said,For this reason I said to you that none can come to me unless the Father enables them to do so.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma se am ka be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro ba m dɔ, má ka m Si ama atin ka i mi yo m susu­fɔ.
