eng: At this, many of his disciples turned away and no longer accompanied him.
local: I seeri sɔ n sin, na i susu­fɔm dɔŋgu akaaki ayaki i ka bu su má i su kun.

eng: Jesus asked the Twelve, Do you also want to leave.
local: Na wɔ bisa i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni ka, Ambɛrɛ gusu koro ka am kaaki yaki m wɔ fan.

eng: Simon Peter answered, Lord, where would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
local: Na Simon Petoro abɔ i nu ka, M Mibiɛ, ya kɔ́ ŋma dɔ a? Wɔrɔ ti sɔnɔ n bo i la nɔaniɛ n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: We believe and know that you are Gods holy one.
local: Ye ya yo u yarada, na ya si ka wɔrɔ ti sɔnɔ casi n bo a fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ n a.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I choose you twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.
local: Na Yesu abɔ nu ka, M ŋa am buru ni nnyɔ ye m yiiri am e, má sɔ? Ama, am nu kun ti Sitana sɔnɔ ɔ.

eng: He was speaking of Judas, Simon Iscariots son, for he, one of the Twelve, was going to betray him.
local: I jɔjɔ niiri Yudasi wɔ, Simon Isikariyoti wa ni. Yiri Yudasi tiiri buru ni nnyɔm ni kun wɔ, ama, yiri bá tɔ Yesu n a.

eng: After this Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He didnt want to travel in Judea, because the Jewish authorities wanted to kill him.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu afa Galili awɔrɛ n nu subara yɛ, wɔ koro má ka i kɔ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, dama Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm koro ka bu kun i wɔ.

eng: When it was almost time for the Jewish Festival of Booths.
local: Nyini saŋga, wɔ ka kaan na Yuda­fɔm di bu cɛɛn­kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Nambue Bo cɛɛn­kpiri ni.

eng: Jesus brothers said to him, Leave Galilee. Go to Judea so that your disciples can see the amazing works that you do.
local: Na Yesu niɛmam ase i ka, Fite ahin awɔrɛ n nu na a kɔ Yudiya, adena u susu­fɔm gusu kɔ́ ŋu sam bo a yo bu ni.

eng: Those who want to be known publicly dont do things secretly. Since you can do these things, show yourself to the world.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka minɛ si kabo i ti barasu, i fa má i sa­yoo­wa fiɛ má minɛm sin, nzɛn a yo alekutura sam wɔ, ma durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ si u sa.

eng: His brothers said this because even they didnt believe in him.
local: I niɛmam n mmɔɔ ayo má i yarada, nyini ti ye bu seeri sɔ n a.

eng: Jesus replied, For you, anytime is fine. But my time hasnt come yet.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, M saŋga nya ju má, ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, saŋga kɛrɛ bo am koro, am kɔ ɔ.

eng: The world cant hate you. It hates me, though, because I testify that its works are evil.
local: Ambɛrɛ ye durunya n nu­fɔm koro kpɔ má am a, ama, bu kpɔ mini wɔ, dama m se bu wɔ, ka bu sa­yoo­wam ti má kpa.

eng: You go up to the festival. Im not going to this one because my time hasnt yet come.
local: Am kɔ cɛɛn­kpiri n di, m kɔ́ má, dama m saŋga nya ju má.

eng: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
local: I seeri bu sɔ ni, na wɔ ka Galili dɔ lee nnyɔ.

eng: However, after his brothers left for the festival, he went too—not openly but in secret.
local: I niɛmam kɔɔri ka bu kɔ di cɛɛn­kpiri ni, na Yesu gusu aba asa bu su, ama, wɔ yi má i ŋu fofoe nu, i fiɛ kɔɔri a.

eng: The Jewish leaders were looking for Jesus at the festival. They kept asking, Where is he.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni suniɛniɛ i, na ba bisa ka, Ye i wo ni wɔ.

eng: The crowds were murmuring about him. Hes a good man, some said, but others were saying, No, he tricks the people.
local: Na jama n sutu asuturi bu beŋgu dɔ i busu. Nbem ŋa, I ti sɔnɔ kpa a. Ye nbem gusu ŋa, Ai, i dadaka minɛm wɔ.

eng: No one spoke about him publicly, though, for fear of the Jewish authorities.
local: Ama, be akoro akan má i jɔrɛ fofoe nu, dama bu kɛrɛ soro Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n wɔ.

eng: Halfway through the festival, Jesus went up to the temple and started to teach.
local: Saŋga bo ba di cɛɛn­kpiri n akɔ aju afiɛn ni, na Yesu akɔ awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, sukere minɛm.

eng: Astonished, the Jewish leaders asked, Hes never been taught! How has he mastered the Law.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ayo alekutura, na ba bisa ka, I yo sɛ ye ahin sɔnɔ n la nyini si n ŋusu sɔ a, bo be akere má i a.

eng: Jesus responded, My teaching isnt mine but comes from the one who sent me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Deke bo m fa kere am ni, i ti má m tiɛŋgu keree­wa, ama, m Si Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ sunma m ni, yiri kere m kabo m kere a.

eng: Whoever wants to do Gods will can tell whether my teaching is from God or whether I speak on my own.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i yó Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa ni, ye i sí kabo m keree­wa n ti a, nzɛn m keree­wa n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ, wara m tiɛŋgu keree­wa.

eng: Those who speak on their own seek glory for themselves. Those who seek the glory of him who sent me are people of truth; theres no falsehood in them.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ i koroya­nu, i mi koro ka bu kansi i wɔ, ama, bo i koro ka bu kansi sɔnɔ bo i sunma i ni, nyini sɔnɔ ni jɔjɔ nahɔrɛ a, na kɔrɛ be wo má i ŋu.

eng: Didnt Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keep the Law. Why do you want to kill me.
local: M ŋa Moyisi yo maari am Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n a, má sɔ? Ama, am be n mmɔɔ su má i atin su. Ye nzu ti ye am koro ka am kun m a.

eng: And Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
local: Na Yesu akɔ buka n bo bu fere i ka Oliva Buka n su, dama oliva bakam gɔ su wɔ.

eng: The crowd answered, You have a demon. Who wants to kill you.
local: Na jama n ase i ka, Ajini wo u ŋu. Ŋma koro ka i kun u a.

eng: Jesus replied, I did one work, and you were all astonished.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Sa kun be lɛɛ, ye ma yo i n a, ye wɔ yo am kɛrɛ alekutura n a.

eng: Early in the morning he returned to the temple. All the people gathered around him, and he sat down and taught them.
local: Alecin ŋgarama fuun na Yesu akaaki akɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Na jama n kɛrɛ aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ i dɔ. Na wɔ tana sukere bu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ.

eng: Because Moses gave you the commandment about circumcision , you circumcise a man on the Sabbath.
local: Moyisi yo maari am nɔɔ ka am wura am nbaa­biɛsɔm kɛnkɛnnɛ le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn, nzɛn haari i tɔ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n mmɔɔ, am yo i, ama, má Moyisi yo boori i bu ka bu wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n a, am nam yo boori i bu a.

eng: The legal experts and Pharisees brought a woman caught in adultery. Placing her in the center of the group.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm nbem afa bara ajaa­fɔ be bo bu kɔ taraari i ka i ni biɛsɔ da, na ba fa i ajina bu afiɛn.

eng: If a man can be circumcised on the Sabbath without breaking Moses Law, why are you angry with me because I made an entire man well on the Sabbath?.
local: Am wura nbaa­biɛsɔm kɛnkɛnnɛ Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wɔ, adena má am bu nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari Moyisi ni ŋgbɛɛn, ye nzɛn ma tuntun sɔnɔ mɔmɔ kun Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n na wɔ nya laifiɛ, nzu ti ye am afa yaa m ŋu a?.

eng: they said to Jesus, Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of committing adultery.
local: Na ba se Yesu ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, ya kɔ atara ahin bara n ka i susaaki ajaa ciire wɔ.

eng: In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone women like this. What do you say.
local: Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari Moyisi n kere ka, nyini nbaram ŋusu sɔ, ya tu bu yabuɛ bo kun bu. Nyini ti, ye wɔrɔ ŋa sɛ.

eng: Dont judge according to appearances. Judge with right judgment.
local: Má am niɛ sɔnɔ nyunu dika na am di i jɔrɛ, ama, am si i wo nahɔrɛ su, na am nya di i jɔrɛ.

eng: They said this to test him, because they wanted a reason to bring an accusation against him. Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
local: Bu seeri sɔ n na bu sɔ i ŋgɛɛ, na bu jaraki i wɔ. Na Yesu abiri i tii asiɛŋgu sufa i saa maraka sukɛrɛ asiɛŋgu.

eng: They continued to question him, so he stood up and replied, Whoever hasnt sinned should throw the first stone.
local: Bu wo i bisa n su ni, na wɔ yɛ i tii su ase bu ka, Bo i ŋa wɔ nya yo má satiɛ am nu, ma i mi du mɔ tu i yabuɛ.

eng: Some people from Jerusalem said, Isnt he the one they want to kill?.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na Yɛrusalem­fɔm nbem abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Má ahin sɔnɔ n ye Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm koro ka bu kun i n a?.

eng: Here he is, speaking in public, yet they arent saying anything to him. Could it be that our leaders actually think he is the Christ?.
local: Am niɛ e. Wɔ yi i ŋu fofoe nu ye i jɔjɔ a. Be suse má i pui mmɔɔ. Beni, Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm asi ka yiri ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n nahɔrɛ su n a.

eng: Bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
local: Na wɔ kaaki abiri i tii sukɛrɛ asiɛŋgu.

eng: We know where he is from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he is from.
local: Ama, saŋga bo Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ba baari, be sí má deke bo i fite. Ahin sɔnɔ n yiri, ya si deke bo i fite.

eng: Those who heard him went away, one by one, beginning with the elders. Finally, only Jesus and the woman were left in the middle of the crowd.
local: Bu tiiri sɔ ni, na bu kɛrɛ alɔrɔki kun kun akɔ. Nkpiɛnm ni du mɔ a. Na wɔ ka Yesu ni bara ni ŋgumi.

eng: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he exclaimed, You know me and where I am from. I havent come on my own. The one who sent me is true, and you dont know him.
local: Na saŋga bo Yesu sukere Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni, na wɔ tiɛn faŋga su ase ka, Am sunsun ka am si m nahɔrɛ su, nzɛn sɛ? Ye am si deke bo m fite? Ma ba má ni m dɛɛ yiko nu wɔ. Yiri n bo wɔ sunma m ni ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ a, ama, am si má i.

eng: Jesus stood up and said to her, Woman, where are they? Is there no one to condemn you.
local: Na Yesu ayɛ i tii su, na wɔ bisa bara n ka, Bara, bu wo ni? Be ase má ka i ti ye ni ka bu kun u, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: I know him because I am from him and he sent me.
local: Mini si i a, dama m fite i dɔ ɔ, yiri sunmaari m a.

eng: She said, No one, sir. Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you. Go, and from now on, dont sin anymore.
local: Na bara n abɔ i nu ka, Ai, m Mibiɛ, be ase má ka i kún m. Na Yesu ase i ka, Mini mmɔɔ se má ka be kún u. Kɔ, ama, má a yo satiɛ kun.

eng: Jesus spoke to the people again, saying, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me wont walk in darkness but will have the light of life.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase jama n ka, Mini ti durunya n nu­fɔm wein a. Bo i su m su kɛrɛ wo má awosin nu kun, ama, i nyá wein bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni.

eng: So they wanted to seize Jesus, but they couldnt because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Nyini ti, na ba kpi ka bu tara i, ama, be afa má i saa akan má i, dama i saŋga nya ju má.

eng: Then the Pharisees said to him, Because you are testifying about yourself, your testimony isnt valid.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm n ase i ka, Wɔrɔ, a sudi u ŋu daani wɔ, be de má u daani n ka i ti nahɔrɛ.

eng: Many from that crowd believed in Jesus. They said, When the Christ comes, will he do more miraculous signs than this man does.
local: Na jama n dɔŋgu ayo i yarada, na ba se ka, Saŋga bo Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ba baari, i yó alekutura sam tara ahin sɔnɔ n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: The Pharisees heard the crowd whispering such things about Jesus, and the chief priests and Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm n ati kabo jama n sutu asuturi sukan Yesu jɔrɛ, na bu ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n asunma tintini­jina­fɔm ka bu kɔ tara i.

eng: Jesus replied, Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, since I know where I came from and where Im going. You dont know where I come from or where Im going.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Haari nzɛn m di m ŋu daani n mmɔɔ, m daani dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama m si deke n bo m fite baari, na m kaaki si deke bo m sukɔ ni. Ama, ambɛrɛ, am si má deke bo m fite, na am kaaki si má deke bo m sukɔ n gusu.

eng: You judge according to human standards, but I judge no one.
local: Ambɛrɛ, am di jɔrɛ kabo durunya n nu­fɔm di bu beŋgum jɔrɛ n wɔ, ama, mini ni m tii, m di má be jɔrɛ.

eng: Therefore, Jesus said, Im still with you for a little while before I go to the one who sent me.
local: Na wɔ se ka, M wó am dɔ saŋga kaan, na m nya bá kɔ yiri n bo i sunmaari m n dɔ.

eng: Even if I do judge, my judgment is truthful, because Im not alone. My judgments come from me and from the Father who sent me.
local: Ama, haari nzɛn m di minɛ jɔrɛ n mmɔɔ, m jɔrɛ dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama má mini ŋgumi di minɛm jɔrɛ a, ama, mini ni m Si bo i sunmaari m ni di a.

eng: You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: Am bá niɛniɛ m busu, na am ŋú má m. Deke bo m bá wo ni, am koro kɔ má dɔ.

eng: In your Law it is written that the witness of two people is true.
local: Ba kɛrɛ am Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu ka, nzɛn minɛ nnyɔ daani dii­wa yo kun, nyini la ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: The Jewish opposition asked each other, Where does he intend to go that we cant find him? Surely he doesnt intend to go where our people have been scattered and are living among the Greeks! He isnt going to teach the Greeks, is he?.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Yiri koro ka i kɔ ni wɔ, ye yɛrɛ koro ŋu má i a? I kɔ́ ya minɛm dɔ ɔ, bo ba sandi awura Giriki­fɔm asiɛn su ni, na i kɔ́ kere Giriki­fɔm ni Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ni, nzɛn sɛ a?.

eng: I am one witness concerning myself, and the Father who sent me is the other.
local: Mini ni m tii di m ŋu daani a, na m Si bo wɔ sunma m gusu di m daani.

eng: What does he mean when he says, You will look for me, but you wont find me, and where I am you cant come.
local: I ŋa sɛ wɔ lɛɛ? Bo i ŋa ya bá niɛniɛ i busu na ya ŋú má i ni, na bekun, deke bo i sukɔ ni, ya koro kɔ má dɔ.

eng: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, All who are thirsty should come to me!.
local: Cɛɛn­kpiri n awieeri cɛɛn n ti cɛɛn­kpiri n tii a. Nyini cɛɛn n na Yesu ayasu ajina, na wɔ tiɛn faŋga su ase ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo nzuehɔɛ ati i, i mi bra m dɔ na i ba nu.

eng: They asked him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, You dont know me and you dont know my Father. If you knew me, you would also know my Father.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, U si wo ni? Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Am si má m ni m Si n be n mmɔɔ. Nzɛn má am si mini, má am si m Si n mmɔɔ.

eng: He spoke these words while he was teaching in the temple area known as the treasury. No one arrested him, because his time hadnt yet come.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu seeri sɔ ni, i wo minɛm kere su Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, fa dodo deke bo bu yi bu saraka ŋwaa fa ma Nyɛmɛ n wɔ. Ama, be akoro atara má i, dama i saŋga nya ju má.

eng: All who believe in me should drink! As the scriptures said concerning me, Rivers of living water will flow out from within him. .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, Nzuetin bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni, i sóŋgi fite i mi ahore­mbaa su a.

eng: Jesus continued, Im going away. You will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Na Yesu akaaki ase bu ka, M bá kɔ yaki am, na am bá niɛniɛ m busu, ama, am ŋú má m, na am bá wu ni am satiɛ ni. Deke bo m sukɔ ni, am koro kɔ má dɔ.

eng: Jesus said this concerning the Spirit. Those who believed in him would soon receive the Spirit, but they hadnt experienced the Spirit yet since Jesus hadnt yet been glorified.
local: Yesu jɔjɔ niiri Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wɔ, ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo yiri Yesu yarada nyá Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni. Nyini saŋga ni, Nyɛmɛ nya fa má i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ma má, dama wɔ nya wura má Yesu kpiri.

eng: The Jewish leaders said, He isnt going to kill himself, is he? Is that why he said, Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm n abisa ka, I ŋa deke bo i sukɔ ni, ya koro kɔ má dɔ. Nyini la ka, i kún i ŋu wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ a.

eng: When some in the crowd heard these words, they said, This man is truly the prophet.
local: Na jama n nu minɛ nbem tiiri kabo i seeri sɔ ni, na ba se ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n wɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who was blind from birth.
local: Na saŋga bo Yesu wo natiiri su ni, na wɔ ŋu sɔnɔ be, bu wuuri i ni, i ti anyinsin wɔ.

eng: He said to them, You are from below; Im from above. You are from this world; Im not from this world.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ fite durunya n nu fɛn wɔ, ama, mini fite aŋgoro wɔ. Ambɛrɛ ti durunya n nu fɛn fɔm wɔ, ama, mini fite má durunya n nu fɛn.

eng: Others said, Hes the Christ. But others said, The Christ cant come from Galilee, can he?.
local: Na nbem ase ka, Yiri ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a. Ama, na nbem gusu ase ka, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni fite má Galili da.

eng: Jesus disciples asked, Rabbi, who sinned so that he was born blind, this man or his parents.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n abisa i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, nzu ti ye bu wuuri ahin sɔnɔ ni anyinsin a? I wu­fɔm yoori satiɛ, nzɛn i dɛɛ satiɛ a.

eng: This is why I told you that you would die in your sins. If you dont believe that I Am, you will die in your sins.
local: Nyini ti ye ma se am ka, am wú ni am satiɛ n a, dama nzɛn am yo má m yarada ka mini la sɔnɔhɔrɛ n bo m se am ka m ti ni, am wú ni am satiɛ n wɔ.

eng: Didnt the scripture say that the Christ comes from Davids family and from Bethlehem, Davids village.
local: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni yó Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma a, na bu wú i Bɛtilɛhɛm, Fɛmɛ Davidi bɔsu wɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Neither he nor his parents. This happened so that Gods mighty works might be displayed in him.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, I ti má i ni i wu­fɔm be mmɔɔ satiɛ dɛɛ ti, ama, Nyɛmɛ koro ka minɛm ŋu kabo i yo alekutura sa fa ma i a.

eng: Who are you? they asked. Jesus replied, Im exactly who I have claimed to be from the beginning.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, Ye wɔrɔ la ŋma a? Na Yesu ase bu ka, Kabo m se am saŋga kɛrɛ ni, m ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: So the crowd was divided over Jesus.
local: Yesu dɛɛ ti, na jama n nɔɔ awo bu ŋguŋgumi.

eng: While its daytime, we must do the works of him who sent me. Night is coming when no one can work.
local: Kabo wiɛ toko wo bɛrɛ ni, am ma ya yo yiri n bo wɔ sunma m juma ni. Ale bá san bo be koro yo má juma be.

eng: I have many things to say in judgment concerning you. The one who sent me is true, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.
local: M la sa dɔŋgu bo m jɔjɔ fa kɔ am dɔ, na m la sa dɔŋgu bo m fa di am jɔrɛ jaraki am, ama, yiri bo i sunmaari m ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ ɔ, na deke bo i se m ni, nyini ye m kan kere durunya n nu­fɔm a.

eng: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one grabbed him.
local: na nbem akoro ka bu tara i, ama, be afa má i saa akan má i.

eng: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.
local: Kabo m toko wo durunya n nu ni, mini ti durunya n nu­fɔm wein a.

eng: They didnt know he was speaking about his Father.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, ba si má ka i jɔjɔ niiri i Si Nyɛmɛ wɔ.

eng: The guards returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked, Why didnt you bring him.
local: Na tintini­jina­fɔm n akpie bu nyi akɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ni, ni Farasi­fɔm n dɔ, na ba bisa bu ka, Nzu ti ye am afa má i aba má.

eng: After he said this, he spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and smeared the mud on the mans eyes.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na wɔ yi nanzue agɔ asiɛŋgu, na wɔ fa akpita naatiɛ afa abɔrɔki anyinsin nyu­mbaa nnyɔ n kɛrɛ.

eng: So Jesus said to them, When the Human One is lifted up, then you will know that I Am. Then you will know that I do nothing on my own, but I say just what the Father has taught me.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am bá yɛɛri mini Adamande Wa n su baka jaanu n su, ye am sí ka mini la sɔnɔhɔrɛ n bo m se am kabo m ti n a, na m yo má deke be m dɛɛ yiko nu, ama, m se kabo m Si akere m wɔ.

eng: The guards answered, No one has ever spoken the way he does.
local: Na tintini­jina­fɔm n abɔ bu nu ka, Be nya koro jɔjɔ má kabo nyini sɔnɔ n jɔjɔ ni.

eng: Jesus said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam . So the man went away and washed. When he returned, he could see.
local: Na wɔ se i ka, Kɔ na a kɔ ŋmisi u nyunu Silowam bura n nu. Silowam n bu la ka, ba sunma i wɔ. Na wɔ kɔ aŋmisi i nyunu n na wɔ ŋu asi, na wɔ kaaki aba awuru.

eng: He who sent me is with me. He doesnt leave me by myself, because I always do what makes him happy.
local: Yiri bo i sunmaari m n wo m dɔ, wɔ nya yaki má m cɛɛn be, dama saŋga kɛrɛ m yo deke bo i sun i nu wɔ.

eng: The Pharisees replied, Have you too been deceived?.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm n abisa bu ka, Wɔ daka ambɛrɛ gusu wɔ fan?.

eng: The mans neighbors and those who used to see him when he was a beggar said, Isnt this the man who used to sit and beg.
local: Na i lamɔkɔm ni minɛm bo bu yo ŋuuri i saŋga kɛrɛ ka i sere ni, abisa ka, Má ahin sɔnɔ ni yo tana fɛn sere n a.

eng: While Jesus was saying these things, many people came to believe in him.
local: I jɔjɔɔri sɔ ni, na jama n dɔŋgu ayo i yarada.

eng: Have any of the leaders believed in him? Has any Pharisee?.
local: Am nya ti ka Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm wara Farasi­fɔm n be ayo i yarada a?.

eng: Some said, It is, and others said, No, its someone who looks like him. But the man said, Yes, its me.
local: Nbem ŋa, Yiri a. Nbem gusu ŋa, Má yiri a. I ferema­fɔ a. Na yiri n mmɔɔ ni i tii suse bu ka, Mini a.

eng: Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, You are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teaching.
local: Na Yesu ase Yuda­fɔm bo bu yo i yarada n ka, Nzɛn am de m nɔaniɛ ni, am ti m susu­fɔm wɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: No, only this crowd, which doesnt know the Law. And they are under Gods curse.
local: Ama, ahin jama n si má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, nyini ti, Nyɛmɛ ayo bu ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: So they asked him, How are you now able to see.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, Ye i yoori sɛ ye u nyu­mbaa n ateke a.

eng: Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
local: Na am sí deke bo i ti nahɔrɛ ni, na nahɔrɛ ni má am nya am tii a.

eng: Nicodemus, who was one of them and had come to Jesus earlier, said.
local: Na Nikodemo bo i ti Farasi­fɔm kun n na i yo kɔ ŋuuri Yesu ni, ase bu ka.

eng: He answered, The man they call Jesus made mud, smeared it on my eyes, and said, Go to the pool of Siloam and wash. So I went and washed, and then I could see.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Sɔnɔ n bo bu fere i ka Yesu ni, yiri kpitaari naatiɛ bɔrɔkiiri m nyu­mbaa n a. Na wɔ se m ka m kɔ Silowam bura n nu, na m kɔ ŋmisi m nyunu. Na ma kɔ aŋmisi, kpeŋga na ma ŋu asi.

eng: They responded, We are Abrahams children; weve never been anyones slaves. How can you say that we will be set free.
local: Na ba se i ka, Ya ti Abraham anumam wɔ, be nya fa má ya yo má i akɔɔm. Ye nzu ti ye a ŋa ya nyá ya tii a.

eng: Our Law doesnt judge someone without first hearing him and learning what he is doing, does it.
local: Ya dɛɛ atin n su, kana ya ba di sɔnɔ jɔrɛ jaraki i, má ka ya bisa i na ya ti sa bo wɔ yo i n ka.

eng: They asked, Where is this man? He replied, I dont know.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, Ye yiri n wo ni? Na wɔ se bu ka, M si má.

eng: Jesus answered, I assure you that everyone who sins is a slave to sin.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ, i mi ti satiɛ akɔɔ.

eng: They answered him, You are not from Galilee too, are you? Look it up and you will see that the prophet doesnt come from Galilee.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, Wɔrɔ n mmɔɔ fite Galili wɔ? Kan Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n na a niɛ. A ŋú má ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n be mmɔɔ fite Galili. .

eng: Then they led the man who had been born blind to the Pharisees.
local: Na ba fa sɔnɔ n bo i yo tiiri anyinsin n akɔ Farasi­fɔm dɔ.

eng: A slave isnt a permanent member of the household, but a son is.
local: Akɔɔ­baa koro ka má awuru n nu saŋga kɛrɛ, ama, awuru­baa yiri wo awuru n nu wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Now Jesus made the mud and smeared it on the mans eyes on a Sabbath day.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n bo Yesu kpitaari naatiɛ na i fa tekeeri anyinsin nyu­mbaa n tiiri Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wɔ.

eng: Therefore, if the Son makes you free, you really will be free.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn mini awuru­baa n nya deeri am tii jiiri, am nyá am tii nahɔrɛ su.

eng: They each went to their own homes.
local: Bu se sɔ wieeri ni, na bu kɛrɛ asandi akɔ awuru.

eng: So Pharisees also asked him how he was able to see. The man told them, He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see.
local: Nyini ti, na Farasi­fɔm gusu akaaki abisa i ka, I yoori sɛ ye a ŋuuri asi a? Na wɔ se bu ka, I faari naatiɛ bɔrɔkiiri m nyu­mbaa n a, na ma ŋmisi i, na ma ŋu asi.

eng: I know that you are Abrahams children, yet you want to kill me because you dont welcome my teaching.
local: M si ka am ti Abraham anumam wɔ, ama, am koro ka am kun m wɔ, dama am ade má m nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Some Pharisees said, This man isnt from God, because he breaks the Sabbath law. Others said, How can a sinner do miraculous signs like these? So they were divided.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm nbem ase ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n fite má Nyɛmɛ dɔ, dama i bita má Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ciire ni. Ama, na nbem gusu ase ka, I yo sɛ ye satiɛ­yo­fɔ koro yo alekutura sam ka ahin ŋusu sɔ a? Na bu kɛrɛ nɔɔ awo i ŋguŋgumi.

eng: Im telling you what Ive seen when I am with the Father, but you are doing what youve heard from your father.
local: Deke bo mini aŋu i m Si dɔ n ye m suse am a, ama, ambɛrɛ, deke bo am ti i am si dɔ, ye am yo a.

eng: Some of the Pharisees questioned the man who had been born blind again: What do you have to say about him, since he healed your eyes? He replied, Hes a prophet.
local: Na ba kaaki abisa sɔnɔ n bo i yo tiiri anyinsin bekun ka, Ye wɔrɔ bo wɔ teke u nyu­mbaa ni, a bu i sɛ wɔ? Na wɔ se ka, I ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ.

eng: They replied, Our father is Abraham. Jesus responded, If you were Abrahams children, you would do Abrahams works.
local: Na ba se i ka, Ya si la Abraham. Na wɔ se bu ka, Nzɛn má am ti Abraham nbaam, má am yo i sa­yoo­wa a.

eng: The Jewish leaders didnt believe the man had been blind and received his sight until they called for his parents.
local: Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ade má ka i yo tiiri anyinsin wɔ, ye i ŋu asi kisa, má ka bu ba fereeri i si, ni i ni.

eng: Instead, you want to kill me, though I am the one who has spoken the truth I heard from God. Abraham didnt do this.
local: Kabo i ti ni, ma se am nahɔrɛ sam bo m tiiri i Nyɛmɛ dɔ n wɔ, ama, am koro ka am kun m wɔ. Abraham ayo má nyini be sɔ.

eng: The Jewish leaders asked them, Is this your son? Are you saying he was born blind? How can he now see.
local: na ba bisa bu ka, Am wa n bo am ŋa am wuuri i na i ti anyinsin ni, yiri la ahin? Ye i yoori sɛ ye i ŋu asi kisa a.

eng: You are doing your fathers works. They said, Our ancestry isnt in question! The only Father we have is God.
local: Ambɛrɛ, am yo am si sa­yoo­wa a. Na ba se i ka, Yɛrɛ, ya ti má soma nbaam. Ya si la Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi wɔ.

eng: I assure you that whoever doesnt enter into the sheep pen through the gate but climbs over the wall is a thief and an outlaw.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, sɔnɔ bo i lɔrɔki wura nbɔɛm sɔɔ n nu, na wɔ fa má anɔ n nu awura má, i mi ti awiefɔ ɔ, na i kaaki ti sɔnɔ bo i nati fin minɛm di wɔ.

eng: His parents answered, We know he is our son. We know he was born blind.
local: Na i si, ni i ni abɔ bu nu ka, Ya si i ka i ti ya wa a, ya wuuri i anyinsin wɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God. Here I am. I havent come on my own. God sent me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nzɛn má Nyɛmɛ ti am Si, má am koro m, dama m fiteeri Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ye m baari fɛn n a. Ma ba má ni m dɛɛ yiko nu, ama, yiri sunmaari m a.

eng: The one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i fa anɔ n wura nbɔɛm sɔɔ n nu, yiri ti nbɔɛm sasa­fɔ n a.

eng: Before the Festival of Passover, Jesus knew that his time had come to leave this world and go to the Father. Having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them fully.
local: Alecin bo má bu di Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n ni, na Yesu asi nu ka saŋga n aju bo i fíte durunya n nu kaaki kɔ i Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Na i dɛɛ tiɛŋgu minɛm bo bu wo durunya n nu na i koro bu ni, i koro bu sɔ ɔ haari i kɔ ju i faŋga nyaari.

eng: But we dont know how he now sees, and we dont know who healed his eyes. Ask him. Hes old enough to speak for himself.
local: Ama, ya si má kabo i yoori barasu na i koro ŋu asi kisa ni, yɛrɛ gusu kaaki si má sɔnɔ bo i tekeeri i nyu­mbaa ni. Wɔ ju i tiɛŋgu sɔnɔ, am bisa i, yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii sé am.

eng: Why dont you understand what Im saying? Its because you cant really hear my words.
local: Nzu ti ye am ti má m jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu a? Dama am koro de má m nɔaniɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: The guard at the gate opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
local: Anɔ niɛ­fɔ ni teke anɔ n ma i a, na i fere i dɛɛ nbɔɛm n duma, na i nbɔɛm ni ti i konvi, na i fa bu fite.

eng: Jesus and his disciples were sharing the evening meal. The devil had already provoked Judas, Simon Iscariots son, to betray Jesus.
local: Na kɔŋguɛ saŋga bo bu wo diire n di su ni, na Sitana awura Yudasi, Simon Isikariyoti wa n tii nu ka i tɔ Yesu ma i kpɔ­fɔm.

eng: His parents said this because they feared the Jewish authorities. This is because the Jewish authorities had already decided that whoever confessed Jesus to be the Christ would be expelled from the synagogue.
local: Deke bo i ti na i si, ni i ni seeri sɔ ni, bu soroori Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ahore wɔ, dama Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n adu mɔ ase ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i seeri ka Yesu ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni, bu fɔ́n i mi fite bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu wɔ.

eng: Your father is the devil. You are his children, and you want to do what your father wants. He was a murderer from the beginning. He has never stood for the truth, because theres no truth in him. Whenever that liar speaks, he speaks according to his own nature, because hes a liar and the father of liars.
local: Sitana ti am si wɔ, na am koro ka am yo kabo am si koro n wɔ, i fite ka i bumboori kɛrɛ, i ti sɔnɔ kun­fɔ ɔ, na i su má nahɔrɛ su, dama nahɔrɛ haari kun mmɔɔ wo má i dɔ, i dɛɛ la ka i bu kɔrɛ wɔ, dama yiri ti kɔrɛ­fɔ a, na yiri kaaki ti kɔrɛ­fɔm kɛrɛ si a.

eng: I am the true vine, and my Father is the vineyard keeper.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Mini ti baka n bo i su suu­wa nahɔrɛ su a, ye m Si Nyɛmɛ ti nyini baka n fie niɛ­fɔ.

eng: Whenever he has gathered all of his sheep, he goes before them and they follow him, because they know his voice.
local: Nzɛn i nya faari i nbɔɛm kɛrɛ fiteeri, i du bu mɔ ɔ, na i dɛɛ nbɔɛm n su i su, dama bu si i konvi.

eng: Jesus knew the Father had given everything into his hands and that he had come from God and was returning to God.
local: Na Yesu asi nu ka i Si Nyɛmɛ afa deke n kɛrɛ awura i saa nu, na i kaaki si ka i fiteeri Nyɛmɛ dɔ baari a, na i káaki kɔ i dɔ ɔ.

eng: Thats why his parents said, Hes old enough. Ask him.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye i si, ni i ni seeri ka, Wɔ ju i tiɛŋgu sɔnɔ, am bisa yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii n a.

eng: Because I speak the truth, you dont believe me.
local: Ama, mini se nahɔrɛ n ti, am de má m nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: He removes any of my branches that dont produce fruit, and he trims any branch that produces fruit so that it will produce even more fruit.
local: Asa­mbaa kɛrɛ bo i wo m su na i su má, m Si kpiɛ i ji wɔ, na asa­mbaa kɛrɛ bo i wo m su na i su, i kpiɛkpiɛ i nyawa n yo i dɔŋgu wɔ, na i su nyunyumi.

eng: They wont follow a stranger but will run away because they dont know the strangers voice.
local: Sɔnɔ bo bu si má i, bu sú má i su fieo, bu ŋmáti yaki i wɔ, dama bu si má i konvi.

eng: So he got up from the table and took off his robes. Picking up a linen towel, he tied it around his waist.
local: Na wɔ nya yasu ajina, na wɔ yi i tanlɛɛ n ase, na wɔ fa papawo abo i wi nu.

eng: Therefore, they called a second time for the man who had been born blind and said to him, Give glory to God. We know this man is a sinner.
local: Na ba kaaki afere sɔnɔ n bo i yo tiiri anyinsin n bekun, na ba se i ka, Niɛ Nyɛmɛ ti, na a jɔjɔ nahɔrɛ. Yɛrɛ si ka ahin sɔnɔ n ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ.

eng: Who among you can show Im guilty of sin? Since I speak the truth, why dont you believe me?.
local: Am ŋma koro kere m satiɛ bo ma yo i a? Nzu ti ye m se am nahɔrɛ, ye am de má m nɔaniɛ n a?.

eng: You are already trimmed because of the word I have spoken to you.
local: Jɔrɛ n bo m jɔjɔ maari am ni, yiri adu mɔ ayo am casi a.

eng: Dont be troubled. Trust in God. Trust also in me.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Má am ma am ahore saaki. Am yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, na am yo m gusu yarada.

eng: Those who heard Jesus use this analogy didnt understand what he was saying.
local: Na Yesu abo ahin miɛɛnu n akere bu, ama, ba ti má i bu.

eng: Then he poured water into a washbasin and began to wash the disciples feet, drying them with the towel he was wearing.
local: Na wɔ sa nzue agɔ nyaŋga nu, na wɔ bo i bu suŋmisi i susu­fɔm n jaa, na wɔ fa papawo n bo i bo i wi nu ni sunuka bu jaam.

eng: The man answered, I dont know whether hes a sinner. Heres what I do know: I was blind and now I see.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nzɛn i ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, i ti má satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, mini si má. Deke bo m si i n la ka, m yo tiiri anyinsin wɔ, ama, kisa m ŋu asi.

eng: Gods children listen to Gods words. You dont listen to me because you arent Gods children.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ, i mi ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ wɔ. Deke bo i ti na am ti má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n la ka, am ti má i dɛɛm wɔ.

eng: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. A branch cant produce fruit by itself, but must remain in the vine. Likewise, you cant produce fruit unless you remain in me.
local: Am wo ni mini na m gusu wo ni ambɛrɛ. Kabo baka asa­mbaa be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro su i ŋgumi, má ka i wo baka n su. Sɔ ye am gusu koro su má, má ka am wo m dɔ.

eng: My Fathers house has room to spare. If that werent the case, would I have told you that Im going to prepare a place for you?.
local: M Si Nyɛmɛ dika n nu, sɔɔm sunnu dɔ, nzɛn má i ti má sɔ, má m se má am. M kɔ́ na m kɔ sɛsɛ woo­biri su se am.

eng: I have said these things to you so that you wont fall away.
local: M suse am ahin sam kɛrɛ bo bu bá yo n wɔ, na bu ba yoori, na má am kaaki am sin bo m.

eng: A certain man, Lazarus, was ill. He was from Bethany, the village of Mary and her sister Martha. .
local: Na sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Lazarosi n ada tukpaki. I ninbaam la Mariya ni Marata. Bu bɔsu la Betani.

eng: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, home of Lazarus, whom Jesus had raised from the dead.
local: Na saŋga bo i ba kaari le­sɛn, na bu di Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni, na Yesu akɔ Betani, Lazarosi bɔsu, yiri bo Yesu teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni.

eng: So Jesus spoke again, I assure you that I am the gate of the sheep.
local: Ba ti má miɛɛnu n bu n ti, na Yesu akaaki ase bu bekun ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, mini ti nbɔɛm anɔ n bo bu fa wura sɔɔ n nu n a.

eng: When Jesus came to Simon Peter, Peter said to him, Lord, are you going to wash my feet.
local: I ba kpuraari Simon Petoro, na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, M Mibiɛ, na wɔrɔ ba ŋmisi m dɛɛ jaa.

eng: They questioned him: What did he do to you? How did he heal your eyes.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, I yoori u sɛ wɔ, ye i tekeeri u nyu­mbaa n sɛ wɔ.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon, werent we.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm n ase i ka, Bo ya ŋa a ti Samariya­fɔ ɔ, na ajini wo u ŋu ni, i ti má nahɔrɛ wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a.

eng: I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, then you will produce much fruit. Without me, you cant do anything.
local: Mini ti baka n a, ye ambɛrɛ ti asa­mbaam ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo m dɔ, na mini mmɔɔ wo i dɔ, i mi su suu­wa nyunyumi wɔ, dama am koro yo má pui, má ka am awo m dɔ.

eng: When I go to prepare a place for you, I will return and take you to be with me so that where I am you will be too.
local: Na nzɛn m kɔ sɛsɛ woo­biri n su seeri am, m káaki ba fa am kɔ deke bo m kɔ́ wo ni, na deke bo m wó ni, am gusu wó dɔ ɔ.

eng: They will expel you from the synagogue. The time is coming when those who kill you will think that they are doing a service to God.
local: Bu bá fɔn yi am fite bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, ama, saŋga suba bo bu bá kun am, na bu súnsun ka bu susu Nyɛmɛ wɔ.

eng: When Jesus finished saying these things, he looked up to heaven and said, Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that the Son can glorify you.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ yɛ i tii su aniɛ aŋgoro na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ. I ŋa, M Si, saŋga n aju. Wura mini u Wa n kpiri, adena m gusu wura u kpiri.

eng: This was the Mary who anointed the Lord with fragrant oil and wiped his feet with her hair. Her brother Lazarus was ill.
local: Nyini Mariya n bo i niwa Lazarosi daari tukpaki ni, yiri ba faari tulaalɛɛ nva­nvani­fɔ gɔɔri Yesu jaa su ye i faari i tii­ŋmiɛ tintiin nunukaari i n a.

eng: Lazarus and his sisters hosted a dinner for him. Martha served and Lazarus was among those who joined him at the table.
local: Na ba su Yesu wɔfɔ dɔ. Marata faari diire n ba maari bu a, Lazarosi gusu woori minɛm bo bu ni Yesu diiri diire n nu.

eng: All who came before me were thieves and outlaws, but the sheep didnt listen to them.
local: Bɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo bu duuri m mɔ baari, na bu yo bu ŋu ka bu niɛ nbɔɛm su ni, bu ti awiefɔm wɔ, ni minɛm bo bu nati fin minɛm di wɔ, ama, nbɔɛm n ade má bu jɔjɔɔ­wa ni.

eng: Jesus replied, You dont understand what Im doing now, but you will understand later.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Sa bo m suyo i ni, a koro ti má i bu kisa, ama, fa kɔ fa ba, a bá ti i bu.

eng: He replied, I already told you, and you didnt listen. Why do you want to hear it again? Do you want to become his disciples too.
local: Na wɔ bɔ bu nu ka, Ma du mɔ ase am kabo i tekeeri m nyu­mbaa ni, ye am ade má. Ye nzu ti ye am koro ka m kan kere am bekun a? Ambɛrɛ gusu, am koro ka am yo i susu­fɔm wɔ, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: I dont have a demon, Jesus replied. But I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ajini wo má m ŋu, ama, m bu m Si n wɔ, ye ambɛrɛ bu má m.

eng: If you dont remain in me, you will be like a branch that is thrown out and dries up. Those branches are gathered up, thrown into a fire, and burned.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo má m dɔ, bu kpiɛ i ji wɔ, kabo bu kpiɛ baka asa­mbaa na i wu n wɔ, na bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ wura sin nu na bu je ni.

eng: You know the way to the place Im going.
local: Na deke bo m sukɔ ni, am si atin ni.

eng: They will do these things because they dont know the Father or me.
local: Bu si má m ni m Si n dɛɛ ti, ye bu yó nyini sam kɛrɛ n a.

eng: You gave him authority over everyone so that he could give eternal life to everyone you gave him.
local: Dama a ma m yiko ka m niɛ minɛm kɛrɛ su, na bo a fa bu ama m ni, m má bu ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri.

eng: So the sisters sent word to Jesus, saying, Lord, the one whom you love is ill.
local: Na i ninbaa nbaa­baram n asunma ka bu kɔ se Yesu ka, Ya Mibiɛ, u danfo n bo a koro i agaya n ada tukpaki.

eng: Then Mary took an extraordinary amount, almost three-quarters of a pound, of very expensive perfume made of pure nard. She anointed Jesus feet with it, then wiped his feet dry with her hair. The house was filled with the aroma of the perfume.
local: Na Mariya afa tulaalɛɛ nva­nvani bo i la gɔ agaya tuwa bambaka kun, na wɔ fa agɔ Yesu jaa su, na wɔ fa i tii­ŋmiɛ tintiin anunuka i jaam kɛrɛ su, na tulaalɛɛ ni nvani ade awuru n kɛrɛ.

eng: I am the gate. Whoever enters through me will be saved. They will come in and go out and find pasture.
local: Mini ti anɔ n a, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i fa m dɛɛ anɔ n wuraari, i nyá tii­de, na i wúra na i fíte, na i nyá i diire di.

eng: No! Peter said. You will never wash my feet! Jesus replied, Unless I wash you, you wont have a place with me.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, Ai, a ŋmísi má m jaa haari durunya n ba yasu. Na Yesu ase i ka, Nzɛn ma ŋmisi má u jaa, m ni wɔrɔ akpaaki lɛɛ.

eng: They insulted him: You are his disciple, but we are Moses disciples.
local: Na ba tɔ i ŋu ni nzukɔ kpiɛ ase i ka, Wɔrɔ ti nyini sɔnɔ n susu­fɔ a, yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti Moyisi dɛɛ susu­fɔm wɔ.

eng: Im not trying to bring glory to myself. Theres one who is seeking to glorify me, and hes the judge.
local: M sukoro má ka minɛ wura m ŋu kpiri wɔ, be wo bɛrɛ bo i koro ka i wura m kpiri, yiri ti jɔrɛ­di­fɔ a.

eng: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask for whatever you want and it will be done for you.
local: Nzɛn am wo m dɔ, na m nɔaniɛ n wo am ahore su, deke kɛrɛ bo am koroori i, na am sereeri Nyɛmɛ, am nyá i.

eng: Thomas asked, Lord, we dont know where you are going. How can we know the way.
local: Na Tomasi ase i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, ya si má deke bo a sukɔ ni, ye ya yo sɛ si atin n a.

eng: But I have said these things to you so that when their time comes, you will remember that I told you about them. I didnt say these things to you from the beginning, because I was with you.
local: Ma se am sam bo bu bá yo lɛɛ, na i saŋga ba juuri, na am téŋge su ka m yo seeri am. Na Yesu asa su ase bu ka, M ni ambɛrɛ yo woori bumboori n dɛɛ ti, ye ma yo ase má am ahin kɛrɛ a.

eng: This is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you sent.
local: Wɔrɔ ŋgumi ti Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su a. Na minɛ bo bu si u, na bu si mini Yesu Kristo n bo a sunmaari m ni, bɛrɛ la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: When he heard this, Jesus said, This illness isnt fatal. Its for the glory of God so that Gods Son can be glorified through it.
local: Yesu tiiri sɔ ni, na wɔ se ka, Ahin tukpaki n ti má yiwee tukpaki, ama, i yo sɔ, na minɛm ŋu Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa kabo i ti, na Nyɛmɛ ma minɛm ŋu mini bo m ti i Wa n gusu jirima woo­wa.

eng: Judas Iscariot, one of his disciples , complained.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔm kun bo bu fere i ka Yudasi Isikariyoti bo i bá tɔ Yesu n abisa ka.

eng: The thief enters only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they could have life—indeed, so that they could live life to the fullest.
local: Awiefɔ dɛɛ la ka i ba wɔ ɔ, na i kun, na i saaki. Mini aba ka bu nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má aŋusu.

eng: Simon Peter said, Lord, not only my feet but also my hands and my head.
local: Dɔ na Simon Petoro anya ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, nzɛn i ti sɔ a, má a ŋmisi m jaa ŋgumi, ama, ŋmisi m saam ni m tii kɛrɛ.

eng: We know that God spoke to Moses, but we dont know where this man is from.
local: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ yo jɔjɔɔri Moyisi dɔ a, ama, nyini sɔnɔ n yiri, ya si má i fitee­biri n mmɔɔ.

eng: I assure you that whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bita m nɔaniɛ ni, i mi wú má fieo.

eng: My Father is glorified when you produce much fruit and in this way prove that you are my disciples.
local: Nzɛn am su suu­wa nyunyumi, nyini kere ka am ti m susu­fɔm a, na i wura m Si kpiri.

eng: Jesus answered, I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Mini ti atin n a, ni nahɔrɛ, ni ŋgɔɔ n a. Mini ŋgumi ti atin n bo sɔnɔ koro fa i na i kɔ m Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

eng: But now I go away to the one who sent me. None of you ask me, Where are you going.
local: Ama, kisa m sukaaki kɔ yiri bo i sunmaari m n dɔ ɔ, ye am be akoro abisa má m ka, Ye a sukɔ ni wɔ.

eng: I have glorified you on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do.
local: Ma wura u kpiri durunya n nu, dama ma yo juma n bo a ma m ka m yo i n awie.

eng: Jesus loved Martha, her sister, and Lazarus.
local: Tɔ, Yesu koroori Marata ni Mariya ni bu niwa Lazarosi.

eng: This perfume was worth a years wages! Why wasnt it sold and the money given to the poor? .
local: Nzu ti ye bara n atɔ má tulaalɛɛ n na i nya ŋwaa beberebe, na ya fa ma yaari­fɔm a.

eng: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
local: Mini ti bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpa n a. Bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpa n tɔ i ŋgɔɔ ɔ, na i wu ma i nbɔɛm ni.

eng: Jesus responded, Those who have bathed need only to have their feet washed, because they are completely clean. You disciples are clean, but not every one of you.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Sɔnɔ anya abiɛ nzue, ŋka i ŋmisi i jaa a, i biɛ má nzue kun, dama i ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ ti casi wɔ. Ambɛrɛ, am ti casi wɔ, ama, má am kɛrɛ.

eng: The Jewish opposition said to Jesus, Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham and the prophets died, yet you say, Whoever keeps my word will never die.
local: Na ba se i ka, Kisa, ye ya si ka ajini wo u ŋu a. Abraham ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni kɛrɛ awu, ama, wɔrɔ ŋa sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bita u nɔaniɛ ni, i mi wú má fieo.

eng: The man answered, This is incredible! You dont know where he is from, yet he healed my eyes!.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin ti alekutura wanla, am si má i fitee­biri, ama, wɔ teke m nyu­mbaa.

eng: As the Father loved me, I too have loved you. Remain in my love.
local: Kabo m Si koro m ni, sɔ ye mini mmɔɔ koro am a, am wo ni m koro ni.

eng: If you have really known me, you will also know the Father. From now on you know him and have seen him.
local: Nzɛn má am siiri m, má am si m Si mmɔɔ, ama, fa nyuma fa kɔ, am asi i, na am aŋu i.

eng: Yet because I have said these things to you, you are filled with sorrow.
local: Bo ma se ka m kɔ́ yaki am dɛɛ ti, am ahore asaaki agaya.

eng: Now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I shared with you before the world was created.
local: M Si, saŋga bo durunya nya bo má i bu ni, m yo woori u dɔ ɔ, ye a wura m kpiri a. Kisa, kaaki wura m kpiri u dɔ bekun.

eng: When he heard that Lazarus was ill, he stayed where he was. After two days.
local: Ama, i tiiri ka Lazarosi ada tukpaki ni, na wɔ ka deke bo i woori n cɛɛn nnyɔ abuka su.

eng: He said this not because he cared about the poor but because he was a thief. He carried the money bag and would take what was in it.
local: I ti awiefɔ n dɛɛ ti ye i seeri sɔ a, má ka i cɛcɛ wo yaari­fɔm nu wɔ. Yiri ti bu ŋwaa bita­fɔ a, na i suwɔ ŋwaa n be saŋga saŋga.

eng: When the hired hand sees the wolf coming, he leaves the sheep and runs away. Thats because he isnt the shepherd; the sheep arent really his. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
local: Pa­di­fɔ n bo ba fa i ka i sasa nbɔɛm n ti má bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ nahɔrɛ su, na nbɔɛm n ti má i dɛɛm. Nyini ti, nzɛn i ba ŋuuri ka bedi suba, i ŋmati yaki nbɔɛm wɔ. Na bedi n wura bu nu tara nbem, na bo ba ka n sandi.

eng: He knew who would betray him. Thats why he said, Not every one of you is clean.
local: Yesu siiri bo i bá tɔ i ma i kpɔ­fɔm ni, nyini ti ye i ŋa má bu kɛrɛ ti casi n a.

eng: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died and the prophets died, so who do you make yourself out to be.
local: A tara ya si Abraham bo wɔ wu n wɔ? I ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ awu. A bu u ŋu sɛ wɔ.

eng: We know that God doesnt listen to sinners. God listens to anyone who is devout and does Gods will.
local: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ de má satiɛ­yo­fɔ nyɛmɛsere, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su Nyɛmɛ atin su, na i yo i sa koroo­wa, Nyɛmɛ de i sere wɔ.

eng: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I kept my Fathers commandments and remain in his love.
local: Ma de m Si nɔaniɛ maa­wam n dɛɛ ti ye i koro m a. Nzɛn am gusu de m nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, m gusu kóro am sɔ ɔ.

eng: Philip said, Lord, show us the Father; that will be enough for us.
local: Na Filipu ase i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, kere ya u Si ni, deke bo ya koro i kɛrɛ lɛɛ.

eng: I assure you that it is better for you that I go away. If I dont go away, the Companion wont come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
local: Ama, nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, i ti am buka su wɔ ka m kɔ, dama nzɛn má m kɔ má, má Buka­fɔ ni bá má, ama, nzɛn m kɔɔri, m súnma i am dɔ.

eng: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me from this world. They were yours and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
local: Minɛm n bo a yiiri bu fiteeri durunya n nu fɛn, ma kere bu kabo a ti barasu. Bu tiiri u dɛɛm wɔ, ye a faari bu maari m a, ye ba de u nɔaniɛ n a.

eng: he said to his disciples, Lets return to Judea again.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm ka, Am ma ya kaaki kɔ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: Then Jesus said, Leave her alone. This perfume was to be used in preparation for my burial, and this is how she has used it.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Yaki Mariya bɛnlɛ, i dɛɛ nzee bo i sufa yo m fa kɔ m yiwee cɛɛn lɛɛ.

eng: Hes only a hired hand and the sheep dont matter to him.
local: I ti pa­di­fɔ n dɛɛ ti, na i cɛcɛ wo má nbɔɛm n nu, nyini ti ye i ŋmati yaki bu n a.

eng: After he washed the disciples feet, he put on his robes and returned to his place at the table. He said to them, Do you know what Ive done for you?.
local: I ŋmisi bu jaam wieeri ni, na wɔ fa i tanlɛɛ n awura, na wɔ kɔ atana i tanaa­biri ni. Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Kabo ma yo am ni, am ati i bu?.

eng: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is meaningless. My Father, who you say is your God, is the one who glorifies me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nzɛn m wura m ŋu kpiri, i ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. M Si wura m kpiri a, yiri bo am ŋa i ti am Nyɛmɛ ni.

eng: No one has ever heard of a healing of the eyes of someone born blind.
local: Fite durunya n bumboori, sɔnɔ be nya ti má ka be ateke sɔnɔ bo bu wuuri i na i ti anyinsin nyu­mbaa.

eng: I have said these things to you so that my joy will be in you and your joy will be complete.
local: Ma se am ahin sam na am ahore jɔ kabo m ahore ajɔ n wɔ, na am ahore jɔ n kɔ sin su.

eng: Jesus replied, Dont you know me, Philip, even after I have been with you all this time? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, Show us the Father?.
local: Na Yesu abisa i ka, Filipu, bo m ni ambɛrɛ awo bɛrɛ acɛ sɔ n na a si má m? Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ ŋu mini aŋu m Si lɛɛ, ye nzu ti ye am ŋa m kere am m Si n a?.

eng: When he comes, he will show the world it was wrong about sin, righteousness, and judgment.
local: I baari, i kére durunya n nu­fɔm deke bo i ti satiɛ, na i kére bu deke bo i ti sakpa na i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na i káaki kere bu kabo Nyɛmɛ bá di sɔnɔ kɛrɛ jɔrɛ barasu.

eng: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
local: Kisa bu si ka deke kɛrɛ bo a fa ama m ni, fite u dɔ ɔ.

eng: The disciples replied, Rabbi, the Jewish opposition wants to stone you, but you want to go back.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n ase i ka, Ya Kere­fɔ, má anzɔni fɛn ye Yuda­fɔm ŋa bu tu yabuɛ bo kun u n a? Ye a sukoro ka ya kpie ya nyi kɔ dɔ bekun n a.

eng: You will always have the poor among you, but you wont always have me.
local: Dama am ni yaari­fɔm ni wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a, ama, am ni mini koro wo má bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: I am the good shepherd. I know my own sheep and they know me.
local: Mini ti bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpa n a. Kabo m Si Nyɛmɛ si m na m si i ni, sɔ ye mini gusu si m dɛɛ nbɔɛm a, na bu gusu si m. Na m tɔ m dɛɛ ŋgɔɔ na m wu ma bu.

eng: You call me Teacher and Lord, and you speak correctly, because I am.
local: Am fere m ka am Kere­fɔ ɔ, na am kaaki fere m ka am Mibiɛ. Am jɔjɔ nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama m ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: You dont know him, but I do. If I said I didnt know him, I would be like you, a liar. But I do know him, and I keep his word.
local: ama, am si má i. Mini si i a. Haari nzɛn m ŋa m si má i, m yó kɔrɛ­fɔ ka ambɛrɛ wɔ. Ama, m si i, na m bita i nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: If this man wasnt from God, he couldnt do this.
local: Nzɛn má ahin sɔnɔ n afite má Nyɛmɛ dɔ, má i koro yo má pui.

eng: This is my commandment: love each other just as I have loved you.
local: M nɔaniɛ maa­wa n la ka o, am koro am beŋgu kabo m koro am ni.

eng: Dont you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I have spoken to you I dont speak on my own. The Father who dwells in me does his works.
local: A yo má yarada ka m ni m Si n ti deke kun wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Jɔrɛ n bo ma jɔjɔ ama am ni, má m tiɛŋgu jɔjɔɔ­wa, ama, m Si bo m ni yiri ti kun ni, yo i juma.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about sin because they dont believe in me.
local: I kére bu deke bo i ti satiɛ, dama bu yo má m yarada.

eng: This is because I gave them the words that you gave me, and they received them. They truly understood that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
local: Dama nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ bo a jɔjɔ fa maari m ni, m gusu ajɔjɔ ama bu, ye ba de i, ye ba si ka i ti nahɔrɛ ka m fiteeri u dɔ ɔ, ye ba yo yarada ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a.

eng: Jesus answered, Arent there twelve hours in the day? Whoever walks in the day doesnt stumble because they see the light of the world.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Fa ŋgarama kɔ ju naasɔ, m ŋa i ti wiɛ kun wɔ e. Sɔnɔ bo i nati wiɛsu bo má i jaa, dama durunya n nu wein ni ma i ŋu asi a.

eng: Many Jews learned that he was there. They came not only because of Jesus but also to see Lazarus, whom he had raised from the dead.
local: Yuda­fɔm beberebe tiiri ka Yesu woori dɔ ni, na ba ba i dɔ, má ka Yesu ŋgumi ti ye bu baari a, ama, bu baari ka bu ba ŋu Lazarosi n bo Yesu teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu n wɔ.

eng: I have other sheep that dont belong to this sheep pen. I must lead them too. They will listen to my voice and there will be one flock, with one shepherd.
local: M la nbɔɛ nyiŋga nbem bo bu ti m dɛɛm, bo bu wo má ahin nbɔɛm n nu. Wɔ yo daka ka m fa bu gusu ba wura ahinm n nu. Bɛrɛ gusu tí m konvi na bu dé i, na bu yó kun na bu sasa­fɔ yó kun.

eng: If I, your Lord and teacher, have washed your feet, you too must wash each others feet.
local: Tɔ. Kisa na mini bo m ti am Mibiɛ, ni am Kere­fɔ, na ma ŋmisi am jaam ni, wɔ yo daka ka ambɛrɛ gusu ŋmisi am beŋgum jaam sɔ.

eng: Your father Abraham was overjoyed that he would see my day. He saw it and was happy.
local: I yoori am si Abraham fiɛ ka i ŋu m ba n nu, i ŋuuri i, na i ahore ajɔ.

eng: They responded, You were born completely in sin! How is it that you dare to teach us? Then they expelled him.
local: Na ba se i ka, Wɔrɔ yiri, bu wuuri u satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, ye wɔrɔ koro ka a kere yɛrɛ a? Na ba fɔn i afite.

eng: No one has greater love than to give up ones life for ones friends.
local: Koro be wo má ahin koro n sin, ka sɔnɔ wu i danfom ti.

eng: Trust me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or at least believe on account of the works themselves.
local: Bo m ŋa, m ni m Si n ti deke kun ni, am yo m yarada. Nzɛn má nyini, am yo m yarada sam bo m yo bu n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Im praying for them. Im not praying for the world but for those you gave me, because they are yours.
local: M susere u ma bɛrɛ wɔ, má bo bu wo durunya n nu ni, ama, minɛm bo a fa bu ama m ni, bɛrɛ ye m sere u ma bu a, dama bu ti u dɛɛ minɛm wɔ.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about righteousness because Im going to the Father and you wont see me anymore.
local: Na i kére bu deke bo i ti kpa na i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, dama m sukɔ m Si n dɔ ɔ, na am ŋú má m kun.

eng: But whoever walks in the night does stumble because the light isnt in them.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ bo i nati kɔŋguɛ bo i jaa, dama wein wo má i dɔ.

eng: The chief priests decided that they would kill Lazarus too.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n asɛsɛ su ka bu kun Lazarosi gusu.

eng: This is why the Father loves me: I give up my life so that I can take it up again.
local: M tɔ m dɛɛ ŋgɔɔ fa ma m minɛm, na m káaki de i bekun, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m Si koro m n a.

eng: I have given you an example: Just as I have done, you also must do.
local: Dama ma yo akere am lɛɛ, na am gusu yo am beŋgum kabo ma yo am ni.

eng: You arent even years old! the Jewish opposition replied. How can you say that you have seen Abraham.
local: Na ba se i ka, A nya ju má afɔ aburu­nu, ye i yo sɛ ye a ŋa a ŋuuri Abraham a.

eng: Jesus heard they had expelled the man born blind. Finding him, Jesus said, Do you believe in the Human One.
local: Yesu tiiri ka ba fɔn ayi sɔnɔ n ni, na wɔ kɔ aŋu i, na wɔ bisa i ka, A yo Adamande Wa n yarada a.

eng: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
local: Nzɛn am su m nɔaniɛ maa­wa n su, am ti m danfom wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: I assure you that whoever believes in me will do the works that I do. They will do even greater works than these because I am going to the Father.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, yiri mmɔɔ yó ahin sam bo m yo bu ni, na i yó bo bu tara ahin mmɔɔ, dama m sukɔ m Si dɔ ɔ.

eng: Everything that is mine is yours and everything that is yours is mine; I have been glorified in them.
local: M minɛm kɛrɛ ti u dɛɛm wɔ, ye u dɛɛm gusu kɛrɛ ti m dɛɛm, ye ba ma minɛm aŋu m jirima woo­wa ni.

eng: He will show the world it was wrong about judgment because this worlds ruler stands condemned.
local: Na i káaki kere bu kabo Nyɛmɛ bá di sɔnɔ kɛrɛ jɔrɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ adi Sitana bo i ti durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ tii n jɔrɛ ayo i ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus went out with his disciples and crossed over to the other side of the Kidron Valley. He and his disciples entered a garden there.
local: Yesu sereeri Nyɛmɛ wieeri ni, na i ni i susu­fɔm n ayasu akɔ akpiɛ Kɛdɔrɔn nzuetin n buekun dɔ, deke bo baka fie be wo ni, na ba kɔ awura baka fie n nu.

eng: It was because of Lazarus that many of the Jews had deserted them and come to believe in Jesus.
local: Dama i dɛɛ ti ye Yuda­fɔm dɔŋgu akaaki bu sin abo bu a, ye ba yo Yesu yarada n a.

eng: He continued, Our friend Lazarus is sleeping, but I am going in order to wake him up.
local: Yesu jɔjɔɔri sɔ n sin, na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Ya danfo Lazarosi adaafi, ama, m sukɔ na m kɔ teŋge i lɛɛ.

eng: No one takes it from me, but I give it up because I want to. I have the right to give it up, and I have the right to take it up again. I received this commandment from my Father.
local: Be sude má m dɛɛ ŋgɔɔ ni m dɔ, ama, mini ni m tii sutɔ i m koroya­nu a. M la atin bo m tɔ m dɛɛ ŋgɔɔ ni, na m kaaki la atin bo m de i bekun. M Si maari m nɔɔ ka m yo sɔ a.

eng: I assure you, servants arent greater than their master, nor are those who are sent greater than the one who sent them.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, akɔɔ koro tara má i mibiɛ, na bekun sunma­fɔ gusu koro tara má bo i sunmaari i ni.

eng: I assure you, Jesus replied, before Abraham was, I Am.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, kana bu ba wuuri Abraham ni, mini wo bɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: He answered, Who is he, sir? I want to believe in him.
local: Na sɔnɔ n abisa i ka, M Mibiɛ, yiri la ŋma a? Se m na m yo i yarada.

eng: I dont call you servants any longer, because servants dont know what their master is doing. Instead, I call you friends, because everything I heard from my Father I have made known to you.
local: M fere am m danfom wɔ, m fere má am ka m akɔɔm kun, dama sɔnɔ akɔɔ si má i mibiɛ sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ, ama, deke kɛrɛ bo ma ti i m Si dɔ ni, ma fa akere am.

eng: I will do whatever you ask for in my name, so that the Father can be glorified in the Son.
local: Na deke kɛrɛ bo am fa m duma fa sereeri i, m yó i ma am, adena mini Baa ni má minɛm wura m Si kpiri.

eng: Im no longer in the world, but they are in the world, even as Im coming to you. Holy Father, watch over them in your name, the name you gave me, that they will be one just as we are one.
local: Kisa, m suba u dɔ lɛɛ, m ká má durunya n nu fɛn kun, ama, bɛrɛ ká fɛn wɔ. M Si, wɔrɔ bo a ti casi ni, yo bu kanda u duma n bo a fa maari m n yiko nu, na bu yo deke kun, kabo mini ni wɔrɔ ti kun ni.

eng: I have much more to say to you, but you cant handle it now.
local: M toko la jɔrɛ dɔŋgu bo má m se am, ama, am koro de má i kɛrɛ kisa.

eng: Judas, his betrayer, also knew the place because Jesus often gathered there with his disciples.
local: Yudasi bo i sutɔ Yesu ma i kpɔ­fɔm ni si dika n nu, dama Yesu ni i susu­fɔm nati tiɛn bu nɔɔ dɔ ɔ saŋga saŋga.

eng: The next day the great crowd that had come for the festival heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem.
local: Na alecin na jama n bo bu baari ka bu ba di Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n ati ka Yesu suba Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: The disciples said, Lord, if hes sleeping, he will get well.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n ase i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, nzɛn wɔ daafi wɔ, i nyá laifiɛ.

eng: There was another division among the Jews because of Jesus words.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, na Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nɔɔ awo i ŋguŋgumi bekun.

eng: Since you know these things, you will be happy if you do them.
local: Nzɛn am sin ahin n kɛrɛ, na am yoori sɔ, tere­fiɛ wo am dɔ.

eng: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na ba fa yabuɛ ka bu bo kun i, ama, na wɔ lɔrɔki afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu.

eng: Jesus said, You have seen him. In fact, he is the one speaking with you.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, A ŋu i e, yiri la mini n bo m sujɔjɔ u dɔ ni.

eng: You didnt choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you could go and produce fruit and so that your fruit could last. As a result, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
local: Má ambɛrɛ yiiri m a, ama, mini ayi am ase ka am kɔ su suu­wa bo i wó bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ, na deke kɛrɛ bo am bá fa m duma n sereeri m Si, i yó i ma am.

eng: When you ask me for anything in my name, I will do it.
local: Na nzɛn am ba faari m duma fa sereeri m deke be, m yó i ma am.

eng: When I was with them, I watched over them in your name, the name you gave to me, and I kept them safe. None of them were lost, except the one who was destined for destruction, so that scripture would be fulfilled.
local: Saŋga bo m woori bu dɔ ni, m yoori bu kanda u duma n bo afa ama m yiko nu wɔ. M niɛɛri bu su, na bu be aka má ŋgbɛɛn, má ka bo wɔ ka ŋgbɛɛn haari i ni kunnu ni, adena kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n yo yii­wa.

eng: However, when the Spirit of Truth comes, he will guide you in all truth. He wont speak on his own, but will say whatever he hears and will proclaim to you what is to come.
local: Ama, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i se nahɔrɛ n ba baari, yiri kére am nahɔrɛ atin kɛrɛ a, dama jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo i kán kere am ni, má i dɛɛ yiko nu ye i fá kan, ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ kére i ni, sɔ ye i kán, na i kére am sam bo bu bá yo ni.

eng: Judas brought a company of soldiers and some guards from the chief priests and Pharisees. They came there carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
local: Na Yudasi aba dɔ. Sojam su i su, ni tintini­jina­fɔm bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ni Farasi­fɔm asunma bu n buka su. Bu bita fitanam, ni wura sinm, ni biɛsɔya neŋgem.

eng: They took palm branches and went out to meet him. They shouted, Hosanna! Blessings on the one who comes in the name of the Lord! Blessings on the king of Israel.
local: Na ba kpiɛkpiɛ kpakpa nyawa akɔ akpa i atin, na bu subo kuyigindi, bu ŋa, Hosana . Ya kansi Nyɛmɛ. Tere­fiɛ wo yiri n bo i suba Micɛra n dɛɛ duma n nu n dɔ. Tere­fiɛ wo Yisareli­fɔm fɛmɛ n dɔ.

eng: They thought Jesus meant that Lazarus was in a deep sleep, but Jesus had spoken about Lazarus death.
local: Kabo Yesu seeri n la ka, Lazarosi awu wɔ, ama, i susu­fɔm bɛrɛ sunsun ka i ŋa wɔ daafi nahɔrɛ su wɔ.

eng: Many of them said, He has a demon and has lost his mind. Why listen to him.
local: Na bu dɔŋgu n ase ka, Ajini wo i ŋu, nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ bo fiɛ a. Nzu ti ye am so am sui i dɔ a.

eng: Im not speaking about all of you. I know those whom Ive chosen. But this is to fulfill the scripture, The one who eats my bread has turned against me.
local: M sujɔjɔ ni má am kɛrɛ wɔ, dama m si bo ma du mɔ ayi bu ni. Ama, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, i ti ye ni ka i yo yii­wa, ka, Sɔnɔ n bo m ni yiri saŋga di diire taalɛ kun nu, yiri akaaki m jaa tu­fɔ n a.

eng: The man said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped Jesus.
local: Na sɔnɔ n ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, ma yo u yarada. Na wɔ kutu Yesu nyunu akansi i.

eng: I give you these commandments so that you can love each other.
local: Nɔaniɛ maa­wam n bo m sufa ma am la ka, am koro am beŋgum.

eng: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nzɛn am koro m, am dé m nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni.

eng: Now Im coming to you and I say these things while Im in the world so that they can share completely in my joy.
local: Kisa m suba u dɔ lɛɛ, ama, m toko wo durunya n nu ye m jɔjɔ ahin jɔjɔɔ­wa n na bu nya m dɛɛ ahorejɔ n kɔ sin su.

eng: He will glorify me, because he will take what is mine and proclaim it to you.
local: Yiri wúra m kpiri a, dama i kán m ŋu jɔrɛ kere am.

eng: Jesus knew everything that was to happen to him, so he went out and asked, Who are you looking for.
local: Yesu si sa kɛrɛ bo i bá tu i ni, na wɔ kpa bu atin abisa bu ka, Am koro ŋma a.

eng: Jesus found a young donkey and sat on it, just as it is written.
local: Na Yesu aŋu kaako­baa be, na wɔ fun atana su, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni.

eng: Jesus told them plainly, Lazarus has died.
local: Nyini ti, na Yesu ase bu kpain ka, Lazarosi awu.

eng: Others said, These arent the words of someone who has a demon. Can a demon heal the eyes of people who are blind.
local: Ama, nbem ŋa, Má sɔnɔ bo ajini wo i ŋu jɔjɔɔ­wa la ahin. Ajini yo sɛ koro teke anyinsin nyu­mbaa.

eng: Im telling you this now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe that I Am.
local: M suse am sa bo i bá yo wɔ lɛɛ, na i ba yoori, na am yo m yarada ka mini la sɔnɔhɔrɛ n bo ma se am kabo m ti n a.

eng: Jesus said, I have come into the world to exercise judgment so that those who dont see can see and those who see will become blind.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, M baari durunya n nu ka m ba di minɛm jɔrɛ wɔ, na bo bu ŋu má asi n ŋú asi, na bo bu ŋu asi n yó anyinsinm.

eng: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
local: Na Yesu asa su ase bu ka, Nzɛn durunya n nu­fɔm kpɔ am, am si nu ka o, ba du mɔ akpɔ mini wɔ, ye bu nya ba sukpɔ am n a.

eng: I will ask the Father, and he will send another Companion, who will be with you forever.
local: M sére m Si Nyɛmɛ, na i má am Buka­fɔ fɔfɔrɛ bo i bá wo am dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: I gave your word to them and the world hated them, because they dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Ma fa u nɔaniɛ n afa ama bu, na kabo m ti má durunya n nu fɛn fɔ be ni, sɔ ye bu gusu ti má durunya n nu fɛn fɔm a, nyini dɛɛ ti, durunya n nu­fɔm koro má bu jɔrɛ.

eng: Everything that the Father has is mine. Thats why I said that the Spirit takes what is mine and will proclaim it to you.
local: Deke kɛrɛ bo i ti m Si dɛɛ, i ti m dɛɛ wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma se am ka, i kán m ŋu jɔrɛ kere am n a.

eng: They answered, Jesus the Nazarene. He said to them, I Am.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ase i ka, Ya koro Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n wɔ. Na wɔ se bu ka, Yiri la mini. Yudasi bo i tɔɔri i ni jina sojam n nu.

eng: For your sakes, Im glad I wasnt there so that you can believe. Lets go to him.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo m fiɛ ka ma wo má dɔ, adena am yo yarada. Am ma ya kɔ i dɔ.

eng: Dont be afraid, Daughter Zion. Look! Your king is coming , sitting on a donkeys colt .
local: Ambɛrɛ Siyon­fɔm, má am soro sɛrɛ. Am niɛ, am fɛmɛ ni suba n a, i fun kaako­baa wɔ.

eng: The time came for the Festival of Dedication in Jerusalem. It was winter.
local: Fufuku saŋga na Yuda­fɔm ba sudi bu cɛɛn­kpiri n Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na bu teŋge su kabo bu yo tekeeri Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n ni, na bu yoori i dɔŋgu ni.

eng: I assure you that whoever receives someone I send receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de sɔnɔ bo m sunma i ni, i mi ade mini wɔ lɛɛ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de mini, i mi ade yiri bo i sunmaari m wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Some Pharisees who were with him heard what he said and asked, Surely we arent blind, are we.
local: Farasi­fɔm nbem bo bu woori i bi dɔ n tiiri sɔ ni, na ba se i ka, Ŋka a ŋa a se ka yɛrɛ gusu ti anyinsinm wɔ lɛɛ fan.

eng: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, I have chosen you out of the world, and you dont belong to the world. This is why the world hates you.
local: Nzɛn má am ti durunya n nu­fɔm dɛɛ minɛm, má bu koro am kabo bu koro bu beŋgu n wɔ. Ama, bo ma yi am afite durunya n nu­fɔm nu, na am ti má durunya n nu­fɔm dɛɛ minɛm n ti, ye bu kpɔ am n a.

eng: Im not asking that you take them out of this world but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
local: M susere má u ka a yi bu fite durunya n nu wɔ, ama, m susere u ka a kanda Sitana satiɛ­yo­fɔ ni, na i yaki bu.

eng: This Companion is the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cant receive because it neither sees him nor recognizes him. You know him, because he lives with you and will be with you.
local: Yiri la Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i se nahɔrɛ ni. Durunya n nu­fɔm koro de má i, dama bu ŋu má i, na bekun bu kaaki si má i, ama, ambɛrɛ si i, dama i wo am dɔ, na i bá wo am nu.

eng: Soon you wont be able to see me; soon after that, you will see me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Wɔ ka kaan na am ŋú má m, ama, i ba yoori kaan buka su na am ŋú m bekun.

eng: When he said, I Am, they shrank back and fell to the ground.
local: Yesu seeri ka, Yiri la mini ni, na bu kɛrɛ akɔkɔ bu sin akpakpaasi asiɛŋgu.

eng: Then Thomas said to the other disciples, Let us go too so that we may die with Jesus.
local: Na Tomasi bo bu fere i ka Ndaa n ase i lamɔkɔ susu­fɔm n ka, Am ma yɛrɛ gusu kɔ, nzɛn bu kɔ kuunri i a, na ya ni yiri kɛrɛ awu.

eng: His disciples didnt understand these things at first. After he was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that they had done these things to him.
local: Nyini saŋga, i susu­fɔm nya si má nyini sam n bo ba yo n kɛrɛ bu. Má ka saŋga bo Yesu ba teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu, na bu ŋuuri i jirima woo­wa n sin, na ba nya ateŋge su, ka ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka bu yó i sɔ ɔ, na ba kaaki ateŋge su, ka ba yo i sɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: and Jesus was in the temple, walking in the covered porch named for Solomon.
local: Na Yesu sunati bara yɛ Solomon Lɛlɛ n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni.

eng: After he said these things, Jesus was deeply disturbed and testified, I assure you, one of you will betray me.
local: Yesu seeri bu sɔ wieeri ni, na i ahore asaaki akɔ asin su, na wɔ se bu kpain ka, Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, am nu kun bá tɔ m ma m kpɔ­fɔm a.

eng: Jesus said to them, If you were blind, you wouldnt have any sin, but now that you say, We see, your sin remains.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Nzɛn má am ti anyinsinm, má am la má ŋgasi, ama, kabo am nya ŋa am ŋu asi ni, am toko la ŋgasi lɛɛ.

eng: Remember what I told you, Servants arent greater than their master. If the world harassed me, it will harass you too. If it kept my word, it will also keep yours.
local: Má am ma am wara fi ka ma se am ka, akɔɔ tara má i mibiɛ. Nzɛn bu yo kereeri m wuwuruwa, sɔ ye bu kére am gusu wuwuruwa a. Na nzɛn bu yo deeri m nɔaniɛ, bu dé am gusu dɛɛ ni.

eng: They dont belong to this world, just as I dont belong to this world.
local: Kabo m ti má durunya n nu fɛn fɔ ni, sɔ ye bu gusu ti má durunya n nu fɛn fɔm a.

eng: I wont leave you as orphans. I will come to you.
local: M kɔɔri, m yaki má am na am ka ayikam, m káaki ba am dɔ.

eng: Some of Jesus disciples said to each other, What does he mean: Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me and Because Im going to the Father?.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n nbem abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Nzu jɔrɛ ye i jɔjɔɔri i sɔ n a? I ŋa, wɔ ka kaan na ya ŋú má i, ama, i ba yoori kaan buka su, ya ŋú i bekun. Ye wɔ kaaki ase bekun ka, i sukɔ i Si dɔ ɔ.

eng: He asked them again, Who are you looking for? They said, Jesus the Nazarene.
local: Na wɔ kaaki abisa bu bekun ka, Am koro ŋma a? Na ba se i ka, Ya koro Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n wɔ.

eng: When Jesus arrived, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu kɔ juuri ni, i ŋuuri ka ba se Lazarosi i le­nna lɛɛ.

eng: The crowd who had been with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead were testifying about him.
local: Na jama n bo bu yo woori Yesu dɔ, saŋga n bo i fereeri Lazarosi fiteeri faŋ­kunma n nu, na i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni, na bu sukan labari ni.

eng: The Jewish opposition circled around him and asked, How long will you test our patience? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
local: Na Yuda­fɔ nbem atiɛn bu nɔɔ abara ayɛ i, na ba bisa i ka, Saŋga bɔnɔ ye a bá kan teke ya nyi a? Nzɛn wɔrɔ ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a, se ya nahɔrɛ kpain.

eng: His disciples looked at each other, confused about which of them he was talking about.
local: Na i susu­fɔm n abirisi, suniɛ bu ŋuŋu nu, ama, ba si má bo i yó nyini sa n ni.

eng: The world will do all these things to you on account of my name, because it doesnt know the one who sent me.
local: Bu yó am nyini n kɛrɛ, m dɛɛ ti, dama bu si má m Si bo i sunmaari m ni.

eng: Make them holy in the truth; your word is truth.
local: U nɔaniɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Ma u nɔaniɛ n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n yo bu casi.

eng: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
local: Wɔ ka kaan na durunya n nu­fɔm ŋu má m kun, ama, ambɛrɛ ŋú m. M la ŋgɔɔ n dɛɛ ti, am gusu nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: What does he mean by soon? We dont understand what hes talking about.
local: Na ba kaaki abisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ye ahin dɛɛ, i ba yoori kaan n bu la ka sɛ wɔ lɛɛ? Ya si má i jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu.

eng: Jesus answered, I told you, I Am. If you are looking for me, then let these people go.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ma se am ka yiri la mini. Nzɛn am koro mini wɔ, am ma ahin minɛm bɛrɛ kɔ.

eng: Bethany was a little less than two miles from Jerusalem.
local: Betani ni Yɛrusalem miɛm waari má ni i beŋgu.

eng: Thats why the crowd came to meet him, because they had heard about this miraculous sign that he had done.
local: Nyini alekutura sa n bo i yoori i na minɛm tiiri n dɛɛ ti, na ba kpa i atin.

eng: Jesus answered, I have told you, but you dont believe. The works I do in my Fathers name testify about me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ma se am, ye am ade má a. Alekutura sam bo m yo bu m Si duma n nu ni, di m ŋu daani a.

eng: One of the disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was at Jesus side.
local: Bu nu kun be bo Yesu koro i agaya n tana mata Yesu.

eng: If I hadnt come and spoken to the people of this world, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
local: Nzɛn má ma ba akan má m ŋu jɔrɛ akere má bu, má bu si má ka ba yo satiɛ, ama, kisa na ma kan akere bu ni, bu koro kan fite má nu ka ba yo má satiɛ.

eng: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
local: Kabo a sunmaari m durunya n nu ni, sɔ ye m gusu asunma bu a.

eng: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, you are in me, and I am in you.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n ye am sí ka mini ni m Si n ti deke kun a, na mini ni ambɛrɛ gusu ti deke kun wɔ.

eng: Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, so he said, Are you trying to find out from each other what I meant when I said, Soon you wont see me, and soon after that you will see me?.
local: Na Yesu afa nu ka bu koro ka bu bisa i nyini jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu wɔ. Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Bo m seeri ka wɔ ka kaan na am ŋú má m, ama, i ba yoori kaan buka su, am ŋú m bekun n ye am subisa am ŋuŋu nu, na am si i bu, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: This was so that the word he had spoken might be fulfilled: I didnt lose anyone of those whom you gave me.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na i nɔaniɛ bo i yo jɔjɔɔri i n ayo yii­wa, ka, Minɛm bo a fa bu ama m ni, m ma má bu be ka má ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Many Jews had come to comfort Martha and Mary after their brothers death.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm dɔŋgu aba Marata, ni Mariya dɔ ka bu ba dada bu ahore, bu niwa n bo wɔ wu n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Therefore, the Pharisees said to each other, See! Youve accomplished nothing! Look! The whole world is following him.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm n ase bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Am aŋu má i? Ya koro yo má pui kun. Yiri ye durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ susu i su n a.

eng: but you dont believe because you dont belong to my sheep.
local: Ama, am yo má m yarada, dama am ti má m dɛɛ nbɔɛm.

eng: Simon Peter nodded at him to get him to ask Jesus who he was talking about.
local: Na Simon Petoro abo i nyu­mbaa i dɔ ka i bisa Yesu ka, i ni ŋma a?.

eng: Whoever hates me also hates the Father.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kpɔ m, i kpɔ mini ni m Si n kɛrɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: I made myself holy on their behalf so that they also would be made holy in the truth.
local: Bɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, ye ma fa m ŋu ama u a, adena bɛrɛ gusu fa bu ŋu ma u nahɔrɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them loves me. Whoever loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de m nɔaniɛ maa­wam, na i su i su, i mi ti bo i koro m a, na bo i koro m ni, m Si gusu koro yiri wɔ, na mini mmɔɔ kóro i mi, na m yí m ŋu kere i.

eng: I assure you that you will cry and lament, and the world will be happy. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will turn into joy.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, ambɛrɛ bá sun na am kóko, ama, na durunya n nu­fɔm bɛrɛ ahore jɔ́. Ambɛrɛ ahore bá saaki, ama, am ahoresaaki ni bá kaaki ahorejɔ.

eng: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priests servant, cutting off his right ear.
local: Nyini saŋga Simon Petoro jina bita takobe, na wɔ cin awɔrɔ i akpiɛ sɔnɔ be sui, i saa fɔmbɔrɔ dɛɛ ni, nyini sɔnɔ n ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n akɔɔ ɔ. Bu fere i ka Malikusi.

eng: When Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary remained in the house.
local: Marata tiiri ka Yesu suba ni, na wɔ kpa i atin, ama, na Mariya aka awuru.

eng: Some Greeks were among those who had come up to worship at the festival.
local: Minɛm bo bu baari Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n saŋga n ka bu ba su Nyɛmɛ ni, bu nbem ti Giriki­fɔm wɔ.

eng: My sheep listen to my voice. I know them and they follow me.
local: M dɛɛ nbɔɛm ti m konvi. M si bu, na bu su m su.

eng: Leaning back toward Jesus, this disciple asked, Lord, who is it.
local: Na yiri n acin adodo Yesu, na wɔ bisa i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, a ni ŋma a.

eng: If I hadnt done works among them that no one else had done, they wouldnt be sinners. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
local: Ma yo alekutura sam bo ba ŋu i. Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro yo nyini alekutura sam sɔ. Nzɛn má ma yo má nyini alekutura sam na ba ŋu má i, má be fa má bu ŋgasi ka bu ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm. Ama, kisa, ba ŋu nyini n kɛrɛ, na bu toko kpɔ m ni m Si ni.

eng: Judas asked, Lord, why are you about to reveal yourself to us and not to the world.
local: Na Yudasi, má Yudasi Isikariyoti ni, ase ka, Ya Mibiɛ, nzu ti ye a yí u ŋu kere yɛrɛ a, ye a yí má u ŋu kere má durunya n nu­fɔm a.

eng: Im not praying only for them but also for those who believe in me because of their word.
local: M susere má u ma má bɛrɛ ŋgumi wɔ, ama, m susere u ma minɛm bo bu bá yo m yarada ahinm dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kaan­wa n ti wɔ.

eng: When a woman gives birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when the child is born, she no longer remembers her distress because of her joy that a child has been born into the world.
local: Nzɛn bara anya akutu ka i wu baa, i la ahoresaaki, dama i wahara saŋga ye wɔ ju a, ama, i ba wuuri baa n wieeri, i téŋge má i wahara n bo wɔ sin n su kun, dama baa n bo wɔ wu i durunya n nu n ti, i ahore ajɔ.

eng: Jesus told Peter, Put your sword away! Am I not to drink the cup the Father has given me.
local: Na Yesu ase Petoro ka, Fa u takobe n wura i buɛ nu. A ŋa má m ŋu anyum­boro n bo m Si afa ama m n wɔ.

eng: Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Na Marata ase Yesu ka, M Mibiɛ, nzɛn má a woori fɛn, má m niwa n awu má.

eng: They came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and made a request: Sir, we want to see Jesus.
local: Na ba ba Filipu dɔ, i fite miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Besayida na i wo Galili awɔrɛ n nu n wɔ. Na ba se i ka, Njabiɛ, ya koro ka ya ŋu Yesu wɔ.

eng: I give them eternal life. They will never die, and no one will snatch them from my hand.
local: M ma bu ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri, na bu ká má ŋgbɛɛn cɛɛn be fieo. Be gusu koro fin má bu fite má m saa nu.

eng: Jesus answered, Its the one to whom I will give this piece of bread once I have dipped into the bowl.Then he dipped the piece of bread and gave it to Judas, Simon Iscariots son.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Sɔnɔ n bo m kpíɛ too n bo toro n nu na m fá ma i ni, yiri lɛɛ. Na wɔ fa too n abo toro n nu, na wɔ fa ama Yudasi, Simon Isikariyoti wa ni.

eng: This fulfills the word written in their Law, They hated me without a reason.
local: Nyini ma kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n ayo yii­wa n a, ka, Bu kpɔ m kpɔ ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.
