eng: Jesus answered, Whoever loves me will keep my word. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro m, i dé m nɔaniɛ ni, na m Si mmɔɔ kóro i mi, na ya bá, na ya bá wo i dɔ.

eng: I pray they will be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. I pray that they also will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
local: M Si, m sere u ka a ma bu kɛrɛ yo kun, kabo a wo m dɔ, na m gusu wo u dɔ ni, ma bɛrɛ gusu wo ya dɔ, adena durunya n nu­fɔm yo yarada ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a.

eng: In the same way, you have sorrow now; but I will see you again, and you will be overjoyed. No one takes away your joy.
local: I ti sɔ ɔ, am gusu dɔ, kisa, am la ahoresaaki, ama, m bá ŋu am bekun, na am ahore jɔ́ bo sɔnɔ koro dé má am ahorejɔ n am dɔ.

eng: Then the company of soldiers, the commander, and the guards from the Jewish leaders took Jesus into custody. They bound him.
local: Na sojam, ni bu kpiɛn, ni Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n tintini­jina­fɔm n atara Yesu acici i nyama.

eng: Even now I know that whatever you ask God, God will give you.
local: Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, m si ka deke kɛrɛ bo a sere Nyɛmɛ, i yó i ma u.

eng: Philip told Andrew, and Andrew and Philip told Jesus.
local: Na Filipu akɔ ase Andere, na bu nnyɔ n kɛrɛ akɔ ase Yesu.

eng: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them from my Fathers hand.
local: M Si bo i faari bu maari m ni, tara deke kɛrɛ faŋga, na be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro fin bu fite m Si saa nu.

eng: After Judas took the bread, Satan entered into him. Jesus told him, What you are about to do, do quickly.
local: Yesu faari too n maari Yudasi wieeri ni, na Sitana awura i tii nu, na Yesu ase i ka, Sa n bo a ŋa a yó i ni, kɔ yo i ndende.

eng: When the Companion comes, whom I will send from the Father—the Spirit of Truth who proceeds from the Father—he will testify about me.
local: Na Buka­fɔ n bo m súnma i fite m Si dɔ na i bá am dɔ ni, yiri ti Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i kere kabo Nyɛmɛ ti nahɔrɛ su n a, yiri dí m ŋusu daani kere am a.

eng: Whoever doesnt love me doesnt keep my words. The word that you hear isnt mine. It is the word of the Father who sent me.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má m, i dé má m nɔaniɛ ni. Nɔaniɛ n bo ma jɔjɔ i ni, i ti má m dɛɛ nɔaniɛ, i ti m Si n bo i sunmaari m dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Ive given them the glory that you gave me so that they can be one just as we are one.
local: U jirima woo­wa n bo a maari m ni, ma fa ama bu, na bu yó kun kabo m ni wɔrɔ ti kun ni.

eng: In that day, you wont ask me anything. I assure you that the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
local: Nyini cɛɛn ni, am bísa má deke be busu m dɔ. Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am a, deke kɛrɛ bo am sereeri m Si ni, m duma n nu, i yó i ma am.

eng: and led him first to Annas. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year. .
local: Bu du mɔ faari i kɔɔri sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Anasi n dɔ ɔ ka. Anasi sibiɛ la Kayafasi. Kayafasi ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n nyini afɔ n a.

eng: Jesus told her, Your brother will rise again.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, U niwa ni téŋge i yiwee n nu bekun.

eng: I and the Father are one.
local: Mini ni m Si ti deke kun wɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, The time has come for the Human One to be glorified.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Saŋga n aju bo Nyɛmɛ wúra mini Adamande Wa n kpiri.

eng: No one sitting at the table understood why Jesus said this to him.
local: Ama, bu kɛrɛ bo bu woori diire n di su ni, bu be mmɔɔ asi má deke n bo i ti na Yesu seeri i sɔ ni.

eng: You will testify too, because you have been with me from the beginning.
local: Na am gusu di m daani, dama m ni ambɛrɛ wo bɛrɛ a, fite m juma n bumboori ba ju ni kisa.

eng: I have spoken these things to you while I am with you.
local: M toko wo am dɔ, ye m suse am ahin kɛrɛ a.

eng: Im in them and you are in me so that they will be made perfectly one. Then the world will know that you sent me and that you have loved them just as you loved me.
local: M wo bu dɔ, na u gusu wo m dɔ, nyini ma bu yo deke kun kabo a koro ka bu ti n a, na durunya n nu­fɔm si ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a, na kabo a koro m ni, a koro u minɛm gusu sɔ ɔ.

eng: Up to now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask and you will receive so that your joy will be complete.
local: Am nya sere má m Si deke be m duma n nu haari ni nyuma. Kisa am sere i, na am nyá i, na am ahore jɔ́ kɔ sin su.

eng: Caiaphas was the one who had advised the Jewish leaders that it was better for one person to die for the people.
local: Yiri Kayafasi ti bo i yo seeri Yuda­fɔm ka i ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kun wu ma minɛm kɛrɛ n a.

eng: Martha replied, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
local: Na Marata ase i ka, M si ka i bá teŋge i yiwee nu jina cɛɛn, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge minɛm bu yiwee nu ni.

eng: Again the Jewish opposition picked up stones in order to stone him.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm afa yabuɛ bekun ka bu bo kun i.

eng: I assure you that unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it can only be a single seed. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse am n a, má ka alɛ­mbaa awura fa nu, na wɔ kpɔrɔ, i ti i tii wɔ. Ama, nzɛn i wuraari fa nu na i kpɔrɔɔri, ye i wúra mbaa beberebe a.

eng: Some thought that, since Judas kept the money bag, Jesus told him, Go, buy what we need for the feast, or that he should give something to the poor.
local: Yudasi tiiri bu ŋwaa bita­fɔ n dɛɛ ti, bu nbem sunsun ka Yesu ŋa i kɔ to deke be na bu fa di cɛɛn­kpiri n wɔ, wara i kɔ yi ŋwaa n be fa ma yaari­fɔm wɔ.

eng: The Companion, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I told you.
local: ama, m Si Nyɛmɛ súnma Buka­fɔ ni, m duma n nu, yiri la Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni, yiri kére am deke kɛrɛ a, na i káaki teŋge am su fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo ma kere am ni.

eng: Father, I want those you gave me to be with me where I am. Then they can see my glory, which you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
local: M Si, m koro ka a ma minɛm bo a fa bu ama m n bá wo m dɔ, deke bo m wo ni, na bu ŋú m jirima woo­wa n bo a fa maari m ni, dama saŋga bo a nya ayi má durunya n ye a koro m a.

eng: Ive been using figures of speech with you. The time is coming when I will no longer speak to you in such analogies. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ma se am ahin n kɛrɛ anyundɛrɛ nu wɔ lɛɛ, saŋga suba bo m bó má am anyundɛrɛ kun, ama, m kán m Si ŋu jɔrɛ kɛrɛ kpain kere am a.

eng: Simon Peter and another disciple followed Jesus. Because this other disciple was known to the high priest, he went with Jesus into the high priests courtyard.
local: Bu taraari Yesu sukɔ ni, na Simon Petoro ni Yesu susu­fɔ n kun asu i su akɔ. Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n si nyini Yesu susu­fɔ n dɛɛ ti, ye i suuri Yesu su wuraari yiri Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n gisan nu a.

eng: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me will live, even though they die.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Mini ti sɔnɔ bo i teŋge minɛm bu yiwee nu, na i má bu ŋgɔɔ n a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, haari nzɛn i wuuri, i nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Jesus responded, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of those works do you stone me.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ma yo alekutura sakpam dɔŋgu, bo m Si kereeri m na am aŋu i, ye sa bɔnɔ ti ye am ŋa am kun m n a.

eng: Those who love their lives will lose them, and those who hate their lives in this world will keep them forever.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i ŋgɔɔ, i mi bá fun i, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má i ŋgɔɔ durunya n nu fɛn, i mi bá nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri.

eng: So when Judas took the bread, he left immediately. And it was night.
local: Yudasi diiri too n wieeri ni, na wɔ yasu afite akɔ. Nyini saŋga ni, i ti kɔŋguɛ wɔ.

eng: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give you. I give to you not as the world gives. Dont be troubled or afraid.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ahoreda n ye m sufa ma am n a. M dɛɛ ahoreda n ye m sufa ma am a. Má kabo durunya n nu­fɔm ma am ahoreda ye m suma am n a. Má am ma am ahore saaki, má am soro sɛrɛ.

eng: Righteous Father, even the world didnt know you, but Ive known you, and these believers know that you sent me.
local: M Si, wɔrɔ yo sa kɛrɛ bo i kɔ atin su n a, durunya n nu­fɔm si má u, ama, mini si u, na ahinm gusu si ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a.

eng: In that day you will ask in my name. Im not saying that I will ask the Father on your behalf.
local: Nyini cɛɛn ni, am fá m duma n fa sere Nyɛmɛ, má mini sere Nyɛmɛ ma am a.

eng: However, Peter stood outside near the gate. Then the other disciple came out and spoke to the woman stationed at the gate, and she brought Peter in.
local: Ama, na Petoro yiri aka ajina dasin anɔ n nu, na yiri susu­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n si i ni afite, na wɔ ba afani n bo i jina kanda anɔ n dɔ, na wɔ sere i atin na wɔ fa Petoro awura.

eng: Everyone who lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la ŋgɔɔ na i yo m yarada, i mi wu má fieo. A yo ahin jɔjɔɔ­wa n yarada a.

eng: The Jewish opposition answered, We dont stone you for a good work but for insulting God. You are human, yet you make yourself out to be God.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm n ase i ka, Má sakpam bo a yo bu n be dɛɛ ti ye ya ŋa ya kún u a, ama, bo a jɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu n ti, dama a ti adamande wɔ, ama, na a se ka a ti Nyɛmɛ wɔ.

eng: Whoever serves me must follow me. Wherever I am, there my servant will also be. My Father will honor whoever serves me.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su m, wɔ yo daka ka i mi su m su, na deke bo m wo ni, m amusui­su­fɔ gusu wó dɔ ɔ. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i suuri m, m Si Nyɛmɛ wúra i mi kpiri.

eng: When Judas was gone, Jesus said, Now the Human One has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.
local: Yudasi fite kɔ wieeri ni, na Yesu ase ka, Kisa ye Nyɛmɛ wúra mini Adamande Wa n kpiri a, na m dɛɛ ti ye minɛ wúra m Si Nyɛmɛ kpiri a.

eng: You have heard me tell you, Im going away and returning to you. If you loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than me.
local: Am ati ka ma se ka, M kɔ́ na m káaki ba am dɔ bekun. Nzɛn má am koro m, má am ahore jɔ ka m sukɔ m Si n dɔ, dama m Si tara m.

eng: Ive made your name known to them and will continue to make it known so that your love for me will be in them, and I myself will be in them.
local: Ma kere bu kabo a ti barasu, na m toko sukere bu wɔ, adena koro n bo a koro m n wó bu nu, na m ni bɛrɛ yó kun.

eng: The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and believed that I came from God.
local: Yiri m Si ni i tii koro am a, dama am koro m na am ayo yarada ka m fite i dɔ ɔ.

eng: The servant woman stationed at the gate asked Peter, Arent you one of this mans disciples? Im not, he replied.
local: Na afani n bo i jina kanda anɔ n abisa Petoro ka, Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ti nyini sɔnɔ n susu­fɔm nu kun wɔ, má sɔ? Na Petoro ase i ka, Ai, m ti má bu kun.

eng: She replied, Yes, Lord, I believe that you are the Christ, Gods Son, the one who is coming into the world.
local: Na Marata ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, ma yo yarada ka wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ Wa, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n a, bo i ŋa i súnma i durunya n nu ni.

eng: Jesus replied, Isnt it written in your Law, I have said, you are gods?.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ba kɛrɛ i am Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Am ti ka Nyɛmɛ wɔ.

eng: Now I am deeply troubled. What should I say? Father, save me from this time? No, for this is the reason I have come to this time.
local: Na Yesu ase ka, Kisa, m ahore asaaki, ye m se sɛ wɔ? M koro se ka, m Si Nyɛmɛ, yi m fite ahin anyum­boro bo i suba m dɔ n nu, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, m koro se má sɔ, dama nyini dɛɛ ti ye m baari a.

eng: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify the Human One in himself and will glorify him immediately.
local: Na nzɛn bu wuraari Nyɛmɛ kpiri m dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ gusu wúra m kpiri, na Nyɛmɛ má i yo kisa bɛrɛ.

eng: I have told you before it happens so that when it happens you will believe.
local: Sa bo wɔ nya yo má lɛɛ, ye ma se am n a, adena i bá yoori, na am yo m yarada.

eng: I left the Father and came into the world. I tell you again: I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
local: M fiteeri m Si dɔ ɔ baari durunya n nu a, ye kisa m sufite durunya n nu kaaki kɔ i dɔ a.

eng: The servants and the guards had made a fire because it was cold. They were standing around it, warming themselves. Peter joined them there, standing by the fire and warming himself.
local: Ayɛrɛ saŋga lɛɛ, na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn ni ngbɛfɛnɛm, ni tintini­jina­fɔm atɔrɔ sin wo tun su, na Petoro gusu ajina bu bi sutun sin n be.

eng: After she said this, she went and spoke privately to her sister Mary, The teacher is here and hes calling for you.
local: Marata seeri sɔ n sin, na wɔ kaaki akɔ afere i niwa Mariya bɛnlɛ, na wɔ se i ka, Ya Mibiɛ n aba, i ŋa a bra.

eng: Scripture calls those to whom Gods word came gods , and scripture cant be abolished.
local: Be koro bu má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi ni ŋgbɛɛn. Nyɛmɛ yo seeri bu ka bu ti ka Nyɛmɛ wɔ, bɛrɛ bo bu deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Father, glorify your name! Then a voice came from heaven, I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.
local: M dɛɛ bo m se la ka, m Si, kere kabo u jirima woo­wa ti. Na akonvi be atiɛn afite aŋgoro ase ka, Ma du mɔ akere kabo m jirima woo­wa ti, na m káaki kere i bekun.

eng: Little children, Im with you for a little while longer. You will look for me—but, just as I told the Jewish leaders, I also tell you now—Where Im going, you cant come.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, M nbaam, m cɛ́ má am nu fɛn. Am bá niɛniɛ m busu fie wɔ, na kabo m yo seeri Yuda­fɔm ni, sɔ ye m suse am kisa, ka am koro kɔ má deke bo m sukɔ ni.

eng: I wont say much more to you because this worlds ruler is coming. He has nothing on me.
local: M jɔ́jɔ má am dɔ jɔrɛ beberebe kun, dama Sitana bo i ti durunya n nu­fɔm tii ni suba. Ama, i koro yo má m pui.

eng: His disciples said, See! Now you speak plainly; you arent using figures of speech.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔm n ase i ka, Kisa a jɔjɔ ya dɔ wein­wein wɔ, a bo má ya anyundɛrɛ kun.

eng: Meanwhile, the chief priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kpiɛn n abisa Yesu fa kɔ i susu­fɔm n jɔrɛ, ni kabo i keree­wa n ti barasu ni.

eng: When Mary heard this, she got up quickly and went to Jesus.
local: Mariya tiiri sɔ ni, i kaba bereki yasuuri wɔ, na wɔ kɔ i dɔ.

eng: So how can you say that the one whom the Father has made holy and sent into the world insults God because he said, I am Gods Son?.
local: ama, mini yiri, m Si Nyɛmɛ ayi m, ye wɔ sunma m durunya n nu a. Ye nzu ti ye ambɛrɛ ŋa ma kan ciire jɔrɛ, dama m ŋa mini ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a?.

eng: Then Pilate had Jesus taken and whipped.
local: Na Pilato ama ba fa Yesu akɔ afin i ŋgbere.

eng: The crowd standing there heard and said, Its thunder. Others said, An angel spoke to him.
local: Jama n bo bu jina Yesu bi n tiiri akonvi ni ŋga ni, na bu nbem ase ka, Nzue akpura. Na nbem gusu ase ka, Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ ajɔjɔ i dɔ a.

eng: I give you a new commandment: Love each other. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love each other.
local: Nɔaniɛ maa­wa fɔfɔrɛ ye m sufa ma am n a, yiri la ka, am koro am beŋgum. Kabo ma koro am ni, am koro am beŋgu sɔ.

eng: Rather, he comes so that the world will know that I love the Father and do just as the Father has commanded me. Get up. Were leaving this place.
local: M koro ka durunya n nu­fɔm si ka, m koro m Si ni, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m yo deke kɛrɛ bo i se m ka m yo i n a. Na wɔ se bu ka, Am yasu na ya kɔ.

eng: Now we know that you know everything and you dont need anyone to ask you. Because of this we believe you have come from God.
local: Ama, kisa ya si ka a si deke kɛrɛ, wɔ ti má ye ni ka be bisa u deke be, dama kana bu ba bisa u deke be, a si kabo bu koro ka bu bisa u ni, nyini ma ya si ka a fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ n a.

eng: Jesus answered, Ive spoken openly to the world. Ive always taught in synagogues and in the temple, where all the Jews gather. Ive said nothing in private.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Saŋga kɛrɛ m jɔjɔ sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nyunu wɔ, na m kere bu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, ni Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, deke bo Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ tiɛn bu nɔɔ ni, m kan má jɔrɛ be fiɛɛ­wa nu.

eng: He hadnt entered the village but was still in the place where Martha had met him.
local: Nyini saŋga, na Yesu nya ju má miɛ n nu, i toko wo deke bo Marata kɔ tuuri i n wɔ.

eng: If I dont do the works of my Father, dont believe me.
local: Nzɛn m yo má m Si juma ni, má am yo m yarada.

eng: The soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple robe.
local: Na sojam n awura i tanlɛɛ kɔkɔrɛ yorom ka fɛmɛya dɛɛ su, na ba fa buwe aŋɔ i ka kaba dɛɛ su, na ba fa aforo i tii.

eng: Jesus replied, This voice wasnt for my benefit but for yours.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye akonvi n jɔjɔɔri a, má mini dɛɛ ti a.

eng: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, when you love each other.
local: Nzɛn am koro am beŋgu, nyini ye sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka am ti m susu­fɔm a.

eng: Jesus replied, Now you believe?.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am ayo yarada kisa?.

eng: Why ask me? Ask those who heard what I told them. They know what I said.
local: Ye nzu ti ye a bisa m a? Bisa minɛm bo bu tiiri m keree­wa n ka m seeri bu sɛ wɔ. Bɛrɛ si kabo m seeri bu n a.

eng: When the Jews who were comforting Mary in the house saw her get up quickly and leave, they followed her. They assumed she was going to mourn at the tomb.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm bo bu woori awuru n nu Mariya bi, na bu sudada i ahore ni, bu ŋuuri kabo i bereki yasu fiteeri ni, na ba su i su. Bu sunsuunri ka i sukɔ deke bo bu seeri Lazarosi n wɔ, na i kɔ sun.

eng: But if I do them, and you dont believe me, believe the works so that you can know and recognize that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.
local: Ama, nzɛn m yo bu na haari am yo má m yarada mmɔɔ a, am yo m juma n yarada, adena am si nahɔrɛ su ka mini ni m Si n ti deke kun wɔ.

eng: Over and over they went up to him and said, Greetings, king of the Jews! And they slapped him in the face.
local: Na bu suba i dɔ suyo i anzasi se i ka, Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ, ya yo u ansɛ. Na ba bo i nyɔɔnu.

eng: Simon Peter said to Jesus, Lord, where are you going? Jesus answered, Where I am going, you cant follow me now, but you will follow later.
local: Na Simon Petoro abisa i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, a kɔ́ ni wɔ? Na Yesu abɔ i nu ka, Deke bo m sukɔ ni, a koro su má m su kisa, ama, saŋga suba bo a bá su m su.

eng: Now is the time for judgment of this world. Now this worlds ruler will be thrown out.
local: Saŋga n aju bo Nyɛmɛ dí durunya n nu­fɔm jɔrɛ, na i fɔ́n yi Sitana bo i ti bu fɛmɛ ni.

eng: Look! A time is coming—and is here!—when each of you will be scattered to your own homes and you will leave me alone. Im not really alone, for the Father is with me.
local: Ama, saŋga suba bo wɔ ju awie, bo am kɛrɛ bá sandi yaki mini ŋgumi, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kɔ́ i dika, ama, m wo má m ŋgumi, dama m Si Nyɛmɛ wo m dɔ.

eng: After Jesus spoke, one of the guards standing there slapped Jesus in the face. Is that how you would answer the high priest? he asked.
local: Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na tintini­jina­fɔ n bo i jina dodo i n abo i nyɔɔnu, na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, Sɔ ye a bɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n nu a.

eng: When Mary arrived where Jesus was and saw him, she fell at his feet and said, Lord, if you had been here, my brother wouldnt have died.
local: Saŋga bo Mariya kɔ juuri deke bo Yesu wo n na i ŋuuri i ni, na wɔ kutu i nyunu, na wɔ se i ka, M Mibiɛ, nzɛn má a woori fɛn, má m niwa n awu má.

eng: Again, they wanted to arrest him, but he escaped from them.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na ba kpi ka bu tara i bekun, na wɔ lɔrɔki afite bu nu.

eng: Pilate came out of the palace again and said to the Jewish leaders, Look! Im bringing him out to you to let you know that I find no grounds for a charge against him.
local: Na Pilato akaaki afite aba Yuda­fɔm dɔ aba ase bu ka, Am ti fɛn, m sufa i fite ba kere am wɔ, na am si ka ma ŋu má ŋgasi bo wɔ do i.

eng: Peter asked, Lord, why cant I follow you now? Ill give up my life for you.
local: Na Petoro akaaki abisa i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, nzu ti ye m koro su má u su kisa? Nzɛn i yo yiwee mmɔɔ a, m wú u dɛɛ ti.

eng: When I am lifted up from the earth, I will draw everyone to me. .
local: Ama, mini, bu ba yɛɛri m su jinaari aŋgoro baka jaanu n su, m cín sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ba m dɔ.

eng: Ive said these things to you so that you will have peace in me. In the world you have distress. But be encouraged! I have conquered the world.
local: Ma se am ahin kɛrɛ na am la ahoreda m dɔ. Am ŋú kekereke durunya n nu, ama, am fa ahoresi, dama ma kunma durunya ni.

eng: Jesus replied, If I speak wrongly, testify about what was wrong. But if I speak correctly, why do you strike me.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nzɛn ma jɔjɔ jɔrɛ bo wɔ kɔ má atin su, kan na m ti. Ye nzɛn bo ma se n ti nahɔrɛ, nzu ti ye a bo m nyɔɔnu a.

eng: When Jesus saw her crying and the Jews who had come with her crying also, he was deeply disturbed and troubled.
local: Yesu ŋuuri ka i susun ni, ni kabo Yuda­fɔm n bo bu suuri i su baari n gusu wo sun su ni, na i ahore asaaki, na wɔ mi i ŋumi.

eng: Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had baptized at first, and he stayed there.
local: Na Yesu akaaki akpiɛ akɔ Yodan kunma n buekun dɔ akɔ aju deke bo Yohane yo biɛɛri minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, na wɔ wo dɔ.

eng: When Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe, Pilate said to them, Heres the man.
local: Yesu tiɛ fiteeri ni tanlɛɛ kɔkɔrɛ yorom ni, ni i tii buwe kaba n ni, na Pilato ase bu ka, Am niɛ, sɔnɔ n yaa.

eng: Jesus replied, Will you give up your life for me? I assure you that you will deny me three times before the rooster crows.
local: Na Yesu abisa i ka, A koro wu m dɛɛ ti? Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u a, kana akɔnyuma bá bo, a kéte kpe nsan su ka a si má m.

eng: He said this to show how he was going to die.
local: Kabo i yo seeri sɔ n kere yiwee n bo i bá wu i n wɔ.

eng: Then Annas sent him, bound, to Caiaphas the high priest.
local: Na Anasi ama ba fa i ni nyama cicii­wa n sɔ akɔ Kayafasi bo i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kpiɛn n dɔ.

eng: He asked, Where have you laid him? They replied, Lord, come and see.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, Am seeri i ni wɔ? Na ba se i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, bra na a ba niɛ.

eng: When the chief priests and their deputies saw him, they shouted out, Crucify, crucify! Pilate told them, You take him and crucify him. I dont find any grounds for a charge against him.
local: Deke bo i tiɛ fiteeri na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni tintini­jina­fɔm ŋuuri i sɔ ni, kpeŋga na ba tiɛn, bu ŋa, Am kpama i baka jaanu n su na i wu. Am kpama i. Na Pilato ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii fa i kɔ kpama i e. Mini, ma ŋu má ŋgasi bo wɔ do i.

eng: Many people came to him. John didnt do any miraculous signs, they said, but everything John said about this man was true.
local: Na jama beberebe aba i dɔ, na ba se ka, Yohane ayo má alekutura sa be, ama, jɔjɔɔ­wa kɛrɛ bo i yo jɔjɔ fa kɔɔri ahin sɔnɔ n ŋusu n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: The crowd responded, We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever. How can you say that the Human One must be lifted up? Who is this Human One.
local: Na jama n ase i ka, Ya ti i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ. I wú má. Ye i yoori sɛ ye wɔrɔ ŋa, wɔ yo daka ka bu bá yɛɛri Adamande Wa n su jinaari aŋgoro baka jaanu n su a? Ye Adamande Wa n la ŋma a.

eng: Meanwhile, Simon Peter was still standing with the guards, warming himself. They asked, Arent you one of his disciples? Peter denied it, saying, Im not.
local: Simon Petoro jina woori sin n tun su ni, na ba bisa i ka, Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ti i susu­fɔm kun wɔ, má sɔ? Na Petoro akete, i ŋa, Ai, m ti má bu kun.

eng: Jesus began to cry.
local: Na Yesu asun.

eng: The Jewish leaders replied, We have a Law, and according to this Law he ought to die because he made himself out to be Gods Son.
local: Na ba se i ka, Ya la nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa be, wɔ yo daka ka i wu, dama i ŋa i ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a.

eng: Many believed in Jesus there.
local: Na minɛ dɔŋgu ayo i yarada dɔ.

eng: Jesus replied, The light is with you for only a little while. Walk while you have the light so that darkness doesnt overtake you. Those who walk in the darkness dont know where they are going.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Mini bo m ti wein ni, m wo am nu kisa, ama, m cɛ́ má. Nyini dɛɛ ti, am nya wo ni wein ni, am sunati wein nu wɔ, na má awosin tu am. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo awosin nu, i mi si má deke bo i sukɔ ni.

eng: A servant of the high priest, a relative of the one whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, Didnt I see you in the garden with him.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n akɔɔ be, i dika­fɔ la sɔnɔ n bo Petoro kpiɛ yiiri i sui ni, na wɔ se Petoro ka, Ma ŋu má u ka a wo i bi baka fie n nu dɔ.

eng: The Jews said, See how much he loved him.
local: Nyini ti na Yuda­fɔm n ase ka, Am niɛ kabo i koro i agaya barasu a.

eng: When Pilate heard this word, he was even more afraid.
local: Pilato tiiri ka Yesu ŋa i ti Nyɛmɛ wa ni, na sɛrɛ ati i abuka su.

eng: As long as you have the light, believe in the light so that you might become people whose lives are determined by the light. After Jesus said these things, he went away and hid from them.
local: Am nya wo ni wein ni, am yo i yarada, na m má am yo wein dɛɛ nbaam. Yesu seeri sɔ ni, na wɔ fite bu nu akɔ afiɛ deke be, na má be ŋu i.

eng: Peter denied it again, and immediately a rooster crowed.
local: Na Petoro akete bekun, kpeŋga akɔnyuma abo.

eng: But some of them said, He healed the eyes of the man born blind. Couldnt he have kept Lazarus from dying.
local: Ama, na bu nbem ase ka, Yiri n bo i teke anyinsin n nyu­mbaa ni, má i koro tuntun má sɔnɔ ni, na i wu má, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: He went back into the residence and spoke to Jesus, Where are you from? Jesus didnt answer.
local: Na wɔ kaaki awura gisan n nu abisa Yesu ka, A fite ni wɔ? Ama, Yesu abɔ má i nu.

eng: Jesus had done many miraculous signs before the people, but they didnt believe in him.
local: Yesu yo yoori alekutura sam dɔŋgu na ba ŋu i, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ba yo má i yarada.

eng: The Jewish leaders led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governors palace. It was early in the morning. So that they could eat the Passover, the Jewish leaders wouldnt enter the palace; entering the palace would have made them ritually impure.
local: Na alecin ŋgarama fun na ba fa Yesu afite Kayafasi dika n nu, na ba fa i akɔ gomina n dika, bu fere gomina n ka Pilato. Yuda­fɔm awura má gisan nu, adena má bu yo bu ŋu fin, na bu koro di má Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni.

eng: Jesus was deeply disturbed again when he came to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone covered the entrance.
local: Na Yesu ahore asaaki bekun, na wɔ kɔ deke bo bu seeri Lazarosi ni. I ti yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma wɔ, ye bu faari i seeri nu a, na ba fa yabuɛ nyiŋga atɔ anɔ ni.

eng: So Pilate said, You wont speak to me? Dont you know that I have authority to release you and also to crucify you.
local: Na Pilato akaaki abisa i ka, A bɔ má m nu? A si má ka m la yiko bo m koro ma bu fa u ji, na m kaaki la yiko bo m koro ma bu kpama u baka jaanu n su.

eng: This was to fulfill the word of the prophet Isaiah: Lord, who has believed through our message? To whom is the arm of the Lord fully revealed?.
local: Nyini ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya jɔrɛ n bo i yo kɛrɛɛri n yo yii­wa. I ŋa, M Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ, ŋma ade jɔrɛ n bo ya kan i n a? Ye ŋma ye a yi u yiko akere i a.

eng: So Pilate went out to them and asked, What charge do you bring against this man.
local: Nyini ti, na Pilato afite aba bu dɔ dasin abisa bu ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n bo am afa i aba m dɔ ni, i ŋgasi la bɔnɔ.

eng: Jesus said, Remove the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said, Lord, the smell will be awful! Hes been dead four days.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am ba yi yabuɛ ni. Na sɔnɔ n bo wɔ wu n niwa Marata ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, kisa wɔ bun, dama bu seeri i nyuma le­nna lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, You would have no authority over me if it had not been given to you from above. Thats why the one who handed me over to you has the greater sin.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nzɛn má Nyɛmɛ ama má u atin, maala má yiko bo a koro yo m pui. Nyini ti, sɔnɔ n bo i faari m wuraari u saa nu ni, i satiɛ yoo­wa tara u dɛɛ.

eng: Isaiah explains why they couldnt believe.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye ba koro ayo má Yesu yarada n a. Kabo Esaya akaaki ase ka.

eng: Jesus replied, Didnt I tell you that if you believe, you will see Gods glory.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Ma se má u ka, nzɛn a yoori m yarada, a ŋú Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa kabo i ti barasu.

eng: They answered, If he had done nothing wrong, we wouldnt have handed him over to you.
local: Na ba se i ka, Nzɛn má wɔ do má ŋgasi be, má ya fa má i ba má u dɔ.

eng: From that moment on, Pilate wanted to release Jesus. However, the Jewish leaders cried out, saying, If you release this man, you arent a friend of the emperor! Anyone who makes himself out to be a king opposes the emperor.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na Pilato akpi kabo i yo na i yaki Yesu nu ji. Ama, na Yuda­fɔm sutiɛntiɛn suse ka, Nzɛn a yakiiri i nu jiiri, u ni Fɛmɛ Siza danfoya awie lɛɛ, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo i ŋu ka i ti fɛmɛ, i mi ti Fɛmɛ Siza kpɔ­fɔ ɔ.

eng: He made their eyes blind and closed their minds so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their minds, and turn their lives around— and I would heal them.
local: Nyɛmɛ ama ba yo anyinsinm na má bu ŋu asi, na wɔ ma bu ahore ayo kekereke na má bu ti i nɔaniɛ n bu, adena má bu kaaki ba i dɔ na bu nya laifiɛ.

eng: So they removed the stone. Jesus looked up and said, Father, thank you for hearing me.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na ba lakati yabuɛ ni. Na wɔ yɛ i tii su aŋgoro, na wɔ se ka, M Si, m da u asi ka a de m sere.

eng: Pilate responded, Take him yourselves and judge him according to your Law. The Jewish leaders replied, The Law doesnt allow us to kill anyone. .
local: Na Pilato ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii fa i kɔ di i jɔrɛ kabo am jɔrɛ­di atin kere ni. Na Yuda­fɔm n ase i ka, Ya la má atin bo ya kun sɔnɔ.

eng: When Pilate heard these words, he led Jesus out and seated him on the judges bench at the place called Stone Pavement .
local: Pilato tiiri sɔ ni, na wɔ fa Yesu afite, na wɔ tana jɔrɛ dii­biri n su bimbindi n su. Yuda­fɔm aniɛ nu bu fere i ka Gabata , i bu la ka, deke bo ba fa yabuɛ asɛsɛ su ayo i dɔŋgu ni.

eng: Isaiah said these things because he saw Jesus glory; he spoke about Jesus.
local: Esaya yo ŋuuri Yesu jirima woo­wa n dɛɛ ti, ye i kaanri i ŋu jɔrɛ n a.

eng: I know you always hear me. I say this for the benefit of the crowd standing here so that they will believe that you sent me.
local: M si ka saŋga kɛrɛ a de m sere wɔ, ama, ahin minɛm bo bu jina fɛn n dɛɛ ti, ye m seeri sɔ a, adena bu yo m yarada ka wɔrɔ sunmaari m a.

eng: This was so that Jesus word might be fulfilled when he indicated how he was going to die.
local: Bu seeri sɔ ni, adena Yesu nɔaniɛ n bo i seeri kabo i bá wu barasu n yo yii­wa.

eng: It was about noon on the Preparation Day for the Passover. Pilate said to the Jewish leaders, Heres your king.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n tiiri bu Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n kpokpo cɛɛn wɔ, wiɛsu kerefi buru ni nnyɔ saŋga a. Na Pilato ase Yuda­fɔm ka, Am fɛmɛ n yaa.

eng: Even so, many leaders believed in him, but they wouldnt acknowledge their faith because they feared that the Pharisees would expel them from the synagogue.
local: Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm dɔŋgu mmɔɔ yoori Yesu yarada, ama, bu soroori Farasi­fɔm n ahore n dɛɛ ti, ba koro ayi má bu ŋu fofoe nu. Nzɛn bu yiiri bu ŋu ka ba yo i yarada, Farasi­fɔm ni fɔ́n bu fite bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu wɔ.

eng: Early in the morning of the first day of the week, while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene came to the tomb and saw that the stone had been taken away from the tomb.
local: Yuda­fɔm bakuɛ n bumboori, mɔnɛ cɛɛn ŋgarama fuun, nyini saŋga dika toko ti liim, na Mariya n bo i fite Magadala n akɔ yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma n su. I kɔ juuri ni, i ŋuuri ka ba yi yabuɛ bambaka n bo bu fa tɔɔri faŋ­kunma n anɔ ni.

eng: Having said this, Jesus shouted with a loud voice, Lazarus, come out.
local: I sereeri Nyɛmɛ n wieeri ni, na wɔ tiɛn ase ka, Lazarosi, fite.

eng: Pilate went back into the palace. He summoned Jesus and asked, Are you the king of the Jews.
local: Na Pilato akaaki awura gisan n nu, na wɔ fere Yesu abisa i ka, Wɔrɔ ti Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n a.

eng: The Jewish leaders cried out, Take him away! Take him away! Crucify him! Pilate responded, What? Do you want me to crucify your king? We have no king except the emperor, the chief priests answered.
local: Na ba kaaki atiɛn ase ka, Am fa i kɔ, am fa i kɔ kpama i baka jaanu n su. Na Pilato abisa bu ka, M fá am fɛmɛ n kɔ kpama i baka jaanu n su? Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ase ka, Ya la má fɛmɛ be sin Fɛmɛ Siza ŋu.

eng: They believed, but they loved human praise more than Gods glory.
local: Dama Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n yo koroori adamande kansi taraari Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ.

eng: She ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple, the one whom Jesus loved, and said, They have taken the Lord from the tomb, and we dont know where theyve put him.
local: Na wɔ ŋmati akɔ Simon Petoro ni Yesu susu­fɔ n bo i koro i agaya n dɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, Ba yi Mibiɛ n faŋ­kunma n nu, ya si má deke bo ba fa i akɔ ase.

eng: The dead man came out, his feet bound and his hands tied, and his face covered with a cloth. Jesus said to them, Untie him and let him go.
local: Na sɔnɔ n bo wɔ wu n afite faŋ­kunma n nu. Tani pɔɔndiiri i saa, ni i jaa, na tani kun pɔɔndiiri i tii. Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am nyaŋgi yi tani n na am ma i kɔ.

eng: Jesus answered, Do you say this on your own or have others spoken to you about me.
local: Na Yesu abisa i ka, U dɛɛ sunsunni nu lɛɛ, nzɛn be seeri u a.

eng: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be crucified. The soldiers took Jesus prisoner.
local: Bu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na Pilato anya afa i ama bu ka bu kɔ kpama i baka jaanu n su. Na ba de Yesu akɔ.

eng: Jesus shouted, Whoever believes in me doesnt believe in me but in the one who sent me.
local: Na Yesu atiɛn ase ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, má mini ŋgumi ye i yo m yarada, ama, i yo bo wɔ sunma m n gusu yarada a.

eng: Peter and the other disciple left to go to the tomb.
local: Na Petoro ni i lamɔkɔ n ayasu ka bu kɔ faŋ­kunma n su.

eng: Therefore, many of the Jews who came with Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.
local: Yuda­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu baari Mariya dɔ ŋuuri sa n bo Yesu yoori i ni, na ba yo i yarada.

eng: Pilate responded, Im not a Jew, am I? Your nation and its chief priests handed you over to me. What have you done.
local: Na Pilato abisa i ka, M ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ? U minɛm ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni faari u baari m dɔ a. A do nzu ŋgasi wɔ.

eng: Carrying his cross by himself, he went out to a place called Skull Place .
local: Na yiri ni i tii asɔ i baka jaanu n akɔ awɔrɛ n bo bu fere i ka Tere­awa Woo­biri ni, Yuda­fɔm aniɛ nu bu fere dɔ ka Gologota .

eng: Whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i niɛ na i ŋuuri m, ti ka wɔ ŋu m Si Nyɛmɛ bo i sunmaari m n lɛɛ.

eng: Later, Jesus himself appeared again to his disciples at the Sea of Tiberias. This is how it happened.
local: Nyini sin, na Yesu ayi i ŋu akere i susu­fɔm n bekun kunma kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Tiberiya n nɔɔ su. Kabo i faari i ŋu kereeri bu n la ka.

eng: They were running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and was the first to arrive at the tomb.
local: Bu minɛ nnyɔ ni suŋmati kɔ a, ama, na kun n aŋmati asin Petoro ŋu adu mɔ aju faŋ­kunma n su.

eng: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
local: Na bu nbem akɔ Farasi­fɔm n dɔ akɔ ase bu kabo Yesu ayo ni.

eng: Jesus replied, My kingdom doesnt originate from this world. If it did, my guards would fight so that I wouldnt have been arrested by the Jewish leaders. My kingdom isnt from here.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, M fɛmɛya n ti má durunya n nu fɛn fɛmɛya. Nzɛn má m fɛmɛya n ti durunya n nu fɛn fɛmɛya, má m susu­fɔm bá kun luɛ m busu, na má be koro tara má m wura má Yuda­fɔm saa nu. Ama, m fɛmɛya n ti má durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ.

eng: Thats where they crucified him—and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
local: Na ba kpama i baka jaanu n su dɔ, na ba kaaki akpama minɛ nnyɔ nbem adodo i, bu kpamaari kun i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, na ba kpama kun i saa biɛ su, na Yesu awo bu afiɛn.

eng: I have come as a light into the world so that everyone who believes in me wont live in darkness.
local: Mini bo m ti wein n aba durunya n nu, adena sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo m yarada, i wó má awosin nu.

eng: Simon Peter, Thomas , Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, Zebedees sons, and two other disciples were together.
local: i susu­fɔ nbem atiɛn bu nɔɔ, bɛrɛ la Simon Petoro, ni Tomasi n bo bu fere i ka Ndaa ni, ni Nataniɛli n bo i fite Kana bo i wo Galili asiɛn n su ni, ni Zɛbɛdi nbaa nnyɔm ni, ni i susu­fɔ nnyɔ nbem.

eng: Bending down to take a look, he saw the linen cloths lying there, but he didnt go in.
local: Na wɔ kasi aniɛ faŋ­kunma n nu, aŋu ka nzee n da, ama, wɔ wura má faŋ­kunma n nu.

eng: Then the chief priests and Pharisees called together the council and said, What are we going to do? This man is doing many miraculous signs!.
local: Na Farasi­fɔm ni Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n afere Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm ni, na ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ. Na ba se ka, Ye ya yo sɛ wɔ? Am niɛ e, ahin sɔnɔ ni suyo alekutura sa­yoo­wam dɔŋgu.

eng: So you are a king? Pilate said. Jesus answered, You say that I am a king. I was born and came into the world for this reason: to testify to the truth. Whoever accepts the truth listens to my voice.
local: Na Pilato abisa i bekun ka, Ŋka a ti fɛmɛ wɔ lɛɛ? Na Yesu ase i ka, Bo a ŋa m ti fɛmɛ ni, i ti sɔ ɔ. Bu wuuri m durunya n nu ka m ba di nahɔrɛ n daani wɔ. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nahɔrɛ n su, i de m nɔaniɛ n wɔ.

eng: Pilate had a public notice written and posted on the cross. It read Jesus the Nazarene, the king of the Jews.
local: Na Pilato akɛrɛ kɛrɛɛ­wa be amata i tii su baka jaanu n su. Kɛrɛɛ­wa n la ka, Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n bo i ti Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n lɛɛ.

eng: If people hear my words and dont keep them, I dont judge them. I didnt come to judge the world but to save it.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti m nɔaniɛ n na i de má i, m di má i mi jɔrɛ, dama ma ba má ka m ba di durunya n nu­fɔm jɔrɛ wɔ, ama, m baari ka m ba de bu tii ji wɔ.

eng: Simon Peter told them, Im going fishing. They said, Well go with you. They set out in a boat, but throughout the night they caught nothing.
local: Na Simon Petoro ase bu ka, M sukɔ jombaa tara lɛɛ. Na ba se i ka, Yɛrɛ mmɔɔ sú u su kɔ ɔ. Na ba wura lee n nu akɔ, bu woori nzue n nu haari ale kɔ sucin, ba nya koro tara má pui.

eng: Following him, Simon Peter entered the tomb and saw the linen cloths lying there.
local: Ama, na Simon Petoro yiri aka sin aba aju, na wɔ wura faŋ­kunma n nu, aŋu ka nzee n da.

eng: If we let him go on like this, everyone will believe in him. Then the Romans will come and take away both our temple and our people.
local: Nzɛn ya yaki i na i suyo sɔ, minɛm kɛrɛ yó i yarada a, na Roma yiko­fɔm bá saaki ya Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni, na bu kún ya minɛm ni.

eng: What is truth? Pilate asked. After Pilate said this, he returned to the Jewish leaders and said, I find no grounds for any charge against him.
local: Na Pilato abisa i ka, Nahɔrɛ la nzu wɔ? Pilato seeri i sɔ, na wɔ kaaki afite akɔ Yuda­fɔm dɔ akɔ ase bu ka, Ma ŋu má i ŋgasi be wɔ do i.

eng: Many of the Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city and it was written in Aramaic, Latin, and Greek.
local: Bu kɛrɛɛri nyini kɛrɛɛ­wa n Yuda­fɔm, ni Roma­fɔm, ni Giriki­fɔm dɛɛ aniɛm kɛrɛ nu wɔ, na Yuda­fɔm dɔŋgu aŋu i, na ba kan i, dama deke bo bu kpamaari i n waari má ni miɛ kunnu.

eng: Whoever rejects me and doesnt receive my words will be judged at the last day by the word I have spoken.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kete m, na i de má m nɔaniɛ ni, i jɔrɛ­di­fɔ wo bɛrɛ wɔ. Nɔaniɛ n bo ma jɔjɔ i ni, yiri dí i mi jɔrɛ jina cɛɛn n a.

eng: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples didnt realize it was Jesus.
local: Saŋga bo ale ba ciinri wein ni, na Yesu aba ajina kunma n nɔɔ su, ama, i susu­fɔm n asi má ka yiri lɛɛ.

eng: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, told them, You dont know anything!.
local: Na bu nu sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Kayafasi ni, bo i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn nyini afɔ n ase ka, Ambɛrɛ, am si má pui.

eng: He also saw the face cloth that had been on Jesus head. It wasnt with the other clothes but was folded up in its own place.
local: na wɔ ŋu ka tani n bo bu fa ciinri Yesu tii ka kaba n gusu bubu nu da i ŋgumi.

eng: You have a custom that I release one prisoner for you at Passover. Do you want me to release for you the king of the Jews.
local: Ama, am la am yoo­wa be bo i ma m atin ka m yi dan­saraka kun ma am Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n di saŋga. Am koro ka m yi ambɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ ni ji ma am a, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: Therefore, the Jewish chief priests complained to Pilate, Dont write, The king of the Jews but This man said, I am the king of the Jews.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n akete ase Pilato ka, Má a kɛrɛ ka, Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n lɛɛ, ama, kɛrɛ ka, Ahin sɔnɔ ŋa, Mini ti Yuda­fɔm Fɛmɛ n a. .

eng: I dont speak on my own, but the Father who sent me commanded me regarding what I should speak and say.
local: Dama má m koroya­nu ye m kan sɔ a, ama, m Si n bo wɔ sunma m ni, yiri maari m nɔɔ, kereeri m nɔaniɛ kun kun kɛrɛ bo m jɔjɔ i n a.

eng: Jesus called to them, Children, have you caught anything to eat? They answered him, No.
local: Na wɔ bisa bu ka, M nbaam, am anya má jombaa, má sɔ? Na ba se i ka, Ai, ya nya má be.

eng: You dont see that it is better for you that one man die for the people rather than the whole nation be destroyed.
local: Am si má kabo i ti n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ka nzɛn am ma sɔnɔ kun wu maari minɛm kɛrɛ, i ti ye ni bo ya minɛm kɛrɛ bá wu ni.

eng: Then the other disciple, the one who arrived at the tomb first, also went inside. He saw and believed.
local: Nyini saŋga na yiri n bo i du mɔ juuri faŋ­kunma n su n awura nu, i ŋuuri i ni, na wɔ yo yarada kabo Yesu yo seeri bu ni.

eng: They shouted, Not this man! Give us Barabbas.
local: Na ba tiɛn ase ka, Má yiri. Ya koro Barabasi wɔ. Barabasi ti sɔnɔ bo i nati fin minɛm di wɔ.

eng: Pilate answered, What Ive written, Ive written.
local: Na Pilato ase bu ka, Bo ma kɛrɛ i ni, nyini lɛɛ wɔ.

eng: I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, whatever I say is just as the Father has said to me.
local: M si ka i nɔaniɛ maa­wa n ti deke bo i ma ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n wɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo m Si seeri m ni, sɔ ye m jɔjɔ a.

eng: He said, Cast your net on the right side of the boat and you will find some. So they did, and there were so many fish that they couldnt haul in the net.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am sandi am diɛndiɛ n lee n fɔmbɔrɔ su dɛɛ bue ni, am nyá be. Bu sandiiri ni, na ba tara jombaa beberebe, haari ba koro ama má diɛndiɛ n su.

eng: He didnt say this on his own. As high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would soon die for the nation.
local: Má i koroya­nu ye i seeri sɔ a, ama, yiri ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn nyini afɔ n ti, ye Nyɛmɛ ma i seeri ka Yesu wú ma Yuda­fɔm n a.

eng: They didnt yet understand the scripture that Jesus must rise from the dead.
local: Ama, ba nya asi má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n bo i ŋa Yesu téŋge i yiwee n nu n bu.

eng: When the soldiers crucified Jesus, they took his clothes and his sandals, and divided them into four shares, one for each soldier. His shirt was seamless, woven as one piece from the top to the bottom.
local: Sojam n kpamaari i baka jaanu n su wieeri ni, na ba kpaaki i neŋge wuraa­wam nu busu nna, na bu kɛrɛ afa kun kun, na wɔ ka i tanlɛɛ yorom bo i wo i bu na i wura be gɔ su ni, i la má gise kpaa­biri, ama, ba wu i kɛrɛ aŋgoro kɔ jura asiɛŋgu wɔ.

eng: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, Its the Lord! When Simon Peter heard it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself and jumped into the water.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔ n bo i koro i agaya n ase Petoro ka, Ya Mibiɛ lɛɛ. Deke bo Petoro tiiri ka, bu Mibiɛ lɛɛ ni, nyini saŋga i jina i ŋuniɛ ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. Na wɔ yo ndende afa i tanlɛɛ abo i wi nu, na wɔ ti ato nzue n nu.

eng: and not only for the nation. Jesus would also die so that Gods children scattered everywhere would be gathered together as one.
local: Na má bɛrɛ ŋgumi dɛɛ ti ye i wú a, ama, adena i tiɛn Nyɛmɛ nbaam nɔɔ, na bu yo kun, bɛrɛ bo ba sandi akɔ asiɛn nbem n su.

eng: Then the disciples returned to the place where they were staying.
local: Na ba kaaki akɔ awuru.

eng: The other disciples followed in the boat, dragging the net full of fish, for they werent far from shore, only about one hundred yards.
local: Ama, na i beŋgu susu­fɔm n bo bu wo lee n nu ni, sucin diɛndiɛ n bo wɔ yi ni jombaa ni, na bu fa ba kunma n nɔɔ su ni. Deke bo bu wo n ni kunma n nɔɔ su n waari má, i tiiri ajaari natii­wa yaa kun wɔ.

eng: From that day on they plotted to kill him.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, fite nyini cɛɛn n fa kɔ, na ba sɛsɛ su ka bu kun Yesu.

eng: They said to each other, Lets not tear it. Lets cast lots to see who will get it. This was to fulfill the scripture, They divided my clothes among themselves, and they cast lots for my clothing. Thats what the soldiers did.
local: Na ba se ka, Má am ma ya sɔnsɔn i nu, ama, am ma ya tu mbaa na ya si bo i dí i. Bu yoori sɔ adena Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n yo yii­wa, bo ba kɛrɛ i ka, Ba kpaaki m neŋge wuraa­wam nu, na ba tu mbaa m tanlɛɛ n bo i wo m bu n busu ni. Nyini ti ye sojam yoori sɔ n a.

eng: Mary stood outside near the tomb, crying. As she cried, she bent down to look into the tomb.
local: Ama, na Mariya aka ajina faŋ­kunma n su, na i susun. I wo sun su ni, na wɔ kasi aniɛ faŋ­kunma n nu.

eng: When they landed, they saw a fire there, with fish on it, and some bread.
local: Bu cin kɔ juuri kunma n nɔɔ su ni, na bu fiteeri lee n nu ni, bu ŋuuri ka sin­mɔnɛ da, jombaa nana su, kpɔnɔ da bɛrɛ.

eng: Therefore, Jesus was no longer active in public ministry among the Jewish leaders. Instead, he left Jerusalem and went to a place near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim, where he stayed with his disciples.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yi má i ŋu fofoe nu anati má Yuda­fɔm nu kun. Ama, i fiteeri bɛrɛ ni, na wɔ kɔ awɔrɛ be bo i wo dodo boro kpantain dɛɛ bue ni, na i ni i susu­fɔm n akɔ awo miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Efirayim n su.

eng: Jesus mother and his mothers sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene stood near the cross.
local: Na Yesu ni, ni Yesu ni niɛma­bara, ni Kolopasi yi Mariya, fa kɔ tuuri Mariya n bo i fite Magadala n jina dodo deke bo bu kpamaari Yesu ni.

eng: She saw two angels dressed in white, seated where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and one at the foot.
local: Na wɔ ŋu mɛrɛkɛm nnyɔ ka bu wura tanlɛɛ fofoe tana deke bo bu faari Yesu ŋuniɛ n daari ni, kun tana deke bo bu faari i tii wɔɔri ni, na nyiŋga gusu tana deke bo bu faari i jaa wɔɔri ni.

eng: Jesus said to them, Bring some of the fish that youve just caught.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Am fa jombaa n bo am atara bu kisa n be bra.

eng: It was almost time for the Jewish Passover, and many people went from the countryside up to Jerusalem to purify themselves through ritual washing before the Passover.
local: Nyini saŋga, wɔ ka kaan, na Yuda­fɔm di bu Butara cɛɛn­kpiri ni, na minɛ dɔŋgu afite ahɛmɛm aba Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, na bu ba kpakpara bu ŋu fin ka, na cɛɛn­kpiri n nya ju.

eng: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, Woman, here is your son.
local: Yesu niɛ ŋuuri i ni ni, ni i susu­fɔ n bo i koro i agaya n ka bu jina dodo i ni, na wɔ se i ni n ka, M ni, niɛ fɛn, u wa la ahin.

eng: The angels asked her, Woman, why are you crying? She replied, They have taken away my Lord, and I dont know where theyve put him.
local: Na ba bisa i ka, Bara, nzu ti ye a sun a? Na wɔ se bu ka, Ba yi m Mibiɛ n wɔ, ye m si má deke bo ba fa i ase.

eng: Simon Peter got up and pulled the net to shore. It was full of large fish, one hundred fifty-three of them. Yet the net hadnt torn, even with so many fish.
local: Na Simon Petoro awura lee n nu, na wɔ cin diɛndiɛ n bo wɔ yi ni jombaa ŋgbiŋgbiri n aba ahɛmɛ. Jombaa n kɛrɛ kɔ yasuuri yaa ni aburu­nu ni nsan a, ama, haari ni nyini jombaa beberebe n kɛrɛ, diɛndiɛ n ati má.

eng: They were looking for Jesus. As they spoke to each other in the temple, they said, What do you think? He wont come to the festival, will he.
local: Na ba niɛniɛ Yesu busu, na saŋga bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Am sunsun sɛ wɔ? I bá cɛɛn­kpiri ni? Ya de má ka i bá.

eng: Then he said to the disciple, Here is your mother. And from that time on, this disciple took her into his home.
local: Na wɔ se i susu­fɔ n gusu ka, Niɛ fɛn, u gusu ni lɛɛ. Nyini cɛɛn n kɛrɛ na Yesu susu­fɔ n afa Yesu ni, na wɔ kɔ awo i dɔ.

eng: As soon as she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, but she didnt know it was Jesus.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na wɔ kaaki aniɛ i sin. I ŋuuri ka Yesu jina, ama, wɔ si má ka Yesu lɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said to them, Come and have breakfast. None of the disciples could bring themselves to ask him, Who are you? They knew it was the Lord.
local: Na Yesu ase i susu­fɔm n ka, Am ba yo daraka. Bu be abo má su, na i bisa i ka, Wɔrɔ ŋma? dama bu kɛrɛ si ka bu Mibiɛ n a.

eng: The chief priests and Pharisees had given orders that anyone who knew where he was should report it, so they could arrest him.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Farasi­fɔm n ama nɔɔ ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i si deke bo Yesu wo, i se bu na bu kɔ tara i.

eng: After this, knowing that everything was already completed, in order to fulfill the scripture, Jesus said, I am thirsty.
local: Nyini sin na Yesu asi nu ka wɔ yo i juma kɛrɛ awie, na wɔ se ka, Nzuehɔɛ ti m wɔ. I seeri sɔ ni, adena Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n yo yii­wa.

eng: Jesus said to her, Woman, why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Thinking he was the gardener, she replied, Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have put him and I will get him.
local: Na Yesu abisa i ka, Bara, nzu ti ye a sun a? A koro ŋma a? Mariya ŋuuri i sɔ ni, i sunsun ka fie n niɛ­fɔ a. Na wɔ se i ka, M jakpiɛn, nzɛn wɔrɔ ayi i akɔ ase deke be a, kere m na m kɔ fa i.

eng: Jesus came, took the bread, and gave it to them. He did the same with the fish.
local: Na Yesu aba afa kpɔnɔ n ama bu, na wɔ fa jombaa n gusu abuka bu su.

eng: This was now the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from the dead.
local: Ahin yoori kpe nsan su, bo i yiiri i ŋu kereeri i susu­fɔm n a, saŋga bo i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni.

eng: A jar full of sour wine was nearby, so the soldiers soaked a sponge in it, placed it on a hyssop branch, and held it up to his lips.
local: Nyini saŋga dundu be taka bɛrɛ, wɔ yi ni nzan gagaraki, na ba fa mɛnɛ afa awɔ baka su afa abo nu alaandi atɔ i nɔɔ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Mary. She turned and said to him in Aramaic, Rabbouni .
local: Na Yesu afere i ka, Mariya. Na wɔ kaaki aniɛ Yesu dɔ, na wɔ se i ka, Raboni . Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ aniɛ n nu, i bu la ka, ya Kere­fɔ.

eng: When they finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, Simon son of John, do you love me more than these? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my lambs.
local: Bu yoori daraka n wieeri ni, na Yesu abisa Simon Petoro ka, Yohane wa Simon, a koro m tara kabo nyiŋgam ni koro m ni? Na wɔ se i ka, Ɛɛn, m Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ mmɔɔ si ka m koro u. Na Yesu ase i ka, Niɛ m nbɔmbaam dika.

eng: When he had received the sour wine, Jesus said, It is completed. Bowing his head, he gave up his life.
local: Yesu de nu wieeri ni, na wɔ se ka, Wɔ wie. Na wɔ biri i tii, na wɔ yo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ.

eng: Jesus said to her, Dont hold on to me, for I havent yet gone up to my Father. Go to my brothers and sisters and tell them, Im going up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Má a fa u saa kan m, dama ma nya akɔ má m Si dɔ, ama, kɔ na a kɔ se m ninbaam n ka, M sukɔ m Si bo i ti am Si ni, ni m Nyɛmɛ bo i ti ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ Nyɛmɛ n dɔ.

eng: Jesus asked a second time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Simon replied, Yes, Lord, you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Take care of my sheep.
local: Na wɔ kaaki abisa i kpe nnyɔ su ka, Yohane wa Simon, a koro m? Na Petoro ase i ka, Ɛɛn, m Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ mmɔɔ si ka m koro u. Na Yesu ase i ka, Niɛ m nbɔɛm dika.

eng: It was the Preparation Day and the Jewish leaders didnt want the bodies to remain on the cross on the Sabbath, especially since that Sabbath was an important day. So they asked Pilate to have the legs of those crucified broken and the bodies taken down.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n ti Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n kpokpo cɛɛn n ti, ba koro má ka minɛm bo ba kpama bu baka jaanu n su n ka sense su bu Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, dama Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n bo i suba n ti cɛɛn­kpiri bambaka bu dɔ ɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye bu sereeri Pilato ka i ma bu kɔ bubu bo ba kpama bu baka jaanu n su jaam n a, na bu yi bu kɔ se bu.

eng: Mary Magdalene left and announced to the disciples, Ive seen the Lord. Then she told them what he said to her.
local: Na Mariya n bo i fite Magadala n akɔ ase bu ka, Ma ŋu Mibiɛ ni. Na wɔ se bu jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo Yesu akan akere i ni.

eng: He asked a third time, Simon son of John, do you love me? Peter was sad that Jesus asked him a third time, Do you love me? He replied, Lord, you know everything; you know I love you. Jesus said to him, Feed my sheep.
local: Na wɔ kaaki abisa i kpe nsan su ka, Yohane wa Simon, a koro m? Na Petoro ahore asaaki kabo wɔ bisa i kpe nsan su kɛrɛ ka, i koro i ni? Na Petoro ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ si deke n kɛrɛ a, a si ka m koro u. Na Yesu ase i ka, Niɛ m nbɔɛm dika.

eng: Therefore, the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men who were crucified with Jesus.
local: Nyini ti, na sojam n akɔ abo abubu sɔnɔ n kun jaam, ni nyiŋga n gusu dɛɛ, bɛrɛ n bo bu kpamaari bu dodoori Yesu ni.

eng: It was still the first day of the week. That evening, while the disciples were behind closed doors because they were afraid of the Jewish authorities, Jesus came and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Na bakuɛ n bumboori, mɔnɛ cɛɛn n naasɔ saŋga, na Yesu susu­fɔm woori sɔɔ be nu, na ba tu anɔ n agɔ bu ŋu, dama bu soroori Yuda­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm wɔ. Na Yesu aba ajina bu afiɛn ase bu ka, Am ma am ahore da.

eng: I assure you that when you were younger you tied your own belt and walked around wherever you wanted. When you grow old, you will stretch out your hands and another will tie your belt and lead you where you dont want to go.
local: Nahɔrɛ ye m suse u n a, ka saŋga bo a yo ti gbɛfɛnɛ ni, wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii bo u wi nu kujara kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo a koro a, ama, a ba yoori kpiɛn, a yɛ́ u saa su aŋgoro wɔ, na be bó u kujara na i fá u kɔ deke bo a koro má ni.

eng: When they came to Jesus, they saw that he was already dead so they didnt break his legs.
local: Ama, saŋga bo bu ba kpuraari Yesu ni, bu ŋuuri ka yiri awu, nyini dɛɛ ti, ba bubu má i jaam.

eng: After he said this, he showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they were filled with joy.
local: I seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ fa i saam deke bo bu boori i bulaari ni, ni i nvin deke bo bu wɔɔri i kpiɛ n akere bu. I susu­fɔm ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na bu ahore ajɔ.

eng: He said this to show the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, Jesus said to Peter, Follow me.
local: I seeri sɔ n na i kere kabo Petoro bá wu barasu, na minɛm ŋú kabo Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa n ti barasu. Nyini sin na wɔ se Petoro ka, Su m su.

eng: However, one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.
local: Ama, na sojam kun afa kpiɛ awɔ Yesu nvin nu, kpeŋga na nzue ni mbunja susoŋgi fite.

eng: Jesus said to them again, Peace be with you. As the Father sent me, so I am sending you.
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, Am ma am ahore da, kabo m Si sunmaari m ni, sɔ ye mini gusu susunma am a.

eng: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Jesus at the meal and asked him, Lord, who is going to betray you.
local: Na Petoro akaaki aniɛ aŋu ka bɛrɛ susu­fɔm nu kun bo Yesu koro i agaya n su bu su. Yiri la bo bu yo sudi diire na i tana mataari Yesu na i bisaari i ka, M Mibiɛ, ŋma tɔ́ u n a.

eng: The one who saw this has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he speaks the truth, and he has testified so that you also can believe.
local: Sɔnɔ n bo i nyu­mbaa ŋuuri i ni, yiri di i daani a, adena am yo yarada. I jɔrɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, na kabo i di daani n gusu ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: Then he breathed on them and said, Receive the Holy Spirit.
local: I seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ yi i ŋumi agɔ bu ŋu, na wɔ se bu ka, Am de Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni.

eng: When Peter saw this disciple, he said to Jesus, Lord, what about him.
local: Petoro ŋuuri ka i su bu su sɔ ni, na wɔ bisa Yesu ka, M Mibiɛ, ye ahin n gusu di.

eng: These things happened to fulfill the scripture, They wont break any of his bones .
local: Ahin n kɛrɛ yoori, adena kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n yo yii­wa. Ba kɛrɛ ka, I buwe kun n kpein mmɔɔ bu má.

eng: If you forgive anyones sins, they are forgiven; if you dont forgive them, they arent forgiven.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo am faari i satiɛ ceeri i, Nyɛmɛ fá i satiɛ ce i. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo am fa má i satiɛ ce má i, Nyɛmɛ fá má i satiɛ ce má i.

eng: Jesus replied, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you? You must follow me.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Nzɛn m koro ka i wo bɛrɛ haari saŋga bo m bá kaaki ba, i nya u sɛ? Wɔrɔ su m su.

eng: And another scripture says, They will look at him whom they have pierced.
local: Ye ba kaaki akɛrɛ ka, Minɛm bá niɛ yiri n bo ba wɔ i kpiɛ n wɔ.

eng: Thomas, the one called Didymus, one of the Twelve, wasnt with the disciples when Jesus came.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu yiiri i ŋu kereeri i susu­fɔm buru ni nnyɔm ni, bu nu kun awo má bu nu. Yiri la Tomasi bo bu fere i ka Ndaa ni.

eng: Therefore, the word spread among the brothers and sisters that this disciple wouldnt die. However, Jesus didnt say he wouldnt die, but only, If I want him to remain until I come, what difference does that make to you.
local: I seeri sɔ n na labari n abara ayɛ i susu­fɔm dɔ, ka i nyini susu­fɔ ni wú má. Yesu ase má ka i wú má, ama, i ŋa, Nzɛn m koro ka i wo bɛrɛ haari saŋga bo m bá kaaki ba, i nya u sɛ.

eng: After this Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate if he could take away the body of Jesus. Joseph was a disciple of Jesus, but a secret one because he feared the Jewish authorities. Pilate gave him permission, so he came and took the body away.
local: Nyini dɛɛ sin, na sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Yosefu n asere Pilato atin na i yi Yesu ŋuniɛ n kɔ se i. Na Pilato ama i atin, na wɔ kɔ ayi i ŋuniɛ n baka jaanu n su akɔ. Yiri Yosefu fite miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Aramatiya n su wɔ, i yo tiiri Yesu susu­fɔm kun wɔ, ama, i soroori Yuda­fɔm ahore ti, wɔ yi má i ŋu fofoe nu.

eng: The other disciples told him, Weve seen the Lord! But he replied, Unless I see the nail marks in his hands, put my finger in the wounds left by the nails, and put my hand into his side, I wont believe.
local: I ba baari ni, na ba se i ka, Ya ŋu Mibiɛ ni. Na wɔ se bu ka, Má ka ma ŋu i saa kunma n bo bu kpamaari i ni, na m fa m saa maraka wura nu, na m fa m saa wura i nvin nu deke bo bu wɔɔri i kpiɛ ni, m yó má yarada fieo.

eng: This is the disciple who testifies concerning these things and who wrote them down. We know that his testimony is true.
local: Nyini Yesu susu­fɔ ni diiri ahin jɔrɛm kɛrɛ daani a, yiri kaaki kɛrɛɛri ahin jɔrɛm kɛrɛ a, na ya si ka i daani dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ n a.

eng: Nicodemus, the one who at first had come to Jesus at night, was there too. He brought a mixture of myrrh and aloe, nearly seventy-five pounds in all.
local: Na Nikodemo bo i yo baari Yesu dɔ kɔŋguɛ be n gusu aba, i bita ŋguin nva­nvani ni tulaalɛɛ saŋgaa­wa, i nɔnɔɔri ju pobu abura­na.

eng: After eight days his disciples were again in a house and Thomas was with them. Even though the doors were locked, Jesus entered and stood among them. He said, Peace be with you.
local: Na i le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn, na ba wo sɔɔ n nu bekun. Nyini cɛɛn ni, Tomasi gusu woori bu nu, na ba yandi atu anɔ ni. Na Yesu aba ajina bu nu, na wɔ se bu ka, Am ma am ahore da.

eng: Jesus did many other things as well. If all of them were recorded, I imagine the world itself wouldnt have enough room for the scrolls that would be written.
local: Yesu yo yoori sam dɔŋgu, bo má bu ŋa bu kɛrɛ i kɛrɛ, ma de má ka bu nyá fofoe durunya n nu, bo bu fá nyini kadasim n kɛrɛ fa se.

eng: Following Jewish burial customs, they took Jesus body and wrapped it, with the spices, in linen cloths.
local: Na ba fa Yesu ŋuniɛ ni, na ba yo i kabo Yuda­fɔm yo bu fuin na bu se i ni, bu faari tulaalɛɛ n gɔɔri i ŋu wɔ, na ba nya afa nzee apɔɔndi i.

eng: Then he said to Thomas, Put your finger here. Look at my hands. Put your hand into my side. No more disbelief. Believe.
local: Na wɔ se Tomasi ka, Fa u saa maraka ba wura m saa kunma n nu, na a fa u saa ba wura m nvin kunma n nu. Na a yaki sika na a yo yarada.

eng: There was a garden in the place where Jesus was crucified, and in the garden was a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.
local: Deke bo bu kpamaari Yesu ni, baka fie be wo dɔ. Nyini baka fie n nu yabuɛ buɛ bo ba lokoti i ayo i faŋ­kunma wo dɔ, bo bu nya se má fuin nu ni.

eng: Because it was the Jewish Preparation Day and the tomb was nearby, they laid Jesus in it.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n ti Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n kpokpo n dɛɛ ti, na bekun, faŋ­kunma n waari má ni bɛrɛ ni, ye bu faari i kɔ seeri nu n a.

eng: Thomas responded to Jesus, My Lord and my God.
local: Na Tomasi ase i ka, Wɔrɔ ti m Mibiɛ, ni m Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: Jesus replied, Do you believe because you see me? Happy are those who dont see and yet believe.
local: Na Yesu ase i ka, Bo a ŋu m ti ye a yo yarada? Tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, bo bu nya ŋu má m na bu yo yarada ni.

eng: Then Jesus did many other miraculous signs in his disciples presence, signs that arent recorded in this scroll.
local: Yesu yo yoori alekutura sam beberebe na i susu­fɔm n aŋu i, ama, ba kɛrɛ má i kɛrɛ awura má ahin kadasi n nu.

eng: But these things are written so that you will believe that Jesus is the Christ, Gods Son, and that believing, you will have life in his name.
local: Ama, ba kɛrɛ ahinm ni, adena am yo yarada ka Yesu Nyɛmɛ Wa ni ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni, na am yarada ni ma am nya ŋgɔɔ i dɛɛ duma n nu.

eng: Theophilus, the first scroll I wrote concerned everything Jesus did and taught from the beginning.
local: Ya si Teyofilosi, m kadasi n bo m du mɔ kɛrɛ maari u ni, m yo seeri u fa kɔ sam bo Yesu yo yoori bu n kɛrɛ, ni kabo i kereeri minɛm, saŋga bo i boori i juma n bu ni.

eng: right up to the day when he was taken up into heaven. Before he was taken up, working in the power of the Holy Spirit, Jesus instructed the apostles he had chosen.
local: haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ faari i fun kɔɔri aŋgoro ni. Kana i ba fun kɔ ni, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu ye i yo seeri i jaramasam bo wɔ yi bu n sam bo bu bá yo n a.

eng: After his suffering, he showed them that he was alive with many convincing proofs. He appeared to them over a period of forty days, speaking to them about Gods kingdom.
local: Yesu wu teŋgeeri n dɛɛ sin, na wɔ wo durunya n nu cɛɛn abura­na, na i suyi i ŋu sukere bu saŋga saŋga su, bo i kere ka i la ŋgɔɔ nahɔrɛ su. Na ba ŋu i, na wɔ jɔjɔ bu dɔ fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n su.

eng: While they were eating together, he ordered them not to leave Jerusalem but to wait for what the Father had promised. He said, This is what you heard from me.
local: Na saŋga bo Yesu ni i jaramasam ba tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ wo diire di su ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Má am se ka am fite Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, ama, am ŋminda kɔ ju saŋga bo m Si Nyɛmɛ fá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bá ma am, yiri ye i yo seeri ka i fá ma am n a. Yiri ye m yo kaanri i jɔrɛ kereeri am n a.

eng: John baptized with water, but in only a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Yohane yo faari nzue biɛɛri am a, ama, i cɛ má, mini fá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ba wura am ŋu a.

eng: As a result, those who had gathered together asked Jesus, Lord, are you going to restore the kingdom to Israel now.
local: Na saŋga bo Yesu ni i jaramasam n ba tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ ni, na ba bisa i ka, Ya Mibiɛ, kisa ye a káaki fa fɛmɛya n ma yɛrɛ Yisareli­fɔm, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: Jesus replied, It isnt for you to know the times or seasons that the Father has set by his own authority.
local: Na Yesu ase bu ka, Deke bo m Si Nyɛmɛ koro ka i yo i, ye i yo a, ambɛrɛ koro si má nyini saŋga wara cɛɛn ni.

eng: Rather, you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you, and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.
local: Ama, saŋga bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ba wuraari am ŋu, i má am faŋga na am dí m ŋusu daani Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, ni Yudiya awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, ni Samariya awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, haari kɔ kpura durunya n kɛrɛ pepere su.

eng: After Jesus said these things, as they were watching, he was lifted up and a cloud took him out of their sight.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na Nyɛmɛ ama wɔ fun akɔ aŋgoro, bu toko jina wo niɛ su ni, na yinyiŋgu akata i ŋu, ba ŋu má i kun.

eng: While he was going away and as they were staring toward heaven, suddenly two men in white robes stood next to them.
local: Bu jina wo niɛ su kabo i sufun kɔ aŋgoro n nu barasu ni, na minɛ nnyɔ nbem akaba aba ajina bu bi, bu wura sutura fofoe.

eng: They said, Galileans, why are you standing here, looking toward heaven? This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way that you saw him go into heaven.
local: na ba bisa bu ka, Galili­fɔm, nzu ti ye am jina niɛ aŋgoro n nu sɔ a? Yiri Yesu ni, kabo am aŋu ka i sufun kɔ aŋgoro n nu barasu ni, am bá kaaki ŋu i sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, saŋga bo i bá sukpie i nyi ba ni.

eng: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives, which is near Jerusalem—a sabbath days journey away.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, Yesu jaramasam yo woori buka n bo bu fere i ka Oliva Buka n su wɔ. Dɔ ni Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu waari má. Na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ Yɛrusalem.

eng: When they entered the city, they went to the upstairs room where they were staying. Peter, John, James, and Andrew; Philip and Thomas; Bartholomew and Matthew; James, Alphaeus son; Simon the zealot; and Judas, James son.
local: Bu kɔ juuri n na ba fun akɔ aŋgoro sɔɔ n bo bu da nu n ni. Bɛrɛ bo bu kɔɔri dɔ n la Petoro, ni Yohane, ni Yakobo, ni Andere, ni Filipu, ni Tomasi, ni Batolomeyo, ni Matio, Yakobo bo i si la Alafeyo ni, ni Simon bo i tu ŋgoni fa kɔ i asiɛn su ni, ni Yudasi bo i si la Yakobo ni.

eng: all were united in their devotion to prayer, along with some women, including Mary the mother of Jesus, and his brothers.
local: Bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ sɔ ɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, na bu susere Nyɛmɛ, bu ni nbaram nbem, ni Yesu ni Mariya, ni Yesu niɛmam ni.

eng: During this time, the family of believers was a company of about one hundred twenty persons. Peter stood among them and said.
local: Cɛɛn be sɔ ɔ, na yarada­yo­fɔm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ, bu kɛrɛ kɔ yasuuri minɛ yaa ni abura­nyɔ ɔ. Dɔ na Petoro ayasu ajina bu afiɛn ase bu ka.

eng: Brothers and sisters, the scripture that the Holy Spirit announced beforehand through David had to be fulfilled. This was the scripture concerning Judas, who became a guide for those who arrested Jesus.
local: M ninbaam, wɔ yo daka ka nɔaniɛ n bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n yo maari Fɛmɛ Davidi nɔɔ na i kɛrɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n yo yii­wa, kabo i kɛrɛɛri fa kɔ Yudasi jɔrɛ ni, ka yiri dú minɛm bo bu ba tara Yesu n mɔ a.

eng: This happened even though he was one of us and received a share of this ministry. .
local: Yudasi yo tiiri ya nu kun wɔ, ya ni yiri saŋga yoori juma n a.

eng: In fact, he bought a field with the payment he received for his injustice. Falling headfirst, he burst open in the middle and all his intestines spilled out.
local: Ya si Teyofilosi, si nu ka Yudasi afa ŋwaa n bo i yoori satiɛ na i nyaari i ni, na wɔ fa ato asiɛn, dɔ na wɔ kpaasi, na wɔ wu, na i kunnu atiɛ, na i ayaya kɛrɛ afite agɔ.

eng: This became known to everyone living in Jerusalem, so they called that field in their own language Hakeldama, or Field of Blood.
local: Na Yɛrusalem­fɔm kɛrɛ ati nyini jɔrɛ ni, nyini ti, na ba fere nyini asiɛn ni, bu aniɛ nu ka Akelidama , i bu la ka, Mbunja Asiɛn.

eng: It is written in the Psalms scroll, Let his home become deserted and let there be no one living in it; and Give his position of leadership to another. .
local: Tɔ, na Petoro akaaki ase bu ka, Bu yo kɛrɛɛri i Kansi Jue kɛrɛɛ­wam n nu, ka, Ma i dika n ka bɔnfɔ, na má be wo nu. Na wɔ kaaki akɛrɛ ka, Ma sɔnɔ be de i jinaa­biri ni.

eng: Therefore, we must select one of those who have accompanied us during the whole time the Lord Jesus lived among us.
local: Nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka i mi ti sɔnɔ bo i woori ya nu, saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu woori bɛrɛ na ya nati tiɛn ya nɔɔ i dɔ ni, fite saŋga n bo Yohane biɛɛri Yesu Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, haari fa kɔ juuri cɛɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ maari Yesu fun kɔɔri aŋgoro yakiiri ya ni.

eng: So they nominated two: Joseph called Barsabbas, who was also known as Justus, and Matthias.
local: Nyini ti, na ba yi minɛ nnyɔ ajina. Bu kun la Yosefu bo bu fere i ka Basabasi, na bu kaaki fere i ka Jusitosi ni, ye kun n la bo bu fere i ka Matiyasi ni.

eng: They prayed, Lord, you know everyones deepest thoughts and desires. Show us clearly which one you have chosen from among these two.
local: Dɔ na ba sere Nyɛmɛ, bu ŋa, Ya Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ si kabo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sunsunni ti barasu a. Ahin minɛ nnyɔm ni nu, kere ya bo a koro ka ya yi i.

eng: to take the place of this ministry and apostleship, from which Judas turned away to go to his own place.
local: na i yo u jaramasa na i yo ahin juma ni, na i jina Yudasi jinaa­biri, yiri bo wɔ yaki u akɔ i koroo­biri ni.

eng: When they cast lots, the lot fell on Matthias. He was added to the eleven apostles.
local: Na ba tu mbaa na bu yi minɛ nnyɔm nu kun, na ba yi Matiyasi abuka Yesu jaramasa buru ni kunm n su.

eng: When Pentecost Day arrived, they were all together in one place.
local: Yuda­fɔm cɛɛn­kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Pɛntɛkoti n di cɛɛn, nyini saŋga ni, na Yesu susu­fɔm kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ dika hɔrɛ kun.

eng: Suddenly a sound from heaven like the howling of a fierce wind filled the entire house where they were sitting.
local: Kpeŋga na ba ti naŋmiɛ be ŋga aŋgoro n nu ka aŋuma bo i sukpiɛ faŋga su, na wɔ de sɔɔ n bo bu tana nu n kɛrɛ abara ayɛ.

eng: They saw what seemed to be individual flames of fire alighting on each one of them.
local: Dɔ na ba ŋu deke be ka sin taferema dɛɛ su, na wɔ sandi atantana bu kun kun n kɛrɛ tii su.

eng: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other languages as the Spirit enabled them to speak.
local: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura bu kɛrɛ ŋu, na bu sujɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ma bu koro jɔjɔ i.

eng: There were pious Jews from every nation under heaven living in Jerusalem.
local: Tɔ, nyini saŋga, Yuda­fɔm bo bu soro Nyɛmɛ n atiɛn bu nɔɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, bu fiteeri durunya n awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ baari a, ka bu ba su Nyɛmɛ.

eng: When they heard this sound, a crowd gathered. They were mystified because everyone heard them speaking in their native languages.
local: Saŋga bo bu tiiri naŋmiɛ ni ŋga sɔ ni, na jama beberebe aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na jɔrɛ n amiɛ bu, dama bu kɛrɛ tiiri ka Yesu susu­fɔm ni sujɔjɔ bɛrɛ kun kun n kɛrɛ bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ n dɛɛ aniɛ nu.

eng: They were surprised and amazed, saying, Look, arent all the people who are speaking Galileans, every one of them?.
local: Na ba birisi na ba yo alekutura, na ba se ka, Ahin minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo jɔjɔ su n ti Galili­fɔm wɔ.

eng: How then can each of us hear them speaking in our native language?.
local: ye i baari sɛ, ye bu sujɔjɔ, ye ya kun kun n kɛrɛ ti ka bu jɔjɔ ya dɛɛ aniɛm n a?.

eng: Parthians, Medes, and Elamites; as well as residents of Mesopotamia, Judea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia.
local: Ya fite awɔrɛ barasu barasu kɛrɛ n nu wɔ, nbem fite Patiya, na nbem fite Mediya, na nbem fite Elam, na nbem fite Mesopotamiya, na nbem fite Yudiya, na nbem fite Kapadosiya, na nbem fite Pontusi ni Esiya.

eng: Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the regions of Libya bordering Cyrene; and visitors from Rome.
local: na nbem fite Firigiya, na nbem fite Pamfiliya, na nbem fite Ijipiti asiɛn n su, na nbem fite Libiya asiɛn bo i wo dodo Sirene miɛ kpiri n ni. Ya nbem gusu fite Roma miɛ kpiri n su wɔ.

eng: Cretans and Arabs—we hear them declaring the mighty works of God in our own languages.
local: Ya nbem ti Yuda­fɔm, ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ba kaaki bu wo ka bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, ye ya nbem gusu fite Kereti asiɛn n su, na nbem fite Arabiya asiɛn n su, ama, ya kɛrɛ ti ka bu jɔjɔ ya dɛɛ aniɛm n nu wɔ, fa kɔ sakpa­kpam kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayo bu n su.

eng: They were all surprised and bewildered. Some asked each other, What does this mean.
local: Na ba yo alekutura, ba si má kabo bu yo, na bu subisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ahin sa n bu la ka sɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Others jeered at them, saying, Theyre full of new wine.
local: Ama, na bu nbem ayo Yesu susu­fɔm n anzasi, na ba se ka, Ai, ba nu nzan abo wɔ.

eng: Peter stood with the other eleven apostles. He raised his voice and declared, Judeans and everyone living in Jerusalem! Know this! Listen carefully to my words!.
local: Dɔ na Petoro ni i beŋgu buru ni kunm n ayasu ajina, na wɔ yɛ i konvi su ase jama n ka, M lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔm, ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, am so am sui nyunyumi na am ti deke n bo m sukan kere am ni.

eng: These people arent drunk, as you suspect; after all, its only nine oclock in the morning!.
local: Ahin minɛm bo am sunsun ka ba nu nzan abo ni, ba nu má nzan, kisa ŋgarama kerefi ŋgɔna ye ya wo nu n a.

eng: Rather, this is what was spoken through the prophet Joel.
local: Ama, nyini n bo am ti ka bu jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu ni, i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Jowɛli yo jɔjɔɔri n wɔ lɛɛ, i ŋa.

eng: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young will see visions. Your elders will dream dreams. .
local: Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, fa kɔ durunya n awieeri, m fá m Awiɛwiɛ n wura minɛm kɛrɛ ŋu. Na am nbaa­biɛsɔm ni nbaa­baram kán Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm, na am ngbɛfɛnɛm ŋú deke nbem nahɔrɛ su ka alɛlɛ nu, na am nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm dá cin alɛlɛ.

eng: Even upon my servants, men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy. .
local: Nahɔrɛ, nzɛn cɛɛn n ba juuri, m fá m Awiɛwiɛ n wura m akɔɔm n ŋu, i bara ni i biɛsɔ kɛrɛ, na bu kán m nɔaniɛ n kere minɛ.

eng: I will cause wonders to occur in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and a cloud of smoke. .
local: Na m yó alekutura sam barasu barasu beberebe aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu fɛn, na am ŋú mbunja, ni sin, ni ŋmisini bambaka.

eng: The sun will be changed into darkness, and the moon will be changed into blood, before the great and spectacular day of the Lord comes. .
local: Na wiɛ n sí biri, na sara n káaki kɔkɔrɛ ka mbunja. Nyini sin, na Mibiɛ n cɛɛn bambaka n bo i la jirima n nyá ju.

eng: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved. .
local: Nyini cɛɛn ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bo fereeri Mibiɛ n duma, i mi nyá tii­de.

eng: Fellow Israelites, listen to these words! Jesus the Nazarene was a man whose credentials God proved to you through miracles, wonders, and signs, which God performed through him among you. You yourselves know this.
local: Na Petoro ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ Yisareli­fɔm, am so am sui na am ti fɛn. Nyɛmɛ akere am ka yiri sunmaari Yesu bo i fite Nazarɛti miɛ n nu n a. Nyini ti, ye i ma i yoori alekutura sam, ni sa ŋgbiŋgbirim am nu n a, kabo ambɛrɛ ni am tii si i ni.

eng: In accordance with Gods established plan and foreknowledge, he was betrayed. You, with the help of wicked men, had Jesus killed by nailing him to a cross.
local: Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu, ye i du mɔ ciciiri ka i má am nya Yesu taraa­biri a, ye am faari i wuraari minɛ tiɛm saa nu, ye bu kpamaari i baka jaanu n su kuunri i n a.

eng: God raised him up! God freed him from deaths dreadful grip, since it was impossible for death to hang on to him.
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i afite i yiwee n nu, yiwee akoro ala má i yiko, dama i tiiri kekereke na yiwee koro kunma i.

eng: David says about him, I foresaw that the Lord was always with me; because he is at my right hand I wont be shaken. .
local: Dama Fɛmɛ Davidi yo jɔjɔɔri fa kɔ yiri Yesu ŋusu jɔrɛ, i ŋa, Ma ŋu i ka Mibiɛ n wo m dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti, puitiri­pui koro yo má m.

eng: Therefore, my heart was glad and my tongue rejoiced. Moreover, my body will live in hope, .
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ahore ti daa­wa, na m jɔjɔ ni ahorejɔ, haari ni bo m bá wu ni, m la tama fa kɔ m ŋuniɛ­mbaa n su.

eng: because you wont abandon me to the grave, nor permit your holy one to experience decay. .
local: dama wɔrɔ Nyɛmɛ, a yáki má m na m ka fuinm n nu, a yáki má na u dɛɛ amusui­su­fɔ casi ka mini sɔ wu kpɔrɔ.

eng: You have shown me the paths of life; your presence will fill me with happiness. .
local: A kere m atin bo m fa na m nya ŋgɔɔ, na kabo a wo m dɔ ni, nyini má m la ahorejɔ nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I can speak confidently about the patriarch David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is with us to this very day.
local: M ninbaam, m koro ka m se am nahɔrɛ su wɔ, fa kɔ ya na Fɛmɛ Davidi ŋusu jɔrɛ. I yo wuuri wɔ, na ba se i, i faŋ­kunma toko wo ya nu fɛn haari ni nyuma.

eng: Because he was a prophet, he knew that God promised him with a solemn pledge to seat one of his descendants on his throne.
local: I tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ si ka Nyɛmɛ atan ndiɛ ka, i yí yiri Fɛmɛ Davidi busu nu­fɔm n nu kun, na i dí i fɛmɛya n kabo yiri Fɛmɛ Davidi yo tiiri barasu ni.

eng: Having seen this beforehand, David spoke about the resurrection of Christ, that he wasnt abandoned to the grave, nor did his body experience decay .
local: Fɛmɛ Davidi du mɔ ŋuuri sa n bo Nyɛmɛ bá yo i ni, na wɔ jɔjɔ fa kɔ Kristo su, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni, ka i bá wu na i téŋge i yiwee nu, i ŋa, Nyɛmɛ yáki má na i ka fuinm nu, na i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kpɔ́rɔ.

eng: This Jesus God raised up. We are all witnesses to that fact.
local: Yiri Yesu n ye Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu a, na ya kɛrɛ aŋu i, ye ya sudi i daani a.

eng: He was exalted to Gods right side and received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit. He poured out this Spirit, and you are seeing and hearing the results of his having done so.
local: I Si Nyɛmɛ ayɛ i su akɔ aŋgoro afa atana i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, ye wɔ ma i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo wɔ se ka i fá ma i ni. Deke bo am aŋu i ni, ni deke n bo am ati i ni, yiri la Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i seeri ka i fá ma ya ni.

eng: David didnt ascend into heaven. Yet he says, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side, .
local: Má Fɛmɛ Davidi ni i tii fun kɔɔri aŋgoro a, ama, na wɔ se ka, Micɛra n ase m Mibiɛ n ka, Bra, ba tana m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su fɛn.

eng: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet. .
local: haari m bá ma a kunma u kpɔ­fɔm. .
