eng: Therefore, let all Israel know beyond question that God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Christ.
local: Na Petoro akaaki ase ka, I ti ye ni ka Yisareli­fɔm kɛrɛ si ka, yiri Yesu n bo ambɛrɛ yo kpamaari i baka jaanu n su n ye Nyɛmɛ afa i ayo Mibiɛ n ni Kristo, ya Tii­de­fɔ n a.

eng: When the crowd heard this, they were deeply troubled. They said to Peter and the other apostles, Brothers, what should we do.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm n tiiri ahin jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na bu ahore asaaki agaya, na ba bisa Petoro ni Yesu jaramasa nyiŋgam n ka, Ya ninbaam, ye ya yo sɛ wɔ.

eng: Peter replied, Change your hearts and lives. Each of you must be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. Then you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
local: Na Petoro ase bu ka, Am kaaki am wo, na ya bíɛ am kun kun kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma n nu, na Nyɛmɛ fá am satiɛ ce am, na i fá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ce am.

eng: This promise is for you, your children, and for all who are far away—as many as the Lord our God invites.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i fá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ma ambɛrɛ ni am busu nu­fɔm, ni minɛm bo bu wo dede ni, ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo ya Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n fere bu ka bu bra yiri ni i tii dɔ n wɔ.

eng: With many other words he testified to them and encouraged them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.
local: Na Petoro ajɔjɔ jɔrɛ beberebe atu bu fɔ ni anyum­boro su, i ŋa, Am yi am ŋu fite sui­cin n bo Nyɛmɛ bá fa cin ahin nyuma­fɔ satiɛ­yo­fɔm n nu.

eng: Those who accepted Peters message were baptized. God brought about three thousand people into the community on that day.
local: Na bu dɔŋgu ayo i nɔaniɛ n yarada, na ba biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue, nyini cɛɛn ni, na ba nya minɛ, ba yo ka akpii nsan abuka bu ŋu su.

eng: The believers devoted themselves to the apostles teaching, to the community, to their shared meals, and to their prayers.
local: Na bu nyunu aboro ka bu sɔnsɔn Yesu jaramasam keree­wam ni, na bu kɛrɛ ayo anɔɔkun, na bu susaŋga di diire, na bu susaŋga sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: A sense of awe came over everyone. God performed many wonders and signs through the apostles.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ ama Yesu jaramasam n ayo alekutura sam barasu barasu beberebe, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ asoro Nyɛmɛ.

eng: All the believers were united and shared everything.
local: Na yarada­yo­fɔm n ayo deke kɛrɛ ni anɔɔsɛ, na ba soro su ka, deke kɛrɛ bo bu la i n ti bu kɛrɛ dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: They would sell pieces of property and possessions and distribute the proceeds to everyone who needed them.
local: Bu tɔ bu neŋge kɛrɛ bo bu la i wɔ, na bu kpaaki ŋwaa n nu, na bo bu la má be n kɛrɛ nya be.

eng: Every day, they met together in the temple and ate in their homes. They shared food with gladness and simplicity.
local: Cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, bu ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu wɔ, na bu nati sutiɛn bu nɔɔ awuru awurum nu, na bu saŋga di diire ni ahorejɔ, ni bu kunnu fofoe.

eng: They praised God and demonstrated Gods goodness to everyone. The Lord added daily to the community those who were being saved.
local: na bu sukansi Nyɛmɛ, na minɛm akoro bu jɔrɛ. Na cɛɛn kɛrɛ cɛɛn na bu sunya minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ ma bu anya tii­de n abuka bu ŋu su.

eng: Peter and John were going up to the temple at three oclock in the afternoon, the established prayer time.
local: Cɛɛn n be naasɔ wiɛ kerefi nsan saŋga, na Petoro ni Yohane akɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ka bu kɔ sere Nyɛmɛ. Saŋga bo bu fa sere Nyɛmɛ lɛɛ.

eng: Meanwhile, a man crippled since birth was being carried in. Every day, people would place him at the temple gate known as the Beautiful Gate so he could ask for money from those entering the temple.
local: Sɔnɔ be woori dɔ, bu wuuri i wɔbuka wɔ, na minɛ nbem asɔ i aba atana Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru nu anɔ n bo bu fere i ka Anɔ Nyunyumi ni, na i tana susere minɛm bo bu ba nyɛmɛsere ni, ka bu ma i ŋwaa. Cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn bu fa i ba wɔ, na i ba susere.

eng: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he began to ask them for a gift.
local: Nyini cɛɛn ni, saŋga bo i ŋuuri Petoro ni Yohane ka bu suwura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni, na wɔ sere bɛrɛ gusu ka bu ma i deke be.

eng: Peter and John stared at him. Peter said, Look at us.
local: Na Petoro ni Yohane afa bu nyu­mbaa akpama i ŋu, na Petoro ase i ka, Niɛ ya dɔ fɛn.

eng: So the man gazed at them, expecting to receive something from them.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ tu i tii suniɛ bu dɔ, i sunsun ka bu má i deke be wɔ.

eng: Peter said, I dont have any money, but I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, rise up and walk.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, M la má ŋwaa bo m ma u, ama, deke bo m la i na m fa ma u yaa, Yesu Kristo Nazarɛti­fɔ n dɛɛ duma nu, m ŋa, a yasu nati.

eng: Then he grasped the mans right hand and raised him up. At once his feet and ankles became strong.
local: Dɔ na Petoro aso wɔbuka n saa fɔmbɔrɔ nu, na wɔ yɛ i su ajina, kpeŋga sɔnɔ n jaam kɛrɛ ayo faŋga.

eng: Jumping up, he began to walk around. He entered the temple with them, walking, leaping, and praising God.
local: nyini ti, na wɔ warakpin, na wɔ jina i jaa su, na wɔ bo i bu sunati bara yɛ. Dɔ na wɔ su bu su awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na i sunati, na i suwarakpin, na i sukansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
local: Na jama n kɛrɛ aŋu ka i sunati, na i sukansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: They recognized him as the same one who used to sit at the temples Beautiful Gate asking for money. They were filled with amazement and surprise at what had happened to him.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm ni siiri nu ka, yiri bo i yo tana Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n Anɔ Nyunyumi n nu sere n lɛɛ ni, na ba miɛ atana, na sa n bo i yoori sɔnɔ n ayo bu alekutura.

eng: While the healed man clung to Peter and John, all the people rushed toward them at Solomons Porch, completely amazed.
local: Saŋga bo wɔbuka n bita Petoro ni Yohane barasu ni, na minɛ kɛrɛ ayo alekutura, na ba ŋmati aba bu dɔ awɔrɛ be bo bu fere i ka Solomon dɛɛ Lɛlɛ n nu.

eng: Seeing this, Peter addressed the people: You Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us as if we made him walk by our own power or piety?.
local: Saŋga bo Petoro ŋuuri minɛm sɔ ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Yisareli­fɔm, nzu ti ye am fa ahin sa n yo alekutura ye am niɛ ya sɔ a? Am sunsun ka ya dɛɛ yiko nu ye ahin sɔnɔ n anati a, wara am sunsun ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n dɛɛ ti, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, i ti má sɔ.

eng: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—the God of our ancestors—has glorified his servant Jesus. This is the one you handed over and denied in Pilates presence, even though he had already decided to release him.
local: Nyɛmɛ bo Abraham ni Izaki ni Jakɔbu ni ya nam suuri i ni, yiri ye wɔ wura i amusui­su­fɔ Yesu n kpiri bambaka a. Ama, am faari i wuraari yiko­fɔm saa nu wɔ, na am akete i Gomina Pilato nyunu, ka am koro má i, haari ni bo má gomina n ŋa i yaki i nu ji ni.

eng: You rejected the holy and righteous one, and asked that a murderer be released to you instead.
local: I ti Nyɛmɛ sɔnɔ fofoe wɔ, bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ama, na am akete i, na am asere gomina n jande ka i yaki sɔnɔ kun­fɔ nu ji.

eng: You killed the author of life, the very one whom God raised from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
local: Na am akun yiri Yesu n bo i ma ya ŋgɔɔ ni, ama, Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i yiwee n nu, ya ŋuuri i wɔ ye ya sudi i daani a.

eng: His name itself has made this man strong. That is, because of faith in Jesus name, God has strengthened this man whom you see and know. The faith that comes through Jesus gave him complete health right before your eyes.
local: Ahin sɔnɔ bo am ŋu i na am si i ni, Yesu dɛɛ duma n yiko nu ma i jaam kɛrɛ ayo faŋga n a. Deke n bo am aŋu i ni, i fite ka sɔnɔ n ayo Yesu duma n yarada n dɛɛ ti, nyini ma wɔ nya laifiɛ ye am kɛrɛ aŋu i n a.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I know you acted in ignorance. So did your rulers.
local: Na Petoro anya ase bu ka, M ninbaam, m si ka ambɛrɛ ni am tere­tere­fɔm kɛrɛ be mmɔɔ asi má sam bo am yo yoori bu ni.

eng: But this is how God fulfilled what he foretold through all the prophets: that his Christ would suffer.
local: Dawa saŋga, Nyɛmɛ ma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ yo jɔjɔɔri ahin jɔrɛ kɛrɛ, ka, Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni bá, na i bá di wahara, nyini lɛɛ ye wɔ yo yii­wa bɛrɛ n a.

eng: Change your hearts and lives! Turn back to God so that your sins may be wiped away.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am kaaki am wo, na am su Nyɛmɛ, na i fá am satiɛ ce am.

eng: Then the Lord will provide a season of relief from the distress of this age and he will send Jesus, whom he handpicked to be your Christ.
local: na i má am nyá ŋumi­yi, na i nyá sunma Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo wɔ yi i ka i bá am dɔ ni, yiri la Yesu.

eng: Jesus must remain in heaven until the restoration of all things, about which God spoke long ago through his holy prophets.
local: Wɔ yo daka ka i wó Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga n bo Nyɛmɛ má deke kɛrɛ yo yii­wa, kabo dawa saŋga na i maari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ casim n nɔɔ na bu seeri ni.

eng: Moses said, The Lord your God will raise up from your own people a prophet like me. Listen to whatever he tells you. .
local: Moyisi yo seeri ka, Am Micɛra Nyɛmɛ súnma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ kabo i sunmaari mini Moyisi n wɔ. Nyini sɔnɔ ni yó am dɛɛ tiɛŋgu sɔnɔ bo i fite am nu wɔ. I ti ye ni ka am de i nɔaniɛ, na am yo deke kɛrɛ bo i se ka am yo i.

eng: Whoever doesnt listen to that prophet will be totally cut off from the people. .
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n nɔaniɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ yí i mi fite i dɛɛ minɛm nu wɔ, na i ká ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: All the prophets who spoke—from Samuel forward—announced these days.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu jɔjɔɔri fite Samuwɛli saŋga haari ba ju ni nyuma, bu kɛrɛ jɔjɔɔri fa kɔ ahin sam bo bu suyo nyuma n su wɔ.

eng: You are the heirs of the prophets and the covenant that God made with your ancestors when he told Abraham, Through your descendants, all the families on earth will be blessed. .
local: Ambɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm a, am dɛɛ la sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ yo diiri i di ni am nam ni. Na wɔ se ya na Abraham ka, U busu nu­fɔm dɛɛ ti ye m yó durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ kisi a.

eng: After God raised his servant, he sent him to you first—to bless you by enabling each of you to turn from your evil ways.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i du mɔ yiiri i amusui­su­fɔ n ye i sunmaari i am dɔ n a, ka i bá yo am kisi, nyini la ka, i má am kaaki am wo, na i yí am fite am satiɛ kɛrɛ nu.

eng: While Peter and John were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees confronted them.
local: Petoro ni Yohane toko jɔjɔ jama n dɔ ni, na minɛm nbem aba bu dɔ, bɛrɛ la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm nbem, ni Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu tintini­jina­fɔm kpiɛn, ni Sadusi­fɔm nbem.

eng: They were incensed that the apostles were teaching the people and announcing that the resurrection of the dead was happening because of Jesus.
local: Bu baari ni yaa su wɔ, kabo Yesu jaramasa nnyɔm ni sukere minɛm ka Yesu wuuri ni, i teŋgeeri i yiwee nu wɔ, nyini kere ka minɛm wu, bu teŋge bu yiwee nu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: They seized Peter and John and put them in prison until the next day.
local: Nyini ti, na ba tara Petoro ni Yohane. Bu taraari bu naasɔ saŋga a, na ba nyi bu haari ale akɔ acin.

eng: Many who heard the word became believers, and their number grew to about five thousand.
local: Ama, na minɛ dɔŋgu bo bu tiiri Yesu jaramasa ni keree­wa n ayo yarada. Nyini ti, bɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo bu yoori yarada ni, bu nbiɛsɔm kɔ yasuuri minɛ akpii nnu wɔ.

eng: The next day the leaders, elders, and legal experts gathered in Jerusalem.
local: Alecin ŋgarama na Yuda­fɔm tere­tere­fɔm, ni bu nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: along with Annas the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and others from the high priests family.
local: Bo bu gusu woori dɔ n la Anasi bo i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn ni, ni minɛ nbem bo bu fere bu ka Kayafasi, ni Yohane, ni Alɛkizanda, ni minɛm bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm busu nu n kɛrɛ tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ ɔ.

eng: They had Peter and John brought before them and asked, By what power or in what name did you do this.
local: Na ba fa Petoro ni Yohane aba ajina bu afiɛn, na ba bisa bu ka, Am yoori sɛ yoori ahin sa n a? Am la yiko bɔnɔ, wara ŋma dɛɛ duma nu ye am fa tuntuunri sɔnɔ n ye wɔ nya laifiɛ n a.

eng: Then Peter, inspired by the Holy Spirit, answered, Leaders of the people and elders.
local: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura Petoro ŋu, na wɔ se bu ka, Ya tere­tere­fɔm ni ya nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm.

eng: are we being examined today because something good was done for a sick person, a good deed that healed him?.
local: sakpa n bo ya yo i ama wɔbuka ni, ni kabo i yoori barasu na wɔ nya laifiɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye am sudi ya jɔrɛ bisa ya sɔ a, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: If so, then you and all the people of Israel need to know that this man stands healthy before you because of the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—whom you crucified but whom God raised from the dead.
local: I ti ye ni ka ambɛrɛ ni Yisareli­fɔm kɛrɛ si ka o, ahin sɔnɔ n bo i jina bɛrɛ na wɔ nya laifiɛ ni, i fite Yesu Kristo Nazarɛti­fɔ n duma n nu wɔ. Yiri ye am kuunri i, ye Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri i fiteeri i yiwee n nu n a.

eng: This Jesus is the stone you builders rejected; he has become the cornerstone!.
local: Ba kɛrɛ i ŋusu jɔrɛ Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Yabuɛ n bo ambɛrɛ bondo ta­fɔm faari i tuuri ni, yiri ye wɔ kaaki bondo n jaa yabuɛm kɛrɛ tii a.

eng: Salvation can be found in no one else. Throughout the whole world, no other name has been given among humans through which we must be saved.
local: Yiri ŋgumi ye Nyɛmɛ ayi i ka i ba de sɔnɔ kɛrɛ tii a, duma be wo má durunya n kɛrɛ nu bo i koro de ya tii ji, má ka Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma ni ŋgumi.

eng: The council was caught by surprise by the confidence with which Peter and John spoke. After all, they understood that these apostles were uneducated and inexperienced. They also recognized that they had been followers of Jesus.
local: Na Yuda­fɔ yiko­fɔm ni tere­tere­fɔm n aŋu kabo Petoro ni Yohane la ahoresi barasu ni, na ba si ka ba kɔ má kara be mmɔɔ, na wɔ yo bu alekutura. Dɔ na ba si nu ka bu yo suuri Yesu su wɔ.

eng: However, since the healed man was standing with Peter and John before their own eyes, they had no rebuttal.
local: Bu ŋuuri ka sɔnɔ n bo wɔ nya laifiɛ n jina Petoro ni Yohane bi ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ba koro ase má jɔrɛ be.

eng: After ordering them to wait outside, the council members began to confer with each other.
local: Na ba se Petoro ni Yohane ka bu fite sɔɔ n nu. Bu fite wieeri ni, na bɛrɛ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n abo Petoro ni Yohane n ŋusu anzama.

eng: What should we do with these men? Everyone living in Jerusalem is aware of the sign performed through them. Its obvious to everyone and we cant deny it.
local: Na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ye ya yó ahin minɛm sɛ wɔ? Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu si ka ba yo ahin alekutura sa bambaka ni, ya koro se má ka ba yo má i e.

eng: To keep it from spreading further among the people, we need to warn them not to speak to anyone in this name.
local: Ama, nzɛn ya koro má ka ahin jɔrɛ n bara yɛ deke n kɛrɛ, am ma ya ŋɔŋɔnɔ bu ka, má bu se ka bu bo Yesu duma mmɔɔ na be ti.

eng: When they called Peter and John back, they demanded that they stop all speaking and teaching in the name of Jesus.
local: Nyini ti, na ba kaaki afere Petoro ni Yohane awura, na ba ma bu nɔɔ ka, má bu se ka bu bo Yesu duma, wara bu kan i ŋusu jɔrɛ kere minɛm.

eng: Peter and John responded, Its up to you to determine whether its right before God to obey you rather than God.
local: Ama, na Petoro ni Yohane ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii, am niɛ nu na am niɛ e, Nyɛmɛ atin su, bɔnɔ ti ye a, ya de am dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n wɔ, nzɛn ya de Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ n wɔ?.

eng: As for us, we cant stop speaking about what we have seen and heard.
local: Ya sukan kere minɛm n wɔ fa kɔ sam n bo yɛrɛ ni ya tii ati bu, na ya ŋu bu ni, ya koro mɔ má ya nɔɔ.

eng: They threatened them further, then released them. Because of public support for Peter and John, they couldnt find a way to punish them. Everyone was praising God for what had happened.
local: Na bɛrɛ yiko­fɔm, ni tere­tere­fɔm n aŋɔŋɔnɔ bu nahɔrɛ su, na ba yaki bu nu aji. Bu niɛɛri nu, bu ŋu ka bu koro yo má bu pui, dama minɛm kɛrɛ wo Nyɛmɛ kansi su wɔ fa kɔ sa n bo wɔ yo ni.

eng: because the man who had experienced this sign of healing was over years old.
local: Sɔnɔ n bo bu yoori nyini alekutura sa n maari i na i nyaari laifiɛ ni, i tara afɔ abura­na.

eng: After their release, Peter and John returned to the brothers and sisters and reported everything the chief priests and elders had said.
local: Saŋga bo Petoro ni Yohane nyaari bu tii ni, bu kaba kpieeri bu nyi kɔɔri bu beŋgu Yesu susu­fɔm n dɔ ɔ, na ba kan jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛnm, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n seeri ni.

eng: They listened, then lifted their voices in unison to God, Master, you are the one who created the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
local: Yarada­yo­fɔm ni tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ sɔ ni, na bu kɛrɛ ayo anɔɔkun, na ba sere Nyɛmɛ, bu ŋa, Ya Micɛra Yiko­fɔ, wɔrɔ yiiri aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu a, ni tieku n ni dekem kɛrɛ bo bu wo nu ni.

eng: You are the one who spoke by the Holy Spirit through our ancestor David, your servant: Why did the Gentiles rage , and the peoples plot in vain? .
local: Wɔrɔ yo ma u Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wuraari ya na Davidi bo i ti u dɛɛ amusui­su­fɔ n ŋu a, na wɔ se ka, Nzu ti ye minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm afa yaa agaya sɔ a. ye minɛm bita sunsunni bo i la má kpɛrɛ a?.

eng: The kings of the earth took their stand and the rulers gathered together as one against the Lord and against his Christ. .
local: Ye durunya n nu nfɛmɛm ayasu ajina, na bu yiko­fɔm atiɛn bu nɔɔ, ka bu tu luɛ ni Micɛra Nyɛmɛ ni, ni Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i n a.

eng: Indeed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with Gentiles and Israelites, did gather in this city against your holy servant Jesus, whom you anointed.
local: Tɔ, kabo Fɛmɛ Davidi yo kaanri ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka Fɛmɛ Herode, ni Gomina Pɔntiyo Pilato fa saŋga ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, ni Yuda­fɔm yo tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ ahin miɛ n nu wɔ, na ba kete Yesu, u amusui­su­fɔ casi n bo a yiiri i ka yiri ti Tii­de­fɔ ni.

eng: They did what your power and plan had already determined would happen.
local: Bu tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ ɔ, na ba yo dekem kɛrɛ bo u dɛɛ yiko n ni u koroya­nu na a du mɔ acici ka bu yo ni.

eng: Now, Lord, take note of their threats and enable your servants to speak your word with complete confidence.
local: Ya Mibiɛ, ti kabo kisa bu ŋɔŋɔnɔ ya barasu, nyini ti, ma yɛrɛ u akɔɔm n ahoresi, na ya kan u dɛɛ labari kpa n na má ya soro sɛrɛ.

eng: Stretch out your hand to bring healing and enable signs and wonders to be performed through the name of Jesus, your holy servant.
local: Fa u yiko n tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu nya laifiɛ, na a ma ya yiko na ya tuntun minɛ, na ya yo alekutura sam barasu barasu, u amusui­su­fɔ casi Yesu n dɛɛ duma nu.

eng: After they prayed, the place where they were gathered was shaken. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking Gods word with confidence.
local: Saŋga bo bu sereeri Nyɛmɛ n wieeri sɔ ni, na dika n bo bu wo nu n akpisi. Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura bu kɛrɛ ŋu, na ba kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ni ahoresi.

eng: The community of believers was one in heart and mind. None of them would say, This is mine! about any of their possessions, but held everything in common.
local: Yarada­yo­fɔm n yo yoori anɔɔsɛ wɔ, na ba saŋga ayo deke n kɛrɛ kun. Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i se ka i neŋge be tiiri i tiɛŋgu dɛɛ. Ama, bu kɛrɛ kpaaki deke n kɛrɛ bo bu la i nu ma bu beŋgu wɔ.

eng: The apostles continued to bear powerful witness to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and an abundance of grace was at work among them all.
local: Na Yesu jaramasam n akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ni yiko bambaka, na ba kan akere minɛm kabo Yesu teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni. Na Nyɛmɛ ama bu kɛrɛ adi ahɔrɔma beberebe.

eng: There were no needy persons among them. Those who owned properties or houses would sell them, bring the proceeds from the sales.
local: Be awo má bu nu bo i miɛɛri deke be busu. Bo bu laari asiɛnm, ni awurum tɔɔri bu wɔ, na ba fa ŋwaa kɛrɛ bo bu nyaari i ni.

eng: and place them in the care and under the authority of the apostles. Then it was distributed to anyone who was in need.
local: aba ama Yesu jaramasam ni, na bɛrɛ gusu akpaaki i nu ama bo bu la má pui ni, na ba fa ayo deke bo i miɛ bu.

eng: Joseph, whom the apostles nicknamed Barnabas , was a Levite from Cyprus.
local: Sɔnɔ be yo woori bu nu, bu fere i ka Yosefu, i ti Levi busu nu­fɔ ɔ, bu wuuri i asiɛn be bo bu fere i ka Sipurusi n su wɔ. Na Yesu jaramasam n atun i duma ka Banabasi, i bu la ka, sɔnɔ bo i ma minɛ ahoresi ni.

eng: He owned a field, sold it, brought the money, and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Nyini sɔnɔ n tɔɔri i asiɛn n bo i la i, na wɔ fa ŋwaa n aba ama Yesu jaramasam ni.

eng: However, a man named Ananias, along with his wife Sapphira, sold a piece of property.
local: Sɔnɔ be yo woori dɔ bu fere i ka Ananiyasi, na bu fere i yi ka Safira, na ba tɔ bu aja be.

eng: With his wifes knowledge, he withheld some of the proceeds from the sale. He brought the rest and placed it in the care and under the authority of the apostles.
local: Ama, na yiri ni i yi n ayo bu nɔɔkun, na wɔ yi ŋwaa n be afiɛ, na wɔ fa bo i kaari n ama Yesu jaramasam ni.

eng: Peter asked, Ananias, how is it that Satan has influenced you to lie to the Holy Spirit by withholding some of the proceeds from the sale of your land?.
local: Na Petoro abisa i ka, Ananiyasi, nzu ti ye a ma Sitana awura u sunsunni nu, ye a fa ŋwaa n bo a tɔɔri u asiɛn n na a nyaari i n be afiɛ a, ye a ŋa a dadaka Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a?.

eng: Wasnt that property yours to keep? After you sold it, wasnt the money yours to do with whatever you wanted? What made you think of such a thing? You havent lied to other people but to God.
local: Saŋga n bo a nya tɔ má i ni, i ti u dɛɛ wɔ, kisa gusu bo a tɔ i ni, ŋwaa n ti u dɛɛ wɔ. Ye nzu ti ye a yo ahin sa n a? Má adamande ŋgumi ye a ŋa a dadaka a, ama, a ŋa a dadaka Nyɛmɛ wɔ.

eng: When Ananias heard these words, he dropped dead. Everyone who heard this conversation was terrified.
local: Ananiyasi tiiri nyini nɔaniɛ n sɔ ni, i kaba kpaasiiri wɔ, na wɔ wu, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tiiri nyini sa n bo wɔ yo ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu agaya.

eng: Some young men stood up, wrapped up his body, carried him out, and buried him.
local: Na ngbɛfɛnɛm ayasu afa tani apɔɔndi i, na ba fa i afite akɔ ase i.

eng: About three hours later, his wife entered, but she didnt know what had happened to her husband.
local: Wɔ cɛ má, na i yi gusu aba, wɔ si má sa bo wɔ yo ni.

eng: Peter asked her, Tell me, did you and your husband receive this price for the field? She responded, Yes, thats the amount.
local: Na Petoro abisa i ka, U ni u hun aja n bo am tɔɔri i n ŋwaa n kɛrɛ lɛɛ? Na wɔ bɔ Petoro n nu ka, Ɛɛn, i kɛrɛ lɛɛ.

eng: He replied, How could you scheme with each other to challenge the Lords Spirit? Look! The feet of those who buried your husband are at the door. They will carry you out too.
local: Dɔ na Petoro abisa i ka, Nzu ti ye u ni u hun ayo am nɔɔkun na am yo Micɛra n Awiɛwiɛ n na am niɛ a? Niɛ fɛn, minɛm bo bu faari u hun kɔ seeri i ni, bu jaa busu wo anɔ n nu, bu fá wɔrɔ mmɔɔ fite lɛɛ.

eng: At that very moment, she dropped dead at his feet. When the young men entered and found her dead, they carried her out and buried her with her husband.
local: Jina n nu bɛrɛ, na wɔ kaba akpaasi Petoro jaa bu, na wɔ wu. Bo bu seeri i hun n ba ŋuuri ka yiri gusu awu, na ba fa i akɔ ase i adodo i hun ni.

eng: Trepidation and dread seized the whole church and all who heard what had happened.
local: Na Kristo jama n kɛrɛ, ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu tiiri nyini sa n bo wɔ yo ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu kɛrɛ agaya.

eng: The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people. They would come together regularly at Solomons Porch.
local: Na Yesu jaramasam n ayo alekutura sam barasu barasu dɔŋgu minɛm n nu bo i kere ka Nyɛmɛ wo bu dɔ. Na yarada­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ Solomon Lɛlɛ n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni.

eng: No one from outside the church dared to join them, even though the people spoke highly of them.
local: Minɛm bo ba yo má Yesu yarada ni soroori sɛrɛ wɔ, ba koro asaŋga má bu nu, ama, na minɛm n awura bu kpiri.

eng: Indeed, more and more believers in the Lord, large numbers of both men and women, were added to the church.
local: Na minɛ beberebe ayo Mibiɛ n yarada, subuka bu su saŋga kɛrɛ, bu sunnu, nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram.

eng: As a result, they would even bring the sick out into the main streets and lay them on cots and mats so that at least Peters shadow could fall on some of them as he passed by.
local: Na alekutura sam bo Yesu jaramasam yoori bu n dɛɛ ti, na minɛ asɔ bu tukpaki­fɔm, ni bu dasum aba ase atiŋgberem nɔɔ su, adena Petoro ba sunati sin, na haari i awiɛwiɛ mmɔɔ tɔ gɔ bu nbem ŋu, na bu nyá laifiɛ.

eng: Even large numbers of persons from towns around Jerusalem would gather, bringing the sick and those harassed by unclean spirits. Everyone was healed.
local: Na minɛ beberebe afite miɛ miɛm bo bu bara yɛ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n su ni, na ba fa tukpaki­fɔm ni minɛm bo ajinim yaki má bu n aba, na bu kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

eng: The high priest, together with his allies, the Sadducees, was overcome with jealousy.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn ni, ni i beŋgum bo bu su Sadusi­fɔm atin su ni, aba asi Yesu jaramasam ni kɔrɛ agaya.

eng: They seized the apostles and made a public show of putting them in prison.
local: Na ba tara Yesu jaramasam ni, akɔ anyi bu dan­saraka.

eng: An angel from the Lord opened the prison doors during the night and led them out. The angel told them.
local: Ama, nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ, na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ aba ateke dan­saraka anɔ ni, na wɔ yi Yesu jaramasam n afite, na wɔ se bu ka.

eng: Go, take your place in the temple, and tell the people everything about this new life.
local: Am kɔ na am kɔ jina Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na am kan ahin ŋgɔɔ fɔfɔrɛ n bo am anya i n jɔrɛ kɛrɛ kere minɛm.

eng: Early in the morning, they went into the temple as they had been told and began to teach. When the high priest and his colleagues gathered, they convened the Jerusalem Council, that is, the full assembly of Israels elders. They sent word to the prison to have the apostles brought before them.
local: Na jaramasam n ade i nɔaniɛ ni, na alecin ŋgarama fuun, na ba kɔ ajina Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na ba bo i bu sukere minɛ. Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ni i dɛɛ jama nbaam n ba juuri ni, na ba fere Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm ni, ni Yisareli nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm kɛrɛ, na ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ na bu fa sɛsɛ­su, dɔ na ba ma nɔɔ ka bu kɔ yi Yesu jaramasam ni dan­saraka n nu bra.

eng: However, the guards didnt find them in the prison. They returned and reported.
local: Ama, minɛm n bo bu sunmaari bu n kɔ juuri ni, ba ŋu má jaramasam ni dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu, na ba kpie bu nyi aba ase bu.

eng: We found the prison locked and well-secured, with guards standing at the doors, but when we opened the doors we found no one inside.
local: Bu ŋa, Saŋga bo ya kɔ juuri ni, dan­saraka anɔ n kɛrɛ nyi wɔ kenken, tintini­jina­fɔm gusu jina anɔ n nu bɛrɛ, ama, saŋga bo ya ba tekeeri anɔ n ni, ya ŋu má sɔnɔ kun n kpein mmɔɔ sɔɔ n nu.

eng: When they received this news, the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests were baffled and wondered what might be happening.
local: Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ n kɛrɛ nkpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu tintini­jina­fɔm kpiɛn n tiiri sɔ ni, na ba birisi, ba si má sa bo wɔ yo.

eng: Just then, someone arrived and announced, Look! The people you put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people.
local: Dɔ na sɔnɔ be aba ase bu ka, Am ti fɛn. Minɛm bo am taraari bu kɔ nyiiri bu ni, bu wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu wo minɛm kere su.

eng: Then the captain left with his guards and brought the apostles back. They didnt use force because they were afraid the people would stone them.
local: Nyini ti, na Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu tintini­jina­fɔm kpiɛn n afa i minɛm akɔ, na ba kɔ atara Yesu jaramasam n aba. Bu kɔ faari bu ni, ba miɛ má bu, dama bu soroori ka minɛm ni tu bu yabuɛ wɔ.

eng: The apostles were brought before the council where the high priest confronted them.
local: Na ba fa jaramasam n aba, na ba fa bu ajina minɛm bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ n nyunu. Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn n abisa bu bisaa­wam.

eng: In no uncertain terms, we demanded that you not teach in this name. And look at you! You have filled Jerusalem with your teaching. And you are determined to hold us responsible for this mans death.
local: I ŋa, Ya maari am nɔɔ nahɔrɛ su ka, má am se ka am kere keree­wa be ahin sɔnɔ n duma nu. Ama, am niɛ kabo am ayo a. Am afa am keree­wa n abara ayɛ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n kɛrɛ nu, ye am koro ka am se ka yɛrɛ kuunri i a.

eng: Peter and the apostles replied, We must obey God rather than humans!.
local: Na Petoro ni jaramasa nyiŋgam n abɔ bu nu ka, Wɔ yo daka ka ya de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ wɔ, má adamande dɛɛ.

eng: The God of our ancestors raised Jesus from the dead—whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
local: Nyɛmɛ bo i ti ya nam dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ ni, yiri teŋgeeri Yesu fiteeri i yiwee n nu, yiri bo am kuunri i na am kpamaari i baka jaanu n su ni.

eng: God has exalted Jesus to his right side as leader and savior so that he could enable Israel to change its heart and life and to find forgiveness for sins.
local: Yiri Yesu, ye Nyɛmɛ afa i atana i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, ka i yo ya Mɔ­fɔ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ, na i ma yɛrɛ Yisareli­fɔm atin, na ya kaaki ya wo, na Nyɛmɛ fa ya satiɛ kɛrɛ ce ya.

eng: We are witnesses of such things, as is the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him.
local: Yɛrɛ ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni di ahin sam n daani kan kere minɛ a. Yiri Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ye Nyɛmɛ afa ama minɛm bo bu de i nɔaniɛ n a.

eng: When the council members heard this, they became furious and wanted to kill the apostles.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ n tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na yaa ati bu agaya, má bu koroori ka bu kun Yesu jaramasam wɔ.

eng: One council member, a Pharisee and teacher of the Law named Gamaliel, well-respected by all the people, stood up and ordered that the men be taken outside for a few moments.
local: Ama, na bu nu kun, i ti Farasi­fɔ ɔ, bu fere i ka Gamɛli, i kaaki ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere­fɔ ɔ, bo minɛm kɛrɛ ma i jirima agaya. Na wɔ yasu ajina, na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu fa jaramasam n fite dasin kaan ka.

eng: He said, Fellow Israelites, consider carefully what you intend to do to these people.
local: Dɔ na wɔ se jama n kɛrɛ ka, M lamɔkɔ Yisareli­fɔm, am niɛ am tii su dɔŋgu fa kɔ sa n bo am koro ka am fa yo ahin minɛm ni.

eng: Some time ago, Theudas appeared, claiming to be somebody, and some four hundred men joined him. After he was killed, all of his followers scattered, and nothing came of that.
local: Am teŋge su ka afɔ be, na sɔnɔ be, bu fere i ka Tiyodasi, na wɔ ba, na wɔ yo i ŋu ka i ti sɔnɔ bo i ŋusu nya má durunya n nu wɔ, na minɛ ba yo ka yaa nna kɛrɛ asu i su. Ama, wɔ cɛ má, na minɛ akun i, na i susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ aŋmati asandi, na i dɛɛ atin n aka ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Afterward, at the time of the census, Judas the Galilean appeared and got some people to follow him in a revolt. He was killed too, and all his followers scattered far and wide.
local: Nyini sin, saŋga bo bu ŋa bu kɛrɛ asiɛn n su jama duma na bu si bu nɔ ni, na sɔnɔ be gusu aba, yiri la Yudasi bo i fite Galili asiɛn n su ni. Na jama beberebe asu i su, ama, na minɛ akun yiri gusu, na i susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ aŋmati asandi.

eng: Heres my recommendation in this case: Distance yourselves from these men. Let them go! If their plan or activity is of human origin, it will end in ruin.
local: Nyini ti, kabo i ti ni, m suse am wɔ, má am se ka am yo ahin minɛm ni pui. Am yaki bu bɛnlɛ. Nzɛn atin n bo bu bita i n ti adamande dɛɛ, i ká ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: If it originates with God, you wont be able to stop them. Instead, you would actually find yourselves fighting God! The council was convinced by his reasoning.
local: Ama, nzɛn i ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ, am kóro yo má ahin minɛ ni pui. Am bá ŋu ka am si má na am sukun luɛ ni Nyɛmɛ.

eng: After calling the apostles back, they had them beaten. They ordered them not to speak in the name of Jesus, then let them go.
local: Na ba de Gamɛli fɔ­tuu­wa ni. Na ba fere Yesu jaramasam n awura, na ba fin bu ŋgbere, na ba ma bu nɔɔ ka, má bu se ka bu bo Yesu duma mmɔɔ kere minɛ kun. Na ba yaki bu, na ba kɔ.

eng: The apostles left the council rejoicing because they had been regarded as worthy to suffer disgrace for the sake of the name.
local: Yesu jaramasam ni fiteeri minɛm bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ n dɔ wieeri ni, na bu ahore ajɔ agaya, ka Nyɛmɛ abu bu awura be nu, dama ba di nyinsɛ Yesu dɛɛ duma n nu.

eng: Every day they continued to teach and proclaim the good news that Jesus is the Christ, both in the temple and in houses.
local: Na cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, na bu suwura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, ni minɛm dika, na bu yaki má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere ni, na bu sukan Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n kere bu ka Yesu ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: The high priest asked, Are these accusations true.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn n abisa Satɛfano ka, Ahin jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo ba kan i n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: Stephen responded, Brothers and fathers, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he settled in Haran.
local: Na Satɛfano abɔ i nu ka, M sim ni m ninbaam, am so am sui na am ti fɛn. Kana ya na Abraham kɔ wo Harani miɛ kpiri n nu ni, Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n yo yiiri i ŋu kereeri Abraham Mesopotamiya dɛɛ asiɛn n su wɔ.

eng: God told him, Leave your homeland and kin, and go to the land that I will show you.
local: Na wɔ se i ka, Fite yaki u asiɛn n bo a tana su ni, ni u minɛm, na m kére u asiɛn bo m koro ka a kɔ tana su ni.

eng: So Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After Abrahams father died, God had him resettle in this land where you now live.
local: Nyini ti, na Abraham ayasu Kaladiya asiɛn n su, na wɔ kɔ awo Harani miɛ kpiri n nu. I woori dɔ ye i si ba wuuri a. Nyini sin, na Nyɛmɛ ama wɔ yasu dɔ aba atana ahin asiɛn n bo ya wo su kisa ni.

eng: God didnt give him an inheritance here, not even a square foot of land. However, God did promise to give the land as his possession to him and to his descendants, even though Abraham had no child.
local: Nyini saŋga, Nyɛmɛ nya fa má asiɛn n haari kampiti sɔ mmɔɔ ma má Abraham na i se ka i ti i dɛɛ. Nyɛmɛ yo seeri i ka i fá asiɛn n ma i ni i busu nu­fɔm wɔ. Saŋga bo i seeri i sɔ ni, na Abraham nya wu má baa.

eng: God put it this way: His descendants will be strangers in a land that belongs to others, who will enslave them and abuse them for four hundred years. .
local: Na Nyɛmɛ ase i ka, U busu nu­fɔm bá kɔ yo wɔfɔm wɔ asiɛn be su, na bu fá bu yo nkɔkɔm, na bu kére bu wahara agaya kɔ ju afɔ yaa nna.

eng: And I will condemn the nation they serve as slaves, God said, and afterward they will leave that land and serve me in this place.
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ase ka, M bá cin minɛm n bo bu fá u minɛm yo bu nkɔkɔm n sui, na m nyá yi u minɛm fite nyini asiɛn n su, na bu káaki ba fɛn, na bu bá su m.

eng: God gave him the covenant confirmed through circumcision. Accordingly, eight days after Isaacs birth, Abraham circumcised him. Isaac did the same with Jacob, and Jacob with the twelve patriarchs.
local: Dɔ na Nyɛmɛ afa sɛsɛ­su na wɔ di i di ni Abraham, i ŋa, Baa kɛrɛ bo a wuuri i, i ti ye ni ka a wura i kɛnkɛnnɛ. Nyini kere ka a bita sɛsɛ­su n bo ya di i di n a. Nyini ti, Abraham ba wuuri Izaki ni, i wuraari i kɛnkɛnnɛ wɔ le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn. Na Izaki gusu aba wu Jakɔbu, na wɔ wura i kɛnkɛnnɛ i le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn, na Jakɔbu gusu aba awu i nbaa buru ni nnyɔm bo bu ti ya nam ni, na wɔ wura bu kɛnkɛnnɛ le­mɔcuɛ cɛɛn.

eng: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him into slavery in Egypt. God was with him, however.
local: Na Jakɔbu nbaam n asi bu niɛma Yosefu kɔrɛ, na ba tɔ i afa ama Ijipiti asiɛn n su­fɔm. Ama, na Nyɛmɛ awo i dɔ.

eng: and rescued him from all his troubles. The grace and wisdom he gave Joseph were recognized by Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and over his whole palace.
local: na wɔ yi i afite kekereke kɛrɛ bo i tuuri i nu, na wɔ ma i alecira, na wɔ di Ijipiti Fɛmɛ Faaro ahɔrɔma. Na fɛmɛ n afa i dika n kɛrɛ, ni Ijipiti asiɛn n ama i ka i niɛ i dika.

eng: A famine came upon all Egypt and Canaan, and great hardship came with it. Our ancestors had nothing to eat.
local: Na ahɔɛ aba atɔ Ijipiti, ni Kanana asiɛnm n su, abi aboro minɛ ŋu, bo ya nam anya má diire bo bu di.

eng: When Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
local: Na bu si Jakɔbu ati ka diire wo Ijipiti asiɛn n su, na wɔ sunma i nbaam. Ciciri su dɛɛ sunma ni, na ba kɔ ato diire n aba.

eng: During their second visit, Joseph told his brothers who he was, and Pharaoh learned about Josephs family.
local: Bu ba kpieeri bu nyi kɔɔri ajaari nnyɔ su ni, na Yosefu ayi i ŋu akere i sinbaam ni, na Fɛmɛ Faaro gusu asi Yosefu dika­fɔm.

eng: Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives—seventy-five in all—and invited them to live with him.
local: Na Yosefu asunma i sinbaam n ka bu kɔ fa bu si, ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ ka bu bra i dɔ Ijipiti asiɛn n su. Minɛm kɛrɛ kɔ yasuuri abura­so ni nnu wɔ, bo bu baari Ijipiti asiɛn n su.

eng: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our ancestors died.
local: Na Jakɔbu, ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ akɔ Ijipiti asiɛn n su, dɔ ye i ni i nbaam n kɛrɛ kɔ wuuri a.

eng: Their bodies were brought back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had purchased for a certain sum of money from Hamors children, who lived in Shechem.
local: Na ba fa bu aba Sɛkɛm miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba se bu faŋ­kunma n bo Abraham toori i jɛtɛ ŋwaa beberebe Hamɔ nbaam dɔ ni.

eng: When it was time for God to keep the promise he made to Abraham, the number of our people in Egypt had greatly expanded.
local: Saŋga n ba dodoori bo deke n bo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i yo ma Abraham n yo yii­wa ni, nyini saŋga ni, na ya minɛm ni atɛrɛ ade Ijipiti asiɛn n kɛrɛ su.

eng: But then another king rose to power over Egypt who didnt know anything about Joseph. .
local: Bo woori dɔ sɔ ɔ, haari na sɔnɔ be aba adi Ijipiti asiɛn n su fɛmɛ, yiri asi má Yosefu.

eng: He exploited our people and abused our ancestors. He even forced them to abandon their newly born babies so they would die.
local: Na yiri ni afa sunsunni tiɛ adadaka ya nam, na wɔ bita bu ni tiɛ bita, na wɔ se ya nam ka bu fa bu nbaam fa tu, adena bu wu.

eng: Thats when Moses was born. He was highly favored by God, and for three months his parents cared for him in their home.
local: Nyini saŋga n ye bu wuuri Moyisi a, i yo ti baa bo i yo nyumi agaya Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ. Na ba bita i i si dika haari sara nsan.

eng: After he was abandoned, Pharaohs daughter adopted and cared for him as though he were her own son.
local: na ba nya ayi i afite, na Fɛmɛ Faaro wa baa­bara afa i ayo i kunnu wa.

eng: Moses learned everything Egyptian wisdom had to offer, and he was a man of powerful words and deeds.
local: Na ba kere i Ijipiti­fɔm ŋgɔnlɛ ni alecira barasu barasu kɛrɛ, na wɔ yasu, na i duma ata fa kɔ i sa­yoo­wa ni i jɔjɔɔ­wa kɛrɛ nu.

eng: When Moses was years old, he decided to visit his family, the Israelites.
local: Saŋga bo Moyisi ba juuri afɔ abura­na ni, na i sunsunni ama i ka i kɔ niɛ i lamɔkɔ Yisareli­fɔm busu.

eng: He saw one of them being wronged so he came to his rescue and evened the score by killing the Egyptian.
local: Na wɔ ŋu ka Ijipiti­fɔm be sukere Yisareli­fɔ kun be wuwuruwa. I ŋuuri i sɔ ni, wɔ sun má i nu, na wɔ kɔ abo akun Ijipiti­fɔ ni.

eng: He expected his own kin to understand that God was using him to rescue them, but they didnt.
local: Moyisi sunsuunri ka, i dɛɛ minɛm ni tí i bu wɔ, ka, Nyɛmɛ ye wɔ yi i ka i ba de bu tii ji a, ama, ba ti má i bu sɔ.

eng: The next day he came upon some Israelites who were caught up in an argument. He tried to make peace between them by saying, You are brothers! Why are you harming each other.
local: I alecin, na wɔ ŋu ka Yisareli­fɔm minɛ nnyɔ sudi aluwa n bu ŋuŋu nu, na wɔ kɔ akpata bu, na wɔ se bu ka, Njam, am ti fɛn, m ŋa am kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ e, ye nzu ti ye am sudi aluwa.

eng: The one who started the fight against his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? .
local: Ama, na yiri n bo i sukere i beŋgu n wuwuruwa n asun Moyisi akɔ i sin, na wɔ bisa i ka, Ŋma fa u yoori ya niɛsu­fɔ ni ya jɔrɛ­di­fɔ a?.

eng: Are you planning to kill me like you killed that Egyptian yesterday? .
local: A koro ka a kun mini gusu, kabo anuma a kuunri Ijipiti­fɔm wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a.

eng: When Moses heard this, he fled to Midian, where he lived as an immigrant and had two sons.
local: Moyisi tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na wɔ ŋmati afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su akɔ awo Madiya asiɛn n su akaaki wɔfɔ dɔ. Dɔ na wɔ ba aja bara, na wɔ wu nbaa­biɛsɔm nnyɔ.

eng: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses in the flame of a burning bush in the wilderness near Mount Sinai.
local: Na afɔ abura­na sin, na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be ayi i ŋu akere Moyisi boro kpantain n nu, fa dodo buka n bo bu fere i ka Sinayi n su, i ŋu ka sin fofoe be je buwe baka be su, ama, i yara má, mɛrɛkɛ n wo sin n nu.

eng: Enthralled by the sight, Moses approached to get a closer look and he heard the Lords voice.
local: Moyisi ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na wɔ yo alekutura, na wɔ cin adodo ka i niɛ i nyunyumi. Dɔ na Micɛra n ajɔjɔ i dɔ, ase i ka.

eng: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Trembling with fear, Moses didnt dare to investigate any further.
local: Mini ti u nam Nyɛmɛ a, u na Abraham, ni Izaki, ni Jakɔbu. Na sɛrɛ ati Moyisi, na i ŋuniɛ subobo i, wɔ bo má su ka i niɛ i kun.

eng: The Lord continued, Remove the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground. .
local: Na Micɛra n ase Moyisi ka, Deke bo a jina n ti m tiɛŋgu dɛɛ asiɛn casi wɔ, cin yi u gbagbaa ni.

eng: I have clearly seen the oppression my people have experienced in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning. I have come down to rescue them. Come! I am sending you to Egypt .
local: Ma ŋu kabo m minɛm wo kekereke nu Ijipiti asiɛn n su barasu. Ma ŋu kabo bu fiɛfiɛ barasu, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma jura aba ka m ba de bu tii ji. Bra na m sunma u Ijipiti asiɛn n su.

eng: This is the same Moses whom they rejected when they asked, Who appointed you as our leader and judge? This is the Moses whom God sent as leader and deliverer. God did this with the help of the angel who appeared before him in the bush.
local: Tɔ, yiri Moyisi ye Yisareli­fɔm keteeri i n a, na ba bisa i ka, Ŋma fa u yoori ya niɛsu­fɔ ni ya jɔrɛ­di­fɔ a? Moyisi, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ sunmaari i mɛrɛkɛ i dɔ baka n bo i je sin fofoe na i yara má n su, na mɛrɛkɛ n ase i ka i kɔ de minɛm tii ji na i yo bu kpiɛn n a.

eng: This man led them out after he performed wonders and signs in Egypt at the Red Sea and for forty years in the wilderness.
local: Na wɔ du Yisareli­fɔm mɔ, na wɔ yi bu afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su, na wɔ yo alekutura sam beberebe dɔ, ni kunma kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Kunma Kɔkɔrɛ n su, ni saŋga bo bu wo boro kpantain n nu afɔ abura­na ni.

eng: This is the Moses who told the Israelites, God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your own people. .
local: Yiri Moyisi yo seeri Yisareli­fɔm ka, Nyɛmɛ bá yi am busu nu­fɔ be na i yó i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ, kabo m ti ni.

eng: This is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness with our ancestors and with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He is the one who received life-giving words to give to us.
local: Yiri Moyisi, i ni ya na Yisareli­fɔm yo woori boro kpantain n nu n a, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ n jɔjɔɔri i dɔ Sinayi Buka n su n a, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n maari i ka i fa ma ya nam n a.

eng: Hes also the one whom our ancestors refused to obey. Instead, they pushed him aside and, in their thoughts and desires, returned to Egypt.
local: Ama, ya nam akoro má ka bu de i, na ba kete i na ba koro ka má bu kpie bu nyi kɔ Ijipiti asiɛn n su.

eng: They told Aaron, Make us gods that will lead us. As for this Moses who led us out of Egypt, we dont know whats happened to him! .
local: Na ba se Moyisi gɔrɔ Aarɔn ka, Ta amɔɛm ma ya na bu du ya mɔ fa ya nati. Ya si má sa bo wɔ yo yiri Moyisi n bo i faari ya fiteeri Ijipiti asiɛn n su ni.

eng: Thats when they made an idol in the shape of a calf, offered a sacrifice to it, and began to celebrate what they had made with their own hands.
local: Na ba yiyi bu sikam, na ba fa ata amɔɛ ka nambaa dɛɛ su, na ba yo tiɛ ama i, na ba su i, na ba di cɛɛn­kpiri fa kɔ nyini deke n bo bɛrɛ ni bu tii ayo i n su.

eng: So God turned away from them and handed them over to worship the stars in the sky, just as it is written in the scroll of the Prophets: Did you bring sacrifices and offerings to me for forty years in the wilderness, house of Israel? .
local: Nyini ti, na Nyɛmɛ akaaki i sin abo bu, na wɔ ma bu susu asara­mbaam bo bu wo aŋgoro ni, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n nu lɛɛ. Bu ŋa, Ambɛrɛ Yisareli­fɔm, am yo yoori yoo­wam boro kpantain n nu afɔ abura­na ni, am yo maari mini wɔ?.

eng: No! Instead, you took the tent of Moloch with you, and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made in order to worship them. Therefore, I will send you far away, farther than Babylon. .
local: Ai, am yo sɔɔri amɔɛ n bo bu fere i ka Mɔlɛki na am afa tani abo i nambue n sɔ i nati wɔ, ni amɔɛ n bo bu fere i ka Rɛfani na i ti ka asara­mbaa be ni. Bɛrɛ ye am ta bu ka amɔɛm ye am su bu a. Nyini ti, ye m má bu taraari am kɔ miɛ be bo i sin Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n ŋu n a.

eng: The tent of testimony was with our ancestors in the wilderness. Moses built it just as he had been instructed by the one who spoke to him and according to the pattern he had seen.
local: Ya nam ni, bɛrɛ yo laari nambue n bo i kere ka Nyɛmɛ wo bu dɔ n boro kpantain n nu a. Bu yoori i kabo Nyɛmɛ kereeri Moyisi ka i yo i n wɔ.

eng: In time, when they had received the tent, our ancestors carried it with them when, under Joshuas leadership, they took possession of the land from the nations whom God expelled. This tent remained in the land until the time of David.
local: Ya nam yo faari nyini nambue n maari bu sin­fɔm wɔ, na bu ni bu mɔ­fɔ Yosuwa afa i akɔ asiɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ fɔn yiiri minɛm su na i fa maari bu ni. Na nyini nambue n awo bu dɔ haari Fɛmɛ Davidi saŋga.

eng: God approved of David, who asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Davidi adi ahɔrɔma Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ka i ma i ta awuru bo Jakɔbu ni i dika­fɔm fa su Nyɛmɛ. Fɛmɛ Davidi akoro ata má i.

eng: But it was Solomon who actually built a house for God.
local: ama, i wa Fɛmɛ Solomon yo taari awuru n maari Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: However, the Most High doesnt live in houses built by human hands. As the prophet says.
local: Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, Micɛra Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n yiri tana má awuru bo adamande ata i nu, kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n ajɔjɔ i ni. I ŋa.

eng: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or where is my resting place? .
local: Micɛra n ŋa o, Aŋgoro ni ti m fɛmɛya tanaa­biri a, na asiɛŋgu nya ti deke bo m jaa gbagbaa tiɛn. Ye am ta nzu awuru barasu ma m a? Am nya nyini fofoe n ni ye m wo nu a?.

eng: Didnt I make all these things with my own hand? .
local: Má mini yoori ahin dekem ni kɛrɛ a.

eng: You stubborn people! In your thoughts and hearing, you are like those who have had no part in Gods covenant! You continuously set yourself against the Holy Spirit, just like your ancestors did.
local: Na Satɛfano akaaki ase bu ka, Ambɛrɛ, am ahore­mbaa ti kekereke, na am sui ti tan­tanantan ka minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni. Am nati kete Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wɔ, kabo am nam yo yoori lɛɛ.

eng: Was there a single prophet your ancestors didnt harass? They even killed those who predicted the coming of the righteous one, and youve betrayed and murdered him!.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ bɔnɔ ye am nam akere má i wuwuruwa? Na ba kun minɛm ni, bo bu seeri ka Sɔnɔ n bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su ni bá ni.

eng: You received the Law given by angels, but you havent kept it.
local: Ambɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ yo sunmaari i mɛrɛkɛm ye bu faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa maari am n a, ama, am ade má i.

eng: Once the council members heard these words, they were enraged and began to grind their teeth at Stephen.
local: Kabo Satɛfano jɔjɔɔri sɔ ni, na yaa ati Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm n agaya, na ba kan bu nɔɔ awura nu, na bu sukpɛsɛ bu jee.

eng: But Stephen, enabled by the Holy Spirit, stared into heaven and saw Gods majesty and Jesus standing at Gods right side.
local: Ama, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura Satɛfano ŋu, na wɔ tu i tii aniɛ aŋgoro, na wɔ ŋu kabo Nyɛmɛ woo­wa ata papasi barasu. Na wɔ ŋu ka Yesu jina Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

eng: He exclaimed, Look! I can see heaven on display and the Human One standing at Gods right side.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Am niɛ, ma ŋu ka aŋgoro n ateke ye Adamande Wa n jina Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

eng: At this, they shrieked and covered their ears. Together, they charged at him.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm n atiɛn kpa, na ba fa bu saa atɔ bu suim. Dɔ na ba kaba abereki atɔ Satɛfano ŋu.

eng: threw him out of the city, and began to stone him. The witnesses placed their coats in the care of a young man named Saul.
local: Na ba cin i afite miɛ n ahɛmɛ, na bu sutu i yabuɛ, na bɛrɛ bo bu di daani jaraki i, na bu sutu i yabuɛ, na ba yi bu suturam ama gbɛfɛnɛ anyunuti­fɔ be ka i suniɛ bu dika, bu fere nyini gbɛfɛnɛ n ka Sɔɔlu.

eng: As they battered him with stones, Stephen prayed, Lord Jesus, accept my life.
local: Bu wo Satɛfano yabuɛ n tu su sɔ ni, na i gusu subo fere Mibiɛ ni, i ŋa, M Mibiɛ Yesu, de m ŋgɔɔ ni.

eng: Falling to his knees, he shouted, Lord, dont hold this sin against them! Then he died.
local: Na wɔ kutu asiɛŋgu, na wɔ bo awie kpa, i ŋa, M Mibiɛ, má a fa bu ŋgasi fa kɔ sa n bo bu suyo i n su. I seeri sɔ wieeri ni, kpeŋga na i ni durunya n akpaaki.

eng: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion in the Italian Company.
local: Sɔnɔ be yo woori Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu, bu fere i ka Koneliyo, i ti Roma­fɔ sojam n kpiɛn wɔ, i niɛ soja mbaa yaa su a. Bu fere nyini soja jama mbaa n ka Itali sojam.

eng: He and his whole household were pious, Gentile God-worshippers. He gave generously to those in need among the Jewish people and prayed to God constantly.
local: Koneliyo ti sɔnɔ bo i su Nyɛmɛ atin su wɔ, i ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ, na bu soro Nyɛmɛ. Na i suyo sakpa ma Yuda­fɔm bo bu ti yaari­fɔm ni, na i susere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: One day at nearly three oclock in the afternoon, he clearly saw an angel from God in a vision. The angel came to him and said, Cornelius.
local: Na cɛɛn n be wiɛsu kerefi nsan saŋga, na wɔ kaba aŋu Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be, na mɛrɛkɛ n aba ase i ka, Koneliyo.

eng: Startled, he stared at the angel and replied, What is it, Lord? The angel said, Your prayers and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to God.
local: Na wɔ niɛ i gbirim, na sɛrɛ ati i, na wɔ bisa i ka, M Mibiɛ, nzu a? Na mɛrɛkɛ n abɔ i nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ade u sere, wɔ ŋu sakpa n bo a yo ama yaari­fɔm ni, i wara koro fi má nyini sam kɛrɛ.

eng: Send messengers to Joppa at once and summon a certain Simon, the one known as Peter.
local: Tɔ, kisa, sunma minɛm na bu kɔ Jopa miɛ kpiri n nu na bu kɔ fere sɔnɔ bo bu fere i ka Simon Petoro ni.

eng: He is a guest of Simon the tanner, whose house is near the seacoast.
local: I wo jɛrɛkɛ be dika a, bu fere i gusu ka Simon, i dika n wo fa dodo tieku n wɔ.

eng: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius summoned two of his household servants along with a pious soldier from his personal staff.
local: Mɛrɛkɛ n jɔjɔɔri i dɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ kɔ, na Koneliyo afere i ngbɛfɛnɛm nnyɔ, ni soja kun be bo i su Nyɛmɛ atin su na i wo i dika ni.

eng: He explained everything to them, then sent them to Joppa.
local: Na wɔ kan jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo mɛrɛkɛ n seeri i ni akere bu, na wɔ sunma bu Jopa miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: At noon on the following day, as their journey brought them close to the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
local: Na alecin ŋgarama, na ba de atin na bu kɔ Jopa, bu kɔ sudodo dɔ fa kɔ wiɛ kekete saŋga, na Petoro gusu ayasu afun sɔɔ su ka i sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: He became hungry and wanted to eat. While others were preparing the meal, he had a visionary experience.
local: Na ahɔɛ ati i, na wɔ koro deke be i di. Saŋga bo bu wo diire n yo su ni, na deke be aba i dɔ ka alɛlɛ nu dɛɛ su.

eng: He saw heaven opened up and something like a large linen sheet being lowered to the earth by its four corners.
local: Na wɔ ŋu ka aŋgoro n ateke, na wɔ ŋu deke be i ti ka tani bambaka wɔ, ba cici i bue nna n kɛrɛ, i sucɔɔndi ba asiɛŋgu.

eng: Inside the sheet were all kinds of four-legged animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
local: Ajaa­nna barasu kɛrɛ, ni cin­bukunnu­su­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ, ni laŋkpɛtɛ­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ ka bu wo nu.

eng: A voice told him, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Na wɔ ti akonvi be ŋga, i ŋa, Petoro, yasu na a kun di.

eng: Peter exclaimed, Absolutely not, Lord! I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.
local: Ama, na Petoro ase i ka, M Mibiɛ, ai. M nya di má deke bo i ti ciire na i la fin.

eng: The voice spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Na akonvi n akaaki ase i bekun ka, Deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayo i casi, má a se ka i ti fin.

eng: This happened three times, then the object was suddenly pulled back into heaven.
local: Deke n yoori sɔ kɔ juuri kun kun kpe nsan a, na wɔ kaba afun akɔ aŋgoro.

eng: Peter was bewildered about the meaning of the vision. Just then, the messengers sent by Cornelius discovered the whereabouts of Simons house and arrived at the gate.
local: Saŋga n bo Petoro subirisi fa kɔ deke n bo wɔ ŋu i n su ni, nyini saŋga, minɛm bo Koneliyo sunmaari bu ni, abisa asi Simon dika, na ba ka ajina awuru n anɔ nu.

eng: Calling out, they inquired whether the Simon known as Peter was a guest there.
local: Na ba fere sɔnɔ be, abisa i ka, Simon Petoro wo dɔ ɔ?.

eng: While Peter was brooding over the vision, the Spirit interrupted him, Look! Three people are looking for you.
local: Petoro toko wo sunsun su, fa kɔ deke bo i baari i dɔ ka alɛlɛ nu n bu. Dɔ na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ase i ka, Ti fɛn. Minɛ nsan nbem wo fɛn suniɛniɛ u busu.

eng: Go downstairs. Dont ask questions; just go with them because I have sent them.
local: Nyini ti, yasu na a jura, na u ni bɛrɛ kɔ, má a yo sika, dama mini sunmaari bu a.

eng: So Peter went downstairs and told them, Im the one you are looking for. Why have you come.
local: Na Petoro ajura akɔ minɛm n dɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, Mini ye am niɛniɛ m n a. Am natiiri bu la ka sɛ wɔ.

eng: They replied, Weve come on behalf of Cornelius, a centurion and righteous man, a God-worshipper who is well-respected by all Jewish people. A holy angel directed him to summon you to his house and to hear what you have to say.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Sojam kpiɛn Koneliyo sunmaari ya u dɔ a. I ti sɔnɔ kpa a, na i soro Nyɛmɛ, na Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ bu i agaya. Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be seeri i ka i fere u, na a ba i dika na a ba kan jɔrɛ bo a la i kere i a.

eng: Peter invited them into the house as his guests. The next day he got up and went with them, together with some of the believers from Joppa.
local: Nyini ti, na Petoro ade minɛm n awura, na ba da i dɔ. Alecin ŋgarama, na Petoro ayasu, na i ni minɛm n akɔ, bu sukɔ ni, na yarada­yo­fɔm nbem bo bu wo Jopa n gusu asu i su akɔ.

eng: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Anticipating their arrival, Cornelius had gathered his relatives and close friends.
local: Na i alecin fɔfɔrɛ, na ba ju Sizariya, bu kɔ ŋu ka Koneliyo atiɛn i lamɔsim, ni i danfo kpam nɔɔ suŋminda yiri Petoro.

eng: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in order to honor him.
local: Saŋga bo Petoro kɔ ŋa i wura awuru n nu ni, na Koneliyo akpa i atin akutu i jaa bu haari asiɛŋgu, na wɔ su i.

eng: But Peter lifted him up, saying, Get up! Like you, Im just a human.
local: Ama, na Petoro ayɛ i su ajina, na wɔ se i ka, Mini ni m tii mmɔɔ, ti adamande wɔ.

eng: As they continued to talk, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
local: Petoro ni Koneliyo bitaari anzama sɔ ɔ haari bu kɔ wuraari awuru n nu, dɔ na wɔ ŋu ka minɛ beberebe atiɛn bu nɔɔ.

eng: He said to them, You all realize that it is forbidden for a Jew to associate or visit with outsiders. However, God has shown me that I should never call a person impure or unclean.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si ka, i ti ciire wɔ ka yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm fa ya ŋu saŋga ni daanfiɛ barasu be nu, wara ya wura bu dika. Ama, Nyɛmɛ akere m ka má m sinndi sɔnɔ be ka i woo­wa ti fin.

eng: For this reason, when you sent for me, I came without objection. I want to know, then, why you sent for me.
local: Nyini ti, ye a sunma fereeri m ye ma kete má ye ma ba n a. M koro ka m bisa u wɔ, nzu ti ye a sunma fereeri m a.

eng: Cornelius answered, Four days ago at this same time, three oclock in the afternoon, I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me.
local: Na Koneliyo ase i ka, I nyuma le­nna lɛɛ, ka ahin saŋga n sɔ, fa kɔ wiɛsu kerefi nsan, m wo m dika n nu wo nyɛmɛsere su, na sɔnɔ be akaba aba ajina m nyunu, i wura sutura fofoe papasi.

eng: He said, Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and your compassionate acts are like a memorial offering to him.
local: na wɔ se m ka, Koneliyo, Nyɛmɛ ade u sere, ye wɔ teŋge u sakpa bo a yo ama yaari­fɔm n su.

eng: Therefore, send someone to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, located near the seacoast.
local: Nyini ti, sunma na bu kɔ Jopa, na bu kɔ fere Simon Petoro ni. I wo jɛrɛkɛ be dika a, bu fere i gusu ka Simon, i dika n wo dodo tieku n wɔ.

eng: I sent for you right away, and you were kind enough to come. Now, here we are, gathered in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has directed you to say.
local: Nyini ti, ye m kaba sunmaari ka bu fere u n a, ye wɔrɔ gusu a yo sakpa ye a ba. Kisa, ya kɛrɛ gusu atiɛn ya nɔɔ fɛn Nyɛmɛ nyunu, na ya ti deke n kɛrɛ bo Mibiɛ n ama u nɔɔ ka a fa ma ya ni.

eng: Peter said, I really am learning that God doesnt show partiality to one group of people over another.
local: Na Petoro anya ase ka, Kisa, ma si nahɔrɛ su ka adamande kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i tambu má nu.

eng: Rather, in every nation, whoever worships him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i soro Nyɛmɛ na i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, i mi koro i ti nzu daanfiɛ sɔnɔ, Nyɛmɛ de i wɔ.

eng: This is the message of peace he sent to the Israelites by proclaiming the good news through Jesus Christ: He is Lord of all!.
local: Am si labari n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari Yisareli­fɔm, nyini labari kpa n kere ka minɛm nya tana­laifiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, yiri ti sɔnɔ kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a.

eng: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism John preached.
local: Am kɛrɛ si sa bo i boori i bu Galili dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, saŋga bo Yohane kaanri Nyɛmɛ nzue biɛ n jɔrɛ ni, haari na wɔ bara ayɛ Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: You know about Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and endowed with power. Jesus traveled around doing good and healing everyone oppressed by the devil because God was with him.
local: Am si Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n jɔrɛ ni, kabo Nyɛmɛ faari i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ni yiko n wuraari i ŋu ni, ni kabo i nati bara yɛɛri deke kɛrɛ suyo sakpa sutuntun minɛm kɛrɛ bo Sitana miɛ bu n na bu nya laifiɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ woori i dɔ a.

eng: We are witnesses of everything he did, both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
local: Yɛrɛ, ya ŋuuri deke n kɛrɛ bo i yoori bu Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ asiɛn su, ni Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni. Na ba kpama i baka jaanu n su, na wɔ wu.

eng: but God raised him up on the third day and allowed him to be seen.
local: Ama, i le­san cɛɛn, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i afite i yiwee n nu, na Nyɛmɛ ama minɛm aŋu i.

eng: not by everyone but by us. We are witnesses whom God chose beforehand, who ate and drank with him after God raised him from the dead.
local: Má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋuuri i a, ama, yɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yiiri ya ka ya di i daani ni, yɛrɛ ŋuuri i a, na ya ni yiri asaŋga adi, na ya nu, saŋga bo i teŋgeeri ni.

eng: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
local: Na wɔ ma ya nɔɔ ka ya kɔ kan labari kpa n kere minɛm na ya di daani ka, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ ayi i ka i bá di ŋgɔɔ­fɔm, ni bo ba wu n kɛrɛ jɔrɛ a.

eng: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ yo jɔjɔɔri Yesu jɔrɛ, bu ŋa, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori i yarada, Nyɛmɛ fá i mi satiɛ bo wɔ yo i fa ce i, Yesu dɛɛ duma n nu.

eng: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell on everyone who heard the word.
local: Saŋga bo Petoro toko jina wo jɔjɔ su ni, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aba awura minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu jina suti labari n ŋu.

eng: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm bo bu ti yarada­yo­fɔm na bu suuri Petoro su fiteeri Jopa miɛ kpiri n nu baari n ayo alekutura, kabo Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura minɛm n bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm mmɔɔ ŋu ni.

eng: They heard them speaking in other languages and praising God. Peter asked.
local: Na bɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm ni, ati ka minɛm n sujɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu sukansi Nyɛmɛ. Na Petoro ase ka.

eng: These people have received the Holy Spirit just as we have. Surely no one can stop them from being baptized with water, can they.
local: Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura ahin minɛm n ŋu, kabo i fa wuraari ya ŋu ni. Ye ŋma koro kete ka má ya biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue a.

eng: He directed that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited Peter to stay for several days.
local: Nyini ti, na Petoro ama nɔɔ ka bu biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue, Yesu Kristo duma n nu. Na ba sere i ka i wo bu dɔ le­nnyɔ.

eng: About that time King Herod began to harass some who belonged to the church.
local: Fa kɔ nyini saŋga n nu, na Fɛmɛ Herode abo i bu sukere Kristo jama minɛ nbem wuwuruwa.

eng: He had James, Johns brother, killed with a sword.
local: Na wɔ ma ba fa takobe akun Yohane gɔrɔ Yakobo.

eng: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he arrested Peter as well. This happened during the Festival of Unleavened Bread.
local: I yoori saŋga bo Yuda­fɔm sudi bu Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n wɔ.

eng: He put Peter in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers, sixteen in all, who guarded him. He planned to charge him publicly after the Passover.
local: Bu taraari Petoro wuraari dan­saraka nu ni, na ba fa i awura sojam buru ni nsɛn saa nu ka bu niɛ i dika. Na bɛrɛ gusu akpaaki nu ka minɛ nna niɛ i dika kɔ ju saŋga su, na nyiŋgam nya de. Na Fɛmɛ Herode asɛsɛ su ka bu diiri Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n siinri, i fa Petoro jina jama n kɛrɛ nyunu wɔ, na i di i jɔrɛ.

eng: While Peter was held in prison, the church offered earnest prayer to God for him.
local: Nyini ti, na wɔ ka dan­saraka n nu, na Kristo jama n asere Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro ama i.

eng: The night before Herod was going to bring Peters case forward, Peter was asleep between two soldiers and bound with two chains, with soldiers guarding the prison entrance.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ n bo i alecin na Fɛmɛ Herode má bu yi Petoro fite na bu di i jɔrɛ ni, i da sojam nnyɔ afiɛn wɔ. Na ba wura i jɔrɔkɔ aminda bu i bue nnyɔ n kɛrɛ, na nbem gusu kaaki jina dan­saraka anɔ n nu ŋminda.

eng: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared and a light shone in the prison cell. After nudging Peter on his side to awaken him, the angel raised him up and said, Quick! Get up! The chains fell from his wrists.
local: Kpeŋga na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be aba ajina dan­saraka n nu, na sɔɔ n ata wein. Na mɛrɛkɛ n abo Petoro gbɛngbɛn, na wɔ teŋge i, na wɔ se i ka, Yasu ndende. Na jɔrɔkɔ n bo i wo Petoro saa n afite atɔ.

eng: The angel continued, Get dressed. Put on your sandals. Peter did as he was told. The angel said, Put on your coat and follow me.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ase i ka, Bo u dambara ni, na a fa u gbagbaa n wura. Na Petoro ayo sɔ, na wɔ kaaki ase i ka, Fa u tanlɛɛ n wura, na a su m su.

eng: Following the angel, Peter left the prison. However, he didnt realize the angel had actually done all this. He thought he was seeing a vision.
local: Na Petoro asu i su afite dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu, wɔ si má ka sa n bo mɛrɛkɛ n suyo i n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, i sunsun ka i yo i ka alɛlɛ dɛɛ su wɔ.

eng: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself. After leaving the prison, they proceeded the length of one street, when abruptly the angel was gone.
local: Na ba kɔ asin sojam ciciri su dɛɛ n ŋu, na ba kɔ asin nnyɔ su dɛɛ n gusu ŋu, na ba kɔ aju bulaari anɔ n bo bu teke i ka na bu sin kɔ miɛ n kunnu ni. Na anɔ n ni i tii ateke, na ba fite. Bu toori atiŋgbere n nu kɔɔri mɔ kaan, na mɛrɛkɛ n akaba ayaki Petoro.

eng: At that, Peter came to his senses and remarked, Now Im certain that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod and from everything the Jewish people expected.
local: Dɔ na Petoro nyunu akoro, na wɔ se ka, Kisa, ma si ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka Micɛra n ye wɔ sunma i mɛrɛkɛ n ka i yi m fite Fɛmɛ Herode saa nu, ni deke n kɛrɛ bo Yuda­fɔm sunsun ka bu yó m ni.

eng: Realizing this, he made his way to Marys house. Many believers had gathered there and were praying.
local: I faari nyini sa n kɛrɛ nu sɔ ni, na wɔ kɔ Mariya dika, i wa la Yohane n bo bu kaaki fere i Marako ni, nyini saŋga, jama beberebe atiɛn bu nɔɔ wo nyɛmɛsere su.

eng: When Peter knocked at the outer gate, a female servant named Rhoda went to answer.
local: Na Petoro abobo dasin anɔ ni, na afani be, bu fere i ka Roda, na wɔ ba ka i ba niɛ sɔnɔ n bo i bobo anɔ ni.

eng: She was so overcome with joy when she recognized Peters voice that she didnt open the gate. Instead, she ran back in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
local: Na wɔ si akonvi n ka Petoro lɛɛ, i ahore ajɔ agaya n dɛɛ ti, wɔ teke má anɔ ni, na wɔ ŋmati akpie i nyi, na wɔ kɔ ase minɛm bo bu wo awuru n nu n ka Petoro jina dasin.

eng: Youve lost your mind! they responded. She stuck by her story with such determination that they began to say, It must be his guardian angel.
local: Na ba se i ka, A bo fiɛ wɔ. Ama, na wɔ kaaki ajɔjɔ ni anyum­boro ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Dɔ na ba se ka, Kisa, i mɛrɛkɛ a.

eng: Meanwhile, Peter remained outside, knocking at the gate. They finally opened the gate and saw him there, and they were astounded.
local: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ Petoro toko jina anɔ n nu wo bobo su. Kasin, na ba ba ateke anɔ ni, na saŋga bo bu ŋuuri ka yiri ni, na ba yo alekutura.

eng: He gestured with his hand to quiet them down, then recounted how the Lord led him out of prison. He said, Tell this to James and the brothers and sisters. Then he left for another place.
local: Na Petoro afa i saa ayo bu ka bu yaki naŋmiɛ. Na wɔ kan akere bu kabo Mibiɛ n yiiri i fiteeri dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu barasu ni. Na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ kan ahin jɔrɛ n kere Yakobo ni yarada­yo­fɔ nyiŋgam n kɛrɛ. Na wɔ fite bu bi bɛrɛ akɔ deke be.

eng: The next morning the soldiers were flustered about what had happened to Peter.
local: Ale ba ciinri ni, na jɔrɛ n amiɛ sojam ni, ba si má kabo bu yo. Na ba se ka, Ye nzu ayo Petoro a.

eng: Herod called for a thorough search. When Peter didnt turn up, Herod interrogated the guards and had them executed. Afterward, Herod left Judea in order to spend some time in Caesarea.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Herode ama nɔɔ ka bu niɛniɛ Petoro busu, ama, ba ŋu má i. Nyini ti, na wɔ bisa sojam bu jɔrɛ ni, na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu kun bu. Nyini sin, na Fɛmɛ Herode afite Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu, na wɔ kɔ awo Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu fa kɔ saŋga su.

eng: Herod had been furious with the people of Tyre and Sidon for some time. They made a pact to approach him together, since their region depended on the kings realm for its food supply. They persuaded Blastus, the kings personal attendant, to join their cause, then appealed for an end to hostilities.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Herode afa yaa agaya Tiro ni Sidon miɛ n nu­fɔm ŋu, nyini ti, na ba fa sɛsɛ­su, na ba kɔ ka bu ŋu i. Bu du mɔ kɔɔri sɔnɔ be dɔ ɔ ka, bu fere i ka Blasitusi, na bu ni i dɛɛ ayo kun ka. Blasitusi ti Fɛmɛ Herode gisan n niɛ­fɔ kpiɛn wɔ. Na wɔ nya fa bu akɔ Fɛmɛ Herode dɔ, na ba kɔ asere ka i ma bu ni yiri nya tana­laifiɛ. Deke bo i ti na bu yoori sɔ n la ka, dama bu diire kɛrɛ fite yiri fɛmɛ n dɛɛ asiɛn n su wɔ.

eng: On the scheduled day Herod dressed himself in royal attire, seated himself on the throne, and gave a speech to the people.
local: Cɛɛn n bo bu kpamaari i ba juuri ni, na Fɛmɛ Herode awura i fɛmɛya sutura, na wɔ tana i fɛmɛya biɛ n su, na wɔ jɔjɔ minɛm n dɔ.

eng: Those assembled kept shouting, over and over, This is a gods voice, not the voice of a mere human.
local: I jɔjɔ wieeri ni, na minɛ kɛrɛ atiɛn, bu ŋa, Ahin yiri, nyɛmɛ be ajɔjɔ a, má adamande.

eng: Immediately an angel from the Lord struck Herod down, because he didnt give the honor to God. He was eaten by worms and died.
local: Kpeŋga na Micɛra n mɛrɛkɛ akaba abo i aji, dama wɔ ma má Nyɛmɛ jirima n dɛɛ ti. Na wɔ da tukpaki, na kakabam awura i, na wɔ wu.

eng: Gods word continued to grow and increase.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n asandi abara ayɛ deke n kɛrɛ, na ba nya minɛ dɔŋgu abuka su.

eng: Barnabas and Saul returned to Antioch from Jerusalem after completing their mission, bringing with them John, who was also known as Mark.
local: Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu yoori bu juma n Yɛrusalem miɛ nu wieeri ni, na ba fa Yohane bo bu kaaki fere i ka Marako ni, na ba fite Yɛrusalem na ba kaaki akɔ Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: The apostles and the brothers and sisters throughout Judea heard that even the Gentiles had welcomed Gods word.
local: Na Yesu jaramasam ni, ni yarada­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ bo bu wo Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu n ati ka, minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n gusu ade Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ayo i yarada.

eng: When Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers criticized him.
local: Nyini ti, saŋga bo Petoro kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na Yuda­fɔ yarada­yo­fɔm nbem ajaraki i.

eng: They accused him, You went into the home of the uncircumcised and ate with them.
local: Bu ŋa, Nzu ti ye a kɔ minɛm bo bu wura má kɛnkɛnɛ n dika ye u ni bɛrɛ kɔ saŋga di diire a.

eng: Step-by-step, Peter explained what had happened.
local: Dɔ na Petoro abo i bu, na wɔ kan kabo jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ boori i bu kun kun n akere bu.

eng: I was in the city of Joppa praying when I had a visionary experience. In my vision, I saw something like a large linen sheet being lowered from heaven by its four corners. It came all the way down to me.
local: I ŋa, M wo Jopa miɛ kpiri n nu, wo nyɛmɛsere su ni, na ma ŋu deke be, i yo m ka alɛlɛ dɛɛ su wɔ. M ŋuuri deke be, i ti ka tani bambaka wɔ, ba cici i bue nna n kɛrɛ, i sucɔɔndi fite aŋgoro n nu suba asiɛŋgu, na wɔ ba ajina m bi.

eng: As I stared at it, wondering what it was, I saw four-legged animals—including wild beasts—as well as reptiles and wild birds.
local: Na ma niɛ i nu nyunyumi, na ma ŋu ajaa­nnam barasu kɛrɛ, ni cin­bukunnu­su­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ, ni laŋkpɛtɛ­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ ka bu wo nu.

eng: I heard a voice say, Get up, Peter! Kill and eat.
local: Na ma ti akonvi be ŋga, i ŋa, Petoro, yasu na a kun di.

eng: I responded, Absolutely not, Lord! Nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.
local: Ama, na ma se i ka, M Mibiɛ, ai. M nya di má deke bo i ti ciire na i la fin.

eng: The voice from heaven spoke a second time, Never consider unclean what God has made pure.
local: Na ma kaaki ati akonvi ni ŋga bekun, i fite aŋgoro wɔ, i ŋa, Deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayo i casi, má a se ka i ti fin.

eng: This happened three times, then everything was pulled back into heaven.
local: Deke n yoori sɔ kɔ juuri kun kun kpe nsan a, na wɔ kaaki afun akɔ aŋgoro.

eng: At that moment three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were staying.
local: Nyini saŋga n nu, na minɛ nsanm n bo bu sunmaari bu m dɔ n afite Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu aba aju awuru n bo m wo nu ni.

eng: The Spirit told me to go with them even though they were Gentiles. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered that mans house.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ase m ka m yasu na m su bu su kɔ, má m yo sika. Na ma su bu su, na ya kɛrɛ akɔ. Na ahin yarada­yo­fɔ nsɛnm n gusu asunma m afite Jopa, na ya kɛrɛ akɔ Sizariya, na ya kɔ awura Koneliyo dika.

eng: He reported to us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is known as Peter.
local: Na wɔ se ya kabo i ŋuuri Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ i dika barasu ni, ni kabo mɛrɛkɛ n ase i ka i sunma na bu kɔ Jopa, na bu kɔ fere sɔnɔ n bo bu fere i ka Simon Petoro ni.

eng: He will tell you how you and your entire household can be saved.
local: na i bá kan jɔrɛ be kere u ni u dika­fɔm kɛrɛ na am nya tii­de.

eng: When I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, just as the Spirit fell on us in the beginning.
local: Na ma bo i bu ka m jɔjɔ. Saŋga bo m wo jɔjɔ su ni, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ajura awura bu ŋu, kabo i yo ba wuraari ya ŋu bumboori ni.

eng: I remembered the Lords words: John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
local: Na ma teŋge su kabo Mibiɛ n yo seeri ni. I ŋa, Yohane yo faari nzue biɛɛri am a, ama, mini fá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ba wura am ŋu a.

eng: If God gave them the same gift he gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, then who am I? Could I stand in Gods way.
local: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka Nyɛmɛ faari i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wuraari yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm bo ya yo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo yarada n ŋu, ye wɔ fa awura bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm gusu ŋu. Mini la ŋma bo m koro kanda Nyɛmɛ ka má i yo sa bo i koro ka i yo i a.

eng: Once the apostles and other believers heard this, they calmed down. They praised God and concluded, So then God has enabled Gentiles to change their hearts and lives so that they might have new life.
local: Saŋga bo bɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm tiiri sɔ ni, ba se má jɔrɛ be kun, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ, bu ŋa, Nyɛmɛ ama minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n atin ka bɛrɛ gusu kaaki bu wo na bu nya ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Now those who were scattered as a result of the trouble that occurred because of Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch. They proclaimed the word only to Jews.
local: Fite saŋga n bo bu kuunri Satɛfano n dɛɛ sin, na ba kere yarada­yo­fɔm wuwuruwa, nyini ti, na ba sandi abara ayɛ deke kɛrɛ, na nbem akɔ Fonisiya awɔrɛ n nu, ni Sipurusi asiɛn n su, ni Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu, na bu kɔ sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n sukere Yuda­fɔm ŋgumi.

eng: Among them were some people from Cyprus and Cyrene. They entered Antioch and began to proclaim the good news about the Lord Jesus also to Gentiles.
local: Ama, bu nbem wo nu, bu fite Sipurusi, na nbem gusu fite Sirene miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba kɔ Antiyoki, na bu sukan ya Mibiɛ Yesu labari kpa n jɔrɛ sukere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

eng: The Lords power was with them, and a large number came to believe and turned to the Lord.
local: Na Mibiɛ n yiko awo bu dɔ nahɔrɛ su, na minɛ beberebe ayo yarada, na ba kaaki bu wo, na ba su Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su.

eng: When the church in Jerusalem heard about this, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
local: Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu n tiiri nyini labari sɔ ni, na ba sunma Banabasi Antiyoki dɔ.

eng: When he arrived and saw evidence of Gods grace, he was overjoyed and encouraged everyone to remain fully committed to the Lord.
local: Saŋga bo i juuri na i ŋuuri sakpa bo Nyɛmɛ ayo ama minɛm sɔ ni, na i ahore ajɔ, na wɔ tu bu fɔ ka bu ma bu nyunu boro yarada n bo bu bita i fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su ni, na bu su i su ni bu ahore­mbaa kun.

eng: Barnabas responded in this way because he was a good man, whom the Holy Spirit had endowed with exceptional faith. A considerable number of people were added to the Lord.
local: Banabasi tiiri sɔnɔ kpa a, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awo i dɔ, na i la yarada agaya. Na minɛ beberebe aba ayo Mibiɛ n yarada.

eng: Barnabas went to Tarsus in search of Saul.
local: Na Banabasi akɔ Tarasu miɛ kpiri n nu ka i kɔ niɛniɛ Sɔɔlu.

eng: When he found him, he brought him to Antioch. They were there for a whole year, meeting with the church and teaching large numbers of people. It was in Antioch where the disciples were first labeled Christians.
local: Saŋga bo Banabasi kɔ ŋuuri Sɔɔlu ni, na wɔ fa i na ba kɔ Antiyoki, na bɛrɛ minɛ nnyɔ n kɛrɛ akɔ awo dɔ afɔ kun, na ba tiɛn Kristo jama n nɔɔ, na ba kere minɛ beberebe. Antiyoki dɔ ye bu du mɔ fereeri yarada­yo­fɔm n ka Kristo­fɔm a.

eng: About that time, some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
local: Nyini saŋga n nu, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nbem afite Yɛrusalem aba Antiyoki dɔ.

eng: One of them, Agabus, stood up and, inspired by the Spirit, predicted that a severe famine would overtake the entire Roman world.
local: Bu fere bu nu kun ka Agabusi, dɔ na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama i yiko, na wɔ yasu ajina ajɔjɔ ka, ahɔɛ bambaka be bá tɔ durunya n kɛrɛ nu. Nyini ahɔɛ n ba tɔɔri saŋga bo Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri bo bu fere i ka Kalodisi n diiri i fɛmɛya n a.

eng: The disciples decided they would send support to the brothers and sisters in Judea, with everyone contributing to this ministry according to each persons abundance.
local: Na Kristo susu­fɔm ni ayo anɔɔkun ka bu kun kun n kɛrɛ yiyi ŋwaa kabo bu faŋga nyaari ti barasu, na bu fa kɔ buka bu beŋgu yarada­yo­fɔm bo bu wo Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu ni.

eng: They sent Barnabas and Saul to take this gift to the elders.
local: Na ba yiyi ŋwaa ni, na ba fa ŋwaa n asunma Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu ka bu fa kɔ ma Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni.

eng: About that time, while the number of disciples continued to increase, a complaint arose. Greek-speaking disciples accused the Aramaic-speaking disciples because their widows were being overlooked in the daily food service.
local: Na fa kɔ saŋga su, na Yesu susu­fɔm n asunnu abuka su, na Yuda­fɔm bo bu jɔjɔ Giriki aniɛ n ni aŋuŋuti Yuda­fɔm bo bu jɔjɔ Hiburu aniɛ ni. Bu ŋa cɛɛn kɛrɛ cɛɛn na bu ce nkuna­fɔm diire ni, bu bu má bu dɛɛm wura má nu.

eng: The Twelve called a meeting of all the disciples and said, It isnt right for us to set aside proclamation of Gods word in order to serve tables.
local: Nyini ti, na Yesu jaramasam buru ni nnyɔm n afere susu­fɔm n kɛrɛ pepere atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na ba se bu ka, I ti má ye ni ka yɛrɛ ni ya tii yaki Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n kan, na ya suniɛ diire jɔrɛ su.

eng: Brothers and sisters, carefully choose seven well-respected men from among you. They must be well-respected and endowed by the Spirit with exceptional wisdom. We will put them in charge of this concern.
local: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, am niɛ am ŋuŋu nu, na am yi minɛ nso bo bu la anyunuyi, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo bu dɔ, na bu la alecira, na bu kaaki la sunsunni kpa, na ya má bɛrɛ niɛ nyini sereyam n kɛrɛ su.

eng: As for us, we will devote ourselves to prayer and the service of proclaiming the word.
local: Na yɛrɛ ni ya tii nyá ya ŋu, na ya nyunu bóro nyɛmɛsere, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kan su.

eng: This proposal pleased the entire community. They selected Stephen, a man endowed by the Holy Spirit with exceptional faith, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
