local: Yesu jaramasam n seeri sɔ ni, na wɔ yo minɛm kɛrɛ fiɛ fa kɔ bu jɔjɔɔ­wa n su. Na ba yi sɔnɔ be, bu fere i ka Satɛfano, i ti sɔnɔ bo i la yarada nahɔrɛ su wɔ, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo i ŋu, ni Filipu, ni Porokoron ni, ni Nikanɔ, ni Timona, ni Pamɛnasi, ni Nikola bo i fite miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Antiyoki n su, na wɔ kaaki i ŋu ka i ti Yuda­fɔ, na i su bu dɛɛ atin n su ni.

eng: The community presented these seven to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
local: Na jama n afa bu aba Yesu jaramasam n dɔ, na ba fa bu saa anana bu ŋu, na ba sere Nyɛmɛ ama bu.

eng: Gods word continued to grow. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased significantly. Even a large group of priests embraced the faith.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni susandi subuka su. Na minɛ beberebe ayo yarada abuka Yesu susu­fɔm su Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu. Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm gusu dɔŋgu ayo yarada.

eng: Stephen, who stood out among the believers for the way Gods grace was at work in his life and for his exceptional endowment with divine power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
local: Satɛfano ti sɔnɔ bo i diiri Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma wɔ beberebe, na Nyɛmɛ ama i yiko nahɔrɛ su, na wɔ yo alekutura sa barasu barasu minɛm n nu.

eng: Opposition arose from some who belonged to the so-called Synagogue of Former Slaves. Members from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and Asia entered into debate with Stephen.
local: Ama, na Yuda­fɔ nbem ayasu ajina ase akuruwa ni yiri. Nyini minɛm wo asɛsɛ n bo bu fere i ka Ma De M Tii tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu wɔ. Bu nbem fite miɛ kpirim n bo bu fere bu ka Sirene, ni Alɛkizanderiya n wɔ. Na bu nbem gusu fite Silisiya ni Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu wɔ.

eng: However, they couldnt resist the wisdom the Spirit gave him as he spoke.
local: Ama, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ama Satɛfano alecira, na wɔ koro akan ayi bu.

eng: Then they secretly enticed some people to claim, We heard him insult Moses and God.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba ma minɛ nbem ŋwaa ka bu kpiɛ kɔrɛ sɔ Satɛfano ka, ba ti ka wɔ kpiɛ Moyisi ni Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ.

eng: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the legal experts. They caught Stephen, dragged him away, and brought him before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Bu seeri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, na ba yɛ minɛm yaa su, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, na ba tara i akɔ Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm dɔ.

eng: Before the council, they presented false witnesses who testified, This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law.
local: Dɔ na ba fa minɛ nbem aba, na ba kpiɛ kɔrɛ asɔ i. Bu ŋa, Saŋga kɛrɛ ahin sɔnɔ n jɔjɔ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn wɔ fa kɔ ya Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru casi n su, na i kaaki jɔjɔ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa su.

eng: In fact, we heard him say that this man Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and alter the customary practices Moses gave us.
local: Ya ti ka i ŋa, Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ ni bó fufu ya Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n kɛrɛ gɔ, na i kaaki ya nana sa­yoo­wam kɛrɛ bo Moyisi fa kereeri ya ni.

eng: Everyone seated in the council stared at Stephen, and they saw that his face was radiant, just like an angels.
local: Na bɛrɛ tere­tere­fɔm n kɛrɛ afa bu nyu­mbaa akpama Satɛfano ŋu, na ba ŋu ka i nyunu­awa akaaki wo ka Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ dɛɛ su.

eng: Some people came down from Judea teaching the family of believers, Unless you are circumcised according to the custom weve received from Moses, you cant be saved.
local: Tɔ, na minɛ nbem afite Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu aba Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu, na bu sukere yarada­yo­fɔm ni bu dɛɛ keree­wam. Bu ŋa, Má ka ba wura am kɛnkɛnnɛ kabo Moyisi yo kereeri ni, am koro nya má tii­de.

eng: Paul and Barnabas took sides against these Judeans and argued strongly against their position. The church at Antioch appointed Paul, Barnabas, and several others from Antioch to go up to Jerusalem to set this question before the apostles and the elders.
local: Na Pɔɔlu, ni Banabasi, ni bɛrɛ ase akuruwa nyannyan fa kɔ nyini jɔrɛ n bu su. Nyini ti, na ba fa sɛsɛ­su ka Pɔɔlu, ni Banabasi, ni yarada­yo­fɔm nbem bo bu wo Antiyoki ni, ka bu kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu na bu kɔ bisa Yesu jaramasam ni, ni Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nyini jɔrɛ ni.

eng: Paul reached Derbe, and then Lystra, where there was a disciple named Timothy. He was the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.
local: Na Pɔɔlu anati haari na wɔ kɔ aju Dɛrɛbi, ni Lisitira miɛ kpirim n nu, sɔnɔ be woori dɔ, i ti Kristo susu­fɔ ɔ, bu fere i ka Timoti. I ni ti Yuda­fɔ bara bo wɔ yo Kristo yarada wɔ, ama, na i si ti Giriki­fɔ.

eng: The church sent this delegation on their way. They traveled through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling stories about the conversion of the Gentiles to everyone. Their reports thrilled the brothers and sisters.
local: Na Kristo jama n akaari bu na ba kɔ. Bu wo kɔ su ni, na ba fa Fonisiya, ni Samariya awɔrɛm n nu, na ba kan akere dɔ­fɔm kabo minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n ayo Nyɛmɛ yarada barasu ni, na nyini labari n ama yarada­yo­fɔm n ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su.

eng: The brothers and sisters in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
local: Na yarada­yo­fɔm bo bu woori Lisitira ni Ikoniyom miɛ kpirim n nu n kɛrɛ ase ka Timoti ti sɔnɔ kpa a.

eng: When they arrived in Jerusalem, the church, the apostles, and the elders all welcomed them. They gave a full report of what God had accomplished through their activity.
local: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri Yɛrusalem ni, na Kristo jama ni, ni Yesu jaramasam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n ade bu ni bu saa nnyɔ, na ba kan akere bu fa kɔ deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ma ba koro ayo i ni.

eng: Saul was in full agreement with Stephens murder. At that time, the church in Jerusalem began to be subjected to vicious harassment. Everyone except the apostles was scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
local: Sɔɔlu yo soroori su ka bu kun Satɛfano a. Nyini cɛɛn n gbingbin ye bu boori i bu kereeri Kristo jamam wuwuruwa agaya, bɛrɛ bo bu wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni. Na bu kɛrɛ aŋmati asandi akɔ Yudiya, ni Samariya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, i kɔ kaari Yesu jaramasam ni ŋgumi wɔ, Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu a.

eng: Paul wanted to take Timothy with him, so he circumcised him. This was because of the Jews who lived in those areas, for they all knew Timothys father was Greek.
local: Pɔɔlu koroori ka i fa Timoti buka i ŋu su wɔ, na bu sunati, na wɔ wura i kɛnkɛnnɛ. Má deke be dɛɛ ti a, ama, Yuda­fɔm bo bu woori dɔ n dɛɛ ti, dama bu kɛrɛ si ka Timoti si ti Giriki­fɔ ɔ.

eng: Some believers from among the Pharisees stood up and claimed, The Gentiles must be circumcised. They must be required to keep the Law from Moses.
local: Ama, na yarada­yo­fɔ nbem bo bu su Farasi­fɔm atin n su n ayasu ajina, ase ka, Wɔ yo daka ka minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ba yo Yesu yarada n awura kɛnkɛnnɛ ka, na bu nya ma bu nɔɔ ka bu su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i fa maari Moyisi n su.

eng: As Paul and his companions traveled through the cities, they instructed Gentile believers to keep the regulations put in place by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
local: Na ba nati miɛ miɛm su, na ba kan akere yarada­yo­fɔm fa kɔ jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo Yesu jaramasam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm bo bu wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu n asɛsɛ su asoro su ka bu yo i n su. Na ba se bu ka bu su nyini atinm su nyunyumi.

eng: Some pious men buried Stephen and deeply grieved over him.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ su­fɔ nbem akɔ ayo Satɛfano sutura, na ba sun sukoko agaya.

eng: The apostles and the elders gathered to consider this matter.
local: Na Yesu jaramasam ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na bu kpokpo jɔrɛ n nu nyunyumi.

eng: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and every day their numbers flourished.
local: Nyini ti, na Kristo jamam n anya faŋga, na bu yarada ayo bii­wa abuka su, na bu sunya minɛm subuka su ale kɛrɛ bo i cin.

eng: Saul began to wreak havoc against the church. Entering one house after another, he would drag off both men and women and throw them into prison.
local: Ama, na Sɔɔlu aboro i nyi kabo i yo na i ka Kristo jama ni ŋgbɛɛn, na i suwura awuru awurum nu, na i sucin yi yarada­yo­fɔm ni, nbaram ni nbiɛsɔm kɛrɛ, na i ma bu kɔ nyi bu dan­saraka.

eng: After much debate, Peter stood and addressed them, Fellow believers, you know that, early on, God chose me from among you as the one through whom the Gentiles would hear the word of the gospel and come to believe.
local: Na ba se nyini jɔrɛ n akuruwa bu ŋuŋu nu acɛ, na Petoro ayasu ajina, na wɔ se bu ka, M ninbaam, am si ka mini ye Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yiiri m fiteeri am nu, ka m ma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n ti labari kpa ni, adena bu yo yarada.

eng: Paul and his companions traveled throughout the regions of Phrygia and Galatia because the Holy Spirit kept them from speaking the word in the province of Asia.
local: Na ba fa Firigiya, ni Galatiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu asin, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama má bu atin na bu kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: Those who had been scattered moved on, preaching the good news along the way.
local: Yarada­yo­fɔm n bo bu sandiiri awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu ni, na ba nati sukan labari kpa n kere minɛm kɛrɛ.

eng: The church at Antioch included prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon , Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen , and Saul.
local: Kristo jama n bo i wo Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu ni, bu nu nbem ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm wɔ, na nbem gusu ti kere­fɔm. Bɛrɛ la Banabasi, ni Simeyon bo bu kaaki fere i ka Nigɛri ni, ni Lukiyosi bo i fite Sirene miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Manaye bo bu yo saŋga taari i ni Fɛmɛ Herode, ni Sɔɔlu ni.

eng: God, who knows peoples deepest thoughts and desires, confirmed this by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as he did to us.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ bo i si kabo adamande ahore­mbaa ti barasu ni, akere ka ba yo yarada nahɔrɛ su, dama wɔ fa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama bu, kabo i yo fa maari yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni.

eng: When they approached the province of Mysia, they tried to enter the province of Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus wouldnt let them.
local: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri Masiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ awieeri ni, má bu koroori ka bu wura Bitiniya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu wɔ, ama, Yesu dɛɛ Awiɛwiɛ n asoro má bu su ka bu wura dɔ.

eng: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and began to preach Christ to them.
local: Na Filipu akɔ Samariya miɛ kpiri n su, akɔ sukan kere minɛm fa kɔ Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: As they were worshipping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Appoint Barnabas and Saul to the work I have called them to undertake.
local: Saŋga bo bu cici bu nɔɔ na bu wo Mibiɛ n su su ni, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ase bu ka, Am yi Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu ma m, na bu yo juma n bo ma yi bu ka bu yo i ni.

eng: He made no distinction between us and them, but purified their deepest thoughts and desires through faith.
local: Nyɛmɛ dɔ, yɛrɛ ni bɛrɛ la má kpakpaaki­nu lɛɛ, dama yarada n bo ba yo i n dɛɛ ti, wɔ ŋmisi ayi satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo bu ahore su ni.

eng: Passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas instead.
local: Nyini ti, na ba fa Masiya awɔrɛ n nu, na ba sin akɔ miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Tɔrasi n nu.

eng: The crowds were united by what they heard Philip say and the signs they saw him perform, and they gave him their undivided attention.
local: Na wɔ yo alekutura sam. Bu tiiri kabo i jɔjɔ na bu ŋuuri alekutura sam bo i yoori bu ni, na bu kɛrɛ aso bu sui nyunyumi, na ba ti kabo i se ni.

eng: After they fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on these two and sent them off.
local: Bu ciciiri bu nɔɔ, na bu sereeri Nyɛmɛ n wieeri ni, na ba fa bu saa anana bu ŋu, na ba ma bu atin na ba kɔ.

eng: Why then are you now challenging God by placing a burden on the shoulders of these disciples that neither we nor our ancestors could bear?.
local: Kisa, nzu ti ye am koro ka am yo Nyɛmɛ na am niɛ a, ye am susɔ yarada­yo­fɔm sɔɔri bo ya nam, ni yɛrɛ ni ya tii akoro asɔ má i a?.

eng: A vision of a man from Macedonia came to Paul during the night. He stood urging Paul, Come over to Macedonia and help us.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ, na Pɔɔlu aŋu deke be ka alɛlɛ nu dɛɛ su, ka sɔnɔ be wo Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu, jina susere i, i ŋa, Jande, bra Makɛdoniya fɛn na a ba buka ya.

eng: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many people, and many who were paralyzed or crippled were healed.
local: Na wɔ fɔn ayi ajinim minɛ dɔŋgu ŋu. Bu ba sufite yaki minɛm ni, bu tiɛn ni faŋga su na bu fite. Na minɛm beberebe bo bu saam ni bu jaam awu ni, ni wɔbukam kɛrɛ anya laifiɛ.

eng: After the Holy Spirit sent them on their way, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.
local: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n asunma Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu na ba kɔ aju miɛ kpiri be su, bu fere i ka Sɛlusiya, na ba fa lee bambaka dɔ, na ba kɔ miɛ be bo i wo lui bambaka be su, bu fere nyini lui n ka Sipurusi n su.

eng: The same thing happened in Iconium. Paul and Barnabas entered the Jewish synagogue and spoke as they had before. As a result, a huge number of Jews and Greeks believed.
local: Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi kɔ juuri Ikoniyom miɛ kpiri n nu ni, bu kaaki yoori sɔ bekun, na ba wura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na ba kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n ni anyum­boro haari na minɛ beberebe ayo yarada, Yuda­fɔm, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ.

eng: On the contrary, we believe that we and they are saved in the same way, by the grace of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ai. Yɛrɛ ayo yarada ka ya Mibiɛ Yesu ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, ye ya nya tii­de a, nyini ma bɛrɛ gusu nya tii­de a.

eng: Immediately after he saw the vision, we prepared to leave for the province of Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them.
local: Pɔɔlu ŋuuri nyini deke n ka alɛlɛ nu n dɛɛ ti, ya kaba yoori siriya a, na ya kɔ Makɛdoniya, dama ya si ka Nyɛmɛ ye wɔ se ka ya kɔ dɔ na ya kɔ kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm n bo bu wo dɔ n a.

eng: There was great rejoicing in that city.
local: Nyini ti, na ahorejɔ bambaka aba Samariya miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: In Salamis they proclaimed Gods word in the Jewish synagogues. John was with them as their assistant.
local: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri lui n miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Salamisi n su ni, na ba kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu. Na Yohane bo bu kaaki fere i ka Marako n awo bu dɔ abuka bu juma.

eng: However, the Jews who rejected the faith stirred up the Gentiles, poisoning their minds against the brothers.
local: Ama, na Yuda­fɔm nbem bo bu yo má yarada n awura minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm bu sin akpura bu ni yarada­yo­fɔm n tii.

eng: The entire assembly fell quiet as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describe all the signs and wonders God did among the Gentiles through their activity.
local: Na minɛm kɛrɛ atɔ amuna, na bu ti kabo Banabasi ni Pɔɔlu sukan kere bu fa kɔ alekutura sa barasu barasum bo Nyɛmɛ ma bu koro yo maari minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

eng: We sailed from Troas straight for Samothrace and came to Neapolis the following day.
local: Na ya fa lee bambaka afite Tɔrasi miɛ kpiri n nu akpiɛ akɔ aju miɛ be bo i wo lui bambaka be su, bu fere nyini lui n ka Samotarase. I alecin fɔfɔrɛ na ya kɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Nɛpolisi n su.

eng: Before Philips arrival, a certain man named Simon had practiced sorcery in that city and baffled the people of Samaria. He claimed to be a great person.
local: Na sɔnɔ be ayo awo Samariya miɛ kpiri n nu, bu fere i ka Simon, i nati yo boboyaya wɔ, na minɛm bo bu wo miɛ n nu ayo alekutura, na i sukere i ŋu ka i ti sɔnɔ bambaka a.

eng: They traveled throughout the island until they arrived at Paphos. There they found a certain man named Bar-Jesus, a Jew who was a false prophet and practiced sorcery.
local: Na ba fa bɛrɛ asin akɔ aju lui bambaka n wiɛ atɔɔri dɛɛ bue ni, miɛ kpiri be su, bu fere i ka Pafɔsi. Na ba kɔ aŋu boboyaya­fɔ be dɔ, bu fere i ka Baari Yesu, i ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ, i yo i ŋu ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ɔ, ama, i ti má i.

eng: Nevertheless, Paul and Barnabas stayed there for quite some time, confidently speaking about the Lord. And the Lord confirmed the word about his grace by the signs and wonders he enabled them to perform.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi awo dɔ ayo le­nnyɔ. Na bu sujɔjɔ Mibiɛ n jɔrɛ ni ahoresi, na Mibiɛ n ama bu faŋga, na wɔ ma bu atin na ba yo alekutura sa barasu barasum, adena minɛ ŋu bu na bu si ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: When Barnabas and Paul also fell silent, James responded, Fellow believers, listen to me.
local: Saŋga bo bu jɔjɔ wieeri ni, na Yakobo gusu ayasu ajɔjɔ. I ŋa, M ninbaam, am ti fɛn.

eng: From there we went to Philippi, a city of Macedonias first district and a Roman colony. We stayed in that city several days.
local: Na ya fite bɛrɛ akɔ miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Filipiya n su, yiri ti Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nyini bue n miɛm kɛrɛ tii a. Roma­fɔm niɛ i su a. Na ya yo le­nnyɔ dɔ.

eng: Everyone, from the least to the greatest, gave him their undivided attention and referred to him as the power of God called Great.
local: Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ, tarakam ni nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ asoro su ka yiri la Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n a. Na ba fere i ka Yiko Bambaka­fɔ n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ n a.

eng: He kept company with the governor of that province, an intelligent man named Sergius Paulus. The governor sent for Barnabas and Saul since he wanted to hear Gods word.
local: I yo wo lui bambaka n su gomina n dɔ ɔ, bu fere gomina n ka Sɛgosi Pɔɔlu, i ti si­fɔ ɔ. Na wɔ fere Banabasi ni Sɔɔlu, dama i koroori ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n wɔ.

eng: The people of the city were divided—some siding with the Jews, others with the Lords messengers.
local: Na kpakpaaki­nu aba miɛ n nu­fɔm dɔ, na nbem ajina Yuda­fɔm n sin, na nbem gusu ajina Yesu jaramasam n sin.

eng: Simon reported how, in his kindness, God came to the Gentiles in the first place, to raise up from them a people of God.
local: Simon ateŋge ya su kabo Nyɛmɛ ni i tii adu mɔ akere ka i cɛcɛ wo minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm nu ni, ni kabo wɔ yi bu nbem afa bu ayo i dɛɛ minɛm ni.

eng: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to the riverbank, where we thought there might be a place for prayer. We sat down and began to talk with the women who had gathered.
local: Na Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni, na ya fite miɛ n nu akɔ kunma nu, dɔ ye ya sunsun ka ya kɔ ŋu ka deke bo Yuda­fɔm n tiɛn bu nɔɔ sere Nyɛmɛ a. Na ya tana, na ya ni nbaram bo ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ n abo anzama.

eng: He had their attention because he had baffled them with sorcery for a long time.
local: Deke bo i ti na bu koro i jɔrɛ n la ka, i yo yoori nyini boboyaya juma n bu dɔ acɛ.

eng: But Elymas the sorcerer opposed them, trying to steer the governor away from the faith.
local: Ama, boboyaya­fɔ ni, bu kaaki fere i duma Giriki aniɛ nu ka Elamasi ni, na wɔ boro i nyi ka i kanda gomina n na má i yo Yesu yarada.

eng: Then some Gentiles and Jews, including their leaders, hatched a plot to mistreat and stone Paul and Barnabas.
local: Na bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, ni Yuda­fɔm ni, fa saŋga ni bu mɔ­fɔm n afa sɛsɛ­su atara kabo bu yo Yesu jaramasam ni tiɛ, na bu tu bu yabuɛ.

eng: Meanwhile, Saul was still spewing out murderous threats against the Lords disciples. He went to the high priest.
local: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ ni, na Sɔɔlu suboro i nyi se kabo i yo na i kere Yesu susu­fɔm n wuwuruwa haari i bá kun bu. Na wɔ koro ka i kɔ Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu, na i kɔ tara nbaram ni nbiɛsɔm bo bu su Yesu atin su ni, na i wura bu jɔrɔkɔ, na i fa bu ba Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ kɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ kpiɛn n dɔ, akɔ ade kadasi bo i koro la atin wura Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu na i tara bu.

eng: The prophets words agree with this; as it is written.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n bo bu yo seeri n ni ahinm n kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ, dama bu kɛrɛɛri ka.

eng: One of those women was Lydia, a Gentile God-worshipper from the city of Thyatira, a dealer in purple cloth. As she listened, the Lord enabled her to embrace Pauls message.
local: Bara be woori bu nu, bu fere i ka Lidiya, i fite miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Tiyatira n su wɔ, i tɔ sanvuin kpakpam bo i la gɔ, i ti bara bo i su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, na Nyɛmɛ ateke i sunsunni, na wɔ so i sui nyunyumi, na wɔ ti kabo Pɔɔlu suse ni.

eng: After they came to believe Philip, who preached the good news about Gods kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
local: Ama, saŋga bo Filipu ba kaanri labari kpa n jɔrɛ n fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ni Yesu Kristo dɛɛ atin n su, na bu deeri i nɔaniɛ ni, na wɔ biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue, nbaram ni nbiɛsɔm kɛrɛ.

eng: Empowered by the Holy Spirit, Saul, also known as Paul, glared at Bar-Jesus and.
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura Sɔɔlu, bo bu kaaki fere i ka Pɔɔlu ŋu, na wɔ niɛ boboyaya­fɔ n gbirim, na wɔ se i ka.

eng: When they learned of it, these two messengers fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding area.
local: Na Yesu jaramasam n afa nu fa kɔ kabo bu koro ka bu yo ni, na ba ŋmati akɔ miɛ kpiri nbem bo bu fere bu ka Lisitira ni Dɛrɛbi n su. Bu wo Likawoniya awɔrɛ n nu wɔ, na bu nya nati subara yɛ ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛm.

eng: During the journey, as he approached Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven encircled him.
local: Na wɔ de atin akɔ, i kɔ sudodo Damasiko, na sin akaba afite aŋgoro ata abara ayɛ i.

eng: After this I will return, and I will rebuild Davids fallen tent; I will rebuild what has been torn down. I will restore it .
local: Micɛra n ŋa, Nyini sin, m káaki ba yɛ Fɛmɛ Davidi awuru n bo wɔ tɔ n su, na m tá i bɔnfɔ ni, na m yó i dɔŋgu.

eng: Once she and her household were baptized, she urged, Now that you have decided that I am a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house. And she persuaded us.
local: Na ba biɛ i ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue, na wɔ sere ya jande ka, Nzɛn am asoro su ka ma yo Mibiɛ n yarada nahɔrɛ su, am ba wo m dika. I yoori ya awaraye n dɛɛ ti, na ya soro su.

eng: Even Simon himself came to believe and was baptized. Afterward, he became one of Philips supporters. As he saw firsthand the signs and great miracles that were happening, he was astonished.
local: Na Simon mmɔɔ ni i tii ayo yarada, na Filipu abiɛ i Nyɛmɛ nzue, na i susu Filipu su deke kɛrɛ bo i kɔ, na wɔ ŋu abonɔ sam barasu beberebe bo Filipu yoori bu ni, na wɔ yo i alekutura.

eng: said, You are a deceiver and trickster! You devil! You attack anything that is right! Will you never stop twisting the straight ways of the Lord into crooked paths?.
local: Wɔrɔ, a ti Sitana dɛɛ wa a. A ti deke kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa n kpɔ­fɔ ɔ. Kɔrɛ ni satiɛ­yo asunnu u ŋu. A ti Mibiɛ n atin kpa n saaki­fɔ ɔ. Cɛɛn bɔnɔ ye a yaki i a?.

eng: where they continued to proclaim the good news.
local: Dɔ na ba kan labari kpa n akere minɛm.

eng: He fell to the ground and heard a voice asking him, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Na wɔ kpaasi haari asiɛŋgu, na wɔ ti akonvi be ŋga, i ŋa, Sɔɔlu, Sɔɔlu e, nzu ti ye a kere m wuwuruwa sɔ a.

eng: so that the rest of humanity will seek the Lord, even all the Gentiles who belong to me. The Lord says this, the one who does these things .
local: Adena daanfiɛm n bo ba ka n kɛrɛ bá kpini Mibiɛ busu, bɛrɛ la minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ma fere bu ka bu ti m dɛɛm ni. Micɛra ni seeri sɔ a.

eng: One day, when we were on the way to the place for prayer, we met a slave woman. She had a spirit that enabled her to predict the future. She made a lot of money for her owners through fortune-telling.
local: Cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, ya sukɔ deke bo Yuda­fɔm n tiɛn bu nɔɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ni, na ya ni suŋguru be ayɛ, i ti afani wɔ, ajini wo i ŋu, na i nati yi munzue. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na i to­fɔm anya ŋwaa beberebe afite i munzue yi n nu.

eng: When word reached the apostles in Jerusalem that Samaria had accepted Gods word, they commissioned Peter and John to go to Samaria.
local: Na Yesu jaramasam bo bu wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu n ati ka Samariya­fɔm gusu ade Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ni, nyini ti, na ba sunma Petoro ni Yohane bu dɔ.

eng: Listen! The Lords power is set against you. You will be blind for a while, unable even to see the daylight. At once, Bar-Jesus eyes were darkened, and he began to grope about for someone to lead him around by the hand.
local: Ti fɛn, Mibiɛ ni bó u ji kisa, na a yó anyinsin fa kɔ saŋga su, na a ŋú má wiɛ ka i ta kun. Kpeŋga na i nyunu akaba asi biri, na i suturiŋgi suniɛniɛ sɔnɔ bo i kere i atin.

eng: In Lystra there was a certain man who lacked strength in his legs. He had been crippled since birth and had never walked. Sitting there, he.
local: Sɔnɔ be woori Lisitira miɛ kpiri n nu, i jaa awu, kabo bu wuuri i ni, i ti wɔbuka a, wɔ nati má.

eng: Saul asked, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus, whom you are harassing, came the reply.
local: Na wɔ bisa ka, M Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ ŋma a? Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, Mini Yesu, mini ye a kere m wuwuruwa n a.

eng: known from earliest times.
local: yiri bo i yo seeri dada kɛrɛ ka i yó i ni.

eng: She began following Paul and us, shouting, These people are servants of the Most High God! They are proclaiming a way of salvation to you.
local: Na i nati susu ya ni Pɔɔlu su, na i sutiɛn suse ka, Ahin minɛm ti Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n dɛɛ amusui­su­fɔm wɔ. Bɛrɛ kere am kabo am yo na am nya tii­de a.

eng: When the governor saw what had taken place, he came to believe, for he was astonished by the teaching about the Lord.
local: Saŋga bo gomina n ŋuuri nyini n sɔ ni, na i tiiri kabo bu kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na wɔ yo i alekutura, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ yo Mibiɛ n yarada.

eng: Peter and John went down to Samaria where they prayed that the new believers would receive the Holy Spirit. .
local: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri dɔ ni, na ba sere Nyɛmɛ ama bɛrɛ bo ba yo yarada ni, adena Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wura bu ŋu.

eng: heard Paul speaking. Paul stared at him and saw that he believed he could be healed.
local: Na wɔ tana aso i sui ati kabo Pɔɔlu sujɔjɔ ni. Na Pɔɔlu afa i nyu­mbaa akpama i ŋu, na wɔ fa nu ka sɔnɔ n ayo yarada, ka i koro tuntun i na i nya laifiɛ.

eng: Now get up and enter the city. You will be told what you must do.
local: Ama, yasu jina na a wura miɛ n kunnu na m má sɔnɔ be kere u deke bo wɔ yo daka ka a yo ni.

eng: Therefore, I conclude that we shouldnt create problems for Gentiles who turn to God.
local: Na Yakobo akaaki ase bu ka, Mini dɛɛ sunsunni la ka, minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu suba Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, wɔ yo má daka ka ya miɛ bu.

eng: She did this for many days. This annoyed Paul so much that he finally turned and said to the spirit, In the name of Jesus Christ, I command you to leave her! It left her at that very moment.
local: I nati yoori sɔ ɔ, cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, haari na yaa aba ati Pɔɔlu, na wɔ kaaki aniɛ i, na wɔ tiɛn ajini n bo i wo suŋguru n ŋu n na wɔ se i ka, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma n dɛɛ yiko nu, fite yaki i. Kpeŋga, na ajini n afite ayaki i.

eng: Paul and his companions sailed from Paphos to Perga in Pamphylia. John deserted them there and returned to Jerusalem.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ni i lamɔkɔm n afa lee bambaka afite Pafɔsi dɛɛ miɛ kpiri n nu akɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Pɛrɛga na i wo Pamfiliya awɔrɛ n nu ni. Na Yohane bo bu kaaki fere i ka Marako n akpaaki ayaki bu, na wɔ kpie i nyi akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: This was because the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen on any of them; they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Dama ba biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue ya Mibiɛ Yesu dɛɛ duma n nu wɔ, ama, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n nya wura má bu ŋu.

eng: Raising his voice, Paul said, Stand up straight on your feet! He jumped up and began to walk.
local: Na wɔ tiɛn kpa ase i ka, Yasu jina cencen. Na sɔnɔ n ayasu kungere­kun awarakpin sunati bara yɛ.

eng: Paul and Silas journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, then came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ni Silasi afa asin Amfipolisi, ni Apoloniya miɛ kpirim su, na ba kɔ aju Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu. Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ wo dɔ.

eng: Those traveling with him stood there speechless; they heard the voice but saw no one.
local: Na minɛm n bo bu su Sɔɔlu su n aka ajina surum, ba koro ase má jɔrɛ be, bu tiiri akonvi ni ŋga a, ama, ba ŋu má sɔnɔ be.

eng: Instead, we should write a letter, telling them to avoid the pollution associated with idols, sexual immorality, eating meat from strangled animals, and consuming blood.
local: Ama, i ti ye ni ka ya kɛrɛ kadasi ma bu, na ya se bu ka, má bu di tiɛ niɛ, na má bu yo sakaraya, na má bu di niɛ bo ba kpiɛ má i kɔm, na má bu di niɛ mbunja.

eng: Her owners realized that their hope for making money was gone. They grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them before the officials in the city center.
local: Tɔ, saŋga bo afani n to­fɔm ŋuuri ka bu koro nya má ŋwaa fite má i n dɔ kun ni, na ba tara Pɔɔlu ni Silasi, na ba cin bu akɔ miɛ n kunnu afa bu awura yiko­fɔm saa nu.

eng: They went on from Perga and arrived at Antioch in Pisidia. On the Sabbath, they entered and found seats in the synagogue there.
local: Na ba fite Pɛrɛga akɔ aju Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Pisidiya awɔrɛ n nu ni. Na Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn, na ba kɔ awura atana bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu.

eng: So Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
local: Na Petoro ni Yohane afa bu saa anana bu tii su, na ba sere Nyɛmɛ ama bu, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura bu ŋu.

eng: Seeing what Paul had done, the crowd shouted in the Lycaonian language, The gods have taken human form and come down to visit us.
local: Saŋga bo jama n ŋuuri sa n bo Pɔɔlu ayo i sɔ ni, na ba tiɛn kpa Likawoniya aniɛ n nu, bu ŋa, Ya nyɛmɛm akaaki ka adamandem wɔ, ye ba jura aba ya dɔ a.

eng: As was Pauls custom, he entered the synagogue and for three Sabbaths interacted with them on the basis of the scriptures.
local: Nyini ti, kabo i nati yo ni, na Pɔɔlu awura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu akɔ aju Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn nsan, na i ni bɛrɛ subo anzama fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n su.

eng: After they picked Saul up from the ground, he opened his eyes but he couldnt see. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.
local: Na Sɔɔlu ayasu ajina, na wɔ teke i nyu­mbaa, ama, wɔ ŋu má asi. Nyini ti, na ba so i saa nu, na ba cin i awura Damasiko miɛ n kunnu.

eng: After all, Moses has been proclaimed in every city for a long time, and is read aloud every Sabbath in every synagogue.
local: Dama ba du mɔ akan Moyisi nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n jɔrɛ ya nam saŋga, haari ni nyuma bu toko kan i kadasi miɛ n kɛrɛ su wɔ, na bu sukan i na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn kɛrɛ.

eng: When her owners approached the legal authorities, they said, These people are causing an uproar in our city. They are Jews.
local: Bu faari bu kɔ juuri jɔrɛ­di­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm dɔ, na ba se ka, Ahin minɛm ni ti Yuda­fɔm wɔ, na bu nati sukpini jɔrɛ ya miɛ n nu.

eng: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders invited them, Brothers, if one of you has a sermon for the people, please speak.
local: Saŋga bo bu kaanri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa kadasi n bo Moyisi kɛrɛɛri i ni, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n wieeri ni, na bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n asunma ase Pɔɔlu ka, Njam, nzɛn am la jɔrɛ be bo am kan kere jama ni, na i ma bu ahoresi, am kan kere bu.

eng: When Simon perceived that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles hands, he offered them money.
local: Simon ŋuuri ka Yesu jaramasam faari bu saa nanaari yarada­yo­fɔm ni tii su, na bu sereeri Nyɛmɛ maari bu, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wuraari bu ŋu ni, na wɔ fa ŋwaa aba ka i fa ma Petoro ni Yohane.

eng: They referred to Barnabas as Zeus and to Paul as Hermes, since Paul was the main speaker.
local: Na ba fere Banabasi duma ka Ziyosi, bu amɔɛ kpiri duma lɛɛ, na ba fere Pɔɔlu ka Hɛrɛmesi, dama yiri ti amɔɛ n cɛm a.

eng: Through his interpretation of the scriptures, he demonstrated that the Christ had to suffer and rise from the dead. He declared, This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.
local: na i sukan, na i suyi i bu kere bu, kabo wɔ yo daka ka Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ba di wahara na i teŋge i yiwee nu ni. Na wɔ se bu ka, Yiri Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n la Yesu n bo m kan i jɔrɛ kere am ni.

eng: For three days he was blind and neither ate nor drank anything.
local: I woori dɔ, i le­san bo wɔ nya koro ŋu má asi, nyini saŋga n gusu, i di má, i nu má.

eng: The apostles and the elders, along with the entire church, agreed to send some delegates chosen from among themselves to Antioch, together with Paul and Barnabas. They selected Judas Barsabbas and Silas, who were leaders among the brothers and sisters.
local: Dɔ na Yesu jaramasam ni, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Kristo jama n kɛrɛ afa sɛsɛ­su asoro su ka bu yi bu nu nbem, na bu sunma bu ni Pɔɔlu, ni Banabasi Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu, bɛrɛ la Yudasi bo bu fere i ka Basabasi ni, ni Silasi. Nyini minɛ nnyɔm kɛrɛ ti Kristo jama n mɔ­fɔm wɔ.

eng: who promote customs that we Romans cant accept or practice.
local: Bu nati sukere sa­yoo­wam bo ya dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa atin n su, wɔ yo má daka ka ya de bu, wara ya su bu su, dama ya ti Roma­fɔm wɔ.

eng: Standing up, Paul gestured with his hand and said, Fellow Israelites and Gentile God-worshippers, please listen to me.
local: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu ayasu ajina, na wɔ yɛ i saa su, na wɔ bo i bu na i jɔjɔ, i ŋa, M lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔm, ni ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm na am su Nyɛmɛ ni, am ti fɛn.

eng: He said, Give me this authority too so that anyone on whom I lay my hands will receive the Holy Spirit.
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Am ma mini mmɔɔ ahin yiko n be, adena sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo m fa m saa nana i ŋu na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wura i mi ŋu.

eng: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was located just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates. Along with the crowds, he wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
local: Na amɔɛ n bo bu fere i ka Ziyosi n sɔɔ jina miɛ n ahɛmɛ, na amɔɛ n fɔ n ni miɛ n nu­fɔm anyɔndi tooram ni baka pum afa aba miɛ n anɔ nu ka bu fa yo tiɛ ma Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi.

eng: Some were convinced and joined Paul and Silas, including a larger number of Greek God-worshippers and quite a few prominent women.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm nbem ade jɔrɛ n bo Pɔɔlu kaanri i ni, ni Giriki­fɔm beberebe bo bu su Nyɛmɛ ni, ni nbaram beberebe bo bu la anyunuyi ni, na bu kɛrɛ afa bu ŋu amata Pɔɔlu ni Silasi.

eng: In Damascus there was a certain disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, Ananias! He answered, Yes, Lord.
local: Yesu susu­fɔ n kun be woori Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu dɔ, bu fere i ka Ananiyasi. Na Mibiɛ ni aba i dɔ ka alɛlɛ nu. Na wɔ jɔjɔ i dɔ ka, Ananiyasi. Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, M Mibiɛ, mini yaa.

eng: They were to carry this letter: The apostles and the elders, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. Greetings!.
local: Na ba kɛrɛ kadasi ama bu ka bu fa kɔ. Kɛrɛɛ­wa n la ka, Yɛrɛ am ninbaam, ni Kristo jaramasam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni subisa am dɔŋgu, ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm na am wo Antiyoki ni, ni bo bu wo Siriya ni Silisiya dɛɛ awɔrɛm nu ni.

eng: The crowd joined in the attacks against Paul and Silas, so the authorities ordered that they be stripped of their clothes and beaten with a rod.
local: Na jama n kɛrɛ atɔ Pɔɔlu ni Silasi ŋu ni jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn. Na jɔrɛ­di­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ama ba yi Pɔɔlu ni Silasi suturam, na ba ma nɔɔ ka bu fin bu ŋgbere.

eng: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors. God made them a great people while they lived as strangers in the land of Egypt. With his great power, he led them out of that country.
local: Yisareli­fɔm Nyɛmɛ ni, yiri yo yiiri ya nam a, na wɔ ma ba tɛrɛ abuka su, saŋga bo bu yo ti wɔfɔm Ijipiti asiɛn su ni. Na Nyɛmɛ afa i dɛɛ yiko bambaka n na wɔ yi bu afite Ijipiti asiɛn n su.

eng: Peter responded, May your money be condemned to hell along with you because you believed you could buy Gods gift with money!.
local: Ama, na Petoro ase i ka, Wɔrɔ, u ni u ŋwaa kɛrɛ ka ŋgbɛɛn, dama a sunsun ka a koro fa ŋwaa to Nyɛmɛ aceeri n wɔ.

eng: When the Lords messengers Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes in protest and rushed out into the crowd. They shouted.
local: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi tiiri kabo minɛm koro ka bu yo ni, na bu ahore asaaki na yaa ati bu, nyini ti, na ba cin asɔn bu sutura bo bu wura i, na ba ŋmati awura jama n afiɛn sutiɛn bu ŋu.

eng: But the Jews became jealous and brought along some thugs who were hanging out in the marketplace. They formed a mob and started a riot in the city. They attacked Jasons house, intending to bring Paul and Silas before the people.
local: Ama, na Yuda­fɔm n asi bu kɔrɛ agaya, na ba fere minɛm ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn nbem bo bu la má kpɛrɛ na bu nati tana gɔɔsu ni, na ba tiɛn jama dɔŋgu nɔɔ. Na ba kpisi miɛ ni, na ba kɔ sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Jason n dika, akɔ ajina i dika anɔ nu, deke bo Pɔɔlu ni Silasi wo ni, na bu sutiɛntiɛn ka i yi bu ma bu na bu fa bu kɔ jama n nu.

eng: The Lord instructed him, Go to Judas house on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul. He is praying.
local: Na Mibiɛ n ase i ka, Yasu, fa atin n bo bu fere i ka Atiŋgbere Teŋgee­wa n su, na a kɔ sɔnɔ n bo bu fere i ka Yudasi n dika, na a bisa ka a koro sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Sɔɔlu ni, na i fite miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Tarasu n wɔ. Yiri Sɔɔlu n wo dɔ wo nyɛmɛsere su.

eng: Weve heard that some of our number have disturbed you with unsettling words we didnt authorize.
local: Ya ti ka ya nbem afite ya nu fɛn aba am dɔ, aba akan jɔrɛ nbem akere am bo wɔ miɛ am, na wɔ yo am sunsunni agaya. Yɛrɛ ni ya tii asunma má bu.

eng: When Paul and Silas had been severely beaten, the authorities threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to secure them with great care.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba bo bu nyunyumi, na ba kɔ anyi bu dan­saraka, na ba ma dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n nɔɔ ka i tu anɔ n na i niɛ bu dika nyunyumi.

eng: For about forty years, God put up with them in the wilderness.
local: na wɔ mindi abita bu boro kpantain n nu akɔ aju afɔ abura­na.

eng: You can have no part or share in Gods word because your heart isnt right with God.
local: U saa wo má juma n nu fieo, dama u sunsunni ti má kpa Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: People, what are you doing? We are humans too, just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you: turn to the living God and away from such worthless things. He made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything in them. .
local: bu ŋa, Njam o e, nzu ti ye am suyo sɔ a? Yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii, ti adamandem kabo am ti n wɔ. Ya ba ka ya kan labari kpa n kere am wɔ, na am yaki nyini deke ŋgbɛɛnm ni, na am su Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni. Yiri yiiri aŋgoro, ni asiɛŋgu, ni tieku n ni dekem kɛrɛ bo bu wo nu n a.

eng: When they didnt find them, they dragged Jason and some believers before the city officials. They were shouting, These people who have been disturbing the peace throughout the empire have also come here.
local: Ama, bu kɔ niɛniɛɛri na ba ŋu má bu ni, na ba tara Jason ni yarada­yo­fɔ nbem acin bu afa bu akɔ miɛ n nu yiko­fɔm dɔ, na bu sutiɛn suse ka, Ahin minɛm n bo ba saaki durunya n abara ayɛ ni, aba ya miɛ n nu fɛn.

eng: We reached a united decision to select some delegates and send them to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul.
local: Nyini ti, ye ya kɛrɛ atana asɛsɛ su, asoro su ayi minɛm asunma bu ni ya danfo kpam Banabasi ni Pɔɔlu am dɔ.

eng: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias enter and put his hands on him to restore his sight.
local: wɔ ŋu sɔnɔ be ka alɛlɛ nu, bu fere i ka Ananiyasi, wɔ wura i dɔ afa i saa anana i ŋu ka i kaaki ŋu asi bekun.

eng: When he received these instructions, he threw them into the innermost cell and secured their feet in stocks.
local: Dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n deeri nyini nɔaniɛ n wieeri sɔ ni, na wɔ fa bu akɔ awura dan­saraka n sɔɔ n bo i wo mɔ n nu, na wɔ bo bu tobarim.

eng: God conquered seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave the Israelites their land as an inheritance.
local: Na wɔ butu daanfiɛ barasu nso n bo bu wo Kanana asiɛn n su ni, na wɔ fa bu asiɛn n ama Yisareli­fɔm ka bu fa yo bu tiɛŋgu dɛɛ.

eng: Therefore, change your heart and life! Turn from your wickedness! Plead with the Lord in the hope that your wicked intent can be forgiven.
local: Kaaki u wo fa kɔ u satiɛ n bo a bita i n su, na a sere Nyɛmɛ, adena nyini sunsunni tiɛ n bo i wo u ahore su ni, i fá ce u.

eng: In the past, he permitted every nation to go its own way.
local: Dawa saŋga ni, yiri Nyɛmɛ yo maari atin wɔ ka daanfiɛ kɛrɛ yo kabo i koro.

eng: What is more, Jason has welcomed them into his home. Every one of them does what is contrary to Caesars decrees by naming someone else as king: Jesus.
local: haari Jason ade bu awura i dika. Ba kete Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n nɔaniɛ n bo wɔ fa ama ka ya su i su ni, bu ŋa fɛmɛ be wo bɛrɛ bekun, bo bu fere i ka Yesu.

eng: These people have devoted their lives to the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Bɛrɛ ye ba fa bu ŋgɔɔ ama ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti n a.

eng: Ananias countered, Lord, I have heard many reports about this man. People say he has done horrible things to your holy people in Jerusalem.
local: Na Ananiyasi abɔ i nu ka, M Mibiɛ, minɛ dɔŋgu akan nyini sɔnɔ n jɔrɛ akere m, kabo i kere u minɛm ni wuwuruwa Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu barasu.

eng: Around midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.
local: Na fa kɔ kɔŋguɛ jinanjin saŋga, na Pɔɔlu ni Silasi susere Nyɛmɛ, na bu sudi jue kansi i, na bu beŋgu dan­sarakam ni suso bu sui suti.

eng: This happened over a period of about four hundred fifty years. After this, he gave them judges until the time of the prophet Samuel.
local: Ahin n kɛrɛ yoori sɔ kɔ juuri afɔ yaa nna ni afɔ aburu­nu wɔ. Nyini sin, na Nyɛmɛ ayi mɔ­fɔm ama bu haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo i yiiri i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Samuwɛli maari bu ni.

eng: for I see that your bitterness has poisoned you and evil has you in chains.
local: Dama ma ŋu ka u ahore­mbaa yo wi agaya, na u satiɛ akunma u.

eng: Nevertheless, he hasnt left himself without a witness. He has blessed you by giving you rain from above as well as seasonal harvests, and satisfying you with food and happiness.
local: Ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ, saŋga kɛrɛ i yo sakpa ma am wɔ, i ma nzue tɔ ma am wɔ, na am alɛ­mbaa bo am dɔ bu n yo nyunyumi, na am di, na am kunnu yi, na i ma am ahorejɔ. Nyini kɛrɛ kere am ka i wo bɛrɛ a.

eng: This provoked the crowd and the city officials even more.
local: Bu seeri sɔ ni, na wɔ yɛ yiko­fɔm ni jama n ahore su.

eng: Therefore, we are sending Judas and Silas. They will confirm what we have written.
local: Nyini ti, ya súsunma Yudasi ni Silasi am dɔ, na bɛrɛ ni bu tii bá teke bu nɔɔ, na bu kán jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo kadasi n nu kere am.

eng: Hes here with authority from the chief priests to arrest everyone who calls on your name.
local: Ye wɔ de yiko afite Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm dɔ, aba Damasiko fɛn, na i ba tara minɛm bo bu su u n kɛrɛ.

eng: All at once there was such a violent earthquake that it shook the prisons foundations. The doors flew open and everyones chains came loose.
local: Jina n nu bɛrɛ, na asiɛn n akpisi agaya, haari na dan­saraka sɔɔ n jaafa bo bu fa boori i bu n kɛrɛ akpisi, na anɔm n kɛrɛ ateke, na tobari n kɛrɛ n bo bu fa boori bu n akporo atɔtɔ.

eng: The Israelites requested a king, so God gave them Saul, Kishs son, from the tribe of Benjamin, and he served as their king for forty years.
local: Na ba ba ase Samuwɛli ka bu koro fɛmɛ wɔ, na Nyɛmɛ ama Sɔɔlu bo i ti Kisi wa n ayo bu fɛmɛ, i fite Bɛnjamin n dɛɛ busu nu wɔ, na wɔ bita bu akɔ aju afɔ abura­na.

eng: Simon replied, All of you, please, plead to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said will happen to me.
local: Dɔ na Simon ase Petoro ni Yohane ka, Jande, am sere Mibiɛ n ma m, adena má ahin sam kɛrɛ bo am ajɔjɔ i n be ba tu m.

eng: Even with these words, they barely kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.
local: Haari ni nyini jɔjɔɔ­wa n kɛrɛ n mmɔɔ ni, i yoori kekereke maari Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi ye bu koro kandaari jama n ye ba yo má tiɛ ama bu n a.

eng: After Jason and the others posted bail, they released them.
local: Na yiko­fɔm n akpiɛ Jason ni i beŋgum kɔbuka, na ba tɔ kaari, na ba nya ayaki bu aji.

eng: The Holy Spirit has led us to the decision that no burden should be placed on you other than these essentials.
local: Kabo ya ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ ayo kun, na ya soro su lɛɛ, ka ya sɔ má am sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri be, sin ahin dekem bo wɔ yo daka ka am yo bu ni.

eng: The Lord replied, Go! This man is the agent I have chosen to carry my name before Gentiles, kings, and Israelites.
local: Ama, na Mibiɛ n ase i ka, Kɔ, dama mini ye ma yi i ka i yo m juma a, na i kɔ bo m duma kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, ni bu nfɛmɛm, ni Yisareli­fɔm, adena bu si m.

eng: When the jailer awoke and saw the open doors of the prison, he thought the prisoners had escaped, so he drew his sword and was about to kill himself.
local: Na dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n abo nu ayasu, na wɔ ŋu ka dan­saraka sɔɔ anɔm n kɛrɛ ateke, i sunsun ka dan­sarakam kɛrɛ aŋmati asandi wɔ, nyini ti, na wɔ cin awɔrɔ i takobe ka i kun i ŋu wɔ.

eng: After God removed him, he raised up David to be their king. God testified concerning him, I have found David, Jesses son, a man who shares my desires. Whatever my will is, he will do.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ ayi Sɔɔlu fɛmɛya n nu, na wɔ fa Fɛmɛ Davidi bo i ti Jese wa n ajina i jinaa­biri. Na Nyɛmɛ adi i ŋusu daani, i ŋa, Yiri Davidi, Jese wa n ti sɔnɔ bo m ahore atara i a, yiri yó m sa koroo­wa kɛrɛ a.

eng: After the apostles had testified and proclaimed the Lords word, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the good news to many Samaritan villages along the way.
local: Petoro ni Yohane diiri daani n na bu kaanri Mibiɛ n labari kpa n jɔrɛ kereeri minɛ wieeri ni, na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu. Bu wo kɔ su ni, na ba kan labari kpa n Samariya miɛ ahɛmɛ ahɛmɛ dɔŋgu su.

eng: Jews from Antioch and Iconium arrived and won the crowds over. They stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing he was dead.
local: Na Yuda­fɔ nbem afite Antiyoki ni Ikoniyom miɛ kpirim n nu aba Lisitira, na ba ma minɛm ade bu nɔaniɛ, na ba tutu Pɔɔlu yabuɛ, bu sunsun ka wɔ wu wɔ, na ba cin i afite miɛ n nu.

eng: As soon as it was dark, the brothers and sisters sent Paul and Silas on to Beroea. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ n kɛrɛ, na yarada­yo­fɔm n ase Pɔɔlu ni Silasi atin nu akɔ aju miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Bɛriya n su. Bu kɔ juuri dɔ n na ba kɔ awura Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu.

eng: refuse food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid such things. Farewell.
local: Má am di tiɛ niɛ, má am di niɛ mbunja, má am di niɛ bo ba kpiɛ má i kɔm, na má am yo sakaraya. Nzɛn am tara am ŋu na am ayo má ahin sam ni, i yó kpa ma am. Tɔ, ya ka saŋga lɛɛ.

eng: I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.
local: Na m kére i wahara n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo daka ka i di i, mini dɛɛ ti ni.

eng: From this mans descendants, God brought to Israel a savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
local: Yiri Fɛmɛ Davidi dɛɛ busu nu ye Nyɛmɛ seeri ka Yisareli­fɔm Tii­de­fɔ Yesu ni fite n a.

eng: But Paul shouted loudly, Dont harm yourself! Were all here.
local: Ama, na Pɔɔlu atiɛn kpa ase ka, Má a ma u ŋu mandim. Ya kɛrɛ wo fɛn.

eng: An angel from the Lord spoke to Philip, At noon, take the road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.
local: Na Micɛra n mɛrɛkɛ aba ase Filipu ka, Yasu na a fa wiɛ atɔɔri u saa biɛ su dɛɛ atin n bo i fa Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu fa kɔ Gaza miɛ kpiri n nu ni. I ti atin bo i fa boro kpantain n nu wɔ.

eng: When the disciples surrounded him, he got up and entered the city again. The following day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.
local: Ama, na Yesu susu­fɔm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ abara ayɛ i, na wɔ yasu, na wɔ kpie i nyi awura miɛ n nu. Na alecin fɔfɔrɛ, na yiri ni Banabasi afite dɔ, na ba kɔ Dɛrɛbi miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: The Beroean Jews were more honorable than those in Thessalonica. This was evident in the great eagerness with which they accepted the word and examined the scriptures each day to see whether Paul and Silas teaching was true.
local: Dɔ dɛɛ Yuda­fɔm joko n ti kpa tara bɛrɛ n bo bu wo Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu ni. Bu soori bu sui wɔ ni nahɔrɛ su, na ba ti keree­wa n bo bu kere bu ni, na ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, bɛrɛ ni bu tii kan Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n wɔ, na bu niɛ ka, deke n bo Pɔɔlu sukere bu n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, nzɛn i ti má nahɔrɛ.

eng: When Barnabas, Paul, and the delegates were sent on their way, they went down to Antioch. They gathered the believers and delivered the letter.
local: Na minɛm bo bu sunmaari bu n akɔ aju Antiyoki. Bu kɔ juuri ni, na ba nya atiɛn yarada­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ nɔɔ, na ba fa kadasi n ama bu.

eng: Ananias went to the house. He placed his hands on Saul and said, Brother Saul, the Lord sent me—Jesus, who appeared to you on the way as you were coming here. He sent me so that you could see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.
local: Nyini ti, na Ananiyasi akɔ Yudasi dika, na wɔ wura Sɔɔlu dɔ, na wɔ fa i saa anana i ŋu, na wɔ se i ka, M niwa Sɔɔlu, ya Mibiɛ ni sunmaari m u dɔ a, yiri Yesu n ye i yiiri i ŋu kereeri u saŋga bo a wo atin n nu suba n a. Yiri sunmaari m ka m ba ma a ŋu asi bekun, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wura u ŋu a.

eng: Before Jesus appearance, John proclaimed to all the Israelites a baptism to show they were changing their hearts and lives.
local: Kana Yesu ba ba, Yohane du mɔ ba kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kereeri Yisareli­fɔm wɔ, ka bu kaaki bu wo na i biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue.

eng: The jailer called for some lights, rushed in, and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.
local: Na dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n afere ka bu fa sin bra, na wɔ bereki awura Pɔɔlu ni Silasi sɔɔ n nu, na sɛrɛ ati i, na i ŋuniɛ subobo i, na wɔ kɔ akutu bu jaa bu.

eng: So he did. Meanwhile, an Ethiopian man was on his way home from Jerusalem, where he had come to worship. He was a eunuch and an official responsible for the entire treasury of Candace.
local: Na Filipu ayasu, na wɔ kɔ, i wo kɔ su n na wɔ kɔ atu Itiyopiya sɔnɔ bo i fite Itiyopiya asiɛn su, i la jinaa­biri bambaka, yiri niɛ Itiyopiya fɛmɛ bara n neŋge kɛrɛ su a. Wɔ kɔ asu Nyɛmɛ Yɛrusalem.

eng: Paul and Barnabas proclaimed the good news to the people in Derbe and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch, where.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi akan labari kpa n Dɛrɛbi miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba nya susu­fɔm beberebe dɔ. Na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ Lisitira, ni Ikoniyom miɛ kpirim n nu, na ba nya akɔ Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Pisidiya awɔrɛ n nu ni.

eng: Many came to believe, including a number of reputable Greek women and many Greek men.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm ni dɔŋgu ayo yarada, fa saŋga ni Giriki­fɔm beberebe, nbaram bo bu la anyunuyi n ni nbiɛsɔm.

eng: Instantly, flakes fell from Sauls eyes and he could see again. He got up and was baptized.
local: Kpeŋga na deke be afite Sɔɔlu nyu­mbaa n nu atɔ, i ti ka jombaa bui wɔ. Na wɔ kaaki aŋu asi bekun. Na wɔ yasu ajina, na Ananiyasi abiɛ i Nyɛmɛ nzue.

eng: The people read it, delighted with its encouraging message.
local: Saŋga bo bu kaanri kadasi n wieeri ni, na bu ahore ajɔ, na wɔ kaaki ama bu ahoresi.

eng: As John was completing his mission, he said, Who do you think I am? Im not the one you think I am, but he is coming after me. Im not worthy to loosen his sandals.
local: Na saŋga bo Yohane ba sukoro ka i wie i juma n ni, na wɔ se minɛm n ka, Am sunsun ka mini la ŋma a? Ai, má mini la sɔnɔ n bo am ŋminda i ka i bá n a. Ama, am ti fɛn. Yiri bo i wo m sin suba ni, m ti kaan mmɔɔ ni bo m dodo i.

eng: He led them outside and asked, Honorable masters, what must I do to be rescued.
local: Dɔ na wɔ fa bu afite, na wɔ bisa bu ka, M mibiɛm, m yo sɛ ye m nya tii­de a.

eng: He was reading the prophet Isaiah while sitting in his carriage.
local: sukpie i nyi kɔ awuru, i tana i kpɔŋgɔ toroku nu wɔ, na i sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya dɛɛ kadasi ni.

eng: they strengthened the disciples and urged them to remain firm in the faith. They told them, If we are to enter Gods kingdom, we must pass through many troubles.
local: Na ba se yarada­yo­fɔm ni ka, bu ma bu yarada n yo bii­wa, na ba ma bu ahoresi. Na ba se bu ka, Wɔ yo daka ka bu kere ya wuwuruwa beberebe ka, na ya nya koro wura Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: The Jews from Thessalonica learned that Paul also proclaimed Gods word in Beroea, so they went there too and were upsetting and disturbing the crowds.
local: Ama, saŋga bo Yuda­fɔm bo bu wo Tɛsalonika n tiiri ka Pɔɔlu akɔ akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ Bɛriya dɔ gusu ni, na ba ba dɔ aba awura minɛm bu sin, na ba yɛ bu yaa su.

eng: After eating, he regained his strength. He stayed with the disciples in Damascus for several days.
local: na wɔ di diire, na wɔ nya faŋga bekun. Na Sɔɔlu awo Yesu susu­fɔm n dɔ le­nnyɔ Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they said many things that encouraged and strengthened the brothers and sisters.
local: Na Yudasi, ni Silasi bo bɛrɛ ni bu tii ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, akan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ beberebe akere bu, na bu ahore asi, na wɔ buka bu faŋga.

eng: Brothers, children of Abrahams family, and you Gentile God-worshippers, the message about this salvation has been sent to us.
local: M ninbaam, ambɛrɛ bo am ti Abraham busu nu­fɔm ni, ni ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm na am su Nyɛmɛ ni, yɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ ma ba fa ahin tii­de labari n ama ya a.

eng: They replied, Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved—you and your entire household.
local: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Yo Mibiɛ Yesu yarada, na a nyá tii­de, wɔrɔ ni u dika­fɔm kɛrɛ.

eng: The Spirit told Philip, Approach this carriage and stay with it.
local: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ase Filipu ka, Cin dodo kpɔŋgɔ toroku n na a su i su.

eng: They appointed elders for each church. With prayer and fasting, they committed these elders to the Lord, in whom they had placed their trust.
local: Miɛ kɛrɛ bo bu wɔɔri su, bu yi nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm wɔ Kristo jama n nu, na bu cici bu nɔɔ, na bu sere Nyɛmɛ, na bu fa bu wura yiri Mibiɛ n bo ba yo i yarada n saa nu ka i niɛ bu dika.

eng: The brothers and sisters sent Paul away to the seacoast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained at Beroea.
local: Kpeŋga na yarada­yo­fɔm n ayo ndende afa Pɔɔlu akɔ ase haari tieku n nɔɔ su, ama, na Silasi ni Timoti yiri aka Bɛriya dɔ.

eng: Right away, he began to preach about Jesus in the synagogues. He is Gods Son, he declared.
local: Na wɔ kaba sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu, ka Yesu ti Nyɛmɛ Wa n a.

eng: Judas and Silas stayed there awhile, then were sent back with a blessing of peace from the brothers and sisters to those who first sent them.
local: Na ba wo dɔ acɛ, na yarada­yo­fɔm n akaari bu ni ahoreda, na ba kpie bu nyi akɔ bɛrɛ n bo bu sunmaari bu n dɔ. .

eng: The people in Jerusalem and their leaders didnt recognize Jesus. By condemning him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.
local: Dama Yɛrusalem­fɔm ni bu nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm kɛrɛ asi má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n bu, haari ni bo bu kan i bu Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni. Ba kaaki asi má kabo Yesu ti barasu, na ba ma ba kun i. Nyini ma Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kadasi n ayo yii­wa a.

eng: They spoke the Lords word to him and everyone else in his house.
local: Na ba kan Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ n akere i, i ni bo bu wo i dika n kɛrɛ.

eng: Running up to the carriage, Philip heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked, Do you really understand what you are reading.
local: Na wɔ ŋmati akɔ ati ka i sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya dɛɛ kadasi ni. Na wɔ bisa sɔnɔ n ka, A ti kadasi n bo a sukan i n bu.

eng: After Paul and Barnabas traveled through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
local: Bu fite dɔ ni, na ba fa Pisidiya awɔrɛ n nu akɔ atiɛ Pamfiliya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: Those who escorted Paul led him as far as Athens, then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible.
local: Na minɛm n bo bu seeri Pɔɔlu atin nu ni, afa i akɔ aju haari miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Atensi n su. Bu kpieeri bu nyi sukɔ ni, na Pɔɔlu akaari bu ka bu kɔ se Silasi ni Timoti ka bu bra na bu ba tu i dɔ ndende.

eng: Everyone who heard him was baffled. They questioned each other, Isnt he the one who was wreaking havoc among those in Jerusalem who called on this name? Hadnt he come here to take those same people as prisoners to the chief priests.
local: Na jɔrɛ n amiɛ minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu tiiri ka i sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ni, na ba bisa bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Má sɔnɔ n bo i wo Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu kun minɛm bo bu su Yesu su n lɛɛ? Ye kisa wɔ ba fɛn ka i ba tara minɛm wura bu jɔrɔkɔ, fa bu kpie bu nyi kɔ ma Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, má sɔ.

eng: Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, where, together with many others, they taught and proclaimed the good news of the Lords word.
local: Ama, na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi aka Antiyoki, na bu ni bu beŋgu dɔŋgu akan na ba kere minɛm Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Even though they didnt find a single legal basis for the death penalty, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
local: Haari ni bo ba yo aŋu má deke n bo i ti na wɔ yo daka ka bu kun i ni, bu sereeri Gomina Pilato wɔ, ka i ma bu kun i.

eng: Right then, in the middle of the night, the jailer welcomed them and washed their wounds. He and everyone in his household were immediately baptized.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ n kɛrɛ, na dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n afa Pɔɔlu ni Silasi akɔ, na wɔ kɔ aŋmisi bu kani n kɛrɛ, na ba kaba abiɛ yiri ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue.

eng: The man replied, Without someone to guide me, how could I? Then he invited Philip to climb up and sit with him.
local: Na sɔnɔ n abɔ i nu ka, M yo sɛ ti i bu a, bo má ka sɔnɔ akan i bu akere m? Na wɔ se Filipu ka i wura toroku n nu ba tana i bi.

eng: They proclaimed the word in Perga, then went down to Attalia.
local: Dɔ na ba kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ Pɛrɛga miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba nya akɔ Ataliya miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: While Paul waited for them in Athens, he was deeply distressed to find that the city was flooded with idols.
local: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu wo Atensi miɛ kpiri n nu suŋminda Silasi ni Timoti ni, na i ahore asaaki agaya kabo i ŋuuri ka amɔɛm asunnu miɛ n nu ni.

eng: But Saul grew stronger and stronger. He confused the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.
local: Ama, na Sɔɔlu anya faŋga abuka su, na wɔ kan akunma Yuda­fɔm bo bu wo Damasiko n nu, na wɔ kan ateke bu nyi na wɔ ma ba si ka Yesu ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: When they finished doing everything that had been written about him, they took him down from the cross and laid him in a tomb.
local: Na saŋga bo bu yoori sa n kɛrɛ bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu fa kɔ i ŋusu jɔrɛ ni, na ba jura i baka jaanu n su, na ba fa i awura yabuɛ buɛ faŋ­kunma nu.

eng: Some time later, Paul said to Barnabas, Lets go back and visit all the brothers and sisters in every city where we preached the Lords word. Lets see how they are doing.
local: Tɔ, fa kɔ saŋga su, na Pɔɔlu ase Banabasi ka, Ma ya kpie ya nyi kɔ miɛm kɛrɛ bo ya kaanri Mibiɛ n jɔrɛ kereeri bu n su, na ya kɔ niɛ kabo ya ninbaam ti barasu.

eng: He brought them into his home and gave them a meal. He was overjoyed because he and everyone in his household had come to believe in God.
local: Dɔ na wɔ fa bu akɔ i dika, na wɔ ma bu diire, na ba di. Na yiri ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ ahore ajɔ agaya ka ba yo Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti.

eng: This was the passage of scripture he was reading: Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent so he didnt open his mouth. .
local: Nɔaniɛ n bo i wo i kan su n la bo ba kɛrɛ i ka, Yiri ti ka bɔɛ bo bu sufa i kɔ kpiɛ i kɔm wɔ, i kaaki ti ka bɔmbaa bo bu sukpiɛkpiɛ i ndɔrɛ, na i sun má, sɔ ye wɔ se má jɔrɛ be a.

eng: From there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been entrusted by Gods grace to the work they had now completed.
local: na ba fa lee bambaka afite dɔ akpie bu nyi akaaki akɔ Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Siriya awɔrɛ n nu. Dɔ ye bu du mɔ sereeri Nyɛmɛ maari bu ka bu di i ahɔrɔma fa kɔ nyini juma n bo kisa ba yo i awie ni.

eng: He began to interact with the Jews and Gentile God-worshippers in the synagogue. He also addressed whoever happened to be in the marketplace each day.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ kɔ awura Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na i ni bɛrɛ, ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu su Nyɛmɛ ni, subo anzama. Na i sukɔ gɔɔsu cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, na i sukan kere minɛm bo bu wo dɔ ni.

eng: After this had gone on for some time, the Jews hatched a plot to kill Saul.
local: Na wɔ yo le­nnyɔ, na Yuda­fɔm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ afa sɛsɛ­su kabo bu yo na bu kun Sɔɔlu.

eng: But God raised him from the dead!.
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i yiwee n nu.

eng: Barnabas wanted to take John Mark with them.
local: Má Banabasi koro ka bu fa Yohane bo bu kaaki fere i ka Marako n na bu kɛrɛ kɔ ɔ.

eng: The next morning the legal authorities sent the police to the jailer with the order Release those people.
local: Alecin ŋgarama, na jɔrɛ­di­fɔm n asunma npulisim ka bu kɔ se dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n ka i yaki nyini minɛm nu ji na bu kɔ.

eng: In his humiliation justice was taken away from him. Who can tell the story of his descendants because his life was taken from the earth? .
local: Bu yo faari i wuraari jɔrɛ­di­fɔm saa nu wɔ, na ba kpiɛ kɔrɛ asɔ i, na ba wura i nyinsɛ. Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i bá koro jɔjɔ fa kɔ i busu nu­fɔm dɔ, dama bu yo yiiri i ŋgɔɔ durunya n nu fɛn.

eng: On their arrival, they gathered the church together and reported everything that God had accomplished through their activity, and how God had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
local: Saŋga bo bu kɔ juuri Antiyoki ni, na ba tiɛn Kristo jama n nɔɔ, na ba kan sam n bo Nyɛmɛ ayo bu ni, ni kabo wɔ teke atin ama bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ba yo yarada barasu ni.

eng: Certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers engaged him in discussion too. Some said, What an amateur! Whats he trying to say? Others remarked, He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.
local: Sifɔm asɛsɛ nbem wo miɛ n nu, bu fere bu kun ka Epikurifɔm, na bu fere nyiŋga n ka Sitɔwa­fɔm, na bu suse akuruwa ni Pɔɔlu, na bu nbem abisa ka, Ye ahin jɔrɛ beberebe­fɔ n ŋa sɛ wɔ lɛɛ? Na bu nbem abɔ nu ka, I jɔjɔ fa kɔ amɔɛ fɔfɔrɛ nbem jɔrɛ wɔ. Deke bo i ti na bu seeri sɔ n la ka, Pɔɔlu sukan Yesu ni yiwee teŋge n su jɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: However, he found out about their scheme. They were keeping watch at the city gates around the clock so they could assassinate him.
local: ama, na wɔ ti kabo ba sɛsɛ­su ka bu kun i ni. Na ba ŋminda i miɛ ni anɔm n nu, wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ, ka nzɛn i ba sufite na bu kun i.

eng: He appeared over many days to those who had traveled with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now his witnesses to the people.
local: na wɔ yi i ŋu bo i la má nɔ akere minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu suuri i su fiteeri Galili awɔrɛ n nu kɔɔri Yɛrusalem ni. Bɛrɛ n bo bu ŋuuri sam bo bu yoori ni, kisa bɛrɛ sudi i daani kere ya minɛm n a.

eng: Paul insisted that they shouldnt take him along, since he had deserted them in Pamphylia and hadnt continued with them in their work.
local: ama, na Pɔɔlu akete ka i sú má bu su, dama saŋga bo bu wo Pamfiliya awɔrɛ n nu ni, i ŋmati yakiiri bu wɔ, wɔ su má bu su abuka má bu ni juma ni.

eng: So the jailer reported this to Paul, informing him, The authorities sent word that you both are to be released. You can leave now. Go in peace.
local: Nyini ti, na dan­saraka niɛ­fɔ n asere Pɔɔlu ka, Jɔrɛ­di­fɔm n ama nɔɔ ka ya yaki am na am kɔ. Nyini ti, am kɔ ni ahoreda.

eng: The eunuch asked Philip, Tell me, about whom does the prophet say this? Is he talking about himself or someone else.
local: Na Itiyopiya sɔnɔ n abisa Filipu ka, Jande, m koro ka a se m, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n yo jɔjɔ ahin jɔrɛ n ni ŋma a? I jɔjɔ niiri i ŋu wɔ, nzɛn be a.

eng: They stayed with the disciples a long time.
local: Na ba wo yarada­yo­fɔm n dɔ miɛ n nu acɛ.

eng: They took him into custody and brought him to the council on Mars Hill. What is this new teaching? Can we learn what you are talking about?.
local: Na ba fa Pɔɔlu akɔ awɔrɛ be bo bu fere i ka Aropagɔsi na bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ di jɔrɛ n nu. Na ba se i ka, A koro kere ya na ya ti ahin keree­wa fɔfɔrɛ n bo a sufa kere n bu?.

eng: But his disciples took him by night and lowered him in a basket through an opening in the city wall.
local: Ama, na cɛɛn n be kɔŋguɛ, na Sɔɔlu susu­fɔm afa i awura kandiɛ nu, na ba fa i acɔɔndi miɛ n kobe tukoro nu ase.

eng: We proclaim to you the good news. What God promised to our ancestors.
local: Nyini ti, ya ba ka ya kan labari kpa sam n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fa ma ya nam ni kere am a.

eng: Their argument became so intense that they went their separate ways. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus.
local: Dɔ na akuruwa bambaka aba bu afiɛn, na ba kpaaki ayaki bu beŋgu, na Banabasi afa Marako abuka i ŋu su, na ba fa lee bambaka akɔ Sipurusi asiɛn n su.

eng: Paul told the police, Even though we are Roman citizens, they beat us publicly without first finding us guilty of a crime, and they threw us into prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? No way! They themselves will have to come and escort us out.
local: Ama, na Pɔɔlu ase npulisim ni ka, Haari ni bo ya ti Roma­fɔm ni, ba di má ya jɔrɛ aŋu má ya ŋgasi be, na ba bo ya jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, na ba nyi ya dan­saraka. Ye kisa bu sukoro ka bu fiɛ yi ya ji bɛnlɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Nyini koro yo má, má ka bɛrɛ ni bu tii aba na bu ba yi ya ji.

eng: Starting with that passage, Philip proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
local: Dɔ na Filipu abo i bu ni nyini Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n bo yiri sɔnɔ n sukan i ni, na wɔ kan Yesu labari kpa n jɔrɛ n akere i.

eng: Youve told us some strange things and we want to know what they mean. .
local: Ahin keree­wa n bo a sukere i ni, ya si má i, ya koro ka a ma ya ti i bu wɔ.

eng: he has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it was written in the second psalm, You are my son; today I have become your father. .
local: ye i ma nɔaniɛ n ayo yii­wa ama yɛrɛ bo ya ti bu busu nu­fɔm n a, wɔ teŋge Yesu i yiwee n nu, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Kansi Jue kɛrɛɛ­wam n nu, i jue nnyɔ su ni. Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Wɔrɔ ti m Wa a, nyuma, m káaki u Si.

eng: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him. They didnt believe he was really a disciple.
local: Saŋga bo Sɔɔlu kɔ juuri Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na wɔ koro ka i fa i ŋu bo Yesu susu­fɔm n su, na i ni bɛrɛ yo kun, na sɛrɛ ati bu, dama ba yo má yarada ka i ti Yesu susu­fɔm n be wɔ.

eng: Paul chose Silas and left, entrusted by the brothers and sisters to the Lords grace.
local: na Pɔɔlu gusu afa Silasi ka i su i su, na yarada­yo­fɔm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ asere Nyɛmɛ ama bu, ka bu di Mibiɛ n ahɔrɔma.

eng: As they went down the road, they came to some water. The eunuch said, Look! Water! What would keep me from being baptized.
local: Bu wo kɔ su ni, na ba kɔ aju awɔrɛ be nu, na ba ŋu nzue, na sɔnɔ n ase i ka, Niɛ, nzue yaa e, ye nzu kanda ka a koro biɛ má m Nyɛmɛ nzue a? .

eng: The police reported this to the legal authorities, who were alarmed to learn that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.
local: Na npulisim n akɔ ase jɔrɛ­di­fɔm ni. Bu tiiri ka Pɔɔlu ni Silasi ti Roma­fɔm ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu.

eng: They said this because all Athenians as well as the foreigners who live in Athens used to spend their time doing nothing but talking about or listening to the newest thing.
local: Dama Atensi­fɔm ni wɔfɔm bo bu wo miɛ n nu n dɛɛ la ka, saŋga kɛrɛ bu sukan na bu suti keree­wa fɔfɔrɛm wɔ.

eng: God raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be subjected to deaths decay. Therefore, God said, I will give to you the holy and firm promises I made to David. .
local: Bekun, kabo i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu na i kpɔrɔ má faŋ­kunma nu ni, Nyɛmɛ akaaki ase ka, M fá deke kpa n bo i ti casi na i la anyunuyi n ma u, yiri ye m seeri ka m fa ma Fɛmɛ Davidi n a.

eng: Then Barnabas brought Saul to the apostles and told them the story about how Saul saw the Lord on the way and that the Lord had spoken to Saul. He also told them about the confidence with which Saul had preached in the name of Jesus in Damascus.
local: Dɔ na Banabasi afa i akɔ Yesu jaramasam n dɔ. Na yiri Banabasi akan akere bu kabo Sɔɔlu wo atin n nu sukɔ Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu, na i ŋuuri Mibiɛ ni, ni kabo Mibiɛ n jɔjɔɔri i dɔ barasu ni, ni kabo Sɔɔlu kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ Yesu duma nu Damasiko, ni ahoresi ni.

eng: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
local: Na wɔ kɔ abara ayɛ Siriya, ni Silisiya awɔrɛm n nu, na wɔ ma Kristo jama n ahoresi.

eng: He ordered that the carriage halt. Both Philip and the eunuch went down to the water, where Philip baptized him.
local: Na wɔ ma toroku n ajina, na bu kɛrɛ ajura awura nzue n nu, na Filipu abiɛ i Nyɛmɛ nzue ni.

eng: They came and consoled Paul and Silas, escorting them out of prison and begging them to leave the city.
local: Nyini ti, na ba ba atara Pɔɔlu ni Silasi jaa, na ba nya ayi bu afite dan­saraka n nu, na ba sere bu jande ka bu fite miɛ n nu.

eng: Paul stood up in the middle of the council on Mars Hill and said, People of Athens, I see that you are very religious in every way.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ayasu ajina miɛ n nu tere­tere­fɔm n afiɛn, na wɔ se ka, Ma ŋu kabo ambɛrɛ Atensi­fɔm su amɔɛm na am yo munzue sam ni anyum­boro.

eng: In another place it is said, You will not let your holy one experience deaths decay. .
local: Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i Kansi Jue kɛrɛɛ­wam n awɔrɛ be nu, ka, A yáki má na u Amusui­su­fɔ Casi n wu kpɔrɔ.

eng: After this, Saul moved freely among the disciples in Jerusalem and was speaking with confidence in the name of the Lord.
local: Banabasi seeri sɔ ni, na ba soro su, na Sɔɔlu awo bu dɔ Yɛrusalem, na i sujeŋgbe sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ Yesu duma nu, ni ahoresi.

eng: When they came up out of the water, the Lords Spirit suddenly took Philip away. The eunuch never saw him again but went on his way rejoicing.
local: Na ba fite nzue n nu, bu fite wieeri ni, na Mibiɛ n Awiɛwiɛ n akaba akpara Filipu akɔ, sɔnɔ n aŋu má i kun, na wɔ kɔ awuru ni ahorejɔ.

eng: As I was walking through town and carefully observing your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: To an unknown God. What you worship as unknown, I now proclaim to you.
local: Dama kabo m nati bara yɛ niɛ yi kɔrɛsi ni, ma ŋu dekem bo am su bu ni, na ma ŋu bimbindi be bo am akɛrɛ su ka, Nyɛmɛ n bo ya si má i n dɛɛ woo­biri lɛɛ. Tɔ, yiri n bo am su i na am si má i ni, yiri ye m kan i jɔrɛ kere am a.

eng: Paul and Silas left the prison and made their way to Lydias house where they encouraged the brothers and sisters. Then they left Philippi.
local: Na ba fite dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu, na ba kaaki akɔ Lidiya dika, na ba kɔ aŋu yarada­yo­fɔm ni, na ba ma bu ahoresi, na ba nya afite miɛ n nu akɔ.

eng: David served Gods purpose in his own generation, then he died and was buried with his ancestors. He experienced deaths decay.
local: Fɛmɛ Davidi yo yoori Nyɛmɛ kun koroo­wa haari na wɔ ba awu, na ba se i deke bo bu seeri i nam ni, na i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n akpɔrɔ faŋ­kunma n nu.

eng: He got into debates with the Greek-speaking Jews as well, but they tried to kill him.
local: Na i ni Yuda­fɔm bo bu jɔjɔ Giriki aniɛ ni abo anzama haari na ba se akuruwa ni bu beŋgu. Na ba koro ka bu kun i.

eng: Philip found himself in Azotus. He traveled through that area, preaching the good news in all the cities until he reached Caesarea.
local: Na Filipu aŋu ka i wo miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Azotusi n nu, na wɔ yasu dɔ sukɔ, na wɔ kan labari kpa n akere minɛm miɛ kɛrɛ bo i siinri su ni, haari na wɔ kɔ aju Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: God, who made the world and everything in it, is Lord of heaven and earth. He doesnt live in temples made with human hands.
local: Nyɛmɛ bo i yiiri durunya n ni deke n kɛrɛ bo i wo nu ni, yiri ti aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a, i wura má amɔɛ sɔɔm bo adamande afa i saa ata bu n nu na i da.

eng: but the one whom God has raised up didnt experience deaths decay.
local: Ama, yiri n bo Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni, wɔ kpɔrɔ má.

eng: When the family of believers learned about this, they escorted him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
local: Saŋga bo yarada­yo­fɔm n tiiri ahin jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na ba fa Sɔɔlu akɔ aju Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu, na ba ma wɔ kɔ Tarasu miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: Nor is God served by human hands, as though he needed something, since he is the one who gives life, breath, and everything else.
local: Sasu bekun, i fun má deke be bo i se adamande ka i yo ma i, dama yiri ni i tii ma adamande ŋgɔɔ ni ŋumi a, ni deke n kɛrɛ a.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, know this: Through Jesus we proclaim forgiveness of sins to you. From all those sins from which you couldnt be put in right relationship with God through Moses Law.
local: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, m koro ka am si ka yiri Yesu dɛɛ ti ye bu kaanri labari kpa n jɔrɛ kereeri am a, ka Nyɛmɛ afa am satiɛ ace am.

eng: From one person God created every human nation to live on the whole earth, having determined their appointed times and the boundaries of their lands.
local: Yiri ma adamande n kɛrɛ fite sɔnɔ kun busu n nu wɔ, na wɔ ma ba tɛrɛ ade durunya n kɛrɛ. Na wɔ nya akpama saŋga saŋga ama bu kɛrɛ, na wɔ kaaki akere bu kabo bu asiɛn nyaari ti.

eng: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria enjoyed a time of peace. God strengthened the church, and its life was marked by reverence for the Lord. Encouraged by the Holy Spirit, the church continued to grow in numbers.
local: Na Kristo jama n anya faŋga abuka su, na bu susoro Mibiɛ n nahɔrɛ su, na bu suwo ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ ahoresi nu, na bu sunya minɛm subuka su.

eng: through Jesus everyone who believes is put in right relationship with God.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori yiri n yarada, Nyɛmɛ afa i mi satiɛ kɛrɛ ace i. Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo Moyisi fa maari ni, wɔ koro afa má minɛm satiɛ ace má bu.

eng: God made the nations so they would seek him, perhaps even reach out to him and find him. In fact, God isnt far away from any of us.
local: Deke n bo i ti na i yoori sɔ n la ka, bu bá turiŋgi niɛniɛ i busu na beni bu ŋú i. Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ ni, i wo má dede ni yɛrɛ kɛrɛ be.

eng: As Peter toured the whole region, he went to visit Gods holy people in Lydda.
local: Na Petoro ajeŋgbe ka i bara yɛ awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ, na cɛɛn n be sɔ ɔ, na wɔ kɔ ka i kɔ niɛ Nyɛmɛ minɛm n bo bu wo Luda dɛɛ miɛ kpiri n nu n busu.

eng: Take care that the prophets words dont apply to you.
local: Am si am ŋu sa, adena má sam bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm yo seeri n be tu am.

eng: In God we live, move, and exist. As some of your own poets said, We are his offspring.
local: Dama yiri dɛɛ ti ye ya la ŋgɔɔ ye ya wo bɛrɛ ye ya nati a. Kabo am jue­di­fɔ nbem gusu ase ni, ka, Yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ti i nbaam wɔ.

eng: There he found a man named Aeneas who was paralyzed and had been confined to his bed for eight years.
local: Dɔ na wɔ kɔ aŋu sɔnɔ be, bu fere i ka Aniyasi, i saam ni i jaam kɛrɛ awu, i da sɔrɔ ɔ, i afɔ mɔcuɛ lɛɛ bo i koro yasu má.

eng: Look, you scoffers , marvel and die . Im going to do work in your day — a work you wont believe even if someone told you .
local: Bu yo seeri ka, Ambɛrɛ bo am yo Nyɛmɛ anzasi ni, am ti fɛn, am yo alekutura na am ka ŋgbɛɛn, dama m suyo juma be am dɛɛ saŋga nu, haari má be kan kereeri am, am dé má i.

eng: Therefore, as Gods offspring, we have no need to imagine that the divine being is like a gold, silver, or stone image made by human skill and thought.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo ya nya ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam ni, má ya sunsun ka i woo­wa ti ka sika, wara jɛtɛ, wara yabuɛ nyunyumi, wara deke be bo juma si­fɔm afa bu alecira ayo i.

eng: Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and make your bed. At once he got up.
local: Na Petoro ase i ka, Aniyasi, Yesu Kristo ama u laifiɛ. Yasu na a nyanya u dasu ni. Kpeŋga na wɔ kaba ayasu.

eng: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to speak about these things again on the next Sabbath.
local: Na saŋga bo Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi sufite tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu ni, na minɛm n asere bu ka bu kaaki bra Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n bo i suba ni, na bu ba kan nyini jɔrɛm bo ba kan akere bu ni, na bu tí bekun.

eng: God overlooks ignorance of these things in times past, but now directs everyone everywhere to change their hearts and lives.
local: Dawa saŋga ni, Nyɛmɛ ayo aniɛ má minɛm fa kɔ bu sa­yoo­wam su, dama bu yo si má. Ama, kisa, wɔ ma nɔɔ ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo deke kɛrɛ kaaki i wo na i yaki i satiɛ.

eng: Everyone who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
local: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo Luda miɛ kpiri n nu, ni bo bu wo Saroni awɔrɛ n nu n kɛrɛ ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na ba kaaki bu wo na ba fa bu ŋu ama Mibiɛ ni.

eng: When the people in the synagogue were dismissed, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism accompanied Paul and Barnabas, who urged them to remain faithful to the message of Gods grace.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm ni ba sandiiri tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n ni, na Yuda­fɔm nbem, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ba kaaki bu woo­wa na bu su Yuda­fɔm atin n su ni, na bu dɔŋgu asu Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi su. Na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi atu bu fɔ, ka má bu kaaki bu sin bo sakpa n bo Nyɛmɛ ayo ama bu ni.

eng: This is because God has set a day when he intends to judge the world justly by a man he has appointed. God has given proof of this to everyone by raising him from the dead.
local: Dama wɔ du mɔ akpama cɛɛn na wɔ yi sɔnɔ be ka i bá di durunya n nu­fɔm jɔrɛ ni nahɔrɛ. Deke bo i kere ya kɛrɛ ka i bá yo sɔ n la bo i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni.

eng: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha . Her life overflowed with good works and compassionate acts on behalf of those in need.
local: Bara be yo woori miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Jopa n nu, bu fere i ka Tabita, i ti Yesu susu­fɔ ɔ, bu fere i duma Giriki aniɛ nu ka Dɔkasi, i bu la ka aja. I yo sakpa wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, na i si yaari­fɔm aŋunfɔ.

eng: On the next Sabbath, almost everyone in the city gathered to hear the Lords word.
local: Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ba juuri ni, na minɛm n dɔŋgu bo bu wo miɛ n nu n aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu na bu ti Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ni.

eng: When they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to ridicule Paul. However, others said, Well hear from you about this again.
local: Saŋga bo bu tiiri ka Pɔɔlu sukan fa kɔ yiwee teŋge n su n jɔrɛ ni, na bu nbem ayo i anzasi. Ama, na bu nbem ase ka, Ya koro ka a kaaki ba kan ahin jɔrɛ n bekun na ya ti.

eng: About that time, though, she became so ill that she died. After they washed her body, they laid her in an upstairs room.
local: Ama, na wɔ ba ada tukpaki na wɔ wu, na ba biɛ i nzue, na ba fa i ada aŋgoro sɔɔ nu.

eng: When the Jews saw the crowds, they were overcome with jealousy. They argued against what Paul was saying by slandering him.
local: Saŋga bo Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni ŋuuri jama n sɔ ni, na ba si Pɔɔlu kɔrɛ, na bu ni yiri awura akuruwa, na ba kpiɛ i nzukɔ.

eng: At that, Paul left the council.
local: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu akɔ ayaki bu.

eng: Since Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two people to Peter. They urged, Please come right away.
local: Jopa gusu waari má ni Luda, na saŋga bo Yesu susu­fɔm bo bu wo Jopa n tiiri ka Petoro wo Luda ni, na ba sunma minɛ nnyɔ i dɔ ka i yo aniɛ na i bra ndende.

eng: Speaking courageously, Paul and Barnabas said, We had to speak Gods word to you first. Since you reject it and show that you are unworthy to receive eternal life, we will turn to the Gentiles.
local: Ama, na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi ajɔjɔ ni ahoresi, bu ŋa, I ti ye ni ka ya du mɔ kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere ambɛrɛ wɔ ka. Ama, kabo am akete i na am koro má ka am nya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, ya yáki am wɔ, na ya kɔ́ kan kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

eng: Peter went with them. Upon his arrival, he was taken to the upstairs room. All the widows stood beside him, crying as they showed the tunics and other clothing Dorcas made when she was alive.
local: Nyini ti, na Petoro ayasu asu bu su na bu kɛrɛ akɔ. Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri ni, na ba fa i afun akɔ aŋgoro sɔɔ n nu, na nkuna­fɔm kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ abara ayɛ i, na bu susun, na ba fa tanlɛɛm ni tani barasu barasu bo Dɔkasi la ŋgɔɔ na i kpa maari bu n akere yiri Petoro.
