eng: Some people joined him and came to believe, including Dionysius, a member of the council on Mars Hill, a woman named Damaris, and several others.
local: Na bu nbem asu i su na ba yo yarada. Bɛrɛ la Diyonusiyɔsi, i ti Aropagɔsi­fɔm n nu kun wɔ, ni bara be bo bu fere i ka Damarisi ni, ni minɛm nbem.

eng: This is what the Lord commanded us: I have made you a light for the Gentiles, so that you could bring salvation to the end of the earth .
local: Dama nɔaniɛ maa­wa bo Mibiɛ n fa maari ya n lɛɛ. I ŋa, Ma yi u ka a yo wein ma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, adena durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ nya tii­de.

eng: Peter sent everyone out of the room, then knelt and prayed. He turned to the body and said, Tabitha, get up! She opened her eyes, saw Peter, and sat up.
local: Na Petoro afɔn bu kɛrɛ afite sɔɔ n nu, na wɔ kutu i naŋgoroma su, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ, na wɔ kaaki i nyunu awɔ fuin ni, na wɔ se i ka, Tabita, yasu. Saŋga bo i tekeeri i nyu­mbaa ni, na wɔ ŋu Petoro, na wɔ yasu atana.

eng: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the Lords word. Everyone who was appointed for eternal life believed.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm n bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm tiiri ahin nɔaniɛ n sɔ ni, na bu ahore ajɔ, na ba kansi Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ ni. Na minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ka bu nya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n ayo yarada.

eng: He gave her his hand and raised her up. Then he called Gods holy people, including the widows, and presented her alive to them.
local: Na Petoro aso i saa nu abuka i, na wɔ yasu ajina, na wɔ fere Nyɛmɛ minɛm n ni nkuna­fɔm ni kɛrɛ, na wɔ fa i akere bu ka wɔ teŋge.

eng: The news spread throughout Joppa, and many put their faith in the Lord.
local: Na nyini labari ni abara ayɛ Jopa miɛ n kɛrɛ nu, na minɛ beberebe ayo Mibiɛ n yarada.

eng: and the Lords word was broadcast throughout the entire region.
local: Na Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ n asandi abara ayɛ nyini awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: Peter stayed for some time in Joppa with a certain tanner named Simon.
local: Na Petoro awo Jopa le­nnyɔ, i yo woori jɛrɛkɛ be dika a, bu fere i ka Simon.

eng: However, the Jews provoked the prominent women among the Gentile God-worshippers, as well as the citys leaders. They instigated others to harass Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their district.
local: Ama, na Yuda­fɔm nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n awura minɛm bu sin, bɛrɛ la nbaram bo bu la wo na bu su Nyɛmɛ ni, ni nbiɛsɔm bo bu wo miɛ n nu na bu se na i bi ni. Na ba kere Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi wuwuruwa, na ba fɔn bu afite bu asiɛn n su.

eng: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from their feet and went to Iconium.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ni Banabasi akpɔnkpɔn bu jaa tuturi agɔ bu ŋu, nyini kere ka ba kete miɛ n nu­fɔm lɛɛ, na ba kɔ miɛ kpiri be bo bu fere i ka Ikoniyom n su.

eng: Because of the abundant presence of the Holy Spirit in their lives, the disciples were overflowing with happiness.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔm bo bu wo Antiyoki dɔ ni, bu ahore ajɔ agaya, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awo bu dɔ.

eng: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
local: Nyini sin, na Pɔɔlu afite Atensi miɛ kpiri n nu, na wɔ kɔ Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus. He had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul visited with them.
local: Na wɔ kɔ atu Yuda­fɔ sɔnɔ be dɔ, bu fere i ka Akuyila, i ti Pontusi asiɛn su fɔ ɔ, i ni i yi, bu fere i ka Pirisila, bu fiteeri Itali asiɛn su baari a, wɔ nya cɛ má ye Pɔɔlu ŋuuri bu n a, dama Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Kalodisi n ama nɔɔ ka Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ fite i asiɛn n su. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Pɔɔlu akɔ ka i kɔ niɛ bu busu.

eng: Because they practiced the same trade, he stayed and worked with them. They all worked with leather.
local: na wɔ kɔ aseke bu wɔfɔ, na i ni bɛrɛ asaŋga ayo juma, dama bu kɛrɛ ti jɛrɛkɛm ni tani wu­fɔm wɔ.

eng: Every Sabbath he interacted with people in the synagogue, trying to convince both Jews and Greeks.
local: Na Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn kɛrɛ, i suwura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na i subo anzama ni bɛrɛ, ni Giriki­fɔm kɛrɛ, adena i kaaki bu sunsunni na bu yo yarada.

eng: Once Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself fully to the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Saŋga bo Silasi ni Timoti fiteeri Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu ba juuri ni, Pɔɔlu nyunu aboro ni labari kpa n jɔrɛ kan su ŋgumi wɔ. Na i sukere Yuda­fɔm n ka, Yesu ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: When they opposed and slandered him, he shook the dust from his clothes in protest and said to them, You are responsible for your own fates! Im innocent! From now on Ill go to the Gentiles.
local: Ama, Yuda­fɔm n keteeri i nɔaniɛ n wɔ, na ba kpiɛ i nzukɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ kpɔnkpɔn i sutura wuraa­wa tuturi agɔ bu ŋu, na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ, nzɛn am nya má tii­de, i ti am dɛɛ jɔrɛ wɔ, má m dɛɛ. Fite kisa fa kɔ, m kɔ́ minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n dɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: He left the synagogue and went next door to the home of Titius Justus, a Gentile God-worshipper.
local: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu afite tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu ayaki bu, na wɔ kɔ sɔnɔ be dika, i ti má Yuda­fɔ, ama, i su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, bu fere i ka, Titosi Jusitosi, i dika n mata tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n wɔ.

eng: Crispus, the synagogue leader, and his entire household came to believe in the Lord. Many Corinthians believed and were baptized after listening to Paul.
local: Na sɔnɔ n be bo bu fere i ka Kirisipusi na i ti bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n kpiɛn n ayo Mibiɛ n yarada, i ni i dika­fɔm kɛrɛ, na Kɔrinto­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu tiiri labari n bo Pɔɔlu kaanri i ni, na bu kɛrɛ ayo yarada, na ba biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue.

eng: One night the Lord said to Paul in a vision, Dont be afraid. Continue speaking. Dont be silent.
local: Cɛɛn n be kɔŋguɛ sɔ ɔ, na Mibiɛ n ajɔjɔ Pɔɔlu dɔ ka alɛlɛ nu dɛɛ su, i ŋa, Má a soro sɛrɛ, ama, kɔ su na a sukan labari kpa n kere bu, má a mɔ u nɔɔ.

eng: Im with you and no one who attacks you will harm you, for I have many people in this city.
local: dama m wo u dɔ. Be koro fa má i saa kan má u na i yo u pui. Niɛ fɛn, m la minɛ beberebe miɛ n nu fɛn bo bu ti m dɛɛ.

eng: So he stayed there for eighteen months, teaching Gods word among them.
local: Na wɔ wo bu dɔ akɔ aju afɔ kun ni kpaaki nu, na i sukere bu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Now when Gallio was the governor of the province of Achaia, the Jews united in their opposition against Paul and brought him before the court.
local: Tɔ, bu wo bɛrɛ na sɔnɔ n be bo bu fere i ka Galiyo n aba adi Akaya awɔrɛ n nu gomina. Nyini saŋga n na Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ nɔɔ ayo kun, na ba kete Pɔɔlu ŋu na ba fa i akɔ jɔrɛ dii­biri.

eng: This man is persuading others to worship God unlawfully, they declared.
local: Na ba se ka, Ahin sɔnɔ ni suboro i nyi sukere minɛm keree­wa bo i wo i ŋgumi ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni.

eng: Just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, If there had been some sort of injury or criminal behavior, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
local: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu tekeeri i nɔɔ ka i jɔjɔ ni, na Gomina Galiyo ase Yuda­fɔm ka, Nzɛn má i ti ka wɔ yo satiɛ be, wara wɔ do ŋgasi be, ye am sujaraki i a, má m ti i bu ma am.

eng: However, since these are squabbles about a message, names, and your own Law, deal with them yourselves. I have no desire to sit in judgment over such things.
local: Ama, kabo i nya ti bisaa­wam fa kɔ nɔaniɛm, ni dumam, ni am dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wam n su ni, ambɛrɛ ni am tii kɔ yo i dɔŋgu. Mini, m koro di má nyini jɔrɛ ni.

eng: He expelled them from the court.
local: Na wɔ ma ba fɔn ayi bu afite jɔrɛ dii­biri sɔɔ n nu.

eng: but everyone seized Sosthenes, the synagogue leader, and gave him a beating in the presence of the governor. None of this mattered to Gallio.
local: Na bu kɛrɛ akaba atɔ sɔnɔ n bo bu fere i ka Sositeni n ŋu na bu subo i wɔ, jɔrɛ dii­biri n nyunu bɛrɛ, yiri ti tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ nkpiɛn a. Ama, Gomina Galiyo cɛcɛ awo má nyini n be nu.

eng: After Paul stayed in Corinth for some time, he said good-bye to the brothers and sisters. At the Corinthian seaport of Cenchreae he had his head shaved, since he had made a solemn promise. Then, accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila, he sailed away to Syria.
local: Na Pɔɔlu awo Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu acɛ, nyini sin na wɔ kaari yarada­yo­fɔm ni na i ni Pirisila ni Akuyila akɔ. Bu kɔ juuri miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Kenciriya n su ni, na Pɔɔlu ayi i tii dɔ, dama wɔ du mɔ atan ndiɛ n dɛɛ ti. Na ba wura lee bambaka be nu dɔ, i sukɔ Siriya awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: After they arrived in Ephesus, he left Priscilla and Aquila and entered the synagogue and interacted with the Jews.
local: Bu kɔ juuri Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na wɔ yaki Pirisila ni Akuyila dɔ, ama, na yiri akɔ awura Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na i ni bɛrɛ abo anzama fa kɔ labari kpa n su.

eng: They asked him to stay longer, but he declined.
local: Na ba sere i ka i wo bu dɔ le­nnyɔ, ama, wɔ soro má su.

eng: As he said farewell to them, though, he added, God willing, I will return. Then he sailed off from Ephesus.
local: Na wɔ sere bu atin na wɔ kaari bu, na wɔ se bu ka, Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ maari m atin, m kpíe m nyi ba am dɔ. Na wɔ yasu Ɛfɛso dɔ, na wɔ fa lee bambaka akɔ.

eng: He arrived in Caesarea, went up to Jerusalem and greeted the church, and then went down to Antioch.
local: Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na wɔ sin akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu akɔ ayo Kristo jama n ansɛ, na wɔ nya kɔ Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: After some time there he left and traveled from place to place in the region of Galatia and the district of Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples.
local: Na wɔ yo le­nnyɔ dɔ, na wɔ nya akaaki ayasu dɔ, abara ayɛ miɛ miɛm bo bu wo Galatiya, ni Firigiya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu n kɛrɛ, na wɔ ma yarada­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ ahoresi.

eng: Meanwhile, a certain Jew named Apollos arrived in Ephesus. He was a native of Alexandria and was well-educated and effective in his use of the scriptures.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, Yuda­fɔ sɔnɔ be aba Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu, bu fere i ka Apolosi, bu wuuri i Alɛkizanderiya miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ. I yo la si fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n su, na i koro jɔjɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord and spoke as one stirred up by the Spirit. He taught accurately the things about Jesus, even though he was aware only of the baptism John proclaimed and practiced.
local: Ba du mɔ akere i keree­wam fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin su, na i ahore abi na i ŋuniɛ ti nyakanyaka, na i sukan na i kere minɛm Yesu jɔrɛ nahɔrɛ su. Ama, i si má nzue biɛɛ­wa be, má ka Yohane dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue biɛɛ­wa ni ŋgumi.

eng: He began speaking with confidence in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they received him into their circle of friends and explained to him Gods way more accurately.
local: Na wɔ bo i bu na i sukan i ni ahoresi Yuda­fɔm tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu. Saŋga bo Pirisila ni Akuyila tiiri i keree­wam n sɔ ni, na ba fa i akɔ awuru, na ba kere i Nyɛmɛ atin n su jɔrɛ nyunyumi abuka su.

eng: When he wanted to travel to Achaia, the brothers and sisters encouraged him and wrote to the disciples so they would open their homes to him. Once he arrived, he was of great help to those who had come to believe through grace.
local: Nyini sin, na Apolosi akoro ka i kɔ Akaya awɔrɛ n nu, na yarada­yo­fɔm ni bo bu wo Ɛfɛso n ama i ahoresi, na ba kɛrɛ kadasi ama bu ninbaam bo bu wo Akaya n ka bu de i wɔfɔ nyunyumi. Saŋga bo i kɔ juuri dɔ ni, na wɔ buka minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n nu na ba yo yarada ni.

eng: He would vigorously defeat Jewish arguments in public debate, using the scriptures to prove that Jesus was the Christ.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm seeri akuruwa n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ fa Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n na wɔ kan ayi bu fieo fieo jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, ka, Yesu ti Kristo n a, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni.

eng: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul took a route through the interior and came to Ephesus, where he found some disciples.
local: Saŋga bo Apolosi toko wo Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na Pɔɔlu anati abara ayɛ dɔ dɛɛ ahɛmɛ miɛ miɛm n kɛrɛ su, haari na wɔ kɔ aju Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu. Dɔ na wɔ kɔ atu Yesu susu­fɔ nbem.

eng: He asked them, Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you came to believe? They replied, Weve not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.
local: na wɔ bisa bu ka, Saŋga bo am yoori yarada ni, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura am ŋu? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Ai, ya nya ti má ka Awiɛwiɛ Casi wo bɛrɛ mmɔɔ.

eng: Then he said, What baptism did you receive, then? They answered, Johns baptism.
local: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu abisa bu ka, Ye nzu Nyɛmɛ nzue ye bu biɛɛri am a? Na ba bɔ i nu ka, Bu biɛɛri ya Yohane dɛɛ nzue biɛɛ­wa n wɔ.

eng: Paul explained, John baptized with a baptism by which people showed they were changing their hearts and lives. It was a baptism that told people about the one who was coming after him. This is the one in whom they were to believe. This one is Jesus.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ase bu ka, Yohane nzue biɛɛ­wa n bu la ka, minɛ kaaki bu wo na bu yaki bu satiɛ wɔ. Na wɔ se minɛm n ka bu yo sɔnɔ n bo i wo i sin suba n yarada, yiri la Yesu.

eng: After they listened to Paul, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Bu tiiri nyini n sɔ wieeri ni, na ba biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue ya Mibiɛ Yesu n dɛɛ duma nu.

eng: When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they began speaking in other languages and prophesying.
local: Na Pɔɔlu afa i saa anana bu ŋu, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura bu ŋu. Na ba jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu nbem, na ba kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ akere minɛm.

eng: Altogether, there were about twelve people.
local: Bu kɛrɛ kɔ yasuuri nbiɛsɔm buru ni nnyɔ ɔ.

eng: Paul went to the synagogue and spoke confidently for the next three months. He interacted with those present and offered convincing arguments concerning the nature of Gods kingdom.
local: Na wɔ wo dɔ haari sara nsan, na Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn kɛrɛ, na i suwura bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na i sukan kere bu fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n su ni ahoresi, na i ni bɛrɛ subo anzama, na wɔ boro i nyi akere bu ka, kabo i kan n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: Some people had closed their minds, though. They refused to believe and publicly slandered the Way. As a result, Paul left them, took the disciples with him, and continued his daily interactions in Tyrannus lecture hall.
local: Ama, na bu nbem afa ahore­mbaa kekereke, na ba kete ka bu yo má yarada. Na ba jɔjɔ jɔrɛ tiɛ fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su, jama n kɛrɛ nyunu. Nyini ti, na Pɔɔlu ayaki bu, na wɔ fa yarada­yo­fɔm n afite akɔ Tiranusi tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ bambaka n nu, na i ni bɛrɛ sutiɛn bu nɔɔ subo anzama, fa kɔ Yesu dɛɛ atin n su cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn.

eng: This went on for two years, so that everyone living in the province of Asia—both Jews and Greeks—heard the Lords word.
local: Pɔɔlu bitaari i sɔ ɔ, haari afɔ nnyɔ, na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu n ati Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ ni, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ.

eng: God was doing unusual miracles through Paul.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ ama Pɔɔlu ayo alekutura sam bo bu la má ŋusu.

eng: Even the small towels and aprons that had touched his skin were taken to the sick, and their diseases were cured and the evil spirits left them.
local: Haari na sanvuinm ni suturam kɛrɛ bo ba kan i ŋuniɛ, na bu fa kɔ nana tukpaki­fɔm ŋu, bu nya laifiɛ wɔ, na ajinim fite yaki minɛm bo bu wo bu ŋu ni.

eng: There were some Jews who traveled around throwing out evil spirits. They tried to use the power of the name of the Lord Jesus against some people with evil spirits. They said, In the name of the Jesus whom Paul preaches, I command you.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm nbem bo bɛrɛ gusu nati jeŋgbe fɔn yi ajinim fite minɛm ŋu ni, aboro bu nyi ka bu fa Yesu duma n fɔn yi ajinim. Bu ŋa, Yesu n bo Pɔɔlu kan i jɔrɛ n dɛɛ duma nu, ye m se am ka am fite yaki sɔnɔ n a.

eng: The seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, were doing this.
local: Yuda­fɔm minɛ nso nbem nati yo nyini sam n a. Bɛrɛ la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn be bo bu fere i ka Sikeva n dɛɛ nbaa­biɛsɔm ni.

eng: The evil spirit replied, I know Jesus and Im familiar with Paul, but who are you.
local: Ama, na ajini n ase bu ka, M si Yesu, na m kaaki si Pɔɔlu, ama, ye ambɛrɛ la ŋma n wɔ.

eng: The person who had an evil spirit jumped on them and overpowered them all with such force that they ran out of that house naked and wounded.
local: Na sɔnɔ n bo ajini n wo i ŋu ni, atɔ bu ŋu na wɔ kunma bu kɛrɛ faŋga, na wɔ bo bu na wɔ ma bu mandim, na ba ŋmati afite i dika n nu bu bu ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: This became known to the Jews and Greeks living in Ephesus. Everyone was seized with fear and they held the name of the Lord Jesus in the highest regard.
local: Saŋga bo Yuda­fɔm ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu n kɛrɛ tiiri nyini sa n sɔ ni, na sɛrɛ ati bu, na ba kansi Mibiɛ Yesu n duma agaya.

eng: Many of those who had come to believe came, confessing their past practices.
local: Na minɛ dɔŋgu bo ba yo yarada n afite ajina, na ba kan bu ŋgasim ni bu satiɛm kɛrɛ bo ba yo bu n akere jama ni.

eng: This included a number of people who practiced sorcery. They collected their sorcery texts and burned them publicly. The value of those materials was calculated at more than someone might make if they worked for one hundred sixty-five years.
local: Na minɛ dɔŋgu bo bu nati yo boboyaya juma n afa bu boboyaya kadasim ni, na ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, na ba yara bu kɛrɛ. Kadasim n kɛrɛ bo bu yaraari bu ni, i ŋwaa sunnu agaya, bu buuri i akunta, i kɛrɛ kɔ yasuuri bu dɛɛ jɛtɛ ŋwaa akpii aburu­nu wɔ.

eng: In this way the Lords word grew abundantly and strengthened powerfully.
local: Nyini n dɛɛ ti, na minɛ beberebe ati Mibiɛ n nɔaniɛ ni, na wɔ sandi abara yɛ ni faŋga.

eng: Once these things had come to an end, Paul, guided by the Spirit, decided to return to Jerusalem, taking a route that would carry him through the provinces of Macedonia and Achaia. He said, After I have been there, I must visit Rome as well.
local: Nyini n kɛrɛ sin, na Pɔɔlu afa sunsunni ka i kɔ Makɛdoniya ni Akaya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu, na i nya sin kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu. Na wɔ se ka, Nzɛn m nya ŋuuri m natiiri n kɛrɛ nyaari, wɔ yo daka ka m kɔ́ Roma miɛ kpiri n nu gusu.

eng: He sent two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, while he remained awhile in the province of Asia.
local: Na wɔ sunma i buka­fɔ nnyɔm ni, bɛrɛ la Timoti ni Irasitusi ka bu kɔ Makɛdoniya, na yiri aka Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, ayo le­nnyɔ.

eng: At that time a great disturbance erupted about the Way.
local: Nyini saŋga n ye Mibiɛ n atin n su su n ayo jɔrɛ agaya Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu n a.

eng: There was a silversmith named Demetrius. He made silver models of Artemis temple, and his business generated a lot of profit for the craftspeople.
local: Jɛtɛ tunmi be yo wo dɔ, bu fere i ka Demetirɔsi, i fa jɛtɛ wɔ, na i fa yo sɔɔ kan kanm, bu ti ka amɔɛ n bo bu fere i ka Atɛmisi n wɔ, na i sufa tɔ ma minɛm. Na minɛm bo bu yo nyini juma n anya kpɛrɛ beberebe afite nu.

eng: He called a meeting with these craftspeople and others working in related trades and said, Friends, you know that we make an easy living from this business.
local: Tɔ, na yiri Demetirɔsi afere minɛm bo bu yo nyini juma atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, Njam, am si ka ya nya ya dɛɛ ajɛkɛ kɛrɛ fite ahin juma n bo ya yo i n nu wɔ e.

eng: And you can see and hear that this Paul has convinced and misled a lot of people, not only in Ephesus but also throughout most of the province of Asia. He says that gods made by human hands arent really gods.
local: Yiri Pɔɔlu ni, kisa, am aŋu i e, am ati kabo wɔ koro akan ateke minɛ beberebe nyi ni, má bo bu wo Ɛfɛso fɛn ŋgumi, ama, ni bo bu wo Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu kɛrɛ, i ŋa amɔɛm bo adamandem yo bu n ti má nyɛmɛ ni.

eng: This poses a danger not only by discrediting our trade but also by completely dishonoring the great goddess Artemis. The whole province of Asia—indeed, the entire civilized world—worships her, but her splendor will soon be extinguished.
local: Nyini n bo i wo su ni, nzɛn ya yakiiri na i yó sɔ ɔ, ya juma n bo ya yo i ni, i ká ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. Má nyini ŋgumi, ama, minɛm fá má ya amɔɛ kpiri Atɛmisi sɔɔ n yo má pui, dama yiri Atɛmisi n ye minɛm bo bu wo Esiya awɔrɛ n nu ni durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ su yiri wɔ. I nya yoori sɔ, Atɛmisi fún nyini jirima n wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Once they heard this, they were beside themselves with anger and began to shout, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians.
local: Jama n tiiri ahin jɔjɔɔ­wa n sɔ ni, na yaa ati bu, na ba tiɛn kpa, bu ŋa, Yɛrɛ Ɛfɛso­fɔm dɛɛ amɔɛ Atɛmisi ni, yiri ti yiko­fɔ a.

eng: The city was thrown into turmoil. They rushed as one into the theater. They seized Gaius and Aristarchus, Pauls traveling companions from the province of Macedonia.
local: Na miɛ n abo nu, na minɛm suyo naŋmiɛ. Na jama n akaba akɔ atara Gayosi ni Arisitakusi, bɛrɛ bo bu suuri Pɔɔlu su fiteeri Makɛdoniya baari ni. Na ba cin bu akɔ tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ fofoe paa n nu.

eng: Paul wanted to appear before the assembly, but the disciples wouldnt allow him.
local: Má Pɔɔlu ni i tii koroori ka i kɔ deke bo jama n atiɛn bu nɔɔ n wɔ, na i jɔjɔ bu dɔ, ama, yarada­yo­fɔm n asoro má i su.

eng: Even some officials of the province of Asia, who were Pauls friends, sent word to him, urging him not to risk going into the theater.
local: Haari na nyini awɔrɛ n nu yiko­fɔm bo bu ti i danfom n asunma asere i jande ka má i kɔ tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ fofoe n nu.

eng: Meanwhile, the assembly was in a state of confusion. Some shouted one thing, others shouted something else, and most of the crowd didnt know why they had gathered.
local: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ ni, bu nyunu ata, na jama n sutiɛntiɛn, suyo naŋmiɛ, nbem ŋa sɔ, nbem gusu ŋa ahin, ama, bu dɔŋgu asi má deke n bo i ti na ba ba atiɛn bu nɔɔ ni.

eng: The Jews sent Alexander to the front, and some of the crowd directed their words toward him. He gestured that he wanted to offer a defense before the assembly.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm asun sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Alɛkizanda n adu jama n nyunu, nyini dɛɛ ti, minɛ nbem sunsun ka yiri faari nyini sam n kɛrɛ baari a. Dɔ na wɔ yɛ i saa su ka jama n yaki naŋmiɛ, na i sukan na minɛm si deke bo i wo nu.

eng: but when they realized he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, Great is Artemis of the Ephesians! This continued for about two hours.
local: Ama, saŋga bo bu faari nu ka i ti Yuda­fɔ ni, na bu kɛrɛ atiɛn kungere­kun, bu ŋa, Yɛrɛ Ɛfɛso­fɔm dɛɛ amɔɛ Atɛmisi ni, yiri ti yiko­fɔ a. Ba tiɛntiɛn sɔ ɔ acɛ haari saŋga su.

eng: The city manager brought order to the crowd and said, People of Ephesus, doesnt everyone know that the city of Ephesus is guardian of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image, which fell from heaven?.
local: Na sɔnɔ be bo i ti bu miɛ n nu kadasi kɛrɛ­fɔ n aba bu dɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, Am yaki naŋmiɛ. Dɔ na wɔ nya se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ Ɛfɛso­fɔm, am ti fɛn, ŋma wo bɛrɛ bo i si má ka Ɛfɛso­fɔm niɛ Atɛmisi amɔɛ kpiri sɔɔ n ni yabuɛ n bo i fiteeri aŋgoro tɔɔri n su a.

eng: Therefore, since these facts are undeniable, you must calm down. Dont be reckless.
local: Sɔnɔ be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro se ka ahin jɔrɛ n ti má nahɔrɛ. Nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka am tara am ŋu, má am koro nde yo sa be bo i kunma am.

eng: The men you brought here have neither robbed the temple nor slandered our goddess.
local: Ahin minɛm bo am afa bu aba fɛn ni, ba wɔ má puitiri­pui afite má amɔɛ sɔɔ n nu, ba kaaki akpiɛ má nzukɔ be ajaraki má ya amɔɛ ni.

eng: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftspeople with him have a charge against anyone, the courts are in session and governors are available. They can press charges against each other there.
local: Nzɛn Demetirɔsi ni i beŋgu juma­yo­fɔm ni, la jaraki fa kɔ bu be dɔ, jɔrɛ­di­fɔm ni gomina nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm wo bɛrɛ, bu koro jaraki nyini minɛm bu dɔ.

eng: Additional disputes can be resolved in a legal assembly.
local: Ama, nzɛn sa be wo bɛrɛ buka su na am koro ka ya yo i dɔŋgu a, am fa bra saŋga bo miɛ n nu jama n kɛrɛ la bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ ni.

eng: As for us, we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since we cant justify this unruly gathering.
local: Naŋmiɛ n bo am ayo i nyuma ni, i la má kpɛrɛ, i koro fa ya wura jɔrɛ nu, dama ya koro kan má deke n bo i ti na am ayo sɔ ni.

eng: After he said this, he dismissed the assembly.
local: I jɔjɔɔri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ ma jama n asandi.

eng: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, said good-bye, and left for the province of Macedonia.
local: Minɛm ni yoori naŋmiɛ n wieeri ni, na Pɔɔlu afere yarada­yo­fɔm n atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na wɔ ma bu ahoresi, na wɔ kaari bu, na wɔ kɔ Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: He traveled through that region with a message of encouragement. When he came to Greece.
local: Na wɔ fa nyini awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu, na wɔ jɔjɔ yarada­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ dɔ, na wɔ ma bu ahoresi, na wɔ fa dɔ akɔ aju Giriki­fɔm dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: he stayed for three months. Because the Jews hatched a plot against Paul as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided instead to return through Macedonia.
local: na wɔ wo dɔ haari sara nsan. Na saŋga bo i wo sɛsɛ­su su ka i kɔ Siriya awɔrɛ n nu ni, na wɔ ti ka Yuda­fɔm akpa bu ŋu akɔ aŋminda i atin nu, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ yo i sunsunni ka i kpie i nyi fa Makɛdoniya a.

eng: He was accompanied by Sopater, Pyrrhus son from Beroea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
local: Na minɛ nbem asu i su akɔ, bɛrɛ la Sopateri bo bu fere i si ka Pirihusi na i fite Bɛriya miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Arisitakusi, ni Sekundosi bo bu fite Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Gayosi bo i fite Dɛrɛbi miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Tikikusi, ni Torofimosi bo bu fite Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu ni, ni Timoti.

eng: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
local: Na bɛrɛ kɛrɛ adu mɔ akɔ aŋminda ya Tɔrasi miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: We sailed from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread and met them five days later in Troas, where we stayed for a week.
local: Na yɛrɛ aka Filipiya miɛ kpiri n nu, haari na Yuda­fɔm aba adi bu cɛɛn­kpiri n asin, bo bu di i ni kpɔnɔ na bu gɔ má ayiri nu na i yasu ni, na ya nya afa lee bambaka, na i le­nnu cɛɛn na ya kɔ atu bu Tɔrasi, na ya wo dɔ akɔ aju le­so.

eng: On the first day of the week, as we gathered together for a meal, Paul was holding a discussion with them. Since he was leaving the next day, he continued talking until midnight.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm bakuɛ n bumboori, mɔnɛ cɛɛn, na ya kɛrɛ atiɛn ya nɔɔ ka ya saŋga di diire. Na Pɔɔlu ajɔjɔ minɛm dɔ, haari kɔŋguɛ jinanjin, dama i koroori ka nyini cɛɛn alecin i kaari bu wɔ, na i kɔ yaki bu.

eng: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we had gathered.
local: Ya tiɛɛnri ya nɔɔ aŋgoro sɔɔ be nu wɔ, fitanam sunnu nu dɔ.

eng: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in the window. He was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell from the third floor and died.
local: Na gbɛfɛnɛ be, bu fere i ka Itikusi, na wɔ fun atana sɔɔ aŋgoro nsan n tukoro n su, na daafiri ati i. Pɔɔlu jɔjɔ cɛɛri na wɔ daafi, na wɔ fite aŋgoro aba atɔ asiɛŋgu. Bu jura kɔ maari i su ni, nyini saŋga wɔ wu.

eng: Paul went down, fell on him and embraced him, then said, Dont be alarmed. Hes alive.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ajura akɔ asiɛŋgu, na wɔ fun abutu i ŋu, na wɔ fa i saa abara ayɛ i, na wɔ se bu ka, Má am ma sɛrɛ ti am, dama i la ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Then Paul went back upstairs and ate. He talked for a long time—right up until daybreak—then he left.
local: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu akpie i nyi afun akɔ aŋgoro sɔɔ n nu dɔ, na i ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ asaŋga adi diire, na ba bo anzama, haari ale akɔ acin, na wɔ kaari bu na wɔ kɔ.

eng: They took the young man away alive, and they were greatly comforted.
local: Na ba nya afa gbɛfɛnɛ n akɔ awuru ni laifiɛ, na bu ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su.

eng: We went on to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we intended to take Paul on board. Paul had arranged this, since he intended to make his way there by land.
local: Na Pɔɔlu afa sɛsɛ­su ka yɛrɛ fa lee bambaka du mɔ ɔ, na yiri nati na ya ni yiri kɔ saa nu Asosi miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
local: Saŋga bo i ba tuuri ya dɔ ni, na wɔ wura lee n nu, na ya ni yiri kɛrɛ akɔ miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Mitulene n su.

eng: The next day we sailed from there and arrived opposite Chios. On the day after, we sailed to Samos, and on the following day we came to Miletus.
local: Na ya yasu dɔ, i alecin, na ya kɔ dodo lui bambaka n bo bu fere i ka Kiyɔsi n su. Na i alecin bekun, na ya kɔ aju lui bambaka nyiŋga n bo bu fere i ka Samosi, na i alecin fɔfɔrɛ, na ya kɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Miletosi n su.

eng: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he wouldnt need to spend too much time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by Pentecost Day.
local: Na Pɔɔlu aboro i nyi ka i ju Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ka, na bu di Pɛntɛkoti cɛɛn­kpiri ni, nyini ti, wɔ koro má ka i cɛ Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ nu, na wɔ san asin Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n dɛɛ bue ni.

eng: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus calling for the churchs elders to meet him.
local: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu wo Miletosi miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na wɔ sunma afere Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm bo bu wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu n ka bu ma bu ni yiri yɛ.

eng: When they arrived, he said to them, You know how I lived among you the whole time I was with you, beginning with the first day I arrived in the province of Asia.
local: Saŋga bo bu ba juuri i dɔ ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Ambɛrɛ ni am tii si kabo m woo­wa ti barasu, saŋga kɛrɛ bo m woori am dɔ ni, fite cɛɛn gbingbin n bo m ba juuri Esiya awɔrɛ n nu ni.

eng: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears in the midst of trials that came upon me because of the Jews schemes.
local: M yoori m ŋu kaan wɔ, na ma yo Mibiɛ n juma n ni nyunvuin. Haari ni bo Yuda­fɔm sɛsɛɛri su ka bu kere m wuwuruwa barasu n kɛrɛ ni, m mindi deeri i wɔ.

eng: You know I held back nothing that would be helpful so that I could proclaim to you and teach you both publicly and privately in your homes.
local: Am si ka deke kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa na i buka am ni, m kaanri i kereeri am wɔ jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, ma fa afiɛ má am su, na m sunati awuru awurum n kɛrɛ nu na m sukere am.

eng: You know I have testified to both Jews and Greeks that they must change their hearts and lives as they turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
local: M kan kereeri Yuda­fɔm ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ wɔ, ka bu kaaki bu wo fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na bu yo ya Mibiɛ Yesu n yarada.

eng: Now, compelled by the Spirit, Im going to Jerusalem. I dont know what will happen to me there.
local: Tɔ, kisa Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ma m sukɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu n a, ama, m gusu si má deke bo i kɔ́ yo m dɔ.

eng: What I do know is that the Holy Spirit testifies to me from city to city that prisons and troubles await me.
local: Deke bo m si i n la ka, miɛ kɛrɛ bo m kɔ su ni, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n se m ka bu kére m wuwuruwa, na bu nyí m dan­saraka.

eng: But nothing, not even my life, is more important than my completing my mission. This is nothing other than the ministry I received from the Lord Jesus: to testify about the good news of Gods grace.
local: Ama, m fa má m tiɛŋgu ŋgɔɔ yo má pui. Deke bo i kpiɛ m daafiri n kɛrɛ la ka, má ka ma yo juma n bo Mibiɛ Yesu n afa ama m n awie. Yiri la ka, m kan labari kpa n fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama minɛm ni.

eng: I know that none of you will see me again—you among whom I traveled and proclaimed the kingdom.
local: Kisa m si ka am be mmɔɔ ŋú má m kun, ambɛrɛ bo m yo nati am nu kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ fa kɔ i fɛmɛya n su kere am ni.

eng: Therefore, today I testify to you that Im not responsible for anyones fate.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ nyuma, ka wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a kaari ŋgbɛɛn, i ti má m jɔrɛ.

eng: I havent avoided proclaiming the entire plan of God to you.
local: Dama deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koroori ka am yo i ni, ma fa afiɛ má am su, ma kan i akere am.

eng: Watch yourselves and the whole flock, in which the Holy Spirit has placed you as supervisors, to shepherd Gods church, which he obtained with the death of his own Son.
local: Nyini ti, am bita am ŋu nyunyumi, na am kaaki niɛ Kristo jama minɛm kɛrɛ su, bɛrɛ bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa ama am ka am niɛ bu dika ni, kabo bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ niɛ i nbɔɛm su ni. Nyini minɛm ye Nyɛmɛ faari i tiɛŋgu Wa mbunja deeri bu jiiri n a.

eng: I know that, after my departure, savage wolves will come in among you and wont spare the flock.
local: M si tenle su ka m kɔ yakiiri am, minɛ tiɛm ba wura am nu, kabo bedi wura nbɔɛm nu sandi bu n wɔ.

eng: Some of your own people will distort the word in order to lure followers after them.
local: Haari na am dɛɛ minɛm nbem bo bu wo am nu fɛn ni, bu bá kaaki nahɔrɛ ni, na bu dádaka yarada­yo­fɔm nbem na bu su bu dɛɛ atin n su.

eng: Stay alert! Remember that for three years I constantly and tearfully warned each one of you. I never stopped warning you!.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am kpa am ŋu, na am teŋge su ka afɔ nsan lɛɛ bo m nati bɔbɔ am ni nyunvuin, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu.

eng: Now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all whom God has made holy.
local: Kisa, m sufa am wura Nyɛmɛ saa nu wɔ lɛɛ, na i niɛ am dika, na m kaaki fa am wura i dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n bo i ma am ahɔrɔma nu, na i buka am na i má am nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i tiɛŋgu minɛm kɛrɛ ni.

eng: I havent craved anyones silver, gold, or clothing.
local: M konviki awo má be ŋwaa, wara sutura nu.

eng: You yourselves know that I have provided for my own needs and for those of my companions with my own hands.
local: Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si ka m yo faari m saa yoori m juma a, ye m fa bukaari m ŋu ni m lamɔkɔm n kɛrɛ a.

eng: In everything I have shown you that, by working hard, we must help the weak. In this way we remember the Lord Jesus words: It is more blessed to give than to receive.
local: Kabo ma yo juma n sɔ ni, m sukere am ka am gusu yo i sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, na am buka yaari­fɔm, na am teŋge su kabo ya Mibiɛ Yesu n ni i tii seeri ni. I ŋa, Tere­fiɛ wo sɔnɔ bo i ce aceeri n dɔ tara sɔnɔ bo i de aceeri ni.

eng: After he said these things, he knelt down with all of them to pray.
local: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu jɔjɔ wieeri ni, na i ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ akutu bu jaa naŋgoroma su, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: They cried uncontrollably as everyone embraced and kissed Paul.
local: Na bu kɛrɛ asun, na ba fa bu saa abara ayɛ i, na ba biti i.

eng: They were especially grieved by his statement that they would never see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
local: Na bu ahore asaaki agaya, dama i seeri bu ka bu ŋú má i kun. Na ba se i atin nu, na wɔ kɔ awura lee bambaka n nu.

eng: After we tore ourselves away from them, we set sail on a straight course to Cos, reaching Rhodes the next day, and then Patara.
local: Na ya kaari bu, na ya fa lee bambaka, na ya wɔ ya tii cein akɔ aju miɛ n bo i wo lui bambaka n su na bu fere i ka Kosi ni. Na i alecin na ya kɔ aju awɔrɛ n bo bu fere i ka Rodesi ni, na ya fite dɔ, na ya kɔ Patara miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, boarded, and put out to sea.
local: Dɔ na ya ŋu lee bambaka be ka i sukɔ Fonisiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na ya wura i akɔ.

eng: We spotted Cyprus, but passed by it on our left. We sailed on to the province of Syria and landed in Tyre, where the ship was to unload its cargo.
local: Ya sukɔ ni, na ya ŋu Sipurusi lui bambaka ni, na ya yaki ya saa biɛ su, na ya sin akɔ Siriya awɔrɛ n nu. Na ya nya akɔ ajina Tiro miɛ kpiri n nu, dɔ ye bu yi bu sɔɔri n lee n nu a.

eng: We found the disciples there and stayed with them for a week. Compelled by the Spirit, they kept telling Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
local: Na ya kɔ aniɛniɛ Yesu susu­fɔm busu, na ya wo bu dɔ le­so. Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama ba se Pɔɔlu ka má i kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: When our time had come to an end, we departed. All of them, including women and children, accompanied us out of town where we knelt on the beach and prayed.
local: Ama, ya yasu cɛɛn n ba juuri ni, na ya fite ka ya kɔ, na bu ni bu yim, ni bu nbaam ase ya atin nu afite miɛ n nu akɔ tieku n nɔɔ su. Dɔ na ya ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ akutu ya jaa naŋgoroma su, na ya sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: We said good-bye to each other, then we boarded the ship and they returned to their homes.
local: Nyini sin, na ya kaari ya beŋgu, na yɛrɛ awura lee bambaka n nu, na bɛrɛ gusu akpie bu nyi akɔ awuru.

eng: Continuing our voyage, we sailed from Tyre and arrived in Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and sisters there and spent a day with them.
local: Na ya fite Tiro akɔ aju miɛ kpiri n nu, na ya kɔ aniɛ yarada­yo­fɔm ni busu, na ya da bu dɔ le­kun.

eng: The next day we left and came to Caesarea. We went to the house of Philip the evangelist, one of the Seven, and stayed with him.
local: I alecin fɔfɔrɛ na ya fite bɛrɛ akɔ aju Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu. Dɔ na ya kɔ awo sɔnɔ be dika, bu fere i ka Filipu, i ti Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kan­fɔ ɔ. Yiri bukaari minɛ nsom bo bu yo yiiri bu ka bu buka Kristo jama n bo bu wo Yɛrusalem n a.

eng: He had four unmarried daughters who were involved in the work of prophecy.
local: I yo la nbaa­baram nna a, bu be nya ja má. Bɛrɛ nati yo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ juma n a.

eng: After staying there for several days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
local: Ya woori dɔ yoori le­nnyɔ, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ be aba dɔ, bu fere i ka Agabusi, i fite Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu wɔ.

eng: He came to us, took Pauls belt, tied his own feet and hands, and said, This is what the Holy Spirit says: In Jerusalem the Jews will bind the man who owns this belt, and they will hand him over to the Gentiles.
local: Na wɔ ba ya dɔ, na wɔ fa Pɔɔlu dambara, acici i dɛɛ jaam ni i saam. Na wɔ nya ase ka, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ŋa o, kabo Yuda­fɔm bo bu wo Yɛrusalem ni cíci ahin dambara n fɔ lɛɛ, na bu nyá fa i ma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

eng: When we heard this, we and the local believers urged Paul not to go up to Jerusalem.
local: Saŋga bo ya tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na ya ni minɛm n bo bu wo dɔ n asere Pɔɔlu jande ka má i kɔ Yɛrusalem.

eng: Paul replied, Why are you doing this? Why are you weeping and breaking my heart? Im ready not only to be arrested but even to die in Jerusalem for the sake of the name of the Lord Jesus.
local: Ama, na wɔ bisa, Nzu ti ye am suyo sɔ na am saaki m ahore a? Mini, ma yo siriya na m kɔ Yɛrusalem, na bu kɔ́ cici m nyama, má nyini ŋgumi, ama, haari na bu kún m, ya Mibiɛ Yesu n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Since we couldnt talk him out of it, the only thing we could say was, The Lords will be done.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, ya koro akanda má i ka má i kɔ, na ya mɔ ya nɔɔ, na ya se i ka, Tɔ, ma Mibiɛ n dɛɛ koroo­wa yo.

eng: After this, we got ready and made our way up to Jerusalem.
local: Ya diiri le­nnyɔ dɔ ni, nyini sin, na ya sɛsɛ ya ŋu su, na ya nya akɔ Yɛrusalem.

eng: Some of the disciples from Caesarea accompanied us and led us to Mnasons home, where we were guests. He was from Cyprus and had been a disciple a long time.
local: Na Yesu susu­fɔm nbem bo bu fite Sizariya n asu ya su, na ba fa ya akɔ ka ya kɔ wo sɔnɔ be dika, bu fere i ka Manesoni, i ti Sipurusi­fɔ ɔ, wɔ su Yesu dɛɛ atin n su acɛ.

eng: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers and sisters welcomed us warmly.
local: Saŋga bo ya kɔ juuri Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na ya ninbaam n ade ya ni ahorejɔ nyunyumi.

eng: On the next day Paul and the rest of us went to see James. All of the elders were present.
local: I alecin na Pɔɔlu afa ya akɔ Yakobo dɔ, na ya kɔ atu Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm kɛrɛ ka bu wo i dɔ.

eng: After greeting them, he gave them a detailed report of what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
local: Ya bisaari ahin n wieeri ni, na Pɔɔlu akan deke n kɛrɛ kun kun fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ ma wɔ yo i juma ama minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

eng: Those who heard this praised God. Then they said to him, Brother, you see how many thousands of Jews have become believers, and all of them keep the Law passionately.
local: Bu tiiri nyini nɔaniɛ n sɔ ni, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ. Ama, na ba se i ka, Ya niwa Pɔɔlu, m ŋa a ŋu Yuda­fɔm akpii beberebe bo bu wo ya nu fɛn na ba yo Yesu yarada, ye bu gusu nyunu aboro ka bu su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su.

eng: They have been informed that you teach all the Jews who live among the Gentiles to reject Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children nor to live according to our customs.
local: Ba ti ka a kere Yuda­fɔm bo bu tana minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n nu ni, ka má bu su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo Moyisi fa maari n su, ye a se bu ka má bu wura bu nbaam kɛnkɛnnɛ, na má bu kaaki su Yuda­fɔm nana sa­yoo­wam su.

eng: What about this? Without a doubt, they will hear that you have arrived.
local: Nahɔrɛ, bu tí ka a ba. Ye ya yo sɛ wɔ?.

eng: You must therefore do what we tell you. Four men among us have made a solemn promise.
local: Ti fɛn, kabo ya yo n la ka, minɛ nna nbem wo ya nu fɛn bo ba tan ndiɛ.

eng: Take them with you, go through the purification ritual with them, and pay the cost of having their heads shaved. Everyone will know there is nothing to those reports about you but that you too live a life in keeping with the Law.
local: Su bu su na a kɔ biɛ fin kabo bu yoo­wa kere ni, na a tɔ bu tii su kaari, adena bu koro yi bu tii. Nyini ni má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo bu kan i fa kɔ u dɔ n ti kɔrɛ wɔ, na bu si ka wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su wɔ.

eng: As for the Gentile believers, we wrote a letter about what we decided, that they avoid food offered to idols, blood, the meat from strangled animals, and sexual immorality.
local: Ama, minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na ba yo yarada n yiri, ya kɛrɛ kadasi ama bu, ka má bu se ka bu di tiɛ niɛ, wara mbunja, wara deke bo wɔ wu i ŋgumi na ba kpiɛ má i kɔm, na má bu yo sakaraya.

eng: The following day Paul took the men with him and went through the purification ritual with them. He entered the temple and publicly announced the completion of the days of purification, when the offering would be presented for each one of them.
local: I alecin, na Pɔɔlu afa minɛm n akɔ na ba kɔ ayo fin biɛɛ­wa yoo­wa ni, na wɔ nya awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ jɔjɔ akere Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ni, cɛɛn n bo bu ba biɛ fin n wie, na bu kun kun n kɛrɛ yo bu tii su sa­yoo­wa ni.

eng: When the seven days of purification were almost over, the Jews from the province of Asia saw Paul in the temple. Grabbing him, they threw the whole crowd into confusion by shouting.
local: Saŋga bo wɔ ka kaan na le­so cɛɛn n wie ni, na Yuda­fɔ nbem afite Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu aba dɔ, na ba ŋu Pɔɔlu Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Na ba yɛ jama n kɛrɛ ahore su, na ba tara Pɔɔlu.

eng: Fellow Israelites! Help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, the Law, and this place. Not only that, he has even brought Greeks into the temple and defiled this holy place. .
local: Na ba tiɛn, bu ŋa, Yisareli­fɔm, am buka ya. Ahin sɔnɔ ni nati jeŋgbe deke n kɛrɛ kere minɛm kɛrɛ ɔ, ka má bu bu Yisareli­fɔm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, ni Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru ni. Ye wɔ kaaki afa minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm aba awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na wɔ yo ahin woo­biri casi n fin.

eng: They said this because they had seen Trophimus the Ephesian in the city with him earlier, and they assumed Paul had brought him into the temple.
local: Deke n bo i ti na bu seeri sɔ n la ka, ba du mɔ aŋu Torofimosi bo i fite Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Pɔɔlu miɛ kunnu, nyini dɛɛ ti ye bu sunsuunri ka Pɔɔlu afa i awura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu n a.

eng: The entire city was stirred up. The people came rushing, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the gates were closed.
local: Na miɛ n abo nu, na minɛm kɛrɛ aŋmati atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na ba tara Pɔɔlu acin ayi i afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu. Na ba kaba anyi Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n anɔ ni ndende.

eng: While they were trying to kill him, a report reached the commander of a company of soldiers that all Jerusalem was in a state of confusion.
local: Má bu koroori ka bu kun Pɔɔlu wɔ. Dɔ na labari n atu Roma­fɔm sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ka Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n abo nu.

eng: Without a moments hesitation, he took some soldiers and officers and ran down to the mob. When the mob saw the commander and his soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
local: Kpeŋga na wɔ yasu ndende, na wɔ fa sojam ni bu nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, na ba ŋmati aju dɔ. Saŋga bo jama n ŋuuri soja kpiɛn n ni i sojam ni, na ba yaki Pɔɔlu, ba bo má i kun.

eng: When the commander arrived, he arrested Paul and ordered him to be bound with two chains. Only then did he begin to ask who Paul was and what he had done.
local: Na sojam kpiɛn n akɔ atara Pɔɔlu, na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu wura i jɔrɔkɔ nnyɔ. Na wɔ bisa ka, Ahin sɔnɔ ti nzu sɔnɔ ɔ? Ye wɔ yo nzu wɔ.

eng: Some in the crowd shouted one thing, others shouted something else. Because of the commotion, he couldnt learn the truth, so he ordered that Paul be taken to the military headquarters.
local: Na jama ni nbem sutiɛntiɛn sujɔjɔ, bu jɔjɔɔ­wa n woori i ŋgumi ŋgumi wɔ, ba jɔjɔ má jɔrɛ kun. Bu naŋmiɛ dɛɛ ti, wɔ koro asi má jɔrɛ n bu. Na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu fa Pɔɔlu kɔ bɛrɛ sojam dika bambaka n nu.

eng: When Paul reached the steps, he had to be carried by the soldiers in order to protect him from the violence of the crowd.
local: Bu faari Pɔɔlu kɔ juuri bimbindi bo bu tiɛntiɛn su fun na bu wura gisan nu ni, na i boori i bu ka i fun kɔ sɔɔ n aŋgoro ni, sojam n kukuuri i wɔ, dama jama n biɛkpa n dɛɛ ti.

eng: The mob that followed kept screaming, Away with him.
local: Na bu suŋmati su i su, sutiɛn kpa, bu ŋa, Am kun i, am kun i.

eng: As Paul was about to be taken into the military headquarters, he asked the commander, May I speak with you? He answered, Do you know Greek?.
local: Saŋga bo bu sukoro ka bu fa Pɔɔlu wura sojam dika bambaka n nu ni, na Pɔɔlu ase sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ka, Ma m kan jɔrɛ be na a ti. Na sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n abisa i ka, A ti Giriki aniɛ?.

eng: Arent you the Egyptian who started a revolt and led four thousand terrorists into the desert some time ago.
local: Nyini yiri, a ti má Ijipiti sɔnɔ n bo i yo duuri minɛ akpii nna mɔ ka bu tu gomina n ni, na i faari nyini minɛ akpii nna n kɛrɛ kɔɔri boro kpantain n nu ni.

eng: Paul replied, Im a Jew from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city. Please, let me speak to the people.
local: Na Pɔɔlu abɔ i nu ka, Ai, mini, m ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ, m ti miɛbaa a, m fite Tarasu wɔ, miɛ kpiri n bo i la duma na i wo Silisiya awɔrɛ n nu ni. Jande, ma m jɔjɔ minɛm ni dɔ.

eng: With the commanders permission, Paul stood on the steps and gestured to the people. When they were quiet, he addressed them in Aramaic.
local: Na sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ama i atin ka i jɔjɔ, na Pɔɔlu ajina bimbindi n bo bu tiɛntiɛn su fun na bu wura gisan nu ni, na wɔ fa i saa ayo minɛm n ka bu yaki naŋmiɛ. Na ba tɔ amuna, na Pɔɔlu ajɔjɔ bu dɔ Hiburu aniɛ nu.

eng: Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.
local: I ŋa, M sim ni m ninbaam, am ti fɛn, m koro ka m kan m jɔrɛ n na am ti.

eng: When they heard him address them in Aramaic, they became even more quiet.
local: Saŋga bo bu tiiri ka i sujɔjɔ bu dɔ Hiburu aniɛ nu ni, na ba yaki naŋmiɛ na ba mɔ bu nɔɔ surum. Na Pɔɔlu ase ka.

eng: Paul continued, Im a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia but raised in this city. Under Gamaliels instruction, I was trained in the strict interpretation of our ancestral Law. I am passionately loyal to God, just like you who are gathered here today.
local: M ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ, bu wuuri m Tarasu miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ bo i wo Silisiya awɔrɛ n nu ni, ama, bu taari m Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu fɛn wɔ, Gamɛli kereeri m kara a, ye m sɔnsɔɔnri ya nam sa­yoo­wa atin n kɛrɛ ni anyum­boro. Na ma fa m ŋu kɛrɛ ama Nyɛmɛ, kabo ambɛrɛ bo am jina fɛn nyuma n afa am ŋu ama i ni.

eng: I harassed those who followed this Way to their death, arresting and delivering both men and women into prison.
local: Na ma kere minɛm bo bu su ahin atin n su n wuwuruwa, haari na ma kun bu nbem. Na ma tara bu nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram anyi bu dan­saraka.

eng: The high priest and the whole Jerusalem Council can testify about me. I received letters from them, addressed to our associates in Damascus, then went there to bring those who were arrested to Jerusalem so they could be punished.
local: Nzɛn am bisaari Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn ni, ni tere­tere­fɔm ni, bu sé am ka nɔaniɛ n bo m sujɔjɔ i n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Haari na ba ma m kadasi ka m fa kɔ ma ya minɛm bo bu wo Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu ni, adena m koro tara Yesu minɛm ni, na m wura bu jɔrɔkɔ fa bu ba Yɛrusalem na bu bá cin bu sui.

eng: During that journey, about noon, as I approached Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven encircled me.
local: Na Pɔɔlu asa su ase bu ka, Saŋga bo m wo kɔ su n na m kɔ dodoori Damasiko miɛ kpiri n ni, wiɛ kekete saŋga lɛɛ, na sin be akaba afite aŋgoro ata abara ayɛ m.

eng: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking me, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me.
local: Na ma kpaasi haari asiɛŋgu, na ma ti akonvi be ŋga, i ŋa, Sɔɔlu, Sɔɔlu e, nzu ti ye a kere m wuwuruwa sɔ a.

eng: I answered, Who are you, Lord? I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are harassing, he replied.
local: Na ma bisa i ka, M Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ ŋma a? Na wɔ bɔ m nu ka, Mini Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n a, mini ye a kere m wuwuruwa n a.

eng: My traveling companions saw the light, but they didnt hear the voice of the one who spoke to me.
local: Na minɛm bo bu su m su n aŋu sin ni, ama, ba ti má akonvi n yiri ŋga.

eng: I asked, What should I do, Lord? Get up, the Lord replied, and go into Damascus. There you will be told everything you have been appointed to do.
local: Na ma bisa i ka, M Mibiɛ, ye a ŋa m yo nzu wɔ? Na wɔ se m ka, Yasu na a kɔ Damasiko, dɔ ye bu sé u deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka a yo i ni.

eng: I couldnt see because of the brightness of that light, so my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.
local: Sin n bo i ta wuraari m nyu­mbaa nu agaya n dɛɛ ti, na ma yo anyinsin, na minɛm ni bo bu su m su n aso m saa nu afa m awura Damasiko.

eng: There was a certain man named Ananias. According to the standards of the Law, he was a pious man who enjoyed the respect of all the Jews living there.
local: Tɔ, sɔnɔ be woori miɛ n nu, bu fere i ka Ananiyasi, i su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, na i su i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, i ti sɔnɔ n bo Yuda­fɔm bo bu wo miɛ n nu n kɛrɛ ma i jirima agaya a.

eng: He came and stood beside me. Brother Saul, receive your sight! he said. Instantly, I regained my sight and I could see him.
local: Na wɔ ba m dɔ ajina adodo m, na wɔ se m ka, M niwa Sɔɔlu, niɛ, a ŋú asi. Jina n nu bɛrɛ na ma ŋu asi bekun, na ma niɛ aŋu i.

eng: He said, The God of our ancestors has selected you to know his will, to see the righteous one, and to hear his voice.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Nyɛmɛ n bo ya nam su i ni, yiri ye wɔ yi u a, na a si i sa koroo­wam, ye wɔ yi Sɔnɔ n bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n akere u, na a ti yiri ni i tii nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa.

eng: You will be his witness to everyone concerning what you have seen and heard.
local: Dama deke bo a ŋu i na a ti i ni, wɔrɔ kán kere minɛm kɛrɛ.

eng: What are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash away your sins as you call on his name.
local: Ye kisa, a ŋminda nzu wɔ? Yasu na bu biɛ u Nyɛmɛ nzue. Bo fere i duma na i ŋmisi yi u satiɛ kɛrɛ.

eng: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I had a visionary experience.
local: Na Pɔɔlu asa su ase bu ka, Nyini ti, na ma kpie m nyi akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu, saŋga bo m wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu wo nyɛmɛsere su ni, na wɔ yo m ka alɛlɛ dɛɛ su wɔ.

eng: I saw the Lord speaking to me. Hurry! he said. Leave Jerusalem at once because they wont accept your testimony about me.
local: na ma ŋu Yesu ka i sujɔjɔ m dɔ, i ŋa, Yasu na a fite Yɛrusalem ndende, dama minɛm bo bu wo fɛn ni dé má m jɔrɛ n bo a kan kere bu ni.

eng: I responded, Lord, these people know I used to go from one synagogue to the next, beating those who believe in you and throwing them into prison.
local: Na ma bɔ i nu ka, M Mibiɛ, bɛrɛ ni bu tii si tenle su ka m yo wuraari bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu wɔ, na ma tara minɛm bo bu yo u yarada ni, na ma nyi bu dan­saraka, na ma bo bu.

eng: When Stephen your witness was being killed, I stood there giving my approval, even watching the clothes that belonged to those who were killing him.
local: Sasu bekun, saŋga bo bu sukun Satɛfano bo i diiri u ŋusu daani kereeri minɛm ni, m yo jinaari bɛrɛ, na ma soro su, mini niɛɛri minɛm bo bu kuunri i n suturam su a.

eng: Then the Lord said to me, Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.
local: Na Mibiɛ n ase m ka, Wɔrɔ kɔ, m súnma u dede minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni dɔ.

eng: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they shouted, Away with this man! Hes not fit to live.
local: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ ni, minɛm ni soori bu sui wɔ, na ba ti jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo Pɔɔlu kaanri i ni, ama, i ba kaanri sɔ ni, na ba tiɛn kpa, bu ŋa, Am yi i fite fɛn. Am mini i mmɔɔ. Wɔ ti má ye ni ka i wo ni ŋgɔɔ.

eng: As they were screaming, throwing off their garments, and flinging dust into the air.
local: Na bu sutiɛntiɛn, yaa ati bu n dɛɛ ti, na bu sufikindi bu suturam tu, na bu susa fa sutu sandi aŋgoro.

eng: the commander directed that Paul be taken into the military headquarters. He ordered that Paul be questioned under the whip so that he could find out why they were shouting at him like this.
local: Na Roma­fɔm soja kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ama nɔɔ ka bu fa Pɔɔlu wura bu dika bambaka n nu, na bu bisa i bisaa­wam na bu fin i ŋgbere na bu si deke bo i ti na Yuda­fɔm ni tiɛntiɛn sɔ ni.

eng: As they were stretching him out and tying him down with straps, Paul said to the centurion standing there, Can you legally whip a Roman citizen who hasnt been found guilty in court.
local: Ama, saŋga bo bu ciciiri Pɔɔlu nyama n ka bu bo i ni, na wɔ bisa soja kpiɛn n bo i jina dodo i ka, Atin wo nu ka am bo Roma miɛbaa na am asi má satiɛ bo wɔ yo i.

eng: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. He asked, What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.
local: Saŋga bo soja kpiɛn n tiiri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ni, na wɔ kɔ sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n dɔ, akɔ abisa i ka, A koro ka a yo ahin sɔnɔ n sɛ wɔ? I ti Roma miɛbaa a.

eng: The commander went to Paul and demanded, Tell me! Are you a Roman citizen? He said, Yes.
local: Nyini ti, na sojam kpiɛn n akɔ Pɔɔlu dɔ, akɔ abisa i ka, A ti Roma miɛbaa a, nahɔrɛ su? Na wɔ bɔ i nu ka, Ɛɛn.

eng: The commander replied, It cost me a lot of money to buy my citizenship. Paul said, Im a citizen by birth.
local: Na sojam kpiɛn n ase i ka, Mini, m tɔɔri ŋwaa beberebe wɔ ye m nya kaakiiri Roma miɛbaa a. Na Pɔɔlu ase i ka, Ama, mini yiri, bu wuuri m miɛbaa a.

eng: At once those who were about to examine him stepped away. The commander was alarmed when he realized he had bound a Roman citizen.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, kpeŋga na sɛrɛ ati sojam bo ba yi bu ka bu bisa i bisaa­wa ni, na ba kɔ bu sin. Na sɛrɛ ati soja kpiɛn ni mmɔɔ, dama wɔ du mɔ abo Pɔɔlu jɔrɔkɔ ka, ye i nya siiri ka i ti Roma miɛbaa a.

eng: The commander still wanted to know the truth about why Paul was being accused by the Jews. Therefore, the next day he ordered the chief priests and the entire Jerusalem Council to assemble. Then he took Paul out of prison and had him stand before them.
local: Na i alecin, sojam kpiɛn n akoro ka i si sa bo Pɔɔlu ayo i na Yuda­fɔm jaraki i ni, na wɔ yi Pɔɔlu jɔrɔkɔ ni, na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm n kɛrɛ tiɛn bu nɔɔ. Na wɔ ma Pɔɔlu aba ajina bu nyunu.

eng: Paul stared at the council and said, Brothers, I have lived my life with an altogether clear conscience right up to this very day.
local: Na Pɔɔlu afa i nyu­mbaa akpama Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm n ŋu, na wɔ se ka, M ninbaam, m sunsunni ti kpa wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, fite m kaan nu haari ni nyuma.

eng: The high priest Ananias ordered those standing beside Paul to strike him in the mouth.
local: Na Ananiyasi bo i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn n ama minɛm bo bu jina dodo Pɔɔlu n nɔɔ, ka bu bo i nɔɔ dika, fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Then Paul said to him, God is about to strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit and judge me according to the Law, yet disobey the Law by ordering that I be struck.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ase i ka, Nyɛmɛ bó wɔrɔ mmɔɔ, wɔrɔ bo a tana dɔ ka kobe dawa bo ba bo i ŋgoro fofoe ni. A tana na a di m jɔrɛ, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɛɛ atin su, ama, wɔrɔ ni u tii asu má i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su na a tana ma nɔɔ ka bu bo m.

eng: Those standing near him asked, You dare to insult Gods high priest.
local: Na minɛm bo bu jina dodo Pɔɔlu n ase i ka, Wɔrɔ la ŋma na a kpiɛ i nzukɔ a, yiri Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ kpiɛn ni.

eng: Paul replied, Brothers, I wasnt aware that he was the high priest. It is written, You will not speak evil about a ruler of your people .
local: Na Pɔɔlu abɔ bu nu ka, M ninbaam, ma si má ka yiri ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ n kɛrɛ kpiɛn n a. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Má a jɔjɔ sɔnɔ bo i niɛ u minɛm su jɔrɛ tiɛ.

eng: Knowing that some of them were Sadducees and the others Pharisees, Paul exclaimed in the council, Brothers, Im a Pharisee and a descendant of Pharisees. I am on trial because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead.
local: Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu faari nu ka, minɛm ni nbem ti Sadusi­fɔm, na bu nbem gusu ti Farasi­fɔm ni, na wɔ tiɛn kpa jama n nu, i ŋa, M ninbaam, m ti Farasi­fɔ ɔ. M sim mmɔɔ suuri nyini atin ni su a. Deke n bo i ti na ya tiɛn ya nɔɔ n la ka, m la tama ka minɛm wu, bu téŋge bu yiwee nu wɔ.

eng: These words aroused a dispute between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
local: Pɔɔlu seeri sɔ wieeri na akuruwa aba Sadusi­fɔm ni Farasi­fɔm n afiɛn, na bu tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ n akpaaki nu nnyɔ.

eng: This is because Sadducees say that theres no resurrection, angel, or spirit, but Pharisees affirm them all.
local: Sadusi­fɔm n ŋa, sɔnɔ wu, i téŋge má i yiwee nu kun, ye bu kaaki ŋa, Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm, wara awiɛwiɛm wo má bɛrɛ. Ama, Farasi­fɔm bɛrɛ yo nyinim n kɛrɛ yarada a.

eng: Council members were shouting loudly. Some Pharisees who were legal experts stood up and insisted forcefully, We find nothing wrong with this man! What if a spirit or angel has spoken to him.
local: Na jama ni sutiɛntiɛn suyo naŋmiɛ agaya buka su. Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔ nbem bo bu wo Farasi­fɔm asɛsɛ n nu n ayasu ajina atiɛn faŋga su, bu ŋa, Yɛrɛ aŋu má ahin sɔnɔ ni ŋgasi bo wɔ do i. Beni, awiɛwiɛ be, wara Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ ajɔjɔ i dɔ a.

eng: The dispute became so heated that the commander feared they might tear Paul to pieces. He ordered soldiers to go down and remove him by force from their midst. Then they took him back to the military headquarters.
local: Saŋga bo akuruwa n ba bi boroori agaya ni, na sɛrɛ ati soja kpiɛn ni, ka minɛm ni bá cincin titi Pɔɔlu nu. Nyini ti, na wɔ ma sojam nɔɔ ka bu wura jama n nu na bu yi Pɔɔlu fite bu nu, na bu fa i ba wura bu dɛɛ dika bambaka n nu.

eng: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, Be encouraged! Just as you have testified about me in Jerusalem, so too you must testify in Rome.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n kɔŋguɛ, na Mibiɛ n ayi i ŋu akere Pɔɔlu, na wɔ se i ka, Ma u ahore si, na má a soro sɛrɛ. Kabo a di m ŋu daani Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu fɛn ni, sɔ ye i ti ye ni ka a kɔ di i Roma miɛ kpiri n nu gusu a.

eng: The next morning some Jewish leaders formulated a plot and solemnly promised that they wouldnt eat or drink until they had killed Paul.
local: I alecin fɔfɔrɛ, na Yuda­fɔ nbem atiɛn bu nɔɔ, na ba fa sɛsɛ­su na ba tan ndiɛ ka bu di má, bu nu má, má ka ba kun Pɔɔlu.

eng: More than forty people were involved in the conspiracy.
local: Bɛrɛ n bo bu tana faari nyini sɛsɛ­su ni, bu taraari minɛ abura­na.

eng: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, We have solemnly promised to eat nothing until we have killed Paul.
local: Na ba kɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, ni Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm ni dɔ, akɔ ase bu ka, Yɛrɛ, ya tiɛn ya nɔɔ atan ndiɛ ka ya dí má pui, má ka ya kun Pɔɔlu.

eng: You and the council must explain to the commander that you need Paul brought down to you. Pretend that you want to examine his case more closely. Were prepared to kill him before he arrives.
local: Nyini ti, ambɛrɛ ni tere­tere­fɔm ni, am sunma Roma­fɔm soja kpiɛn n dɔ kɔ se i ka, i fa Pɔɔlu bra am dɔ ka am koro ka am niɛ i jɔrɛ n nu nyunyumi bekun wɔ. Ama, na ya yó siriya ŋminda i, na ya kún i atin nu na i nya ba ju fɛn.

eng: Pauls sister had a son who heard about the ambush and he came to the military headquarters and reported it to Paul.
local: Ama, na Pɔɔlu niwa bara be wa ati sɛsɛ­su n bo ba fa i ni, na wɔ kɔ awura sojam sɔɔ bambaka n nu, na wɔ se Pɔɔlu.

eng: Paul called for one of the centurions and said, Take this young man to the commander because he has something to report to him.
local: Dɔ na Pɔɔlu afere sojam n kun, na wɔ se i ka, Fa ahin gbɛfɛnɛ n kɔ am kpiɛn n dɔ, i la jɔrɛ be bo i kán kere i.

eng: He took him to the commander and said, The prisoner Paul asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.
local: Na soja n afa i akɔ bu kpiɛn n dɔ, na wɔ se i ka, Pɔɔlu bo ba nyi i dan­saraka ni fereeri m a, na wɔ se m ka m fa ahin gbɛfɛnɛ n ba u dɔ, dama i la jɔrɛ bo i kán kere u.

eng: The commander took him by the hand and withdrew to a place where they could speak privately. He asked, What do you have to report to me.
local: Na sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n aso i saa nu, na i ni yiri ŋgumi afite ahɛmɛ, na wɔ bisa i ka, A koro ka a kan nzu jɔrɛ kere m a.

eng: He replied, The Jewish leaders have conspired to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow. They will pretend that they want to investigate his case more closely.
local: Na wɔ se sojam kpiɛn n ka, Yuda­fɔm n asɛsɛ su ye ba soro su ka ahunma bu bá sere u na a fa Pɔɔlu ma bu na bu fa i kɔ bu tere­tere­fɔm ni dɔ, na bu yó bu ŋu ka bu koro ka bu niɛ i jɔrɛ n nu nyunyumi wɔ.

eng: Dont fall for it! More than forty of them are waiting to ambush him. They have solemnly promised not to eat or drink until they have killed him. They are ready now, awaiting your consent.
local: Ama, má a soro bu su, dama bu nbem ŋa bu kɔ ŋminda i atin nu wɔ, bu tara minɛm abura­na. Ba tan ndiɛ ka bu dí má, bu nú má, má ka ba kun Pɔɔlu. Kisa ba yo siriya su aŋminda na bu ti kabo a sé.

eng: The commander dismissed the young man, ordering him, Dont tell anyone that you brought this to my attention.
local: Na sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn n abɔbɔ i ka, Má a ma be si ka a kan ahin jɔrɛ n akere m. Na wɔ ma gbɛfɛnɛ n atin na wɔ kɔ.

eng: The commander called two centurions and said, Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to leave for Caesarea at nine oclock tonight.
local: Na sojam kɛrɛ kpiɛn ni, afere soja nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni nnyɔ, na wɔ ma bu nɔɔ ka, Am fa sojam yaa nnyɔ, ni soja kpɔŋgɔ­fun­fɔm abura­so, ni soja kpiɛ­bita­fɔm yaa nnyɔ, na am yo siriya kɔŋguɛ n nu, kerefi ŋgɔna saŋga, na am kɔ Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: Have horses ready for Paul to ride, so they may take him safely to Governor Felix.
local: Na am nya kpɔŋgɔ ma Pɔɔlu gusu, na am fa i haari na am kɔ ju Gomina Felika dɔ, na má pui yo i.

eng: He wrote the following letter.
local: Na sojam kpiɛn n akɛrɛ kadasi ama gomina n ka.

eng: Claudius Lysias, to the most honorable Governor Felix: Greetings.
local: Mini Kalodisi Lisiyasi sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n bisa wɔrɔ, m si Gomina Jirima­fɔ Felika dɔŋgu a.

eng: This man was seized by the Jews and was almost killed by them. I was nearby with a unit of soldiers, and I rescued him when I discovered that he was a Roman citizen.
local: Yuda­fɔm taraari ahin sɔnɔ n a, má bu koro ka bu kun i wɔ. Ama, na ma ti ka i ti Roma miɛbaa a, nyini ti, na ma fa m sojam, na ya kɔ ade i aji.

eng: I wanted to find out why they were accusing him, so I brought him to their council.
local: Na ma koro ka m si deke n bo i ti na bu jarakiiri i ni, nyini ti, na ma fa i akɔ Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm dɔ.

eng: I discovered that they were accusing him about questions related to their Law. I found no charge deserving of death or imprisonment.
local: Na ma ŋu ka fa kɔ bu nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɛɛ atin su ye bu jarakiiri i a, ama, wɔ yo má sa be bo i ti ye ni ka bu kun i, wara bu nyi i dan­saraka.

eng: When I was informed of a conspiracy against his life, I sent him to you at once and ordered his accusers to bring their case against him before you.
local: M tiiri ka Yuda­fɔm asɛsɛ su akɔ ada aŋminda atin nu ka bu kun i, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m ma ba kaba afa i aba u dɔ n a. Ma se minɛm bo bu jarakiiri i n ka bu fá jɔrɛ n bra u dɔ.

eng: Following their orders, the soldiers took Paul during the night and brought him to Antipatris.
local: Na sojam n ayo kabo i kereeri bu ka bu yo ni, na ba fa Pɔɔlu nyini kɔŋguɛ n kɛrɛ, na ba fa i akɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Antipatiriso n su.

eng: The following day they let the horsemen continue on with Paul while they returned to the military headquarters in Jerusalem.
local: Na i alecin fɔfɔrɛ na sojam bo bu nati bu jaa su n akpie bu nyi akɔ sojam sɔɔ bambaka n nu, na ba ma soja kpɔŋgɔ­fun­fɔm n afa i akɔ aju deke bo bu sukɔ ni.

eng: The horsemen entered Caesarea, delivered the letter to the governor, and brought Paul before him.
local: Bu kɔ juuri Sizariya ni, na ba fa kadasi n ama gomina ni, na ba nya afa Pɔɔlu awura i saa nu.

eng: After he read the letter, he asked Paul about his home province. When he learned that he was from Cilicia.
local: Gomina n kaanri kadasi n wieeri ni, na wɔ bisa Pɔɔlu awɔrɛ bo i fite nu. I faari nu ka i fite Silisiya awɔrɛ n nu wɔ.

eng: the governor said, I will hear your case when your accusers arrive. Then he ordered that Paul be kept in custody in Herods palace.
local: na wɔ se i ka, Minɛm bo bu jarakiiri u n ba juuri, m tí u nɔaniɛ. Na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu fa Pɔɔlu wura Fɛmɛ Herode dika n nu, na bu niɛ i dika nyunyumi.

eng: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. They pressed charges against Paul before the governor.
local: Na i le­nnu cɛɛn sin, na Ananiyasi bo i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ n kɛrɛ kpiɛn n aba aju Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu, i ni Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔ nbem, na ba fa jɔrɛ­si­fɔ be bo bu fere i ka Tɛtulɔsi n abuka bu ŋu su. Na ba jaraki Pɔɔlu gomina n nyunu.

eng: After the governor summoned Paul, Tertullus began to make his case against him. He declared, Under your leadership, we have experienced substantial peace, and your administration has brought reforms to our nation.
local: Bu faari Pɔɔlu ba juuri n na Tɛtulɔsi abo i bu na i kan ŋgasi bo Pɔɔlu ado i. I ŋa, Ya si Felika jirima­fɔ, u ŋgɔnlɛ kpa n bo a la i n dɛɛ ti, nyini ma ya nya tana­laifiɛ acɛ a, ye kaaki aba ya asiɛn n su a, fa kɔ nyini sunsunni n bo a la i ni. Deke kɛrɛ bo ya wo ni saŋga kɛrɛ, ya nyunu ayi u fa kɔ deke n kɛrɛ bo a yo ama ya ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ya da u asi beberebe.

eng: I dont want to take too much of your time, so I ask that you listen with your usual courtesy to our brief statement of the facts.
local: Tɔ, m koro má ka m jɔjɔ saaki u saŋga, ama, m sere u wɔ, sawɔrɛ n bo a la i n dɛɛ ti, ti ya ahin jɔrɛ kaan ni.

eng: We have found this man to be a troublemaker who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the empire. Hes a ringleader of the Nazarene faction.
local: Ya ŋu ka ahin sɔnɔ n ti miɛbo­fɔ ɔ, na i nati sukpukpura Yuda­fɔm tii durunya n kɛrɛ nu, na bu sudi aluwa ni bu beŋgu. I ti mɔ­fɔ ɔ, fa kɔ minɛm bo bu su Nazarɛti­fɔ n atin n su ni.

eng: and even tried to defile the temple. Thats when we arrested him.
local: Haari i yo koroori ka i wura Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu wɔ na i saaki i na i yo i fin, ye ya taraari i a. Má ya koroori ka ya fa ya nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n atin su di i jɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: By examining him yourself, you will be able to verify the allegations we are bringing against him.
local: na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka, bo bu ni yiri la jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bra u dɔ. Na nzɛn wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii niɛɛri jɔrɛ n nu, a sí deke n bo i ti na ya jaraki i ni.

eng: The Jews reinforced the action against Paul, affirming the truth of these accusations.
local: Na Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ ajina su ka, jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo bu jɔjɔ i n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: The governor nodded at Paul, giving him permission to speak. He responded, I know that you have been judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly offer my own defense.
local: Na Gomina Felika ama Pɔɔlu atin ka i jɔjɔ, na Pɔɔlu ase ka, M si ka a di jɔrɛ ahin asiɛn n su acɛ, nyini ti, m ahore ti sii­wa ka ma ba u dɔ na m kan de m tii.

eng: You can verify that I went up to worship in Jerusalem no more than twelve days ago.
local: Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii koro bisa na a ti, ka i nyuma le­buru le­nnyɔ lɛɛ, bo m kɔɔri Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ka m kɔ su Nyɛmɛ.

eng: They didnt find me arguing with anyone in the temple or stirring up a crowd, whether in the synagogue or anywhere else in the city.
local: Bɛrɛ n aŋu má ka m ni be suse akuruwa Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, ba kaaki aŋu má ka m ni be suwura minɛm bu sin na bu yo sa be tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm n nu, wara miɛ n nu.

eng: Nor can they prove to you the allegations they are now bringing against me.
local: Bɛrɛ gusu koro kan kere má u deke n bo i ti na kisa bu sujaraki m ni.

eng: I do admit this to you, that I am a follower of the Way, which they call a faction. Accordingly, I worship the God of our ancestors and believe everything set out in the Law and written in the Prophets.
local: Ama, deke bo m soro i su ka i ti nahɔrɛ n la ka, m su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, kabo ya nam su i ni, nyini atin n gusu ye bu ŋa i ti má nahɔrɛ n a. Ama, m yo deke bo ba kɛrɛ i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu n yarada, na m kaaki yo deke bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n nu n yarada.

eng: The hope I have in God I also share with my accusers, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the unrighteous.
local: M la tama Nyɛmɛ dɔ ka minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, ni bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ má atin su n kɛrɛ bá teŋge bu yiwee n nu, kabo bɛrɛ n bo bu jaraki m n gusu la nyini tama ni.

eng: On account of this, I have committed myself to maintaining a clear conscience before God and with all people.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, saŋga kɛrɛ m boro m nyi wɔ, na m sunsunni ti casi fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ ni adamandem dɔ.

eng: After an absence of several years, I came to Jerusalem to bring gifts for the poor of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
local: M fiteeri Yɛrusalem, wɔ cɛ, nyini sin, na ma kpie m nyi akɔ dɔ ka m kɔ fa ŋwaa ce m minɛm bo bu ti yaari­fɔm n na m yo m dɛɛ yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ.

eng: When they found me in the temple, I was ritually pure. There was no crowd and no disturbance.
local: M wo nyini yoo­wa n yo su ye bu ŋuuri m Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu a. Nyini saŋga ni, ma yo fin biɛ yoo­wa n awie mmɔɔ. Jama be awo má m dɔ, be gusu ayo má naŋmiɛ be.

eng: But there were some Jews from the province of Asia. They should be here making their accusations, if indeed they have something against me.
local: Ama, Yuda­fɔm nbem bo bu fite Esiya awɔrɛ n nu ni, yo woori dɔ a. Wɔ yo daka ka má bɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni bu tii wo fɛn, na bu kan m ŋgasi bo ma do i n kere u.

eng: In their absence, have these people who are here declare what crime they found when I stood before the Jerusalem Council.
local: Nzɛn má nyini, yaki na ahin minɛm bo bu jina fɛn n kan ŋgasi bo m doori, saŋga bo m jinaari bu tere­tere­fɔm nyunu na bu diiri m jɔrɛ ni.

eng: Perhaps it concerns this one statement that I blurted out when I was with them: I am on trial before you today because of the resurrection of the dead.
local: Má ka deke kun n kpein be bo bu yo sudi m jɔrɛ na m jinaari bu nyunu ni. Yiri la bo m seeri ka minɛm wu, bu káaki teŋge bu yiwee nu ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye a sudi m jɔrɛ nyuma n a.

eng: Felix, who had an accurate understanding of the Way, adjourned the meeting. He said, When Lysias the commander arrives from Jerusalem, I will decide this case.
local: Na Gomina Felika si Mibiɛ n atin n sereya n kɛrɛ nyunyumi n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ se bu ka, Am kɔ ka, na sojam kpiɛn Lisiyasi ba baari, na m nyá di jɔrɛ ni.

eng: He arranged for a centurion to guard Paul. He was to give Paul some freedom, and his friends were not to be hindered in their efforts to provide for him.
local: Na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka soja kpiɛn n niɛ Pɔɔlu dika nyunyumi, ama, i ma i atin na i nati i koroya­nu, na i ma i danfom gusu atin na bu koro ba buka i.

eng: After several days, Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and summoned Paul. He listened to him talk about faith in Christ Jesus.
local: I yoori le­nnyɔ, na Gomina Felika akaaki aba, i ni i yi Durusila, i ti Yuda­fɔ baa a. Na wɔ sunma afere Pɔɔlu na i ba kan kabo sɔnɔ yo Yesu Kristo yarada barasu ni. Na wɔ so i sui ati.

eng: When he spoke about upright behavior, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became fearful and said, Go away for now! When I have time, Ill send for you.
local: Ama, saŋga bo Pɔɔlu wo jɔjɔ su fa kɔ sa bo i kɔ atin su, ni tara­buŋu, ni kabo Nyɛmɛ bá di minɛm n jɔrɛ ni, na sɛrɛ ati Gomina Felika na wɔ se ka, Tɔ, kisa, wɔ sɛ su. A koro kɔ, na m ba nya saŋga na m fére u.

eng: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him some money, so he often sent for him and talked with him.
local: Nyini saŋga n kɛrɛ ni, i sunsunni la ka Pɔɔlu má i ŋwaa a, nyini ti ye saŋga saŋga i fere Pɔɔlu, na bu subo anzama n a.

eng: When two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. Since Felix wanted to grant a favor to the Jews, he left Paul in prison.
local: Na afɔ nnyɔ aba asin, na sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Pɔkiyɔsi Fɛsitosi ade Felika jinaa­biri ni. Na Gomina Felika akoro ka i jɔ Yuda­fɔm n ahore, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ yaki Pɔɔlu dan­saraka n nu, i ma ba yi má i.

eng: Three days after arriving in the province, Festus went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
local: Gomina Fɛsitosi ba juuri nyini awɔrɛ n nu ni, i le­san, na wɔ fite Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu, na wɔ fun akɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: The chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. Appealing to him.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm n aba akan ŋgasi bo Pɔɔlu ado i n akere Gomina Fɛsitosi. Na ba sere i jande.

eng: they asked as a favor from Festus that he summon Paul to Jerusalem. They were planning to ambush and kill him along the way.
local: ka i boro i nyi na i ma bu fa Pɔɔlu ba Yɛrusalem. Deke n bo i ti na bu seeri sɔ n la ka, ba sɛsɛ su ka bu kɔ ŋminda i atin nu wɔ na bu kun i.

eng: But Festus responded by keeping Paul in Caesarea, since he was to return there very soon himself.
local: Na Gomina Fɛsitosi abɔ bu nu ka, Pɔɔlu wo dan­saraka nu Sizariya, mini ni m tii, m kpie m nyi kɔ dɔ kisa.

eng: Some of your leaders can come down with me, he said. If hes done anything wrong, they can bring charges against him.
local: Am ma am nu nbem bo bu la yiko n su m su, na ya kɔ Sizariya, na nzɛn wɔ do ŋgasi be a, na bu koro kan i na bu jaraki i.

eng: He stayed with them for no more than eight or ten days, then went down to Caesarea. The following day he took his seat in the court and ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Na Gomina Fɛsitosi awo bu dɔ, le­mɔcuɛ ni le­buru afiɛn, na wɔ nya aba Sizariya. I alecin na wɔ kɔ atana jɔrɛ dii­biri ni, na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka bu fa Pɔɔlu bra.

eng: When he arrived, many Jews who had come down from Jerusalem surrounded him. They brought serious charges against him, but they couldnt prove them.
local: Saŋga bo bu faari Pɔɔlu ba juuri n ni, na Yuda­fɔm bo bu fiteeri Yɛrusalem baari n atiɛn bu nɔɔ abara ayɛ i. Na ba kan i ŋgasi tiɛtiɛ beberebe bo bu ŋa wɔ do i, bu gusu akoro akere má ŋgasi n bo bu ŋa wɔ do i n tenle su.

eng: In his own defense, Paul said, Ive done nothing wrong against the Jewish Law, against the temple, or against Caesar.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ase ka, Ma do má ŋgasi be fa kɔ Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, ma do má ŋgasi be fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n su, ma kaaki ado má ŋgasi be fa kɔ Roma­fɔm Fɛmɛ Kpiri Siza n dɔ.

eng: Festus, wanting to put the Jews in his debt, asked Paul, Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me concerning these things.
local: Ama, na Gomina Fɛsitosi akoro ka i buka Yuda­fɔm ni, na i da bu ahore, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ bisa Pɔɔlu ka, A soro su ka ya kɔ Yɛrusalem na bu kɔ di u jɔrɛ n dɔ m nyunu, fa kɔ ahin sereyam kɛrɛ su.

eng: Paul replied, Im standing before Caesars court. I ought to be tried here. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you well know.
local: Ama, na Pɔɔlu abɔ i nu ka, M jina Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n jɔrɛ dii­biri wɔ lɛɛ, fɛn ye wɔ yo daka ka bu di m jɔrɛ a. Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii si tenle su ka ma do má Yuda­fɔm ŋgasi be.

eng: If Im guilty and have done something that deserves death, then I wont try to avoid death. But if there is nothing to their accusations against me, no one has the authority to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.
local: Nzɛn ma do ŋgasi be bo wɔ yo daka ka bu kun m, ma kete má ka má bu kun m. Ama, nzɛn jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo ba kan i n be ti má nahɔrɛ, be la má atin bo i koro fa m wura bu saa nu. Nyini ti, m sere ka a fa m jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ.

eng: After Festus conferred with his advisors, he responded, You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go.
local: Na Gomina Fɛsitosi ni i nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n atana aŋu bu ŋu, na ba soro su, na wɔ se Pɔɔlu ka, A sere ka ya fa u jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ. Tɔ, ya fá u kɔ i dɔ.

eng: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived in Caesarea to welcome Festus.
local: Na wɔ yo le­nnyɔ, na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ni i siwa baa­bara Bɛrɛnisi aba Sizariya miɛ kpiri n nu, ka bu ba niɛ Gomina Fɛsitosi busu.

eng: Since they were staying there for many days, Festus discussed the case against Paul with the king. He said, There is a man whom Felix left in prison.
local: Bu woori dɔ cɛɛri kaan n dɛɛ ti, na Gomina Fɛsitosi akan Pɔɔlu jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ n akere fɛmɛ ni. I ŋa, Felika yo yakiiri sɔnɔ be dan­saraka n nu fɛn.

eng: When I was in Jerusalem, the Jewish chief priests and elders brought charges against him and requested a guilty verdict in his case.
local: Saŋga bo m kɔɔri Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na Yuda­fɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Yuda­fɔ tere­tere­fɔm n aba m dɔ, na ba kan nyini sɔnɔ n jɔrɛ, ka m boro m nyi di i jɔrɛ n na m se ka wɔ do ŋgasi.

eng: I told them it is contrary to Roman practice to hand someone over before they have faced their accusers and had opportunity to offer a defense against the charges.
local: Ama, na ma se bu ka yɛrɛ Roma­fɔm, ya yo má sɔ, nzɛn minɛm ajaraki sɔnɔ, ya ma bu kɛrɛ tiɛn bu nɔɔ ɔ, na i mi si deke n bo i ti na bu jaraki i ni, na i gusu nya kan i dɛɛ bo i la i ni.

eng: When they came here, I didnt put them off. The very next day I took my seat in the court and ordered that the man be brought before me.
local: Saŋga bo bu suuri m su ba juuri fɛn ni, ma wura má nnyɔ nu, i alecin m kaba kɔ tanaari jɔrɛ dii­biri n wɔ, na ma ma nɔɔ ka bu fa sɔnɔ n bra.

eng: When the accusers took the floor, they didnt charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.
local: Saŋga bo bu yasu jinaari na bu jaraki i ni, ba kan má satiɛ bo má m sunsun ka wɔ yo i na bu fa jaraki i ni.

eng: Instead, they quibbled with him about their own religion and about some dead man named Jesus, who Paul claimed was alive.
local: Ama, i ti akuruwa ye bu seeri a, fa kɔ bu nyɛmɛsu atin su, ni sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Yesu n busu, bu ŋa wɔ wu, ama, Pɔɔlu ŋa i toko la ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Since I had no idea how to investigate these matters, I asked if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem to stand trial there on these issues.
local: Ma si má kabo m tara ahin jɔrɛ n tii ni i jaa barasu, na ma bisa Pɔɔlu ka, i koro ka ya fa i kɔ Yɛrusalem na bu kɔ di i jɔrɛ n dɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: However, Paul appealed that he be held in custody pending a decision from His Majesty the emperor, so I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.
