local: Ama, na Pɔɔlu asere ka, i koro ka i ka dan­saraka n nu haari i ba kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ. Nyini ti, na ma ma nɔɔ ka bu subita i haari m ba nya atin na m ma bu fa i kɔ fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, I want to hear the man myself. Tomorrow, Festus replied, you will hear him.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ase Gomina Fɛsitosi ka, M koro ka mini ni m tii ti ahin sɔnɔ n jɔrɛ ni. Na Gomina Fɛsitosi ase i ka, Ahunma a tí i jɔrɛ ni.

eng: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great fanfare. They entered the auditorium with the military commanders and the citys most prominent men. Festus then ordered that Paul be brought in.
local: Tɔ, na i alecin, na Fɛmɛ Agiripa, ni Bɛrɛnisi afite ni bu fɛmɛya jirima bambaka, na bu ba wura tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ bambaka n nu, bu ni soja nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni miɛ n nu minɛ anyunuyi­fɔm. Na Gomina Fɛsitosi ama nɔɔ, na ba fa Pɔɔlu awura.

eng: Festus said, King Agrippa and everyone present with us: You see this man! The entire Jewish community, both here and in Jerusalem, has appealed to me concerning him. Theyve been calling for his immediate death.
local: Na Gomina Fɛsitosi ase ka, Ya si Fɛmɛ Agiripa, ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am atiɛn am nɔɔ fɛn ni, am niɛ ahin sɔnɔ ni. Yiri dɛɛ ti ma Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo Yɛrusalem, ni bo bu wo fɛn n yo baari m dɔ a, na bu sutiɛntiɛn ka i ti má ye ni ka i wo ni ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Ive found that he has done nothing deserving death. When he appealed to His Majesty, I decided to send him to Rome.
local: Ama, m gusu afiiti i jɔrɛ n nu, ma ŋu má i ŋgasi bo wɔ do i na i ti ye ni ka i wu. Nyini ti, kabo yiri ni i tii asere ka m fa i jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ ni, m fá i kɔ dɔ ɔ.

eng: I have nothing definite to write to our lord emperor. Therefore, Ive brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this investigation, I might have something to write.
local: Ama, m la má deke be tenle su bo m kɛrɛ fa ma fɛmɛ kpiri ni, fa kɔ ahin n jɔrɛ n su. Nyini ti ye ma fa i aba wɔrɔ Fɛmɛ Agiripa dɔ a, adena m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ niɛ i jɔrɛ n nu, na m si deke bo m kɛrɛ kɔ ma fɛmɛ kpiri ni.

eng: After all, it would be foolish to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.
local: Dama mini dɔ, wɔ kɔ má atin su ka m fa sɔnɔ bo ba nyi i dan­saraka kɔ ma fɛmɛ kpiri n na m la má jɔrɛ bo m kan kere i fa kɔ deke n bo i ti na bu jaraki i n su.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, You may speak for yourself. So Paul gestured with his hand and began his defense.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ase Pɔɔlu ka, Atin wo nu ka wɔrɔ ni u tii jɔjɔ. Na Pɔɔlu ayɛ i saa su, na wɔ bo i jɔrɛ n bu, i ŋa.

eng: King Agrippa, I consider myself especially fortunate that I stand before you today as I offer my defense concerning all the accusations the Jews have brought against me.
local: Fɛmɛ Agiripa, wɔ yo m fiɛ nyuma ka m jina u nyunu, na m kan m jɔrɛ n nahɔrɛ su fa kɔ deke n kɛrɛ bo Yuda­fɔm jaraki m n su.

eng: This is because you understand well all the Jewish customs and controversies. Therefore, I ask you to listen to me patiently.
local: Dama wɔrɔ ni u tii asi kabo Yuda­fɔm nana sa­yoo­wam n ti, ni kabo ya se bu akuruwam barasu ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, m sere u wɔ, ka a fa sawɔrɛ na a ti m jɔrɛ ni.

eng: Every Jew knows the way of life I have followed since my youth because, from the beginning, I was among my people and in Jerusalem.
local: Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ si kabo m woo­wa yo ti barasu fite m gbɛfɛnɛya nu, saŋga bo m wo m bɔsu­fɔm nu ni, haari ni bo m kɔ woori Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni.

eng: They have known me for a long time. If they wanted to, they could testify that I followed the way of life set out by the most exacting group of our religion. I am a Pharisee.
local: Bu si m sa kabo m woo­wa yo ti, má nyuma. Nzɛn bu ŋa bu kan nahɔrɛ, bu se u ka m yo ti Farasi­fɔm be wɔ, na ma su ya dɛɛ nyini atin n bo i su ti kekereke n su ni anyum­boro.

eng: Today I am standing trial because of the hope in the promise God gave our ancestors.
local: Kisa, deke bo i ti na m jina am nu nyuma na am sudi m jɔrɛ ni, yiri la tama n bo m la i fa kɔ deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó ma ya nam ni.

eng: This is the promise our twelve tribes hope to receive as they earnestly worship night and day. The Jews are accusing me, King Agrippa, because of this hope!.
local: Nyini n ye ya anɔ buru ni nnyɔ n bo ya su Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ n ŋminda ka ya nya i n a. Fɛmɛ, ti fɛn, nyini tama n bo m la i n dɛɛ ti, ye Yuda­fɔm jaraki m n a.

eng: Why is it inconceivable to you that God raises the dead?.
local: Nzu ti ye am de má ka Nyɛmɛ koro teŋge minɛm bu yiwee nu a?.

eng: I really thought that I ought to oppose the name of Jesus the Nazarene in every way possible.
local: Nahɔrɛ mini ni m tii yo sunsuunri ka wɔ yo daka ka m yo sam dɔŋgu na m saaki Yesu Nazarɛti­fɔ n duma.

eng: And thats exactly what I did in Jerusalem. I locked up many of Gods holy people in prison under the authority of the chief priests. When they were condemned to death, I voted against them.
local: Kabo m yo yoori Yɛrusalem lɛɛ. Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm yo maari m yiko, na ma tara Nyɛmɛ minɛm beberebe anyi bu dan­saraka, na saŋga bo bu diiri bu jɔrɛ na bu ŋa bu kun bu ni, mini mmɔɔ jina bu sin wɔ.

eng: In one synagogue after another—indeed, in all the synagogues—I would often torture them, compelling them to slander God. My rage bordered on the hysterical as I pursued them, even to foreign cities.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga, m nati wura ya tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔm nu wɔ, na m sucin bu sui, na m sumiɛ bu ka bu kete Yesu. Na ma fa yaa bu ŋu agaya, haari na m sukɔ miɛ nyiŋgam su, kɔ sukere bu wuwuruwa.

eng: On one such journey, I was going to Damascus with the full authority of the chief priests.
local: Na Pɔɔlu asa su ase ka, Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ma sere atin Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n dɔ, na ba ma m yiko ka m kɔ Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: While on the road at midday, King Agrippa, I saw a light from heaven shining around me and my traveling companions. That light was brighter than the sun.
local: Fɛmɛ, ti fɛn, m wo atin n nu wo kɔ su ni, fa kɔ wiɛ kekete saŋga, na ma ŋu sin be ka i fite aŋgoro, i ta tara wiɛ, na i wein ata abara ayɛ m ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu su m su ni.

eng: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice that said to me in Aramaic, Saul, Saul, why are you harassing me? Its hard for you to kick against a spear.
local: Na yɛrɛ kɛrɛ akpaasi haari asiɛŋgu, na ma ti akonvi be ŋga, ka i sujɔjɔ m dɔ Hiburu dɛɛ aniɛ n nu, i ŋa, Sɔɔlu, Sɔɔlu e, nzu ti ye a kere m wuwuruwa sɔ a? A sukere u ŋu yaari ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, kabo bu fa toora dɔdɔɔri­fɔ sudɔ na i kete, na bu fin i baka n wɔ.

eng: Then I said, Who are you, Lord? The Lord replied, I am Jesus, whom you are harassing.
local: Na ma bisa ka, M Mibiɛ, wɔrɔ ŋma a? Na Mibiɛ n abɔ nu ka, Mini Yesu ye a sukere m wuwuruwa n a.

eng: Get up! Stand on your feet! I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you as my servant and witness of what you have seen and what I will show you.
local: Ama, yasu jina. Deke n bo i ti na ma yi m ŋu akere u n la ka, m fa u yo m amusui­su­fɔ, na a di m daani kere minɛm fa kɔ deke bo a ŋu i nyuma n su, ni dekem bo m bá yi m ŋu kere u bekun n su.

eng: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you.
local: Na m dé u tii fite minɛm saa nu, Yuda­fɔm ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, dama m susunma u bɛrɛ dɔ a.

eng: to open their eyes. Then they can turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, and receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are made holy by faith in me.
local: na a teke bu nyi, na a yi bu fite awosin nu ba wein nu, na a yi bu fite Sitana saa nu, na a fa bu wura Nyɛmɛ saa nu, adena bu yo m yarada na m fa bu satiɛ ce bu, na bu nya jinaa­biri Nyɛmɛ minɛ n nu, na bu kɛrɛ saŋga wo.

eng: So, King Agrippa, I wasnt disobedient to that heavenly vision.
local: Na Pɔɔlu asa su ase ka, Fɛmɛ Agiripa, nyini dɛɛ ti ni, ma kete má deke n bo Nyɛmɛ yi kereeri m ka alɛlɛ nu dɛɛ su ni.

eng: Instead, I proclaimed first to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to the whole region of Judea and to the Gentiles. My message was that they should change their hearts and lives and turn to God, and that they should demonstrate this change in their behavior.
local: Nyini ti, m du mɔ boori i bu Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na ma kan akere bu ka bu kaaki bu wo na bu su Nyɛmɛ, na bu yo sakpa bo i kere ka ba kaaki bu wo. Na ma kɔ akan akere Yɛrusalem­fɔm n gusu, na ma nya akɔ akan akere Yudiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu fɔm kɛrɛ. Nyini sin, na ma kɔ akan akere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n gusu.

eng: Because of this, some Jews seized me in the temple and tried to murder me.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Yuda­fɔm taraari m Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, ka bu kun m n a.

eng: God has helped me up to this very day. Therefore, I stand here and bear witness to the lowly and the great. Im saying nothing more than what the Prophets and Moses declared would happen.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ abuka m dɛɛ ti, ye m toko wo bɛrɛ haari ni nyuma n a, adena m jina fɛn di daani kere nfaŋga­fɔm ni tarakam kɛrɛ, ni minɛ barasum kɛrɛ. M sukan má jɔrɛ fɔfɔrɛ be wɔ, má ka sam bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni Moyisi yo seeri ka bu yó ni.

eng: that the Christ would suffer and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to my people and to the Gentiles.
local: ka, wɔ yo daka ka Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i ni bá di wahara. Yiri ti sɔnɔ bo i wú na i dú mɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu a. Na i kán wein n jɔrɛ kere Yuda­fɔm, ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ.

eng: At this point in Pauls defense, Festus declared with a loud voice, Youve lost your mind, Paul! Too much learning is driving you mad.
local: Pɔɔlu wo nyini jɔjɔɔri n su na i kan i nahɔrɛ ni, na Gomina Fɛsitosi atiɛn i ŋu kpa, i ŋa, Pɔɔlu, a bo fiɛ wɔ. U kara sɔnsɔn ni u si agaya n ma u tii yo má juma.

eng: But Paul replied, Im not mad, most honorable Festus! Im speaking what is sound and true.
local: Na Pɔɔlu abɔ i nu ka, Gomina Fɛsitosi jirima­fɔ, ma bo má fiɛ, ama, m jɔjɔ anyuŋkoro ni nahɔrɛ jɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: King Agrippa knows about these things, and I have been speaking openly to him. Im certain that none of these things have escaped his attention. This didnt happen secretly or in some out-of-the-way place.
local: Fɛmɛ Agiripa si ahin jɔrɛm kɛrɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m la ahoresi jɔjɔ i nyunu n a. M si tenle su ka i si ahin sam n kɛrɛ bo m sukan kere am ni, dama i be mmɔɔ ayo má fiɛɛ­wa nu.

eng: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.
local: Fɛmɛ Agiripa, a yo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm jɔrɛ n yarada a? M si tenle su ka a yo yarada.

eng: Agrippa said to Paul, Are you trying to convince me that, in such a short time, youve made me a Christian.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ase Pɔɔlu ka, Ahin saŋga kaan nu, a sunsun ka a koro jɔjɔ na a ma m kaaki Kristo susu­fɔ ɔ, nzɛn sɛ.

eng: Paul responded, Whether it is a short or a long time, I pray to God that not only you but also all who are listening to me today will become like me, except for these chains.
local: Na Pɔɔlu abɔ i nu ka, Nzɛn i cɛ o, i cɛ má o, mini yiri, m nyɛmɛsere la ka, wɔrɔ ni minɛm n kɛrɛ bo m sujɔjɔ am dɔ nyuma ni, am ba kaaki na am yo kabo m ti barasu ni, ama, bu wura má am jɔrɔkɔ kabo ba wura m ni.

eng: The king stood up, as did the governor, Bernice, and those sitting with them.
local: I seeri sɔ ni, na fɛmɛ n ayasu ajina, yiri ni gomina ni, ni Bɛrɛnisi, ni nyiŋgam n kɛrɛ bo bu tana bɛrɛ ni.

eng: As they left, they were saying to each other, This man is doing nothing that deserves death or imprisonment.
local: Bu yasuuri na bu wo kɔ su ni, na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n ayo má sa be bo i ti ye ni ka bu kun i, wara bu nyi i dan­saraka nu.

eng: Agrippa said to Festus, This man could have been released if he hadnt appealed to Caesar.
local: Na Fɛmɛ Agiripa ase Gomina Fɛsitosi ka, Nzɛn má ahin sɔnɔ n asere má ka bu fa i jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ, má ya koro yaki i nu ji.

eng: When it was determined that we were to sail to Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were placed in the custody of a centurion named Julius of the Imperial Company.
local: Tɔ, saŋga bo bu faari sɛsɛ­su ka ya fa lee bambaka kɔ Roma miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Itali n asiɛn n su ni, na ba fa Pɔɔlu ni dan­saraka nyiŋga nbem ama soja kpiɛn be bo bu fere i ka Juliyosi ni, yiri niɛ soja asɛsɛ be su a, bu fere i ka Roma­fɔm Fɛmɛ Kpiri Agusito dɛɛm.

eng: We boarded a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail for ports along the coast of the province of Asia. So we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, came with us.
local: Na ya kɔ awura lee bambaka be, i fite miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Adaramuteno n wɔ. Lee n ayo siriya sukɔ Esiya awɔrɛ n nu dɛɛ miɛ nbem su, na ya wura i akɔ. Yɛrɛ ni sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Arisitakusi n kɔɔri a, i fite Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n bo i wo Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu wɔ.

eng: The next day we landed in Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly and permitted him to go to some friends so they could take care of him.
local: Na i alecin, na ya kɔ aju Sidon miɛ kpiri n nu, na Juliyosi ayo Pɔɔlu sakpa, i maari i atin wɔ, na wɔ kɔ aniɛ i danfom busu, adena i kɔ nya deke bo i koro.

eng: From there we sailed off. We passed Cyprus, using the island to shelter us from the headwinds.
local: Ya yasuuri Sidon ka ya kɔ ni, ya faari Sipurusi lui bambaka n dɛɛ bue n wɔ, ya koro fa má deke bo má ya sukoro ka ya fa ni, dama aŋuma n cin ya kɔ ya sin wɔ.

eng: We sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, and landed in Myra in Lycia.
local: Ya yasu bɛrɛ ni, na ya kpiɛ tieku n bo i wo Silisiya, ni Pamfiliya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu ni, na ya nya akɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Mira na i wo Lisiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu ni.

eng: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship headed for Italy and put us on board.
local: Dɔ na soja kpiɛn n aŋu lee bambaka be ka i fite Alɛkizanderiya miɛ kpiri n nu sukɔ Itali, na wɔ fa ya awura nu.

eng: After many days of slow and difficult sailing, we arrived off the coast of Cnidus. The wind wouldnt allow us to go farther, so we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
local: Ya yasuuri bɛrɛ ka ya kɔ ni, aŋuma n kpa ya atin wɔ, ya koro akɔ má ndende. I yoori kekereke ye ya nya kɔ dodoori miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Kinidosi n a. Aŋuma n dɛɛ ti, ya koro akɔ má deke bo má ya sukɔ ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ya fa lui bambaka n bo bu fere i ka Kereti dɛɛ bue ni, akpiɛ akɔ adodo miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Salamone ni.

eng: We sailed along the coast only with difficulty until we came to a place called Good Harbors, near the city of Lasea.
local: Ya faari bɛrɛ ni, i yoori ya kekereke ye ya nya kɔ juuri miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Dika Nyunyumi n a, lee ŋgbiŋgbirim jinaa­biri ni, i mata miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Lasiya n wɔ.

eng: Much time had been lost, and the voyage was now dangerous since the Day of Reconciliation had already passed. Paul warned them.
local: Na ya wo dɔ acɛ, haari na Yuda­fɔm nɔɔ cici n aba asin, bu ŋa nyini saŋga ni, tieku n su natiiri ti tiɛ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Pɔɔlu abɔbɔ bu, i ŋa.

eng: Men, I see that our voyage will suffer damage and great loss, not only for the cargo and ship but also for our lives.
local: Njam, ma ŋu ka nzɛn ya ŋa ya kɔ ya nyunu, i yó kekereke, na deke dɔŋgu sáaki, má lee bambaka n ni neŋgem ŋgumi, ama, ya ŋgɔɔ mmɔɔ wo nu.

eng: But the centurion was persuaded more by the ships pilot and captain than by Pauls advice.
local: Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, soja kpiɛn n keteeri Pɔɔlu nɔaniɛ n wɔ, na wɔ de lee­kan­fɔ n ni lee n fɔ n dɛɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Since the harbor was unsuitable for spending the winter, the majority supported a plan to put out to sea from there. They thought they might reach Phoenix in Crete and spend the winter in its harbor, which faced southwest and northwest.
local: Deke bo ya wo ni, i ti má kpa ka ya wo dɔ haari fufuku ba tu ya, nyini dɛɛ ti, na ya dɔŋgu asoro su ka ya yasu dɔ, na ya niɛ, beni, ya koro kɔ ju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Foniki n su na i wo Kereti lui bambaka n su ni. Lee ŋgbiŋgbirim jinaa­biri wo dɔ ɔ, i nɔɔ wɔ wiɛ atɔɔri dɛɛ bue n wɔ, ya koro kɔ wo dɔ, haari na fufuku n ba sin.

eng: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they could carry out their plan. They pulled up anchor and sailed closely along the coast of Crete.
local: Na aŋuma fafari be sukpiɛ fite ya nyunu ndiɛma biɛ su. Na ba sunsun ka bu koro kɔ deke bo ba fa bu tii awɔ ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba cin atu bulaari n bo bu fa kpamaari lee ni, na ya san tieku n ahɛmɛ akɔ adodo Kereti lui bambaka n dɛɛ bue ni.

eng: Before long, a hurricane-strength wind known as a northeaster swept down from Crete.
local: Ama, wɔ cɛ má, na sɔɔ sin aŋuma be bo i nati kpiɛ fite wiɛ afiiri saa fɔmbɔrɔ su n akpiɛ faŋga su afite Kereti dɔ.

eng: The ship was caught in the storm and couldnt be turned into the wind. So we gave in to it, and it carried us along.
local: Ya kpa aŋuma n atin wɔ, ya koro akɔ má deke bo ya fa ya tii awɔ ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ya yaki lee bambaka ni, na wɔ su aŋuma n su.

eng: After sailing under the shelter of an island called Cauda, we were able to control the lifeboat only with difficulty.
local: Na ya kɔ adodo lui be bo bu fere i ka Kawuda ni, na ya fa i sin. Lee kaan be sa lee bambaka n su, i yoori kekereke ye ya nya koro taraari i jinaari a.

eng: They brought the lifeboat aboard, then began to wrap the ship with cables to hold it together. Fearing they might run aground on the sandbars of the Gulf of Syrtis, they lowered the anchor and let the ship be carried along.
local: Na ba nya acin i afun awura lee bambaka n nu. Na ba fa nyama awura lee bambaka n bu, na ba cici i kenken, na i bu n ayo faŋga. Bu soro sɛrɛ ka lee ni kpama tɛtɛkɛ n dika wɔ, miɛ kaan be wo bɛrɛ, bu fere i ka Siritisi. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba jura lee tani n bo i bo aŋuma na lee n kɔ ni, na ba nyanya i, na lee n asu aŋuma n su.

eng: We were so battered by the violent storm that the next day the men began throwing cargo overboard.
local: Kabo aŋuma n kereeri ya wahara sɔ ni, i alecin, na ba yiyi neŋgem bo bu wo lee n nu ni nbem ajiji nzue n nu.

eng: On the third day, they picked up the ships gear and hurled it into the sea.
local: I le­san cɛɛn, na bu kɛrɛ afa bu saa ayi lee n dɛɛ neŋge kɛrɛ aji nzue n nu.

eng: When neither the sun nor the moon appeared for many days and the raging storm continued to pound us, all hope of our being saved from this peril faded.
local: Na wɔ cɛ bo ya ŋu má wiɛ, wara asara­mbaam, na aŋuma n sukpiɛ agaya sɔ, haari na ya ba ayi tama ka ya fite má nu.

eng: For a long time no one had eaten. Paul stood up among them and said, Men, you should have complied with my instructions not to sail from Crete. Then we would have avoided this damage and loss.
local: Tɔ, wɔ cɛ bo minɛm n adi má diire n dɛɛ ti, na Pɔɔlu ayasu ajina bu afiɛn, na wɔ se bu ka, Njam, nzɛn má am tiiri m nɔaniɛ ni, má ya yasu má Kereti, má ya neŋgem ni saaki má, na bu ka ŋgbɛɛn sɔ.

eng: Now I urge you to be encouraged. Not one of your lives will be lost, though we will lose the ship.
local: Ama, kisa, m sutu am fɔ ɔ, am ma am ahoresi. Am be mmɔɔ ŋgɔɔ ká má nu, má ka lee bambaka ni ŋgumi saaki a.

eng: Last night an angel from the God to whom I belong and whom I worship stood beside me.
local: Dama anuma kɔŋguɛ, Nyɛmɛ n bo m su i na i dɛɛ la m ni, i mɛrɛkɛ be ba jinaari m bi.

eng: The angel said, Dont be afraid, Paul! You must stand before Caesar! Indeed, God has also graciously given you everyone sailing with you.
local: na wɔ se m ka, Pɔɔlu, má a ma sɛrɛ ti u. Wɔ yo daka ka a kɔ́ jina Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n nyunu wɔ, na i dí u jɔrɛ. Ti fɛn, Nyɛmɛ afa minɛm kɛrɛ bo u ni bɛrɛ saŋga wo lee n nu n ama u.

eng: Be encouraged, men! I have faith in God that it will be exactly as he told me.
local: Nyini ti, njam, am fa ahoresi. Dama m daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu ka i yó kabo i seeri m n wɔ.

eng: However, we must run aground on some island.
local: Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, aŋuma ni sún ya kɔ jina lui bambaka be su wɔ.

eng: On the fourteenth night, we were being carried across the Adriatic Sea. Around midnight the sailors began to suspect that land was near.
local: Tɔ, i bita ya fikindi Adiriya tieku n nu sɔ ɔ, haari i le­buru le­nna cɛɛn kɔŋguɛ fa kɔ i jinanjin saŋga, na lee juma­yo­fɔm n afa nu ka bu sudodo asiɛn.

eng: They dropped a weighted line to take soundings and found the water to be about one hundred twenty feet deep. After proceeding a little farther, we took soundings again and found the water to be about ninety feet deep.
local: Nyini ti, na ba fa nyama bo bu bita sunsun niɛ kabo nzue ŋgoŋgo ti barasu. Na ba ŋu ka i toko wo ŋgo. Na ba kɔ mɔ kaan abuka su, na ba sunsun bekun, na ba ŋu ka i wo má ŋgo kun.

eng: Afraid that we might run aground somewhere on the rocks, they hurled out four anchors from the stern and began to pray for daylight.
local: Na sɛrɛ ati bu, bu sunsun ka lee bambaka ni kɔ́ si yabuɛm wɔ, na i sandi nu, nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba cɔɔndi lee n sin bulaari nɔnɔɔri nna asiɛŋgu. Na bu susere Nyɛmɛ ka ale cin bu.

eng: The sailors tried to abandon the ship by lowering the lifeboat into the sea, pretending they were going to lower anchors from the bow.
local: Na lee juma­yo­fɔm n akoro ka bu wɔ ŋmati, na ba yi lee kaan n afa ase nzue n su, na ba kpiɛ mbɛnlɛ ka bu suyi bulaari nɔnɔɔri nbem lee bambaka n tii dika a.

eng: Paul said to the centurion and his soldiers, Unless they stay in the ship, you cant be saved from peril.
local: Ama, na Pɔɔlu ase soja kpiɛn n ni i sojam ni ka, Má ka ahin minɛm n aka lee n nu, am be mmɔɔ fíte má nu.

eng: The soldiers then cut the ropes to the lifeboat and let it drift away.
local: Na sojam n akpiɛ nyamam bo bu bita lee kaan n aji, na wɔ kɔ.

eng: Just before daybreak, Paul urged everyone to eat. He said, This is the fourteenth day youve lived in suspense, and youve not had even a bite to eat.
local: Saŋga bo ale ba sukoro cin ni, na Pɔɔlu asere minɛm kɛrɛ ka, bu nya diire be di. I ŋa, I nyuma le­buru le­nna lɛɛ, bo am wo ahoresaaki nu, na am adi má pui.

eng: I urge you to take some food. Your health depends on it. None of you will lose a single hair from his head.
local: Nyini ti, m sere am wɔ, am di diire, adena i buka am na am nya ŋgɔɔ. Haari am be mmɔɔ tii­ŋmiɛ kun n kpein ká má ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: After he said these things, he took bread, gave thanks to God in front of them all, then broke it and began to eat.
local: Pɔɔlu seeri sɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi bu kɛrɛ nyunu, na wɔ bu i nu, na wɔ di.

eng: Everyone was encouraged and took some food. .
local: Na bu kɛrɛ afa ahoresi, na bɛrɛ gusu adi diire.

eng: In all, there were two hundred seventy-six of us on the ship.
local: Yɛrɛ bo ya yo woori lee bambaka n nu ni, ya ti minɛ yaa nnyɔ ni minɛ abura­so ni nsɛn wɔ.

eng: When they had eaten as much as they wanted, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.
local: Bɛrɛ kɛrɛ diiri na bu kunnu yiiri ni, na ba sasa diire mbaa n bo wɔ ka n agɔ nzue n nu, adena lee bambaka n ka fafaki.

eng: In the morning light they saw a bay with a sandy beach. They didnt know what land it was, but they thought they might possibly be able to run the ship aground.
local: Saŋga bo ale ba ciinri ni, lee juma­yo­fɔm ŋuuri asiɛn be, ba si má i, ama, na ba ŋu ka nzue be ati ada bɛrɛ, ŋmiɛ abara ayɛ i. Na ba fa sunsunni ka nzɛn má bu koro yo, na ba boro bu nyi na bu fa lee bambaka n kɔ wura ŋmiɛ n nu na i kpama.

eng: They cut the anchors loose and left them in the sea. At the same time, they untied the ropes that ran back to the rudders. They raised the foresail to catch the wind and made for the beach.
local: Na ba nyaŋgi lee bambaka n bulaari nɔnɔɔrim n aji nzue n nu, na ba nyaŋgi bakam bo bu fa kan lee n dɛɛ nyamam ni, na ba yɛ tani n bo i wo lee n tii su n aŋgoro ka aŋuma n fa lee n kɔ, na bu sukan sukɔ ahɛmɛ.

eng: But they struck a sandbar and the ship ran aground. The bow was stuck and wouldnt move, and the stern was broken into pieces by the force of the waves.
local: Ama, na ba kɔ aju kunma kpiri saŋgaa­biri, na ba wura ŋmiɛ be nu, na lee bambaka n tii dika n aka akpama, wɔ koro akɔ má. Na nzue nyama ni subo lee ni, na wɔ paparaki i sin dika ni.

eng: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming to shore and escaping.
local: Na sojam n asɛsɛ su ka bu kun dan­sarakam n kɛrɛ wɔ, adena má be wiɛ kpiɛ na i ŋmati kɔ.

eng: However, the centurion wanted to save Paul, so he stopped them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and head for land.
local: Ama, soja kpiɛn n koroori ka i de Pɔɔlu tii ji n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ kete bu ŋu ka má bu yo sɔ. Na wɔ ma nɔɔ ka, bo bu si nzue wiɛ n kɛrɛ, bu du mɔ ti to nu na bu wiɛ kpiɛ kpɛrɛ su.

eng: He ordered the rest to grab hold of planks or debris from the ship. In this way, everyone reached land safely.
local: na minɛm bo bu kaari n kɛrɛ nya so baka sinsinm, ni lee buebuem n nu, na bu wiɛ kɔ ju ahɛmɛ. Kabo i yoori na ya kɛrɛ koro fiteeri nu, na pui ayo má be n lɛɛ.

eng: After reaching land safely, we learned that the island was called Malta.
local: Saŋga bo ya fiteeri nzue n nu wieeri ni, na ba se ya ka bu fere lui bambaka n ka Malite.

eng: The islanders showed us extraordinary kindness. Because it was rainy and cold, they built a fire and welcomed all of us.
local: Na miɛ n nu­fɔm ade ya na ba yo ya sakpa agaya. Nzue sutɔ na ayɛrɛ wo bɛrɛ n dɛɛ ti, na ba tɔrɔ sin ama ya, na ya kɛrɛ atun.

eng: Paul gathered a bunch of dry sticks and put them on the fire. As he did, a poisonous snake, driven out by the heat, latched on to his hand.
local: Na Pɔɔlu atiɛntiɛn yee nɔɔ agɔ sin n nu, jaa pumpuni wo yee n nu, na sin n waraŋgu awura i, na wɔ fite akan i saa aka asense su.

eng: When the islanders saw the snake hanging from his hand, they said to each other, This man must be a murderer! He was rescued from the sea, but the goddess Justice hasnt let him live.
local: Miɛ n nu­fɔm ni ŋuuri ka woo n akan asense Pɔɔlu saa su sɔ ni, na ba se bu ŋuŋu nu ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n ti sɔnɔ kun­fɔ ɔ nahɔrɛ su, haari ni bo wɔ koro afite n nzue n nu ni, i nyɛmɛ atin su, wɔ yo má daka ka i nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Paul shook the snake into the fire and suffered no harm.
local: Ama, na Pɔɔlu akpisi woo n aji sin n nu, wɔ ŋu má yaari be.

eng: They expected him to swell up with fever or suddenly drop dead. After waiting a long time and seeing nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and began to claim that he was a god.
local: Na minɛm n asunsun ka i cɛ́ má, i ŋuniɛ wúra a, wara i kába kpaasi wu. Ama, bu ŋminda cɛɛri na bu ŋu má ka deke be ayo i ni, na ba kaaki bu sunsunni, na ba se ka, I ti nyɛmɛ be wɔ.

eng: Publius, the islands most prominent person, owned a large estate in that area. He welcomed us warmly into his home as his guests for three days.
local: Deke bo ya sekeeri ni, fie be wo dodo dɔ, i fɔ n ti lui bambaka n su sɔnɔ anyumbaa­fɔ ɔ, bu fere i ka Pubiliyosi. Na wɔ de ya wɔfɔ nyunyumi, na ya ka ada i dika haari le­san.

eng: Publius father was bedridden, sick with a fever and dysentery. Paul went to see him and prayed. He placed his hand on him and healed him.
local: Nyini saŋga Pubiliyosi si gusu la má laifiɛ, i da sɔrɔ, i ŋuniɛ ado, na i suje tɔtɔkɔ. Na Pɔɔlu awura sɔɔ n bo i da nu ni, na wɔ fa i saa anana i ŋu, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ama i, na wɔ nya laifiɛ.

eng: Once this happened, the rest of the sick on the island came to him and were healed.
local: I yoori sɔ n dɛɛ sin, na tukpaki­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo lui bambaka n su n gusu aba Pɔɔlu dɔ, na wɔ tuntun bu, na ba nya laifiɛ.

eng: They honored us in many ways. When we were getting ready to sail again, they supplied us with what we needed.
local: Na ba wura ya kpiri, na ba ce ya aceerim beberebe. Na saŋga bo ya ba suyasu ka ya fa lee na ya kɔ ni, na ba fa deke kɛrɛ bo i ti ya miɛɛ­wa awura lee bambaka n nu ama ya.

eng: After three months we put out to sea in a ship that had spent the winter at the island. It was an Alexandrian ship with carvings of the twin gods Castor and Pollux as its figurehead.
local: Ya woori dɔ haari sara nsan, na ya nya fa lee bambaka be bo i fite Alɛkizanderiya dɛɛ miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ. Amɔɛ ndaam anyaari see­wa mata i. Fufuku saŋga n dɛɛ ti, wɔ jina dɔ acɛ.

eng: We landed in Syracuse where we stayed three days.
local: Na ya kɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Sarakusasi n su, na ya wo dɔ le­san.

eng: From there we sailed to Rhegium. After one day a south wind came up, and we arrived on the second day in Puteoli.
local: Na ya nya akaaki ayasu bɛrɛ bekun, akɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Regiyoni n su. Na i alecin, na aŋuma abo i bu sukpiɛ fite ya nyunu ndiɛma saa biɛ su. Na i le­nnyɔ cɛɛn n na ya kɔ aju miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Potiyoli n su.

eng: There we found brothers and sisters who urged us to stay with them for a week. In this way we came to Rome.
local: Na ya ŋu yarada­yo­fɔ nbem dɔ, na ba sere ya ka ya wo bu dɔ le­so. Na ya nya yasu aba Roma miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: When the brothers and sisters there heard about us, they came as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns to meet us. When Paul saw them, he gave thanks to God and was encouraged.
local: Saŋga bo yarada­yo­fɔm n bo bu wo Roma n tiiri ya ŋga ka ya suba ni, na ba fite akpa ya atin, na ya ni bu nbem akɔ ayɛ miɛ n bue bo bu fere i ka Apiyɔsi Gɔɔ n su, na ya ni bu nbem gusu akɔ ayɛ Wɔfɔm Daa­biri Awuru Nsan miɛ n nu. Saŋga bo Pɔɔlu ŋuuri bu ni, na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ nya ahoresi.

eng: When we entered Rome, Paul was permitted to live by himself, with a soldier guarding him.
local: Saŋga bo ya kɔ juuri Roma miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na ba ma Pɔɔlu atin ka i nya awuru be na yiri ni i tii suwo nu, na ba ma i soja kun ka i suniɛ i dika.

eng: Three days later, Paul called the Jewish leaders together. When they gathered, he said, Brothers, although I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our ancestors, Im a prisoner from Jerusalem. They handed me over to the Romans.
local: I woori dɔ i le­san, nyini sin, na Pɔɔlu afere Yuda­fɔm bo bu la anyunuyi na bu wo miɛ n nu ni, ka bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ. Bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ wieeri ni, na wɔ se bu ka, M ninbaam, ma yo má ya minɛm n sa be, ma bu má ya nana sa­yoo­wa n be ŋgbɛɛn, ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, bu taraari m Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na ba nya afa m awura Roma­fɔm saa nu.

eng: who intended to release me after they examined me, because they couldnt find any reason for putting me to death.
local: Bɛrɛ gusu bisaari m bisaa­wam wieeri ni, má bu ŋa bu fa m ji wɔ, dama ba ŋu má satiɛ bo ma yo i na i ti ye ni ka bu kun m.

eng: So what are we going to say? Should we continue sinning so grace will multiply?.
local: Tɔ, ye ya se sɛ wɔ? Ya koro kɔ su suyo satiɛ, adena Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma buka su, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: From Paul, a slave of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for Gods good news.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n fa ma am a. M ti Yesu Kristo akɔɔ ɔ, ye wɔ fere m ka m kaaki i jaramasa, na wɔ yi m ka m kan labari kpa n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ n jɔrɛ.

eng: When the Jews objected, I was forced to appeal to Caesar. Dont think I appealed to Caesar because I had any reason to bring charges against my nation.
local: Ama, na Yuda­fɔm n akete ka má Roma­fɔm n yaki m nu ji, na wɔ yo daka ka m sere ka bu fa m jɔrɛ n kɔ Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri n dɔ, haari ni bo m la má jɔrɛ be bo m fa jaraki m tiɛŋgu minɛm ni.

eng: So what are we going to say? Are we going to find that Abraham is our ancestor on the basis of genealogy?.
local: Tɔ, am ma ya niɛ ya na Abraham jɔrɛ. Ya busu nu, yiri ti ya kɛrɛ na a.

eng: Absolutely not! All of us died to sin. How can we still live in it?.
local: Ai. I koro yo má sɔ. I ti ka ya ti minɛm bo ba du mɔ awu wɔ, fa kɔ satiɛ su. Ye nzu ti ye ya toko yo satiɛ bekun a?.

eng: So every single one of you who judge others is without any excuse. You condemn yourself when you judge another person because the one who is judging is doing the same things.
local: Tɔ, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a kan nyini minɛm jɔrɛ ni, a la má kabo a yo na a kan fite nu Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Dama jɔrɛ n bo a kan jaraki u beŋgu na wɔrɔ ni u tii yo nyini sam ni, nyini ma a kan jaraki u ŋu n a.

eng: Therefore, since we have been made righteous through his faithfulness, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɔ, ya yo yarada, na Nyɛmɛ ade ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, ye ya ni yiri afiɛn ayo dɔŋgu a, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Nyini labari kpa n ye Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i fá ma ya n a, na wɔ ma i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm akɛrɛ i ŋusu jɔrɛ ase i jɔrɛ kadasi n nu.

eng: This is why I asked to see you and speak with you: its because of the hope of Israel that I am bound with this chain.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m fere am a, na m jɔjɔ am dɔ na am si bo i wo bɛrɛ, ka, sɔnɔ n bo Yisareli­fɔm yo i tama n dɛɛ ti, ye m wo ahin jɔrɔkɔ n nu a.

eng: Because if Abraham was made righteous because of his actions, he would have had a reason to brag, but not in front of God.
local: Nzɛn má i sakpa yoo­wa dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, má i koro fa fɔ yanfɔya, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i ti má sɔ.

eng: Or dont you know that all who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?.
local: Am si má Nyɛmɛ nzue n biɛ n bu wɔ? Yɛrɛ bo bu biɛɛri ya Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun lɛɛ, na ya ni yiri asaŋga awu yiwee kun lɛɛ.

eng: We know that Gods judgment agrees with the truth, and his judgment is against those who do these kinds of things.
local: Ya si kabo Nyɛmɛ di minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu yo sɔ n jɔrɛ, na i jaraki bu ni, i jɔrɛ dii­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: We have access by faith into this grace in which we stand through him, and we boast in the hope of Gods glory.
local: Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti ma ya la atin jina deke bo ya wo kisa n a, yiri la Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama ya ni, na ya la ahorejɔ, dama ya la tama ka ya nyá Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n be.

eng: God promised this good news about his Son ahead of time through his prophets in the holy scriptures. His Son was descended from David.
local: Nyini labari kpa n kere ya i Wa n jɔrɛ wɔ, nzɛn ya niɛ i fa kɔ i adamande dɛɛ woo­wa su, bu yo wuuri i fiteeri Fɛmɛ Davidi dɛɛ busu n nu n wɔ.

eng: They responded, We havent received any letters about you from Judea, nor have any of our brothers come and reported or said anything bad about you.
local: Na ba se ka, Ya nya de má kadasi be Yudiya­fɔm dɔ fa kɔ u jɔrɛ su. Ya minɛm bo bu fiteeri dɔ baari n gusu, be nya kan má, wara i jɔjɔ jɔrɛ tiɛ be fa kɔ u dɔ.

eng: What does the scripture say? Abraham had faith in God, and it was credited to him as righteousness .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Abraham yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada, nyini dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a.

eng: Therefore, we were buried together with him through baptism into his death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too can walk in newness of life.
local: Saŋga bo bu biɛɛri ya Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, i ti ka ya ni Yesu asaŋga awu, na ba kaaki ase ya ni yiri wɔ lɛɛ. Na kabo ya Si Nyɛmɛ Yiko­fɔ n teŋgeeri Yesu i yiwee nu ni, sɔ ye i má ya gusu nya nyini ŋgɔɔ fɔfɔrɛ n a.

eng: If you judge those who do these kinds of things while you do the same things yourself, think about this: Do you believe that you will escape Gods judgment?.
local: Ye wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a kan minɛm n bo bu yo nyini sam n jɔrɛ jaraki bu, na wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii yo sɔ ni, a sunsun ka a koro fite Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ dii­wa n nu, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: But not only that! We even take pride in our problems, because we know that trouble produces endurance.
local: Na nzɛn ya gusu la ahorejɔ saŋga bo ya di wahara, ya si ka wahara di ni ma ya mindi a.

eng: He was publicly identified as Gods Son with power through his resurrection from the dead, which was based on the Spirit of holiness. This Son is Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ faari i yiko teŋgeeri i yiwee nu ni, nyini ma Awiɛwiɛ Casi n kere ka i ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a, yiri la ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ni.

eng: So whats the advantage of being a Jew? Or whats the benefit of circumcision?.
local: Tɔ, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, bu la nzu kpɛrɛ wɔ? Ye kɛnkɛnnɛ wura buka nzu wɔ?.

eng: But we think its important to hear what you think, for we know that people everywhere are speaking against this faction.
local: Ama, ya koro ka ya ti fite u dɔ kabo u sunsunni ti, dama ya si ka nyini atin n bo a bita i ni, deke kɛrɛ bo a kɔ, minɛ jaraki i wɔ.

eng: Workers salaries arent credited to them on the basis of an employers grace but rather on the basis of what they deserve.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ yo juma na ba tɔ i kaari, i ti má ka bu ŋa ba ce i aceeri wɔ, i ti i juma ni ŋkatɔ ɔ.

eng: If we were united together in a death like his, we will also be united together in a resurrection like his.
local: Ya ti kabo i ti n wɔ, saŋga bo i wuuri ni, sɔ ye wɔ yo daka ka ya bá ti kabo i ti n a, i yiwee teŋge n nu.

eng: Or do you have contempt for the riches of Gods generosity, tolerance, and patience? Dont you realize that Gods kindness is supposed to lead you to change your heart and life?.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ ayo sakpa bambaka ama u na i la mindi ni sawɔrɛ ni, nzu ti ye a bu i ŋgbɛɛn a? A si má ka deke bo i ti na i yoori nyini sakpa n la ka, i koro ka a kaaki u wo wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: endurance produces character, and character produces hope.
local: Nzɛn ya la mindi, nyini ma ya la wo kpa a, nzɛn ya la wo kpa, nyini ma ya la tama a.

eng: Through him we have received Gods grace and our appointment to be apostles. This was to bring all Gentiles to faithful obedience for his names sake.
local: Yiri dɛɛ ti, ma di Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma, ye wɔ ma ma yo i jaramasa, adena i duma n dɛɛ ti, na m ma daanfiɛ barasu kɛrɛ de i nɔaniɛ ni, na bu yo i yarada.

eng: Plenty in every way. First of all, the Jews were trusted with Gods revelations.
local: Nahɔrɛ, Yuda­fɔm tara minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni deke dɔŋgu nu. Ciciri su, Nyɛmɛ du mɔ faari i nɔaniɛm n wuraari bɛrɛ saa nu a, ka bu niɛ i su.

eng: On the day scheduled for this purpose, many people came to the place where he was staying. From morning until evening, he explained and testified concerning Gods kingdom and tried to convince them about Jesus through appealing to the Law from Moses and the Prophets.
local: Na ba kpama cɛɛn ka bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ. Nyini cɛɛn n na minɛ beberebe aba atiɛn bu nɔɔ deke bo Pɔɔlu da ni. Na wɔ kan akere bu kabo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n atin ti barasu. Fite ŋgarama haari kɔŋguɛ, na wɔ boro i nyi na i ma bu si kabo Yesu ti barasu, fite kabo ba kɛrɛ i Moyisi dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa kadasi n nu ni, ni kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm dɛɛ kadasi n nu ni.

eng: But faith is credited as righteousness to those who dont work, because they have faith in God who makes the ungodly righteous.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ bo i yoori yarada, má ka i sakpa bo wɔ yo i n dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a, ama, wɔ yo yarada ka Nyɛmɛ de satiɛ­yo­fɔ ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n dɛɛ ti a.

eng: This is what we know: the person that we used to be was crucified with him in order to get rid of the corpse that had been controlled by sin. That way we wouldnt be slaves to sin anymore.
local: Ya si ka, ya woo­wa dawa ni, ba saŋga akpama i ni Kristo i baka jaanu n su, adena má ya yo akɔɔm fa kɔ satiɛ su kun, dama aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i yo satiɛ n wo má bɛrɛ kun.

eng: You are storing up wrath for yourself because of your stubbornness and your heart that refuses to change. Gods just judgment will be revealed on the day of wrath.
local: Ama, kabo a yo u ahore­mbaa kekereke na a kete ka a kaaki má u wo ni, a sukpini sui­cin ma u ŋu wɔ lɛɛ, cɛɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ bá di minɛm jɔrɛ nahɔrɛ su, na i cín bu sui na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋú i ni.

eng: This hope doesnt put us to shame, because the love of God has been poured out in our hearts through the Holy Spirit, who has been given to us.
local: Na tama n bo ya la i ni, i ma ya di má nyinsɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama ya, yiri ma ya si ya ahore su kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ya agaya barasu a.

eng: You who are called by Jesus Christ are also included among these Gentiles.
local: Ambɛrɛ Roma­fɔm gusu, am wo nyini minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ afere bu n nu wɔ, ka am kaaki Yesu Kristo dɛɛ minɛm.

eng: What does it matter, then, if some werent faithful? Their lack of faith wont cancel Gods faithfulness, will it?.
local: Ye nzɛn Nyɛmɛ fa wuraari bɛrɛ saa nu a, ye bu nbem ayo má yarada, nyini kere ka Nyɛmɛ la má dabuŋu lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: Some were persuaded by what he said, but others refused to believe.
local: Na bu nbem ade i nɔaniɛ ni, ama, na nbem akete, ba yo má yarada.

eng: In the same way, David also pronounces a blessing on the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from actions.
local: Fɛmɛ Davidi mmɔɔ yo kaanri nyini minɛm ŋusu jɔrɛ, ka i mi la tere­fiɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, má ka i sakpa n dɛɛ ti a. I ŋa.

eng: because a person who has died has been freed from sins power.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ wu, wɔ de i tii afite satiɛ nu lɛɛ.

eng: God will repay everyone based on their works .
local: Na i tɔ́ sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋkatɔ kabo i sa­yoo­wa ti barasu.

eng: While we were still weak, at the right moment, Christ died for ungodly people.
local: Saŋga bo ya la má faŋga mmɔɔ ni, nyini saŋga n ye Kristo ba wu maari satiɛ­yo­fɔm n a.

eng: To those in Rome who are dearly loved by God and called to be Gods people. Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ bo am wo Roma miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ye m sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma am a. Ambɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro am na wɔ fere am na am ayo i dɛɛ minɛm ni, ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ma am wura bu ahɔrɔma nu na am nya ahoreda.

eng: Absolutely not! God must be true, even if every human being is a liar, as it is written: So that it can show that you are right in your words; and you will triumph when you are judged. .
local: Ai, má sɔ. Haari ni bo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti kɔrɛ­fɔm ni, Nyɛmɛ yiri ti i nahɔrɛ­fɔ ɔ, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Deke bo a kan ni, bu ŋú ka nyini ti nahɔrɛ a, na nzɛn bu di u jɔrɛ jarakiiri u, a kúnma bu.

eng: They disagreed with each other and were starting to leave when Paul made one more statement: The Holy Spirit spoke correctly when he said to your ancestors through Isaiah the prophet.
local: Nyini ti, bu nɔɔ ayo má kun, na ba yasu ka bu kɔ awuru, na Pɔɔlu ase bu jɔrɛ kun be abuka su, i ŋa, Nahɔrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n jɔjɔɔri am jɔrɛ n a. I yo maari Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya seeri am nam ka.

eng: But if we died with Christ, we have faith that we will also live with him.
local: Tɔ, kabo ya ni Kristo saŋga wuuri ni, sɔ ye ya yo yarada ka ya ni yiri sáŋga nya ŋgɔɔ a.

eng: On the one hand, he will give eternal life to those who look for glory, honor, and immortality based on their patient good work.
local: Minɛ nbem dɛɛ la ka, bu mindi nati yo sakpa, na bu kpini kansi, ni jirima, ni aŋuniɛ­mbaa bo i kpɔrɔ má n busu ni, bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ má bu ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: Happy are those whose actions outside the Law are forgiven, and whose sins are covered. .
local: Tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ fa bu ŋgasi ce bu ni. Tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ kata bu satiɛ su ni.

eng: It isnt often that someone will die for a righteous person, though maybe someone might dare to die for a good person.
local: I ti kekereke ka sɔnɔ soro su na i tɔ i ŋgɔɔ na i wu ma sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, ama, beni, be koro fa ahoresi na i soro su na i de sɔnɔ bo i yo sakpa agaya jinaa­biri na i wu ma i.

eng: First of all, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because the news about your faithfulness is being spread throughout the whole world.
local: Ciciri su, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, m da m Nyɛmɛ asi ma am kɛrɛ, dama durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ ati kabo am ayo Yesu Kristo yarada ni.

eng: But if our lack of righteousness confirms Gods justice, what will we say? That God, who brings wrath upon us, isnt just ?.
local: Ye nzɛn ya satiɛm n bo ya yo bu n kere kabo Nyɛmɛ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, na i cin ya sui, ya koro se ka wɔ yo má sakpa, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má sɔ. Adamande sunsunni lɛɛ. Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ yo má sa bo i kɔ atin su, i yo sɛ koro di durunya n nu­fɔm jɔrɛ a?.

eng: Go to this people and say: You will hear, to be sure, but never understand; and you will certainly see but never recognize what you are seeing. .
local: Kɔ na a kɔ se nyini minɛm ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, Am só am sui ni nahɔrɛ su, ama, am tí má deke bo am so am sui ka am ti i n bu. Am káaki niɛ ni nahɔrɛ su, ama, am ŋú má pui.

eng: We know that Christ has been raised from the dead and he will never die again. Death no longer has power over him.
local: Ya si ka Kristo teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni, i wú má kun, dama yiwee la má i ŋusu yiko kun.

eng: But on the other hand, there will be wrath and anger for those who obey wickedness instead of the truth because they are acting out of selfishness and disobedience.
local: Ama, minɛm bo bu nati yo anyii sam, na bu kete deke kɛrɛ bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na bu su satiɛ atin su ni, bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ fá yaa bu ŋu na i cín bu sui n a.

eng: Happy are those whose sin isnt counted against them by the Lord .
local: Tere­fiɛ­fɔ la sɔnɔ bo Micɛra n ŋa i la má satiɛ be ni.

eng: But God shows his love for us, because while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ yiri akere ya kabo i koro ya barasu, dama saŋga bo ya toko yo satiɛm ni, ye Kristo ba wu maari ya a.

eng: I serve God in my spirit by preaching the good news about Gods Son, and God is my witness that I continually mention you.
local: Nyɛmɛ bo m su i ni m ahore­mbaa kun ni, na m kan i Wa n labari kpa n jɔrɛ ni, yiri di m daani a, kabo m nati sere Nyɛmɛ ma am ni.

eng: But if Gods truth is demonstrated by my lie and it increases his glory, why am I still judged as a sinner?.
local: Be koro se ka nzɛn m bu kɔrɛ, na i ma minɛm ŋu kabo Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ ti barasu, adena bu ma i jirima, ye nzu ti ye i di m jɔrɛ cin m sui ka m ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ a?.

eng: This peoples senses have become calloused, and theyve become hard of hearing, and theyve shut their eyes so that they wont see with their eyes or hear with their ears or understand with their minds, and change their hearts and lives that I may heal them. .
local: Dama ahin minɛm tii ti kekereke, ba tɔ bu sui na má bu ti, ba kata bu nyu­mbaa na má bu ŋu. Má nyini, bu nyu­mbaa ŋú i wɔ, na bu sui tí, na bu sí bu ahore su, na bu káaki bu wo, na m má bu nya laifiɛ.

eng: He died to sin once and for all with his death, but he lives for God with his life.
local: Yiwee n bo i wuuri ni, i wuuri kpe­kun n kpein wɔ, na i yi satiɛ, ama, ŋgɔɔ n bo i la i ni, i wo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wo wɔ.

eng: There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu nati yo satiɛ ni, bu dí wahara na bu ŋú anyum­boro, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni dú mɔ ŋu nyini kekereke n a, na bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm gusu nyá tɔ su.

eng: Is this state of happiness only for the circumcised or is it also for those who arent circumcised? We say, Faith was credited to Abraham as righteousness.
local: Tɔ, minɛm bo bu wuraari kɛnkɛnnɛ n ŋgumi la nyini tere­fiɛ ni, nzɛn sɛ a? Ai, minɛm bo bu wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ ni mmɔɔ la i. Dama ya du mɔ ase ka, Abraham yarada n dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a.

eng: So, now that we have been made righteous by his blood, we can be even more certain that we will be saved from Gods wrath through him.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, i yiwee n bo i wu maari ya ni, ye Nyɛmɛ ade ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a, nyini ti, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka i dé ya fite sui­cin n bo Nyɛmɛ fá cin satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni.

eng: in all my prayers. Im always asking that somehow, by Gods will, I might succeed in visiting you at last.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ m sere i wɔ, ka nzɛn i ti i dɛɛ koroya­nu, i teke atin ma m kisa, na i awieeri su na m koro ba niɛ am busu.

eng: Why not say, Lets do evil things so that good things will come out of it?.
local: I ti kabo minɛ nbem nati kan wɔ, bu ŋa, am ma ya yo satiɛ adena Nyɛmɛ ba ma i kaaki sakpa. Bu kpiɛ m nzukɔ ka mini mmɔɔ se sɔ ɔ. Bu bu kɔrɛ wɔ. Nyɛmɛ dí bu jɔrɛ na i cín bu sui.

eng: Therefore, be certain of this: Gods salvation has been sent to the Gentiles. They will listen.
local: Na Pɔɔlu akun i jɔjɔɔ­wa n nɔɔ, na wɔ se bu ka, Nyini ti, m koro ka am si ka Nyɛmɛ asunma ya ka ya fa tii­de labari kpa n kɔ ma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni. Bɛrɛ dé i a. .

eng: In the same way, you also should consider yourselves dead to sin but alive for God in Christ Jesus.
local: Sɔ ye i ti ye ni ka ambɛrɛ ni am tii si ka am awu fa kɔ satiɛ su a, na am ni Yesu ayo kun, ye am la ŋgɔɔ wo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wo a.

eng: But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does what is good, for the Jew first and also for the Greek.
local: Ama, minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu nati yo sakpa ni, Nyɛmɛ má bu nya kansi ni jirima ni ahoreda, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni dú mɔ nya nyini deke kpam n a, na bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n gusu nyá tɔ su.

eng: So how was it credited? When he was circumcised, or when he wasnt circumcised? In fact, it was credited while he still wasnt circumcised, not after he was circumcised.
local: Saŋga bɔnɔ ye Nyɛmɛ seeri sɔ n a? Nyini saŋga Abraham awura kɛnkɛnnɛ, nzɛn wɔ nya wura má? Ai, nyini saŋga wɔ nya wura má.

eng: If we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son while we were still enemies, now that we have been reconciled, how much more certain is it that we will be saved by his life?.
local: Saŋga bo ya yo ti Nyɛmɛ kpɔ­fɔm ni, ye i wa Yesu wu maari ya n a, na wɔ yo ya ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu, kisa na ya ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn ayo dɔŋgu ni, i dé ya tii ji nahɔrɛ su, dama Yesu la ŋgɔɔ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: I really want to see you to pass along some spiritual gift to you so that you can be strengthened.
local: Dama m nyunu aboro ka m bá ŋu am wɔ, adena m fa aceeri n bo i fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ ba ma am na am koro jina faŋga kenken ni nyɛmɛsu ni.

eng: So what are we saying? Are we better off? Not at all. We have already stated the charge: both Jews and Greeks are all under the power of sin.
local: Tɔ, ya se sɛ wɔ? Yɛrɛ bo ya ti Yuda­fɔm ni, ya tara bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n nzu nu wɔ? Ai, ya tara má bu pui, dama ma du mɔ ase am ka yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm ni, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ.

eng: Paul lived in his own rented quarters for two full years and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
local: Na Pɔɔlu ade sɔɔ, na i sutɔ kaari, na wɔ wo nu haari afɔ nnyɔ, na i sude minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ba i dɔ ni.

eng: So then, dont let sin rule your body, so that you do what it wants.
local: Nyini ti, má am ma satiɛ la yiko fa kɔ am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n su, na am yo am kunnu koroo­wa.

eng: God does not have favorites.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ tambu má nu.

eng: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that comes from the faith he had while he still wasnt circumcised. It happened this way so that Abraham could be the ancestor of all those people who arent circumcised, who have faith in God, and so are counted as righteous.
local: Nyini saŋga n sin, ye i ba wuraari kɛnkɛnnɛ n a. Na i kɛnkɛnnɛ wuraa­wa n kere ka i yarada n dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ deeri i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, saŋga bo i nya wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ ni. I yoori sɔ ni, adena yiri Abraham bá yo minɛm bo ba yo yarada n kɛrɛ si, haari ni bo ba wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ ni, ye Nyɛmɛ ade bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a.

eng: And not only that: we even take pride in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, the one through whom we now have a restored relationship with God.
local: Má nyini ŋgumi a, ama, ya la ahorejɔ na ya bo tuntun Nyɛmɛ ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, dama wɔ yo ya ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu.

eng: What I mean is that we can mutually encourage each other while I am with you. We can be encouraged by the faithfulness we find in each other, both your faithfulness and mine.
local: Nahɔrɛ, i ti deke bo ya fa ma ya beŋgu na ya nya ahoresi wɔ, m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ, kabo ya yo Yesu yarada n dɛɛ ti.

eng: As it is written, There is no righteous person, not even one. .
local: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, haari sɔnɔ kun n kpein mmɔɔ.

eng: Unhindered and with complete confidence, he continued to preach Gods kingdom and to teach about the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: na i sukan Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ŋusu jɔrɛ, na i sukere minɛm ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo jɔrɛ ni ahoresi, be akanda má i.

eng: Dont offer parts of your body to sin, to be used as weapons to do wrong. Instead, present yourselves to God as people who have been brought back to life from the dead, and offer all the parts of your body to God to be used as weapons to do right.
local: Má am fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaa be ma satiɛ, na i kaaki deke bo i yo satiɛ, ama, am fa am ŋu ma Nyɛmɛ ka minɛm bo ba teŋge yiwee nu anya ŋgɔɔ kisa, na am fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ ma Nyɛmɛ na i fa am yo deke bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su.

eng: Those who have sinned outside the Law will also die outside the Law, and those who have sinned under the Law will be judged by the Law.
local: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu la má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n na bu yoori satiɛ, bu ká ŋgbɛɛn, haari ni bo bu la má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ni. Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu la Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n na bu yoori satiɛ, bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ fá i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n di bu jɔrɛ, na i bó bu asi a.

eng: He could also be the ancestor of those circumcised people, who arent only circumcised but who also walk in the path of faith, like our ancestor Abraham did while he wasnt circumcised.
local: Yiri gusu kaaki ti minɛm bo ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n si a, má ka ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n dɛɛ ti, ama, bo ba yo yarada kabo i yoori yarada saŋga bo i nya wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ ni.

eng: Just as through one human being sin came into the world, and death came through sin, so death has come to everyone, since everyone has sinned.
local: Am ma ya teŋge ya kɛrɛ na Adam jɔrɛ su. Yiri sɔnɔ kun dɛɛ sa bo i yoori i ni faari satiɛ baari durunya n nu a, ye satiɛ n gusu faari yiwee ba kuunri sɔnɔ kɛrɛ n a, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ yo satiɛ wɔ.

eng: I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that I planned to visit you many times, although I have been prevented from coming until now. I want to harvest some fruit among you, just as I have done among the other Gentiles.
local: M ninbaam, m koro ka am si ka saŋga kɛrɛ, má m koro ka m bá niɛ am busu wɔ, ama, m nya má atin. Má m koro ka m ba nya minɛ am nu ma Nyɛmɛ wɔ, kabo ma nya am beŋgu nbem bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni.

eng: There is no one who understands. There is no one who looks for God. .
local: Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i la ŋgɔnlɛ, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i niɛniɛ Nyɛmɛ busu.

eng: Sin will have no power over you, because you arent under Law but under grace.
local: Satiɛ la má yiko fa kɔ am dɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ má am su kun, ama, Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma ni niɛ am su a.

eng: It isnt the ones who hear the Law who are righteous in Gods eyes. It is the ones who do what the Law says who will be treated as righteous.
local: Má minɛm bo bu sui ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni ye Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a. Ama, bo bu yo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ni, bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a.

eng: Although sin was in the world, since there was no Law, it wasnt taken into account until the Law came.
local: Satiɛ yo woori durunya n nu ka, ye Nyɛmɛ nya ba faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari a, ama, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ nya fa má i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ma má ni, minɛm yo kan má satiɛ jɔrɛ, dama nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa be wo má bɛrɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: The promise to Abraham and to his descendants, that he would inherit the world, didnt come through the Law but through the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Nyɛmɛ yo seeri Abraham ni i busu nu­fɔm ka i fá durunya n kɛrɛ ce bu ni, má ka i su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su dɛɛ ti a, ama, kabo wɔ yo Nyɛmɛ yarada n ti ye Nyɛmɛ deeri i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin n su i dɔ a.

eng: I have a responsibility both to Greeks and to those who dont speak Greek, both to the wise and to the foolish.
local: Dama wɔ yo daka ka m yo sɔ ma minɛ barasu kɛrɛ, bo bu nyunu ateke, ni bo bu nyunu ateke má, ni bo ba sɔnsɔn kara, ni bo ba sɔnsɔn má kara.

eng: They all turned away. They have become worthless together. There is no one who shows kindness. There is not even one. .
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ akaaki bu sin abo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ atin ni, bu kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i yo sakpa, haari sɔnɔ kun n kpein mmɔɔ wo má bɛrɛ.

eng: So what? Should we sin because we arent under Law but under grace? Absolutely not!.
local: Tɔ, nzu bekun a? Kisa, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ má ya su kun, ama, Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma ni niɛ ya su a. Nyini ti, ya koro yo satiɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má sɔ.

eng: Gentiles dont have the Law. But when they instinctively do what the Law requires they are a Law in themselves, though they dont have the Law.
local: Minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu la má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, ama, na bu yo kabo nɔaniɛ n kere ni, bu sunsunni ni bu ahore­mbaa kpa n dɛɛ ti, bɛrɛ ni bu tii akpama nɔaniɛ ama bu ŋu lɛɛ.

eng: But death ruled from Adam until Moses, even over those who didnt sin in the same way Adam did—Adam was a type of the one who was coming.
local: Ama, haari nyini mmɔɔ, fite Adam saŋga n kɛrɛ kɔ ju Moyisi saŋga ni, yiwee la yiko kun minɛm sɔ ɔ, haari ni bo ba yo má satiɛ kabo Adam yoori ni. Adam woo­wa kere ya deke be fa kɔ yiri n bo i bá ba n jɔrɛ a.

eng: If they inherit because of the Law, then faith has no effect and the promise has been canceled.
local: Nzɛn minɛ su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ye bu nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i cé bu ni, nyini la ka yarada­yo n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ, na kabo Nyɛmɛ ase n gusu la má kpɛrɛ.

eng: Thats why Im ready to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m ahore abi ka m ba kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere ambɛrɛ gusu bo am wo Roma miɛ kpiri n nu n a.

eng: Their throat is a grave that has been opened. They are deceitful with their tongues, and the poison of vipers is under their lips. .
local: Ba kaaki akɛrɛ ka, Bu konvi ti ka faŋ­kunma bo ba teke i su wɔ, na bu fa bu taferema sudadaka minɛm. Na jɔrɛ bo i fite bu nɔɔ nu yo ya ka pumpuni bɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: Dont you know that if you offer yourselves to someone as obedient slaves, that you are slaves of the one whom you obey? Thats true whether you serve as slaves of sin, which leads to death, or as slaves of the kind of obedience that leads to righteousness.
local: Am si ka nzɛn am faari am ŋu maari be na am deeri i mi nɔaniɛ, am ti i mi n akɔɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Kabo i nya ti sɔ ni, nzɛn am faari am ŋu maari satiɛ, am ti satiɛ akɔɔ ɔ, na am wú ɔ lɛɛ. Ama, nzɛn am gusu deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, i dé am ka am ayo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: They show the proof of the Law written on their hearts, and their consciences affirm it. Their conflicting thoughts will accuse them, or even make a defense for them.
local: Kabo ba de i sɔ ni, nyini kere ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n wo bu ahore su a, na bu sunsunni gusu kere ka i ti sɔ ɔ, na i kaaki jaraki bu ka ba yo sa bo i kɔ má atin su, wara i koro jina bu sin ka ba yo sa bo i kɔ atin su.

eng: But the free gift of Christ isnt like Adams failure. If many people died through what one person did wrong, Gods grace is multiplied even more for many people with the gift—of the one person Jesus Christ—that comes through grace.
local: Ama, aceeri n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya ni, i ti má ka Adam dɛɛ satiɛ ni. Sɔnɔ kun satiɛ dɛɛ ti ye minɛ dɔŋgu wuuri a, ama, deke bo i tara i kɛrɛ n la ka, minɛ dɔŋgu anya ahɔrɔma bambaka, i ti aceeri bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, sɔnɔ kun ahɔrɔma dɛɛ ti. Yiri la Yesu Kristo.

eng: The Law brings about wrath. But when there isnt any law, there isnt any violation of the law.
local: Nzɛn minɛm saaki nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, nyini ma Nyɛmɛ fa yaa na i cin bu sui a, ama, nzɛn má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa be wo má bɛrɛ, má deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i kere ka sɔnɔ ayo satiɛ.

eng: Im not ashamed of the gospel: it is Gods own power for salvation to all who have faith in God, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
local: Nyinsɛ ti má m fa kɔ labari kpa n su, dama yiri ti Nyɛmɛ yiko bo i ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo yarada n nya tii­de a, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni du mɔ a, na bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n nya tɔ su.

eng: Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness. .
local: Nzukɔlɛɛ ni jɔrɛ tiɛ ŋgumi fite bu nɔɔ nu a.

eng: But thank God that although you used to be slaves of sin, you gave wholehearted obedience to the teaching that was handed down to you, which provides a pattern.
local: Saŋga be am yo ti satiɛ akɔɔm wɔ, ama, kisa ya da Nyɛmɛ asi ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo bu fa kereeri am, na am ade i ni am ahore­mbaa kɛrɛ ni.

eng: on the day when, according to my gospel, God will judge the hidden truth about human beings through Christ Jesus.
local: Nyini kɛrɛ yó cɛɛn bo Nyɛmɛ má Yesu Kristo ba di adamande kɛrɛ jɔrɛ bo i wo asiri nu n wɔ. M labari kpa n bo m kan i kere minɛ ni, kabo m kere i lɛɛ.

eng: The gift isnt like the consequences of one persons sin. The judgment that came from one persons sin led to punishment, but the free gift that came out of many failures led to the verdict of acquittal.
local: Bekun, Nyɛmɛ aceeri n ti má ka sɔnɔ kun n dɛɛ satiɛ ni, dama sɔnɔ kun n dɛɛ satiɛ ni, nyini ma Nyɛmɛ di minɛ jɔrɛ cin bu sui a, ama, minɛ dɔŋgu satiɛ bo ba yo i n dɛɛ sin, ye Nyɛmɛ ace bu aceeri a, na wɔ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ.

eng: Thats why the inheritance comes through faith, so that it will be on the basis of Gods grace. In that way, the promise is secure for all of Abrahams descendants, not just for those who are related by Law but also for those who are related by the faith of Abraham, who is the father of all of us.
local: Nyini ti, deke bo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i fá ma ni, i baari minɛm bo bu yo yarada n dɛɛ ti wɔ. I yoori sɔ ni, adena deke n bo i yo seeri ka i yó n yó aceeri kpama ma Abraham busu nu­fɔm kɛrɛ, má bo bu su nɔaniɛ n kpamaa­wa n su ŋgumi, ama, bo ba yo yarada kabo Abraham yoori ni. Yiri ti ya kɛrɛ si a.

eng: Gods righteousness is being revealed in the gospel, from faithfulness for faith, as it is written, The righteous person will live by faith. .
local: Labari kpa n kere kabo Nyɛmɛ de sɔnɔ ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo yarada, i fite i yarada­yo n dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ de i mi ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, na nyini ma i yarada n yo bii­wa. Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su m dɔ, i nyá ŋgɔɔ, i yarada n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Their feet are quick to shed blood; .
local: Bu cɛ má na bu yi minɛ ŋgɔɔ ndende.

eng: Now that you have been set free from sin, you have become slaves of righteousness.
local: am ade am tii afite satiɛ nu lɛɛ, am akaaki sa bo i kɔ atin su n akɔɔm wɔ.

eng: But, if you call yourself a Jew; if you rely on the Law; if you brag about your relationship to God.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am ŋa am ti Yuda­fɔm, na am daafi Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ŋu, na am fa fɔ ka am si Nyɛmɛ ni.

eng: If death ruled because of one persons failure, those who receive the multiplied grace and the gift of righteousness will even more certainly rule in life through the one person Jesus Christ.
local: Ya ŋu ka sɔnɔ kun dɛɛ satiɛ ti, ye yiwee la yiko kun minɛ sɔ n a, yiri sɔnɔ kun n satiɛ dɛɛ ti. Ama, deke bo i tara i kɛrɛ n la kabo minɛ anya ŋgɔɔ adi fɛmɛya n wɔ, dama ba nya ahɔrɔma bambaka n bo i ti Nyɛmɛ aceeri ni, Nyɛmɛ ade bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a. I kɛrɛ fite sɔnɔ kun n dɛɛ ti wɔ, yiri la Yesu Kristo.

eng: As it is written: I have appointed you to be the father of many nations. So Abraham is our father in the eyes of God in whom he had faith, the God who gives life to the dead and calls things that dont exist into existence.
local: kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, M má a yo minɛ barasu dɔŋgu si. Nyɛmɛ ŋuuri ka Abraham ayo yarada ka yiri Nyɛmɛ teŋge minɛm bo ba wu n yiwee nu, na yiri ma deke n bo i yo wo má bɛrɛ n wo bɛrɛ a.

eng: Gods wrath is being revealed from heaven against all the ungodly behavior and the injustice of human beings who silence the truth with injustice.
local: Nyɛmɛ wo aŋgoro akere kabo wɔ fa yaa acin minɛm sui ni, bɛrɛ bo bu sunsunni ti tiɛ na bu yo satiɛ ni, bu satiɛ n dɛɛ ti, ba kete ka má minɛm si Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: destruction and misery are in their ways; .
local: Deke kɛrɛ bu kɔ, bu dɛɛ la ka bu yo minɛm ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, na bu saaki bu.

eng: Once, you offered the parts of your body to be used as slaves to impurity and to lawless behavior that leads to still more lawless behavior. Now, you should present the parts of your body as slaves to righteousness, which makes your lives holy.
local: M fa adamande dɛɛ woo­wa, ye m fa kere kabo Nyɛmɛ ti a, dama am suti má i bu. Am yo fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n be yoori daŋgba sam wɔ, ni satiɛ barasum wɔ, nyini ma am saaki nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n a, sɔ ye kisa am fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaam yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, nyini ma am yo Nyɛmɛ minɛ casim a.

eng: if you know the will of God; if you are taught by the Law so that you can figure out the things that really matter.
local: na am si kabo Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa ti, na am sunsun nyunyumi, na am yi sa bo i ti kpa, dama am asi Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ awie.

eng: So now the righteous requirements necessary for life are met for everyone through the righteous act of one person, just as judgment fell on everyone through the failure of one person.
local: Nyini ti, kabo sɔnɔ kun dɛɛ satiɛ ti na Nyɛmɛ di minɛm kɛrɛ jɔrɛ cin bu sui ni, sɔ bekun ye sɔnɔ kun dɛɛ sakpa­yo ni ma Nyɛmɛ de minɛm kɛrɛ ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ na bu nya ŋgɔɔ a.

eng: When it was beyond hope, he had faith in the hope that he would become the father of many nations, in keeping with the promise God spoke to him: Thats how many descendants you will have. .
local: Nyini ti, saŋga bo Abraham yoori yarada na i la tama ni, i ti kekereke na i yo tama ka i bá yo minɛ barasu dɔŋgu si, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, U busu nu­fɔm bá sunnu beberebe ni.

eng: This is because what is known about God should be plain to them because God made it plain to them.
local: Deke bo i ti na wɔ fa yaa n la ka, ba ŋu deke n kɛrɛ bo i ma bu si Nyɛmɛ ni, dama yiri ni i tii ayi i kpain afite fofoe nu.

eng: and they dont know the way of peace. .
local: Bu dɛɛ la ka bu nati sukpini jɔrɛ, bu koro má tana­laifiɛ.

eng: When you were slaves of sin, you were free from the control of righteousness.
local: Saŋga bo am yo ti satiɛ akɔɔm ni, be miɛ má am ka am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su.

eng: if you have persuaded yourself that you are: a guide for the blind; a light to those who are in darkness.
local: am yo am ŋu ka ambɛrɛ bita anyinsinm kpama a, na am ti wein ma minɛm bo bu wo awosin nu ni.

eng: Many people were made righteous through the obedience of one person, just as many people were made sinners through the disobedience of one person.
local: Na kabo sɔnɔ kun keteeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na minɛm kɛrɛ ayo satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni, sɔ bekun ye sɔnɔ kun gusu ade Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a, nyini ye Nyɛmɛ dé minɛm kɛrɛ ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a.

eng: Without losing faith, Abraham, who was nearly years old, took into account his own body, which was as good as dead, and Sarahs womb, which was dead.
local: Haari ni saŋga bo wɔ ju afɔ yaa, na i si ka wɔ yo kpiɛn ni, na i yi Saara ti ŋɔrɔka na wɔ yo buke n kɛrɛ ni, i ŋuniɛ ayɔrɔki má i, i yoori yarada a.

eng: Ever since the creation of the world, Gods invisible qualities—Gods eternal power and divine nature—have been clearly seen, because they are understood through the things God has made. So humans are without excuse.
local: Fite saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri durunya n ba juuri ni kisa, minɛ aŋu i woo­wa, i ti má ka bu nyu­mbaa aŋu i kabo i ti barasu wɔ, ama, ba ŋu dekem bo wɔ yi bu ni. Nyini dekem ni kere ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su a, ni kabo i la yiko bo i la má awieeri ni. Nyini ti, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro se ka i si má i.

eng: There is no fear of God in their view of the world. .
local: Bu nyunu yi má Nyɛmɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti, bu soro má i.

eng: What consequences did you get from doing things that you are now ashamed of? The outcome of those things is death.
local: Satiɛm bo am yo yoori bu ni, nzu kpɛrɛ ye am nyaari i a? Pui. Kisa, bu ti am nyinsɛ wɔ, na i awieeri kɛrɛ la yiwee wɔ.

eng: an educator of the foolish; a teacher of infants.
local: Am ŋa ambɛrɛ teke minɛm bo bu la má ŋgɔnlɛ nyi a, na am kaaki kere minɛm bo bu ti ka nbaatumam ni, dama am ŋa am si Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɛɛ ti, am sunsun ka am si ŋgɔnlɛ ni nahɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo i nu ni.

eng: The Law stepped in to amplify the failure, but where sin increased, grace multiplied even more.
local: Nyɛmɛ faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kereeri a, na minɛ si kabo satiɛ­yo asunnu barasu a, ama, deke bo satiɛ­yo sunnu, Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama n sunnu tara sɔ.

eng: He didnt hesitate with a lack of faith in Gods promise, but he grew strong in faith and gave glory to God.
local: Wɔ yo má sika fa kɔ deke bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó i n su, ama, yarada n bo i yoori i n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ nya faŋga na wɔ kansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Although they knew God, they didnt honor God as God or thank him. Instead, their reasoning became pointless, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
local: Haari ni bo bu si i n kɛrɛ, ba kete ka bu ma má i jirima ka i ti Nyɛmɛ, na bu kaaki da má i asi. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye bu sunsunni kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn ye ŋgɔnlɛ akpiɛ bu su, ye bu ahore­mbaa ayo biri n a.

eng: Now we know that whatever the Law says, it speaks to those who are under the Law, in order to shut every mouth and make it so the whole world has to answer to God.
local: Ya si ka deke n kɛrɛ bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu ni, i jɔjɔ ni Yuda­fɔm bo bu su i su n wɔ, adena má be koro teke i nɔɔ kan jɔrɛ be, ama, durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ bá jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu na i dí bu jɔrɛ na i cín bu sui.

eng: But now that you have been set free from sin and become slaves to God, you have the consequence of a holy life, and the outcome is eternal life.
local: Ama, kisa na am ade am tii afite satiɛ nu, na am ayo Nyɛmɛ akɔɔm ni, kpɛrɛ bo am anya i ni, yiri la bo am ayo i minɛ casim ni, na i awieeri, am nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: then why dont you who are teaching others teach yourself? If you preach, No stealing, do you steal?.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am kere minɛm ni, nzu ti ye am kere má am ŋu a? Ye ambɛrɛ bo am se minɛm ka má bu wɔ ni, am wɔ, nzɛn am wɔ má?.

eng: The result is that grace will rule through Gods righteousness, leading to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord, just as sin ruled in death.
local: Nyini ti, kabo satiɛ la yiko na i ma yiwee kun minɛm sɔ ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, ye i de minɛm ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, na bu nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri a, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: He was fully convinced that God was able to do what he promised.
local: Na wɔ si tenle su ka Nyɛmɛ koro yo deke n bo i yo seeri ka i yó i ni.

eng: While they were claiming to be wise, they made fools of themselves.
local: Bu bu bu ŋu ka bu ti ŋgɔnlɛ­fɔm wɔ, ama, bu ti sinnzinm wɔ.

eng: It follows that no human being will be treated as righteous in his presence by doing what the Law says, because the knowledge of sin comes through the Law.
local: Dama be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro se ka i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ de i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, ama, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni ma minɛm si ka bu ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm a.

eng: The wages that sin pays are death, but Gods gift is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: Dama satiɛ ŋkatɔ la yiwee wɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ aceeri n bo wɔ fa ama ya n la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri na i wo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo nu n wɔ.

eng: If you say, No adultery, do you commit adultery? If you hate idols, do you rob temples?.
local: Am ŋa má be saaki ajaa ciire ni, ye am saaki i, nzɛn am saaki má i? Am ŋa am koro má amɔɛ jɔrɛ ni, ye am wɔ neŋgem amɔɛ sɔɔm n nu, nzɛn am wɔ má?.

eng: Therefore, it was credited to him as righteousness.
local: Abraham yoori yarada a, nyini dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a.

eng: They exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images that look like mortal humans: birds, animals, and reptiles.
local: Jirima bo má bu fa ma Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, ba fa ama amɔɛm bo ba yo bu na bu ferema ni adamandem, ni laŋkpɛtɛ­fɔm, ni ajaa­nnam, ni cin­bukunnu­su­fɔm wɔ.

eng: But now Gods righteousness has been revealed apart from the Law, which is confirmed by the Law and the Prophets.
local: Ama, kisa Nyɛmɛ ayi akere ya kabo ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, má yiri la bo ya su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ni, ama, yiri la bo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm diiri i ŋusu daani ka i yó n wɔ.

eng: If you brag about the Law, do you shame God by breaking the Law?.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am bita Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n fa fɔ ka am si i, na am su má i su ni, ambɛrɛ kaaki wura Nyɛmɛ nyinsɛ a.

eng: But the scripture that says it was credited to him wasnt written only for Abrahams sake.
local: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i ka, Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, má Abraham ŋgumi dɛɛ ti ye bu kɛrɛɛri i sɔ a.

eng: So God abandoned them to their hearts desires, which led to the moral corruption of degrading their own bodies with each other.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ ayaki bu ye bu yo bu kunnu koroo­wa n a, na bu suyo daŋgba sam bu ŋuŋu nu suwura bu ŋu nyinsɛ.

eng: Gods righteousness comes through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ for all who have faith in him. Theres no distinction.
local: Yiri la ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo Yesu Kristo yarada, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ ŋa i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, nyini ti, ya la má kpakpaaki­nu.

eng: As it is written: The name of God is discredited by the Gentiles because of you .
local: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Ambɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ ti, ye minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm jɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu a.

eng: It was written also for our sake, because it is going to be credited to us too. It will be credited to those of us who have faith in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
local: ama, yɛrɛ gusu dɛɛ ti ye bu kɛrɛɛri sɔ n a, yɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ bá de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n na ya yo i yarada ni, yiri bo i teŋgeeri ya Mibiɛ Yesu i yiwee n nu ni.

eng: They traded Gods truth for a lie, and they worshipped and served the creation instead of the creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Dama ba kete Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ­fɔ ni, na bu nya susu kɔrɛ su. Na bu su dekem bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ni, bu su má yiri bo i yiiri deke n kɛrɛ ni. Yiri dɛɛ la kansi saŋga kɛrɛ a. Ami.

eng: All have sinned and fall short of Gods glory.
local: dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ayo satiɛ afun Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa ni.

eng: Circumcision is an advantage if you do what the Law says. But if you are a person who breaks the Law, your status of being circumcised has changed into not being circumcised.
local: Nzɛn am yo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka am yo ni, nyini yiri, am kɛnkɛnnɛ n bo am awura i n la kpɛrɛ. Ama, nzɛn am yo má kabo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ni, i ti ka am awura má kɛnkɛnnɛ lɛɛ.

eng: He was handed over because of our mistakes, and he was raised to meet the requirements of righteousness for us.
local: Ya satiɛ dɛɛ ti ye Yesu wuuri a, na wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu, adena Nyɛmɛ de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ.

eng: Thats why God abandoned them to degrading lust. Their females traded natural sexual relations for unnatural sexual relations.
local: Ba kete Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ yaki bu a, na bu suyo nyinsɛ sam kabo bu kunnu koro n a, haari na nbaram yaki nbiɛsɔm dɔ daari, na bu sufa bu beŋgu nbaram da, na bu yo kabo má wɔ yo má daka ka bu yo ni.

eng: but all are treated as righteous freely by his grace because of a ransom that was paid by Christ Jesus.
local: ama, Yesu Kristo ade sɔnɔ kɛrɛ tii aji, nyini dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ŋa sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ayo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a, i ti Nyɛmɛ aceeri wɔ.

eng: So if the person who isnt circumcised keeps the Law, wont his status of not being circumcised be counted as if he were circumcised?.
local: Nzɛn bo ba wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ n yo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka bu yo ni, Nyɛmɛ de bu ka ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Also, in the same way, the males traded natural sexual relations with females, and burned with lust for each other. Males performed shameful actions with males, and they were paid back with the penalty they deserved for their mistake in their own bodies.
local: Na nbiɛsɔm n gusu yaki nbaram dɔ daari, na bu la konviki agaya fa kɔ bu beŋgu nbiɛsɔm dɔ, na bu ni bɛrɛ saŋga da ka bara ni biɛsɔ dɛɛ su. Nyini nyinsɛ sam dɛɛ ti, ye ba nya sui­cin kabo wɔ yo daka ka bu nya i n a.

eng: The one who isnt physically circumcised but keeps the Law will judge you. You became a lawbreaker after you had the written Law and circumcision.
local: Nyini ti, i sukere ka, bɛrɛ n bo ba wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ na bu yo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ni, bu dí ambɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm jɔrɛ jaraki am, dama am awura kɛnkɛnnɛ na am la nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, ama, am yo má kabo i kere ni.

eng: Through his faithfulness, God displayed Jesus as the place of sacrifice where mercy is found by means of his blood. He did this to demonstrate his righteousness in passing over sins that happened before.
local: Yiri Yesu ye Nyɛmɛ ma i deeri adamande jinaa­biri n a, na wɔ fa i ŋu ayo yoo­wa na wɔ wu, adena nzɛn minɛm yoori i yarada, i mbunja n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ fá bu satiɛ ce bu. Deke bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ yoori sɔ n la ka, i koro ka i kere kabo yiri ni i tii yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n wɔ, dama dawa saŋga Nyɛmɛ yo laari sawɔrɛ fa kɔ minɛm bo bu yo satiɛ n dɔ. Ama, kisa wɔ kere ka yiri ni i tii yo sa bo i kɔ atin su wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori Yesu yarada, Nyɛmɛ de i mi ka yiri yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a.

eng: Since they didnt think it was worthwhile to acknowledge God, God abandoned them to a defective mind to do inappropriate things.
local: Kabo ba kete ka bu si má Nyɛmɛ ni, nyini ma wɔ yaki bu, ye bu sunsunni tiɛ n ma bu yo sa bo má wɔ yo má daka ka bu yo i n a.

eng: It isnt the Jew who maintains outward appearances who will receive praise from God, and it isnt people who are outwardly circumcised on their bodies.
local: Dama má Yuda­fɔm bo ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa su ni ŋgumi ti Yuda­fɔm nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: What happens to our bragging? Its thrown out. With which law? With what we have accomplished under the Law?.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, nzu deke ye ya koro fa fɔ a? Deke be wo má bɛrɛ. Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni ma minɛm yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ a, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má sɔ. Ama, bo minɛm yo Yesu yarada n dɛɛ ti a.

eng: So they were filled with all injustice, wicked behavior, greed, and evil behavior. They are full of jealousy, murder, fighting, deception, and malice. They are gossips.
local: Bu ahore­mbaa ayi ni satiɛ barasu kɛrɛ ŋgumi wɔ, bu konviki wura bu beŋgu neŋgem nu wɔ, na bu suyo bu beŋgum tiɛ, na bu susi bu beŋgum kɔrɛ, na bu sukun bu beŋgum, na bu sudi aluwa, na bu sudadaka bu beŋgum, na bu sukpɔ bu beŋgum, na bu sukan bu beŋgum jɔrɛ.

eng: Instead, it is the person who is a Jew inside, who is circumcised in spirit, not literally. That persons praise doesnt come from people but from God.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ bo i ti Yuda­fɔ nahɔrɛ su la bo wɔ kaaki wo i ahore­mbaa su ni, na kɛnkɛnnɛ wuraa­wa nahɔrɛ su fite ahore­mbaa su wɔ, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni kaaki sɔnɔ a, má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni. Nyini sɔnɔ sɔ, Nyɛmɛ kansi i a, má adamande.

eng: they slander people, and they hate God. They are rude and proud, and they brag. They invent ways to be evil, and they are disobedient to their parents.
local: na bu susaaki bu beŋgum duma, na bu sukpɔ Nyɛmɛ, na bu sukpiɛ bu beŋgum nzukɔ, na bu la karambani, na bu sufɔ, na bu susunsun kabo bu yo na bu yo satiɛm, na bu sukete bu wu­fɔm nɔaniɛ.

eng: No, not at all, but through the law of faith. We consider that a person is treated as righteous by faith, apart from what is accomplished under the Law.
local: Ya niɛ ka deke bo i kere ka Nyɛmɛ de sɔnɔ ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n la kabo wɔ yo Yesu yarada n wɔ, má ka i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su n dɛɛ ti a.
