eng: They are without understanding, disloyal, without affection, and without mercy.
local: na bu la má ŋgɔnlɛ, na bu yo má sa bo ba se ka bu yo i ni, na bu koro má minɛm, na bu si má bu beŋgum aŋunfɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Im talking to you as people who know the Law. Dont you know that the Law has power over someone only as long as he or she lives?.
local: Tɔ, m ninbaam, m sujɔjɔ ambɛrɛ bo am si nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni dɔ a. Am si má ka nzɛn sɔnɔ toko la ŋgɔɔ, ye nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ i su a? Ama, i nya wu wieeri, i koro niɛ má i su kun.

eng: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isnt God the God of Gentiles also? Yes, God is also the God of Gentiles.
local: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka Nyɛmɛ ti má Yuda­fɔm ŋgumi Nyɛmɛ, i ti bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n gusu Nyɛmɛ wɔ.

eng: Though they know Gods decision that those who persist in such practices deserve death, they not only keep doing these things but also approve others who practice them.
local: Bu si kabo Nyɛmɛ akere ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nati yo sɔ, wɔ yo daka ka i mi wú ni, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, bu toko yo nyini sam wɔ, ama, bo i kɔ tara i kɛrɛ n la ka, bu jina minɛ bo bu gusu nati yo nyini sam ni sin wɔ.

eng: A married woman is united with her husband under the Law while he is alive. But if her husband dies, she is released from the Law concerning her husband.
local: Ya fa i ka, nzɛn bara aja, na i hun toko la ŋgɔɔ, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ma má atin ka i yaki i ajaa, ama, nzɛn i hun n nya wuuri, i la atin bo i koro kɔ ja biɛsɔ fɔfɔrɛ.

eng: Since God is one, then the one who makes the circumcised righteous by faith will also make the one who isnt circumcised righteous through faith.
local: Nyɛmɛ ti kun wɔ. Yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ ŋa Yuda­fɔm bo ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, ye minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ ni, bu yarada ma Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a.

eng: So then, if she lives with another man while her husband is alive, shes committing adultery. But if her husband dies, shes free from the Law, so she wont be committing adultery if she marries someone else.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn i hun n toko la ŋgɔɔ na i kɔ jaari biɛsɔ fɔfɔrɛ, bu fére i ka bara bo wɔ saaki ajaa ciire wɔ, ama, nzɛn i hun n awu ye i kɔ jaari biɛsɔ fɔfɔrɛ, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ma i atin wɔ, wɔ saaki má ajaa ciire lɛɛ.

eng: Do we then cancel the Law through this faith? Absolutely not! Instead, we confirm the Law.
local: Nyini sukere má ka ya yo Yesu yarada n dɛɛ ti, ya fa má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n yo má juma a. I ti má sɔ, ama, nyini ma ya bita nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kenken a.

eng: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, you also died with respect to the Law through the body of Christ, so that you could be united with someone else. You are united with the one who was raised from the dead so that we can bear fruit for God.
local: Tɔ, nyini ti, m ninbaam, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ dɔ. Kabo am ni Kristo ayo kun na wɔ wu ni, bu saŋga kuunri am ni yiri wɔ lɛɛ. Nyini ti, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ má am su kun, nyini ma am la atin na am kaaki be dɛɛm n a. Nyini la ka, am ti Kristo dɛɛm wɔ lɛɛ, yiri bo i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu ni. Deke n bo i ti na i yo sɔ n la ka, ya koro yo sakpa ma Nyɛmɛ.

eng: When we were self-centered, the sinful passions aroused through the Law were at work in all the parts of our body, so that we bore fruit for death.
local: Saŋga bo ya yo su ya adamande woo­wa su ni, ya koroo­wa la ka, ya sufa ya ŋuniɛ­mbaam suyo satiɛm, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɛɛ ti. Nyini satiɛm n awieeri la yiwee wɔ.

eng: But now we have been released from the Law. We have died with respect to the thing that controlled us, so that we can be slaves in the new life under the Spirit, not in the old life under the written Law.
local: Ama, kisa, ya nya ya tii afite nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i yo niɛ ya su ni, dama ya wu wɔ. Nyini ti, ya su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, ya su má atin dawa n bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo ba kɛrɛ ase n su, ama, ya su Nyɛmɛ ni atin fɔfɔrɛ n bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa ama ya ni su a.

eng: So what are we going to say? That the Law is sin? Absolutely not! But I wouldnt have known sin except through the Law. I wouldnt have known the desire for what others have if the Law had not said, Dont desire to take what others have .
local: Tɔ, ye ya se sɛ wɔ? Ya koro se ka nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ti satiɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má sɔ. Nzɛn má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n awo má bɛrɛ, má m si má kabo satiɛ ti. Dama nzɛn má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ase má ka, Má a ma u konviki wura be dɛɛ neŋge nu, má m si má ka nzɛn u konviki wuraari be dɛɛ neŋge nu, i ti satiɛ wɔ.

eng: But sin seized the opportunity and used this commandment to produce all kinds of desires in me. Sin is dead without the Law.
local: Nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo i seeri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, satiɛ anya atin m ahore su, nyini ma m la konviki barasu barasu fa kɔ minɛm neŋge nu n a. Nyini kere ka nzɛn nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n wo má bɛrɛ, satiɛ la má yiko, i ti ka deke bo wɔ wu wɔ.

eng: I used to be alive without the Law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life.
local: Saŋga be m yo la ŋgɔɔ ɔ, na m si má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n jɔrɛ, ama, saŋga bo nɔaniɛ maa­wa n baari ni, na wɔ yɛ satiɛ su m ahore su.

eng: and I died. So the commandment that was intended to give life brought death.
local: na ma wu. Na ma ŋu ka nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo má i ma ŋgɔɔ ni, mini dɔ, i faari yiwee baari a.

eng: Sin seized the opportunity through the commandment, deceived me, and killed me.
local: Nɔaniɛ maa­wa n dɛɛ ti, ye satiɛ anya atin awura m ahore su adadaka m, ye i kun m a.

eng: So the Law itself is holy, and the commandment is holy, righteous, and good.
local: Nyini kɛrɛ kere ka nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ni i tii, ti deke kpa fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, na nɔaniɛ maa­wa kun kun n kɛrɛ ti deke kpa a, na i kere sa bo i kɔ atin su.

eng: So did something good bring death to me? Absolutely not! But sin caused my death through something good so that sin would be exposed as sin. That way sin would become even more thoroughly sinful through the commandment.
local: Nyini ti, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i ti kpa ni, wɔ ma ma wu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má sɔ. Ama, satiɛ fa nyini nɔaniɛ kpa n su wɔ, ye i ma m wu a, adena satiɛ kere kabo i ti barasu nahɔrɛ su, na nɔaniɛ maa­wa n ma minɛm si kabo satiɛ ti deke tiɛ agaya ni.

eng: We know that the Law is spiritual, but Im made of flesh and blood, and Im sold as a slave to sin.
local: Ya si ka nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ti awiɛwiɛ jɔrɛ wɔ, ama, mini yiri, m bita adamande dɛɛ woo­wa a, na m ti satiɛ akɔɔ.

eng: I dont know what Im doing, because I dont do what I want to do. Instead, I do the thing that I hate.
local: Sam bo m yo bu ni, m koro si má deke n bo i ti na m yo bu ni, dama deke n bo m koro ka m yo bu ni, m yo má nyini, ama, deke n bo m kpɔ bu ni, m yo nyini wɔ.

eng: But if Im doing the thing that I dont want to do, Im agreeing that the Law is right.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn m yoori satiɛ be, na m koro má i, ma soro su ka nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ti kpa wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: But now Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, its sin that lives in me.
local: Ma ŋu ka má m koroya­nu ye m yo nyini satiɛm n a, ama, satiɛ n bo i wo m ahore su ni ma m yo sɔ a.

eng: I know that good doesnt live in me—that is, in my body. The desire to do good is inside of me, but I cant do it.
local: M si ka woo­wa n bo m bita i ni, i ti má kpa haari kaan sɔ, i ti adamande woo­wa a, nyini ti, sakpa­yo wo m ahore su, ama, m koro yo má i.

eng: I dont do the good that I want to do, but I do the evil that I dont want to do.
local: M koro ka m yo sakpa, ama, m koro yo má i, satiɛ n bo m koro má ka m yo i ni, m yo nyini wɔ.

eng: But if I do the very thing that I dont want to do, then Im not the one doing it anymore. Instead, it is sin that lives in me that is doing it.
local: Satiɛ n bo m yo bu na m koro má ka m yo bu ni, nyini la ka, má mini ni m tii yo bu a, ama, satiɛ n bo i wo m ahore su ni yo bu a.

eng: So I find that, as a rule, when I want to do what is good, evil is right there with me.
local: Ma ŋu ka nyini jɔrɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka, saŋga kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m yo sakpa, nyini saŋga ye m yo satiɛ a.

eng: I gladly agree with the Law on the inside.
local: Dama m sunsunni ni m ahore su, m koro Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni.

eng: but I see a different law at work in my body. It wages a war against the law of my mind and takes me prisoner with the law of sin that is in my body.
local: Ama, ma ŋu ka nɔaniɛ barasu be wo m ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ nu, nyini nɔaniɛ tiɛ n kun luɛ ni nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i wo m sunsunni ni m ahore su n wɔ, na i kunma m ŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ nu, na i tara m nyi m.

eng: Im a miserable human being. Who will deliver me from this dead corpse?.
local: Oi. Mini aŋunfɔ­fɔ sɔ. Ŋma dé m tii ji a, na i yí m fite ahin adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa n nu a, bo i sucin m kɔ yiwee n nu, na m ka ŋgbɛɛn n a?.

eng: Thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then Im a slave to Gods Law in my mind, but Im a slave to sins law in my body.
local: M da Nyɛmɛ asi, dama wɔ de m tii aji, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Tɔ, mini dɔ, m sunsunni ni m ahore su, m su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su wɔ, ama, m adamande dɛɛ woo­wa su, m su satiɛ atin su wɔ.

eng: So now there isnt any condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
local: Nyini ti, kisa, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun, Nyɛmɛ di má i jɔrɛ cin má i sui.

eng: The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
local: Dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i ma ya ŋgɔɔ, na i fite Yesu Kristo dɔ ni, ade u tii aji, na wɔ yi u afite nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i ma a yo satiɛ na a wu n nu ni.

eng: God has done what was impossible for the Law, since it was weak because of selfishness. God condemned sin in the body by sending his own Son to deal with sin in the same body as humans, who are controlled by sin.
local: Nyini nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n la má faŋga, dama adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n ayɔrɔki. Deke bo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n akoro ayo má i ni, Nyɛmɛ ayo i, yiri la bo i sunmaari i tiɛŋgu Wa durunya n nu fɛn, ni aŋuniɛ­mbaa bo i ferema ni adamande dɛɛ n bo i yo satiɛ ni, na wɔ di satiɛ bo i wo adamande ŋuniɛ n nu n jɔrɛ, na wɔ yo i ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: He did this so that the righteous requirement of the Law might be fulfilled in us. Now the way we live is based on the Spirit, not based on selfishness.
local: Deke bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ yoori sɔ n la ka, adena ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su kabo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kereeri ni, dama yɛrɛ, ya su má adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n atin su kun, ama, ya su awiɛwiɛ n dɛɛ atin ni su a.

eng: People whose lives are based on selfishness think about selfish things, but people whose lives are based on the Spirit think about things that are related to the Spirit.
local: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu su adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n atin su ni, bu sunsunni kɛrɛ wo adamande sa koroo­wa su wɔ, ama, minɛm bo bu su Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ atin n su ni, bu sunsunni kɛrɛ wo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n jɔrɛ su wɔ.

eng: The attitude that comes from selfishness leads to death, but the attitude that comes from the Spirit leads to life and peace.
local: Bo i sunsunni wo adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n su, i mi wú ɔ, ama, bo i sunsunni wo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n jɔrɛ su, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ, na i nyá ahoreda.

eng: So the attitude that comes from selfishness is hostile to God. It doesnt submit to Gods Law, because it cant.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sunsunni kɛrɛ wo adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n su ni, i mi ti Nyɛmɛ kpɔ­fɔ ɔ, i de má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, i koro de má i wɔ mmɔɔ fieo.

eng: People who are self-centered arent able to please God.
local: na bo i su adamande dɛɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n su ni, i koro yo má sa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu.

eng: But you arent self-centered. Instead you are in the Spirit, if in fact Gods Spirit lives in you. If anyone doesnt have the Spirit of Christ, they dont belong to him.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, am su má adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n atin n su, am su Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ atin ni su a, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo am nu n wɔ. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la má Kristo Awiɛwiɛ ni, i mi ti má Kristo dɛɛ.

eng: If Christ is in you, the Spirit is your life because of Gods righteousness, but the body is dead because of sin.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ bo Kristo wo am dɔ ni, am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n bá wu wɔ, satiɛ dɛɛ ti, ama, Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ama am ŋgɔɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i de am ka am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su wɔ.

eng: If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead lives in you, the one who raised Christ from the dead will give life to your human bodies also, through his Spirit that lives in you.
local: Nahɔrɛ su, Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri Yesu i yiwee n nu, nyini ti, nzɛn i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo am nu, haari am ŋuniɛ­mbaa wuuri mmɔɔ, i téŋge i kabo i teŋgeeri Yesu i yiwee n nu n wɔ, dama i Awiɛwiɛ n wo am nu n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Im speaking the truth in Christ—Im not lying, as my conscience assures me with the Holy Spirit.
local: Tɔ, deke bo m sukan kere am ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, m bu má kɔrɛ, m ti Kristo dɛɛ wɔ, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n gusu kere m sunsunni nu a, ye i di daani m ahore su a.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we have an obligation, but it isnt an obligation to ourselves to live our lives on the basis of selfishness.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaam, ya di kaari, má yɛrɛ adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n su dɛɛ kaari, bo má ya fa su yɛrɛ adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n atin su ni.

eng: I have great sadness and constant pain in my heart.
local: Yiri la ka, m ahore asaaki agaya, na saŋga kɛrɛ i suyo m yaa.

eng: If you live on the basis of selfishness, you are going to die. But if by the Spirit you put to death the actions of the body, you will live.
local: Dama nzɛn am su adamande dɛɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa n atin su, am wú wɔ lɛɛ, ama, nzɛn am ma Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bukaari am, na am yakiiri adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa woo­wa ni, am nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: I wish I could be cursed, cut off from Christ if it helped my brothers and sisters, who are my flesh-and-blood relatives.
local: fa kɔ m ninbaa Yisareli­fɔm dɛɛ busu. Nzɛn má mini ni m tii kaari ŋgbɛɛn, na m kpaaki yakiiri Kristo, adena i buka m ninbaam, m dɛɛ tiɛŋgu minɛm ni, má m koro.

eng: All who are led by Gods Spirit are Gods sons and daughters.
local: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ma Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n kere bu atin, bɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam a.

eng: Brothers and sisters, my hearts desire is for Israels salvation. Thats my prayer to God for them.
local: M ninbaam, deke bo m koro i m ahore su la ka, m minɛm Yisareli­fɔm nya tii­de, m nyɛmɛsere lɛɛ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: They are Israelites. The adoption as Gods children, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the Law, the worship, and the promises belong to them.
local: Bɛrɛ la Yisareli­fɔm bo Nyɛmɛ maari bu atin ka bu yo i nbaam, na bu ŋuuri i jirima woo­wa ni, na i faari sɛsɛ­sum diiri i di ni bɛrɛ ni, na i faari nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari bu ni, na i kereeri bu kabo bu su i barasu ni, na i seeri ka i yó sakpa beberebe ma bu ni.

eng: You didnt receive a spirit of slavery to lead you back again into fear, but you received a Spirit that shows you are adopted as his children. With this Spirit, we cry, Abba, Father.
local: Dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo am ade i ni, i yaki má am na am kaaki yo akɔɔm kun na am soro sɛrɛ, ama, yiri ma am yo Nyɛmɛ nbaam a, na yiri ma am koro bo fere Nyɛmɛ ka, Abba , i bu la ka, M Si.

eng: I can vouch for them: they are enthusiastic about God. However, it isnt informed by knowledge.
local: M si tenle su ka bu nyunu aboro ka bu su Nyɛmɛ atin n su nahɔrɛ su, ama, bu si má kabo bu yo na bu su i su.

eng: The Jewish ancestors are theirs, and the Christ descended from those ancestors. He is the one who rules over all things, who is God, and who is blessed forever. Amen.
local: Bu ti ya nam busu nu­fɔm wɔ, haari Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ sunmaari i na i kaaki adamande baari durunya n nu ni, bu wuuri i fite bu dɛɛ busu n nu wɔ. Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni la yiko tara deke n kɛrɛ, yiri ti Nyɛmɛ a, yiri ti ye ni kansi saŋga kɛrɛ a. Ami.

eng: The same Spirit agrees with our spirit, that we are Gods children.
local: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ni i tii buka ya dɛɛ awiɛwiɛ n ye i di daani ya ahore su se ka ya ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam a.

eng: They dont submit to Gods righteousness because they dont understand his righteousness, and they try to establish their own righteousness.
local: Dama bu si má atin bo Nyɛmɛ de minɛm ka ba yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, ama, bu kpini bu tiɛŋgu atin wɔ, na bu su i su. Nyini ti, na ba kete Nyɛmɛ atin ni, ba de má kabo Nyɛmɛ ŋa bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni.

eng: But its not as though Gods word has failed. Not all who are descended from Israel are part of Israel.
local: I ti má ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ti má nahɔrɛ wɔ, dama má bu kɛrɛ bo bu wuuri bu Yisareli­fɔm busu nu ni n ti Yisareli­fɔm nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: But if we are children, we are also heirs. We are Gods heirs and fellow heirs with Christ, if we really suffer with him so that we can also be glorified with him.
local: Kabo ya ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam ni, ya nyá deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i minɛm ni, na ya ni Yesu Kristo gusu sáŋga nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i minɛm n a. Nzɛn ya ni yiri saŋga diiri wahara, ya ni yiri gusu sáŋga nya i jirima woo­wa ni.

eng: So, brothers and sisters, because of Gods mercies, I encourage you to present your bodies as a living sacrifice that is holy and pleasing to God. This is your appropriate priestly service.
local: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, m sere am wɔ, am niɛ kabo Nyɛmɛ asi am aŋunfɔ ayo am sakpa ni, na am fa am ŋu ma i, kabo má am yo yoo­wa fa ma i ni, ama, am toko la ŋgɔɔ, na am kaaki Nyɛmɛ minɛm na am su i ni anyum­boro. Nyini ti nyɛmɛsu bo i sun i nu a, dama wɔ yo sakpa ama am.

eng: Not all of Abrahams children are called Abrahams descendants, but instead your descendants will be named through Isaac. .
local: Nahɔrɛ, má nbaam kɛrɛ bo Abraham wuuri bu ni ti i nbaam nahɔrɛ su a, ama, Nyɛmɛ yo seeri Abraham ka, U wa Izaki sin­fɔm ŋgumi yó u busu nu­fɔm a.

eng: I believe that the present suffering is nothing compared to the coming glory that is going to be revealed to us.
local: M sunsun ka wahara n bo ya sudi i kisa ni, i ti má pui, ya koro fa má i sunsun má jirima woo­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ bá yi i kere ya ni.

eng: Christ is the goal of the Law, which leads to righteousness for all who have faith in God.
local: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka Kristo juma n bo i yoori i ni, yiri kuunri nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ nɔɔ a, adena sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori i yarada, Nyɛmɛ de i mi ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ.

eng: Dont be conformed to the patterns of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds so that you can figure out what Gods will is—what is good and pleasing and mature.
local: Má am ma am woo­wa ti kabo durunya n nu­fɔm woo­wa ti barasu ni, ama, am kaaki am sunsunni fɔfɔrɛ, na am kaaki am wo, nyini ma am koro si Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu sa a, bo i ti kpa, na i sun i nu, na i ti kabo i koro ka i ti ni.

eng: That means it isnt the natural children who are Gods children, but it is the children from the promise who are counted as descendants.
local: Nyini la ka, má nbaam kɛrɛ bo Abraham wuuri bu kabo adamande wu nbaam ni ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam nahɔrɛ su a, ama, i nbaam bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i wú bu ni ŋgumi, ti i busu nu­fɔm nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: The whole creation waits breathless with anticipation for the revelation of Gods sons and daughters.
local: Deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu n ŋminda ni anyum­boro wɔ, na bu bá ŋu kabo Nyɛmɛ yí i nbaam fite fofoe n nu.

eng: Moses writes about the righteousness that comes from the Law: The person who does these things will live by them. .
local: Moyisi yo kɛrɛ fa kɔɔri kabo minɛ koro su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, na bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i ŋa, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ su, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Because of the grace that God gave me, I can say to each one of you: dont think of yourself more highly than you ought to think. Instead, be reasonable since God has measured out a portion of faith to each one of you.
local: Tɔ, Nyɛmɛ aceeri n bo wɔ fa ama m dɛɛ ti, m sutu am kɛrɛ fɔ ɔ ka, má be wura i ŋu kpiri tara kabo i ti ni, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sunsun na i niɛ i ŋu kabo i ti nahɔrɛ su barasu, na i si kabo Nyɛmɛ ama i mi yarada barasu ni.

eng: The words in the promise were: A year from now I will return, and Sarah will have a son .
local: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri i n yaa, i ŋa, Ti fɛn, ŋgɔɔ cɛɛn ka buusɔ, m káaki ba na m bá ŋu ka u yi Saara bita baa­biɛsɔ.

eng: Creation was subjected to frustration, not by its own choice—it was the choice of the one who subjected it—but in the hope.
local: Dama deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu n wo bɛrɛ wɔ, bu la má kpɛrɛ, má ka bɛrɛ koro sɔ a, ama, Nyɛmɛ ma bu la má kpɛrɛ a. Deke bo i ti na i yo sɔ n la ka, i la tama.

eng: But the righteousness that comes from faith talks like this: Dont say in your heart , Who will go up into heaven? .
local: Ama, fa kɔ kabo yarada dɛɛ ti, na sɔnɔ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, i ŋa, Má a sunsun u ahore su ka, ŋma fún kɔ aŋgoro a? Nyini la ka, i kɔ́ fa Kristo jura ba lɛɛ.

eng: We have many parts in one body, but the parts dont all have the same function.
local: Adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa n ti mɔmɔ kun wɔ, ama, i la bue bue, i kɛrɛ ni i juma bo i yo i.

eng: Not only that, but also Rebecca conceived children with one man, our ancestor Isaac.
local: Má nyini ŋgumi a, am teŋge su ka ya na Izaki yo maari i yi Rɛbɛka akunnu, ye i wuuri ndaam n a.

eng: that the creation itself will be set free from slavery to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of Gods children.
local: ka deke n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yi bu ni, bu nyá bu tii, na má bu yo akɔɔm kun, na bu wu na bu kpɔrɔ, ama, na bu nyá tii­de ni jirima woo­wa kabo Nyɛmɛ nbaam kɛrɛ nya i barasu ni.

eng: or Who will go down into the region below? .
local: Wara Ŋma júra kunma ŋgoŋgo n bo i la má awieeri nu a? Nyini la ka, i kɔ́ fa Kristo fun fite bo ba wum n nu ba lɛɛ.

eng: In the same way, though there are many of us, we are one body in Christ, and individually we belong to each other.
local: Ya gusu dɔ i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ya sunnu, ama, ya ni Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti, ya ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa mɔmɔ kun wɔ, na ya kɛrɛ ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be bo ya buka ya beŋgu wɔ.

eng: When they hadnt been born yet and when they hadnt yet done anything good or bad, it was shown that Gods purpose would continue because it was based on his choice.
local: Saŋga bo Rɛbɛka nya wu má i ndaam ni, na bu nya yo má sakpa wara satiɛ be ni, nyini saŋga n ye Nyɛmɛ seeri i ka, Baa­kpiɛn ni bá su baa­kan n a. Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu ye i yoori sɔ n a, yiri koro i sɔnɔ ye i yiiri i a, má ka bu be sa­yoo­wa dɛɛ ti a.

eng: We know that the whole creation is groaning together and suffering labor pains up until now.
local: Ya si ka deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ni, wo waraki ni yaari nu wɔ, haari ni kisa, kabo bara ba suwu baa na i di yaari ni.

eng: But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart .
local: Deke bo i suse n la ka, Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n waari má ni wɔrɔ. I wo u nɔɔ nu, ni u ahore­mbaa mmɔɔ su. Yiri la labari n bo ya kaanri i n fa kɔ yarada­yo su ni.

eng: So I ask you, has God rejected his people? Absolutely not! Im an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
local: M koro ka m bisa ka, Nyɛmɛ akete i minɛm wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a? Ai, wɔ kete má bu e. Am ti fɛn. Mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii, m ti Yisareli­fɔ ɔ. M ti Abraham busu nu­fɔ ɔ, m fite Bɛnjamin dɛɛ lɛɛ anɔ n nu wɔ.

eng: We have different gifts that are consistent with Gods grace that has been given to us. If your gift is prophecy, you should prophesy in proportion to your faith.
local: Nyini ti, aceeri barasu barasum bo Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti na wɔ fa ama ya ni, am ma ya fa yo i juma ni. Nzɛn aceeri n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama be n la ka i sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, i ti ye ni ka i mi sufa yo juma, kabo i yarada n ti barasu ni.

eng: As it is written, I loved Jacob, but I hated Esau .
local: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Jakɔbu, ye m koro i a, ama, Isawu yiri, m kpɔ i wɔ.

eng: And its not only the creation. We ourselves who have the Spirit as the first crop of the harvest also groan inside as we wait to be adopted and for our bodies to be set free.
local: Má deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ni ŋgumi wo waraki su a, ama, yɛrɛ mmɔɔ bo ya de i Awiɛwiɛ bo i ti ciciri su aceeri ni, ya wo waraki su suŋminda ni anyum­boro wɔ, ka Nyɛmɛ bá fa ya yo i nbaam, na i de ya ŋuniɛ ni.

eng: Because if you confess with your mouth Jesus is Lord and in your heart you have faith that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
local: Nzɛn a teke u nɔɔ jɔjɔɔri minɛm nyunu ka, Yesu ti u Mibiɛ nahɔrɛ su, na a yoori yarada u ahore su ka Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu, a nyá tii­de.

eng: God hasnt rejected his people, whom he knew in advance. Or dont you know what the scripture says in the case of Elijah, when he pleads with God against Israel?.
local: Nyɛmɛ akete má i minɛm bo i du mɔ yiiri bu ni. Am nya kan má Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu fa kɔ i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Eliya n su, kabo i jarakiiri Yisareli­fɔm Nyɛmɛ dɔ n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: If your gift is service, devote yourself to serving. If your gift is teaching, devote yourself to teaching.
local: Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ ama be aceeri ka i su amusui, i ti ye ni ka i mi sufa su. Nzɛn wɔ ma be aceeri ka i fa kere minɛm, i ti ye ni ka i mi sufa kere bu.

eng: So what are we going to say? Isnt this unfair on Gods part? Absolutely not!.
local: Ye ya se sɛ wɔ? Ya koro se ka Nyɛmɛ ayo má sa bo i kɔ atin su, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, ya koro se má sɔ fieo.

eng: We were saved in hope. If we see what we hope for, that isnt hope. Who hopes for what they already see?.
local: Tii­de n bo ya nya i ni, nyini ma ya la tama n a. Nzɛn a la deke, a se má ka a la tama ka a nya i. Deke bo sɔnɔ la i, i se má ka i la i tama.

eng: Trusting with the heart leads to righteousness, and confessing with the mouth leads to salvation.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori yarada i ahore su, ye Nyɛmɛ de i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i teke i nɔɔ jɔjɔɔri minɛm nyunu ka wɔ yo yarada, nyini ma i nya tii­de a.

eng: Lord, they have killed your prophets, and they have torn down your altars. Im the only one left, and they are trying to take my life. .
local: Bo i ŋa, Micɛra, ba kun u nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛ, ba kaaki afufu u yoo­wa yoo­birim n kɛrɛ, wɔ kɔ aka mini ŋgumi kpein wɔ, ye bu koro ka bu kun m gusu n a.

eng: If your gift is encouragement, devote yourself to encouraging. The one giving should do it with no strings attached. The leader should lead with passion. The one showing mercy should be cheerful.
local: Nzɛn wɔ ma be aceeri ka i sutu minɛm fɔ na i wura bu kpɛkpɛrɛ kekereke, i ti ye ni ka i mi sutu fɔ yo sɔ. Nzɛn wɔ ma be aceeri ka i suyo sakpa na i ce minɛm aceeri, i ti ye ni ka i mi yo i ni ahore­mbaa kun. Nzɛn wɔ ma be aceeri ka i yo minɛm mɔ­fɔ, i ti ye ni ka i yo sɔ ni anyum­boro. Ye nzɛn wɔ ma be aceeri ka i suyo sakpa ma minɛm bo bu wo ahoresaaki nu ni, i ti ye ni ka i yo sɔ ni ahorejɔ.

eng: He says to Moses, Ill have mercy on whomever I choose to have mercy, and Ill show compassion to whomever I choose to show compassion .
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ yo seeri Moyisi ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m buka i, m búka i wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m si i aŋunfɔ, m sí i aŋunfɔ ɔ.

eng: But if we hope for what we dont see, we wait for it with patience.
local: Ama, nzɛn ya la tama ka ya nyá deke be na ya nya nya má i, ya mindi ŋminda ni anyum­boro wɔ, na ya bá nya i.

eng: The scripture says, All who have faith in him wont be put to shame .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori i yarada, i mi dí má nyinsɛ.

eng: But what is Gods reply to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand people who havent bowed their knees to Baal. .
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ yo seeri i sɛ wɔ? I ŋa, Ma yaki Yisareli­fɔm minɛ akpii nso afa ase Yisareli asiɛn n su. Bɛrɛ ye bu nya kutu má amɔɛ Baali n na bu su i a.

eng: Love should be shown without pretending. Hate evil, and hold on to what is good.
local: Am koro minɛm ni akunnu fofoe, am kpɔ satiɛ, na am su deke bo i ti kpa su.

eng: So then, it doesnt depend on a persons desire or effort. It depends entirely on God, who shows mercy.
local: Nyini ti, má adamande koroya­nu, wara deke bo i boro i nyi yo i ni ti jɔrɛ a, ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ si aŋunfɔ ni, nyini ti jɔrɛ a.

eng: In the same way, the Spirit comes to help our weakness. We dont know what we should pray, but the Spirit himself pleads our case with unexpressed groans.
local: I ti sɔ lɛɛ, saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya yɔrɔki na ya si má kabo ya yo ni, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ba buka ya, beni, ya si má kabo ya sere Nyɛmɛ barasu, ama, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ni i tii waraki sɔ ɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ma ya, ni waraki bo adamande koro jɔjɔ má i.

eng: There is no distinction between Jew and Greek, because the same Lord is Lord of all, who gives richly to all who call on him.
local: Nyini ti, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, bu la má kpakpaaki­nu, Micɛra kun ni ti bu kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a. Yiri ma minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu bo fere i duma deke kpa bo i la má aŋusu n a.

eng: So also in the present time there is a remaining group by the choice of Gods grace.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, kisa gusu, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Nyɛmɛ ayi Yisareli­fɔm nu minɛ kaan sɔ ɔ, ye wɔ fere bu a, fa kɔ i ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Love each other like the members of your family. Be the best at showing honor to each other.
local: Am koro am beŋgu Kristo­fɔm ka am ti nibaam wɔ, na am bu am beŋgum ka bu tara am.

eng: Scripture says to Pharaoh, I have put you in this position for this very thing: so I can show my power in you and so that my name can be spread through the entire earth. .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ yo seeri Ijipiti Fɛmɛ Faaro ka, Deke n bo i ti na m yiiri u tanaari fɛmɛya biɛ n su n la ka, m kére m yiko u ŋgɔɔ su, na m duma tá bara yɛ durunya n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: The one who searches hearts knows how the Spirit thinks, because he pleads for the saints, consistent with Gods will.
local: Nyɛmɛ fiiti adamande ahore­mbaa su na i si kabo i ti barasu a, yiri si deke bo i wo i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n sunsunni nu a, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n su kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni su a, na i sere Nyɛmɛ ma i minɛm.

eng: All who call on the Lords name will be saved. .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i bo fereeri Mibiɛ n duma, i mi nyá tii­de.

eng: But if it is by grace, it isnt by whats done anymore. If it were, Gods grace wouldnt be grace.
local: I ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ yi bu a, má ka bu sa­yoo­wa dɛɛ ti a. Nzɛn má i ti ka bu sa­yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti, má ahɔrɔma n ti má aceeri kun.

eng: Dont hesitate to be enthusiastic—be on fire in the Spirit as you serve the Lord!.
local: Am su Mibiɛ n ni anyum­boro, má am yo nviɛya, am ma am ahore­mbaa bi ni nyɛmɛsu.

eng: So then, God has mercy on whomever he wants to, but he makes resistant whomever he wants to.
local: Nyini kɛrɛ kere ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i si i aŋunfɔ, ye i si i mi aŋunfɔ a, na minɛm bo i koro ka i yo bu ahore­mbaa kekereke ni, na i yo bu.

eng: We know that God works all things together for good for the ones who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.
local: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ wo minɛm bo bu koro i n dɔ, na i buka bu, adena i yo deke kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma bu, bɛrɛ la minɛm bo i du mɔ sɛsɛɛri su ka i fere bu ni, na wɔ fere bu.

eng: So how can they call on someone they dont have faith in? And how can they have faith in someone they havent heard of? And how can they hear without a preacher?.
local: Tɔ, bu yo sɛ koro bo fere i duma, má ka ba du mɔ ayo i yarada? Ye bu yo sɛ koro yo i yarada, má ka ba du mɔ ati i jɔrɛ? Ye bu yo sɛ koro ti i jɔrɛ a, má ka be adu mɔ akan i labari kpa n akere bu?.

eng: So what? Israel didnt find what it was looking for. Those who were chosen found it, but the others were resistant.
local: Nzu bekun a? Yisareli­fɔm n anya má deke n bo bu kpiiri i busu ni, ama, minɛm n bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri bu ni, bɛrɛ nyaari i a, na bo bu kaari n kɛrɛ ahore­mbaa ayo kekereke.

eng: Be happy in your hope, stand your ground when youre in trouble, and devote yourselves to prayer.
local: Am la ahoresi n dɛɛ ti, am ma am ahore jɔ. Saŋga kɛrɛ bo am sudi wahara, am mindi, na am sere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni anyum­boro.

eng: So you are going to say to me, Then why does he still blame people? Who has ever resisted his will.
local: Nyini ti, beni, be koro bisa m ka, Nzɛn i ti sɔ, nzu ti ye Nyɛmɛ di minɛm bo ba kete i n jɔrɛ na i jaraki bu a? Ŋma koro kete na i kanda sa bo Nyɛmɛ koro i n a.

eng: We know this because God knew them in advance, and he decided in advance that they would be conformed to the image of his Son. That way his Son would be the first of many brothers and sisters.
local: Nyɛmɛ du mɔ siiri nyini minɛm wɔ, na wɔ cici ka bu bá yo kabo i Wa n ti ni, nyini ma i Wa n yoori baa­kpiɛn, i nbaam kɛrɛ nu n a.

eng: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written, How beautiful are the feet of those who announce the good news. .
local: Ye bu yo sɛ koro kan i labari kpa n kere minɛm a, má ka be asunma bu minɛm dɔ? Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu, ka, Bo bu suba kan Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n ma ya ni, bu natiiri yo fiɛ.

eng: As it is written, God gave them a dull spirit, so that their eyes would not see and their ears not hear, right up until the present day. .
local: I ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n wɔ, ka, Nyɛmɛ ama bu ahore­mbaa nɔnɔɔri, na i ma bu nyu­mbaa ŋu má asi, na i ma bu sui ti má asui, haari ni nyuma.

eng: Contribute to the needs of Gods people, and welcome strangers into your home.
local: Am kpaaki am neŋgem nu ma Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu la má be ni, na am boro am nyi sude minɛm wɔfɔ.

eng: You are only a human being. Who do you think you are to talk back to God? Does the clay say to the potter, Why did you make me like this? .
local: M ŋa, wɔrɔ la ŋma na a se Nyɛmɛ akuruwa a? Dundu koro bisa má yakati ta­fɔ n ka, Nzu ti ye a taari m sɔ a.

eng: Those who God decided in advance would be conformed to his Son, he also called. Those whom he called, he also made righteous. Those whom he made righteous, he also glorified.
local: Nyɛmɛ du mɔ ciciiri nyini n kɛrɛ wɔ ka, na wɔ nya fere bu, na bɛrɛ n bo wɔ fere bu n ye wɔ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a, na bɛrɛ n bo wɔ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, ye wɔ ma bu i jirima woo­wa n be n a.

eng: But everyone hasnt obeyed the good news. As Isaiah says, Lord, who has had faith in our message? .
local: Ama, má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ soroori su deeri labari kpa n a, kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya yo seeri ni, ka, Ya Mibiɛ, ŋma ayo ya labari n yarada? Ai, be ade má i.

eng: And David says, Their table should become a pitfall and a trap, a stumbling block and payback to them for what they have done. .
local: Na Fɛmɛ Davidi ase ka, Ma bu cɛɛn­kpiri diire dii­biri n kaaki toroku ni diɛndiɛ tara bu. Ma bu kpaasi, na bu nya bu satiɛ ŋkatɔ.

eng: Bless people who harass you—bless and dont curse them.
local: Am sere deke kpa Nyɛmɛ dɔ ma minɛm bo bu kere am wuwuruwa ni, am sere deke kpa ma bu, má am sere deke tiɛ ma bu.

eng: Doesnt the potter have the power over the clay to make one pot for special purposes and another for garbage from the same lump of clay?.
local: Yakati ta­fɔ n la atin bo i fa i yakati n ta dundu bo i sufa yo juma cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn, na i kaaki la atin bo i fa be ta dundu bo i fa yo juma cɛɛn kpiri cɛɛn.

eng: So what are we going to say about these things? If God is for us, who is against us?.
local: Tɔ, kisa, ye ya se sɛ wɔ bekun a, fa kɔ ahin sam kɛrɛ su a? Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ wo ya dɔ, ŋma koro yo ya sa be a?.

eng: So, faith comes from listening, but its listening by means of Christs message.
local: Nyini ti, kana sɔnɔ ba yo Yesu Kristo yarada, má ka wɔ ti i labari kpa n ka, kana sɔnɔ gusu ba ti labari kpa ni, má ka be akan Kristo nɔaniɛ ni akere i.

eng: Their eyes should be darkened so they cant see, and their backs always bent. .
local: Ma bu nyunu si biri na bu koro ŋu má asi, ma bu wi biri saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga ka ba sɔ sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri wɔ.

eng: Be happy with those who are happy, and cry with those who are crying.
local: Nzɛn minɛm wo ahorejɔ nu, am ma am ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ ahore ti jɔɔ­wa. Nzɛn minɛm wo ahoresaaki nu, am ma am ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ wo ahoresaaki nu.

eng: What if God very patiently puts up with pots made for wrath that were designed for destruction, because he wanted to show his wrath and to make his power known?.
local: Nyini ti, Nyɛmɛ la atin bo i koro fa yaa, na minɛm ŋu kabo i yiko ti barasu. I gusu koro mindi agaya fa kɔ minɛm bo bu ma yaa ati i ni, bɛrɛ bo má wɔ yo daka ka bu ka ŋgbɛɛn ni.

eng: He didnt spare his own Son but gave him up for us all. Wont he also freely give us all things with him?.
local: Nyɛmɛ akete má ka i fa má i tiɛŋgu Wa ni mmɔɔ ma má ya, ama, i faari i maari ya kɛrɛ wɔ. Nyini ti, i yo sɛ kete ka i má má ya deke n kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa n a?.

eng: But I ask you, didnt they hear it? Definitely! Their voice has gone out into the entire earth, and their message has gone out to the corners of the inhabited world. .
local: Ama, minɛ bo ba de má labari kpa ni, ba ti má jɔrɛ n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ a? Nahɔrɛ, ba ti i. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Ba bo ndoori abara ayɛ durunya n kɛrɛ nu, na bu nɔaniɛ aju asiɛn n kɛrɛ su.

eng: So Im asking you: They havent stumbled so that theyve fallen permanently, have they? Absolutely not! But salvation has come to the Gentiles by their failure, in order to make Israel jealous.
local: M koro ka m bisa ka, bo bu boori bu jaa kpaasi ni, bu yasu má kun wɔ? Ai, má sɔ. Ama, nahɔrɛ, bu kpaasi sɔ n dɛɛ ti, ye minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm nyaari tii­de a, adena Yuda­fɔm ŋu i na bu si bu kɔrɛ.

eng: Consider everyone as equal, and dont think that youre better than anyone else. Instead, associate with people who have no status. Dont think that youre so smart.
local: Am ma am ni am beŋgum saŋga wo ni sawɔrɛ, má am koro kpiri, ama, am fa am ŋu mata tarakam. Má am se ka ambɛrɛ ŋgumi la ŋgɔnlɛ a.

eng: What if he did this to make the wealth of his glory known toward pots made for mercy, which he prepared in advance for glory?.
local: Na i gusu la atin bo i fa i jirima woo­wa bo i la má aŋusu n fa kere minɛm bo wɔ si bu aŋunfɔ ni, na wɔ du mɔ asɛsɛ su ka bu bá nya i jirima woo­wa n be ni.

eng: Who will bring a charge against Gods elect people? It is God who acquits them.
local: Ŋma koro fa jɔrɛ fa sɔ Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo wɔ yi bu n a? Be wo má bɛrɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ ni i tii ade bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a.

eng: But I ask you again, didnt Israel understand? First, Moses says, I will make you jealous of those who arent a people, of a people without understanding. .
local: M koro ka m bisa bekun ka, Yisareli­fɔm ni, ba si má? Ba si. Moyisi du mɔ seeri ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, M má am si kɔrɛ fa kɔ minɛm bo bu ti má daanfiɛ n nahɔrɛ su ni, m má am fa yaa fa kɔ minɛm bo bu la má ŋgɔnlɛ n ŋu.

eng: But if their failure brings riches to the world, and their defeat brings riches to the Gentiles, how much more will come from the completion of their number!.
local: Nyini ti, ba kpaasi n dɛɛ ti, ye durunya n nu­fɔm anya deke kpa beberebe a, ba fun deke n bo má bu nya i n dɛɛ ti, ye bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n anya deke kpa beberebe n a. Tɔ, nzɛn i ti sɔ, na Yuda­fɔm bo bu bá yo yarada n búka su, i yó deke kpa tara sɔ.

eng: Dont pay back anyone for their evil actions with evil actions, but show respect for what everyone else believes is good.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ yo am satiɛ, má am wɔrɔ tiɛ tɔ i kaari. Ama, am boro am nyi na am suyo sa bo i ti kpa, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋu i, na bu se ka i ti kpa.

eng: Who is going to convict them? It is Christ Jesus who died, even more, who was raised, and who also is at Gods right side. It is Christ Jesus who also pleads our case for us.
local: Nyini ti, ŋma koro di ya jɔrɛ cin ya sui a? Be wo má bɛrɛ. Dama Kristo ni i tii wuuri wɔ, nahɔrɛ na wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu, i tana Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su, susere i ma ya.

eng: We are the ones God has called. We dont come only from the Jews but we also come from the Gentiles.
local: Yɛrɛ la nyini minɛm bo i si ya aŋunfɔ, na ya nya i jirima woo­wa n be ni, má ka yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm ŋgumi, ye wɔ fere ya nbem a, ama, wɔ fere bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni mmɔɔ nbem abuka su.

eng: And Isaiah even dares to say, I was found by those who didnt look for me; I revealed myself to those who didnt ask for me. .
local: Na Esaya ayo ahoresi, na wɔ se bu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Haari minɛm bo bu yo niɛniɛ má m busu ni, m ma ba ŋu m. Na minɛm bo bu yo bisa má m busu ni, ma bɔ bu nu afa m ŋu akere bu.

eng: Im speaking to you Gentiles. Considering that Im an apostle to the Gentiles, I publicize my own ministry.
local: Kisa m sujɔjɔ ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm ni gusu dɔ a. Juma n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama m ni, i sunmaari m ka m yo i jaramasa ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm dɔ ɔ, nyini ti ye m fa m juma n fa fɔ a.

eng: If possible, to the best of your ability, live at peace with all people.
local: Nzɛn am koro yo i, am boro am nyi na am ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ tana ni laifiɛ.

eng: Who will separate us from Christs love? Will we be separated by trouble, or distress, or harassment, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?.
local: Koro n bo Kristo koro ya ni, nzu deke koro kpaaki ya ni yiri a? Ai. Deke be koro kpaaki má ya ni yiri n nu. Kekereke o, wahara o, wuwuruwa o, ahɔɛ o, yaari o, anyum­boro o, yiwee o. Deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro kpaaki ya ni yiri nu.

eng: As it says also in Hosea, I will call my people those who arent my people, and the one who isnt well loved, I will call loved one. .
local: I ti kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Hoseya akɛrɛ i dɛɛ kadasi n nu n wɔ ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Minɛm bo bu yo ti má m minɛm ni, m fére bu ka m minɛm. Na yiri n bo ma koro má i ni, m fére i ka m sɔnɔ koroo­wa.

eng: But he says about Israel, All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people. .
local: Na Esaya akaaki ase fa kɔ Yisareli­fɔm su ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, m teŋge m saa fere nyini minɛm n wɔ, ama, bu ti atii kekereke ni ketema­fɔm wɔ.

eng: in the hope that somehow I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
local: na i má m tiɛŋgu minɛm si kɔrɛ, adena bu nbem nya tii­de.

eng: Dont try to get revenge for yourselves, my dear friends, but leave room for Gods wrath. It is written, Revenge belongs to me; I will pay it back, says the Lord .
local: M danfo kpam, nzɛn be ayo am satiɛ, má am wɔrɔ tiɛ tɔ i kaari, ama, am yaki i, na Nyɛmɛ cín i mi sui, dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Micɛra n ŋa, Mini cín bu sui a, mini tɔ́ bu kaari a.

eng: As it is written, We are being put to death all day long for your sake. We are treated like sheep for slaughter. .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, bu kun ya sɔ ɔ, ya ti ka nbɔɛm wɔ, ye bu kpiɛ ya kɔm a.

eng: And in the place where it was said to them, You arent my people, there they will be called the living Gods children .
local: Dika n bo i nu na m fereeri bu ka, Am ti má m minɛm ni, dɔ ye bu fére bu ka Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ nbaam n a.

eng: If their rejection has brought about a close relationship between God and the world, how can their acceptance mean anything less than life from the dead?.
local: Dama saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ keteeri bu ni, ye i ni durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ ayo kun a, ama, nzɛn i kaaki deeri bu bekun, i tí sɛ wɔ? I yó ka ba nya ŋgɔɔ afite yiwee nu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Instead, If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. By doing this, you will pile burning coals of fire upon his head .
local: Ama, am yo kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Nzɛn ahɔɛ ati u kpɔ­fɔ na a la diire, ma i na i di. Nzɛn nzuehɔɛ ati i, ma i nzue na i nu. Nzɛn a yoori sɔ, i ti ka a tɔrɔ sin­mɔnɛ agɔ i mi tii su wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: But in all these things we win a sweeping victory through the one who loved us.
local: Haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni, ya di nasara a, na ya toko di nasara buka su, Kristo bo i koro ya n dɛɛ ti.

eng: But Isaiah cries out for Israel, Though the number of Israels children will be like the sand of the sea, only a remaining part will be saved, .
local: Tɔ, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya abo awie fa kɔ Yisareli­fɔm jɔrɛ su, i ŋa, Haari ni bo Yisareli­fɔm sunnu beberebe ka ŋmiɛ ni, bu nu minɛ kaan sɔ nyá tii­de a.

eng: But if part of a batch of dough is offered to God as holy, the whole batch of dough is holy too. If a root is holy, the branches will be holy too.
local: Nzɛn be faari i alɛ­mbaa fɔfɔrɛ be fa yoori yoo­wa na i fa maari Nyɛmɛ, nyini la ka, i alɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wɔ lɛɛ. Ye nzɛn baka bunlui n ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ, nyini la ka, i asa­mbaa n kɛrɛ gusu ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Dont be defeated by evil, but defeat evil with good.
local: Má a ma satiɛ yoo­wa kunma u, ama, ma u kpa­yo n kunma satiɛ ni.

eng: Im convinced that nothing can separate us from Gods love in Christ Jesus our Lord: not death or life, not angels or rulers, not present things or future things, not powers.
local: M si tenle su ka, deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro kpaaki ya ni koro n bo Nyɛmɛ koro ya, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti ni, yiwee wara ŋgɔɔ o, mɛrɛkɛm wara Sitana yiko­fɔm o, deke n bo bu wo bɛrɛ o, wara deke n bo bu bá yo o, yiko bo i wo aŋgoro wara asiɛn bu o. Deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ni, bu be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro kpaaki koro n bo i wo ya ni Kristo afiɛn ni.

eng: because the Lord does what he says completely and quickly. .
local: Dama sa n bo Micɛra ni yó i durunya n nu ni, i yó i wie ndende.

eng: If some of the branches were broken off, and you were a wild olive branch, and you were grafted in among the other branches and shared the root that produces the rich oil of the olive tree.
local: Nahɔrɛ, Yisareli­fɔm ti ka baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n wɔ, ye ba kpiɛ i asa­mbaa ni nbem atu, na ambɛrɛ ti ka boro baka bo i suu­wa la má faŋga, ye ba kpiɛ am asa baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n dɛɛ asa­mbaa n su a, nyini ti, am ni baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n asa­mbaa n kɛrɛ saŋga nya ŋgɔɔ fite i bunlui n dɔ ɔ.

eng: As Isaiah prophesied, If the Lord of the heavenly forces had not left descendants for us, we would have been like Sodom, and we would have become like Gomorrah. .
local: I ti kabo Esaya du mɔ seeri n wɔ, i ŋa, Nzɛn má Micɛra Yiko­fɔ n ayaki má ya busu nu­fɔm nbem, má i yo ya kabo i yoori Sodom­fɔm ni Gomora­fɔm wɔ, miɛ tiɛm bo Nyɛmɛ saaki bu ni.

eng: then dont brag like youre better than the other branches. If you do brag, be careful: its not you that sustains the root, but its the root that sustains you.
local: Nzɛn i nya ti sɔ, má am fa fɔ ka am ti kpa tara baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n ni i tii, dama má asa­mbaa ni ma bunlui ni ŋgɔɔ a, ama, bunlui ni ma asa­mbaa ni ŋgɔɔ a.

eng: So what are we going to say? Gentiles who werent striving for righteousness achieved righteousness, the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Tɔ, ye ya se sɛ wɔ? Minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, akpini má atin kabo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ama, kisa, Nyɛmɛ ade bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, bu yarada n dɛɛ ti.

eng: You will say then, Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
local: Beni, a se ka, Ba kpiɛ baka n bo i su suu­wa n dɛɛ asa­mbaa n be, adena mini nya sasuu­biri a.

eng: Every person should place themselves under the authority of the government. There isnt any authority unless it comes from God, and the authorities that are there have been put in place by God.
local: I ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ de tere­tere­fɔm nɔaniɛ, bɛrɛ bo bu la yiko na bu niɛ am su ni, dama yiko be wo má bɛrɛ bo i fite má Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Bo bu la yiko kisa n kɛrɛ, Nyɛmɛ akpama bu a.

eng: But though Israel was striving for a Law of righteousness, they didnt arrive.
local: Ama, na Yisareli­fɔm bɛrɛ, akpini atin kabo bu su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, adena Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su. Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ba koro asu má i su nyunyumi, na bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: Fine. They were broken off because they werent faithful, but you stand only by your faithfulness. So dont think in a proud way; instead be afraid.
local: Tɔ, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, Yuda­fɔm ayo má yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ kpiɛɛri bu jiiri n a, ye u yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye a jina jinaa­biri n bo a jina n a. Nyini ti, má a yo kpirikoro, ama, si u ŋu sa.

eng: So anyone who opposes the authority is standing against what God has established. People who take this kind of stand will get punished.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri tere­tere­fɔm nɔaniɛ ni, i mi sukete deke bo Nyɛmɛ akpama i n wɔ lɛɛ. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori sɔ, i sukpini sui­cin ma i ŋu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Why? Its because they didnt go for it by faith but they went for it as if it could be reached by doing something. They have tripped over a stumbling block.
local: Nzu ma i yoori sɔ n a? Dama ba kpini má atin kabo bu yo yarada na Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, ama, bu kpiniiri atin ka bu sa­yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a. Na ba bo bu jaa yabuɛ n su na ba kpaasi.

eng: If God didnt spare the natural branches, he wont spare you either.
local: Dama nzɛn Nyɛmɛ ayaki má baka kpa n ni i tii dɛɛ asa­mbaa ni, beni, i bá yaki má wɔrɔ boro baka ni mmɔɔ dɛɛ asa­mbaa ni.

eng: The authorities dont frighten people who are doing the right thing. Rather, they frighten people who are doing wrong. Would you rather not be afraid of authority? Do whats right, and you will receive its approval.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sakpa soro má tere­tere­fɔm ni sɛrɛ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori satiɛ ni, yiri soro tere­tere­fɔm n a. Nyini ti, nzɛn a koro má ka a soro tere­tere­fɔm ni, yo sakpa, na bu kansi u.

eng: As it is written: Look! Im putting a stumbling block in Zion, which is a rock that offends people. And the one who has faith in him will not be put to shame. .
local: Kabo ba du mɔ akɛrɛ i ase ni, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Am ti fɛn, m sufa yabuɛ be se Siyon miɛ kpiri n nu lɛɛ, nyini yabuɛ ni, minɛm bó bu jaa su na bu kpáasi. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori i yarada, i mi dí má nyinsɛ.

eng: So look at Gods kindness and harshness. Its harshness toward those who fell, but its Gods kindness for you, provided you continue in his kindness; otherwise, you could be cut off too.
local: M ŋa a ŋu kabo Nyɛmɛ yo sakpa barasu ni, ni kabo i di minɛ jɔrɛ na i cin bu sui ni anyum­boro barasu ni. Minɛm bo bu yo má i yarada n ye i di bu jɔrɛ cin bu sui ni anyum­boro n a, ama, wɔrɔ yiri, i yo sakpa ma u wɔ, nyini ti, susu i sakpa n bo i yo ma u n su, má nyini, i kpíɛ wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ji wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: It is Gods servant given for your benefit. But if you do whats wrong, be afraid because it doesnt have weapons to enforce the law for nothing. It is Gods servant put in place to carry out his punishment on those who do what is wrong.
local: Nyɛmɛ ma tere­tere­fɔm wo bɛrɛ yo bu juma a, na bu buka u na a yo sakpa. Ama, nzɛn a yoori satiɛ, ma sɛrɛ ti u, dama Nyɛmɛ ama bu yiko ka bu yo bu juma ma yiri Nyɛmɛ a, na bu cin minɛm bo bu yo satiɛ n sui. Bu cin má minɛm sui ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: And even those who were cut off will be grafted back in if they dont continue to be unfaithful, because God is able to graft them in again.
local: Nzɛn Yuda­fɔm kaakiiri bu wo, na bu bá yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada, i koro fa bu sa bu woo­biri dawa n su, dama i la yiko bo i koro yo sɔ.

eng: If you were naturally part of a wild olive tree and you were cut off from it, and then, contrary to nature, you were grafted into the cultivated olive tree, wont these natural branches stand an even better chance of being grafted back onto their own olive tree?.
local: Ti fɛn. Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii ti ka boro baka asa­mbaa n bo ba kpiɛ i afa asa baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n su wɔ. Nyini ti, a si ka Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i ti má kekereke na i káaki ba fa baka n bo i su suu­wa kpa n asa­mbaam n ba sa bu woo­biri dawa n su bekun.

eng: That is why it is necessary to place yourself under the governments authority, not only to avoid Gods punishment but also for the sake of your conscience.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, i ti ye ni ka am de tere­tere­fɔm n nɔaniɛ, má ka am soro ka bu cin am sui n ŋgumi dɛɛ ti a, ama, am ahore su, ni am sunsunni kere ka am yo sɔ n dɛɛ ti a.

eng: I dont want you to be unaware of this secret, brothers and sisters. That way you wont think too highly of yourselves. A part of Israel has become resistant until the full number of the Gentiles comes in.
local: M ninbaam, asiri nu jɔrɛ be yaa, ye m koro ka am si i a, adena má am yo am ŋu kpiri ka am la si. Yiri la ka, kabo Yisareli­fɔm nbem ayo bu ahore­mbaa kekereke ni, bu bíta i sɔ haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm kɛrɛ bá ba Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: In this way, all Israel will be saved, as it is written: The deliverer will come from Zion. He will remove ungodly behavior from Jacob. .
local: Tɔ, i yó sɔ ɔ, na Yisareli­fɔm kɛrɛ bá nya tii­de, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Tii­de­fɔ ni fíte Siyon miɛ kpiri n nu ba, na i bá yi deke n kɛrɛ bo i kɔ má atin su fa tu, na i yó Jakɔbu busu nu­fɔm casi.

eng: You should also pay taxes for the same reason, because the authorities are Gods assistants, concerned with this very thing.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, i kaaki ti ye ni ka am tɔ am lampom, dama gominam n yo bu juma ni anyum­boro wɔ, Nyɛmɛ akere ka bu yo sɔ a.

eng: So pay everyone what you owe them. Pay the taxes you owe, pay the duties you are charged, give respect to those you should respect, and honor those you should honor.
local: Nyini ti, deke bo i ti ye ni ka am yo ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ni, am yo ma bu. Am tɔ am lampom kaari, wara nzɛn ba kpiɛ am kaari be, am tɔ i, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo i ti ye ni ka am ma i jirima, am ma i.

eng: This is my covenant with them, when I take away their sins .
local: Kabo m fá sɛsɛ­su di i di ni bɛrɛ lɛɛ, saŋga bo m ba yiiri satiɛ n bo i wo bu ŋu ni.

eng: According to the gospel, they are enemies for your sake, but according to Gods choice, they are loved for the sake of their ancestors.
local: Kabo Yuda­fɔm n akete labari kpa n ni, nyini ma ba kaaki Nyɛmɛ kpɔ­fɔm kisa n a, ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm n dɛɛ ti. Ama, fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ fereeri bu nam na i yiiri bu ni, bu ti i minɛ koroo­wam wɔ.

eng: Dont be in debt to anyone, except for the obligation to love each other. Whoever loves another person has fulfilled the Law.
local: Má am ma be deke be kaari su am su. Deke bo wɔ yo daka ka am bita am beŋgu kaari la ka, am sukoro am beŋgu. Dama sɔnɔ bo i koro i beŋgu, i mi su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ su wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: The commandments, Dont commit adultery, dont murder, dont steal, dont desire what others have, and any other commandments, are all summed up in one word: You must love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Dama am si Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam n kɛrɛ, ka, Má a saaki ajaa ciire, má a kun sɔnɔ, má a wɔ, ni bo i ŋa, Má a ma u konviki wura be dɛɛ neŋge nu, haari ni bo ba ka n kɛrɛ jina nyini nɔaniɛ kun ni su a, ka, Koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni.

eng: Gods gifts and calling cant be taken back.
local: Dama nzɛn Nyɛmɛ faari i aceeri fa ceeri minɛ nbem, wara nzɛn wɔ fere sɔnɔ, i kaaki má i sunsunni.

eng: Once you were disobedient to God, but now you have mercy because they were disobedient.
local: Ambɛrɛ, am yo keteeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n wɔ, ama, kisa, wɔ si am aŋunfɔ, bɛrɛ bo ba kete i nɔaniɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Love doesnt do anything wrong to a neighbor; therefore, love is what fulfills the Law.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i beŋgu yo má i mi satiɛ. Nyini kere ka nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ jina koro su wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: As you do all this, you know what time it is. The hour has already come for you to wake up from your sleep. Now our salvation is nearer than when we first had faith.
local: I ti ye ni ka am yo sɔ, dama am si kabo ahin saŋga n ti ni. Saŋga n aju, i ti ye ni ka am teŋge, má am daafi. Dama saŋga n bo ya nyá tii­de n adodo, i waari má ka saŋga bo ya du mɔ yoori yarada ni.

eng: In the same way, they have also been disobedient because of the mercy that you received, so now they can receive mercy too.
local: Tɔ, kisa, i ti sɔ ɔ, bɛrɛ akete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, dama Nyɛmɛ asi am aŋunfɔ, adena i awieeri su, na i bá si bɛrɛ gusu aŋunfɔ.

eng: The night is almost over, and the day is near. So lets get rid of the actions that belong to the darkness and put on the weapons of light.
local: Wɔ ka kaan na ya fíte kɔŋguɛ nu, ale sucin lɛɛ. Nyini ti, am ma ya yaki awosin nu juma kɛrɛ, na ya yo wein sa­yoo­wa, kabo luɛ­kun­fɔm sana bu biɛsɔya neŋgem na bu kɔ kun luɛ ni.

eng: God has locked up all people in disobedience, in order to have mercy on all of them.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ abo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kun ka bu kɛrɛ kete i wɔ, adena i awieeri su na i bá si bu kɛrɛ aŋunfɔ.

eng: Lets behave appropriately as people who live in the day, not in partying and getting drunk, not in sleeping around and obscene behavior, not in fighting and obsession.
local: Ya wo wiɛsu wɔ lɛɛ, nyini ti, am ma ya woo­wa kɔ atin su. Má ya sudi ya tii, sunu nzan subo, suyo ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, má ya suyo sakaraya, suyo konvikiya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, má ya sukpini jɔrɛ, na má ya susi kɔrɛ.

eng: Gods riches, wisdom, and knowledge are so deep! They are as mysterious as his judgments, and they are as hard to track as his paths!.
local: Nahɔrɛ, Nyɛmɛ alecira ni i si bo i la i n wo ŋgo. Kabo i di i jɔrɛ ni, be koro fiiti má nu na i ti i bu, i atin n gusu kɛrɛ, sɔnɔ koro si má i bu.

eng: Instead, dress yourself with the Lord Jesus Christ, and dont plan to indulge your selfish desires.
local: Ama, am ma ya fa ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo n yo deke bo ya fa wura, kabo sɔnɔ fa sutura wura barasu ni, na má ya su ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa n su.

eng: Who has known the Lords mind? Or who has been his mentor? .
local: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Ŋma nya si deke bo i wo Micɛra n sunsunni nu a? Wara ŋma nya koro tu i fɔ a.

eng: Or who has given him a gift and has been paid back by him? .
local: Ŋma nya ce Nyɛmɛ deke be a, adena Nyɛmɛ kaaki tɔ i mi kaari a.

eng: All things are from him and through him and for him. May the glory be to him forever. Amen.
local: Dama deke kɛrɛ fite yiri dɔ a, deke kɛrɛ ti i dɛɛ wɔ, na deke kɛrɛ bá yo i dɛɛ wɔ. Yiri ti ye ni ka ya kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.

eng: Welcome the person who is weak in faith—but not in order to argue about differences of opinion.
local: Am de minɛm bo bu yarada ti má bii­wa n ni am saa nnyɔ, má am se bu akuruwa fa kɔ deke n bo bu ci bu n su na am tanda bu ahore.

eng: One person believes in eating everything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
local: Ma ya fa i ka, nbem la yarada bo bu koro di deke kɛrɛ bo bu koro, ama, nbem gusu wo bɛrɛ, bu yarada ti má bii­wa, bu ciire la niɛ, bu di toro nyawa n ŋgumi wɔ.

eng: Those who eat must not look down on the ones who dont, and the ones who dont eat must not judge the ones who do, because God has accepted them.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i di niɛ kɛrɛ niɛ ni, má i mi bu bo i di má niɛ ni ŋgbɛɛn. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo i di nyawa n ŋgumi ni, má i mi jaraki bo i ci má pui ni, dama Nyɛmɛ ade i a.

eng: Who are you to judge someone elses servants? They stand or fall before their own Lord .
local: Wɔrɔ la ŋma na a jaraki be dɛɛ gbɛfɛnɛ? I dɛɛ tiɛŋgu mibiɛ ni sé ka i jína wara i kpáasi a. I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka i jína a, dama i mibiɛ ni koro ma i jina nyunyumi a.

eng: One person considers some days to be more sacred than others, while another person considers all days to be the same. Each person must have their own convictions.
local: Bekun, minɛ nbem dɔ, bu sunsun ka cɛɛn nbem ti kpa tara nbem, ama, nbem gusu sunsun ka cɛɛn n kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ, be tara má i beŋgu. I ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ su kabo i sunsunni nu kere ka i ti kpa n su.

eng: Someone who thinks that a day is sacred, thinks that way for the Lord. Those who eat, eat for the Lord, because they thank God. And those who dont eat, dont eat for the Lord, and they thank the Lord too.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sunsun ka cɛɛn be ti kpa tara cɛɛn nbem ni, i mi koro ka i bu Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ti, ye i yo sɔ a, ye sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ci má pui n gusu, i mi koro ka i bu Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ti, ye i yo sɔ a, dama i da Nyɛmɛ asi fa kɔ diire n su, ye i nya di a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo i di má deke nbem ni, i mi gusu bu Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ti, ye i yo sɔ a, dama i mi gusu da Nyɛmɛ asi fa kɔ diire n su, ye i nya di a.

eng: We dont live for ourselves and we dont die for ourselves.
local: Sɔnɔ be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro se ka i wo i tii su wo wɔ, be gusu wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro se ka i wu i tii su yiwee.

eng: If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or die, we belong to God.
local: Ai, nzɛn ya la ŋgɔɔ a, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti ye ya la ŋgɔɔ a. Nzɛn ya wu gusu a, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti ye ya wu a. Nyini ti, nzɛn ya la ŋgɔɔ wara ya wu wɔ, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ la ya a.

eng: This is why Christ died and lived: so that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
local: Dama nyini dɛɛ ti ma Kristo wuuri, ye i teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu n a, adena i yó sɔnɔ kɛrɛ Mibiɛ, bo bu la ŋgɔɔ n ni bo ba du mɔ awu n kɛrɛ.

eng: But why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? We all will stand in front of the judgment seat of God.
local: Wɔrɔ la ŋma, wɔrɔ bo a jaraki u beŋgu na a bu i ŋgbɛɛn ni? A si má ka ya kɛrɛ bá jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu na i dí ya jɔrɛ?.

eng: Because it is written, As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to me, and every tongue will give praise to God. .
local: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Micɛra n ŋa, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bá kutu m nyunu, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bá teke i nɔɔ se ka mini ti Nyɛmɛ a. Kabo ma se ni, nyini wo bɛrɛ a.

eng: So then, each of us will give an account of ourselves to God.
local: Nyini ti, ya kɛrɛ bá kan ya sa bo ya yo i n kɛrɛ kere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: So stop judging each other. Instead, this is what you should decide: never put a stumbling block or obstacle in the way of your brother or sister.
local: Nyini ti, am ma ya yaki ya beŋgum jɔrɛ kan, na má ya jaraki bu kun, ama, am ma ya sunsun kabo ya yo na má ya ma ya beŋgu Kristo­fɔm bo bu jaa, wara na bu kpaasi.

eng: I know and Im convinced in the Lord Jesus that nothing is wrong to eat in itself. But if someone thinks something is wrong to eat, it becomes wrong for that person.
local: Kabo m ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu ayo kun ni, nyini ma m si ka deke be mmɔɔ ni i tii ti má ciire n a. Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ sunsun ka deke be ti ciire i dɔ, má i mi di i.

eng: If your brother or sister is upset by your food, you are no longer walking in love. Dont let your food destroy someone for whom Christ died.
local: Nzɛn diire n bo a di i n dɛɛ ti, na a saaki u beŋgu Kristo­fɔ n ahore, nyini yiri, a suyo má koro sa lɛɛ. Kristo yo wuuri i dɛɛ ti wɔ, má a ma i mi ka ŋgbɛɛn, u diire n bo a di i n dɛɛ ti.

eng: And dont let something you consider to be good be criticized as wrong.
local: Má am ma be jɔjɔ jɔrɛ tiɛ fa kɔ deke kpa n bo am si i n su.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about eating food and drinking but about righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
local: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ niɛ i minɛm su ni, i ti má di ni nu jɔrɛ wɔ, ama, i ti aceeri bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n afa ama minɛm wɔ, yiri la ka, ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ni tana­laifiɛ, ni ahorejɔ.

eng: Whoever serves Christ this way pleases God and gets human approval.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i suuri nyini atin n su, na i su Kristo sɔ, i mi yo sa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu a, na adamande kansi i ka i ti sɔnɔ kpa.

eng: So lets strive for the things that bring peace and the things that build each other up.
local: Nyini ti, am ma ya boro ya nyi na ya su deke n bo i ma ya tana­laifiɛ n su, na ya buka ya beŋgum, na ya kɔ mɔ ni ya nyɛmɛsu ni.

eng: Dont destroy what God has done because of food. All food is acceptable, but its a bad thing if it trips someone else.
local: Má am saaki Nyɛmɛ juma ni, diire dɛɛ ti. Diire kɛrɛ ti kpa a. Ama, nzɛn be di diire be na i ma i beŋgu bo i jaa na i kpaasi, nyini yiri, i ti má kpa.

eng: Its a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything that trips your brother or sister.
local: Nzɛn a di niɛ, wara a nu nzan, wara a yo nyini n be sɔ, na i ma u beŋgu Kristo­fɔ bo i jaa na i kpaasi, i ti má ye ni ka a yo sɔ.

eng: Keep the belief that you have to yourself—its between you and God. People are blessed who dont convict themselves by the things they approve.
local: Yarada n bo a yo i u ahore su fa kɔ nyini jɔrɛm su, ma i wo u ni u Nyɛmɛ afiɛn. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, na i la má sika i ahore su ka wɔ yo satiɛ, i mi la tere­fiɛ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: But those who have doubts are convicted if they go ahead and eat, because they arent acting on the basis of faith. Everything that isnt based on faith is sin.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ba sudi diire barasu nbem na i yo sika, Nyɛmɛ adi i mi jɔrɛ ajaraki i ka wɔ yo satiɛ, dama diire n bo i sudi i ni, wɔ yo má yarada. Deke kɛrɛ gusu bo sɔnɔ yo i, na i la má yarada, i ti satiɛ wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: We who are powerful need to be patient with the weakness of those who dont have power, and not please ourselves.
local: Yɛrɛ bo ya la faŋga fa kɔ yarada su ni, wɔ yo daka ka ya yo sawɔrɛ ni bɛrɛ n bo bu yarada la má faŋga ni, na ya buka bu, na ya dada bu ahore, má ya yo sa bo i sun yɛrɛ ni ya tii nu.

eng: Each of us should please our neighbors for their good in order to build them up.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ yo deke bo i sun i beŋgu nu, na i yo sakpa, na i kɔ mɔ ni i nyɛmɛsu ni.

eng: Christ didnt please himself, but, as it is written, The insults of those who insulted you fell on me. .
local: Dama haari Yesu Kristo ni i tii ayo má sa bo i sun yiri ni i tii nu, ama, i ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n wɔ, ka, Nzukɔlɛɛ barasu n kɛrɛ bo minɛm fa kpiɛɛri wɔrɔ Nyɛmɛ ni, bu fa kpiɛɛri mini wɔ.

eng: Whatever was written in the past was written for our instruction so that we could have hope through endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures.
local: Deke n kɛrɛ bo bu du mɔ kɛrɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, bu kɛrɛɛri i ka i kere ya a, adena ya la tama, dama deke bo ba kɛrɛ i ni, nyini ma ya ahoresi a, na ya koro mindi a.

eng: May the God of endurance and encouragement give you the same attitude toward each other, similar to Christ Jesus attitude.
local: Nyɛmɛ ma ya mindi ni ahoresi a, yiri ma am la anɔɔsɛ ni am beŋgu a, kabo Yesu Kristo kereeri am ni.

eng: That way you can glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ together with one voice.
local: adena am kɛrɛ tiɛn am nɔɔ fa anɔɔsɛ, na am kansi Nyɛmɛ, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo Si.

eng: So welcome each other, in the same way that Christ also welcomed you, for Gods glory.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am de am beŋgum, ni am saa nnyɔ kabo Kristo ade am ni, nyini ma minɛ kansi Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: Im saying that Christ became a servant of those who are circumcised for the sake of Gods truth, in order to confirm the promises given to the ancestors.
local: Dama m suse am ka Kristo baari ka i ba buka Yuda­fɔm n wɔ, i yoori sɔ n dɛɛ ti, nyini ma deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yo ma Yuda­fɔm nam n ayo bii­wa nahɔrɛ su n a.

eng: and so that the Gentiles could glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, Because of this I will confess you among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to your name .
local: Bekun i baari ka i ba buka minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm wɔ, na bu gusu kansi Nyɛmɛ fa kɔ kabo wɔ si bu aŋunfɔ ni. Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Nyini dɛɛ ti, m kánsi wɔrɔ Nyɛmɛ, m kánsi u duma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm nu, na m dí jue ma u.

eng: And again, it says, Rejoice, Gentiles, with his people .
local: Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i bekun ka, Ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, am ma am ahore jɔ ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo wɔ yi bu ni.

eng: And again, Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and all the people should sing his praises .
local: Bekun, i ŋa, Ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, am kansi Micɛra ni, na am ma daanfiɛm kɛrɛ kansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: And again, Isaiah says, There will be a root of Jesse, who will also rise to rule the Gentiles. The Gentiles will place their hope in him .
local: Na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ Esaya gusu ase ka, Sɔnɔ be bá fite Jese dɛɛ lɛɛ anɔ n nu, i ti kabo baka tiɛ i bunlui su n wɔ, na i bá yo daanfiɛm kɛrɛ fɛmɛ, yiri má bu nya ahoresi a.

eng: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in faith so that you overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
local: Nyɛmɛ ma ya tama, m sere i ka i ma am ahorejɔ ni tana­laifiɛ kɔ sin su, kabo am ayo i yarada n dɛɛ ti, adena Awiɛwiɛ Casi n yiko ma am tama bo i la má aŋusu.

eng: My brothers and sisters, I myself am convinced that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and are able to teach each other.
local: Tɔ, m ninbaam, m si tenle su ka am la wo kpa, na am la si dɔŋgu, na am kaaki koro fa tu am beŋgu fɔ.

eng: But Ive written to you in a sort of daring way, partly to remind you of what you already know. Im writing to you in this way because of the grace that was given to me by God.
local: Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ma fa ahoresi, ye m sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n fa ma am a, adena m teŋge am ahin sam n su, dama Nyɛmɛ ayo sakpa ama m.

eng: It helps me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. Im working as a priest of Gods gospel so that the offering of the Gentiles can be acceptable and made holy by the Holy Spirit.
local: ka m yo Yesu Kristo amusui­su­fɔ ma minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, adena m kan Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n kere bɛrɛ bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ma Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu ti i dɛɛ minɛ casim. M yoori sɔ ni, i ti ka Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ n bo i yo yoo­wa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu ni.

eng: So in Christ Jesus I brag about things that have to do with God.
local: Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, m la atin bo m koro fɔ fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ juma n bo wɔ fa ama m su.

eng: I dont dare speak about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring about the obedience of the Gentiles. He did it by what Ive said and what Ive done.
local: Nahɔrɛ, i la m sɛrɛ na m kan sam bo nyiŋgam ayo bu ni, má ka sam n bo Kristo ma ma yo bu ni ŋgumi, yiri la bo wɔ ma ma kan i nɔaniɛ n akere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, na ma yo alekutura sam, na ba de i nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of Gods Spirit. So Ive completed the circuit of preaching Christs gospel from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum.
local: I ma m faari alekutura sam, ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu fa yoori nyini juma n a. M boori i bu Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na ma bara ayɛ akɔ aju haari Ilirikum awɔrɛ n nu, na ma kan Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ akere bu.

eng: In this way, I have a goal to preach the gospel where they havent heard of Christ yet, so that I wont be building on someone elses foundation.
local: Deke bo m nyunu boro su agaya n la ka, m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm awɔrɛm n kɛrɛ bo bu nya ti má Kristo duma ni, dama m koro má ka m ta be dɛɛ ataari su.

eng: Instead, as its written, Those who hadnt been told about him will see, and those who hadnt heard will understand .
local: kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Haari ni bo minɛm nya kan má labari kpa n kere má bu ni, bu ŋú i, haari ni bo bu nya ti má labari kpa ni, bu tí i, na bu sí i bu.

eng: Thats why Ive been stopped so many times from coming to see you.
local: Tɔ, nyini n kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, saŋga kɛrɛ, má m koro ka m ba niɛ am busu wɔ, ama, m nya má atin.

eng: But now, since I dont have any place to work in these regions anymore, and since Ive wanted to come to see you for many years.
local: Ama, kisa, miɛ fɔfɔrɛ be wo má bɛrɛ bo m kɔ dɔ na m kɔ yo juma ahin awɔrɛm n nu, nyini ti, kabo má saŋga kɛrɛ na m koro ka m ba niɛ am busu ni.

eng: Ill visit you when I go to Spain. I hope to see you while Im passing through. And I hope you will send me on my way there, after I have first been reenergized by some time in your company.
local: m la tama ka m ba susin kɔ Esipani dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, m jína am dɔ na m niɛ am busu, na m ni ambɛrɛ nya tana, na am buka m na m koro kɔ ju deke bo m sukɔ ni.

eng: But now Im going to Jerusalem, to serve Gods people.
local: Ama, kisa yiri, m sukɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, m la buka be bo m kɔ fa ma Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu wo dɔ ni.

eng: Macedonia and Achaia have been happy to make a contribution for the poor among Gods people in Jerusalem.
local: Dama Kristo­fɔm bo bu wo Makɛdoniya ni, ni bo bu wo Akaya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu ni, wɔ yo bu fiɛ ka bu yiyi ŋwaa, na m kɔ fa ma Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu wo Yɛrusalem na bu ti yaari­fɔm ni.

eng: They were happy to do this, and they are actually in debt to Gods people in Jerusalem. If the Gentiles got a share of the Jewish peoples spiritual resources, they ought to minister to them with material resources.
local: Bɛrɛ ni bu tii afa sunsunni kpa aŋu ka Yɛrusalem­fɔm kaari su bu su, dama Yɛrusalem­fɔm du mɔ faari Nyɛmɛ deke kpa maari bɛrɛ n bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo daka ka bɛrɛ gusu nya durunya n nu neŋge ma Yɛrusalem­fɔm ni.

eng: So then after I have finished this job and have safely delivered the final amount of the Gentiles offering to them, I will leave for Spain, visiting you on the way.
local: Tɔ, nyini ti, nzɛn m koro yoori nyini juma n kɛrɛ wieeri, na bu nya nyini buka n kɛrɛ, m bá niɛ am busu, na m nyá sin kɔ Esipani.

eng: And I know that when I come to you I will come with the fullest blessing of Christ.
local: M si tenle su ka m baari, m fá deke kpa fite Kristo dɔ ba ma am.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggles in your prayers to God for me.
local: M ninbaam, kabo Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo wo ya dɔ, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n koro ya agaya n dɛɛ ti, m sere am wɔ, ka am buka m na ya sere Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro.

eng: Pray that I will be rescued from the people in Judea who dont believe. Also, pray that my service for Jerusalem will be acceptable to Gods people there.
local: ka i dé m fite Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu­fɔ nbem bo bu yo má yarada n saa nu, na Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu wo Yɛrusalem n dé buka n bo m sufa kɔ ma bu ni.

eng: so that I can come to you with joy by Gods will and be reenergized with your company.
local: Na nzɛn i ti Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa, na m bá am dɔ ni ahorejɔ, na m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ koro dada ya beŋgu ahore.

eng: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
local: Nyɛmɛ bo i ma tana­laifiɛ n buka ya kɛrɛ. Ami.

eng: Im introducing our sister Phoebe to you, who is a servant of the church in Cenchreae.
local: Tɔ, m koro ka am si ya niwa baa­bara n bo bu fere i ka Fibi ni, i yo Nyɛmɛ juma buka Kristo jama n bo i wo Kenciriya miɛ kpiri n nu n wɔ.

eng: Welcome her in the Lord in a way that is worthy of Gods people, and give her whatever she needs from you, because she herself has been a sponsor of many people, myself included.
local: Am de i kabo má am de Nyɛmɛ minɛm barasu ni, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, na am buka i ni deke kɛrɛ bo i koro, dama yiri ni i tii abuka minɛ dɔŋgu, haari ni mini mmɔɔ.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus.
local: Am bisa Pirisila ni Akuyila dɔŋgu ma m. M ni bɛrɛ saŋga yo juma ma Yesu Kristo wɔ.

eng: who risked their own necks for my life. Im not the only one who thanks God for them, but all the churches of the Gentiles do the same.
local: Bu yo tɔɔri bu ŋgɔɔ maari m wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m da bu asi n a, má mini ŋgumi yo bu ansɛ a, ama, Kristo jamam kɛrɛ bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm gusu yo bu ansɛ.

eng: Also say hello to the church that meets in their house. Say hello to Epaenetus, my dear friend, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
local: Am bisa Kristo jama n kɛrɛ dɔŋgu, bo bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ bu dika ni. Am bisa m danfo kpa Ɛpanɛtosi n dɔŋgu ma m, yiri du mɔ yoori Kristo yarada Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu a.

eng: Say hello to Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
local: Am bisa Mariya dɔŋgu, bo wɔ yo juma agaya abuka am ni.

eng: Say hello to Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners. They are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
local: Am bisa Andɔrɔnikusi ni Juniya dɔŋgu, m lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔm bo m ni bɛrɛ yo woori dan­saraka nu ni, bu la dumaye Kristo jaramasam kɛrɛ nu, bɛrɛ du mɔ yoori Kristo susu­fɔm ka, ye m nya ba yoori a.

eng: Say hello to Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
local: Am bisa m danfo Ampiliyatusi dɔŋgu, m Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, m koro i agaya.

eng: Say hello to Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
local: Am bisa Wuribanisi dɔŋgu ma m, bo yiri ni yɛrɛ saŋga yo juma ma Kristo ni, na am kaaki bisa m danfo Sitakisi dɔŋgu.

eng: Say hello to Apelles, who is tried and true in Christ. Say hello to the members of the household of Aristobulus.
local: Am bisa Apɛlɛsi dɔŋgu, bo wɔ yi i ŋu ka i ti Kristo susu­fɔ nahɔrɛ su ni, na am bisa Arisitobulosi dika­fɔm kɛrɛ dɔŋgu.

eng: Say hello to my relative Herodion. Say hello to the members of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
local: Am bisa m lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔ n bo bu fere i ka Herodiyon n dɔŋgu, na am bisa Naracisi dika­fɔm bo bu ti Mibiɛ n dɛɛ minɛm n buka su.

eng: Say hello to Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who are workers for the Lord. Say hello to my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
local: Am bisa Tirifena ni Tirifosa dɔŋgu, nbara nnyɔm bo bu yo juma ma Mibiɛ ni, ni m danfo Pɛrisisi, yiri mmɔɔ ayo juma agaya ama Mibiɛ n ni.

eng: Say hello to Rufus, who is an outstanding believer, along with his mother and mine.
local: Am bisa Rufusu dɔŋgu, yiri bo Nyɛmɛ ayi i ka i yo i juma n ni, ni i ni bo i ti mini mmɔɔ ni ni.

eng: Say hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
local: Am bisa ahin minɛm kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma m, bɛrɛ la Asinkiritusi, ni Felegon, ni Hɛrɛmesi, ni Patorobasi, ni Hɛrɛmasi, ni ya ninbaam kɛrɛ bo bu wo bu dɔ ni.

eng: Say hello to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
local: Am bisa Filolokusi ni Juliya dɔŋgu, ni Nɛrɛyusi, ni i niɛma­bara ni, ni Olimpasi, ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo bu dɔ ni.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ say hello to you.
local: Am bisa am beŋgu dɔŋgu ni koro. Kristo jamam kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn n bisa am dɔŋgu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I urge you to watch out for people who create divisions and problems against the teaching that you learned. Keep away from them.
local: M ninbaam, m sere am wɔ, am si am ŋu sa ni minɛm bo bu saaki keree­wam bo am adu mɔ ade i ni, na bu fa kpakpaaki­nu ba ni, na bu ma minɛm kpaasi fa kɔ bu yarada n su ni. Má am dodo bu.

eng: People like that arent serving the Lord. They are serving their own feelings. They deceive the hearts of innocent people with smooth talk and flattery.
local: Dama nyini minɛm sɔ, bu su má ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo su, ama, bu su bu dɛɛ kunnu koroo­wa su wɔ, na bu bo minɛm bo bu si má pui n anɔɔfiɛ, na bu dadaka bu.

eng: The news of your obedience has reached everybody, so Im happy for you. But I want you to be wise about whats good, and innocent about whats evil.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ati kabo am ade Kristo nɔaniɛ nahɔrɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti, m ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su. M koro ka am la si fa kɔ deke n bo i ti kpa n su, ama, m koro má ka am la si haari kaan sɔ mmɔɔ fa kɔ deke n bo i ti má kpa n su.

eng: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Nyɛmɛ bo i ma tana­laifiɛ ni, i cɛ́ má, i má am yiko na am fún tiɛntiɛn Sitana ŋu mɛrɛki i. Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am.

eng: Timothy my coworker says hello to you, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
local: Timoti bo m ni yiri saŋga yo juma n bisa am dɔŋgu, na Lukiyosi, ni Jason, ni Sosipateri, m lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔm n gusu bisa am dɔŋgu.

eng: Im Tertius, and Im writing this letter to you in the Lord—hello!.
local: Mini Tɛrɛtiyusi gusu bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ duma n nu, mini ye Pɔɔlu ma m kɛrɛɛri ahin kadasi n sufa ma am n a.

eng: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, says hello to you. Erastus the city treasurer says hello to you, along with our brother Quartus.
local: M awuru­fɔ­biɛ Gayosi bisa am dɔŋgu. Kristo jama n kɛrɛ tiɛn bu nɔɔ i dika a. Irasitusi bo i ti miɛ n nu ŋwaa bita­fɔ ni, ni ya niwa Kuwatosi kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu. .

eng: May the glory be to God who can strengthen you with my good news and the message that I preach about Jesus Christ. He can strengthen you with the announcement of the secret that was kept quiet for a long time.
