local: Am ma ya kansi Nyɛmɛ. Yiri koro ma am jina faŋga kenken fa kɔ Yesu Kristo labari kpa n bo m kaanri i jɔrɛ na am ati i n su a. I ti jɔrɛ bo bu yo fa fiɛɛri dada kɛrɛ wɔ, ama, kisa wɔ fite fofoe nu.

eng: Now that secret is revealed through what the prophets wrote. It is made known to the Gentiles in order to lead to their faithful obedience based on the command of the eternal God.
local: Yiri ye Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛɛri i n a, na Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n ama daanfiɛ kɛrɛ ati i, na ba de i, na ba yo i yarada.

eng: May the glory be to God, who alone is wise! May the glory be to him through Jesus Christ forever! Amen.
local: Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi la alecira a, yiri ti ye ni ka ya kansi i a, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.

eng: When I came to you, brothers and sisters, I didnt come preaching Gods secrets to you like I was an expert in speech or wisdom.
local: M ninbaam, saŋga bo m baari am dɔ na m ba kaanri labari kpa n kereeri am, na am si Nyɛmɛ asiri nu n jɔrɛ ni, ma fa má adamande alecira ni si jɔrɛ ajɔjɔ akere má am.

eng: I had made up my mind not to think about anything while I was with you except Jesus Christ, and to preach him as crucified.
local: Dama m yo baari ni, ma koro má ka m kan jɔrɛ be am dɔ, má ka Yesu Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n dɛɛ jɔrɛ, ni kabo i wuuri baka jaanu n su ni.

eng: I stood in front of you with weakness, fear, and a lot of shaking.
local: Saŋga bo m yo woori am dɔ ni, m ŋuniɛ ti m yɔrɔkii­wa a, na sɛrɛ ati m agaya, na m ŋuniɛ subobo m.

eng: My message and my preaching werent presented with convincing wise words but with a demonstration of the Spirit and of power.
local: M nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa ni labari kpa n bo m kaanri i ni, i ti má adamande alecira ni si jɔrɛ, ama, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni yoori alekutura sam kereeri ka m nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: I did this so that your faith might not depend on the wisdom of people but on the power of God.
local: Adena má am yo yarada adamande alecira n dɛɛ ti, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n dɛɛ ti.

eng: What we say is wisdom to people who are mature. It isnt a wisdom that comes from the present day or from todays leaders who are being reduced to nothing.
local: Tɔ, ama, ya jɔjɔ alecira jɔrɛ fa kere minɛm bo ba bi fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin n su n a. I ti má durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ alecira, wara i ti má alecira bo durunya n nu nfaŋga­fɔm la i ni, bɛrɛ kɛrɛ bá ka ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: We talk about Gods wisdom, which has been hidden as a secret. God determined this wisdom in advance, before time began, for our glory.
local: Ama, yɛrɛ kan Nyɛmɛ alecira asiri nu jɔrɛ n bo i yo fa fiɛɛri adamande ŋu n wɔ, nyini alecira ni, Nyɛmɛ du mɔ ciciiri i wɔ ka, ye i nya ba yiiri durunya n a. I yoori sɔ ɔ, adena ya nya i jirima woo­wa ni.

eng: It is a wisdom that none of the present-day rulers have understood, because if they did understand it, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory!.
local: Durunya n nu nfaŋga­fɔm be mmɔɔ asi má nyini alecira ni, nzɛn má bu siiri i, má bu kpama má ya Mibiɛ Jirima­fɔ n baka jaanu n su.

eng: But this is precisely what is written: God has prepared things for those who love him that no eye has seen, or ear has heard, or that havent crossed the mind of any human being .
local: Ama, i ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n wɔ, ka, Deke bo be nyu­mbaa nya ŋu má i ni, wara deke bo be sui nya ti má i ni, wara deke bo be nya sunsun má i, i ahore­mbaa su ni, nyini ye Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ i su, na i fá ma bo bu koro i n a.

eng: God has revealed these things to us through the Spirit. The Spirit searches everything, including the depths of God.
local: Ama, yɛrɛ yiri, Nyɛmɛ ama i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ayi nyini n sɔ akere ya, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni fiiti si deke n kɛrɛ a, haari ni Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ bo i wo ŋgo ni.

eng: Who knows a persons depths except their own spirit that lives in them? In the same way, no one has known the depths of God except Gods Spirit.
local: Adamande be koro si má sa bo i wo i beŋgu adamande ahore su, má ka i mi ni i tii awiɛwiɛ ni si deke bo i wo i ahore su a. Sɔ ye be koro si má sa bo i wo Nyɛmɛ ahore su a, má ka Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni ŋgumi si a.

eng: We havent received the worlds spirit but Gods Spirit so that we can know the things given to us by God.
local: Awiɛwiɛ n bo yɛrɛ anya i ni, i ti má durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ, ama, i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, adena ya si aceerim kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya ni.

eng: These are the things we are talking about—not with words taught by human wisdom but with words taught by the Spirit—we are interpreting spiritual things to spiritual people.
local: Nyini ye ya fa kere minɛm a. I fite má adamande alecira keree­wa n nu, ama, i ti deke bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni kereeri ya a, ye ya fa kere minɛm bo bu la Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a.

eng: But people who are unspiritual dont accept the things from Gods Spirit. They are foolishness to them and cant be understood, because they can only be comprehended in a spiritual way.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i la má Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni, koro de má deke n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n dɔ, dama yiri dɔ, nyini jɔrɛm ti ka sinnzinya jɔrɛ wɔ, na i koro ti má i bu, dama be koro si má i bu, má ka Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni buka i na i koro fiiti si i bu.

eng: Spiritual people comprehend everything, but they themselves arent understood by anyone.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i la Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni, i mi koro fiiti deke n kɛrɛ a, ama, be koro fiiti má i mi yiri.

eng: Who has known the mind of the Lord, who will advise him? But we have the mind of Christ.
local: I ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu barasu n wɔ ka, Ŋma asi sa bo i wo Micɛra n sunsunni nu, na i kére i kabo i yo a? Ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya si deke bo i wo Kristo sunsunni nu.

eng: From Paul, called by Gods will to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Sosthenes our brother.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu bo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu na wɔ fere m ka m yo Yesu Kristo jaramasa ni, m ni ya niwa Sositeni ni sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi ma am a.

eng: To Gods church that is in Corinth: To those who have been made holy to God in Christ Jesus, who are called to be Gods people. Together with all those who call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place—hes their Lord and ours!.
local: Ya sukɛrɛ i na ya fa ma ambɛrɛ Kristo jama n bo am wo Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu, na Nyɛmɛ afere am afa am ayo i dɛɛ minɛ casim ni, na wɔ ma am ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun ni, fa saŋga ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu bo fere ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo duma n deke kɛrɛ ni, yiri ti ya ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na bu ma am tana­laifiɛ.

eng: I thank my God always for you, because of Gods grace that was given to you in Christ Jesus.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ m da Nyɛmɛ asi wɔ, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, fa kɔ sakpa n bo wɔ yo ama am na i fite Yesu Kristo dɔ ni.

eng: That is, you were made rich through him in everything: in all your communication and every kind of knowledge.
local: Yiri dɛɛ ti, am anya deke kpa dɔŋgu, haari fa saŋga ni jɔrɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa, ni si kɛrɛ.

eng: in the same way that the testimony about Christ was confirmed with you.
local: Daani n bo ya diiri i fa kɔ Kristo ŋusu jɔrɛ ni, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: The result is that you arent missing any spiritual gift while you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to be revealed.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am fun má Nyɛmɛ aceeri kpa n haari kun n kpein mmɔɔ, na am ŋminda fa kɔ saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo bá yi i ŋu fite ni.

eng: He will also confirm your testimony about Christ until the end so that you will be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Yiri má am faŋga haari kɔ ju awieeri a, na am la má ŋgasi be ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ cɛɛn ni.

eng: God is faithful, and you were called by him to partnership with his Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
local: Nyɛmɛ la dabuŋu, yiri afere am ka am ni i Wa ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo n la wo fiɛ ni am beŋgu a.

eng: Now I encourage you, brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: Agree with each other and dont be divided into rival groups. Instead, be restored with the same mind and the same purpose.
local: M ninbaam, m sere am wɔ, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma n nu, ka am kɛrɛ fa anɔɔsɛ na má am ma kpakpaaki­nu be wo am nu, ama, am ma am sunsunni ni am nɔaniɛ yo kun nahɔrɛ su.

eng: My brothers and sisters, Chloes people gave me some information about you, that youre fighting with each other.
local: M ninbaam, deke bo i ti na m se sɔ ni, Kolowe dika­fɔm nbem ase m ka am la akuruwa am ŋuŋu nu.

eng: What I mean is this: that each one of you says, I belong to Paul, I belong to Apollos, I belong to Cephas, I belong to Christ.
local: Yiri la ka, am nbem ŋa, Mini su Pɔɔlu su a, nbem ŋa, Mini su Apolosi su a, nbem ŋa, Mini su Petoro su a, ye nbem gusu ŋa, Mini su Kristo su a.

eng: Has Christ been divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in Pauls name?.
local: Kristo akpakpaaki i ŋu nu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ye bu kpamaari Pɔɔlu baka jaanu n su maari am a? Bu biɛɛri am Nyɛmɛ nzue Pɔɔlu dɛɛ duma nu wɔ? Ai.

eng: Thank God that I didnt baptize any of you, except Crispus and Gaius.
local: M da Nyɛmɛ asi ka ma biɛ má am be Nyɛmɛ nzue, má ka Krisipusi ni Gayosi n ŋgumi.

eng: so that nobody can say that you were baptized in my name!.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, be koro se má ka m biɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ nzue m dɛɛ duma nu.

eng: Oh, I baptized the house of Stephanas too. Otherwise, I dont know if I baptized anyone else.
local: Ɛɛnhɛɛn, ma teŋge su bekun ka m biɛɛri Satɛfanasi dika­fɔm n gusu Nyɛmɛ nzue. Sin nyini sin, m koro teŋge má su ka m biɛɛri be Nyɛmɛ nzue kun.

eng: Christ didnt send me to baptize but to preach the good news. And Christ didnt send me to preach the good news with clever words so that Christs cross wont be emptied of its meaning.
local: Dama Kristo asunma má m ka m ba biɛ minɛm Nyɛmɛ nzue wɔ, ama, i sunmaari m ka m ba kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm wɔ. Ma fa má adamande dɛɛ alecira, ye m fa kan kereeri minɛm a, nzɛn má m yo sɔ, má yiwee n bo Kristo wuuri baka jaanu n su n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: The message of the cross is foolishness to those who are being destroyed. But it is the power of God for those of us who are being saved.
local: Dama nzɛn ya kan yiwee n bo Kristo wuuri i baka jaanu n su n jɔrɛ kere minɛm bo bu suka ŋgbɛɛn ni, bɛrɛ dɔ i ti sinnzinya jɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ. Ama, yɛrɛ n bo ya nya tii­de ni, i ti Nyɛmɛ yiko sa bo wɔ yo ama ya a lɛɛ.

eng: It is written in scripture: I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and I will reject the intelligence of the intelligent .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, M yó alecira­fɔm alecira ni ŋgbɛɛn, na m tɔ́ si­fɔm si n atin.

eng: Where are the wise? Where are the legal experts? Where are todays debaters? Hasnt God made the wisdom of the world foolish?.
local: Tɔ, alecira­fɔm wo ni? Ye si­fɔm wo ni? Ye bo bu fiiti na bu si ahin durunya n nu jɔrɛ n wo ni? Durunya n nu alecira ye Nyɛmɛ afa akaaki sinnzinya n a.

eng: In Gods wisdom, he determined that the world wouldnt come to know him through its wisdom. Instead, God was pleased to save those who believe through the foolishness of preaching.
local: Nyɛmɛ la alecira, be koro fa má durunya n nu alecira na i fa si Nyɛmɛ. Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu ye i ma ya kan labari kpa n bo i ti ka sinnzinya jɔrɛ n kere minɛm a, na bo ba yo yarada ni, Nyɛmɛ ade bu tii aji.

eng: Jews ask for signs, and Greeks look for wisdom.
local: Dama bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, bɛrɛ koro ka bu ŋu alekutura sam wɔ, ye bo bu ti Giriki­fɔm n gusu koro ka bu ti alecira jɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: but we preach Christ crucified, which is a scandal to Jews and foolishness to Gentiles.
local: Ama, yɛrɛ kan Kristo Tii­de­fɔ labari kpa n jɔrɛ wɔ, kabo bu kpamaari i baka jaanu n su ni. Yuda­fɔm dɔ, i ti ciire wɔ, ye bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n dɔ, i ti sinnzinya jɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: But to those who are called—both Jews and Greeks—Christ is Gods power and Gods wisdom.
local: Ama, minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ afere bu ni, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, ni bo bu ti Giriki­fɔm ni, Kristo ti Nyɛmɛ yiko ni alecira a.

eng: This is because the foolishness of God is wiser than human wisdom, and the weakness of God is stronger than human strength.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ sa bo wɔ yo i na i ti ka sinnzinya sa n tara adamande alecira kɛrɛ bo i la i, na Nyɛmɛ sa bo wɔ yo i na i ti ka i la má faŋga ni, i tara adamande faŋga kɛrɛ bo i la i.

eng: Look at your situation when you were called, brothers and sisters! By ordinary human standards not many were wise, not many were powerful, not many were from the upper class.
local: M ninbaam, am sunsun na am niɛ kabo am yo ti na Nyɛmɛ ba fereeri am ni. Nzɛn ya ŋa ya niɛ, durunya n nu­fɔm yo bu má ka am dɔŋgu la alecira, am dɔŋgu yo ti má yiko­fɔm, na am dɔŋgu gusu yo fite má nfaŋga­fɔm n busu nu.

eng: But God chose what the world considers foolish to shame the wise. God chose what the world considers weak to shame the strong.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ ayi dekem bo bu ti sinnzinya sa durunya n nu ni, na wɔ fa awura alecira­fɔm nyinsɛ, na wɔ yi dekem bo bu la má faŋga durunya n nu ni, na wɔ fa awura nfaŋga­fɔm nyinsɛ.

eng: And God chose what the world considers low-class and low-life—what is considered to be nothing—to reduce what is considered to be something to nothing.
local: Nyɛmɛ ayi bo bu ti tarakam durunya n nu fɛn, na wɔ yi minɛm bo bu bu má bu ni, ni dekem bo bu ti ŋgbɛɛn ni, na i ma dekem bo bu la faŋga n ka ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: So no human being can brag in Gods presence.
local: Deke bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ yoori i sɔ n la ka, adena má be koro fɔ yiri Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: It is because of God that you are in Christ Jesus. He became wisdom from God for us. This means that he made us righteous and holy, and he delivered us.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ ama am ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun, na wɔ ma wɔ yo ya dɛɛ alecira­fɔ. Yesu dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a, ye wɔ yo ya i minɛ casim, na wɔ de ya tii aji.

eng: This is consistent with what was written: The one who brags should brag in the Lord! .
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i fɔ, i ti ye ni ka i fɔ Mibiɛ n ti.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I couldnt talk to you like spiritual people but like unspiritual people, like babies in Christ.
local: M ninbaam, ma koro ajɔjɔ má am dɔ ka am ti minɛm bo bu si Awiɛwiɛ Casi n jɔrɛ, ama, m jɔjɔɔri am dɔ ka am ti bo bu bita adamande woo­wa a, Kristo dɛɛ atin n su, am ti ka nbaatumam wɔ.

eng: I gave you milk to drink instead of solid food, because you werent up to it yet.
local: Nbaatumam koro nu nyufini ŋgumi wɔ, bu koro di má diire nɔnɔɔri, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m kereeri am bumboori su dɛɛ keree­wam n a, dama am koro ti má keree­wam bo bu ti kekereke n bu. Haari ni kisa, am nya koro ti má i bu.

eng: Now you are still not up to it because you are still unspiritual. When jealousy and fighting exist between you, arent you unspiritual and living by human standards?.
local: Dama am toko bita adamande woo­wa n wɔ. Am si am beŋgu kɔrɛ wɔ, na am suse akuruwa am ŋuŋu nu. Kabo am yo sɔ ni, durunya n nu adamande woo­wa atin lɛɛ, ye am bita i n a.

eng: When someone says, I belong to Paul, and someone else says, I belong to Apollos, arent you acting like people without the Spirit?.
local: Dama nzɛn am nbem ŋa, Mini su Pɔɔlu su a, ye nbem gusu ŋa, Mini su Apolosi su a, am ti má ka durunya n nu­fɔm wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: After all, what is Apollos? What is Paul? They are servants who helped you to believe. Each one had a role given to them by the Lord.
local: Apolosi la ŋma? Wara Pɔɔlu la ŋma? Ya kɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ amusui­su­fɔm bo ya ba abuka am wɔ, ya kɛrɛ ni ya juma bo wɔ fa ama ya na ya yo, ye am ayo yarada a.

eng: I planted, Apollos watered, but God made it grow.
local: Fa kɔ am yarada­yo n su, i ti ka mini Pɔɔlu dɔɔri alɛ­mbaa n a, ye Apolosi gɔɔri i nzue a, ama, Nyɛmɛ yiko n nu ma wɔ nyi awura mbaa.

eng: Because of this, neither the one who plants nor the one who waters is anything, but the only one who is anything is God who makes it grow.
local: Nyini ti, bo i dɔ mbaa n ni bo i gɔ nzue n be mmɔɔ ti má pui. Deke bo i ti jɔrɛ n la sa bo Nyɛmɛ yo i n wɔ, dama yiri ma i nyi wura mbaa a.

eng: The one who plants and the one who waters work together, but each one will receive their own reward for their own labor.
local: Bo i dɔ mbaa n ni bo i gɔ nzue n kɛrɛ yo juma kun wɔ. Bu kɛrɛ nyá bu ŋkatɔ kabo bu juma­yoo­wa ti barasu n wɔ.

eng: We are Gods coworkers, and you are Gods field, Gods building.
local: Dama ya kɛrɛ saŋga yo juma kun ma Nyɛmɛ wɔ, ye ambɛrɛ gusu ti ka Nyɛmɛ fie ye ya dɔ i a, na am kaaki ti ka Nyɛmɛ awuru ye ya ta i a.

eng: I laid a foundation like a wise master builder according to Gods grace that was given to me, but someone else is building on top of it. Each person needs to pay attention to the way they build on it.
local: Aceeri n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama m ti ka sɔnɔ bo i si ataari ta nyunyumi wɔ, ye m se sɔɔ n bumboori fa n a. Ye be gusu dɛɛ la ka i ta taka nyini n su a. Ama, wɔ yo daka ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si i ŋu sa kabo i ta taka su barasu.

eng: No one can lay any other foundation besides the one that is already laid, which is Jesus Christ.
local: Dama Yesu Kristo ŋgumi ti ka sɔɔ fa bumboori n bo bu fa se sɔɔ n bu n a, be koro se má sɔɔ fa be bekun sin yiri ŋu.

eng: So, whether someone builds on top of the foundation with gold, silver, precious stones, wood, grass, or hay.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ta taka su, na i ti ka i faari sika, na be gusu faari jɛtɛ, na be gusu faari afere bo i la anyunuyi ta takaari su, na be faari baka, na be faari wura, na be faari kara ta takaari su.

eng: each ones work will be clearly shown. The day will make it clear, because it will be revealed with fire—the fire will test the quality of each ones work.
local: Ama, jina cɛɛn, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ juma­yoo­wa bá fite fofoe nu, na bu ŋú i kabo i ti, dama sin bá yara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ juma, na sin ni yó i na i níɛ kabo i ti.

eng: If anyones work survives, theyll get a reward.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ta takaari bumboori fa see­wa n su, na nzɛn sin n yaraari i juma­yoo­wa na wɔ di má i, i mi nyá ŋkatɔ.

eng: But if anyones work goes up in flames, theyll lose it. However, they themselves will be saved as if they had gone through a fire.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo sin n diiri i juma­yoo­wa ni, i nyá má ŋkatɔ, ama, yiri ni i tii nyá tii­de, se i ti ka sin n afundi i mi wɔ.

eng: Dont you know that you are Gods temple and Gods Spirit lives in you?.
local: Am si má ka am ti ka awuru bo Nyɛmɛ wo am nu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Am si má ka Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo am nu wɔ?.

eng: If someone destroys Gods temple, God will destroy that person, because Gods temple is holy, which is what you are.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i saakiiri Nyɛmɛ awuru ni, Nyɛmɛ sáaki i mi, dama Nyɛmɛ awuru n ti casi wɔ, ye ambɛrɛ ni am tii ti ka nyini awuru n a.

eng: Dont fool yourself. If some of you think they are worldly-wise, then they should become foolish so that they can become wise.
local: Má be se ka i dadaka i ŋu. Nzɛn be wo am nu, na i sunsun ka i la durunya n nu alecira, ma i mi yo i ŋu ka i ti sinnzin wɔ, adena i kaaki alecira­fɔ nahɔrɛ su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: This worlds wisdom is foolishness to God. As its written, He catches the wise in their cleverness. .
local: Dama deke bo durunya n nu­fɔm bu i ka i ti alecira ni, Nyɛmɛ dɔ i ti sinnzinya a. Nyini ti ye ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Alecira­fɔm ye Nyɛmɛ fa bu ŋgɔnlɛ­tiɛ fa tara bu n a.

eng: And also, The Lord knows that the thoughts of the wise are silly .
local: Ye ba kaaki akɛrɛ i ka, Micɛra ni si deke kɛrɛ bo i wo alecira­fɔm sunsunni nu a, i si ka i ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: So then, no one should brag about human beings. Everything belongs to you.
local: Nyini ti, má be se ka i fa adamande be fa fɔ, dama deke n kɛrɛ ti am dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Paul, Apollos, Cephas, the world, life, death, things in the present, things in the future—everything belongs to you.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu ti am dɛɛ wɔ, ni Apolosi, ni Petoro gusu, ni durunya, ni ŋgɔɔ, ni yiwee, ni kisa dɛɛ saŋga n bo ya wo nu ni, ni saŋga bo i bá ba ni, deke n kɛrɛ ti am dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: but you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
local: na ambɛrɛ gusu ti Kristo dɛɛm, ye Kristo gusu ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ.

eng: Everyone has heard that there is sexual immorality among you. This is a type of immorality that isnt even heard of among the Gentiles—a man is having sex with his fathers wife!.
local: Ma ti jɔrɛ be, ka daŋgba sa wo am nu, yiri la ka sɔnɔ be bita i si yi da a. Haari bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni mmɔɔ yo má nyini daŋgba sa ni.

eng: And youre proud of yourselves instead of being so upset that the one who did this thing is expelled from your community.
local: Am ayo nyini sa n ye am toko koro kpiri n a fan? Ye am ahore saaki má n a? Wɔ yo daka ka am yi nyini sɔnɔ n fite am nu.

eng: Though Im absent physically, Im present in the spirit and Ive already judged the man who did this as if I were present.
local: Haari ni bo mini wo má am dɔ ni, m awiɛwiɛ wo am dɔ, na sɔnɔ bo i yoori nyini sa ni, ma du mɔ adi i jɔrɛ ajaraki i ya Mibiɛ Yesu dɛɛ duma n nu, kabo má mini ni m tii wo am dɔ na m yo ni. Nzɛn am ba tiɛɛnri am nɔɔ, na m awiɛwiɛ wo am dɔ, na ya Mibiɛ Yesu yiko n wo ya dɔ.

eng: At that time we need to hand this man over to Satan to destroy his human weakness so that his spirit might be saved on the day of the Lord.
local: am fa nyini sɔnɔ n fa wura Sitana saa nu, na i kere i kekereke fa kɔ i adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa n su, adena i awiɛwiɛ n nya tii­de cɛɛn n bo Mibiɛ ni bá di minɛm jɔrɛ ni.

eng: Your bragging isnt good! Dont you know that a tiny grain of yeast makes a whole batch of dough rise?.
local: Am yanfɔya bo am nati fɔ i ni, i ti má kpa. Am si má ka ayiri kaan sɔ ma kpɔnɔ n yasu a, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: Clean out the old yeast so you can be a new batch of dough, given that youre supposed to be unleavened bread. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed.
local: Am sunsun fa kɔ Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n su, bo bu di kpɔnɔ na bu gɔ má ayiri nu ni, kana bu ba di cɛɛn­kpiri n ni, bu du mɔ fa ayiri dawa n tu wɔ. Am yi nyini ayiri dawa n fa tu, na am yo ka kpɔnɔ fɔfɔrɛ bo ba fa má ayiri agɔ má nu. Deke bo m suse n la ka, nzɛn satiɛ kaan woori am nu, i sáaki am kɛrɛ wɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, am yi satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo am nu ni. Nahɔrɛ, am ti minɛ casim wɔ, am ti ka kpɔnɔ fɔfɔrɛ n wɔ, dama ba fa Kristo ayo yoo­wa ama ya. Yiri ti ka ya Butara cɛɛn­kpiri bɔmbaa n bo ba kpiɛ i kɔm n a.

eng: so lets celebrate the feast with the unleavened bread of honesty and truth, not with old yeast or with the yeast of evil and wickedness.
local: Nyini ti, am ma ya bita Butara cɛɛn­kpiri nyunyumi, na ya yaki satiɛ bo i ti ka ayiri dawa ni, ama, am ma ya di i ka ya ti minɛm bo ya ti casi na ya la nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo i ti ka kpɔnɔ bo ba gɔ má ayiri nu ni.

eng: I wrote to you in my earlier letter not to associate with sexually immoral people.
local: Kadasi n bo m yo du mɔ kɛrɛ maari am ni, m seeri am ka, má am ni minɛm bo bu yo daŋgba sam saŋga wo.

eng: But I wasnt talking about the sexually immoral people in the outside world by any means—or the greedy, or the swindlers, or people who worship false gods—otherwise, you would have to leave the world entirely!.
local: Tɔ, m sukan má durunya n nu­fɔm ni jɔrɛ a, bɛrɛ bo bu nati yo daŋgba sam ni, ni bo bu nyunu aboro neŋge kpini su ni, ni bo bu fin bu beŋgum ni, ni bo bu su amɔɛm ni. Nzɛn má i ti sɔ yiri, ŋka má am fite yaki durunya n wɔ.

eng: But now Im writing to you not to associate with anyone who calls themselves brother or sister who is sexually immoral, greedy, someone who worships false gods, an abusive person, a drunk, or a swindler. Dont even eat with anyone like this.
local: Tɔ, bo m sukan n la ka, má am saŋga ni minɛm bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti am lamɔkɔ Kristo­fɔm na bu yo daŋgba sam, wara bu nyunu boro neŋge kpini su, wara bo bu su amɔɛm ni, wara bo bu nati kan minɛm jɔrɛ, wara bo bu nati nu nzan bo ni, wara bo bu nati fin minɛ ni. Má am se ka am ni nyini sɔnɔ n be sɔ mmɔɔ saŋga di diire.

eng: What do I care about judging outsiders? Isnt it your job to judge insiders?.
local: Má m juma la ka m di bo bu wo má Kristo jama n nu jɔrɛ. Nyɛmɛ dí bu jɔrɛ a. Bo bu wo Kristo jama n nu ni, ambɛrɛ ti bo am di bu jɔrɛ a. Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Am fɔn yi sɔnɔ tiɛ n bo i wo am nu ni.

eng: So a person should think about us this way—as servants of Christ and managers of Gods secrets.
local: Tɔ, nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka am niɛ ya ka ya ti Kristo amusui­su­fɔm wɔ, ya ti minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ afa i asiri nu jɔrɛ n awura ya saa nu wɔ.

eng: In this kind of situation, what is expected of a manager is that they prove to be faithful.
local: Jɔrɛ n bo i kaaki wo bɛrɛ la ka, wɔ yo daka ka amusui­su­fɔ yo i juma ni nahɔrɛ su ma i mibiɛ.

eng: I couldnt care less if Im judged by you or by any human court; I dont even judge myself.
local: Mini yiri, nzɛn am di m jɔrɛ, wara adamande jɔrɛ­di­fɔm di m jɔrɛ, m cɛcɛ wo má nu, haari mini mmɔɔ ni m tii di má m ŋu jɔrɛ.

eng: Im not aware of anything against me, but that doesnt make me innocent, because the Lord is the one who judges me.
local: M sunsunni ni m ahore su, ma yo má satiɛ be, ama, nyini kere má ka m la má ŋgasi be wɔ lɛɛ, má ka Mibiɛ ni ŋgumi ti bo i di m jɔrɛ a.

eng: So dont judge anything before the right time—wait until the Lord comes. He will bring things that are hidden in the dark to light, and he will make peoples motivations public. Then there will be recognition for each person from God.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, má sɔnɔ be di i beŋgu jɔrɛ, dama saŋga nya ju má bo Mibiɛ ni bá. I ba baari, yiri yí deke kɛrɛ bo i wo awosin nu na i fiɛ n fite fofoe nu a, na i yí deke kɛrɛ bo i wo adamande ahore su fite fofoe nu. Nyini saŋga ni, ye sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nyá i kansi Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit. Ive done this so that you can learn what it means not to go beyond what has been written and so none of you will become arrogant by supporting one of us against the other.
local: Tɔ, m ninbaam, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye m kaanri ahin n kɛrɛ fa kɔ m ni Apolosi jɔrɛ n a, na am niɛ ya woo­wa, na am su kabo ba kɛrɛ i ase ni ŋgumi su, adena má am be yo kpirikoro ka mɔ­fɔ be tara i lamɔkɔ nyiŋga ni.

eng: When someone in your assembly has a legal case against another member, do they dare to take it to court to be judged by people who arent just, instead of by Gods people?.
local: Nzɛn am be ni i beŋgu Kristo­fɔ be nyaari jɔrɛ be, i ti ye ni ka i mi fa jɔrɛ n kɔ Nyɛmɛ minɛm n dɔ, má i se ka i fa kɔ durunya n nu jɔrɛ­di­fɔm bo bu si má Nyɛmɛ n dɔ fieo.

eng: Or dont you know that Gods people will judge the world? If the world is to be judged by you, are you incompetent to judge trivial cases?.
local: Am si má ka Nyɛmɛ minɛm bá di durunya n kɛrɛ jɔrɛ a, nzɛn sɛ? Ye nzɛn am bá di durunya n kɛrɛ jɔrɛ, nzu ti ye am koro yo má am ŋuŋu nu jɔrɛ mumukum dɔŋgu a?.

eng: Who says that you are better than anyone else? What do you have that you didnt receive? And if you received it, then why are you bragging as if you didnt receive it?.
local: Ŋma ase am ka am tara am beŋgu a? Nzu deke ye am la i, ye Nyɛmɛ afa ace má am a? Deke bo am deeri i ni, ye nzu ti ye am koro kpiri ka Nyɛmɛ afa ace má am a?.

eng: Youve been filled already! Youve become rich already! You rule like kings without us! I wish you did rule so that we could be kings with you!.
local: Am sunsun ka am anya deke n kɛrɛ bo am koro i n lɛɛ fan. Am ayo neŋge­fɔm lɛɛ fan. Am akaaki nfɛmɛm ayaki ya lɛɛ fan. Nzɛn má am adi fɛmɛya nahɔrɛ su, má i yo m fiɛ, adena ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ saŋga di fɛmɛya ni.

eng: Dont you know that we will judge angels? Why not ordinary things?.
local: Am si má ka ya bá di haari ni mɛrɛkɛm jɔrɛ? Ye nzu ti ye am koro di má jɔrɛm fa kɔ dekem bo bu wo durunya n nu n su a?.

eng: So then if you have ordinary lawsuits, do you appoint people as judges who arent respected by the church?.
local: Ambɛrɛ, nzɛn am la nyini jɔrɛ n be sɔ, ye nzu ti ye am fa kɔ minɛm bo Kristo jama n bu má bu n dɔ a?.

eng: I suppose that God has shown that we apostles are at the end of the line. We are like prisoners sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle in the world, both to angels and to humans.
local: Mini dɔ, yɛrɛ n bo ya ti Kristo jaramasam ni, i ti ka Nyɛmɛ ma ya ka sin wɔ. Ya ti ka minɛm bo ba di ya jɔrɛ abo ya asi ye ya wu a, na ya di nyini nyinsɛ n na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nyu­mbaa ŋu i, mɛrɛkɛm ni adamandem kɛrɛ.

eng: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise through Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, but we are dishonored!.
local: Yɛrɛ ti sinnzinm wɔ, Kristo dɛɛ ti, ama, ambɛrɛ ti anyunuteke­fɔm wɔ, Kristo atin n su. Yɛrɛ ti daburuka­fɔm wɔ, ye ambɛrɛ ti nfaŋga­fɔm. Bu bu ambɛrɛ wɔ, ama, yɛrɛ yiri, bu bu má ya.

eng: Im saying this because you should be ashamed of yourselves! Isnt there one person among you who is wise enough to pass judgment between believers?.
local: Deke bo m sukan i ni, i ti am nyinsɛ wɔ. Alecira­fɔ haari kun n kpein mmɔɔ wo má am nu bo i koro di am ŋuŋu nu jɔrɛm, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: But instead, does a brother or sister have a lawsuit against another brother or sister, and do they do this in front of unbelievers?.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ, am fa am beŋgum kɔ jɔrɛ dii­biri wɔ, na am kɔ jina minɛm bo bu ti má yarada­yo­fɔm nyunu, na bu di am jɔrɛ.

eng: Up to this very moment we are hungry, thirsty, wearing rags, abused, and homeless.
local: Haari fa ba ju ni kisa, ahɔɛ ni nzuehɔɛ sukun ya a, ya la má sutura wuraa­wa kpa, bu bita ya bo wɔ, ya kaaki la má deke bo ya da.

eng: The fact that you have lawsuits against each other means that youve already lost your case. Why not be wronged instead? Why not be cheated?.
local: Nahɔrɛ, am akpaasi, kabo am nati fa am beŋgu kɔ jɔrɛ dii­biri ni. Nzɛn u niwa doori u ŋgasi be, i ti ye ni ka a mindi de i. Nzɛn u niwa be sisiiri u, yaki ce i, i ti ye ni bo a fa i kɔ jɔrɛ dii­biri.

eng: We work hard with our own hands. When we are insulted, we respond with a blessing; when we are harassed, we put up with it.
local: Ya yo ya saa nu juma ni anyum­boro wɔ, ye ya di a. Nzɛn minɛm kpiɛ ya nzukɔ, ya sere awieeri kpa ma bu wɔ. Nzɛn bu kere ya wuwuruwa, ya mindi de i wɔ.

eng: when our reputation is attacked, we are encouraging. We have become the scum of the earth, the waste that runs off everything, up to the present time.
local: Nzɛn bu saaki ya duma, yɛrɛ jɔjɔ kpa ma bu wɔ. Ya ti ka wura tiɛ bo durunya n nu­fɔm fa gɔ bɔnfɔ su n wɔ, haari ni kisa.

eng: But instead you are doing wrong and cheating—and youre doing it to your own brothers and sisters.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ ni am tii wura am beŋgu ŋgasi nu wɔ, na am susisi am beŋgum, haari ni am beŋgu Kristo­fɔm kɛrɛ.

eng: Dont you know that people who are unjust wont inherit Gods kingdom? Dont be deceived. Those who are sexually immoral, those who worship false gods, adulterers, both participants in same-sex intercourse.
local: Am si má ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, nzɛn sɛ? Má am dadaka am ŋu, minɛm bo bu yo daŋgba sa koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, wara bo bu su amɔɛm, wara bo bu saaki ajaa ciire, wara nbiɛsɔm bo bu fa bu ŋu kaaki ka nbaram na bu ni bu beŋgu nbiɛsɔm saŋga da ka bara ni biɛsɔ dɛɛ su ni.

eng: Im not writing these things to make you ashamed but to warn you, since you are my loved children.
local: I ti má ka m koro ka m wura am nyinsɛ dɛɛ ti, ye m sukɛrɛ ahin jɔrɛm ma am a, ama, am ti m nbaa koroo­wam dɛɛ ti, ye m subɔbɔ am a.

eng: You may have ten thousand mentors in Christ, but you dont have many fathers. I gave birth to you in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
local: Dama nzɛn haari má am la kere­fɔm akpii buru mmɔɔ fa kɔ Kristo dɛɛ atin n su, má am la má sim beberebe, mini ti am si a, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ atin n su, labari kpa n bo m kan kereeri am dɛɛ ti.

eng: thieves, the greedy, drunks, abusive people, and swindlers wont inherit Gods kingdom.
local: wara awiefɔm, wara bo bu nyunu aboro neŋge kpini su, wara bo bu nu nzan na bu bo, wara bo bu nati kan minɛm jɔrɛ, wara bo bu nati fin minɛm ni, bɛrɛ be mmɔɔ koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu.

eng: That is what some of you used to be! But you were washed clean, you were made holy to God, and you were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and in the Spirit of our God.
local: Am nbem yo ti sɔ ɔ. Ama, Nyɛmɛ aŋmisi ayi am satiɛm ni, na wɔ fa am ayo i dɛɛ minɛ casim, na wɔ de am ka am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo duma n dɛɛ ti, ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: so I encourage you to follow my example.
local: Nyini ti, m sere am wɔ, am sɔn m su, na am ma am woo­wa ti kabo m woo­wa ti barasu ni.

eng: This is why Ive sent Timothy to you; hes my loved and trusted child in the Lord; hell remind you about my way of life in Christ Jesus. Hell teach the same way as I teach everywhere in every church.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye m susunma Timoti am dɔ n a. Yiri ti m wa koroo­wa nahɔrɛ su a, na i kaaki la dabuŋu Mibiɛ n atin n su. I téŋge am su kabo m woo­wa ti Kristo atin n su, ni kabo m kere minɛm ka bu wo barasu, deke kɛrɛ bo m kɔ ni, Kristo jamam n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: I have the freedom to do anything, but not everything is helpful. I have the freedom to do anything, but I wont be controlled by anything.
local: Am nbem se ka, M la atin bo m koro yo deke kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m yo i, ama, má deke kɛrɛ la kpɛrɛ a. Mini mmɔɔ koro se ka m la atin bo m koro yo deke kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m yo i, ama, m yaki má na deke be kunma m.

eng: Some have become arrogant as if Im not coming to see you.
local: Am nbem sunsun ka m ba má am dɔ kun, nyini ti, ba fa kpirikoro.

eng: But, if the Lord is willing, Ill come to you soon. Then I wont focus on what these arrogant people say, but Ill find out what power they possess.
local: Ama, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ soro su, i cɛ́ má, m bá niɛ am busu, na m bá si nyini minɛm bo bu la kpirikoro ni, má ka bu nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa ni ŋgumi, ama, m níɛ kabo bu yiko ti barasu.

eng: Food is for the stomach and the stomach is for food, and yet God will do away with both. The body isnt for sexual immorality but for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
local: Nbem gusu ŋa, Diire ti akunnu dɛɛ wɔ, na akunnu gusu ti diire dɛɛ. I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ bá ma akunnu n ni diire n kɛrɛ ka ŋgbɛɛn a. Ama, aŋuniɛ­mbaa n yiri, i ti má sakaraya dɛɛ, ama, i ti Mibiɛ n dɛɛ wɔ, na Mibiɛ n gusu ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa n dɛɛ.

eng: God has raised the Lord and will raise us through his power.
local: Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n nu ye i teŋgeeri Mibiɛ ni i yiwee nu a. I yiko n nu ye i bá teŋge ya gusu a.

eng: Gods kingdom isnt about words but about power.
local: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ niɛ i minɛm su ni, i ti má nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n wɔ, ama, i ti yiko wɔ.

eng: Dont you know that your bodies are parts of Christ? So then, should I take parts of Christ and make them a part of someone who is sleeping around? No way!.
local: Am si má ka am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n ti Kristo aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ye m koro fa Kristo aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be yo sakaraya, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, atin wo má nu.

eng: Dont you know that anyone who is joined to someone who is sleeping around is one body with that person? The scripture says, The two will become one flesh .
local: Am si má ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i faari i ŋu mataari bara sakara na bu saŋga woori, bɛrɛ nnyɔ n ayo deke kun wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu fa kɔ biɛsɔ ni i yi jɔrɛ su, ka, Bu minɛ nnyɔ n ayo deke kun lɛɛ.

eng: Which do you want? Should I come to you with a big stick to punish you, or with love and a gentle spirit?.
local: Ahin jɔrɛ nnyɔm nu, am koro bɔnɔ ɔ? Am koro ka m bá cin am sui, nzɛn sɛ? Wara am koro ka m bá am dɔ bɛnlɛ, ni koro wɔ?.

eng: The one who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i faari i ŋu mataari Mibiɛ n na bu saŋga woori ni, i ni yiri dɛɛ awiɛwiɛ n ayo kun lɛɛ.

eng: Avoid sexual immorality! Every sin that a person can do is committed outside the body, except those who engage in sexual immorality commit sin against their own bodies.
local: Am ŋmati fite sakaraya nu. Má satiɛ kɛrɛ bo sɔnɔ yo i ye i saaki i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n a. Ama, bo i yo sakaraya ni, i mi saaki i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n wɔ.

eng: Or dont you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you? Dont you know that you have the Holy Spirit from God, and you dont belong to yourselves?.
local: Am si má ka am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n ti ka awuru bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo nu n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Yiri ye Nyɛmɛ fa maari am n a, má am dɛɛ la am ŋu.

eng: You have been bought and paid for, so honor God with your body.
local: Nyɛmɛ ato am atɔ kaari wɔ, nyini ti, am fa am ŋuniɛ­mbaa n fa yo deke bo i ma Nyɛmɛ jirima.

eng: Now, about what you wrote: Its good for a man not to have sex with a woman.
local: Tɔ, jɔrɛ n bo am kɛrɛ maari m ni, am ŋa, I ti ye ni ka má biɛsɔ si bara.

eng: Each man should have his own wife, and each woman should have her own husband because of sexual immorality.
local: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ama, mini dɔ, sakaraya dɛɛ ti, i ti ye ni ka biɛsɔ kɛrɛ la i tiɛŋgu yi, na bara kɛrɛ la i tiɛŋgu hun.

eng: The husband should meet his wifes sexual needs, and the wife should do the same for her husband.
local: I ti má ye ni ka biɛsɔ kete na má i ni i yi saŋga, i kaaki ti má ye ni ka bara kete na má i ni i hun saŋga.

eng: The wife doesnt have authority over her own body, but the husband does. Likewise, the husband doesnt have authority over his own body, but the wife does.
local: Má bara n dɛɛ la i ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, i ti i hun n dɛɛ wɔ, sɔ ye i kaaki ti a, má biɛsɔ n gusu dɛɛ la i ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni, i ti i yi n dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Dont refuse to meet each others needs unless you both agree for a short period of time to devote yourselves to prayer. Then come back together again so that Satan might not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
local: Má am kete am beŋgu daari, má ka am ayo anɔɔkun ka am fa nyini saŋga kaan sere Nyɛmɛ, nyini sin, na am kaaki saŋga bekun, adena má Sitana yo am niɛ na i nya am, dama am koro tara má am ŋu dɛɛ ti.

eng: Im saying this to give you permission; its not a command.
local: I ti má ka m suma am nɔɔ ka am yaki i wɔ, ama, m suma am atin ka am koro yo sɔ ɔ.

eng: I wish all people were like me, but each has a particular gift from God: one has this gift, and another has that one.
local: Mini dɔ, nzɛn má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wo kabo m wo ni, i yo m fiɛ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ni i aceeri bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama i, be dɛɛ wo i ŋgumi, na be gusu dɛɛ wo i ŋgumi, i kɛrɛ ti má kun.

eng: Im telling those who are single and widows that its good for them to stay single like me.
local: Kisa m sujɔjɔ ambɛrɛ nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram bo am nya ja má ni dɔ a, ni nkuna­fɔm, nzɛn má am wo kabo m wo ni, na am ja má, i ti kpa.

eng: But if they cant control themselves, they should get married, because its better to marry than to burn with passion.
local: Ama, nzɛn am koro tara má am ŋu, am ja. Nzɛn am yo sɔ, i ti ye ni bo saŋga kɛrɛ am la konviki agaya fa kɔ bara ni biɛsɔ dika, na am koro tara má am ŋu ni.

eng: Im passing on the Lords command to those who are married: A wife shouldnt leave her husband.
local: Ahin ti nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram ajaa­fɔm dɛɛ jɔrɛ ye m sukan i n a, má mini suse a, ama, i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa a, ka má bara yaki i hun ajaa.

eng: but if she does leave him, then she should stay single or be reconciled to her husband. And a man shouldnt divorce his wife.
local: Ama, nzɛn i kɔ yoori ka be afite i hun dika, má i ja kun, i tana sɔ, wara i kaaki kɔ i hun n dika. Má biɛsɔ n gusu se ka i yaki i yi ajaa.

eng: Im telling everyone else : If a believer has a wife who doesnt believe, and she agrees to live with him, then he shouldnt divorce her.
local: Minɛm bo ba ka ni, m dɛɛ fɔ ye m sufa tu am n a, má Nyɛmɛ seeri sɔ a. Nzɛn Kristo­fɔ be yi ti má yarada­yo­fɔ, na wɔ soro su ka i tana i hun n ajaa bu, má i yaki i ajaa, i bita i.

eng: If a woman has a husband who doesnt believe and he agrees to live with her, then she shouldnt divorce him.
local: Nzɛn bara be ti Kristo­fɔ, na i hun ti má yarada­yo­fɔ, na biɛsɔ n asoro su ka i ni i yi n saŋga tana, má bara n yaki i ajaa, i tana.

eng: The husband who doesnt believe belongs to God because of his wife, and the wife who doesnt believe belongs to God because of her husband. Otherwise, your children would be contaminated by the world, but now they are spiritually set apart.
local: Dama biɛsɔ n bo i yo má yarada ni, Nyɛmɛ ade i ayo i dɛɛ sɔnɔ, i yi n bo i ti Kristo­fɔ n dɛɛ ti, na bara n gusu bo i yo má yarada ni, Nyɛmɛ ade i ayo i dɛɛ sɔnɔ, i hun n bo i ti Kristo­fɔ n dɛɛ ti. Nzɛn má nyini, Nyɛmɛ dé má bu nbaam, ama, kabo i ti ni, Nyɛmɛ ade bu.

eng: But if a spouse who doesnt believe chooses to leave, then let them leave. The brother or sister isnt tied down in these circumstances. God has called you to peace.
local: Ama, nzɛn bo i ti má yarada­yo­fɔ n kete ka i ja má, yaki i na i kɔ, nyini saŋga, Kristo­fɔ n la má ŋgasi be. Nyɛmɛ afere ya ka ya wo ni tana­laifiɛ wɔ.

eng: How do you know as a wife if you will save your husband? Or how do you know as a husband if you will save your wife?.
local: Wɔrɔ bara ni, beni, wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, u hun ni nyá tii­de, na wɔrɔ biɛsɔ n gusu, beni, wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, u yi ni nyá tii­de.

eng: Nevertheless, each person should live the kind of life that the Lord assigned when he called each one. This is what I teach in all the churches.
local: Tɔ, am ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wo kabo Mibiɛ n kereeri i saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ fereeri i ni. Deke kɛrɛ bo m kɔ Kristo jamam n nu, kabo m kere bu ka bu yo lɛɛ.

eng: If someone was circumcised when called, he shouldnt try to reverse it. If someone wasnt circumcised when he was called, he shouldnt be circumcised.
local: Nzɛn be wo bɛrɛ, na wɔ du mɔ awura kɛnkɛnnɛ ka, ye Nyɛmɛ ba fereeri i, i ti ye ni ka i mi yaki i kɛnkɛnnɛ yoo­wa n sɔ. Nzɛn be gusu wo bɛrɛ, na wɔ wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ, ye Nyɛmɛ afere i, i ti ye ni ka i mi wo sɔ, má i se ka i wura kɛnkɛnnɛ.

eng: Circumcision is nothing; not being circumcised is nothing. What matters is keeping Gods commandments.
local: Bo wɔ wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n ni bo wɔ wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ ni, i be mmɔɔ ti má pui, ama, deke bo i ti jɔrɛ nahɔrɛ su n la ka, sɔnɔ bita Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni.

eng: Each person should stay in the situation they were in when they were called.
local: I ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wo kabo i yo ti barasu na Nyɛmɛ nya ba fereeri i ni.

eng: If you were a slave when you were called, dont let it bother you. But if you are actually able to be free, take advantage of the opportunity.
local: Nzɛn a ti akɔɔ, ye Nyɛmɛ afere u, má a ma i yo u yaa, ama, nzɛn a si ka a koro nya atin na a de u tii, a koro yo sɔ.

eng: Anyone who was a slave when they were called by the Lord has the status of being the Lords free person. In the same way, anyone who was a free person when they were called is Christs slave.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti akɔɔ na Mibiɛ n afere i, i ti Mibiɛ n dɛɛ sɔnɔ bo wɔ de i tii aji wɔ, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo i ti má akɔɔ na Mibiɛ n afere i, sɔ ye wɔ kaaki Kristo akɔɔ a.

eng: You were bought and paid for. Dont become slaves of people.
local: Nyɛmɛ ato am atɔ kaari wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, má am yo adamandem akɔɔm.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, each of you should stay with God in the situation you were in when you were called.
local: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, kabo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti na Nyɛmɛ afere i ni, i ti ye ni ka i mi wo sɔ ni Nyɛmɛ.

eng: I dont have a command from the Lord about people who have never been married, but Ill give you my opinion as someone you can trust because of the Lords mercy.
local: Fa kɔ minɛm bo ba nya aja má ajaa n dɔ ni, m la má keree­wa bo i fite Mibiɛ n dɔ fa kɔ nyini n dika, ama, Mibiɛ n asi m aŋunfɔ na m la dabuŋu n dɛɛ ti, ye m koro ka m se am deke bo i wo m sunsunni nu n a.

eng: So I think this advice is good because of the present crisis: Stay as you are.
local: Miɛ n bo ya wo nu kisa n dɛɛ ti, mini dɔ, nzɛn má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro wo i wo, má i ti kpa.

eng: If you are married, dont get a divorce. If you are divorced, dont try to find a spouse.
local: Nzɛn a du mɔ aja bara, má a kpini atin na a yaki ajaa ni. Nzɛn a nya ja má gusu, má a kpini atin na a ja.

eng: But if you do marry, you havent sinned; and if someone who hasnt been married gets married, they havent sinned. But married people will have a hard time, and Im trying to spare you that.
local: Nzɛn a ti biɛsɔ ye a ja bara, a yo má satiɛ, nzɛn a ti suŋguru gusu ye a ja biɛsɔ, a yo má satiɛ. Ama, wahara n bo ajaaja­fɔm di i durunya n nu n ye m koro má ka am di i be a.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: The time has drawn short. From now on, those who have wives should be like people who dont have them.
local: M ninbaam, deke bo m suse n la ka, saŋga n waari má kun. Nyini ti, fa kisa fa kɔ, bo bu la nbaram ni, i ti ye ni ka bu wo ka bu la má be.

eng: Those who are sad should be like people who arent crying. Those who are happy should be like people who arent happy. Those who buy something should be like people who dont have possessions.
local: Na bo bu sun ni, ma bu wo ka bu sun má, na bo bu ahore ajɔ ni, ma bu wo ka bu ahore ajɔ má, na bo bu to neŋgem ni, ma bu wo ka má bu dɛɛ la neŋge ni.

eng: Those who use the world should be like people who arent preoccupied with it, because this world in its present form is passing away.
local: Na bo bu fa durunya n nu neŋgem fa yo bu jumam ni, ma bu wo ka bu cɛcɛ wo má bu nu, dama ahin durunya n ni deke kɛrɛ bo i wo nu n ti siin­wa a.

eng: I want you to be free from concerns. A man who isnt married is concerned about the Lords concerns—how he can please the Lord.
local: M koro má ka deke be miɛ am, am sunsunni nu. Sɔnɔ bo i la má bara ni, i sunsunni kɛrɛ wo Mibiɛ n dɛɛ juma ni su a, i dɛɛ la ka, i yo sa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu wɔ.

eng: But a married man is concerned about the worlds concerns—how he can please his wife.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ bo wɔ ja bara, i sunsunni kɛrɛ wo durunya n nu dɛɛ dekem ni su a, i dɛɛ la ka, i yo sa bo i sun i yi nu wɔ.

eng: His attention is divided. A woman who isnt married or who is a virgin is concerned about the Lords concerns so that she can be dedicated to God in both body and spirit. But a married woman is concerned about the worlds concerns—how she can please her husband.
local: nyini ti, i sunsunni ti nnyɔ nnyɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Na bara bo i ja má, wara suŋguru bo i si má biɛsɔ, i sunsunni kɛrɛ wo Mibiɛ n juma ni su a, adena i wo casi i ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni i awiɛwiɛ nu. Ama, bara bo i la hun, i sunsunni kɛrɛ wo durunya n nu dɛɛ dekem ni su a, i dɛɛ la ka, i yo sa bo i sun i hun nu wɔ.

eng: Im saying this for your own advantage. Its not to restrict you but rather to promote effective and consistent service to the Lord without distraction.
local: M koro ka m buka am wɔ, nyini ti ye m se sɔ n a, i ti má ka m koro ka m kanda am atin wɔ. Ama, m koro ka am yo sa kɛrɛ bo i kɔ atin su wɔ, na am fa am ŋu ma Mibiɛ n nahɔrɛ su, na am su i nyunyumi.

eng: If someone thinks he is acting inappropriately toward an unmarried woman whom he knows, and if he has strong feelings and it seems like the right thing to do, he should do what he wants—hes not sinning—they should get married.
local: Ama, nzɛn suŋguru be wo bɛrɛ na wɔ nya si má biɛsɔ, na ba ce i, na i ni biɛsɔ n kɛrɛ ayo bu sunsunni nu ka bu ja má, ama, kasin na biɛsɔ n ba ŋu ka i koro tara má i ŋu, na bara n aju ajaa, i koro ja i, i ti má satiɛ.

eng: But if a man stands firm in his decision, and doesnt feel the pressure, but has his own will under control, he does right if he decides in his own heart not to marry the woman.
local: Ama, nzɛn wɔ jina su kekereke i sunsunni nu, na i ti má deke bo bu miɛ i, na i koro tara i ŋu fa kɔ i kunnu koroo­wa su, na wɔ yo i sunsunni nu ka i ja má suŋguru ni, ama, bu wo ka bu ti danfoya a, bo bu si má bu beŋgu ka bara ni biɛsɔ, nyini ti kpa a.

eng: Therefore, the one who marries the unmarried woman does right, and the one who doesnt get married will do even better.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jaari i suŋguru cee­wa ni, nyini ti kpa, ama, bo i ja má i fieo ni, i mi yo sakpa tara bo i ja i ni.

eng: A woman is obligated to stay in her marriage as long as her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free to marry whomever she wants, only it should be a believer in the Lord.
local: Nzɛn bara ni i hun wo bɛrɛ, na biɛsɔ n nya wu má, bara n la má atin bo i fite i ajaa n bu, ama, nzɛn i hun wuuri, i la atin bo i kɔ ja sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro, ama, wɔ yo daka ka i mi ti Mibiɛ n dɛɛ sɔnɔ.

eng: But in my opinion, she will be happier if she stays the way she is. And I think that I have Gods Spirit too.
local: M dɛɛ sunsunni nu, nzɛn má i wo sɔ na i ja má, má i la tere­fiɛ agaya tara bo i ja. M niɛ ka mini mmɔɔ ni m tii la Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ ni.

eng: Now concerning meat that has been sacrificed to a false god: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge makes people arrogant, but love builds people up.
local: Tɔ, fa kɔ dekem bo bu kpiɛ bu kɔm yo tiɛ fa ma amɔɛm dɛɛ jɔrɛ, ya si ka ya kɛrɛ la si. Ama, si ka nyini n sɔ ma minɛ wura bu ŋu kpiri wɔ, ama, koro yiri, i buka sɔnɔ ɔ, na i woo­wa ti kpa.

eng: If anyone thinks they know something, they dont yet know as much as they should know.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo i ŋu ka i si deke be, i mi nya la má si nahɔrɛ su kabo má i sunsun ka i si i ni.

eng: But if someone loves God, then they are known by God.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro Nyɛmɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ si yiri wɔ.

eng: So concerning the actual food involved in these sacrifices to false gods, we know that a false god isnt anything in this world, and that there is no God except for the one God.
local: Nyini ti, fa kɔ kabo minɛm di amɔɛ tiɛ diirem ni, ya si ka amɔɛ ti má pui durunya n nu, na nyɛmɛ be wo má bɛrɛ sin ya Nyɛmɛ ni ŋgumi kpein ŋu.

eng: Granted, there are so-called gods, in heaven and on the earth, as there are many gods and many lords.
local: Haari nzɛn dekem bo minɛm ŋa bu ti amɔɛm wo aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu fɛn, na nzɛn i ti nahɔrɛ ka amɔɛm ni mibiɛm dɔŋgu wo bɛrɛ mmɔɔ.

eng: However, for us believers, There is one God the Father. All things come from him, and we belong to him. And there is one Lord Jesus Christ. All things exist through him, and we live through him.
local: yɛrɛ dɔ, Nyɛmɛ kun n kpein wo bɛrɛ a, yiri la ya Si Nyɛmɛ, yiri yo yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a, ya wo bɛrɛ ma yiri wɔ. Ya kaaki la Mibiɛ kun n kpein wɔ, yiri la Yesu Kristo, Nyɛmɛ ma yiri yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a, i dɛɛ ti ye ya wo bɛrɛ a.

eng: But not everybody knows this. Some are eating this food as though it really is food sacrificed to a real idol, because they were used to idol worship until now. Their conscience is weak because it has been damaged.
local: Ama, má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la nyini si n a, nbem wo bɛrɛ bo ba su amɔɛm acɛ, nyini ti, haari ni kisa nzɛn bu diiri nyini diire n be, bu sunsun ka bu toko di amɔɛ tiɛ niɛ wɔ, na bu sunsunni suma bu nnyɔ nnyɔ ɔ, ka bu ti má casi.

eng: Food wont bring us close to God. Were not missing out if we dont eat, and we dont have any advantage if we do eat.
local: Nyɛmɛ de má ya diire dɛɛ ti, nzɛn ya diiri i, i buka má ya pui, nzɛn ya di má i gusu, i kpiɛ má ya su.

eng: But watch out or else this freedom of yours might be a problem for those who are weak.
local: Ama, am si am ŋu sa, ambɛrɛ bo am ade am tii ni, na má am yo sa be bo i ma am niwa n bo i jaa kpaasi, yiri n bo i yarada ti má bii­wa ni.

eng: Suppose someone sees you eating in an idols temple. Wont the person with a weak conscience be encouraged to eat the meat sacrificed to false gods?.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ bo i sunsunni n ti nnyɔ nnyɔ n ŋu ka wɔrɔ bo a la nyini si n sɔ wo amɔɛ awuru n nu sudi amɔɛ diire be, nyini ma i ahoresi ka i di be na i niɛ a, má sɔ? Ɛɛn, i mi dí ɔ.

eng: The weak brother or sister for whom Christ died is destroyed by your knowledge.
local: A yoori sɔ, u si n dɛɛ ti, a suma u niwa n ka ŋgbɛɛn lɛɛ, yiri n bo i yarada ti má bii­wa ni. Yiri gusu ti u niwa bo Kristo wuuri i dɛɛ ti wɔ.

eng: You sin against Christ if you sin against your brothers and sisters and hurt their weak consciences this way.
local: Nzɛn am yo sɔ, am suyo am ninbaam satiɛ wɔ lɛɛ, na am sufɔndɔ bu sunsunni bo i ti nnyɔ nnyɔ n lɛɛ, nyini n sɔ ni, am suyo Kristo gusu satiɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: This is why, if food causes the downfall of my brother or sister, I wont eat meat ever again, or else I may cause my brother or sister to fall.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn diire bo m di i, na i ma m niwa Kristo­fɔ bo i jaa kpaasi, m dí má niɛ bekun fieo, adena má m niwa n bo i jaa kpaasi.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to be sure of the fact that our ancestors were all under the cloud and they all went through the sea.
local: M ninbaam, m koro ka m teŋge am su ka saŋga bo ya nam yo suuri Moyisi su fiteeri Ijipiti asiɛn n su ni, yinyiŋgu n yo kataari bu kɛrɛ ŋu wɔ, na bu kɛrɛ ayo awura kunma nzue bambaka n nu na ba kpiɛ.

eng: All were baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea.
local: Kabo i yoori sɔ ni, i yo ti ka ba biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue, afa bu ama Moyisi wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: All ate the same spiritual food.
local: Bu kɛrɛ yo saŋga diiri diire n bo i ti má durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ diire n a.

eng: and all drank the same spiritual drink. They drank from a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.
local: na bu kɛrɛ asaŋga anu deke nuu­wa kun n bo i ti má durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ ni, dama yabuɛ n bo nzue n soŋgi fiteeri nu na bu nuuri i n na i suuri bu su ni, i fite má durunya n nu fɛn, nyini yabuɛ n la Kristo Tii­de­fɔ ni.

eng: However, God was unhappy with most of them, and they were struck down in the wilderness.
local: Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, bu dɔŋgu sa­yoo­wa asun má Nyɛmɛ nu, nyini dɛɛ ti na ba wu aka ŋgbɛɛn boro kpantain n nu.

eng: These things were examples for us, so we wont crave evil things like they did.
local: Tɔ, ahin sam kɛrɛ bo bu yo yoori ni, bu ti deke bo bu sufa kere ya a, ka má ya sufa konviki, na ya su satiɛm su, kabo bu yo yoori ni.

eng: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Havent I seen Jesus our Lord? Arent you my work in the Lord?.
local: M ti má sɔnɔ bo ma de m tii wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? M ti má Kristo jaramasa a, nzɛn sɛ? Ma ŋu má ya Mibiɛ Yesu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Kabo am ayo yarada ni, má ambɛrɛ ti m juma­yoo­wa, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti a, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: Dont worship false gods like some of them did, as it is written, The people sat down to eat and drink and they got up to play .
local: Na má am su amɔɛm kabo bu nbem yo yoori ni. Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Minɛm n yo tanaari wɔ, na ba di, na ba nu, na ba nya ayasu agɔ agɔrɛ.

eng: If Im not an apostle to others, at least I am to you! You are the seal that shows Im an apostle.
local: Haari nzɛn nyiŋgam de má m ka m ti jaramasa, ambɛrɛ si ka m ti i jaramasa a, dama kabo am ni Mibiɛ n ayo kun ni, nyini kere am ka m ti i jaramasa a.

eng: Lets not practice sexual immorality, like some of them did, and twenty-three thousand died in one day.
local: Má am ma ya yo sakaraya kabo bu nbem yo yoori, na cɛɛn kun nu na bu minɛm kɔ akpii abura­nyɔ ni akpii nsan wuuri ni.

eng: This is my defense against those who criticize me.
local: Nzɛn minɛ se m jɔrɛ, ka m ti má Kristo jaramasa, má m bisa bu ka.

eng: Lets not test Christ, like some of them did, and were killed by the snakes.
local: Má am ma ya yo Mibiɛ n na ya niɛ, kabo bu nbem yo yoori i na bu niɛ, na woom kan kuunri bu ni.

eng: Dont we have the right to eat and drink?.
local: mini ni Banabasi, ya la má atin ka ya de deke bo ya di i, ni deke bo ya nu i, Kristo jama n dɔ ɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: Lets not grumble, like some of them did, and were killed by the destroyer.
local: Má am suŋuŋuti kabo bu nbem yo ŋuŋutiiri na mɛrɛkɛ saaki­fɔ n ba kuunri bu na bu kaari ŋgbɛɛn ni.

eng: Dont we have the right to travel with a wife who believes like the rest of the apostles, the Lords brothers, and Cephas?.
local: Ya la má atin ka nzɛn ya sukɔ atin, ya koro fa ya yim bo bu ti yarada­yo­fɔm bita nu, kabo ya beŋgu Kristo jaramasa nbem yo ni, ni kabo ya Mibiɛ niɛmam, ni Kefasi bo bu kaaki fere i ka Petoro gusu yo ni, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: These things happened to them as an example and were written as a warning for us to whom the end of time has come.
local: Ahin sam kɛrɛ bo bu yo yoori ni, bu ti deke bo bu sufa kere ya wɔ, nyini ma bu kɛrɛɛri bu seeri n a, na bu fa bɔbɔ yɛrɛ n bo ya wo ahin awieeri su saŋga n nu ni.

eng: Or is it only I and Barnabas who dont have the right to not work for our living?.
local: Wara m ni Banabasi ŋgumi ye i ti ye ni ka ya yo ya saa nu juma ka, na ya nya di a?.

eng: So those who think they are standing need to watch out or else they may fall.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i sunsun ka i jina nyunyumi, i ti ye ni ka i mi si i ŋu sa, na má i kpaasi.

eng: No temptation has seized you that isnt common for people. But God is faithful. He wont allow you to be tempted beyond your abilities. Instead, with the temptation, God will also supply a way out so that you will be able to endure it.
local: Gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ be wo má bɛrɛ bo wɔ tu am, na i be nya tu má adamande. Nyɛmɛ la dabuŋu, i yáki má na gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ bo i kúnma am bá tu am, ama, haari nzɛn gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ be bá tuuri am, i kpíni atin ma am, na i má am koro mindi de i.

eng: Who joins the army and pays their own way? Who plants a vineyard and doesnt eat its fruit? Who shepherds a flock and doesnt drink its milk?.
local: Ŋma wo bɛrɛ bo i yo soja juma na i tɔ i tii su kaari a? Ai, bu tɔ bu wɔ. Ŋma wo bɛrɛ bo i ta bakam, na i di má suu­wam be a? Ai, bu di wɔ. Wara ŋma wo bɛrɛ bo i sasa neŋge­ndɔrɛm na i nu má nɔnɔ a? Ai, bu nu wɔ.

eng: So then, my dear friends, run away from the worship of false gods!.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaa koroo­wam, am ŋmati fite amɔɛ su nu.

eng: Im not saying these things just based on common sense, am I? Doesnt the Law itself say these things?.
local: M sufa má adamande dɛɛ sunsunni nu ye m sufa se sɔ n a, ama, kabo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere lɛɛ.

eng: Im talking to you like you are sensible people. Think about what Im saying.
local: M sujɔjɔ am dɔ ka am ti minɛm bo bu nyunu ateke wɔ, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii, am si deke n bo m sujɔjɔ i n bu.

eng: In Moses Law its written: You will not muzzle the ox when it is threshing . Is God worried about oxen.
local: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i fa maari Moyisi n nu ka, Nzɛn a bita toora subobo ŋgani, má a cici i nɔɔ. Tɔ, naanim ŋgumi ye Nyɛmɛ cɛcɛ wo bu nu a, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, má bɛrɛ ŋgumi.

eng: Isnt the cup of blessing that we bless a sharing in the blood of Christ? Isnt the loaf of bread that we break a sharing in the body of Christ?.
local: Nzɛn ya sudi ya Mibiɛ n diire ni, na ya faari awa n ni nzan n na ya yo kisi gɔ su, na ya kɛrɛ saŋga nuuri i, i ti Kristo mbunja wɔ lɛɛ, ye ya nu i n a. Kpɔnɔ n gusu bo ya kpiɛkpiɛ i nu na ya saŋga di i ni, i ti Kristo ŋuniɛ­mbaa n wɔ lɛɛ, ye ya di i n a.

eng: or did he say this entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake because the one who plows and the one who threshes should each do so with the hope of sharing the produce.
local: Nahɔrɛ, ya ti ka dɔdɔɔri­fɔm wɔ, yɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye bu kɛrɛɛri sɔ a, adena bo i dɔ ŋgani n ni bo i bobo i n kɛrɛ la tama ka bu nyá be na bu kpáaki nu.

eng: If I speak in tongues of human beings and of angels but I dont have love, Im a clanging gong or a clashing cymbal.
local: Nzɛn m jɔjɔ adamande aniɛ barasu barasu n kɛrɛ, na nzɛn m jɔjɔ mɛrɛkɛm aniɛ, na m koro má minɛm, m ti ka adaŋwore wɔ, wara m ti ka coco bo i bobo i beŋgu na i yo naŋmiɛ ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: Since there is one loaf of bread, we who are many are one body, because we all share the one loaf of bread.
local: Kpɔnɔ n bo ya kɛrɛ saŋga di i n ti mɔmɔ kun wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, yɛrɛ bo ya ti minɛ beberebe ni, ya ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun wɔ lɛɛ, dama ya kɛrɛ saŋga di kpɔnɔ kun n wɔ.

eng: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it so much to ask to harvest some material things from you?.
local: Yɛrɛ kaanri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni kereeri am a, kabo dɔdɔɔri­fɔ dɔ alɛ­mbaa ni, na ambɛrɛ ade i. Ye nzɛn i ti sɔ, yɛrɛ la má atin bo ya koro nya deke be am dɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: If I have the gift of prophecy and I know all the mysteries and everything else, and if I have such complete faith that I can move mountains but I dont have love, Im nothing.
local: Nzɛn m de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ, na m kan kere minɛm, na m la si kɛrɛ na m si deke n kɛrɛ bo bu wo asiri nu ni, na nzɛn m la yarada haari bo m koro se bukam ka bu yasu bu tanaa­biri, ama, na m koro má minɛm, m ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Look at the people of Israel. Dont those who eat the sacrifices share from the altar?.
local: Am niɛ kabo Yisareli­fɔm yoori ni. Minɛm bo bu di dekem bo bu fa yo yoo­wa Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n su n ni, bu ni Nyɛmɛ la wo fiɛ bo i ma bu yo anɔɔkun, fa kɔ bu nyɛmɛsu atin n su.

eng: If others have these rights over you, dont we deserve them all the more? However, we havent made use of this right, but we put up with everything so we dont put any obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
local: Nzɛn nyiŋgam la atin ka bu de neŋgem am dɔ, yɛrɛ la má atin bo ya de tara sɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ama, yɛrɛ akere má ya yiko ka ya de nyini n be sɔ, dama ya koro má ka ya kanda minɛm, na má bu de Kristo labari kpa ni.

eng: If I give away everything that I have and hand over my own body to feel good about what Ive done but I dont have love, I receive no benefit whatsoever.
local: Na nzɛn m faari m neŋgem kɛrɛ fa kpaaki nu maari yaari­fɔm, na m faari m ŋuniɛ­mbaa fa maari, na bu yara m sin, na m koro má minɛm, nyini n kɛrɛ bo m yo i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: What am I saying then? That food sacrificed to a false god is anything, or that a false god is anything?.
local: M suse má ka dekem bo bu yo tiɛ n ma bu n wo má bɛrɛ wɔ, wara amɔɛm ni bu tii wo má bɛrɛ. Ai, má sɔ.

eng: Dont you know that those who serve in the temple get to eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share part of what is sacrificed on the altar?.
local: Am si má ka, minɛm bo bu yo juma Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu ni di nyini nu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Na bo bu nati yo juma Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n su ma minɛm gusu di nyini nu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: Love is patient, love is kind, it isnt jealous, it doesnt brag, it isnt arrogant.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i la koro, i mi la sawɔrɛ wɔ, na i yo kpa, i mi si má minɛ kɔrɛ, i mi fɔ má yanfɔya, i mi koro má kpiri.

eng: No, but this kind of sacrifice is sacrificed to demons and not to God. I dont want you to be sharing in demons.
local: Ama, dekem bo amɔɛ­su­fɔm yo tiɛ ma bu ni, bu yo ma ajinim wɔ, bu yo ma má Nyɛmɛ. M koro má ka am di amɔɛ tiɛ diirem, na am ni ajinim saŋga.

eng: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who preach the gospel should get their living from the gospel.
local: Sɔ ye Micɛra n ama nɔɔ a, ka bo bu kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ n nya deke bo bu di i fite labari kpa n kan nu a.

eng: it isnt rude, it doesnt seek its own advantage, it isnt irritable, it doesnt keep a record of complaints.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i la koro, i mi la má anyumbaa wuruu­wa, i mi yo má sa bo yiri ŋgumi yo ye, i mi fa má yaa ndende, i mi fa má jɔrɛ se má i kunnu na i teŋge su.

eng: You cant drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons; you cant participate in the table of the Lord and the table of demons.
local: Nahɔrɛ, m koro ka am si ka am koro nu má ya Mibiɛ ni nzan awa n nu, na am kaaki nu ajinim dɛɛ gusu buka su, nyini koro yo má, na am kaaki koro di má ya Mibiɛ n diire ni, na am kaaki di ajinim dɛɛ buka su, nyini koro yo má.

eng: But I havent taken advantage of this. And Im not writing this so that it will be done for me. Its better for me to die than to lose my right to brag about this!.
local: Ama, haari ni bo má m la atin ka m yo sɔ ni, ma yo má nyini n be sɔ, na m sukɛrɛ má ahin kadasi n ka m de deke be am dɔ ɔ, dama nzɛn m wuuri, má i ti ye tara bo be se ka m fɔ yanfɔya ŋgbɛɛn ni.

eng: it isnt happy with injustice, but it is happy with the truth.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i la koro, i mi ahore ti má jɔɔ­wa fa kɔ satiɛ su, ama, i mi n ahore ti jɔɔ­wa fa kɔ deke bo i ti nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: Follow my example, just like I follow Christs.
local: Am sɔn m su, na am ma am woo­wa ti kabo m woo­wa ti barasu n na m sɔn Kristo su ni.

eng: Or should we make the Lord jealous? We arent stronger than he is, are we?.
local: wara ya koro ka Mibiɛ n si ya kɔrɛ na i fa yaa ya ŋu wɔ. Ya la faŋga tara i wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, ya lá má faŋga.

eng: If I preach the gospel, I have no reason to brag, since Im obligated to do it. Im in trouble if I dont preach the gospel.
local: Nzɛn m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛ, m la má atin bo m fɔ, dama i ti m juma bo Nyɛmɛ ama m ka m yo i wɔ. Nzɛn m keteeri ka m yo má i, m nyá sui­cin.

eng: Love puts up with all things, trusts in all things, hopes for all things, endures all things.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i la koro mindi de deke kɛrɛ wɔ, na i la yarada fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ nu, na i la ahoresi fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ nu, na i la mindi saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: I praise you because you remember all my instructions, and you hold on to the traditions exactly as I handed them on to you.
local: Tɔ, m kansi am wɔ, dama saŋga kɛrɛ am nati teŋge m su wɔ, na am bita keree­wam bo m fa kereeri am n ni nahɔrɛ su ni.

eng: Everything is permitted, but everything isnt beneficial. Everything is permitted, but everything doesnt build others up.
local: Am nbem se ka, Ya la atin bo ya koro yo deke kɛrɛ bo ya koro ka ya yo i ni. I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ama, má deke kɛrɛ la kpɛrɛ a. Am ŋa, Ya la atin bo ya koro yo deke kɛrɛ bo ya koro ka ya yo i ni. I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ama, má deke kɛrɛ buka ya a.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I dont want you to be ignorant about spiritual gifts.
local: Tɔ, m ninbaam, m koro ka am si sa bo i wo bɛrɛ fa kɔ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim su.

eng: If I do this voluntarily, I get rewarded for it. But if Im forced to do it, then Ive been charged with a responsibility.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ m koroya­nu, m nya ŋkatɔ, ama, i ti má m koroya­nu, dama Nyɛmɛ afa nyini juma n ama m ka m yo i a.

eng: Love never fails. As for prophecies, they will be brought to an end. As for tongues, they will stop. As for knowledge, it will be brought to an end.
local: Koro la má awieeri. Ama, aceerim bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n fa ma minɛm na bu koro kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni, i la saŋga bo i ba sin wɔ, minɛm bo bu jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum ni, bu bá mɔ bu nɔɔ ɔ, na bo bu la si ni, i la saŋga bo i bá sin wɔ.

eng: Now I want you to know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.
local: Ama, deke bo m koro ka am si i la ka, biɛsɔ kɛrɛ tii­fɔ la Kristo wɔ, na bara kɛrɛ tii­fɔ la i hun, na Kristo gusu tii­fɔ la Nyɛmɛ.

eng: No one should look out for their own advantage, but they should look out for each other.
local: Má am niɛ kabo am yo na ambɛrɛ ŋgumi yo ye, ama, am niɛ kabo am yo na am beŋgum gusu yo ye.

eng: You know that when you were Gentiles you were often misled by false gods that cant even speak.
local: Am si ka saŋga bo am yo su má Nyɛmɛ ni, bu yo taari am nyi wɔ, na am susu amɔɛm bo bu koro jɔjɔ má ni.

eng: What reward do I get? That when I preach, I offer the good news free of charge. Thats why I dont use the rights to which Im entitled through the gospel.
local: M dɛɛ ŋkatɔ la bɔnɔ? Yiri la ka m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ fa kere minɛm ŋgbɛɛn, m la atin bo m sere deke be bu dɔ, ama, m yo má i.

eng: We know in part and we prophesy in part.
local: Kisa ya si má deke n kɛrɛ wie má, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo ya kan kere minɛm ni, ya si má i kɛrɛ wie má.

eng: Every man who prays or prophesies with his head covered shames his head.
local: Nyini ti, biɛsɔ kɛrɛ bo i susere Nyɛmɛ, wara i sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm, na i kata i tii su, i mi awura i tii­fɔ n nyinsɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Eat everything that is sold in the marketplace, without asking questions about it because of your conscience.
local: Niɛ kɛrɛ bo naŋkɔsim tɔ i ni, am di i, má am yo sika.

eng: So I want to make it clear to you that no one says, Jesus is cursed! when speaking by Gods Spirit, and no one can say, Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
local: Nyini ti, m koro ka am si ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo i dɔ, i mi koro se má ka, Yesu aka ŋgbɛɛn, na be gusu koro se má ka Yesu ti m Mibiɛ, má ka Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo i mi dɔ.

eng: Although Im free from all people, I make myself a slave to all people, to recruit more of them.
local: Haari ni bo ma de m tii na m ti má be dɛɛ akɔɔ ni, m fa m ŋu yo akɔɔ ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ, adena m koro nya minɛ dɔŋgu na bu su Kristo su.

eng: but when the perfect comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.
local: Ama, nzɛn Kristo ba baari, nyini saŋga deke n bo ya si má i kɛrɛ ni aba i awieeri lɛɛ.

eng: Every woman who prays or prophesies with her head uncovered disgraces her head. It is the same thing as having her head shaved.
local: Na bara kɛrɛ bo i susere Nyɛmɛ, wara i sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm, na wɔ kata má i tii su, nyini bara n suwura i tii­fɔ nyinsɛ wɔ lɛɛ. I ti ka wɔ yi i tii wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: The earth and all that is in it belong to the Lord .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Durunya n ni deke n kɛrɛ bo i wo nu n ti Micɛra n dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: There are different spiritual gifts but the same Spirit.
local: Aceerim barasu barasu wo bɛrɛ, ama, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ti kun wɔ.

eng: I act like a Jew to the Jews, so I can recruit Jews. I act like Im under the Law to those under the Law, so I can recruit those who are under the Law .
local: Nzɛn m kɔɔri Yuda­fɔm dɔ, m yo kabo bu yo n wɔ, adena m koro nya bu nbem na bu su Kristo su. Nzɛn m kɔɔri minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ bu su n dɔ, haari ni bo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ má mini ni m tii su kun ni, m yo kabo bu yo n wɔ, adena m koro nya minɛm bo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ bu su ni nbem na bu su Kristo su.

eng: When I was a child, I used to speak like a child, reason like a child, think like a child. But now that I have become a man, Ive put an end to childish things.
local: Saŋga bo m yo ti baatuma ni, m yo jɔjɔ baatumaya jɔrɛ wɔ, na m sunsun kabo baatuma sunsun ni, ama, m nyunu ba tiiri ni, na ma yaki baatumaya jɔrɛm n kɛrɛ.

eng: If a woman doesnt cover her head, then she should have her hair cut off. If it is disgraceful for a woman to have short hair or to be shaved, then she should keep her head covered.
local: Nzɛn bara kete ka i kata má i tii su, nyini yiri, i ti ye ni ka i kpiɛkpiɛ i tii ni, ama, nzɛn i ti i nyinsɛ ka i kpiɛkpiɛ i tii, wara i yi i, nyini yiri, i ti ye ni ka i fa deke kata su.

eng: If an unbeliever invites you to eat with them and you want to go, eat whatever is served, without asking questions because of your conscience.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ bo i yo má yarada baandiiri am ka am ba di diire, na am soro su, am di deke kɛrɛ bo bu fa ma am ni, má am kɛrɛ nu, sika dɛɛ ti.

eng: and there are different ministries and the same Lord.
local: Amusuim bo ya yo buka minɛm wo bɛrɛ barasu barasu, ama, ya su Mibiɛ kun wɔ.

eng: I act like Im outside the Law to those who are outside the Law, so I can recruit those outside the Law .
local: Nzɛn m kaaki kɔɔri minɛm bo bu la má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɔ, m yo kabo bu yo n wɔ, i ti má ka m su má kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere n su wɔ, ama, Kristo dɛɛ nɔaniɛ ni niɛ m su a, adena m koro nya minɛm bo bu la má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n na bu nbem su Kristo su.

eng: Now we see a reflection in a mirror; then we will see face-to-face. Now I know partially, but then I will know completely in the same way that I have been completely known.
local: Kisa dekem bo ya ŋu i ni, i ti ka ya niɛ ŋu i niɛɛnu nu wɔ, ama, ya bá ŋu i fofoe nu, kabo ya ŋu ya ŋu n wɔ. Kisa m si má deke n kɛrɛ, ama, saŋga suba bo m bá si deke n kɛrɛ, kabo Nyɛmɛ si m nahɔrɛ su ni.

eng: A man shouldnt have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God; but the woman is mans glory.
local: Nyini ti, i ti má ye ni ka biɛsɔ susere Nyɛmɛ na i fa deke be kata i tii su, dama i ferema ni Nyɛmɛ wɔ, i de Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa a, ama, bara n yiri, i de biɛsɔ n jirima woo­wa a.

eng: But if someone says to you, This meat was sacrificed in a temple, then dont eat it for the sake of the one who told you and for the sake of conscience.
local: Ama, nzɛn be se am ka, ahin niɛ n ti tiɛ niɛ wɔ, nyini yiri, má am di, yiri n bo wɔ se sɔ ni, i sunsunni suma i nnyɔ nnyɔ n dɛɛ ti, má am di.

eng: and there are different activities but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la atin barasu barasu kabo i mi koro fa yo i juma, ama, Nyɛmɛ kun ni yo nyini n kɛrɛ a.

eng: I act weak to the weak, so I can recruit the weak. I have become all things to all people, so I could save some by all possible means.
local: Nzɛn m kɔɔri minɛm bo bu yarada la má faŋga n dɔ, m yo m ŋu ka mini mmɔɔ la má faŋga wɔ, adena m koro nya bu nbem na bu su Kristo su. Deke kɛrɛ bo m kɔ, m yo m ŋu kabo nyini minɛm yo barasu n wɔ, adena m koro ma bu nbem nya tii­de.

eng: Now faith, hope, and love remain—these three things—and the greatest of these is love.
local: Tɔ, kisa ahin deke nsanm ni wo bɛrɛ a, bɛrɛ la yarada, ni tama, ni koro, ama, bo i tara bu kɛrɛ n la koro wɔ.

eng: Man didnt have his origin from woman, but woman from man.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ afa má bara ye i fa yoori biɛsɔ a, ama, i du mɔ yiiri biɛsɔ ɔ ka, na wɔ nya ayi bara afite i ŋuniɛ nu.

eng: Now when I say conscience I dont mean yours but the other persons. Why should my freedom be judged by someone elses conscience?.
local: I mi n dɛɛ sunsunni nnyɔ nnyɔ n dɛɛ ti, má a di i, má u dɛɛ ti a. M dɛɛ la m tii, ye nzu ti ye m ma be dɛɛ sunsunni nnyɔ nnyɔ n di m jɔrɛ, na má m yo kabo m koro a.

eng: A demonstration of the Spirit is given to each person for the common good.
local: Nyɛmɛ ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim n bo i kere ka Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo i mi dɔ a, na i fa buka Kristo jama n kɛrɛ.

eng: All the things I do are for the sake of the gospel, so I can be a partner with it.
local: M yo nyini n kɛrɛ sɔ ɔ, labari kpa n dɛɛ ti, adena m ni nyiŋgam kɛrɛ saŋga di i kpɛrɛ.

eng: and man wasnt created for the sake of the woman, but the woman for the sake of the man.
local: Má bara dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ yoori biɛsɔ a, ama, biɛsɔ dɛɛ ti, ye i yoori bara a.

eng: If I participate with gratitude, why should I be blamed for food I thank God for?.
local: Nzɛn m da Nyɛmɛ asi gɔ diire n su na m di i, be yo sɛ koro jaraki m diire n dɛɛ ti a? Dama ma du mɔ ada Nyɛmɛ asi agɔ diire n su.

eng: Dont you know that all the runners in the stadium run, but only one gets the prize? So run to win.
local: Am si ka nzɛn minɛm awura akuruwa ka bu ŋmati, na bu niɛ bo i kunma i beŋgum, bu kɛrɛ ŋmati wɔ, ama, bu nu sɔnɔ kun bo i kɔ kunmaari bu kɛrɛ ni, yiri nya aceeri n a. Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ miɛ i nyi, na i wo woo­wa kpa fa kɔ Kristo atin n su, kabo ŋmatiiri­fɔ n bo i kunma i beŋgu n ti ni, adena i nya aceeri ni.

eng: A word of wisdom is given by the Spirit to one person, a word of knowledge to another according to the same Spirit.
local: Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ma sɔnɔ be jɔjɔ alecira jɔrɛ a, nyini Awiɛwiɛ ni ma be gusu jɔjɔ si jɔrɛ a.

eng: Because of this a woman should have authority over her head, because of the angels.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo daka ka bara cin acici, na i kere ka be la i yiko, mɛrɛkɛm dɛɛ ti.

eng: So, whether you eat or drink or whatever you do, you should do it all for Gods glory.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn am diiri diire, wara am nuuri deke nuu­wa, wara deke kɛrɛ bo am yoori i, am yo i na i wura Nyɛmɛ kpiri.

eng: Everyone who competes practices self-discipline in everything. The runners do this to get a crown of leaves that shrivel up and die, but we do it to receive a crown that never dies.
local: Minɛm bo bu ŋmati ŋmatiiri ni, wara bo bu bo baalɔ ni, bu kɛrɛ boro bu nyi wɔ ni anyum­boro fa kɔ nyini kɔkɔrɔ n su. Bu yo sɔ ɔ na bu nya aceeri n bo i cɛ má ni, ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya nyá aceeri n bo i wie má na i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n wɔ.

eng: faith to still another by the same Spirit, gifts of healing to another in the one Spirit.
local: Nyini Awiɛwiɛ ni ma be yarada ye i yo yiko juma n a, nyini Awiɛwiɛ kun ni ma be yiko ye i mi n koro tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu nya laifiɛ a.

eng: However, woman isnt independent from man, and man isnt independent from woman in the Lord.
local: Ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ ni, fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su, bara wo má i ŋgumi, na biɛsɔ gusu wo i ŋgumi.

eng: Dont offend either Jews or Greeks, or Gods church.
local: Má am yo sa bo i ma minɛm bo bu jaa na bu kpaasi, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, haari ni Kristo jama ni.

eng: So now this is how I run—not without a clear goal in sight. I fight like a boxer in the ring, not like someone who is shadowboxing.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, mini, m ŋmati má ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn ka sɔnɔ bo i si má bo i wo nu ni. M ti má sɔnɔ bo m bo acɔrɛ na m bo fun.

eng: performance of miracles to another, prophecy to another, the ability to tell spirits apart to another, different kinds of tongues to another, and the interpretation of the tongues to another.
local: Nyini Awiɛwiɛ ni ma be yiko ye i koro yo alekutura sam a, yiri ma be yiko ye i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm a, yiri ma be yiko ye i koro si jɔrɛ n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ a, yiri ma be yiko ye i koro jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu a, yiri gusu ma be yiko ye i koro kere aniɛ barasu barasum bu a.

eng: As woman came from man so also man comes from woman. But everything comes from God.
local: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ yo yiiri bara fiteeri biɛsɔ ŋuniɛ nu ni, sɔ ye bara gusu wu biɛsɔ a, ama, deke n kɛrɛ fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: This is the same thing that I do. I please everyone in everything I do. I dont look out for my own advantage, but I look out for many people so that they can be saved.
local: Sɔ ye mini mmɔɔ ni m tii boro m nyi a, na m yo sa bo i sun sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nu, m suniɛ má kabo m yo na mini ŋgumi yo ye wɔ, ama, m yo deke bo i buka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ, adena bu nya tii­de.

eng: Rather, Im landing punches on my own body and subduing it like a slave. I do this to be sure that I myself wont be disqualified after preaching to others.
local: Ama, m kere m ŋuniɛ n wahara a, na i yo kabo m koro ni, adena má m yo sɔnɔ bo m kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm, na mini mmɔɔ fun aceeri ni.

eng: All these things are produced by the one and same Spirit who gives what he wants to each person.
local: Nyini Awiɛwiɛ kun ni yo nyinim kɛrɛ a, yiri ce sɔnɔ kɛrɛ aceeri barasu barasu n kabo i koro a.

eng: Judge for yourselves: Is it appropriate for a woman to pray to God with her head uncovered?.
local: Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii, am sunsun na am niɛ, i ti kpa ka bara sere Nyɛmɛ na má i cin acici?.

eng: Doesnt nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a disgrace to him.
local: Am niɛ kabo adamande woo­wa ti a, i kere ka nzɛn biɛsɔ yaki i tii­ŋmiɛ na i yo tintiin jɔnjɔndi, minɛm bu má i.

eng: Christ is just like the human body—a body is a unit and has many parts; and all the parts of the body are one body, even though there are many.
local: Aŋuniɛ­mbaa n la bue barasu barasu beberebe wɔ, haari ni bo i la bue barasu barasu ni, i ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun wɔ, Kristo gusu ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, yɛrɛ ti i bue barasu barasu n a.

eng: We were all baptized by one Spirit into one body, whether Jew or Greek, or slave or free, and we all were given one Spirit to drink.
local: Awiɛwiɛ kun n ye ba fa abiɛ ya kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue a, ye ya kɛrɛ ayo aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun, bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm, ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, bo bu ti akɔɔm, ni bo bu ti má akɔɔm, ya kɛrɛ ade nyini Awiɛwiɛ kun ni ya ahore su a.

eng: Certainly the body isnt one part but many.
local: Tɔ, aŋuniɛ­mbaa n la bue barasu barasu beberebe wɔ, bu ti má kun.

eng: but if a woman has long hair, it is her glory? This is because her long hair is given to her for a covering.
local: Ama, nzɛn bara la tii­ŋmiɛ tintiin ye i la jirima a, dama Nyɛmɛ ama i tii­ŋmiɛ n ka i fa kata i tii su wɔ.

eng: But if someone wants to argue about this, we dont have such a custom, nor do Gods churches.
local: Nzɛn be wo am nu na i yo i ŋu ka i se akuruwa fa kɔ kabo m suse ni, m koro ka m se am ka, yɛrɛ, ya su má nyini yoo­wa n su, haari ni deke kɛrɛ bo Kristo jamam tiɛn bu nɔɔ ni, be yo má sɔ.

eng: If the foot says, Im not part of the body because Im not a hand, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Nzɛn má ajaari n seeri ka, M ti má asaa, nyini ti, m ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n be fieo, nyini kere má ka i ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be kun wɔ.
