eng: If the ear says, Im not part of the body because Im not an eye, does that mean its not part of the body?.
local: Na nzɛn má asui n gusu seeri ka, M ti má anyumbaa, nyini ti, m ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n be fieo, nyini kere má ka i ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be kun wɔ.

eng: Now I dont praise you as I give the following instruction because when you meet together, it does more harm than good.
local: Tɔ, ahin keree­wam bo m koro ka m fa kere am ni, m koro kansi má am, dama saŋga bo am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ, sam bo am yo bu ni, bu buka má am, bu ma am kɔ am sin wɔ.

eng: If the whole body were an eye, what would happen to the hearing? And if the whole body were an ear, what would happen to the sense of smell?.
local: Nzɛn má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue n kɛrɛ tiiri anyumbaa, má bu yo sɛ koro ti deke ŋga a? Ye nzɛn má bu kɛrɛ tiiri asui, má bu yo sɛ koro de nvan a?.

eng: Pursue love, and use your ambition to try to get spiritual gifts but especially so that you might prophesy.
local: Tɔ, am bita koro atin na am koro am beŋgu ni anyum­boro, na am kpini Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim busu, maala bo i ma am koro kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni.

eng: First of all, when you meet together as a church, I hear that there are divisions among you, and I partly believe it.
local: Ciciri su, ma ti ka saŋga bo am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ na am su Nyɛmɛ ni, am la kpakpaaki­nu wɔ. Ma yo yarada ka, beni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: But as it is, God has placed each one of the parts in the body just like he wanted.
local: Ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ koro n ye wɔ fa ama aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue barasu n kɛrɛ a, ye wɔ yo i aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun a.

eng: This is because those who speak in a tongue dont speak to people but to God; no one understands it—they speak mysteries by the Spirit.
local: Dama bo i jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu n jɔjɔ má adamandem dɔ, ama, i jɔjɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ a. Nahɔrɛ, be koro ti má kabo i sujɔjɔ n bu, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko n nu ye i jɔjɔ asiri nu jɔrɛm a.

eng: Its necessary that there are groups among you, to make it clear who is genuine.
local: Nahɔrɛ, wɔ yo daka ka am la kpakpaaki­nu am nu, adena am si bo bu ti yarada­yo­fɔm nahɔrɛ su ni.

eng: If all were one and the same body part, what would happen to the body?.
local: Nzɛn má i kɛrɛ ti bue barasu kun, má i ti má aŋuniɛ­mbaa lɛɛ.

eng: Those who prophesy speak to people, building them up, and giving them encouragement and comfort.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ bo i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni, i jɔjɔ ma minɛm wɔ, na i buka bu na bu nya faŋga buka su, na i ma bu ahoresi, na i kaaki ma bu ahoreda.

eng: So when you get together in one place, it isnt to eat the Lords meal.
local: Am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ ni, má ya Mibiɛ n diire n ye am tiɛn am nɔɔ di i n a.

eng: But as it is, there are many parts but one body.
local: Kabo i ti ni, aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue barasu n sunnu, ama, i ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa kun wɔ.

eng: People who speak in a tongue build up themselves; those who prophesy build up the church.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu n buka má be, i buka yiri ni i tii ŋgumi wɔ. Ama, bo i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni, yiri buka Kristo jama n wɔ, na i nya faŋga buka su.

eng: Each of you goes ahead and eats a private meal. One person goes hungry while another is drunk.
local: Dama saŋga bo am ba sudi ni, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ di i tiɛŋgu diire wɔ, be ŋminda má i beŋgu, na ahɔɛ suti nbem, na nbem nu nzan subo.

eng: So the eye cant say to the hand, I dont need you, or in turn, the head cant say to the feet, I dont need you.
local: Nyini ti, anyumbaa n koro se má asaa n ka, Wɔrɔ, a kun má m kpɛrɛ be, m koro má u. Wara atii n koro se má ajaari n ka, Wɔrɔ, a kun má m kpɛrɛ be, m koro má u.

eng: I wish that all of you spoke in tongues, but Id rather you could prophesy. Those who prophesy are more important than those who speak in tongues, unless they are able to interpret them so that the church might be built up.
local: Tɔ, nzɛn má am kɛrɛ koro jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu, má i yo m fiɛ. Ama, bo i yo m fiɛ kɔ tara i kɛrɛ n la ka, am koro kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm. Sɔnɔ bo i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm n tara bo i jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum ni, má ka be akoro akere nyini aniɛ barasu barasum bu, na i buka Kristo jama n na i nya faŋga.

eng: Dont you have houses to eat and drink in? Or do you look down on Gods churches and humiliate those who have nothing? What can I say to you? Will I praise you? No, I dont praise you in this.
local: Am la má am tiɛŋgu awurum wɔ, bo am koro di na am nu wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Wara am bu Kristo jama ni ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, haari na am suwura bo bu la má pui nyinsɛ? Am ŋa m se am sɛ wɔ? Am ŋa m kansi am wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, m koro kansi má am ni nyini yiri.

eng: Instead, the parts of the body that people think are the weakest are the most necessary.
local: Ama, haari aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue barasum n bo ya ŋa bu la má faŋga ni mmɔɔ kun kpɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: After all, brothers and sisters, if I come to you speaking in tongues, how will I help you unless I speak to you with a revelation, some knowledge, a prophecy, or a teaching?.
local: M ninbaam, nzɛn kisa na m baari am dɔ, na m jɔjɔɔri aniɛ barasu barasum, nzu kpɛrɛ ye am nyá i a? Ai, am nyá má kpɛrɛ be. Má ka ma kan sa bo Nyɛmɛ ayi i akere m n ama am, wara m kan si jɔrɛ be kere am, wara m kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere am, wara m kan keree­wa bo i fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ kere am.

eng: I received a tradition from the Lord, which I also handed on to you: on the night on which he was betrayed, the Lord Jesus took bread.
local: Dama deke n bo mini ni m tii deeri i fiteeri Mibiɛ n dɔ ni, nyini ye ma fa akere am n a. Kɔŋguɛ n bo bu tɔɔri ya Mibiɛ Yesu maari i kpɔ­fɔm ni, na wɔ fa kpɔnɔ.

eng: The parts of the body that we think are less honorable are the ones we honor the most. The private parts of our body that arent presentable are the ones that are given the most dignity.
local: Na aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue barasu n bo i la má jirima ni, yiri ye ya niɛ i dika nyunyumi a. Na aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue barasu n bo ya ŋu i ka i la ya nyinsɛ ni, ya yo yiri dɔŋgu a, na má ya di nyinsɛ.

eng: Likewise, things that arent alive like a harp or a lyre can make a sound, but if there arent different notes in the sounds they make, how will the tune from the harp or the lyre be recognized?.
local: Sɔ ye i ti a, nzɛn be fitaari tere, wara be bo biɛku, na wɔ tɔ má su, bu yo sɛ koro di jue soro su a?.

eng: After giving thanks, he broke it and said, This is my body, which is for you; do this to remember me.
local: na wɔ da Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ bubu i nu, na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin kpɔnɔ n ti m ŋuniɛ­mbaa lɛɛ ye m sufa ma am n a. Am yo ahin sɔ adena am fa teŋge m su.

eng: The parts of our body that are presentable dont need this. But God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the part with less honor.
local: Na aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue n bo bu wo fofoe nu ni, ya niɛ má bu dika nyunyumi. Ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ yoori aŋuniɛ­mbaa n ni, deke n bo yɛrɛ fa má i yo má pui ni, yiri ye wɔ wura i jirima a.

eng: And if a trumpet call is unrecognizable, then who will prepare for battle?.
local: Bekun nzɛn be kaanri luɛ awiɛ, na wɔ sun má kabo luɛ awiɛ sun ni, bu yo sɛ koro cici luɛ a?.

eng: He did the same thing with the cup, after they had eaten, saying, This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Every time you drink it, do this to remember me.
local: Bu diiri diire n wieeri ni, na wɔ kaaki afa awa n ni nzan ni, na wɔ se bu ka, Ahin nzan n ti sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ sufa m mbunja n sudi i di wɔ. Saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ba sunu nyini nzan ni, am teŋge m su.

eng: so that there wont be division in the body and so the parts might have mutual concern for each other.
local: adena má i la kpakpaaki­nu, ama, na bue barasu n kɛrɛ cɛcɛ wo i beŋgu nu.

eng: Its the same way with you: If you dont use language that is easy to understand when you speak in a tongue, then how will anyone understand what is said?.
local: I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ dɔ, nzɛn am jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu, na i la má bu, minɛ yo sɛ koro si kabo am suse n a? I ti ka am sujɔjɔ gɔ nzue awa sin wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Every time you eat this bread and drink this cup, you broadcast the death of the Lord until he comes.
local: Nyini la ka, saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ba sudi ahin kpɔnɔ n na am sunu ahin nzan ni, am subo Mibiɛ n yiwee ni ndoori wɔ lɛɛ, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo i bá kaaki ba bekun ni.

eng: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it; if one part gets the glory, all the parts celebrate with it.
local: Nzɛn aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue kun diiri yaari, aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue n kɛrɛ sudi yaari n a, ye nzɛn aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bue be sunya kansi, aŋuniɛ­mbaa bue n kɛrɛ ahore ti jɔɔ­wa wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: It will be as if you are speaking into the air! There are probably many language families in the world, and none of them are without meaning.
local: Daanfiɛ aniɛ barasu barasum sunnu durunya n nu, i gusu be wo má bɛrɛ bo i la má bu, i kɛrɛ la bu.

eng: This is why those who eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord inappropriately will be guilty of the Lords body and blood.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri ya Mibiɛ n kpɔnɔ ni, wara i nuuri i nzan ni, na i bu má i, Nyɛmɛ dí i mi jɔrɛ jaraki i, dama wɔ fa má ya Mibiɛ ŋuniɛ n ni i mbunja n ayo má juma.

eng: You are the body of Christ and parts of each other.
local: Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ ti Kristo aŋuniɛ­mbaa n wɔ, na am kɛrɛ kun kun n ti i bue barasu be wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: So if I dont know the meaning of the language, then I will be like a foreigner to those who speak it, and they will be like foreigners to me.
local: Tɔ, nyini ti, nzɛn be jɔjɔ aniɛ be na m ti má i, m ti daanfiɛ barasu be wɔ lɛɛ i mi dɔ, ye i gusu ti daanfiɛ barasu be wɔ lɛɛ m dɔ.

eng: Each individual should test himself or herself, and eat from the bread and drink from the cup in that way.
local: Wɔ yo daka ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ niɛ i ahore­mbaa su ka, na i nya di ahin kpɔnɔ ni, na i nya nu nzan ni.

eng: In the church, God has appointed first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, the ability to help others, leadership skills, different kinds of tongues.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ ayi i minɛ nbem Kristo jamam n nu, na wɔ ma bu kɛrɛ ni bu juma. I du mɔ yiiri jaramasam wɔ, nnyɔ su, bo bu kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni, nsan su la kere­fɔm, bo bu buka su la bo bu yo alekutura sam ni, ni bo bu tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu nya laifiɛ ni, ni bo bu buka minɛm ni, ni Nyɛmɛ minɛ mɔ­fɔm bo bu kere nyaari ni, ni bo bu jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu ni.

eng: The same holds true for you: since you are ambitious for spiritual gifts, use your ambition to try to work toward being the best at building up the church.
local: Kabo am nyunu aboro ka am nyá Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim ni, am boro am nyi ni aceerim bo bu koro buka Kristo jama n na i nya faŋga buka su.

eng: Those who eat and drink without correctly understanding the body are eating and drinking their own judgment.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri kpɔnɔ n na i nuuri nzan n na wɔ si má ka ya Mibiɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa n lɛɛ, Nyɛmɛ bá di i mi jɔrɛ jaraki i.

eng: All arent apostles, are they? All arent prophets, are they? All arent teachers, are they? All dont perform miracles, do they?.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti jaramasa a? Ai. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kan kere minɛm a? Wara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro kere a? Ai. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la yiko koro yo alekutura sam a? Ai, i ti má sɔ.

eng: Therefore, those who speak in a tongue should pray to be able to interpret.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum, i ti ye ni ka i sere Nyɛmɛ na i ma i aceeri n bo i koro yi i bu kere minɛm ni.

eng: Because of this, many of you are weak and sick, and quite a few have died.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye am nu, minɛ dɔŋgu ayɔrɔki ada tukpaki, haari nbem awu n a.

eng: All dont have gifts of healing, do they? All dont speak in different tongues, do they? All dont interpret, do they?.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la yiko koro tuntun tukpaki­fɔm na bu nya laifiɛ a? Ai. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasu a? Ai. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ koro yi aniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa barasu barasu n bu a? Ai.

eng: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays but my mind isnt productive.
local: Dama saŋga kɛrɛ bo m sere Nyɛmɛ na m sere aniɛ barasu barasu be nu, m awiɛwiɛ susere Nyɛmɛ a, ama, m ti má i bu m sunsunni nu.

eng: But if we had judged ourselves, we wouldnt be judged.
local: Nzɛn yɛrɛ ni ya tii du mɔ fiiti niɛɛri kabo ya woo­wa ti, Nyɛmɛ di má ya jɔrɛ cin má ya sui.

eng: Use your ambition to try to get the greater gifts. And Im going to show you an even better way.
local: Ama, am kɛrɛ boro am nyi na am nya aceerim n bo bu la má aŋusu ni. Kisa, m koro ka m kere am atin n bo i ti kpa tara i kɛrɛ ni.

eng: What should I do? Ill pray in the Spirit, but Ill pray with my mind too; Ill sing a psalm in the Spirit, but Ill sing the psalm with my mind too.
local: Nyini ti, deke bo i ti ye ni ka m yo la ka, m sere Nyɛmɛ fite m awiɛwiɛ nu wɔ, na m kaaki sere i fite m sunsunni nu, na m di jue fite m awiɛwiɛ nu kansi Nyɛmɛ, na m kaaki di jue fite m sunsunni nu kansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: However, we are disciplined by the Lord when we are judged so that we wont be judged and condemned along with the whole world.
local: Ama, nzɛn Mibiɛ n fiiti ya na i cin ya sui, i yo sɔ ɔ adena i di má ya jɔrɛ na i yo ya ŋgbɛɛn, cɛɛn n bo i bá di durunya n nu­fɔm jɔrɛ ni.

eng: After all, if you praise God in the Spirit, how will the people who arent trained in that language say Amen! to your thanksgiving, when they dont know what you are saying?.
local: Nzɛn a sukansi Nyɛmɛ na i fite u awiɛwiɛ nu, na be wo u bi bo i nya si má Nyɛmɛ atin ni, i mi yo sɛ koro ti u nyɛmɛsere n bu a, na i soro su se ka, Ami a?.

eng: For these reasons, my brothers and sisters, when you get together to eat, wait for each other.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaam, saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ, na am di, am kɛrɛ ŋminda am beŋgu, na am saŋga di.

eng: You may offer a beautiful prayer of thanksgiving, but the other person is not being built up.
local: Haari nzɛn u nyɛmɛsere ti kpa, i buka má yiri n bo i ti má i bu ni.

eng: If some of you are hungry, they should eat at home so that getting together doesnt lead to judgment. I will give directions about the other things when I come.
local: Nzɛn ahɔɛ ati be, i ti ye ni ka i mi di diire awuru ka, na i nya ba, adena nzɛn am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ, na má Nyɛmɛ di am jɔrɛ cin am sui. Jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo wɔ ka ni, nzɛn m ba baari, m yó i dɔŋgu.

eng: I thank God that I speak in tongues more than all of you.
local: M da Nyɛmɛ asi ka m jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum tara am kɛrɛ.

eng: But in the church Id rather speak five words in my right mind than speak thousands of words in a tongue so that I can teach others.
local: Ama, nzɛn m ni Kristo jama n aba atiɛn ya nɔɔ susu Nyɛmɛ, na m jɔjɔɔri jɔrɛ kaan bo i fite m sunsunni nu, na bu tiiri i bu, i tara bo m jɔjɔ jɔrɛ beberebe aniɛ barasu nu ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont be like children in the way you think. Well, be babies when it comes to evil, but be adults in your thinking.
local: M ninbaam, má am sunsun kabo nbaatumam sunsun ni, ama, fa kɔ satiɛ­yo dika, am yo kabo nbaatumam yo ni, na am ma am sunsunni ti kabo anyunuti­fɔm dɛɛ ti ni.

eng: In the Law it is written: I will speak to this people with foreign languages and foreigners lips, but they will not even listen to me this way , says the Lord.
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu ka, Micɛra n ŋa, M fá minɛm bo bu jɔjɔ daanfiɛ aniɛ barasu barasum na m má bu jɔjɔ m tiɛŋgu minɛm dɔ. Ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ, bu dé má m nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: So then, tongues are a sign for those who dont believe, not for those who believe. But prophecy is a sign for believers, not for those who dont believe.
local: Nyini ti, aniɛ barasu barasum jɔjɔɔ­wa n kere minɛm bo ba nya yo má Kristo yarada ni nyaari a, má bo ba yo yarada ni. Na bo bu kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni, bu kere bo ba yo yarada ni nyaari a, má bo bu yo má yarada ni.

eng: So suppose that the whole church is meeting and everyone is speaking in tongues. If people come in who are outsiders or unbelievers, wont they say that you are out of your minds?.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn ambɛrɛ Kristo jama n atiɛn am nɔɔ, na am kɛrɛ sujɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum, na bo bu nya si má Nyɛmɛ atin ni, wara bo bu nya yo má yarada wuraari am nu, bu sé ka am nyunu akan am wɔ, má sɔ?.

eng: But if everyone is prophesying when an unbeliever or outsider comes in, they are tested by all and called to account by all.
local: Ama, nzɛn am atiɛn am nɔɔ, na am kɛrɛ sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na bo i nya si má Nyɛmɛ atin ni, wara, bo i ti má yarada­yo­fɔ aba awura am nu, na i ti kabo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sujɔjɔ ni, i mi n sunsunni nu kere i ka i ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n di i jɔrɛ jaraki i.

eng: The secrets of their hearts are brought to light. When that happens, they will fall on their faces and worship God, proclaiming out loud that truly God is among you!.
local: Nyini ma i mi n kan asiri jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo i ahore su n fite fofoe nu a, na i kutu i jaa su, na i kansi Nyɛmɛ, na i se ka, Nyɛmɛ wo am dɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: What is the outcome of this, brothers and sisters? When you meet together, each one has a psalm, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, or an interpretation. All these things must be done to build up the church.
local: Tɔ, m ninbaam, nzu deke ye i ti ye ni ka am yo i a? Deke bo m koro ka m kan kere am la ka, nzɛn am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ, na be la jue bo i di, wara be la keree­wa be bo i kere, wara be la sa­yikeree­wa bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ, wara be la aniɛ barasu be bo i jɔjɔ, wara be yi aniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu kere am, deke n kɛrɛ bo am yo i ni, am yo i na i buka Kristo jama ni, na i nya faŋga buka su.

eng: If some speak in a tongue, then let two or at most three speak, one at a time, and someone must interpret.
local: Nzɛn nbem ba sujɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum, i ni i agaya kɛrɛ, bu yo minɛ nnyɔ, wara minɛ nsan, má bu tara sɔ. Bu ŋa bu jɔjɔ, bu jɔjɔ kun kun, i ti ye ni ka be gusu kaaki yi i bu kere am.

eng: However, if there is no interpreter, then they should keep quiet in the meeting. They should speak privately to themselves and to God.
local: Ama, nzɛn be koro kaaki yi má i bu kere má am, i ti ye ni ka i mi n bo i sujɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum n mɔ i nɔɔ Kristo jamam nu, na i jɔjɔ ma i ŋu ni Nyɛmɛ.

eng: In the case of prophets, let two or three speak and have the rest evaluate what is said.
local: Minɛm bo bu kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm ni, i ti ye ni ka bu minɛ nnyɔ wara bu minɛ nsan jɔjɔ a, na nyiŋgam bo ba ka n fiiti i nu.

eng: And if some revelation comes to someone else who is sitting down, the first one should be quiet.
local: Nzɛn be sujɔjɔ, na Nyɛmɛ ayi deke be akere sɔnɔ nyiŋga, i ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ n bo i du mɔ sujɔjɔ n mɔ i nɔɔ, na yiri n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi deke be akere i n jɔjɔ.

eng: You can all prophesy one at a time so that everyone can learn and be encouraged.
local: Dama am kɛrɛ koro kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kun kun kere am beŋgu wɔ, adena am kɛrɛ sɔnsɔn deke be nu, na i ma am ahoresi.

eng: The spirits of prophets are under the control of the prophets.
local: I ti ye ni ka bo bu kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm n kɛrɛ la tara­buŋu fa kɔ bu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kan su.

eng: God isnt a God of disorder but of peace. Like in all the churches of Gods people.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ ti má bo i fa kpakpaaki­nu ba i minɛm dɔ, ama, i ma bu la tana­laifiɛ wɔ. I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, Kristo jama n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: the women should be quiet during the meeting. They are not allowed to talk. Instead, they need to get under control, just as the Law says.
local: nzɛn am ba tiɛɛnri am nɔɔ, i ti ye ni ka nbaram mɔ bu nɔɔ, bu la má atin ka bu jɔjɔ. Ama, wɔ yo daka ka bu yo bu ŋu bɛnlɛ, na bu su kabo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere n su.

eng: If they want to learn something, they should ask their husbands at home. It is disgraceful for a woman to talk during the meeting.
local: Nzɛn bu koro ka bu bisa jɔrɛ be, i ti ye ni ka bu bisa bu hunm awuru, dama i ti nyinsɛ wɔ ka bara jɔjɔ Kristo jama n nu.

eng: Did the word of God originate with you? Has it come only to you?.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n du mɔ fite ambɛrɛ dɔ a, nzɛn sɛ? Wara ambɛrɛ ŋgumi ade Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a?.

eng: If anyone thinks that they are prophets or spiritual people, then let them recognize that what Im writing to you is the Lords command.
local: Nzɛn be sunsun ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ, wara i sunsun ka i si Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim jɔrɛ, i ti ye ni ka i mi si tenle su ka ahin nɔaniɛ n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am ni, i ti Mibiɛ n dɛɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa a.

eng: If someone doesnt recognize this, they arent recognized.
local: Nzɛn be wo am nu, na i de má ahin jɔrɛ n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am ni, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ bu i mi ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, use your ambition to try to get the gift of prophecy, but dont prevent speaking in tongues.
local: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, i ti ye ni ka am boro am nyi na am sukan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm, na má am kanda minɛm ka má bu jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu barasum ni.

eng: Everything should be done with dignity and in proper order.
local: Na am yo deke kɛrɛ nyunyumi na i kɔ atin su.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want to call your attention to the good news that I preached to you, which you also received and in which you stand.
local: Tɔ, m ninbaam, kisa m koro ka m teŋge am su fa kɔ labari kpa n bo m yo kan kereeri am n su. Am yo yoori i yarada a, na am ajina i su kenken.

eng: You are being saved through it if you hold on to the message I preached to you, unless somehow you believed it for nothing.
local: Nzɛn am bitaari labari kpa n bo m kan kereeri am kenken, nyini ma am tii­de a, nzɛn má nyini yiri, am yarada n bo am ayo i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: I passed on to you as most important what I also received: Christ died for our sins in line with the scriptures.
local: Dama labari kpa n bo m deeri i na m kan kereeri am ni, i jɔrɛ anyumbaa la ka, Kristo wuuri ya satiɛ ti wɔ, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu lɛɛ.

eng: he was buried, and he rose on the third day in line with the scriptures.
local: ka i wuuri na bu seeri i ni, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i yiwee n nu i le­san cɛɛn, kabo Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n kere lɛɛ.

eng: He appeared to Cephas, then to the Twelve.
local: I teŋgeeri ni, na wɔ yi i ŋu akere Kefasi bo bu kaaki fere i ka Petoro ni, na wɔ ba ayi i ŋu akere i susu­fɔ buru ni nnyɔm ni.

eng: and then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers and sisters at once—most of them are still alive to this day, though some have died.
local: Nyini sin, na wɔ yi i ŋu akere ya ninbaam nbem, bu tara minɛ yaa nnu, bu kɛrɛ saŋga wo woo­biri kun wɔ. Bu nu, bu dɔŋgu toko la ŋgɔɔ, nbem gusu awu.

eng: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
local: Na wɔ yi i ŋu akere Yakobo, na wɔ ba akaaki ayi i ŋu akere i jaramasam kɛrɛ.

eng: and last of all he appeared to me, as if I were born at the wrong time.
local: Awieeri su, na wɔ yi i ŋu akere mini, bo m ti ka baa bo ba wu i na i saŋga nya ju má ni.

eng: Im the least important of the apostles. I dont deserve to be called an apostle, because I harassed Gods church.
local: Dama mini ti jaramasam kɛrɛ nu kaan, haari má wɔ ti má ye ni ka bu fere m jaramasa mmɔɔ, dama m yo kereeri Kristo jama n wuwuruwa wɔ.

eng: I am what I am by Gods grace, and Gods grace hasnt been for nothing. In fact, I have worked harder than all the others—that is, it wasnt me but the grace of God that is with me.
local: Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, ye m ti kabo m ti n a. Ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama m ni, i ti má ŋgbɛɛn, ama, m koro se ka ma yo juma atara nyiŋgam kɛrɛ mmɔɔ. Má mini ni m tii yoori i a, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama m ni, nyini ma m koro yoori i a.

eng: So then, whether you heard the message from me or them, this is what we preach and this is what you have believed.
local: Nyini ti, mini, wara bɛrɛ o, labari kpa n ye ya kan i a, labari kpa n gusu dɛɛ ti ye am yoori yarada n a.

eng: So if the message that is preached says that Christ has been raised from the dead, then how can some of you say, Theres no resurrection of the dead?.
local: Tɔ, labari kpa n bo ya kan i ni, ya ŋa, Nyɛmɛ ateŋge Kristo i yiwee n nu. Ye nzu ti ye am nbem ŋa yiwee teŋge wo má bɛrɛ a?.

eng: If theres no resurrection of the dead, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Nzɛn yiwee teŋge wo má bɛrɛ, nyini la ka, Nyɛmɛ ateŋge má Kristo mmɔɔ i yiwee n nu lɛɛ.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then our preaching is useless and your faith is useless.
local: Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ ateŋge má Kristo i yiwee n nu, nyini la ka, labari kpa n bo ya kan i ni, i ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ, na yarada n bo am ayo i n gusu ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: We are found to be false witnesses about God, because we testified against God that he raised Christ, when he didnt raise him if its the case that the dead arent raised.
local: Má nyini ŋgumi, nyini la ka, ya bu kɔrɛ asɔ Nyɛmɛ wɔ lɛɛ, dama ya ŋa wɔ teŋge Kristo i yiwee n nu, ama, bu ŋa bo ba wu n teŋge má yiwee nu.

eng: If the dead arent raised, then Christ hasnt been raised either.
local: Na nzɛn bo ba wu n teŋge má bu yiwee nu, nyini la ka, wɔ teŋge má Kristo mmɔɔ i yiwee n nu lɛɛ.

eng: If Christ hasnt been raised, then your faith is worthless; you are still in your sins.
local: Na nzɛn wɔ teŋge má Kristo i yiwee n nu, yarada n bo am ayo i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ, na am toko wo am satiɛm nu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: and whats more, those who have died in Christ are gone forever.
local: na bo bu yo yoori Kristo yarada na ba wu n kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn lɛɛ.

eng: If we have a hope in Christ only in this life, then we deserve to be pitied more than anyone else.
local: Nzɛn durunya n nu fɛn ŋgumi ye ya la tama n fa kɔ Kristo dɔ, nyini yiri, ya ti minɛm bo ya la aŋunfɔ tara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

eng: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead. Hes the first crop of the harvest of those who have died.
local: Ama, nahɔrɛ su, Nyɛmɛ ateŋge Kristo i yiwee n nu. Minɛm bo ba wu n kɛrɛ nu, yiri tiiri bo i du mɔ teŋgeeri i yiwee nu a.

eng: Since death came through a human being, the resurrection of the dead came through one too.
local: Adamande kun dɛɛ ti, ye yiwee kun sɔnɔ kɛrɛ n a, adamande kun gusu dɛɛ ti, ye minɛ bá teŋge bu yiwee n nu a.

eng: In the same way that everyone dies in Adam, so also everyone will be given life in Christ.
local: Na kabo Adam dɛɛ ti na adamande kɛrɛ wu ni, sɔ ye Kristo dɛɛ ti ma adamande kɛrɛ bá teŋge bu yiwee nu na bu nyá ŋgɔɔ a.

eng: Each event will happen in the right order: Christ, the first crop of the harvest, then those who belong to Christ at his coming.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ni i saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ bá teŋge i. Bo ba wu n kɛrɛ nu, Kristo ti bo Nyɛmɛ du mɔ teŋgeeri i yiwee nu a, na bo bu ti i minɛm n gusu bá teŋge saŋga bo i bá kaaki ba ni.

eng: and then the end, when Christ hands over the kingdom to God the Father, when he brings every form of rule, every authority and power to an end.
local: Nyini sin na deke n kɛrɛ bá awieeri, na i nyá fa fɛmɛya n wura i Si Nyɛmɛ saa nu, nyini saŋga, wɔ kunma fɛmɛya kɛrɛ, ni nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ, ni yiko­fɔm kɛrɛ lɛɛ.

eng: It is necessary for him to rule until he puts all enemies under his feet .
local: Dama wɔ yo daka ka i di fɛmɛya ni haari i bá kunma i kpɔ­fɔm, na i nana bu kɛrɛ ŋu.

eng: Death is the last enemy to be brought to an end.
local: I kpɔ­fɔ n bo i bá kunma i awieeri su n la yiwee wɔ.

eng: since he has brought everything under control under his feet. When it says that everything has been brought under his control, this clearly means everything except for the one who placed everything under his control.
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi nu ka, Wɔ fa deke n kɛrɛ awura i saa nu ka i niɛ bu su. Bo i ŋa wɔ fa deke n kɛrɛ awura i saa nu ka i niɛ bu su ni, nyini kere má ka Nyɛmɛ bo i faari deke n kɛrɛ wuraari yiri Kristo saa nu afa i tii awura i saa nu lɛɛ.

eng: But when all things have been brought under his control, then the Son himself will also be under the control of the one who gave him control over everything so that God may be all in all.
local: Na saŋga bo i ba faari deke n kɛrɛ wuraari i saa nu ni, na yiri Baa n ni i tii nya fa i ŋu wura i Si Nyɛmɛ saa nu. Nyini saŋga Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ la deke n kɛrɛ, na deke n kɛrɛ wo bɛrɛ yiri ti wɔ.

eng: Otherwise, what are those who are getting baptized for the dead doing? If the dead arent raised, then why are they being baptized for them?.
local: Tɔ, nzɛn bu ŋa yiwee teŋge wo má bɛrɛ, ye bo bu de minɛm bo ba wu n jinaa­biri na bu biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue ma bu n di, bu yo sɛ wɔ? Nzɛn bo ba wu n teŋge má bu yiwee nu, nzu ti ye bu biɛ Nyɛmɛ nzue ma bu a?.

eng: And what about us? Why are we in danger all day every day?.
local: Ye nzu ti ye yɛrɛ tɔ ya ŋgɔɔ saŋga kɛrɛ n a?.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I swear by the pride I have in you in Christ Jesus our Lord, Im facing death every day.
local: Ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, m wo yiwee nu wɔ, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, m ninbaam, m fa ambɛrɛ bo m hoe ye m se sɔ n a, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: From a human point of view, what good does it do me if I fought wild animals in Ephesus? If the dead arent raised, lets eat and drink because tomorrow well die .
local: Nzɛn m ni minɛm bo bu ti ka boro neŋgem, kun luɛ, Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu, na i kɔ yo ka adamande woo­wa dɛɛ ti m la tama, nzu kpɛrɛ ye ma nya i a? Nzɛn bo ba wu n teŋge má bu yiwee nu, nyini yiri, i ti kabo minɛ nbem n nati se n wɔ, bu ŋa, Am ma ya di, na ya nu, dama ahunma ya wú ɔ.

eng: Dont be deceived, bad company corrupts good character.
local: Má am ma be dadaka am, kabo minɛm nati se ni, ka, Sɔnɔ tiɛ suuri sɔnɔ kpa su, i sáaki i woo­wa kpa n wɔ.

eng: Sober up by acting like you should and dont sin. Some of you are ignorant about God—I say this because you should be ashamed of yourselves!.
local: Am kaaki sunsun nyunyumi, na má am yo satiɛ kun, dama am nbem si má Nyɛmɛ. Nyini n bo m se sɔ ni, i ti am nyinsɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: But someone will say, How are the dead raised? What kind of body will they have when they come back.
local: Tɔ, beni, be koro bisa ka, bo ba wu ni, bu teŋge bu yiwee nu sɛ wɔ? Aŋuniɛ­mbaa barasu bɔnɔ ye bu fá ba?.

eng: Look, fool! When you put a seed into the ground, it doesnt come back to life unless it dies.
local: Wɔrɔ sinnzin ni, a si má ka nzɛn a dɔ deke mbaa, má ka i wu wɔ ka, ye i nya fi a, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: What you put in the ground doesnt have the shape that it will have, but its a bare grain of wheat or some other seed.
local: Deke mbaa n bo a dɔ i ni, beni, ŋgani mbaa wara abuwe ye a dɔ i a, ama, i ba fiiri, i kaaki nyawa a, i ti má ka mbaa n bo a dɔɔri i ni.

eng: God gives it the sort of shape that he chooses, and he gives each of the seeds its own shape.
local: Nyɛmɛ ma i kɛrɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa n kabo i koro a, deke mbaa barasu n kɛrɛ, ni i tiɛŋgu aŋuniɛ­mbaa.

eng: All flesh isnt alike. Humans have one kind of flesh, animals have another kind of flesh, birds have another kind of flesh, and fish have another kind.
local: Aŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ wo i ŋgumi wɔ, i ti má kun, adamande dɛɛ wo bɛrɛ i ŋgumi, ajaa­nnam dɛɛ wo bɛrɛ bu ŋgumi, laŋkpɛtɛ­fɔm dɛɛ wo bɛrɛ bu ŋgumi, na jombaam dɛɛ wo bɛrɛ bu ŋgumi.

eng: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. The heavenly bodies have one kind of glory, and the earthly bodies have another kind of glory.
local: Dekem bo bu wo aŋgoro n wo bɛrɛ, na a yaki bo bu wo durunya n nu fɛn ni. Aŋgoro dɛɛm nyumi wo bɛrɛ bu ŋgumi, na durunya n nu fɛn n dɛɛm gusu nyumi wo bɛrɛ bu ŋgumi.

eng: The sun has one kind of glory, the moon has another kind of glory, and the stars have another kind of glory .
local: Wiɛ nyumi wo bɛrɛ i ŋgumi, sara nyumi wo bɛrɛ i ŋgumi, asara­mbaam nyumi wo bɛrɛ bu ŋgumi, ama, bu nbem yo nyumi tara nbem.

eng: Its the same with the resurrection of the dead: a rotting body is put into the ground, but what is raised wont ever decay.
local: Tɔ, kabo i ti lɛɛ fa kɔ yiwee teŋge n su. Aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo bu se i ni, i kpɔrɔ ɔ, na bo i teŋge, i kpɔrɔ má fieo.

eng: Its degraded when its put into the ground, but its raised in glory. Its weak when its put into the ground, but its raised in power.
local: Deke n bo bu se i ni, i la má anyunuyi, ama, i bá teŋgeeri, i woo­wa yo nyumi wɔ. Deke n bo bu se i ni, i la má faŋga, ama, i bá teŋgeeri, i la faŋga wɔ.

eng: Its a physical body when its put into the ground, but its raised as a spiritual body. If theres a physical body, theres also a spiritual body.
local: Deke n bo bu se i ni, i ti durunya n nu adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa a, i bá teŋgeeri, i ti aŋuniɛ­mbaa bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ma i ŋgɔɔ n wɔ. Kabo durunya n nu adamande aŋuniɛ­mbaa wo bɛrɛ ni, sɔ ye aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ma i ŋgɔɔ n gusu wo bɛrɛ a.

eng: So it is also written, The first human, Adam, became a living person, and the last Adam became a spirit that gives life.
local: I ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n wɔ, ka, Ciciri su dɛɛ adamande ŋgɔɔ­fɔ n la Adam wɔ, na Adam n bo i ka sin n ti awiɛwiɛ bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni.

eng: But the physical body comes first, not the spiritual one—the spiritual body comes afterward.
local: Bo i ti awiɛwiɛ ni, má yiri du mɔ baari a, ama, bo i ti adamande ŋgɔɔ­fɔ ni du mɔ baari a, na bo i ti awiɛwiɛ n anya tɔ su.

eng: The first human was from the earth made from dust; the second human is from heaven.
local: Ciciri su dɛɛ adamande n fite durunya n nu fɛn wɔ, Nyɛmɛ yo faari natiɛ yoori i a, ama, bo i tɔ su nnyɔ su n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro baari a.

eng: The nature of the person made of dust is shared by people who are made of dust, and the nature of the heavenly person is shared by heavenly people.
local: Minɛm bo bu ti durunya n nu­fɔm ni, bu ti kabo sɔnɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ faari natiɛ yoori i n ti n wɔ. Na bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ minɛm n gusu ti kabo sɔnɔ n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro n ti ni.

eng: We will look like the heavenly person in the same way as we have looked like the person made from dust.
local: Kabo ya ferema ni sɔnɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ faari natiɛ yoori i ni, sɔ ye ya bá ferema ni sɔnɔ n bo i fite aŋgoro n a.

eng: This is what Im saying, brothers and sisters: Flesh and blood cant inherit Gods kingdom. Something that rots cant inherit something that doesnt decay.
local: M ninbaam, m suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i ti niɛ ni mbunja ni, i koro wura má Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu, na deke n bo i kpɔrɔ n koro nya má deke n bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni.

eng: Listen, Im telling you a secret: All of us wont die, but we will all be changed.
local: Am ti fɛn, m suse am asiri nu jɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ, ka, má ya kɛrɛ wú a, ama, ya kɛrɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa ni bá kaaki fɔfɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: in an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the final trumpet. The trumpet will blast, and the dead will be raised with bodies that wont decay, and we will be changed.
local: I yó kungere­kun wɔ, anyumbaa kata ni i teke, saŋga bo bu bá fitaari awieeri su dɛɛ awiɛ ni, na bo ba wu n téŋge bu yiwee nu, bu kpɔ́rɔ má kun, na yɛrɛ gusu ŋuniɛ­mbaa n káaki fɔfɔrɛ.

eng: Its necessary for this rotting body to be clothed with what cant decay, and for the body that is dying to be clothed in what cant die.
local: Dama wɔ yo daka ka ahin aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i kpɔrɔ n kaaki aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i kpɔrɔ má ni, na aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i wu n gusu kaaki aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i wu má.

eng: And when the rotting body has been clothed in what cant decay, and the dying body has been clothed in what cant die, then this statement in scripture will happen: Death has been swallowed up by a victory .
local: Saŋga bo aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i kpɔrɔ n bá kpɔrɔ má kun ni, na aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i wu n gusu bá wu má kun ni, nyini saŋga Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni yó yii­wa lɛɛ, bo ba kɛrɛ i ka, Ba di nasara yiwee ŋu ayo i ŋgbɛɛn ni.

eng: Where is your victory, Death? Where is your sting, Death? .
local: Keto, yiwee, u yiko n bo a fa kan n wo ni? Keto, u nasara n wo ni.

eng: Deaths sting is sin, and the power of sin is the Law.
local: Deke n bo yiwee bita kan n la satiɛ wɔ, ye satiɛ nya i yiko n fite Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu wɔ.

eng: Thanks be to God, who gives us this victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!.
local: Ama, ya da Nyɛmɛ asi, dama ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, i ma ya di nasara.

eng: As a result of all this, my loved brothers and sisters, you must stand firm, unshakable, excelling in the work of the Lord as always, because you know that your labor isnt going to be for nothing in the Lord.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaa koroo­wam, am jina kenken na má am fa am ŋu ji, am boro am nyi suyo Mibiɛ n juma n saŋga kɛrɛ, dama am si ka juma n bo am suyo i ma Mibiɛ n ti má ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Concerning the collection of money for Gods people: You should do what I have directed the churches in Galatia to do.
local: Tɔ, fa kɔ ŋwaa n bo am yiyi i na am fa kɔ buka Nyɛmɛ minɛm ni, am yo kabo m kereeri Kristo jamam n bo bu wo Galatiya awɔrɛ n nu n ka bu yo ni.

eng: On the first day of the week, each of you should set aside whatever you can afford from what you earn so that the collection wont be delayed until I come.
local: Yuda­fɔm bakuɛ n bumboori, mɔnɛ kɛrɛ mɔnɛ, am niɛ kabo Nyɛmɛ awura am abaraka fa kɔ dekem bo am la bu n su, na am suyiyi ŋwaa be se, adena nzɛn m bá baari, am yi má ŋwaa kun.

eng: Then when I get there, Ill send whomever you approve to Jerusalem with letters of recommendation to bring your gift.
local: Nyini saŋga na m bá juuri, m kɛ́rɛ kadasi ma minɛm n bo am ayi bu ni, na m fá sunma bu na bu fá am aceerim kɔ Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: If it seems right for me to go too, theyll travel with me.
local: Na nzɛn i ti ye ni ka mini mmɔɔ kɔ, na m ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ kɔ́.

eng: Ill come to you after I go through Macedonia, and because Im going through Macedonia.
local: M koro ka m fa Makɛdoniya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na m sin ba niɛ am busu.

eng: I may stay with you or even spend the winter there in Corinth so that you can send me on my way to wherever Im off to next.
local: Beni, m yó le­nnyɔ am dɔ, wara fufuku ni sín ka, ye m nyá ba yasu a, adena am buka m na m kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo m koro ka m kɔ ni.

eng: I dont want to make a quick visit to you, since I hope to spend some time with you if the Lord lets it happen.
local: Dama m koro má ka m bá jina aŋgoro niɛ am busu na m sin, m la tama ka nzɛn Mibiɛ n soroori su, m yó le­nnyɔ am dɔ.

eng: Ill stay here in Ephesus until the Festival of Pentecost.
local: M wó Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu fɛn haari Butara Cɛɛn­kpiri n saŋga.

eng: In spite of the fact that there are many opponents, a big and productive opportunity has opened up for my mission here.
local: Dama atin bambaka ateke ama m, na m yo juma n nahɔrɛ su, ama, m kpɔ­fɔm gusu asunnu.

eng: If Timothy comes to you, be sure that he has no reason to be afraid while hes with you, because he does the work of the Lord just like I do.
local: Nzɛn Timoti bá baari am dɔ, am de i ni am saa nnyɔ, má am ma i ahore saaki, yiri mmɔɔ yo Mibiɛ n juma n kabo m yo i n wɔ.

eng: So dont let anyone disrespect him, but send him on in peace so he can join me. Im waiting for him along with the brothers and sisters.
local: Má be se ka i bu i ŋgbɛɛn, ama, am kaari i ni ahoreda na i ba tu m, dama m suniɛ i ni ya ninbaam n atin nu ka bu bá.

eng: Concerning Apollos our brother: I strongly encouraged him to visit you with the brothers and sisters, but he didnt want to go now. Hell come when he has an opportunity.
local: Fa kɔ ya niwa Apolosi yiri, ma sere i ni anyum­boro ka i yo aniɛ, na i ni ya ninbaam bá na bu bá niɛ am busu, ama, wɔ soro má su ka i bá kisa, ama, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ ba maari i atin, i bá.

eng: Stay awake, stand firm in your faith, be brave, be strong.
local: Má am fa am ŋu ji, am jina kenken ni am yarada ni, na am yo nbiɛsɔm, na am fa ahoresi.

eng: Everything should be done in love.
local: Deke n kɛrɛ bo am yo i ni, am yo i ni koro.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I encourage you to do something else. You know that the people in Stephanas household were the first crop of the harvest to come from the mission to Achaia. They have dedicated themselves to the service of Gods people.
local: M ninbaam, am si Satɛfanasi ni i dika­fɔm jɔrɛ, bɛrɛ duuri mɔ yoori Kristo yarada Akaya awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu a, na ba fa bu ŋu asu Nyɛmɛ minɛm amusui, nyini ti, m sere am wɔ jande.

eng: So accept the authority of people like them and of anyone who cooperates and works hard.
local: ka am bu nyini minɛm bo bu ti sɔ ni, ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu buka su, na bu yo bu juma n ni anyum­boro ni.

eng: Im so happy that Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus have arrived; theyve made up for my missing you.
local: Saŋga bo Satɛfanasi, ni Fɔ­tunatusi, ni Akayakusi ba tuuri m ni, na wɔ yo m fiɛ agaya, dama bu deeri am dɛɛ jinaa­biri wɔ, na ba yo deke n bo má am yo i ma m ni.

eng: Indeed theyve provided my spirit and yours with a much-needed rest. Therefore, give them proper recognition.
local: Dama kabo bu baari ni, bu ma m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ anya ahoresi, nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka am bu minɛm bo bu ti sɔ ni.

eng: The churches in the province of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you warmly in the Lord, together with the church that meets in their house.
local: Kristo jamam bo bu wo Esiya awɔrɛ n nu n kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu. Akuyila, ni i yi Pirisila, ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ bu dika n kɛrɛ gusu bisa am dɔŋgu, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: All the brothers and sisters greet you. You in turn should greet each other with a holy kiss.
local: Ya ninbaam kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn n bisa am dɔŋgu. Am gusu bisa am beŋgum dɔŋgu ni koro.

eng: Here is my greeting in my own handwriting—Paul.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii sufa m saa sukɛrɛ ahin kɛrɛɛ­wa n bisa am dɔŋgu a.

eng: A curse on anyone who doesnt love the Lord. Come, Lord!.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má Mibiɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ yó i mi ŋgbɛɛn. Marana ta , i bu la ka, Ya Mibiɛ, bra.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
local: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu buka am kɛrɛ.

eng: My love is with all of you in Christ Jesus.
local: M koro am kɛrɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Ami.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, and Timothy our brother. To Gods church that is in Corinth, along with all of Gods people throughout Achaia.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu bo m ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu ni, m ni ya niwa Timoti sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma am a. Ya sukɛrɛ i na ya fa ma ambɛrɛ Kristo jama n bo am wo Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo Akaya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu ni.

eng: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and from our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na am nya tana­laifiɛ.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! He is the compassionate Father and God of all comfort.
local: Am ma ya kansi Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo Si ni. Yiri ti ya Si bo i si ya aŋunfɔ a. Yiri ti Nyɛmɛ bo i dada ya ahore a.

eng: Hes the one who comforts us in all our trouble so that we can comfort other people who are in every kind of trouble. We offer the same comfort that we ourselves received from God.
local: Yiri dada ya ahore a, ya wahara kɛrɛ nu, adena yɛrɛ gusu koro dada minɛm ahore, bɛrɛ n bo bu wo wahara n kɛrɛ nu ni. Kabo Nyɛmɛ ama yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii anya ahoreda ni, yɛrɛ ma bɛrɛ n gusu nya nyini ahoreda n a.

eng: That is because we receive so much comfort through Christ in the same way that we share so many of Christs sufferings.
local: Dama kabo ya ni Kristo susaŋga di i wahara n agaya ni, sɔ ye yiri dɛɛ ti, ye ya nya ahoreda agaya n a.

eng: So if we have trouble, it is to bring you comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is to bring you comfort from the experience of endurance while you go through the same sufferings that we also suffer.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya ŋu kekereke, i ma am nya ahoreda ni tii­de wɔ. Na saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya nya ahoreda ni, i ma am ahoreda a, na am yo sawɔrɛ mindi de nyini wahara n bo ya di i n be.

eng: Our hope for you is certain, because we know that as you are partners in suffering, so also you are partners in comfort.
local: Ya la tama fa kɔ am dɔ, ya yo má sika. Dama ya si ka kabo am ni yɛrɛ kɛrɛ susaŋga di ya wahara n be ni, sɔ ye ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ susaŋga nya ya ahoreda n a.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we dont want you to be unaware of the troubles that we went through in Asia. We were weighed down with a load of suffering that was so far beyond our strength that we were afraid we might not survive.
local: M ninbaam, ya koro ka am si kabo ya ŋuuri kekereke Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu barasu ni, ya ŋuuri anyum­boro bo i la má aŋusu, haari na ya yi tama ka ya wu awie.

eng: It certainly seemed to us as if we had gotten the death penalty. This was so that we would have confidence in God, who raises the dead, instead of ourselves.
local: Nahɔrɛ, ya sunsun ka ya wu wɔ. Ama, i yo sɔ na má ya daafi ya ŋu su, ama, na ya daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu, dama yiri teŋge bo ba wu n a.

eng: God rescued us from a terrible death, and he will rescue us. We have set our hope on him that he will rescue us again.
local: Wɔ yi ya afite nyini yiwee n bo i la má aŋusu n nu, na i yí ya fite nu bekun. Ya yo yiri tama ka i toko súyi ya fite nu bekun a.

eng: since you are helping with your prayer for us. Then many people can thank God on our behalf for the gift that was given to us through the prayers of many people.
local: kabo am buka ya ni am nyɛmɛsere ni. Nyini má minɛ dɔŋgu bá da Nyɛmɛ asi, fa kɔ kabo wɔ de minɛ dɔŋgu nyɛmɛsere n na wɔ yo sakpam ama ya ni.

eng: We have conducted ourselves with godly sincerity and pure motives in the world, and especially toward you. This is why we are confident, and our conscience confirms this. We didnt act with human wisdom but we relied on the grace of God.
local: Deke n bo ya fa fɔ n la ka, ya sunsunni nu kere ya ka ya bo má anɔɔfiɛ, na ya ahore­mbaa ti casi wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ durunya n nu, maala fa kɔ am dɔ, má ka ya su durunya n nu alecira su n dɛɛ ti a, ama, Nyɛmɛ yo bukaari ya n dɛɛ ti a.

eng: We dont write anything to you except what you can read and also understand. I hope that you will understand totally.
local: Ya kɛrɛ má jɔrɛ be ma má am, má ka jɔrɛ bo am kan i na am kaaki koro ti i bu ni. M la tama ka am bá si i kɛrɛ haari ni i awieeri.

eng: since you have already understood us partly. Understand that in the day of our Lord Jesus, we will make you proud as you will also make us proud.
local: dama am adu mɔ asi ya ŋusu jɔrɛ kaan, nyini ma am bá koro fa ya fɔ n a, kabo ya bá fa am fɔ, cɛɛn n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá kaaki ba n a.

eng: Because I was sure of this, I wanted to visit you first so that you could have a second opportunity to see me.
local: M la ahoresi fa kɔ nyini n su dɛɛ ti, ma sɛsɛ su ka m du mɔ ba niɛ am busu, adena má am nya buka kpe nnyɔ.

eng: I wanted to visit you on my way to Macedonia, and then come to you again on my way back from Macedonia, at which point I was hoping you would help me on my way to Judea.
local: Deke bo i ti na m se sɔ n la ka, ma sɛsɛ su ka má m fa am dɔ na m niɛ am busu, na m sin kɔ Makɛdoniya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu, na m ba sufite dɔ ba, na má m fa am dɔ bekun, adena am buka m na m koro kɔ ju Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: So I wasnt unreliable when I planned to do this, was I? Or do I make decisions with a substandard human process so that I say Yes, yes and No, no at the same time?.
local: Nyini n bo m sɛsɛɛri su sɔ ni, m la má jinakun­su wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Wara m sɛsɛ su kabo durunya n nu­fɔm n yo n wɔ, na m se ka Ɛɛn, na m kaaki se ka Ai, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: But as God is faithful, our message to you isnt both yes and no.
local: Nyɛmɛ la dabuŋu ni nahɔrɛ n dɛɛ ti, nɔaniɛm bo ya kan kereeri am ni, i ti má Ɛɛn, na i kaaki ti má Ai.

eng: Gods Son, Jesus Christ, is the one who was preached among you by us—through me, Silvanus, and Timothy—he wasnt yes and no. In him it is always yes.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ Wa Yesu Kristo n bo ya kaanri i jɔrɛ kereeri am ni, mini ni Silivanusi ni Timoti, yiri Yesu dɛɛ jɔrɛ ti má Ɛɛn, na i kaaki ti má Ai, ama, i deke kɛrɛ ti Ɛɛn ɔ.

eng: All of Gods promises have their yes in him. That is why we say Amen through him to the glory of God.
local: Dama dekem kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó bu ni, bu kɛrɛ ti Ɛɛn ɔ, Yesu dɛɛ ti. Nyini ti, i dɛɛ ti ye saŋga bo ya kansi Nyɛmɛ, ya se ka, Ami n a.

eng: God is the one who establishes us with you in Christ and who anointed us.
local: Tɔ, Nyɛmɛ ni i tii ma ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ koro jina kenken a, kabo ya ni Kristo ayo kun ni. Yiri ayi ya ka ya su i n a.

eng: God also sealed us and gave the Spirit as a down payment in our hearts.
local: Yiri ase ya nyaari ka ya ti i dɛɛ minɛm a, na wɔ fa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura ya ahore su na i kere kabo i má ya deke n kɛrɛ bo i seeri ka i fá ma i minɛm ni.

eng: I call on God as my witness—I didnt come again to Corinth because I wanted to spare you.
local: Nyɛmɛ di m daani m ahore su a, ma koro má ka m kan am ndiɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma kaaki aba má Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu n a.

eng: It isnt that we are trying to control your faith, but we are working with you for your happiness, because you stand firm in your faith.
local: I ti má ka ya sumiɛ am kabo am yo yarada n wɔ, ama, ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ buka ya beŋgu wɔ, na am nya ahorejɔ, dama yarada n dɛɛ ti ye am jina kenken n a.

eng: So I decided that, for my own sake, I wouldnt visit you again while I was upset.
local: Nyini ti, na ma yo m sunsunni nu ka m ba niɛ má am busu kun na am ahore saaki.

eng: If I make you sad, who will be there to make me glad when you are sad because of me?.
local: Dama nzɛn m ma am ahore saakiiri, ŋmam ma m ahore da a? Má ambɛrɛ bo ma saaki am ahore ni?.

eng: Thats why I wrote this very thing to you, so that when I came I wouldnt be made sad by the ones who ought to make me happy. I have confidence in you, that my happiness means your happiness.
local: Deke bo i ti na m kɛrɛɛri sɔ n la ka, ma koro má ka m ba na má am ma m ahore saaki, ambɛrɛ bo má i ti ye ni ka am ma m ahore jɔ ni. M la ahoresi fa kɔ am dɔ, ka deke n bo i ma m ahore jɔ ni, nyini má am gusu kɛrɛ ahore jɔ n a.

eng: I wrote to you in tears, with a very troubled and anxious heart. I didnt write to make you sad but so you would know the overwhelming love that I have for you.
local: Dama m woori ahoresaaki, ni ahore­mbaa yaari, ni nyunvuin agaya nu, ye m kɛrɛɛri am n a. I ti má ka m koroori ka m saaki am ahore wɔ, ama, m koroori ka am si kabo m koro am agaya barasu n wɔ.

eng: But if someone has made anyone sad, that person hasnt hurt me but all of you to some degree .
local: Kabo be ayo ahoresaaki sa ni, má mini ye wɔ saaki m ahore a, ama, wɔ saaki am kɛrɛ ahore a, nzɛn má nyini, wɔ saaki am nu be ahore. M gusu koro má ka m jɔjɔ jɔrɛ n na i kɔ sin su agaya.

eng: The punishment handed out by the majority is enough for this person.
local: Sui­cin n bo ambɛrɛ minɛ beberebe n kɛrɛ fa kereeri i mi ni, wɔ sɛ i su sɔ.

eng: This is why you should try your best to forgive and to comfort this person now instead, so that this person isnt overwhelmed by too much sorrow.
local: Ama, kisa, kabo i ti ye ni ka am yo i n la ka, am kaaki de i mi, na am fa i ŋgasi ce i, na am dada i ahore, má sɔ, ahoresaaki agaya ni má i kpaasi wɔ.

eng: So I encourage you to show your love for this person.
local: Nyini ti, m sere am wɔ, am kaaki de i na am kere i kabo am la koro fa kɔ i dɔ ni.

eng: This is another reason why I wrote you. I wanted to test you and see if you are obedient in everything.
local: Deke bo i ti na m kɛrɛɛri am n la ka, m koro ka m yo am na m niɛ ka am de deke n kɛrɛ bo m se am ka am yo i ni.

eng: If you forgive anyone for anything, I do too. And whatever Ive forgiven , I did it for you in the presence of Christ.
local: Sɔnɔ bo am fa i ŋgasi ce i ni, mini mmɔɔ fa ce i mi. Sa bo i ti na ma fa i ŋgasi ace i ni, nzɛn i la ŋgasi bo i ti ye ni ka m fa ce i mmɔɔ, ma yo sɔ Kristo nyunu, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti wɔ.

eng: This is so that we wont be taken advantage of by Satan, because we are well aware of his schemes.
local: Ya yo sɔ ɔ, adena má Sitana koro kunma ya. Dama ya si tenle su kabo i la ŋgɔnlɛ­tiɛ barasu ni.

eng: When I came to Troas to preach Christs gospel, the Lord gave me an opportunity to preach.
local: Tɔ, m kɔ juuri Tɔrasi miɛ kpiri n nu ka m kan Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere bu ni, na ma ŋu ka Mibiɛ n adu mɔ ateke atin ama m.

eng: But I was worried because I couldnt find my brother Titus there. So I said good-bye to them and went on to Macedonia.
local: Haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ, m ahore ada má, dama ma ŋu má ya niwa Titosi dɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na ma kaari bu, na ma sin akɔ Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu.

eng: But thank God, who is always leading us around through Christ as if we were in a parade. He releases the fragrance of the knowledge of him everywhere through us.
local: Ama, ya da Nyɛmɛ asi, dama kabo Kristo adi nasara na ya ni yiri ayo kun ni, saŋga kɛrɛ, yiri Nyɛmɛ ma ya nati su Kristo su, i nasara natiiri nu a, na deke kɛrɛ bo ya kɔ ni, yiri fa ya bara yɛ a, na ya ma minɛm si i jɔrɛ, kabo deke nvani bara yɛ deke kɛrɛ ni.

eng: We smell like the aroma of Christs offering to God, both to those who are being saved and to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Dama ya ti ka ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani bo Kristo fa ma Nyɛmɛ n wɔ, na i nva­nvani n bara yɛ minɛm bo bu sunya tii­de ni, ni bo bu suka ŋgbɛɛn n dɔ ɔ.

eng: We smell like a contagious dead person to those who are dying, but we smell like the fountain of life to those who are being saved. Who is qualified for this kind of ministry?.
local: Bo bu suka ŋgbɛɛn ni, bɛrɛ bu ya ka nva­nvani n bo i sufa yiwee ba bu dɔ ɔ, ama, bo bu sunya tii­de ni, bɛrɛ bu ya ka nva­nvani n bo i suma bu nya ŋgɔɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ. Ahin juma n yiri, ŋma koro fa i tiɛŋgu faŋga yo i a?.

eng: We arent like so many people who hustle the word of God to make a profit. We are speaking through Christ in the presence of God, as those who are sincere and as those who are sent from God.
local: Ya ti má ka minɛ dɔŋgu bo bu bita Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n yo wata na bu di i kpɛrɛ ni, ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti nahɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ, haari na ya ti minɛ bo wɔ yi ya asunma ya wɔ, na ya jina i nyunu ye ya kan i jɔrɛ a, kabo ya ni Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? We dont need letters of introduction to you or from you like other people, do we?.
local: Ya sukaaki kan kere am kabo ya ti na am si ya, nzɛn sɛ? Wɔ yo má daka ka ya yo kabo nbem yo ni, ka ya fa kadasi bo i kere kabo ya ti barasu n bita nu, wara ya de kadasi am dɔ na i kere minɛ nyiŋgam kabo ya ti barasu, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: You are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone.
local: Ai, ya kadasi n la ambɛrɛ ni am tii wɔ, kadasi n bo ba kɛrɛ i awura ya ahore su ni, na minɛ kɛrɛ koro kan i na bu si kabo i ti.

eng: You show that you are Christs letter, delivered by us. You werent written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God. You werent written on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
local: Na bu koro ŋu ka ambɛrɛ ti kadasi n bo Kristo akɛrɛ awura ya saa nu afa asunma ya n a. Wɔ fa má deke kɛrɛɛ­wa nzue fa kɛrɛɛri i a. Ama, Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ Awiɛwiɛ n ye i fa kɛrɛɛri i a. Wɔ kɛrɛ má i agɔ má yabuɛ pɛpɛrɛpɛ su wɔ, kabo Nyɛmɛ yo kɛrɛɛri i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari Moyisi ni, ama, i kɛrɛɛri i gɔɔri adamandem ahore su wɔ.

eng: This is the confidence that we have through Christ in the presence of God.
local: Deke bo i ti na ya koro jɔjɔ sɔ n la ka, ya la ahoresi Nyɛmɛ dɔ, Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: It isnt that we ourselves are qualified to claim that anything came from us. No, our qualification is from God.
local: Ya la má deke bo i kere ka yɛrɛ ni ya tii koro yo juma n a. Deke bo i ma ya koro yo i n fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: He has qualified us as ministers of a new covenant, not based on what is written but on the Spirit, because what is written kills, but the Spirit gives life.
local: Yiri Nyɛmɛ ama ya koro ayo i amusui­su­fɔm a, fa kɔ sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n bo wɔ di i di n su a. Nyini sɛsɛ­su n fite má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo wɔ kɛrɛ i n dɔ, ama, i fite i Awiɛwiɛ n dɔ ɔ. Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa kɛrɛɛ­wa n fa yiwee ba, ama, Awiɛwiɛ n ma ŋgɔɔ ɔ.

eng: The ministry that brought death was carved in letters on stone tablets. It came with such glory that the Israelites couldnt look for long at Moses face because his face was shining with glory, even though it was a fading glory.
local: Tɔ, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ yo kɛrɛɛri i gɔɔri yabuɛ pɛpɛrɛpɛ n su na i fa maari Moyisi ni, nyini saŋga, i yoori sɔ ni, i jirima bambaka n taari wɔ, na Yisareli­fɔm n akoro aniɛ má Moyisi nyunu­awa dika, dama i nyunu­awa taari wein agaya, haari ni bo i ba saari wɔ. Kabo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i fa yiwee ba n ti n na Nyɛmɛ boori i juma n bu ni jirima bambaka n sɔ ni.

eng: Wont the ministry of the Spirit be much more glorious?.
local: i Awiɛwiɛ ni yó i dɛɛ juma n ni jirima bambaka bo i tara nyini ni, má sɔ?.

eng: If the ministry that brought condemnation has glory, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness?.
local: Kabo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i yo diiri minɛm jɔrɛ ciinri bu sui n ti n na i juma yo la jirima bambaka sɔ ni, nyini yiri, juma n bo i ma Nyɛmɛ de minɛm ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n dɛɛ jirima bambaka tara sɔ.

eng: In fact, what was glorious isnt glorious now, because of the glory that is brighter.
local: Nahɔrɛ, deke n bo i yo la jirima bambaka ni, i la má jirima kun, deke n bo i la jirima bambaka tara sɔ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: If the glory that fades away was glorious, how much more glorious is the one that lasts!.
local: Kabo deke n bo i ti siin­wa n ti n na i yo la jirima bambaka sɔ ni, nyini yiri, deke n bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ jirima bambaka n tara sɔ.

eng: So, since we have such a hope, we act with great confidence.
local: Kabo ya la tama ka i ti sɔ n dɛɛ ti, ye ya la ahoresi agaya koro jɔjɔ sɔ n a.

eng: We arent like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites couldnt watch the end of what was fading away.
local: Yɛrɛ ti má ka Moyisi bo i yo faari sanvuin kataari na má Yisareli­fɔm ŋu i nyunu­awa bo i ta wein na i susaari ni.

eng: But their minds were closed. Right up to the present day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. The veil is not removed because it is taken away by Christ.
local: Ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni, bu sui yo tiiri tan­tanantan a. Haari ni nyuma, saŋga kɛrɛ bo bu ti ka be sukan sɛsɛ­su dawa n dɛɛ kadasim ni, i yoori bu ka nyini sanvuin n toko kata bu sunsunni su wɔ, má ka Kristo ŋgumi koro yi i a.

eng: Even today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts.
local: Haari ni nyuma, saŋga kɛrɛ bo bu kan kadasim n bo Nyɛmɛ ma Moyisi kɛrɛɛri bu ni, i yoori bu ka sanvuin n toko kata bu sunsunni su wɔ.

eng: But whenever someone turns back to the Lord, the veil is removed.
local: Ama, bu koro yi i, kabo ba kɛrɛ i fa kɔ Moyisi jɔrɛ n su ni, ka, Saŋga bo i fa i nyunu wɔ Mibiɛ ni, sanvuin n kata má i nyunu­awa kun.

eng: The Lord is the Spirit, and where the Lords Spirit is, there is freedom.
local: Tɔ, deke n bo bu kɛrɛɛri i ka Mibiɛ n ni, bu kɛrɛ niiri i Awiɛwiɛ n wɔ. Deke bo Mibiɛ n Awiɛwiɛ n wo ni, dɔ ye minɛ nya bu tii a.

eng: All of us are looking with unveiled faces at the glory of the Lord as if we were looking in a mirror. We are being transformed into that same image from one degree of glory to the next degree of glory. This comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
local: Yɛrɛ yiri, ya la má sanvuin bo i kata ya nyunu. Kabo ya niɛ ŋu deke niɛɛnu nu ni, sɔ ye ya niɛ ŋu Mibiɛ n jirima woo­wa n a, na i ma ya woo­wa kaaki kabo i ti ni, na ya sunya i jirima woo­wa ni, subuka su saŋga kɛrɛ. Ahin n fite Mibiɛ n Awiɛwiɛ n dɔ ɔ.

eng: This is why we dont get discouraged, given that we received this ministry in the same way that we received Gods mercy.
local: Nyini ti, kabo Nyɛmɛ asi ya aŋunfɔ na wɔ fa ahin juma n ama ya ni, ya ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki má ya.

eng: Instead, we reject secrecy and shameful actions. We dont use deception, and we dont tamper with Gods word. Instead, we commend ourselves to everyones conscience in the sight of God by the public announcement of the truth.
local: Yɛrɛ, ya kete deke kɛrɛ bo i ti nyinsɛ sa na bu kata i su asiri nu ni, ya dadaka má be, ya kaaki má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni. Ama, ya kan nahɔrɛ n fofoe nu wɔ, nyini ma ya fa kere sɔnɔ kɛrɛ na i si i sunsunni nu kabo ya wo ti Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

eng: And even if our gospel is veiled, it is veiled to those who are on the road to destruction.
local: Na haari nzɛn deke be akata labari kpa n bo ya kan i n su, bɛrɛ n bo bu suka ŋgbɛɛn ni ŋgumi dɔ, ye deke be akata i su a.

eng: The god of this age has blinded the minds of those who dont have faith so they couldnt see the light of the gospel that reveals Christs glory. Christ is the image of God.
local: Dama Sitana bo i ti durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ nyɛmɛ n ata bu nyi bu sunsunni nu na má bu yo yarada, adena má bu ŋu labari kpa n dɛɛ wein n fa kɔ Kristo jirima woo­wa n su, yiri bo i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ ti ni.

eng: We dont preach about ourselves. Instead, we preach about Jesus Christ as Lord, and we describe ourselves as your slaves for Jesus sake.
local: Ya kan má yɛrɛ ni ya tii jɔrɛ wɔ, ama, ya kan Yesu Kristo jɔrɛ a, ka yiri ti sɔnɔ kɛrɛ Mibiɛ a. Yɛrɛ, ya ti am dɛɛ akɔɔm wɔ, yiri Yesu dɛɛ ti.

eng: God said that light should shine out of the darkness. He is the same one who shone in our hearts to give us the light of the knowledge of Gods glory in the face of Jesus Christ.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ bo i yo seeri ka, Ma wein ta fite awosin nu ni, yiri ma i wein ata ya ahore su a, na i ma ya si i jirima woo­wa n bo i ta fite Yesu Kristo nyunu­awa dika ni.

eng: But we have this treasure in clay pots so that the awesome power belongs to God and doesnt come from us.
local: Ahin jɔrɛ n ti ajɛkɛ bo ya la i Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti ka dundu bo ba fa yakati ata ya n ye ya la ahin ajɛkɛ n a. I ti sɔ ɔ, adena minɛ ŋu i na bu si ka nyini yiko n bo i la má aŋusu n ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wɔ, i fite má ya dɔ.

eng: We are experiencing all kinds of trouble, but we arent crushed. We are confused, but we arent depressed.
local: Bu miɛ ya sɔ ɔ deke kɛrɛ nu, ama, Nyɛmɛ yi ya nu wɔ. Ya tanda sɔ ɔ, ama, ya yi má tama.

eng: We are harassed, but we arent abandoned. We are knocked down, but we arent knocked out.
local: Bu kere ya wuwuruwa sɔ ɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ yaki má ya. Bu bo ya ji asiɛŋgu sɔ ɔ, ama, ya ka má ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: We always carry Jesus death around in our bodies so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ ya nati ŋu yiwee n bo Yesu wuuri i n be wɔ, adena minɛm koro ŋu ŋgɔɔ n gusu bo Yesu la i n be, ya ŋuniɛ nu.

eng: We who are alive are always being handed over to death for Jesus sake so that Jesus life can also be seen in our bodies that are dying.
local: Nahɔrɛ, yɛrɛ bo ya la ŋgɔɔ ni, saŋga kɛrɛ bu fa ya wura yiwee woo­biri wɔ, Yesu dɛɛ ti, adena bu koro ŋu ŋgɔɔ n gusu bo Yesu la i n ya ŋuniɛ nu.

eng: So death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
local: Tɔ, yɛrɛ, ya wo yiwee nu wɔ, ama, ambɛrɛ wo ŋgɔɔ nu wɔ, Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: We have the same faithful spirit as what is written in scripture: I had faith, and so I spoke. We also have faith, and so we also speak.
local: Ya jɔrɛ n ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu wɔ, ka, M yo yoori yarada a, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m jɔjɔɔri sɔ n a. Tɔ, yɛrɛ gusu ayo nyini yarada kun n wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ya jɔjɔ sɔ n a.

eng: We do this because we know that the one who raised the Lord Jesus will also raise us with Jesus, and he will bring us into his presence along with you.
local: Dama ya si tenle su ka Nyɛmɛ bo i teŋgeeri Yesu i yiwee n nu ni, yiri sáŋga teŋge ya ni Yesu yiwee n nu a, na i fá ya ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ kɔ i nyunu.

eng: All these things are for your benefit. As grace increases to benefit more and more people, it will cause gratitude to increase, which results in Gods glory.
local: Ahin n kɛrɛ ma am nya kpɛrɛ a, adena minɛ dɔŋgu sunya Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma ni subuka su, na i la má aŋusu, nyini ma bu suda Nyɛmɛ asi subuka su a, na i wura Nyɛmɛ kpiri.

eng: So we arent depressed. But even if our bodies are breaking down on the outside, the person that we are on the inside is being renewed every day.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ya ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki má ya. Haari ni bo ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa sugbaraki ni, ama, ya ahore su, ya sunya faŋga fɔfɔrɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ale kɛrɛ bo i cin.

eng: Our temporary minor problems are producing an eternal stockpile of glory for us that is beyond all comparison.
local: Ahin waharam bo ya sudi bu ni, bu ti má pui, na bu cɛ má, nyini ni má ya nya jirima woo­wa bo i la má aŋusu na i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n a.

eng: We dont focus on the things that can be seen but on the things that cant be seen. The things that can be seen dont last, but the things that cant be seen are eternal.
local: Ya fa má ya nyu­mbaa kpama má dekem bo ya ŋu bu n su, ama, dekem bo ya ŋu má bu ni, ya fa ya nyu­mbaa kpama bɛrɛ su a, dama bo ya koro ŋu i ni, i cɛ má, ama, bo ya koro ŋu má i ni, i wo bɛrɛ wɔ, saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: We know that if the tent that we live in on earth is torn down, we have a building from God. Its a house that isnt handmade, which is eternal and located in heaven.
local: Tɔ, ya si durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ aŋuniɛ­mbaa n jɔrɛ, i ti ka nambue wɔ, nzɛn wɔ saaki, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la ataari kpa bo Nyɛmɛ ata i afa ase i. Má adamande taari i a, nyini awuru n wo aŋgoro wɔ, i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: We groan while we live in this residence. We really want to dress ourselves with our building from heaven.
local: Kabo i ti sɔ na ya toko wo fɛn ni, ya wo waraki su wɔ. Ya ahore su, ya koro ka Nyɛmɛ fa ya wura ya awuru n bo i fite aŋgoro n nu wɔ, na i kata ya ŋu.

eng: since we assume that when we take off this tent, we wont find out that we are naked.
local: Ya koro i sɔ ɔ, na ya wura i ka sutura na má ya nati ya bu ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Yes, while we are in this tent we groan, because we are weighed down. We want to be dressed not undressed, so that what is dying can be swallowed up by life.
local: Yɛrɛ bo ya toko wo ahin nambue n nu ni, ya waraki wɔ, na ya wo ahore tanda nu sɔ ɔ lɛɛ. I ti má ka ya koro ka ya yi i se wɔ, ama, ya koro ka ya wura aŋgoro dɛɛ n nu wɔ, na i kata ya ŋu, adena Nyɛmɛ bá kaaki ahin aŋuniɛ­mbaa n bo i wu ni, na i ma ya i dɛɛ ŋgɔɔ ni.

eng: Now the one who prepared us for this very thing is God, and God gave us the Spirit as a down payment for our home.
local: Nyɛmɛ ni i tii adu mɔ asɛsɛ nyini deke n su ase ya a. Na wɔ fa i Awiɛwiɛ n ama ya, na i kere kabo i má ya deke n kɛrɛ bo i seeri ka i fá ma i minɛm ni.

eng: So we are always confident, because we know that while we are living in the body, we are away from our home with the Lord.
local: Tɔ, nyini ma ya la ahoresi saŋga kɛrɛ n a. Ya si tenle su ka kabo ya toko wo ahin aŋuniɛ­mbaa n nu ni, ya nya wo má Mibiɛ n dika n nu lɛɛ.

eng: We live by faith and not by sight.
local: Kisa ya la yarada ye ya nati a, má ka ya nyu­mbaa ye ya fa ŋu asi nati a.

eng: We are confident, and we would prefer to leave the body and to be at home with the Lord.
local: Yɛrɛ, ya la ahoresi n dɛɛ ti, ye ya ŋa ya yaki ahin aŋuniɛ­mbaa n a, na ya kɔ awuru Mibiɛ n dɔ.

eng: So our goal is to be acceptable to him, whether we are at home or away from home.
local: Nyini ti, yɛrɛ yiri, nzɛn ya wo ya awuru n nu, wara ya wo má nu, ya boro ya nyi ka ya yo sa bo i sun Mibiɛ n nu wɔ.

eng: We all must appear before Christ in court so that each person can be paid back for the things that were done while in the body, whether they were good or bad.
local: Dama wɔ yo daka ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bá jina Kristo nyunu, i jɔrɛ dii­biri ni, adena i mi nya i ŋkatɔ fa kɔ dekem bo i yoori bu i ŋgɔɔ n su ni, bo i ti kpa, ni bo i ti má kpa ni.

eng: So we try to persuade people, since we know what it means to fear the Lord. We are well known by God, and I hope that in your heart we are well known by you as well.
local: Ya si kabo i ti na ya soro Mibiɛ n ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye ya boro ya nyi na ya ma minɛm kaaki bu sunsunni fa kɔ i jɔrɛ n su a. Nyɛmɛ si kabo ya woo­wa kɛrɛ ti barasu. Na m la tama ka am sunsunni nu, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ si ya woo­wa.

eng: Since we work together with him, we are also begging you not to receive the grace of God in vain.
local: Yɛrɛ bo ya ni Nyɛmɛ saŋga yo juma ni, ya sere am wɔ, ahɔrɔma n bo am ade i ni, má am ma i ka ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: We arent trying to commend ourselves to you again. Instead, we are giving you an opportunity to be proud of us so that you could answer those who take pride in superficial appearance, and not in what is in the heart.
local: Ya sukaaki kan kere má am bekun kabo ya ti wɔ, ama, ya sukere am deke bo i ti na am koro fa ya fɔ n wɔ, adena nzɛn minɛm bá koro ka bu fɔ deke bo bu koro ŋu i n su, na i fite má ahore­mbaa su, dama bu koro ka bu bu am ŋgbɛɛn n dɛɛ ti, tɔ, am koro bɔ bu nu ni jɔrɛ kpa.

eng: If we are crazy, its for Gods sake. If we are rational, its for your sake.
local: Nzɛn bu ŋa ya bo fiɛ wɔ, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ti ye ya bo fiɛ a, ye nzɛn ya nyunu ti koroo­wa gusu a, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye ya nyunu ti koroo­wa a.

eng: He says, I listened to you at the right time, and I helped you on the day of salvation. Look, now is the right time! Look, now is the day of salvation!.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Saŋga kpa n ba juuri ni, ye m deeri am sere a, na tii­de cɛɛn n ye m bukaari am a. Am ti fɛn, kisa ti saŋga kpa n a. Am ti fɛn, kisa ti tii­de cɛɛn n a.

eng: We dont give anyone any reason to be offended about anything so that our ministry wont be criticized.
local: Ya sukanda má be atin wɔ, adena má be jaraki ya fa kɔ ya juma n bo ya yo i n su.

eng: The love of Christ controls us, because we have concluded this: one died for the sake of all; therefore, all died.
local: Koro n bo Kristo fa koro ya ni, yiri niɛ ya su a, dama ya yo ya sunsunni ka sɔnɔ kun n bo i wu maari sɔnɔ kɛrɛ n dɛɛ ti ni, nyini la ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ awu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: He died for the sake of all so that those who are alive should live not for themselves but for the one who died for them and was raised.
local: I wu maari sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ, adena bo bu wo ni ŋgɔɔ n bita bu ŋgɔɔ n ka i ti má bu dɛɛ, ama, i ti i dɛɛ wɔ, yiri n bo i wu maari bu na wɔ kaaki ateŋge i yiwee n nu bekun ni.

eng: Instead, we commend ourselves as ministers of God in every way. We did this with our great endurance through problems, disasters, and stressful situations.
local: Ama, deke n kɛrɛ nu, ya kere ka ya ti Nyɛmɛ amusui­su­fɔm wɔ. Dama haari nzɛn ya wo wahara nu, ni kekereke nu, ni anyum­boro nu, ya mindi de i wɔ.

eng: We went through beatings, imprisonments, and riots. We experienced hard work, sleepless nights, and hunger.
local: Haari na ba bo ya, na ba nyi ya dan­saraka, na ba di aluwa ya busu, na ya yo juma kekereke, na ya kpiɛ daafiri, na ya da ahɔɛ.

eng: So then, from this point on we wont recognize people by human standards. Even though we used to know Christ by human standards, that isnt how we know him now.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, fa i kisa fa kɔ, kabo durunya n nu­fɔm n bu sɔnɔ ni, yɛrɛ, ya bu má be sɔ kun, haari ni bo ya yo buuri Kristo mmɔɔ sɔ ni, kisa ya bu má i sɔ kun.

eng: So then, if anyone is in Christ, that person is part of the new creation. The old things have gone away, and look, new things have arrived!.
local: Nyini yiri, nzɛn be ni Kristo ayo kun, i mi n ayo deke fɔfɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ, i woo­wa dawa n asin, na i deke n kɛrɛ akaaki fɔfɔrɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: We displayed purity, knowledge, patience, and generosity. We served with the Holy Spirit, genuine love.
local: nyini n kɛrɛ ni, na ya kere ka ya woo­wa ti casi wɔ, na ya la si Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na ya mindi, na ya yo kpa, na ya wo ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni, na ya la koro nahɔrɛ su.

eng: telling the truth, and Gods power. We carried the weapons of righteousness in our right hand and our left hand.
local: Ya kan nahɔrɛ jɔrɛ wɔ, na ya yo juma n ni Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n nu. Ya bita biɛsɔya neŋge bo i ti sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, i wo ya saa fɔmbɔrɔ, ni ya saa biɛ nu wɔ.

eng: All of these new things are from God, who reconciled us to himself through Christ and who gave us the ministry of reconciliation.
local: Nyini n kɛrɛ fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ. Yiri ama Kristo ayo ya ni yiri ni i tii afiɛn dɔŋgu a, ye wɔ ma ya juma n bo i yo i ni minɛm afiɛn dɔŋgu.

eng: We were treated with honor and dishonor and with verbal abuse and good evaluation. We were seen as both fake and real.
local: Bu wura ya kpiri wɔ, na bu kaaki yo ya kaan. Bu kansi ya a, na bu kaaki jaraki ya. Bu ŋa ya dadaka minɛm wɔ, ama, bu kaaki ŋa ya kan nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: as unknown and well known, as dying—and look, we are alive! We were seen as punished but not killed.
local: Ya ti ka minɛm bo be si má ya wɔ, ama, deke kɛrɛ minɛm si ya sɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Ya ti ka ya wu wɔ, ama, ya la ŋgɔɔ sɔ ɔ lɛɛ. Bu bo ya ni anyum­boro sɔ ɔ, ama, ya wu má.

eng: as going through pain but always happy, as poor but making many rich, and as having nothing but owning everything.
local: Ya wo ahoresaaki nu sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ama, saŋga kɛrɛ ya la ahorejɔ ɔ. Ya wo ka ya ti yaari­fɔm wɔ, ama, ya fa ajɛkɛ ma minɛm dɔŋgu wɔ. Ya ti ka ya la má pui wɔ, ama, deke n kɛrɛ ti ya dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: In other words, God was reconciling the world to himself through Christ, by not counting peoples sins against them. He has trusted us with this message of reconciliation.
local: Nyini la ka, Nyɛmɛ ma Kristo yo i ni durunya n nu­fɔm afiɛn dɔŋgu a, na i teŋge má bu satiɛ jɔrɛ su, haari kaan sɔ, ye wɔ fa nyini afiɛn dɔŋgu­yo n jɔrɛ n awura ya saa nu, ka ya kan i.

eng: So we are ambassadors who represent Christ. God is negotiating with you through us. We beg you as Christs representatives, Be reconciled to God.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ya ti sunma­fɔm bo ya jina Kristo jaa nu wɔ. I ti ka Nyɛmɛ ni i tii sufa ya susere am jande a. Ya jina Kristo dɛɛ jaa nu susere am wɔ, ka, am ma yiri yo am ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu.

eng: Corinthians, we have spoken openly to you, and our hearts are wide open.
local: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ ya danfo Kɔrinto­fɔm, ya fa má deke be afiɛ má am su, ya teke ya ahore­mbaa ama am.

eng: There are no limits to the affection that we feel for you. You are the ones who placed boundaries on your affection for us.
local: Yɛrɛ, ya kpiɛ má am ya koro, ambɛrɛ yiri, am akpiɛ ya am koro.

eng: God caused the one who didnt know sin to be sin for our sake so that through him we could become the righteousness of God.
local: Yiri Kristo n bo i la má satiɛ be ni, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ ma wɔ de satiɛ­yo­fɔm n jinaa­biri a, yɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, adena ya kaaki minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ de bu ka bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ.

eng: But as a fair trade—Im talking to you like you are children—open your hearts wide too.
local: Am ti ka m nbaam dɛɛ ti ye m suse am sɔ n a, ka am gusu teke am ahore­mbaa ma ya, kabo ya teke ya dɛɛ ama am ni.

eng: Dont be tied up as equal partners with people who dont believe. What does righteousness share with that which is outside the Law? What relationship does light have with darkness?.
local: Má am fa am ŋu bo minɛm bo bu yo má Kristo yarada n su, dama bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n ni ketema­fɔ ni, i yo sɛ ye bu koro saŋga wo a? Wara wein ni awosin yo sɛ koro saŋga wo a? Ai, i koro yo má.

eng: What harmony does Christ have with Satan? What does a believer have in common with someone who doesnt believe?.
local: Nzu anɔɔsɛ wo Kristo ni Sitana afiɛn a? Wara nzu tana wo sɔnɔ bo wɔ yo Kristo yarada n ni bo wɔ yo má i yarada n afiɛn a? Ai, bu la má tana.

eng: What agreement can there be between Gods temple and idols? Because we are the temple of the living God. Just as God said, I live with them, and I will move among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Nzu anɔɔsɛ wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n ni amɔɛm afiɛn a? Ai, bu la má anɔɔsɛ be. Yɛrɛ ti ka Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n a, ye yiri Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n wo nu n a. Kabo Nyɛmɛ ni i tii ase ni, ka, M wó bu dɔ, na m ni bɛrɛ sáŋga nati, na m yó bu Nyɛmɛ, na bu yó m minɛm.

eng: Therefore, come out from among them and be separated, says the Lord. Dont touch what is unclean. Then I will welcome you. .
local: Nyini ti, Nyɛmɛ akaaki ase ka, Am fite yaki bu, na am kpaaki yaki bu, Micɛra ni seeri sɔ a. Na má am fa am ŋu mata deke bo i ti má casi, na m dé am.

eng: I will be a father to you, and you will be my sons and daughters, says the Lord Almighty .
local: Na m yó am Si, na ambɛrɛ gusu yó m dɛɛ nbaam. Kabo Micɛra Kpiri n ase lɛɛ.

eng: My dear friends, since we have these promises, lets cleanse ourselves from anything that contaminates our body or spirit so that we make our holiness complete in the fear of God.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, ahin deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i yó ma ya n dɛɛ ti, am ma ya kpakpara ya ŋu fite deke kɛrɛ bo i ma ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa n ni ya awiɛwiɛ n yo fin ni, na am ma ya soro Nyɛmɛ na ya woo­wa kɛrɛ ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni.

eng: Make room in your hearts for us. We didnt do anything wrong to anyone. We didnt ruin anyone. We didnt take advantage of anyone.
local: Am teke am ahore­mbaa na am de ya. Ya do má am be ŋgasi, ya saaki má am be, ya sisi má am be.

eng: Im not saying this to make you feel guilty. Ive already said that you are in our hearts so that we die and live together with you.
local: Deke bo i ti na m se ahin sɔ ni, i ti má ka m sujaraki am wɔ, dama ma du mɔ ase am ka am jɔrɛ wo ya ahore su, ya ni ambɛrɛ ti kun wɔ, haari yiwee nu wara ŋgɔɔ nu.

eng: I have every confidence in you. Im terribly proud of you. Im filled with encouragement. Im overwhelmed with happiness while in the middle of our problems.
local: M la ahoresi agaya fa kɔ am dɔ, na m fa am fɔ agaya, na m la ahoreda agaya ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, haari ni wahara n bo ya di i n kɛrɛ ni, m la ahorejɔ bo i la má aŋusu.

eng: Even after we arrived in Macedonia, we couldnt rest physically. We were surrounded by problems. There was external conflict, and there were internal fears.
local: Dama saŋga bo ya ba juuri Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu ni, ya nya má ŋumi­yi haari kaan sɔ mmɔɔ, ama, ya ŋuuri kekereke wɔ deke kɛrɛ nu. Minɛm fa aluwa kpura ya sɔ ɔ, na sɛrɛ suti ya, ya ahore su.

eng: However, God comforts people who are discouraged, and he comforted us by Titus arrival.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ yiri, yiri dada minɛm bo bu ahore asaaki n ahore a, yiri adada ya ahore a, Titosi baawa n dɛɛ ti.

eng: We werent comforted only by his arrival but also by the comfort he had received from you. He told us about your desire to see me, how you were sorry, and about your concern for me, so that I was even happier.
local: Má ka i baawa ni ŋgumi dɛɛ ti a, ama, ahoreda n bo am ni yiri kɛrɛ nyaari i n dɛɛ ti a. Wɔ se ya kabo am wara ado m na am koro ka am ŋu m barasu ni, ni kabo am ahore asaaki fa kɔ jɔrɛ n su ni, ni kabo am ahore abi m busu barasu ni, nyini ma m ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su n a.

eng: Even though my letter hurt you, I dont regret it. Well—I did regret it just a bit because I see that that letter made you sad, though only for a short time.
local: Haari nzɛn kadasi n bo m yo kɛrɛ maari am n asaaki am ahore, ma yo má m si i n sɔ, fa kɔ nyini n dika. Nzɛn má m ŋuuri kabo nyini kadasi n ama am ahore asaaki, haari ni bo wɔ cɛ má ni, má m yoori m si i n sɔ.

eng: Now Im glad—not because you were sad but because you were made sad enough to change your hearts and lives. You felt godly sadness so that no one was harmed by us in any way.
local: Kisa m ahore ajɔ, má ka am ahore asaaki n dɛɛ ti a, ama, am ahoresaaki ni, wɔ ma am akaaki am wo n dɛɛ ti a. Nyini ahoresaaki ni, yiri ama am akaaki kabo Nyɛmɛ koro n a, adena má am kpaasi fieo, yɛrɛ dɛɛ ti.

eng: Godly sadness produces a changed heart and life that leads to salvation and leaves no regrets, but sorrow under the influence of the world produces death.
local: Dama ahoresaaki bo i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni, yiri ma ya kaaki ya wo ye ya nya tii­de a, na ya yo má m si i n sɔ a. Ama, durunya n nu dɛɛ ahoresaaki n fa yiwee ba.

eng: Look at what this very experience of godly sadness has produced in you: such enthusiasm, what a desire to clear yourselves of blame, such indignation, what fear, what purpose, such concern, what justice! In everything you have shown yourselves to be innocent in the matter.
local: Am niɛ sa bo Nyɛmɛ ma am ayo i a, nyini ahoresaaki n dɛɛ ti, kabo am nyunu aboro ka am yo jɔrɛ n dɔŋgu na má be jaraki am ni, ni kabo yaa ti am fa kɔ yiri n bo wɔ do ŋgasi n su ni, ni kabo jɔrɛ n ama sɛrɛ ati am ni, ni kabo am wara ado m na am koro ka am ŋu m ni, ni kabo am ahore abi m busu ni, ni kabo am nyunu aboro ka am fa nahɔrɛ cin i sui ni. Deke n kɛrɛ nu, am akere ka am la má ŋgasi be lɛɛ, fa kɔ nyini jɔrɛ n su.

eng: So although I wrote to you, it wasnt for the sake of the one who did wrong, or for the sake of the one who was wronged, but to show you your own enthusiasm for us in the sight of God.
