local: Deke bo i ti na m yo kɛrɛɛri kadasi n maari am ni, má yiri n bo i doori ŋgasi n dɛɛ ti a, wara sɔnɔ n bo wɔ do i ŋgasi n dɛɛ ti a, ama, deke n bo i ti na m yo kɛrɛɛri i sɔ n la ka, adena Nyɛmɛ ma am si kabo am cɛcɛ wo ya nu ni anyum­boro su barasu ni.

eng: Because of this we have been encouraged. And in addition to our own encouragement, we were even more pleased at how happy Titus was. His mind has been put at rest by all of you.
local: Nyini n kɛrɛ adada ya ahore a. Má nyini ahoreda n ŋgumi ye ya la i a, ama, deke bo i ma ya ahore ajɔ agaya n la kabo Titosi ahore ajɔ ni, dama ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ adada i ahore a.

eng: If Ive bragged about you to him in any way, I havent been embarrassed. Instead, our bragging to Titus has also been proven to be true, just like everything we said to you was true.
local: Nyini ma ya ahore ajɔ sɔ a, dama ma du mɔ afa am afɔ kabo am ti barasu n akere yiri Titosi, na am ama ma di má nyinsɛ. Kabo jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo ya kan kereeri am n ti nahɔrɛ ni, sɔ ye minɛm koro ŋu kabo ya fɔɔri am ŋusu jɔrɛ n kereeri Titosi n gusu n ti nahɔrɛ a.

eng: His devotion to you is growing even more as he remembers how all of you were obedient when you welcomed him with fear and trembling.
local: Deke bo i ma yiri Titosi koro am agaya ni, maala bo i teŋge su kabo am kɛrɛ deeri i nɔaniɛ n ni, ni kabo am deeri i ni am saa nnyɔ n na sɛrɛ ti am agaya na am ŋuniɛ subobo am ni.

eng: Im happy, because I can completely depend on you.
local: M ahore ti jɔɔ­wa, dama deke n kɛrɛ nu, m la ahoresi fa kɔ am dɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want to let you know about the grace of God that was given to the churches of Macedonia.
local: Tɔ, m ninbaam, ya koro ka am si kabo Nyɛmɛ ama Kristo jamam bo bu wo Makɛdoniya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu n adi i ahɔrɔma barasu ni.

eng: While they were being tested by many problems, their extra amount of happiness and their extreme poverty resulted in a surplus of rich generosity.
local: Saŋga bo bu wo wahara ni kekereke nu agaya ni, na ba kere kabo bu ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su, na ba ce aceerim beberebe ni bu ahore­mbaa kun, haari ni bo yaari ati bu agaya ni.

eng: I assure you that they gave what they could afford and even more than they could afford, and they did it voluntarily.
local: M koro di daani ka bu maari bu faŋga nyaari wɔ, haari ba ma atara kabo má bu ma ni mmɔɔ. Bu koroya­nu.

eng: They urgently begged us for the privilege of sharing in this service for the saints.
local: na ba sere ya jande ka ya ma bɛrɛ mmɔɔ nya atin na bu saa wo nyini juma n nu, bo ya de aceerim fa ma Nyɛmɛ minɛm ni.

eng: They even exceeded our expectations, because they gave themselves to the Lord first and to us, consistent with Gods will.
local: Haari na ba yo akɔ atara kabo má ya sunsun ka bu yo ni. Bu du mɔ faari bu ŋu maari Mibiɛ n wɔ ka, na ba nya fa bu ŋu ama yɛrɛ gusu, kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni.

eng: As a result, we challenged Titus to finish this work of grace with you the way he had started it.
local: Tɔ, kabo Titosi ti sɔnɔ bo i boori ahin aceeri juma n bu am nu ni, wɔ yo daka ka ya sere i jande na i suyo i sɔ ma am, haari i kɔ wie.

eng: Be the best in this work of grace in the same way that you are the best in everything, such as faith, speech, knowledge, total commitment, and the love we inspired in you.
local: Ambɛrɛ, am boro am nyi deke n kɛrɛ nu wɔ, bɛrɛ la yarada n bo am la i ni, ni kabo am koro jɔjɔ ni, ni kabo am la si ni, ni kabo am cɛcɛ wo deke n kɛrɛ nu ni, ni kabo am la koro ma ya ni. Kabo i ti sɔ ni, wɔ yo daka ka am boro am nyi yo ahin aceeri juma n gusu.

eng: Im not giving an order, but by mentioning the commitment of others, Im trying to prove the authenticity of your love also.
local: I ti má ka m suma am nɔɔ ka am yo sɔ ɔ, ama, m koro ka m kere am kabo nyiŋgam la koro ni anyum­boro na bu buka n wɔ, adena m yo am na m niɛ ka am la koro nahɔrɛ su.

eng: You know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ. Although he was rich, he became poor for your sakes, so that you could become rich through his poverty.
local: Am si juma kpa n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo yoori i ni, i yo ti ajɛkɛ­fɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ama, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti na wɔ kaaki yaari­fɔ, adena am kaaki ajɛkɛ­fɔm Nyɛmɛ dɔ, kabo wɔ kaaki yaari­fɔ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Im giving you my opinion about this. Its to your advantage to do this, since you not only started to do it last year but you wanted to do it too.
local: Ahin jɔrɛ n yiri, afara, ambɛrɛ du mɔ boori nyini juma n bu a, má ka am du mɔ boori i bu ni ŋgumi, ama, ni kabo am koroori ka am yo i n gusu ni, nyini ti, fɔ bo m koro ka m fa tu am n la ka.

eng: Now finish the job as well so that you finish it with as much enthusiasm as you started, given what you can afford.
local: i ti ye ni ka am yo juma n bo am ŋa am yo i n kɔ wie kisa, adena kabo am yo la anyum­boro am sunsunni nu, na am koro ka am yo i ni, sɔ ye am kere anyum­boro a, na am fa deke n bo am la i n fa yo i wie.

eng: A gift is appreciated because of what a person can afford, not because of what that person cant afford, if its apparent that its done willingly.
local: Nzɛn be la anyum­boro na i koro ka i ce aceeri, Nyɛmɛ de i aceeri n fa kɔ deke bo i koro ce n su wɔ, má deke bo i koro ce má ni.

eng: It isnt that we want others to have financial ease and you financial difficulties, but its a matter of equality.
local: I ti má ka ya koro ka ya yi nbem fie nu, na ambɛrɛ fie wɔ. Ai, ama, ya koro ka i sɛ su ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: At the present moment, your surplus can fill their deficit so that in the future their surplus can fill your deficit. In this way there is equality.
local: Tɔ, kisa am la i, na nbem la má i ni, am buka bu. Na nzɛn bu gusu bá la i, na am la má i, bu gusu buka am. Sɔ ye i sɛ su ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ a.

eng: As it is written, The one who gathered more didnt have too much, and the one who gathered less didnt have too little. .
local: I ti kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu wɔ, ka, Bo i tambuuri beberebe ni, wɔ sunnu asin má su, ye bo i tambuuri kaan n gusu, wɔ kpiɛ má i su.

eng: But thank God, who put the same commitment that I have for you in Titus heart.
local: Ya da Nyɛmɛ asi, yiri ma Titosi nyunu aboro na i cɛcɛ wo am dɔ a, kabo mini mmɔɔ dɛɛ ti ni.

eng: Not only has he accepted our challenge but hes on his way to see you voluntarily, and hes excited.
local: Ya sere yiri Titosi ka i ba am dɔ, na wɔ soro su, ama, yiri ni i tii dɛɛ koroya­nu ye i ŋa i kɔ a, dama i cɛcɛ du mɔ wo am dɔ.

eng: We are sending the brother who is famous in all the churches because of his work for the gospel along with him.
local: Bo i buka i su, ya susunma ya niwa be gusu am dɔ. I ti sɔnɔ bo i yo labari kpa n juma a, nyini dɛɛ ti, Kristo jama n kɛrɛ kansi i wɔ.

eng: In addition to this, he is chosen by the churches to be our traveling companion in this work of grace, which we are taking care of for the sake of the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our desire to help.
local: Má nyini ŋgumi, ama, yiri ye Kristo jama n kɛrɛ ayi i ka i susu ya su buka ya a, na ya niɛ ahin aceeri cee­wa juma n su n a. Ahin juma n wura Nyɛmɛ kpiri wɔ, na i kere kabo ya koro ka ya buka minɛm ni anyum­boro ni.

eng: We are trying to avoid being blamed by anyone for the way we take care of this large amount of money.
local: Ya koro má ka be jaraki ya fa kɔ kabo ya niɛ ahin aceeri beberebe n su ni.

eng: We care about doing the right thing, not only in the Lords eyes but also in the eyes of other people.
local: Nyini ti, ya boro ya nyi wɔ, na ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, má Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi dɔ, ama, adamande mmɔɔ dɔ.

eng: We are sending our brother with them. We have tested his commitment in many ways and many times. Now hes even more committed, because he has so much confidence in you.
local: Ya kaaki susunma ya niwa be buka bu su. I ti sɔnɔ bo ya yo i aniɛ atin barasu nu, aŋu ka i la anyum­boro fa kɔ deke dɔŋgu su. Kisa ya ŋu ka i la anyum­boro tara sɔ, dama i la ahoresi fa kɔ am dɔ.

eng: If there is any question about Titus, he is my partner and coworker among you. If there is any question about our brothers, they are the churches apostles and an honor to Christ.
local: Titosi yiri, i ti m mata­fɔ bo m ni yiri saŋga yo juma ma am wɔ. Ya ninbaa nnyɔm bɛrɛ yiri, bu ti minɛm bo Kristo jamam n asunma bu wɔ, na bu kaaki wura Kristo kpiri.

eng: So show them the proof of your love and the reason we are so proud of you, in such a way that the churches can see it.
local: Nyini ti, am yi am ŋu fofoe nu, na am kere Kristo jamam ni kabo am la koro ni, na bu si deke n bo i ti na ya fa am fɔ ni.

eng: Its unnecessary for me to write to you about this service for Gods people.
local: Tɔ, aceerim bo am fa kɔ buka Nyɛmɛ minɛm ni, mini dɔ, wɔ ti má ye ka m kɛrɛ i jɔrɛ.

eng: I know about your willingness to help. I brag about you to the Macedonians, saying, Greece has been ready since last year, and your enthusiasm has motivated most of them.
local: Dama m si kabo am koro ka am buka barasu ni, haari ma du mɔ afa am afɔ Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu­fɔm dɔ, ka ambɛrɛ Akaya awɔrɛ n nu­fɔm n adu mɔ akpa am ŋu haari afara kɛrɛ. Kabo am nyunu aboro na am koro ka am ce ni, wɔ yo bu dɔŋgu gusu ŋuniɛ nyanyaka ye bu koro ka bu ce n a.

eng: But Im sending the brothers so that our bragging about you in this case wont be empty words, and so that you can be prepared, just as I keep telling them you will be.
local: Nyini ye m susunma ahin ya ninbaam am dɔ n a, adena kabo ya fa am afɔ akere bu ni, na má i ka ŋgbɛɛn, ama, am yo siriya, kabo ma du mɔ ase bu ka am yo siriya ni.

eng: If some Macedonians should come with me and find out that you arent ready, we would be embarrassed as far as this project goes.
local: Má nyini, nzɛn Makɛdoniya­fɔm nbem suuri m su baari, na bu ba ŋu ka am nya yo má siriya, i ti ya nyinsɛ wɔ, ya kan má am dɛɛ yiri, kabo ya yo am yarada n dɛɛ ti.

eng: This is why I thought it was necessary to encourage the brothers to go to you ahead of time and arrange in advance the generous gift you have already promised. I want it to be a real gift from you. I dont want you to feel like you are being forced to give anything.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ma ŋu ka wɔ yo daka ka m sere ya ninbaam ni, na bu du m mɔ am dɔ, na bu buka am, na am sɛsɛ am aceeri n bo am yo sereeri ka am yi fa ma n su. Nyini ma i yo koro aceeri a, i ti má ka bu miɛ am wɔ.

eng: What I mean is this: the one who sows a small number of seeds will also reap a small crop, and the one who sows a generous amount of seeds will also reap a generous crop.
local: Am teŋge su, ka sɔnɔ bo i dɔ kaan, i bá kpiɛ kaan wɔ. Na sɔnɔ bo i dɔ dɔŋgu, i bá kpiɛ dɔŋgu wɔ.

eng: Everyone should give whatever they have decided in their heart. They shouldnt give with hesitation or because of pressure. God loves a cheerful giver.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ce aceeri n kabo wɔ du mɔ asunsun i ahore su ka i ce barasu ni, má ka bu miɛ i wɔ, ama, i mi ma ni ahorejɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ koro bo i ce ni ahorejɔ ɔ.

eng: God has the power to provide you with more than enough of every kind of grace. That way, you will have everything you need always and in everything to provide more than enough for every kind of good work.
local: Yiri Nyɛmɛ ni, yiri koro ma am nya i aceeri barasu kɛrɛ a, na am nya deke kɛrɛ bo am koro saŋga kɛrɛ, na i sɛ am su, na am nya i buka su, adena am koro yo juma kpa kɛrɛ.

eng: As it is written, He scattered everywhere; he gave to the needy; his righteousness remains forever. .
local: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Wɔ sandi i aceerim afa ama yaari­fɔm ni, na i kpa­yo n wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: The one who supplies seed for planting and bread for eating will supply and multiply your seed and will increase your crop, which is righteousness.
local: Nyɛmɛ ti bo i ma dɔdɔɔri­fɔ ni alɛ­mbaa n a, na i kaaki ma minɛm diire na bu di a, yiri ma am alɛ­mbaa n a, na i ma i sunnu buka su, na i kaaki ma am kpa­yo subuka su saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: You will be made rich in every way so that you can be generous in every way. Such generosity produces thanksgiving to God through us.
local: Na i ma am sunya beberebe deke n kɛrɛ nu, adena am koro suyo kpa ma minɛm saŋga kɛrɛ, nyini ma minɛm da Nyɛmɛ asi, fa kɔ deke n bo ya yo i n su a.

eng: Your ministry of this service to Gods people isnt only fully meeting their needs but it is also multiplying in many expressions of thanksgiving to God.
local: Ahin aceeri juma n bo am yo i ni, i ti má ka i subuka Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo ba fun i ni ŋgumi wɔ, ama, i kaaki ma minɛm dɔŋgu da Nyɛmɛ asi wɔ.

eng: They will give honor to God for your obedience to your confession of Christs gospel. They will do this because this service provides evidence of your obedience, and because of your generosity in sharing with them and with everyone.
local: Ahin juma n bo am suyo i kɔ wie ni, nyini kere kabo am woo­wa ti barasu a. Na minɛm ŋu i, na bu kansi Nyɛmɛ kabo am ade yiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na am kan kere minɛm nahɔrɛ, ka am su Kristo labari kpa n jɔrɛ n su ni. Bo i buka nyini n su la kabo am yo kpa, na am kpaaki am neŋgem nu ma bɛrɛ ni nyiŋgam kɛrɛ ni.

eng: They will also pray for you, and they will care deeply for you because of the outstanding grace that God has given to you.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ ama am anya i ahɔrɔma akɔ asin su ni, bɛrɛ, bu koro am ni anyum­boro na bu sere Nyɛmɛ ma am.

eng: Thank God for his gift that words cant describe!.
local: Am ma ya da Nyɛmɛ asi fa kɔ i aceeri n bo wɔ fa ama ya na i la má aŋusu ni.

eng: I hope that you will put up with me while I act like a fool. Well, in fact, you are putting up with me!.
local: Tɔ, má am soro su, na am yo sawɔrɛ ma m, na m jɔjɔ sinnzin jɔrɛ kaan sɔ. Jande, am yo sawɔrɛ ma m.

eng: Im deeply concerned about you with the same concern that God has. As your father, I promised you in marriage to one husband. I promised to present you as an innocent virgin to Christ himself.
local: M si am kɔrɛ kabo Nyɛmɛ si am kɔrɛ ni, dama am ti m dɛɛ nbaam wɔ, ka baa­bara bo i nya si má biɛsɔ, ye ma fa am ace biɛsɔ kun, yiri la Kristo.

eng: But Im afraid that your minds might be seduced in the same way as the snake deceived Eve with his devious tricks. You might be unable to focus completely on a genuine and innocent commitment to Christ.
local: Ama, m soro sɛrɛ wɔ, dama nzɛn m teŋgeeri Hawa jɔrɛ n su, yiri la kabo woo n yo faari ŋgɔnlɛ­tiɛ dadakaari i na i nyaari i na i kunmaari i ni, nyini ma m soro ka beni, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ bá mini fa kɔ am sunsunni nu, na am yaki Kristo dɛɛ atin n bo am su i su, na am ahore­mbaa ti kun, na am woo­wa ti casi Kristo dɔ ni.

eng: If a person comes and preaches some other Jesus than the one we preached, or if you receive a different Spirit than the one you had received, or a different gospel than the one you embraced, you put up with it so easily!.
local: Dama nzɛn be ba baari am dɔ na i ba kaanri Yesu be jɔrɛ, na i ti má Yesu n bo ya kan i jɔrɛ kereeri am ni, wara nzɛn am deeri awiɛwiɛ be, na i ti má Awiɛwiɛ n bo am du mɔ deeri i ya dɔ ni, wara labari kpa barasu be bo i ti má bo am deeri i ni, ambɛrɛ koro yo nyini sawɔrɛ na am de nyini n sɔ a.

eng: I dont consider myself as second-rate in any way compared to the super-apostles.
local: Bɛrɛ bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Kristo jaramasam na bu la má aŋusu ni, mini yiri, m sunsun má ka m ti kaan ni bɛrɛ fieo.

eng: But even if Im uneducated in public speaking, Im not uneducated in knowledge. We have shown this to you in every way and in everything we have done.
local: Haari ni bo m ti má sɔnɔ bo i koro jɔjɔ kabo bu jɔjɔ ni, m la si yiri, ma du mɔ akere am ka i ti sɔ ɔ, deke n kɛrɛ nu ni saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself to give you an advantage because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?.
local: Am aŋu má i? Saŋga bo m ba kaanri Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ labari kpa n kereeri am ŋgbɛɛn, na ma de má deke be ni, m yoori m ŋu kaan wɔ, na m wura am kpiri, ma do ŋgasi wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: I robbed other churches by taking a salary from them in order to serve you!.
local: M koro se ka m yo fiinri Kristo jamam nbem wɔ lɛɛ, dama m yo deeri buka bu dɔ ɔ, ye m fa ba bukaari am a.

eng: While I was with you, I didnt burden any of you even though I needed things. The believers who came from Macedonia gave me everything I needed. I kept myself from being a financial drain on you in any way, and I will continue to keep myself from being a burden.
local: Saŋga bo m yo wo am dɔ na m la má pui ni, ma fa má m fie afie má be, dama ya ninbaam bo bu fite Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu yo bukaari m ni deke kɛrɛ bo wɔ kpiɛ m su ni. Ma koro má ka m fa m fie fie am be deke be nu. M woori sɔ ɔ, na m toko wo sɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Since Christs truth is in me, I wont stop telling the entire area of Greece that Im proud of what I did.
local: Kristo dɛɛ nahɔrɛ n bo i wo m nu n dɛɛ ti, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro kanda m na m fɔ má nyini yanfɔya ni, Akaya awɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: Why? Is it because I dont love you? God knows that I do!.
local: Nzu ti a? Ka m koro má am, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, Nyɛmɛ si ka m koro am.

eng: But Im going to continue to do what Im doing. I want to contradict the claims of the people who want to be treated like they are the same as us because of what they brag about.
local: Nyini ti, deke n bo m yo i ni, m kɔ́ su yo i sɔ ɔ, adena m tɔ bu atin, bɛrɛ kɔrɛ­fɔm ni bo bu koro ka bu nya atin na bu fɔ, na bu se ka ya ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ juma­yoo­wa sɛ ni.

eng: Such people are false apostles and dishonest workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
local: Dama nyini minɛm sɔ ti jaramasa kɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ, bu nati bu kɔrɛ wɔ, fa kɔ bu juma­yoo­wa su, na bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Kristo jaramasam wɔ.

eng: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
local: Nyini n sɔ n ti má alekutura, dama haari Sitana ni i tii mmɔɔ yo i ŋu ka i ti mɛrɛkɛ bo i ta wein n wɔ.

eng: It is no great surprise then that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
local: Nyini ti, i ti má alekutura, ka i amusui­su­fɔm mmɔɔ yo bu ŋu ka bu su nahɔrɛ atin su wɔ. Ama, bu awieeri bá kere kabo bu juma­yoo­wa ti a.

eng: I repeat, no one should take me for a fool. But if you do, then allow me to be a fool so that I can brag like a fool for a bit.
local: M koro ka m kaaki se am bekun wɔ, ka má be sunsun ka m ti sinnzin wɔ, ama, nzɛn am sunsun sɔ yiri, am yo sawɔrɛ na am de m ka m ti sinnzin wɔ, adena mini mmɔɔ koro fɔ kaan sɔ.

eng: Im not saying what Im saying because the Lord tells me to. Im saying it like Im a fool. Im putting my confidence in this business of bragging.
local: Ahin ahoresi n bo m fa fɔ sɔ ni, i fite má Mibiɛ n dɔ, ama, m jɔjɔ ka m ti sinnzin wɔ.

eng: Since so many people are bragging based on human standards, that is how Im going to brag too.
local: Kabo minɛm dɔŋgu fɔ kabo durunya n nu­fɔm yo ni, sɔ ye mini gusu fɔ́ n a.

eng: Because you, who are so wise, are happy to put up with fools.
local: Am aŋu má i? Ambɛrɛ la alecira a, am mindi de minɛm bo bu ti sinnzin n wɔ, ni ahoreda a.

eng: You put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone places themselves over you, or if someone hits you in the face.
local: Nahɔrɛ, haari minɛm bo bu miɛ am fa am yo bu akɔɔm ni, am mindi de bu wɔ, wara nzɛn be asisi am, am mindi de i wɔ, wara nzɛn be anya am taraa­biri, am mindi de i wɔ, wara nzɛn be kere i ŋu am dɔ, am mindi de i wɔ, wara nzɛn be boori am nyɔɔnu, am mindi de i wɔ.

eng: Im ashamed to say that we have been weak in comparison! But in whatever they challenge me, I challenge them .
local: Yɛrɛ, ya yo má nyini n be sɔ am nu, i ti m nyinsɛ wɔ, beni, ya yɔrɔki ya ŋu n dɛɛ ti a. Ama, kabo nyini minɛm soro má sɛrɛ na bu fɔ deke kɛrɛ ti ni, mini mmɔɔ, kisa m gusu soro má sɛrɛ na m fɔ fa kɔ nyini dekem n su. Beni, am sunsun ka m jɔjɔ ka m ti sinnzin wɔ.

eng: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
local: Bu ŋa bu ti Hiburu­fɔm wɔ? Tɔ, mini mmɔɔ, m ti sɔ ɔ. Bu ŋa bu dɛɛ la Yisareli­fɔ nyɛmɛsu atin ni? Tɔ, mini mmɔɔ, m su nyini n su a. Bu ŋa bu ti Abraham anumam wɔ? Tɔ, mini mmɔɔ, m ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: Are they ministers of Christ? Im speaking like a crazy person. What Ive done goes well beyond what theyve done. Ive worked much harder. Ive been imprisoned much more often. Ive been beaten more times than I can count. Ive faced death many times.
local: Bu ŋa bu ti Kristo amusui­su­fɔm wɔ? Tɔ, m tara bu. Am niɛ, i ti ka m nyunu akan m ye m jɔjɔ sɔ n a. Ama, nahɔrɛ, ma yo juma atara bu, ba nyi m dan­saraka bo i la má nɔ, bu boori m haari m ŋgɔɔ ba kaari kaan sɔ ɔ, saŋga dɔŋgu, kaan sɔ, má ma wu. Nyinim kɛrɛ nu, m tara bu.

eng: I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews five times.
local: Kpe nnu lɛɛ bo Yuda­fɔm n afin m ŋgbere mbaa kun kun abura­na kun kpiɛ su.

eng: I was beaten with rods three times. I was stoned once. I was shipwrecked three times. I spent a day and a night on the open sea.
local: Kpe nsɛn lɛɛ bo ba fin m ni bulaari ŋgbere. Ba tu m yabuɛ kpe­kun. Kpe nsan lɛɛ bo m wo lee bambakam nu tieku n su na ba sandi nu, haari na ma da tieku n nu kpe­kun, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu.

eng: Ive been on many journeys. I faced dangers from rivers, robbers, my people, and Gentiles. I faced dangers in the city, in the desert, on the sea, and from false brothers and sisters.
local: M nati sɔ lɛɛ saŋga kɛrɛ, m la má tana, ma ŋu anyum­boro nzuetinm kpiɛɛ­biri, ma ŋu anyum­boro minɛm bo bu nati fin bu beŋgum n saa nu, ma ŋu anyum­boro m tiɛŋgu minɛm saa nu, ma ŋu anyum­boro minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm saa nu, ma ŋu anyum­boro miɛ kpirim su, ma ŋu anyum­boro boro kpantainm nu, ma ŋu anyum­boro tieku n su, ma ŋu anyum­boro ya ninbaa kɔrɛ­fɔm n dɔ, bɛrɛ bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Kristo minɛm ni.

eng: I faced these dangers with hard work and heavy labor, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, and in the cold without enough clothes.
local: Ma yo juma nahɔrɛ su adi wahara, ma kpiɛ daafiri saŋga dɔŋgu, ma da ahɔɛ ni nzuehɔɛ, ma fun diire saŋga dɔŋgu, na ayɛrɛ ati m, na ma nati m ŋuniɛ ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Besides all the other things I could mention, theres my daily stress because Im concerned about all the churches.
local: Dekem bo ba ka ni, m kán má bu jɔrɛ, ama, deke bo i kpiɛ m daafiri ale kɛrɛ bo i cin ni, yiri la kabo m sunsun Kristo jama n kɛrɛ jɔrɛ n wɔ.

eng: I, Paul, make a personal request to you with the gentleness and kindness of Christ. Im shy when Im with you, but Im bossy when Im away from you!.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii, m susere am wɔ, Kristo sawɔrɛ n ni i bɛnlɛ woo­wa n dɛɛ ti, mini bo nbem ŋa nzɛn m wo am dɔ, m san nya ni, ama, nzɛn m wo má am dɔ yiri, m la ahore wɔ.

eng: Who is weak without me being weak? Who is led astray without me being furious about it?.
local: Bu nu ŋma agbaraki ye i gbarakii­wa n asa má m a? Bu nu ŋma ye bu ma i yo satiɛ ye i yo má m yaa?.

eng: I beg you that when Im with you in person, I wont have to boss you around. Im afraid that I may have to use that kind of behavior with those people who think we live by human standards.
local: Deke bo i ti na m susere am n bu la ka, m koro má ka am ma m yo ahore, saŋga bo m ba baari am dɔ ni, na m yo ahoresi jɔjɔ am, kabo m sunsun ka wɔ yo daka ka m jɔjɔ bu ni, bɛrɛ bo bu ŋa ya su durunya n nu­fɔm woo­wa n su ni.

eng: If its necessary to brag, Ill brag about my weaknesses.
local: Tɔ, saŋga bo wɔ yo daka ka m fɔ, m fɔ fa kɔ dekem bo bu kere ka m la má faŋga n su wɔ.

eng: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, the one who is blessed forever, knows that Im not lying.
local: Nyɛmɛ si ka m bu má kɔrɛ, yiri ti ya Mibiɛ Yesu Si a, yiri ti ye ni ka ya kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ a.

eng: Although we live in the world, we dont fight our battles with human methods.
local: Haari ni bo ya wo durunya n nu ni, ya kun má luɛ n kabo durunya n nu­fɔm kun luɛ ni.

eng: Our weapons that we fight with arent human, but instead they are powered by God for the destruction of fortresses. They destroy arguments.
local: Biɛsɔya neŋgem bo ya fa kun luɛ n ti má durunya n nu fɛn dɛɛ biɛsɔya neŋgem. Ama, i fite Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n nu wɔ, na i kunma na i saaki faŋga woo­biri kɛrɛ, na i kunma na i saaki sunsunni kɛrɛ bo i ti má nahɔrɛ.

eng: At Damascus the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to capture me.
local: Saŋga bo m yo woori Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na gomina be wo bɛrɛ, yiri ye Fɛmɛ Aretasi yo faari miɛ n yiko wuraari i saa nu, na nyini gomina n ama tintini­jina­fɔm n akɔ aŋminda miɛ n ka bu tara m.

eng: but I got away from him by being lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall.
local: Ama, na minɛm n afa m awura kandiɛ bambaka nu, na ba fa m acɔɔndi kobe n tukoro nu, ajura asiɛŋgu, na ma foro gomina n saa nu.

eng: and every defense that is raised up to oppose the knowledge of God. They capture every thought to make it obedient to Christ.
local: na i kunma na i saaki deke kɛrɛ bo i wura i ŋu kpiri na i kanda minɛm na má bu si Nyɛmɛ ni, na i ma ya kunma sunsunni kɛrɛ bo i ti ka ya kpɔ­fɔm ni, na bu de Kristo nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Once your obedience is complete, we are ready to punish any disobedience.
local: Ambɛrɛ, am deeri i nɔaniɛ n wieeri, ye ya bá cin bu kɛrɛ sui a, bɛrɛ bo ba kete ka bu de má i nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: Look at what is right in front of you! If anyone is sure about belonging to Christ, that person should think again. We belong to Christ just like that person.
local: Ambɛrɛ, deke bo am nyu­mbaa ŋu i ni, nyini ye am sunsunni wo i su a. Nzɛn be la ahoresi ka i ti Kristo dɛɛ sɔnɔ, i ti ye ni ka i mi kaaki sunsun ka yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ti Kristo minɛm wɔ, kabo yiri mmɔɔ ti ni.

eng: Even if I went on to brag about our authority, I wouldnt be ashamed of it. The Lord gave us that authority to build you up and not to destroy you.
local: Haari nzɛn m fɔ yanfɔya tara kabo má m fɔ ni, fa kɔ yiko n bo Mibiɛ n afa ama ya n su, i la má m nyinsɛ, nyini yiko n ye Mibiɛ n maari ya, ka ya fa buka am, na am kɔ mɔ ni am nyɛmɛsu ni, wɔ ma má ya ka ya fa saaki am ji wɔ.

eng: I dont want it to seem like Im trying to intimidate you with my letters.
local: M koro má ka i yo ka m sukɛrɛ am kadasim n na m wura am sɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: I know what some people are saying: His letters are severe and powerful, but in person he is weak and his speech is worth nothing.
local: Dama nbem bo bu kan m jɔrɛ ni, bu ŋa, m kadasim wo nɔ na bu la faŋga, ama, saŋga bo mini ni m tii ba wo am dɔ, m ti sɔnɔ yɔrɔkii­wa a, kabo m jɔjɔ ni, be fa yo má pui.

eng: These people need to think about this—that when we are with you, our actions will show that we are the same as the words we wrote when we were away from you.
local: I ti ye ni ka nyini minɛm sɔ n si ka, kabo saŋga bo ya wo má dɔ na ya kɛrɛ kadasim n nu ni, sɔ ye ya yó a, saŋga bo ya wo am dɔ ni.

eng: We wont dare to place ourselves in the same league or to compare ourselves with some of those who are promoting themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves, and compare themselves with themselves, they have no understanding.
local: Ahin minɛm ni, bɛrɛ ni bu tii kan kere minɛm kabo bu ti wɔ, ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya soro sɛrɛ ni bo ya fa ya ŋu bo bu su, wara na ya fa ya ŋu bu bu. Ama, kabo bu fa bu ŋu bo bu beŋgum su ni, na bu fa bu ŋu bu bu ni, nyini kere ka bu la má ŋgɔnlɛ haari kaan sɔ lɛɛ.

eng: We wont take pride in anything more than what is appropriate. Lets look at the boundaries of our work area that God has assigned to us. Its an area that includes you.
local: Yɛrɛ yiri, ya wura má ya ŋu kpiri, na ya fɔ tara kabo i ti ye ni ka ya fɔ ni. Ya yo kɔ jina ya juma yoo­biri nyaari wɔ, deke bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya ka ya yo ni, haari ya yo aba aju ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ dɔ.

eng: We arent going out of bounds, as if our work area doesnt extend as far as you. We were the first ones to travel as far as Corinth with the gospel of Christ.
local: Nzɛn má ya du má mɔ aba má am dɔ, nyini yiri, ya suyo kɔ sin ya juma yoo­biri nyaari wɔ lɛɛ, fa kɔ ahin jɔrɛ n su. Ama, kabo i ti ni, yɛrɛ ti minɛm bo ya duuri mɔ a, haari bo ya ba juuri am dɔ, na ya kan Kristo labari kpa n akere am.

eng: We dont take pride in what other people do outside of our boundaries. We hope that our work will be extended even more by you as your faith grows, until it expands fully .
local: Yɛrɛ, ya gusu fa má nbem juma fa fɔ má, na má ya fɔ tara ya juma yoo­biri nyaari. Ama, ya la tama ka kabo am yarada subuka su ni, ya juma ni sandi bara yɛ am nu, kabo ya juma yoo­biri nyaari ti ni.

eng: We hope that our work grows even to the point of the gospel being preached in places beyond Corinth, without bragging about what has already been done in another persons work area.
local: Adena ya koro kɔ kan labari kpa n kere minɛm, bo bu wo asiɛnm bo bu wo am nyunu dɔ ni. Ya koro má ka ya yo wura nbem dɛɛ juma yoo­biri bo ba du mɔ ayo i n nu, na ya fa fɔ.

eng: But, the one who brags should brag in the Lord. .
local: Ama, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i fɔ, i fɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: It isnt the person who promotes himself or herself who is approved but the person whom the Lord commends.
local: Dama má sɔnɔ bo yiri ni i tii kan kere kabo i ti ni, ye Mibiɛ n de i ka i ti sɔnɔ kpa, ama, bo Mibiɛ n kere ni, yiri ti sɔnɔ kpa.

eng: This is the third time that Im coming to visit you. Every matter is settled on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
local: Tɔ, ahin baawa n bo m súba ni, yiri yo m kpe nsan su bo m ba niɛ am busu a. Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo ba kan i na i ti nahɔrɛ, má ka minɛ nnyɔ, wara minɛ nsan adi i daani.

eng: When I was with you on my second visit, I already warned those who continued to sin. Now Im repeating that warning to all the rest of you while Im at a safe distance: if I come again, I wont spare anyone.
local: Saŋga bo m yo baari am dɔ ba niɛɛri am kpe nnyɔ su ni, m yo bɔbɔɔri am nbem. Kisa na m wo má am dɔ ni, m koro ka m bɔbɔ bu bekun fa kɔ nyini n su, ka nzɛn m baari, m yáki má bɛrɛ n bo bu yo yoori satiɛ ni, ni bo bu buka bu su kisa n kɛrɛ.

eng: Since you are demanding proof that Christ speaks through me, Christ isnt weak in dealing with you but shows his power among you.
local: Dama am ŋa am koro ka am ŋu deke bo i kere ka Kristo fa m su, ye i jɔjɔ am dɔ sɔ a. Tɔ, am aŋu ka yiri ti má bo i la má faŋga, ama, i la yiko am nu.

eng: Certainly he was crucified because of weakness, but he lives by the power of God. Certainly we also are weak in him, but we will live together with him, because of Gods power that is directed toward you.
local: Nahɔrɛ, saŋga bo bu yo kpamaari i baka jaanu n su ni, i yo la má faŋga, ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ, kisa i la ŋgɔɔ wo bɛrɛ, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n dɛɛ ti. Sɔ ye i ti a, yɛrɛ bo ya ni yiri kɛrɛ ayo kun ni, yɛrɛ mmɔɔ la má faŋga lɛɛ, ama, Nyɛmɛ yiko n dɛɛ ti, ye ya ni yiri súwo a, na ya yo juma ma am.

eng: Examine yourselves to see if you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Dont you understand that Jesus Christ is in you? Unless, of course, you fail the test.
local: Ambɛrɛ ni am tii, am fiiti am ŋu na am niɛ ka am woo­wa kere ka am ayo yarada nahɔrɛ su, wara am ayo má. Am si má ka Yesu Kristo wo am dɔ ɔ, nzɛn sɛ? I wo am dɔ yiri, ŋka am woo­wa kere ka am ayo má yarada nahɔrɛ su wɔ.

eng: But I hope that you will realize that we dont fail the test.
local: M la tama ka am bá si ka, yɛrɛ, ya ti má bo bu woo­wa kere ka ba yo má yarada nahɔrɛ su ni.

eng: We pray to God that you dont do anything wrong, not because we want to appear to pass the test but so that you might do the right thing, even if we appear to fail.
local: Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ya sere Nyɛmɛ ma am wɔ, ka, má am yo sa be bo i kɔ má atin su, má ka ya koro ka minɛ ŋu ya woo­wa na i kere ka ya yo yarada nahɔrɛ su, ama, ya koro ka am yo sa bo i ti kpa a, haari nzɛn má minɛm ŋu ya woo­wa na i kere ka ya yo má yarada nahɔrɛ su.

eng: We cant do anything against the truth but only to help the truth.
local: Yɛrɛ, ya koro yo má deke bo i jaraki nahɔrɛ ni, ama, ya yo deke bo i su nahɔrɛ ni ŋgumi su a.

eng: We are happy when we are weak but you are strong. We pray for this: that you will be made complete.
local: Ya ahore ti jɔɔ­wa a, saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya la má faŋga, na ambɛrɛ la yiko Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Ahin n ye ya sere Nyɛmɛ ma am a, ka, i ma am woo­wa yo kabo i koro ni.

eng: This is why Im writing these things while Im away. Im writing so that I wont need to act harshly when Im with you by using the authority that the Lord gave me. He gave it to me so that I could build you up, not tear you down.
local: Nyini mmɔɔ dɛɛ ti, ye m wo má am dɔ ye m sukɛrɛ am ahin kadasi n sɔ n a, adena m ba baari am dɔ, na má m fa Mibiɛ n yiko n fa yi am anyum­boro, nyini yiko n ye i maari m ka m fa buka am, na am kɔ mɔ ni am nyɛmɛsu n a, wɔ ma má m ka m fa saaki am ji wɔ.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, good-bye. Put things in order, respond to my encouragement, be in harmony with each other, and live in peace—and the God of love and peace will be with you.
local: M ninbaam, jɔrɛ bo wɔ ka n la ka, am ma am ahore jɔ, am boro am nyi na am wo wo kpa, am ti kabo m sereeri am ni, am la anɔɔsɛ ni am beŋgum, am wo ni tana­laifiɛ. Nyɛmɛ ti tana­laifiɛ ni koro­fɔ a, yiri wó am dɔ a.

eng: Say hello to each other with a holy kiss. All of Gods people say hello to you.
local: Am bisa am beŋgu dɔŋgu ni koro. Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn n bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu.

eng: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, the love of God, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
local: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ahɔrɔma ni, ni Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koro ni, ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ wo fiɛ n wo am kɛrɛ dɔ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle who is not sent from human authority or commissioned through human agency, but sent through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu bo m ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa n ni ya ninbaam bo bu wo m dɔ fɛn n kɛrɛ, sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ Kristo jama minɛm bo am wo Galatiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu n wɔ. Yesu Kristo ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ bo i teŋgeeri Kristo i yiwee n nu ni, afa m ayo i jaramasa, má adamande yoori m a, bekun má minɛm gusu faari juma n maari m a.

eng: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na bu ma am ahoreda.

eng: He gave himself for our sins, so he could deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father.
local: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ ma Yesu ade ya jinaa­biri awu ya satiɛ dɛɛ ti, adena i de ya fite ahin durunya tiɛ n bo ya wo nu n nu.

eng: To God be the glory forever and always! Amen.
local: Yiri ti ye ni ka minɛm kɛrɛ kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.

eng: Im amazed that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ to follow another gospel.
local: Am jɔrɛ amiɛ m bo m si má kabo m kan. Kristo dɛɛ aŋunfɔ si ti, ye Nyɛmɛ afere am ka am ba su i, wɔ nya cɛ má, ye am akaaki am sin abo i ndende, susu labari barasu be bo i wo i ŋgumi ni labari kpa n ni.

eng: Its not really another gospel, but certain people are confusing you and they want to change the gospel of Christ.
local: Labari kpa nyiŋga be wo má bɛrɛ, deke bo i ti na m se sɔ n la ka, minɛm nbem koro ka bu ta am nyi wɔ, na bu fa keree­wa barasu nbem kaaki Kristo labari kpa ni.

eng: However, even if we ourselves or a heavenly angel should ever preach anything different from what we preached to you, they should be under a curse.
local: Ama, haari nzɛn yɛrɛ be, wara nzɛn Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ be fiteeri aŋgoro ba kaanri labari barasu be, na i ti má kabo ya du mɔ kaanri i ni, Nyɛmɛ yó i mi ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Im repeating what weve said before: if anyone preaches something different from what you received, they should be under a curse!.
local: Kabo ya du mɔ ase am ni, nyini ye m kaaki suse am bekun n a, ka, nzɛn be ba kaanri labari barasu be bo i wo i ŋgumi ni bo am ade i ni, Nyɛmɛ yó i mi ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Am I trying to win over human beings or God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I wouldnt be Christs slave.
local: Bu ŋa m suyo m juma n ka minɛ kansi m wɔ, ama, i ti má sɔ, m koro ka m yo bo Nyɛmɛ kansi m wɔ, m suyo má sa bo minɛ se ka m ti kpa. Nzɛn má m ŋa m yo sa bo i sun minɛ nu, má m koro yo má Kristo akɔɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the gospel I preached isnt human in origin.
local: M ninbaam, m koro ka am si ka labari kpa n bo m kaanri i jɔrɛ kereeri am ni, i ti má deke bo i fite adamande sunsunni nu wɔ.

eng: I didnt receive it or learn it from a human. It came through a revelation from Jesus Christ.
local: I ti má ka m deeri i fiteeri adamande dɔ ɔ, má be gusu kaaki fa kereeri m a. Ama, Yesu Kristo ni i tii yiiri i kereeri m a.

eng: It is necessary to brag, not that it does any good. Ill move on to visions and revelations from the Lord.
local: Tɔ, wɔ yo daka ka m fɔ, haari ni bo i la má kpɛrɛ be ni. M la deke nbem bo m kan buka su, bɛrɛ la kabo Nyɛmɛ yiiri deke nbem kereeri m ka alɛlɛ nu ni, ni kabo i yiiri sa­yoo­wam kereeri m ni.

eng: You heard about my previous life in Judaism, how severely I harassed Gods church and tried to destroy it.
local: Am yo tiiri kabo m woo­wa yo ti barasu, saŋga bo m yo suuri Yuda­fɔm dɛɛ atin n su ni. M yo kereeri Kristo jama n wuwuruwa agaya, na má m butu bu.

eng: I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my peers, because I was much more militant about the traditions of my ancestors.
local: M yo suuri Yuda­fɔm atin n su nahɔrɛ su taraari m sara su­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu su nyini atin n su ni. M nyunu yo boroori agaya kabo m yo na m su ya nana sa­yoo­wam su.

eng: I know a man in Christ who was caught up into the third heaven fourteen years ago. I dont know whether it was in the body or out of the body. God knows.
local: M si sɔnɔ be jɔrɛ, i ti Kristo sɔnɔ ɔ, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ yo faari i fun kɔɔri aŋgoro woo­biri nsan su ni i dɔ n a, i nyuma afɔ buru ni nna lɛɛ. Nzɛn i kɔɔri ni i ŋuniɛ­mbaa a, wara i awiɛwiɛ nu, mini, m si má, Nyɛmɛ si a.

eng: But God had set me apart from birth and called me through his grace. He was pleased.
local: Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo bu nya wu má m ni, ye i yiiri m ka m yo i juma n a. Saŋga n bo i suunri Nyɛmɛ nu n ba juuri ni.

eng: I know that this man was caught up into paradise and that he heard unspeakable words that were things no one is allowed to repeat. I dont know whether it was in the body or apart from the body. God knows.
local: Nahɔrɛ, m si nyini sɔnɔ n jɔrɛ, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ faari i fun kɔɔri alecɛɛma nu n a, na wɔ ti jɔrɛm bo bu la má kan, na be gusu la má atin ka i jɔjɔ bu, ama, kabo i yoori ni, ma si má, nzɛn i kɔɔri ni i ŋuniɛ­mbaa a, wara i awiɛwiɛ nu, mini, m si má, Nyɛmɛ si a.

eng: Ill brag about this man, but I wont brag about myself, except to brag about my weaknesses.
local: Tɔ, nyini sɔnɔ n sɔ, ye m fa fɔ n a, ama, mini ni m tii, m koro fɔ má deke be ti, má ka dekem bo bu kere ka m la má faŋga n ye m fa fɔ a.

eng: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might preach about him to the Gentiles. I didnt immediately consult with any human being.
local: na wɔ yi i Wa n akere m, adena m kɔ kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, ye ma kɔ má be dɔ na i kɔ kere m kabo m yo n a.

eng: I didnt go up to Jerusalem to see the men who were apostles before me either, but I went away into Arabia and I returned again to Damascus.
local: Ma kaaki akɔ má Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu na m kɔ ŋu minɛm bo bu du mɔ ti Kristo jaramasam ni. M kɔɔri Arabiya asiɛn n su wɔ, na ma fite dɔ, na ma nya akaaki akɔ Damasiko miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: If I did want to brag, I wouldnt make a fool of myself because Id tell the truth. Im holding back from bragging so that no one will give me any more credit than what anyone sees or hears about me.
local: Haari nzɛn má m ŋa m fɔ mmɔɔ, má m ti má sinnzin wɔ, dama má m kan nahɔrɛ wɔ. Ama, m yaki i sɔ ɔ, adena má am be wura m kpiri tara dekem bo i ŋu ka m yo bu, ni dekem bo i ti ka m kan bu ni.

eng: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days.
local: I dɛɛ afɔ nsan sin, na ma kɔ Yɛrusalem ka m kɔ niɛ Kefasi busu, yiri bo bu kaaki fere i ka Petoro ni, na ma kɔ awo i dɔ cɛɛn buru ni nnu.

eng: I was given a thorn in my body because of the outstanding revelations Ive received so that I wouldnt be conceited. Its a messenger from Satan sent to torment me so that I wouldnt be conceited.
local: Tɔ, fa kɔ sa­yikeree­wa beberebe n bo Nyɛmɛ fa kereeri m su, wɔ koro má ka nyini dɛɛ ti na m wura m ŋu kpiri. Nyini ti, i maari Sitana faari tɔrɛ be wuraari m ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu, i ti ka buwe ye i wɔwɔ m a, na i sukere m wahara, adena má m wura m ŋu kpiri.

eng: I pleaded with the Lord three times for it to leave me alone.
local: Kpe nsan lɛɛ bo ma sere Mibiɛ n fa kɔ ahin jɔrɛ n su, ka i ma i fite yaki m.

eng: But I didnt see any other of the apostles except James the brother of the Lord.
local: Saŋga bo m woori dɔ ni, ma ŋu má Kristo jaramasam n be abuka má su, má ka Yakobo, ya Mibiɛ niɛma ni.

eng: He said to me, My grace is enough for you, because power is made perfect in weakness. So Ill gladly spend my time bragging about my weaknesses so that Christs power can rest on me.
local: Ama, na wɔ se m ka, M ahɔrɔma n asɛ u su, dama saŋga bo a la má faŋga ni, ye m kere m yiko kabo m koro a. Kabo i seeri sɔ n dɛɛ ti, nyini ma m la ahoreda na m fɔ fa kɔ kabo m la má faŋga n a, adena Kristo yiko n wo m dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ a.

eng: Before God, Im not lying about the things that Im writing to you!.
local: Nɔaniɛ n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, m bu má kɔrɛ. Nyɛmɛ ti m daani­fɔ a.

eng: Therefore, Im all right with weaknesses, insults, disasters, harassments, and stressful situations for the sake of Christ, because when Im weak, then Im strong.
local: Nyini ma Kristo dɛɛ ti ye m ahore ti daa­wa n a, saŋga bo m la má faŋga n a, ni saŋga bo bu kpiɛ m nzukɔ ni, ni saŋga bo m ŋu kekere ni, ni saŋga bo bu kere m wuwuruwa ni, ni saŋga bo m ŋu anyum­boro ni. Dama saŋga bo m la má faŋga ni, nyini saŋga n ye m la yiko Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

eng: Ive become a fool! You made me do it. Actually, I should have been commended by you. Im not inferior to the super-apostles in any way, even though Im a nonentity.
local: Tɔ, kabo m jɔjɔ ni, ma yo ka sinnzin wɔ lɛɛ, ambɛrɛ miɛ m a, ye m yo sɔ n a. Má ambɛrɛ ti minɛm bo am kan kereeri kabo m ti n a, má i ti ye. Dama bɛrɛ n bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Kristo jaramasam na bu la má aŋusu ni, mini yiri, haari ni bo m ti má pui ni, m ti má kaan ni bɛrɛ fieo.

eng: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
local: Nyini sin na ma fite Yɛrusalem, na ma kɔ Siriya ni Silisiya dɛɛ awɔrɛm n nu.

eng: but I wasnt known personally by the Christian churches in Judea.
local: Nyini saŋga Kristo jamam n bo bu wo Yudiya awɔrɛ n nu n be nya ŋu má m na bu ba si m.

eng: The signs of an apostle were performed among you with continuous endurance through signs, wonders, and miracles.
local: Nahɔrɛ, sam bo bu kere ka m ti jaramasa ni, ye m yoori bu am dɔ n a, ni mindi kɛrɛ, ni alekutura sam, ni yiko sam, ni abonɔ sam n a.

eng: How were you treated worse than the other churches, except that I myself wasnt a financial burden on you? Forgive me for this wrong!.
local: Nzu deke ye m yo maari Kristo jamam bo bu wo miɛ nyiŋgam su n ye ma yo ama má am a? Má ka bo ma fa má m fie be afie má am ni. Tɔ, ma do ŋgasi, am fa ce m.

eng: They only heard a report about me: The man who used to harass us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.
local: se ba ti ka minɛ ŋa, Ahin sɔnɔ n yo kere ya wuwuruwa a, na i kun minɛ bo ba de Kristo labari kpa n na i mini bu, kisa i nati kan labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛ wɔ.

eng: So they were glorifying God because of me.
local: Deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ma m koro yoori i n dɛɛ ti, na ba kansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Look, Im ready to visit you a third time, and I wont be a burden on you. I dont want your things; I want you. It isnt the childrens responsibility to save up for their parents but parents for children.
local: Am ti fɛn, kisa ma sɛsɛ m ŋu su ka m ba niɛ am busu kpe nsan su lɛɛ, ama, m toko koro má ka m ba fa m fie fie am. M suba má ka m koro am neŋge be wɔ, ama, m koro ambɛrɛ wɔ. Dama má nbaam kpini neŋgem se bu wu­fɔm a, ama, bu wu­fɔm ni kpini se bu nbaam n a.

eng: I will very gladly spend and be spent for your sake. If I love you more, will you love me less?.
local: Mini, deke bo m la i ni, m di i ka a, na m fa buka am, na m fa m ŋu kɛrɛ ma am, nyini ma m ahoreda. Ma si má, m koro am agaya n sɔ n dɛɛ ti, ye ambɛrɛ koro m kaan sɔ n a? Ai, atin wo má nu.

eng: We all know that I didnt place a burden on you, but in spite of that you think Im a con artist who fooled you with a trick.
local: Tɔ, am nbem ŋa ma fa má m fie be afie má am, ama, bu ŋa m fa ŋgɔnlɛ­tiɛ wɔ, na ma dadaka am anya am taraa­biri.

eng: I havent taken advantage of you through any of the people I sent to you, have I?.
local: Ka sɛ? Minɛm bo m yo sunmaari bu am dɔ ni, m ma bu be sisiiri am be maari m, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, be asisi má am ama má m.

eng: I strongly encouraged Titus to go to you and sent the brother with him. Titus didnt take advantage of you, did he? Didnt we live by the same Spirit? Didnt we walk in the same footsteps?.
local: M yo sereeri Titosi wɔ, na wɔ ba am dɔ, na ma ma ya niwa n asunma i. Titosi yo sisiiri am, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, wɔ sisi má am. Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ aŋu ka m ni yiri kɛrɛ sunsunni ti kun wɔ, na ya kɛrɛ su atin kun su a.

eng: Have you been thinking up to now that we are defending ourselves to you? Actually, we are speaking in the sight of God and in Christ. Dear friends, everything is meant to build you up.
local: Beni, nyini n kɛrɛ bo ya jɔjɔ i sɔ ni, am sunsun ka ya sukan de ya tii wɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, i ti má sɔ. M ninbaa koroo­wam, kabo ya ni Kristo ayo kun na ya jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu n ye ya se sɔ n a. Deke n kɛrɛ bo ya yo i ni, i buka am na am kɔ mɔ ni am nyɛmɛsu n wɔ.

eng: Im afraid that maybe when I come you will be different from the way I want you to be, and that Ill be different from the way you want me to be. Im afraid that there might be fighting, obsession, losing your temper, competitive opposition, backstabbing, gossip, conceit, and disorderly conduct.
local: M soro sɛrɛ wɔ, m koro má ka m ba baari am dɔ, na má m ba tu am kabo m koro ka am ti ni, wara na ambɛrɛ gusu ŋu m kabo má am koro ka m ti ni. M soro sɛrɛ ka, beni, m bá ŋu ka, am suse akuruwa, na am susi kɔrɛ, na am sufa yaa, na am suyo anyii sam, na am sukan am beŋgu jɔrɛ susaaki bu, na am sukpura tere, na am suyo kpirikoro, na am la kpakpaaki­nu.

eng: Im afraid that when I come again, my God may embarrass me in front of you. I might have to go into mourning over all the people who have sinned before and havent changed their hearts and lives from what they used to practice: moral corruption, sexual immorality, and doing whatever feels good.
local: M soro sɛrɛ ka m ba juuri, na má Nyɛmɛ ma m yo m ŋu kaan am nyunu, na má m ahore saaki fa ma bu, bɛrɛ bo ba du mɔ ayo satiɛ, na bu nya kaaki yaki má bu daŋgba sa­yoo­wam, ni sakaraya, ni konvikiya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm ni.

eng: Then after fourteen years I went up to Jerusalem again with Barnabas, and I took Titus along also.
local: Saŋga bo ya fite Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n nu ni, i afɔ buru ni nna sin, na m ni Banabasi akaaki akɔ dɔ bekun, na ya fa Titosi abuka ya ŋu su.

eng: I went there because of a revelation, and I laid out the gospel that I preach to the Gentiles for them. But I did it privately with the influential leaders to make sure that I wouldnt be working or that I hadnt worked for nothing.
local: Deke n bo i ti na m kɔɔri n la ka, Nyɛmɛ kereeri m ka m kɔ a. Na m ni Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n atiɛn ya nɔɔ ya ŋgumi deke be, na ma kan ateke bu nyi, akere bu fa kɔ labari kpa n jɔrɛ bo m kan kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni. Dama ma koro má ka juma n bo m suyo i n kɛrɛ ka ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: However, not even Titus, who was with me and who was a Greek, was required to be circumcised.
local: Tɔ, haari ni bo Titosi yo woori m dɔ na i ti má Yuda­fɔ ni, ba miɛ má i ka i wura kɛnkɛnnɛ.

eng: But false brothers and sisters, who were brought in secretly, slipped in to spy on our freedom, which we have in Christ Jesus, and to make us slaves.
local: Ama, be abisa má ya i jɔrɛ be, se minɛ nbem ba saŋgaari ya nu, bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Kristo dɛɛ minɛm wɔ. Bɛrɛ seeri ka wɔ yo daka ka i wura kɛnkɛnnɛ n a. Bu faari bu ŋu mataari ya a, adena bu niɛ kabo Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti na ya nya ya tii ni. Má bu koro ka ya su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su wɔ, na ya yo akɔɔm.

eng: We didnt give in and submit to them for a single moment, so that the truth of the gospel would continue to be with you.
local: Ama, na ya fa nu, ya ma má bu anyunu fieo, adena má be kaaki i sin bo labari kpa n bo i ti nahɔrɛ, na ya kan kere am su.

eng: The influential leaders didnt add anything to what I was preaching—and whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God doesnt show favoritism.
local: Bɛrɛ n bo minɛ bu bu ka bu ti nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni, m cɛcɛ awo má bu nu ka bu ti nkpiɛnm, dama Nyɛmɛ tambu má nu, nyini nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n gusu ala má kaakii­wa bo bu kaaki m fa kɔ m labari kpa n su.

eng: But on the contrary, they saw that I had been given the responsibility to preach the gospel to the people who arent circumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised.
local: Se deke bo bu ŋuuri i n la ka, Nyɛmɛ ye wɔ yi m ka m kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n a, kabo wɔ yi Petoro ka i kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere Yuda­fɔm ni.

eng: The one who empowered Peter to become an apostle to the circumcised empowered me also to be one to the Gentiles.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ ama m yiko ka m kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, kabo wɔ ma Petoro yiko ka i kan i labari kpa n jɔrɛ kere Yuda­fɔm ni.

eng: James, Cephas, and John, who are considered to be key leaders, shook hands with me and Barnabas as equals when they recognized the grace that was given to me. So it was agreed that we would go to the Gentiles, while they continue to go to the people who were circumcised.
local: Na Yakobo, ni Petoro, ni Yohane bo minɛ bu bu ka bu ti Kristo jama mɔ­fɔm n aŋu ka, Nyɛmɛ ama m nyini juma n ka m yo i a, nyini dɛɛ ti, na ba fa bu saa awura m ni Banabasi saa nu, na ba de ya ni bu saa nnyɔ. Na ya kɛrɛ asoro su ka m ni Banabasi kɔ yo juma ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, na bɛrɛ gusu kɔ yo juma ni Yuda­fɔm ni.

eng: They asked only that we would remember the poor, which was certainly something I was willing to do.
local: Ama, jɔrɛ n bo bu seeri ya n la ka, má ya wara fi yaari­fɔm sa. Nyini ye m yo la anyum­boro ka m yo i a.

eng: But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he was wrong.
local: Ama, Petoro ba baari Antiyoki miɛ kpiri n nu na ma jaraki i, ma fiɛ má i su, dama sa n bo i yoori i ni, wɔ kɔ má atin su.

eng: He had been eating with the Gentiles before certain people came from James. But when they came, he began to back out and separate himself, because he was afraid of the people who promoted circumcision.
local: Saŋga bo i du mɔ ba tuuri ya ni, i ni ya ninbaa Kristo­fɔm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni saŋga di diire a. Na minɛ nbem afite Yakobo dɔ aba ka bu ba niɛ ya busu. Bu ba juuri ni, na wɔ cin i ŋu afite minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n nu. I ni bɛrɛ asaŋga adi má diire kun, dama i soro Yuda­fɔm n ahore wɔ.

eng: And the rest of the Jews also joined him in this hypocrisy so that even Barnabas got carried away with them in their hypocrisy.
local: I yoori sɔ ni, na Yuda­fɔ nyiŋgam bo bu wo Antiyoki n gusu ayo kabo i yoori ni. Bu si kabo má bu yo, ama, ba yo má sɔ. Na Banabasi gusu asɔn bu su.

eng: But when I saw that they werent acting consistently with the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of everyone, If you, though youre a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you require the Gentiles to live like Jews.
local: M ŋuuri ka bu su má Yesu labari kpa n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n su ni, na ma se Petoro jama n kɛrɛ nyunu ka, Wɔrɔ, a ti Yuda­fɔ ɔ, ama, u woo­wa ti ka bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm wɔ. Ye nzu ti ye a koro ka a miɛ minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm na bu wo kabo Yuda­fɔm wo barasu n a.

eng: We are born Jews—were not Gentile sinners.
local: Nahɔrɛ, ya fitee­biri, yɛrɛ ti Yuda­fɔm wɔ, ya ti má minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ na ya fere bu ka satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni.

eng: However, we know that a person isnt made righteous by the works of the Law but rather through the faithfulness of Jesus Christ. We ourselves believed in Christ Jesus so that we could be made righteous by the faithfulness of Christ and not by the works of the Law—because no one will be made righteous by the works of the Law.
local: Ya si ka na nzɛn sɔnɔ yo Yesu Kristo yarada, ye Nyɛmɛ de i mi ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, ama, i ti má ka i mi su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, ye Nyɛmɛ de i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a. Tɔ, ye yɛrɛ gusu ayo Yesu Kristo yarada, adena Nyɛmɛ de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, i ti má ka ya su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su n dɛɛ ti a. Dama be wo má bɛrɛ bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su na Nyɛmɛ de i ka wɔ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ.

eng: But if it is discovered that we ourselves are sinners while we are trying to be made righteous in Christ, then is Christ a servant of sin? Absolutely not!.
local: Kabo ya boro ya nyi na ya su Kristo su na Nyɛmɛ de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, nyini kere ka yɛrɛ Yuda­fɔm gusu ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm ka minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ti n wɔ. Ama, nyini n sukere má ka Kristo subuka minɛm na bu yo satiɛ wɔ lɛɛ. Ai, i ti má sɔ.

eng: If I rebuild the very things that I tore down, I show that I myself am breaking the Law.
local: M yo su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su wɔ, ama, ma yaki i, nzɛn kisa na m ŋa m kaaki bo i bu su i su bekun, nyini saŋga, ma fa m ŋu ayo satiɛ­yo­fɔ lɛɛ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: I died to the Law through the Law, so that I could live for God.
local: Mini dɔ, fa kɔ nyini nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, i yo m ka ma wu wɔ, m su má i su kun, adena m nya ŋgɔɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. I ti ka m ni Yesu Kristo asaŋga awu baka jaanu n su a.

eng: I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live, but Christ lives in me. And the life that I now live in my body, I live by faith, indeed, by the faithfulness of Gods Son, who loved me and gave himself for me.
local: Ahin ŋgɔɔ n bo m la i ni, durunya n nu fɛn ni, yiri Nyɛmɛ Wa n koro m dɛɛ ti, na wɔ wu ama m, na ma yo i yarada, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m la ŋgɔɔ n a.

eng: I dont ignore the grace of God, because if we become righteous through the Law, then Christ died for no purpose.
local: Ma kete má kabo Nyɛmɛ ayo ya sakpa akere i koro barasu ni. Ama, deke bo m suse n la ka, nzɛn sɔnɔ ŋa i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, na nyini kere ka wɔ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ, nyini la ka, yiwee n bo Kristo wuuri i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: You irrational Galatians! Who put a spell on you? Jesus Christ was put on display as crucified before your eyes!.
local: Ambɛrɛ Galatiya­fɔm, am la má ŋgɔnlɛ wɔ. Ŋma adadaka am a? M yo seeri am, kabo Yesu Kristo wuuri baka jaanu n su barasu ni, ma fiɛ má am su.

eng: I just want to know this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by doing the works of the Law or by believing what you heard?.
local: Am ma m bisa am ahin bisaa­wa ni, am suuri nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su n dɛɛ ti, ye am deeri Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a? Wara nzɛn bo am tiiri Yesu labari kpa n jɔrɛ na am yoori yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye am deeri i a?.

eng: Are you so irrational? After you started with the Spirit, are you now finishing up with your own human effort?.
local: Nzu ti ye am la má ŋgɔnlɛ sɔ a? Am boori i bu ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wɔ, ye i baari sɛ ye am koro ka am fa am faŋga wiewie i a?.

eng: Did you experience so much for nothing? I wonder if it really was for nothing.
local: Deke n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayo i na am aŋu i n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Mini dɔ, i ti má ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: So does the one providing you with the Spirit and working miracles among you do this by you doing the works of the Law or by you believing what you heard?.
local: Bo am ati labari kpa n na am ayo yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ afa i Awiɛwiɛ n ama am, ye Nyɛmɛ yoori alekutura sam am nu a, nzɛn bo am su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su n a?.

eng: Understand that in the same way that Abraham believed God and it was credited to him as righteousness, .
local: Nyini ye ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Abraham yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a.

eng: those who believe are the children of Abraham.
local: Nyini kere ya ka, bo ba yo Nyɛmɛ yarada ni, bɛrɛ ti Abraham busu nu­fɔm nahɔrɛ su a.

eng: But when it saw ahead of time that God would make the Gentiles righteous on the basis of faith, scripture preached the gospel in advance to Abraham: All the Gentiles will be blessed in you .
local: Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n kere ka, minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm bá yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, na i de bu ka ba yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ. Nyɛmɛ du mɔ kaanri labari kpa n jɔrɛ kereeri Abraham wɔ, na wɔ se i ka, Wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, ye m yó adamande kɛrɛ kisi a.

eng: Therefore, those who believe are blessed together with Abraham who believed.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo yarada, Nyɛmɛ yó i kisi, kabo Abraham yoori yarada, na Nyɛmɛ yoori i kisi barasu ni.

eng: All those who rely on the works of the Law are under a curse, because it is written, Everyone is cursed who does not keep on doing all the things that have been written in the Law scroll .
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su na i sunsun ka Nyɛmɛ dé i ka wɔ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, Nyɛmɛ nɔɔ dí i mi. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ nɔɔ dí sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, na i su má i kɛrɛ kpakpati su kɔ wie má.

eng: But since no one is made righteous by the Law as far as God is concerned, it is clear that the righteous one will live on the basis of faith .
local: Ya si ka, má ka sɔnɔ su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ye Nyɛmɛ de i mi ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su m dɔ, i nyá ŋgɔɔ, i yarada n dɛɛ ti.

eng: The Law isnt based on faith; rather, the one doing these things will live by them .
local: Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n wo bɛrɛ i ŋgumi, na yarada n bo ya yo Nyɛmɛ n gusu wo bɛrɛ i ŋgumi. Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ su, i mi nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the Law by becoming a curse for us—because it is written, Everyone who is hung on a tree is cursed .
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, Nyɛmɛ nɔɔ dí i mi, nyini dɛɛ ti ye Kristo kaari ŋgbɛɛn wuuri baka jaanu n su deeri ya tii jiiri n a. Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ yó sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo ba sense i baka n su ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: He redeemed us so that the blessing of Abraham would come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, and that we would receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
local: Deke n bo i ti na Yesu Kristo wuuri nyini yiwee n la ka, nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di ni Abraham ka i yó minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm kisi ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, nzɛn ya yo Kristo yarada, Nyɛmɛ ŋa i ma ya Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo wɔ di i di ka i fa ma ya ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, Ill use an example from human experience. No one ignores or makes additions to a validated will.
local: M ninbaam, m koro ka m fa adamande woo­wa na m fa kere kabo Nyɛmɛ woo­wa ti barasu. Nzɛn minɛ nnyɔ afa sɛsɛ­su adi i di ka bu yo deke be, sɔnɔ wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro kete i, wara i kaaki i.

eng: The promises were made to Abraham and to his descendant. It doesnt say, and to the descendants, as if referring to many rather than just one. It says, and to your descendant, who is Christ.
local: Nyini ti, Nyɛmɛ diiri nyini di n ni Abraham ni i busu nu­fɔ n wɔ, wɔ se má ka i busu nu­fɔm kɛrɛ, ama, i ŋa i busu nu­fɔm kun, yiri la Kristo.

eng: Im saying this: the Law, which came four hundred thirty years later, doesnt invalidate the agreement that was previously validated by God so that it cancels the promise.
local: Deke bo m suse n la ka, Nyɛmɛ yo faari sɛsɛ­su wɔ, na wɔ di i di ni Abraham ka i yo i. Na i dɛɛ afɔ yaa nna ni afɔ abura­san sin, na wɔ fa i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ama Moyisi, ama, sɛsɛ­su n bo wɔ du mɔ adi i di ni, i koro saaki má i.

eng: If the inheritance were based upon the Law, it would no longer be from the promise. But God has given it graciously to Abraham through a promise.
local: Sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ ceeri Abraham aceeri n a. Má ka bo i su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su dɛɛ ti ye i ce i aceeri n a.

eng: So why was the Law given? It was added because of offenses, until the descendant would come to whom the promise had been made. It was put in place through angels by the hand of a mediator.
local: Nzɛn i ti sɔ, nzu ti ye Nyɛmɛ ma Moyisi yo faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari minɛm a? Nyɛmɛ koro ka minɛ si kabo satiɛ n ti wɔ, ye i faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari a, fa kɔ ju saŋga bo Abraham busu nu­fɔ ni bá ba. Yiri ye Nyɛmɛ diiri i di ni Abraham n a. Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari i mɛrɛkɛm wɔ, na ba kan akere Moyisi bo i wo Nyɛmɛ ni i minɛm afiɛn, na i gusu anya akan akere minɛm ni.

eng: Now the mediator does not take one side; but God is one.
local: Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n maari mɛrɛkɛm, ni Moyisi wɔ, ye bu kan kereeri minɛm a, ama, sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ yo diiri i di ni Abraham ni, be wo má bu afiɛn, se Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi.

eng: So, is the Law against the promises of God? Absolutely not! If a Law had been given that was able to give life, then righteousness would in fact have come from the Law.
local: Nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kete dekem bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó i ni, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, i kete má. Nzɛn má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n be wo bɛrɛ bo i koro ma ŋgɔɔ, nyini la ka, nzɛn sɔnɔ suuri nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, ye Nyɛmɛ de i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a.

eng: But scripture locked up all things under sin, so that the promise based on the faithfulness of Jesus Christ might be given to those who have faith.
local: Ama, ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, adena minɛm bo bu yoori Yesu yarada ni, bɛrɛ nyá deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma yarada­yo­fɔm n a.

eng: Before faith came, we were guarded under the Law, locked up until faith that was coming would be revealed.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu nya ba má na ya yo i yarada ni, ya yo su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su a, nyini nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, i kere ya kabo ya yo, ye i niɛ ya su a, haari ba fa juuri saŋga n bo Yesu baari na ya yo i yarada ni.

eng: so that the Law became our custodian until Christ so that we might be made righteous by faith.
local: Nyini ti, kana Yesu bá ba ni, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ti ka sɔnɔ bo i bita ya a, haari na Yesu aba aba, adena Nyɛmɛ de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, ya yarada n dɛɛ ti.

eng: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian.
local: Ama, ahin saŋga n bo ya yo Yesu yarada ni, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bita má ya kun.

eng: You are all Gods children through faith in Christ Jesus.
local: Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am ayo Yesu Kristo yarada ni, nyini dɛɛ ti ye am ayo Nyɛmɛ nbaam a.

eng: All of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
local: Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo bu biɛɛri am Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, am ni Kristo ayo kun lɛɛ, nyini kere ka am ade i dɛɛ woo­wa n wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: There is neither Jew nor Greek; there is neither slave nor free; nor is there male and female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
local: Nyini ti, kabo am ni Kristo ayo kun ni, am kɛrɛ ti deke kun wɔ lɛɛ. Bo bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm, bo bu ti akɔɔ­nbaam ni bo bu ti má akɔɔ­nbaam, nbiɛsɔm ni nbaram kɛrɛ akaaki deke kun wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Now if you belong to Christ, then indeed you are Abrahams descendants, heirs according to the promise.
local: Kisa am ti Kristo dɛɛ minɛm wɔ, nyini la ka, am ti Abraham busu nu­fɔm wɔ, bo am nyá deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma i minɛm ni.

eng: Im saying that as long as the heirs are minors, they are no different from slaves, though they really are the owners of everything.
local: Deke n bo m suse am la ka, awuru­baa bo i bá di i si neŋge n kɛrɛ aja, na i nyunu nya ti má ni, i ni akɔɔ­baa ti kun wɔ, haari bo i si aja n kɛrɛ ti i dɛɛ ni.

eng: However, they are placed under trustees and guardians until the date set by the parents.
local: Saŋga bo i toko ti kaan ni, i wo i minɛm saa nu wɔ, ye bu niɛ i su a, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo i si seeri ka i di aja ni.

eng: In the same way, when we were minors, we were also enslaved by this worlds system.
local: Ya gusu yo ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, dama saŋga bo ya yo si má Yesu jɔrɛ ni, ya yo ti akɔɔm wɔ, fa kɔ durunya n nu satiɛ yoo­wam su.

eng: But when the fulfillment of the time came, God sent his Son, born through a woman, and born under the Law.
local: Ama, saŋga n ba juuri ni, na Nyɛmɛ asunma i Wa, na ba wu i kabo bu wu adamande ni, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni bita i a.

eng: This was so he could redeem those under the Law so that we could be adopted.
local: adena i koro de minɛm bo nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bita bu n tii ji, na bu yo Nyɛmɛ nbaam nahɔrɛ su.

eng: Because you are sons and daughters, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father.
local: Ya ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam dɛɛ ti ye i sunmaari i Wa n dɛɛ Awiɛwiɛ n ka i ba wura ya ahore­mbaa su. Nyini ti, ye ya koro bo fere Nyɛmɛ ka, Abba , i bu la ka, M Si.

eng: Therefore, you are no longer a slave but a son or daughter, and if you are his child, then you are also an heir through God.
local: Nyini ti, a ti má akɔɔ kun, a ti Nyɛmɛ wa a. Nzɛn a nya ti Nyɛmɛ wa, nyini la ka, i má u deke n kɛrɛ bo i fa ma i nbaam ni.

eng: At the time, when you didnt know God, you were enslaved by things that arent gods by nature.
local: Saŋga bo am nya si má Nyɛmɛ ni, am yo suuri dekem bo bu ti má Nyɛmɛ n wɔ, ye am ayo bu akɔɔm a.

eng: But now, after knowing God , how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless world system? Do you want to be slaves to it again?.
local: Ama, kisa, am asi Nyɛmɛ, wara yiri Nyɛmɛ asi am. Nzu ti ye am koro ka am kaaki am sin bo Nyɛmɛ a, na am su durunya n nu satiɛ yoo­wam, bo bu la má faŋga n su a? Nzu ti ye am koro ka am yo bu akɔɔm na am kaaki su bu bekun a?.

eng: You observe religious days and months and seasons and years.
local: Am nyunu aboro cɛɛn­kpirim ni yoo­wam n su a, bɛrɛ la yoo­wa n bo am bu le­mɔcuɛ kɛrɛ ni, ni bo am yo i sara kɛrɛ ni, ni bo afɔ ba yɛ na am yo bu ni.

eng: Im afraid for you! Perhaps my hard work for you has been for nothing.
local: M koro má ka juma n kɛrɛ bo ma yo i ama am n ka ŋgbɛɛn, nyini ti ye am sa­yoo­wa kpiɛ m daafiri cɛɛn kɛrɛ n a.

eng: I beg you to be like me, brothers and sisters, because I have become like you! You havent wronged me.
local: M ninbaam, m susere am wɔ, am ma am woo­wa ti kabo m woo­wa ti barasu ni, dama m woo­wa ti kabo am woo­wa ti n wɔ. Am ado má mini ŋgasi be.

eng: You know that I first preached the gospel to you because of an illness.
local: Am si ka saŋga bo m du mɔ ba kaanri labari kpa n jɔrɛ kereeri am ni, m yo la má laifiɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye m koro nyaari atin a.

eng: Though my poor health burdened you, you didnt look down on me or reject me, but you welcomed me as if I were an angel from God, or as if I were Christ Jesus!.
local: Haari ni bo m yo la má laifiɛ n kɛrɛ ni, na i yoori kekereke ma am ni, am akete má m, abu má m ŋgbɛɛn, ama, am deeri m ni am saa nnyɔ ɔ, kabo má am de Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ wara Yesu Kristo barasu n wɔ.

eng: Where then is the great attitude that you had? I swear that, if possible, you would have dug out your eyes and given them to me.
local: Nzu ti ye kisa am ahore ti má jɔɔ­wa kabo i yo ti dawa n a? Nyini saŋga ni, mini mmɔɔ ni m tii si ka, nzɛn má m yo seeri ka am lokoti yi am nyu­mbaa fa ma m, na má am koro yo i, má am yó i wɔ.

eng: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?.
local: Bo m tu am fɔ seeri am nahɔrɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye kisa ma kaaki am kpɔ­fɔ a?.

eng: They are so concerned about you, though not with good intentions. Rather, they want to shut you out so that you would run after them.
local: Nyini minɛm bo bu boro bu nyi na bu cɛcɛ wo am nu ni, bu sunsunni ti má kpa. Bu koro ka am kaaki yaki Kristo atin n wɔ, na am boro am nyi su bu dɛɛ atin n su.

eng: However, its always good to have people concerned about you with good intentions, and not just when Im there with you.
local: Nzɛn má am nyunu aboro nahɔrɛ su ka am yo sakpa saŋga kɛrɛ, má i ti kpa, nzɛn haari m wo má am dɔ mmɔɔ.

eng: My little children, Im going through labor pains again until Christ is formed in you.
local: M nbaam, am jɔrɛ yo m yaa, kabo bara ba suwu baa na i yo i yaa barasu n wɔ, i suyo m yaa sɔ ɔ, má ka Kristo aba awo am nu, na am woo­wa ti kabo Kristo woo­wa ti barasu ni.

eng: But I wish I could be with you now and change how I sound, because Im at a loss about you.
local: M ahore su, má m koro ka m wo am dɔ kisa, na m da m bɔɛ na ya bo anzama, dama ma tanda fa kɔ am dɔ.

eng: Tell me—those of you who want to be under the Law—dont you listen to the Law?.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am koro ka am su Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ni, am si má kabo i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere n wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: Its written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
local: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Abraham yo la nbaa nbiɛsɔm nnyɔ ɔ, baa n bo afani n wuuri i ni, ni baa n bo awuru­bara wuuri i ni.

eng: The son by the slave woman was conceived the normal way, but the son by the free woman was conceived through a promise.
local: Baa n bo afani n wuuri i ni, kabo bara kɛrɛ nyinsɛ, na bu wu baa ni, i nyinsɛ wuuri i sɔ ɔ. Ama, awuru­bara n dɛɛ wa n bo i wuuri i ni, kabo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri Abraham n ye i wuuri i a.

eng: These things are an allegory: the women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, which gives birth to slave children; this is Hagar.
local: Kabo nbara nnyɔm woo­wa n ti má kun na bu woo­wa wo i ŋgumi ni, i la bu wɔ. Bu ti ka sɛsɛ­su nnyɔm bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di n wɔ. Kun la Haga, afani n bo i dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti akɔɔm ni. I kere kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i yo fa maari Moyisi Sinayi Buka n su n maari minɛm yoori akɔɔm barasu n wɔ.

eng: Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present-day Jerusalem, because the city is in slavery with her children.
local: Haga ade Sinayi Buka n bo i wo Arabiya asiɛn n dɛɛ jinaa­biri wɔ, i ti ka Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n bo i wo durunya n nu fɛn kisa n wɔ, i ni i busu nu­fɔm kɛrɛ ti akɔɔm wɔ.

eng: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
local: Ama, Yɛrusalem n bo i wo aŋgoro n dɛɛ minɛm anya bu tii, bɛrɛ ti awuru­bara Saara dɛɛ nbaam a, yiri ti ya ni a.

eng: Its written: Rejoice, barren woman, you who have not given birth . Break out with a shout, you who have not suffered labor pains; because the woman who has been deserted will have many more children than the woman who has a husband .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Wɔrɔ bara ŋɔrɔka ni, bo a wu má baa ni, ma u ahore jɔ kɔ sin su. Tiɛn, na a siri ni ahorejɔ, wɔrɔ bara n bo a si má baa­wu yaari ni, dama bara n bo i hun akete i n la nbaam tara bara n bo i la hun ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are children of the promise like Isaac.
local: M ninbaam, kisa ambɛrɛ ti ka Izaki bo i ti awuru­baa n a, dama yiri ye Nyɛmɛ seeri Abraham ka bu wu i n a, ye ambɛrɛ gusu ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam n a.

eng: But just as it was then, so it is now also: the one who was conceived the normal way harassed the one who was conceived by the Spirit.
local: Kabo nyini saŋga na afani n wa n yo kereeri awuru­bara n wa Izaki n wahara ni, yiri ye bu wuuri i ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu a. Minɛm bo bu su Moyisi nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ni, sumiɛ yɛrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n yiko nu na ya kaaki Nyɛmɛ nbaam n sɔ lɛɛ.

eng: But what does the scripture say? Throw out the slave woman and her son, because the slave womans son wont share the inheritance with the free womans son .
local: Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n se sɛ wɔ? I ŋa, Fɔn yi ahin afani n ni i wa n fite fɛn. Wɔ ti má ye ni ka ahin bara n wa n nya aja neŋge n be. Ama, i ti ye ni ka awuru­bara n wa ni fá i kɛrɛ a.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we arent the slave womans children, but we are the free womans children.
local: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, ya ti má afani n dɛɛ nbaam, ama, ya ti awuru­bara n dɛɛ nbaam wɔ.

eng: Christ has set us free for freedom. Therefore, stand firm and dont submit to the bondage of slavery again.
local: Kristo ade ya tii aji, nyini ti, am wo ni nyini tii­de n bo am anya i n nu, am bita i kenken na má am wo ka am ti akɔɔm kun.

eng: Look, I, Paul, am telling you that if you have yourselves circumcised, having Christ wont help you.
local: Am ti kabo mini Pɔɔlu suse am ni. Nzɛn am soro su na be wuraari am kɛnkɛnnɛ, nyini la ka, deke n bo Kristo ayo ama am n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Again I swear to every man who has himself circumcised that he is required to do the whole Law.
local: M koro ka m bɔbɔ am wɔ lɛɛ bekun, ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i soro su na bu wuraari i kɛnkɛnnɛ, wɔ yo daka ka i mi su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ su, má i yaki kun n kpein mmɔɔ.

eng: You people who are trying to be made righteous by the Law have been estranged from Christ. You have fallen away from grace!.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am sunsun ka am su nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su, na am ŋa nyini ma Nyɛmɛ de am ka am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ni, am ni Kristo akpaaki lɛɛ, am di má Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n kun.

eng: We eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness through the Spirit by faith.
local: Ama, yɛrɛ yiri, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama ya yo yarada, ye ya la ahoresi ŋminda ka Nyɛmɛ bá de ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ.

eng: Being circumcised or not being circumcised doesnt matter in Christ Jesus, but faith working through love does matter.
local: Nzɛn ya ni Kristo ayo kun, na ya wura kɛnkɛnnɛ, i ti má pui, nzɛn ya wura má kɛnkɛnnɛ gusu, i ti má pui. Ama, nzɛn ya yo Yesu Kristo yarada, na ya koro ya beŋgum na ya yo sakpa, nyini ti jɔrɛ n a.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if a person is caught doing something wrong, you who are spiritual should restore someone like this with a spirit of gentleness. Watch out for yourselves so you wont be tempted too.
local: M ninbaam, nzɛn am ŋu ka am nu be suyo satiɛ, ambɛrɛ bo am wo kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n kere ka am wo barasu ni, am tara am ŋu bɛnlɛ, na am buka i mi na i yo sa bo i ti kpa. Na am si am ŋu sa, na má am gusu yo satiɛ be.

eng: You were running well—who stopped you from obeying the truth?.
local: Kabo am yo su Kristo atin n su ni, am yo boro am nyi wɔ nahɔrɛ su. Kisa ŋma adaka am ye am akaaki am sunsunni ka má am de nahɔrɛ n a?.

eng: Carry each others burdens and so you will fulfill the law of Christ.
local: Nzɛn am ŋu ka am beŋgu wo kekereke nu, am buka i. Nzɛn am yo sɔ, nyini kere ka am ade Kristo keree­wam n bo wɔ fa ama ya n nahɔrɛ su lɛɛ.

eng: If anyone thinks they are important when they arent, theyre fooling themselves.
local: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a ti má pui, na a sunsun ka a ti be ni, a dadaka u ŋu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: This line of reasoning doesnt come from the one who calls you.
local: Nyini nɔaniɛ n bo ba fa akaaki am sunsunni n fite má Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ fere am ka am ba su i n dɔ.

eng: Each person should test their own work and be happy with doing a good job and not compare themselves with others.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ niɛ i sa­yoo­wa, nzɛn i mi ŋu ka i sa­yoo­wa ti kpa, nyini saŋga, i mi koro kansi i ŋu. Ama, má i niɛ ka i sa­yoo­wa tara i beŋgum dɛɛ ti, na i fa fɔ.

eng: A little yeast works through the whole lump of dough.
local: Bu ŋa, ayiri n bo bu fa gɔ kpɔnɔ nu n ti kaan sɔ ɔ, ama, yiri ma i yasu yo dɔŋgu a.

eng: Each person will have to carry their own load.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ la i tiɛŋgu sa­yoo­wa.

eng: Im convinced about you in the Lord that you wont think any other way. But the one who is confusing you will pay the penalty, whoever that may be.
local: Ama, m la ahoresi fa kɔ am dɔ, kabo ya ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ayo kun ni, ka, am káaki má am sunsunni na am yaki atin n bo ya bita i ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka am kaaki am sunsunni ni, Nyɛmɛ cín i mi sui.

eng: Those who are taught the word should share all good things with their teacher.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo bu sukere am Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ni, am buka bɛrɛ n bo bu kere am ni, ni deke kɛrɛ bo am la i.

eng: Brothers and sisters, if Im still preaching circumcision, why am I still being harassed? In that case, the offense of the cross would be canceled.
local: M ninbaam, nbem ŋa m toko nati kan i ka i ti ye ni ka Kristo minɛm kɛrɛ wura kɛnkɛnnɛ. Nzɛn má i ti nahɔrɛ ka m kan i sɔ ɔ, ye nzu ti ye Yuda­fɔm kere m wuwuruwa n a? Nzɛn má m kan i sɔ, ye haari nzɛn m kan fa kɔ yiwee n bo Yesu wuuri i baka jaanu n su ni, má i ti má jɔrɛ.

eng: Make no mistake, God is not mocked. A person will harvest what they plant.
local: Má am dadaka am ŋu, na am sunsun ka be koro dadaka Nyɛmɛ, dama deke kɛrɛ bo sɔnɔ yoori, Nyɛmɛ fá nyini tɔ i mi kaari a.

eng: I wish that the ones who are upsetting you would castrate themselves!.
local: Minɛm bo bu miɛ am ka am wura kɛnkɛnnɛ ni, má i yo m fiɛ ka bu sa bu ŋu na má bu biɛsɔ dika yo juma.

eng: You were called to freedom, brothers and sisters; only dont let this freedom be an opportunity to indulge your selfish impulses, but serve each other through love.
local: M ninbaam, Nyɛmɛ afere am ka i de am tii ji wɔ. Ama, má am niɛ ka am anya am tii n dɛɛ ti, na am yo am kunnu koroo­wa, ama, am koro am beŋgu nahɔrɛ su, na am buka am beŋgu.

eng: Those who plant only for their own benefit will harvest devastation from their selfishness, but those who plant for the benefit of the Spirit will harvest eternal life from the Spirit.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ yo kabo adamande woo­wa koro barasu ni, nyini má i mi wu a na i ka ŋgbɛɛn. Nzɛn i yo kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n koro ka i yo ni, nyini má i mi bá nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri a.

eng: All the Law has been fulfilled in a single statement: Love your neighbor as yourself .
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ jina nɔaniɛ maa­wa kun su a, yiri la ka, Koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni.

eng: Lets not get tired of doing good, because in time well have a harvest if we dont give up.
local: Nyini ti, má am ma ya fie ni sakpa­yo, dama nzɛn ya fie má, na saŋga n ba juuri, Nyɛmɛ tɔ́ ya ŋkatɔ.

eng: But if you bite and devour each other, be careful that you dont get eaten up by each other!.
local: Ama, nzɛn saŋga kɛrɛ na am sudi aluwa na am ŋumi sukpi am beŋgu ŋu, na am asi má am ŋu sa, am sáaki am ŋuŋu nu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: So then, lets work for the good of all whenever we have an opportunity, and especially for those in the household of faith.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn ya la deke ya saa nu bo ya fa yo sakpa, ya yo buka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ, maala ya lamɔkɔ Kristo­fɔm ni, bɛrɛ yiri, i ti ye ni ka ya buka bu saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: I say be guided by the Spirit and you wont carry out your selfish desires.
local: M suse am wɔ, am wo kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n kere am ka am wo ni, adena má am yo am kunnu koroo­wa kabo dawa na am yo yoori barasu ni.

eng: Look at the large letters Im making with my own handwriting!.
local: Am niɛ, mini ni m tii sufa m saa sukɛrɛ ahin kɛrɛɛ­wa n ŋgbiŋgbiri n kɛrɛ ma am a.

eng: A persons selfish desires are set against the Spirit, and the Spirit is set against ones selfish desires. They are opposed to each other, so you shouldnt do whatever you want to do.
local: Dama deke bo yɛrɛ adamande kunnu koro i ni, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n koro má i, ye deke bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n koro i ni, yɛrɛ adamande dɛɛ woo­wa n koro má i. Nyini ti, saŋga kɛrɛ yɛrɛ adamande dɛɛ woo­wa n ni Awiɛwiɛ Casi n se akuruwa ni i beŋgu wɔ. Nyini ye ya koro yo má kabo ya koro ka ya yo n a.

eng: Whoever wants to look good by human standards will try to get you to be circumcised, but only so they wont be harassed for the cross of Christ.
local: Minɛm bo bu miɛ am ka am wura kɛnkɛnnɛ ni, bu koro ka minɛ kansi bu wɔ. Deke n bo i ti na bu yo sɔ n la ka, bu koro má ka minɛ kere bu wuwuruwa fa kɔ yiwee n bo Kristo wuuri i baka jaanu n su ni.

eng: But if you are being led by the Spirit, you arent under the Law.
local: Nzɛn am yo kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n koro ka am yo ni, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n niɛ má am su kun.

eng: The actions that are produced by selfish motives are obvious, since they include sexual immorality, moral corruption, doing whatever feels good.
