local: Ama, kisa wɔ yo m fiɛ kabo m sudi wahara ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ni, na wahara n bo m di i m ŋuniɛ n nu ni, m sudi i sɔ haari m kɔ wie i, kabo Kristo gusu diiri wahara ni, m dí i ma i jamam bo bu ti i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n wɔ.

eng: I became a servant of the church by Gods commission, which was given to me for you, in order to complete Gods word.
local: Nyɛmɛ ayi m ka m yo Kristo jama n amusui­su­fɔ a, ye wɔ ma m ahin juma n ka m yo buka am a. Nyini la bo i ŋa m kan i nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ pepere kere am ni.

eng: Im completing it with a secret plan that has been hidden for ages and generations but which has now been revealed to his holy people.
local: I ti jɔrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ yo fa fiɛɛri dawa­fɔm kɛrɛ su wɔ, ama, kisa wɔ fa i akere i minɛm bo wɔ yi bu ni.

eng: God wanted to make the glorious riches of this secret plan known among the Gentiles, which is Christ living in you, the hope of glory.
local: Bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ koro ka i kere bu jɔrɛ bo i wo fiɛɛ­wa nu n a, yiri la alekutura sa bo i la má aŋusu na i koro ka i yo ma ambɛrɛ bo am ti má Yuda­fɔm ni. Yiri la ka, Kristo wo am nu, na am la ahoresi ka am nyá i dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n be.

eng: This is what we preach as we warn and teach every person with all wisdom so that we might present each one mature in Christ.
local: Ya kan Kristo jɔrɛ kere sɔnɔ kɛrɛ wɔ, na ya kere bu na ya bɔbɔ bu, ni alecira bo wɔ fa ama ya ni, na bu ba i dɔ ba su i ka minɛm bo bu nyunu ati Kristo dɛɛ atin su.

eng: I work hard and struggle for this goal with his energy, which works in me powerfully.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m yo ahin juma n ni anyum­boro n a. I dɛɛ yiko n bo i la má aŋusu ni ma m faŋga ye m koro yo juma n a.

eng: I want you to know how much I struggle for you, for those in Laodicea, and for all who havent known me personally.
local: M koro ka am si kabo m tu ŋgoni yo juma n ni anyum­boro ma ambɛrɛ, ni Lawodikaya­fɔm, ni minɛm bo bu nya si má m ni.

eng: My goal is that their hearts would be encouraged and united together in love so that they might have all the riches of assurance that come with understanding, so that they might have the knowledge of the secret plan of God, namely Christ.
local: M koro ka m yo sɔ na i wura am kpɛrɛ kekereke, na am la anɔɔsɛ, na am koro am beŋgu ni nahɔrɛ su, na am si Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su, na am de i nɔaniɛ ni, na i ma am ahorejɔ, na am si jɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ yo fa fiɛɛri ni. Yiri la Yesu Kristo.

eng: All the treasures of wisdom and knowledge are hidden in him.
local: Yiri ŋgumi ti bo i koro ma ya alecira ni si kɛrɛ fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n bo i wo fiɛɛ­wa nu wo i dɔ ni.

eng: Im telling you this so that no one deceives you with convincing arguments.
local: M koro má ka be fa anɔɔfiɛfiɛ daka am, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m se am sɔ n a.

eng: because even though I am absent physically, Im with you in spirit. Im happy to see the discipline and stability of your faith in Christ.
local: Haari ni bo m wo má am dɔ ni, m sunsunni yiri wo am dɔ, kabo am ayo Kristo yarada nahɔrɛ su na am la anɔɔsɛ bita i atin kenken ni, nyini ayo m fiɛfiɛ agaya.

eng: So live in Christ Jesus the Lord in the same way as you received him.
local: Kabo am asoro su ade Yesu Kristo ka am Mibiɛ ni, am ma am woo­wa ti ka minɛm bo bu ni yiri ayo kun ni.

eng: Be rooted and built up in him, be established in faith, and overflow with thanksgiving just as you were taught.
local: Am bita i kekereke, na am subuka su fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin su, na am ma am yarada yo bii­wa nahɔrɛ su kabo ba kere am ni. Na am suda i asi agaya.

eng: See to it that nobody enslaves you with philosophy and foolish deception, which conform to human traditions and the way the world thinks and acts rather than Christ.
local: Am si am ŋu sa, na má be fa adamande ŋgɔnlɛ, ni sa ŋgbɛɛn be dadaka am na am su bu su. Bɛrɛ la adamande keree­wam, ni durunya n nu satiɛ yoo­wam bo bu fa kere minɛm na i ti má Kristo dɛɛ keree­wa ni.

eng: All the fullness of deity lives in Christs body.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ woo­wa kɛrɛ wo Kristo dɛɛ ŋuniɛ n nu wɔ.

eng: And you have been filled by him, who is the head of every ruler and authority.
local: Kabo am ni yiri ayo kun ni, nyini ma am anya deke kɛrɛ pepere a. Yiri ti yiko­fɔm, ni nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ tii a.

eng: In him you were also circumcised with a circumcision not administered by human hands. The circumcision of Christ is realized in the stripping away of the whole self dominated by sin.
local: Kabo am ni Kristo ayo kun ni, nyini kere ka am awura kɛnkɛnnɛ nahɔrɛ su a, má aŋuniɛ­mbaa su yoo­wa bo adamande yo i ni, ama, yiri la bo Kristo ayi am satiɛ n kɛrɛ ni.

eng: You were buried with him through baptism and raised with him through faith in the power of God, who raised him from the dead.
local: Saŋga bo bu biɛɛri am Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, i kere ka ba saŋga ase am ni Kristo wɔ lɛɛ, na am ni yiri asaŋga ateŋge i yiwee n nu lɛɛ, dama am la yarada ka Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ yiko n nu ye wɔ teŋge Kristo i yiwee n nu a.

eng: When you were dead because of the things you had done wrong and because your body wasnt circumcised, God made you alive with Christ and forgave all the things you had done wrong.
local: Saŋga be, am yo ti fuinm wɔ, dama am ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ, na am ayo awura má kɛnkɛnnɛ na am su má Nyɛmɛ. Ama, kisa Nyɛmɛ ama am ni Kristo asaŋga anya ŋgɔɔ fɔfɔrɛ. Wɔ fa ya satiɛ kɛrɛ ace ya.

eng: He destroyed the record of the debt we owed, with its requirements that worked against us. He canceled it by nailing it to the cross.
local: Satiɛ kɛrɛ bo ya yo yoori na ba kɛrɛ i ka i ti ŋgasi jɔrɛ ni, Yesu yiwee n bo i wuuri baka jaanu n su n dɛɛ ti, wɔ ŋmisi ayi nyini ŋgasi sa n kɛrɛ.

eng: When he disarmed the rulers and authorities, he exposed them to public disgrace by leading them in a triumphal parade.
local: na wɔ kunma yiko­fɔm ni nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ, awura bu nyinsɛ fofoe nu.

eng: So dont let anyone judge you about eating or drinking or about a festival, a new moon observance, or sabbaths.
local: Nyini ti, má am ma be jaraki am fa kɔ sa­yoo­wa be su, kabo am yo fa kɔ diire dii­wam ni bu ciirem, wara deke nuu­wam ni bu ciirem, wara cɛɛn­kpiri ciirem, wara sara fɔfɔrɛ ciire yoo­wam, wara Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn n ciirem.

eng: These religious practices are only a shadow of what was coming—the body that cast the shadow is Christ.
local: Nyini yoo­wam kɛrɛ ferema ni deke n bo i suba n wɔ, yiri la Kristo.

eng: Dont let anyone who wants to practice harsh self-denial and worship angels rob you of the prize. They go into detail about what they have seen in visions and have become unjustifiably arrogant by their selfish way of thinking.
local: Am si am ŋu sa ni minɛm bo bu ŋa ba ŋu deke yikeree­wa nbem, na bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti tarakam, na bu su mɛrɛkɛm ni. Adamande dɛɛ sunsunni nu kpirikoro la nyini n kɛrɛ bo bu yo sɔ ni. Má am ma bu ŋu am ŋgasi be.

eng: They dont stay connected to the head. The head nourishes and supports the whole body through the joints and ligaments, so the body grows with a growth that is from God.
local: Nyini minɛm sɔ da má Kristo bo i ti i jama n tii n ŋu. I jama n ti i ŋuniɛ­mbaa a. Yiri niɛ i ŋuniɛ­mbaa barasu barasu n kɛrɛ suyo i kun a, na i ma i wura i beŋgu nu, na i sasa suyo faŋga, na i buka nyɛmɛsu atin su, kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni.

eng: If you died with Christ to the way the world thinks and acts, why do you submit to rules and regulations as though you were living in the world?.
local: Kabo am ni Kristo asaŋga awu ni, am anya am tii afite durunya n nu satiɛ yoo­wam kɛrɛ nu. Nzu ti ye am su bu nyini ciirem su a?.

eng: Dont handle! Dont taste! Dont touch.
local: Bo bu ŋa, má am tara ahin, má am di ahin, ni bo bu ŋa má am fa am saa kan ahin dekem ni.

eng: All these things cease to exist when they are used. Such rules are human commandments and teachings.
local: Nyini ciirem kɛrɛ ti adamande dɛɛ keree­wam wɔ, nzɛn sɔnɔ suuri bu su, bu kɛrɛ kaari ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: They look like they are wise with this self-made religion and their self-denial by the harsh treatment of the body, but they are no help against indulging in selfish immoral behavior.
local: Nyini ciirem ni, minɛ sunsun ka bɛrɛ n bo bu su nyini n kɛrɛ su n ti alecira­fɔm wɔ, ama, bu ti má alecira­fɔm, bu su dekem bo bu ahore­mbaa koro ni su a, na bu yo bu ŋu taraka, na bu sukere bu ŋu wahara, ama, nyini n kɛrɛ bo bu yo i ni, bu koro tara má bu ŋu fa kɔ adamande konviki dika.

eng: Therefore, if you were raised with Christ, look for the things that are above where Christ is sitting at Gods right side.
local: Nyini ti, kabo am ni Kristo asaŋga ateŋge i yiwee n nu ni, am ma am ahore­mbaa kɛrɛ wo dekem bo bu wo aŋgoro n su, dika n bo Kristo tana Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su ni.

eng: Think about the things above and not things on earth.
local: Am ma am sunsunni kɛrɛ wo dekem bo bu wo aŋgoro n su, ama, má dekem bo bu wo durunya n nu fɛn ni.

eng: You died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
local: Dama am ni Kristo asaŋga awu wɔ, ye am ni yiri ayo kun a, ye Nyɛmɛ afa am ni yiri ŋgɔɔ afiɛ yiri ni i tii dɔ a.

eng: When Christ, who is your life, is revealed, then you also will be revealed with him in glory.
local: Kristo ti am ŋgɔɔ nahɔrɛ su a, i ba baari, am ni yiri kɛrɛ sáŋga fite a, na am nyá i jirima woo­wa ni.

eng: So put to death the parts of your life that belong to the earth, such as sexual immorality, moral corruption, lust, evil desire, and greed .
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am yaki am ŋuniɛ­mbaa sa koroo­wa kɛrɛ, má am yo sakaraya, am yaki daŋgba sa­yoo­wam, am yaki konviki ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, má am ma am nyunu boro satiɛ­yo su. Má am ma am nyunu boro neŋge kpini su, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sɔ, i mi su má Nyɛmɛ, i su neŋgem wɔ.

eng: The wrath of God is coming upon disobedient people because of these things.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ fá yaa cin minɛm sui a, bɛrɛ bo ba kete ka bu de má i nɔaniɛ na bu yo nyini sam ni.

eng: You used to live this way, when you were alive to these things.
local: Saŋga be, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii yo yo nyini sam wɔ, dama kabo am woo­wa yo ti lɛɛ.

eng: But now set aside these things, such as anger, rage, malice, slander, and obscene language.
local: Ama, kisa, am yaki nyini kɛrɛ fieo, yaa­fa ni aŋunkpini, na má am ma nzukɔ kpiɛ ni jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn fite am nɔɔ nu.

eng: Dont lie to each other. Take off the old human nature with its practices.
local: má am bu kɔrɛ sɔ am beŋgu, dama am ayaki am dawa dɛɛ yoo­wa n kɛrɛ fieo fieo.

eng: and put on the new nature, which is renewed in knowledge by conforming to the image of the one who created it.
local: am afa wo fɔfɔrɛ. Nyɛmɛ suma am nyini woo­wa fɔfɔrɛ n a, adena i má am woo­wa ti kabo i dɛɛ woo­wa ti barasu ni, na am koro si i nahɔrɛ su.

eng: In this image there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcised nor uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave nor free, but Christ is all things and in all people.
local: Nyini woo­wa fɔfɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ ayo i n dɛɛ ti, Yuda­fɔm ni minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ti kun wɔ, minɛm bo ba wura kɛnkɛnnɛ o, ni bo ba wura má kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ, ni minɛm bo bu jɔjɔ aniɛ barasu nbem, ni minɛm bo bu nyunu nya teke má, ni akɔɔm ni awuru­nbaam, bu kɛrɛ ti kun wɔ. Ama, bo i kɔ tara bu kɛrɛ n la Kristo, yiri wo bu kɛrɛ ahore su a.

eng: Therefore, as Gods choice, holy and loved, put on compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience.
local: Am ti minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ koro am, na wɔ yi am afa ayo i dɛɛm wɔ. Nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka am si aŋunfɔ na am buka minɛm, na am yo am ŋu kaan, na am yo bɛnlɛ, na am la mindi.

eng: Be tolerant with each other and, if someone has a complaint against anyone, forgive each other. As the Lord forgave you, so also forgive each other.
local: na am yo sawɔrɛ de am beŋgum. Nzɛn be ni i beŋgu anya jɔrɛ, am fa ce am beŋgu. Kabo Kristo afa am ŋgasi ace am ni, ambɛrɛ gusu fa am beŋgum ŋgasi ce bu.

eng: And over all these things put on love, which is the perfect bond of unity.
local: Bo i tara i kɛrɛ n la ka, am koro am beŋgu, dama koro ma am yo kun a, na am yo anɔɔsɛ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: The peace of Christ must control your hearts—a peace into which you were called in one body. And be thankful people.
local: Kristo afere am ayo am deke kun ama am ahoreda, nyini dɛɛ ti, am ma nyini ahoreda n wo am dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, na am suda Nyɛmɛ asi.

eng: The word of Christ must live in you richly. Teach and warn each other with all wisdom by singing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs. Sing to God with gratitude in your hearts.
local: Am ma Kristo jɔrɛ n wo am ahore su ni nahɔrɛ su. Am fa Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ na am fa kere am beŋgu, ni alecira kɛrɛ bo am la i ni, na am fa tu am beŋgu fɔ. Am fa Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛm bo bu wo kansi jue kɛrɛɛ­wam n nu n fa di jue kansi Nyɛmɛ, na am di jue n bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n kere am, na am di jue da Nyɛmɛ asi am ahore su.

eng: Whatever you do, whether in speech or action, do it all in the name of the Lord Jesus and give thanks to God the Father through him.
local: Am sa­yoo­wa, ni am jɔjɔɔ­wa kɛrɛ nu, am yo i ya Mibiɛ Yesu dɛɛ duma n nu, na am da ya Si Nyɛmɛ asi Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: Wives, submit to your husbands in a way that is appropriate in the Lord.
local: Nbaram, am de am hunm nɔaniɛ kabo i ti ye ni ka Mibiɛ n susu­fɔm yo ni.

eng: Husbands, love your wives and dont be harsh with them.
local: Nbiɛsɔm, am koro am yim, má am bita bu ni joko tiɛ.

eng: Children, obey your parents in everything, because this pleases the Lord.
local: Nbaatumam, am de am sim ni am nim nɔaniɛ fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ su. Nzɛn am yo sɔ, nyini yo Mibiɛ n fiɛ a.

eng: Parents, dont provoke your children in a way that ends up discouraging them.
local: Nbaa­sim, má am yo sa bo i yɛ am nbaam yaa su, na bu ahore saaki.

eng: Slaves, obey your masters on earth in everything. Dont just obey like people pleasers when they are watching. Instead, obey with the single motivation of fearing the Lord.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti akɔɔm ni, am de am mibiɛm bo bu wo durunya n nu fɛn n nɔaniɛ deke kɛrɛ nu, má am yo ka bu niɛ am dɛɛ ti, na am koro ka am yo na bu ahore jɔ am ŋu, ama, am yo deke kɛrɛ bo bu ŋa am yo i n ni am ahore­mbaa kun, ka am su Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Whatever you do, do it from the heart for the Lord and not for people.
local: Am yo am juma kɛrɛ ni am ahore­mbaa kun, ka am suyo ma Mibiɛ n wɔ, ama, má ka am suyo ma adamande wɔ.

eng: You know that you will receive an inheritance as a reward. You serve the Lord Christ.
local: Am si ka am nyá ŋkatɔ bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i minɛm ni. Dama am Mibiɛ n bo am su i ni, yiri la Kristo wɔ.

eng: But evildoers will receive their reward for their evil actions. There is no discrimination.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori sa bo i ti má kpa, Nyɛmɛ tɔ́ i mi kaari, dama Nyɛmɛ tantambu má nu.

eng: Masters, be just and fair to your slaves, knowing that you yourselves have a master in heaven.
local: Ambɛrɛ gusu bo am ti mibiɛm ni, am yo am akɔɔm kpa, na am yo deke bo i ti ye ni ka am yo ma bu ni, dama am si ka am gusu la am Mibiɛ n bo i wo aŋgoro.

eng: Keep on praying and guard your prayers with thanksgiving.
local: Am susere Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni anyum­boro, má am fa am ŋu ji, am suda i asi.

eng: At the same time, pray for us also. Pray that God would open a door for the word so we can preach the secret plan of Christ—which is why Im in chains.
local: Nzɛn am sere Nyɛmɛ, am sere ma ya gusu, na Nyɛmɛ teke atin ma ya na ya kan labari kpa n fa kɔ Kristo jɔrɛ n bo i yo wo fiɛɛ­wa nu n kere minɛm. Dama labari kpa n dɛɛ jɔrɛ ti, ye m wo dan­saraka nu n a.

eng: Pray that I might be able to make it as clear as I ought to when I preach.
local: Am sere Nyɛmɛ ma m, na i jina m sin na m koro kan labari kpa n kabo i ti ye ni ka m yi i bu nyunyumi kere minɛm na bu ti i bu.

eng: Act wisely toward outsiders, making the most of the opportunity.
local: Am fa ŋgɔnlɛ na am si kabo am wo fa kɔ minɛm bo bu ti má Kristo­fɔm dɔ. Saŋga kɛrɛ bo am nya atin, am fa yo sakpa, kabo i ti ye ni ka am yo ni. Má am saaki am saŋga n bo am la i ni.

eng: Your speech should always be gracious and sprinkled with insight so that you may know how to respond to every person.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo am ŋa am jɔjɔ minɛm dɔ, am ma am nɔaniɛ fite kpa na i yo bu fiɛfiɛ, na am si kabo am bɔ sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nu barasu.

eng: Tychicus, our dearly loved brother, faithful minister, and fellow slave in the Lord, will inform you about everything that has happened to me.
local: Ya niwa Tikikusi bá kan m ŋusu jɔrɛ kɛrɛ kere am. Yiri ye ya koro i agaya, i bita nahɔrɛ fa kɔ i juma su, i ti ya lamɔkɔ akɔɔ ɔ, fa kɔ Mibiɛ n juma n su.

eng: This is why I sent him to you, so that youll know all about us and so he can encourage your hearts.
local: Nyini ti, ye m susunma i am dɔ n a, ka i ba kan kere am kabo ya kɛrɛ ti barasu, na am ahore si.

eng: I sent him with Onesimus, our faithful and dearly loved brother, who is one of you. They will let you know about everything here.
local: I ni Onisimɔ suba a. Yiri mmɔɔ ti ya niwa bo ya koro i wɔ, i bita nahɔrɛ fa kɔ i juma su, i ti am be wɔ. Bu bá kan deke kɛrɛ bo i suyo fɛn kere am.

eng: Aristarchus, my fellow prisoner, says hello to you. So does Mark, Barnabas cousin .
local: Arisitakusi bo m ni yiri kɛrɛ wo dan­saraka nu fɛn ni, bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu. Banabasi niɛma Marako gusu bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu. Ma du mɔ ase am kabo am yo barasu, ka i baari, am de i ni am saa nnyɔ.

eng: Jesus, called Justus, also says hello. These are my only fellow workers for Gods kingdom who are Jewish converts. They have been an encouragement to me.
local: Yesu n bo bu kaaki fere i ka Jusitosi n gusu bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu. Bɛrɛ minɛ nsan n ŋgumi ti m lamɔkɔ Yuda­fɔm bo ba yo yarada, na m ni bɛrɛ saŋga yo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu juma n a. Bu ti minɛm bo bu ma m ahoresi.

eng: Epaphras, who is one of you, says hello. Hes a slave of Christ Jesus who always wrestles for you in prayers so that you will stand firm and be fully mature and complete in the entire will of God.
local: Ipafarasi gusu bo i ti am be na i ti Kristo akɔɔ n bisa am dɔŋgu. Saŋga kɛrɛ i sere Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro ma am wɔ, adena am la jinakun­su na am su Nyɛmɛ ni nahɔrɛ, na am yarada yo bii­wa, na am de i nɔaniɛ ni kabo Nyɛmɛ koro n kɛrɛ.

eng: I can vouch for him that he has worked hard for you and for those in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
local: Mini mmɔɔ ni m tii koro di i daani, kabo i yi anyum­boro yo juma ma ambɛrɛ, ni Lawodikaya­fɔm, ni Hɛrapolisi­fɔm barasu.

eng: Luke, the dearly loved physician, and Demas say hello.
local: Luka bo ya koro i na i ti dɔkɔtiɛ ni, i ni Demasi kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu.

eng: Say hello to the brothers and sisters in Laodicea, along with Nympha and the church that meets in her house.
local: Am bisa ya ninbaam kɛrɛ bo bu wo Lawodikaya ni, ni Nimfa, ni Kristo jama n kɛrɛ bo bu nati tiɛn bu nɔɔ i dika n dɔŋgu.

eng: After this letter has been read to you publicly, make sure that the church in Laodicea reads it and that you read the one from Laodicea.
local: Nzɛn am kaanri ahin kadasi n wieeri, am yo aniɛ na am fa ma Lawodikaya­fɔm na bu gusu kan i. Na am boro am nyi na am gusu kan m kadasi n bo bɛrɛ Lawodikaya­fɔm fa ma am ni.

eng: And tell Archippus, See to it that you complete the ministry that you received in the Lord.
local: Am se Akipusi ka i boro i nyi, na i yo juma n bo Mibiɛ n fa maari i ni, na i wie i.

eng: I, Paul, am writing this greeting personally. Remember that Im in prison. Grace be with you.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii sufa m saa sukɛrɛ ahin kɛrɛɛ­wa n bisa am dɔŋgu a. Má am ma am wara fi ka m wo dan­saraka nu wɔ. Nyɛmɛ buka am kɛrɛ.

eng: From Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy. To the Thessalonians church that is in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Grace and peace to all of you.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu, ni Silivanusi, ni Timoti sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ Kristo jama n bo am wo Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu na am ti ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ minɛm ni. Nyɛmɛ buka am, na i ma am ahoreda.

eng: We always thank God for all of you when we mention you constantly in our prayers.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ ya da Nyɛmɛ asi ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti wɔ, na ya susere Nyɛmɛ ma am.

eng: This is because we remember your work that comes from faith, your effort that comes from love, and your perseverance that comes from hope in our Lord Jesus Christ in the presence of our God and Father.
local: na ya suteŋge am su ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ, kabo am fa am yarada fa yo juma barasu ni, ni kabo am la koro, na am fa yo juma barasu ni, ni kabo am bita am tama n kenken fa kɔ ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɔ ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you are loved by God, and we know that he has chosen you.
local: Ya ninbaam, Nyɛmɛ koro am, na ya si ka wɔ yi am ka am yo i tiɛŋgu dɛɛ minɛm, ya si ka i ti sɔ ɔ.

eng: We know this because our good news didnt come to you just in speech but also with power and the Holy Spirit and with deep conviction. You know as well as we do what kind of people we were when we were with you, which was for your sake.
local: dama saŋga bo ya kaanri labari kpa n kereeri am ni, wɔ ti má nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa ŋgumi wɔ, ama, i fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko n nu wɔ, ye am siiri tenle su ka i ti nahɔrɛ a. Saŋga bo ya woori am dɔ ni, am ŋuuri kabo ya wo tiiri barasu, i fiteeri ka ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti ye ya woori sɔ a, adena i buka am.

eng: You became imitators of us and of the Lord when you accepted the message that came from the Holy Spirit with joy in spite of great suffering.
local: Na am asɔn ya ni Mibiɛ n dɛɛ wo n su, haari ni bo am diiri wahara agaya n kɛrɛ ni, am deeri labari kpa n wɔ, ni ahorejɔ n bo i fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ ni.

eng: As a result you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and Achaia.
local: Am nyini wo n ti, yarada­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo Makɛdoniya ni Akaya awɔrɛm n nu aŋu ka i ti ye ni ka bu sɔn am su.

eng: The message about the Lord rang out from you, not only in Macedonia and Achaia but in every place. The news about your faithfulness to God has spread so that we dont even need to mention it.
local: Má ka Makɛdoniya ni Akaya­fɔm tiiri Mibiɛ n labari n fiteeri am dɔ n ŋgumi, ama, yarada n bo am yoori Nyɛmɛ ni, i jɔrɛ abara ayɛ deke kɛrɛ. Nyini ti, ya la má jɔrɛ be bo ya kan kun.

eng: People tell us about what sort of welcome we had from you and how you turned to God from idols. As a result, you are serving the living and true God.
local: Nyini minɛm n kɛrɛ kan kabo am deeri ya barasu, saŋga bo ya baari am dɔ ni, ni kabo am kaakiiri am sin boori amɔɛm na am su Nyɛmɛ bo i ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ na i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni.

eng: and you are waiting for his Son from heaven. His Son is Jesus, who is the one he raised from the dead and who is the one who will rescue us from the coming wrath.
local: na am ŋminda i Wa Yesu, yiri fíte Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro ba a, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ ateŋge i yiwee n nu n a, yiri yí ya fite jina cɛɛn dɛɛ kekereke n nu a.

eng: As you yourselves know, brothers and sisters, our visit with you wasnt a waste of time.
local: Ya ninbaam, ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si ka, ba n bo ya baari am dɔ ni, wɔ ka má ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: On the contrary, we had the courage through God to speak Gods good news in spite of a lot of opposition, although we had already suffered and were publicly insulted in Philippi, as you know.
local: Am si kabo bu boori ya, na bu wuraari ya nyinsɛ Filipiya miɛ kpiri n nu ka, ye ya nya baari am dɔ Tɛsalonika a. Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ama ya ahoresi na ya koro akan i labari kpa n akere am, haari ni bo minɛ dɔŋgu asoro má ya su mmɔɔ ni.

eng: Our appeal isnt based on false information, the wrong motives, or deception.
local: Nɔaniɛ n bo ya kan kereeri am ni, i ti má ka ya mini atin wɔ, wara i ti má ka ya la sunsunni tiɛ be wɔ, i sa su ti má ka ya koro ka ya dadaka be wɔ.

eng: So when we couldnt stand it any longer, we thought it was a good idea to stay on in Athens by ourselves.
local: Kabo ya ni ambɛrɛ akpaaki acɛ n dɛɛ ti, ya wara ado am agaya, nyini ti, ya ŋa yɛrɛ ka Atensi miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: Rather, we have been examined and approved by God to be trusted with the good news, and thats exactly how we speak. We arent trying to please people, but we are trying to please God, who continues to examine our hearts.
local: Ama, saŋga kɛrɛ ya jɔjɔɔri kabo Nyɛmɛ koro n wɔ, dama yiri jina ya sin a, ye wɔ fa labari kpa n ama ya ka ya kan i. Ya yo má ka i sun adamande nu wɔ, ya yo ka i sun Nyɛmɛ nu a, dama yiri fiiti si deke bo i wo ya ahore su a.

eng: As you know, we never used flattery, and God is our witness that we didnt have greedy motives.
local: Am si tenle su ka ya ba abo má am anɔɔfiɛ wɔ, na ya ba akan má labari kpa n ka ya nyunu aboro neŋge kpini su wɔ. Nyɛmɛ wo ya daani.

eng: and we sent you Timothy, who is our brother and Gods coworker in the good news about Christ. We sent him to strengthen and encourage you in your faithfulness.
local: Na ya sunma ya niwa Timoti ka i ba niɛ am busu. Ya ni yiri saŋga yo juma fa kɔ Kristo labari kpa n kan su, ye ya sunmaari i ka i ma am nya ahoresi, na i ma am jina kenken fa kɔ am yarada n su.

eng: We didnt want any of you to be shaken by these problems. You know very well that we were meant to go through this.
local: Adena má am be ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki i fa kɔ ahin wuwuruwam bo bu sukere am n ti. Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si ka, nyini sam bo bu tú yɛrɛ yarada­yo­fɔm ni, Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa lɛɛ.

eng: We didnt ask for special treatment from people—not from you or from others.
local: ya yo akoro má kansi am dɔ, wara be dɔ.

eng: although we could have thrown our weight around as Christs apostles. Instead, we were gentle with you like a nursing mother caring for her own children.
local: Má kabo ya ti Kristo jaramasam ni, má ya koro se am ka am yo deke kɛrɛ ma ya. Ama, saŋga bo ya woori am dɔ ni, ya woori bɛnlɛ wɔ, kabo bara baa­wu­fɔ niɛ i nbaam dika n wɔ.

eng: In fact, when we were with you, we kept on predicting that we were going to face problems exactly like what happened, as you know.
local: Dama saŋga bo ya toko wo am dɔ ni, ya yo seeri am ka bu bá kere ya wuwuruwa. Kabo am si ni, nyini lɛɛ ye wɔ tu ya n a.

eng: We were glad to share not only Gods good news with you but also our very lives because we cared for you so much.
local: Koro bo ya fa koro am n dɛɛ ti, ye ya kaanri Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n maari am a, na ya gusu akun ya ŋu ama am, dama ya koroori am agaya n dɛɛ ti.

eng: You remember, brothers and sisters, our efforts and hard work. We preached Gods good news to you, while we worked night and day so we wouldnt be a burden on any of you.
local: Nahɔrɛ, ya ninbaam, am teŋge su kabo ya boroori ya nyi yoori juma nahɔrɛ su, ya yoori juma wiɛsu, ni kɔŋguɛ, adena má ya fa ya fie fie be, saŋga bo ya kaanri Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n maari am ni.

eng: Thats why I sent Timothy to find out about your faithfulness when I couldnt stand it anymore. I was worried that the tempter might have tempted you so that our work would have been a waste of time.
local: Nyini ti, ma koro amindi má, ye m sunmaari Timoti na i ba niɛ ka am toko bita am yarada n kenken n a. Beni, kisa Sitana akoro anya atin adadaka am, wɔ ma ya juma n bo ya yo i am dɔ n kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Now Timothy has returned to us from you and has given us good news about your faithfulness and love! He says that you always have good memories about us and that you want to see us as much as we want to see you.
local: Ye kisa, Timoti akpie i nyi afa labari aba ama ya fa kɔ kabo am yarada, ni am koro n ti barasu. Wɔ se ya ka am la sunsunni kpa fa ma ya saŋga kɛrɛ, na am koro ka am ŋu ya kabo ya gusu koro ka ya ŋu am ni.

eng: You and God are witnesses of how holy, just, and blameless we were toward you believers.
local: Ambɛrɛ yarada­yo­fɔm ni, am ni Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ kɛrɛ wo ya daani, kabo ya wo tiiri, saŋga bo ya woori am dɔ ni. Ya woori am dɔ ni akunnu fofoe wɔ, na ya sa kɛrɛ akɔ atin n su, ya yo má sa be bo i fa jaraki ba.

eng: Because of this, brothers and sisters, we were encouraged in all our distress and trouble through your faithfulness.
local: Nyini ti, ya ninbaam, haari ni bo ya wo kekereke nu na ya sudi wahara n kɛrɛ ni, am yarada n ama ya ahoresi.

eng: For now we are alive if you are standing your ground in the Lord.
local: Na nzɛn am jinaari kun su, fa kɔ Mibiɛ n dɛɛ atin n su, ya koro da na ya daafi.

eng: Likewise, you know how we treated each of you like a father treats his own children.
local: Am si ka saŋga bo ya woori ni ambɛrɛ ni, ya bitaari am kabo sɔnɔ bita i nbaam n wɔ.

eng: How can we thank God enough for you, given all the joy we have because of you before our God?.
local: Kisa ya si má kabo ya da Nyɛmɛ asi fa ma am mmɔɔ, fa kɔ ahorejɔ bambaka n bo wɔ fa ama ya, am dɛɛ ti.

eng: Night and day, we pray more than ever to see all of you in person and to complete whatever you still need for your faith.
local: Kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu kɛrɛ ya sereeri Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su wɔ, ka, i ma ya ba ŋu ya ŋu, na ya kere am dekem bo i kpiɛ am su fa kɔ am yarada n su.

eng: We appealed to you, encouraged you, and pleaded with you to live lives worthy of the God who is calling you into his own kingdom and glory.
local: Ya maari am ahoresi, na ya dada am ahore, na ya yɛ am nyi ka am wo kabo i ti ye ni ka Nyɛmɛ minɛm wo barasu n a. Yiri fereeri am a, ka, am ba yo i dɛɛ minɛm bo i níɛ am dika, na am bá nya i dɛɛ jirima ni.

eng: We also thank God constantly for this: when you accepted Gods word that you heard from us, you welcomed it for what it truly is. Instead of accepting it as a human message, you accepted it as Gods message, and it continues to work in you who are believers.
local: Na ya gusu da Nyɛmɛ asi saŋga kɛrɛ, dama saŋga bo am deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo ya kan kereeri am ni, am ade má i ka i fite adamande dɔ ɔ, ama, am ade i ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ wɔ, na i ti sɔ ɔ nahɔrɛ su, ye i wo ambɛrɛ yarada­yo­fɔm ahore su yo juma a.

eng: Now may our God and Father himself and our Lord Jesus guide us on our way back to you.
local: Ya sere ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu ka bu teke atin ma ya, na ya koro ba am dɔ.

eng: May the Lord cause you to increase and enrich your love for each other and for everyone in the same way as we also love you.
local: Na ya sere Mibiɛ ni, ka, i ma am la koro buka su, na am fa koro am beŋgu ni minɛm kɛrɛ kɔ sin su, kabo ya gusu koro am ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, you became imitators of the churches of God in Judea, which are in Christ Jesus. This was because you also suffered the same things from your own people as they did from the Jews.
local: Ya ninbaam, sam bo bu yo tuuri Kristo jamam bo bu wo Yudiya asiɛn n su ni, nyini sam ye ba tu ambɛrɛ gusu n a. Kabo am tiɛŋgu minɛm akere am wahara barasu ni, sɔ ye Yuda­fɔm yo fa kereeri Kristo jamam n a.

eng: May the love cause your hearts to be strengthened, to be blameless in holiness before our God and Father when our Lord Jesus comes with all his people. Amen.
local: Na i ma am nya ahoresi, na am jina kenken, na am ba yo minɛ casim bo be koro jaraki má am, ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ, saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá baari ni i tiɛŋgu minɛm kɛrɛ ni.

eng: They killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets and drove us out. They dont please God, and they are hostile to the entire human race.
local: Bɛrɛ kuunri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ye bu kaaki kuunri ya Mibiɛ Yesu, na ba kere ya wuwuruwa, na ba yo sa bo i sun má Nyɛmɛ nu, na ba yo adamande kɛrɛ kpɔ­fɔm.

eng: when they try to stop us from speaking to the Gentiles so they can be saved. Their sins are constantly pushing the limit. Gods wrath has caught up with them in the end.
local: Na ba koro ka bu kanda ya ka má ya kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, adena má bu nya tii­de. Na nyini satiɛ n aba abuka bu satiɛ n kɛrɛ su akɔ asin su. Ama, kisa, Nyɛmɛ afa yaa bu ŋu, i sudi bu jɔrɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we were separated from you for a while physically but not in our hearts. We made every effort in our desire to see you again face-to-face.
local: Ya ninbaam, saŋga bo ya kpaaki yakiiri am kaan sɔ ni, ya sunsunni yiri woori am dɔ ɔ, na ya wara ado am agaya, na ya boro ya nyi ka ya ba niɛ am busu.

eng: We wanted to come to you—I, Paul, tried over and over again—and Satan stopped us.
local: Má ya koroori ka ya kpie ya nyi ba am dɔ. Haari na mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii akoro ka m kpie m nyi ba am dɔ, kpe nyɛnyɛ kɛrɛ, ama, Sitana akanda ya.

eng: What is our hope, joy, or crown that we can brag about in front of our Lord Jesus when he comes? Isnt it all of you?.
local: Ya koro ka ya kpie ya nyi ba am dɔ, dama saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá kpie i nyi ba ni, ya lá ahoresi ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, na ya lá ahorejɔ, na ya káaki koro fɔ ka ya nya teremasu i dɔ.

eng: You are our glory and joy!.
local: Nahɔrɛ, ambɛrɛ ti, ye ya la ahorejɔ koro bo ya hoe n a.

eng: From Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy: To the church of the Thessalonians, which is in God our Father, and in the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu, ni Silivanusi, ni Timoti sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ Kristo jama n bo am wo Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu na am ti ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ minɛm n a.

eng: Grace and peace to all of you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na bu ma am ahoreda.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we must always thank God for you. This is only right because your faithfulness is growing by leaps and bounds, and the love that all of you have for each other is increasing.
local: Ya ninbaam, wɔ yo daka ka ya da Nyɛmɛ asi, ambɛrɛ ti, saŋga kɛrɛ. Atin wo nu ka ya yo sɔ, dama am yarada n buka su wɔ nahɔrɛ su, na koro bo am koro am beŋgu n gusu subuka su sɔ ɔ.

eng: Thats why we ourselves are bragging about you in Gods churches. We tell about your endurance and faithfulness in all the harassments and trouble that you have put up with.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ya fa am fɔ sɔ ɔ, deke bo Kristo jamam wo kɛrɛ. Ya fɔ fa kɔ kabo am ayo yarada na am la mindi fa kɔ wuwuruwa n kɛrɛ bo minɛm sukere am, ni wahara n kɛrɛ bo am wo i nu ni.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, we ask and encourage you in the Lord Jesus to keep living the way you already are and even do better in how you live and please God—just as you learned from us.
local: Ya ninbaam, ya yo kereeri am kabo i ti ye ni ka am wo barasu na i yo Nyɛmɛ fiɛ. Ya si ka am wo nyini wo n wɔ nahɔrɛ su. Ama, ya sere am jande wɔ, ya Mibiɛ Yesu duma n nu, ka, am wo wo bo i kɔ tara sɔ.

eng: We dont need to write to you about the timing and dates, brothers and sisters.
local: Ya ninbaam, fa kɔ saŋga n bo Mibiɛ ni bá ba ni, ya la má jɔrɛ be bo i ti ye ni ka ya kɛrɛ ma am fa kɔ nyini saŋga n su kun.

eng: This shows that Gods judgment is right, and that you will be considered worthy of Gods kingdom for which you are suffering.
local: Nyini kere ka Nyɛmɛ di minɛm jɔrɛ ni i nahɔrɛ atin su a, na i se ka ambɛrɛ ti ye ni ka am wura i fɛmɛya n nu a, wahara n bo am adi i n dɛɛ ti.

eng: You know the instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
local: Dama am si nɔaniɛ n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu fa maari ya na ya gusu fa maari am ni.

eng: You know very well that the day of the Lord is going to come like a thief in the night.
local: Dama ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni am tii si tenle su ka cɛɛn n bo Mibiɛ ni bá kaaki ba ni, i yó kabo awiefɔ ba kɔŋguɛ ba wɔ n wɔ.

eng: After all, its right for God to pay back the ones making trouble for you with trouble.
local: Nahɔrɛ, Nyɛmɛ yo sa bo i kɔ atin su wɔ, i má minɛm bo bu sukere am wahara n gusu di wahara.

eng: Gods will is that your lives are dedicated to him. This means that you stay away from sexual immorality.
local: Nyɛmɛ koro ka am wo wo casi wɔ, na má be yo sakaraya.

eng: When they are saying, There is peace and security, at that time sudden destruction will attack them, like labor pains start with a pregnant woman, and they definitely wont escape.
local: Saŋga bo minɛm ba suse ka tana­laifiɛ ni ahoreda wo bɛrɛ ni, nyini saŋga n nu ye kekereke kába ba tu bu a, kabo bara ba kutu ka i wu baa na i ti barasu ni. Nyini kekereke ni, sɔnɔ be kóro fite má nu.

eng: and to pay back you who are having trouble with relief along with us. This payback will come when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his powerful angels.
local: na i má ambɛrɛ bo am sudi wahara n ni yɛrɛ kɛrɛ nya ya ŋu. Nyini sam kɛrɛ bá yo sɔ saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá fite aŋgoro ba ni i mɛrɛkɛm, ni bu yiko bambaka ni.

eng: and learn how to control your own body in a pure and respectable way.
local: I ti ye ni ka biɛsɔ kɛrɛ ni i yi tara bu ŋu, na bu wo wo bo i ti casi na i la kansi.

eng: But you arent in darkness, brothers and sisters, so the day wont catch you by surprise like a thief.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ, ya ninbaam ni, am wo má awosin nu bo Mibiɛ n ba cɛɛn ni bá fura am nu, kabo awiefɔ ba fura sɔnɔ nu na i wɔ n wɔ.

eng: He will give justice with blazing fire to those who dont recognize God and dont obey the good news of our Lord Jesus.
local: ni sin, na i bá cin minɛm bo ba kete Nyɛmɛ n sui, ni bo ba de má ya Mibiɛ Yesu labari kpa ni.

eng: Dont be controlled by your sexual urges like the Gentiles who dont know God.
local: Má am ma am konviki wo sakaraya sereya nu kabo minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n yo ni.

eng: All of you are children of light and children of the day. We dont belong to night or darkness.
local: Ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ ti minɛm bo am wo wein n nu wɔ, kabo wiɛsu ti ni. Ya wo má kɔŋguɛ nu, wara awosin nu.

eng: They will pay the penalty of eternal destruction away from the Lords presence and away from his mighty glory.
local: Nyɛmɛ cín bu sui haari bu duma bá bo má, na bu ŋú má Mibiɛ ni, ni i yiko bambaka ni.

eng: No one should mistreat or take advantage of their brother or sister in this issue. The Lord punishes people for all these things, as we told you before and sternly warned you.
local: Kabo i nya ti sɔ ni, má Kristo susu­fɔ be se ka i bu i niwa Kristo susu­fɔ ŋgbɛɛn fa kɔ nyini sam su. Ya yo seeri am wɔ ni nahɔrɛ kekereke, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori sɔ, Mibiɛ ni cín i mi sui.

eng: So then, lets not sleep like the others, but lets stay awake and stay sober.
local: Nyini ti, má am ma ya daafi kabo nyiŋgam daafi ni, am ma ya nyunu wo su na ya tara ya ŋu.

eng: This will happen when he comes on that day to receive honor from his holy people and to be admired by everyone who has believed—and our testimony to you was believed.
local: Nyini cɛɛn n bo i ba baari n ye i minɛm kɛrɛ wúra i kpiri a, na nyini minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu yo i yarada n má i jirima. Ambɛrɛ mmɔɔ wo nyini minɛm n nu, dama am ayo labari n bo ya fa maari am n yarada.

eng: God didnt call us to be immoral but to be dedicated to him.
local: Nyɛmɛ fereeri ya ka ya wo wo bo i ti casi wɔ, i koro má ka ya fa ya ŋu wura sakaraya sereya n nu.

eng: Therefore, whoever rejects these instructions isnt rejecting a human authority. They are rejecting God, who gives his Holy Spirit to you.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri ahin keree­wa ni, i ti má ka i sukete adamande wɔ, ama, i sukete Nyɛmɛ wɔ, yiri ma am i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n a.

eng: People who sleep sleep at night, and people who get drunk get drunk at night.
local: Kɔŋguɛ ye minɛm daafi a, kɔŋguɛ gusu ye minɛm nu nzan bo a.

eng: We are constantly praying for you for this: that our God will make you worthy of his calling and accomplish every good desire and faithful work by his power.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye ya sere Nyɛmɛ ma am saŋga kɛrɛ n a. Ya sere i ka i ma am wo wo bo i ti ye ni ka i dɛɛ minɛm bo wɔ fere bu n wo ni. Na ya kaaki sere i ka i fa i yiko n buka am, na am yo am kunnu koroo­wa, ni juma kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa kɔ wie.

eng: You dont need us to write about loving your brothers and sisters because God has already taught you to love each other.
local: Tɔ, fa kɔ kabo am koro am ninbaam ni, ya la má jɔrɛ be bo i ti ye ni ka ya kɛrɛ ma am, dama Nyɛmɛ akere ambɛrɛ ni am tii kabo am koro am beŋgu barasu.

eng: Since we belong to the day, lets stay sober, wearing faithfulness and love as a piece of armor that protects our body and the hope of salvation as a helmet.
local: Ama, yɛrɛ wo wein nu wɔ, nyini ti, am ma ya tara ya ŋu, na ya fa yarada ni koro fa kanda ya ŋu ka bulaari pɛpɛrɛpɛ bo ya fa kanda ya hoe su, na ya fa ahoresi ka ya nya tii­de yo ka bulaari kere bo ya fa butu kanda ya tii.

eng: Then the name of our Lord Jesus will be honored by you, and you will be honored by him, consistent with the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Nzɛn am nya yoori sɔ, nyini má minɛm ma ya Mibiɛ Yesu duma n jirima a, am dɛɛ ti, na i gusu má am nya jirima. Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n má i yo sɔ a.

eng: In fact, you are doing loving deeds for all the brothers and sisters throughout Macedonia. Now we encourage you, brothers and sisters, to do so even more.
local: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka, am koro ya ninbaam kɛrɛ bo bu wo Makɛdoniya awɔrɛ n nu ni. Haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ya susere am wɔ, am koro bu koro bo i kɔ tara sɔ.

eng: God didnt intend for us to suffer his wrath but rather to possess salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ ayi má ya ka i fá yaa na i cin ya sui wɔ, ama, i yiiri ya ka ya nyá tii­de, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti wɔ.

eng: Aim to live quietly, mind your own business, and earn your own living, just as I told you.
local: Am yo aniɛ na am wo bɛnlɛ, na am niɛ am tiɛŋgu juma bo am yo i, na am fiɛn aŋuye na am di, kabo ya du mɔ seeri am ni.

eng: That way youll behave appropriately toward outsiders, and you wont be in need.
local: Nzɛn am yoori sɔ, nyini má minɛm bo bu nya yo má Yesu yarada n bu am a, na am niɛ má be anyumbaa fa kɔ am sa koroo­wa su.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we want you to know about people who have died so that you wont mourn like others who dont have any hope.
local: Tɔ, ya ninbaam, ya koro ka am si deke bo i ti nahɔrɛ fa kɔ minɛm bo ba wu n dɔ, adena má am ahore saaki ka minɛm bo bu la má tama be ni.

eng: Jesus died for us so that, whether we are awake or asleep, we will live together with him.
local: I wuuri yɛrɛ dɛɛ ti wɔ, na saŋga bo i bá ba ni, nzɛn ya la ŋgɔɔ o, wara nzɛn ya wu wɔ o, ya ni yiri sáŋga wo a.

eng: So continue encouraging each other and building each other up, just like you are doing already.
local: Nyini ti, am ma am beŋgu ahoresi, na am buka am beŋgu na am kɔ mɔ ni am nyɛmɛsu ni, kabo kisa na am suyo ni.

eng: Since we believe that Jesus died and rose, so we also believe that God will bring with him those who have died in Jesus.
local: Ya yo yarada ka Yesu wuuri wɔ na wɔ teŋge bekun, nyini ti, ya kaaki la yarada ka Nyɛmɛ téŋge minɛm bo bu yoori Yesu yarada na ba wu ni, na i má bu su Yesu su ba.

eng: What we are saying is a message from the Lord: we who are alive and still around at the Lords coming definitely wont go ahead of those who have died.
local: Keree­wa bo ya sufa kere am kisa n fite Mibiɛ n dɔ ɔ, ka, yɛrɛ bo ya toko la ŋgɔɔ ni, cɛɛn n bo Mibiɛ n ba baari ni, yɛrɛ, ya dú má bɛrɛ n bo ba wu n mɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we ask you to respect those who are working with you, leading you, and instructing you.
local: Ya ninbaam, ya sere am wɔ, ka, am bu mɔ­fɔm ni, bɛrɛ bo bu yo Nyɛmɛ juma ni anyum­boro ma am ni, na bu niɛ am su, na bu kere am Nyɛmɛ atin ni.

eng: This is because the Lord himself will come down from heaven with the signal of a shout by the head angel and a blast on Gods trumpet. First, those who are dead in Christ will rise.
local: Dama Mibiɛ n ni i tii bá fite aŋgoro n nu jura na i má nɔɔ, na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ kpiɛn n tíɛn, na bu kán Nyɛmɛ awiɛ ni. Na minɛm bo bu yoori Yesu yarada na ba wu n dú mɔ teŋge bu yiwee n nu.

eng: Think of them highly with love because of their work. Live in peace with each other.
local: Am ma bu jirima nahɔrɛ su, ni koro, fa kɔ bu juma n bo bu yo i n dɛɛ ti. Am ma tana­laifiɛ wo am ni am beŋgum dɔ.

eng: Then, we who are living and still around will be taken up together with them in the clouds to meet with the Lord in the air. That way we will always be with the Lord.
local: Nyini saŋga, na Nyɛmɛ fá bɛrɛ, ni yɛrɛ bo ya toko la ŋgɔɔ ni, na ya kpá Mibiɛ n atin aŋgoro yinyiŋgu n nu. Na ya kɔ́ wo Mibiɛ n dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we urge you to warn those who are disorderly. Comfort the discouraged. Help the weak. Be patient with everyone.
local: Ya ninbaam, ya kaaki sere am wɔ, ka, am bɔbɔ nviɛya­fɔm ni, na am ma sɛrɛ­fɔm n ahoresi, na am buka daburuka­fɔm ni, na am fa sawɔrɛ ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

eng: So encourage each other with these words.
local: Nyini ti, am fa ahin nɔaniɛm fa si am beŋgum ahore.

eng: Make sure no one repays a wrong with a wrong, but always pursue the good for each other and everyone else.
local: Má be se ka i fa i beŋgu satiɛ bo i yo i fa tɔ i mi kaari, ama, saŋga kɛrɛ am ma am sunsunni la ka, am yo sakpa ma am beŋgum ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

eng: Rejoice always.
local: Am ma am ahore jɔ, saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Pray continually.
local: na má am se ka am yaki am nyɛmɛsere.

eng: Give thanks in every situation because this is Gods will for you in Christ Jesus.
local: na am da Nyɛmɛ asi, haari fa kɔ sa kɛrɛ bo i tu am. Kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka am yo lɛɛ, dama am ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun n ti.

eng: Dont suppress the Spirit.
local: Má am kanda Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni, am ma i yo i juma.

eng: Dont brush off Spirit-inspired messages.
local: Nɔaniɛ bo minɛm deeri i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ kan kereeri am ni, má am bu i ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: but examine everything carefully and hang on to what is good.
local: ama, am fiiti i kɛrɛ nu na am niɛ, na am de bo i ti kpa ni, na am su i su.

eng: Avoid every kind of evil.
local: na am kete sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ pepere.

eng: Now, may the God of peace himself cause you to be completely dedicated to him; and may your spirit, soul, and body be kept intact and blameless at our Lord Jesus Christs coming.
local: Ya sere Nyɛmɛ, yiri n bo i ma ahoreda n ka, i ŋmisi am ahore­mbaa, ni am ŋuniɛ, ni am sunsunni kɛrɛ casi, adena am wo wo bo fin be mata má am, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo bá kpie i nyi ba ni.

eng: The one who is calling you is faithful and will do this.
local: Yiri bo wɔ fere am ni, yiri la dabuŋu, i yó nyini sam n kɛrɛ.

eng: Brothers and sisters, pray for us.
local: Ya ninbaam, am sere Nyɛmɛ ma ya gusu.

eng: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
local: na am bisa ya ninbaam kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ni koro.

eng: By the Lords authority, I order all of you to have this letter read aloud to all the brothers and sisters.
local: M susere am wɔ, am niɛ Mibiɛ n ti, na am kan ahin kadasi n kere ya ninbaa yarada­yo­fɔm n kɛrɛ na bu ti.

eng: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
local: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have a request for you concerning our Lord Jesus Christs coming and when we are gathered together to be with him.
local: Tɔ, ya ninbaam, m sujɔjɔ am dɔ fa kɔ ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ba n su wɔ, ni kabo ya tíɛn ya nɔɔ na ya ni yiri kɔ́ yɛ n wɔ. Ya sere am wɔ.

eng: We dont want you to be easily confused in your mind or upset if you hear that the day of the Lord is already here, whether you hear it through some spirit, a message, or a letter supposedly from us.
local: ka, má am kaaki am sunsunni wara am birisi, dama nbem ŋa Mibiɛ n adu mɔ aba asin n dɛɛ ti. Haari nzɛn be ŋa wɔ de nɔaniɛ Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ, wara nzɛn be jɔjɔɔri nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ, wara nzɛn be seeri ka yɛrɛ akɛrɛ i kadasi n nu ase sɔ a, má am de i.

eng: Dont let anyone deceive you in any way. That day wont come unless the rebellion comes first and the person who is lawless is revealed, who is headed for destruction.
local: Má am ma be dadaka am fa kɔ deke be su fieo, dama nyini cɛɛn ni bá má, má ka saŋga n bo minɛm dɔŋgu bá kete ka bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni, na sɔnɔtiɛ n bo i bu má ka pui wo bɛrɛ n nyá yi i ŋu fite fofoe nu, yiri n bo Nyɛmɛ fá i ji cɛɛma sin n nu ni.

eng: He is the opponent of every so-called god or object of worship and promotes himself over them. So he sits in Gods temple, displaying himself to show that he is God.
local: I kéte deke kɛrɛ bo bu fere i ka Nyɛmɛ, ni deke kɛrɛ bo adamande kutu su i, na i kére i ŋu ka i tara bɛrɛ kɛrɛ pepere. Haari na i kɔ́ wura tana Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, na i sé ka yiri ti Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: You remember that I used to tell you these things while I was with you, dont you?.
local: Am teŋge má su ka saŋga bo m woori am dɔ ni, m yo seeri am ahin jɔrɛm kɛrɛ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: Now you know what holds him back so that he can be revealed when his time comes.
local: Kisa, am si deke bo i kanda i na wɔ nya ba má ni, má ka saŋga n aju bo i yí i ŋu fite fofoe nu.

eng: The hidden plan to live without any law is at work now, but it will be secret only until the one who is holding it back is out of the way.
local: Ama, sɔnɔtiɛ n bo i bu má ka pui wo bɛrɛ ni, yiri suyo i asiri nu juma wɔ kisa, má ka yiri n bo i kanda i ni, aba acin ayaki atin ni.

eng: Then the person who is lawless will be revealed. The Lord Jesus will destroy him with the breath from his mouth. When the Lord comes, his appearance will put an end to him.
local: Dɔ na sɔnɔtiɛ n nyá yi i ŋu fite fofoe nu, ama, saŋga n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu bá ni, na i fíta kun i, na i baawa n dɛɛ wein n gusu yó i ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: When the person who is lawless comes, it will happen through Satans effort, with all kinds of fake power, signs, and wonders.
local: Tɔ, kabo sɔnɔtiɛ ni bá ba n yaa. I bá ni Sitana dɛɛ yiko n na i bá yo alekutura sam beberebe fa dadaka minɛm ka nyini sam fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: It will happen with every sort of wicked deception of those who are heading toward destruction because they have refused to love the truth that would allow them to be saved.
local: na i bá bu kɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn barasu barasu dadaka minɛm bo bu ŋgɔɔ ka ŋgbɛɛn ni. Bu ŋgɔɔ ka ŋgbɛɛn, dama ba koro má ka bu su atin n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n su na bu nya tii­de.

eng: This is why God will send them an influence that will mislead them so that they will believe the lie.
local: Nyini ti, na Nyɛmɛ tá bu nyi, adena bu yó deke kɛrɛ bo i ti kɔrɛ n yarada.

eng: The result will be that everyone will be judged who is not convinced by the truth but is happy with injustice.
local: Nyini minɛm sɔ, bo ba yo má nahɔrɛ n yarada ni, ama, na ba fa satiɛ­yo acici bu tii ni, Nyɛmɛ dí bu jɔrɛ na i cín bu sui.

eng: But we always must thank God for you, brothers and sisters who are loved by God. This is because he chose you from the beginning to be the first crop of the harvest. This brought salvation, through your dedication to God by the Spirit and through your belief in the truth.
local: Ya ninbaam, ambɛrɛ bo Mibiɛ n koro am ni, wɔ yo daka ka ya da Nyɛmɛ asi ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti saŋga kɛrɛ, dama fite bumboori n ye Nyɛmɛ yiiri am ka am nya tii­de a. Deke bo i ma am nya tii­de n la ka, Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ma am yo Nyɛmɛ minɛ casim a, na am bita nahɔrɛ atin, na am yo yarada.

eng: God called all of you through our good news so you could possess the honor of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Labari kpa n bo ya kan kereeri am na am deeri n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ fereeri am ka am nya tii­de a, na am nyá ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n be.

eng: So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm and hold on to the traditions we taught you, whether we taught you in person or through our letter.
local: Nyini ti, ya ninbaam, am bita nahɔrɛ atin n kenken, bo ya kaanri i na ya kɛrɛɛri i maari am ni, má am kaaki am sin bo i.

eng: Our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father loved us and through grace gave us eternal comfort and a good hope.
local: Ya sere ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ni i tii, ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i koro ya, na i dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n nu, na wɔ ma ya ahoresi saŋga kɛrɛ, ni tama kpiri ni.

eng: May he encourage your hearts and give you strength in every good thing you do or say.
local: ya sere bu ka bu ma am ahoresi buka su, na bu ma am faŋga, na am yo sakpa, na am jɔjɔ jɔrɛ bo i kɔ atin su saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Finally, brothers and sisters, pray for us so that the Lords message will spread quickly and be honored, just like it happened with you.
local: Ya ninbaam, jɔrɛ bo wɔ ka n la ka, am sere Nyɛmɛ ma ya, na Mibiɛ n labari kpa n sandi bara yɛ ndende na bu de i ni jirima, kabo am deeri i ni.

eng: Pray too that we will be rescued from inappropriate and evil people since everyone that we meet wont respond with faith.
local: Am kaaki sere Nyɛmɛ, na i yí ya fite minɛ tiɛm saa nu, dama má sɔnɔ kɛrɛ yo labari kpa n yarada a.

eng: But the Lord is faithful and will give you strength and protect you from the evil one.
local: Ama, Mibiɛ n la dabuŋu, yiri má am faŋga, na i yó am kanda bo Sitana koro yo má am pui.

eng: We are confident about you in the Lord—that you are doing and will keep doing what we tell you to do.
local: Ya Mibiɛ n ama ya ahoresi ka am suyo deke n kɛrɛ bo ya ma am nɔɔ ka am yo i ni, am kɔ su suyo i saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: May the Lord lead your hearts to express Gods love and Christs endurance.
local: Ya sere Mibiɛ n ka i ma am si kabo Nyɛmɛ koro am barasu, ni kabo Kristo mindiiri barasu ni.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we command you in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to stay away from every brother or sister who lives an undisciplined life that is not in line with the traditions that you received from us.
local: Ya ninbaam, ya maari am nɔɔ ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma n nu ka, má am fa am ŋu gɔ ya ninbaam bo bu koro má ka bu yo juma, na bu ti nviɛya­fɔm n ŋu, ba kete ka bu su má nɔaniɛ n bo ya fa maari am n su lɛɛ.

eng: You yourselves know how you need to imitate us because we were not undisciplined when we were with you.
local: Ambɛrɛ ni am tii si tenle su ka, i ti ye ni ka am yo kabo ya yo ni. Am si ka saŋga bo ya woori am dɔ ni, ya yo ti má nviɛya­fɔm.

eng: We didnt eat anyones food without paying for it. Instead, we worked night and day with effort and hard work so that we would not impose on you.
local: Ya di má sɔnɔ be diire ŋgbɛɛn. Ama, ya yoori juma wɔ nahɔrɛ su, wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ, adena má ya bo be kaari.

eng: We did this to give you an example to imitate, not because we didnt have a right to insist on financial support.
local: Ya yoori sɔ ni, i ti má ka ya la má atin bo ya se ka am buka ya a, ama, ya yoori sɔ, adena am ŋu i na am gusu sɔn ya su wɔ.

eng: Even when we were with you we were giving you this command: If anyone doesnt want to work, they shouldnt eat.
local: Saŋga bo ya woori am dɔ ni, ya maari am ahin nɔɔ ni, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kete ka i yo má juma, má am ma i na i di.

eng: We hear that some of you are living an undisciplined life. They arent working, but they are meddling in other peoples business.
local: Má pui ti ye ya se sɔ a, dama ya ti labari ka am nbem wo bɛrɛ bo bu ti nviɛya­fɔm wɔ, bu yo má juma be, se bu nati ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, na bu sufa bu nɔɔ bɔ minɛm jɔrɛ nu.

eng: By the Lord Jesus Christ, we command and encourage such people to work quietly and put their own food on the table.
local: Nyini ti, ye ya sutu bu fɔ ni anyum­boro su ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ duma n nu n a, ka, bu yo aniɛ tara bu ŋu, na bu yo juma na bu koro bita bu ŋu.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont get discouraged in doing what is right.
local: Ama, ya ninbaam, ambɛrɛ yiri, má am ma am ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki am ni sakpa­yo.

eng: Take note of anyone who doesnt obey what we have said in this letter. Dont associate with them so they will be ashamed of themselves.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri nɔaniɛ n bo ya kɛrɛ i ahin kadasi n nu afa ama am ni, am yi kɔrɛsi na am si i mi. Má am dodo i mi, adena nyinsɛ ti i.

eng: Dont treat them like enemies, but warn them like you would do for a brother or sister.
local: Ama, má am fa i mi yo am kpɔ­fɔ, am tu i fɔ ka am niwa a.

eng: May the Lord of peace himself give you peace always in every way. The Lord be with all of you.
local: Ya sere Mibiɛ n ni i tii, yiri bo i ma ahoreda ni, ka, i ma am ahoreda saŋga kɛrɛ fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ nu, na i wo am kɛrɛ dɔ.

eng: I, Paul, am writing this greeting with my own hand. This verifies that the letter is from me, as in every letter of mine. This is how I write.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii sufa m saa sukɛrɛ ahin kɛrɛɛ­wa n bisa am dɔŋgu a. Kabo saŋga kɛrɛ na m kɛrɛ lɛɛ.

eng: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
local: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am kɛrɛ.

eng: From Paul, who is an apostle of Jesus Christ by the command of God our savior and of Christ Jesus our hope.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n a. M ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa a, Nyɛmɛ bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, yiri ama m nɔɔ ka m yo nyini juma n a, i ni Yesu Kristo bo i ma ya tama ni.

eng: To Timothy, my true child in the faith. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: M sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma wɔrɔ Timoti wɔ, a ti ka m wa a nahɔrɛ su, dama mini ni wɔrɔ yo yarada kun wɔ. Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka u, na bu si u aŋunfɔ, na bu ma u ahoreda.

eng: When I left for Macedonia, I asked you to stay behind in Ephesus so that you could instruct certain individuals not to spread wrong teaching.
local: M koro ka a wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu, kabo saŋga bo m sukɔ Makɛdoniya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu na m seeri u ni. Minɛ nbem wo dɔ bo bu keree­wa kɔ má atin su, i ti ye ni ka a ma bu nɔɔ ka bu yaki nyini keree­wa ni.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to myths and endless genealogies. Their teaching only causes useless guessing games instead of faithfulness to Gods way of doing things.
local: Se bu na bu yaki kɔrɛ jɔrɛm, ni nana dumam beberebe ni. Nyini jɔrɛm fa akuruwa ba a. Bu koro buka má sɔnɔ na i yo deke n bo Nyɛmɛ koroori ka i yo i. Be koro yo má kabo Nyɛmɛ koro, má ka i mi ayo i yarada.

eng: The goal of instruction is love from a pure heart, a good conscience, and a sincere faith.
local: Deke n bo i ti na m maari u ahin nɔaniɛ n la ka, i fa koro ba a, nyini koro n fite ahore­mbaa fofoe wɔ, ni sunsunni kpa, ni yarada nahɔrɛ su.

eng: Because they missed this goal, some people have been distracted by talk that doesnt mean anything.
local: Nyini minɛ n nbem amini ahin keree­wam ni, na bu subo anzama ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: They want to be teachers of Law without understanding either what they are saying or what they are talking about with such confidence.
local: Bu koro ka bu yo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, bu kan i ni ahoresi wɔ, ama, bɛrɛ ni bu tii si má bu tiɛŋgu nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu.

eng: Now we know that the Law is good if used appropriately.
local: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni ti kpa a, nzɛn ya bitaari i kabo i ti ye ni ka ya bita i ni.

eng: We understand this: the Law isnt established for a righteous person but for people who live without laws and without obeying any authority. They are the ungodly and the sinners. They are people who are not spiritual, and nothing is sacred to them. They kill their fathers and mothers, and murder others.
local: Ya kaaki si nu ka, ba fa má nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ase má sakpa­yo­fɔm n ti wɔ, ama, ba fa ase ama minɛm bo bu ma má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n jirima n wɔ, ni bo bu kete i ni, ni bo bu soro má Nyɛmɛ ni, ni bo bu nati yo satiɛ ni, ni bo bu woo­wa ti má casi ni, ni bo bu bu Nyɛmɛ ŋgbɛɛn ni, ni bo bu nati kun minɛ ni, ni bo bu kun bu nim ni bu sim mmɔɔ ni.

eng: They are people who are sexually unfaithful, and people who have intercourse with the same sex. They are kidnappers, liars, individuals who give false testimonies in court, and those who do anything else that is opposed to sound teaching.
local: Fa kɔ bo bu nati yo sakaraya ni, ni bo bu nati fa bu ŋu kaaki ka nbaram na bu ni bu beŋgu nbiɛsɔm saŋga da ka bara ni biɛsɔ dɛɛ su ni, ni bo bu tara minɛm faŋga tɔ bu ni, ni bo bu bu kɔrɛ ni, ni bo bu di kɔrɛ daani ni, ni sa kɛrɛ bo i kɔ má keree­wa kpa atin su ni, bɛrɛ kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, ye ba fa nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n ase n a.

eng: Sound teaching agrees with the glorious gospel of the blessed God that has been trusted to me.
local: Nyini keree­wa n fite Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n nu wɔ, bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama m ka m kan kere minɛm ni. Labari kpa n ti Nyɛmɛ kpiri n dɛɛ wɔ, yiri ti ye ni ka minɛm kansi i a.

eng: I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me strength because he considered me faithful. So he appointed me to ministry.
local: M da ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo asi ka, yiri ye wɔ ma m faŋga, na m koro yo m juma n a. M da i asi ni kabo wɔ bu m ka m la dabuŋu, na wɔ yi m ka m su i ni.

eng: even though I used to speak against him, attack his people, and I was proud. But I was shown mercy because I acted in ignorance and without faith.
local: haari ni bo m yo jɔjɔ wuraari i ŋgasi nu, na m kpiɛɛri i nzukɔ, na m kereeri i wuwuruwa ni, ama, nyini n kɛrɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ siiri m aŋunfɔ ɔ, dama m yo nya yo má i yarada, nyini ti, m yo si má sa bo m yo i ni.

eng: Our Lords favor poured all over me along with the faithfulness and love that are in Christ Jesus.
local: Na yiri Mibiɛ n ama ma di i ahɔrɔma akɔ asin su, na wɔ ma ma yo i yarada na m koro minɛm, kabo ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti.

eng: This saying is reliable and deserves full acceptance: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners—and Im the biggest sinner of all.
local: Ahin nɔaniɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, i ti ye ni ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ de i ni bu saa nnyɔ, yiri la ka, Yesu Kristo baari durunya n nu ka i ba de satiɛ­yo­fɔm tii ji wɔ. Mini ti sɔnɔ bo m satiɛ taraari sɔnɔ kɛrɛ dɛɛ a.

eng: But this is why I was shown mercy, so that Christ Jesus could show his endless patience to me first of all. So Im an example for those who are going to believe in him for eternal life.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ siiri m aŋunfɔ. I yoori sɔ ɔ, adena Yesu Kristo koro kere kabo i la sawɔrɛ beberebe ni mini bo m satiɛ sunnu taraari sɔnɔ kɛrɛ dɛɛ ni. I yoori nyini sɔ ɔ, adena i fa m fa kere minɛm bo bu ba ka sin ni, na bu yo i yarada nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: Now to the king of the ages, to the immortal, invisible, and only God, may honor and glory be given to him forever and always! Amen.
local: Yiri ti Fɛmɛ Kpiri a, na i fɛmɛya n la má awieeri, i wo bɛrɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, na be gusu koro ŋu má i, yiri ŋgumi kpein ti Nyɛmɛ a, i ti ye ni ka minɛm kɛrɛ kansi i na bu ma i jirima. Ami.

eng: Timothy, my child, Im giving you these instructions based on the prophecies that were once made about you. So if you follow them, you can wage a good war.
local: M wa Timoti, m sufa ahin nɔaniɛ n fa wura u saa n nu wɔ lɛɛ, i su kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm du mɔ jɔjɔɔri i fa kɔ u dɔ n su wɔ. Fa nyini nɔaniɛm ni yo ka u biɛsɔya neŋgem na a fa kun luɛ nyunyumi.

eng: because you have faith and a good conscience. Some people have ruined their faith because they refused to listen to their conscience.
local: na a bita u yarada ni sunsunni kpa. Nbem akete ka bu fa má sunsunni kpa na bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, nyini ti, bu yarada bo bu yo yoori i ni aka ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: such as Hymenaeus and Alexander. Ive handed them over to Satan so that they can be taught not to speak against God.
local: Bɛrɛ nbem la Himɛnasi ni Alɛkizanda, bɛrɛ ye ma fa bu awura Sitana saa nu ka i kere bu ŋgɔnlɛ, adena bu sɔnsɔn kabo má bu wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu kun.

eng: First of all, then, I ask that requests, prayers, petitions, and thanksgiving be made for all people.
local: Deke bo i du mɔ la ka, m susere am ni anyum­boro wɔ, ka, am sere Nyɛmɛ ma minɛm kɛrɛ, fa kɔ deke bo i ti bu miɛɛ­wa su, na am da i asi fa ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

eng: Pray for kings and everyone who is in authority so that we can live a quiet and peaceful life in complete godliness and dignity.
local: Sere Nyɛmɛ ma nfɛmɛm, ni bo bu wo yiko woo­biri n kɛrɛ, na ya koro wo ni tana­laifiɛ, na ya woo­wa kere ka ya su Nyɛmɛ atin n su ni nahɔrɛ, na ya wo jirima wo ni ya beŋgu.

eng: This is right and it pleases God our savior.
local: Nyini nyɛmɛsere n ti kpa a, na i sun Nyɛmɛ nu, yiri ti ya Tii­de­fɔ a.

eng: who wants all people to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.
local: Yiri koro ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nya tii­de a, na bu si nahɔrɛ atin ni.

eng: There is one God and one mediator between God and humanity, the human Christ Jesus.
local: Dama ya kɛrɛ si ka Nyɛmɛ kun n kpein wo bɛrɛ a, na sɔnɔ kun be gusu wo bɛrɛ bo i yo adamande ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu a, yiri la Yesu Kristo.

eng: who gave himself as a payment to set all people free. This was a testimony that was given at the right time.
local: i wu maari adamande na i de bu kɛrɛ tii ji. Kabo i yoori sɔ n kere ka, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i de adamande kɛrɛ tii ji lɛɛ.

eng: I was appointed to be a preacher and apostle of this testimony—Im telling the truth and Im not lying! Im a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
local: Nyini ti ye Nyɛmɛ yiiri m ka m ba yo Yesu jaramasa, na m kan i labari kpa n kere minɛm bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ni, na m kere bu keree­wam bo bu ti nahɔrɛ ni. M sukan nahɔrɛ wɔ, m bu má kɔrɛ.

eng: Therefore, I want men to pray everywhere by lifting up hands that are holy, without anger or argument.
local: Deke kɛrɛ bo Kristo jamam tiɛn bu nɔɔ, m koro ka nbiɛsɔm yɛ bu saa su sere Nyɛmɛ. Nyini nbiɛsɔm ni, i ti ye ni ka bu nyɛmɛsu kɔ atin n su, na má yaa­fa wara akuruwa be wo bu ahore su.

eng: In the same way, I want women to enhance their appearance with clothing that is modest and sensible, not with elaborate hairstyles, gold, pearls, or expensive clothes.
local: M koro ka nbaram gusu di suŋguruya nyunyumi, bo i sɛ su. I ti má ye ni ka bu di bu tii­ŋmiɛ suŋguruya kɔ sin su, na má bu wura sika, wara aferem bo bu la gɔ agaya, wara suturam bo bu la gɔ agaya.

eng: They should make themselves attractive by doing good, which is appropriate for women who claim to honor God.
local: Ama, bu nyumi kɛrɛ fite bu sakpa yoo­wa a, kabo i ti ye ni ka nbaram bo bu se ka bu su Nyɛmɛ atin n su n yo ni.

eng: A wife should learn quietly with complete submission.
local: I ti ye ni ka nbaram mɔ bu nɔɔ na bu yo bu ŋu bɛnlɛ, na bu so bu sui ti keree­wam bo i ti ye ni ka bu sɔnsɔn i ni.

eng: I dont allow a wife to teach or to control her husband. Instead, she should be a quiet listener.
local: M soro má su na bu kere nbiɛsɔm keree­wam, wara bu la yiko niɛ nbiɛsɔm su. Bu dɛɛ la ka, bu mɔ bu nɔɔ.

eng: Adam was formed first, and then Eve.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yiiri Adam wɔ ka, ye i nya ba yiiri Awa.

eng: Adam wasnt deceived, but rather his wife became the one who stepped over the line because she was completely deceived.
local: Má Adam ye woo dadakaari i a, ama, i dakaari Awa wɔ, na wɔ kete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: But a wife will be brought safely through childbirth, if they both continue in faith, love, and holiness, together with self-control.
local: Ama, nzɛn nbaram laari yarada, ni koro, ni wo casi, na bu bita bu ŋu nyunyumi saŋga kɛrɛ, bu nyá bu tii­de fite bu baa­wu nu.

eng: This saying is reliable: if anyone has a goal to be a supervisor in the church, they want a good thing.
local: Ahin nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i boro i nyi na i yo Kristo jama n mɔ­fɔ, i mi koro juma kpa lɛɛ.

eng: So the churchs supervisor must be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, sober, modest, and honest. They should show hospitality and be skilled at teaching.
local: Kristo jama n mɔ­fɔ ni, wɔ yo daka ka má i la ŋgasi be, na i ja bara kun n kpein, na i bita i ŋu, na i la tara­buŋu, na i yo sa bo i la anyunuyi. I ti ye ni ka i de wɔfɔm, na i koro kere minɛm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ nyunyumi.

eng: They shouldnt be addicted to alcohol or be a bully. Instead, they should be gentle, peaceable, and not greedy.
local: I ti má ye ni ka i nu nzan agaya, wara i ti aluwa koro­fɔ, ama, i yo i ŋu bɛnlɛ, na i koro tana­laifiɛ. I kaaki ti má ye ni ka i koro ŋwaa agaya.

eng: They should manage their own household well—they should see that their children are obedient with complete respect.
local: Wɔ yo daka ka i koro bita i awuru­fɔm nyunyumi, na i ma i nbaam de i nɔaniɛ na bu ma i jirima deke kɛrɛ nu.

eng: because if they dont know how to manage their own household, how can they take care of Gods church?.
local: Dama nzɛn sɔnɔ koro bita má i awuru­fɔm, i mi yo sɛ koro bita Kristo jama n a?.

eng: They shouldnt be new believers so that they wont become proud and fall under the devils spell.
local: I ti má ye ni ka i mi ti Yesu susu­fɔ fɔfɔrɛ, adena má i wura i ŋu kpiri na Nyɛmɛ di i jɔrɛ yo i ŋgbɛɛn kabo i yoori Sitana ni.

eng: They should also have a good reputation with those outside the church so that they wont be embarrassed and fall into the devils trap.
local: Wɔ kaaki ayo daka ka i mi ti sɔnɔ bo minɛm bo bu ti má Kristo jama n nu­fɔm ma i jirima, adena, má Sitana nya i taraa­biri na i yo sa be bo i di nyinsɛ.

eng: In the same way, servants in the church should be dignified, not two-faced, heavy drinkers, or greedy for money.
local: I ti sɔ ɔ fa kɔ Kristo jama n buka­fɔm gusu dɔ. Wɔ yo daka ka bu ti minɛm bo bu la anyunuyi, na má bu yo anɔɔ nnyɔ nnyɔ­fɔm, na má bu nu nzan agaya, na má bu nyunu boro ŋwaa kpini su.

eng: They should hold on to the faith that has been revealed with a clear conscience.
local: Wɔ yo daka ka bu subita Kristo jama n keree­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi akere n kenken, na bu la má ŋgasi be bu sunsunni nu.

eng: They should also be tested and then serve if they are without fault.
local: I ti ye ni ka bu du mɔ fiiti bɛrɛ Kristo jama n buka­fɔm ni na bu niɛ. Na nzɛn bu la má ŋgasi be, am ma bu yo buka­fɔm n juma ni.

eng: In the same way, women who are servants in the church should be dignified and not gossip. They should be sober and faithful in everything they do.
local: I ti sɔ ɔ nbaram gusu dɔ. Wɔ yo daka ka bu ti minɛm bo bu la anyunuyi, na má bu susaaki minɛ duma. Wɔ yo daka ka bu bita bu ŋu, na bu la dabuŋu deke n kɛrɛ nu.

eng: Servants must be faithful to their spouse and manage their children and their own households well.
local: Kristo jama n buka­fɔ, i ti ye ni ka i ja bara kun n kpein, na i koro bita i nbaam, ni i dika­fɔm nyunyumi.

eng: Those who have served well gain a good standing and considerable confidence in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Nyini buka­fɔm bo bu yo bu juma na i kɔ atin su ni, bu kpini duma kpa ma bu ŋu wɔ, na bu kaaki koro jɔjɔ ni ahoresi kabo bu yo Yesu Kristo yarada ni.

eng: I hope to come to you quickly. But Im writing these things to you so that.
local: Kabo m sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma u ni, m la tama ka m bá u dɔ kisa.

eng: if Im delayed, youll know how you should behave in Gods household. It is the church of the living God and the backbone and support of the truth.
local: Ama, nzɛn haari ma ba aba má nde, ahin kadasi ni má a si kabo wɔ yo daka ka Nyɛmɛ minɛm wo ti barasu a. Bɛrɛ ti Kristo jama n a, yiri n bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛm a, bɛrɛ bita i keree­wa n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n kenken a, kabo sɔɔ fa see­wa n ni i jantaraka n bita i kenken ni.

eng: Without question, the mystery of godliness is great: he was revealed as a human, declared righteous by the Spirit, seen by angels, preached throughout the nations, believed in around the world, and taken up in glory.
local: Be koro kete má ka ya nyɛmɛsu atin n bo i wo asiri nu la má aŋusu, ka o, Na wɔ kaaki adamande ayi i ŋu fofoe nu. Na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n akere ka wɔ yo sa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu. Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm aŋu i. Na i susu­fɔm n akɔ ajɔjɔ i jɔrɛ durunya n kɛrɛ nu. Na durunya n nu­fɔm dɔŋgu ayo i yarada. Na Nyɛmɛ afa i akɔ awo i bi aŋgoro, jirima kɛrɛ woo­biri ni.

eng: The Spirit clearly says that in latter times some people will turn away from the faith. They will pay attention to spirits that deceive and to the teaching of demons.
local: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ajɔjɔ wein ka, ahin awieeri su saŋga n bo ya wo nu ni, minɛ nbem yáki bu yarada atin ni, na bu su ajinim bo bu dadaka minɛm su, ni bu keree­wam ni.

eng: They will be controlled by the pretense of lying, and their own consciences will be seared.
local: Nyini keree­wam sɔ fite minɛm bo bu ti dadaka­fɔm na bu nati bu kɔrɛ n dɔ ɔ, bo bu sunsunni akaaki haari na bu yo satiɛ mmɔɔ, na bu sunsunni ma má bu ka ba yo satiɛ.

eng: They will prohibit marriage and eating foods that God created—and he intended them to be accepted with thanksgiving by those who are faithful and have come to know the truth.
local: Nyini minɛm sɔ kere minɛm wɔ, ka, má bu ja ajaa, na má bu kaaki di diire barasu nbem. Ama, Nyɛmɛ ayi nyini diirem n ka ya di bu wɔ, nyini ti, yɛrɛ bo ya yo i yarada na ya si kabo nahɔrɛ atin n ti barasu ni, i ti ye ni ka ya sere Nyɛmɛ, na ya da i asi ka, na ya nya di bu.

eng: Everything that has been created by God is good, and nothing that is received with thanksgiving should be rejected.
local: Deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi i ti kpa a, deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo ya kete i, ama, i ti ye ni ka ya de i na ya da Nyɛmɛ asi.

eng: These things are made holy by Gods word and prayer.
local: dama Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ni nyɛmɛsere ma nyini diire n yo casi wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: If you point these things out to the believers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus who has been trained by the words of faith and the good teaching that youve carefully followed.
local: Nzɛn a faari ahin keree­wam kere ya ninbaam ni, nyini kere ka a ti Yesu Kristo amusui­su­fɔ kpa. Nyini keree­wam bo a du mɔ ade bu ni, na a yo bu yarada, na a si ka bu ti nahɔrɛ, na a su bu su ni, bu ti ka diire bo i ma u faŋga n wɔ.

eng: But stay away from the godless myths that are passed down from the older women. Train yourself for a holy life!.
local: Ama, kete jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn bo bu kɔ má Nyɛmɛ atin su ni, na a boro u nyi na a sɔnsɔn sa bo i kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin n su nahɔrɛ su.

eng: While physical training has some value, training in holy living is useful for everything. It has promise for this life now and the life to come.
local: Dama sa bo a boro u nyi yo i na i buka aŋuniɛ­mbaa n faŋga n la kpɛrɛ kaan, ama, nzɛn a boro u nyi yo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ atin n su sa, i la kpɛrɛ beberebe, nyini ma a si ka a nyá deke kpa kisa, na a káaki nya deke kpa buka su fa kɔ fa ba.

eng: This saying is reliable and deserves complete acceptance.
local: Nyini nɔaniɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo i ti ye ni ka ya de i na ya yo i yarada.

eng: We work and struggle for this: Our hope is set on the living God, who is the savior of all people, especially those who believe.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye ya boro ya nyi, na ya yo juma ni nahɔrɛ su, dama ya daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu, yiri wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a, yiri ti minɛ kɛrɛ Tii­de­fɔ a, maala bo bu yo i yarada ni.

eng: Command these things. Teach them.
local: Fa ahin keree­wam n fa kere bu, na a ma bu su bu su.

eng: Dont let anyone look down on you because you are young. Instead, set an example for the believers through your speech, behavior, love, faith, and by being sexually pure.
local: Má a ma be bu u ŋgbɛɛn ka a ti gbɛfɛnɛ n dɛɛ ti, ama, ma yarada­yo­fɔm ni ŋu u sa­yoo­wam na bu sɔn u su. Bɛrɛ la u nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa, ni deke bo a yo i, ni kabo a koro minɛm ni, ni u yarada n bo a la i ni, ni u woo­wa casi ni.

eng: Until I arrive, pay attention to public reading, preaching, and teaching.
local: Yo aniɛ na a sukan Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n na Kristo jama n ti, na a sukan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, na a sukere minɛm Nyɛmɛ atin ni, na m nya ba.

eng: Dont neglect the spiritual gift in you that was given through prophecy when the elders laid hands on you.
local: Má a ma u wara fi aceeri n bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n fa maari u ni, saŋga bo i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm jɔjɔɔri na Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm faari bu saa nanaari u ŋu sereeri Nyɛmɛ maari u ni, ma u cɛcɛ wo i nu, na a fa i yo juma.

eng: Practice these things, and live by them so that your progress will be visible to all.
local: Yo ahin juma ni anyum­boro na a fa u ŋu ma nyinim kɛrɛ, adena minɛm kɛrɛ ŋu kabo u nyɛmɛsu n kɔ atin su barasu.

eng: Focus on working on your own development and on what you teach. If you do this, you will save yourself and those who hear you.
local: Si u ŋu sa ni u keree­wam ni. Kɔ su suyo ahin sam ni, dama nzɛn a nya yoori sɔ, wɔrɔ ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ti u nɔaniɛ ni nyá tii­de.

eng: Dont correct an older man, but encourage him like hes your father; treat younger men like your brothers.
local: Má a tiɛn kpiɛn ŋu, ama, jɔjɔ i dɔ kabo a jɔjɔ u si dɔ ni, na a tu ngbɛfɛnɛm fɔ ka u ninbaam.

eng: treat older women like your mother, and treat younger women like your sisters with appropriate respect.
local: na a fa nbara nkpiɛnm yo ka u nim, na a fa tarawam ni nsuŋgurum yo ka u ninbaam, na u ni bɛrɛ wo ni ahore­mbaa casi.

eng: Take care of widows who are truly needy.
local: Bita nkuna­fɔm bo bu la má buka­fɔ be n ni jirima.

eng: But if a particular widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn to respect their own family and repay their parents, because this pleases God.
local: Ama, nzɛn kuna­fɔ la nbaam, wara anumam, i ti ye ni ka bu suniɛ i dika kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni. Nyini kere ka, bu sufa bu wu­fɔm ŋkatɔ tɔ bu kaari wɔ lɛɛ. Dama nyini n sɔ yo Nyɛmɛ fiɛ a.

eng: A widow who is truly needy and all alone puts her hope in God and keeps on going with requests and prayers, night and day.
local: Nzɛn kuna­fɔ wo i ŋgumi na i la má buka­fɔ be, wɔ fa i tama kɛrɛ awura Nyɛmɛ saa nu lɛɛ. Na i susere Nyɛmɛ jande wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ ka i buka i.

eng: But a widow who tries to live a life of luxury is dead even while she is alive.
local: Ama, kuna­fɔ kɛrɛ bo i nati yo i kunnu koroo­wa, haari ni bo i la ŋgɔɔ mmɔɔ ni, i ti ka wɔ wu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Teach these things so that the families will be without fault.
local: Fa ahin keree­wam kɛrɛ kere nkuna­fɔm, ni bu dika­fɔm, adena má be ŋu bu ŋgasi.

eng: But if someone doesnt provide for their own family, and especially for a member of their household, they have denied the faith. They are worse than those who have no faith.
local: Ama, nzɛn be cɛcɛ wo má nu ka i niɛ i lamɔsim dika, maala i dika­fɔm, i ti ka i mi akete Kristo atin n lɛɛ. Nyini saŋga ni, sɔnɔ bo wɔ yo má yarada tara i mi co lɛɛ.

eng: Put a widow on the list who is older than years old and who was faithful to her husband.
local: Kuna­fɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ nya ju má afɔ abura­sɛn, má a kɛrɛ i duma buka Kristo jama nkuna­fɔm su. Bekun, na i ti bo wɔ du mɔ aja biɛsɔ kun wɔ.

eng: She should have a reputation for doing good: raising children, providing hospitality to strangers, washing the feet of the saints, helping those in distress, and dedicating herself to every kind of good thing.
local: na minɛm di i daani ka i yo sakpa a, má pui ti, i ti bo wɔ ta nbaam ataari kpa, na wɔ de wɔfɔm i dika, na wɔ yo i ŋu kaan ka i su Nyɛmɛ minɛm, na wɔ buka minɛm bo bu woori kekereke nu, na wɔ su amusui kpa.

eng: But dont accept younger widows for the list. When their physical desires distract them from Christ, they will want to get married.
local: Ama, nkuna­fɔm bo bu nyunu nya ti má ni, má a kɛrɛ bu duma buka nkuna­fɔm n su, dama nzɛn ajaa konvi ba wuraari bu na bu koro ka bu kaaki ja ajaa, nyini saŋga, sɛsɛ­su bo bu faari i na bu diiri i di ni Kristo ni, bu koro su má i su ni nahɔrɛ su lɛɛ, bu yaki i wɔ, na bu kaaki bu sin bo Kristo lɛɛ. Nyini ti ye Nyɛmɛ dí bu jɔrɛ cin bu sui a.

eng: Also, they learn to be lazy by going from house to house. They are not only lazy, but they also become gossips and busybodies, talking about things they shouldnt.
local: Bekun, nyini ma bu koro ba yo nviɛya­fɔm na bu ba sunati awuru awurum nu, i ti má ka bu yo nviɛya­fɔm ŋgumi wɔ, ama, na bu sukpura tere, na bu sufa bu nɔɔ suwura minɛm jɔrɛ nu, na bu sujɔjɔ jɔrɛm bo wɔ ti má ye ni ka bu jɔjɔ bu.

eng: So I want younger widows to marry, have children, and manage their homes so that they wont give the enemy any reason to slander us. .
local: Nyini ti, mini dɔ, nzɛn nkuna­fɔm toko ti tarawam, i ti ye ni ka bu ja na bu wu nbaam, na bu bita bu awurum, na má ya kpɔ­fɔm koro ŋu ŋgasi be bo bu koro nya atin jɔjɔ ya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kabo bu koro.

eng: Some have already turned away to follow Satan.
local: Dama nkuna­fɔ nbem adu mɔ akete Kristo atin n asu Sitana su.

eng: If any woman who is a believer has widows in her family, she should take care of them and not burden the church, so that it can help other widows who are truly needy.
local: Ama, Kristo susu­fɔ bara kɛrɛ bo nkuna­fɔm wo i dika, i ti ye ni ka yiri ni i tii niɛ bu dika, na má bu fa bu fie fie Kristo jama n ni, adena Kristo jama n koro niɛ nkuna­fɔm bo bu la má buka­fɔ be fieo n su.

eng: Elders who lead well should be paid double, especially those who work with public speaking and teaching.
local: Kristo jama ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm bo bu yo mɔ­fɔ juma nyunyumi ni, i ti ye ni ka bu nya bu ŋkatɔ busu nnyɔ, maala bɛrɛ bo bu yo bu juma ni anyum­boro ka bu kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ na bu kaaki kere minɛm Nyɛmɛ atin n ni.

eng: The scripture says, Dont put a muzzle on an ox while it treads grain , and Workers deserve their pay .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nzɛn a bita toora subobo ŋgani, má a cici i nɔɔ. Na ba kaaki akɛrɛ ka, I ti ye ni ka juma­yo­fɔ nya i ŋkatɔ.
