eng: Dont accept an accusation made against an elder unless it is confirmed by two or three witnesses.
local: Má a se ka a de jɔrɛ be bo ba kpiɛ asɔ Kristo jama n buka­fɔ n kpiɛn be, má ka minɛ nnyɔ, wara nsan adi jɔrɛ n daani ka i ti nahɔrɛ.

eng: Discipline those who are sinning in front of everyone so that all the others will be afraid.
local: Ama, bo bu yo satiɛ ni, se bu nahɔrɛ jama n kɛrɛ nyunu, adena nyiŋgam kɛrɛ soro sɛrɛ.

eng: I charge you before God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels to follow these practices without bias, and without playing favorites.
local: M suma u nɔaniɛ lɛɛ, Nyɛmɛ ni Yesu Kristo ni mɛrɛkɛm kɛrɛ nyunu, ka, a de ahin keree­wam ni, na má a fa tantambu­nu, wara má a koro be tara be fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo a yo i n su.

eng: Dont rush to commission anyone to leadership, and dont participate in the sins of others. Keep yourself morally pure.
local: Má a koro nde na a fa u saa nana sɔnɔ be ŋu, na a sere Nyɛmɛ ma i mi ka i yo Nyɛmɛ juma, má a ma u saa n wo be dɛɛ satiɛ yoo­wa n nu. Ma u woo­wa yo casi.

eng: Dont drink water anymore, but use a little wine because of your stomach problems and your frequent illnesses.
local: A la má laifiɛ saŋga saŋga n dɛɛ ti, má a nu nzue ŋgumi, ama, nu nzan mmɔɔ kaan, na i buka u fa kɔ akunnuyaari n bo i yaki má u ni.

eng: The sins of some people are obvious, and the sins are judged before the people must face judgment, but the sins of other people show up later.
local: Minɛ nbem satiɛ yoo­wa kɛrɛ kaba da fofoe nu wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋu i, nyini satiɛm ye Nyɛmɛ fá di bu jɔrɛ a, ama, na nbem gusu dɛɛ satiɛ bo bu yo i bá fite fofoe nu kasin.

eng: In the same way, the good that people do is also obvious and cant be hidden.
local: I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ bekun, sakpa ti deke bo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ŋu i fofoe nu wɔ, na nzɛn haari sakpa bo i wo má fofoe nu ni mmɔɔ, i ti deke bo bu koro fiɛ má i.

eng: Those who are under the bondage of slavery should consider their own masters as worthy of full respect so that Gods name and our teaching wont get a bad reputation.
local: Kristo jama nbaam bo bu ti akɔɔm ni, i ti ye ni ka bu ma bu mibiɛm jirima nyunyumi, adena be koro saaki má Nyɛmɛ duma, wara ya keree­wam ni.

eng: And those who have masters who are believers shouldnt look down on them because they are brothers. Instead, they should serve them more faithfully, because the people who benefit from your good service are believers who are loved. Teach and encourage these things.
local: Akɔɔm bo bu mibiɛm gusu ti yarada­yo­fɔm ni, i ti má ye ni ka bu bu bu mibiɛm ŋgbɛɛn ka bu kɛrɛ ti yarada­yo­fɔm n ti, ama, i ti ye ni ka bu su bu tara mibiɛm bo bu ti má yarada­yo­fɔm ni, dama bɛrɛ n bo bu sudi bu amusui n kpɛrɛ n ti bu ninbaam bo bu koro bu wɔ. Fa ahin keree­wam n kan kere u jama n na a se ka bu yo bu.

eng: If anyone teaches anything different and doesnt agree with sound teaching about our Lord Jesus Christ and teaching that is consistent with godliness.
local: Minɛ nbem wo bɛrɛ bo bu kere keree­wa nbem bo bu ni ya dɛɛ n ti má kun, na bu sunsun ka ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ nɔaniɛ keree­wam n ti kɔrɛ wɔ, na bu keree­wam kaaki su má Kristo dɛɛ atin n su.

eng: that person is conceited. They dont understand anything but have a sick obsession with debates and arguments. This creates jealousy, conflict, verbal abuse, and evil suspicions.
local: Nyini minɛm n ti kpirikoro­fɔm wɔ, na bu kaaki si má pui. Na bu la sunsunni tiɛ, na bu se akuruwa ni minɛm fa kɔ nɔaniɛ keree­wam bu. Nyini ma bu si kɔrɛ a, na bu di aluwa, na bu kpiɛ bu beŋgu nzukɔ, na bu la sunsun­mase fa kɔ bu beŋgu dɔ.

eng: There is constant bickering between people whose minds are ruined and who have been robbed of the truth. They think that godliness is a way to make money!.
local: na bu nati suse akuruwa. Bu la má sunsunni kpa, na nahɔrɛ wo má bu dɔ. Na bu sunsun ka nzɛn sɔnɔ su Nyɛmɛ atin su, i nyá kpɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: Actually, godliness is a great source of profit when it is combined with being happy with what you already have.
local: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nyunu yi deke bo i la i, na i su Nyɛmɛ atin su, i mi nyá kpɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: We didnt bring anything into the world and so we cant take anything out of it.
local: Dama ya fa má pui aba má durunya n nu fɛn. Ya kaaki koro fa má pui fite má fɛn.

eng: well be happy with food and clothing.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn ya la diirem ni suturam, i ti ye ni ka ya nyunu yi bu.

eng: But people who are trying to get rich fall into temptation. They are trapped by many stupid and harmful passions that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
local: Ama, minɛm bo bu nyunu aboro neŋge kpini su agaya, na bu koro ka bu nya ajɛkɛ beberebe ni, bu nyini neŋge koro n sɔ bu ŋgɛɛ wɔ, na i nya bu taraa­biri fa kɔ bu konvikiya sunsunni ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn n su. Nyini ma bu kpaasi a, na bu ka ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: The love of money is the root of all kinds of evil. Some have wandered away from the faith and have impaled themselves with a lot of pain because they made money their goal.
local: Dama ŋwaa koro awaraye ti satiɛ kɛrɛ bunlui a. Nbem wo bɛrɛ bo bu koro ŋwaa agaya, nyini sɔ ma bu kaaki bu sin bo Kristo atin n wɔ, na i fa bu wura ahoresaaki bambaka nu.

eng: But as for you, man of God, run away from all these things. Instead, pursue righteousness, holy living, faithfulness, love, endurance, and gentleness.
local: Ama, wɔrɔ Timoti, a ti Nyɛmɛ sɔnɔ ɔ, nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka má a dodo nyini sam kɛrɛ be. Boro u nyi na a yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, na a su nyɛmɛsu atin n su, na a yo i yarada, na a koro minɛm, na a la mindi, na a wo bɛnlɛ.

eng: Compete in the good fight of faith. Grab hold of eternal life—you were called to it, and you made a good confession of it in the presence of many witnesses.
local: Boro u nyi na a tu ŋgoni kpa bita u yarada n kenken. Nyini má a di nasara, na a nyá ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, ahin ŋgɔɔ n ye Nyɛmɛ fereeri u ka i fá ma u a, na wɔrɔ ni u tii diiri i daani minɛ beberebe nyunu ni.

eng: I command you in the presence of God, who gives life to all things, and Christ Jesus, who made the good confession when testifying before Pontius Pilate.
local: Nyɛmɛ ma deke n kɛrɛ ŋgɔɔ a, na Yesu Kristo gusu jinaari Gomina Pɔntiyo Pilato nyunu diiri daani kpa, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m suma u nɔɔ Nyɛmɛ ni Yesu Kristo nyunu n a.

eng: Obey this order without fault or failure until the appearance of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: ka, a yo u juma n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama u n ni nahɔrɛ su, na u woo­wa ti casi na má be koro ŋu u ŋgasi be, haari fa kɔ ju cɛɛn n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo bá kpie i nyi ba ni.

eng: The timing of this appearance is revealed by God alone, who is the blessed and only master, the King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Nyɛmɛ afa nyini saŋga n bo i koro ka Yesu ba n afa ase, nyini saŋga n juuri, ye Nyɛmɛ má i ba a. Yiri ŋgumi niɛ deke n kɛrɛ su ye kansi wo i dɔ a. Yiri ti nfɛmɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim kɛrɛ Fɛmɛ a, na i ti mibiɛm kɛrɛ Mibiɛ.

eng: He alone has immortality and lives in light that no one can come near. No human being has ever seen or is able to see him. Honor and eternal power belong to him. Amen.
local: Yiri ŋgumi kpein wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a, na i woo­biri n bo i wo n ti wein wɔ, na be koro dodo má nyini wein ni. Be nya ŋu má i cɛɛn be da, be gusu koro ŋu má i wɔ mmɔɔ cɛɛn be cɛɛn be. I dɛɛ la jirima ni faŋga, saŋga kɛrɛ. Ami.

eng: Tell people who are rich at this time not to become egotistical and not to place their hope on their finances, which are uncertain. Instead, they need to hope in God, who richly provides everything for our enjoyment.
local: Se bo bu la ajɛkɛ durunya n nu fɛn n ka má bu fɔ, na má bu fa bu tama gɔ nyini ajɛkɛm bo bu cɛ má n ŋu, ama, bu fa gɔ Nyɛmɛ ŋu, yiri bo i ce ya deke n kɛrɛ kɔ sin su n na ya fa yo ya koroo­wa ni.

eng: Tell them to do good, to be rich in the good things they do, to be generous, and to share with others.
local: Se bu ka bu yo sakpa dɔŋgu, na bu ce aceerim, na deke kɛrɛ bo bu la i, na bu sukpaaki nu ma nyiŋgam.

eng: When they do these things, they will save a treasure for themselves that is a good foundation for the future. That way they can take hold of what is truly life.
local: Nzɛn bu nya yoori sɔ, i ti ka ba tiɛn ajɛkɛ nɔɔ abo i bu afa ase fa kɔ fa ba lɛɛ, adena bu nyá ŋgɔɔ n bo i ti ŋgɔɔ nahɔrɛ su ni.

eng: Timothy, protect what has been given to you in trust. Avoid godless and pointless discussions and the contradictory claims of so-called knowledge.
local: Timoti, niɛ deke bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama u ka a niɛ i dika n su nyunyumi. Cin u ŋu yaki minɛm bo bu jɔjɔ jɔjɔɔ­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn na bu bu má Nyɛmɛ, na bu sunsun ka bu si wɔ, ama, bu si má pui.

eng: When some people adopted this false knowledge, they missed the goal of faith. May grace be with you all.
local: Dama nbem afun ajina su ka bu la si, nyini dɛɛ ti, ba mini Kristo atin ni. Nyɛmɛ buka am kɛrɛ.

eng: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by Gods will, to promote the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n a. M ti Yesu Kristo jamarasa a, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koroya­nu, yiri Nyɛmɛ asunma m ka m kɔ kan kere minɛm kabo i yo seeri ka minɛm bo bu ni Yesu Kristo yo kun ni, bu nyá ŋgɔɔ.

eng: To Timothy, my dear child. Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
local: M sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma wɔrɔ Timoti bo a ti ka m wa koroo­wa n wɔ. Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka u, na bu si u aŋunfɔ, na bu ma u ahoreda.

eng: Im grateful to God, whom I serve with a good conscience as my ancestors did. I constantly remember you in my prayers day and night.
local: M da Nyɛmɛ asi, yiri ye m su i ni sunsunni kpa, kabo m nam yo suuri i ni. M sere Nyɛmɛ sɔ ɔ, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu, na m nati suda i asi ma u.

eng: When I remember your tears, I long to see you so that I can be filled with happiness.
local: M teŋge su kabo a yo suunri ni nyunvuin ni, m wara ado u, m koro ka m ŋu u, adena m ahore jɔ.

eng: Im reminded of your authentic faith, which first lived in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice. Im sure that this faith is also inside you.
local: M wara fi má u yarada n bo a yo i nahɔrɛ su n sa, nyini yarada n gusu ye u nabara Loyisi, ni u ni Yunisi laari i a. Ma yo yarada ka wɔrɔ mmɔɔ la i sɔ ɔ.

eng: Because of this, Im reminding you to revive Gods gift that is in you through the laying on of my hands.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, aceeri n bo Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n fa ceeri u ni, saŋga bo m faari m saa nanaari u ŋu, na m sereeri Nyɛmɛ maari u ni, m koro ka m teŋge u su ka a kaaki yɛ i su fa yo juma.

eng: God didnt give us a spirit that is timid but one that is powerful, loving, and self-controlled.
local: Dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i fa maari ya ni, wɔ fa ama má ya ka ya yo sɛrɛ­fɔm wɔ, ama, i fa maari ya ka i buka ya faŋga na ya koro minɛm na ya la tara­buŋu wɔ.

eng: So dont be ashamed of the testimony about the Lord or of me, his prisoner. Instead, share the suffering for the good news, depending on Gods power.
local: Nyini ti, má a ma nyinsɛ ti u ka a kan Mibiɛ n jɔrɛ n kere minɛm, wara má a ma nyinsɛ ti u ka m wo dan­saraka nu, Kristo dɛɛ ti. Ama, wɔrɔ mmɔɔ di wahara fa kɔ labari kpa n kan su, kabo m diiri i ni, na Nyɛmɛ ma u faŋga na a koro mindi de i.

eng: God is the one who saved and called us with a holy calling. This wasnt based on what we have done, but it was based on his own purpose and grace that he gave us in Christ Jesus before time began.
local: I deeri ya tii ji wɔ, na wɔ fere ya ka ya yo i dɛɛ tiɛŋgu minɛm, má ka ya sakpa bo ya yo i n dɛɛ ti, ama, i dɛɛ kunnu koroo­wa ni i ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti a. Saŋga bo i nya yi má durunya ni, ye i yoori ya nyini sakpa n a, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: Now his grace is revealed through the appearance of our savior, Christ Jesus. He destroyed death and brought life and immortality into clear focus through the good news.
local: Ama, kisa na Yesu Kristo, yiri bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ n aba durunya n nu ni, ya ŋu Nyɛmɛ koro ni. Labari kpa n dɛɛ ti, ya si ka Kristo akunma yiwee faŋga, na ya koro nya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: I was appointed a messenger, apostle, and teacher of this good news.
local: Nyɛmɛ ayi m ka m yo Yesu jaramasa na m kan Nyɛmɛ labari kpa n kere minɛm.

eng: This is also why Im suffering the way I do, but Im not ashamed. I know the one in whom Ive placed my trust. Im convinced that God is powerful enough to protect what he has placed in my trust until that day.
local: nyini dɛɛ ti ye m di ahin waharam n kɛrɛ n a. Ama, nyinsɛ ti má m, dama m si sɔnɔ bo ma daafi i ŋu ni, na labari kpa n bo wɔ fa ama m ni, m si tenle su ka i koro niɛ i su fa kɔ ju haari jina cɛɛn.

eng: Hold on to the pattern of sound teaching that you heard from me with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
local: Bita nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ bo ma kan akere u na i ti nahɔrɛ n kenken, na a yo yarada na a koro minɛm, kabo ya ni Yesu Kristo ayo kun n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Protect this good thing that has been placed in your trust through the Holy Spirit who lives in us.
local: Bita labari kpa n nyunyumi. I ti aceeri kpiri wɔ, ye Nyɛmɛ afa awura u saa nu a. Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i wo ya dɔ n buka u, na a bita i nyunyumi.

eng: You know that everyone in Asia has turned away from me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
local: A si ka yarada­yo­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo Esiya dɛɛ awɔrɛ n nu ni, fa saŋga ni Figelosi ni Hɛmogenesi n kɛrɛ, aŋmati ayaki m.

eng: May the Lord show mercy to Onesiphorus household, because he supported me many times and he wasnt ashamed of my imprisonment.
local: M sere Nyɛmɛ ka i si Onesifɔrɔsi dika­fɔm kɛrɛ aŋunfɔ, dama saŋga kɛrɛ yiri titi m nyunu a. Nyinsɛ ati má i ka m wo dan­saraka nu.

eng: After I arrived in Rome, he quickly looked for me and found me.
local: ama, i ba juuri Roma miɛ kpiri n nu fɛn ni, i niɛniɛɛri m busu wɔ ni anyum­boro, haari na wɔ ba aŋu m.

eng: May the Lord allow him to find his mercy on that day .
local: M sere Nyɛmɛ ka i si Onesifɔrɔsi aŋunfɔ, jina cɛɛn. Wɔrɔ ni u tii kaaki si buka bo i fa bukaari m barasu, saŋga bo m wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu ni.

eng: So, my child, draw your strength from the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔrɔ m wa, ahɔrɔma n bo Yesu Kristo ma a diiri i n dɛɛ ti, má a yɔrɔki u ŋu, yo u ŋu kenken.

eng: Take the things you heard me say in front of many other witnesses and pass them on to faithful people who are also capable of teaching others.
local: Jɔrɛ n bo a tiiri i m dɔ, na minɛ beberebe woori dɔ tiiri i ni, fa wura minɛm bo bu la dabuŋu n saa nu, na bɛrɛ gusu koro fa kere nyiŋgam.

eng: Accept your share of suffering like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
local: Kabo a ti Yesu Kristo dɛɛ soja kpa ni, yo siriya na wɔrɔ mmɔɔ di wahara n kabo nyiŋgam di i barasu ni.

eng: Nobody who serves in the military gets tied up with civilian matters, so that they can please the one who recruited them.
local: Sɔnɔ bo i yo soja juma, i cɛcɛ wo má juma bo i ti má soja juma nu, se i dɛɛ la ka i yo i soja juma a, na i yo i kpiɛn fiɛ.

eng: Also in the same way, athletes dont win unless they follow the rules.
local: Bekun, sɔnɔ bo i ŋmati ŋmatiiri ka i nya teremasu na i nya aceeri, má ka i mi asu atin bo bu fa kere nyini dɛɛ ŋmatiiri­fɔm n su, i koro nya má aceeri ni.

eng: A hardworking farmer should get the first share of the crop.
local: Dɔdɔɔri­fɔ bo i yo juma nahɔrɛ su ni, wɔ yo daka ka yiri du mɔ nya diire fɔfɔrɛ n be di a.

eng: Think about what Im saying; the Lord will give you understanding about everything.
local: Sunsun fa kɔ deke bo m suse u n su, na Mibiɛ n buka u na a ti i kɛrɛ bu.

eng: Remember Jesus Christ, who was raised from the dead and descended from David. This is my good news.
local: Teŋge su fa kɔ Yesu Kristo jɔrɛ n su, ka i fiteeri Fɛmɛ Davidi dɛɛ busu n nu wɔ, ye i wuuri a, na wɔ teŋge i yiwee n nu, m labari kpa n lɛɛ, sɔ ye m kan i a.

eng: This is the reason Im suffering to the point that Im in prison like a common criminal. But Gods word cannot be imprisoned.
local: Labari kpa n dɛɛ ti, ye m di wahara n a, haari ba wura m jɔrɔkɔ ase ka m ti satiɛ­yo­fɔ ɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n yiri, i wo má jɔrɔkɔ nu.

eng: This is why I endure everything for the sake of those who are chosen by God so that they too may experience salvation in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
local: Nyini ti, minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ka bu ti i dɛɛ minɛm n dɛɛ ti, ma mindi ade deke n kɛrɛ, adena bɛrɛ mmɔɔ nyá tii­de n bo i fite Yesu Kristo dɔ ni, na i má bu jirima n bo i la má awieeri Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni.

eng: This saying is reliable: If we have died together, we will also live together.
local: Ahin jɔjɔɔ­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo i ŋa, Nzɛn ya ni yiri saŋga wuuri, ya ni yiri bá saŋga nya ŋgɔɔ.

eng: If we endure, we will also rule together. If we deny him, he will also deny us.
local: Nzɛn ya mindi suuri i, ya ni yiri bá saŋga di i fɛmɛya n a. Nzɛn ya keteeri i, yiri gusu bá kete ya.

eng: If we are disloyal, he stays faithful because he cant be anything else than what he is.
local: Nzɛn yɛrɛ la má dabuŋu, yiri la dabuŋu wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, dama i koro kete má i ŋu.

eng: Remind them of these things and warn them in the sight of God not to engage in battles over words that arent helpful and only destroy those who hear them.
local: Teŋge u minɛm su fa kɔ nyini jɔrɛm n su, na a se bu ka Nyɛmɛ wo u daani, ka má bu se akuruwa fa kɔ nɔaniɛ keree­wam bo i la má kpɛrɛ, i saaki minɛm bo bu so bu sui ti nyini nɔaniɛ n wɔ.

eng: Make an effort to present yourself to God as a tried-and-true worker, who doesnt need to be ashamed but is one who interprets the message of truth correctly.
local: Boro u nyi na a fa u ŋu ma Nyɛmɛ juma na i sun i nu. Na a yo u juma nyunyumi na má nyinsɛ ti u, na a kere keree­wa bo i ti nahɔrɛ na i kɔ atin su nyunyumi.

eng: Avoid their godless discussions, because they will lead many people into ungodly behavior.
local: Má a so u sui ti jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm bo bu kɔ má nyɛmɛsu atin su ni, dama i ma minɛm wo dede ni Nyɛmɛ wɔ, na bu yo satiɛ kɔ sin su.

eng: and their ideas will spread like an infection. This includes Hymenaeus and Philetus.
local: Nyini keree­wam sɔ ti ka kambue bo i wie má na i tɛrɛki saŋga kɛrɛ wɔ. Minɛ nnyɔm bo bu kere nyini keree­wam n la Himɛnasi, ni Filetosi.

eng: who have deviated from the truth by claiming that the resurrection has already happened. This has undermined some peoples faith.
local: bɛrɛ kere ka saŋga bo minɛm wu na bu teŋge bu yiwee nu asin n a. Ahin keree­wam n ti kɔrɛ wɔ, nyini keree­wa n sɔ ma minɛ nbem ŋuniɛ ayɔrɔki bu fa kɔ bu yarada bo bu la i n su wɔ.

eng: Gods solid foundation is still standing with this sign: The Lord knows the people who belong to him, and Everyone who confesses the Lords name must avoid wickedness. .
local: Ama, deke bo Nyɛmɛ ayi akere i jama ni, i ti ka fa bumboori see­wa a, pui koro kankan má i. Nyini fa bumboori see­wa ni, ye ba kɛrɛ ahin nɔaniɛm n agɔ su n a, ka, Micɛra n si minɛm bo bu ti i dɛɛ minɛm ni, na Bo bu ŋa bu ti Micɛra n dɛɛ n kɛrɛ, i ti ye ni ka bu yaki satiɛ­yo.

eng: In a mansion, there arent just gold and silver bowls but also some bowls that are made of wood and clay. Some are meant for special uses, some for garbage.
local: Ya si ka awuru kpiri nu la taalɛ ni ayaba barasu barasu kɛrɛ, bu faari jɛtɛ ni sika yoori nbem a, na nbem gusu ti baka ni yakati dɛɛ. Bu fa nbem fa yo juma cɛɛn­kpiri cɛɛn, na bu fa nbem gusu yo juma cɛɛn n kɛrɛ cɛɛn.

eng: So if anyone washes filth off themselves, they will be set apart as a special bowl. They will be useful to the owner of the mansion for every sort of good work.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yi i ŋu fiteeri nyini kɔrɛ keree­wam n nu, Nyɛmɛ fá i mi yo ka cɛɛn­kpiri ayaba a, dama wɔ fa i ŋu ama Mibiɛ ni, na i yo juma kpa bo i kun kpɛrɛ ma i, má pui ti, wɔ sɛsɛ i ŋu su ka i yo juma kpa ma i Mibiɛ ni.

eng: Run away from adolescent cravings. Instead, pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace together with those who confess the Lord with a clean heart.
local: Tara u ŋu fa kɔ ngbɛfɛnɛm konviki tiɛ sam su. Boro u nyi, na a yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, na a yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, na a koro minɛm, na a la tana­laifiɛ, nyini má a buka minɛm bo bu su Nyɛmɛ ni ahore casi n su a.

eng: Avoid foolish and thoughtless discussions, since you know that they produce conflicts.
local: Ama, má a fa u nɔɔ bɔ sinnzinya jɔrɛm n nu, ni bisaa­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm. A si ka nyini n sɔ kpura minɛ tii wɔ.

eng: Gods slave shouldnt be argumentative but should be kind toward all people, able to teach, patient.
local: Wɔ yo daka ka má Mibiɛ n akɔɔ n suse akuruwa. I ti ye ni ka i yo aye ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ, na i koro fa sawɔrɛ kere minɛm Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n nyunyumi.

eng: and should correct opponents with gentleness. Perhaps God will change their mind and give them a knowledge of the truth.
local: Haari minɛm bo bu se i akuruwa ni mmɔɔ, i ti ye ni ka i fa sawɔrɛ ni bɛrɛ, na i kere bu atin bo bu fa i, beni, a ŋu ka Nyɛmɛ má bu kaaki bu wo na bu si nahɔrɛ atin ni.

eng: They may come to their senses and escape from the devils trap that holds them captive to do his will.
local: Nyini ma bu kaaki fa sunsunni kpa na bu ŋmati fite toroku n nu a, bo Sitana n asɔ atara bu na i ma bu yo i koroo­wa ni.

eng: Understand that the last days will be dangerous times.
local: M koro ka a si ka, fa kɔ durunya n awieeri, i yó kekereke.

eng: People will be selfish and love money. They will be the kind of people who brag and who are proud. They will slander others, and they will be disobedient to their parents. They will be ungrateful, unholy.
local: Minɛ yó anyii sam, na bu kóro ŋwaa agaya, na bu fɔ́, na bu súnsun ka bɛrɛ ŋgumi wo bɛrɛ a, na bu kpíɛ minɛ nzukɔ, na bu dé má bu wu­fɔm nɔaniɛ, na bu sí má aye, na bu kéte Nyɛmɛ woo­wa casi n jɔrɛ.

eng: unloving, contrary, and critical. They will be without self-control and brutal, and they wont love what is good.
local: na bu kóro má minɛm, na bu sí má aŋunfɔ, na bu sáaki minɛm duma, na bu lá má tara­buŋu, na bu yó wi, na bu kpɔ́ deke kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa.

eng: They will be people who are disloyal, reckless, and conceited. They will love pleasure instead of loving God.
local: Na bu lá má dabuŋu, na bu yó sa bo bu sunsun má bu, na bu lá karambani agaya, na bu kóro bu tii di tara nyɛmɛsu.

eng: They will look like they are religious but deny Gods power. Avoid people like this.
local: na bu yó bu ŋu ka bu su Nyɛmɛ atin n su wɔ, ama, na bu kéte Nyɛmɛ yiko ni. Má a dodo nyini minɛm sɔ.

eng: Some will slither into households and control immature women who are burdened with sins and driven by all kinds of desires.
local: Bu nbem nati wura awuru awurum nu wɔ, na bu bo nbaram bo bu la má tara­buŋu n anɔɔfiɛ na bu su bu su. Nyini nbaram sɔ satiɛ­yo akunma bu, na bu yo bu kunnu koroo­wam.

eng: These women are always learning, but they can never arrive at an understanding of the truth.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ bu ti keree­wam wɔ, ama, bu koro si má nahɔrɛ atin n fieo.

eng: These people oppose the truth in the same way that Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses. Their minds are corrupt and their faith is counterfeit.
local: Kabo Janesi ni Jambaresi yo keteeri jɔrɛ kɛrɛ bo Moyisi yo jɔjɔɔri ni, sɔ ye ahin kɔrɛ kere­fɔm n gusu akete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a. Bu sunsunni yo má juma, na ba kete yarada atin n su su ni.

eng: But they wont get very far. Their foolishness will become obvious to everyone like those others.
local: Ama, bu juma koro kɔ má i nyunu, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bá si ka bu ti sinnzinm wɔ, bu si má pui. Kabo minɛ yo siiri ka Janesi, ni Jambaresi yo ti sinnzinm ni.

eng: But you have paid attention to my teaching, conduct, purpose, faithfulness, patience, love, and endurance.
local: Ama, wɔrɔ yiri, a ŋu m keree­wa, ni m woo­wa, ni m sunsunni bo m taraari i ni, ni m yarada ni, ni m sawɔrɛ ni, ni m koro, ni m mindi ni.

eng: You have seen me experience physical abuse and ordeals in places such as Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I put up with all sorts of abuse, and the Lord rescued me from it all!.
local: ni m wuwuruwa bo bu kereeri m ni, ni wahara bo m diiri i n su. A si sam bo bu tuuri m, miɛ kpirim n bo bu fere bu ka Antiyoki, ni Ikoniyom, ni Lisitira ni, má wuwuruwa lɛɛ ye minɛm kereeri m a. Ama, na Mibiɛ n ayi m afite nyinim kɛrɛ nu.

eng: In fact, anyone who wants to live a holy life in Christ Jesus will be harassed.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i su Nyɛmɛ atin su na i ni Yesu Kristo yo kun, i mi ŋú wuwuruwa.

eng: But evil people and swindlers will grow even worse, as they deceive others while being deceived themselves.
local: na minɛ tiɛm ni kere­fɔ kɔrɛ­fɔm yo satiɛ kɔ sin su, bu dadaka minɛ, na minɛ dadaka bɛrɛ gusu.

eng: But you must continue with the things you have learned and found convincing. You know who taught you.
local: Ama, wɔrɔ yiri, nahɔrɛ atin n bo bu fa kereeri u, na a yoori i yarada nahɔrɛ su ni, su i su sɔ, dama a si minɛm bo bu kereeri u nyini atin ni.

eng: Since childhood you have known the holy scriptures that help you to be wise in a way that leads to salvation through faith that is in Christ Jesus.
local: A si ka fite u kaan nu kɛrɛ, ye a sɔnsɔɔnri Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n kɛrɛ n ye a siiri i a. Nyini koro ma u alecira a, na a yo Yesu Kristo yarada na a nya tii­de.

eng: Every scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for showing mistakes, for correcting, and for training character.
local: Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n bo ba kɛrɛ i n kɛrɛ fite yiri dɔ a, na i la kpɛrɛ bo a koro fa kere minɛm nahɔrɛ atin ni, na a fa kan sɔnɔ bo i yo satiɛ ndiɛ, na a fa kere sɔnɔ atin fa kɔ deke bo i kɔ má atin su, na a fa kere sɔnɔ na i yo sa bo i ti kpa.

eng: so that the person who belongs to God can be equipped to do everything that is good.
local: Nyini n kɛrɛ koro ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su Nyɛmɛ kpama bi a, na i koro yo juma kpa barasu kɛrɛ.

eng: Im giving you this commission in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is coming to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearance and his kingdom.
local: M suma u nɔaniɛ lɛɛ, Nyɛmɛ ni Yesu Kristo n nyunu, yiri bá di ŋgɔɔ­fɔm ni yiwee­fɔm n jɔrɛ a, na i bá di fɛmɛ na i niɛ i minɛm su.

eng: Preach the word. Be ready to do it whether it is convenient or inconvenient. Correct, confront, and encourage with patience and instruction.
local: Boro u nyi kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, sukan i saŋga kɛrɛ. Teŋge yarada­yo­fɔm su fa kɔ keree­wa bo i kɔ atin su. Jaraki bu na bu yaki satiɛ­yo. Jɔjɔ bu dɔ na bu yo sakpa buka su. Na a yo sawɔrɛ ni nyini n kɛrɛ na a kere bu nyunyumi.

eng: There will come a time when people will not tolerate sound teaching. They will collect teachers who say what they want to hear because they are self-centered.
local: Dama fa kɔ fa ba, minɛm kóro má ka bu so bu sui, na bu ti Kristo keree­wa n bo i kɔ atin su ni. Ama, bu sú bu dɛɛ koroo­wam su wɔ, na bu tíɛn kere­fɔm dɔŋgu nɔɔ bo bu kere bu dekem bo bu koro ka bu ti i ni.

eng: They will turn their back on the truth and turn to myths.
local: Bu káaki bu sin bo nahɔrɛ atin ni, na bu só bu sui ti kɔrɛ jɔrɛm.

eng: But you must keep control of yourself in all circumstances. Endure suffering, do the work of a preacher of the good news, and carry out your service fully.
local: Ama, wɔrɔ yiri, tara u ŋu saŋga kɛrɛ, na a mindi ni wahara di, na a boro u nyi kan Yesu labari kpa n jɔrɛ, na a yo u juma n kɛrɛ kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka a yo i ni.

eng: Im already being poured out like a sacrifice to God, and the time of my death is near.
local: Mini yiri, saŋga n aju bo bu yí m ŋgɔɔ ni, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, saŋga n aju bo m fíte durunya n nu fɛn.

eng: I have fought the good fight, finished the race, and kept the faith.
local: Ma boro m nyi atu ŋgoni Nyɛmɛ atin n su, ma yo m juma awie, ma kan labari kpa n ni m yarada kun.

eng: At last the champions wreath that is awarded for righteousness is waiting for me. The Lord, who is the righteous judge, is going to give it to me on that day. Hes giving it not only to me but also to all those who have set their heart on waiting for his appearance.
local: Kisa Nyɛmɛ bita teremasu aceeri ŋminda m, dama wɔ de m ka m yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ɔ. Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo, yiri di minɛm jɔrɛ ye i kɔ atin su a, yiri fá nyini teremasu aceeri n ce m jina cɛɛn a, má mini ŋgumi, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro Yesu na i tana ŋminda ka i ba n a.

eng: Do your best to come to me quickly.
local: Yo aniɛ na a bra m dɔ kisa kisa.

eng: Demas has fallen in love with the present world and has deserted me and has gone to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
local: Dama Keresensi akɔ Galatiya awɔrɛ n nu, ye Titosi akɔ Dalamatiya awɔrɛ n nu. Demasi aŋmati ayaki m, wɔ kɔ Tɛsalonika miɛ kpiri n nu, kisa i koro ka i wo kabo durunya n nu­fɔm wo n wɔ.

eng: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark, and bring him with you. He has been a big help to me in the ministry.
local: Luka ŋgumi wo m dɔ a. Fa Marako na u ni yiri bra, dama i koro buka m ni juma ni.

eng: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
local: Ma sunma Tikikusi Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu.

eng: When you come, bring along the coat I left with Carpus in Troas. Also bring the scrolls and especially the parchments.
local: A ŋa a ba, bita m ayɛrɛ tanlɛɛ n ba ma m, bo m yakiiri i Tɔrasi miɛ kpiri n nu, Kapusi dika ni. Fa m kadasim n gusu bita m nu, maala bo ba fa neŋge­ndɔrɛ ŋuma ayo bu ni.

eng: Alexander, the craftsman who works with metal, has really hurt me. The Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
local: Alɛkizanda bo i ti tunmi n ayo m satiɛ agaya, Mibiɛ ni tɔ́ i kaari fa kɔ sa bo wɔ yo i n su a.

eng: But watch out for him, because he opposes our teaching.
local: Si u ŋu sa ni yiri, dama wɔ jina su kekereke akete nɔaniɛ n bo ya kan i ni.

eng: No one took my side at my first court hearing. Everyone deserted me. I hope that God doesnt hold it against them!.
local: Ciciri su bo m sukan na m de m tii jɔrɛ dii­biri ni, be ajina má m sin, bu kɛrɛ ŋmati yakiiri m wɔ. M sere Nyɛmɛ ka i fa bu ŋgasi ce bu.

eng: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that the entire message would be preached through me and so all the nations could hear it. I was also rescued from the lions mouth!.
local: Ama, na Mibiɛ n ajina m sin a, na wɔ ma m faŋga, na ma koro akan labari kpa n akere minɛm, na bo bu ti má Yuda­fɔm n kɛrɛ akoro ati. Na wɔ de m afite minɛm bo bu koro ka bu kun m n saa nu.

eng: The Lord will rescue me from every evil action and will save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Yiri Mibiɛ ni yí m fite jɔrɛ tiɛ kɛrɛ nu a, na i fá m kɔ wura i fɛmɛya n nu aŋgoro ni laifiɛ a. Jirima bambaka ti i dɛɛ saŋga kɛrɛ. Ami.

eng: Say hello to Prisca and Aquila and the household of Onesiphorus.
local: Bisa Pirisila ni i hun Akuyila fa tu Onesifɔrɔsi dika­fɔm kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma m.

eng: Erastus stayed in Corinth, and I left Trophimus in Miletus because of his illness.
local: Irasitusi kaari Kɔrinto miɛ kpiri n nu wɔ, na ma yaki Torofimosi gusu Miletosi miɛ kpiri n nu, dama wɔ la má i ŋu.

eng: Try hard to come to me before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters say hello.
local: Yo aniɛ na a bra ka, na fufuku nya ju. Ya ninbaam bisa u dɔŋgu, bɛrɛ la Ɛyabulusi, ni Pudensi, ni Linosi, ni ya niɛma­bara Kalodiya, ni ya ninbaam nyiŋgam kɛrɛ.

eng: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all.
local: Ma Mibiɛ n wo ni wɔrɔ. Nyɛmɛ buka am kɛrɛ.

eng: From Paul, a slave of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. Im sent to bring about the faith of Gods chosen people and a knowledge of the truth that agrees with godliness.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n a. M ti Nyɛmɛ akɔɔ ɔ, na m kaaki ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa. Nyɛmɛ ayi m na wɔ sunma m ka m kɔ buka minɛm bo wɔ yi bu n na bu yarada yo bii­wa, na bu si nahɔrɛ fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin n su.

eng: Their faith and this knowledge are based on the hope of eternal life that God, who doesnt lie, promised before time began.
local: Kana Nyɛmɛ ba yiiri durunya n ni, i yo seeri ka i má ya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni. Nyɛmɛ bu má kɔrɛ. Nyini ŋgɔɔ n tama dɛɛ ti, ye ya la yarada ni si jina kun su n a.

eng: God revealed his message at the appropriate time through preaching, and I was trusted with preaching this message by the command of God our savior.
local: Na saŋga n ba juuri ni, na wɔ yi i nɔaniɛ n afite fofoe nu, na wɔ fa ama m ka m kɔ kan i kere minɛm. Nyɛmɛ ni i tii, yiri bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, maari m nɔɔ ka m kɔ kan i nɔaniɛ n a.

eng: To Titus, my true child in a common faith. Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our savior.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma wɔrɔ Titosi a. A ti m wa a, nahɔrɛ su, dama m ni wɔrɔ kɛrɛ asaŋga ayo Yesu Kristo yarada. Ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ Yesu Kristo buka u, na bu ma u ahoreda.

eng: The reason I left you behind in Crete was to organize whatever needs to be done and to appoint elders in each city, as I told you.
local: Deke bo i ti na m yakiiri u Kereti asiɛn n su n la ka, m koro ka a yo juma n bo wɔ wie má n wie, na a sɛsɛ i su, na a yi Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm se miɛm kɛrɛ su, kabo m seeri u ni.

eng: Elders should be without fault. They should be faithful to their spouse, and have faithful children who cant be accused of self-indulgence or rebelliousness.
local: Nyini nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n kɛrɛ bo a yi bu ni, i ti ye ni ka bu be la má ŋgasi be. I ti ye ni ka bu ja bara kun n kpein. I ti ye ni ka bu nbaam yo Kristo yarada, na be koro jaraki má nbaam ka bu nati yo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn na bu de má bu wu­fɔm nɔaniɛ.

eng: This is because supervisors should be without fault as Gods managers: they shouldnt be stubborn, irritable, addicted to alcohol, a bully, or greedy.
local: Nyɛmɛ afa i juma n awura nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm saa nu n dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo daka ka, má bu la ŋgasi be. I ti má ye ni ka bu ti aŋukere­fɔm, wara bu fa yaa ndende, wara bu nu nzan agaya, wara bu ti aluwa koro­fɔm, wara bu koro ŋwaa agaya.

eng: Instead, they should show hospitality, love what is good, and be reasonable, ethical, godly, and self-controlled.
local: Ama, i ti ye ni ka nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm kɛrɛ ti minɛm bo bu de wɔfɔm, na bu ti minɛm bo bu koro sakpa­yo, na bu la tara­buŋu, na bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin n su, na bu ti casi, na bu bita bu ŋu.

eng: They must pay attention to the reliable message as it has been taught to them so that they can encourage people with healthy instruction and refute those who speak against it.
local: I kaaki ti ye ni ka bu ti minɛm bo bu bita Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo i la dabuŋu n kenken, kabo ya kereeri bu ni, adena bu koro fa keree­wa n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n fa kere minɛm na bu nya ahoresi, na bu kaaki koro kan kere minɛm bo ba kete nyini keree­wa n kabo ba mini barasu ni.

eng: In fact, there are many who are rebellious people, loudmouths, and deceivers, especially some of those who are Jewish believers.
local: Dama minɛm dɔŋgu wo bɛrɛ bo ba kete keree­wa bo i ti kpa ni, na bu sujɔjɔ jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn na bu sudadaka minɛm, maala yarada­yo­fɔm bo bu toko bita Yuda­fɔm sa­yoo­wa ni.

eng: They must be silenced because they upset entire households. They teach what they shouldnt to make money dishonestly.
local: Ma bu mɔ bu nɔɔ, dama bu sufa nyini keree­wa n bo i ti má ye ni ka bu fa kere ni, sufa saaki awuru­kpa lɛɛ. Ŋwaa dɛɛ ti ye bu yo sɔ a.

eng: Someone who is one of their own prophets said, People from Crete are always liars, wild animals, and lazy gluttons.
local: Haari bɛrɛ Kereti­fɔm mmɔɔ dɛɛ tiɛŋgu si­fɔ be yo seeri ka, Bɛrɛ Kereti­fɔm ti kɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ dada kɛrɛ, na bu ti ka boro neŋge tiɛm, na bu ti nviɛya­fɔm, ni ayaa kpiri­fɔm wɔ.

eng: This statement is true. Because of this, correct them firmly, so that they can be healthy in their faith.
local: Kabo i yo seeri n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, jaraki bu, na bu yarada yo faŋga.

eng: They shouldnt pay attention to Jewish myths and commands from people who reject the truth.
local: na má bu su Yuda­fɔm nana jɔrɛm su, na má bu su deke bo minɛm bo ba kete nahɔrɛ n akpama i n su.

eng: Everything is clean to those who are clean, but nothing is clean to those who are corrupt and without faith. Instead, their mind and conscience are corrupted.
local: Minɛm bo bu woo­wa ti casi ni, deke kɛrɛ ti casi wɔ bu dɔ, ama, minɛm bo bu woo­wa ti má casi na bu yo má Yesu Kristo yarada ni, deke be mmɔɔ ti má casi bu dɔ. Bu sunsunni, ni bu ahore kɛrɛ ti fin wɔ, na nzɛn haari bu yo satiɛ mmɔɔ, bu si má ka i ti satiɛ.

eng: They claim to know God, but they deny God by the things that they do. They are detestable, disobedient, and disqualified to do anything good.
local: Bu ŋa bu si Nyɛmɛ, ama, bu sa­yoo­wa kere ka bu si má i, dama bu sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ ti ciire wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na bu de má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na bu koro yo má sakpa be.

eng: But you should talk in a way that is consistent with sound teaching.
local: Ama, wɔrɔ yiri, i ti ye ni ka a fa keree­wa bo i ti nahɔrɛ na i kɔ atin su, na a sufa kere yarada­yo­fɔm ni.

eng: Tell the older men to be sober, dignified, sensible, and healthy in respect to their faith, love, and patience.
local: Se nbiɛsɔ anyunuti­fɔm ka, bu bita bu ŋu, na bu yo sa bo i la anyunuyi, na bu la tara­buŋu. Kaaki se bu ka, bu ma bu yarada yo faŋga, na bu koro minɛm nahɔrɛ su, na bu la mindi nahɔrɛ su.

eng: Likewise, tell the older women to be reverent in their behavior, teaching what is good, rather than being gossips or addicted to heavy drinking.
local: Se nbara anyunuti­fɔm gusu ka, bu ma bu woo­wa kere ka bu soro Nyɛmɛ. Nyini la ka, má bu susaaki minɛ duma, na má bu ma nzan ja bu. Kaaki se bu ka, bu kere keree­wa kpa.

eng: That way they can mentor young women to love their husbands and children.
local: na bu tu tarawam fɔ ka, bu koro bu hunm ni bu nbaam.

eng: and to be sensible, morally pure, working at home, kind and submissive to their own husbands, so that Gods word wont be ridiculed.
local: na bu la tara­buŋu, na bu ma bu woo­wa ti casi, na bu bita bu awurum nyunyumi, na bu yo sakpa, na bu de bu hunm nɔaniɛ, adena má be koro bu Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Remind them to submit to rulers and authorities. They should be obedient and ready to do every good thing.
local: Teŋge yarada­yo­fɔm su ka, bu yo bu ŋu bɛnlɛ ma nfɛmɛm ni gomina nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, na bu de bu nɔaniɛ, na bu kpa bu ŋu na bu yo sa kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa.

eng: Likewise, encourage the younger men to be sensible.
local: Tu ngbɛfɛnɛm gusu fɔ ka, bu tara bu ŋu.

eng: They shouldnt speak disrespectfully about anyone, but they should be peaceful, kind, and show complete courtesy toward everyone.
local: na má bu saaki minɛm duma, na má bu se akuruwa ni minɛm, ama, bu yo bu ŋu kaan, na bu yo bu ŋu bɛnlɛ ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

eng: We were once foolish, disobedient, deceived, and slaves to our desires and various pleasures too. We were spending our lives in evil behavior and jealousy. We were disgusting, and we hated other people.
local: Má pui ti, yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii yo ti sinnzinm wɔ, ya si má Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ, na ya kaaki de má i nɔaniɛ, na ya mini i atin ni, ya yo ti minɛm bo ya yo deke kɛrɛ bo i wura ya konvi wɔ, na ya suyo ya sa koroo­wa, na ya la aŋunkpini, na ya si kɔrɛ, na ya yo ti minɛm bo minɛm kpɔ ya a, na ya gusu kpɔ ya beŋgu.

eng: in every way. Offer yourself as a role model of good actions. Show integrity, seriousness.
local: ni deke kɛrɛ. Wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii, ma u sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ kɔ atin su, na minɛm ŋu i, na bu sɔn u su. Fa sunsunni kpa ni atin bo i la jirima fa kere minɛm.

eng: and a sound message that is above criticism when you teach, so that any opponent will be ashamed because they wont find anything bad to say about us.
local: keree­wa kpa, na be koro kan má jɔrɛ be. Kere bu sɔ, adena minɛm bo bu kete ya keree­wa n di nyinsɛ, dama bu ŋu má ŋgasi be bo bu fa saaki ya.

eng: But when God our saviors kindness and love appeared.
local: Ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, Nyɛmɛ bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, yo faari i sakpa, ni i koro kereeri ya.

eng: he saved us because of his mercy, not because of righteous things we had done. He did it through the washing of new birth and the renewing by the Holy Spirit.
local: na wɔ nya ade ya tii aji. Má ka ya sakpa yoo­wa dɛɛ ti, ye i deeri ya tii jiiri a, ama, i aŋunfɔ si dɛɛ ti. Kabo i deeri ya tii n la ka, i Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni ŋmisiiri ya a, na wɔ ma ya wo fɔfɔrɛ, ni ŋgɔɔ fɔfɔrɛ.

eng: Tell slaves to submit to their own masters and please them in everything they do. They shouldnt talk back.
local: Se akɔɔm gusu ka, bu de bu mibiɛm nɔaniɛ, na bu yo sa kɛrɛ bo bu mibiɛm koro, na má bu se jɔrɛ be.

eng: which God poured out upon us generously through Jesus Christ our savior.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ ama ya Tii­de­fɔ Yesu Kristo afa Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama ya akɔ asin su.

eng: or steal. Instead, they should show that they are completely reliable in everything so that they might make the teaching about God our savior attractive in every way.
local: Kaaki se bu ka, má bu wɔ awie mumuku, ama, bu kere ka bu la dabuŋu deke kɛrɛ nu. Nzɛn bu yoori sɔ, bu woo­wa kpa ni kere minɛm ka, Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, i dɛɛ keree­wa n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: The grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all people.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ ayi i ahɔrɔma afite fofoe nu, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nya tii­de.

eng: So, since we have been made righteous by his grace, we can inherit the hope for eternal life.
local: Nyɛmɛ yoori sɔ ni, adena ya yó i nbaam, na ya yó tama ka ya nyá ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri. Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n dɛɛ ti, wɔ de ya ka ya la má ŋgasi be i dɔ, nyini ti ye wɔ fa ya ayo i nbaam n a.

eng: It educates us so that we can live sensible, ethical, and godly lives right now by rejecting ungodly lives and the desires of this world.
local: I ahɔrɔma ni kere ya ka ya yaki sam bo bu kɔ má nyɛmɛsu atin n su a, ni dekem bo bu wura ya konvi durunya n nu ni, na ya tara ya ŋu, na ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su, na ya bita nyɛmɛsu atin, ahin durunya n bo ya wo nu ni.

eng: At the same time we wait for the blessed hope and the glorious appearance of our great God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Ya yo ahin na ya ŋminda deke n bo ya la i tama bambaka ka i fite fofoe nu, yiri la ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo, kabo i fite fofoe nu ni i jirima woo­wa ni. Yiri ti ya Nyɛmɛ kpiri, ni ya Tii­de­fɔ a.

eng: This saying is reliable. And I want you to insist on these things, so that those who have come to believe in God might give careful attention to doing good. These things are good and useful for everyone.
local: Ahin jɔrɛ n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. M koro ka a kere ahin dekem ni anyum­boro, adena bo bu yo Nyɛmɛ yarada ni, bu fa bu sunsunni kɛrɛ yo sa bo i ti kpa. Ahin keree­wam n la kpɛrɛ, na bu kaaki ti kpa ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ.

eng: He gave himself for us in order to rescue us from every kind of lawless behavior, and cleanse a special people for himself who are eager to do good actions.
local: Yiri wu maari ya, adena i ma ya nya ya tii, na i yi ya fite ya satiɛ nu, na i kpakpara yi ya fin kɛrɛ, na i yo ya casi, na ya yo i dɛɛ minɛm bo bu nyunu aboro sakpa­yo su.

eng: Avoid stupid controversies, genealogies, and fights about the Law, because they are useless and worthless.
local: Ama, má a fa u ŋu wura akuruwa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn barasu kɛrɛ nu, ni ya nana Yuda­fɔm dumam, ni aluwa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su. Ahinm kɛrɛ ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, bu kunma kpɛrɛ be.

eng: Talk about these things. Encourage and correct with complete authority. Dont let anyone disrespect you.
local: Ahin keree­wam kɛrɛ, fa kere yarada­yo­fɔm, na a ma bu ahoresi, na a kan bu ndiɛ. Fa yiko n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama u n yo sɔ, na má a ma be bu u ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: After a first and second warning, have nothing more to do with a person who causes conflict.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i fa kpakpaaki­nu ba, i ti ye ni ka a tu i mi fɔ kpe nnyɔ ɔ, na nzɛn wɔ nya de má i, má a bu i wura am nu.

eng: because you know that someone like this is twisted and sinful—so they condemn themselves.
local: Si ka nyini sɔnɔ n sɔ akete keree­wa kpa n lɛɛ, na i yo satiɛ bo yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii si ka i ti satiɛ wɔ.

eng: When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, try to come to me in Nicopolis, because Ive decided to spend the winter there.
local: M súnma Aratɛma, wara Tikikusi u dɔ, nzɛn bu be baari, boro u nyi na m ni wɔrɔ sa nu Nikopoli miɛ kpiri n nu. M koro ka m wo dɔ fufuku saŋga a.

eng: Help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey with enthusiasm so that they wont need anything.
local: Zena bo i ti jɔrɛ­di­fɔ ni, i ni Apolosi, bu ba ŋa bu yasu, boro u nyi na a buka bu, na má deke be kpiɛ bu su.

eng: But our people should also learn to devote themselves to doing good in order to meet pressing needs so they arent unproductive.
local: I ti ye ni ka a ma ya minɛm gusu sɔnsɔn kabo bu boro bu nyi suyo sa bo i ti kpa, na bu buka minɛm ni deke bo i ti bu miɛɛ­wam ni, adena má ya minɛm ni wo wo ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Everyone with me greets you; greet those who love us faithfully. Grace be with all of you.
local: M labari n kɛrɛ lɛɛ. Minɛm bo bu wo m dɔ n kɛrɛ bisa u. Bisa ya danfom kɛrɛ bo bu yo Yesu yarada ni. Nyɛmɛ buka am kɛrɛ.

eng: From Paul, who is a prisoner for the cause of Christ Jesus, and our brother Timothy. To Philemon our dearly loved coworker.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu bo Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti na kisa m wo dan­saraka nu ni, ni ya niwa Timoti sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n a. Wɔrɔ Filemon bo ya koro u na ya ni wɔrɔ saŋga yo juma ni, ya bisa u dɔŋgu.

eng: Apphia our sister, Archippus our fellow soldier, and the church that meets in your house.
local: Ya kaaki bisa Kristo jama n bo bu tiɛn bu nɔɔ u dika ni, ni ya niwa baa­bara Afiya, ni ya niwa Akipusi bo ya ni yiri kɛrɛ ti ka soja ni, Yesu dɛɛ atin n su ni.

eng: May the grace and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
local: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am, na bu ma am ahoreda.

eng: Philemon, I thank my God every time I mention you in my prayers.
local: M niwa Filemon, saŋga kɛrɛ bo m sere Nyɛmɛ, m wara fi má u sa, m da i asi ma u wɔ.

eng: because Ive heard of your love and faithfulness, which you have both for the Lord Jesus and for all Gods people.
local: Dama ma ti kabo a yo ya Mibiɛ Yesu yarada ni, ni kabo a koro Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ ni.

eng: I pray that your partnership in the faith might become effective by an understanding of all that is good among us in Christ.
local: M kaaki sere Nyɛmɛ ka i ma wɔrɔ ni bo ba yo yarada na am saŋga su Nyɛmɛ ni la wo fiɛ, nyini ma a si nahɔrɛ su kabo Nyɛmɛ afa deke kpa ama ya, Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: I have great joy and encouragement because of your love, since the hearts of Gods people are refreshed by your actions, my brother.
local: M niwa, koro n bo a bita i ni, wɔ ma m ahore ajɔ agaya, na wɔ kaaki ama m ahoresi, dama wɔrɔ dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ minɛm anya ahoresi.

eng: Therefore, though I have enough confidence in Christ to command you to do the right thing.
local: Tɔ, kabo Kristo dɛɛ ti na a ti m niwa ni, m la ahoresi, na m la atin bo m koro se u ka a yo deke bo i ti ye ni ka a yo i.

eng: I would rather appeal to you through love. I, Paul—an old man, and now also a prisoner for Christ Jesus.
local: Ama, m koro u n dɛɛ ti, ye m susere u a, ka a buka m, haari ni bo m jina Yesu Kristo dɛɛ jaa nu n ni, na i dɛɛ ti na m kaaki ti dan­saraka kisa ni.

eng: appeal to you for my child Onesimus. I became his father in the faith during my time in prison.
local: Deke bo i ti na m susere u n la ka, m susere u ma m wa Onisimɔ n wɔ, bo Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti na wɔ kaaki m wa dan­saraka nu n ni.

eng: He was useless to you before, but now he is useful to both of us.
local: Saŋga be, i yo kun má kpɛrɛ be ma má u, ama, kisa, i kun kpɛrɛ ma wɔrɔ ni mini kɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: Im sending him back to you, which is like sending you my own heart.
local: Yiri bo m koro i ni m ahore­mbaa kun ni, ye m suma i ba u dɔ n a.

eng: I considered keeping him with me so that he might serve me in your place during my time in prison because of the gospel.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kaan­wa n dɛɛ ti na m wo dan­saraka n nu ni, má m koro ka i wo m dɔ fɛn na i buka m, kabo má wɔrɔ wo fɛn na a buka m ni.

eng: However, I didnt want to do anything without your consent so that your act of kindness would occur willingly and not under pressure.
local: Ama, m koro má ka m yo deke be bo a soro má su, ama, m koro ka wɔrɔ mmɔɔ ni u tii buka m u koroya­nu, má ka i yo ka m miɛ u wɔ.

eng: Maybe this is the reason that Onesimus was separated from you for a while so that you might have him back forever.
local: Beni, Onisimɔ fiteeri u saa nu ni, wɔ cɛ kaan, adena i ba kaaki ba wo u dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: no longer as a slave but more than a slave—that is, as a dearly loved brother. He is especially a dearly loved brother to me. How much more can he become a brother to you, personally and spiritually in the Lord!.
local: Kisa, i ti má u akɔɔ ŋgumi kun, ama, i tara akɔɔ ɔ. I ti ya niwa koroo­wa a. Koro bo m fa koro i ni, wɔrɔ koro i tara sɔ. I ti u akɔɔ ɔ, ama, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, i kaaki ti u niwa a.

eng: So, if you really consider me a partner, welcome Onesimus as if you were welcoming me.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn a fa i ka m ti u mata­fɔ, de i kabo má a de m ni.

eng: If he has harmed you in any way or owes you money, charge it to my account.
local: Nzɛn wɔ do u ŋgasi be, wara i bita u kaari, ma nyini kaari n yo m dɛɛ kaari.

eng: I, Paul, will pay it back to you . Of course, I wont mention that you owe me your life.
local: Mini Pɔɔlu ni m tii sufa m saa sukɛrɛ ahin jɔrɛ n a, ka m tɔ́ kaari n kɛrɛ bo i bita i n ma u. M kaaki koro se u ka, m kaari su u su, fa kɔ u ŋgɔɔ bo a nyaari i Yesu dɔ ni.

eng: Yes, brother, I want this favor from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
local: Nyini ti, m niwa, m koro ka a yo ahin sakpa n ma m, Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti. Kabo ya kɛrɛ ti nibaa Kristo dɛɛ ti ni, da m ahore.

eng: Im writing to you, confident of your obedience and knowing that you will do more than what I ask.
local: M la ahoresi ka a dé m nɔaniɛ ni, nyini ti ye m sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n a. Na kabo ma se ni, m si ka a yó i kɔ tara sɔ.

eng: Also, one more thing—prepare a guest room for me. I hope that I will be released from prison to be with you because of your prayers.
local: Bo i buka su la ka, m koro ka a yo sɔɔ dɔŋgu se m, dama m la tama ka Nyɛmɛ bá de am sere, na i má m kaaki ba am dɔ.

eng: Epaphras, who is in prison with me for the cause of Christ Jesus, greets you.
local: M lamɔkɔ Ipafarasi bo m ni yiri wo dan­saraka nu, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti ni, i ŋa m bisa u dɔŋgu.

eng: as well as my coworkers Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke.
local: Marako, ni Arisitakusi, ni Demasi, ni Luka kɛrɛ gusu ŋa m bisa u dɔŋgu, m ni bɛrɛ saŋga yo juma.

eng: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
local: Ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo buka am kɛrɛ.

eng: In the past, God spoke through the prophets to our ancestors in many times and many ways.
local: Dawa saŋga Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ maari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm wɔ, saŋga barasu ni atin barasu barasum, na bu sukan i kere ya nam.

eng: In these final days, though, he spoke to us through a Son. God made his Son the heir of everything and created the world through him.
local: Ama, ahin awieeri su saŋga n bo ya wo nu ni, Nyɛmɛ afa i Wa n su ye i fa i nɔaniɛ n jɔjɔ ya sɔ a. Nyɛmɛ maari yiri yiiri durunya n a. Na wɔ se ka, awieeri su, deke n kɛrɛ bá yo i dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: The Son is the light of Gods glory and the imprint of Gods being. He maintains everything with his powerful message. After he carried out the cleansing of people from their sins, he sat down at the right side of the highest majesty.
local: I jirima woo­wa wein ni kere kabo Nyɛmɛ ti a. Kabo Nyɛmɛ ti ni, i woo­wa ti sɔ ɔ. I nɔaniɛ n bo i la yiko ni, i fa nyini bita durunya n kɛrɛ a. I faari i ŋu fa yoori yoo­wa, adena Nyɛmɛ fa adamande satiɛ ce i. Nyini dɛɛ sin, na wɔ nya akɔ atana aŋgoro, Micɛra Kpiri n saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

eng: And the Son became so much greater than the other messengers, such as angels, that he received a more important title than theirs.
local: Nyɛmɛ maari i jinaa­biri bambaka bo i tara i mɛrɛkɛm dɛɛ, na wɔ kaaki ama i duma bo i tara bu dɛɛ ni.

eng: After all, when did God ever say to any of the angels: You are my Son. Today I have become your Father? Or, even, I will be his Father, and he will be my Son? .
local: Dama kabo Nyɛmɛ seeri i Wa n ni, ka, Wɔrɔ ti m Wa a, nyuma, m káaki u Si, wɔ se má i mɛrɛkɛ n be sɔ. Bekun, i ŋa M yó u Si, na u gusu yó m Wa.

eng: But then, when he brought his firstborn into the world, he said, All of Gods angels must worship him. .
local: Sasu bekun, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ yo ŋa i sunma i Wa kun n kpein n durunya n nu ni, na wɔ se ka, Ma Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm kɛrɛ su i.

eng: He talks about the angels: Hes the one who uses the spirits for his messengers and who uses flames of fire as ministers. .
local: Fa kɔ kabo Nyɛmɛ jɔjɔɔri i mɛrɛkɛm jɔrɛ ni, i ŋa, Mini, m ma m mɛrɛkɛm yo bu juma kabo aŋuma, ni sin yo bu juma barasu n wɔ.

eng: But he says to his Son, God, your throne is forever and your kingdoms scepter is a rod of justice. .
local: Ama, kabo Nyɛmɛ jɔjɔɔri i Wa n dɔ ni, i ŋa, Wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ a, u fɛmɛya biɛ n la má awieeri. A bita u fɛmɛya n ni nahɔrɛ atin su wɔ.

eng: You loved righteousness and hated lawless behavior. That is why God, your God, has anointed you more than your companions with the oil of joy. .
local: A koro sa bo i kɔ atin su wɔ, na a koro má sa bo i kɔ má atin su a. Nyini dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ ayi u a, ye wɔ ma u ahoreda bo i tara u beŋgum kɛrɛ n a.

eng: And he says, You, Lord, laid the earths foundations in the beginning, and the heavens are made by your hands. .
local: Na Nyɛmɛ akaaki ase i Wa n ka, Ya Mibiɛ, bumboori ni, wɔrɔ yiiri asiɛŋgu a. U sa bo a yoori i dɛɛ ti, ye aŋgoro wo bɛrɛ a.

eng: They will pass away, but you remain. They will all wear out like old clothes. .
local: I kɛrɛ bá ka ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, ama, wɔrɔ wo bɛrɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ. Aŋgoro n ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ yó dawa kabo sutura yo dawa barasu n wɔ.

eng: You will fold them up like a coat. They will be changed like a person changes clothes, but you stay the same, and the years of your life wont come to an end. .
local: A pɔ́ɔndi bu ka sanvuin bo wɔ do wɔ, na a bá kaaki i fɔfɔrɛ. Ama, wɔrɔ kaaki má fieo, na u afɔ la má awieeri.

eng: When has he ever said to any of the angels, Sit at my right side until I put your enemies under your feet like a footstool? .
local: Na Nyɛmɛ akaaki ase i Wa n ka, Ba tana m saa fɔmbɔrɔ su fɛn, haari m bá ma a kunma u kpɔ­fɔm na a nana bu kɛrɛ ŋu. Kabo i seeri sɔ ni, wɔ nya ase má i mɛrɛkɛ n be sɔ.

eng: Arent all the angels ministering spirits who are sent to serve those who are going to inherit salvation?.
local: Ye i mɛrɛkɛm ti nzum wɔ? Bu ti awiɛwiɛm bo bu su Nyɛmɛ wɔ, na i sunma bu na bu kɔ buka minɛm bo bu bá nya tii­de ni.

eng: This is why its necessary for us to pay more attention to what we have heard, or else we may drift away from it.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo daka ka ya bita Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo ya du mɔ ati i nyunyumi, adena má ya mini.

eng: If the message that was spoken by angels was reliable, and every offense and act of disobedience received an appropriate consequence.
local: Ya si ka nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ yo fa maari i mɛrɛkɛm, na bu kan kereeri ya nam ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, na minɛm bo bu tiiri i na bu keteeri, na ba de má i ni, bɛrɛ nyaari sui­cin n a.

eng: how will we escape if we ignore such a great salvation? It was first announced through the Lord, and then it was confirmed by those who heard him.
local: Nzɛn yɛrɛ gusu keteeri nyini nɔaniɛ kpiri n bo i kere kabo Nyɛmɛ de ya tii ji ni, ya yo sɛ koro fite nu na ya nya má sui­cin a? Ahin tii­de n bo ya kan i jɔrɛ ni, Mibiɛ n ni i tii du mɔ kan kereeri minɛm a, na bo bu tiiri i n adi i daani, ase ya ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: God also vouched for their message with signs, amazing things, various miracles, and gifts from the Holy Spirit, which were handed out the way he wanted.
local: Nyini saŋga n na Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ adi i daani abuka su, yiri la bo i yoori alekutura sa barasu barasum kereeri i yiko, na i maari minɛm nyaari i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aceerim kabo i koro barasu ni.

eng: God didnt put the world that is coming under the angels control.
local: Nahɔrɛ, Nyɛmɛ afa má durunya fɔfɔrɛ n bo ya kan i jɔrɛ, na i suba ni, awura má i mɛrɛkɛm saa nu.

eng: Instead, someone declared somewhere, What is humanity that you think about them? Or what are human beings that you care about them? .
local: Ama, be akɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n awɔrɛ be nu, ka, Ya Micɛra Nyɛmɛ, adamande gusu ti nzu wɔ, ye u cɛcɛ wo i nu a? Adamande wa ti nzu wɔ, ye a niɛ i dika?.

eng: For a while you made them lower than angels. You crowned human beings with glory and honor. .
local: Kabo a yoori i ni, a ma i jinaa­biri kpiɛɛri su saŋga kaan afiɛn ni u mɛrɛkɛm dɛɛ, na a nya awura i kpiri na a ma i jirima bambaka.

eng: You put everything under their control. When he puts everything under their control, he doesnt leave anything out of control. But right now, we dont see everything under their control yet.
local: na a fa deke kɛrɛ awura i saa nu. Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu kabo Nyɛmɛ afa deke n kɛrɛ awura adamande saa nu ka i niɛ i dika ni, nyini kere ka deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i ti má i dɛɛ. Ama, kisa, kabo i ti ni, ya nya ŋu má i sɔ.

eng: However, we do see the one who was made lower in order than the angels for a little while—its Jesus! Hes the one who is now crowned with glory and honor because of the suffering of his death. He suffered death so that he could taste death for everyone through Gods grace.
local: Ama, ya ŋu Yesu kabo Nyɛmɛ yoori i ni, i ma i jinaa­biri kpiɛɛri su saŋga kaan afiɛn ni i mɛrɛkɛm dɛɛ wɔ. Na i dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n nu na wɔ wu sɔnɔ kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti. Kisa, wahara n bo i diiri i na i wu n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ awura i kpiri, na wɔ ma i jirima bambaka.

eng: It was appropriate for God, for whom and through whom everything exists, to use experiences of suffering to make perfect the pioneer of salvation. This salvation belongs to many sons and daughters whom hes leading to glory.
local: Nyɛmɛ yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a, yiri la deke n kɛrɛ yiko a. I tiiri ye ni ka Nyɛmɛ kpini atin bo i minɛ beberebe ba nya i jirima woo­wa n be. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i ma Yesu ba diiri wahara n a, adena i yo minɛm mɔ­fɔ bo i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni, dama yiri ma bu nya tii­de a.

eng: This is because the one who makes people holy and the people who are being made holy all come from one source. That is why Jesus isnt ashamed to call them brothers and sisters when he says.
local: Yiri Yesu bo i yo minɛm casi ni, i ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo bu casi n saŋga Si wɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, i la má i nyinsɛ na i fere bu ka i ninbaam n a.

eng: I will publicly announce your name to my brothers and sisters. I will praise you in the middle of the assembly. .
local: Na wɔ se Nyɛmɛ ka, M bó u duma kere m ninbaam. Na saŋga kɛrɛ bo bu tiɛɛnri bu nɔɔ, m dí jue kansi u duma.

eng: He also says, I will rely on him. And also, Here I am with the children whom God has given to me. .
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase ka, Mini, m daafi Nyɛmɛ ŋu, Na wɔ kaaki ase bekun ka, Mini ni nbaam bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama m ni, yɛrɛ yaa.

eng: Therefore, since the children share in flesh and blood, he also shared the same things in the same way. He did this to destroy the one who holds the power over death—the devil—by dying.
local: Kabo nbaam n ti adamande ni, nyini dɛɛ ti ye yiri mmɔɔ kaakiiri adamande a, adena i wu na i yo sɔnɔ bo i la yiwee yiko ni ŋgbɛɛn, yiri la Sitana a.

eng: He set free those who were held in slavery their entire lives by their fear of death.
local: na i de minɛm tii ji, bɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo bu ŋgɔɔ su na bu soro yiwee n dɛɛ ti, na ba kaaki akɔɔm ni.

eng: Of course, he isnt trying to help angels, but rather hes helping Abrahams descendants.
local: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka, má mɛrɛkɛm ye i buka deeri bu tii a, ama, Abraham busu nu­fɔm ye i buka deeri bu tii a.

eng: Therefore, he had to be made like his brothers and sisters in every way. This was so that he could become a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating to God, in order to wipe away the sins of the people.
local: Nyini kere ka, wɔ yo daka ka i woo­wa ti ka i ninbaam i deke n kɛrɛ nu wɔ, adena i yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn bo i la dabuŋu na i si aŋunfɔ fa kɔ i nyɛmɛsu n nu, na i fa i ŋu yo yoo­wa ma minɛm na Nyɛmɛ fa bu satiɛ ce bu.

eng: Hes able to help those who are being tempted, since he himself experienced suffering when he was tempted.
local: Yiri ni i tii yo diiri wahara, na ba sɔ i ŋgɛɛ na bu niɛ ni. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i koro buka minɛm bo bu sɔ bu ŋgɛɛ na bu niɛ n a.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters who are partners in the heavenly calling, think about Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession.
local: M ninbaam, ambɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi am ni yɛrɛ ka mata­fɔ casim ni, am fa am sunsunni se Yesu ŋu, yiri bo Nyɛmɛ asunma i na i ti ya sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn n ni. Ya su yiri su a, ye ya di i daani kere minɛm a.

eng: Jesus was faithful to the one who appointed him just like Moses was faithful in Gods house.
local: I yoori i juma n bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri i ka i yo i n ni nahɔrɛ su wɔ, kabo Moyisi mmɔɔ yo yoori i juma kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ awuru n nu, bukaari Nyɛmɛ minɛm ni nahɔrɛ su n wɔ.

eng: But he deserves greater glory than Moses in the same way that the builder of the house deserves more honor than the house itself.
local: Kabo sɔnɔ bo i ta awuru n la jirima tara awuru n ni i tii ni, sɔ ye Yesu la jirima tara Moyisi a.

eng: Every house is built by someone, but God is the builder of everything.
local: Awuru kɛrɛ la i ta­fɔ, ama, Nyɛmɛ ti deke n kɛrɛ tii a.

eng: Moses was faithful in all Gods house as a servant in order to affirm the things that would be spoken later.
local: Moyisi yo laari dabuŋu yoori i juma kɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ awuru n nu, bukaari Nyɛmɛ minɛm n ka amusui­su­fɔ ɔ, na wɔ kere bu deke bo Nyɛmɛ koroori ka i bá se bu ni.

eng: But Jesus was faithful over Gods house as a Son. We are his house if we hold on to the confidence and the pride that our hope gives us.
local: Ama, Kristo yiri ti Nyɛmɛ Wa a, yiri la dabuŋu Nyɛmɛ awuru n nu koro bita Nyɛmɛ minɛm a. Nzɛn ya bita ya tama n ni ahoresi, yɛrɛ ti i awuru n nu­fɔm a.

eng: So, as the Holy Spirit says, Today, if you hear his voice, .
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n se ni, Nyuma, nzɛn am tiiri kabo Nyɛmɛ suse.

eng: dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion, on the day when they tested me in the desert. .
local: má am ma am sui yo tan­tanantan, kabo am nam yo yoori bu sui tan­tanantan na bu yoori Nyɛmɛ na bu niɛ ni, saŋga bo bu wo boro kpantain n nu na bu keteeri i nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: That is where your ancestors challenged and tested me, though they had seen my work for forty years. .
local: Na Nyɛmɛ ase ka, Dɔ ye am nam yo yoori m na bu niɛ a, haari ni bo bu ŋuuri sam bo m yoori bu, nyini afɔ abura­na n kɛrɛ nu ni, bu yoori m wɔ na ba niɛ.

eng: So I was angry with them. I said, Their hearts always go off course, and they dont know my ways. .
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye m faari yaa bu ŋu a, ye m seeri ka, ba mini atin n a, dama ba yo má m sa koroo­wam ni.

eng: Because of my anger I swore: They will never enter my rest! .
local: Na yaa ati m, na ma tan ndiɛ ka, bu ny má atin wura má asiɛn n bo má m ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu ni.

eng: Watch out, brothers and sisters, so that none of you have an evil, unfaithful heart that abandons the living God.
local: M ninbaam, am si am ŋu sa na má am be ahore­mbaa yo kekereke, na i kaaki i sin bo Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ atin ni.

eng: Instead, encourage each other every day, as long as its called today, so that none of you become insensitive to God because of sins deception.
local: Cɛɛn kɛrɛ bo ale acin na bu fere i ka, Nyuma, am sutu am beŋgu fɔ, na má am ma be dadaka am na am yo satiɛ na am sui yo tan­tanantan.

eng: We are partners with Christ, but only if we hold on to the confidence we had in the beginning until the end.
local: Nzɛn ya koro bitaari bumboori su nɔaniɛ n kenken haari kɔ juuri i awieeri, nyini n kere ka ya ni Kristo saŋga yo juma kun wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: When it says, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts as they did in the rebellion. .
local: Kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, ka, Nyuma, nzɛn am tiiri kabo Nyɛmɛ suse, má am yo am sui tan­tanantan, kabo am nam yo yoori bu sui tan­tanantan.

eng: Who was it who rebelled when they heard his voice? Wasnt it all of those who were brought out of Egypt by Moses?.
local: Ŋmam yo tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ye bu yoori bu sui tan­tanantan n a? Má minɛm kɛrɛ bo Moyisi yo yiiri bu fiteeri Ijipiti asiɛn n su ni, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: And with whom was God angry for forty years? Wasnt it with the ones who sinned, whose bodies fell in the desert?.
local: Ye ŋmam ye Nyɛmɛ yo faari yaa bu ŋu afɔ abura­na n a? Má bo bu yo yoori satiɛ na bu wuuri boro kpantain n nu ni?.

eng: And against whom did he swear that they would never enter his rest, if not against the ones who were disobedient?.
local: Ŋmam ye Nyɛmɛ yo taanri ndiɛ ka, Bu nya má atin wura má asiɛn n bo má m ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu n a? Má bɛrɛ n bo bu yo keteeri i nɔaniɛ ni, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: We see that they couldnt enter because of their lack of faith.
local: Deke bo i ti na ya ŋu ka ba wura má asiɛn n su na bu nya ŋumi­yi n la ka, ba yo má i yarada.

eng: Therefore, since the promise that we can enter into rest is still open, lets be careful so that none of you will appear to miss it.
local: Nyini ti, am ma ya si ya ŋu sa, na má be fun nyini ŋumi­yi n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma ya na atin toko wo bɛrɛ ka ya nya i ni.

eng: We also had the good news preached to us, just as the Israelites did. However, the message they heard didnt help them because they werent united in faith with the ones who listened to it.
local: Dama yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ati labari kpa n kabo bɛrɛ mmɔɔ yo tiiri i ni, ama, bɛrɛ ayo má i yarada, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ kun má kpɛrɛ ama má bu n a.

eng: We who have faith are entering the rest. As God said, And because of my anger I swore: They will never enter into my rest! And yet Gods works were completed at the foundation of the world.
local: Ama, yɛrɛ bo ya yo Yesu yarada ni, ya nya nyini ŋumi­yi n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma i minɛm ni. I ti kabo Nyɛmɛ seeri n wɔ, i ŋa, Na yaa ati m, na ma tan ndiɛ ka, bu nyá má atin wura má asiɛn n bo má m ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu ni. Fite saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri durunya n kɛrɛ wieeri ni, i juma awieeri lɛɛ.

eng: Then somewhere he said this about the seventh day of creation: God rested on the seventh day from all his works. .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ le­so cɛɛn n jɔrɛ, Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n awɔrɛ be nu, ka, Nyɛmɛ yo yiiri i ŋumi, i juma­yoo­wa kɛrɛ nu wɔ le­so cɛɛn ni.

eng: But again, in the passage above, God said, They will never enter my rest! .
local: Ya kaaki aŋu ka wɔ jɔjɔ nyini jɔrɛ n sɔ ka, bu nya má atin wura má asiɛn n bo m ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu.

eng: Therefore, its left open for some to enter it, and the ones who had the good news preached to them before didnt enter because of disobedience.
local: Bo bu du mɔ tiiri labari kpa n ni, ba wura má nyini ŋumi­yi n nu, dama ba yo má Nyɛmɛ yarada. Ama, atin toko wo bɛrɛ bo nbem nya nyini ŋumi­yi ni.

eng: Just as it says in the passage above, God designates a certain day as today, when he says through David much later, Today, if you hear his voice, dont have stubborn hearts. .
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ ma wɔ cɛ ka, na wɔ kpama cɛɛn fɔfɔrɛ be, na wɔ fere i ka nyuma, na wɔ ma Fɛmɛ Davidi ajɔjɔ nyini cɛɛn n jɔrɛ, kabo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyuma, nzɛn am tiiri kabo Nyɛmɛ suse, má am ma am sui yo tan­tanantan.

eng: If Joshua gave the Israelites rest, God wouldnt have spoken about another day later on.
local: Nzɛn má Yosuwa koro maari Yisareli­fɔm nyaari atin wuraari asiɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ ma bu nya ŋumi­yi n nu, má Nyɛmɛ ayo akaaki ase má ka cɛɛn n be wo bɛrɛ bo i ma i minɛm nya ŋumi­yi kun.

eng: So you see that a sabbath rest is left open for Gods people.
local: Ama, kabo i ti ni, ŋumi­yi cɛɛn wo bɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ bá fa ma i minɛm.

eng: The one who entered Gods rest also rested from his works, just as God rested from his own.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nya ŋumi­yi Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i mi yi i ŋumi fa kɔ i juma su, kabo Nyɛmɛ yo yoori i juma wieeri na i yiiri i ŋumi n wɔ.

eng: Therefore, lets make every effort to enter that rest so that no one will fall by following the same example of disobedience.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am ma ya boro ya nyi, na ya nya ŋumi­yi n bo Nyɛmɛ bá fa ma ya ni. Ama, má am ma ya be kete Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, adena má ya fun ŋumi­yi ni, kabo Yisareli­fɔm nbem yo keteeri na bu fuunri i ni.

eng: because Gods word is living, active, and sharper than any two-edged sword. It penetrates to the point that it separates the soul from the spirit and the joints from the marrow. Its able to judge the hearts thoughts and intentions.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n la ŋgɔɔ na i la faŋga. I kaaki wo na tara takobe, i kpiɛ wura sɔnɔ ahore­mbaa su wɔ, ni i ŋumi, ni i awiɛwiɛ nu, na i kpiɛ kpaaki buwe sasuu­biri, na i kɔ kpura bɔrɔkɔ nu, na i fiiti sɔnɔ sunsunni nu, na i si deke bo i ahore­mbaa koro, deke bo i ti kpa, ni bo i ti má kpa.

eng: No creature is hidden from it, but rather everything is naked and exposed to the eyes of the one to whom we have to give an answer.
local: Deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro fiɛ Nyɛmɛ, dama deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi i na i nyu­mbaa ŋu má i. Yiri ye ya kɛrɛ bá kan ya sa­yoo­wa kere i a.

eng: Also, lets hold on to the confession since we have a great high priest who passed through the heavens, who is Jesus, Gods Son.
local: Kisa, na ya la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn bo i la má aŋusu, na wɔ kɔ aŋgoro, na wɔ wura Nyɛmɛ dika n nu ni, yiri la Nyɛmɛ Wa Yesu, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye am ma ya bita i dɛɛ atin n bo ya ŋa ya yo i yarada n kenken.

eng: because we dont have a high priest who cant sympathize with our weaknesses but instead one who was tempted in every way that we are, except without sin.
local: Ya dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn n ti má be bo i koro si má ya aŋunfɔ ɔ. I koro si ya aŋunfɔ fa kɔ saŋga bo ya faŋga akpiɛ su, ni i dɛɛ atin su, dama ŋgɛɛ barasu barasu kɛrɛ bo bu fa sɔ ya ni, bu yo fa sɔɔri yiri mmɔɔ sɔ ɔ, ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ ni, wɔ yo má satiɛ be.

eng: Finally, lets draw near to the throne of favor with confidence so that we can receive mercy and find grace when we need help.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am ma ya fa ahoresi, na ya cin dodo Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya tanaa­biri ni, yiri ti ahɔrɔma­fɔ a, adena i si ya aŋunfɔ, na saŋga kɛrɛ bo ya ba koro buka, na i buka ya.

eng: Every high priest is taken from the people and put in charge of things that relate to God for their sake, in order to offer gifts and sacrifices for sins.
local: Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn kɛrɛ, bo Nyɛmɛ yo yiiri i minɛm n nu ka i yo juma ni, i dɛɛ la ka, i jina minɛm dɛɛ jinaa­biri Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na i de bu aceerim ni yoo­wam na i fa ma Nyɛmɛ fa kɔ minɛm satiɛ bo ba yo i n dɛɛ ti.

eng: The high priest is able to deal gently with the ignorant and those who are misled since he himself is prone to weakness.
local: Yiri mmɔɔ ni i tii koro si minɛm aŋunfɔ bo ba si má na ba mini atin ayo satiɛ ni, dama yiri mmɔɔ faŋga akpiɛ su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: Because of his weakness, he must offer sacrifices for his own sins as well as for the people.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo daka ka i du mɔ yo yoo­wa ma i ŋu fa kɔ i ŋgasi bo wɔ do i ka, ye i nya koro yo yoo­wa ma minɛm kɛrɛ ŋgasi n a.

eng: No one takes this honor for themselves but takes it only when they are called by God, just like Aaron.
local: Be koro wura má i ŋu kpiri na i se ka i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn, má ka Nyɛmɛ ni i tii ayi i mi, kabo i yo yiiri Aarɔn ni.

eng: In the same way Christ also didnt promote himself to become high priest. Instead, it was the one who said to him, You are my Son. Today I have become your Father .
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, Kristo gusu awura má i ŋu kpiri ayo má i ŋu Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn, se Nyɛmɛ yiiri i a, na wɔ se i ka, Wɔrɔ ti m Wa a, nyuma, m káaki u Si.

eng: as he also says in another place, You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Ba kaaki akɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n awɔrɛ be nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ, kabo Mɛlɛkisidɛki busu nu ti n a, a wo bɛrɛ sɔ ɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, be de má u.

eng: During his days on earth, Christ offered prayers and requests with loud cries and tears as his sacrifices to the one who was able to save him from death. He was heard because of his godly devotion.
local: Saŋga bo Yesu wo durunya n nu fɛn ni, i yo sereeri Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro su, na wɔ bo afere Nyɛmɛ na wɔ sun, ka, i de i fite yiwee n nu. I yo yoori i ŋu kaan, na i soro Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ ade i sere.

eng: Although he was a Son, he learned obedience from what he suffered.
local: Haari ni bo i ti Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, Nyɛmɛ maari i diiri wahara, adena i si kabo i deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni.

eng: After he had been made perfect, he became the source of eternal salvation for everyone who obeys him.
local: Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ ma i tiiri kabo i koro ka i ti ni, ye wɔ ma sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i deeri i nɔaniɛ n nya tii­de bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: He was appointed by God to be a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ yiiri i ka i yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn, kabo Mɛlɛkisidɛki yo ti barasu n a.

eng: We have a lot to say about this topic, and its difficult to explain, because you have been lazy and you havent been listening.
local: Má ya la deke dɔŋgu bo ya kan kere am fa kɔ jɔrɛ bo ya jɔjɔ i n su, ama, i ti kekereke kabo ya kan teke am nyi, dama am koro ti má i bu ndende.

eng: Although you should have been teachers by now, you need someone to teach you an introduction to the basics about Gods message. You have come to the place where you need milk instead of solid food.
local: Saŋga bo am boori i bu ka am su Nyɛmɛ ni, am acɛ nu, má am aju bo am sukere minɛm Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, ama, am toko tana koro ka bu kere am dekem bo ba du mɔ afa akere am, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n su. Am toko nu nyufini wɔ, am nya koro di má diire nɔnɔɔri.

eng: Everyone who lives on milk is not used to the word of righteousness, because they are babies.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i nu nyufini toko ti baa­kan wɔ lɛɛ, i nya koro si má deke bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: But solid food is for the mature, whose senses are trained by practice to distinguish between good and evil.
local: Minɛm bo bu nyunu ati Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ atin n su, bɛrɛ di diire nɔnɔɔri a. Ba sɔnsɔn Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, na bu su i su kabo i kere ni. Nyini ti ye bu la alecira koro si deke bo i ti kpa, ni bo i ti má kpa n a.

eng: So lets press on to maturity, by moving on from the basics about Christs word. Lets not lay a foundation of turning away from dead works, of faith in God.
local: Nyini ti, má am ma ya ka Kristo ciciri su nɔaniɛ keree­wam ŋgumi su, ama, am ma ya boro ya nyi na ya kɔ mɔ. I ti má ye ni ka ya sukan nyini jɔrɛm ni saŋga kɛrɛ. Nyini jɔrɛm ni la kaaki bu wo, fa kɔ bu satiɛ su na bu yo Nyɛmɛ yarada ni.

eng: of teaching about ritual ways to wash with water, laying on of hands, the resurrection from the dead, and eternal judgment—all over again.
local: ni kabo bu biɛ bu Nyɛmɛ nzue barasu, na bu fa bu sa nana yarada­yo­fɔm ŋu ka Nyɛmɛ yo bu kisi, ni kabo minɛ ba wu na bu teŋge bu yiwee nu ni, ni kabo Nyɛmɛ ba di minɛ jɔrɛ na be koro kaaki má i kun ni.

eng: Were going to press on, if God allows it.
local: Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ soroori su, ya koro kɔ mɔ bo i tara sɔ.

eng: Because its impossible to restore people to changed hearts and lives who turn away once they have seen the light, tasted the heavenly gift, become partners with the Holy Spirit.
local: Minɛm bo ba yo asi ka Nyɛmɛ atin n ti wein, na ba kaaki bu sin abo i ni, bu yo sɛ koro kaaki ba Nyɛmɛ dɔ bekun a? Dama bu yo nyaari Nyɛmɛ aceerim wɔ, na ba de Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni.

eng: and tasted Gods good word and the powers of the coming age.
local: Ba yo asi kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ti kpa barasu, ni kabo durunya n bo i suba n yiko ti.

eng: They are crucifying Gods Son all over again and exposing him to public shame.
local: Ama, na ba kaaki bu sin abo i, i ti kekereke bo nyini minɛm ŋusu koro kaaki ba i dɔ bekun, dama bu sa­yoo­wa kere ka bu sukaaki kpama Nyɛmɛ Wa n baka jaanu n su bekun wɔ lɛɛ, na bu suwura i nyinsɛ minɛm nyunu.

eng: The ground receives a blessing from God when it drinks up the rain that regularly comes and falls on it and yields a useful crop for those people for whom it is being farmed.
local: Asiɛn kɛrɛ bo nzue tɔ saŋga kɛrɛ na minɛ dɔ su, na i yo diire nyunyumi, Nyɛmɛ wura nyini asiɛn n abaraka beberebe.

eng: But if it produces thorns and thistles, its useless and close to being cursed. It ends up being burned.
local: Ama, asiɛn bo buwe ni wura ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm fi su na i kunma kpɛrɛ, i cɛ má, Nyɛmɛ yó i ŋgbɛɛn, na i awieeri su, sin bá yara i kɛrɛ.

eng: But we are convinced of better things in your case, brothers and sisters, even though we are talking this way—things that go together with salvation.
local: Ama, m ninbaam, haari ni bo ya kan sɔ n kɛrɛ ni, ya la ahoresi fa kɔ ambɛrɛ dɔ, dama am bita atin kpa n wɔ, yiri la tii­de n bo am anya i ni.

eng: God isnt unjust so that he forgets your efforts and the love you have shown for his names sake when you served and continue to serve Gods holy people.
local: Nyɛmɛ ti kpa, i wara fi má sakpa n bo am yo i ni, kabo am abuka i minɛm, na am toko buka bu ni. Nyini kere ka am koro i a.

eng: But we desperately want each of you to show the same effort to make your hope sure until the end.
local: Ya koro ka am kɛrɛ boro am nyi haari kɔ ju awieeri, adena deke n bo am la i tama n yo yii­wa.

eng: This is so you wont be lazy but follow the example of the ones who inherit the promises through faith and patience.
local: Má am ma nviɛya ti am, ama, minɛm bo bu yo yarada na bu la sawɔrɛ, na bu nya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i fá ma bu ni, am su bu dɛɛ sa­yoo­wam su.

eng: When God gave Abraham his promise, he swore by himself since he couldnt swear by anyone greater.
local: Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ seeri Abraham ka i bá yo i kisi ni, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i tara yiri Nyɛmɛ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i faari i duma taanri ndiɛ maari i a.

eng: He said, I will certainly bless you and multiply your descendants. .
local: Na wɔ se i ka, M yó u kisi, na u busu nu­fɔm bá sunnu beberebe.

eng: So Abraham obtained the promise by showing patience.
local: Na Abraham amindi haari na wɔ ba anya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i má i ni.

eng: People pledge by something greater than themselves. A solemn pledge guarantees what they say and shuts down any argument.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ ŋa i tan ndiɛ, i tan deke bo i tara i mi n wɔ. Deke bo akuruwa nya yoori na bu taanri ndiɛ, wɔ kun akuruwa n kɛrɛ nɔɔ lɛɛ.

eng: When God wanted to further demonstrate to the heirs of the promise that his purpose doesnt change, he guaranteed it with a solemn pledge.
local: Nyɛmɛ yo koroori ka i kere i minɛm ka deke bo i bá ma bu ni, i wɔrɔ má i nɔɔ i busu, nyini ti ye i kaaki taanri ndiɛ n a.

eng: So these are two things that dont change, because its impossible for God to lie. He did this so that we, who have taken refuge in him, can be encouraged to grasp the hope that is lying in front of us.
local: Deke bo i seeri ka i yó i ni, ni i ndiɛ taan­wa n ti deke nnyɔ lɛɛ, bo bu kaaki má. Nyini kere ka Nyɛmɛ koro bu má kɔrɛ a. Nyini ti ye yɛrɛ bo ya ba i dɔ ka ya nya tii­de n la ahoresi bita ya tama n bo ya la i ni anyum­boro n a.

eng: This hope, which is a safe and secure anchor for our whole being, enters the sanctuary behind the curtain.
local: Tama n bo ya la i ni, i ti kabo ba kpama lee barasu n wɔ. Nyini ma ya ahore­mbaa abi kekereke, ye ya kaaki yaki má atin n bo ya bita i n a. Nyini tama n ti bo wɔ sin tani bambaka n bo i kanda n ŋu awura Sɔɔ Casi n nu a. Yiri bita ya a.

eng: Thats where Jesus went in advance and entered for us, since he became a high priest according to the order of Melchizedek.
local: Dɔ ye Yesu adu ya mɔ awura, ade ya dɛɛ jinaa­biri a. Yiri ti ya Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn n bo i la má de­fɔ fieo, kabo Mɛlɛkisidɛki busu nu n ti n a.

eng: This Melchizedek, who was king of Salem and priest of the Most High God, met Abraham as he returned from the defeat of the kings, and Melchizedek blessed him.
