local: Mɛlɛkisidɛki ni, yiri yo ti miɛ n bo bu fere i ka Salem dɛɛ fɛmɛ a, na i kaaki ti Micɛra Kpiri sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ. Saŋga bo Abraham kɔ kuunri luɛ ni nfɛmɛ nna nbem na i kunmaari bu na i sukaaki kɔ awuru ni, yiri Mɛlɛkisidɛki ni kpaari Abraham atin ye i yoori kisi gɔɔri i ŋu n a.

eng: Abraham gave a tenth of everything to him. His name means first king of righteousness, and then king of Salem, that is, king of peace.
local: Na Abraham ayi dekem kɛrɛ bo i kun luɛ n na i deeri i ni, deke buru kɛrɛ nu, na wɔ yi i kun kun ama Mɛlɛkisidɛki. I duma Mɛlɛkisidɛki n bu la ka, fɛmɛ bo i sa­yoo­wa kɔ atin su. I kaaki ti Salem fɛmɛ wɔ, i bu la ka, fɛmɛ bo i fa tana­laifiɛ ba.

eng: He is without father or mother or any family. He has no beginning or end of life, but hes like Gods Son and remains a priest for all time.
local: Mɛlɛkisidɛki ni, be si má i si, wara i ni, wara i busu nu­fɔm, wara kabo bu wuuri i, wara kabo i wuuri. I yo ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo wɔ la má de­fɔ, kabo Nyɛmɛ Wa n ti n wɔ.

eng: See how great Melchizedek was! Abraham, the father of the people, gave him a tenth of everything he captured.
local: Am niɛ kabo i yo ti sɔnɔ bambaka barasu a. Dama ya kɛrɛ na Abraham yo yiiri deke n kɛrɛ bo i kɔ kuunri luɛ n na i deeri i ni, deke buru kɛrɛ nu, i yiiri kun kun maari yiri Mɛlɛkisidɛki n wɔ.

eng: The descendants of Levi who receive the office of priest have a commandment under the Law to collect a tenth of everything from the people who are their brothers and sisters, though they also are descended from Abraham.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n kun bo i wo i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu n kere ka, deke buru kɛrɛ nu, bu yi kun kun fa ma Levi­fɔm, bu ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n dɛɛ ti, haari ni bo bu kɛrɛ ti minɛ kunm na bu ti Abraham busu nu­fɔm ni.

eng: But Melchizedek, who isnt related to them, received a tenth of everything from Abraham and blessed the one who had received the promises.
local: Mɛlɛkisidɛki yo fite má Levi­fɔm dɛɛ busu n nu, ama, Abraham yo yiiri deke n kɛrɛ buru nu kun kun maari i wɔ, na wɔ yo kisi agɔ Abraham ŋu, yiri Abraham ye Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i bá yo i kisi n a.

eng: Without question, the less important person is blessed by the more important person.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka, sɔnɔ bo i yo kisi gɔ minɛm ŋu n tara bo bu de kisi ni.

eng: In addition, in one case a tenth is received by people who die, and in the other case, the tenth is received by someone who continues to live, according to the record.
local: Levi­fɔm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm na minɛm yo yiiri bu deke kɛrɛ buru nu kun kun maari bu ni, bu ti yiwee­fɔm wɔ. Ama, Mɛlɛkisidɛki bo Abraham yiiri i deke kɛrɛ buru nu kun kun maari i ni, Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n kere ka i toko wo bɛrɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: It could be said that Levi, who received a tenth, paid a tenth through Abraham.
local: Nyini ti, saŋga bo Abraham yiiri deke n kɛrɛ nu buru kun kun maari Mɛlɛkisidɛki ni, ya koro se ka i wa Levi bo i ni i busu nu­fɔ yo deeri nyini n sɔ ni, bu minɛm gusu mmɔɔ atɔ lɛɛ.

eng: because he was still in his ancestors body when Abraham paid the tenth to Melchizedek.
local: Dama saŋga bo Mɛlɛkisidɛki ni Abraham yɛɛri ni, na Abraham nya wu má Levi bo i fite i busu nu ni, nyini ti, ya koro se ka Abraham deeri i wa Levi dɛɛ jinaa­biri, ye i yi maari Mɛlɛkisidɛki n a.

eng: So if perfection came through the levitical office of priest , why was there still a need to speak about raising up another priest according to the order of Melchizedek rather than one according to the order of Aaron?.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, i yo wo Levi­fɔm bo bu ti i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm n saa nu a. Ama, nzɛn má Levi busu nu­fɔm dɛɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm dɛɛ juma n koro yoori deke n kɛrɛ bo má i ti ye ni ka bu yo i ni, má Nyɛmɛ ayi má i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo i ti ka Mɛlɛkisidɛki. Má i kaaki yi i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo i fite Aarɔn dɛɛ Levi­fɔm busu n nu ba.

eng: When the order of the priest changes, there has to be a change in the Law as well.
local: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ akaaki anɔ fɔfɔrɛ fa kɔ i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm dɛɛ atin n su, wɔ yo daka ka i kaaki i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n gusu.

eng: The person we are talking about belongs to another tribe, and no one ever served at the altar from that tribe.
local: Kabo bu kɛrɛɛri Mibiɛ n ŋusu jɔrɛ ni, i fite anɔ barasu be nu a, nyini anɔ n nu­fɔ be ayo má Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ juma be fieo.

eng: Its clear that our Lord came from the tribe of Judah, but Moses never said anything about priests from that tribe.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ si ka i fite Yuda dɛɛ anɔ n nu wɔ, ama, fa kɔ Yuda dɛɛ anɔ n nu, ya ti má ka Moyisi kɛrɛɛri ka bu be ba yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ.

eng: And its even clearer if another priest appears who is like Melchizedek.
local: Deke bo i ma ya ti jɔrɛ n bu nyunyumi n tara nyini su n la ka, Nyɛmɛ ayi i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo i ti ka Mɛlɛkisidɛki ama ya.

eng: He has become a priest by the power of a life that cant be destroyed, rather than a legal requirement about physical descent.
local: Má i nam busu dɛɛ ti ye i la atin ka i yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ a, se i la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n dɛɛ ti, ye i la atin a.

eng: This is confirmed: You are a priest forever, according to the order of Melchizedek. .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo a la má de­fɔ fieo, kabo Mɛlɛkisidɛki busu n nu ti n a.

eng: On the one hand, an earlier command is set aside because it was weak and useless.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa dawa n la má kpɛrɛ ni faŋga, dama wɔ ma má deke n ati má kabo má Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti n a. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ acɛ i ase, ye wɔ ma ya tama n bo i tara nyini n a. Nyini tama n dɛɛ ti, ye ya koro cin dodo Nyɛmɛ n a.

eng: And this was not done without a solemn pledge! The others have become priests without a solemn pledge.
local: Nyini sin, na Nyɛmɛ atan ndiɛ. Ama, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ yo yiiri Levi­fɔm n ka bu yo i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm ni, wɔ yo atan má ndiɛ.

eng: but this priest was affirmed with a solemn pledge by the one who said, The Lord has made a solemn pledge and will not change his mind: You are a priest forever. .
local: Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri Yesu ka i yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ ni, i taanri ndiɛ wɔ, ka, Mini Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n atan ndiɛ, m wɔrɔ má m nɔɔ, wɔrɔ ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo a la má de­fɔ fieo a.

eng: As a result, Jesus has become the guarantee of a better covenant.
local: Nyɛmɛ taanri nyini ndiɛ n dɛɛ ti, nyini ma ya si ka Yesu jina sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n sin ka i ti kpa tara dawa n a.

eng: The others who became priests are numerous because death prevented them from continuing to serve.
local: Deke bo i ma i kaaki wo i ŋgumi n la ka, Levi­fɔm dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm yo wu wɔ, na bu nbem de bu jinaa­biri, nyini dɛɛ ti ye bu yo sunnu n a.

eng: In contrast, he holds the office of priest permanently because he continues to serve forever.
local: Ama, Yesu yiri, i ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ ti, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro de i jinaa­biri na i yo yoo­wa.

eng: This is why he can completely save those who are approaching God through him, because he always lives to speak with God for them.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti ye i koro de minɛm tii ji fieo n a, bɛrɛ bo i ma bu cin dodo Nyɛmɛ ni, dama yiri wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ de bu nyɛmɛsere, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

eng: Its appropriate for us to have this kind of high priest: holy, innocent, incorrupt, separate from sinners, and raised high above the heavens.
local: Nyini ti, Yesu ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn bo i koro yo deke n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo daka ka i yo ma ya nahɔrɛ su a, yiri ti bo i ti casi na i la má ŋgasi be a, i la má satiɛ be, i woo­wa wo bɛrɛ i ŋgumi ni satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ye Nyɛmɛ awura i kpiri atara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n a.

eng: He doesnt need to offer sacrifices every day like the other high priests, first for their own sins and then for the sins of the people. He did this once for all when he offered himself.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn nyiŋgam yo ti ni, yiri ti má sɔ. Bɛrɛ dɔ, ale kɛrɛ bo i cin, bu du mɔ yo yoo­wa ma bu ŋu fa kɔ satiɛ bo ba yo i wɔ ka, na bu nya ba yo ma minɛm satiɛ bo ba yo i. Ama, yiri yiri, yoo­wa kpe­kun n kpein ye i yoori i a, yiri la bo i faari i ŋu yoori i ni.

eng: The Law appoints people who are prone to weakness as high priests, but the content of the solemn pledge, which came after the Law, appointed a Son who has been made perfect forever.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ni, haari ni bo minɛm bo bu yiiri bu ka bu yo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛnm n akoro ayo má deke n kɛrɛ bo má wɔ yo daka ka bu yo i ni, bu yiiri bu wɔ. Ama, nyini nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɛɛ sin, na Nyɛmɛ afa i tiɛŋgu duma atan ndiɛ, na wɔ yi i Wa n ka i yo i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn. Yiri ti bo Nyɛmɛ ma i tiiri kabo i koro ka i ti saŋga kɛrɛ n a.

eng: Now the main point of what we are saying is this: We have this kind of high priest. He sat down at the right side of the throne of the majesty in the heavens.
local: Deke n bo m koro ka m se n kɛrɛ la ka, ya la ahin Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn n bo i tana aŋgoro, Micɛra Kpiri n saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

eng: Hes serving as a priest in the holy place, which is the true meeting tent that God, not any human being, set up.
local: I yo i Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn juma n dika n nu wɔ, bo i ti Nyɛmɛ seree­biri nahɔrɛ su, Nyɛmɛ ni i tii taari nyini dika n a, má adamande taari i a.

eng: Every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices. So its necessary for this high priest also to have something to offer.
local: Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ yo yiiri i, i dɛɛ la ka, i de minɛm aceerim, ni bu yoo­wam na i fa ma Nyɛmɛ. Nyini dɛɛ ti ye wɔ yo daka ka ahin Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn n gusu la yoo­wa be bo i yo ma Nyɛmɛ n a.

eng: If he was located on earth, he wouldnt be a priest because there are already others who offer gifts based on the Law.
local: Nzɛn má Yesu wo durunya n nu, má i yo má Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ fieo, dama minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ adu mɔ ayi bu ka bu yo sa­yoo­wa n wo bɛrɛ wɔ, kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere lɛɛ.

eng: They serve in a place that is a copy and shadow of the heavenly meeting tent. This is indicated when Moses was warned by God when he was about to set up the meeting tent: See that you follow the pattern that I showed you on the mountain in every detail .
local: Juma n bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm yo i ni, i ti kabo i wo aŋgoro n wɔ, ama, i ti má kun. Moyisi dɛɛ n gusu yo ti sɔ ɔ, nyini ti ye saŋga bo i ŋa i bo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Nambue n ye Nyɛmɛ seeri i ka, Si nu, na a yo deke n kɛrɛ kabo m kereeri u buka n su ka a yo i ni.

eng: But now, Jesus has received a superior priestly service just as he arranged a better covenant that is enacted with better promises.
local: Ama, kisa, Yesu dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ juma n tara nyiŋgam dɛɛ ni. Sɔ ye sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n bo Yesu afa aba na i yo Nyɛmɛ ni minɛm afiɛn dɔŋgu n tara dawa dɛɛ n a. Dama sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ ŋa i fa deke n bo i ti kpa na i la má aŋusu n fa ma ya n a.

eng: If the first covenant had been without fault, it wouldnt have made sense to expect a second.
local: Nzɛn má sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ du mɔ diiri i di n koro yoori deke n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo daka ka i yo ni, má i kaaki fa má sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ na i di i di kun.

eng: But God did find fault with them, since he says, Look, the days are coming, says the Lord, when I will make a covenant with the house of Israel, and I will make a new covenant with the house of Judah. .
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ aŋu i minɛm ni ŋgasi, na wɔ se bu ka, Micɛra n ŋa o, saŋga suba bo m fá sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ na m dí i di ni Yisareli­fɔm ni Yuda­fɔm.

eng: It will not be like the covenant that I made with their ancestors on the day I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt, because they did not continue to keep my covenant, and I lost interest in them, says the Lord. .
local: I ti má ka dawa dɛɛ sɛsɛ­su ni, bo m yo faari i na m diiri i di ni am nam ni, saŋga bo m yiiri bu fiteeri Ijipiti asiɛn n su ni. Ba yo abita má i kabo m yo seeri bu ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye m cɛcɛ awo má bu nu n a. Kabo Micɛra n seeri lɛɛ.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, says the Lord. I will place my laws in their minds, and write them on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people. .
local: Sɛsɛ­su n bo m bá di i di ni Yisareli­fɔm la ka, fa kɔ fa ba, m fá m nɔaniɛ n wura bu sunsunni nu, na bu bíta i bu ahore su, mini yó bu Nyɛmɛ a, na bu yó m minɛm. Kabo Nyɛmɛ seeri lɛɛ.

eng: And each person wont ever teach a neighbor or their brother or sister, saying, Know the Lord, because they will all know me, from the least important of them to the most important; .
local: Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i bá kere i lamɔkɔ ka i si Micɛra ni. Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bá si m, fa i tarakam kɔ tu i nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ.

eng: because I will be lenient toward their unjust actions, and I wont remember their sins anymore. .
local: M sí bu aŋunfɔ, na m fá bu satiɛ bo ba yo i n fa ce bu, na m téŋge má i su fieo.

eng: When it says new, it makes the first obsolete. And if something is old and outdated, its close to disappearing.
local: Deke bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ yo kaanri sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ be jɔrɛ ni, i bu la ka, ciciri su dɛɛ n ayo dawa. Deke kɛrɛ bo i nya yoori dawa wieeri, i cɛ má, i busu míni wɔ.

eng: So then the first covenant had regulations for the priests service and the holy place on earth.
local: Kabo i ti n la ka, ciciri su dɛɛ sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di ni, yo la atin keree­wa keree­wam bo bu fa su Nyɛmɛ, na i kaaki la dika bo ba ta i ase durunya n nu fɛn na bu wo nu su Nyɛmɛ.

eng: They pitched the first tent called the holy place. It contained the lampstand, the table, and the loaves of bread presented to God.
local: Bu yo boori nambue be wɔ, na ba fa sanvuin ati i nu nnyɔ. Anɔ nu dɛɛ sɔɔ ni, fitana takaa­biri, ni teebiri, ni kpɔnɔ bo bu fa ma Nyɛmɛ n wo dɔ, bu fere bɛrɛ n ka Sɔɔ Casi.

eng: There was a tent behind the second curtain called the holy of holies.
local: Mɔ dɛɛ sɔɔ ni, bu fere i ka Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ.

eng: It had the gold altar for incense and the chest containing the covenant, which was covered with gold on all sides. In the chest there was a gold jar containing manna, Aarons rod that budded, and the stone tablets of the covenant.
local: Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n bo ba mata i sika n wo dɔ, nyini yoo­biri n su ye bu kunkun tulaalɛɛ nva­nvani fa ma Nyɛmɛ a. Alaka n bo ba mata i sika n gusu wo dɔ, nyini alaka n kere kabo Nyɛmɛ faari sɛsɛ­su diiri i di n a. Deke bo bu wo alaka n nu n la sika torosɛ n bo ba fa diire n bo bu fere i ka mana n awura nu ni, ni Aarɔn kpama n bo i fiɛfiɛɛri nyawa ni, ni yabuɛ pɛpɛrɛpɛ nnyɔm bo Nyɛmɛ akɛrɛ i nɔaniɛ maa­wa burum n agɔ su ni.

eng: Above the chest there were magnificent winged creatures casting their shadow over the seat of the chest, where sin is taken care of. Right now we cant talk about these things in detail.
local: Na ba yo deke nnyɔ nbem bo bu la laŋkpɛtɛ ase alaka n tii su, nyini dekem ni kere kabo Nyɛmɛ wo i minɛm dɔ barasu a, bu fere bu ka Cɛrubim , ba fa bu laŋkpɛtɛm akata dika n bo Nyɛmɛ si i minɛm aŋunfɔ n su. Kisa, ya koro kere má deke n kɛrɛ kun kun kabo bu ti barasu.

eng: When these things have been prepared in this way, priests enter the first tent all the time as they perform their service.
local: Kabo i ti ni, bu nya sɛsɛ nyini neŋgem kɛrɛ su wieeri, nyini saŋga, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm suwura sɔɔ n bo i wo anɔ nu n wɔ, na bu suyo bu Nyɛmɛ juma saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: But only the high priest enters the second tent once a year. He never does this without blood, which he offers for himself and for the sins the people committed in ignorance.
local: Ama, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn ni ŋgumi koro wura sɔɔ n bo i wo mɔ n nu a. Afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ, i wura dɔ kpe­kun wɔ, nzɛn i ŋa i wura dɔ gusu, i bita neŋge­ndɔrɛm mbunja a, na i du mɔ yo yoo­wa ma i ŋu ka, na i nya yo ma minɛm dɛɛ satiɛ bo ba yo i na bu si má ni.

eng: With this, the Holy Spirit is showing that the way into the holy place hadnt been revealed yet while the first tent was standing.
local: Deke bo i ti na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n fa nyini atin n sɔ ni, na i kere ya ka, saŋga bo bu yo boori nyini nambue na bu sɛsɛ deke n kɛrɛ su ni, Nyɛmɛ nya teke má atin na minɛm kɛrɛ koro wura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu.

eng: This is a symbol for the present time. It shows that the gifts and sacrifices that are being offered cant perfect the conscience of the one who is serving.
local: Nyini n kɛrɛ bo bu yo yoori i ni, i la bu ma ya kisa. I bu la ka, aceerim ni neŋgem kɛrɛ bo bu yo fa yoori yoo­wa suuri Nyɛmɛ ni, ba koro ama má bu sunsunni ati má kabo má i ti ye ni ka i ti ni.

eng: These are superficial regulations that are only about food, drink, and various ritual ways to wash with water. They are regulations that have been imposed until the time of the new order.
local: Dama nyini yoo­wa atin n kere diire dii­wam, ni deke nuu­wam, ni bu yoo­wam kabo bu ŋmisi bu ŋu na bu yo bu ŋu casi ŋgumi wɔ. Nyini n kɛrɛ la i saŋga nyaari wɔ, yiri la bo bu suyo i sɔ ɔ, haari kɔ ju saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ bá yo deke n kɛrɛ dɔŋgu.

eng: But Christ has appeared as the high priest of the good things that have happened. He passed through the greater and more perfect meeting tent, which isnt made by human hands .
local: Ama, kisa, Kristo aba, yiri ti Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn n a, yiri bita atin kpa n bo bu yo seeri ka i fa ba n a. Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Nambue n bo wɔ wura nu, na i suyo i Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn juma ni, nyini nambue n la má aŋusu durunya n nu fɛn, i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti n wɔ, dama i ti má deke bo adamande taari i ni bu saa a, wara dekem bo Nyɛmɛ yiiri bu durunya n nu fɛn n be wɔ.

eng: He entered the holy of holies once for all by his own blood, not by the blood of goats or calves, securing our deliverance for all time.
local: Má naanim ni sirim dɛɛ mbunja ye i fa wuraari nambue n nu a, ama, i faari i tiɛŋgu mbunja a, na wɔ fa awura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu kpe­kun n kpein, na wɔ fa ayo yoo­wa ama ya, adena ya koro nya tii­de n bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: If the blood of goats and bulls and the sprinkled ashes of cows made spiritually contaminated people holy and clean.
local: Minɛm bo fin yo wo bu ŋu, na bu koro cin dodo má Nyɛmɛ ni, bu fa sirim ni tooram mbunja ye bu fa yɔyɔkɔti gɔ bu ŋu a, na bu yara nambaa, na bu fa nzuin n gɔ bu ŋu. Nyini saŋga, ba kpakpara bu fin ayo bu casi lɛɛ.

eng: how much more will the blood of Jesus wash our consciences clean from dead works in order to serve the living God? He offered himself to God through the eternal Spirit as a sacrifice without any flaw.
local: Nzɛn nyini yoo­wam bo bu yo bu n ma minɛ yo casi, nyini yiri, Kristo dɛɛ mbunja ni koro yo bo i tara sɔ ɔ. Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni, yiri bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ yiko n nu ma Kristo koro faari i ŋu yoori yoo­wa bo i la má gela be maari Nyɛmɛ n a. I dɛɛ mbunja ni koro kpakpara yi satiɛm bo bu ma ya ka ŋgbɛɛn ya ahore su, ni ya sunsunni nu n a, nyini ma ya koro su Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n a.

eng: This is why hes the mediator of a new covenant : so that those who are called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance on the basis of his death. His death occurred to set them free from the offenses committed under the first covenant.
local: Kristo yoori sɔ n dɛɛ ti, yiri ti afiɛn dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ n bo wɔ fa sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n aba, adena minɛm kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ afere bu n nya deke n bo i diiri i di ka i ma bu ni. Nyini deke n la aja n bo bu di i na i la má awieeri ni. Yiwee n bo Kristo wuuri ni, nyini de minɛm tii ji fa kɔ bu satiɛ ti, na ba do Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi, ni ciciri su sɛsɛ­su dɛɛ atin n su.

eng: When there is a will, you need to confirm the death of the one who made the will.
local: Sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di ni, i ti kabo sɔnɔ toko la ŋgɔɔ na i kere kabo bu ba kpaaki i aja nu barasu n wɔ, má ka wɔ wu ka, ye bu koro di i aja n a.

eng: This is because a will takes effect only after a death, since its not in force while the one who made the will is alive.
local: Saŋga bo i toko la ŋgɔɔ ni, be koro di má i aja ni, má ka wɔ wu ka.

eng: So not even the first covenant was put into effect without blood.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye haari kana ciciri su sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ diiri i di n bá yo juma, má ka ba yi neŋge­ndɔrɛ mbunja.

eng: Moses took the blood of calves and goats, along with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the Law scroll itself and all the people after he had proclaimed every command of the Law to all the people.
local: Nyini saŋga, Moyisi du mɔ kaanri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam n kɛrɛ kereeri minɛm n wɔ, kabo bu wo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu n ka, na i nya fa tooram ni sirim mbunja na i saŋga nzue nu, na i fa nyawa ni bɔɛ ndɔrɛ kɔkɔrɛ bo ba wu i tani, na i fa bo nu, na i fa yɔkɔti gɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa kadasi n su ka, na i nya yɔkɔti gɔ minɛm kɛrɛ ŋu.

eng: While he did it, he said, This is the blood of the covenant that God established for you. .
local: na i nya se bu ka, Ahin mbunja ni ti deke bo i kere sɛsɛ­su n bo Nyɛmɛ adi i di ase am ka am bita i nɔaniɛ n a.

eng: And in the same way he sprinkled the meeting tent and also all the equipment that would be used in the priests service with blood.
local: Sɔ ye Moyisi yo fa mbunja n yɔkɔti gɔ nambue n su a, ni neŋge n kɛrɛ bo bu bita yo juma ma Nyɛmɛ n gusu.

eng: Almost everything is cleansed by blood, according to the Laws regulations, and there is no forgiveness without blood being shed.
local: Nahɔrɛ, kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ni, i ti ka deke kɛrɛ bo bu ŋa bu yo i casi, má ka ba fa mbunja ayɔkɔti su ka. Nyɛmɛ fa má bu ŋgasi ce má bu, má ka ba yi deke mbunja.

eng: So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be cleansed with these sacrifices, but the heavenly things had to be cleansed with better sacrifices than these.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Nambue ni, ni dekem n kɛrɛ bo bu wo nu ni na bu ferema ni aŋgoro dɛɛm ni, wɔ yo daka ka bu fa neŋge­ndɔrɛ mbunja yo bu casi. Ama, neŋgem n bo bu wo aŋgoro n yiri, wɔ yo daka ka bu fa deke bo i mbunja tara neŋge­ndɔrɛm dɛɛ fa yo bu casi a.

eng: Christ didnt enter the holy place made by human hands, but into heaven itself, so that he now appears in Gods presence for us.
local: Kristo awura má dika casi n bo i ferema ni aŋgoro dɛɛ n nu a, bo minɛm taari i ni, ama, i wuraari aŋgoro dɛɛ n ni i tii nu a, ye kisa, wɔ de ya jinaa­biri Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: He didnt enter to offer himself over and over again, like the high priest enters the earthly holy place every year with blood that isnt his.
local: Afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm kpiɛn ni yo wura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu wɔ, na i fa neŋge­ndɔrɛ mbunja n yo yoo­wa. Ama, Kristo yiri awura má aŋgoro dɛɛ dika n nu ka i sufa i ŋu yo yoo­wa saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga.

eng: If that were so, then Jesus would have to suffer many times since the foundation of the world. Instead, he has now appeared once at the end of the ages to get rid of sin by sacrificing himself.
local: Nzɛn má i ŋa i sufa i ŋu yo yoo­wa saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga, má i sudi wahara a saŋga kɛrɛ, fite kabo Nyɛmɛ yiiri durunya n ba ju ni nyuma. Ama, kabo i ti n la ka, ahin saŋga n bo deke n kɛrɛ aba awieeri su ni, na wɔ ba kpe­kun n kpein, afa i ŋu ayo yoo­wa, adena Nyɛmɛ fa adamande satiɛ ce i.

eng: People are destined to die once and then face judgment.
local: Nyɛmɛ akpama i ka adamande kɛrɛ wu kpe­kun wɔ, nyini sin, na Nyɛmɛ dí i jɔrɛ.

eng: In the same way, Christ was also offered once to take on himself the sins of many people. He will appear a second time, not to take away sin but to save those who are eagerly waiting for him.
local: Kabo Kristo gusu yoori lɛɛ. Kpe­kun n kpein ye i faari i ŋu yoori yoo­wa, adena i de minɛ dɔŋgu satiɛ bo ba yo i ni. Nyini sin, i káaki ba bekun. Má minɛ satiɛ bo ba yo i n dɛɛ ti ye i bá, ama, i bá de minɛm bo bu niɛ i atin nu n tii ji wɔ.

eng: The Law is a shadow of the good things that are coming, not the real things themselves. It never can perfect the ones who are trying to draw near to God through the same sacrifices that are offered continually every year.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni, i ferema ni deke kpa n bo Nyɛmɛ ŋa i fá ba n wɔ, ama, má yiri lɛɛ. Nyini ti, yoo­wa n bo bu yo bu saŋga kɛrɛ akoro ama má bɛrɛ bo bu cin dodo Nyɛmɛ ni, ati má kabo má i koro ka bu ti ni.

eng: Otherwise, wouldnt they have stopped being offered? If the people carrying out their religious duties had been completely cleansed once, no one would have been aware of sin anymore.
local: Nzɛn má nyini yoo­wam n kɛrɛ koro yiiri minɛm n bo bu su Nyɛmɛ n fiteeri bu satiɛm nu, má bu sunsunni ma má bu cɛɛn be ka ba yo satiɛ, má bu yaki nyini yoo­wam wɔ.

eng: Instead, these sacrifices are a reminder of sin every year.
local: Ama, kabo i ti n la ka, nyini yoo­wa n bo bu yo i afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ n teŋge bu su fa kɔ bu satiɛm bo ba yo bu n su wɔ.

eng: because its impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
local: Dama tooram ni sirim mbunja koro yi má satiɛ bo i wo adamande ahore su ni, cɛɛn be.

eng: Therefore, when he comes into the world he says, You didnt want a sacrifice or an offering, but you prepared a body for me; .
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo Yesu koro ka i ba durunya n nu fɛn ni, na wɔ se Nyɛmɛ ka, A koro má ka minɛ fa aceerim ni neŋge­ndɔrɛm n yo yoo­wa ma u, ama, adamande ŋuniɛ­mbaa ye a fa ama m ka m fa yo yoo­wa n a.

eng: you werent pleased with entirely burned offerings or a sin offering. .
local: U nyunu ayi má yoo­wam bo bu yo bu na bu kpiɛ neŋge­ndɔrɛm kɔm, na bu yara bu sa­yoo­wa yoo­biri n su ni, ni yoo­wam n bo bu yo satiɛ na bu yo bu ni.

eng: So then I said, Look, Ive come to do your will, God. This has been written about me in the scroll. .
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase Nyɛmɛ ka, Mini yaa, ma ba ka m ba yo u koroo­wa n wɔ, kabo ba kɛrɛ m ŋusu jɔrɛ u nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n dɛɛ kadasi n nu ni.

eng: He says above, You didnt want and you werent pleased with a sacrifice or an offering or with entirely burned offerings or a purification offering, which are offered because the Law requires them.
local: I du mɔ seeri Nyɛmɛ ka, A koro má ka minɛ fa aceerim ni neŋge­ndɔrɛm n fa yo yoo­wa ma u, u nyunu ayi má yoo­wam bo bu yo na bu kpiɛ neŋge­ndɔrɛm kɔm, na bu yara bu sa­yoo­wa yoo­biri n su ni, ni yoo­wam n bo bu yo satiɛ na bu yo bu ni. I seeri sɔ ɔ, haari ni bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere sɔ ni.

eng: Then he said, Look, Ive come to do your will . He puts an end to the first to establish the second.
local: Na wɔ se Nyɛmɛ ka, Mini yaa, ma ba ka m ba yo u koroo­wa n wɔ. Nyini la ka, Nyɛmɛ akun ciciri su dɛɛ yoo­wam kɛrɛ nɔɔ lɛɛ, ye wɔ fa fɔfɔrɛ aba.

eng: We have been made holy by Gods will through the offering of Jesus Christs body once for all.
local: Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa n lɛɛ ye i yoori n a. Kpe­kun n kpein ye i faari i ŋu yoori yoo­wa. Nyini ti ye wɔ kpakpara ya kɛrɛ fin ayo ya casi n a.

eng: Every priest stands every day serving and offering the same sacrifices over and over, sacrifices that can never take away sins.
local: Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kɛrɛ yo i Nyɛmɛ juma cɛɛn n kɛrɛ wɔ, na i suyo nyini yoo­wa kun n saŋga kɛrɛ. Haari ni nyini yoo­wam n kɛrɛ, bu be koro yi má minɛm satiɛ bo i wo bu sunsunni nu ni.

eng: But when this priest offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right side of God.
local: Ama, Yesu yiri yoori yoo­wa kpe­kun n kpein wɔ, bo i yo juma saŋga kɛrɛ, fa kɔ minɛ satiɛ bo bu yo i su. Na wɔ nya akɔ atana aŋgoro Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su.

eng: Since then, hes waiting until his enemies are made into a footstool for his feet.
local: Fite nyini saŋga ni, i ŋminda kabo Nyɛmɛ ba ma i kunma i kpɔ­fɔm na i nana bu kɛrɛ ŋu.

eng: because he perfected the people who are being made holy with one offering for all time.
local: Nyini yoo­wa kun n dɛɛ ti, wɔ kpakpara minɛm fin kɛrɛ na wɔ ma ba ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka bu ti ni, saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: The Holy Spirit affirms this when saying.
local: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n gusu kaanri i sɔ kereeri ya. Wɔ du mɔ ase ka.

eng: This is the covenant that I will make with them. After these days, says the Lord, I will place my laws in their hearts and write them on their minds. .
local: Micɛra ŋa, sɛsɛ­su n bo m ni bɛrɛ adi i di fa kɔ saŋga bo i suba n la ka, m fá m nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n wura bu sunsunni nu, na m kɛ́rɛ i gɔ bu ahore su.

eng: And I wont remember their sins and their lawless behavior anymore. .
local: Na wɔ kaaki ase ka, M téŋge má bu satiɛm bo ba yo bu n su, kabo bu keteeri na ba su má m nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su kun.

eng: When there is forgiveness for these things, there is no longer an offering for sin.
local: Tɔ, Nyɛmɛ afa satiɛ bo minɛm ayo i ace bu, nyini la ka, yoo­wa be wo má bɛrɛ bo be yo i fa kɔ satiɛ su kun.

eng: Brothers and sisters, we have confidence that we can enter the holy of holies by means of Jesus blood.
local: M ninbaam, Yesu dɛɛ mbunja n ti ye ya la ahoresi kɛrɛ ka ya koro cin dodo Nyɛmɛ a, na ya koro wura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu a.

eng: through a new and living way that he opened up for us through the curtain, which is his body.
local: Yesu ateke atin fɔfɔrɛ bo i ma ŋgɔɔ nahɔrɛ su ama ya. Kabo i faari i ŋu yoori yoo­wa ni, i ti ka wɔ teke atin ama ya na ya koro sin tani bambaka n bo bu fa kanda n ŋu, na ya wura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu.

eng: and we have a great high priest over Gods house.
local: Tɔ, kisa na ya la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ bambaka bo i niɛ yɛrɛ n bo ya ti Nyɛmɛ minɛm n su ni.

eng: Therefore, lets draw near with a genuine heart with the certainty that our faith gives us, since our hearts are sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies are washed with pure water.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am ma ya cin dodo Nyɛmɛ ni ya ahore­mbaa kun, na má ya yo sika be. Yesu mbunja n akpakpara ayi satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo ya ahore su a, na má ya sunsunni nu suma ya saŋga kɛrɛ ka ya yo satiɛ. Nyɛmɛ nzue n gusu bo ba fa abiɛ ya ni ama ya ŋuniɛ ayo casi a.

eng: Lets hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, because the one who made the promises is reliable.
local: Tama n bo ya ŋa ya la i ni, am ma ya bita i ni ya saa nnyɔ, má am ma ya fa sika wura nu, dama ya si ka Nyɛmɛ yo deke n bo wɔ fa sɛsɛ­su adi i di ka i yo i ni.

eng: And let us consider each other carefully for the purpose of sparking love and good deeds.
local: Am ma ya sutu ya beŋgum fɔ, na ya buka ya beŋgu, na koro wo ya afiɛn na ya yo sakpa.

eng: Dont stop meeting together with other believers, which some people have gotten into the habit of doing. Instead, encourage each other, especially as you see the day drawing near.
local: Kabo i ti ye ni ka ya sutiɛn ya nɔɔ na ya su Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, má am ma ya yaki i kabo nbem suyo barasu ni, ama, am ma ya sutu ya beŋgu fɔ na bu nya ahoresi buka su, dama ya kɛrɛ ŋu ka Micɛra n dɛɛ cɛɛn n adodo lɛɛ.

eng: If we make the decision to sin after we receive the knowledge of the truth, there isnt a sacrifice for sins left any longer.
local: Tɔ, kisa na ya si keree­wa n bo i ti nahɔrɛ ni, na nzɛn ya toko kete i yo satiɛ ni, yoo­wa be wo má bɛrɛ bo ya yo i na Nyɛmɛ fa ya satiɛ ce ya kun.

eng: Theres only a scary expectation of judgment and of a burning fire thats going to devour Gods opponents.
local: má ka ya ŋminda ni sɛrɛ fa kɔ cɛɛn n bo i suba na Nyɛmɛ bá di minɛm jɔrɛ, na i fa bo ba kete i nɔaniɛ n wura sin n bo i ti kɛkɛrɛ agaya n nu, na bu ka ŋgbɛɛn ni.

eng: When someone rejected the Law from Moses, they were put to death without mercy on the basis of the testimony of two or three witnesses.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i yo fa maari Moyisi ni, nzɛn sɔnɔ be keteeri na i yoori satiɛ, na minɛ nnyɔ wara nsan diiri daani ka i ti sɔ, nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm si má i mi aŋunfɔ, bu kun i wɔ.

eng: How much worse punishment do you think is deserved by the person who walks all over Gods Son, who acts as if the blood of the covenant that made us holy is just ordinary blood, and who insults the Spirit of grace?.
local: Ye nzɛn i nya ti sɔ, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i buuri Nyɛmɛ Wa ni ŋgbɛɛn na i sunsun ka Yesu mbunja n bo i yo ya casi n ti ŋgbɛɛn, na i jɔjɔ wura Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i si ya aŋunfɔ ni ŋgasi nu, sui­cin barasu bɔnɔ ye am sunsun ka Nyɛmɛ fá cin i a?.

eng: We know the one who said, Judgment is mine; I will pay people back. And he also said, The Lord will judge his people. .
local: Dama ya si ka Micɛra n ase ka, Mini cín bu sui a. Mini tɔ́ bu kaari a, na wɔ kaaki ase ka, Mini dí m minɛm jɔrɛ a.

eng: Its scary to fall into the hands of the living God!.
local: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a to Nyɛmɛ saa nu, yiri bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, i la sɛrɛ agaya.

eng: But remember the earlier days, after you saw the light. You stood your ground while you were suffering from an enormous amount of pressure.
local: Am teŋge su, na am niɛ saŋga bo am du mɔ nyaari anyunuteke fa kɔ labari kpa n su ni, haari ni bo am yo diiri wahara agaya ni, am afa ahoresi ade nyini kekereke n kɛrɛ.

eng: Sometimes you were exposed to insults and abuse in public. Other times you became partners with those who were treated that way.
local: Saŋga be bu yo kpiɛ am nzukɔ, na bu kere am wuwuruwa jama nu. Na saŋga be gusu na am asaŋga adi wahara ni am beŋgum bo bu kere bu wuwuruwa ni.

eng: You even showed sympathy toward people in prison and accepted the confiscation of your possessions with joy, since you knew that you had better and lasting possessions.
local: I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka, am asi dan­sarakam aŋunfɔ. Bekun, haari nzɛn minɛ fiinri am neŋgem kɛrɛ bo am la i ni, am ahore ti jɔɔ­wa a, dama am si ka am la deke bo i tara nyinim bo ba de am dɔ ni, nyini deke n yiri wo bɛrɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: So dont throw away your confidence—it brings a great reward.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, má am ma am ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki am, dama ahoresi n bo am la i ni, nyini ma am nya aceeri bambaka Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

eng: You need to endure so that you can receive the promises after you do Gods will.
local: Kisa, wɔ yo daka ka am la mindi, adena am yo Nyɛmɛ koroo­wa, na am nyá deke n bo Nyɛmɛ afa sɛsɛ­su adi i di ka i ma am ni.

eng: In a little while longer, the one who is coming will come and wont delay; .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Wɔ ka kaan, i cɛ́ má, yiri n bo i suba ni, i bá.

eng: but my righteous one will live by faith, and my whole being wont be pleased with anyone who shrinks back. .
local: M sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su m dɔ, i nyá ŋgɔɔ, i yarada n dɛɛ ti, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kaakiiri i sin boori m, m ahore jɔ má i mi ŋu.

eng: But we arent the sort of people who timidly draw back and end up being destroyed. Were the sort of people who have faith so that our whole beings are preserved.
local: Yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti má minɛm bo bu kaaki bu sin, na bu ka ŋgbɛɛn ni, ama, ya ti minɛm bo ya yo Nyɛmɛ yarada na ya nya tii­de wɔ.

eng: So then, with endurance, lets also run the race that is laid out in front of us, since we have such a great cloud of witnesses surrounding us. Lets throw off any extra baggage, get rid of the sin that trips us up.
local: Nyini minɛm bo bu yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n sunnu, ba bara ayɛ ya, na bu niɛ kabo ya su Nyɛmɛ atin n su. Nyini ti, am ma yɛrɛ gusu yaki deke kɛrɛ bo i kanda ya na ya koro su má Nyɛmɛ ni, na ya yaki satiɛ n bo ya koro i agaya ni, na am ma ya fa anyum­boro na ya su atin n bo i ti ye ni ka ya su i n su.

eng: and fix our eyes on Jesus, faiths pioneer and perfecter. He endured the cross, ignoring the shame, for the sake of the joy that was laid out in front of him, and sat down at the right side of Gods throne.
local: Am ma ya suniɛ Yesu. Yiri ti ya mɔ­fɔ a, na i ma ya yo Nyɛmɛ yarada kabo wɔ yo daka ka ya yo ni. I yo diiri wahara baka jaanu n su, na ba wura i nyinsɛ, ama, haari ni nyini n kɛrɛ, i cɛcɛ awo má nu, dama i niɛ ŋuuri fiɛfiɛ n bo i bá di i ni, nyini ti, ye kisa, i tana Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su i fɛmɛya n nu n a.

eng: Think about the one who endured such opposition from sinners so that you wont be discouraged and you wont give up.
local: Am niɛ kabo i yo diiri wahara satiɛ­yo­fɔm saa nu, na ba koro má i jɔrɛ, na i laari mindi barasu a. Am su i su, adena má am ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki am.

eng: In your struggle against sin, you havent resisted yet to the point of shedding blood.
local: Kabo satiɛ­yo­fɔm kere am wuwuruwa na am mindi ni, am nya mindi ju má juu­biri bo bu yi am ŋgɔɔ.

eng: and you have forgotten the encouragement that addresses you as sons and daughters: My child, dont make light of the Lords discipline or give up when you are corrected by him, .
local: Kabo am ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam na wɔ tu am fɔ asi am ahore ni, má am ma am wara afi nyini ni, bo i ŋa, M wa, nzɛn Nyɛmɛ tu u fɔ, má a kete, nzɛn i cin u sui gusu a, má a ma u ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki u.

eng: because the Lord disciplines whomever he loves, and he punishes every son or daughter whom he accepts. .
local: Dama minɛm bo i koro bu n ye i tu bu fɔ a, na minɛm kɛrɛ bo wɔ fa bu ayo i nbaam ye i cin bu sui a.

eng: Bear hardship for the sake of discipline. God is treating you like sons and daughters! What child isnt disciplined by his or her father?.
local: Wahara n bo am sudi i ni, am mindi na am fa i ka am si sucin am sui a. Nyini sukere am ka am ti i nbaam wɔ lɛɛ, dama baa be wo má bɛrɛ bo i si cin má i sui.

eng: But if you dont experience discipline, which happens to all children, then you are illegitimate and not real sons and daughters.
local: Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ acin má am sui kabo i cin bo bu ti i nbaam kɛrɛ sui ni, nyini la ka, am ti má i nbaam nahɔrɛ su, am ti ka soma nbaam wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Whats more, we had human parents who disciplined us, and we respected them for it. How much more should we submit to the Father of spirits and live?.
local: Ya sim bo bu wuuri ya ni, bu ba yo, bu cin ya sui wɔ, na ya ma bu jirima. Nzɛn i nya ti sɔ yiri, wɔ yo daka ya de ya Si Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ tara bu dɛɛ, adena ya nya ŋgɔɔ.

eng: Our human parents disciplined us for a little while, as it seemed best to them, but God does it for our benefit so that we can share his holiness.
local: Saŋga bo ya yo ti nbaatumam ni, ya sim yo cin ya sui saŋga kaan wɔ, kabo i ti ye ni ka bu yo ni, ama, Nyɛmɛ yiri cin ya sui, i yo i ni kpa dɛɛ ti wɔ, i koro ka ya nya i dɛɛ woo­wa n be wɔ.

eng: No discipline is fun while it lasts, but it seems painful at the time. Later, however, it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness for those who have been trained by it.
local: Saŋga kɛrɛ bo bu cin ya sui ni, i ti ka ya ahore ti saakii­wa a, i yo má ya fiɛ, ama, nyini n kɛrɛ sin, minɛm kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ cin bu sui na bu si ka ba yo sa bo i kɔ má atin su, bu nya ahoreda na bu wo wo kpa.

eng: So strengthen your drooping hands and weak knees!.
local: Nyini ti, má am ma am ŋuniɛ yɔrɔki am, ama, am ma am kpɛkpɛrɛ yo kenken.

eng: Make straight paths for your feet so that if any part is lame, it will be healed rather than injured more seriously.
local: Am susu Nyɛmɛ atin n su, na am nya faŋga, adena má am gbaraki buka su.

eng: Pursue the goal of peace along with everyone—and holiness as well, because no one will see the Lord without it.
local: Am kɛrɛ boro am nyi na am tana laifiɛ ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ, na am wo wo bo i ti casi, dama wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a ti má casi, a ŋú má Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Make sure that no one misses out on Gods grace. Make sure that no root of bitterness grows up that might cause trouble and pollute many people.
local: Am si am ŋu sa, na má be fun Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma ni. Am kaaki si am ŋu sa, na má be fa sunsunni tiɛ be ba saaki minɛ dɔŋgu na bu kaaki bu sin bo Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Make sure that no one becomes sexually immoral or ungodly like Esau. He sold his inheritance as the oldest son for one meal.
local: Am si am ŋu sa, na má be sukpini nbaram, na má be gusu yo i ŋu ŋgbɛɛn na i cɛcɛ wo má Nyɛmɛ su nu, ka Esawu, bo diire dɛɛ ti na i tɔɔri i tiɛŋgu baa­wu kpiɛn jinaa­biri maari i niɛma ni.

eng: You know that afterward, when he wanted to inherit the blessing, he was rejected because he couldnt find a way to change his heart and life, though he looked for it with tears.
local: I tɔɔri i baawu kpiɛn jinaa­biri n dɛɛ sin, saŋga bo i seeri i si ka, i yo nyini kisi gɔ i ŋu ni, na i si akete, haari ni bo i sun ni nyunvuin ni, wɔ koro akaaki má deke n bo wɔ yo i n kun.

eng: Faith is the reality of what we hope for, the proof of what we dont see.
local: Nzɛn ya yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, ya la ahoresi ka deke n bo ya yo i tama ni, ya nya i, na ya kaaki la ahoresi ka deke n bo ya nyu­mbaa ŋu má i n wo bɛrɛ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: The elders in the past were approved because they showed faith.
local: Dawa­fɔm yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i se ka i nyunu ayi bu sa­yoo­wa n a.

eng: You havent drawn near to something that can be touched: a burning fire, darkness, shadow, a whirlwind.
local: Juu­biri n bo am aba aju ni, i ti má deke bo am koro ŋu i, i ti má ka buka n bo Yisareli­fɔm yo kɔ juuri su na bu fere i ka Sinayi ni. Bu kɔ juuri su ni, na ba ŋu ka i suje sin fofoe, na awosin awura dika n nu, na wɔ kata liim, na aŋuma akpisi dika ni.

eng: a blast of a trumpet, and a sound of words that made the ones who heard it beg that there wouldnt be one more word.
local: Na ba ti awiɛ ŋga ka i fita ni faŋga su, na ba ti akonvi ŋga ka i sujɔjɔ faŋga su. Bu tiiri akonvi ni ŋga sɔ ni, na bu seeri ni anyum­boro ka bu koro má ka bu ti nyini jɔjɔɔ­wa n kun.

eng: They couldnt stand the command, If even a wild animal touches the mountain, it must be stoned. .
local: Dama ba koro ade má nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari bu ni, i ŋa, haari nzɛn neŋge­ndɔrɛ be faari i ŋu kaanri buka ni, bu tu i yabuɛ bo kun i.

eng: By faith we understand that the universe has been created by a word from God so that the visible came into existence from the invisible.
local: Ya yo Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye ya koro fa si ka Nyɛmɛ yo jɔjɔɔri nɔaniɛ wɔ, ye i fa yiiri durunya n a. Nyini ma ya si ka, dekem bo ya nyu­mbaa koro ŋu bu ni, Nyɛmɛ afa deke bo ya koro ŋu má i ayi bu n a.

eng: The sight was so frightening that Moses said, Im terrified and shaking.
local: Dekem bo bu ŋuuri bu buka n su n laari sɛrɛ agaya n dɛɛ ti, na Moyisi mmɔɔ ase ka, Sɛrɛ ati m agaya, na m ŋuniɛ subobo m.

eng: But you have drawn near to Mount Zion, the city of the living God, heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels in a festival gathering.
local: Ama, deke bo am aba aju ni, am aba aju buka n bo bu fere i ka Siyon ni su a, ni Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n dɛɛ miɛ kpiri ni, yiri la miɛ kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Yɛrusalem na i wo aŋgoro ni, dɔ ye Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ akpiim atiɛn bu nɔɔ ni ahorejɔ n a.

eng: to the assembly of Gods firstborn children who are registered in heaven, to God the judge of all, to the spirits of the righteous who have been made perfect.
local: Am aba jama n bo bu ti ka Nyɛmɛ nbaa­kpiɛnm, na ba kɛrɛ bu duma Nyɛmɛ dika n nu ni dɔ a. Am aba Nyɛmɛ dɔ, yiri bo i di sɔnɔ kɛrɛ jɔrɛ ni. Am aba dawa­fɔm woo­biri ni, bɛrɛ bo bu yoori sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ na wɔ ma ba ti kabo i koro ka bu ti n wɔ.

eng: to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the sprinkled blood that speaks better than Abels blood.
local: Am aba Yesu dɔ ɔ, yiri ti afiɛn dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ a, yiri ye wɔ fa sɛsɛ­su fɔfɔrɛ n aba. I mbunja n bo wɔ fite agɔ ni, ayo ya casi a, i la kpɛrɛ tara Abɛli dɛɛ.

eng: By faith Abel offered a better sacrifice to God than Cain, which showed that he was righteous, since God gave approval to him for his gift. Though he died, hes still speaking through faith.
local: Abɛli yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i koro yoori yoo­wa bo i ti kpa tara i gɔrɔ Keen dɛɛ n bo i yo maari Nyɛmɛ n a. I yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ nyunu yiiri i aceeri n ye i seeri ka i ti sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ n a. Abɛli ti sɔnɔ bo wɔ wu wɔ, ama, haari ni kisa, i yarada sa­yoo­wa toko yo juma ya dɔ.

eng: See to it that you dont resist the one who is speaking. If the people didnt escape when they refused to listen to the one who warned them on earth, how will we escape if we reject the one who is warning from heaven?.
local: Am si am ŋu sa, Nyɛmɛ sujɔjɔ am dɔ, má am kete i, dama saŋga bo i yo jɔjɔɔri Yisareli­fɔm dɔ durunya n nu fɛn na bu keteeri ni, wɔ yaki má bu. Nyini ti, nzɛn i wo aŋgoro sujɔjɔ ya dɔ, na ya keteeri, ya yo sɛ koro fite nu a?.

eng: His voice shook the earth then, but now he has made a promise: Still once more I will shake not only the earth but heaven also .
local: Nyini saŋga, i konvi ŋgumi mmɔɔ ma asiɛn kpisi wɔ. Ama, kisa, wɔ se ka, M kpísi asiɛn n ni aŋgoro n kɛrɛ kpe­kun buka su.

eng: By faith Enoch was taken up so that he didnt see death, and he wasnt found because God took him up. He was given approval for having pleased God before he was taken up.
local: Henoki yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, wɔ wu má, na Nyɛmɛ afa i akɔ i bi aŋgoro, nyini ti ye be aŋu má i n a, dama Nyɛmɛ afa i akɔ i bi. Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ nya fa má i kɔ má i bi aŋgoro ni, Nyɛmɛ nyunu yiiri i sa­yoo­wa a.

eng: The words still once more reveal the removal of what is shaken—the things that are part of this creation—so that what isnt shaken will remain.
local: Kabo i seeri ka i kpísi i kpe­kun buka su n kere ka, deke n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yi bu ni, i bá kpisi yi bu, adena dekem bo be koro kpisi má bu n bá ka.

eng: Therefore, since we are receiving a kingdom that cant be shaken, lets continue to express our gratitude. With this gratitude, lets serve in a way that is pleasing to God with respect and awe.
local: Nyini ti, kabo Nyɛmɛ ama ya atin ka ya ni yiri saŋga di i fɛmɛya n bo be koro kpisi má i ni, am ma ya da i asi. Am ma ya kaaki da i asi, na ya bu i, na ya soro i ahore, na ya su i, kabo wɔ yo daka ka ya su i ni.

eng: Its impossible to please God without faith because the one who draws near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards people who try to find him.
local: Nzɛn sɔnɔ ayo má Nyɛmɛ yarada, i mi koro yo má sa bo i yi Nyɛmɛ nyi, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ba Nyɛmɛ dɔ, wɔ yo daka ka i mi yo yarada ka Nyɛmɛ wo bɛrɛ, na i tɔ minɛ n bo bu niɛniɛ i busu ni ŋkatɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: because our God really is a consuming fire.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ bo ya su i n ti ka sin bo i di deke kɛrɛ bo i ti má kpa n wɔ.

eng: By faith Noah responded with godly fear when he was warned about events he hadnt seen yet. He built an ark to deliver his household. With his faith, he criticized the world and became an heir of the righteousness that comes from faith.
local: Nowa yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i yo deeri kabo Nyɛmɛ bɔbɔɔri i fa kɔ sam bo bu bá yo n su n a. I deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kpaari lee bambaka n ye i ni i dika­fɔm wuraari nu ye ba wu má n a. I yoori sɔ n dɛɛ ti, nyini kere ka minɛm bo bu kaari durunya n nu n ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm a, ama, Nowa yiri, i yarada n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ɔ.

eng: By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was going to receive as an inheritance. He went out without knowing where he was going.
local: Abraham yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ fereeri i, na i seeri i ka i kɔ asiɛn be bo i seeri ka i fá ma i n su ni, haari ni bo i si má deke bo i sukɔ ni, yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i yo deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ye i fiteeri i nam asiɛn su kɔɔri nyini asiɛn n su a.

eng: By faith he lived in the land he had been promised as a stranger. He lived in tents along with Isaac and Jacob, who were coheirs of the same promise.
local: Yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i kɔ juuri asiɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma i n su n a, dɔ ye i tanaari ka i ti wɔfɔ n a. I ni i wa Izaki, ni i anuma Jakɔbu yo boori nambue woori dɔ saŋga be su wɔ. Haari ni bo bɛrɛ yo ti minɛm bo bu nyaari deke n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i fá ma bu n a.

eng: He was looking forward to a city that has foundations, whose architect and builder is God.
local: Abraham boroori i nyi niɛniɛɛri miɛ kpiri n bo Nyɛmɛ ni i tii ata i na i la má awieeri n a.

eng: By faith even Sarah received the ability to have a child, though she herself was barren and past the age for having children, because she believed that the one who promised was faithful.
local: Abraham yi Saara mmɔɔ yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, haari ni bo wɔ yo buke na i ti ŋɔrɔka ni, yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i koro wuuri baa n a. I yo yarada ka Nyɛmɛ yó deke n bo i seeri ka i yó i ni.

eng: So descendants were born from one man . They were as many as the number of the stars in the sky and as countless as the grains of sand on the seashore.
local: Haari ni bo má Abraham ti ka sɔnɔ bo wɔ wu ni, yiri nyini sɔnɔ kun n ye i busu nu­fɔm asunnu ka asara­mbaam bo bu wo aŋgoro n a, ni kabo kunma ŋmiɛ sunnu na i la má nɔ n a.

eng: All these people died in faith without receiving the promises, but they saw the promises from a distance and welcomed them. They confessed that they were strangers and immigrants on earth.
local: Nyini minɛm kɛrɛ yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada a, haari bu ba wuuri. Ba nya má deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó ma bu, bu ŋgɔɔ su ni. Ama, yarada dɛɛ n ti, bu ŋuuri deke n ka i wo dede wɔ, na ba de i ni ahorejɔ, na ba soro su ase ka bu ti wɔfɔm wɔ, wara nanatiiri­fɔm wɔ, durunya n nu fɛn.

eng: People who say this kind of thing make it clear that they are looking for a homeland.
local: Nyini minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu seeri sɔ ni, i kere ka, bu boro bu nyi niɛniɛ bu tiɛŋgu asiɛn busu wɔ.

eng: Keep loving each other like family.
local: Tɔ, kabo Kristo dɛɛ ti na am ayo ka nibaa kun ni, am sukoro am beŋgu.

eng: Dont neglect to open up your homes to guests, because by doing this some have been hosts to angels without knowing it.
local: Am teŋge su na am sude wɔfɔm. Nbem yo yoori sɔ ɔ haari na ba de mɛrɛkɛm wɔfɔ, ama, ba si má ka bu ti mɛrɛkɛm.

eng: If they had been thinking about the country that they had left, they would have had the opportunity to return to it.
local: Nzɛn má bu faari bu ahore­mbaa seeri asiɛn n bo ba yo ayaki i n su ni anyum­boro, má bu koro nya atin bo bu kaaki kɔ nyini asiɛn n su bekun.

eng: Remember prisoners as if you were in prison with them, and people who are mistreated as if you were in their place.
local: Má am ma am wara fi minɛm bo bu wo dan­saraka nu n sa, am fa i ka am ni bɛrɛ ye ba saŋga anyi am a. Na má am ma am wara fi minɛm bo bu sudi wahara n gusu sa, am fa i ka ambɛrɛ sudi wahara n a.

eng: Marriage must be honored in every respect, with no cheating on the relationship, because God will judge the sexually immoral person and the person who commits adultery.
local: Am ma jirima wo am ajaam nu, na biɛsɔ n ni bara n kɛrɛ bita bu ŋu, na má be yo ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, dama Nyɛmɛ bá di minɛm kɛrɛ jɔrɛ bo bu kpini nbaram, ni bo bu kɔ somaya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn n kɛrɛ.

eng: But at this point in time, they are longing for a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore, God isnt ashamed to be called their God—he has prepared a city for them.
local: Ama, bu yo koroori asiɛn be bo i tara bo bu yo yakiiri i n wɔ, yiri la Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ woo­biri n bo i wo aŋgoro ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i ti má nyinsɛ Nyɛmɛ dɔ ka bu fereeri i ka bu Nyɛmɛ n a, dama yiri ye wɔ sɛsɛ miɛ kpiri be su ase bu a.

eng: By faith Abraham offered Isaac when he was tested. The one who received the promises was offering his only son.
local: Abraham yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i yo faari i wa Izaki ka i fa yo yoo­wa, saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ yo koroori ka i yo i na i niɛ n a. Yiri bo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri i ka i yó sakpa ma i ni, i soroori su wɔ, ka i fa i wa kun n kpein n yo yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Your way of life should be free from the love of money, and you should be content with what you have. After all, he has said, I will never leave you or abandon you .
local: Má am ma am nyunu boro ŋwaa koro su agaya, ama, am ma am nyunu yi deke n bo am la i ni, dama Nyɛmɛ ase ka, M yáki má am ŋgbɛɛn, m káaki fa má am ji má.

eng: This is why we can confidently say, The Lord is my helper, and I wont be afraid. What can people do to me? .
local: Nyini ti, am ma ya fa ahoresi, na ya se ka, Micɛra ni ti m buka­fɔ, m soro má sɛrɛ. Adamande koro yo m nzu wɔ.

eng: He had been told concerning him, Your legitimate descendants will come from Isaac. .
local: haari ni bo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri i ka, Izaki dɛɛ ti, ye a nyá busu nu­fɔm a.

eng: Remember your leaders who spoke Gods word to you. Imitate their faith as you consider the way their lives turned out.
local: Am niɛ am mɔ­fɔm bo bu kan Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n kereeri am ni. Am niɛ kabo bu woori wo kpa barasu, na am sɔn bu yarada bo bu yoori i n su.

eng: He figured that God could even raise him from the dead. So in a way he did receive him back from the dead.
local: Abraham siiri ka Nyɛmɛ koro teŋge Izaki fite yiwee nu. Nahɔrɛ, i yarada n dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge Izaki yiwee nu, dama Abraham dɔ, Izaki ti ka sɔnɔ bo wɔ wu ye wɔ teŋge a.

eng: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever!.
local: Yesu Kristo kaaki má, kabo i yo ti ni, i toko ti sɔ ɔ, na i ti sɔ ɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Dont be misled by the many strange teachings out there. Its a good thing for the heart to be strengthened by grace rather than by food. Food doesnt help those who live in this context.
local: Má am su keree­wa fɔfɔrɛ barasu nbem su, na bu ma am mini atin kpa ni. I ti ye ni ka ya de Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ ahɔrɔma n bo i ma ya ahoresi ni, na má ya de atin bo bu ciirem kere fa kɔ diire dii­wam su, bo bu su nyini ciirem su ni, i buka má bu.

eng: By faith Isaac also blessed Jacob and Esau concerning their future.
local: Izaki yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i yo seeri i nbaa nnyɔm ni Jakɔbu ni Esawu ka, fa kɔ fa ba, Nyɛmɛ yo bu kisi n a.

eng: By faith Jacob blessed each of Josephs sons as he was dying and bowed in worship over the head of his staff. .
local: Jakɔbu yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo i ba sudodo ka i wu ni, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ayo Yosefu nbaam nnyɔ ni kisi, na wɔ miɛ i kpama su ayasu asu Nyɛmɛ.

eng: We have an altar, and those who serve as priests in the meeting tent dont have the right to eat from it.
local: Sa bo Yesu ayo ama ya n dɛɛ ti, ya fa i ka ya la Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa yoo­biri n dɛɛ diire bo Yuda­fɔm Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm bo bu yo juma Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Nambue n nu n la má atin na bu di be.

eng: The blood of the animals is carried into the holy of holies by the high priest as an offering for sin, and their bodies are burned outside the camp.
local: Afɔ kɛrɛ afɔ, Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn n fa neŋge­ndɔrɛm mbunja a, na i fa wura Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n nu. Na i fa yo yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ, fa kɔ minɛm satiɛ bo ba yo i n su, na i ma bu nya fa niɛ n kɛrɛ fite miɛ n ahɛmɛ, na bu yara i sin.

eng: By faith Joseph recalled the exodus of the Israelites at the end of his life, and gave instructions about burying his bones.
local: Yosefu yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo i ba suwu ni, ye i jɔjɔɔri ka, Yisareli­fɔm bá fite Ijipiti asiɛn n su n a, na wɔ kere kabo bu yo i fuin n barasu.

eng: And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy with his own blood.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Yesu gusu wuuri miɛ n ahɛmɛ n a, adena i koro fa i mbunja n yo minɛm casi.

eng: So now, lets go to him outside the camp, bearing his shame.
local: Sa n bo wɔ yo ama ya n dɛɛ ti, am ma ya kɔ i dɔ miɛ n ahɛmɛ, na ya su i atin su, na ya ni yiri kɛrɛ saŋga di nyinsɛ n bo i diiri n be.

eng: By faith Moses was hidden by his parents for three months when he was born, because they saw that the child was beautiful and they werent afraid of the kings orders.
local: Moyisi si, ni i ni yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, bu wuuri i ni, ba soro má nɔaniɛ n bo Ijipiti­fɔm fɛmɛ n fa maari ni, na ba fa i afiɛ sara nsan, dama bu ŋuuri ka i yo nyumi agaya.

eng: By faith Moses refused to be called the son of Pharaohs daughter when he was grown up.
local: Moyisi yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye saŋga bo i ba biiri biɛsɔ, i keteeri ka má bu fere i ka fɛmɛ wa baa­bara n dɛɛ wa n a.

eng: We dont have a permanent city here, but rather we are looking for the city that is still to come.
local: Dama durunya n nu fɛn, ya la má miɛ be bo ya suwo nu saŋga kɛrɛ, ama, ya boro ya nyi suniɛniɛ miɛ kpiri n bo i bá ba n wɔ.

eng: So lets continually offer up a sacrifice of praise through him, which is the fruit from our lips that confess his name.
local: Nyini ti, buka n bo Yesu ayo ama ya n dɛɛ ti, am ma ya fa kansi fa ma Nyɛmɛ saŋga kɛrɛ. Ya saraka yoo­wa n bo ya fa ma Nyɛmɛ n la ka, ya teke ya nɔɔ, na ya kan kere minɛm ka Yesu ti Mibiɛ n a.

eng: He chose to be mistreated with Gods people instead of having the temporary pleasures of sin.
local: I koroori kabo i ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm saŋga di wahara n tara bo i di fiɛfiɛ n bo i cɛ má na i do Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi ni.

eng: Dont forget to do good and to share what you have because God is pleased with these kinds of sacrifices.
local: Má am yaki kpa­yo, am buka am beŋgum saŋga kɛrɛ, dama nyini saraka yoo­wam sɔ, ye Nyɛmɛ koro a.

eng: Rely on your leaders and defer to them, because they watch over your whole being as people who are going to be held responsible for you. They need to be able to do this with pleasure and not with complaints about you, because that wouldnt help you.
local: Am de am mɔ­fɔm nɔaniɛ, na am su atin n bo bu fa kere am su, dama bɛrɛ sukpiɛ daafiri am busu n dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ bá bisa bu kabo bu yoori bu juma n barasu n a. Nzɛn am deeri bu nɔaniɛ, bu yó bu juma ni ahoreda a, ama, nzɛn am ade má bu nɔaniɛ, nyini ma bu yo bu juma ni ahoresaaki a, na nyini sɔ gusu buka má am.

eng: Pray for us. Were sure that we have a good conscience, and we want to do the right thing in every way.
local: Am susere Nyɛmɛ ma ya saŋga kɛrɛ. Yɛrɛ dɔ, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su wɔ, na ya sunsunni nu ma ya ka ya yo sa bo i kɔ atin su wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: He thought that the abuses he suffered for Christ were more valuable than the treasures of Egypt, since he was looking forward to the reward.
local: Yiri dɔ, nzɛn bu kpiɛɛri i nzukɔ kabo bu kpiɛ Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ni, nyini la kpɛrɛ tara deke kɛrɛ bo má i nya i Ijipiti asiɛn n su ni, dama i nyunu yo boroori aceeri n bo i bá nya i Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni su a.

eng: By faith he left Egypt without being afraid of the kings anger. He kept on going as if he could see what is invisible.
local: I yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i fiteeri Ijipiti asiɛn n su n a, haari ni bo fɛmɛ n faari yaa i ŋu ni, wɔ soro má sɛrɛ. I ti ka i niɛ ŋuuri Nyɛmɛ wɔ, yiri bo be koro ŋu má i ni, nyini dɛɛ ti ye i koro mindi jinaari ni nahɔrɛ su n a.

eng: Im particularly asking you to do this so that I can be returned to you quickly.
local: M sere am wɔ, am boro am nyi sere Nyɛmɛ ma m, na i buka m, adena i cɛ má, na m kaaki ba am dɔ bekun.

eng: May the God of peace, who brought back the great shepherd of the sheep, our Lord Jesus, from the dead by the blood of the eternal covenant.
local: Nyɛmɛ ahoreda­fɔ n ateŋge ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo i yiwee n nu. Nyɛmɛ faari Yesu mbunja n faari sɛsɛ­su diiri i di n ti ye Yesu yoori ya sasa­fɔ kpiri n a. Nyini sɛsɛ­su n la má awieeri.

eng: By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood, in order that the destroyer could not touch their firstborn children.
local: I yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye i yo kpamaari Butara cɛɛn­kpiri n a, na wɔ kaaki ase Yisareli­fɔm ka, bu kun nbɔmbaam, na bu fa mbunja bɔrɔki bu sɔɔ anɔm su, adena Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ n bo i kun n ba baari, na má i kun Yisareli­fɔm nbaa­biɛsɔ nkpiɛnm n a.

eng: By faith they crossed the Red Sea as if they were on dry land, but when the Egyptians tried it, they were drowned.
local: Yisareli­fɔm yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, ye saŋga bo bu ŋa bu kpiɛ kunma kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Kunma Kɔkɔrɛ ni, ye i tiiri nu nnyɔ na atin ada afiɛn, na ba nati asiɛn wuruu­wa su asin n a. Ama, saŋga bo Ijipiti­fɔm koroori ka bu kpiɛ ni, nzue n diiri bu kɛrɛ wɔ.

eng: equip you with every good thing to do his will, by developing in us what pleases him through Jesus Christ. To him be the glory forever and always. Amen.
local: Nyini ti, m sereeri Nyɛmɛ ka i ma am deke kpa n kɛrɛ bo i ti ye ni ka am nya i, na am yo i sa koroo­wa, na i kaaki ya wo, na ya yo minɛ n bo i ahore jɔ ya ŋu. Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti, ye i yo nyini n kɛrɛ a. Yiri ti ye ni ka ya kansi i saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.

eng: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to put up with this message of encouragement, since Ive only written a short letter to you!.
local: M ninbaam, m sere am wɔ, am yo sawɔrɛ, na am so am sui, na am de ahin jɔrɛ n bo i wo ahin kadasi n nu, na m sufa tu am fɔ ni. Dama jɔrɛ n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am n sunnu má.

eng: By faith Jerichos walls fell after the people marched around them for seven days.
local: Yisareli­fɔm yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo bu bara yɛɛri Yeriko miɛ kpiri n le­so ni, na kobe n bo ba ta abara ayɛ miɛ n kɛrɛ atɔ.

eng: By faith Rahab the prostitute wasnt killed with the disobedient because she welcomed the spies in peace.
local: Bara sakara n bo bu fere i ka Rahabu yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, saŋga bo Yisareli­fɔm kuunri i minɛm bo bu keteeri na ba de má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ni, ba kun má i, dama yiri yo deeri Yisareli­fɔm kɔrɛsi­yi­fɔm wɔfɔ faari bu fiɛɛri a.

eng: You should know that our brother Timothy has been set free. If he comes soon, we will travel together to see you.
local: M koro ka am si ka, ya niwa Timoti wo má dan­saraka nu kun, ba yaki i, nzɛn i ba tuuri m nde, na m ba suba, m ni yiri kɛrɛ bá niɛ am busu.

eng: Greet your leaders and all of Gods holy people. The group from Italy greets you.
local: Am bisa am mɔ­fɔm kɛrɛ ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma ya. Ya ninbaa yarada­yo­fɔm bo bu wo Itali asiɛn n su n kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu.

eng: What more can I say? I would run out of time if I told you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
local: Tɔ kisa, m si má kabo m se kun, m la má saŋga bo m kan fa kɔ Gɛdɛyɔn, ni Balaki, ni Samson, ni Yɛfɛta, ni Fɛmɛ Davidi, ni Samuwɛli, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n jɔrɛ n su.

eng: Through faith they conquered kingdoms, brought about justice, realized promises, shut the mouths of lions.
local: Bu yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, bu nbem kunmaari bu kpɔ­fɔ nfaŋga­fɔm luɛ nu wɔ, na nbem ayo sa bo bu kɔ atin su, na nbem anya deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó ma bu ni, na nbem gusu akata jaram nɔɔ bo ba koro ayo má pui.

eng: May grace be with all of you.
local: Nyɛmɛ buka am kɛrɛ.

eng: put out raging fires, escaped from the edge of the sword, found strength in weakness, were mighty in war, and routed foreign armies.
local: Na ba fa nbem awura sin bambaka nu ka bu yara bu, ama, na sin n anɔ, wɔ koro ayo má bu pui. Na ba koro ka bu kpiɛ nbem takobe ka bu kun bu, ama, na ba fite nu. Haari ni bo nbem ti daburuka­fɔm ni, bu nyaari faŋga a, na ba tu luɛ ni asiɛn nbem su sojam, na ba kunma bu.

eng: Women received back their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured and refused to be released so they could gain a better resurrection.
local: Nbara nbem yo yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ ateŋge bu lamɔkɔm bo ba wu n yiwee nu ama bu. Ama, na ba kere nbem wahara akun bu. Ba saaki má bu nɔɔ na bu yaki bu nu ji, dama bu si ka bu bá teŋge bu yiwee nu, na bu nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má aŋusu.

eng: But others experienced public shame by being taunted and whipped; they were even put in chains and in prison.
local: Na ba yo bu nbem anzasi afin bu ŋgbere, na ba cici bu nbem jɔrɔkɔ, na ba fa bu awura dan­saraka nu.

eng: They were stoned to death, they were cut in two, and they died by being murdered with swords. They went around wearing the skins of sheep and goats, needy, oppressed, and mistreated.
local: Na ba tu nbem yabuɛ abo akun bu, na ba kpiɛ ati nbem nu nnyɔ, na ba fa takobe akun bu, na yaari ati nbem na bu sufa nbɔɛm ni sirim ŋuma sufa yo bu suturam kata bu ŋu, na minɛm akere bu wahara ayo bu ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ.

eng: The world didnt deserve them. They wandered around in deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground.
local: Bu ti minɛ kpam bo bu woo­wa tara durunya n nu­fɔm woo­wa, bu wo má woo­biri kun, bu nati yereŋgi boro kpantain n nu wɔ, ni bukam su, na bu suwura yabuɛ buɛm, ni asiɛŋgu kunma n nu suda.

eng: All these people didnt receive what was promised, though they were given approval for their faith.
local: Nyɛmɛ nyunu yo yiiri nyini minɛm kɛrɛ sa­yoo­wa, bu yarada n dɛɛ ti, ama, ba nya má deke n bo Nyɛmɛ seeri ka i yó ma bu ni.

eng: God provided something better for us so they wouldnt be made perfect without us.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ma yɛrɛ deke kpa nahɔrɛ su, kana deke n bo i koro ka i yo n yo yii­wa, má ka ya ni bɛrɛ abo kun na i ma ya kɛrɛ ti kabo i koro ka ya ti ni.

eng: From James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ. To the twelve tribes who are scattered outside the land of Israel. Greetings!.
local: Mini Yakobo bo m ti Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ akɔɔ ni, m bisa Kristo jamam n kɛrɛ dɔŋgu, bɛrɛ bo ba sandi abara ayɛ durunya n kɛrɛ nu ni.

eng: My brothers and sisters, think of the various tests you encounter as occasions for joy.
local: M ninbaam, nzɛn gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ be tu am, am ma am ahore jɔ.

eng: After all, you know that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
local: Dama am si ka am yarada bo am ayo Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye nyini gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ n tu am a, na am mindi ŋuuri i awieeri, am kóro mindi ŋu gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛm bo bu tara nyini n mmɔɔ awieeri.

eng: Let this endurance complete its work so that you may be fully mature, complete, and lacking in nothing.
local: Am ma am mindi n sun am nu, adena am woo­wa kɛrɛ ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni na má pui kpiɛ am su.

eng: But anyone who needs wisdom should ask God, whose very nature is to give to everyone without a second thought, without keeping score. Wisdom will certainly be given to those who ask.
local: Ama, nzɛn alecira akpiɛ am nu be su, i ti ye ni ka i mi sere i Nyɛmɛ dɔ, yiri bo i ce sɔnɔ kɛrɛ aceeri ni i kunnu kun ni, na i wura má be nyinsɛ ka i mi asere i n dɛɛ ti. Nyini ti, nzɛn be sere Nyɛmɛ, yiri má i mi alecira n a.

eng: Whoever asks shouldnt hesitate. They should ask in faith, without doubting. Whoever doubts is like the surf of the sea, tossed and turned by the wind.
local: Ama, nzɛn be sere Nyɛmɛ, i ti ye ni ka i mi yo yarada ka deke bo i sere i ni, Nyɛmɛ má i wɔ, na má i mi yo sika, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sika, i ti ka nzue bo aŋuma bo i kɔ i sin ba i nyunu wɔ.

eng: People like that should never imagine that they will receive anything from the Lord.
local: Nyini sɔnɔ n bo i yo sika sɔ ni, i sunsunni ti nnyɔ nnyɔ ɔ, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, i si má i jinaa­biri. Má i mi n sunsun ka i nya pui fite Mibiɛ n dɔ.

eng: Brothers and sisters who are poor should find satisfaction in their high status.
local: Tɔ, ya niwa bo i ti yaari­fɔ, na Nyɛmɛ awura i kpiri, i ti ye ni ka i mi ma i ahore da.

eng: Those who are wealthy should find satisfaction in their low status, because they will die off like wildflowers.
local: Na ya niwa bo i ti neŋge­fɔ gusu, na Nyɛmɛ ayo i kaan, i ti ye ni ka i mi ma i ahore da. Dama i cɛ má na i ni i neŋge n kɛrɛ bá wo má bɛrɛ, kabo wura pum cɛ má, na i ni i nyumi n kɛrɛ kpɔnkpɔn barasu ni.

eng: The sun rises with its scorching heat and dries up the grass so that its flowers fall and its beauty is lost. Just like that, in the midst of their daily lives, the wealthy will waste away.
local: I ti sɔ ɔ, kabo wiɛ ba bi, na i kɛkɛrɛ n bo kpɔnkpɔn wura pum na i wu, na i nyumi wie ni. Kabo neŋge­fɔ n gusu ni i neŋge n ti lɛɛ. Saŋga bo i nyunu aba aboro i juma su ni, i ni i neŋge n kɛrɛ bá wo má bɛrɛ kun.

eng: Those who stand firm during testing are blessed. They are tried and true. They will receive the life God has promised to those who love him as their reward.
local: Tere­fiɛ wo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ tu i, na i mi saaki má i nɔɔ n dɔ. Nyini sɔnɔ n koro mindi ŋuuri nyini gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ n awieeri, i nyá ŋgɔɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, nyini la aceeri n bo i yo seeri ka i fá ma bɛrɛ bo bu koro i ni.

eng: No one who is tested should say, God is tempting me! This is because God is not tempted by any form of evil, nor does he tempt anyone.
local: Nzɛn gbɛrɛ­gbɛrɛ n sɔ n atu be na i ma i wura satiɛ nu, má i mi se ka, Nyɛmɛ ayo m ye m wura satiɛ nu a. Dama pui koro yo má Nyɛmɛ na i wura satiɛ nu, na i gusu yo má be na i mi wura satiɛ nu.

eng: Everyone is tempted by their own cravings; they are lured away and enticed by them.
local: Ama, deke bo i ma sɔnɔ wura satiɛ nu n la i mi ni i tii dɛɛ konvikiya a, nyini ma i cin dodo satiɛ­yo a.

eng: Once those cravings conceive, they give birth to sin; and when sin grows up, it gives birth to death.
local: na nyini konvikiya n nya cin i wura satiɛ nu na i mi yo satiɛ be, na nyini satiɛ­yo n nya ba bi wieeri, i fa yiwee ba a.

eng: Dont be misled, my dear brothers and sisters.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, má am daka am ŋu.

eng: Every good gift, every perfect gift, comes from above. These gifts come down from the Father, the creator of the heavenly lights, in whose character there is no change at all.
local: Aceeri kɛrɛ bo i ti kpa na i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni, i kɛrɛ fite aŋgoro ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, yiri yiiri deke n kɛrɛ bo i ta aŋgoro n a. I wo ti i wo wɔ, na i ma má awosin yo má na i kaaki deke be.

eng: He chose to give us birth by his true word, and here is the result: we are like the first crop from the harvest of everything he created.
local: I dɛɛ koroya­nu, na wɔ fa i nɔaniɛ n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n ama ya, ye ya yo i nbaam. I yoori sɔ ɔ, adena ya yo deke n kɛrɛ bo wɔ yi bu n tii.

eng: Know this, my dear brothers and sisters: everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to grow angry.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, i ti ye ni ka am ti ahin sereya n bu, ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ so i sui ndende na i ti, má i mi koro nde jɔjɔ, má i kaaki fa yaa ndende.

eng: This is because an angry person doesnt produce Gods righteousness.
local: Dama yaa kere má sɔnɔ na i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: Therefore, with humility, set aside all moral filth and the growth of wickedness, and welcome the word planted deep inside you—the very word that is able to save you.
local: Nyini ti, am yaki sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, ni satiɛ kɛrɛ yo. Am yo am ŋu kaan fa ma Nyɛmɛ, na am de i nɔaniɛ n bo wɔ fa awura am ahore­mbaa su ni. Dama nyini nɔaniɛ ni koro de am tii ji a.

eng: You must be doers of the word and not only hearers who mislead themselves.
local: Am yo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kere ni. Má am dadaka am ŋu ka am ati i na am yo má kabo i kere ni.

eng: Those who hear but dont do the word are like those who look at their faces in a mirror.
local: Dama nzɛn be tiiri nɔaniɛ n na i yo má kabo i kere ni, i mi ti ka sɔnɔ bo i fa niɛɛnu niɛ, na i ŋu i ŋu wɔ.

eng: They look at themselves, walk away, and immediately forget what they were like.
local: ama, na i nya faari niɛɛnu n seeri, na i kɔ wieeri, a ŋu ka i wara afi kabo i nyunu­awa ti barasu.

eng: But there are those who study the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continue to do it. They dont listen and then forget, but they put it into practice in their lives. They will be blessed in whatever they do.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ gusu bo i fiitiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n nu, nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti n na i ma minɛm nyaari bu tii ni, na nzɛn i mi boro i nyi su nɔaniɛ n su, na i mi ti má sɔnɔ bo i ti nɔaniɛ n na i wara afi i, ama, na i ti sɔnɔ bo i yo kabo nɔaniɛ n kere ka i yo ni, i mi yiri, Nyɛmɛ yó i kisi fa kɔ i sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ nu.

eng: If those who claim devotion to God dont control what they say, they mislead themselves. Their devotion is worthless.
local: Sasu bekun, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i su Nyɛmɛ atin su, na i koro tara má i nɔɔ, i mi daka i ŋu wɔ lɛɛ, na i nyɛmɛsu atin n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: True devotion, the kind that is pure and faultless before God the Father, is this: to care for orphans and widows in their difficulties and to keep the world from contaminating us.
local: Nyɛmɛsu atin bo be su i su n na i la má gela be ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ n la ahin, ka, i mi niɛ ayikam ni nkuna­fɔm su, bɛrɛ bo bu di yaari ni, na i mi kaaki tara i ŋu na má durunya n nu­fɔm ma i yo fin be Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, when you show favoritism you deny the faithfulness of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has been resurrected in glory.
local: M ninbaam, kabo am yo Yesu Kristo yarada ni, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ Jirima­fɔ ni, má am fa tantambu­nu ni am beŋgu.

eng: Imagine two people coming into your meeting. One has a gold ring and fine clothes, while the other is poor, dressed in filthy rags.
local: Ma m fa i ka, nzɛn minɛ nnyɔ nbem ba wuraari am tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n nu, na kun wura sutura kpakpa ni sika ŋgaa, na nyiŋga n ti yaari­fɔ na i wura sutura titii­wa.

eng: Then suppose that you were to take special notice of the one wearing fine clothes, saying, Heres an excellent place. Sit here. But to the poor person you say, Stand over there; or, Here, sit at my feet.
local: na nzɛn am nu kun se bo i wura sutura kpakpa n ka, Jande, ba tana ahin tanaa­biri nyunyumi n su, na i nya se yaari­fɔ n ka, Wɔrɔ, ka jina dɔ, nzɛn má nyini, ba tana m jaa bu fɛn.

eng: Wouldnt you have shown favoritism among yourselves and become evil-minded judges?.
local: nyini kere ka am sufa tantambu­nu atin lɛɛ, na am sunsunni tiɛ ma am sufa kpakpaaki­nu ba lɛɛ, má sɔ?.

eng: My dear brothers and sisters, listen! Hasnt God chosen those who are poor by worldly standards to be rich in terms of faith? Hasnt God chosen the poor as heirs of the kingdom he has promised to those who love him?.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, am ti fɛn. Má Nyɛmɛ yiiri yaari­fɔm bo bu wo durunya n nu n ka bu ti ajɛkɛ­fɔm i dɔ, bu yarada n dɛɛ ti a, na bu nyá fɛmɛya n bo wɔ se ka i fa ma minɛm bo bu koro i n a, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: But you have dishonored the poor. Dont the wealthy make life difficult for you? Arent they the ones who drag you into court?.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ bu yaari­fɔm ŋgbɛɛn wɔ. Ŋmam kere am wahara n a? Ŋmam kaaki cin am kɔ jɔrɛ dii­birim n a? Má neŋge­fɔm n a?.

eng: Arent they the ones who insult the good name spoken over you at your baptism?.
local: Má bɛrɛ jɔjɔ wura duma n bo i la anyunuyi ni ŋgasi nu n a? Yiri ye Nyɛmɛ afa ama am na i kere ka am ti i dɛɛm ni.

eng: You do well when you really fulfill the royal law found in scripture, Love your neighbor as yourself. .
local: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Koro u beŋgu kabo a koro u ŋu ni. Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa bambaka n lɛɛ. Nzɛn am yo sɔ, nyini ti kpa a.

eng: But when you show favoritism, you are committing a sin, and by that same law you are exposed as a lawbreaker.
local: Ama, nzɛn am laari tantambu­nu, am ayo satiɛ, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kere ka am ti ketema­fɔm wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Anyone who tries to keep all of the Law but fails at one point is guilty of failing to keep all of it.
local: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a suuri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ su, ama, na a saakiiri i kun n kpein mmɔɔ, a saaki nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kɛrɛ lɛɛ.

eng: The one who said, Dont commit adultery , also said, Dont commit murder . So if you dont commit adultery but do commit murder, you are a lawbreaker.
local: Nyɛmɛ bo wɔ se ka, Má a kpini u beŋgu yi ni, yiri akaaki ase ka, Má a kun sɔnɔ n a. Ama, nzɛn haari a kpini má u beŋgu yi, ye a kuunri sɔnɔ, a yo ketema­fɔ fa kɔ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su lɛɛ.

eng: In every way, then, speak and act as people who will be judged by the law of freedom.
local: Am ma am jɔjɔɔ­wa ni am sa­yoo­wa kere ka am ti minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ bá fa i nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa ni fá di am jɔrɛ a, nyini nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n bo i ma minɛm nya bu tii ni.

eng: There will be no mercy in judgment for anyone who hasnt shown mercy. Mercy overrules judgment.
local: Dama jɔrɛ­di cɛɛn, minɛm bo bu si má bu beŋgum aŋunfɔ ni, Nyɛmɛ si má bɛrɛ mmɔɔ aŋunfɔ. Ama, aŋunfɔ si­fɔm ni, bɛrɛ dí nasara, jɔrɛ­di cɛɛn ni.

eng: My brothers and sisters, what good is it if people say they have faith but do nothing to show it? Claiming to have faith cant save anyone, can it?.
local: M ninbaam, nzɛn sɔnɔ ŋa i yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, na i sa­yoo­wa kere má ka i la yarada, nyini yarada ni kóro de i tii ji, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, nyini ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Imagine a brother or sister who is naked and never has enough food to eat.
local: Ma m fa i ka, nzɛn am ninbaa Kristo jama nbem wo bɛrɛ bo bu la má diire, na bu la má sutura.

eng: What if one of you said, Go in peace! Stay warm! Have a nice meal!? What good is it if you dont actually give them what their body needs?.
local: na nzɛn am nu kun seeri i ka, Kɔ ni ahoreda. Nyɛmɛ ma u na a di, na i ma a nya sutura. Nzɛn a seeri sɔ, na a ma má i mi koroo­wa ni, i kpa la bɔnɔ?.

eng: In the same way, faith is dead when it doesnt result in faithful activity.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn a la yarada n ŋgumi, na u sa­yoo­wa kere má ka a la yarada, i ti yarada ŋgbɛɛn wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Someone might claim, You have faith and I have action. But how can I see your faith apart from your actions? Instead, Ill show you my faith by putting it into practice in faithful action.
local: Be koro se ka, Ahin sɔnɔ n yiri yo Nyɛmɛ yarada wɔ, ama, ahin n yiri yo sakpa a. Tɔ, ye sɔnɔ yo sɛ kere ka i yo Nyɛmɛ yarada, bo i yo má sakpa a? Mini yo sakpa a, nyini kere ka m yo Nyɛmɛ yarada.

eng: Its good that you believe that God is one. Ha! Even the demons believe this, and they tremble with fear.
local: A ŋa a yo yarada ka Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi wo bɛrɛ a. Nfan. Haari ajinim mmɔɔ si i sɔ ɔ, na bu ŋuniɛ subobo bu ni sɛrɛ.

eng: Are you so slow? Do you need to be shown that faith without actions has no value at all?.
local: Wɔrɔ sinnzin ŋgbɛɛn ni, a koro ka m kere u ka yarada yoo­wa bo i kere má sakpa n ti yarada ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, nzɛn sɛ?.

eng: What about Abraham, our father? Wasnt he shown to be righteous through his actions when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?.
local: I yo sɛ ye Nyɛmɛ deeri ya na Abraham ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a? Abraham sa­yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti ye Nyɛmɛ deeri i sɔ a, dama Abraham yo faari i wa Izaki fa seeri Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri n ka i fa i yo yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ ni.

eng: See, his faith was at work along with his actions. In fact, his faith was made complete by his faithful actions.
local: A ŋu má i? I yarada yoo­wa fa saŋga ni i sakpa yoo­wa kɛrɛ yoori kun wɔ. Na i sa­yoo­wa n akere ka i yarada n ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti n wɔ.

eng: So the scripture was fulfilled that says, Abraham believed God, and God regarded him as righteous . What is more, Abraham was called Gods friend.
local: Sɔ ye deke n bo bu kɛrɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n ayo yii­wa a, ka, Abraham yoori Nyɛmɛ yarada n dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ. Nyini ti, ye bu fereeri i ka Nyɛmɛ danfo n a.

eng: So you see that a person is shown to be righteous through faithful actions and not through faith alone.
local: Tɔ, m ŋa am aŋu i e, ka sɔnɔ sa­yoo­wa ma Nyɛmɛ de i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ a, má ka i mi yarada ŋgumi ti.

eng: In the same way, wasnt Rahab the prostitute shown to be righteous when she received the messengers as her guests and then sent them on by another road?.
local: I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, fa kɔ bara sakara be bo bu fere i ka Rahabu n dɔ. Dama dawa saŋga, yiri yo bukaari Yisareli­fɔm dɛɛ kɔrɛsi­yi­fɔm n a, na wɔ kere bu atin na ba foro bu kpɔ­fɔm saa nu. Nyini sa­yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ ade i ka i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ ɔ.

eng: As the lifeless body is dead, so faith without actions is dead.
local: Tɔ, kabo ŋumi awo má aŋuniɛ­mbaa n nu na i ti fuin ni, sɔ ye i ti a, nzɛn a la yarada na u sa­yoo­wa kere má ka a la yarada, yarada ŋgbɛɛn lɛɛ.

eng: My brothers and sisters, not many of you should become teachers, because we know that we teachers will be judged more strictly.
local: M ninbaam, má am dɔŋgu yo am ŋu kere­fɔm. Dama am si ka yɛrɛ bo ya ti kere­fɔm ni, ya jɔrɛ dii­wa bá yo kekereke agaya a.

eng: We all make mistakes often, but those who dont make mistakes with their words have reached full maturity. Like a bridled horse, they can control themselves entirely.
local: Ya kɛrɛ ba yo, ya yo sam bo bu kɔ má atin su wɔ. Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i jɔjɔɔ­wa kɛrɛ kɔ atin su, i mi woo­wa n ayo kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i yo n wɔ lɛɛ. I kere ka i mi n la tara­buŋu sa kɛrɛ nu lɛɛ.

eng: When we bridle horses and put bits in their mouths to lead them wherever we want, we can control their whole bodies.
local: Am ma ya niɛ kpɔŋgɔ. Ya fa karafi wura kpɔŋgɔm nɔɔ ɔ, na ya kunma bu faŋga na bu yo deke kɛrɛ bo ya koro ka bu yo i ni.
