eng: Consider ships: They are so large that strong winds are needed to drive them. But pilots direct their ships wherever they want with a little rudder.
local: Am ma ya niɛ lee bambaka gusu, i ni i bambaka n kɛrɛ ni, haari nzɛn aŋuma bambaka subo i kɔ deke be, lee kan­fɔ n koro fa napɔku baka n bo i ti kaan ni lee ni, na i kan i kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo i koro.

eng: In the same way, even though the tongue is a small part of the body, it boasts wildly. Think about this: A small flame can set a whole forest on fire.
local: I ti sɔ lɛɛ ni taferema n gusu, i ni bo i ti kaan aŋuniɛ­mbaa n nu n kɛrɛ ni, i koro saaki deke dɔŋgu. Niɛ kabo sin ti barasu a, haari i ti kaan sɛ, i koro je boo bambaka.

eng: The tongue is a small flame of fire, a world of evil at work in us. It contaminates our entire lives. Because of it, the circle of life is set on fire. The tongue itself is set on fire by the flames of hell.
local: Taferema n ti ka sin wɔ lɛɛ. Bekun, ya ŋuniɛ­mbaa n nu, i kaaki ti ka satiɛm bo ba yi durunya n nu n wɔ, i satiɛ n sa aŋuniɛ­mbaa n kɛrɛ wɔ. I yo juma ka sin wɔ, na i saaki sɔnɔ ŋuniɛ­mbaa n fite cɛɛn n bo bu wuuri i n fa kɔ ju haari i yiwee cɛɛn. Yiko bo taferema n la i na i yo sɔ n fite cɛɛma sin n nu wɔ.

eng: People can tame and already have tamed every kind of animal, bird, reptile, and fish.
local: Adamande kunma deke n kɛrɛ faŋga, i ajaa­nnam o, i laŋkpɛtɛ­fɔm o, i cin­bukunnu­su­fɔm o, ni jombaam o. Na wɔ koro ata bu kɛrɛ.

eng: No one can tame the tongue, though. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
local: Ama, taferema yiri, sɔnɔ be nya koro kunma má i faŋga. I ti deke tiɛ wɔ, bo be koro tara má i. I ti ka wɔ yi ni bɔrɛ bo i kun wɔ.

eng: With it we both bless the Lord and Father and curse human beings made in Gods likeness.
local: Ya fa yiri fa kansi Micɛra n bo i ti ya Si n a. Na ya kaaki fa yiri saaki minɛm bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu ka bu ferema ni yiri n a.

eng: Blessing and cursing come from the same mouth. My brothers and sisters, it just shouldnt be this way!.
local: Nyini anɔɔ kun n ye kansi ni saaki fite nu a. M ninbaam, wɔ yo má daka ka i ti sɔ.

eng: Both fresh water and salt water dont come from the same spring, do they?.
local: Bura kun koro tiɛ nzue kpa, ni nzue tiɛ, nzɛn sɛ? Ai, i koro yo má.

eng: My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree produce olives? Can a grapevine produce figs? Of course not, and fresh water doesnt flow from a saltwater spring either.
local: M ninbaam, am si ka damba baka koro su má ŋguin­mbaa. Cumi baka gusu koro su má damba. I ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, bura bo i nzue ti lalaraki, i koro tiɛ má nzue bo i yo fiɛ.

eng: Are any of you wise and understanding? Show that your actions are good with a humble lifestyle that comes from wisdom.
local: Ŋma wo am nu bo i la alecira ni si a? I ti ye ni ka i mi kere ka i ti sɔ ɔ, fa kɔ i woo­wa kpa su, na i yo sakpa, na i wo bɛnlɛ, nyini kere ka i la alecira.

eng: However, if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, then stop bragging and living in ways that deny the truth.
local: Ama, nzɛn am susi kɔrɛ ahore­mbaa su, na am suyo wi, na am bita anyii sunsunni, má am bita fɔ ka am la alecira, na má am bu kɔrɛ jaraki nahɔrɛ n sɔ.

eng: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above. Instead, it is from the earth, natural and demonic.
local: Nyini alecira n sɔ fite má Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ama, i fite durunya n nu fɛn wɔ, i nati má Nyɛmɛ atin su, i ti Sitana dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Wherever there is jealousy and selfish ambition, there is disorder and everything that is evil.
local: Deke bo kɔrɛsi, ni anyii wo, kpakpaaki­nu ni satiɛ yoo­wa barasu kɛrɛ wo dɔ ɔ.

eng: What of the wisdom from above? First, it is pure, and then peaceful, gentle, obedient, filled with mercy and good actions, fair, and genuine.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ bo i la alecira n bo i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ n la ka, i woo­wa ti casi. Bo i tɔ su n la ka, i koro tana­laifiɛ, na i la wo bɛnlɛ, na i de fɔ­tu, na i si aŋunfɔ agaya, na i yo sakpa beberebe, na i la má tantambu­nu, na i kaaki la má burukɔɔ­nu.

eng: Those who make peace sow the seeds of justice by their peaceful acts.
local: Tana­laifiɛ­fɔm bo bu yo tana­laifiɛ juma ni, bu ŋkatɔ n la sam bo bu kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ n wɔ.

eng: What is the source of conflict among you? What is the source of your disputes? Dont they come from your cravings that are at war in your own lives?.
local: Nzu ti ma am di aluwa, na am suse akuruwa am ŋuŋu nu a? I fite ka am sunsunni ti tiɛ, fa kɔ am ahore­mbaa koroo­wa beberebe n bo i wo am ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu, na i kpiɛ am daafiri n dɛɛ ti a, má sɔ?.

eng: You long for something you dont have, so you commit murder. You are jealous for something you cant get, so you struggle and fight. You dont have because you dont ask.
local: Am koro deke nbem, na am nya má bu n ti, ye am koro ka am kun am beŋgum n a. Am konviki wura minɛm neŋge nu, na am nya má i n ti, ye am se akuruwam, na am di aluwa n a. Am sere má Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye am nya má am koroo­wa n a.

eng: You ask and dont have because you ask with evil intentions, to waste it on your own cravings.
local: Deke bo i ti na am sere Nyɛmɛ na am nya má i n la ka, am sere ni sunsunni tiɛ wɔ. Am sere dekem ka am fa yo am kunnu koroo­wa a.

eng: You unfaithful people! Dont you know that friendship with the world means hostility toward God? So whoever wants to be the worlds friend becomes Gods enemy.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am su má Nyɛmɛ ni, am si má ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro durunya n nu neŋgem agaya, ti Nyɛmɛ kpɔ­fɔ ɔ, nzɛn sɛ? Nyini ti, sɔnɔ bo i fa i ahore­mbaa se durunya n nu neŋgem su, i mi ti Nyɛmɛ kpɔ­fɔ ɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Or do you suppose that scripture is meaningless? Doesnt God long for our faithfulness in the life he has given to us?.
local: Má am sunsun ka deke bo ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n ti ŋgbɛɛn wɔ, ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i fa i awiɛwiɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama i n suuri deke be sin Nyɛmɛ ŋu, Nyɛmɛ si i kɔrɛ agaya.

eng: But he gives us more grace. This is why it says, God stands against the proud, but favors the humble .
local: Ama, ahɔrɔma n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya n sunnu. Nyini ti, ye ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ kete kpirikoro­fɔm wɔ, ama, na i fa i ahɔrɔma n ma tarakam.

eng: Therefore, submit to God. Resist the devil, and he will run away from you.
local: Nyini ti, am fa am ŋu ma Nyɛmɛ. Am kete Sitana, na i ŋmáti yaki am.

eng: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.
local: Am cin dodo Nyɛmɛ, na i gusu cín dodo am. Ambɛrɛ satiɛ­yo­fɔm, am yi am saa fite satiɛ nu. Ambɛrɛ anɔɔ nnyɔ nnyɔ­fɔm, am yo am ahore­mbaa casi.

eng: Cry out in sorrow, mourn, and weep! Let your laughter become mourning and your joy become sadness.
local: Am satiɛ dɛɛ ti, am ma am ahore saaki, na am sun. Am fa am siri fa kaaki sun, na am fa am ahorejɔ fa kaaki ahoresaaki.

eng: Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will lift you up.
local: Am da am ŋu asiɛŋgu ma Nyɛmɛ, na yiri bá wura am kpiri.

eng: Brothers and sisters, dont say evil things about each other. Whoever insults or criticizes a brother or sister insults and criticizes the Law. If you find fault with the Law, you are not a doer of the Law but a judge over it.
local: M ninbaam, má am jɔjɔ tiɛ ma am beŋgu. Wɔrɔ bo a jɔjɔ tiɛ ma u lamɔkɔ, wara a jaraki i, a jɔjɔ tiɛ afa akɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n su ajaraki i lɛɛ. Nzɛn a nya yoori sɔ, a nya ti má sɔnɔ bo i de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n lɛɛ, ama, a yo u ŋu ka jɔrɛ­di­fɔ lɛɛ.

eng: There is only one lawgiver and judge, and he is able to save and to destroy. But you who judge your neighbor, who are you?.
local: Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi ti i nɔaniɛ n kpama­fɔ a, na yiri ŋgumi kaaki ti i jɔrɛ­di­fɔ a, yiri bo i koro de minɛm tii ji na i kaaki koro ma bu ka ŋgbɛɛn ni. Wɔrɔ la ŋma na a jaraki u beŋgu a?.

eng: Pay attention, you who say, Today or tomorrow we will go to such-and-such a town. We will stay there a year, buying and selling, and making a profit.
local: Am ti fɛn, ambɛrɛ bo am se ka, Nyuma, wara ahunma, ya kɔ́ ahin miɛ n su, na ya kɔ́ yo afɔ kun dɔ, na ya yó juma, na ya nyá ŋwaa beberebe ni.

eng: You dont really know about tomorrow. What is your life? You are a mist that appears for only a short while before it vanishes.
local: Ambɛrɛ, haari am si má sa bo i bá yo am ahunma mmɔɔ. Am ni am ŋgɔɔ ti ka sɛ? Am ti ka bɔrɛ wɔ, bo i tɔ na i cɛ má, na wiɛ ba tiɛ, a ŋu ka wɔ kpɔnkpɔn ni.

eng: Heres what you ought to say: If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.
local: Deke bo i ti ye ni ka am se n la ka, Nzɛn Nyɛmɛ diiri su, ya wó bɛrɛ, na ya yó ahin ni ahin.

eng: But now you boast and brag, and all such boasting is evil.
local: Ama, kabo i ti ni, ambɛrɛ koro kpiri wɔ, na am sufɔ. Nyini yanfɔya sɔ n kɛrɛ ti má kpa.

eng: It is a sin when someone knows the right thing to do and doesnt do it.
local: Tɔ, nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i si sakpa bo i yo i, na i yo má i, i mi yo satiɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Pay attention, you wealthy people! Weep and moan over the miseries coming upon you.
local: Ambɛrɛ neŋge­fɔm, am ti fɛn. Sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm bo bu bá tu am n dɛɛ ti, am sun na am subo awie.

eng: Your riches have rotted. Moths have destroyed your clothes.
local: Am ajɛkɛ n kɛrɛ susaaki, na nvɔrɛm sumuka am suturam n kɛrɛ.

eng: Your gold and silver have rusted, and their rust will be evidence against you. It will eat your flesh like fire. Consider the treasure you have hoarded in the last days.
local: Am sika ni am jɛtɛ kɛrɛ suwura ŋgambulaari, na bu ŋgambulaari wuraa­wa ni bá ma Nyɛmɛ jaraki am, na i káaki ba yara am ka sin, am ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu. Am atiɛn ajɛkɛ nɔɔ afa ase fa kɔ durunya n awieeri wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Listen! Hear the cries of the wages of your field hands. These are the wages you stole from those who harvested your fields. The cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of heavenly forces.
local: Sasu bekun, am akete ka am tɔ má juma­yo­fɔm ŋkatɔ, bɛrɛ bo bu tambu am fiesu neŋge mbaa ni. Am ti kabo bu kan am ndiɛ a, bu awie bo n kɛrɛ ato Micɛra Yiko­fɔ n sui nu.

eng: You have lived a self-satisfying life on this earth, a life of luxury. You have stuffed your hearts in preparation for the day of slaughter.
local: Kabo am wo durunya n nu fɛn ni, am adi fiɛ, na am ayo am kunnu koroo­wa. Am adi ayo ŋgbiŋgbiri ka toora dɔdɔɔri­fɔm bo bu sukɔ ka bu kpiɛ bu kɔm ni.

eng: You have condemned and murdered the righteous one, who doesnt oppose you.
local: Minɛm bo bu la má ŋgasi be ni, bɛrɛ n ye am ama ba di bu jɔrɛ acin bu sui akun bu a. Bu kete má am wɔ.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, you must be patient as you wait for the coming of the Lord. Consider the farmer who waits patiently for the coming of rain in the fall and spring, looking forward to the precious fruit of the earth.
local: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, am yo sawɔrɛ ŋminda fa kɔ cɛɛn n bo Mibiɛ ni bá ni. Am niɛ kabo dɔdɔɔri­fɔ yo sawɔrɛ ŋminda, haari kabo bu fiesu diire bá yo kpakpa barasu. I yo sawɔrɛ ŋminda kpanyini nzue kɔ tu takoku wɔ.

eng: You also must wait patiently, strengthening your resolve, because the coming of the Lord is near.
local: Am gusu yo sawɔrɛ. Am ma am ahore si, dama Mibiɛ n ba saŋga n adodo.

eng: Dont complain about each other, brothers and sisters, so that you wont be judged. Look! The judge is standing at the door!.
local: M ninbaam, má am wo waraki su, sukan am beŋgu ndiɛ, adena má Nyɛmɛ bá di am jɔrɛ. Am ti fɛn, yiri Jɔrɛ­di­fɔ n lɛɛ, yiri jina anɔ n nu n a.

eng: Brothers and sisters, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patient resolve and steadfastness.
local: M ninbaam, am ma ya teŋge su, fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n bo bu yo jɔjɔɔri Micɛra n duma n nu ni. Am su bu jaa yii­wa su, kabo bu yo yoori sawɔrɛ mindi diiri wahara ni.

eng: Look at how we honor those who have practiced endurance. You have heard of the endurance of Job. And you have seen what the Lord has accomplished, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
local: Nahɔrɛ, minɛm bo sa tu bu, na bu la mindi kɔ ŋu i awieeri ni, bɛrɛ ye ya fere bu ka tere­fiɛ­fɔm a. Am tiiri kabo sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Yobo n yo laari mindi barasu, fa kɔ ni wahara n kɛrɛ bo i yo diiri i ni. Am si kabo kasin na Micɛra n abuka i ni. Dama Micɛra n si aŋunfɔ na i la koro agaya.

eng: Most important, my brothers and sisters, never make a solemn pledge—neither by heaven nor earth, nor by anything else. Instead, speak with a simple Yes or No, or else you may fall under judgment.
local: M ninbaam, jɔrɛ bo i buka i kɛrɛ su n la ka, má am tan deke be ndiɛ fieo. Má am se ka am tan asiɛŋgu, wara am tan aŋgoro. Nzɛn u jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu la ka ɛɛn, am se ka, Ɛɛn. Na nzɛn u jɔjɔɔ­wa n bu la ka ai, am se ka, Ai. Adena, má Nyɛmɛ di am jɔrɛ na i yo am ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: If any of you are suffering, they should pray. If any of you are happy, they should sing.
local: Nzɛn be wo am nu bo sa be atu i, i ti ye ni ka i mi sere Nyɛmɛ. Na nzɛn be wo am nu bo i ahore ajɔ, i ti ye ni ka i mi di jue kansi Nyɛmɛ.

eng: If any of you are sick, they should call for the elders of the church, and the elders should pray over them, anointing them with oil in the name of the Lord.
local: Nzɛn be wo am nu bo i la má laifiɛ, i ti ye ni ka i mi fere Kristo jama nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm na bu ba sere Nyɛmɛ ma i, na bu nunuka i ŋguin, Mibiɛ n duma n nu.

eng: Prayer that comes from faith will heal the sick, for the Lord will restore them to health. And if they have sinned, they will be forgiven.
local: Nyini nyɛmɛsere n bo bu sere i ni yarada ni, i má tukpaki­fɔ n nya laifiɛ, na Mibiɛ ni má i yasu. Na nzɛn haari wɔ yo satiɛ mmɔɔ, Nyɛmɛ fá i satiɛ n ce.

eng: For this reason, confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of the righteous person is powerful in what it can achieve.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am kan am satiɛ bo am ayo i kere am beŋgu, na am sere Nyɛmɛ ma am beŋgu, adena am nya laifiɛ. Sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ni, i nyɛmɛsere la faŋga, na i yo juma nahɔrɛ su.

eng: Elijah was a person just like us. When he earnestly prayed that it wouldnt rain, no rain fell for three and a half years.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n bo bu fere i ka Eliya ni, i yo tiiri adamande ka yɛrɛ wɔ, na wɔ sere Nyɛmɛ ni anyum­boro su, ka, má nzue tɔ, na nzue n atɔ má asiɛn n su afɔ nsan ni sara nsɛn.

eng: He prayed again, God sent rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
local: Na wɔ kaaki asere Nyɛmɛ, ka, i ma nzue n tɔ, na wɔ tɔ, na asiɛn n diire mbaa kɛrɛ akaaki ayo.

eng: My brothers and sisters, if any of you wander from the truth and someone turns back the wanderer.
local: M ninbaam, nzɛn am nu kun miniiri nahɔrɛ atin ni, na am nu kun be faari i kpieeri i nyi baari atin n su.

eng: recognize that whoever brings a sinner back from the wrong path will save them from death and will bring about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: i ti ye ni ka i mi n si nu ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yiiri satiɛ­yo­fɔ fiteeri i satiɛ atin n su, i mi de nyini sɔnɔ n tii fite yiwee nu, na i ma Nyɛmɛ fa i mi satiɛ beberebe n fa ce i.

eng: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To Gods chosen strangers in the world of the diaspora, who live in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
local: Mini Petoro bo m ti Yesu Kristo jaramasa ni, sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n na m fa ma ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo wɔ yi am na am ti wɔfɔm na am asandi abara ayɛ, na am wo Pontusi, ni Galatiya, ni Kapadosiya, ni Esiya, ni Bitiniya awɔrɛm n nu ni.

eng: God the Father chose you because of what he knew beforehand. He chose you through the Holy Spirits work of making you holy and because of the faithful obedience and sacrifice of Jesus Christ. May Gods grace and peace be multiplied to you.
local: Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ du mɔ ayi am ye i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ama am ayo i dɛɛ minɛm a, adena am de Yesu Kristo nɔaniɛ ni, na i mbunja n ŋmisi am na i yo am casi. Nyɛmɛ buka am na i ma am ahoreda kɔ sin su.

eng: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ be blessed! On account of his vast mercy, he has given us new birth. You have been born anew into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead.
local: Am ma ya kansi Nyɛmɛ, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo Si. Wɔ si ya aŋunfɔ agaya n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ ma ya yo minɛ fɔfɔrɛ, ye ya la tama wo ni ŋgɔɔ a, dama i teŋgeeri Yesu Kristo i yiwee n nu wɔ.

eng: You have a pure and enduring inheritance that cannot perish—an inheritance that is presently kept safe in heaven for you.
local: Na am nyá aceerim bo Nyɛmɛ afa ase aŋgoro aŋminda ka i fá ma am ni. I ti aceeri bo i saaki má, i yo má fin, na i kaaki wie má.

eng: Through his faithfulness, you are guarded by Gods power so that you can receive the salvation he is ready to reveal in the last time.
local: Am ayo Kristo yarada n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ la yiko bo i sasa am haari ni bo am bá nya tii­de n bo wɔ sɛsɛ i su ka am bá nya i awieeri su saŋga ni.

eng: You now rejoice in this hope, even if its necessary for you to be distressed for a short time by various trials.
local: Nyini kɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, ye am la ahorejɔ n a, haari nzɛn wɔ yo daka ka am ahore saaki kaan fa kɔ kekereke barasu barasu n bo kisa am suŋu i ni.

eng: This is necessary so that your faith may be found genuine. Your genuine faith will result in praise, glory, and honor for you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Nzɛn am koro mindi deeri nyini kekereke barasu n kɛrɛ, nyini kere ka am ayo Kristo yarada nahɔrɛ su a. Kabo am ayo Kristo yarada ni, i la anyunuyi tara sika bo i ka ŋgbɛɛn ni. Sika yiri, bu fa i wura sin nu wɔ, na bu niɛ kabo i ti barasu, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ má am di nyini waharam n a, na i niɛ kabo am ayo Kristo yarada n barasu ni. Nzɛn am ayo i yarada ni nahɔrɛ su, cɛɛn n bo Yesu Kristo bá yi i ŋu fite fofoe nu ni, am nyá kansi, na bu wúra am kpiri, na bu má am jirima.

eng: Although youve never seen him, you love him. Even though you dont see him now, you trust him and so rejoice with a glorious joy that is too much for words.
local: Am nyu­mbaa nya ŋu má yiri Yesu ni, ama, am koro i, haari ni bo am nyu­mbaa koro ŋu má i kisa ni, am yo i yarada a, na am ahore ajɔ akɔ asin su, na am si má kabo am se barasu.

eng: You are receiving the goal of your faith: your salvation.
local: dama am anya am yarada n kpɛrɛ, yiri la tii­de n bo am anya i ni.

eng: The prophets, who long ago foretold the grace that youve received, searched and explored, inquiring carefully about this salvation.
local: Nyini tii­de n jɔrɛ ye dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm fiitiiri i nu na bu si i n a, ye bu kan kereeri minɛm fa kɔ sakpa n bo Nyɛmɛ bá yo ma am n su n a.

eng: They wondered what the Spirit of Christ within them was saying when he bore witness beforehand about the suffering that would happen to Christ and the glory that would follow. They wondered what sort of person or what sort of time they were speaking about.
local: Na Kristo Awiɛwiɛ n bo i wo bu dɔ n adu mɔ akere bu kabo Kristo, Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka i súnma i n bá diiri wahara ni, ni kabo i woo­wa ti kpiri barasu ni, i wahara di n sin. Na ba boro bu nyi na bu si kabo yiri Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n ti barasu, ni saŋga n bo nyini sam ni bá yo ni.

eng: It was revealed to them that in their search they were not serving themselves but you. These things, which even angels long to examine, have now been proclaimed to you by those who brought you the good news. They did this in the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ ama bu asi nu ka jɔrɛ n bo ba kan i ni, i ti má ka i buka bɛrɛ ni bu tii wɔ, ama, i ti deke bo i buka ambɛrɛ wɔ. Yiri ye kisa am ati i afite minɛm bo bu kan labari kpa n kereeri am dɔ n a. Nyɛmɛ maari i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n fiteeri aŋgoro ba maari bu yiko ye bu kaanri i jɔrɛ n kereeri minɛ a. Nyini jɔrɛm ye Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm mmɔɔ koro ka bu niɛ nu na bu si i bu n a.

eng: Therefore, once you have your minds ready for action and you are thinking clearly, place your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you when Jesus Christ is revealed.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am sɛsɛ am sunsunni su, na am tara am ŋu, na am fa am tama kɛrɛ wura sakpa n bo Nyɛmɛ bá yo ma am n nu, saŋga bo Yesu Kristo bá yi i ŋu fite fofoe nu ni.

eng: Dont be conformed to your former desires, those that shaped you when you were ignorant. But, as obedient children.
local: Am de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n ka nbaatumam bo bu de bu si nɔaniɛ ni, má am yo am kunnu koroo­wa, na am suyo satiɛm n bo am yo bu saŋga bo am yo si má Nyɛmɛ atin ni.

eng: you must be holy in every aspect of your lives, just as the one who called you is holy.
local: Ama, am ma am woo­wa kɛrɛ ti casi, kabo yiri Nyɛmɛ woo­wa ti casi ni, yiri bo wɔ yi am ka i dɛɛm ni.

eng: It is written, You will be holy, because I am holy. .
local: dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Mini, m ti casi wɔ, nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka ambɛrɛ gusu ti casi.

eng: Since you call upon a Father who judges all people according to their actions without favoritism, you should conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your dwelling in a strange land.
local: Kabo am sere Nyɛmɛ, na am fere i ka am Si ni, am tara am ŋu na am bu i fa kɔ deke kɛrɛ bo am yo i am ŋgɔɔ su ni, ahin durunya n bo i ti má am woo­biri n nu ni, dama kabo i di minɛm jɔrɛ fa kɔ bu kɛrɛ sa­yoo­wa su ni, i tambu má nu.

eng: Live in this way, knowing that you were not liberated by perishable things like silver or gold from the empty lifestyle you inherited from your ancestors.
local: Am si kabo Nyɛmɛ ade am tii aji afite am sa­yoo­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm n bo am nam yo fa maari am n nu ni. Deke n bo wɔ fa ade am tii aji n ti má ka sika wara jɛtɛ bo bu koro ka ŋgbɛɛn ni.

eng: Instead, you were liberated by the precious blood of Christ, like that of a flawless, spotless lamb.
local: ama, wɔ fa Yesu Kristo dɛɛ mbunja n bo i la má aŋusu na i ti ka bɔmbaa bo i la laifiɛ na i la má fin be n dɛɛ n wɔ.

eng: Christ was chosen before the creation of the world, but was only revealed at the end of time. This was done for you.
local: Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yiiri i ka i ba yo sɔ ka, ye i nya ba yiiri durunya n a. Ama, ahin awieeri su saŋga n bo ya wo nu n ye Nyɛmɛ ma wɔ fite fofoe nu, ambɛrɛ dɛɛ ti a.

eng: who through Christ are faithful to the God who raised him from the dead and gave him glory. So now, your faith and hope should rest in God.
local: Yiri dɛɛ ti ye am ayo Nyɛmɛ yarada n a. Nyɛmɛ teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu, na wɔ wura i kpiri, nyini dɛɛ ti ye am yo yarada na am la tama fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ n a.

eng: As you set yourselves apart by your obedience to the truth so that you might have genuine affection for your fellow believers, love each other deeply and earnestly.
local: Kabo am ade Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo i ti nahɔrɛ, na wɔ yo am casi ni, nyini ma am koro am beŋgu bo dadaka wo má nu a. Nyini ti, am sukoro am beŋgu ni am ahore­mbaa fofoe ni nahɔrɛ su.

eng: Do this because you have been given new birth—not from the type of seed that decays but from seed that doesnt. This seed is Gods life-giving and enduring word.
local: Am akaaki minɛ fɔfɔrɛm, i ti má kabo adamande wu baa na i wu ni, ama, bo i wuuri am ni, i wu má. Nyɛmɛ faari i nɔaniɛ n bo i la ŋgɔɔ na i la má awieeri n ye i ma am akaaki minɛ fɔfɔrɛm n a.

eng: Thus, All human life on the earth is like grass, and all human glory is like a flower in a field. The grass dries up and its flower falls off, .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Adamande kɛrɛ ti ka wura a, i cɛ má na i wu. Na bu nyumi kɛrɛ ti kabo wura pum yo nyumi barasu n wɔ, i cɛ má na i yɔrɔki na i kpɔnkpɔn.

eng: but the Lords word endures forever. This is the word that was proclaimed to you as good news.
local: Ama, Micɛra n dɛɛ nɔaniɛ n wo bɛrɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ. Nyini nɔaniɛ n ti labari kpa n bo ba kan akere am n a.

eng: Therefore, get rid of all ill will and all deceit, pretense, envy, and slander.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, am yaki sa kɛrɛ bo i ti má kpa, má am kpɔ am beŋgum, má am sisi am beŋgum, má am yo am beŋgum burukɔɔ­nu sa, má am si am beŋgum kɔrɛ, má am kan am beŋgum jɔrɛ saaki bu.

eng: Instead, like a newborn baby, desire the pure milk of the word. Nourished by it, you will grow into salvation.
local: Kabo baa­kan koro nyufini ni, am gusu, am koro Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo i ti ka nɔnɔ casi ni, nyini buka am ye am koro nyi na am nya tii­de a.

eng: since you have tasted that the Lord is good.
local: dama ambɛrɛ ni am tii si kabo Micɛra n ti kpa barasu.

eng: Now you are coming to him as to a living stone. Even though this stone was rejected by humans, from Gods perspective it is chosen, valuable.
local: Am bra Kristo dɔ, yiri ti ka yabuɛ n bo i la ŋgɔɔ n a. Yiri ye fata­fɔm ayi i atu n a. Ama, Nyɛmɛ ayi i ka i la anyunuyi.

eng: You yourselves are being built like living stones into a spiritual temple. You are being made into a holy priesthood to offer up spiritual sacrifices that are acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
local: Ambɛrɛ gusu, am ti ka yabuɛm n bo bu la ŋgɔɔ n wɔ, na Nyɛmɛ sufa am ta awuru n bo i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo nu, na am yo i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ casim, na Awiɛwiɛ Casi buka am na am yo yoo­wa bo i sun Nyɛmɛ nu, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: Thus it is written in scripture, Look! I am laying a cornerstone in Zion, chosen, valuable. The person who believes in him will never be shamed. .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋa, Am ti fɛn, m sufa yabuɛ bo i la anyunuyi sufa bo Siyon miɛ kpiri n bu, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori i yarada, i mi dí má nyinsɛ.

eng: So God honors you who believe. For those who refuse to believe, though, the stone the builders tossed aside has become the capstone.
local: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ bo am ayo i yarada ni, am anya deke bo i la anyunuyi ni, ama, minɛm bo bu yo má i yarada ni, ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Yabuɛ n bo bondo ta­fɔm n faari i tuuri ni, yiri ye wɔ kaaki bondo n jaa yabuɛm n kɛrɛ tii a.

eng: This is a stone that makes people stumble and a rock that makes them fall. Because they refuse to believe in the word, they stumble. Indeed, this is the end to which they were appointed.
local: Na ba kaaki akɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyini yabuɛ ni, minɛm bo bu jaa su, na bu kpaasi. Deke n bo i ti na bu kpaasi n la ka, ba de má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, kabo wɔ yo daka ka i yo lɛɛ.

eng: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people who are Gods own possession. You have become this people so that you may speak of the wonderful acts of the one who called you out of darkness into his amazing light.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ, am ti daanfiɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi am wɔ, am ti sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm bo am su Nyɛmɛ bo i ti am fɛmɛ n wɔ, am ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛ casim wɔ, am ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ awuru­nbaam wɔ. Yiri ye wɔ yi am ka am kan i sakpam kere minɛm a, dama wɔ yi am afite awosin nu, afa am awura i dɛɛ wein n bo i la má aŋusu n nu.

eng: Once you werent a people, but now you are Gods people. Once you hadnt received mercy, but now you have received mercy.
local: Ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Saŋga be, am yo ti má i dɛɛ minɛm, ama, kisa, am ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛm wɔ. Nyini saŋga, Nyɛmɛ yo si má am aŋunfɔ, ama, kisa, wɔ si am aŋunfɔ.

eng: Dear friends, since you are immigrants and strangers in the world, I urge that you avoid worldly desires that wage war against your lives.
local: M danfo kpam, am ti wɔfɔm wɔ, ni minɛm bo bu la má woo­biri durunya n nu fɛn, nyini dɛɛ ti, m sere am ni anyum­boro wɔ, má am ma am nyunu boro am aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa su, dama am aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wam sukoro ka bu kunma am wɔ.

eng: Live honorably among the unbelievers. Today, they defame you, as if you were doing evil. But in the day when God visits to judge they will glorify him, because they have observed your honorable deeds.
local: Am ma am woo­wa ti kpa, na nzɛn minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n jaraki am fa kɔ satiɛ be su, bu ba ŋu am sakpa n bo am yo i ni, na bu kánsi Nyɛmɛ, cɛɛn n bo i bá niɛ i minɛm su ni.

eng: For the sake of the Lord submit to every human institution. Do this whether it means submitting to the emperor as supreme ruler.
local: Ya Mibiɛ n dɛɛ ti, am de nfaŋga­fɔm kɛrɛ nɔaniɛ, fɛmɛ bo i niɛ adamande kɛrɛ su ni.

eng: or to governors as those sent by the emperor. They are sent to punish those doing evil and to praise those doing good.
local: ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm bo wɔ yi bu ka bu suniɛ am su ni, am de bu nɔaniɛ, dama ba fa bu ase ka bu cin minɛm bo bu yo satiɛ n sui wɔ, na bu kansi minɛm bo bu yo sakpa ni.

eng: Submit to them because its Gods will that by doing good you will silence the ignorant talk of foolish people.
local: Nyɛmɛ koro ka am yo sakpa a, na minɛm bo bu ti sinnzinm na bu si má be ni, na bu si má kabo bu kan.

eng: Do this as Gods slaves, and yet also as free people, not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil.
local: Am ti minɛm bo am ade am tii wɔ, má am niɛ ka am anya am tii n dɛɛ ti, na am sufiɛ yo satiɛ. Ama, am ma am woo­wa ti kabo Nyɛmɛ akɔɔm woo­wa ti barasu ni.

eng: Honor everyone. Love the family of believers. Have respectful fear of God. Honor the emperor.
local: Am bu sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sɔnɔ, am koro am ninbaam kɛrɛ, am soro Nyɛmɛ, na am bu Roma­fɔm fɛmɛ kpiri ni.

eng: Household slaves, submit by accepting the authority of your masters with all respect. Do this not only to good and kind masters but also to those who are harsh.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am ti akɔɔm ni, am de am mibiɛm nɔaniɛ, na am bu bu nahɔrɛ su, má ka bo bu ti bɛnlɛ na bu yo am sakpa ni ŋgumi, ama, haari bo bu ti tiɛ ni mmɔɔ, am bu bu.

eng: Now, it is commendable if, because of ones understanding of God, someone should endure pain through suffering unjustly.
local: Nzɛn am ayo má satiɛ be, na bu kere am wahara na am mindi deeri i, ka am soro Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti, nyini ti kpa a.

eng: But what praise comes from enduring patiently when you have sinned and are beaten for it? But if you endure steadfastly when youve done good and suffer for it, this is commendable before God.
local: Nzu kpɛrɛ ye am nyá i a, nzɛn am yoori satiɛ na bu boori am na am mindi deeri i? Ama, nzɛn am yoori sakpa, na bu kere am wahara, na am mindi deeri i, nyini ti kpa Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

eng: You were called to this kind of endurance, because Christ suffered on your behalf. He left you an example so that you might follow in his footsteps.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye Nyɛmɛ afere am a, dama Kristo ni i tii adi wahara ama am, nyini kere am ka i ti ye ni ka am sɔn i su di i wahara n be.

eng: He committed no sin, nor did he ever speak in ways meant to deceive.
local: Bu ŋa, yiri, Wɔ yo má satiɛ be, wɔ kaaki abu má kɔrɛ be.

eng: When he was insulted, he did not reply with insults. When he suffered, he did not threaten revenge. Instead, he entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
local: Bu kpiɛɛri i nzukɔ ni, wɔ wɔrɔ má nzukɔ ama má bu. Bu kereeri i wahara ni, wɔ se má bu ka bu ŋú i. Ama, yiri faari i deke kɛrɛ maari Nyɛmɛ wɔ, yiri bo i di jɔrɛ bo i kɔ atin su ni.

eng: He carried in his own body on the cross the sins we committed. He did this so that we might live in righteousness, having nothing to do with sin. By his wounds you were healed.
local: Ya satiɛ dɛɛ ti, ye Kristo deeri ya jinaa­biri diiri wahara wuuri baka jaanu n su a. I yoori sɔ, adena i kere ka ya wu wɔ, fa kɔ ya satiɛ su, na ya la ŋgɔɔ bo ya koro yo sakpa. Wahara n bo Kristo diiri i ŋuniɛ nu n dɛɛ ti, ye am anya laifiɛ a.

eng: Though you were like straying sheep, you have now returned to the shepherd and guardian of your lives.
local: Am yo ti ka nbɔɛm bo ba mini wɔ, ama, kisa, am akaaki aba yiri bo i ti am sasa­fɔ na i niɛ am dika n dɔ.

eng: Wives, likewise, submit to your own husbands. Do this so that even if some of them refuse to believe the word, they may be won without a word by their wives way of life.
local: Tɔ, ambɛrɛ nbaram gusu, am de am hunm nɔaniɛ. Na nzɛn bu nbem nya yo má Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n yarada, na am woo­wa ti kpa, nyini ma bu yo yarada, má ka am nɔaniɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n dɛɛ ti.

eng: After all, they will have observed the reverent and holy manner of your lives.
local: ama, nzɛn bu ŋuuri ka am woo­wa ti casi, na am ma bu jirima, nyini ma bu kaaki bu wo na bu su Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: Dont try to make yourselves beautiful on the outside, with stylish hair or by wearing gold jewelry or fine clothes.
local: Deke bo i kere ka am yo nyumi ni, i ti má kabo am yo am ŋu di am tii­ŋmiɛ suŋguruya ni, ni kabo am wura sikam, ni kabo am kara sutura kpakpam n barasu ni.

eng: Instead, make yourselves beautiful on the inside, in your hearts, with the enduring quality of a gentle, peaceful spirit. This type of beauty is very precious in Gods eyes.
local: ama, deke bo i kere ka am yo nyumi n la wo kpa bo i fite am ahore­mbaa su, na i saari má ni. I ti ye ni ka am yo am ŋu kaan, na am wo bɛnlɛ, nyini ti deke bo i la anyunuyi nahɔrɛ su Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

eng: For it was in this way that holy women who trusted in God used to make themselves beautiful, accepting the authority of their own husbands.
local: Dama kabo dawa saŋga, nbaram nbem bo bu ti casi na bu la tama ni Nyɛmɛ n yo yoori bu ŋu nyumi lɛɛ, bu deeri bu hunm nɔaniɛ wɔ.

eng: For example, Sarah accepted Abrahams authority when she called him master . You have become her children when you do good and dont respond to threats with fear.
local: kabo Saara gusu yo yoori lɛɛ, i deeri i hun Abraham nɔaniɛ wɔ, na wɔ fere i ka M awuru­fɔ­biɛ. Nzɛn am yoori sa bo i ti kpa na am soro má sɛrɛ, am akaaki Saara nbaam wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Husbands, likewise, submit by living with your wife in ways that honor her, knowing that she is the weaker partner. Honor her all the more, as she is also a coheir of the gracious care of life. Do this so that your prayers wont be hindered.
local: Ambɛrɛ nbiɛsɔm, i ti ye ni ka am gusu yo sɔ. Am si kabo am fa ŋgɔnlɛ bita am yim barasu. Am si ka nbaram faŋga ni am dɛɛ sɛ má, nyini ti, am bu am yim. Dama am ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ sáŋga nya Nyɛmɛ aceeri n a, yiri la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni. Am bita bu nyunyumi, adena má deke be kanda am nyɛmɛsere.

eng: Finally, all of you be of one mind, sympathetic, lovers of your fellow believers, compassionate, and modest in your opinion of yourselves.
local: Tɔ, deke bo m koro ka m fa kun jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ nɔɔ n la ka, am kɛrɛ fa anɔɔsɛ, na am cɛcɛ wo am beŋgu nu, na am koro am beŋgu ka am ti nibaam wɔ, na am si am beŋgu aŋunfɔ, na am yo am ŋu kaan ma am beŋgu.

eng: Dont pay back evil for evil or insult for insult. Instead, give blessing in return. You were called to do this so that you might inherit a blessing.
local: Nzɛn be yo am satiɛ, má am yo tiɛ tɔ i kaari, na nzɛn be kpiɛɛri am nzukɔ, má am wɔrɔ nzukɔ ma i, ama, kabo am yo la ka, am yo bu kisi, dama deke n bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ afere am la ka, i yó am kisi wɔ.

eng: For those who want to love life and see good days should keep their tongue from evil speaking and their lips from speaking lies. .
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ŋu i ŋu fiɛ, na i wo wo bo i yo fiɛ, i mi tara i taferema na má i sujɔjɔ jɔrɛ tiɛ, na má i sudadaka minɛm.

eng: They should shun evil and do good; seek peace and chase after it. .
local: I mi yaki satiɛ­yo, na i yo sa bo i ti kpa, i mi boro i nyi na i sukpini tana­laifiɛ busu.

eng: The Lords eyes are on the righteous and his ears are open to their prayers. But the Lord cannot tolerate those who do evil. .
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ nyu­mbaa ta minɛm bo bu yo sa bo i kɔ atin su ni, na i so i sui ti bu nyɛmɛsere, ama, i koro má ka i nyu­mbaa ŋu bo bu yo satiɛ ni.

eng: Who will harm you if you are zealous for good?.
local: Nzɛn am nyunu aboro ka am yo sa bo i ti kpa, ŋma koro yo am pui a?.

eng: But happy are you, even if you suffer because of righteousness! Dont be terrified or upset by them.
local: Ama, nzɛn am yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n dɛɛ ti, ye am sudi wahara, am tere­fiɛ lɛɛ, má am soro bu, má am ma am ahore saaki.

eng: Instead, regard Christ the Lord as holy in your hearts. Whenever anyone asks you to speak of your hope, be ready to defend it.
local: Ama, am ma Kristo yo am Mibiɛ n na am bu i am ahore su. Na am kpa am ŋu ŋminda saŋga kɛrɛ, na nzɛn be ba bisa am fa kɔ deke n bo i ti na am la tama ni, na am si kabo am bɔ i nu barasu.

eng: Yet do this with respectful humility, maintaining a good conscience. Act in this way so that those who malign your good lifestyle in Christ may be ashamed when they slander you.
local: ama, am jɔjɔ bɛnlɛ, na am bu i mi, na am si am sunsunni nu ka am ayo sakpa, adena nzɛn minɛm jɔjɔɔri am ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn fa kɔ am wo kpa n su ka am su Kristo dɛɛ ti, na bu dí nyinsɛ.

eng: It is better to suffer for doing good than for doing evil.
local: Dama nzɛn am yo sakpa na am di wahara na i ti Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu, i ti ye ni bo am yo satiɛ na bu kere am wahara.

eng: Christ himself suffered on account of sins, once for all, the righteous one on behalf of the unrighteous. He did this in order to bring you into the presence of God. Christ was put to death as a human, but made alive by the Spirit.
local: Ya satiɛ dɛɛ ti ye Kristo gusu diiri wahara kpe­kun n kpein a, na yiri bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su n awu, yɛrɛ bo ya ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm dɛɛ ti, na i yo ya ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu. Bu kuunri i ni, na Nyɛmɛ ama i ŋgɔɔ awiɛwiɛ n nu.

eng: And it was by the Spirit that he went to preach to the spirits in prison.
local: na wɔ nya akɔ akan jɔrɛ akere awiɛwiɛm bo bu wo dan­saraka n nu ni.

eng: In the past, these spirits were disobedient—when God patiently waited during the time of Noah. Noah built an ark in which a few lives were rescued through water.
local: Dawa saŋga, bɛrɛ yo keteeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a, na Nyɛmɛ ayo sawɔrɛ aŋminda, saŋga bo Nowa sukpa lee bambaka n ni. Nyini lee ni, minɛ kaan sɔ wuraari nu n a, bu mɔcuɛ nyaari tii­de, ye nzue n adi má bu a.

eng: Baptism is like that. It saves you now—not because it removes dirt from your body but because it is the mark of a good conscience toward God. Your salvation comes through the resurrection of Jesus Christ.
local: Nyini nzue n ti deke bo i kere fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ nzue biɛɛ­wa bo i ma am anya tii­de nyuma n wɔ. Nyɛmɛ nzue biɛ ti má deke bo i yi am ŋuniɛ su fin, ama, i kere ka am si am sunsunni nu ka, am la má ŋgasi be Nyɛmɛ dɔ, dama Yesu Kristo teŋgeeri i yiwee n nu n dɛɛ ti.

eng: who is at Gods right side. Now that he has gone into heaven, he rules over all angels, authorities, and powers.
local: Yiri ye wɔ kɔ aŋgoro akɔ atana Nyɛmɛ saa fɔmbɔrɔ su n a, ye i la yiko niɛ mɛrɛkɛm kɛrɛ su a, ni yiko­fɔm, ni nfaŋga­fɔm barasu kɛrɛ.

eng: Therefore, since Christ suffered as a human, you should also arm yourselves with his way of thinking. This is because whoever suffers is finished with sin.
local: Tɔ, kabo Kristo diiri wahara i ŋuniɛ n nu ni, i ti ye ni ka am gusu kpa am ŋu bita nyini sunsunni ni, dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri wahara i ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu yo má satiɛ kun.

eng: As a result, they dont live the rest of their human lives in ways determined by human desires but in ways determined by Gods will.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, má am yo am kunnu koroo­wa, ama, am yo kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni, haari am ŋgɔɔ awieeri.

eng: You have wasted enough time doing what unbelievers desire—living in their unrestrained immorality and lust, their drunkenness and excessive feasting and wild parties, and their forbidden worship of idols.
local: Saŋga be am yo yoori am kunnu koroo­wa, deke bo minɛm bo bu si má Nyɛmɛ n yo i ni, wɔ sɛ su sɔ. Am yo tara má am ŋu, na am yo su konvikiya ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm, na am nu nzan bo, na am nu nzan yo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn kabo nzan nu­fɔm yo ni, na am susu amɔɛm, deke bo Nyɛmɛ koro má i ni.

eng: They think its strange that you dont join in these activities with the same flood of unrestrained wickedness. So they slander you.
local: Kisa na am ni bɛrɛ saŋga wo má nyini wo ŋgbɛɛn ni, jɔrɛ n amiɛ bu wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye bu kpiɛ am nzukɔ n a.

eng: They will have to reckon with the one who is ready to judge the living and the dead.
local: Ama, bɛrɛ ni bu tii, bu bá jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu, na bu kán deke kɛrɛ bo ba yo i. Yiri ye wɔ yo siriya ka i dí minɛm bo bu la ŋgɔɔ ni bo ba wu n jɔrɛ a.

eng: Indeed, this is the reason the good news was also preached to the dead. This happened so that, although they were judged as humans according to human standards, they could live by the Spirit according to divine standards.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye bo ba wu n gusu tiiri labari kpa n jɔrɛ kaan­wa n a, adena haari ni bo bu wuuri kabo adamande kɛrɛ wu ni, bu nyá ŋgɔɔ awiɛwiɛ n nu kabo Nyɛmɛ la ŋgɔɔ ni.

eng: The end of everything has come. Therefore, be self-controlled and clearheaded so you can pray.
local: Deke n kɛrɛ sukɔ awieeri lɛɛ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, má am fa am ŋu ji, am tara am ŋu na am koro sere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Above all, show sincere love to each other, because love brings about the forgiveness of many sins.
local: Sa n bo i tara deke n kɛrɛ n la ka, am koro am beŋgu ni nahɔrɛ su, dama koro kata satiɛ dɔŋgu su wɔ.

eng: Open your homes to each other without complaining.
local: Nzɛn am beŋgum aseke am wɔfɔ, am de bu, má am ŋuŋuti.

eng: And serve each other according to the gift each person has received, as good managers of Gods diverse gifts.
local: Nyɛmɛ ama am kɛrɛ aceeri barasu barasu, nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka am fa nyini aceerim buka am beŋgu. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ suyo i juma nyunyumi kabo Nyɛmɛ afa ama am ni.

eng: Whoever speaks should do so as those who speak Gods word. Whoever serves should do so from the strength that God furnishes. Do this so that in everything God may be honored through Jesus Christ. To him be honor and power forever and always. Amen.
local: Wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ama u aceeri ka a kan i jɔrɛ, si ka Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ ye a sukan i a, wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ama u aceeri ka a fa buka minɛm, buka bu ni faŋga n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama u ni, adena am sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ nu na Nyɛmɛ nya kansi, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Yiri la yiko ni jirima saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.

eng: Dear friends, dont be surprised about the fiery trials that have come among you to test you. These are not strange happenings.
local: M danfo kpam, má am ma wahara kekereke n bo am sudi i n yo am fura nu, dama Nyɛmɛ suyo am na i niɛ a. Má am sunsun ka i ti sa bo wɔ nya tu má be wɔ.

eng: Instead, rejoice as you share Christs suffering. You share his suffering now so that you may also have overwhelming joy when his glory is revealed.
local: Ama, kabo am sudi Kristo dɛɛ wahara n bo i diiri i n be ni, am ma am ahore jɔ, adena cɛɛn n bo Kristo jirima woo­wa bo i la má aŋusu n bá fite fofoe nu, am ahore jɔ́ na i yó am fiɛ.

eng: If you are mocked because of Christs name, you are blessed, for the Spirit of glory—indeed, the Spirit of God—rests on you.
local: Nzɛn bu kpiɛɛri am nzukɔ, ka am ti Kristo dɛɛm ti, nyini la ka am tere­fiɛ lɛɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo am dɔ lɛɛ, yiri bo i la jirima woo­wa n na i la má aŋusu ni.

eng: Now none of you should suffer as a murderer or thief or evildoer or rebel.
local: Ama, nzɛn am be diiri wahara, má i yo ka i mi akun sɔnɔ, wara wɔ wɔ, wara wɔ yo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn be, wara wɔ wura minɛm jɔrɛ nu, nyini n sɔ ti má kpa.

eng: But dont be ashamed if you suffer as one who belongs to Christ. Rather, honor God as you bear Christs name. Give honor to God.
local: Ama, nzɛn sɔnɔ sudi wahara ka i ti Kristo susu­fɔ n dɛɛ ti, má i mi ma nyinsɛ ti i, ama, i mi kansi Nyɛmɛ, ka i ti Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: because its time for judgment to begin with Gods own household. But if judgment starts with us, what will happen to those who refuse to believe Gods good news?.
local: Dama saŋga n aju bo Nyɛmɛ dí minɛm jɔrɛ. I dú mɔ bo i bu di i tiɛŋgu minɛm jɔrɛ wɔ ka. Ye nzɛn i nya du mɔ diiri yɛrɛ jɔrɛ, minɛm n bo ba ka na ba de má i labari kpa ni, bu wíe sɛ wɔ lɛɛ?.

eng: If the righteous are barely rescued, what will happen to the godless and sinful?.
local: Dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nzɛn i ti kekere ka minɛm bo bu ti kpam nya tii­de, ye satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ni bo bu bu má Nyɛmɛ ni, i yó bu sɛ wɔ.

eng: So then, those who suffer because they follow Gods will should commit their lives to a trustworthy creator by doing what is right.
local: Nyini ti, minɛm bo bu sudi wahara Nyɛmɛ koroya­nu n dɛɛ ti ni, i ti ye ni ka bu boro bu nyi na bu suyo sakpa, na bu fa bu ŋu wura Nyɛmɛ saa nu, dama yiri bo wɔ yi bu ni, i yáki má bu cɛɛn n be da.

eng: Therefore, I have a request for the elders among you. I urge the elders.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am wo Kristo jama n nu, na am ti nkpiɛnm ni, mini mmɔɔ ti Kristo jama n kpiɛn kun wɔ, ma ŋu kabo Kristo diiri wahara barasu ni, na m bá nya i dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n be, saŋga bo i bá fite fofoe nu. Nyini ti, m susere am jande wɔ.

eng: Like shepherds, tend the flock of God among you. Watch over it. Dont shepherd because you must, but do it voluntarily for God. Dont shepherd greedily, but do it eagerly.
local: am niɛ Nyɛmɛ minɛm bo bu wo am dɔ n su, kabo bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ niɛ i nbɔɛm su barasu ni. Am niɛ bu dika nyunyumi, má ka bu miɛ am wɔ, ama, am koroya­nu, dama Nyɛmɛ koro ka am yo i a, má am yo i ka ŋwaa kpini dɛɛ ti, ama, am yo i ka am nyunu aboro ka am yo i wɔ.

eng: Dont shepherd by ruling over those entrusted to your care, but become examples to the flock.
local: Bɛrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ afa bu ama am ka am niɛ bu su ni, má am miɛ bu ka am ti bu mibiɛm wɔ. Ama, am ma am sa­yoo­wa kɔ atin su, na bu ŋu i na bu sɔn am su.

eng: And when the chief shepherd appears, you will receive an unfading crown of glory.
local: Nzɛn am yoori sɔ, cɛɛn bo bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpiɛn n bá fiteeri fofoe nu, am nyá aceeri bo i la má aŋusu na i wie má fieo ni.

eng: In the same way, I urge you who are younger: accept the authority of the elders. And everyone, clothe yourselves with humility toward each other. God stands against the proud, but he gives favor to the humble.
local: Ambɛrɛ gusu bo am nyunu nya ti má ni, am bu nkpiɛnm ni, na am de bu nɔaniɛ. Am kɛrɛ yo am ŋu kaan na am buka am beŋgu, dama ba kɛrɛ i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ka, Nyɛmɛ kete kpirikoro­fɔm wɔ, ama, na i fa i ahɔrɔma n ma tarakam.

eng: Therefore, humble yourselves under Gods power so that he may raise you up in the last day.
local: Nyini ti, am da am ŋu asiɛŋgu fa ma Nyɛmɛ yiko­fɔ ni, na saŋga n ba juuri, yiri wúra am kpiri a.

eng: Throw all your anxiety onto him, because he cares about you.
local: Deke kɛrɛ bo i miɛ am, am fa tu Nyɛmɛ nyunu, dama yiri cɛcɛ wo am nu a.

eng: Be clearheaded. Keep alert. Your accuser, the devil, is on the prowl like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
local: Am tara am ŋu saŋga kɛrɛ, má am fa am ŋu ji. Dama am kpɔ­fɔ Sitana ni, i sansan am kabo jara sansan ŋuundi koro sɔnɔ na i kpɛsɛ i n wɔ.

eng: Resist him, standing firm in the faith. Do so in the knowledge that your fellow believers are enduring the same suffering throughout the world.
local: Am yo Nyɛmɛ yarada jina kenken na am kete Sitana, dama am si ka am ninbaam bo bu wo durunya n kɛrɛ nu n gusu sudi wahara n bo am sudi i n be n wɔ.

eng: After you have suffered for a little while, the God of all grace, the one who called you into his eternal glory in Christ Jesus, will himself restore, empower, strengthen, and establish you.
local: Nzɛn am diiri wahara saŋga kaan su, Nyɛmɛ sɛ́sɛ am su yo am dɔŋgu a, yiri má am jina kun su a, na i wúra am faŋga, na i kpáma am. Buka kɛrɛ fite yiri Nyɛmɛ dɔ a, Kristo dɛɛ ti ye wɔ fere am ka am bá nya i dɛɛ jirima woo­wa n bo i la má awieeri n be a.

eng: To him be power forever and always. Amen.
local: Yiri la yiko saŋga kɛrɛ a. Ami.

eng: I have written and sent these few lines to you by Silvanus. I consider him to be a faithful brother. In these lines I have urged and affirmed that this is the genuine grace of God. Stand firm in it.
local: Ya niwa Silivanusi, m si i ka i la dabuŋu am dɔ, yiri bukaari m ye ma kɛrɛ ahin kadasi kaan n sufa ma am n a, na am ahore si, na m ma am si ka jɔrɛ n bo ma kɛrɛ ama am ti Nyɛmɛ aceeri wɔ nahɔrɛ su, na am jina kenken.

eng: The fellow-elect church in Babylon greets you, and so does my son Mark.
local: Kristo jamam bo Nyɛmɛ asaŋga ayi am ni bɛrɛ kɛrɛ, na bu wo Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n nu ni, ni m wa Marako kɛrɛ bisa am dɔŋgu.

eng: Greet each other with the kiss of love. Peace to you all who are in Christ.
local: Am bisa am beŋgu dɔŋgu ni koro. Nyɛmɛ ma ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am ti Kristo dɛɛm ni ahoreda.

eng: From Simon Peter, a slave and apostle of Jesus Christ. To those who received a faith equal to ours through the justice of our God and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Mini Simon Petoro bo m ti Yesu Kristo akɔɔ na m kaaki ti i jaramasa ni, mini sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ama am ayo Kristo yarada n a, kabo wɔ ma yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ayo i yarada ni. Yesu Kristo bo i ti ya Nyɛmɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, i sakpa yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti, ye ya kɛrɛ ayo i yarada.

eng: May you have more and more grace and peace through the knowledge of God and Jesus our Lord.
local: Am Si Nyɛmɛ ni ya Mibiɛ Yesu n dɛɛ ti, Nyɛmɛ buka am agaya, na i ma am ahoreda buka su.

eng: By his divine power the Lord has given us everything we need for life and godliness through the knowledge of the one who called us by his own honor and glory.
local: Nyɛmɛ la yiko, nyini yiko n nu, wɔ ce ya deke kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo daka ka ya la i na ya nya ŋgɔɔ na ya su i nahɔrɛ su. Ya si i n dɛɛ ti, ye wɔ yo nyini n kɛrɛ ama ya n a. Yiri ye wɔ yi ya ka ya nya i woo­wa kpa ni i jirima n a.

eng: Through his honor and glory he has given us his precious and wonderful promises, that you may share the divine nature and escape from the worlds immorality that sinful craving produces.
local: Nyini n dɛɛ ti, wɔ se ka i cé ya aceerim bambaka bo bu la má aŋusu, adena i yi ya fite durunya n nu satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wura minɛ konviki na i saaki bu n nu, na ya nya Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ woo­wa n be.

eng: This is why you must make every effort to add moral excellence to your faith; and to moral excellence, knowledge.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, kabo am ayo Kristo yarada ni, wɔ yo daka ka am boro am nyi, na am yo yarada buka su. Má am yo yarada ŋgumi, ama, am yo sakpa gusu. Má am yo sakpa ŋgumi, ama, am kpini si.

eng: and to knowledge, self-control; and to self-control, endurance; and to endurance, godliness.
local: Má am kpini si ŋgumi, ama, am la tara­buŋu. Má am la tara­buŋu ŋgumi, ama, am la mindi. Má am la mindi ŋgumi, ama, am ma am woo­wa kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin su.

eng: and to godliness, affection for others; and to affection for others, love.
local: Má am ma am woo­wa kɔ Nyɛmɛ atin su ŋgumi, ama, am yo am beŋgu kpa. Má am yo am beŋgu kpa ŋgumi, ama, am koro minɛm kɛrɛ.

eng: If all these are yours and they are growing in you, theyll keep you from becoming inactive and unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Nzɛn am la nyini woo­wam ni, na am woo­wa ti sɔ nahɔrɛ su saŋga kɛrɛ, nyini kere ka, kabo am asi ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo ni, am woo­wa la kpɛrɛ, i ti má ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Whoever lacks these things is shortsighted and blind, forgetting that they were cleansed from their past sins.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la má nyini woo­wam ni, i mi ti ka sɔnɔ bo i koro ŋu má nyunyumi, wara i ti ka anyinsin wɔ, na i kaaki ti ka sɔnɔ bo i wara afi ka Nyɛmɛ akpakpara ayi i afite i satiɛ dawam nu ayo i casi.

eng: Therefore, brothers and sisters, be eager to confirm your call and election. Do this and you will never ever be lost.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, m ninbaam, kabo Nyɛmɛ afere am na wɔ yi am ni, am boro am nyi, adena am woo­wa kere ka am ti i dɛɛ minɛm. Nzɛn am yoori nyinim sɔ, am kpáasi má fieo.

eng: In this way you will receive a rich welcome into the everlasting kingdom of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ.
local: Nyini má am nya aceeri bambaka bo am la atin, na am wura Yesu Kristo dɛɛ fɛmɛya woo­biri n nu a, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ na i fɛmɛya n wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni.

eng: So Ill keep reminding you about these things, although you already know them and stand secure in the truth you have.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i wo m ahore su ka m suteŋge am ahin jɔrɛm su saŋga kɛrɛ n a, haari ni bo am si bu na am ade keree­wam bo bu ti nahɔrɛ n na am bita bu kenken n ni.

eng: I think its right that I keep stirring up your memory, as long as Im alive.
local: Mini dɔ, wɔ yo daka ka m suteŋge am su sɔ, saŋga bo m toko la ŋgɔɔ ni.

eng: After all, our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me that I am about to depart from this life.
local: Dama m si ka i cɛ́ má, m bá wo má bɛrɛ, kabo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo yi kereeri m ni.

eng: Im eager for you always to remember these things after my death.
local: Nyini ma m boro m nyi yo deke kɛrɛ bo m koro yo i n a, adena saŋga bo m bá wo má bɛrɛ, na am koro teŋge nyini jɔrɛm su saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: We didnt repeat crafty myths when we told you about the powerful coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. Quite the contrary, we witnessed his majesty with our own eyes.
local: Ya yo kan kereeri am ka, kabo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo la yiko na i káaki ba barasu ni, bo ya kan sɔ ni, i ti má ka ya faari ŋgɔnlɛ boori ya tiɛɛnu kaanri i a, ama, yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii nyu­mbaa ŋuuri kabo i la yiko bambaka barasu ni.

eng: He received honor and glory from God the Father when a voice came to him from the magnificent glory, saying, This is my dearly loved Son, with whom I am well-pleased.
local: Saŋga bo ya Si Nyɛmɛ yo wuraari i kpiri na i maari i woo­wa taari wein ni, Nyɛmɛ konvi yo fiteeri aŋgoro dika n nu na i jɔjɔɔri i dɔ ni, na yiri bo i woo­wa la má aŋusu n ase ka, M dɛɛ Wa bo m koro i n lɛɛ, m ahore ajɔ i ŋu.

eng: We ourselves heard this voice from heaven while we were with him on the holy mountain.
local: Yɛrɛ ni ya tii tiiri kabo akonvi n fite aŋgoro ni, saŋga bo ya woori i dɔ, Nyɛmɛ buka n su ni.

eng: In addition, we have a most reliable prophetic word, and you would do well to pay attention to it, just as you would to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
local: Nyini n kɛrɛ ma ya la ahoresi buka su, ka jɔrɛm bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm yo kaanri bu n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ. Nyini ti, am boro am nyi na am de kabo bu yo seeri ni, na am su i su. Nɔaniɛ n bo bu jɔjɔɔri i n ti ka fitana bo i taari awosin nu n ti n wɔ, i teke am nyi kɔ ju cɛɛn bo Kristo káaki ba ni, na i fa wein n ba teke am nyi nahɔrɛ su, kabo alecin na asaakojo tiɛ fa wein ba ni.

eng: Most important, you must know that no prophecy of scripture represents the prophets own understanding of things.
local: Jɔrɛ bo i tara bu kɛrɛ na am si i n la ka, nɔaniɛ n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm na bu kɛrɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu ni, be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro fa adamande si ŋgumi na i fa kere nyini nɔaniɛ n bu.

eng: because no prophecy ever came by human will. Instead, men and women led by the Holy Spirit spoke from God.
local: Dama má adamande koroya­nu ye bu kaanri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a, ama, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni maari bu nɔɔ ye bu kaanri nɔaniɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ koro ka bu kan i n a.

eng: But false prophets also arose among the people. In the same way, false teachers will come among you. They will introduce destructive opinions and deny the master who bought them, bringing quick destruction on themselves.
local: Kabo minɛ kɔrɛ­fɔm woori Nyɛmɛ minɛm nu dawa saŋga ni, bɛrɛ bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, sɔ ye kere­fɔ kɔrɛ­fɔm bá wo am gusu nu a. Bu fíɛ wɔ, na bu fá bu dɛɛ keree­wam kere am, keree­wa bo bu ma am mini Nyɛmɛ atin ni, haari na bu káaki bu sin bo Mibiɛ ni, yiri bo wɔ tɔ bu tii su ŋkatɔ ni. Nyini ti, bu sa­yoo­wam má Nyɛmɛ yo bu ŋgbɛɛn anyumbaa kata ni i teke.

eng: Many will follow them in their unrestrained immorality, and because of these false teachers the way of truth will be slandered.
local: Na minɛ dɔŋgu sɔ́n bu sa­yoo­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm su. Bɛrɛ dɛɛ ti, minɛ bá bu atin n bo i ti nahɔrɛ ni ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: In their greed they will take advantage of you with lies. The judgment pronounced against them long ago hasnt fallen idle, nor is their destruction sleeping.
local: Nyini kere­fɔ kɔrɛ­fɔm ni, bu nyunu aboro neŋge su dɛɛ ti, bu bó bu tiɛɛnu jɔrɛ kan ma am a, na bu fá am kpini. Ama, Nyɛmɛ daafi má, wɔ du mɔ adi bu jɔrɛ, i bá yo bu ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: God didnt spare the angels when they sinned but cast them into the lowest level of the underworld and committed them to chains of darkness, keeping them there until the judgment.
local: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm bo bu yo yoori satiɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ ayaki má bu. I faari bu tuuri cɛɛma sin n nu wɔ, na wɔ bo bu tobari ase awosin n nu, ka bu suwo dɔ haari jina cɛɛn n bo i bá cin bu sui ni.

eng: And he didnt spare the ancient world when he brought a flood on the world of ungodly people, even though he protected Noah, a preacher of righteousness, along with seven others.
local: Ya si ka, dawa saŋga, minɛm bo bu soro má Nyɛmɛ ni, wɔ yaki má bu, i cin bu sui wɔ, na wɔ ma wɔmbu bambaka adi bu kɛrɛ, ama, na wɔ de Nowa tii aji, i ni minɛ nso nbem, yiri kan kereeri minɛm deke bo i ti sakpa na i kɔ atin su n a.

eng: God condemned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah to total destruction, reducing them to ashes as a warning to ungodly people.
local: Ya si ka Nyɛmɛ yo saakiiri Sodom ni Gomora miɛm ni, i faari sin jiiri su yaraari bu wɔ, na i kɛrɛ aje atɔ nzuin. I yoori sɔ ni, i kere kabo i bá yo minɛm n bo bu soro má Nyɛmɛ n wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: And he rescued righteous Lot, who was made miserable by the unrestrained immorality of unruly people. .
local: Ya si kabo i yo deeri Loti tii jiiri ni, i yo ti sɔnɔ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, na i ahore yo ti saakii­wa fa kɔ minɛm bo bu bu má deke be na bu nati yo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm ni.

eng: While that righteous man lived among them he felt deep distress every day on account of the immoral actions he saw and heard.
local: Nyini sɔnɔ n bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su ni, yiri ni nyini minɛm ni yo saŋga wo a, cɛɛn kɛrɛ cɛɛn, na i suŋu sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm bo bu yo bu ni, na i sui suti jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn bo bu jɔjɔ bu ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, na wɔ di wahara ahoresaaki nu, yiri bo i ahore ti fofoe ni.

eng: These things show that the Lord knows how to rescue the godly from their trials, and how to keep the unrighteous for punishment on the Judgment Day.
local: Tɔ, nyini n kɛrɛ kere ka Nyɛmɛ si kabo i yó na i dé minɛm bo bu su i atin su n fite kekereke nu a. Na i si kabo i sucin minɛm bo bu su má i atin su n sui, kɔ ju saŋga bo i bá di bu jɔrɛ ni.

eng: This is especially true for those who follow after the corrupt cravings of the sinful nature and defy the Lords authority. These reckless, brash people arent afraid to insult the glorious ones.
local: Bɛrɛ n bo i cín bu sui tara minɛm kɛrɛ n la bo bu su aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa ni daŋgba sam su, na bu kete ka má Nyɛmɛ niɛ bu su ni. Nyini minɛm ni, bu tii ti kekereke, bu soro má be. Haari ŋgɔɔ­fɔm bo bu wo aŋgoro na bu la jirima woo­wa ni, bu kpiɛ bu nzukɔ ɔ, bu soro má sɛrɛ.

eng: yet angels, who are stronger and more powerful, dont use insults when pronouncing the Lords judgment on them.
local: Haari Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm bo bu la yiko ni faŋga tara nyini minɛm ni, bu kpiɛ má nyini ŋgɔɔ­fɔm ni nzukɔ na bu jaraki má bu kere má Mikun ni.

eng: These false teachers are like irrational animals, mere creatures of instinct, born to be captured and destroyed. They slander what they dont understand and, like animals, they will be destroyed.
local: Nyini minɛm sɔ, kabo bu koro, ye bu yo a, bu ti ka boro neŋge tiɛm bo minɛ tara bu na bu kpiɛ bu kɔm n wɔ. Bu kpiɛ nzukɔ fa kɔ dekem n bo bu si má bu n su wɔ. Kabo minɛ ma boro neŋge tiɛm ka ŋgbɛɛn ni, sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ má bu gusu bá ka ŋgbɛɛn a.

eng: In this way, they will receive payment for their wrongdoing. They even enjoy unruly parties in broad daylight. They are blots and blemishes, taking delight in their seductive pleasures while feasting with you.
local: Kabo bu ma minɛm di wahara ni, bɛrɛ gusu bá di wahara sɔ ɔ. Bu konvikiya sam bo bu yo bu ni, i yo bu fiɛ wɔ, bu yo bu wiɛsu wɔ, bu fiɛ má su. Bu ti daŋgba­fɔm wɔ, na bu wura am nyinsɛ saŋga bo am ni bɛrɛ tiɛn am nɔɔ sudi diire ni, bu sunsunni kɛrɛ la ka bu daka am.

eng: They are always looking for someone with whom to commit adultery. They are always on the lookout for opportunities to sin. They ensnare people whose faith is weak. They have hearts trained in greed. They are under Gods curse.
local: Bu nyunu boro minɛ yim kpini su wɔ, na bu kunnu yi má bu satiɛ­yo. Bu dadaka minɛm bo bu nya si má Nyɛmɛ atin n nyunyumi n wɔ, na bu yaki i, adena bu su bu dɛɛ keree­wam n su. Bu ahore­mbaa abi neŋge kpini su wɔ. Nyɛmɛ ayo bu ŋgbɛɛn awie lɛɛ.

eng: Leaving the straight path, they have gone off course, following the way of Balaam son of Bosor, who loved the payment of doing wrong.
local: Ba yaki atin n bo i ti kpa ni, ba mini. Atin n bo Baalam bo i si la Bɛori n yo bitaari i ni, ba sɔn i su lɛɛ. I yo koroori ŋwaa bo minɛm ma i n wɔ, na i yo satiɛ.

eng: But Balaam was rebuked for his wrongdoing. A donkey, which has no voice, spoke with a human voice and put a stop to the prophets madness.
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ akan i ndiɛ, i satiɛ yoo­wa n dɛɛ ti. Ya si ka kaako koro jɔjɔ má, ama, Nyɛmɛ ama kaako ajɔjɔ Baalam dɔ, kabo adamande jɔjɔ ni. Na Baalam bo i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ni, na wɔ yaki sa n bo má i suyo i na i ti ka i nyunu ti má koroo­wa ni.

eng: These false teachers are springs without water, mists driven by the wind. The underworld has been reserved for them.
local: Nyini minɛm ti ka bura bo i tiɛ má nzue wɔ, na bu kaaki ti ka nzue munaa­wa bo aŋuma fa i sin n wɔ. Bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ awosin n bo i la má aŋusu ase bu n a.

eng: With empty, self-important speech, they use sinful cravings and unrestrained immorality to ensnare people who have only just escaped life with those who have wandered from the truth.
local: Bu fɔ yanfɔya a, na bu sujɔjɔ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, na bu fa aŋuniɛ koroo­wa na bu fa dadaka minɛm bo ba kaba afite kɔrɛ jɔrɛm nu na ba nya acɛ má ni.

eng: These false teachers promise freedom, but they themselves are slaves of immorality; whatever overpowers you, enslaves you.
local: Bu se minɛm ka bu má bu nya bu tii wɔ. Ama, bɛrɛ ni bu tii bo bu kan sɔ ni, bu ti ka akɔɔm wɔ, satiɛ bo i ma bu ka ŋgbɛɛn ni aja bu, dama deke bo wɔ kunma sɔnɔ ni, i mi n ti nyini deke n akɔɔ lɛɛ.

eng: If people escape the moral filth of this world through the knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ, then get tangled up in it again and are overcome by it, they are worse off than they were before.
local: Minɛm bo bu si Yesu Kristo ni, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, na i ma ba yaki durunya n nu dɛɛ daŋgba sam ni, na ba kaaki akpie bu nyi ama nyini sam atara bu akunma bu bekun ni, bu awieeri ti tiɛ tara bumboori ni.

eng: It would be better for them never to have known the way of righteousness than, having come to know it, to turn back from the holy commandment entrusted to them.
local: Nzɛn má ba si má woo­wa bo i kɔ atin su ni, má i ti ye tara bo ba kaaki bu sin abo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo ba du mɔ ade i ni.

eng: They demonstrate the truth of the proverb: A dog returns to its own vomit, and a washed sow wallows in the mud.
local: Kabo bu yoori sɔ ni, nyini kere ka ahin anyundɛrɛm ni ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo bu ŋa, Caa kaaki fa deke n bo wɔ fi i n di bekun wɔ, na bekun, Nzɛn a taraari afa biɛɛri i nzue, i kaaki kɔ wura natiɛ nu wɔ bekun.

eng: My dear friends, this is now my second letter to you. I have written both letters to stir up your sincere understanding with a reminder.
local: M danfo kpam, ahin kadasi ni ti kadasi nnyɔ su bo m sukɛrɛ ma am a. Ahin kadasi nnyɔ n kɛrɛ, m koro ka m teŋge am su na am bita sunsunni kpa.

eng: I want you to recall what the holy prophets foretold as well as what the Lord and savior commanded through your apostles.
local: M koro ka am teŋge su fa kɔ nɔaniɛm bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm yo seeri bu dawa saŋga ni, na am kaaki teŋge su fa kɔ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo Mibiɛ n bo i ti ya Tii­de­fɔ n kaanri i, na i jaramasam seeri am ni.

eng: Most important, know this: in the last days scoffers will come, jeering, living by their own cravings.
local: Jɔrɛ bo i tara bu kɛrɛ na am si i n la ka, fa kɔ durunya n awieeri su, minɛ nbem bá ba, na bu susu aŋuniɛ­mbaa koroo­wa su, na bu yó am anzasi fa kɔ am yarada n su.

eng: and saying, Where is the promise of his coming? After all, nothing has changed—not since the beginning of creation, nor even since the ancestors died.
local: na bu sé ka, I seeri ka i káaki ba bekun, ye i wo ni? Ya nam wo má bɛrɛ kun, ama, deke n kɛrɛ toko ti i tii wɔ, kabo Nyɛmɛ yiiri durunya n ba ju ni nyuma.

eng: But they fail to notice that, by Gods word, heaven and earth were formed long ago out of water and by means of water.
local: Bu si tenle su, ama, ba kete ka bu teŋge má su ka, dada saŋga, Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔɔ jɔjɔɔri ye aŋgoro wo bɛrɛ a, na wɔ jɔjɔ na wɔ kpaaki nzue n ni asiɛn n nu, na asiɛn n afite nzue n nu.

eng: And it was through these that the world of that time was flooded and destroyed.
local: Nyini nzue n ye i fa saakiiri durunya n a dawa saŋga, saŋga bo wɔmbu nzue n diiri deke n kɛrɛ ni.

eng: But by the same word, heaven and earth are now held in reserve for fire, kept for the Judgment Day and destruction of ungodly people.
local: Ama, aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu n bo i wo bɛrɛ ni, Nyɛmɛ yo jɔjɔɔri i nɔaniɛ bekun wɔ, ka, i bita bu wɔ, na bu wó bɛrɛ haari saŋga bo i bá fa sin saaki i kɛrɛ. I má i kɛrɛ wo bɛrɛ wɔ, kɔ ju saŋga bo i bá di minɛm jɔrɛ, bɛrɛ bo bu soro má i ni, na bu ká ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: Dont let it escape your notice, dear friends, that with the Lord a single day is like a thousand years and a thousand years are like a single day.
local: M danfo kpam, am si tenle na má am wara fi ahin jɔrɛ kun ni, yiri la ka, cɛɛn kun ti ka afɔ akpii wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, na afɔ akpii gusu ti ka cɛɛn kun wɔ i dɔ.

eng: The Lord isnt slow to keep his promise, as some think of slowness, but he is patient toward you, not wanting anyone to perish but all to change their hearts and lives.
local: Kabo Micɛra n seeri ka i yo ni, minɛ nbem bita kan ka i ti niniki. I ti má ka i ti niniki wɔ, ama, i la mindi ni ambɛrɛ wɔ, i koro má ka be ka ŋgbɛɛn, ama, i koro ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ kaaki i wo wɔ.

eng: But the day of the Lord will come like a thief. On that day the heavens will pass away with a dreadful noise, the elements will be consumed by fire, and the earth and all the works done on it will be exposed.
local: Ama, Micɛra n cɛɛn ni, i bá kabo awiefɔ ba fura nu na i wɔ n wɔ. Nyini cɛɛn ni, aŋgoro ni yó naŋmiɛ faŋga su, na i busu míni, na sin yára deke kɛrɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni, na i ká ŋgbɛɛn. Na asiɛn n ni deke kɛrɛ bo i wo asiɛn n su n bá wo má bɛrɛ.

eng: Since everything will be destroyed in this way, what sort of people ought you to be? You must live holy and godly lives.
local: Kabo nyini deke n kɛrɛ bá ka ŋgbɛɛn sɔ ni, wɔ yo daka ka am ti nzu minɛ barasum wɔ? Wɔ yo daka ka am woo­wa ti casi, na am fa am ŋu ma Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi.

eng: waiting for and hastening the coming day of God. Because of that day, the heavens will be destroyed by fire and the elements will melt away in the flames.
local: na am ŋminda fa kɔ Nyɛmɛ cɛɛn ni, na am boro am nyi yo sa bo i ma i ba ndende. Nyini cɛɛn n ye sin yára aŋgoro n kɛrɛ na i ká ŋgbɛɛn n a, na i kɛkɛrɛ má deke n kɛrɛ bo i wo aŋgoro n koro.

eng: But according to his promise we are waiting for a new heaven and a new earth, where righteousness is at home.
local: Ama, ya ŋminda sa bo Nyɛmɛ yo seeri ka i bá yo ni, ka, i yí aŋgoro ni asiɛn fɔfɔrɛ, dɔ ye bu nati yo sa bo i kɔ atin su ŋgumi a.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, while you are waiting for these things to happen, make every effort to be found by him in peace—pure and faultless.
local: Tɔ, m danfo kpam, kabo am ŋminda fa kɔ nyini cɛɛn n ni, am boro am nyi, adena Nyɛmɛ bá ŋu ka am ni yiri nɔɔ ayo kun, na am woo­wa ti casi na am la má ŋgasi be i dɔ.

eng: Consider the patience of our Lord to be salvation, just as our dear friend and brother Paul wrote to you according to the wisdom given to him.
local: Am teŋge su ka ya Mibiɛ mindi n dɛɛ ti, ye minɛ nyá tii­de a. Sɔ ye ya niwa koroo­wa Pɔɔlu yo kɛrɛ maari am a, Nyɛmɛ ceeri i alecira n a.

eng: speaking of these things in all his letters. Some of his remarks are hard to understand, and people who are ignorant and whose faith is weak twist them to their own destruction, just as they do the other scriptures.
local: Wɔ kɛrɛ ahin n be sɔ i kadasi n kɛrɛ n nu, ama, jɔrɛ nbem wo nu, bo i ti kekereke na sɔnɔ ti i bu. Nyini ye minɛm bo bu si má be na bu nya bi má Nyɛmɛ su atin su ni, nbem fa bu dɛɛ ŋgɔnlɛ kaaki i, ye i kɔ má atin su n a, kabo bu kaaki kɛrɛɛ­wa nyiŋgam bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ Jɔrɛ Kadasi n nu n lɛɛ. Kabo bu yo sɔ ni, nyini ma bu ka ŋgbɛɛn a.

eng: Therefore, dear friends, since you have been warned in advance, be on guard so that you arent led off course into the error of sinful people, and lose your own safe position.
local: M danfo kpam, ambɛrɛ, am du mɔ si nyini ni. Nyini ti, am si am ŋu sa, adena má am sɔn minɛm bo bu bu má be n su, na am mini atin n bo am su i su ni.

eng: Instead, grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and savior Jesus Christ. To him belongs glory now and forever. Amen.
local: Ama, am kɔ su, na am subita Yesu Kristo dɛɛ atin ni, yiri bo i ti ya Mibiɛ ni ya Tii­de­fɔ ni, adena am koro di i ahɔrɔma, na am si i buka su. Yiri Yesu Kristo ni, i ti ye ni ka minɛm kɛrɛ wura i kpiri, kisa ni saŋga kɛrɛ. Ami.

eng: We announce to you what existed from the beginning, what we have heard, what we have seen with our eyes, what we have seen and our hands handled, about the word of life.
local: Ya sukɛrɛ ma am fa kɔ nɔaniɛ n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n su wɔ. Kana Nyɛmɛ ba yi durunya n ni, nyini nɔaniɛ n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n woori bɛrɛ wɔ. Deke bo ya ti i, na ya nyu­mbaa aŋu i, na ya niɛ aŋu i kabo i ti barasu, na ya fa ya saa akan i, ye ya sukɛrɛ fa ma am n a.

eng: The life was revealed, and we have seen, and we testify and announce to you the eternal life that was with the Father and was revealed to us.
local: Nyini nɔaniɛ n la Yesu bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni, i yo fiteeri fofoe nu wɔ, na ya nyu­mbaa aŋu i, nyini ti ye ya di ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n daani kan i kere am n a. Yiri bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri n yo woori i Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, ye wɔ yi i ŋu akere ya a.

eng: What we have seen and heard, we also announce it to you so that you can have fellowship with us. Our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
local: Deke n bo ya ŋu i, na ya ti i ni, ye ya sukan i kere am n a, adena ya ni ambɛrɛ la wo fiɛ ni ya beŋgu, na ya kɛrɛ la wo fiɛ ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ ni i Wa Yesu Kristo ni.

eng: We are writing these things so that our joy can be complete.
local: Deke n bo i ti na ya sukɛrɛ ahin dekem ma am n la ka, adena ya nya ahorejɔ nahɔrɛ su.

eng: This is the message that we have heard from him and announce to you: God is light and there is no darkness in him at all.
local: Ahin labari n bo ya yo tiiri i Nyɛmɛ Wa n dɔ n lɛɛ, ye ya sukan kere am n a, ka Nyɛmɛ ti wein wɔ, na awosin haari kaan sɔ wo má i dɔ.

eng: If we claim, We have fellowship with him, and live in the darkness, we are lying and do not act truthfully.
local: Nzɛn ya ŋa ya ni yiri la wo fiɛ ni ya beŋgu, ama, na ya toko wo awosin nu yo satiɛ, ya ti kɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ lɛɛ, ya bita má nahɔrɛ.

eng: But if we live in the light in the same way as he is in the light, we have fellowship with each other, and the blood of Jesus, his Son, cleanses us from every sin.
local: Ama, nzɛn ya bita wein atin kabo yiri Nyɛmɛ atin n ti wein ni, ya ni yiri la wo fiɛ ni ya beŋgu lɛɛ, na i Wa Yesu mbunja ni kpakpara yi ya satiɛ kɛrɛ bo i wo ya ŋu ni. Nyini ti, am ma ya bita wein n atin ni.

eng: If we claim, We dont have any sin, we deceive ourselves and the truth is not in us.
local: Nzɛn ya ŋa ya ti má satiɛ­yo­fɔm, ya dadaka ya ŋu wɔ lɛɛ, na nahɔrɛ wo má ya ahore su.

eng: But if we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and cleanse us from everything weve done wrong.
local: Ama, nzɛn ya kan ya satiɛ kereeri Nyɛmɛ, i fá ya satiɛ n ce ya a, na i kpákpara yi ya fite ya sa kɛrɛ bo i kɔ má atin su nu, dama i la dabuŋu, na i yo sa bo i kɔ atin n su. Nyini ti, am ma ya kan ya satiɛ kɛrɛ kere Nyɛmɛ.

eng: If we claim, We have never sinned, we make him a liar and his word is not in us.
local: Nzɛn ya ŋa ya yo má satiɛ, ya yi Nyɛmɛ kɔrɛ­fɔ lɛɛ, na i nɔaniɛ n wo má ya ahore su lɛɛ.

eng: My little children, Im writing these things to you so that you dont sin. But if you do sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous one.
local: M nbaam, m koro má ka am yo satiɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti ye m sukɛrɛ ahin jɔrɛ n ma am n a, ama, nzɛn am be yoori satiɛ, ya la sɔnɔ be bo i yo ya ni ya Si Nyɛmɛ afiɛn dɔŋgu, yiri la Yesu Kristo bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin su saŋga kɛrɛ ni.

eng: He is Gods way of dealing with our sins, not only ours but the sins of the whole world.
local: Ya satiɛ ti, na yiri Yesu ade ya jinaa­biri, na wɔ fa i ŋu ayo tiɛ afa ama Nyɛmɛ, adena Nyɛmɛ fá ya satiɛ ce ya, má yɛrɛ ŋgumi dɛɛ satiɛ, ama, durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ dɛɛ satiɛ.

eng: This is how we know that we know him: if we keep his commandments.
local: Am ma ya de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, nyini kere ka ya si yiri Nyɛmɛ lɛɛ.

eng: The one who claims, I know him, while not keeping his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in this person.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa, i si Nyɛmɛ, na i de má i nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, i mi ti kɔrɛ­fɔ ɔ, na nahɔrɛ wo má i ahore su.

eng: But the love of God is truly perfected in whoever keeps his word. This is how we know we are in him.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni, i mi ti sɔnɔ bo i koro Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su a. Na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i wo ni Nyɛmɛ, wɔ yo daka ka i mi wo kabo Yesu wo ti ni. Nyini ye ya fa si ka ya wo ni Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: Dear friends, Im not writing a new commandment to you, but an old commandment that you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the message you heard.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, má nɔaniɛ maa­wa fɔfɔrɛ ye m sukɛrɛ ma am n a, ama, nɔaniɛ maa­wa dawa n bo am du mɔ tiiri i n a. Nyini nɔaniɛ maa­wa dawa n la jɔrɛ n bo am yo tiiri i saŋga bo am boori i bu suuri Yesu su ni.

eng: On the other hand, I am writing a new commandment to you, which is true in him and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light already shines.
local: Tɔ, ama, nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo m sukɛrɛ ma am ni, i kaaki ti nɔaniɛ maa­wa fɔfɔrɛ wɔ bekun, na ya ŋu ka i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, fite kabo Yesu Kristo wo ti ni, ni kabo am wo ti ni, dama awosin n susin, ye wein nahɔrɛ su wo ta su.

eng: The one who claims to be in the light while hating a brother or sister is in the darkness even now.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i wo wein n nu, na i kpɔ i niwa, i mi toko wo awosin nu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: The person loving a brother and sister stays in the light, and there is nothing in the light that causes a person to stumble.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i niwa wo wein n nu wɔ, i yo má sa be bo i ma i niwa bo i jaa kpaasi.

eng: But the person who hates a brother or sister is in the darkness and lives in the darkness, and doesnt know where to go because the darkness blinds the eyes.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kpɔ i niwa, i mi wo awosin nu wɔ, na i suyo satiɛm, dama awosin n ata i nyi, nyini ti, ye i si má dekem bo i yo bu n a.

eng: Little children, Im writing to you because your sins have been forgiven through Jesus name.
local: M nbaam, deke bo i ti na m sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma am n la ka, Nyɛmɛ afa am satiɛ ace am, Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti.

eng: Parents, Im writing to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, Im writing to you because you have conquered the evil one.
local: Sim, deke bo i ti na m sukɛrɛ ma ambɛrɛ n la ka, am si Yesu, yiri bo i wo bɛrɛ kana Nyɛmɛ ba yiiri durunya n kɛrɛ ni. Ngbɛfɛnɛm, deke bo i ti na m sukɛrɛ ma ambɛrɛ n la ka, am akunma Sitana n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Little children, I write to you because you know the Father. Parents, I write to you because you have known the one who has existed from the beginning. Young people, I write to you because you are strong, the word of God remains in you, and you have conquered the evil one.
local: M nbaam, deke bo i ti na m toko kɛrɛ ma am n la ka, am si ya Si Nyɛmɛ n dɛɛ ti. Sim, deke bo i ti na m toko kɛrɛ ma am n la ka, am si Yesu bo i wo bɛrɛ durunya n bumboori n kɛrɛ ti. Ngbɛfɛnɛm, deke bo i ti na m toko kɛrɛ ma am n la ka, am yarada n ti faŋga, na Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n wo am ahore su, na am akunma Sitana n dɛɛ ti.

eng: Dont love the world or the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in them.
local: Tɔ, má am koro durunya n ni, ni deke n kɛrɛ bo bu wo nu ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro durunya ni, i mi koro má ya Si Nyɛmɛ lɛɛ.

eng: Everything that is in the world—the craving for whatever the body feels, the craving for whatever the eyes see and the arrogant pride in ones possessions—is not of the Father but is of the world.
local: Dama deke n kɛrɛ bo i wo durunya n nu fɛn na i wura sɔnɔ konvi, ni deke bo sɔnɔ nyu­mbaa ŋu i na i koro i, ni deke bo sɔnɔ nya i na i fa fɔ, i fite má ya Si Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ama, i kɛrɛ fite durunya n nu fɛn wɔ.

eng: And the world and its cravings are passing away, but the person who does the will of God remains forever.
local: Durunya n ni deke kɛrɛ bo i wura minɛ konvi kɛrɛ la awieeri, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo Nyɛmɛ sa koroo­wa, i nyá ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri.

eng: Little children, it is the last hour. Just as you have heard that the antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have appeared. This is how we know it is the last hour.
local: M nbaam, durunya ni sukɔ awieeri lɛɛ. Am yo tiiri ka nzɛn durunya n ba sukɔ awieeri, Kristo kpɔ­fɔ ni bá, ama, kisa Kristo kpɔ­fɔm dɔŋgu ayi bu ŋu fofoe nu. Nyini ti, ya si ka durunya ni sukɔ awieeri lɛɛ.

eng: They went out from us, but they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But by going out from us, they showed they all are not part of us.
local: Nyini minɛm n bo bu ti Kristo kpɔ­fɔm na ba fite ya nu ni, nahɔrɛ su, bu ti má ya minɛm. Nzɛn má bu ti ya minɛm, má bu ka ya dɔ ɔ. Ama, ba fite ya nu, nyini ye ya si ka, bu be mmɔɔ ti má ya minɛm a.

eng: But you have an anointing from the holy one, and all of you know the truth.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, Kristo n afa i Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura am ahore su, na am si deke bo i ti nahɔrɛ ni.

eng: I dont write to you because you dont know the truth but because you know it. You know that no lie comes from the truth.
local: Má ka am si má nahɔrɛ n dɛɛ ti, ye m sukɛrɛ ma am n a, ama, am si i, na am si ka kɔrɛ koro yo má nahɔrɛ.

eng: Who is the liar? Isnt it the person who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This person is the antichrist: the one who denies the Father and the Son.
local: Ŋma ti kɔrɛ­fɔm kɛrɛ tii n a? Kɔrɛ­fɔ n la sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa Yesu ti má Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ sunmaari i ni, yiri n bo i yo sɔ n akete ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni i Wa n lɛɛ, i mi ni ti Kristo kpɔ­fɔ n a.

eng: Everyone who denies the Son does not have the Father, but the one who confesses the Son has the Father also.
local: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, akete i Si Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ lɛɛ. Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa Kristo ti Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, i mi n ade i Si Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ lɛɛ.

eng: As for you, what you heard from the beginning must remain in you. If what you heard from the beginning remains in you, you will also remain in relationship to the Son and in the Father.
local: Nyini ti, nɔaniɛ n bo am tiiri i saŋga bo am boori i bu suuri Kristo su ni, am ma i wo am ahore su. Nzɛn am bita nyini nɔaniɛ ni am ahore su, nyini ma am ni Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, ni i Si n yo deke kun a.

eng: This is the promise that he himself gave us: eternal life.
local: Deke bo Kristo seeri ka i fá ma ya n la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: I write these things to you about those who are attempting to deceive you.
local: M sukɛrɛ ahin nɔaniɛ n ma am, na am si am ŋu sa fa kɔ minɛm bo bu koro ka bu dadaka am ni.

eng: As for you, the anointing that you received from him remains on you, and you dont need anyone to teach you the truth. But since his anointing teaches you about all things , remain in relationship to him just as he taught you.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ yiri, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo Kristo afa ama am wo am dɔ. Nyini ti, i ti má ye ni ka be fa keree­wa be kere am kun, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni kere am deke kɛrɛ a, na deke kɛrɛ bo i kere am ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, kɔrɛ wo má nu. Nyini dɛɛ ti, am wo ni Kristo kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n akere am ni.

eng: And now, little children, remain in relationship to Jesus, so that when he appears we can have confidence and not be ashamed in front of him when he comes.
