local: M nbaam, kisa am wo ni Kristo, na cɛɛn n bo i ba yiiri i ŋu fiteeri fofoe nu n na ya la ahoresi, na má nyinsɛ ti ya.

eng: If you know that he is righteous, you also know that every person who practices righteousness is born from him.
local: Am si ka Yesu yo sa bo i kɔ atin n su, nyini ti, am si ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sa bo i kɔ atin n su, i ti Nyɛmɛ wa a.

eng: See what kind of love the Father has given to us in that we should be called Gods children, and that is what we are! Because the world didnt recognize him, it doesnt recognize us.
local: Am niɛ koro bo ya Si Nyɛmɛ fa koro ya barasu a. Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye i fere ya ka i nbaam n a, i ti nahɔrɛ ka ya ti i nbaam wɔ. Durunya n nu­fɔm si má ya Si Nyɛmɛ, nyini dɛɛ ti, ye bu si má ka ya ti i nbaam n a.

eng: Dear friends, now we are Gods children, and it hasnt yet appeared what we will be. We know that when he appears we will be like him because well see him as he is.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, kisa ya ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam wɔ, na Nyɛmɛ nya yi kere má ya kabo ya bá ti barasu, ama, ya si ka Yesu bá baari, ya tí kabo i ti n wɔ, dama ya ŋú i kabo i ti barasu.

eng: And all who have this hope in him purify themselves even as he is pure.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la tama ka i bá yo kabo Yesu ti ni, i bita i ŋu casi, kabo Yesu ti casi ni.

eng: Every person who practices sin commits an act of rebellion, and sin is rebellion.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ, i mi ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kete­fɔ ɔ, dama satiɛ­yo ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kpamaa­wa n kete wɔ.

eng: You know that he appeared to take away sins, and there is no sin in him.
local: Am si ka Yesu baari durunya n nu, ka i ba yi satiɛ bo i wo ya ŋu n wɔ, ama, yiri ni i tii la má satiɛ be.

eng: Every person who remains in relationship to him does not sin. Any person who sins has not seen him or known him.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo ni Yesu, koro suyo má satiɛ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i toko yo satiɛ, nyini kere ka, wɔ nya ŋu má i lɛɛ, na i mi kaaki si má i.

eng: Little children, make sure no one deceives you. The person who practices righteousness is righteous, in the same way that Jesus is righteous.
local: M nbaam, má am ma be dadaka am. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sakpa, Nyɛmɛ de i ka i mi yo sa bo i kɔ atin su i dɔ, kabo Yesu ti ni.

eng: The person who practices sin belongs to the devil, because the devil has been sinning since the beginning. Gods Son appeared for this purpose: to destroy the works of the devil.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ, i mi ti Sitana dɛɛ sɔnɔ ɔ, dama fite durunya n bumboori n kɛrɛ ye Sitana n yoori satiɛ a. Deke bo i ti na Nyɛmɛ Wa n baari durunya n nu la ka, i ba yo Sitana n juma ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: Those born from God dont practice sin because Gods DNA remains in them. They cant sin because they are born from God.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti Nyɛmɛ wa koro suyo má satiɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ woo­wa n wo i mi dɔ, na Nyɛmɛ ti i Si n dɛɛ ti, i koro suyo má satiɛ.

eng: This is how Gods children and the devils children are apparent: everyone who doesnt practice righteousness is not from God, including the person who doesnt love a brother or sister.
local: Deke bo ya fa si minɛm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam, ni bo bu ti Sitana n nbaam n la ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo má sakpa, wara sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má i niwa, i mi ti má Nyɛmɛ wa.

eng: This is the message that you heard from the beginning: love each other.
local: Jɔrɛ n bo am tiiri i saŋga bo am boori i bu suuri Yesu su n la ka, am ma ya koro ya beŋgum.

eng: Dont behave like Cain, who belonged to the evil one and murdered his brother. And why did he kill him? He killed him because his own works were evil, but the works of his brother were righteous.
local: Má am ma ya yo ka Keen, bo i yo ti Sitana dɛɛ wa ni, na i kuunri i niɛma ni. Nzu ti ye i kuunri i niɛma n a? I sa­yoo­wa ti má kpa n dɛɛ ti, na i niɛma n dɛɛ n ti kpa n ti.

eng: Dont be surprised, brothers and sisters, if the world hates you.
local: Nyini ti, m ninbaam, nzɛn durunya n nu­fɔm kpɔ am ka am yo sakpa n dɛɛ ti, má am ma i yo am alekutura.

eng: We know that we have transferred from death to life, because we love the brothers and sisters. The person who does not love remains in death.
local: Kabo yɛrɛ koro ya ninbaam n ti, nyini ma ya si ka ya fite yiwee n nu anya ŋgɔɔ nahɔrɛ su a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má i niwa toko wo yiwee nu wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Everyone who hates a brother or sister is a murderer, and you know that murderers dont have eternal life residing in them.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kpɔ i niwa ti sɔnɔ kun­fɔ ɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Am si ka sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kun sɔnɔ la má ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: This is how we know love: Jesus laid down his life for us, and we ought to lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.
local: Yesu deeri ya dɛɛ jinaa­biri wɔ, na wɔ tɔ i ŋgɔɔ awu ama ya, nyini ma ya fa si kabo koro ti barasu a. Nyini ti, wɔ yo daka ka ya gusu tɔ ya ŋgɔɔ ma ya ninbaam, haari nzɛn i yo yiwee mmɔɔ.

eng: But if someone has material possessions and sees a brother or sister in need but refuses to help—how can the love of God dwell in a person like that?.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la neŋge, na i ŋu ka i niwa wo yaari nu, na i si má i aŋunfɔ ka i buka i, Nyɛmɛ koro wo má i mi n ahore su lɛɛ.

eng: Little children, lets not love with words or speech but with action and truth.
local: M nbaam, má am ma ya jɔjɔɔ­wa n ŋgumi kere ka ya la koro, ama, am ma ya sa­yoo­wa kere ka ya koro ya ninbaam nahɔrɛ su.

eng: This is how we will know that we belong to the truth and reassure our hearts in Gods presence.
local: Nyini ma ya fa si ka ya su nahɔrɛ n su a, na i ma ya la ahoresi Nyɛmɛ dɔ a.

eng: Even if our hearts condemn us, God is greater than our hearts and knows all things.
local: na nzɛn ya sunsunni maari ya ka ya yo satiɛ, ya si ka Nyɛmɛ tara ya sunsunni ni, na i kaaki si deke n kɛrɛ.

eng: Dear friends, if our hearts dont condemn us, we have confidence in relationship to God.
local: Nyini ti, m ninbaa koroo­wam, nzɛn ya sunsunni ase má ya ka ya yo satiɛ, ya la ahoresi Nyɛmɛ dɔ lɛɛ.

eng: We receive whatever we ask from him because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him.
local: Na deke kɛrɛ bo ya sereeri i, ya nya i wɔ, dama ya de nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari ya ni, na ya yo sa bo i sun i nu.

eng: This is his commandment, that we believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love each other as he commanded us.
local: Nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo Nyɛmɛ fa maari ya n la ka, ya yo i Wa Yesu Kristo yarada, na ya koro ya ninbaam ni, kabo wɔ se ya ni.

eng: Those who keep his commandments dwell in God and God dwells in them. This is how we know that he dwells in us, because of the Spirit he has given us.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam n wo ni Nyɛmɛ, na Nyɛmɛ mmɔɔ wo ni i mi ni. Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo wɔ fa ama ya ni, ma ya si ka Nyɛmɛ wo ni yɛrɛ a.

eng: Dear friends, dont believe every spirit. Test the spirits to see if they are from God because many false prophets have gone into the world.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, má am yo minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ŋa Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n wo bu dɔ n yarada, ama, am yo bu na am niɛ ka bu keree­wa n fite Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɔ, nzɛn i fite má. Dama kɔrɛ­fɔm dɔŋgu bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n asandi abara ayɛ durunya n nu.

eng: This is how you know if a spirit comes from God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come as a human is from God.
local: Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ kɛrɛ bo i se ka Yesu Kristo kaakiiri adamande baari durunya n nu fɛn, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n wo i dɔ a. Nyini ye am fa si bo Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo bu dɔ, ni bo i wo má bu dɔ a.

eng: and every spirit that doesnt confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, which you have heard is coming and is now already in the world.
local: Ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i keteeri ka, Yesu akaaki má adamande ni, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n wo má i mi dɔ, ama, i dɛɛ keree­wa n fite Kristo kpɔ­fɔ n dɔ ɔ. Yiri ye am yo tiiri ka i bá durunya n nu, ye kisa wɔ ba n a.

eng: You are from God, little children, and you have defeated these people because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
local: M nbaam, am ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛm wɔ, ye am akunma nyini kɔrɛ­fɔm bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kan­fɔm ni, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i wo am nu n la yiko tara Sitana n bo i wo durunya n nu­fɔm nu ni.

eng: They are from the world. So they speak from the worlds point of view and the world listens to them.
local: Nyini kɔrɛ­fɔm bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, bu kan durunya n nu jɔrɛ kere minɛm a, na durunya n nu­fɔm de kabo bu kere ni, dama bu ti durunya n nu­fɔm wɔ.

eng: We are from God. The person who knows God listens to us. Whoever is not from God doesnt listen to us. This is how we recognize the Spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
local: Ama, yɛrɛ yiri, ya ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛm wɔ, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i si Nyɛmɛ de kabo ya kere n wɔ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti má Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ de má kabo ya kere ni. Nyini ye ya fa si minɛm bo bu la Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ n bo i kere nahɔrɛ n a, ni bo bu bu kɔrɛ n fite Sitana dɔ n a.

eng: Dear friends, lets love each other, because love is from God, and everyone who loves is born from God and knows God.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, am ma ya koro ya niwa, dama koro fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i beŋgu ti Nyɛmɛ wa a, na i mi si Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: The person who doesnt love does not know God, because God is love.
local: Nyɛmɛ ti koro wɔ, nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro má i beŋgu si má Nyɛmɛ.

eng: This is how the love of God is revealed to us: God has sent his only Son into the world so that we can live through him.
local: Kabo Nyɛmɛ kereeri i koro fa kɔ ya dɔ n la ka o, wɔ sunma i kunnu Wa kun n kpein durunya n nu, adena yiri dɛɛ ti na ya nya ŋgɔɔ.

eng: This is love: it is not that we loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son as the sacrifice that deals with our sins.
local: Kabo koro ti lɛɛ, má ka yɛrɛ koroori Nyɛmɛ a, ama, yiri koroori ya a, na wɔ sunma i Wa, na wɔ de ya jinaa­biri na wɔ fa i ŋu ayo tiɛ afa ama Nyɛmɛ, adena Nyɛmɛ fa ya satiɛ ce ya.

eng: Dear friends, if God loved us this way, we also ought to love each other.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ya sɔ ni, wɔ yo daka ka ya gusu koro ya beŋgu.

eng: No one has ever seen God. If we love each other, God remains in us and his love is made perfect in us.
local: Be nya ŋu má Nyɛmɛ, ama, nzɛn ya koroori ya beŋgu, Nyɛmɛ wo ya dɔ lɛɛ, na nyini ma i dɛɛ koro n wo ya dɔ na i ti kabo i koro ka i ti n a.

eng: This is how we know we remain in him and he remains in us, because he has given us a measure of his Spirit.
local: Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama ya ni, nyini ma ya si ka ya wo ni yiri a, na i gusu wo ni yɛrɛ a.

eng: We have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son to be the savior of the world.
local: Na yɛrɛ aŋu kabo ya Si Nyɛmɛ n asunma i Wa n ka i ba yo durunya n nu­fɔm Tii­de­fɔ, ye ya di i daani a.

eng: If any of us confess that Jesus is Gods Son, God remains in us and we remain in God.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i tekeeri i nɔɔ seeri ka Yesu ti Nyɛmɛ Wa, Nyɛmɛ wo i mi n dɔ, na i mi n gusu wo ni Nyɛmɛ lɛɛ.

eng: We have known and have believed the love that God has for us. God is love, and those who remain in love remain in God and God remains in them.
local: Yɛrɛ mmɔɔ ni ya tii si kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ya barasu, ye ya yo yarada ka i koro ya. Nyɛmɛ ti koro wɔ, nyini dɛɛ ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro i beŋgu ni, i mi wo ni Nyɛmɛ, na Nyɛmɛ wo ni yiri.

eng: This is how love has been perfected in us, so that we can have confidence on the Judgment Day, because we are exactly the same as God is in this world.
local: Nyɛmɛ koro wo ya dɔ, i ti kabo i koro ka i ti n wɔ, nyini ti ye ya bá koro jina i nyunu ni ahoresi, cɛɛn n bo Nyɛmɛ bá di durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ jɔrɛ ni, má pui ti, kabo Kristo ti ni, ya gusu wo ti sɔ ɔ, durunya n nu.

eng: There is no fear in love, but perfect love drives out fear, because fear expects punishment. The person who is afraid has not been made perfect in love.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ koro wo i dɔ soro má sɛrɛ, dama koro bo i ti kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni, i fɔn yi sɛrɛ wɔ, nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i soro sɛrɛ, Nyɛmɛ koro wo má i dɔ kabo má Nyɛmɛ koro ka i ti ni, dama sɛrɛ ni sui­cin nati a.

eng: We love because God first loved us.
local: Nyɛmɛ du mɔ koroori ya, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ya gusu la koro n a.

eng: Those who say, I love God and hate their brothers or sisters are liars. After all, those who dont love their brothers or sisters whom they have seen can hardly love God whom they have not seen!.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋa i koro Nyɛmɛ, na i koro má i ninbaam ni, i mi ti kɔrɛ­fɔ ɔ. Nzɛn i koro má i niwa bo i nyu­mbaa ŋu i ni, i yó sɛ koro Nyɛmɛ bo i nyu­mbaa ŋu má i n a?.

eng: This commandment we have from him: Those who claim to love God ought to love their brother and sister also.
local: Nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo Yesu afa ama ya n la ka, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro Nyɛmɛ, wɔ yo daka ka i mi koro i ninbaam gusu.

eng: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born from God. Whoever loves someone who is a parent loves the child born to the parent.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo yarada ka Yesu ti Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n bo Nyɛmɛ asunma i ni, i mi ti Nyɛmɛ wa a, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koroori baa n wu­fɔ ni, i koro baa n gusu.

eng: This is how we know that we love the children of God: when we love God and keep Gods commandments.
local: Deke bo ya fa si ka ya koro minɛm bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam n la ka, ya koro Nyɛmɛ, na ya de nɔaniɛ maa­wam n bo wɔ fa ama ya ni.

eng: This is the love of God: we keep Gods commandments. Gods commandments are not difficult.
local: Nzɛn ya deeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, nyini kere ka ya koro i a. I nɔaniɛ maa­wam n gusu ti má kekereke.

eng: because everyone who is born from God defeats the world. And this is the victory that has defeated the world: our faith.
local: dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti Nyɛmɛ wa kunma durunya n wɔ. Deke bo ya fa kunma durunya n la kabo ya yo Yesu yarada n wɔ.

eng: Who defeats the world? Isnt it the one who believes that Jesus is Gods Son?.
local: Ŋma koro kunma durunya n a? Bo i koro kunma durunya n la sɔnɔ bo wɔ yo yarada ka Yesu ti Nyɛmɛ Wa ni.

eng: This is the one who came by water and blood: Jesus Christ. Not by water only but by water and blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
local: Yiri Yesu Kristo yo baari durunya n nu n a, na ba biɛ i Nyɛmɛ nzue, nyini sin, na wɔ ba awu, na i mbunja afite agɔ. I baari ni, má ka bu biɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ nzue n ŋgumi wɔ, ama, i wuuri wɔ, na i mbunja afite agɔ. Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni di i daani a, dama Awiɛwiɛ Casi n ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ ɔ.

eng: The three are testifying.
local: Deke nsan di i daani a.

eng: the Spirit, the water, and the blood—and the three are united in agreement.
local: Bu daani dii­wa n ti kun wɔ. Bɛrɛ la Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni, ni kabo bu biɛɛri i Nyɛmɛ nzue ni, ni kabo i wuuri na i mbunja fite gɔɔri ni.

eng: If we receive human testimony, Gods testimony is greater, because this is what God testified: he has testified about his Son.
local: Sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ ni i tii adi i tiɛŋgu Wa n daani a. Ya de minɛm daani dii­wa, nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka ya de Nyɛmɛ daani dii­wa, dama i daani dii­wa n la faŋga tara minɛm dɛɛ.

eng: The one who believes in Gods Son has the testimony within; the one who doesnt believe God has made God a liar, because that one has not believed the testimony that God gave about his Son.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yoori Nyɛmɛ Wa n yarada, i mi la daani di­fɔ, yiri la Awiɛwiɛ Casi n bo i wo i ahore su ni, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ yo má i yarada ayi Nyɛmɛ kɔrɛ­fɔ lɛɛ, dama wɔ yo má daani n bo Nyɛmɛ adi i fa kɔ i Wa n su yarada.

eng: And this is the testimony: God gave eternal life to us, and this life is in his Son.
local: Nyɛmɛ daani dii­wa n la ka o, wɔ ma ya ŋgɔɔ n bo i la má awieeri ni, nyini ŋgɔɔ n wo i Wa n dɔ ɔ.

eng: The one who has the Son has life. The one who doesnt have Gods Son does not have life.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ni Nyɛmɛ Wa n ayo kun, i mi la nyini ŋgɔɔ ni, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i de má Nyɛmɛ Wa ni, i la má ŋgɔɔ.

eng: I write these things to you who believe in the name of Gods Son so that you can know that you have eternal life.
local: M koro ka ambɛrɛ bo am ayo Nyɛmɛ Wa n yarada n si ka am la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni, nyini dɛɛ ti ye ma kɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ama am n a.

eng: This is the confidence that we have in our relationship with God: If we ask for anything in agreement with his will, he listens to us.
local: Ya la ahoresi ni Nyɛmɛ, ka, deke kɛrɛ bo ya sereeri i, na i ti i dɛɛ koroo­wa, i de ya sere wɔ.

eng: If we know that he listens to whatever we ask, we know that we have received what we asked from him.
local: Ya si ka deke kɛrɛ bo ya sereeri i, na i de ya sere, ya si ka wɔ yo i ama ya lɛɛ.

eng: If anyone sees a brother or sister committing a sin that does not result in death, they should pray, and God will give life to them—that is, to those who commit sins that dont result in death. There is a sin that results in death—Im not saying that you should pray about that.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn sɔnɔ ŋu ka i niwa be ayo satiɛ be bo i kpaaki má i ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn fieo, i mi sére Nyɛmɛ ma yiri n bo wɔ yo satiɛ ni, na Nyɛmɛ má i ŋgɔɔ. Ama, satiɛ be wo bɛrɛ bo i kpaaki sɔnɔ ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn fieo, m suse má ka am sere Nyɛmɛ ma minɛm bo bu yo nyini satiɛ n wɔ.

eng: Every unrighteous action is sin, but there is a sin that does not result in death.
local: Sa kɛrɛ bo minɛm yo i na i ti má kpa ti satiɛ wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ, ama, satiɛ­yo nbem wo bɛrɛ bo i kpaaki má sɔnɔ ni Nyɛmɛ afiɛn fieo.

eng: We know that everyone born from God does not sin, but the ones born from God guard themselves, and the evil one cannot touch them.
local: Ya si ka minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nbaam koro suyo má satiɛ, dama Nyɛmɛ Wa ni ŋminda i a, na Sitana koro yo má i mi pui.

eng: We know we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.
local: Ya si ka durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ wo Sitana n saa nu wɔ, ama, yɛrɛ ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛm wɔ.

eng: We know that Gods Son has come and has given us understanding to know the one who is true. We are in the one who is true by being in his Son, Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
local: Ye ya kaaki si ka Nyɛmɛ Wa n aba na wɔ ba ateke ya nyi wɔ, adena ya si yiri n bo i ti Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su ni. Ye ya wo ni Nyɛmɛ n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n ni i wa Yesu Kristo ni. Yiri ti Nyɛmɛ nahɔrɛ su n a, na i ma ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: Little children, guard yourselves from idols!.
local: Tɔ, m nbaam, am tara am ŋu, na má am su amɔɛm.

eng: From the elder. To the chosen gentlewoman and her children, whom I truly love.
local: Mini bo m ti am kpiɛn ni, sukɛrɛ ahin jɔrɛ n fa ma wɔrɔ bara bo Nyɛmɛ ayi u, ni u nbaam n a. M koro am nahɔrɛ su, má mini ŋgumi koro am a, ama, bo bu si nahɔrɛ atin n kɛrɛ koro am wɔ.

eng: because of the truth that remains with us and will be with us forever.
local: dama nahɔrɛ n wo ya dɔ ɔ, na i bá wo ya dɔ ɔ, saŋga kɛrɛ bo i la má awieeri.

eng: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, will be ours who live in truth and love.
local: Ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni i Wa Yesu Kristo buka ya, na bu si ya aŋunfɔ, na bu ma ya ahoreda, na ya su nahɔrɛ atin su ni koro.

eng: I was overjoyed to find some of your children living in the truth, just as we had been commanded by the Father.
local: Ma ŋu ka am nbaam nbem bita nahɔrɛ n kabo ya Si Nyɛmɛ afa i nɔaniɛ n ama ya ni, nyini dɛɛ ti, wɔ yo m fiɛ agaya.

eng: Now, dear friends, I am requesting that we love each other. Its not as though Im writing a new command to you, but its one we have had from the beginning.
local: Kisa, bara, m susere am wɔ, ka am ma ya koro ya beŋgu. Ahin ti má nɔaniɛ maa­wa fɔfɔrɛ ye m sukɛrɛ ma am n a, ama, i ti nɔaniɛ maa­wa kun n bo ya tiiri i saŋga bo ya boori i bu suuri Yesu su n wɔ.

eng: This is love: that we live according to his commands. This is the command that you heard from the beginning: live in love.
local: Koro n bo m sujɔjɔ i jɔrɛ n la ka, ya wo kabo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wa n kere ni. Nɔaniɛ maa­wa n bo am du mɔ tiiri i n la ka, am koro am beŋgu.

eng: Many deceivers have gone into the world who do not confess that Jesus Christ came as a human being. This kind of person is the deceiver and the antichrist.
local: Dadaka­fɔm beberebe asandi abara ayɛ durunya n nu, bu ŋa Yesu Kristo akaaki má adamande aba má durunya n nu. Bɛrɛ ti dadaka­fɔm wɔ, na bu ti Kristo kpɔ­fɔm.

eng: Watch yourselves so that you dont lose what weve worked for but instead receive a full reward.
local: Nyini ti, am si am ŋu sa, na má deke n bo ya yi anyum­boro afa akere am n ka ŋgbɛɛn, ama, na am ba nya i kɛrɛ ŋkatɔ.

eng: Anyone who goes too far and does not continue in the teaching about Christ does not have God. Whoever continues in this teaching has both the Father and the Son.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su má Kristo dɛɛ keree­wa n su, na i kere i dɛɛ keree­wa kɔ sin nyini ŋu, Nyɛmɛ wo má i mi dɔ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i su Kristo dɛɛ keree­wa n su, ya Si Nyɛmɛ, ni i Wa n wo i mi n dɔ lɛɛ.

eng: Whoever comes to you who does not affirm this teaching should neither be received nor welcomed into your home.
local: Nyini ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i baari am dɔ, na i bita má Kristo dɛɛ keree­wa ni, má am ma i mi wura am dika, má am yo i ansɛ ni ba mmɔɔ.

eng: because welcoming people like that is the same thing as sharing in their evil actions.
local: Dama wɔrɔ kɛrɛ bo a yoori nyinim be sɔ ansɛ, a jina i mi sin fa kɔ i satiɛm n bo i yo bu n su wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: I have a lot to tell you. I dont want to use paper and ink, but I hope to visit you and talk with you face-to-face, so that our joy can be complete.
local: Má m la jɔrɛ beberebe bo má m se am, ama, m koro má ka m kɛrɛ i kɛrɛ wura ahin kadasi n nu, ama, m la tama ka m bá niɛ am busu, na ya ŋú ya ŋu, na ya bó anzama, bo ya ahore jɔ ni nahɔrɛ su.

eng: Your chosen sisters children greet you.
local: U niɛma­bara n bo Nyɛmɛ ayi i ni i nbaam kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn n bisa am kɛrɛ dɔŋgu.

eng: From the elder. To my dear friend Gaius, whom I truly love.
local: Mini bo m ti u kpiɛn ni, sukɛrɛ ahin jɔrɛ n fa ma wɔrɔ m dɔn Gayosi bo m koro u nahɔrɛ su n a.

eng: Dear friend, Im praying that all is well with you and that you enjoy good health in the same way that you prosper spiritually.
local: M dɔn koroo­wa, m nyɛmɛsere la ka, Nyɛmɛ ma u aŋuniɛ­mbaa nu laifiɛ, na u deke kɛrɛ kɔ atin su nyunyumi, kabo u nyɛmɛsu ni sukɔ atin su ni.

eng: I was overjoyed when the brothers and sisters arrived and spoke highly of your faithfulness to the truth, shown by how you live according to the truth.
local: Dama ya ninbaa nbem baari fɛn, na ba se m kabo a bita nahɔrɛ ni, na wɔ yo m fiɛ agaya. M si ka a su nahɔrɛ atin n su wɔ.

eng: I have no greater joy than this: to hear that my children are living according to the truth.
local: Deke be wo má bɛrɛ bo i yo m fiɛ agaya tara bo m ti kabo m nbaam su nahɔrɛ atin n su ni.

eng: Dear friend, you act faithfully in whatever you do for our brothers and sisters, even though they are strangers.
local: M dɔn koroo­wa, a bita nahɔrɛ kabo a nati yo sakpa ma u ninbaam bo bu ba u dɔ wɔfɔ ni, haari ni bo a si má bu ni mmɔɔ.

eng: They spoke highly of your love in front of the church. You all would do well to provide for their journey in a way that honors God.
local: Bɛrɛ bo a buka bu ni, ba kan kabo a koro bu n akere Kristo jama n bo bu wo fɛn ni. Boro u nyi na a buka bu kabo Nyɛmɛ koro ni, na bu koro kɔ deke bo bu sukɔ ni.

eng: because they left on their journey for the sake of Jesus Christ without accepting any support from the Gentiles.
local: Kristo dɛɛ ti, bu nati kan i nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm wɔ, na bu koro má pui minɛm n bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ n dɔ.

eng: Therefore, we ought to help people like this so that we can be coworkers with the truth.
local: Nyini ti, yɛrɛ bo ya ti Kristo susu­fɔm ni, wɔ yo daka ka ya buka bu ni juma n bo bu yo i ni. Nzɛn ya bukaari bu, nyini la ka, ya ni bɛrɛ abo kun sukan Kristo nɔaniɛ n bo i ti nahɔrɛ n kere minɛm wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: I wrote something to the church, but Diotrephes, who likes to put himself first, doesnt welcome us.
local: M yo kɛrɛɛri jɔrɛ be maari Kristo jama ni, ama, Diyoterefesi bo i koro ka i kere ka yiri ti mɔ­fɔ n atana su, wɔ bɔ má jɔrɛ be nu.

eng: Because of this, if I come, I will bring up what he has done—making unjustified and wicked accusations against us. And as if that were not enough, he not only refuses to welcome the brothers and sisters but stops those who want to do so and even throws them out of the church!.
local: Nyini ti, nzɛn m baari, m yí i sa­yoo­wa fite fofoe nu, dama wɔ jɔjɔ jɔrɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn abu kɔrɛ asɔ ya, má nyini ŋgumi, wɔ kete ka i de má Kristo minɛm wɔfɔ, na nzɛn nbem koro ka bu de minɛm wɔfɔ mmɔɔ, i soro má su, na i koro ka i fɔn yi bɛrɛ bo bu koro ka bu de wɔfɔm n fite Kristo jamam n nu ni.

eng: Dear friend, dont imitate what is bad but what is good. Whoever practices what is good belongs to God. Whoever practices what is bad has not seen God.
local: M dɔn koroo­wa, má a sɔn satiɛ­yo su, ama, sɔn sakpa­yo su. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo sakpa ti Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wɔ, ama, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo satiɛ si má Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Everyone speaks highly of Demetrius, even the truth itself. We also speak highly of him, and you know that what we say is true.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ di daani kpa fa kɔ kabo Demetirɔsi bita nahɔrɛ n wɔ, na i woo­wa gusu kere ka i bita nahɔrɛ wɔ. Yɛrɛ mmɔɔ di i daani wɔ, ye a si ka kabo ya se n ti nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: I have a lot to say to you, but I dont want to use pen and ink.
local: Má m la jɔrɛ beberebe bo má m se u, ama, m koro má ka m kɛrɛ i kɛrɛ wura kadasi n nu.

eng: I hope to see you soon, and we will speak face-to-face.
local: M la tama ka m bá niɛ u busu kisa, na ya ŋú ya ŋu, na ya bó anzama.

eng: Peace be with you. Your friends here greet you. Greet our friends there by name.
local: Nyɛmɛ ma u ahoreda. U danfom kɛrɛ bo bu wo fɛn bisa u dɔŋgu. Wɔrɔ gusu bisa ya danfom kɛrɛ dɔŋgu ma ya.

eng: Jude, a slave of Jesus Christ and brother of James. To those who are called, loved by God the Father and kept safe by Jesus Christ.
local: Mini Juda bo m ti Yakobo niɛma na m ti Yesu Kristo akɔɔ ni, sukɛrɛ ahin kadasi n ma ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo ya Si Nyɛmɛ koro am na wɔ fere am, na Yesu Kristo bita am ni.

eng: May you have more and more mercy, peace, and love.
local: Nyɛmɛ si am aŋunfɔ agaya, na i ma am ahoreda buka su, na i koro am kɔ sin su.

eng: Dear friends, I wanted very much to write to you concerning the salvation we share. Instead, I must write to urge you to fight for the faith delivered once and for all to Gods holy people.
local: M ninbaa koroo­wam, má m suboro m nyi wɔ, na m kɛrɛ kadasi ma am fa kɔ tii­de n bo m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ anya i n su ni. Ama, wɔ yo daka ka m du mɔ kɛrɛ am, na m sere am jande ka am boro am nyi tu ŋgoni bita am yarada n kenken, nyini yarada ni, yiri ye Nyɛmɛ afa ama i minɛm kpe­kun n kpein n a.

eng: Godless people have slipped in among you. They turn the grace of our God into unrestrained immorality and deny our only master and Lord, Jesus Christ. Judgment was passed against them a long time ago.
local: Dama minɛm nbem awɔ awura am nu bɛnlɛ, nyini minɛm ni, bu soro má Nyɛmɛ, bu kaaki Nyɛmɛ ahɔrɔma n bo wɔ fa ama ya n wɔ, na bu su bu dɛɛ konviki koroo­wa atin n su, na bu kete Kristo. Yiri ŋgumi ti ya Tii­de­fɔ ni ya Mibiɛ a. Dawa dɛɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kɛrɛɛri bu jɔrɛ, ka, Nyɛmɛ dí bu jɔrɛ na i yo bu ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: I want to remind you of something you already know very well. The Lord, who once saved a people out of Egypt, later destroyed those who didnt maintain their faith.
local: Haari ni bo am adu mɔ asi ahin jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ ni, m koro ka m teŋge am su wɔ, fa kɔ kabo Micɛra n yo yiiri Yisareli­fɔm fiteeri Ijipiti asiɛn su ni, ama, kasin na wɔ ma bo ba yo má i yarada n kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: I remind you too of the angels who didnt keep their position of authority but deserted their own home. The Lord has kept them in eternal chains in the underworld until the judgment of the great day.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm nbem bo bu keteeri ka bu wo má bu yiko woo­biri ni, na bu yakiiri bu jinaa­biri ni, bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ awura bu jɔrɔkɔ bo bu fite má nu kun, afa bu ase awosin liim nu n a, ka bu wó dɔ, haari fa kɔ ju jɔrɛ­di bambaka n cɛɛn.

eng: In the same way, Sodom and Gomorrah and neighboring towns practiced immoral sexual relations and pursued other sexual urges. By undergoing the punishment of eternal fire, they serve as a warning.
local: Sɔ ye Sodom ni Gomora­fɔm, ni miɛm bo bu bara yɛ bu n mmɔɔ yoori a, bu faari bu ŋu wuraari sakaraya sereyam nu wɔ, na bu suyo konviki sam bo bu kɔ má atin su. Nyini dɛɛ ti, na Nyɛmɛ acin bu sui, afa bu awura sin n bo i je saŋga kɛrɛ n nu. Nyɛmɛ yoori sɔ ɔ, na minɛ ŋu i, na bu fa tu bu ŋu fɔ.

eng: Yet, even knowing this, these dreamers in the same way pollute themselves, reject authority, and slander the angels.
local: Sɔ ye nyini minɛm nati cin alɛlɛ ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn a, na bu suyo daŋgba sam, na bu kete Nyɛmɛ yiko ni, na bu kpiɛ ŋgɔɔ­fɔm nzukɔ, bɛrɛ bo bu wo aŋgoro na bu woo­wa la jirima ni.

eng: The archangel Michael, when he argued with the devil about Moses body, did not dare charge him with slander. Instead, he said, The Lord rebuke you.
local: Haari Mikali bo i ti Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm n kɛrɛ tii ni mmɔɔ, wɔ yo abo má su ka i jaraki Sitana kpiɛ i nzukɔ, saŋga bo Moyisi wuuri na bu seeri akuruwa fa kɔ i ŋuniɛ­mbaa n busu ni. Ama, kabo i seeri i n la ka, Nyɛmɛ bisa u.

eng: But these people slander whatever they dont understand. They are destroyed by what they know instinctively, as though they were irrational animals.
local: Ama, ahin minɛm ni, bɛrɛ yiri, deke bo bu si má i ni, nyini ye bu kpiɛ i nzukɔ a. Haari nzɛn bu ŋa ba ti deke bu, bu sunsunni ti ka neŋge­ndɔrɛm dɛɛ wɔ. Bu nyini sunsunni n bo bu bita i na i ti má kpa ni, nyini ma bu ka ŋgbɛɛn a.

eng: They are damned, for they follow in the footsteps of Cain. For profit they give themselves over to Balaams error. They are destroyed in the uprising of Korah.
local: Keto, keto, bu ŋú kekereke. Dama ba fa satiɛ atin n bo Keen faari i ni, na ŋwaa dɛɛ ti, bu su Baalam dɛɛ dadaka atin n su wɔ, na ba kete nahɔrɛ atin kabo Kora yo keteeri ni. Nyini ti, kabo i kaari ŋgbɛɛn ni, bɛrɛ gusu ka ŋgbɛɛn sɔ ɔ.

eng: These people are like jagged rocks just below the surface of the water waiting to snag you when they join your love feasts. They feast with you without reverence. They care only for themselves. They are waterless clouds carried along by the winds; fruitless autumn trees, twice dead, uprooted.
local: Nzɛn am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ sudi anɔɔsɛ diire, bu sata am afiɛn kabo fin ta sutura n wɔ, dama bu ni ambɛrɛ saŋga di a, na bu san má nya, bu koro ka bɛrɛ ŋgumi kunnu yi a, bu la má kpɛrɛ be. Bu ti ka nzue bo i muna na aŋuma fa i sin n wɔ. Na bu kaaki ti ka baka bo i su má suu­wa bakam su saŋga, na i wu kpɔrɔ na i bu tɔ n wɔ.

eng: wild waves of the sea foaming up their own shame; wandering stars for whom the darkness of the underworld is reserved forever.
local: Bu nyinsɛ sam bo bu yo bu ni, bu ti kabo tieku nzue bo na i yasu fufuku n wɔ. Bu kaaki ti ka asara­mbaa bo bu jeŋgbe bara yɛ n wɔ. Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ awosin liim su aŋminda bu ka bu kɔ́ wo dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Enoch, who lived seven generations after Adam, prophesied about these people when he said, See, the Lord comes with his countless holy ones.
local: Sɔnɔ be bo bu fere i ka Henoki, na i tɔ ya na Adama su nso su ni, Nyɛmɛ yo faari i nɔaniɛ n maari i wɔ, na wɔ yo ajɔjɔ nyini jɔrɛ n ka, Am ti fɛn, Micɛra ni suba ni i mɛrɛkɛm akpii akpii buru.

eng: to execute judgment on everyone and to convict everyone about every ungodly deed they have committed in their ungodliness as well as all the harsh things that sinful ungodly people have said against him.
local: na i bá di sɔnɔ kɛrɛ jɔrɛ, na i cín bu sui fa kɔ bu satiɛm bo ba yo bu n su, ni kabo bu jɔjɔɔri ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn na bu keteeri Nyɛmɛ ni. Bu ti satiɛ­yo­fɔm wɔ, nyini ti, ye bu keteeri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n a.

eng: These are faultfinding grumblers, living according to their own desires. They speak arrogant words and they show partiality to people when they want a favor in return.
local: Nyini minɛm ni sɔ, bu ŋuŋuti wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, na bu sujaraki bu beŋgu, na bu susu bu konvikiya sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn bo bu bita i n su, na bu sufɔ yanfɔya, na bu subo minɛm anɔɔfiɛ, adena bu nya bu dɛɛ sa koroo­wam.

eng: But you, dear friends, remember the words spoken beforehand by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ, m danfo kpam, wɔ yo daka ka am teŋge su fa kɔ jɔrɛ n bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo jaramasam yo seeri am ni.

eng: They said to you, In the end time scoffers will come living according to their own ungodly desires.
local: Bu seeri am ka, Saŋga bo durunya n ba sukɔ awieeri su ni, minɛ nbem bá ba, na bu yó am anzasi, fa kɔ am yarada n su, na bu sú bu dɛɛ tiɛŋgu aŋuniɛ­mbaa sa koroo­wam n su bo bu kɔ má Nyɛmɛ atin su ni.

eng: These people create divisions. Since they dont have the Spirit, they are worldly.
local: Ahin minɛm sɔ, bu sutiti am nu wɔ lɛɛ, bu su adamande dɛɛ ŋgɔnlɛ su a, Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n wo má bu dɔ.

eng: But you, dear friends: build each other up on the foundation of your most holy faith, pray in the Holy Spirit.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ, m danfo kpam, am boro am nyi na am ma am yarada casi n buka su, na am sere Nyɛmɛ, Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu.

eng: keep each other in the love of God, wait for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ, who will give you eternal life.
local: na am ma Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ koro n wo am dɔ. Na am ŋminda fa kɔ saŋga bo ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo bá si am aŋunfɔ, na am nya ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: Have mercy on those who doubt.
local: Nbem wo am nu bo bu la sika bu sunsunni nu, am si bu aŋunfɔ.

eng: Save some by snatching them from the fire. Fearing God, have mercy on some, hating even the clothing contaminated by their sinful urges.
local: nbem gusu, am cin yi bu fite sin nu, na am de bu tii ji, na nyiŋgam gusu, am si bu aŋunfɔ, ama, am si am ŋu sa haari na má am ma bu sutura fin mmɔɔ ta am.

eng: To the one who is able to protect you from falling, and to present you blameless and rejoicing before his glorious presence.
local: Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi ti ya Tii­de­fɔ a, ya Mibiɛ Yesu Kristo dɛɛ ti. Yiri koro bita am na am kpaasi má a, yiri koro fa am ba jina i jirima biɛ n nyunu a, na am la má ŋgasi be na i ma am ahorejɔ a. Yiri ye i ti ye ni ka ya kansi i na ya wura i kpiri a, yiri ti yiko­fɔ a, yiri ti faŋga­fɔ a, yiri yo woori bɛrɛ a, yiri toko wo bɛrɛ kisa ni saŋga kɛrɛ a. Ami.

eng: A revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show his servants what must soon take place. Christ made it known by sending it through his angel to his servant John.
local: Ahin jɔrɛ n ye Nyɛmɛ seeri Yesu Kristo ka i yi kere i akɔɔm fa kɔ sam bo i cɛ má na bu bá yo n a. Na Yesu asunma i mɛrɛkɛ i akɔɔ Yohane dɔ, na i kɔ se i jɔrɛ ni.

eng: who bore witness to the word of God and to the witness of Jesus Christ, including all that John saw.
local: Jɔrɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ ni Yesu Kristo yi kereeri Yohane na i ŋuuri i ni, nyini ye wɔ di i daani akɛrɛ i n a.

eng: Favored is the one who reads the words of this prophecy out loud, and favored are those who listen to it being read, and keep what is written in it, for the time is near.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i kaanri ahin kadasi ni, tere­fiɛ wo i dɔ. Na minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu tiiri i jɔrɛ na bu deeri i, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, dama saŋga n adodo bo nyini sam kɛrɛ bá yo.

eng: John, to the seven churches that are in Asia: Grace and peace to you from the one who is and was and is coming, and from the seven spirits that are before Gods throne.
local: Mini Yohane akɛrɛ ahin kadasi n na m fa ma Kristo jama asɛsɛ nso n bo bu wo Esiya awɔrɛ n nu n a. Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ, na i yo woori bɛrɛ, na i bá wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, ni i awiɛwiɛ busu nsom n bo bu wo yiri Nyɛmɛ kpiri n nyunu ni ma am ahoreda, na am di bu ahɔrɔma. Bekun, Yesu Kristo, yiri bo i di i daani na i la dabuŋu ni, na yiri ti bo i du mɔ teŋgeeri yiwee nu ni, na i kaaki ti durunya n nu nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ tii ni, ma am ahoreda, na am di i ahɔrɔma. Yiri koro ya, na wɔ fa i mbunja ade ya afite ya satiɛ kɛrɛ nu.

eng: who made us a kingdom, priests to his God and Father—to him be glory and power forever and always. Amen.
local: na wɔ yo ya i fɛmɛya n nu minɛm na ya yo i sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm, na ya su i Si Nyɛmɛ. Yiri Yesu Kristo ni, yiri ti ye ni ka ya wura i kpiri a, i dɛɛ la yiko saŋga kɛrɛ. Ami.

eng: Look, he is coming with the clouds! Every eye will see him, including those who pierced him, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn because of him. This is so. Amen.
local: Am niɛ, i fíte aŋgoro yinyiŋgu n nu ba, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ nyu­mbaa bá ŋu i, fa saŋga ni minɛm bo bu wɔɔri i kpiɛ n kɛrɛ. Yiri ti, durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ sún. I ti sɔ ɔ. Ami.

eng: I am the Alpha and the Omega, says the Lord God, the one who is and was and is coming, the Almighty.
local: Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri bo i wo bɛrɛ ni, na i yo woori bɛrɛ, na i bá wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n ase ka, Mini ti mɔ­fɔ a, na m kaaki ti kasin­fɔ.

eng: I, John, your brother who shares with you in the hardship, kingdom, and endurance that we have in Jesus, was on the island called Patmos because of the word of God and my witness about Jesus.
local: Mini Yohane ti am niwa bo m ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ saŋga di wahara ni mindi Yesu dɛɛ ti, na ya ti i fɛmɛya n nu minɛm ni. Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n bo ma kan i ni, ni Yesu daani n bo ma di i n dɛɛ ti, na ba fa m akɔ aji miɛ be bo i wo lui bambaka n bo i wo tieku n nu na bu fere i ka Patamosi n su.

eng: I was in a Spirit-inspired trance on the Lords day, and I heard behind me a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
local: Ya Mibiɛ n dɛɛ cɛɛn ni, na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n awura m ŋu, na ma ti akonvi bambaka be ŋga m sin, ka awiɛ dɛɛ su.

eng: It said, Write down on a scroll whatever you see, and send it to the seven churches: to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Kɛrɛ deke n kɛrɛ bo a ŋú i n wura kadasi n nu, na a fa ma Kristo jama asɛsɛ nsom bo bu wo ahin miɛ kpirim n su ni, bɛrɛ la Ɛfɛso, ni Samirina, ni Pɛrɛgamon, ni Tiyatira, ni Saradisa, ni Filadɛlɛfiya, ni Lawodikaya.

eng: I turned to see who was speaking to me, and when I turned, I saw seven oil lamps burning on top of seven gold stands.
local: Na ma cɛ aniɛ m sin ka m ŋu sɔnɔ n bo i jɔjɔ m dɔ ni, m cɛ niɛɛri ni, na ma ŋu fitana takaa­biri nsom bo bu ti sika ni.

eng: In the middle of the lampstands I saw someone who looked like the Human One. He wore a robe that stretched down to his feet, and he had a gold sash around his chest.
local: Na ma ŋu be ka adamande wa ni, i wo bu afiɛn, i wura tanlɛɛ tintiin yorom, na wɔ fa sika dambara abo i hoe su.

eng: His head and hair were white as white wool—like snow—and his eyes were like a fiery flame.
local: I tii­ŋmiɛ kɛrɛ ti fofoe papasi ka gise wɔ, na i nyu­mbaa kɛrɛ je ka sin fofoe.

eng: His feet were like fine brass that has been purified in a furnace, and his voice sounded like rushing water.
local: Na i jaa gbagbaa kɛrɛ boro ka jawii bo ba fa awura sin nu na wɔ boro sonsoen, na i konvi bo awie kabo nzue beberebe soŋgi na i bo awie barasu ni.

eng: He held seven stars in his right hand, and from his mouth came a sharp, two-edged sword. His appearance was like the sun shining with all its power.
local: I bita asara­mbaam nso i saa fɔmbɔrɔ nu, na takobe nana afite i nɔɔ nu. I nyunu je kabo wiɛ ba bi na i je n wɔ.

eng: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. But he put his right hand on me and said, Dont be afraid. Im the first and the last.
local: M ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na ma kaba ada i jaa bu asiɛŋgu ka ma wu dɛɛ su wɔ, na wɔ fa i saa fɔmbɔrɔ anana m ŋu, na wɔ se m ka, Má a soro sɛrɛ. Mini ti deke kɛrɛ bumboori ni i awieeri a.

eng: and the living one. I was dead, but look! Now Im alive forever and always. I have the keys of Death and the Grave.
local: Mini ti bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n a. Niɛ fɛn. M yo wuuri wɔ, ama, kisa, ma teŋge yiwee nu. M la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri. Mini bita yiwee ni Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri kadombaa a.

eng: So write down what you have seen, both the scene now before you and the things that are about to unfold after this.
local: Nyini ti, kɛrɛ sam bo a ŋu bu ni, ni bo bu suyo kisa ni, ni bo bu bá yo ahin dɛɛ sin ni.

eng: As for the mystery of the seven stars that you saw in my right hand and the seven gold lampstands, here is what they mean: the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the seven lampstands are the seven churches.
local: Asara­mbaa nsom bo a ŋu bu m saa fɔmbɔrɔ n bu la ka, Kristo jama asɛsɛ nsom n mɛrɛkɛm. Na fitana takaa­biri nsom bo bu ti sika ni gusu bu la ka, Kristo jama asɛsɛ nsom ni.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Ephesus: These are the words of the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among the seven gold lampstands.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Ɛfɛso miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m bita asara­mbaa nsom m saa fɔmbɔrɔ nu ni, na m kaaki nati fitana takaa­biri nsom bo bu ti sika n afiɛn n dɔ ɔ.

eng: I know your works, your labor, and your endurance. I also know that you dont put up with those who are evil. You have tested those who say they are apostles but are not, and you have found them to be liars.
local: M si am sa­yoo­wam, m si kabo am ayo am juma ni anyum­boro barasu, ni kabo am la mindi barasu ni, na m si kabo am de má satiɛ­yo­fɔm ni. Na m kaaki si kabo am ayi kɔrɛsi aŋu minɛm bo bu ŋa bu ti m jaramasam, ama, bu woo­wa kere ka bu ti má sɔ, na am aŋu ka bu ti kɔrɛ­fɔm ni.

eng: You have shown endurance and put up with a lot for my names sake, and you havent gotten tired.
local: Mini dɛɛ ti, am ayo sawɔrɛ amindi am wahara nu, am nya fie má, am toko su m su.

eng: But I have this against you: you have let go of the love you had at first.
local: Ama, jɔrɛ kun bo m fa jaraki am la ka, kisa am koro má m kabo am yo koro m ni.

eng: So remember the high point from which you have fallen. Change your hearts and lives and do the things you did at first. If you dont, Im coming to you. I will move your lampstand from its place if you dont change your hearts and lives.
local: Nyini ti, am bu am tii nu fɔ na am si deke bo am akpaasi ni, na am kaaki am wo, na am yo sam bo am yoori bu bumboori ni. Nzɛn am akaaki má am wo, m bá cin yi am fitana takaa­biri n deke bo i taka ni.

eng: But you have this in your favor: you hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, which I also hate.
local: Ama, am sa kun bo i yo m fiɛ n la kabo am koro má Nikolaita­fɔm sa­yoo­wam ni, mini mmɔɔ koro má bu sa­yoo­wam ni.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will allow those who emerge victorious to eat from the tree of life, which is in Gods paradise.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni suse Kristo jamam barasu ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara, m má i atin na i di baka n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n dɛɛ suu­wa na i wo Nyɛmɛ dika n nu ni.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Smyrna: These are the words of the one who is the first and the last, who died and came back to life.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Samirina miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m ti deke kɛrɛ bumboori ni i awieeri ni, na m yo wuuri na ma teŋge yiwee nu ni dɔ ɔ.

eng: I know your hardship and poverty . I also know the hurtful things that have been spoken about you by those who say they are Jews .
local: M si kabo am adi wahara agaya barasu, ni kabo am ti yaari­fɔm ni, ama, am ti neŋge­fɔm wɔ Nyɛmɛ dɔ. Na m si kabo minɛm kpiɛ am nzukɔ ni, bo bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Yuda­fɔm, ama, bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ŋgba, bu ti Sitana dɛɛ jama n wɔ.

eng: Dont be afraid of what you are going to suffer. Look! The devil is going to throw some of you into prison in order to test you. You will suffer hardship for ten days. Be faithful even to the point of death, and I will give you the crown of life.
local: Am dí wahara kisa, ama, má am soro sɛrɛ. Am ti fɛn, Sitana wúra am nbem saraka na i yó am na i níɛ na am ŋú kekereke le­buru. Ama, am su m ni nahɔrɛ haari am bá wu, na m tɔ́ am ŋkatɔ, yiri la ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. Those who emerge victorious wont be hurt by the second death.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni suse Kristo jamam barasu ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara ŋú má yiwee kpe nnyɔ su n kun.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Pergamum: These are the words of the one who has the sharp, two-edged sword.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Pɛrɛgamon miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m la takobe nana n dɔ ɔ.

eng: I know that you are living right where Satans throne is. You are holding on to my name, and you didnt break faith with me even at the time that Antipas, my faithful witness, was killed among you, where Satan lives.
local: M si deke bo am woori ni, am woo­biri n ti Sitana fɛmɛya tanaa­biri wɔ, ama, am toko su m ni nahɔrɛ wɔ, am ayaki má m yarada­yo, haari ni saŋga bo bu kuunri Antipasi bo i diiri m daani nahɔrɛ su am miɛ n nu, deke bo Sitana wo ni.

eng: But I have a few things against you, because you have some there who follow Balaams teaching. Balaam had taught Balak to trip up the Israelites so that they would eat food sacrificed to idols and commit sexual immorality.
local: Ama, m la jɔrɛ nbem bo m fa jaraki am, am nbem bita Baalam keree­wa atin n wɔ. Yiri yo kereeri Fɛmɛ Balaki, ye i maari Yisareli­fɔm yoori satiɛ diiri tiɛ niɛ, na ba yo sakaraya.

eng: In the same way, you have some who follow the Nicolaitans teaching.
local: Am nbem gusu ti sɔ ɔ, bu su Nikolaita­fɔm dɛɛ atin keree­wa n su wɔ.

eng: So change your hearts and lives. If you dont, I am coming to you soon, and I will make war on them with the sword that comes from my mouth.
local: Nyini ti, am kaaki am wo, má nyini, m bá am dɔ kisa, na m fá takobe n bo i wo m nɔɔ nu kun luɛ ni minɛm bo bu yo sɔ ni.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches. I will give those who emerge victorious some of the hidden manna to eat. I will also give to each of them a white stone with a new name written on it, which no one knows except the one who receives it.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni suse Kristo jamam barasu ni. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara, m má i mi diire n bo bu fere i ka mana * ni, na i wo fiɛɛ­wa nu ni. Na m káaki ma i mi yabuɛ fofoe be, ba kɛrɛ duma fɔfɔrɛ su, be koro si má duma n bo i wo su ni, má ka yiri n bo i de i ni si i a.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Thyatira: These are the words of Gods Son, whose eyes are like a fiery flame, and whose feet are like fine brass.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Tiyatira miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini Nyɛmɛ Wa n dɔ ɔ, mini bo m nyu­mbaa je ka sin fofoe, na m jaa gbagbaa kɛrɛ boro ka jawii dɛɛ su ni.

eng: I know your works, your love and faithfulness, your service and endurance. I also know that the works you have done most recently are even greater than those you did at first.
local: M si am sa­yoo­wam, m si kabo am la koro barasu, ni kabo am su m ni nahɔrɛ su ni, ni amusui bo am su i ni, na m kaaki si kabo am la mindi, na m si kabo kisa am boro am nyi tara bumboori ni.

eng: But I have this against you: you put up with that woman, Jezebel, who calls herself a prophet. You allow her to teach and to mislead my servants into committing sexual immorality and eating food sacrificed to idols.
local: Ama, jɔrɛ bo m fa jaraki am la ka, am amɔ am nɔɔ ye am niɛ Yɛzɛbɛli, bara n bo i yo i ŋu ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ ni. I kere m akɔɔm wɔ na i daka bu, na bu suyo sakaraya, na i ma m minɛm di tiɛ niɛ.

eng: I gave her time to change her heart and life, but she refuses to change her life of prostitution.
local: Ma se i ka i kaaki i wo na i yaki somaya kɔ ni, wɔ kete ka i kaaki má.

eng: Look! Im throwing her onto a sickbed. I am casting those who have committed adultery with her into terrible hardship—if they dont change their hearts from following her practices.
local: Nyini ti, m má i da tukpaki kekereke, na i ni minɛm bo bu yo sakaraya n dí wahara agaya, nzɛn ba kaaki má bu wo ayaki má sam bo bu ni yiri yo bu ni.

eng: and I will even put her children to death with disease. Then all the churches will know that Im the one who examines minds and hearts, and that I will give to each of you what your actions deserve.
local: Na m kún i nbaam, na Kristo jama asɛsɛ n kɛrɛ sí ka, mini fiiti si deke bo i wo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ ahore su, ni i sunsunni nu. Na m fá sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sa­yoo­wa tɔ i kaari.

eng: As for the rest of you in Thyatira—those of you who dont follow this teaching and havent learned the so-called deep secrets of Satan—I wont burden you with anything else.
local: Ama, ambɛrɛ Tiyatira­fɔm nbem bo am aka, na am su má i keree­wa atin n su ni, ambɛrɛ asɔnsɔn má deke bo nbem fere i ka Sitana asiri nu sa bambaka ni. M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, m sɔ́ má am sɔɔri nɔnɔɔri buka má su.

eng: Just hold on to what you have until I come.
local: Ama, atin bo am bita i ni, am bita i kenken haari saŋga bo m bá kaaki ba.

eng: To those who emerge victorious, keeping my practices until the end, I will give authority over the nations.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara na i yo m sa koroo­wa kɔ juuri awieeri, m má i yiko kabo m Si ama m yiko ni, na i níɛ daanfiɛ daanfiɛ kɛrɛ su. Na i bíta bu kekereke kabo naŋkasi fa bulaari kɔrɔma tu bo i naanim barasu ni, na i fúfu bu kabo bu fufu yakati­sɛ barasu ni. Na m má i asaakojo.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni suse Kristo jamam barasu ni.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Sardis: These are the words of the one who holds Gods seven spirits and the seven stars: I know your works. You have the reputation of being alive, and you are in fact dead.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Saradisa miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m la Nyɛmɛ awiɛwiɛ busu nso n na m bita asara­mbaam nso m saa nu dɔ ɔ. M si am sa­yoo­wam, am wo ka am la ŋgɔɔ, ama, am ti fuinm wɔ.

eng: Wake up and strengthen whatever you have left, teetering on the brink of death, for Ive found that your works are far from complete in the eyes of my God.
local: Nyini ti, am yasu na am bita deke n bo am toko la i n kenken, má nyini, i wú wɔ. Dama ma ŋu ka am sa­yoo­wam n akpiɛ su Nyɛmɛ dɔ.

eng: So remember what you received and heard. Hold on to it and change your hearts and lives. If you dont wake up, I will come like a thief, and you wont know what time I will come upon you.
local: Am teŋge su, kabo am yo tiiri Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n na am deeri i ni, am kaaki am wo na am bita i, nzɛn am ayasu má, am si má saŋga bo m bá ni, dama m bá fura am nu ka awiefɔ dɛɛ su wɔ.

eng: But you do have a few people in Sardis who havent stained their clothing. They will walk with me clothed in white because they are worthy.
local: Ama, m la minɛ nbem bo bu wo Saradisa Kristo jama n nu, bo ba saaki má bu sutura ayo má bu fin, bu sa­yoo­wam nu. Bɛrɛ bá wura tanlɛɛ fofoe na m ni bɛrɛ sáŋga nati a, dama bu ti ye ni sɔ ɔ.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will wear white clothing like this. I wont scratch out their names from the scroll of life, but will declare their names in the presence of my Father and his angels.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara, i wúra nyini tanlɛɛ fofoe n be sɔ ɔ. Na m yí má i mi duma fite má ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu, ama, m bá jina m Si ni i mɛrɛkɛm nyunu, na m sé bu ka i ti m dɛɛ sɔnɔ ɔ.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni suse Kristo jamam barasu ni.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: These are the words of the one who is holy and true, who has the key of David. Whatever he opens, no one will shut; and whatever he shuts, no one opens.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Filadɛlɛfiya miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m ti casi na m ti nahɔrɛ­fɔ, na m bita Fɛmɛ Davidi kadombaa n bo i kere ka m la i dɛɛ yiko n be wɔ, na nzɛn m tekeeri anɔ, be koro tu má i, na nzɛn m tuuri i gusu, be koro teke má i.

eng: I know your works. Look! I have set in front of you an open door that no one can shut. You have so little power, and yet you have kept my word and havent denied my name.
local: M si am sa­yoo­wam, am niɛ, ma teke anɔ ama am bo be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro tu i. M si ka am la má faŋga agaya, ama, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, am fite má m sin, am toko bita m nɔaniɛ n ni nahɔrɛ wɔ.

eng: Because of this I will make the people from Satans synagogue —I will make them come and bow down at your feet and realize that I have loved you.
local: Am yaki bo bu su Sitana n na bu yo bu ŋu ka bu ti Yuda­fɔm ni, bu ti kɔrɛ­fɔm wɔ, bu ti má Yuda­fɔm ŋgba. M má bu ba kutu am nyunu na bu si ka m koro am.

eng: Because you kept my command to endure, I will keep you safe through the time of testing that is about to come over the whole world, to test those who live on earth.
local: Am ade m nɔaniɛ ni, na am amindi n dɛɛ ti, m yí am fite kekereke n bo Nyɛmɛ bá fa yo durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ na i niɛ ni.

eng: Im coming soon. Hold on to what you have so that no one takes your crown.
local: M cɛ́ má, m káaki ba kisa. Atin n bo am bita i ni, am bita i kenken na má be fin am ŋkatɔ ni.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will make them pillars in the temple of my God, and they will never leave it. I will write on them the name of my God and the name of the city of my God, the New Jerusalem that comes down out of heaven from my God. I will also write on them my own new name.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara, m fá i yo jantaraka Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu na i wó nu dɔ saŋga kɛrɛ. Na m kɛ́rɛ Nyɛmɛ duma mata i, na m kɛ́rɛ Nyɛmɛ miɛ n duma, miɛ n bo i fite aŋgoro Nyɛmɛ dɔ na bu fere i ka Yɛrusalem fɔfɔrɛ, na m káaki kɛrɛ m duma fɔfɔrɛ n gusu mata i mi ŋu.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni suse Kristo jamam barasu ni.

eng: Write this to the angel of the church in Laodicea: These are the words of the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the ruler of Gods creation.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Kɛrɛ kadasi fa ma mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ Kristo jama n bo i wo Lawodikaya miɛ kpiri n nu ni, na a se bu ka, Ahin nɔaniɛ n fite mini bo m ti Ami na m la dabuŋu, na m di daani ni nahɔrɛ su n dɔ ɔ. Mini ni Nyɛmɛ du mɔ yo woori bɛrɛ a, ye ya yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a.

eng: I know your works. You are neither cold nor hot. I wish that you were either cold or hot.
local: M si am sa­yoo­wam, am ti má niniki, am kaaki ti má kɛkɛrɛ, nzɛn má am ti niniki wara kɛkɛrɛ, má m koro.

eng: So because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, Im about to spit you out of my mouth.
local: Ama, am ti bɔbɔrɔki n dɛɛ ti, m púuti am m nɔɔ nu butu.

eng: After all, you say, Im rich, and Ive grown wealthy, and I dont need a thing. You dont realize that you are miserable, pathetic, poor, blind, and naked.
local: Am ŋa am ti neŋge­fɔm wɔ, deke be kpiɛ má am su, ama, am si má ka am la aŋunfɔ, na am si má ka am ti yaari­fɔm, na am kaaki si má ka am ti anyinsinm, na am kaaki si má ka am nati am ŋuniɛ ŋgbɛɛn wɔ.

eng: My advice is that you buy gold from me that has been purified by fire so that you may be rich, and white clothing to wear so that your nakedness wont be shamefully exposed, and ointment to put on your eyes so that you may see.
local: Nyini ti, m koro ka m tu am fɔ, na am bra m dɔ ba to sika kpa bo ba fa awura sin nu na wɔ fite casi ni, adena am yo neŋge­fɔm nahɔrɛ su, na am ba to sutura fofoe m dɔ, na am fa kata am ŋu, na má minɛ ŋu ka am nati am ŋuniɛ ŋgbɛɛn na i la nyinsɛ. Na am ba to anyumbaa ayiri m dɔ, na am fa wura am nyu­mbaa nu na am koro ŋu asi nyunyumi.

eng: I correct and discipline those whom I love. So be earnest and change your hearts and lives.
local: Bo m koro bu ni, bɛrɛ ye m jaraki bu na m cin bu sui a, nyini ti, am boro am nyi na am kaaki am wo.

eng: Look! Im standing at the door and knocking. If any hear my voice and open the door, I will come in to be with them, and will have dinner with them, and they will have dinner with me.
local: Am ti fɛn, m jina sɔɔ anɔ n sin subobo, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ti m konvi ŋga na i tekeeri anɔ ni, m bá wura i mi dɔ, na m ni yiri sáŋga di diire.

eng: As for those who emerge victorious, I will allow them to sit with me on my throne, just as I emerged victorious and sat down with my Father on his throne.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i di nasara, m má i atin na i bá tana m bi m fɛmɛya biɛ n su, kabo ma di nasara na m ni m Si saŋga tana i fɛmɛya biɛ n su ni.

eng: If you can hear, listen to what the Spirit is saying to the churches.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti kabo Awiɛwiɛ Casi ni suse Kristo jamam barasu ni.

eng: After this I looked and there was a door that had been opened in heaven. The first voice that I had heard, which sounded like a trumpet, said to me, Come up here, and I will show you what must take place after this.
local: Nyini sin, na wɔ yo m ka alɛlɛ dɛɛ su, na ma ŋu ka anɔ be ateke aŋgoro. Na ma ti akonvi ŋga ka awiɛ dɛɛ su sujɔjɔ m dɔ, i ti akonvi n bo i du mɔ jɔjɔɔri m dɔ n wɔ. I ŋa, Fun bra aŋgoro fɛn, na m kére u sam bo bu bá yo ahin sin.

eng: At once I was in a Spirit-inspired trance and I saw a throne in heaven, and someone was seated on the throne.
local: Jina nu n bɛrɛ na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n aba awura m ŋu na ma ŋu ka fɛmɛya biɛ wo aŋgoro, be tana su.

eng: The one seated there looked like jasper and carnelian, and surrounding the throne was a rainbow that looked like an emerald.
local: Bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su je piɛpiɛu kabo cɛncɛɛri nbem je barasu wɔ, nbem je fofoe na nbem je kɔkɔrɛ, na i fɛmɛya biɛ ni, deke be je piɛpiɛu aŋgoro kabo nzue ba muna na ayi kpiɛ nu ni.

eng: Twenty-four thrones, with twenty-four elders seated upon them, surrounded the throne. The elders were dressed in white clothing and had gold crowns on their heads.
local: Fɛmɛya biɛ abura­nyɔ ni nna nbem taka bara yɛ i fɛmɛya biɛ ni, na nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm abura­nyɔ ni nna tana biɛm su, bu wura tanlɛɛ fofoem na bu butu sika kere.

eng: From the throne came lightning, voices, and thunder. In front of the throne were seven flaming torches, which are the seven spirits of God.
local: Na fɛmɛya biɛ ni sukpɛsɛ ka nzue dɛɛ su, na i sutiɛntiɛn, na i suyo naŋmiɛ. Fitana nso taka fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu wo je su, bɛrɛ la Nyɛmɛ awiɛwiɛ busu nsom ni.

eng: Something like a glass sea, like crystal, was in front of the throne. In the center, by the throne, were four living creatures encircling the throne. These creatures were covered with eyes on the front and on the back.
local: Deke be gusu da fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu ka nzue bambaka dɛɛ su, i nyunu da cain ka niɛɛnu wɔ. Deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nna nbem gusu abara ayɛ fɛmɛya biɛ ni, bu la anyumbaa beberebe, bu nyunu ni bu sin n kɛrɛ.

eng: The first living creature was like a lion. The second living creature was like an ox. The third living creature had a face like a human being. And the fourth living creature was like an eagle in flight.
local: Ciciri su dɛɛ n ti ka jara a, nnyɔ su n ti ka toora, nsan su n nyunu­awa ti ka adamande wɔ, na nna su n ti ka kajiri bo i sutu landi.

eng: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and each was covered all around and on the inside with eyes. They never rest day or night, but keep on saying, Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is coming.
local: Bu kun kun kɛrɛ la laŋkpɛtɛ nsɛn nsɛn wɔ, bu la anyumbaam bu ŋuniɛ­mbaa nu, ni bu ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ su. Na bu sudi jue kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu kɛrɛ, ka, Casi, casi, casi la ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri, i yo woori bɛrɛ, na i toko wo bɛrɛ, na i bá wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni.

eng: Whenever the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to the one seated on the throne, who lives forever and always.
local: Nzɛn bu nya sudi jue na bu fa kansi, ni jirima, ni ansɛ ma yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su ni, na i wo bɛrɛ saŋga bo i la má awieeri ni.

eng: the twenty-four elders fall before the one seated on the throne. They worship the one who lives forever and always. They throw down their crowns before the throne and say.
local: nyini saŋga, a ŋu ka nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm abura­nyɔ ni nna n sukutu i nyunu, na bu su i, yiri bo i wo bɛrɛ na i la má awieeri na i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su ni. Na bu yi bu kere, sufa se fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu, na bu sudi jue, ka.

eng: You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, because you created all things. It is by your will that they existed and were created.
local: Ya Micɛra ni ya Nyɛmɛ, wɔrɔ ti ye ni ka a de kansi a, ni jirima, ni yiko. Dama wɔrɔ yiiri deke n kɛrɛ a, u koroya­nu ye a maari deke n kɛrɛ la ŋgɔɔ a.

eng: Then I saw a scroll in the right hand of the one seated on the throne. It had writing on the front and the back, and it was sealed with seven seals.
local: Na ma ŋu ka kadasi nyanyaa­wa be wo yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n saa fɔmbɔrɔ n nu, kɛrɛɛ­wa wo i kunnu ni i sin kɛrɛ su, ba fa wiɛ mɛnɛ amiɛ amata i busu nso.

eng: I saw a powerful angel, who proclaimed in a loud voice, Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals.
local: Na ma ŋu Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ faŋga­fɔ be ka i subo ndoori faŋga su ka, Ŋma ti ye ni bo i koro cin ŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ n na i teke kadasi n a.

eng: But no one in heaven or on earth or under the earth could open the scroll or look inside it.
local: Ama, aŋgoro, ni asiɛŋgu, ni bo bu wo asiɛn bu, be awo má bɛrɛ bo i koro teke kadasi ni, na a ba kan ka i koro niɛ nu.

eng: So I began to weep and weep, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to look inside it.
local: Be awo má bɛrɛ bo i ti ye ni bo i koro teke kadasi ni, na a ba kan ka i koro niɛ nu n dɛɛ ti, na ma sun ni nyunvuin ŋgba.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Dont weep. Look! The Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has emerged victorious so that he can open the scroll and its seven seals.
local: Na nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nu kun, ase m ka, Má a sun. Niɛ fɛn, Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma kpiri ni, bo bu fere i ka Yuda­fɔm busu nu jara n adi nasara, i koro cin ŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nso n na i teke kadasi ni.

eng: Then, in between the throne and the four living creatures and among the elders, I saw a Lamb, standing as if it had been slain. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are Gods seven spirits, sent out into the whole earth.
local: Na ma ŋu bɔmbaa be, i ti ka ba kpiɛ i kɔm wɔ, i jina fɛmɛya biɛ n afiɛn. Deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n abara ayɛ i. I la mbiɛ nso wɔ, i nyu­mbaa gusu ti nso wɔ. Bɛrɛ la Nyɛmɛ awiɛwiɛ busu nsom bo wɔ sunma bu durunya n kɛrɛ nu ni.

eng: He came forward and took the scroll from the right hand of the one seated on the throne.
local: Na wɔ kɔ yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n dɔ, na wɔ de kadasi n afite i saa fɔmbɔrɔ nu.

eng: When he took the scroll, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each held a harp and gold bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.
local: I kɔ deeri kadasi n ni, na deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm abura­nyɔ ni nnam n akutu i nyunu. Bu kɛrɛ la biɛku, na bu kɛrɛ bita sika ayaba bo wɔ yi ni ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani ŋgumi wɔ. Ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani n ti nyɛmɛsere bo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛ sere i wɔ.

eng: They took up a new song, saying, You are worthy to take the scroll and open its seals, because you were slain, and by your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe, language, people, and nation.
local: Na ba di jue fɔfɔrɛ ama yiri Bɔmbaa n ka, Wɔrɔ ti ye ni ka a de kadasi ni, na a ŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ n a, dama wɔrɔ ye bu kpiɛɛri u kɔm, ye a fa u mbunja ade minɛm tii aji a. Adamande kɛrɛ, ni daanfiɛ kɛrɛ, ni aniɛm kɛrɛ, ni asiɛn barasu barasu kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu ni, bu nbem ayo Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ minɛm.

eng: You made them a kingdom and priests to our God, and they will rule on earth.
local: A fa bu ayo u fɛmɛya n nu minɛm, na bu yo u sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm, na bu niɛ durunya n kɛrɛ su.

eng: Then I looked, and I heard the sound of many angels surrounding the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. They numbered in the millions—thousands upon thousands.
local: Na ma niɛ aŋu Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛm beberebe, akpii akpii buru nɔ, ka, ba bara ayɛ fɛmɛya biɛ ni, ni deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm ni. Na ma ti ka.

eng: They said in a loud voice, Worthy is the slaughtered Lamb to receive power, wealth, wisdom, and might, and honor, glory, and blessing.
local: ba yɛ bu konvi su sudi jue, ka, Bɔmbaa n bo bu yo kpiɛɛri i kɔm ni, yiri ti ye ni ka i de yiko a, ni neŋge, ni alecira, ni faŋga, ni jirima, ni kpiri, ni kansi a.

eng: And I heard every creature in heaven and on earth and under the earth and in the sea—I heard everything everywhere say, Blessing, honor, glory, and power belong to the one seated on the throne and to the Lamb forever and always.
local: Na ma ti ka deke kɛrɛ bo bu wo aŋgoro, ni asiɛŋgu, ni bo bu wo asiɛn bu, ni bo bu wo nzue nu, ni deke kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ayi bu, ka bu sudi jue, ka, Yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ni Bɔmbaa ni, bɛrɛ ti ye ni ka bu de kansi a, ni jirima, ni kpiri, ni yiko, saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga.

eng: Then the four living creatures said, Amen, and the elders fell down and worshipped.
local: Na deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam n ase ka, Ami. Na nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n akutu asu Nyɛmɛ ni Bɔmbaa ni.

eng: Then I looked on as the Lamb opened one of the seven seals. I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, Come.
local: Na ma ŋu ka Bɔmbaa n aba acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nso n bo bu fa mataari kadasi n nu kun. Na ma ti deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam n nu kun konvi ŋga, kabo nzue tiɛntiɛn barasu ni, ka, Fite.

eng: So I looked, and there was a white horse. Its rider held a bow and was given a crown. And he went forth from victory to victory.
local: M niɛɛri na ma ŋu ka, kpɔŋgɔ fofoe lɛɛ. Bo i fun i n bita taa ni awiɛ, na ba butu i fɛmɛya kere, na wɔ kɔ ka luɛ­kun­fɔ na i di nasara.

eng: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, Come.
local: Na Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nnyɔ su ni, na ma ti ka deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnyɔ su ni suse ka, Fite.

eng: Out came another horse, fiery red. Its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth so that people would kill each other. He was given a large sword.
local: Na kpɔŋgɔ kɔkɔrɛ afite, na ba ma i fun­fɔ n takobe tintiin, na ba ma i yiko ka i yi tana­laifiɛ fite durunya n nu, na minɛm kun bu beŋgum.

eng: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, Come! So I looked, and there was a black horse. Its rider held a balance for weighing in his hand.
local: Na Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nsan su ni, na ma ti ka deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nsan su n suse ka, Fite. M niɛɛri na ma ŋu ka, kpɔŋgɔ biri lɛɛ. I fun­fɔ n bita deke bo bu bita sunsun diire ni i saa nu.

eng: I heard what sounded like a voice from among the four living creatures. It said, A quart of wheat for a denarion, and three quarts of barley for a denarion, but dont damage the olive oil and the wine.
local: Na ma ti akonvi be ŋga deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam n nu, i ŋa, Ŋgani ayaba ŋgboroko kun ti juma­yo­fɔ le­kun ŋkatɔ ɔ. Na abuwe ayaba ŋgboroko nsan ti juma­yo­fɔ le­kun ŋkatɔ. Ama, má am saaki ŋguin ni nzan ni.

eng: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, Come.
local: Na Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nna su ni, na ma ti ka deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nna su n suse ka, Fite.

eng: So I looked, and there was a pale green horse. Its riders name was Death, and the Grave was following right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill by sword, famine, disease, and the wild animals of the earth.
local: M niɛɛri na ma ŋu kpɔŋgɔ nzuin faa, i fun­fɔ n duma la Yiwee, na bo bu fere i ka Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri n gusu su i su. Na Nyɛmɛ ama bu durunya kpaaki nu nna buekun yiko ka bu fa luɛ, ni ahɔɛ, ni tukpaki, ni boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ tiɛm, na bu kun bu.

eng: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar those who had been slaughtered on account of the word of God and the witness they had given.
local: Na Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nnu su ni, na ma ŋu minɛm awiɛwiɛ Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n bu, bɛrɛ la minɛm bo bu yo kuunri bu fa kɔ bu Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ kaan­wa, ni bu daani dii­wa n dɛɛ ti.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice, Holy and true Master, how long will you wait before you pass judgment? How long before you require justice for our blood, which was shed by those who live on earth.
local: Na bu sutiɛn faŋga su ka, Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ casi, nahɔrɛ­fɔ, cɛɛn nzu ye a dí minɛm bo bu yo kuunri ya n jɔrɛ na a cín bu sui ma ya.

eng: Each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little longer, until their fellow servants and brothers and sisters—who were about to be killed as they were—were finished.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ awura bu kun kun kɛrɛ sutura fofoe, na wɔ se bu ka, Am yi am ŋumi kaan ka, na bu bá kun am beŋgu akɔɔm, ni am beŋgu Kristo­fɔm kɛrɛ kɔ wie ka, kabo bu yo kuunri am barasu ni, na m dí bu jɔrɛ.

eng: I looked on as he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as funeral clothing, and the entire moon turned red as blood.
local: Na ma ŋu ka Bɔmbaa n acin aŋmaŋgi wiɛ mɛnɛ nsɛn su ni, na asiɛn akpisi faŋga su, na wiɛ akaaki biri didiki ka nambiri ŋuma, na sara n akaaki kɔkɔrɛ ka mbunja dɛɛ su.

eng: The stars of the sky fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its fruit when shaken by a strong wind.
local: Na asara­mbaam afite aŋgoro ati atɔtɔ kabo aŋuma ba kpisi damba baka na i poorim titi tɔtɔ barasu ni.

eng: The sky disappeared like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
local: Na aŋgoro n kɛrɛ anyanya kabo bu nyanya biɛ ni, na wɔ mini, na bukam, ni nzue nu lui bambakam kɛrɛ ayasu bu tanaa­biri.

eng: Then the kings of the earth, the officials and the generals, the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
local: Na durunya n nu nfɛmɛm, ni tere­tere­fɔm, ni soja nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni neŋge­fɔm, ni nfaŋga­fɔm, ni akɔɔm, ni sɔnɔ kɛrɛ akɔ afiɛ yabuɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim buɛm nu, ni yabuɛ bambakam bu, bukam su.

eng: They called to the mountains and the rocks, Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the Lambs wrath!.
local: Na ba se ka, Má bukam bereki gɔ ya ŋu, na má yabuɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim kata ya ŋu, má i ti ye, adena má sɔnɔ n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ŋu ya, na má Bɔmbaa n gusu cin ya sui.

eng: The great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand.
local: Dama cɛɛn bo Nyɛmɛ ni Bɔmbaa ni cin sɔnɔ kɛrɛ sui n aju, ye ŋma koro jina bu nyunu a.

eng: Then, when the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
local: Saŋga bo Bɔmbaa n cin ŋmaŋgiiri wiɛ mɛnɛ nso su n ni, na deke kɛrɛ atɔ amuna aŋgoro n nu, haari miti abura­san.

eng: Then I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.
local: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ nsom bo bu jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu ni, na ba fa awiɛ fitaawa nso ama bu.

eng: Another angel came and stood at the altar, and he held a gold bowl for burning incense. He was given a large amount of incense, in order to offer it on behalf of the prayers of all the saints on the gold altar in front of the throne.
local: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ nyiŋga ka i bita sika ayaba, bo bu fa ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani gɔ nu na bu kunkun i ni, na wɔ kɔ ajina Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri n bi. Na ba ma i ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani beberebe ka i fa buka Nyɛmɛ minɛm nyɛmɛsere su, na i fa kunkun Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n bo bu fa sika mata su, na i wo fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu ni.

eng: The smoke of the incense offered for the prayers of the saints rose up before God from the angels hand.
local: Na ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani n ŋmisini n ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm nyɛmɛsere n ayasu mɛrɛkɛ n saa nu afun akɔ Nyɛmɛ nyunu.

eng: Then the angel took the incense container and filled it with fire from the altar. He threw it down to the earth, and there were thunder, voices, lightning, and an earthquake.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ayi sin Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri n su, na wɔ fa awura ayaba n nu, na wɔ yi, na wɔ fa atu asandi durunya n nu. Na nzue sukpura sutiɛntiɛn, na i sujeje, na i sukpɛkpɛsɛ, na asiɛn n akpisi.

eng: Then the seven angels who held the seven trumpets got ready to blow them.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsom n bo bu bita awiɛ fitaawam n asɛsɛ bu ŋu su ka bu kan awiɛm ni.

eng: The first angel blew his trumpet, and hail and fire mixed with blood appeared, and was thrown down to the earth. A third of the earth was burned up. A third of the trees were burned up. All the green grass was burned up.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo i du mɔ n akan i dɛɛ awiɛ ni. Na nzue yabuɛ, ni sin, saŋga ni mbunja atɔ durunya n nu, na sin aje durunya n kpaaki nu nsan buekun, ni bakam kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun, ni wura mɔnɛ kɛrɛ.

eng: Then the second angel blew his trumpet, and something like a huge mountain burning with fire was thrown down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo i tɔ su nnyɔ su n akan i awiɛ ni, na deke be, i ti ka buka bambaka wɔ, ye wɔ so sin su a, na ba fa i atu tieku n nu, na tieku n kpaaki nu nsan buekun akaaki mbunja.

eng: a third of the creatures living in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
local: Na deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo tieku n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun awu, na lee bambakam bo bu wo tieku n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun gusu asaaki.

eng: Then the third angel blew his trumpet, and a great star, burning like a torch, fell from heaven. It fell on a third of the rivers and springs of water.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo i tɔ su nsan su n akan i awiɛ ni, na asara­mbaa bambaka bo i je sin ka wura sin afite aŋgoro aba atɔ kunmam ni nzuetinm bo bu wo durunya n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun nu.

eng: The stars name is Wormwood, and a third of the waters became wormwood, and many people died from the water, because it became so bitter.
local: Asara­mbaa n duma la Wiwi, na nzue n bo i wo durunya n nu n kpaaki nu nsan akaaki wiwi, nzue n ti wiwi n dɛɛ ti, na minɛm dɔŋgu bo bu nuuri nyini nzue n awu.

eng: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth. They held back the earths four winds so that no wind would blow against the earth, the sea, or any tree.
local: Nyini sin na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛm nna, ka bu jina durunya n awɔrɛ nna n kɛrɛ nu na bu kanda aŋuma, na má i kpiɛ asiɛn n su, ni tieku nu, wara i kpiɛ gɔ baka su.

eng: I saw another angel coming up from the east, holding the seal of the living God. He cried out with a loud voice to the four angels who had been given the power to damage the earth and sea.
local: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ be ka i fite wiɛ afiiri, i bita deke be bo Nyɛmɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ n fa se i minɛm nyaari. Na wɔ tiɛn faŋga su ajɔjɔ mɛrɛkɛ nnam bo Nyɛmɛ ama bu yiko ka bu saaki asiɛn n ni tieku n dɔ.

eng: Then the fourth angel blew his trumpet, and a third of the sun was struck, and a third of the moon, and a third of the stars so that a third of them became dark. The day lost a third of its light, and the night lost a third of its light too.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo i tɔ su nna su n akan i awiɛ ni, na deke be aba atɔ wiɛ, ni sara, ni asara­mbaam kɛrɛ kpaaki nu nsan buekun su asaaki bu, na ba kaaki biri tuum. Wiɛsu ni kɔŋguɛ n kpaaki nu nsan buekun, ayo má wein.

eng: Then I looked and I heard an eagle flying high overhead. It said with a loud voice, Horror, horror, oh! The horror for those who live on earth because of the blasts of the remaining trumpets that the three angels are about to blow.
local: Na ma niɛ, na ma ŋu ka kajiri be sulaandi tu aŋgoro, na ma ti kabo i tiɛn barasu ni, i ŋa, Oi, keto, keto, keto wo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i wo durunya n nu n ŋu, kabo mɛrɛkɛ nsanm bo ba ka n koro ka bu kan bu awiɛm ni, na bu tiiri.

eng: He said, Dont damage the earth, the sea, or the trees until we have put a seal on the foreheads of those who serve our God.
local: na wɔ se bu ka, Má am se ka am saaki asiɛn n ni tieku n ni bakam ni, má ka ya ba ase Nyɛmɛ minɛm kɛrɛ ŋuma su nyaari awie ka.

eng: Then I heard the number of those who were sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed from every tribe of the Israelites.
local: Na wɔ kan minɛm bo ba se bu nyaari bu ŋuma su ni, na wɔ kere m bu nɔ, bu kɛrɛ kɔ yasuuri minɛ akpii yaa ni akpii abura­na ni nna a, bu fite Yisareli­fɔm dɛɛ busu n kɛrɛ nu wɔ.

eng: From the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand were sealed; from the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand.
local: Yuda dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ bo ba se bu nyaari, na Ruben dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ, na Gadi dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ.

eng: from the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand.
local: Na Aseri dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ, na Nafatali dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ, na Manase dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ.

eng: from the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand.
local: Na Simeyon dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ, na Levi dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ. Na Izaki dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ.

eng: from the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; from the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand were sealed.
local: Na Zabulon dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ, na Yosefu dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ, na Bɛnjamin dɛɛ busu nu­fɔm ti minɛ akpii buru ni akpii nnyɔ.

eng: After this I looked, and there was a great crowd that no one could number. They were from every nation, tribe, people, and language. They were standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands.
local: Nyini sin na ma ŋu jama beberebe, bu la má nɔ, bu fite durunya asiɛn n kɛrɛ su, ni adamande kɛrɛ nu, ni durunya daanfiɛ n kɛrɛ nu, ni durunya aniɛm kɛrɛ nu, ka, bu jina yiri n bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ni Bɔmbaa n nyunu. Bu wura tanlɛɛ fofoe yorom, bu bita kpakpa nyawam bu saa nu.

eng: They cried out with a loud voice: Victory belongs to our God who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb.
local: Na ba bo afere faŋga su ase ka, Nyɛmɛ bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ni Bɔmbaa n ama ya nya ya tii a.

eng: All the angels stood in a circle around the throne, and around the elders and the four living creatures. They fell facedown before the throne and worshipped God.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛm n kɛrɛ ajina abara ayɛ fɛmɛya biɛ, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam ni, na ba dada bu nyunu su asiɛŋgu Nyɛmɛ bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n nyunu, na ba su i.

eng: saying, Amen! Blessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honor and power and might be to our God forever and always. Amen.
local: Na bu suse ka, Ami. Kansi, ni kpiri, ni alecira, ni ansɛ, ni jirima, ni yiko, ni faŋga ti ya Nyɛmɛ dɛɛ wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ saŋga. Ami.

eng: Then one of the elders said to me, Who are these people wearing white robes, and where did they come from.
local: Na nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nu kun abisa m ka, Ŋmam ye a ŋu ka bu wura tanlɛɛ fofoe yorom n a, ye bu fite ni wɔ.
