eng: I said to him, Sir, you know. Then he said to me, These people have come out of great hardship. They have washed their robes and made them white in the Lambs blood.
local: Na ma se i ka, M mibiɛ, wɔrɔ si a, mini si má. Na wɔ se m ka, Bɛrɛ ti minɛm bo ba fite anyum­boro kekereke nu n a, bɛrɛ ye ba ŋmisi bu sutura Bɔmbaa ni mbunja n nu ayo bu fofoe n a.

eng: This is the reason they are before Gods throne. They worship him day and night in his temple, and the one seated on the throne will shelter them.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, ye bu jina Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, i fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu ye bu su i kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu n a, na Nyɛmɛ bo i tana i fɛmɛya biɛ n su n wó bu dɔ na i yó bu kanda.

eng: They wont hunger or thirst anymore. No sun or scorching heat will beat down on them.
local: Ahɔɛ ni nzuehɔɛ tí má bu kun cɛɛn be, wiɛ bó má bu, na i kɛkɛrɛ gusu wúra má bu.

eng: because the Lamb who is in the midst of the throne will shepherd them. He will lead them to the springs of life-giving water, and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.
local: Dama Bɔmbaa n bo i wo fɛmɛya biɛ n afiɛn ni yó bu sasa­fɔ a, na yiri fá bu kɔ bura nzue bo i tiɛ na i ma ŋgɔɔ bo i la má awieeri n a. Na Nyɛmɛ núnuka bu nyunvuin kɛrɛ bu nyunu.

eng: Then the fifth angel blew his trumpet, and I saw a star that had fallen from heaven to earth, and he was given the key to the shaft of the abyss.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nnu su n akan i awiɛ ni, na ma ŋu ka asara­mbaa be afite aŋgoro n aba atɔ asiɛŋgu. Na ba ma i kadombaa ka i teke kunma n bo i kɔ ŋgoŋgo na i la má awieeri ni.

eng: He opened the shaft of the abyss; and smoke rose up from the shaft, like smoke from a huge furnace. The sun and air were darkened by the smoke from the shaft.
local: Na wɔ teke nyini kunma n ni. I tekeeri i ni, na ŋmisini afite kunma n nu, i ti ka sin bambaka tɔrɔɔ­wa ŋmisini wɔ. Na ŋmisini n bo i fite kunma n nu n ama wiɛ ni aŋgoro n kɛrɛ asi biri didiki.

eng: Then locusts came forth from the smoke and onto the earth. They were given power like the power that scorpions have on the earth.
local: Na lalom afite ŋmisini n nu asandi ade asiɛn n su, na Nyɛmɛ ama bu yiko, ka bundaanim dɛɛ su.

eng: They were told not to hurt the grass of the earth or any green plant or any tree. They could only hurt the people who didnt have the seal of God on their foreheads.
local: Na wɔ se bu ka má bu di wura ni diire mbaa wara bakam, ama, bu kan minɛm n bo bu la má Nyɛmɛ anyaari see­wa bu ŋuma su ni.

eng: The locusts werent allowed to kill them, but only to make them suffer for five months—and the suffering they inflict is like that of a scorpion when it strikes a person.
local: Wɔ ma má bu yiko ka bu kun minɛm wɔ, ama, bu kere bu wahara sara nnu. Wahara ni ti kabo bundaani kan sɔnɔ na i yo ya n wɔ.

eng: In those days people will seek death, but they wont find it. They will want to die, but death will run away from them.
local: Nyini saŋga ni, adamande sún kpini yiwee busu na bu wú, ama, bu nyá má i.

eng: The locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads were what seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces were like human faces.
local: Lalom n ti ka kpɔŋgɔm bo ba cici bu ka bu kɔ luɛ dɛɛ su wɔ, deke be wo bu tii su, ka sika kere dɛɛ su, na bu nyunu­awa ti ka adamande dɛɛ su.

eng: their hair was like womens hair, and their teeth were like lions teeth.
local: Bu ndɔrɛ ti tintiin kabo bara bo i la tii­ŋmiɛ tintiin ti n wɔ, na bu jee ti ka jara dɛɛ.

eng: In front they had what seemed to be iron armor upon their chests, and the sound of their wings was like the sound of many chariots and horses racing into battle.
local: Deke be kata bu hoe su pɛpɛrɛpɛ ka bulaari dɛɛ su, na bu laŋkpɛtɛm suyo naŋmiɛ kabo kpɔŋgɔm ni bu torokum n beberebe suŋmati kɔ luɛ na bu yo naŋmiɛ n wɔ.

eng: They also have tails with stingers, just like scorpions; and in their tails is their power to hurt people for five months.
local: Bu lɔ, bo bu fa kan n ti ka bundaani dɛɛ su wɔ. Bu la yiko bu lɔ n nu, bo bu fa kere minɛ wahara sara nnu.

eng: Their king is an angel from the abyss, whose Hebrew name is Abaddon, and whose Greek name is Apollyon.
local: Bu fɛmɛ n la mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ kunma ŋgoŋgo n bo i la má awieeri n su ni. Hiburu aniɛ nu bu fere i ka, Abadon , ye Giriki aniɛ nu bu fere i ka, Apolin , i bu la ka, saaki­fɔ.

eng: The first horror has passed. Look! Two horrors are still coming after this.
local: Tɔ. Ciciri su dɛɛ ahoresaaki n asin, nnyɔ wo sin suba.

eng: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet, and I heard a voice from the four horns of the gold altar that is before God.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsɛn su n akan i awiɛ n na ma ti akonvi be ŋga ka i fite Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n bue nna n kɛrɛ, ba fa sika amata su na i wo Nyɛmɛ nyunu ni.

eng: It said to the sixth angel, who had the trumpet, Release the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates.
local: Na akonvi n ajɔjɔ mɛrɛkɛ nsɛn su n bo i bita awiɛ n dɔ, i ŋa, Kɔ mɛrɛkɛ nnam bo bu wo kunma kpiri n bo bu fere i ka, Yufarati n dɔ, na a nyaŋgi bu ji.

eng: Then the four angels who had been made ready for that hour, day, month, and year were released to kill a third of humankind.
local: Na wɔ nyaŋgi mɛrɛkɛ nnam bo ba du mɔ akpa bu ŋu aŋminda nyini cɛɛn n gbingbin na bu kun minɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun ni.

eng: The number of cavalry troops was two hundred million. I heard their number.
local: Na ma ti kabo bu sojam bo bu fun kpɔŋgɔ nɔ ti, bu nɔ kɔ yasuuri akpii akpii busu yaa nnyɔ ɔ.

eng: And this is the way I saw the horses and their riders in the vision: they had breastplates that were fiery red, dark blue, and yellow as sulfur. The horses heads were like lions heads, and out of their mouths came fire, smoke, and sulfur.
local: Deke n bo i yo m ka alɛlɛ ni, m ŋuuri kpɔŋgɔm, ni bo bu fun bu ni, ka bulaari pɛpɛrɛpɛ be kata bu hoe su, nbem boro kɔkɔrɛ ka sin wɔ, na nbem ti biri ka bura nzue, na nbem ti ka ndɛrɛ nzue, kpɔŋgɔm tii dika ti ka jara tii wɔ. Na sin, ni ŋmisini, ni ferewita sufite bu nɔɔ nu.

eng: By these three plagues a third of humankind was killed: by the fire, smoke, and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
local: Ahin deke kekereke nsanm ni kuunri minɛ n bo bu wo durunya n nu kpaaki nu nsan buekun n a, bɛrɛ la sin, ni ŋmisini, ni ferewita n bo i fite bu nɔɔ nu ni.

eng: The horses power is in their mouths and their tails, for their tails are like snakes with heads that inflict injuries.
local: Dama kpɔŋgɔm n yiko kɛrɛ wo bu nɔɔ, ni bu lɔm nu wɔ. Bu lɔm ti ka woom wɔ, bu la atiim, ye bu fa kere minɛ wahara a.

eng: The rest of humankind, who werent killed by these plagues, didnt change their hearts and lives and turn from their handiwork. They didnt stop worshipping demons and idols made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood—idols that cant see or hear or walk.
local: Minɛm bo ahin wahara n akun má bu na bu kaari ni, ba kaaki má bu wo, ba yaki má amɔɛm su, ni dekem bo ba fa sikam, ni jɛtɛm, ni jawiim, ni yabuɛm, ni bakam ayo bu ka bu amɔɛm, nyini dekem koro ŋu má asi, na bu koro ti má asui, na bu koro nati má ni, ba yaki má bu su.

eng: They didnt turn away from their murders, their spells and drugs, their sexual immorality, or their stealing.
local: Ba kaaki má bu wo fa kɔ kabo bu kun minɛm ni, ni kabo bu di ayiri ni, ni kabo bu yo sakaraya ni, ni kabo bu wɔ awie ni.

eng: Then I saw another powerful angel coming down from heaven. He was robed with a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet were like fiery pillars.
local: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ faŋga­fɔ be bekun ka i fite aŋgoro sujura ba. Na yinyiŋgu akata i ŋu ka tani dɛɛ su. Na ayi bo i kpiɛ aŋgoro n abara ayɛ i tii, i nyunu ti ka wiɛ wɔ, na i jaam ti ka sin taferema.

eng: He held an open scroll in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
local: I bita kadasi kaan be i saa nu, i ti tekee­wa a. Na wɔ fa i jaa fɔmbɔrɔ atiɛn tieku n su, na wɔ fa i jaa biɛ atiɛn asiɛn n su.

eng: He called out with a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he called out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
local: Na wɔ tiɛn faŋga su kabo jara ŋuundi ni, na nzue tiɛɛn­wa nso n abɔ i nu.

eng: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven say, Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and dont write it down.
local: Nzue n tiɛɛnri kpe nso ni, má m ŋa m kɛrɛ wɔ, na ma ti akonvi be ŋga aŋgoro. I ŋa, Má a kɛrɛ jɔjɔɔ­wa n bo nzue tiɛɛn­wa nso n ase ni. Má a kan kere be.

eng: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo m ŋuuri i ka i jina tieku n ni asiɛn n su, n ayɛ i saa fɔmbɔrɔ su akere aŋgoro.

eng: He swore by the one who lives forever and always, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, and said, The time is up.
local: na wɔ tan Nyɛmɛ bo i la ŋgɔɔ na i wo bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ ni, yiri bo i yiiri aŋgoro, ni asiɛn, ni tieku, ni deke kɛrɛ bo bu wo nu ni, na wɔ se ka, Nyɛmɛ ŋminda má kun.

eng: In the days when the seventh angel blows his trumpet, Gods mysterious purpose will be accomplished, fulfilling the good news he gave to his servants the prophets.
local: Ama, saŋga n bo mɛrɛkɛ nso su n ba sukan i awiɛ n ye Nyɛmɛ asiri nu jɔrɛ ni yó yii­wa, kabo i yo seeri i akɔɔm bo bu ti i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni.

eng: Then the voice I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, Go, take the opened scroll from the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.
local: Na akonvi n bo i jɔjɔɔri m dɔ aŋgoro n nu n ajɔjɔ m dɔ bekun, i ŋa, Kɔ de kadasi kaan n bo wɔ teke na i wo mɛrɛkɛ n bo i jina tieku n ni asiɛn n su n saa nu.

eng: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the scroll. He said to me, Take it and eat it. It will make you sick to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.
local: Na ma kɔ mɛrɛkɛ n dɔ, na ma se i ka, Fa kadasi kaan n ma m. Na wɔ se m ka, De na a kpɛsɛ i, i yó u nɔɔ nu mamari ka wiɛ wɔ, ama, i to u kunnu, i yó wiwi wɔ.

eng: So I took the scroll from the angels hand and ate it. And it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it made my stomach churn.
local: Na ma de kadasi kaan n mɛrɛkɛ n dɔ, na ma kpɛsɛ i. I ti mamari m nɔɔ nu ka wiɛ wɔ, m miiri na wɔ yo wiwi m kunnu.

eng: I was told, You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Kaaki jɔjɔ sam bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka bu bá tu minɛ barasu dɔŋgu, ni daanfiɛ barasu dɔŋgu, ni nfɛmɛm dɔŋgu.

eng: Then I was given a measuring rod, which was like a pole. And I was told, Get up and measure Gods temple, the altar, and those who worship there.
local: Na ba fa deke be bo bu bita sunsun ama m, i ti ka kpama a, na ba se m ka, Kɔ sunsun Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n ni Mikun n yoo­wa yoo­biri ni, na a kan minɛm bo bu wo nu dɔ su Nyɛmɛ ni.

eng: But dont measure the court outside the temple. Leave that out, because it has been given to the nations, and they will trample the holy city underfoot for forty-two months.
local: Ama, má a sunsun awɔrɛ n bo i bara yɛɛri Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n kɛrɛ na ba ta kobe abara ayɛ i n buka su, dama ba fa nyini fofoe n ama minɛm bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni, na bu náti saaki Nyɛmɛ miɛ casi ni, sara abura­na ni nnyɔ afiɛn.

eng: And I will allow my two witnesses to prophesy for one thousand two hundred sixty days, wearing mourning clothes.
local: M súnma m daani­di­fɔ nnyɔm ni, na bu fá bɔtɔ kara ka tani dɛɛ su, bo i kere ka bu wo ahoresaaki nu, na bu kán m nɔaniɛ n kere minɛm, nyini cɛɛn akpii kun ni yaa nnyɔ ni abura­sɛn n nu.

eng: These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand before the Lord of the earth.
local: Daani­di­fɔ nnyɔm ni, bɛrɛ la ŋguin­mbaa baka nnyɔm, ni fitana takaa­biri nnyɔm bo bu jina Nyɛmɛ bo i dɛɛ la asiɛn nyunu ni.

eng: If anyone wants to hurt them, fire comes out of their mouth and burns up their enemies. So if anyone wants to hurt them, they have to be killed in this way.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i yo bu sa be, sin fite bu nɔɔ nu ba yara bu kpɔ­fɔm. Nyini ti, nzɛn be ŋa i yo bu sa be, kabo i ti ye ni ka i wu lɛɛ.

eng: They have the power to close up the sky so that no rain will fall for as long as they prophesy. They also have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with any plague, as often as they wish.
local: Bu la yiko bo bu koro kanda nzue aŋgoro na má nzue tɔ, saŋga bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n kan su ni, na bu la yiko bo bu koro ma nzuem kaaki mbunja, na bu kaaki la yiko bo bu koro ma alɔɔba tɔ durunya n nu, saŋga kɛrɛ bo bu koro.

eng: When they have finished their witnessing, the beast that comes up from the abyss will make war on them, gain victory over them, and kill them.
local: Nzɛn bu ba diiri bu daani kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ n wieeri, na boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo i fite kunma n bo i kɔ ŋgoŋgo na i la má awieeri ni kún luɛ ni bɛrɛ, na i kúnma bu, na i kún bu.

eng: Their dead bodies will lie on the street of the great city that is spiritually called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.
local: Na bu fuin ká da miɛ bambaka n atin afiɛn. Nyini miɛ n nu­fɔm sa­yoo­wa ti tiɛ wɔ, nyini ti ye bu fa bu sunsun Sodom­fɔm wara Ijipiti­fɔm n a. Nyini miɛ n nu ye minɛm kpamaari bu Mibiɛ baka jaanu n su n a.

eng: And for three and a half days, members of the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations will look at their dead bodies, but they wont let their dead bodies be put in a tomb.
local: Bu fuin ni ká da bɛrɛ le­san ni wiɛ sin, na minɛm, ni daanfiɛm, ni aniɛm, ni asiɛnm kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu, bu nu nbem sín na bu niɛ bu, na bu dú má su na be se bu.

eng: Those who live on earth will rejoice over them. They will celebrate and give each other gifts, because these two prophets had brought such pain to those who live on earth.
local: Bu yiwee n dɛɛ ti, durunya n nu­fɔm síri taari, na bu gɔ́ agɔrɛ, na bu cé bu beŋgum aceerim, dama nyini Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nnyɔm bo ba wu n akere bu kɛrɛ wuwuruwa.

eng: But after three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear came over those who saw them.
local: Le­san ni wiɛ sin n dɛɛ sin, na Nyɛmɛ ayi i ŋumi bo i ma ŋgɔɔ n agɔ bu ŋu, na ba yasu ajina, na sɛrɛ ati sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo bu ŋuuri bu ni.

eng: Then they heard a loud voice from heaven say to them, Come up here. And they went up to heaven in a cloud, while their enemies watched them.
local: Na bɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ nnyɔm n ati akonvi bambaka be ka i jɔjɔ aŋgoro nu, i ŋa, Am fun bra aŋgoro fɛn. Na ba wura yinyiŋgu n nu akɔ aŋgoro, bu kpɔ­fɔm jina suniɛ bu.

eng: At that hour there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed by the earthquake, and the rest were afraid and gave glory to the God of heaven.
local: Jina n nu bɛrɛ na asiɛn akpisi faŋga su, na miɛ bambaka n kpaaki nu buru buekun afufu, na wɔ kun minɛ akpii nso, na sɛrɛ ati minɛm bo bu kaari ni, na bu sukansi Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni.

eng: The second horror is over. The third horror is coming soon.
local: Tɔ. Na nnyɔ su dɛɛ ahoresaaki n gusu asin, bo i tɔ su nsan su ni suba kisa.

eng: Then the seventh angel blew his trumpet, and there were loud voices in heaven saying, The kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord and his Christ, and he will rule forever and always.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nso su n akan i awiɛ ni, na akonvim atiɛn faŋga su aŋgoro n nu, bu ŋa, Durunya n nu fɛmɛya kɛrɛ faŋga ti ya Micɛra ni Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n dɛɛ wɔ. I dɛɛ fɛmɛya ni wó bɛrɛ saŋga kɛrɛ a.

eng: Then the twenty-four elders, who were seated on their thrones before God, fell on their faces and worshipped God.
local: Na nkpiɛn­kpiɛn abura­nyɔ ni nnam n bo bu tana bu fɛmɛya biɛm su Nyɛmɛ nyunu n ada bu nyunu su asiɛŋgu na bu subo tuntun Nyɛmɛ.

eng: They said, We give thanks to you, Lord God Almighty, who is and was, for you have taken your great power and enforced your rule.
local: Bu ŋa, Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Kpiri bo a la má aŋusu ni, wɔrɔ bo a wo bɛrɛ na a yo woori bɛrɛ ni, ya da u asi, dama a de u yiko bambaka n ye a suniɛ durunya n kɛrɛ su a.

eng: The nations were enraged, but your wrath came. The time came for the dead to be judged. The time came to reward your servants, the prophets and saints, and those who fear your name, both small and great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.
local: Durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ afa yaa, ye saŋga aju bo u gusu ahore ayasu, na a dí minɛm bo ba wu n jɔrɛ. Na saŋga n akaaki aju bo a tɔ́ u akɔɔm ŋkatɔ, bɛrɛ bo bu ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm ni, ni u minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu su u ni, nfaŋga­fɔm ni tarakam kɛrɛ. Ye saŋga n akaaki aju bo a sáaki bɛrɛ n bo bu saaki durunya ni.

eng: Then Gods temple in heaven was opened, and the chest containing his covenant appeared in his temple. There were lightning, voices, thunder, an earthquake, and large hail.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n bo i wo aŋgoro n ateke, na ma ŋu Nyɛmɛ alaka n bo i kereeri ka wɔ fa sɛsɛ su adi i di n ka i wo dɔ, na nzue sukpɛsɛ na i sutiɛntiɛn, na i suyo naŋmiɛ, na asiɛn sukpisi, na nzue yabuɛ ŋgbiŋgbiri sutɔtɔ.

eng: Then a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars on her head.
local: Na alekutura sa be ayo aŋgoro dɔ. Bara be awo dɔ, wɔ fa wiɛ akara ka tani dɛɛ su, na sara gusu awo i jaa bu, asara­mbaa buru ni nnyɔ butu i tii ka fɛmɛya kere dɛɛ su.

eng: She was pregnant, and she cried out because she was in labor, in pain from giving birth.
local: I bita akunnu, na baa­wu yaari afa i na i subo awie.

eng: Then another sign appeared in heaven: it was a great fiery red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and seven royal crowns on his heads.
local: Na alekutura sa be gusu ayo aŋgoro dɔ, m ŋuuri woo tiɛ kɔkɔrɛ bambaka be, i tii ti nso wɔ, na i la mbiɛ buru, fɛmɛya kere nso butu atii nso kun kun n kɛrɛ su.

eng: His tail swept down a third of heavens stars and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood in front of the woman who was about to give birth so that when she gave birth, he might devour her child.
local: Na wɔ fa i lɔ abaandi asara­mbaam kpaaki nu nsan buekun aji asiɛŋgu. Na wɔ ba ajina bara n nyunu, adena i wuuri baa n na i kaba fa baa n mi.

eng: She gave birth to a son, a male child who is to rule all the nations with an iron rod. Her child was snatched up to God and his throne.
local: Na bara n awu baa­biɛsɔ, bo i bá niɛ daanfiɛ daanfiɛm kɛrɛ su, na i bíta bu kekereke kabo naŋkasi fa bulaari kɔrɔma tu bo i naanim barasu ni. Na ba fa baa n akɔ yiri Nyɛmɛ ni, i fɛmɛya tanaa­biri n dɔ.

eng: Then the woman fled into the desert, where God has prepared a place for her. There she will be taken care of for one thousand two hundred sixty days.
local: Na bara n aŋmati akɔ boro kpantain awɔrɛ be nu, bo Nyɛmɛ asɛsɛ i su ama i na bu suniɛ i dika haari kɔ ju cɛɛn akpii kun ni yaa nnyɔ ni abura­sɛn.

eng: Then there was war in heaven: Michael and his angels fought the dragon. The dragon and his angels fought back.
local: Tɔ, na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ Mikali ni i jamam, ni woo tiɛ ni i dɛɛ jamam sukun luɛ ni bu beŋgu aŋgoro.

eng: but they did not prevail, and there was no longer any place for them in heaven.
local: Ama, na Mikali ni i dɛɛ jamam n akunma woo tiɛ n ni i dɛɛ jamam ni, na ba fɔn bu afite aŋgoro, ba nya má woo­biri dɔ kun. Na ba fa woo tiɛ bambaka n ni i dɛɛ jamam n kɛrɛ aji asiɛŋgu. Yiri ti dada dɛɛ woo tiɛ n a, yiri la Sitana, bu kaaki fere i ka Dadaka­fɔ, yiri dadaka durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ n a.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice in heaven say, Now the salvation and power and kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ have come. The accuser of our brothers and sisters, who accuses them day and night before our God, has been thrown down.
local: Na ma ti akonvi bambaka be ŋga ka i sutiɛn faŋga su aŋgoro. I ŋa, Kisa, ya ŋu kabo ya Nyɛmɛ n la faŋga na i de i minɛm tii ji, na i di i fɛmɛya ni. Kisa, ye Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n akere i yiko a, dama ba fɔn ayi Sitana aŋgoro aji durunya n nu fɛn, yiri bo i ti saaki­fɔ na i jina Nyɛmɛ nyunu na i jaraki ya ninbaam kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu ni.

eng: They gained the victory over him on account of the blood of the Lamb and the word of their witness. Love for their own lives didnt make them afraid to die.
local: Bɔmbaa n dɛɛ mbunja, ni bu daani dii­wa ye bu fa kunmaari i a, ba soro má bu yiwee, bu soroori su wɔ, ka bu wu.

eng: Therefore, rejoice, you heavens and you who dwell in them. But oh! The horror for the earth and sea! The devil has come down to you with great rage, for he knows that he only has a short time.
local: Nyini ti, aŋgoro ni ambɛrɛ kɛrɛ bo am wo aŋgoro ni, am ma am ahore jɔ, dama Sitana la má am faŋga kun. Ama, ahoresaaki bá tɔ asiɛn n ni tieku n su, dama Sitana ajura am dɔ. Yaa ti i agaya, dama i si ka i saŋga sunnu má kun.

eng: When the dragon saw that he had been thrown down to the earth, he chased the woman who had given birth to the male child.
local: Saŋga bo woo tiɛ n ŋu ka ba yi i aŋgoro aji asiɛŋgu ni, na wɔ nya ŋmati afɔn bara n bo i wuuri baa­biɛsɔ n ka i tara i.

eng: But the woman was given the two wings of the great eagle so that she could fly to her place in the desert. There she would be taken care of—out of the snakes reach—for a time and times and half a time.
local: Ama, na Nyɛmɛ ama bara n kajiri bambaka laŋkpɛtɛ nnyɔ, adena i tu kɔ boro kpantain n awɔrɛ n bo wɔ sɛsɛ su ama i ni, na i niɛ i dika afɔ nsan ni kpaaki nu, na má woo tiɛ n tu i.

eng: Then from his mouth the snake poured a river of water after the woman so that the river would sweep her away.
local: Na woo tiɛ n apuuti nzue afite i nɔɔ nu ka wɔmbu nzue dɛɛ su, na má i sɔ bara n kɔ.

eng: But the earth helped the woman. The earth opened its mouth and swallowed the river that the dragon poured out of his mouth.
local: Ama, na asiɛn n ade bara n aji, i kpaaki nu nnyɔ ɔ, na nzue n bo i soŋgi fiteeri woo tiɛ n nɔɔ nu n awura asiɛn bu, nyini ti, nzue n atu má bara ni.

eng: So the dragon was furious with the woman, and he went off to make war on the rest of her children, on those who keep Gods commandments and hold firmly to the witness of Jesus.
local: Woo tiɛ n atu má bara n dɛɛ ti, na yaa ati i agaya, na wɔ kaaki ka i kɔ kun luɛ ni bara n busu nu­fɔm, bo ba de Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ maa­wam n na bu di daani ni Yesu labari kpa n ni.

eng: Then the dragon stood on the seashore.
local: Na woo tiɛ n akɔ ajina tieku n nɔɔ su.

eng: and I saw a beast coming up out of the sea. It had ten horns and seven heads. Each of its horns was decorated with a royal crown, and on its heads were blasphemous names.
local: Na ma ŋu boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ be ka i sufite tieku n nu, i la atii nso wɔ, na i mbiɛ ti buru, mbiɛ n kɛrɛ butu fɛmɛya kere, ye duma kɛrɛɛ­wa be bo i kpiɛ Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ n wo atii kun kun n kɛrɛ su.

eng: The beast I saw was like a leopard. Its feet were like a bears, and its mouth was like a lions mouth. The dragon gave it his power, throne, and great authority.
local: Boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo m ŋuuri i n ti ka awɔba a, ama, i jaa ti bambaka, na i la faŋga, na i nɔɔ ti ka jara dɛɛ. Woo tiɛ n afa i dɛɛ yiko, ni i fɛmɛya, ni i faŋga afa ama i.

eng: One of its heads appeared to have been slain and killed, but its deadly wound was healed. So the whole earth was amazed and followed the beast.
local: Na ma ŋu ka ba kpiɛ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n kun tii, i ti ka ba kpiɛ i kɔm wɔ, ama, kani n awie, na wɔ yo durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ alekutura na bu susu i su.

eng: They worshipped the dragon because it had given the beast its authority. They worshipped the beast and said, Who is like the beast, and who can fight against it.
local: Na bu kɛrɛ sukutu na bu su woo tiɛ ni, dama wɔ fa i yiko ama boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ ni, na bu sukutu na bu su boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n gusu, na bu suse ka, Ŋma ti ka boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n a? Ŋma ni yiri koro kun luɛ na i kunma i a.

eng: The beast was given a mouth that spoke boastful and blasphemous things, and it was given authority to act for forty-two months.
local: Na ba ma boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n atin ka i jɔjɔ kpiri koroya jɔjɔɔri, na i jɔjɔ wura Nyɛmɛ ŋgasi nu, na ba ma i atin ka i la yiko haari sara abura­na ni nnyɔ.

eng: It opened its mouth to speak blasphemies against God. It blasphemed Gods name and his dwelling place .
local: Na i jɔjɔ sukpiɛ Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ, na i susaaki i duma ni i dika ni, ni bo bu wo Nyɛmɛ dika n nu aŋgoro n kɛrɛ.

eng: It was also allowed to make war on the saints and to gain victory over them. It was given authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation.
local: Na wɔ nya yiko ka i kun luɛ ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm na i kunma bu, na wɔ kaaki anya yiko na i niɛ adamande kɛrɛ, ni daanfiɛ kɛrɛ, ni aniɛm kɛrɛ, ni asiɛn barasu kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu.

eng: All who live on earth worshipped it, all whose names hadnt been written—from the time the earth was made—in the scroll of life of the Lamb who was slain.
local: Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu ni su i wɔ, má ka minɛm n bo ba du mɔ akɛrɛ bu duma agɔ ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu, na durunya n boori i bu ni. Kadasi n ti Bɔmbaa n bo ba kpiɛ i kɔm n dɛɛ wɔ.

eng: Whoever has ears must listen.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i koro ka i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu, i ti ye ni ka i mi so i sui na i ti.

eng: If any are to be taken captive, then into captivity they will go. If any are to be killed by the sword, then by the sword they will be killed. This calls for endurance and faithfulness on the part of the saints.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka bu wura i dan­saraka, bu wúra i wɔ, na sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ ase ka bu kun i ni takobe, bu kún i wɔ. Nyini dɛɛ ti, i ti ye ni ka Nyɛmɛ minɛm fa sawɔrɛ ni mindi ni yarada na bu fa su Nyɛmɛ.

eng: Then I saw another beast coming up from the earth. It had two horns like a lamb, but it was speaking like a dragon.
local: Na ma ŋu boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n be bekun ka i fite asiɛn n nu, i la mbiɛm nnyɔ, bu ti kabo Bɔmbaa dɛɛ ti n wɔ, ama, i jɔjɔ kabo woo tiɛ n jɔjɔ n wɔ.

eng: It exercises all the authority of the first beast in its presence. It also makes the earth and those who live in it worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.
local: I jina bumboori dɛɛ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n jinaa­biri wɔ, na i la i yiko n kɛrɛ, na i sumiɛ durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ ka bu su bumboori dɛɛ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ bo ba kpiɛ i tii ni, i ti ka ba kpiɛ i kɔm wɔ, ama, kani n awie.

eng: It does great signs so that it even makes fire come down from heaven to earth in the presence of the people.
local: Na boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ nnyɔ su n ayo alekutura sam beberebe, na i ma sin fiteeri aŋgoro ba tɔɔri asiɛŋgu wɔ, na minɛm aŋu i.

eng: It deceives those who live on earth by the signs that it was allowed to do in the presence of the beast. It told those who live on earth to make an image for the beast who had been wounded by the sword and yet came to life again.
local: Alekutura sam n bo i nya yiko yoori bu bumboori dɛɛ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n nyunu n dɛɛ ti, na wɔ dadaka durunya n nu­fɔm. Na wɔ se bu ka bu ta amɔɛ na i ferema ni boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo bu faari takobe fɔndɔɔri i ni, ama, i toko la ŋgɔɔ.

eng: It was allowed to give breath to the beasts image so that the beasts image would even speak and cause anyone who didnt worship the beasts image to be put to death.
local: Na boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ nnyɔ su n anya atin, na wɔ ma amɔɛ ŋumi, na wɔ nya ŋgɔɔ na i koro jɔjɔ na i koro kun minɛm kɛrɛ bo ba kete ka bu su má i ni.

eng: It forces everyone—the small and great, the rich and poor, the free and slaves—to have a mark put on their right hand or on their forehead.
local: Na i sumiɛ minɛ barasu kɛrɛ, nfaŋga­fɔm ni tarakam, neŋge­fɔm ni yaari­fɔm, minɛm bo ba de bu tii, ni akɔɔm, na wɔ ma ba se bu nyaari bu saa fɔmbɔrɔ wara bu ŋuma su.

eng: It will not allow anyone to make a purchase or sell anything unless the person has the mark with the beasts name or the number of its name.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i la má nyini nyaari see­wa n be, koro tɔ má deke be na i kaaki koro to má deke be gusu, dama nyini nyaari see­wa n ti boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n duma, wara i duma lamba.

eng: This calls for wisdom. Let the one who understands calculate the beasts number, for its a human beings number. Its number is six hundred sixty-six.
local: Sɔnɔ koro si má boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ lamba n bu, má ka i mi la alecira, i ti adamande duma a. I lamba n ti yaa nsɛn ni abura­sɛn ni nsɛn wɔ.

eng: Then I looked, and there was the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion. With him were one hundred forty-four thousand who had his name and his Fathers name written on their foreheads.
local: Na ma niɛ aŋu Bɔmbaa n ka i jina Siyon Buka n su. Minɛ akpii yaa ni akpii abura­na ni nnam wo i dɔ. Bɛrɛ ye ba kɛrɛ Bɔmbaa n ni i Si Nyɛmɛ duma agɔ bu ŋuma su n a.

eng: I heard a sound from heaven that was like the sound of rushing water and loud thunder. The sound I heard was like that of harpists playing their harps.
local: Na ma ti ka minɛ nbem sudi jue aŋgoro, i yo naŋmiɛ kabo nzue soŋgi na i yo naŋmiɛ n wɔ, na i yo kabo nzue tiɛntiɛn na i yo naŋmiɛ ni, na i sun kabo bu bo biɛku na i sun barasu ni.

eng: They sing a new song in front of the throne, the four living creatures, and the elders. And no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand who had been purchased from the earth.
local: Na minɛ akpii yaa ni akpii abura­na ni nnam n jina fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu, ni neŋge ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam ni, ni nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm n nyunu, na bu sudi jue fɔfɔrɛ. Be wo má bɛrɛ bo i koro sɔnsɔn i, má ka bɛrɛ ŋgumi bo Nyɛmɛ ade bu afite durunya n nu­fɔm n nu ni.

eng: They werent defiled with women, for these people who follow the Lamb wherever he goes are virgins. They were purchased from among humankind as early produce for God and the Lamb.
local: Bɛrɛ la minɛm n bo bu ni bara be ada má, na bu ti casi ni. Bɛrɛ la bo deke kɛrɛ bo Bɔmbaa n kɔ na bu su i su ni. Bɛrɛ ye Nyɛmɛ ade bu tii aji afite durunya n nu­fɔm nu n a, na ba yo i ni Bɔmbaa n dɛɛ ciciri su minɛm.

eng: No lie came from their mouths; they are blameless.
local: Kɔrɛ be mmɔɔ wo má bu nɔɔ nu. Na bu kaaki la má ŋgasi be.

eng: Then I saw another angel flying high overhead with eternal good news to proclaim to those who live on earth, and to every nation, tribe, language, and people.
local: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ be bekun ka i wo tu su aŋgoro, na i sukan labari kpa n bo i la má awieeri n jɔrɛ kere adamande kɛrɛ, ni daanfiɛ kɛrɛ, ni aniɛm kɛrɛ, ni asiɛn barasu kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu ni.

eng: He said in a loud voice, Fear God and give him glory, for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship the one who made heaven and earth, the sea and springs of water.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ayɛ i konvi su atiɛn faŋga su agaya ase ka, Am soro Nyɛmɛ na am wura i kpiri, dama saŋga n aju bo i dí minɛm jɔrɛ. Am su Nyɛmɛ bo i yiiri aŋgoro ni asiɛn, ni tieku ni nzue ni.

eng: Another angel, a second one, followed and said, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She made all the nations drink the wine of her lustful passion.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nnyɔ su n afite asu ciciri dɛɛ n su, na i suse ka, Wɔ kpaasi, Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n akpaasi, yiri bo i maari durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ nuuri i nzan n na bu yoori sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn ni.

eng: Then another angel, a third one, followed them and said in a loud voice, If any worship the beast and its image, and receive a mark on their foreheads or their hands.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsan su n afite agɔ ciciri dɛɛm su. Na i sujɔjɔ faŋga su suse ka, Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i suuri boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni i amɔɛ ni, na wɔ se bu ŋuma su wara bu saa fɔmbɔrɔ su n nyaari.

eng: they themselves will also drink the wine of Gods passionate anger, poured full strength into the cup of his wrath. They will suffer the pain of fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels and the Lamb.
local: Nyɛmɛ fá yaa bu ŋu, na i cín bu sui agaya, na i má sin ni ferewita tɔ bu ŋu na bu dí wahara agaya, i mɛrɛkɛ casim n ni Bɔmbaa n nyunu.

eng: The smoke of their painful suffering goes up forever and always. There is no rest day or night for those who worship the beast and its image, and those who receive the mark of its name.
local: Na ŋmisini sufite nyini sin n bo i kere bu wahara n nu kɔ aŋgoro saŋga kɛrɛ, i la má awieeri. Minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu suuri boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni i amɔɛ n na wɔ se bu nyaari ni, bu nya má ŋumi­yi, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu kɛrɛ.

eng: This calls for the endurance of the saints, who keep Gods commandments and keep faith with Jesus.
local: Nyini ti, i ti ye ni ka Nyɛmɛ minɛm la mindi, bɛrɛ bo bu de i nɔaniɛ maa­wam ni, na bu bita bu yarada n kenken fa kɔ Yesu dɔ ni.

eng: And I heard a voice from heaven say, Write this: Favored are the dead who die in the Lord from now on. Yes, says the Spirit, so they can rest from their labors, because their deeds follow them.
local: Na ma ti ka akonvi be sujɔjɔ aŋgoro suse m ka m kɛrɛ ka, Tere­fiɛ wo minɛm n bo bu suuri Mibiɛ n na bu wuuri n dɔ fite kisa fa kɔ. Na Nyɛmɛ Awiɛwiɛ Casi n asoro su ka, I ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, bo ba wu ni, bu yí bu ŋumi, bu ŋkatɔ ye bu sudi i n a, dama bu sakpa­yo su bu su a.

eng: Then I looked, and there was a white cloud. On the cloud was seated someone who looked like the Human One. He had a gold crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.
local: Na ma niɛ aŋu yinyiŋgu fofoe be aŋgoro n su, be tana su, i ferema ni adamande wɔ, i butu sika fɛmɛya kere, i bita gɔrɔku nana i saa nu.

eng: Another angel came out of the temple, calling in a loud voice to the one seated on the cloud: Use your sickle to reap the harvest, for the time to harvest has come, and the harvest of the earth is ripe.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ n be bekun afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru bo i wo aŋgoro n nu, na i sutiɛn faŋga su suse yiri n bo i tana yinyiŋgu n su ka, Durunya n nu alɛ­mbaa n abi, fa u gɔrɔku n fa wura nu na a kpiɛ i, dama saŋga n a ju.

eng: So the one seated on the cloud swung his sickle over the earth, and the earth was harvested.
local: Na yiri n bo i tana yinyiŋgu n su n afa i gɔrɔku n ada awura durunya n nu na wɔ kpiɛ alɛ­mbaa ni.

eng: Then another angel came out of the temple in heaven, and he also had a sharp sickle.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n be bekun afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru bo i wo aŋgoro n nu, i gusu bita gɔrɔku nana be.

eng: Still another angel, who has power over fire, came out from the altar. He said in a loud voice to the one who had the sharp sickle, Use your sharp sickle to cut the clusters in the vineyard of the earth, because its grapes are ripe.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n be bekun bo i niɛ sin su afite Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n dika, na wɔ tiɛn faŋga su ase nyini mɛrɛkɛ n bo i bita gɔrɔku n ka, Fa u gɔrɔku n fa kpiɛ durunya n nu baka suu­wam gɔ, dama baka suu­wa n aboro.

eng: So the angel swung his sickle into the earth, and cut the vineyard of the earth, and he put what he reaped into the great winepress of Gods passionate anger.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n afa i gɔrɔku n ada awura durunya n nu akpiɛ baka suu­wam n kɛrɛ atiɛn bu nɔɔ agɔ kunma bambaka n bo bu miɛmiɛ yiiri baka suu­wa nzue n nu ni. Nyini kere kabo Nyɛmɛ ahore­mbaa ayasu na i cin minɛm sui.

eng: Then the winepress was trampled outside the city, and the blood came out of the winepress as high as the horses bridles for almost two hundred miles.
local: Na ba tiɛntiɛn baka suu­wa n su, kunma bambaka n bo i wo miɛ n ahɛmɛ n nu, na mbunja asoŋgi afite kunma n nu. Nzɛn kpɔŋgɔ wura nu, na i kɔ se ka wɔ dodo ŋgoŋgo dika, i tii nyaari ŋgumi kɔ ka. I tintiin gusu waari agaya.

eng: Then I saw another great and awe-inspiring sign in heaven. There were seven angels with seven plagues—and these are the last, for with them Gods anger is brought to an end.
local: Na ma ŋu alekutura sa bambaka be aŋgoro, ka Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ nso nbem bita kekereke barasu nso n bo bu ti awieeri su dɛɛ ni. Nyini kekereke n dɛɛ sin, Nyɛmɛ fá má yaa kun.

eng: Then I saw what appeared to be a sea of glass mixed with fire. Those who gained victory over the beast, its image, and the number of its name were standing by the glass sea, holding harps from God.
local: Na ma ŋu deke be, i ti ka nzue bambaka a, i je ka niɛɛnu wɔ, wɔ saŋga ni sin. Na ma ŋu minɛm bo ba kunma boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni, ba kɔ ayaki i ni i amɔɛ n ni i duma lamba. Bu jina tieku n bo i je ka niɛɛnu n su, bu bita biɛkum n bo Nyɛmɛ afa ama bu ni.

eng: They sing the song of Moses, Gods servant, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and awe-inspiring are your works, Lord God Almighty. Just and true are your ways, king of the nations.
local: Na ba di Nyɛmɛ akɔɔ Moyisi ni Bɔmbaa n dɛɛ jue, ka, Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri, wɔrɔ ti adamande kɛrɛ fɛmɛ durunya n nu a. U sa­yoo­wa ti bambaka na i ti alekutura. U sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ kɔ atin su wɔ, na i ti nahɔrɛ.

eng: Who wont fear you, Lord, and glorify your name? You alone are holy. All nations will come and fall down in worship before you, for your acts of justice have been revealed.
local: Micɛra, ŋma wo bɛrɛ bo i soro má u a, na i wura má u kpiri a? Dama wɔrɔ ŋgumi ti casi a. Adamande kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu, bu bá kutu u na bu kánsi u, dama u nahɔrɛ sam n afite fofoe nu.

eng: After this I looked, and the temple in heaven—that is, the tent of witness—was opened.
local: Nyini sin, na ma ŋu Casi Kɛrɛ Casi Sɔɔ n bo i wo Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Nambue n nu, na i wo aŋgoro n ka bu suteke i.

eng: The seven angels, who have the seven plagues, came out of the temple. They were clothed in pure bright linen and had gold sashes around their waists.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsom n bo bu bita kekereke nsom n afite Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, bu wura sutura fofoe papasi, ba fa sika dambara abo bu hoe su.

eng: Then one of the four living creatures gave the seven angels seven gold bowls full of the anger of the God who lives forever and always.
local: Na deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam nu kun, afa sika ayabam n nso afa ama Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ nsom n ni. Ayabam n ayi ni Nyɛmɛ ahore­mbaa yasu, bo i kere ka Nyɛmɛ bo i la ŋgɔɔ na i la má awieeri n afa yaa agaya.

eng: The temple was filled with smoke from Gods glory and power, and no one could go into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven last angels were brought to an end.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ yiko ni i jirima woo­wa n ama Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n ayi ni ŋmisini, bo be akoro awura má awuru n nu, fa kɔ ju saŋga bo mɛrɛkɛ nsom fa kekereke nsom n fa jiiri durunya n nu n kɔ wieeri.

eng: Then I heard a loud voice from the temple say to the seven angels, Go and pour out the seven bowls of Gods anger on the earth.
local: Na ma ti akonvi bambaka be ŋga Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, i sujɔjɔ mɛrɛkɛ nsom dɔ, i ŋa, Am kɔ na am kɔ kaaki Nyɛmɛ ahore yasu ayaba nsom n gɔ durunya n nu.

eng: So the first angel poured his bowl on the earth, and a nasty and terrible sore appeared on the people who had the beasts mark and worshipped its image.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ ciciri su n akɔ akaaki i ayaba n agɔ durunya n nu, na kani tiɛm, bo bu yo ya agaya, akpiɛ minɛm bo bu la boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n nyaari see­wa n na bu su i amɔɛ n ni.

eng: The second angel poured his bowl into the sea, and the sea turned into blood, like the blood of a corpse, and every living thing in the sea died.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nnyɔ su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ tieku nzue n nu, na tieku nzue n akaaki ka sɔnɔ bo wɔ wu mbunja. Na deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ kɛrɛ bo bu wo tieku n nu awu.

eng: The third angel poured his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they turned into blood.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsan su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ kunmam nzue ni buram nzue nu, na i kɛrɛ akaaki mbunja.

eng: Then I heard the angel of the waters say, You are just, holy one, who is and was, because you have given these judgments.
local: Na ma ti ka mɛrɛkɛ n bo i niɛ nzue n su n ase ka, Nyɛmɛ, wɔrɔ ti casi a, wɔrɔ bo a wo bɛrɛ na a yo woori bɛrɛ ni, kabo a cin minɛm sui sɔ ni, u sa­yoo­wa akɔ atin su.

eng: They poured out the blood of saints and prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve it.
local: Dama ba kun u dɛɛ minɛm, ni u nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm na bu mbunja afite, ye u gusu ama bu nzuem n akaaki mbunja na bu nu. I ti ye ni ka a yo bu sɔ ɔ.

eng: And I heard the altar say, Yes, Lord God Almighty, your judgments are true and just.
local: Na ma ti akonvi be ŋga ka i fite Mikun yoo­wa yoo­biri n bi, i ŋa, Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri, kabo a di bu jɔrɛ ni, i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, na i kɔ atin su.

eng: The fourth angel poured his bowl on the sun, and it was allowed to burn people with fire.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nna su n akaaki i ayaba, n agɔ wiɛ n ŋu, na Nyɛmɛ ama i kɛkɛrɛ agaya suyara minɛm ka sin dɛɛ su.

eng: The people were burned by intense heat, and they cursed the name of the God who had power over these plagues. But they didnt change their hearts and lives and give him glory.
local: Na sin n kɛkɛrɛ ayara minɛm, na ba kpiɛ Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ, yiri bo i la yiko na i ma bu di nyini wahara ni, na ba kete ka bu kaaki má bu wo na bu ma i jirima.

eng: The fifth angel poured his bowl over the beasts throne, and darkness covered its kingdom. People bit their tongues because of their pain.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nnu su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n fɛmɛya biɛ n su, na i fɛmɛya asiɛn n kɛrɛ asi biri didiki. Na minɛm sukan bu saa, yaari n ti.

eng: and they cursed the God of heaven because of their pains and sores; but they didnt turn away from what they had done.
local: Kambue n ni yaari n bo i wo bu ŋu n dɛɛ ti, ye bu sukpiɛ Nyɛmɛ bo i wo aŋgoro ni nzukɔ a, haari ni nyini mmɔɔ, ba kaaki má bu wo na bu yaki bu satiɛ ni.

eng: Then the sixth angel poured his bowl on the great river Euphrates. Its water was dried up so that the way was ready for the kings from the east.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nsɛn su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ kunma kpiri n bo bu fere i ka Yufarati n su, na nzue n kɛrɛ anu, na wɔ kpiɛ atin ama nfɛmɛm bo bu fite wie afiiri ni.

eng: Then I saw three unclean spirits, like frogs, come from the dragons mouth, the beasts mouth, and the mouth of the false prophet.
local: Na ma ŋu awiɛwiɛ tiɛ nsan nbem, bu ti ka pɔntirim wɔ, kun sufite woo tiɛ n nɔɔ nu, kun sufite boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n nɔɔ nu, na kun gusu sufite sɔnɔ bo wɔ yi i ŋu ka i ti Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔ n nɔɔ nu.

eng: These are demonic spirits that do signs. They go out to the kings of the whole world, to gather them for battle on the great day of God the Almighty. .
local: Bu ti Sitana dɛɛ awiɛwiɛ barasu nbem wɔ, na bu suyo alekutura sam, na bu kɔ durunya n nu nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ dɔ kɔ tiɛn bu nɔɔ, na bu cici luɛ ŋminda ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri n jina cɛɛn.

eng: Look! Im coming like a thief! Favored are those who stay awake and clothed so that they dont go around naked and exposed to shame.
local: Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ ŋa o, Am ti fɛn, m ba ni yó am fura nu ka awiefɔ dɛɛ su wɔ, tere­fiɛ wo sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i ŋminda fa kɔ m ba n dɛɛ su, i fa má i ŋu ti má, na i wura i sutura, adena má i nati i bu ŋgbɛɛn, na má minɛ ŋu i, na nyinsɛ ti i.

eng: The spirits gathered them at the place that is called in Hebrew, Harmagedon.
local: Na Sitana awiɛwiɛm n atiɛn nfɛmɛm nɔɔ dika n bo bu fere i Hiburu aniɛ nu ka, Amagɛdon n ni.

eng: Then the seventh angel poured his bowl into the air, and a loud voice came out from the temple, from the throne, saying, It is done.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ nso su n akaaki i ayaba n agɔ aŋuma n nu. Na akonvi bambaka be ajɔjɔ afite fɛmɛya biɛ n nu, Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu, i ŋa, Wɔ yo awie.

eng: There were lightning strikes, voices, and thunder, and a great earthquake occurred. The earthquake was greater than any that have occurred since there have been people on earth.
local: Na nzue sukpɛkpɛsɛ, na i sutiɛntiɛn, na i suyo naŋmiɛ, na asiɛn sukpisi faŋga su. Kabo Nyɛmɛ yiiri adamande ni, be nya ŋu má nyini asiɛn kpisii­wa n ŋusu sɔ.

eng: The great city split into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. God remembered Babylon the great so that he gave her the wine cup of his furious anger.
local: Na miɛ kpiri n ati nu nsan, na miɛm kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu n atɔtɔ, dama Nyɛmɛ ateŋge Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n jɔrɛ su, nyini ti ye wɔ fa yaa i ŋu, sucin i sui n a.

eng: Every island fled, and the mountains disappeared.
local: Na lui bambakam kɛrɛ bo bu da nzue afiɛn, ni bukam kɛrɛ amini.

eng: Huge hailstones weighing about one hundred pounds came down from heaven on the people. They cursed God for the plague of hail, because the plague was so terrible.
local: Na nzue yabuɛm sutɔtɔ minɛm ŋu, i kun kun nɔnɔɔri la ŋma. Na minɛm sukpiɛ Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ, dama nzue yabuɛ n bo i tɔtɔ ni sukere bu wahara agaya.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the judgment upon the great prostitute, who is seated on deep waters.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ nsom n nu kun, bo i bita ayaba nsom n nu kun, aba m dɔ, aba ase m ka, Bra fɛn, na m kére u kabo Nyɛmɛ cín bara sakara tiɛ n sui ni, yiri bo i tana kunma nzue beberebem n su ni.

eng: The kings of the earth have committed sexual immorality with her, and those who live on earth have become drunk with the wine of her whoring.
local: Yiri ye durunya n nu nfɛmɛm akpini i afa ayo bu soma, na wɔ fa i sakaraya asa durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ, na bu gusu akpini i afa ayo bu soma.

eng: Then he brought me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a desert. There I saw a woman seated on a scarlet beast that was covered with blasphemous names. It had seven heads and ten horns.
local: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu, na mɛrɛkɛ n afa m akɔ boro kpantain nu. Na ma ŋu bara be ka i tana boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ kɔkɔrɛ be su, duma kɛrɛɛ­wa nbem bo bu kpiɛ Nyɛmɛ nzukɔ n wo boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ŋuniɛ kɛrɛ su. I la atii nso, na i mbiɛ ti buru.

eng: The woman wore purple and scarlet clothing, and she glittered with gold and jewels and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup full of the vile and impure things that came from her activity as a prostitute.
local: Bara n adi suŋguruya anyɔnyɔndi i ŋu, i wura sutura kɔkɔrɛ nyunyumi, na wɔ wura i kɔm, ni i saam, ni i suim jɔrɔkɔm, ni lalankundim, ni afere nyunyumim, ni sikam, ni afere fofoem bo bu la gɔ agaya. I bita sika kɔpu i saa nu, wɔ yi ni daŋgba ni i sa­yoo­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn n wɔ.

eng: A name—a mystery—was written on her forehead: Babylon the great, the mother of prostitutes and the vile things of the earth.
local: Ba kɛrɛ i asiri nu duma i ŋuma su, nyini duma n bu la ka, Babilɔn miɛ kpiri, bo i ti sakaram kɛrɛ ni, na i kaaki ti saa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn yo­fɔm kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu n ni.

eng: I saw that the woman was drunk on the blood of the saints and the blood of Jesus witnesses. I was completely stunned when I saw her.
local: Na ma ŋu ka bara n anu Nyɛmɛ minɛm mbunja abo, ni minɛm bo bu di Yesu daani n dɛɛ ti na ba kun bu ni gusu mbunja abo. M ŋuuri i sɔ ni, na wɔ yo m alekutura.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Why are you amazed? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and the seven-headed, ten-horned beast that carries her.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n abisa m ka, Nzu ti ye a ma wɔ yo u alekutura? M kére u asiri nu jɔrɛ bo i wo bɛrɛ fa kɔ bara n ni boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo bara n tana su, na i la atii nso ni mbiɛm buru ni.

eng: The beast that you saw was and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. Those who live on earth, whose names havent been written in the scroll of life from the time the earth was made, will be amazed when they see the beast, because it was and is not and will again be present.
local: Boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo a ŋuuri i ni, ti deke bo i yo woori bɛrɛ wɔ, ama, kisa i wo má bɛrɛ kun. Wɔ ka kaan na i fíte kunma ŋgoŋgo n bo i la má awieeri n nu, na i kɔ́ di wahara na i ká ŋgbɛɛn. Na durunya n nu­fɔm bo bu duma wo má ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu, fite saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ nya yi má durunya ni, bu ba ŋuuri i, i yó bu alekutura, dama i yo woori bɛrɛ, ye kisa, i wo má bɛrɛ kun, na i bá kaaki ba cɛɛn be.

eng: This calls for an understanding mind. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman is seated. They are also seven kings.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, sɔnɔ kɛrɛ fa i alecira ni sunsunni na i ti ahin jɔrɛ n bu. Boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n tii nso n ti buka nso bo bara n afun atana su wɔ. Bu gusu kaaki ti nfɛmɛm nso wɔ.

eng: Five kings have fallen, the one is, and the other hasnt yet come. When that king comes, he must remain for only a short time.
local: Bu nu nnu akpaasi, nsɛn su n toko wo bɛrɛ, nso su n nya ba má, ama, i ba baari, i cɛ má, i fɛmɛya ni kpáasi wɔ.

eng: As for the beast that was and is not, it is itself an eighth king that belongs to the seven, and it is going to destruction.
local: Boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n bo i yo woori bɛrɛ, ama, kisa i wo má bɛrɛ kun ni, yiri ti fɛmɛ n bo i buka su mɔcuɛ su n a, ama, i ti nfɛmɛ nsom nu kun wɔ, na i kɔ́ di wahara na i ká ŋgbɛɛn.

eng: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings, who havent yet received royal power. But they will receive royal authority for an hour, along with the beast.
local: Mbiɛ burum bo a ŋuuri bu n ti nfɛmɛ burum bo bu nya di má fɛmɛya, ama, bu nyá yiko na bu dí fɛmɛ buka boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n su saŋga kaan sɔ.

eng: These kings will be of one mind, and they will give their power and authority to the beast.
local: Nyini nfɛmɛ burum n yó bu nɔɔkun, na bu fá bu faŋga ni bu yiko kɛrɛ na bu fá ma boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ ni.

eng: They will make war on the Lamb, but the Lamb will emerge victorious, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings. Those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.
local: Bu kún luɛ ni Nyɛmɛ Bɔmbaa ni, ama, i ni i minɛm ni kúnma bu, dama yiri ti mibiɛm kɛrɛ Mibiɛ, na i ti nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ Fɛmɛ. I minɛm bo bu wo i dɔ n la bo wɔ fere bu ni, ni bo wɔ yi bu na bu jina i sin nahɔrɛ su ni.

eng: Then he said to me, The waters that you saw, where the prostitute is seated, are peoples, crowds, nations, and languages.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ase m ka, Kunmam nzue n bo a ŋuuri i ni, na bara sakara n tana su ni, ti durunya n nu minɛm, ni durunya n nu asiɛn barasu barasu kɛrɛ, ni daanfiɛ kɛrɛ wɔ lɛɛ.

eng: As for the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the prostitute. They will destroy her and strip her bare. They will devour her flesh and burn her with fire.
local: Mbiɛ burum bo a ŋuuri bu ni, ni boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ ni, bu bá kpɔ bara sakara ni, bu dé i neŋge kɛrɛ na i ká i bu ŋgbɛɛn, na bu dí i ŋuniɛ­mbaa kɛrɛ, na bu yára bo wɔ ka n sin.

eng: because God moved them to carry out his purposes. That is why they will be of one mind and give their royal power to the beast, until Gods words have been accomplished.
local: Dama Nyɛmɛ maari bu yoori kabo i koro a. I maari bu yoori bu nɔɔkun na bu fa bu fɛmɛya n kɛrɛ buka boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ, na i dí fɛmɛya n kɔ ju saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni bá yo yii­wa ni.

eng: The woman whom you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.
local: Bara n bo a ŋuuri i n la miɛ kpiri n bo i ti durunya n nu nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ tii n a.

eng: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven. He had great authority, and the earth was filled with light because of his glory.
local: Nyini sin, na ma kaaki aŋu mɛrɛkɛ be ka i fite aŋgoro sujura, i la yiko agaya, na i woo­wa n ata wein abara ayɛ durunya n kɛrɛ.

eng: He called out with a loud voice, saying, Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! She has become a home for demons and a lair for every unclean spirit. She is a lair for every unclean bird, and a lair for every unclean and disgusting beast.
local: Na wɔ yɛ i konvi su atiɛn faŋga su ka, Wɔ kpaasi. Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n akpaasi. Wɔ kaaki awiɛwiɛ tiɛm, ni ajinim, ni deke ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm, ni anuma tiɛm woo­biri wɔ.

eng: because all the nations have fallen due to the wine of her lustful passion. The kings of the earth committed sexual immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth became rich from the power of her loose and extravagant ways.
local: Dama durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ nuuri i nzan ye bu kɔɔri somaya yiri dɔ a. Na durunya n nu nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ kɔ kpiniiri yiri yoori bu soma. Na kabo i la ajɛkɛ na i koro tara má i ŋu ni, durunya n nu wata­fɔm gusu nya bu ajɛkɛ fite i dɔ ɔ.

eng: Then I heard another voice from heaven say, Come out of her, my people, so that you dont take part in her sins and dont receive any of her plagues.
local: Na ma kaaki ati akonvi be ŋga bekun, ka i sujɔjɔ aŋgoro n nu, i ŋa, M minɛm, am fite miɛ n nu, na má am yo bu satiɛ yoo­wa n be, na má wahara n bo i suba bu dɔ n tu am.

eng: Her sins have piled up as high as heaven, and God remembered her unjust acts.
local: Dama miɛ n nu­fɔm satiɛ yoo­wa n aju Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro, na i wara koro fi má bu satiɛ yoo­wa n sa.

eng: Give her what she has given to others. Give her back twice as much for what she has done. In the cup that she has poured, pour her twice as much.
local: Sam bo bara n yoori bu ni, yo i gusu sɔ, tɔ i kaari i ŋusu nnyɔ. Ma i ŋu kekereke tara kabo i yo ma minɛm ŋuuri kekereke ni.

eng: To the extent that she glorified herself and indulged her loose and extravagant ways, give her pain and grief. In her heart she says, I sit like a queen! Im not a widow. Ill never see grief.
local: Kabo i yo wuraari i ŋu kpiri na i diiri i tii barasu ni, kere i kekereke na i ahore gusu saaki sɔ. Dama i ŋa, Ma di fɛmɛ, m ti má kuna­fɔ, m ahore sáaki má cɛɛn be.

eng: This is why her plagues will come in a single day—deadly disease, grief, and hunger. She will be consumed by fire because the Lord God who judges her is powerful.
local: Nyini dɛɛ ti, le­kun ye kekereke bá tɔ i ŋu a. Yiri la tukpaki, ni ahoresaaki, ni ahɔɛ. Na sin yára i, dama Micɛra Nyɛmɛ n bo i cin i sui n la yiko agaya.

eng: The kings of the earth, who committed sexual immorality with her and shared her loose and extravagant ways, will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke from her burning.
local: Na durunya n nu nfɛmɛm bo bu kɔ kpiniiri i na bu ni yiri diiri bu tii ni, bu ŋu kabo sin je i, na bu ŋu kabo ŋmisini yasu kɔ aŋgoro barasu, na bu sún, na bu bó awie.

eng: They will stand a long way off because they are afraid of the pain she suffers, and they will say, Oh, the horror! Babylon, you great city, you powerful city! In a single hour your judgment has come.
local: Kekereke n bo wɔ tu i n dɛɛ ti, i ma bu sɛrɛ wɔ, na bu jina dede, na bu nya suse ka, Keto, keto, am niɛ ahin miɛ bambaka ni. Babilɔn bo i ti miɛ kpiri n lɛɛ ye ba kaba acin i sui, ye i kɛrɛ aka ŋgbɛɛn, anyumbaa kata ni i teke n nu bɛrɛ n a.

eng: The merchants of the earth will weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their cargoes anymore.
local: Na wata­fɔm bo bu wo durunya n nu ni susun, na bu sukoko miɛ ni, dama be to má bu gɔɔri n kun.

eng: cargoes of gold, silver, jewels, and pearls; fine linen, purple, silk, and scarlet; all those things made of scented wood, ivory, fine wood, bronze, iron, and marble.
local: Be to má bu sika, ni jɛtɛ, ni afere barasu nbem bo bu yo nyumi na bu la gɔ ni. Be to má bu suturam barasu barasu bo bu yo nyumi na bu la gɔ ni. Be to má bu bakam bo bu fa yo biɛ kpakpam ni, ni bu ayaba barasu kɛrɛ bo bu fa sui jee yo bu ni, ni deke kɛrɛ bo bu fa baka fa sense bu, ni deke kɛrɛ bo bu fa jawii bulaari, ni yabuɛ fa yo bu na bu la gɔ agaya.

eng: cinnamon, incense, fragrant ointment, and frankincense; wine, oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle, sheep, horses, and carriages; and slaves, even human lives.
local: Be kaaki to má bu toro neŋge barasum bo bu la nva­nvani, ni ayiri kunkuun­wa nva­nvani, ni baka nyunvuin nva­nvani­fɔ, ni nzan, ni ŋguin, ni kpɔnɔ samɛnɛ, ni diire, ni naanim, ni nbɔɛm, ni kpɔŋgɔm, ni kpɔŋgɔ torokum, ni akɔɔm, ni domuɛm.

eng: The fruit your whole being craved has gone from you. All your glitter and glamour are lost to you, never ever to be found again.
local: Na wata­fɔm n ase i ka, Dekem bo a yo koro bu agaya n kɛrɛ aŋmati ayaki u, u ajɛkɛm, ni u neŋge kpakpam kɛrɛ bo a la bu akɔ ayaki u, a nyá má i be kun.

eng: The merchants who sold these things, and got so rich by her, will stand a long way off because they fear the pain she suffers. They will weep and mourn, and say.
local: Wata­fɔm bo bu yoori neŋge­fɔm nyini miɛ n dɛɛ ti ni, bu kɔ́ jina dede wɔ, dama sɛrɛ ti bu fa kɔ kekereke n bo wɔ tɔ miɛ n nu ni, na bu ahore saaki agaya na bu sun.

eng: Oh, the horror! The great city that wore fine linen, purple, and scarlet, who glittered with gold, jewels, and pearls.
local: Na bu se ka, Keto, keto, am niɛ ahin miɛ bambaka ni. I yo kara sutura barasu barasu nyunyumi, na i la sika jɔrɔkɔm, ni aferem barasu barasu bo bu yo nyumi na bu la gɔ agaya ni.

eng: in just one hour such great wealth was destroyed. Every sea captain, every seafarer, sailors, and all who make their living on the sea stood a long way off.
local: Nyini neŋge n kɛrɛ akaba aka ŋgbɛɛn, anyumbaa kata ni i teke n nu bɛrɛ n a. Na lee bambaka kan­fɔm, ni lee­fɔm, ni lee wura­fɔm, ni bo bu juma nati tieku nzue n su n kɛrɛ akɔ ajina dede.

eng: They cried out as they saw the smoke from her burning and said, What city was ever like the great city.
local: Na bu jina niɛ kabo miɛ ni suje sin barasu na ŋmisini yasu kɔ aŋgoro ni, na bu bo awie se ka, Miɛ bɔnɔ wo bɛrɛ bo i ju ahin miɛ bambaka n a.

eng: They threw dust on their heads, and they cried out, weeping and mourning. They said, Oh, the horror! The great city, where all who have ships at sea became so rich by her prosperity—in just one hour she was destroyed.
local: Na bu susasa tuturi gɔ bu tii su na bu kere ka bu ahore asaaki agaya, na bu susun, na bu suse ka, Keto, keto, am niɛ ahin miɛ bambaka n ni. Dama minɛm kɛrɛ bo bu la lee bambakam na bu juma nati tieku nzue n su n nya bu neŋgem fite nyini miɛ n dɛɛ ajɛkɛ n nu wɔ. Ye wɔ kaba aka ŋgbɛɛn, anyumbaa kata ni i teke n nu bɛrɛ n a.

eng: Rejoice over her, heaven—you saints, apostles, and prophets—because God has condemned her as she condemned you.
local: Ambɛrɛ bo am wo aŋgoro nu, ni Nyɛmɛ minɛm, ni Yesu jaramasam, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm, am ma am ahore jɔ fa kɔ kabo miɛ kpiri n aka ŋgbɛɛn barasu bɛrɛ ni, dama Nyɛmɛ subisa i nɔɔ nu ma am a.

eng: Then a powerful angel picked up a stone that was like a huge millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, With such violent force the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, and it wont be found anymore.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ mɛrɛkɛ faŋga­fɔ be n afa yabuɛ bambaka, na wɔ fa atu aji tieku nzue n nu, na wɔ se ka, Sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ fá Babilɔn miɛ kpiri n fa ji wuum a, na i busu be bá ŋu má.

eng: The sound of harpists and musicians, of pipers and trumpeters, will never be heard among you again. No craftsman of any kind will ever be found among you again. The sound of the hand mill will never be heard among you again.
local: Be ti má deke be ŋga miɛ n nu kun, i biɛku kan­fɔ o, i jue di­fɔ o, i tere kan­fɔ o, ni awiɛ kan­fɔm. Na juma­yo­fɔ barasu be mmɔɔ gusu wo má miɛ n nu kun, be ti má ŋgani san yabuɛ be ŋga miɛ n nu kun.

eng: The light of a lamp will never shine among you again. The sound of a bridegroom and bride will never be heard among you again because your merchants ran the world, because all the nations were deceived by the spell you cast, and because.
local: Na fitana haari kun n kpein je má miɛ n nu cɛɛn be na be ŋu i. Be ti má konya ni konyafan kuyigindi ŋga miɛ n nu kun, dama wata­fɔm bo bu yo wo nyini miɛ n nu yo ti nfaŋga­fɔm wɔ durunya n kɛrɛ nu, na miɛ nu­fɔm yo boboyaya sam dadaka durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ.

eng: the blood of prophets, of saints, and of all who have been slaughtered on the earth was found among you.
local: Nyɛmɛ acin miɛ n nu­fɔm sui a, dama bɛrɛ yo kuunri Nyɛmɛ minɛm, ni i nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm n a, ni minɛm kɛrɛ bo ba kun bu durunya n nu ni, bɛrɛ yiiri bu mbunja.

eng: After this I heard what sounded like a huge crowd in heaven. They said, Hallelujah! The salvation and glory and power of our God!.
local: Nyini sin, na ma ti akonvi be ŋga aŋgoro, ka jama beberebe akonvi, bu ŋa, Haleluya , ya kansi Nyɛmɛ, ya Mi Nyɛmɛ n ama ya nya tii­de a, yiri ti kpiri ni yiko­fɔ a.

eng: His judgments are true and just, because he judged the great prostitute, who ruined the earth by her whoring, and he exacted the penalty for the blood of his servants from her hand.
local: I di jɔrɛ ni i atin su, ni nahɔrɛ wɔ. Wɔ di bara sakara n jɔrɛ, yiri bo i maari durunya n nu­fɔm kɛrɛ kpini nbaram na bu kɔ somaya na bu yo sa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛnm kɛrɛ, na wɔ cin i sui, dama yiri yo kuunri Nyɛmɛ akɔɔm n a.

eng: Then they said a second time, Hallelujah! Smoke goes up from her forever and always.
local: Na jama n bo bu wo aŋgoro n akaaki ayɛ bu konvi su atiɛn ase ka, Haleluya . Sin n bo i sudi miɛ ni ŋmisini n suyasu sukɔ aŋgoro haari ni saŋga bo i la má awieeri.

eng: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshipped God, who is seated on the throne, and they said, Amen. Hallelujah.
local: Na nkpiɛn­kpiɛn abura­nyɔ ni nnam, ni deke ŋgɔɔ­fɔ nnam sukutu sukansi Nyɛmɛ bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su ni, na bu se ka, Ami. Haleluya .

eng: Then a voice went out from the throne and said, Praise our God, all you his servants, and you who fear him, both small and great.
local: Na akonvi be afite fɛmɛya biɛ n nu, i ŋa, Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ akɔɔm kɛrɛ, tarakam ni nfaŋga­fɔm, bo am soro Nyɛmɛ ni, am kɛrɛ kansi i duma.

eng: And I heard something that sounded like a huge crowd, like rushing water and powerful thunder. They said, Hallelujah! The Lord our God, the Almighty, exercised his royal power!.
local: Na ma ti akonvi be naŋmiɛ ŋga, i ti ka jama beberebe suyo naŋmiɛ wɔ, na i yo naŋmiɛ, kabo nzue soŋgi na i yo naŋmiɛ ni, na i kaaki ti kabo nzue tiɛntiɛn na i yo naŋmiɛ ni, i ŋa, Haleluya , Ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri n adi i fɛmɛya n kisa.

eng: Let us rejoice and celebrate, and give him the glory, for the wedding day of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready.
local: Am ma ya ahore jɔ na ya yo ya kunnu koroo­wa, na ya wura i kpiri, dama saŋga n aju bo Bɔmbaa ni já i konya ni, konya n gusu asɛsɛ i ŋu su aŋminda i.

eng: She was given fine, pure white linen to wear, for the fine linen is the saints acts of justice.
local: Nyɛmɛ ama i sutura casi fofoe papasi ka i fa kara. Sutura fofoe papasi n la Nyɛmɛ minɛm sakpa yoo­wam ni.

eng: Then the angel said to me, Write this: Favored are those who have been invited to the wedding banquet of the Lamb. He said to me, These are the true words of God.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n ase m ka m kɛrɛ ka, Tere­fiɛ wo minɛm bo Bɔmbaa n abaandi bu ka bu ba di konyaya diire n be i dɔ. Na wɔ kaaki ase ka, Ahin nɔaniɛ ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ ɔ.

eng: Then I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said, Dont do that! Im a servant just like you and your brothers and sisters who hold firmly to the witness of Jesus. Worship God! The witness of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.
local: Na ma kutu i jaa bu asiɛŋgu ka m su i, na wɔ se m ka má m yo sɔ, ama, m kansi Nyɛmɛ ŋgumi. Na wɔ kaaki ase m ka, M ti u beŋgu akɔɔ, ni u ninbaam bo bu di Yesu daani kere minɛ n be wɔ. Jɔrɛ n kɛrɛ bo Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kan­fɔm kaanri i ni, bu diiri ahin Yesu n daani wɔ.

eng: Then I saw heaven opened, and there was a white horse. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he judges and makes war justly.
local: Na ma ŋu ka aŋgoro ateke, kpeŋga na ma ŋu kpɔŋgɔ fofoe be, bu fere bo i tana su n duma ka, Bo i la Dabuŋu ni Nahɔrɛ­fɔ, i di minɛm jɔrɛ kabo i ti ye ni ka i di bu nahɔrɛ su wɔ, na i kun i luɛ ni nahɔrɛ su.

eng: His eyes were like a fiery flame, and on his head were many royal crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows but he himself.
local: I nyu­mbaa kɛrɛ je ka sin fofoe, na i butu fɛmɛya kere beberebe i tii su. Duma kɛrɛɛ­wa be mata i, be si má nyini duma ni, má ka yiri ŋgumi si a.

eng: He wore a robe dyed with blood, and his name was called the Word of God.
local: I wura sutura yorom barasu be bo ba fa i awura mbunja nu, na bu fere sɔnɔ n duma ka Nyɛmɛ Nɔaniɛ.

eng: Heavens armies, wearing fine linen that was white and pure, were following him on white horses.
local: Na Nyɛmɛ sojam bo bu wo aŋgoro n fun kpɔŋgɔ fofoe su i su, bu wura sutura casi fofoe papasi.

eng: From his mouth comes a sharp sword that he will use to strike down the nations. He is the one who will rule them with an iron rod. And he is the one who will trample the winepress of the Almighty Gods passionate anger.
local: Takobe nana bo i fá kunma durunya n nu­fɔm wo i nɔɔ nu, na i bíta bu kekereke kabo naŋkasi fa bulaari kɔrɔma tu bo i naanim barasu ni, na i tíɛntiɛn bu su kabo bu fa baka suu­wa gɔ kunma nu na bu miɛmiɛ yi i nzue ni, dama Micɛra Nyɛmɛ Kpiri n afa yaa agaya.

eng: He has a name written on his robe and on his thigh: King of kings and Lord of lords.
local: Ba kɛrɛ i duma i sutura n su, ni i jaa sɔɔwa su ka, Nfɛmɛm kɛrɛ Fɛmɛ ni mibiɛm kɛrɛ Mibiɛ.

eng: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and he called out with a loud voice and said to all the birds flying high overhead, Come and gather for Gods great supper.
local: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ be ka i jina wiɛ n su, na wɔ yɛ i konvi su atiɛn faŋga su ase anumam kɛrɛ bo bu tu aŋgoro na bu di niɛ n ka, Am bra na am ba tiɛn am nɔɔ na am di ahin diire beberebe n bo Nyɛmɛ sufa ma am ni.

eng: Come and eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful, and the flesh of horses and their riders. Come and eat the flesh of all, both free and slave, both small and great.
local: am ba di nfɛmɛm ŋuniɛ­mbaa, ni sojam nkpiɛnm ŋuniɛ­mbaa, ni nfaŋga­fɔm ŋuniɛ­mbaa, ni kpɔŋgɔ niɛ, ni minɛm bo bu fun kpɔŋgɔm n ŋuniɛ­mbaa, ni adamande barasu kɛrɛ ŋuniɛ­mbaa, bo bu ti akɔɔm ni bo ba de bu tii, ni bo bu ti minɛ ŋgbiŋgbirim, ni bo bu ti minɛ kan kanm ni.

eng: Then I saw that the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies had gathered to make war against the rider on the horse and his army.
local: Na ma ŋu boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni durunya n nu nfɛmɛm, ni bu sojam, ka, ba tiɛn bu nɔɔ acici na bu kun luɛ ni yiri n bo i tana kpɔŋgɔ fofoe n su ni, ni i jama nbaam.

eng: But the beast was seized, along with the false prophet who had done signs in the beasts presence. The two of them were thrown alive into the fiery lake that burns with sulfur.
local: Na ba tara boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni kɔrɛ­fɔ n bo i yo i ŋu ka i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ ni. Yiri yo yoori alekutura sam boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n nyunu, na wɔ dadaka minɛm bo bu deeri boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n anyaari see­wa na ba su i amɔɛ ni. Na ba fa boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni kɔrɛ­fɔ n ni bu ŋgɔɔ aji sin n nu, bo i je ka ferewita na i da ka tieku ni.

eng: The rest were killed by the sword that comes from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds ate their fill of their flesh.
local: Minɛm bo bu kaari ni, na yiri n bo i tana kpɔŋgɔ n su n afa takobe n bo i wo i nɔɔ nu n akun bu kɛrɛ. Na anumam kɛrɛ adi niɛ na bu kunnu ayi.

eng: Then I saw an angel coming down from heaven, holding in his hand the key to the abyss and a huge chain.
local: Na ma ŋu mɛrɛkɛ be ka i fite aŋgoro suba, i bita deke nbem i saa nu, kun la kadombaa bo i tu kunma ŋgoŋgo bo i la má awieeri ni, na kun gusu la jɔrɔkɔ bambaka faŋga­fɔ be.

eng: He seized the dragon, the old snake, who is the devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years.
local: Na wɔ tara woo tiɛ ni, dada dɛɛ woo tiɛ n bo i ti Sitana ni, na bu kaaki fere i ka Dadaka­fɔ ni, na wɔ fa jɔrɔkɔ n acici i, na wɔ fa i atu kunma ŋgoŋgo n bo i la má awieeri n nu, ka i wó dɔ haari afɔ akpii, na wɔ tu kunma n anɔ ni, na wɔ ta akata su, na má i koro fite na i dadaka durunya n nu­fɔm bekun, ama, i wo dɔ haari afɔ akpii n ba ju. Nyini sin, na bu nyáŋgi i ji kaan.

eng: Then I saw thrones, and people took their seats on them, and judgment was given in their favor. They were the ones who had been beheaded for their witness to Jesus and Gods word, and those who hadnt worshipped the beast or its image, who hadnt received the mark on their forehead or hand. They came to life and ruled with Christ for one thousand years.
local: Na ma ŋu fɛmɛya biɛm, Nyɛmɛ ama bo bu tana sum n atin ka bu di minɛm jɔrɛ. Na ma kaaki aŋu bɛrɛ bo bu yo wuuri Yesu ti ni. Minɛ yo kpiɛɛri bu tii a, dama bu yo kaanri Nyɛmɛ jɔrɛ na ba di Yesu daani n dɛɛ ti. Bɛrɛ keteeri ka bu su má boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni i amɔɛ n a, bɛrɛ la bo bu kaaki keteeri ka bu de má i nyaari see­wa bu ŋuma wara bu saa su n a, ye ba teŋge bu yiwee n nu anya ŋgɔɔ, na bu ni Kristo Tii­de­fɔ n saŋga di fɛmɛya afɔ akpii.

eng: The rest of the dead didnt come to life until the thousand years were over. This is the first resurrection.
local: Ahin ti yiwee teŋge ciciri su n a. Ama, minɛm bo ba wu na ba ka ni, bɛrɛ teŋge má haari kɔ ju afɔ akpii n awieeri.

eng: Favored and holy are those who have a share in the first resurrection. The second death has no power over them, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and will rule with him for one thousand years.
local: Bɛrɛ n bo bu du mɔ teŋgeeri bu yiwee nu ni, na bu ti minɛ casim n ni, tere­fiɛ wo bu dɔ, bɛrɛ ŋú má yiwee nnyɔ su n kun, bɛrɛ yó Nyɛmɛ ni Kristo Tii­de­fɔ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔm a. Na bu ni yiri sáŋga di fɛmɛya n afɔ akpii.

eng: When the thousand years are over, Satan will be released from his prison.
local: Afɔ akpii n nyaari kɔ juuri, na Nyɛmɛ má bu yi Sitana n dan­saraka sɔɔ n nu ji.

eng: He will go out to deceive the nations that are at the four corners of the earth—Gog and Magog. He will gather them for battle. Their number is like the sand of the sea.
local: Na i kɔ́ dadaka adamande barasu barasu kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu ni, Gogi ni Magogi asiɛn n kɛrɛ, na i tíɛn bu kɛrɛ nɔɔ na bu kɔ́ kun luɛ. Bu sunnu beberebe ka kunma ŋmiɛ wɔ.

eng: They came up across the whole earth and surrounded the saints camp, the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and consumed them.
local: Na ba tɛrɛki abara ayɛ durunya n deke kɛrɛ, na ba bara ayɛ miɛ n bo Nyɛmɛ koro i agaya ni, deke bo i minɛm wo ni, ama, na sin afite aŋgoro aba ayara bu.

eng: Then the devil, who had deceived them, was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet also were. There painful suffering will be inflicted upon them day and night, forever and always.
local: Na ba fa Sitana n bo i dadaka bu n aji cɛɛma sin n nu, bo i je ka ferewita na i da ka tieku ni, deke bo bu faari boro neŋge­ndɔrɛ n ni kɔrɛ­fɔ n bo i yo i ŋu ka i kan Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ n jiiri nu ni. Dɔ na bu dí wahara, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu saŋga kɛrɛ.

eng: Then I saw a great white throne and the one who is seated on it. Before his face both earth and heaven fled away, and no place was found for them.
local: Na ma ŋu fɛmɛya biɛ bambaka fofoe be, ni bo i tana su ni. Na aŋgoro ni asiɛŋgu kɛrɛ amini i nyunu, bu wo má bɛrɛ kun.

eng: I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne, and scrolls were opened. Another scroll was opened too; this is the scroll of life. And the dead were judged on the basis of what was written in the scrolls about what they had done.
local: Na ma ŋu minɛm bo ba wu ni, nfaŋga­fɔm ni tarakam kɛrɛ, ka bu jina fɛmɛya biɛ n nyunu. Na ma kaaki aŋu ka be suteke kadasim, na be gusu suteke kadasi bo i ti ŋgɔɔ dɛɛ ni. Nyini kadasim ye bu fa diiri minɛm bo bu wuuri n jɔrɛ a, bu sa­yoo­wa kɛrɛ bo bu yo yoori wo nyini kadasim n nu.

eng: The sea gave up the dead that were in it, and Death and the Grave gave up the dead that were in them, and people were judged by what they had done.
local: Na tieku n awɔrɔ minɛm bo ba wu n ama, na Yiwee, ni Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri n awɔrɔ minɛm bo ba wu n ama, na Nyɛmɛ afa bu sa­yoo­wa bo bu yo yoori adi bu jɔrɛ.

eng: Then Death and the Grave were thrown into the fiery lake. This, the fiery lake, is the second death.
local: Na wɔ fa Yiwee, ni Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri n aji sin n nu, bo i je ka ferewita na i da ka tieku ni. Ahin sin ni ti yiwee nnyɔ su n a.

eng: Then anyone whose name wasnt found written in the scroll of life was thrown into the fiery lake.
local: Na ba fa sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i duma awo má ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu aji sin ni.

eng: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth, for the former heaven and the former earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
local: Na ma ŋu aŋgoro fɔfɔrɛ ni asiɛn fɔfɔrɛ, dama aŋgoro dawa n ni asiɛn dawa n kɛrɛ wo má bɛrɛ kun, na ma ŋu ka tieku nzue n gusu wo má bɛrɛ kun.

eng: I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, made ready as a bride beautifully dressed for her husband.
local: Na ma ŋu miɛ casi ni, Yɛrusalem fɔfɔrɛ n ka i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro sujura ba asiɛŋgu, wɔ sɛsɛ i ŋu su ka konya bo i sukɔ ajaa.

eng: I heard a loud voice from the throne say, Look! Gods dwelling is here with humankind. He will dwell with them, and they will be his peoples. God himself will be with them as their God.
local: Na ma ti akonvi bambaka be ŋga fɛmɛya biɛ n nu, i ŋa, Am niɛ fɛn, Nyɛmɛ woo­biri wo adamandem dɔ, na i wó bu dɔ. Na i fá bu yo i minɛm. Yiri ni i tii wó bu dɔ na i yó bu Nyɛmɛ a.

eng: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more. There will be no mourning, crying, or pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.
local: Yiri núnuka bu nyunvuin kɛrɛ bu nyu­mbaa nu a, yiwee wo má bɛrɛ kun, ahoresaaki wó má bɛrɛ kun, minɛm sún má kun, na minɛm dí má yaari kun, dama deke dawam kɛrɛ asin.

eng: Then the one seated on the throne said, Look! Im making all things new. He also said, Write this down, for these words are trustworthy and true.
local: Na bo i tana fɛmɛya biɛ n su n ase ka, Niɛ fɛn, m suyo deke kɛrɛ fɔfɔrɛ. Na wɔ kaaki ase m ka, Kɛrɛ ahin nɔaniɛ ni, dama i ti nahɔrɛ wɔ, na i la dabuŋu.

eng: Then he said to me, All is done. I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will freely give water from the life-giving spring.
local: Na wɔ se m ka, Wɔ yo awie, mini ti mɔ­fɔ a, na m kaaki ti kasin­fɔ, mini ti deke kɛrɛ bumboori ni i awieeri a. Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo nzuehɔɛ tiiri i, m má i nu nzue n bo i ma ŋgɔɔ ni, na i mi tɔ́ má kaari.

eng: Those who emerge victorious will inherit these things. I will be their God, and they will be my sons and daughters.
local: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i diiri nasara, m má i ahin aceerim ni, na m yó i Nyɛmɛ, na m fá i yo m wa.

eng: But for the cowardly, the faithless, the vile, the murderers, those who commit sexual immorality, those who use drugs and cast spells, the idolaters and all liars—their share will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur. This is the second death.
local: Ama, sɛrɛ­fɔm, ni minɛm bo ba yo má m yarada, ni daŋgba yo­fɔm, ni minɛ kun­fɔm, ni nbara kpini­fɔm, ni somaya kɔ­fɔm, ni bo bu di ayiri ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn, ni munzue­yi­fɔm, ni amɔɛ su­fɔm, ni kɔrɛ­fɔm kɛrɛ, bu woo­biri la cɛɛma sin n bo i je ka ferewita na i da ka tieku ni. Nyini yiwee ni ti yiwee nnyɔ su n a.

eng: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues spoke with me. Come, he said, I will show you the bride, the Lambs wife.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ kun aba m dɔ, i ti mɛrɛkɛ nsom bo bu bita awieeri su dɛɛ kekereke ayaba nsom nu kun wɔ, na wɔ ba ase m ka, Bra, na m kere u Bɔmbaa n konya ni.

eng: He took me in a Spirit-inspired trance to a great, high mountain, and he showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God.
local: Na Awiɛwiɛ Casi n dɛɛ yiko nu, na mɛrɛkɛ n afa m akɔ buka bambaka n bo i ti lenleŋge n tii su, na wɔ kere m Yɛrusalem miɛ kpiri n bo i ti casi ni, i fite Nyɛmɛ dɔ aŋgoro sujura ba.

eng: The city had Gods glory. Its brilliance was like a priceless jewel, like jasper that was as clear as crystal.
local: Na i je na i ta wein kabo Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa n ti ni, na i sujeje kɔkɔrɛ ni sosofura nzue ka afere nyunyumi barasu be bo i da cain ka niɛɛnu.

eng: It had a great high wall with twelve gates. By the gates were twelve angels, and on the gates were written the names of the twelve tribes of Israels sons.
local: Ba ta kobe bambaka bo i wo aŋgoro abara ayɛ miɛ ni, na i la anɔ buru ni nnyɔ, anɔm kɛrɛ su la mɛrɛkɛ kun kun bo bu jina ŋminda. Ba kɛrɛ Yisareli anɔ barasu buru ni nnyɔ n duma agɔ anɔ n kun kun kɛrɛ su.

eng: There were three gates on the east, three gates on the north, three gates on the south, and three gates on the west.
local: Kobe n bue nna n kɛrɛ la anɔ nsan nsan, nsan wo wiɛ afiiri, na nsan wo wiɛ atɔɔri, na nsan nsan wo gbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn bue nnyɔ n kɛrɛ.

eng: The city wall had twelve foundations, and on them were the twelve names of the Lambs twelve apostles.
local: Miɛ n kobe n ajaari see­wa yabuɛ n ti buru ni nnyɔ ɔ, ye bu nya taari kobe n taka su. Ba kɛrɛ Bɔmbaa n jaramasa buru ni nnyɔm duma agɔ yabuɛ n kun kun kɛrɛ su.

eng: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring rod with which to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n bo i jɔjɔɔri m dɔ n bita sika kara i saa nu, bo i fa sunsun niɛ kabo miɛ n ti bambaka barasu, ni kabo kobem n anɔm ti tɛtɛrɛ barasu, na i kaaki sunsun niɛ kabo kobe n aŋgoro ti barasu.

eng: Now the city was laid out as a square. Its length was the same as its width. He measured the city with the rod, and it was fifteen hundred miles. Its length and width and height were equal.
local: Miɛ n ataari n ti ka sɔɔ laŋgba dɛɛ su wɔ, i tintiin, ni i tɛtɛrɛ kɛrɛ sɛ wɔ. Na mɛrɛkɛ n afa i sika kara sunsuun­wa n asunsun miɛ ni, i tintiin su ti kilo akpii nnyɔ ni yaa nna wɔ, nyini ti, i tintiin, ni i tɛtɛrɛ, ni i aŋgoro kɛrɛ sɛ wɔ.

eng: He also measured the thickness of its wall. It was two hundred sixteen feet thick, as a person—or rather, an angel—measures things.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n asunsun kobe n gusu, kabo adamande bo ajaari ni, na i aŋgoro akɔ ayasu ka ajaari boo­wa kun kun kɔ ju yaa ni abura­na ni nna.

eng: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was pure gold, like pure glass.
local: Bu faari yabuɛ nyunyumi barasu be bo bu fere i ka jasapa ni taari kobe n a, na ba fa sika bo i da cain ka niɛɛnu, ata miɛ n ni i tii.

eng: The city walls foundations were decorated with every kind of jewel. The first foundation was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was chalcedony, and the fourth was emerald.
local: Yabuɛ n bo bu fa seeri miɛ n kobe n ajaari n ti yabuɛ barasu nyunyumi, na i kɛrɛ busu wo i ŋgumi ŋgumi. I ti kabo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ kpiɛn n sana i tanlɛɛ su na i wo i hoe su ni. Bu fere bu ka jasapa, ni safiri, ni agata, ni ɛmɛradi.

eng: The fifth was sardonyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, and the eighth was beryl. The ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
local: ni oniki, ni caniliya, ni krisolite, ni berili, ni topasi, ni krisoparasi, ni jasinti, ni amɛtisiti.

eng: The twelve gates were twelve pearls; each one of the gates was made from a single pearl. And the citys main street was pure gold, as transparent as glass.
local: Bu faari afere fofoe nyunyumi barasu be fa yoori anɔ buru ni nnyɔm n a. Bu faari sika fa yoori miɛ n atin natii­wa kɛrɛ a, na i da cain ka niɛɛnu.

eng: I didnt see a temple in the city, because its temple is the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb.
local: Ma ŋu má Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru be miɛ n nu, dama ya Mi Nyɛmɛ Fɛmɛ Kpiri n ni Bɔmbaa n ade Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n jinaa­biri a.

eng: The city doesnt need the sun or the moon to shine on it, because Gods glory is its light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
local: Miɛ n koro má wiɛ ni sara na bu ta wein ma i, dama Nyɛmɛ jirima woo­wa ni ti i wein a, na Bɔmbaa n ti i fitana.

eng: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
local: Na durunya n nu adamande barasu kɛrɛ sunati i wein nu wɔ, na durunya n nu nfɛmɛm fa bu ajɛkɛ kɛrɛ ba wura nu.

eng: Its gates will never be shut by day, and there will be no night there.
local: Miɛ n anɔ ni téke wɔ saŋga kɛrɛ, dama ka kɔŋguɛ wo má dɔ.

eng: They will bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.
local: Na adamande barasu kɛrɛ bo bu wo durunya n nu ni fá bu neŋge kpakpam ni bu jirima na bu fá wura nu.

eng: Nothing unclean will ever enter it, nor anyone who does what is vile and deceitful, but only those who are registered in the Lambs scroll of life.
local: Ama, deke kɛrɛ bo i ti má casi wúra má miɛ n nu, ni minɛm bo bu su amɔɛm, ni minɛm bo bu bu kɔrɛm ni. Má ka minɛm bo bu duma wo Bɔmbaa ni ŋgɔɔ kadasi n nu ni ŋgumi wúra nu a.

eng: Then the angel showed me the river of life-giving water, shining like crystal, flowing from the throne of God and the Lamb.
local: Na mɛrɛkɛ n akaaki akere m kunma nzue bo i ma ŋgɔɔ, i da cain kabo niɛɛnu ti n wɔ, i soŋgi fite Nyɛmɛ ni Bɔmbaa n fɛmɛya biɛ n nu wɔ.

eng: through the middle of the citys main street. On each side of the river is the tree of life, which produces twelve crops of fruit, bearing its fruit each month. The trees leaves are for the healing of the nations.
